# GENESIS

## Chapter 1

1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

2 The earth was without form and void, and darkness was over the face of the deep, and the spirit of God was hovering over the face of the waters.

3 And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

4 And God saw the light that it was good, and God separated between the light and between the darkness.

5 God called the light Day, and the darkness Night; and there was evening and there was morning, one day.

6 God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it separate between waters and waters.

7 God made the firmament and divided between the waters which were under the firmament and between the waters which were above the firmament and it was so.

8 God called the firmament heavens, and there was evening and there was morning, a second day.

9 God said, Let the waters under the heavens be gathered to one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

10 God called the dry land earth, and the gathering of the waters he called seas, and God saw that it was good.

11 God said, Let the land bring forth tender grass, herb yielding seed according to its kind, and tree bearing fruit whose seed is in it upon the land, and it was so.

12 The earth brought forth tender grass, herb yielding seed according to its kind, and tree making fruit whose seed is in it according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.

13 And there was evening and there was morning, day three.

14 And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heavens to divide between the day and the night; and let them be for signs and for appointed seasons and for days and years.

15 And they were for lights in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth and it was so.

16 God made the two great lights: the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night, and the stars.

17 And God gave them in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth.

18 And to rule during the day and during the night season, and to separate between the light and the darkness; and God saw that it was good.

19 And there was evening, and there was morning, day four.

20 God said, Let the waters swarm with swarming creatures of living soul, and let birds fly across the face of the firmament of the heavens over the earth.

21 God created the great sea-monsters and every living creature that moves, with which the waters swarmed according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.

22 And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the land.

23 And there was evening and there was morning, the fifth day.

24 And God said, Let the earth bring forth a living creature according to its kind, cattle and that which creeps and a wild beast of the earth according to its kind, and it was so.

25 God made the living creatures of the land according to their kind, and the cattle according to their kind, and every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.

26 God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over every thing upon the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

27 God created the human in his image, in the image of God he created him, male and female he created them.

28 And God blessed them, and God said to them, Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it, and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the bird of the heavens and over every living creature that moves upon the earth.

29 And God said, Behold, I have given to you every herb seeding seed which upon the face of all the earth, and every tree in which fruit of a tree seeding seed; to you it shall be for food.

30 And to every living creature of the earth, and to every bird of the heavens, and to all the creeping things upon the earth, in which is a living soul, I have given every green herb for food, and it was so.

31 And God saw all that was made, and indeed it was very good, and there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.


## Chapter 2

1 And the heavens and the earth and all their host were completed.

2 And God ceased at the day the seventh from the work which he had made, and rested at the day the seventh from all the work which he had made.

3 And God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it, because in it he rested from all his work which God had created to make.

4 These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made earth and heavens.

5 And all manner of shrub of the field not yet being in the land, and all manner of grass of the field not yet growing, because the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the ground, and there was no man to till the soil.

6 But a mist would rise from the earth and water the whole face of the ground.

7 And Jehovah God formed the man of dust from the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living soul.

8 And Jehovah God planted a garden in the east, and there he placed the man whom he had formed.

9 And the LORD God caused to spring up from the ground every tree desirable to look upon and good for food, and the tree of life in the middle of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

10 And a river issues from Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides and becomes four bands.

11 The name of the first is Pison; it is the one that encircles all the land of Havilah, where there is gold.

12 And the gold of that land is good; there is bdellium and onyx stone.

13 And the name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one encircling all the land of Cush.

14 And the name of the third river is Hiddekel, which goes eastward of Assyria, and the fourth river is the Euphrates.

15 And Jehovah God took the man and placed him in the garden of Eden to work it and to keep it.

16 And Jehovah, the Lord, God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou shalt freely eat.

17 But from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat of it, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.

18 And the LORD God said, It is not good for the man to be alone; I will make him a help as his counterpart.

19 And the LORD God formed from the ground every living creature of the field and every fowl of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call to it, and whatever the man would call to it, that would be the name of the living being.

20 The man named all the cattle, and the birds of the air, and every living creature of the field; but for the man, he did not find a help corresponding to him.

21 Then the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh underneath.

22 And the LORD God built the rib that he had taken from the man into a woman, and brought her to the man.

23 The man said, This now is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called woman, because from man this was taken.

24 Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and clings to his wife, and they become one flesh.

25 And the man and his wife were both naked, and they were not ashamed.


## Chapter 3

1 Now the serpent was more crafty than any beast of the field that the LORD God had made; and he said to the woman, "Indeed, did God say, 'You shall not eat from any tree of the garden'?"

2 And the woman said to the serpent, From the fruit of the tree of the garden we eat.

3 And from the fruit of the tree that is in the midst of the garden, God said, You shall not eat from it, and you shall not touch it, lest you die.

4 The serpent said to the woman, You will not surely die.

5 For God knows that in the day you eat from it your eyes will be opened and you will be like God knowing good and evil.

6 The woman saw that the tree was good for food and that it was a delight to the eyes and that the tree was desirable to make one wise, so she took of its fruit and ate, and she gave also to her husband with her, and he ate.

7 Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew that they were naked; so they sewed together fig leaves and made for themselves girdles.

8 And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden at the breeze of the day, and the man and his wife hid from the presence of the LORD God among the trees of the garden.

9 And the LORD God called to the man and said to him, Where are you?

10 And he said, I heard the sound of you in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked, and I hid myself.

11 And he said, Who told you that you are naked? Have you eaten from the tree from which I commanded you not to eat?

12 The man said, "The woman whom you gave to be with me, she gave me from the tree, and I ate."

13 The LORD God said to the woman, "What is this that you have done?" And the woman said, "The serpent deceived me, and I ate."

14 And the LORD God said to the serpent, Because you have done this, cursed are you above all the cattle and above all the wild beasts of the field; on your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.

15 And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your seed and her seed; he will strike your head, and you will strike his heel.

16 To the woman he said, "I will greatly multiply your toil and your conception; in sorrow you shall bring forth children; and your desire shall be to your husband, and he shall rule over you."

17 And to Adam he said, Because you listened to the voice of your wife, and ate from the tree about which I commanded you, saying, You shall not eat from it, cursed is the ground because of you; in sorrow you shall eat from it all the days of your life.

18 Thorn and thistle shall it yield to you, and you shall eat the herb of the field.

19 By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread until you return to the ground, for from it you were taken; for dust you are and to dust you shall return.

20 The man called the name of his wife Eve, because she was the mother of all living.

21 And Jehovah God made for the man and for his wife garments of skin and clothed them.

22 And the LORD God said, "Behold, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil; and now, lest he stretch out his hand and take also from the tree of life, and eat and live forever."

23 And the LORD God sent him forth from the garden of Eden to till the ground from whence he was taken.

24 And he drove out the man, and stationed at the east of the garden of Eden the cherubims and the flaming sword of the axe turning continually to guard the way of the tree of life.


## Chapter 4

1 The man knew Eve his wife, and she conceived and bore Cain, and said, I have acquired a man, the LORD.

2 And she again bore his brother Abel, and Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a worker of the ground.

3 After days, Cain brought some of the fruit of the ground as an offering to the LORD.

4 And Abel also brought from the firstlings of his flock and from their fat, and the LORD turned to Abel and his offering.

5 And to Cain and to his offering he did not look; so Cain was very angry, and his face fell.

6 The LORD said to Cain, "Why is your anger burned, and why has your countenance fallen?"

7 Is there not, if you do well, a raising up, and if you do not do well, at the entrance a sin offering lies crouching, and to you its desire, but you must rule over it.

8 Cain said to his brother Abel, and it happened when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel and killed him.

9 And the LORD said to Cain, Where is Abel your brother? And he said, I do not know; am I my brother's keeper?

10 And he said, What have you done? the voice of the blood of your brother is crying to me from the ground.

11 Now cursed are you from the ground which opened its mouth to receive the blood of your brother from your hand.

12 When you work the ground, it will no longer yield its strength to you; you will be a wanderer and fugitive on the earth.

13 And Cain said to the LORD, "My fault is too great to bear."

14 Behold, you have driven me today from upon the face of the ground and from your presence I shall be hidden, and I shall be a wanderer and fugitive on the earth, and it shall come to pass that whoever finds me will kill me.

15 And the LORD said to him, Therefore whoever kills Cain, sevenfold shall he be avenged; and the LORD set for Cain a mark, so that whoever found him would not strike him.

16 And Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and dwelt in the land of Nod east of Eden.

17 Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch, and he built a city and called the name of the city after the name of his son Enoch.

18 Enoch became the father of Irad, and Irad fathered Mehujael, and Mehujael fathered Methusael, and Methusael fathered Lamech.

19 Lamech took for himself two wives; the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other was Zillah.

20 Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the chief of those who dwell in tents and [own] livestock.

21 And the name of his brother was Jubal; he was the father of all who play the harp and organ.

22 And Zillah also gave birth to Tubal-cain, a forger of every kind of tool of brass and iron; and the sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.

23 Lamech said to his wives, Adah and Zillah: Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my speech. For I have killed a man for wounding me, and a boy for bruising me.

24 For sevenfold shall Cain be avenged, and for Lamech seventy and seven.

25 Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and called his name Seth, for God has granted me another seed in place of Abel, because Cain killed him.

26 And to Seth also, even to him, was born a son, and he called his name Enos; then began to call upon the name of the LORD.


## Chapter 5

1 This is the book of the generations of Adam: on the day that God created man, in the likeness of God He made him.

2 Male and female he created them, and he blessed them and called their name "person" on the day they were created.

3 And Adam lived one hundred and thirty years, and he begot a son in his similitude, according to his image; and he named his name Seth.

4 And the age of Adam after he fathered Seth was eight hundred years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

5 And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred years and thirty years and he died.

6 And Seth lived a hundred and five years, and then begot Enos.

7 And Seth lived after he begot Enos seven years and eight hundred years, and he begot sons and daughters.

8 And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years, and he died.

9 And Enos lived ninety years and became the father of Kenan.

10 And Enos lived after he begot Kenan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begot sons and daughters.

11 All the days of Enos were five hundred and nine years, and he died.

12 And Cainan lived seventy years and fathered Mahalaleel.

13 And Cainan lived after he begot Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and he begot sons and daughters.

14 All the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died.

15 And Mahalaleel lived five and sixty years and he begot Jared.

16 And Mahalaleel lived after he begot Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begot sons and daughters.

17 And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred and ninety-five years, and he died.

18 Jared lived two hundred and sixty-two years and begot Enoch.

19 And Jared lived after he fathered Enoch eight hundred years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

20 All the days of Jared were two hundred and sixty years and nine hundred years, and he died.

21 Enoch lived sixty-five years and became the father of Methuselah.

22 Enoch walked with God after he begot Methuselah, three hundred years, and he begot sons and daughters.

23 And all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.

24 Enoch walked with God, and he was not, for God took him.

25 Methuselah lived a hundred and eighty-seven years, and he fathered Lamech.

26 And Methuselah lived after he fathered Lamech two and eighty years and seven hundred years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

27 And all the days of Methuselah were nine and sixty years and nine hundred years, and he died.

28 And Lamech lived two and eighty years and one hundred years and he begot a son.

29 He called his name Noah, saying, "This one will bring us relief from our labor and the toil of our hands, from the ground which the LORD has cursed."

30 And Lamech lived after he fathered Noah five hundred and ninety-five years, and he fathered sons and daughters.

31 And all the days of Lamech were seven and seventy years and seven hundred years, and he died.

32 When Noah was five hundred years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.


## Chapter 6

1 And it came to pass when the common sort began to multiply upon the face of the land, that daughters were born to them.

2 The sons of God saw that the daughters of mankind were fair, and they took wives for themselves from all that they chose.

3 And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for indeed he is flesh; and his days shall be a hundred and twenty years.

4 The giants were on the land in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came to the daughters of man and bore children for them; these were the mighty men, who from ancient times were the men of renown.

5 And the LORD saw that the wickedness of humankind was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of their heart was only evil continually.

6 And Jehovah regretted that He had made the man on the earth, and He was grieved to His heart.

7 And the LORD said, I will blot out man whom I have created from upon the face of the ground, from man to beast, to the creeping thing, and to the fowl of the heavens; for I am grieved that I have made them.

8 Noah found favour in the eyes of the LORD.

9 These are the generations of Noah: Noah, a righteous man, was blameless in his generations; Noah walked with God.

10 Noah became the father of three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

11 The earth was ruined in the sight of God, and the earth was filled with violence.

12 And God saw the land, and behold, it was corrupted, for all flesh had corrupted its way upon the land.

13 And God said to Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me, for the earth is filled with violence because of them, and behold, I will destroy them with the earth.

14 Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and outside with pitch.

15 This is what you shall do to it: three hundred cubits the length of the ark, fifty cubits its breadth, and thirty cubits its height.

16 Make a window for the ark, and finish it one cubit above; and set the door of the ark in its side; make it with lower, second, and third stories.

17 And I, behold, I am bringing the flood of water upon the land to destroy all flesh in which is the spirit of life from upon the face of the earth; everything on the land shall perish.

18 I will establish my covenant with you, and you shall enter the ark—you, your sons, your wife, and your sons’ wives with you.

19 And from all living, from all flesh, two of each kind you shall bring to the ark to keep alive with you, male and female they shall be.

20 Of the bird according to its kind, and of the beast according to its kind, of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every kind shall come to you to keep alive.

21 And you, take for yourself from all food that may be eaten, and gather it to yourself, and it shall be for you and for them to eat.

22 Noah did according to all which God commanded him; so he did.


## Chapter 7

1 And the LORD said to Noah, Come thou and all thy family into the ark, for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.

2 Of every clean beast take for yourself seven pairs, male and female, and of the beasts that are not clean, two, male and female.

3 Also of birds of the air, seven and seven, male and female, to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.

4 For yet seven days and I will send rain upon the land forty days and forty nights, and I will blot out every living substance that I have made from upon the face of the ground.

5 Noah did according to all that the LORD commanded him.

6 Noah was six hundred years old when the flood came, waters upon the land.

7 Noah and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him entered the ark because of the waters of the flood.

8 Of the clean beast, and of the beast which is not clean, and of the bird, and of every thing that creepeth upon the ground:

9 Two by two they came to Noah to the ark, male and female, as God commanded Noah.

10 And it came to pass at the end of seven days, that the waters of the flood were upon the earth.

11 In the year six hundred of Noah's life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on that day all the fountains of the great deep were broken open, and the windows of the heavens were opened.

12 And the rain was upon the land forty days and forty nights.

13 On the very day Noah and Shem and Ham and Japheth, the sons of Noah, and the wife of Noah and the three wives of his sons with them, entered the ark.

14 They and every living creature according to its kind, and all the cattle according to its kind, and every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth according to its kind, and every bird according to its kind, all birds of every wing.

15 And they came to Noah into the ark, two and two of all body in which was breath of life.

16 And they came, male and female of every kind of flesh, as God had commanded him, and the LORD closed behind him.

17 The flood was forty days upon the earth, and the waters increased and lifted up the ark, and it rose above the earth.

18 The waters increased and multiplied greatly upon the earth, and the ark went on the face of the waters.

19 And the water was very, very high over the earth, and covered every high mountain that was under all the heavens.

20 Fifteen cubits upward the waters increased, and they covered the mountains.

21 And all flesh that moved upon the earth perished, the birds, the cattle, the living creatures, and every creeping thing that crept upon the earth, and all humankind.

22 Every thing in which the breath of the spirit of life was in its nostrils, from all that was on the dry land, died.

23 And he blotted out the living substance from upon the face of the ground, from man to cattle, to that which creepeth and to birds of the air, and they were blotted out from off the earth, and only Noah remained, and those who were with him in the ark.

24 And the waters prevailed upon the land a hundred and fifty days.


## Chapter 8

1 God remembered Noah and every living creature and all the cattle that were with him in the ark, and God caused a wind to pass over the earth and the waters subsided.

2 The fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were stopped up, and the rain was restrained from the heavens.

3 The waters returned from upon the land continually, and the waters decreased at the end of one hundred and fifty days.

4 The ark came to rest in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, upon the mountains of Ararat.

5 And the waters were receding continually until the tenth month; on the first of the tenth month the mountain tops became visible.

6 After forty days Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.

7 And he sent out the raven, and it went out and returned until the water had dried up from upon the earth.

8 Then he sent out the dove from him to see whether the waters had diminished from the surface of the ground.

9 The dove did not find a place of rest for the sole of her foot, so she returned to him to the ark, for water was over the face of the whole earth; and he stretched out his hand, took her, and brought her to himself into the ark.

10 And he waited again seven days and again sent the dove from the ark.

11 And the dove came in to him at evening time, and behold, a leaf of olive torn off was in her mouth; and Noah knew that the waters had abated from upon the land.

12 And he waited yet another seven days and sent the dove, and she did not return to him again.

13 In the six hundredth year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, the waters receded from upon the earth, and Noah removed the covering of the ark and saw, and behold, the surface of the ground was dry.

14 And in the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.

15 And God spoke to Noah, saying:

16 Go out from the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.

17 Every living creature with you, of all flesh, in the birds, in cattle, and in every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth, shall be with you; they shall swarm upon the earth, and they shall be fruitful and multiply upon the earth.

18 Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and the wives of his sons with him.

19 All the living creatures, all the creeping things, and all the birds—every creature that moves upon the land, according to their families—went out from the ark.

20 Noah built an altar to the LORD, and he took from all the clean cattle and from all the clean birds and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

21 And the LORD smelled the savour of the sweet odour, and the LORD said in his mind, I will not again curse the ground because of the man; for the imagination of the heart of the man is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite every living thing as I have done.

22 As long as all the days of the earth, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall not cease.


## Chapter 9

1 And God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth.

2 Your dread and your fear shall be upon every living creature of the earth, and upon all the birds of the heavens, in everything that moves upon the ground and in all the fish of the sea; they are delivered into your hand.

3 Every creeping thing that is alive for you shall be for food; as the green grass I have given you all.

4 But flesh, with its life, its blood, you shall not eat.

5 But your blood-guiltiness I will require, from the hand of every living creature I will require it, and from the hand of man, from the hand of each his brother, I will require the life of man.

6 Whoever sheds the blood of a person, by a person will their blood be shed, for in the image of God he made the person.

7 And you, be fruitful and multiply, swarm in the land and multiply in it.

8 And God said to Noah and to his sons with him:

9 But I, behold, I am establishing my covenant with you, and with your seed after you.

10 And with every living soul that is with you, among the birds, among the livestock, and among all the wild animals of the earth with you, from all that went out of the ark, to all the wild animals of the earth.

11 I will confirm my covenant with you, and all flesh will no longer be cut off by the waters of the flood, and there will no longer be a flood to destroy the land.

12 And God said, This is the sign of the covenant which I give between me and you, and between all living soul that is with you, for everlasting generations.

13 My bow in the cloud I have set, and it shall be for a sign of the covenant between me and the land.

14 And it shall be, when I bring a cloud over the land, the bow shall be seen in the cloud.

15 And I will remember my covenant between me and you and between me and every living creature in all flesh, and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy every body.

16 And the bow shall be in the cloud, and I will see it to remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature, all flesh that is upon the earth.

17 And God said to Noah, This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between me and all flesh which is on the earth.

18 The sons of Noah who went out from the ark were Sem, Ham, and Japheth, and Ham was the father of Canaan.

19 Three of these were the sons of Noah, and from these were dispersed all the earth.

20 Noah, a certain man of the land, began and planted vines.

21 He drank of the wine and became drunk, and he was uncovered within the tent.

22 Ham, father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father and told his two brothers outside.

23 And Shem and Japheth took the garment, and laid it upon the shoulder of the two of them, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father, and their faces were backward, and the nakedness of their father they did not see.

24 Noah awoke from his wine-bibbing and knew what his younger son had done to him.

25 And he said, Cursed be Canaan, a servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.

26 And he said, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem, and let Canaan be a servant to them."

27 May God enlarge Japheth, and may he dwell in the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be a servant to him.

28 Noah lived after the flood three hundred years and fifty years.

29 All the days of Noah were nine hundred years and fifty years and he died.


## Chapter 10

1 These are the generations of the sons of Noah: Shem, Ham, and Japheth; sons were born to them after the flood.

2 The sons of Japheth: Gomer and Magog and Madai and Javan and Tubal and Meshech and Tiras.

3 And the sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz and Riphath and Togarmah.

4 The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Chittim, and Dodanim.

5 From these the islands of the nations were divided in their lands, each according to his language, by their families among their peoples.

6 The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.

7 The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raamah, and Sabtecha; and the sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.

8 And Cush became the father of Nimrod; he was the first to be a mighty one on the earth.

9 He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, "Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD."

10 The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

11 From that land Asshur went forth and built Nineveh, and Rehoboth city, and Calah.

12 And Resen, between Nineveh and Calah, that is the great city.

13 And Mizraim became the father of the Ludim, and of the Anamim, and of the Lehabim, and of the Naptuhim.

14 And the Pathrusim, and the Casluhim (from whom came the Philistines), and the Caphthorim.

15 And Canaan begot Sidon his eldest son and Heth.

16 and the Jebusite and the Amorite and the Girgashite

17 and the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite.

18 And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite; and afterward the families of the Canaanite were crushed.

19 And the border of the Canaanite was from Sidon, going toward Gerar as far as Gaza, going toward Sodom and Gomorrah and Admah and Zeboiim as far as Lasha.

20 These are the sons of Ham, according to their families, by their languages, in their lands, among their nations.

21 And to Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder, even to him children were born.

22 The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.

23 The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.

24 Arphaxad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.

25 To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and the name of his brother was Joktan.

26 Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, and Jerah.

27 and Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah.

28 and Obal and Abimael and Sheba.

29 And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were sons of Joktan.

30 And their dwelling-place was from Mesha, as far as Sephar, a mountain of the east.

31 These are the children of Shem by their families according to their languages in their lands among their nations.

32 These are the families of the sons of Noah according to their generations among their nations, and from these the nations were divided on the earth after the flood.


## Chapter 11

1 And the whole earth was one language and the same words.

2 And it came to pass when they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.

3 And they said, one to his fellow, Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly; and the brick served them for stone, and the slime served them for mortar.

4 Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered over the face of all the earth.

5 And the LORD went down to see the city and the tower which the sons of man built.

6 And Jehovah said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; this is the beginning of what they will do; and now nothing will be restrained from them of all that they have purposed to do.

7 Come, let us go down and confuse there their language, so that no one will understand the speech of his companion.

8 And the LORD scattered them from there over the face of all the land, and they ceased building the city.

9 Therefore he named it Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth, and from there the LORD scattered them over the face of all the earth.

10 These are the generations of Shem: Shem, being one hundred years old, became the father of Arphaxad two years after the flood.

11 Sem, Shem lived five hundred years after he became the father of Arphaxad, and he fathered sons and daughters.

12 Arphaxad lived thirty-five years and became the father of Salah.

13 And Arphaxad lived after he fathered Salah four hundred and three years and fathered sons and daughters.

14 Salah was thirty years old when he became the father of Eber.

15 And Shelah lived after he fathered Eber three years and four hundred years and he fathered sons and daughters.

16 And Eber lived thirty and four years, and he begot Peleg.

17 And Eber lived after he begot Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begot sons and daughters.

18 And Peleg lived thirty years and begot Reu.

19 And Peleg lived after he fathered Reu nine years and two hundred years and begot sons and daughters.

20 Reu lived thirty-two years and became the father of Serug.

21 Reu lived after he fathered Serug seven years and two hundred years and he fathered sons and daughters.

22 Serug lived thirty years and became the father of Nahor.

23 Serug lived two hundred years after he fathered Nahor, and he fathered sons and daughters.

24 And Nahor lived twenty-nine years and became the father of Terah.

25 And Nahor lived after he begot Terah nine hundred and eighteen years, and begot sons and daughters.

26 Tarah lived seventy years and became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

27 These are the generations of Terah: Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran became the father of Lot.

28 Haran died in the presence of Terah his father, in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldeans.

29 Abram and Nahor took wives for themselves; the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah, daughter of Haran, father of Milcah and father of Iscah.

30 And Sarai was barren; she had no child.

31 Tarah took Abram his son and Lot the son of Haran the son of his son and Sarai his daughter-in-law the wife of Abram his son and they went out with them from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan and they came to Haran and settled there.

32 And the age of Terah was two hundred and five years, and Terah died in Haran.


## Chapter 12

1 The Lord said to Abram, Go for yourself from your land and from your kindred and from the house of your father to the land that I will show you.

2 I will make you a great nation, and I will bless you, and I will make your name renowned, and you will be a blessing.

3 I will bless those who bless you, and I will curse those who curse you, and in you all families of the land shall be blessed.

4 Abram went as Jehovah had spoken to him, and Lot went with him; Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.

5 Abram took Sarai his wife and Lot the son of his brother and all their goods that they had acquired and the persons that they had made in Haran, and they went out to go to the land of Canaan and they came to the land of Canaan.

6 Abram passed through the land to the place of Shechem, to the plain of Moreh, and the Canaanite was then in the land.

7 And the LORD appeared to Abram and said, To your seed I give this land, and he built there an altar to the LORD who appeared to him.

8 And he moved from there to the hill country east of Beth-el and pitched his tent, Beth-el being on the west and Ai on the east; and he built there an altar to the LORD and called upon the name of the LORD.

9 Abram journeyed on, going toward the south country.

10 And there was a famine in the land, and Abram went down to Egypt to sojourn there because the famine was severe in the land.

11 When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife, "Indeed, I now know that you are a woman of beautiful appearance."

12 And it shall be when the Egyptians see you, they will say, “His wife is this woman,” and they will kill me but let you live.

13 Say now, I pray thee, Thou art my sister: that it may be well with me for thy sake; and that my soul may live because of thee.

14 When Abram came to Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.

15 And the captains of Pharaoh saw her and praised her to Pharaoh, and the woman was taken to the house of Pharaoh.

16 And the LORD did good to Abram because of her, and there was to him sheep, and beeve, and (he) asses, and man-servants, and maid-servants, and (she) asses, and camels.

17 The LORD struck Pharaoh with great plagues and his household because of Sarai, Abram's wife.

18 Pharaoh called to Abram and said, "What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she is your wife?"

19 Why did you say she was your sister, so that I took her for my wife? Now therefore, here is your wife; take her and go.

20 Pharaoh commanded men concerning him, and they sent him and his wife and all that he had.


## Chapter 13

1 Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife and all that he had, and Lot with him, to the south country.

2 And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.

3 And he journeyed from the south as far as Beth-el, to the place where he had been at the beginning, between Beth-el and Ai.

4 to the place of the altar which he had made there at first and there Abram called in the name of the LORD

5 And also to Lot who was going with Abram there was small cattle, herd, and tent.

6 And the land could not support them to dwell together, for their possessions were great, so that they were unable to dwell together.

7 And there was strife between the herdsmen of Abram’s herd and the herdsmen of Lot’s herd, and the Canaanite and the Perizzite were then dwelling in the land.

8 Abram said to Lot, "Let there be no provocation between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen, for we are brothers."

9 Is not the whole land before you? Please separate from me: if you take the left, then I will go to the right; and if you take the right, then I will go to the left.

10 Lot lifted his eyes and saw all the plain of the Jordan, that it was entirely watered, before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, toward Zoar.

11 Lot chose for himself all the plain of the Jordan, and Lot journeyed eastward, and they separated, each from his brother.

12 Abram stayed in the land of Canaan, and Lot stayed in the cities of the plain and pitched his tent as far as Sodom.

13 And the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners against the LORD exceedingly.

14 The LORD said to Abram after Lot had parted from him, "Now lift up your eyes from where you are and look northward and southward and eastward and westward."

15 For all the land that you see I will give to you and to your seed forever.

16 I will make your offspring like the dust of the earth, so that if anyone can count the dust of the earth, then your offspring can also be counted.

17 Arise, walk through the land, its length and its breadth, for I will give it to you.

18 Abram journeyed and came and dwelt by the plain of Mamre which is in Hebron, and he built there an altar to the LORD.


## Chapter 14

1 In the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations.

2 They fought a battle against Bera king of Sodom and against Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, that is, Zoar.

3 All these joined together toward the Valley of Siddim, that is, the Salt Sea.

4 Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and thirteen years they rebelled.

5 In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer came and the kings who were with him, and they struck the Rephaim in Ashtoreth Karnaim, and the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaveh Kiriathaim.

6 and the Horites in their hill country of Seir, as far as El-paran, which is by the wilderness.

7 Then they turned and went to En-mishpat, which is Kadesh, and struck all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorite who lived in Hazezon-tamar.

8 The king of Sodom and the king of Gomorrah and the king of Admah and the king of Zeboiim and the king of Bela, that is Zoar, went out and engaged them in battle in the valley of Siddim.

9 Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of the Gentiles, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar—four kings against the five.

10 And the valley of Siddim, which was full of pits of slime, so the king of Sodom and Gomorrah fled and fell there, and the remaining survivors fled to the hill country.

11 They took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food, and they went away.

12 They took Lot and his goods, the nephew of Abram, and went away, and he was dwelling in Sodom.

13 The fugitive came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was dwelling at the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner, and they were confederates of Abram.

14 Abram heard that his brother had been taken captive, and he led out his trained home-born men, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued as far as Dan.

15 He divided against them by night, he and his servants, and struck them and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is on the left of Damascus.

16 And he brought back all the goods, and also his brother Lot and his goods, and also the women and the people.

17 And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from striking Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, to the dale of Shaveh, that is, the dale of the king.

18 And Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine, and he was priest to God Most High.

19 And he blessed him and said, "Blessed be Abram to God Most High, possessor of heaven and earth."

20 And blessed be God Most High, who delivered your enemies into your hand, and gave him a tenth of everything.

21 And the king of Sodom said to Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods for yourself.

22 Abram said to the king of Sodom, "I have raised my hand to the LORD, God Most High, possessor of heaven and earth."

23 If a thread and to a shoe-latchet, and if I take anything of all that is yours, then you shall not say, I have made Abram rich.

24 Except that the boys ate, and the portion of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre—they shall take their portion.


## Chapter 15

1 After these acts, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision, saying, Do not fear, Abram, I am a shield to you, your reward is very great.

2 Abram said, (my) Lord God, what will you give me, seeing I go childless, and the steward of my household is Eliezer of Damascus?

3 Then Abram said, "You have given me no child, and here, a servant born in my household will inherit me."

4 And behold, the word of the LORD came to him, saying, "This one will not inherit you; but he who will come forth from your belly—he will inherit you."

5 And he brought him forth abroad and said: 'Look now toward the heavens, and count the stars, if thou art able to count them'; and he said unto him: 'So shall thy seed be.'

6 And he believed in the LORD, and he accounted it to him as justice.

7 And he said to him, I am the LORD who brought you out from Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to inherit it.

8 And he said, Lord GOD, by what shall I know that I will inherit it?

9 And he said to him, Take for me a three-year-old heifer, and a three-year-old she goat, and a three-year-old ram, and a turtle dove, and a young pigeon.

10 And he took for himself all these and cut them in the middle and placed each piece opposite its fellow, but the bird he did not cut.

11 And the ravenous bird descended upon the carcases, and Abram drove them away.

12 And the sun was going down, and deep sleep fell upon Abram, and behold, a dread, great darkness fell upon him.

13 And he said to Abram, "Know surely that your seed will be an alien in a land not theirs, and they will serve them and afflict them four hundred years."

14 And also the nation that they serve I will judge, and afterward they will go out with great riches.

15 And you will go to your fathers in peace, buried in good gray hairs.

16 And in the fourth generation they shall return here, for the iniquity of the Amorite is not yet complete here.

17 And the sun was coming in and the darkness was descending, and behold, a smoking furnace and a flaming torch passing between these pieces.

18 On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, "To your seed I have given this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates."

19 the Kenite, the Kenezite, and the Kadmonite.

20 and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaim.

21 and the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Girgashite, and the Jebusite.


## Chapter 16

1 Sarai, Abram’s wife, had borne him no children, and she had an Egyptian maidservant whose name was Hagar.

2 And Sarai said to Abram, "Behold now, the LORD has restrained me from bearing; go in to my handmaid, perhaps I may be built up from her." And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.

3 Sarai, wife of Abram, took Hagar the Egyptian, her bondwoman, at the end of ten years of Abram's dwelling in the land of Canaan, and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.

4 And he came to Hagar, and she conceived; and she saw that she had conceived, and her lady was lightly esteemed in her eyes.

5 And Sarai said to Abram, "My wrong is upon you: I gave my handmaid into your bosom, and she saw that she had conceived, and I became light in her eyes; may the LORD judge between me and you."

6 Abram said to Sarai, "Here, your bondwoman is in your power; do to her what seems best in your eyes." But Sarai afflicted her, and she fled from her presence.

7 And the ambassador of the LORD found her by the fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain on the way to Shur.

8 And he said, "Hagar, bondwoman of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?" And she said, "I am fleeing from the presence of my lady Sarai."

9 The angel of the LORD said to her, Return to your mistress and submit yourself under her authority.

10 And the LORD's messenger said to her, I will greatly multiply your offspring, and they shall not be numbered for multitude.

11 The angel of the LORD said to her, "You are with child and will bear a son; you shall name his name Ishmael, because the LORD has paid heed to your affliction."

12 And he will be a wild man, his hand against everyone, and everyone's hand against him, and he will dwell in the presence of all his brothers.

13 And she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, "You are God of my gazing," saying, "Have I not even here seen after he saw me?"

14 Therefore he called the well Beer-lahai-roi; behold, it is between Kadesh and Bered.

15 Hagar bore a son to Abram, and Abram named the son whom Hagar bore Ishmael.

16 And Abram was eighty years and six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to Abram.


## Chapter 17

1 Abram was ninety years old and nine years when the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, I am God Almighty, walk before me and be blameless.

2 I will place my covenant between me and you, and I will greatly increase you.

3 Abram fell on his face, and God spoke to him, saying:

4 I am about to make my covenant with you, and you shall become the father of a multitude of nations.

5 You shall no longer be named Abram, but your name shall be Abraham, for I have made you a chief of a multitude of nations.

6 And I will greatly multiply you, and give you to nations, and kings will come forth from you.

7 I will confirm my covenant between me and you and your seed after you throughout their generations, a covenant for alway, to be your God and the God of your seed after you.

8 I will give to you and to your seed after you the land of your sojournings, all the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession, and I will be their God.

9 And God said to Abraham, You shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.

10 This is the covenant that you shall keep between me and you and your seed after you: every male among you shall be circumcised.

11 And you shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.

12 And on the eighth day every male among you shall be circumcised, whether born in the house or purchased with silver from any foreigner who is not of your offspring.

13 Every home-born child of your household and every (he that is) bought with your money shall surely be circumcised: my covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant.

14 And any uncircumcised male who will not be circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin, that person shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.

15 And God said to Abraham, "Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, for Sarah is her name."

16 I will bless her, and I will also give you a son from her, and I will bless her, and she will become nations; kings of peoples will come from her.

17 Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said in his heart, "Shall a child be born to one of a hundred years, or shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, give birth?"

18 Abraham said to God, "O that Ishmael might live before you."

19 God said, "But Sarah your wife will bear a son for you, and you will name his name Isaac, and I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his seed after him."

20 As for Ishmael, I have heard you: indeed, I will bless him, and I will multiply him, and I will increase him exceedingly; twelve chiefs he will father, and I will make him a great nation.

21 And I will confirm my covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear to you at this appointed time next year.

22 And he ceased speaking with him, and God went up from above Abraham.

23 Abraham took Ishmael his son and all the home-born children of his house and all the purchased possession of his silver, every male among the men of Abraham's household, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins on the very same day, as God had spoken to him.

24 Abraham was ninety-nine years old when his foreskin was circumcised.

25 And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when they circumcised him in the flesh of his foreskin.

26 On the very day, Abraham was circumcised, and Ishmael his son.

27 And all the men of his household, those born in the house and those purchased with silver from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.


## Chapter 18

1 And Jehovah appeared to him in the plains of Mamre, and he was sitting at the entrance of the tent in the heat of the day.

2 He lifted his eyes and saw, and behold, three men were standing over him, and he saw and ran to meet them from the entrance of the tent and bowed to the ground.

3 And he said, O lord, if now I have found favour in your eyes, do not please pass away from your servant.

4 Please take a little water, wash your feet, and recline under the tree.

5 And I will take a piece of bread, and you shall eat and be comforted, after you pass over, for on this account you have passed over upon your servant; and they said, Do so, as you have spoken.

6 Abraham hurried to the tent to Sarah and said, "Hurry, three measures of fine flour! Knead and make cakes upon the hearth."

7 And Abraham ran to the beeve, and took a young calf, tender and good, and gave it to the servant, and he hastened to prepare it.

8 He took butter and cheese and milk and the young calf that he had prepared, and set it before them; and he himself stood by them under the tree, and they ate.

9 And they said to him, Where is Sarah your wife? and he said, Behold, in the tent.

10 And he said, I will return to you at the appointed time, and behold, Sarah your wife shall have a son; and Sarah was listening at the entrance of the tent, and he was behind him.

11 And Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; Sarah had ceased to have the manner of women.

12 Sarah laughed inwardly, saying, "After I have grown old, shall pleasure come to me again, and my lord is advanced in years?"

13 And the LORD said to Abraham, Why did Sarah laugh, saying, Shall I indeed bear a child, when I am old?

14 Shall anything be too hard for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, according to this season, and Sarah shall have a son.

15 Sarah denied it, saying, "I did not laugh," for she was afraid; and he said, "No, but you did laugh."

16 Then the men rose up from there and looked toward the face of Sodom, and Abraham was walking with them to send them off.

17 And the LORD said, Shall I hide [anything] from Abraham?

18 And Abraham shall become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him.

19 For I have known him, so that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing justice and judgment, in order that the LORD may bring upon Abraham what He has spoken concerning him.

20 And the LORD said, "The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and their sin is very grievous."

21 Let me go down now and see whether, according to the cry coming to me, they have altogether consumed, or if not, I will know.

22 Then the men turned from there and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the LORD.

23 Abraham came forward and said, "Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?"

24 Perhaps there are fifty righteous within the city; will You indeed sweep away and not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous who are in the midst of it?

25 Far be it from You to do such a thing, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be as the wicked—far be it from You! Shall the Judge of all the earth not do justice?

26 And the LORD said, If I find in Sodom fifty righteous within the city, then I will spare the whole place because of them.

27 Abraham answered and said, "Behold now, I have ventured to speak to (my) Lord, though I am dust and ashes."

28 Perhaps the fifty righteous will lack five; wilt thou destroy the whole city for lack of five? And he said, I will not destroy it, if I find there forty and five.

29 And he added yet again to speak to him, and said, Perhaps there will be found there forty; and he said, I will not do it because of the forty.

30 And he said, Please do not let (my) Lord be angry, and I will speak: perhaps thirty will be found there. And he said, I will not do it if I find thirty there.

31 And he said, Behold now, I have ventured to speak to my Lord; perhaps twenty may be found there. And he said, I will not destroy because of the twenty.

32 And he said, Please do not be angry, (my) Lord, and I will speak only this once: perhaps ten will be found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for the sake of the ten.

33 And the LORD went as soon as He had finished speaking to Abraham, and Abraham returned to his place.


## Chapter 19

1 And the two ambassadors came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting at the gate of Sodom. And Lot saw them, and he rose up to meet them, and he bowed his face to the ground.

2 And he said, "Behold now, my lord, please turn aside to the house of your servant and lodge, and wash your feet; then you may rise early and go on your way." But they said, "No, we will lodge in the broad place."

3 And he urged them greatly, and they turned to him, and they came to his house, and he prepared for them a feast and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.

4 Before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people from every quarter, surrounded the house.

5 And they called to Lot and said to him, Where are the men who came to you this night? Bring them out to us, and we will know them.

6 Then Lot went out to them at the door, and the two-leaved door he shut behind him.

7 And he said, "Please, brother, do not harm."

8 Behold now, I have two daughters who have not known a man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as is good in your sight, only to these men do nothing, because on account of this they have come under the shade of my roof.

9 And they said, “Step aside; now this one has come to sojourn, and he acts as judge. Now we will treat you worse than them.” And they pressed hard against the man, even Lot, exceedingly, and they drew near to break the door.

10 Then the men reached out their hands and brought Lot to them into the house, and they shut the door.

11 And they struck the men who were at the entrance of the house with blindness, from small to great, and they wearied of finding the door.

12 The men said to Lot, “Whom else do you have here? Son in law, your sons, your daughters, and all that belong to you in the city—bring them out of this place.”

13 For we are destroying this country, because their cry has become great before the face of the LORD, and the LORD has sent us to destroy it.

14 Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law who were taking his daughters, and he said, "Get up, go out from this place, for the LORD will destroy the city," but he seemed to his sons-in-law as one who was joking.

15 And as the morning light arose, the messengers urged Lot, saying, "Rise, take your wife and your two daughters who are found, lest you be swept away in the sin of the city."

16 And he lingered, so the men seized him by his hand and by the hand of his wife and by the hands of his two daughters, because of the LORD's pity for him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.

17 And it came to pass, when they had brought them forth abroad, that he said, Flee for your life; do not look behind you, and do not stand in all the plain; flee to the mountain, lest you be swept away.

18 And Lot said to them, "Oh, not so, my lords."

19 Behold now, your servant has found favour in your eyes, and you have magnified your kindness which you have shown to me by keeping alive my soul; but I am not able to flee to the hill lest the evil overtake me and I die.

20 Behold now, this city is near to flee to, and it is small; please let me escape there—indeed, is it not a small one?—that my soul may live.

21 And he said to him, Behold, I have accepted your presence, even concerning this matter, so as not to overthrow the city which you have spoken of.

22 Hurry, escape there, for I cannot perform any act until you arrive there; therefore he called the name of the town Zoar.

23 The sun rose upon the land and Lot came to Zoar.

24 The LORD rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the LORD out of heaven.

25 And he overthrew those cities and all the plain and all the inhabitants of the cities and the vegetation of the ground.

26 And his wife looked behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.

27 Abraham rose early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the LORD.

28 He looked toward the face of Sodom and Gomorrah and toward the face of all the land of the plain, and he saw, and behold, the smoke of the land went up like the smoke of a furnace.

29 And it happened that when God overthrew the cities of the plain, God remembered Abraham and sent Lot out from the midst of the overthrow when He overthrew the cities where Lot had lived.

30 Lot went up from Zoar and lived in the hill country, and his two daughters with him, for he was afraid to live in Zoar; so he lived in the cave, he and his two daughters.

31 The firstborn said to the younger, "Our father is old, and there is no man on the land to come upon us after the manner of all the earth."

32 Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, and preserve seed from our father.

33 They made their father drink wine that night; then the firstborn went in and lay with her father, but he did not know when she lay down nor when she arose.

34 And it was on the morrow, that the firstborn said to the younger, Behold, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine also this night, and come thou in and lie with him, that we may preserve seed from our father.

35 And also on that night she made her father drink wine, and the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know in her lying down or in her rising up.

36 And the two daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.

37 The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moab; he is the father of Moab to this day.

38 And the younger also bore a son, and she called his name Ben-ammi; he is the father of the sons of Ammon to this day.


## Chapter 20

1 Abraham journeyed from there to the land of the south and dwelt between Kadesh and Shur, and sojourned in Gerar.

2 Abraham said to Sarah his wife, She is my sister; and Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.

3 God came to Abimelech in a dream of the night and said to him, "Behold, you are a dead man because of the woman whom you have taken, for she is a married woman."

4 And Abimelech did not come near her, and he said, "(My) Lord, a nation, even a righteous one, will you kill?"

5 Did he not say to me, “She is my sister,” and she herself also said, “He is my brother”? With integrity of heart and with innocency of my hand I have done this.

6 And God said to him in a dream, I also knew that in the integrity of your heart you did this, and I also kept you from sinning against me, therefore I did not let you touch her.

7 Now therefore restore the man's wife, for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you, and you will live; but if you do not restore her, know that you shall surely die, you and all that is yours.

8 And Abimelech rose up early in the morning and called all his servants and spoke all these words in their ears, and the men feared greatly.

9 Abimelech called to Abraham and said to him, "What have you done to us? And what have I sinned against you, that you have brought upon me and my kingdom a great sin? Acts which should not be done, you have done with me."

10 And Abimelech said to Abraham, "What have you seen, that you have done this act?"

11 Abraham said, "Because I thought, 'Surely there is no fear of God in this place'; they will kill me on account of my wife."

12 And indeed, she is my sister, the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother, and she became my wife.

13 And it came to pass, when God had caused me to wander from my father's house, that I said to her, "This is the kindness you shall show me: in every place where we go, say of me, 'He is my brother.'"

14 And Abimelech took sheep, cattle, male servants, and female servants, and gave them to Abraham, and returned to him Sarah his wife.

15 Abimelech said, "Behold, my land is before you; dwell in it wherever you see fit."

16 And to Sarah he said, Behold, I have given to your brother a thousand silver money; behold, it is for you a covering of the eyes, for all that are with you, and for all, and you are righted.

17 Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech and his wife and his female servants, and they bore children.

18 For the LORD had completely closed every womb in the household of Abimelech because of Sarah, the wife of Abraham.


## Chapter 21

1 The LORD visited Sarah as He had said, and the LORD did for Sarah as He had spoken.

2 Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the appointed time that God had promised him.

3 Abraham named his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore to him, Isaac.

4 Abraham circumcised his son Isaac on the eighth day, as God commanded him.

5 Abraham was one hundred years old when Isaac his son was born to him.

6 And Sarah said, "God has made me laugh; everyone who hears will laugh at me."

7 And she said, Who spoke to Abraham, Sarah has nursed children? For I have borne a son in his old age.

8 The boy grew and was weaned, and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.

9 Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.

10 And she said to Abraham, "Cast out this bondwoman and her son, for the son of this bondwoman shall not inherit with my son, even with Isaac."

11 The act was very displeasing in Abraham's eyes on account of his son.

12 And God said to Abraham, Let it not be affliction in your sight on account of the boy and on account of your bondwoman; all that Sarah says to you, listen in her voice; for in Isaac shall be called to you seed.

13 And also the son of the handmaid I will make into a nation, for he is your seed.

14 Abraham rose early in the morning and took bread and a bottle of water and gave them to Hagar, placing them on her shoulder along with the boy, and sent her away; and she went and wandered in the wilderness of Beer-shebah.

15 And the water from the bottle was spent, and she cast the child under one of the shrubs.

16 So she went and sat down opposite at a distance, about an archer’s shot away, for she said, “I will not see the death of the child”; and as she sat opposite, she lifted up her voice and wept.

17 And God heard the voice of the boy, and the angel of God called to Hagar from the heavens and said to her, What ails you, Hagar? Do not fear, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.

18 Arise, take the child and hold your hand upon him, for a great nation I will make him.

19 Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water; so she went and filled the bottle with water and gave the boy a drink.

20 And God was with the boy and he grew, and he lived in the wilderness, and he became an archer.

21 He settled in the wilderness of Paran, and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.

22 At that time Abimelech and Phichol the captain of his army said to Abraham, "God is with you in all that you do."

23 Now therefore swear to me by God here that you will not deal falsely with me, my son, or my son's son; as I have dealt kindly with you, so you shall deal with me and with the land in which you have sojourned.

24 Abraham said, "I will swear."

25 And Abraham contended with Abimelech concerning the cause of the well of water that Abimelech's servants had seized.

26 And Abimelech said, I did not know who did this act, and also you did not tell me, and also I did not hear except today.

27 Abraham took small cattle and herd and gave to Abimelech, and the two of them cut covenant.

28 Abraham set apart seven ewe lambs from the flock.

29 Abimelech said to Abraham, "What are these seven (ewe) lambs that you have set apart?"

30 And he said, "Indeed, seven (ewe) lambs you shall take from my hand, that it may be a witness for me, because I have dug this well."

31 Therefore he called that place Beer-shebah, because there they swore an oath, both of them.

32 They made a confederacy at Beer-shebah, and Abimelech and Phichol the captain of his host rose up and returned to the land of the Philistines.

33 And he planted a grove in Beer-shebah, and there he called upon the name of the LORD, God alway.

34 Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.


## Chapter 22

1 And it came to pass after these acts, that God tested Abraham, and said to him, Abraham, and he said, Here I am.

2 And he said, Take now your son, your only one, whom you love, even Isaac, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering upon one of the mountains which I will tell you.

3 Abraham rose early in the morning and saddled his ass and took his two servants with him and Isaac his son and split the wood for the burnt offering and arose and went to the place of which God had spoken to him.

4 On the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place from afar.

5 Abraham said to his servants, "Stay here for yourselves with the donkey, and I and the boy will go to that place, and we will bow down and return to you."

6 Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on Isaac his son, and he took in his hand the fire and the knife, and the two of them went together.

7 And Isaac said to Abraham his father, and he said, My father; and he said, Here I am, my son; and he said, Behold, the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?

8 Abraham said, God will provide for himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son; and they went both of them together.

9 They came to the place that God had told him, and there Abraham built the altar and arranged the wood; he bound Isaac his son and laid him on the altar on top of the wood.

10 Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his son.

11 And an ambassador of the LORD called to him from the heavens, saying, Abraham, Abraham! And he said, Here I am.

12 Do not lay your hand on the boy, and do not do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only one, from me.

13 Abraham lifted his eyes and saw, and behold, a ram, caught in the thicket by its horns; and Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up for a burnt offering instead of his son.

14 Abraham named the place Jehovah-jireh, so that it may be said today, "In the mount of the LORD he will be seen."

15 And the LORD's messenger called to Abraham a second time from the heavens.

16 And he said, By myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, because you have done this thing and have not withheld your son, your only one.

17 For I will surely bless you and greatly multiply your seed like the stars of the heavens and like the sand that is on the seashore, and your seed shall possess the gate of his enemy.

18 And in your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed, because you have heeded my voice.

19 Abraham returned to the lads and they arose and went together to Beer-shebah, and Abraham dwelt in Beer-shebah.

20 After these acts, word came to Abraham, saying, "Behold, Milcah has also borne sons to Nahor your brother."

21 Uz the firstborn and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram.

22 and Chesed and Hazo and Pildash and Jidlaph and Bethuel.

23 Bethuel fathered Rebekah; these eight Milcah bore to Nahor, brother of Abraham.

24 And his concubine whose name was Reumah also bore Tebah and Gaham and Thahash and Maachah.


## Chapter 23

1 The age of Sarah was one hundred years and twenty years and seven years; these were the years of the life of Sarah.

2 Sarah died in Kirjath-arba, that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan, and Abraham came to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.

3 Abraham rose up from before the presence of his dead and spoke to the sons of Heth, saying:

4 I am an alien and a sojourner with you; give me a possession of a burying place with you, that I may bury my dead out of my sight.

5 And the sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him:

6 Hear us, my lord: thou art a prince of God among us; in the choice of our sepulchres bury thy dead; every man among us will not withhold his sepulchre from thee, so that thou mayest not be prevented from burying thy dead.

7 Abraham rose and bowed to the folk of the land, to the sons of Heth.

8 And he spoke to them, saying, If it is agreeable to your souls to bury my dead before me, hear me and entreat for me to Ephron son of Zohar.

9 And give me the cave of Machpelah that belongs to him, which is at the edge of his field; for the full price in silver let him give it to me among you for a possession of a burying place.

10 And Ephron was sitting among the Hethites, and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of all who entered the gate of his city, saying:

11 My lord, hear me: the field I give to you, and the cave that is in it, I give it to you; in the sight of the sons of my people I give it to you: bury your dead.

12 Abraham bowed down before the people of the land.

13 And he spoke to Ephron in the audience of the people of the land, saying, If you will but hear me, I have given the silver for the field; take it from me, and I will bury my dead there.

14 Then Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him:

15 My lord, listen to me: a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver—what is that between me and you? Bury your dead.

16 Abraham listened to Ephron, and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver money that he had named in the hearing of the sons of Heth: four hundred shekels of silver, current money among traders.

17 Then the field of Ephron which was in Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the field and the cave which was in it and all the trees which were in the field, which were in all its borders round about, was established.

18 Abraham purchased it before the eyes of the sons of Heth, in the presence of all who entered the gate of his city.

19 After this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah, facing Mamre, that is Hebron, in the land of Canaan.

20 The field and the cave that is in it, which belonged to Abraham as a possession for a burying place, was acquired from the children of Heth.


## Chapter 24

1 And Abraham was old, advanced in age, and the LORD had blessed Abraham in everything.

2 Abraham said to his bond-servant, the elder of his household who ruled over all that he had, "Put now your hand under my thigh."

3 And I will make you swear by the LORD, God of heaven and God of the earth, that you shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanite, among whom I dwell.

4 Go to my country and to my kindred and take a wife for my son Isaac.

5 And the servant said to him, Perhaps the woman will not be willing to follow me to this land: shall I then return your son to the land from which you came?

6 Abraham said to him, "Beware for yourself, lest you return my son there."

7 The LORD, God of the heavens, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my birth, and who spoke to me and swore to me, saying, "To your seed I will give this land"—he will send his messenger before you, and you will take a wife for my son from there.

8 But if the woman is unwilling to go with you, you will be clear of my oath—only do not take my son back there.

9 And the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and swore to him concerning this matter.

10 The servant took ten camels from the camels of his master and went, and all the good of his master was in his hand; he arose and went to Aram-naharaim, to the city of Nahor.

11 And he made the camel stop outside the city at the well of water, at the time of evening, at the time when the women go out to draw water.

12 And the LORD, God of my lord Abraham, I pray You, grant me success today and show kindness to my lord Abraham.

13 Behold, I am standing by the well of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.

14 And it shall be, the damsel to whom I shall say, 'Please let down your pitcher,' and she says, 'Drink, and I will also water your camels'—her will you have shown as bond servant to Isaac, and by her I shall know that you have done kindness with my lord.

15 And it came to pass, before he had finished speaking, that behold, Rebekah came out, who was born to Bethuel the son of Milcah, wife of Nahor, brother of Abraham, and her pitcher was upon her shoulder.

16 And the damsel was very goodly to look upon, a virgin whom no man had known; and she went down to the fountain, filled her pitcher, and came up.

17 And the servant ran toward her and said, Please let me drink a little water from your pitcher.

18 And she said, "Drink, my lord," and quickly lowered her pitcher upon her hand and gave him to drink.

19 And when she had finished giving him drink, she said, "I will also draw for your camels until they have finished drinking."

20 And she hurried and emptied her pitcher into the trough and ran again to the well to draw water and drew for all his camels.

21 And the man gazed at her in silence, not knowing whether the LORD would make his way succeed or not.

22 When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold earring weighing a bekah, and two gold bracelets for her arms weighing ten [shekels] in total weight.

23 And he said, "Whose daughter are you? Please tell me. Is there room in your father's house for us to lodge?"

24 And she said to him, "I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor."

25 And she said to him, "Both straw and abundant provender are with us, and also room to lodge."

26 The man bowed and prostrated himself to the LORD.

27 Blessed be the LORD, God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken His kindness and faithfulness toward my master; I, walking in the way, the LORD has led me to the house of my master’s kinsmen.

28 And the damsel ran and told her mother's household according to these words.

29 And to Rebekah there was a brother, and his name was Laban; and Laban ran to the man outside at the fountain.

30 And it came to pass, when he saw the earring and the bracelets upon his sister's hands, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister, saying, Thus spoke the man to me, that he went to the man; and behold, he was standing by the camels at the fountain.

31 And he said, Come in, blessed be Jehovah; why do you stand abroad? I have cleared the house, and room for the camels.

32 The man came into the house and unloaded the camels, giving straw and provender to the camels and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.

33 Before him to eat, and he said, I will not eat until I have spoken my word; and he said, Speak.

34 And he said, I am a servant of Abraham.

35 And the LORD blessed my lord greatly, and he grew great, and gave him sheep and beeve and silver and gold and bondmen and bondwomen and camels and asses.

36 Sarah bore a son to my lord after her old age, and he gave him all that was his.

37 And my lord made me swear, saying, You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanite, in whose land I dwell.

38 If you do not go to the family of my father and to my kindred, then you shall take a wife for my son.

39 And I said to my lord, Perhaps the woman will not follow me.

40 And he said to me, “Jehovah, the Lord, before whom I walked, will send his messenger with you and prosper your journey, and you will take a wife for my son from my family and from the house of my father.”

41 Then you will be clear of my curse when you come to my family; but if they will not give her to you, then you will be free of my curse.

42 Today I came to the spring, and I said, “O LORD, God of my master Abraham, please let me succeed in the way that I am going.”
