43 Behold, I am standing by the fountain of water, and it shall be that the virgin who comes out to draw water, I will say to her, “Please give me a little water from your pitcher.”

44 And she shall say to me, “You too shall drink, and I will also draw for your camels”—she is the woman whom the LORD has appointed for my lord’s son.

45 I had not yet finished speaking to my mind when behold Rebekah came out with her pitcher on her shoulder and went down to the fountain and drew water, and I said to her, “Please give me a drink.”

46 And she hurried and let down her pitcher from upon her and said, “Drink, and I will also give your camels to drink”; so I drank, and she also gave the camels to drink.

47 I asked her and said, “Whose daughter are you?” and she said, “The daughter of Bethuel, son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to him.” Then I put the earring on her nose and the bracelets on her hands.

48 I bowed and worshiped Jehovah, and I blessed Jehovah, the God of my lord Abraham, who led me in the true way to take the daughter of my lord's brother for his son.

49 Now then, if you will show favour and truth to my lord, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right or to the left.

50 Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, "The act has come from the LORD; we are not able to speak to you bad or good."

51 Behold, Rebekah is before you; take her and go, and let her be wife to the son of your lord, as the LORD has spoken.

52 When Abraham's bondman heard their words, he bowed to the ground before the LORD.

53 The servant brought out silver and gold items and clothing and gave them to Rebekah, and he gave precious things to her brother and to her mother.

54 They ate and drank, he and the men who were with him, and they lodged; and they rose in the morning, and he said, "Send me to my lord."

55 And her brother and her mother said, Let the maiden abide with us days or a ten, afterward she will go.

56 And he said to them, Do not delay me, for the LORD has made my journey successful; send me away so I may go to my lord.

57 And they said, Let us call the damsel and ask her mouth.

58 And they called to Rebekah and said to her, "Will you go with this man?" And she said, "I will go."

59 And they sent Rebekah their sister and her nurse and Abraham's servant and his men.

60 And they blessed Rebekah and said to her, "May you, our sister, become the mother of thousands of ten thousands, and may your seed possess the gate of those who hate them."

61 And Rebekah arose, and her damsels, and they rode upon the camels and went after the man; and the servant took Rebekah and went.

62 And Isaac came from going to Beer-lahai-roi, and he was dwelling in the land of the south.

63 Isaac went out to walk in the country toward evening, and he lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, camels were coming.

64 Rebekah lifted her eyes and saw Isaac, and she fell from upon the camel.

65 And she said to the servant, "Who is the man walking in the field toward us?" And the servant said, "He is my lord." And she took the vail and covered herself.

66 The servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.

67 Isaac brought her into the covering of Sarah his mother, and he took Rebekah and she became his wife, and he loved her, and Isaac was comforted after his mother.


## Chapter 25

1 Abraham added and took a wife, and her name was Keturah.

2 And she bore him Zimran and Jokshan and Medan and Midian and Ishbak and Shuah.

3 Jokshan fathered Sheba and Dedan, and the sons of Dedan were the Asshurim, the Letushim, and the Leummim.

4 And the sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Enoch, Abida, and Eldaah; all these were sons of Keturah.

5 Abraham gave to Isaac all that he had.

6 And to the sons of the concubines who were Abraham's, Abraham gave gifts, and he sent them away from Isaac his son while he was still alive, eastward to the land of the east.

7 These are the years of the age of Abraham which he lived: one hundred years and seventy years and five years.

8 Abraham breathed his last and died, an old man satisfied at a good age, and was gathered to his folk.

9 Isaac and Ishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite, which is opposite Mamre.

10 The field that Abraham bought from the children of Heth—there Abraham was buried, and Sarah his wife.

11 After the death of Abraham, God blessed Isaac his son, and Isaac settled near Beer-lahai-roi.

12 These are the generations of Ishmael son of Abraham, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s handmaid, bore to Abraham.

13 These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, according to their names in their generations: the eldest son of Ishmael, Nebaioth, and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam.

14 Mishma, Dumah, and Massa.

15 Hadad and Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.

16 These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names in their villages and their rows, twelve princes according to their nations.

17 These are the years of Ishmael's life: one hundred years and thirty years and seven years, and he expired and died, and was gathered to his people.

18 They settled from Havilah to Shur, which is opposite Egypt, toward Assyria; he fell against all his brothers.

19 These are the generations of Isaac son of Abraham: Abraham fathered Isaac.

20 Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Syrian of Padan-aram, sister of Laban the Syrian, to be his wife.

21 Isaac entreated the LORD on behalf of his wife because she was barren, and the LORD let Himself be entreated by him, and Rebekah his wife conceived.

22 The children struggled within her, and she said, "If so, why is this happening to me?" and she went to seek the LORD.

23 And the LORD said to her, Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples shall be separated from your bowels, and one people shall be stronger than the other people, and the greater shall serve the lesser.

24 And the days of her pregnancy were fulfilled for giving birth, and behold, twins in her womb.

25 And the first came out red, all of him like a garment of hair; and they called his name Esau.

26 Then his brother came out, and his hand grasped the heel of Esau, and he called his name Jacob, and Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.

27 The boys grew, and Esau became a man who knew hunting, a man of the field, while Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.

28 Isaac loved Esau because he had food in his mouth, but Rebekah loved Jacob.

29 Jacob made pottage, and Esau came in from the field and he was weary.

30 Esau said to Jacob, "Please feed me some of that red red stuff, for I am weary"; therefore his name was named Edom.

31 Jacob said, "Sell your birthright to me today."

32 And Esau said, "Here I am going to die, so of what use to me is the birthright?"

33 Then Jacob said, Swear to me today; so he swore to him, and he sold his birthright to Jacob.

34 Jacob gave Esau bread and lentile pottage, and he ate and he drank, and he rose and he went, and Esau despised the birthright.


## Chapter 26

1 There was a famine in the land besides the famine that had been in the days of Abraham, and Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines at Gerar.

2 And the LORD appeared to him and said, "Do not go down to Egypt; dwell in the land which I will tell you."

3 Dwell in the land and I will be with you and bless you, for to you and to your seed I will give all the lands, and I will confirm the oath that I swore to Abraham your father.

4 I will greatly multiply your seed like the stars of heaven, and give to your seed all the lands, and all the nations of the earth will be blessed by your seed.

5 because Abraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws

6 Isaac settled in Gerar.

7 The men of the place asked about his wife, and he said, "My sister she is," because he feared to say "my wife," lest the men of the place kill him on account of Rebekah, for she was goodly in appearance.

8 And it came to pass, when the days had grown long for him there, that Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out through the window and saw, and behold, Isaac was laughing with Rebekah his wife.

9 Abimelech called to Isaac and said, "Indeed, she is your wife! How could you say, 'She is my sister'?" And Isaac said to him, "Because I said, 'Lest I die on account of her.'"

10 Abimelech said, "What is this you have done to us? Almost one of the folk would have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guiltiness upon us."

11 Abimelech commanded all the people, saying, "Any man who touches this man and his wife shall surely be put to death."

12 Isaac sowed in that land and found in that year a hundredfold, and the LORD blessed him.

13 And the man grew, and went on growing and became very great.

14 He had flocks, herds of cattle, and a great household, and the Philistines were envious of him.

15 And all the wells that the servants of his father had dug in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines stopped up and filled with dust.

16 And Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us, for you have become exceedingly powerful among us.”

17 And Isaac departed from there and encamped in the valley of Gerar and dwelt there.

18 Isaac returned and reopened the water pits that his father Abraham had dug in the days of Abraham, which the Philistines had stopped up after Abraham's death, and he named them according to the names by which his father had named them.

19 And Isaac's servants dug in the stream and found there a well of living water.

20 And the herdsmen of Gerar strove with Isaac's herdsmen, saying, "The water is ours!" So he called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.

21 And they dug another well, and they quarreled over it also, and he called its name Sitnah.

22 And he moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it, and he named its name Rehoboth, and he said, "For now the LORD has made room for us, and we will be fruitful in the land."

23 And he went up from there to Beer-shebah.

24 And Jehovah appeared to him in the night of that night and said, I am the God of Abraham your father; do not fear, for I am with you and I have blessed you and multiplied your seed because of Abraham my bondman.

25 And he built there an altar and called on the name of the LORD and pitched his tent there, and there the servants of Isaac dug a well.

26 And Abimelech went to him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath his friend, and Phichol the captain of his host.

27 Isaac said to them, "Why have you come to me, when you hate me and have sent me away from you?"

28 And they said, We have seen that Jehovah, the Lord, has been with you. Now may there be, we pray, an oath between us, between us and you, and let us make a covenant with you.

29 If you will do us no adversity, as we have not touched you and as we have done to you only good and sent you away in peace, you are now blessed by the LORD.

30 And he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

31 They rose early in the morning, and each man swore to his brother, and Isaac sent them off, and they went from him in peace.

32 On that day Isaac's servants came to him and reported concerning the well they had dug, and said to him, "We have found water."

33 And he called it seven; therefore the name of the town is Beer-shebah to this day.

34 Esau was forty years old when he took as wife Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Bashemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite.

35 And they were a grief of spirit to Isaac and to Rebekah.


## Chapter 27

1 And it came to pass when Isaac was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, that he called Esau his elder son and said to him, My son; and he said to him, Here am I.

2 And he said, Behold, now I am old, I do not know the day of my death.

3 Now therefore, take, I pray, your weapons, your quiver and your bow, go out into the field, and hunt game for me.

4 And prepare me savory meat, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat, so that my soul may bless you before I die.

5 And Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau, and Esau went to the field to hunt game to bring back.

6 Rebekah said to Jacob her son, "Behold, I heard your father speaking to Esau your brother, saying:"

7 Bring me venison, and prepare savory meat for me, that I may eat, and bless you before the LORD before my death.

8 And now, my son, listen to my voice, to what I command you.

9 Go now to the flock and bring me two good young goats, so that I may prepare delicious food for your father, such as he loves.

10 And you shall bring it to your father, and he shall eat, so that he may bless you before his death.

11 Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, "Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man."

12 Perhaps my father will feel me, and I will be in his sight as a deceiver, and I will bring upon myself a curse and not a blessing.

13 And his mother said to him, "Upon me be your curse, my son; only obey my voice and go, bring me.

14 And he went and took and brought to his mother, and his mother made dainty meats such as his father loved.

15 Rebekah took the goodly apparel of Esau her son, the elder, which was in the house, and put it on Jacob her son, the younger.

16 And she put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.

17 And she gave the savoury meat and the bread that she had made into the hand of Jacob her son.

18 And he came to his father and said, "My father," and he said, "Here I am; who are you, my son?"

19 Jacob said to his father, I am Esau your eldest; I have done as you told me; now please arise, sit down, and eat of my hunting, that your soul may bless me.

20 Isaac said to his son, Why have you made haste to find it, my son? And he said, Because the LORD your God caused it to happen before me.

21 Isaac said to Jacob, "Come near, please, and let me feel you, my son—whether you are really my son Esau or not."

22 Jacob approached Isaac his father, and he touched him; and he said, "The voice is the voice of Jacob, but the hands are the hands of Esau."

23 And he did not recognize him, because his hands were like the hands of Esau his brother, hairy; so he blessed him.

24 And he said, Are you this, my son Esau? And he said, I am.

25 And he said, Bring me food from the hunting of my son, that my soul may bless you; and he brought it to him, and he ate, and he brought him wine, and he drank.

26 And Isaac his father said to him, Come now, and kiss me, my son.

27 And he drew near and kissed him, and smelled the scent of his garments and blessed him, and said, “See, the savour of my son is as the savour of the field which the LORD has blessed.”

28 And God will give you dew of the heavens and fat of the earth and abundance of wheat and wine.

29 Nations will serve you, peoples will bow down to you; be lord over your brothers, and your mother’s sons will bow down to you; cursed be those who curse you, and blessed be those who bless you.

30 When Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had scarcely left the presence of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.

31 And he also prepared dainty meat, and brought it to his father, and said to his father, “Let my father arise and eat of the venison of his son, that your soul may bless me.”

32 And Isaac his father said to him, Who are you? And he said, I am your son, your firstborn, Esau.

33 And Isaac trembled a great trembling exceedingly and said Who then is he that hath brought food and brought it me and I have eaten of everything before thou camest and I have blessed him also blessed shall he be

34 When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, Bless me also, my father.

35 And he said, Your brother came with deceit and took your blessing.

36 And he said, Is he rightly named Jacob? This twice he has supplanted me: my birthright he took, and now he has taken my blessing. And he said, Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

37 And Isaac answered and said to Esau, Behold, I have made him a lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with wheat and wine I have sustained him; and what then shall I do for you, my son?

38 Esau said to his father, Have you but one blessing, my father? bless me also, father. And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.

39 Isaac his father answered and said to him, "Behold, the fatness of the land shall be your dwelling, and from the dew of the heavens above.

40 By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; and it shall come to pass when you grow restive, you shall break his yoke from off your neck.

41 Esau harbored resentment against Jacob on account of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esau said in his mind, "Let the days of mourning for my father draw near, then I will kill Jacob my brother."

42 And the acts of Esau her elder son were told to Rebekah, so she sent and called Jacob her younger son and said to him, "Behold, your brother Esau is comforting himself concerning you to kill you."

43 And now, my son, listen to my voice; arise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran.

44 and you shall dwell with him for some days, until your brother's anger turns away.

45 Until your brother's anger turns from you and he forgets what you did to him, then I will send and bring you from there; why should I lose both of you in one day?

46 And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth; if Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth like these, from the daughters of the land, why do I have life?


## Chapter 28

1 Isaac called Jacob and blessed him and charged him, saying, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan."

2 Arise, go to Padan-aram, to the house of Bethuel, your mother's father, and take for yourself a wife from the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.

3 And God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become a multitude of peoples.

4 And he will give to you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your seed with you, to possess the land of your sojournings, which God gave to Abraham.

5 Isaac sent Jacob away, and he went to Padan-aram, to Laban the son of Bethuel the Aramean, brother of Rebekah, mother of Jacob and Esau.

6 Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Padan-aram to take a wife from there, and when blessing him had commanded him, saying, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan."

7 Jacob listened to his father and to his mother and went to Padan-aram.

8 Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan were evil in the eyes of Isaac his father.

9 Esau went to Ishmael and took Mahalath daughter of Ishmael son of Abraham, sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his wives, to be his wife.

10 Jacob went out from Beer-shebah and went toward Haran.

11 He came upon the place and spent the night there because the sun had set; he took some of the stones of the place and put them under his head, and lay down in that place.

12 And he dreamed, and behold, a ladder was set on the ground, and its top reached to the heavens, and behold, the messengers of God were ascending and descending on it.

13 And behold, the LORD stood above him and said, I am the LORD God of Abraham your father and God of Isaac; the land on which you are lying I will give to you and to your seed.

14 And your seed will be like the dust of the land, and you will spread westward and eastward and northward and southward, and all families of the earth will be blessed by you and by your seed.

15 And behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go and will bring you back to this land, for I will not leave you until I have done what I have spoken to you.

16 Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, "Surely Jehovah, the Lord, is in this place, and I did not know it."

17 He was afraid and said, "How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven."

18 Jacob rose early in the morning and took the stone that he had put under his head and set it up as a pillar and poured oil on its top.

19 He called the place named Beth-el, but Luz was the city's name formerly.

20 Jacob vowed a vow, saying, "If God will be with me and will keep me along the way that I am going, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear."

21 And I will return in peace to my father's house, and the LORD will be my God.

22 And this stone which I have set as a pillar shall be God's house, and of all that You give me I will surely tithe to You.


## Chapter 29

1 Jacob set out on his journey and went to the land of the people of the east.

2 And he saw, and behold, a well in the field, and behold, there were three flocks of sheep lying beside it, for from that well the flocks would drink, and the great stone was upon the mouth of the well.

3 And there all the flocks were gathered, and they rolled the stone from the mouth of the well and watered the flock, and they returned the stone to the mouth of the well to its place.

4 Then Jacob said to them, “My brothers, where do you come from?” And they said, “We are from Haran.”

5 And he said to them, "Do you know Laban son of Nahor?" And they said, "We know him."

6 He said to them, "Do you fare well?" and they said, "We fare well," and behold, Rachel, his daughter, was coming with the flock.

7 And he said, "Behold, the day is still long, it is not yet time to gather the flock; water the sheep and go feed them."

8 And they said, We cannot, until all the flocks are gathered, and they roll the stone from the mouth of the well; then we will water the sheep.

9 While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with the flock that belonged to her father, for she was a shepherdess.

10 And it happened, when Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban the brother of his mother, and the sheep of Laban the brother of his mother, that Jacob came near and rolled the stone from the mouth of the well and watered the sheep of Laban the brother of his mother.

11 Jacob kissed Rachel and lifted up his voice and wept aloud.

12 Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's brother and that he was Rebekah's son, and Rachel ran and told her father.

13 When Laban heard the report of Jacob the son of his sister, he ran to meet him, embraced him, kissed him, and brought him to his house; and Jacob told Laban all these things.

14 And Laban said to him, "Surely you are my very body and my very flesh," and he stayed with him a month of days.

15 Then Laban said to Jacob, "Am I your brother, that you should serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?"

16 Laban had two daughters; the name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.

17 Leah's eyes were tender, but Rachel was beautiful in form and beautiful in appearance.

18 Jacob loved Rachel and said, I will serve you seven years for Rachel your daughter the younger.

19 Laban said, "It is better to give her to you than to give her to another man; stay with me."

20 Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him like a few days because of his love for her.

21 Jacob said to Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are full, that I may go in to her.

22 Laban gathered all the men of the place and made a feast.

23 And it came to be in the evening that he took Leah his daughter and brought her to him, and he went in to her.

24 Laban gave his handmaiden Zilpah to Leah his daughter as a handmaiden.

25 And it was in the morning, and behold, she was Leah; and he said to Laban, What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served with you? Why then have you deceived me?

26 And Laban said, It is not done in our country to give the younger before the firstborn.

27 Complete this week of work, and she will also be given to you in addition, if you will work with me for another seven years of service.

28 Jacob did so, and he completed that week, and Laban gave him Rachel his daughter as wife.

29 Laban gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah her handmaid to be her handmaid.

30 And he came also to Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah, and he served with him seven other years.

31 And Jehovah saw that Leah was hated, and he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.

32 Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben, for she said, "Because the LORD has seen my affliction, for now my husband will love me."

33 She conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved," and he gave me this one also, and she called his name Simeon.

34 And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons"; therefore she called his name Levi.

35 And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, "This once I will praise the LORD," so she called his name Judah; then she stopped bearing.


## Chapter 30

1 Rachel saw that she had borne no children to Jacob, and Rachel envied her sister, and said to Jacob, "Give me children, or else I am dead."

2 Jacob's anger burned against Rachel, and he said to her, "Am I in the place of God, who has withheld the fruit of the womb from you?"

3 Then she said, "Here, my maid Bilhah; go in to her, and she will bear upon my knees, and I will also be built up from her."

4 And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid as wife, and Jacob went in to her.

5 Bilhah conceived and bore a son to Jacob.

6 Rachel said, "God has judged me; indeed, He has heeded my voice and given me a son." Therefore she named his name Dan.

7 And she conceived again and bore a second son to Jacob, Bilhah, Rachel’s handmaid.

8 Rachel said, "With mighty wrestling I have wrestled with my sister; indeed, I have prevailed," and she called his name Naphtali.

9 Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, so she took Zilpah her handmaid and gave her to Jacob as wife.

10 Zilpah, Leah's handmaid, bore a son to Jacob.

11 Leah said, and she called his name Gad, saying, "A troop has come."

12 Zilpah, Leah's handmaid, bore a second son to Jacob.

13 And Leah said, "Happy am I, for daughters have declared me happy," and she called his name Asher.

14 Reuben went in the days of the wheat harvest and found mandrakes in the field and brought them to Leah his mother, and Rachel said to Leah, "Please give me some of the mandrakes of your son."

15 And she said to her, Is it a small matter that you have taken my husband, and would you also take the mandrakes of my son? And Rachel said, Therefore he shall lie with you tonight in exchange for the mandrakes of your son.

16 Jacob came from the country in the evening, and Leah went out to meet him and said, “To me you shall come, for I have surely hired you with the mandrakes of my son”; so he lay with her that night.

17 And God heard Leah, and she conceived and bore to Jacob a fifth son.

18 Leah said, "God has given me my reward because I gave my maid to my husband," and she called his name Issachar.

19 Then Leah conceived again and bore a sixth son to Jacob.

20 Leah said, "God has endowed me with a good dowry; this time my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons," and she called his name Zebulun.

21 And afterward she bore a daughter and called her name Dinah.

22 And God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her, and opened her womb.

23 And she conceived and bore a son, and said, "God has taken away my reproach."

24 And she called his name Joseph, saying, "May the LORD add to me another son."

25 When Rachel bore Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Send me away that I may go to my country and to my land.

26 Give me my wives and my children for whom I served you, that I may go; for you know my service which I served you.

27 And Laban said to him, If now I have found favour in your eyes, I have divined, and the LORD has blessed me because of you.

28 And he said, On your hire shall be my wages, and I will give it.

29 And he said to him, You know the work I have done for you, and the cattle that were with me.

30 For though you had little before me, it has increased greatly, and the LORD has blessed you since you came, and now when shall I also do something for my household?

31 And he said, "What shall I give you?" And Jacob said, "You shall not give me anything, if you do for me this thing, I will return, I will shepherd your sheep."

32 I will pass through all your flock today, removing from there every speckled and spotted sheep and every dark-colored lamb among the lambs and every spotted and speckled goat among the goats, and that will be my wages.

33 And my justice will answer for me in time to come, when you come concerning my wages before you: every one that is not speckled among the goats and brown among the lambs, it is stolen with me.

34 Then Laban said, "Behold, may it be according to your word."

35 On that day he removed the streaked and spotted male goats and all the spotted and spotted female goats, everything that had white in it, and all the brown among the lambs, and he gave them into the hand of his sons.

36 And he set a journey of three days between him and Jacob, and Jacob was shepherding the flock of Laban that remained.

37 Jacob took for himself a rod of poplar, hazel, and chestnut tree, and peeled white strakes in them, making appear the white which was on the rods.

38 And he set the rods that he had peeled in the gutters, in the water-troughs where the flocks came to drink, facing the flocks; and the flocks became heated when they came to drink.

39 The flock became heated toward the rods, and the flock bore lambs streaked, speckled, and spotted.

40 And Jacob separated the lambs, and he set the faces of the flock toward the striped and all the dark ones in Laban's flock, and he put apart flocks for himself, and did not put them with Laban's sheep.

41 And it came to pass that when the flocks were in heat, all those that were mated, Jacob set the rods before the eyes of the flock in the troughs, to make them conceive among the rods.

42 And whenever the feebler of the flock bred, he did not lay the rods; so the feebler ones became Laban's, and the stronger Jacob's.

43 And the man became exceedingly, exceedingly rich, and he had abundant sheep, female servants, male servants, camels, and donkeys.


## Chapter 31

1 And he heard the words of Laban's sons saying, Jacob has taken all that belonged to our father, and from what belonged to our father he has made all this glory.

2 And Jacob saw the countenance of Laban, and behold, it was not toward him as yesterday and the day before.

3 The LORD said to Jacob, Return to the land of your fathers and to your native country, and I will be with you.

4 Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field to his flock.

5 And he said to them, "I see the countenance of your father is not toward me as it was in former times, but the God of my father has been with me."

6 And you yourselves know that with all my strength I served your father.

7 But your father cheated me and changed my wages ten times, yet God did not allow him to harm me.

8 If he says, "Your reward shall be speckled," then all the sheep will give birth to speckled offspring; and if he says, "Your reward shall be streaked," then all the sheep will give birth to streaked offspring.

9 And God delivered your fathers' cattle and gave it to me.

10 And it came to pass at the time when the flock was in heat, I lifted up my eyes and saw in a dream, and behold, the he-goats that went up onto the flock were streaked, speckled, and mottled.

11 And the angel of God said to me in a dream, 'Jacob,' and I said, 'Here I am.'

12 And he said, "Lift up now your eyes and see all the rams that are mounting the flock, speckled, spotted, and streaked, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you."

13 I am the God of Beth-el, where you set up a pillar and made a vow to me; now arise, go out from this land and return to your native country.

14 Then Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, "Is there yet any portion or inheritance left to us in our father's house?"

15 Are we not counted as foreigners by him, seeing he has sold us and also consumed our money?

16 For all the riches that God has delivered from our father belong to us and to our children; now, do all that God has said to you.

17 Jacob rose and lifted his sons and his wives onto the camels.

18 And he drove all his cattle and all his substance that he had acquired—the cattle of his getting that he had acquired in Padan-aram—to come to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.

19 And Laban went to shear his flock, and Rachel stole the idols that belonged to her father.

20 Jacob stole the heart of Laban the Syrian by not telling him that he was fleeing.

21 And he fled, he and all that he had, and arose and crossed the river, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.

22 On the third day it was told to Laban that Jacob had fled.

23 He took his brothers with him and pursued after him a journey of seven days and overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.

24 And God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night and said to him, "Beware for yourself lest you speak with Jacob from good to evil."

25 Laban overtook Jacob, and Jacob had pitched his tent on the hill, and Laban pitched his tent with his brothers on Mount Gilead.

26 Then Laban said to Jacob, "What have you done, that you have stolen my heart and carried off my daughters like captives of the sword?"

27 Why have you hidden yourself to flee and stolen me, and not declared it to me, that I might send you away with gladness and with songs, with timbrel and with harp?

28 And I have not forsaken you to kiss my sons and my daughters; now you have acted wisely—do it.

29 I have the power to harm you, but the God of your father last night said to me, “Beware of speaking with Jacob from good to evil.”

30 Now go, for you have longed to return to your father's house; why have you stolen my god?

31 Jacob answered and said to Laban, for I was afraid, for I said, Lest you take your daughters from me.

32 With whomever you find your gods, he shall not live; before our kinsmen discern what is with me and take it for yourself. But Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.

33 Laban came into Jacob's covering, and into Leah's covering, and into the covering of the two bondmaids, but he did not find it; and he went out from Leah's covering, and entered into Rachel's covering.

34 And Rachel took the idols and put them in the camel's saddle and sat on them, and Laban felt through all the tent and did not find.

35 And she said to her father, "Do not let my lord be displeased in his eyes, for I cannot rise from before you, because the way of women is upon me." And he searched, but did not find the idols.

36 Jacob became angry and contended with Laban, and Jacob answered and said to Laban, "What is my rebellion, what is my sin, that you have pursued after me?"

37 For you have searched all my things; what have you found from all the vessels of your house? Set it here before my brothers and your brothers, that they may decide between us.

38 This twenty year I have been with you: your ewes and she-goats have not miscarried, and the rams of your flock I have not eaten.

39 That which was torn, I did not bring to you; I myself would make it good from my hand—you shall seek it from me: if stolen by day, if stolen by night.

40 I was by day consumed with heat and with frost by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.

41 This to me twenty years in your household: I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times.

42 If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had not been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty: the affliction and the toil of my hands, God has seen, and yesterday He rebuked you.

43 Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, "The daughters are my daughters, and the sons are my sons, and the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine; and as for my daughters, what can I do today, or for their sons whom they have borne?"

44 And now come, let us cut a covenant between me and you, and let it be a witness between me and you.

45 Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.

46 Jacob said to his brothers, "Gather stones," and they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there upon the heap.

47 Laban called it Jegar-Sahadutha, and Jacob called it Galeed.

48 Then Laban said, "This heap is a witness between me and you today," therefore he named it Galeed.

49 And the Mizpah, of which he said, "May the LORD watch between me and you, when we are hidden, one from his fellow."

50 If you afflict my daughters or take wives besides my daughters, there is no man with us; see, God is witness between me and you.

51 And Laban said to Jacob, Behold the heap and behold the pillar which I have set between me and you.

52 This heap is a witness, and this pillar is a testimony, that I will not pass over this heap to you, nor will you pass over this heap and this pillar to adversity.

53 The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor shall judge between us, the God of their father; and Jacob swore by the fear of his father Isaac.

54 Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and invited his brothers to eat bread; they ate bread and spent the night on the mountain.

55 Laban rose early in the morning, kissed his sons and his daughters, blessed them, and went away; and Laban returned to his place.


## Chapter 32

1 And Jacob went on his journey, and the angels of God encountered him.

2 Then Jacob said, "As I see them, this is God's camp," and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

3 Jacob sent ambassadors ahead of him to Esau his brother, to the land of Seir in the country of Edom.

4 And he commanded them, saying, "Thus you shall say to my lord Esau: 'Thus says your servant Jacob: With Laban I have dwelt, and I have delayed until now.'"

5 I had a bull and an ass, flocks and male servant and female servant, and I sent to inform my lord, to find favour in your eyes.

6 The messengers returned to Jacob, saying, "We came to your brother Esau, and indeed he is coming to meet you, and four hundred men are with him."

7 And Jacob feared greatly, and it was straitened to him; so he divided the folk who were with him, and the flocks, and the herds, and the camels, into two camps.

8 And he said, If Esau comes to one camp and strikes it, then the remaining camp will be for escape.

9 Jacob said, "God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, the LORD who said to me, Return to your land and to your kindred, and I will deal well with you."

10 I am too small for all the kindness and all the truth that you have done for your servant, for with my staff I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.

11 Rescue me, I pray, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I am afraid of him, lest he come and strike me, mother against children.

12 But you said, "I will surely treat you well, and I will set your seed like the sand of the sea, which cannot be counted for its abundance."

13 And he lodged there that night and took from the gift in his hand an offering for Esau his brother.

14 Two hundred (she) goats and twenty he goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams.

15 Camels bearing young, their children thirty; cows forty, and bulls ten; female donkeys twenty, and young donkeys ten.

16 And he gave into the hand of his servants, a drove by itself, and said to his servants, Pass over before me, and put space between drove and drove.

17 He commanded the first one, saying, "When Esau, my brother, meets you and asks you, saying, 'To whom do you belong, and where are you going, and to whom do these before you belong?' "

18 And you shall say, "It is your servant Jacob's; it is a gift sent to my lord Esau, and behold, he is also behind us."

19 He also commanded the second, and also the third, and also all who were going after the droves, saying, "Thus you shall speak to Esau when you meet him."

20 And you shall say also: Behold, your servant Jacob is behind us; for he said, I will appease his face with the gift that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face; perhaps he will accept my face.

21 The gift passed before his face, and he lodged that night in the camp.

22 He arose that night, took his two wives, his two bondwomen, and his eleven children, and crossed the Jabbok ford.

23 And he took them and brought them across the brook, and he brought across that which was his.

24 Jacob remained alone, and a man wrestled with him until the morning arose.

25 And he saw that he could not prevail against him, so he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh, and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was dislocated as he wrestled with him.

26 And he said, "Send me away, for the morning has come up"; but he said, "I will not send you away unless you bless me."

27 And he said to him, "What is your name?" And he said, "Jacob."

28 No longer shall your name be called Jacob, but Israel, because you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed.

29 Jacob asked, saying, "Please tell me your name," and he said, "Why is this, that you ask for my name?" and he blessed him there.

30 Jacob named the place Peniel, because I have seen God face to face, and my soul was delivered.

31 The sun rose upon him as he passed beyond Peniel, and he was limping on his side.

32 Therefore the children of Israel do not eat the sinew which shrank, that is on the thigh joint, to this day, because he touched the joint of Jacob's thigh in the sinew which shrank.


## Chapter 33

1 Jacob lifted his eyes and saw, and behold, Esau was coming, and with him four hundred men; so he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two female servants.

2 He placed the maids and their children first, and Leah and her children last, and Rachel and Joseph last.

3 And he passed on before them and bowed himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.

4 And Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.

5 He lifted his eyes and saw the women and the children, and said, "Who are these to you?" And he said, "The children whom God has granted to your servant."

6 Then drew near those bondwomen and their children and bowed down.

7 Then Leah also came near, with her sons, and they bowed down; afterward Joseph and Rachel came near, and they bowed down.

8 And he said, Who is to you all this company that I have met? And he said, To find favour in the eyes of my lord.

9 And Esau said, I have plenty, my brother; let what you have remain yours.

10 Jacob said, Please, if I have found favour in your eyes, accept my gift from my hand; for indeed I have seen your face as one sees the face of God, and you have accepted me.

11 Please take my blessing that I have brought you, for God has favored me, and for I have everything; and he urged him, so he took it.

12 And he said, Let us go and walk on, and I will walk before you.

13 My lord, you know that the children are tender and the flocks and the herds are precious to me; if they are driven hard for one day, all the flocks will die.

14 Let my lord, I pray, pass on before his servant, and I will proceed gently, according to the pace of the work that is before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.

15 Esau said, "Let me leave with you some of the folk who are with me." But he said, "Why is this? Let me find favour in the eyes of my lord."

16 On that day Esau turned back on his way to Seir.

17 Jacob journeyed to Succoth and built for himself a house, and for his cattle he made booths; therefore he called the name of the place Succoth.

18 Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan-aram, and he camped before the city.

19 And he bought the parcel of land where he had pitched his tent from the hand of the sons of Hamor, the father of Shechem, for one hundred pieces of silver.

20 And he set up an altar there and called it El, the God of Israel.


## Chapter 34

1 Then Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom Leah bore to Jacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.

2 Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, a prince of the land, saw her, took her, lay with her, and afflicted her.

3 And his soul clung to Dinah daughter of Jacob, and he loved the damsel, and he spoke to the heart of the damsel.

4 And Shechem said to Hamor his father, saying, Take for me this girl as wife.

5 Jacob heard that Shechem had defiled Dinah his daughter, but his sons were in the field with the cattle; so Jacob kept silent until they came.

6 And Hamor the chief of Shechem went out to Jacob to speak with him.

7 And the sons of Jacob came from the field when they heard, and the men were grieved and greatly angered because a vile thing had been done in Israel, to lie with the daughter of Jacob, and such a thing must not be done.

8 And Hamor spoke with them, saying: "Shechem my son has taken a liking to your daughter; give her now to him as wife."

9 And you shall intermarry with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.

10 And with us you shall dwell, and the land shall be before you; dwell and trade in it, and take hold of it.

11 And Shechem said to her father and to her brothers, "Let me find favour in your eyes; whatever you say to me I will give."

12 Greatly increase upon me the dowry and gift, and I will give whatever you say to me; only give me the damsel as a wife.

13 And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father with deceit, because he had defiled Dinah their sister.

14 They said to them, We cannot do this act, to give our sister to a man who has a foreskin, for it would be a reproach to us.

15 Only in this one thing will you be at peace with us: if you will be like us, by circumcising every male among you.

16 And we will give our daughters to you and take your daughters for us, and we will dwell with you and be one people.

17 But if you will not listen to us to be circumcised, then we will take our daughters and go.

18 Their advice was pleasing in the eyes of Hamor and in the eyes of Shechem the son of Hamor.

19 And the lad did not delay the act, for he was pleased with Jacob's daughter, and he was more honored than all his father's household.

20 And Hamor and his son Shechem came to the gate of their city, and they spoke to the men of the city, saying:

21 These men are at peace with us; let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for indeed the land is wide open before them; let us take their daughters for wives and give them our daughters.

22 Only in this will the men consent to dwell with us, to become one people: if every male among us is circumcised as they are circumcised.

23 Their cattle, their goods, and all their beasts—surely these are ours; only let them agree with us, and they will dwell with us.

24 And all the men who went out the gate of his city listened to Hamor and to Shechem his son, and every male, everyone who went out the gate of his city, was circumcised.

25 On the third day, when they were in pain, two sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, each took his sword, came against the city securely, and killed every male.

26 And Hamor and Shechem his son they slew with the sword, and they took Dinah from Shechem's house and went out.

27 The sons of Jacob came upon the slain and looted the city where they had defiled their sister.

28 Their sheep and their beeve and their asses, and that which was in the city and that which was in the country they took.

29 And all their forces and all their little children and their wives they took and they plundered and all that was in the house.

30 Then Jacob said to Simeon and to Levi, "You have troubled me, making me odious among the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanite and among the Perizzite; and I am few in number, and they will gather themselves against me and strike me, and I and my household will be destroyed."

31 They said, "Will he treat our sister as a prostitute?"


## Chapter 35

1 God said to Jacob, "Arise, go up to Beth-el and dwell there, and make there an altar to God who appeared to you when you fled from the presence of Esau your brother."

2 Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, "Put away the foreign gods that are among you, and purify yourselves, and change your garments."

3 And we will rise and go up to Beth-el, and I will make there an altar to God, who answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me in the way that I walked.

4 And they gave to Jacob all the foreign gods which were in their hand, and the earrings which were in their ears; and Jacob buried them under the oak which was by Shechem.

5 They journeyed, and a great terror of God fell upon the cities around them, and they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.

6 Jacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan, that is, Beth-el, he and all the people who were with him.

7 And he built there an altar and called the place Beth-el, because there God appeared to him when he fled from the presence of his brother.

8 And Deborah, Rebekah's milch nurse, died, and she was buried beneath Beth-el, beneath the oak; and he called its name Allon-bachuth.

9 And God appeared again to Jacob when he came from Padan-aram, and blessed him.

10 God said to him, Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel will be your name, and he called his name Israel.

11 And God said to him, I am God Almighty; be fruitful and multiply: a nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come forth from your loins.

12 And the land that I gave to Abraham and to Isaac, I will give it to you and to your seed after you, the land.

13 And God went up from him at the place where He had spoken with him.

14 Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a stone pillar, and poured a drink offering on it and anointed it with oil.

15 Jacob named the place where God spoke with him Beth-el.

16 They journeyed from Beth-el and there was still little land to go to Ephrath when Rachel gave birth and she struggled in childbirth.

17 And when she struggled in childbirth, the midwife said to her, "Do not fear, for this one also is a son for you."

18 And it came to pass, as her soul was departing because she died, that she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.

19 And Rachel died, and was buried along the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.

20 And Jacob set up a pillar upon her grave; that is the pillar of Rachel's grave to this day.

21 Israel moved and pitched his tent beyond Migdal-eder.

22 And it happened, when Israel was living in that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah, his father's concubine, and Israel heard; and the sons of Jacob were twelve.

23 The sons of Leah, Jacob’s firstborn, were Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.

24 The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

25 And the sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maidservant, were Dan and Naphtali.

26 And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's handmaid, were Gad and Asher; these are the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Padan-aram.

27 Jacob came to Isaac his father at Mamre, Kirjath-arba—which is Hebron—where Abraham and Isaac had resided.

28 And the age of Isaac was one hundred and eighty years.

29 Isaac breathed his last and died and was gathered to his folk, an old man and full of days, and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.


## Chapter 36

1 These are the generations of Esau, who is Edom.

2 Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Aholibamah daughter of Anah daughter of Zibeon the Hivite.

3 And Bashemath the daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebaioth.

4 Adah bore to Esau Eliphaz, and Basmath bore Raguel.

5 Aholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

6 Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, all the persons of his household, his cattle, all his livestock, and all his substance that he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went to another land because of Jacob his brother.

7 For their possessions were great, so that the land of their sojournings could not support them because of their livestock.

8 Esau dwelt in the hill country of Seir, Esau being Edom.

9 These are the generations of Esau, the chief of Edom, in Mount Seir.

10 These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz, son of Adah, Esau's wife; and Reuel, son of Bashemath, Esau's wife.

11 The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.

12 Timna was a concubine to Eliphaz son of Esau, and she bore to Eliphaz Amalek; these are the sons of Adah wife of Esau.

13 And these were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah; these were the sons of Bashemath, Esau's wife.

14 These were the sons of Aholibamah, daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon, wife of Esau, and she bore to Esau Jeush and Jalam and Korah.

15 These are the captains of Esau's sons: the sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau; captain Teman, captain Omar, captain Zepho, captain Kenaz.

16 The captain of Korah, the captain of Gatam, the captain of Amalek—these are the captains of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Adah.

17 And these are the sons of Reuel son of Esau: Nahath the captain, Zerah the captain, Shammah the captain, Mizzah the captain; these are the captains of Reuel in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Bashemath wife of Esau.

18 These are the sons of Aholibamah, Esau's wife: Jeush, Jalam, and Korah, chiefs of Aholibamah, daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.

19 These are the sons of Esau and these are his captains; he is Edom.

20 These are the children of Seir the Horites, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah.

21 Dishon and Ezer and Dishan—these were chiefs of the Horites, the sons of Seir, in the land of Edom.

22 The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam, and Timna was a sister of Lotan.

23 These are the sons of Shobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam.

24 These are the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah, he is Anah who found the mules in the wilderness when he was pasturing the donkeys of Zibeon his father.

25 These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Aholibamah, the daughter of Anah.

26 These are the sons of Dishan: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.

27 These are the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.

28 These are the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.

29 These are the captains of the Horites: captain Lotan, captain Shobal, captain Zibeon, captain Anah.

30 Dishon the captain, Ezer the captain, Dishan the captain—these were the captains of the Hori, according to their captains in the land of Seir.

31 These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before a king reigned over the children of Israel.

32 Bela son of Beor reigned in Edom, and the name of his city was Dinhaban.

33 And Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.

34 And Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

35 Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Avith.

36 Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.

37 And Samlah died, and Saul reigned in his place, from Rehoboth of the river.

38 Saul died, and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.

39 Baal-hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Pau, and the name of his wife was Mehetabeel the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.

40 And these are the names of the captains of Esau according to their families, by their places, by their names: captain Timna, captain Alvah, captain Jetheth.

41 The captain of Aholibamah, the captain of Elah, the captain of Pinon.

42 captain Kenaz, captain Teman, captain Mibzar.

43 The captain Magdiel, the captain Iram—these were the captains of Edom according to their dwellings in the land of their possession; he is Esau, the chief of Edom.


## Chapter 37

1 Jacob dwelt in the land of his father's dwelling, in the land of Canaan.

2 These are the generations of Jacob: Joseph, being seventeen years old, was shepherding with his brothers among the flock, and the young man was with the sons of Bilhah and the sons of Zilpah, his father’s wives; and Joseph brought an evil report of them to their father.

3 Israel loved Joseph more than all his sons because he was a child of his old age, and he made him a coat of many colors.

4 And his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, and they hated him and could not speak to him peacefully.

5 Joseph dreamed a dream and told it to his brothers, and they hated him even more.

6 And he said to them, "Please listen to this dream that I dreamed."

7 And behold, we are binding sheaves in the midst of the field, and behold, my sheaf rose up and also stood upright, and behold, your sheaves gathered around and bowed down to my sheaf.

8 And his brothers said to him, Will you indeed reign over us? Will you indeed have dominion over us? And they hated him yet more for his dreams and for his words.

9 And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it to his brothers; and he said, Behold, I have dreamed yet another dream: and behold, the sun and the moon and eleven stars bowed down to me.

10 He reported to his father and to his brothers, and his father rebuked him and said to him, “What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow down to you to the ground?”

11 His brothers were jealous of him, and his father kept the matter.

12 And his brothers went to pasture the flock of their father in Shechem.

13 Israel said to Joseph, "Are not your brothers pasturing at Shechem? Come, and I will send you to them." And he said to him, "Here I am."

14 So he said to him, “Go now, see how your brothers are faring and how the flock is faring, and bring me word.” So he sent him from the valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

15 And a certain man found him, and behold, he was wandering in the country, and the man asked him, saying, What do you seek?

16 And he said, I am seeking my brothers; please tell me where they are feeding the flock.

17 And the man said, They have departed from here, for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan; so Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.

18 And they saw him from afar, and before he came near to them, they conspired against him to kill him.

19 And each man said to his brother, "Here comes the master of dreams."

20 Now therefore come, let us kill him and cast him into one of the pits, and we will say, "A wild beast has devoured him," and we will see what will become of his dreams.

21 Reuben heard and rescued him from their hand and said, Let us not kill him.

22 Reuben said to them, Do not shed blood; cast him into this cistern that is in the wilderness, but do not lay a hand on him—in order to rescue him from their power, to return him to his father.

23 And it came to pass, when Joseph came to his brothers, that they stripped Joseph of his coat, the coat of many colors that was on him.

24 They took him and cast him into the cistern, and the cistern was empty, there was no water in it.

25 They sat down to eat bread; they lifted their eyes and saw, and behold, a travelling company of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, their camels bearing spiceries, balm, and myrrh, going down to Egypt.

26 Judah said to his brothers, "What profit is it if we kill our brother and cover his blood?"

27 Go and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother, our flesh; and his brothers listened to him.

28 And certain Midianite traders passed by, and they pulled and lifted Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they brought Joseph to Egypt.

29 Reuben returned to the cistern and there was no Joseph in the cistern, and he tore his garments.

30 And he returned to his brothers and said, The boy is not, and where am I to go?

31 They took Joseph's garment and slaughtered a goat and dipped the coat in the blood.

32 They sent the coat of divers colours and brought it to their father and said, “This we have found; recognize, please, whether this coat of your son is his or not.”

33 He recognized it and said, "It is my son's coat; a wild beast has devoured him; Joseph is surely torn in pieces."

34 Jacob tore his apparel, put sackcloth on his loins, and mourned for his son many days.

35 All his sons and all his daughters arose to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, and he said, "Indeed I will go down to my son mourning to the grave," and his father wept for him.

36 And the Midianites sold him to Egypt, to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, captain of the guard.


## Chapter 38

1 And it came to pass at that time that Judah went down from his brothers and turned aside to a certain Adullamite whose name was Hirah.

2 Judah saw there the daughter of a certain Canaanite man whose name was Shua, and he took her and went in to her.

3 And she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Er.

4 She conceived again and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.

5 And she added yet again and bore a son, and called his name Shelah; and she was at Chezib when she bore him.

6 Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

7 Er, Judah's firstborn, was evil in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD put him to death.

8 Judah said to Onan, "Go to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her; raise up seed for your brother."

9 Onan knew that the child would not be his, and whenever he went in to his brother's wife, he would spill his seed on the ground so as not to give seed to his brother.

10 It displeased the LORD what he had done, and he also died.

11 Judah said to Tamar his bride, “Remain a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up,” for he thought, “Lest he also die like his brothers.” So Tamar went and lived in her father’s house.

12 The days passed, and Shua’s daughter, the wife of Judah, died; then Judah was comforted, and he went up to Timnah to his sheep-shearers, he and Hirah his friend the Adullamite.

13 And it was told to Tamar, saying, "Behold, your father in law is going up to Timnah to shear his flock."

14 She removed the garments of her widowhood from upon her and covered herself with a veil, wrapped herself, and sat at the entrance of Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah, for she saw that Shelah was grown, yet she had not been given to him as a wife.

15 And Judah saw her, and he thought her to be a prostitute because she had covered her face.

16 He turned aside to her along the way and said, “Please let me come to you,” for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. And she said, “What will you give me, that you may come to me?”

17 Then he said, I will send a kid of the goats from the flock; and she said, Unless you give a pledge until you send it.

18 He said, “What pledge shall I give you?” and she said, “Your signet, your bracelet, and your staff that is in your hand.” So he gave them to her, and went in to her, and she conceived by him.

19 So she rose and went, and she removed her vail from upon her, and she put on the apparel of her widowhood.

20 Judah sent the kid by the hand of his friend the Adullamite to receive the pledge from the woman, but he did not find her.

21 And he asked the men of her place, saying, Where is the cult prostitute who was at Enaim by the road? And they said, There has been no cult prostitute here.

22 And he returned to Judah and said, I did not find her; and also the men of that place said, There was no prostitute in this place.

23 Then Judah said, “Take her, lest we be shamed. Behold, I sent this kid, but you did not find her.”

24 After about three months, it was reported to Judah, saying, "Tamar your daughter-in-law has committed whoredom, and indeed she is pregnant by whoredom." And Judah said, "Bring her out; let her be burned."

25 As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law, saying, "I am with child by the man to whom these belong—the signet, the cords, and the staff"; and she said, "Please identify to whom these belong—the signet, the cords, and the staff."

26 Judah acknowledged and said, "She has been more righteous than I, because I did not give her to Shelah my son," and he did not continue to have relations with her.

27 And it came to pass when she was in labor, behold, there were twins in her womb.

28 And when she gave birth, his hand emerged; so the midwife took and tied a crimson thread on his hand, saying, "This one came out first."

29 And it came to pass, as he drew back his hand, that behold, his brother came forth; and she said, "Why have you made a breach upon yourself?" So she called his name Perez.

30 Then his brother came out, whose hand had the scarlet thread, and he called his name Zarah.


## Chapter 39

1 And Joseph was brought down to Egypt, and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guards, an Egyptian man, bought him from the hand of the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.

2 And the LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man, and he was in the house of his Egyptian master.

3 And his master saw that the LORD was with him and that all which he did the LORD made successful by his hand.

4 Joseph found favour in his eyes, and he served him; so he appointed him over his household, and all that he had he put into his hand.

5 From then on, when he had put him in charge of his house and of all that he had, the LORD blessed the house of the Egyptian because of Joseph, and the blessing of the LORD was on all that he had, in the house and in the field.

6 And he left all that he had in Joseph's hand, and he knew not anything with him except the bread that he ate; and Joseph was beautiful of form and beautiful of appearance.

7 After these acts, the wife of his master lifted her eyes to Joseph and said, “Lie with me.”

8 But he refused and said to his master's wife, "Behold, my master does not concern himself with what is in the house, and everything he owns he has placed in my hand."

9 He is not greater in this house than I am, nor has he withheld anything from me except you, since you are his wife; how then could I do this great evil and sin against God?

10 And it happened, as she spoke to Joseph day by day, that he did not listen to her to lie beside her, to be with her.

11 And it came to pass on that day, he came into the house to do his work, and there was no man of the men of the house there in the house.

12 And she caught him by his garment, saying, "Lie with me," but he left his garment in her hand and fled, going out outside.

13 And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, that he fled outside.

14 And she called to the men of her house and said to them, "See, he has brought among us a Hebrew man to mock us; he came to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice."

15 When he heard that I had raised my voice and cried out, he abandoned his garment beside me and fled outside.

16 And she left his apparel with her until his lord came to his house.

17 And she spoke to him according to these words, saying, The Hebrew servant whom you brought to us has come to me to mock me.

18 And it happened, when I raised my voice and cried, that he abandoned his garment beside me and fled outside.

19 When his lord heard the words of his wife, which she spoke to him, saying, "According to these words your servant has done to me," then his anger was kindled.

20 And Joseph's master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king's prisoners were confined, and he was there in the prison.

21 And the LORD was with Joseph and showed him favour, and gave his favour in the eyes of the keeper of the prison.

22 Then the captain of the prison committed into Joseph’s hand all the prisoners who were in the prison, and whatever was done there, he was the one who did it.

23 There was no captain of the prison seeing anything in his hand because the LORD was with him and whatever he did the LORD made successful.


## Chapter 40

1 After these acts, the king of Egypt's cupbearer and his baker offended their lord, the king of Egypt.

2 Pharaoh was angry with his two chamberlains, the captain of the butlers and the captain of the bakers.

3 And he gave them into custody in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison where Joseph was confined there.

4 The captain of the guard appointed Joseph to be with them and he served them, and they remained some days in custody.

5 Both of them dreamed a dream, each man his dream interpretation, on the same night—the royal cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison house.

6 Joseph came to them in the morning and saw them, and behold, they were distressed.

7 And he asked Pharaoh's chamberlains who were with him in the prison, his master's officers, saying, "Why are your faces so sad today?"

8 They said to him, "We dreamed a dream, but there is no interpreter for it." Then Joseph said to them, "Are not interpretations from God? Please tell them to me."

9 The captain of the butlers told his dream to Joseph and said to him, In my dream, there was a vine before me.

10 And in the vine three branches; and it budded, brought forth blossoms, and ripened clusters of grapes.

11 And Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and squeezed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.

12 Joseph said to him, "This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days."

13 Yet in three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office, and you will place Pharaoh’s cup in his hand according to the former custom when you were his cupbearer.

14 But remember me when it goes well with you, and please do for me a kindness by remembering me to Pharaoh and bringing me out of this house.

15 For I was stolen away from the land of the Hebrews, and even here I have done no fault, for they put me into the dungeon.

16 And the captain of the bakers saw that the interpretation was good, and he said to Joseph, "Also I in my dream, and behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head."

17 And in the upper basket were all kinds of Pharaoh's food, the work of a baker, and the birds were eating them from the basket upon my head.

18 Joseph answered and said, "This is its interpretation: the three baskets are three days."

19 In yet three days Pharaoh will lift up your head from upon you and will hang you on a tree, and the birds will eat your flesh from upon you.

20 On the third day, the day of Pharaoh's birth, he made a banquet for all his servants, and he lifted up the head of the chief of the cupbearers and the head of the chief of the bakers among his servants.

21 And he restored the captain of the cupbearers to his butlership, and he placed the cup upon Pharaoh's hand.

22 And the captain of the bakers he hanged, as Joseph had interpreted to them.

23 But the captain of the cupbearers did not remember Joseph, and he forgot him.


## Chapter 41

1 After two years of days, Pharaoh dreamed, and behold, he was standing by the river.

2 And behold, from the river came up seven cows, handsome in appearance and healthy in flesh, and they grazed in the meadow.

3 And behold, seven other cows, evil in appearance and lean in flesh, came up after them out of the river, and stood beside the cows by the bank of the river.

4 The lean cows with poor appearance and lean flesh ate the seven cows with good appearance and healthy flesh, and Pharaoh awoke.

5 And he slept again and dreamed a second dream, and behold, seven ears of grain came up on one stalk, healthy and good.

6 And behold, seven ears, thin and scorched by the east wind, were growing after them.

7 The thin ears devoured the seven healthy and full ears, and Pharaoh awoke, and behold, it was a dream.

8 And it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men; and Pharaoh told them his dream, but none could interpret it for Pharaoh.

9 The captain of the butlers spoke to Pharaoh, saying, "My fault I remember today."

10 Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me in the prison of the captain of the guards, me and the chief of the bakers.

11 And we dreamed a dream in one night, I and he; each man according to his interpretation of the dream we dreamed.

12 And there with us was a Hebrew lad, a servant to the chief of the cooks, and we told him our dreams, and he interpreted for us each according to his dream.

13 And it was that as he interpreted for us, so it was: he restored me to my place, but he hanged him.

14 Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they hurried him out of the dungeon, and he shaved and changed his apparel, and he came to Pharaoh.

15 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I dreamed a dream, but there is no interpreter for it, and I have heard about you, saying, 'You hear a dream to interpret it.'"

16 Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, "Not I; God will answer concerning the welfare of Pharaoh."

17 Pharaoh spoke to Joseph, "In my dream, behold, I was standing on the bank of the river."

18 And behold, from the river came up seven cows, healthy in body and fine in form, and they grazed in the meadow.

19 And behold, seven other cows, lean and very uncomely in form and lean in flesh, came up after them; I have never seen such in all the land of Egypt for badness.

20 And the lean and bad cows ate the seven first healthy cows.

21 And they came into my midst, but it was not known that they came into my midst, and their appearance was bad as at the beginning, and I awoke.

22 And I saw in my dream: behold, seven ears of corn growing on one stalk, full and good.

23 And behold, seven ears, thin and scorched by the east wind, were growing after them.

24 And the thin ears devoured the seven good ears, and I said to the magicians, but there was no one to tell me.

25 Joseph said to Pharaoh, "The dream of Pharaoh is one: what God is doing, he has declared to Pharaoh."

26 Seven good cows are seven years; and the seven good ears are seven years; one dream it is.

27 And the seven lean and ill-favored cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears scorched by the east wind shall be seven years of famine.

28 This is the act that I spoke to Pharaoh: God is doing; He has shown Pharaoh.

29 Behold, seven years are coming, a great abundance throughout all the land of Egypt.

30 Then seven years of famine will arise after them, and all the abundance will be forgotten in the land of Egypt, and the famine will consume the land.

31 And abundance shall not be known in the land because of the famine that shall come afterward, for it shall be very severe.

32 And concerning the repeating of the dream to Pharaoh twice, it is because the matter is established by God, and God is hastening to do it.

33 And now let Pharaoh see a man discerning and wise, and set him over the land of Egypt.

34 Let Pharaoh act and appoint overseers over the land of Egypt, and let him take one-fifth of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.

35 And they shall gather all the food of the good years that are coming, and store up grain under the authority of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and preserve it.

36 And the food shall be a store for the land against the seven years of famine which will be in the land of Egypt, so that the land is not cut off by the famine.

37 The advice pleased Pharaoh and all his servants.

38 Then Pharaoh said to his servants, "Can we find such a man as this, in whom is the spirit of God?"

39 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, After God has made known to you all this, there is no one so discerning and wise as you.

40 You shall be over my household, and by your mouth shall all my people be ruled; only the throne will I make greater than you.

41 Pharaoh said to Joseph, See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt.

42 Pharaoh removed the ring from his hand and gave it to Joseph's hand, and clothed him in linen garments and placed the gold chain upon his neck.

43 And he caused him to ride in his second chariot which belonged to him, and they cried before him "Bow down!" and he set him over all the land of Egypt.

44 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I am Pharaoh, and without you no man will raise his hand or his foot in all the land of Egypt."

45 Pharaoh named Joseph Zaphnath-paaneah and gave him Asenath daughter of Poti-pherah priest of On as wife, and Joseph went out over the land of Egypt.

46 And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood before Pharaoh king of Egypt, and Joseph went out from the presence of Pharaoh and passed throughout all the land of Egypt.

47 And the land produced in seven years of abundance by handfuls.

48 He gathered in all the food of the seven years that were in the land of Egypt, and he placed food in the cities; the food of the field of each city he placed within it.

49 And Joseph gathered corn like the sand of the sea, very much, exceedingly; until he stopped numbering it, for there was no number.

50 And to Joseph were born two sons before the year of the famine came, whom Asenath daughter of Poti-pherah priest of On bore to him.

51 Joseph named the eldest the name Manasseh, because God has made him forget all his toil and all the house of his father.

52 And the name of the second he called Ephraim, because God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.

53 The seven years of abundance that were in the land of Egypt came to an end.

54 The seven years of famine began to come as Joseph had said, and there was dearth in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.

55 And all the land of Egypt was famished, and the people cried out to Pharaoh for food, and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, Go to Joseph; what he says to you, do.

56 And the famine was upon all the face of the land, and Joseph opened all that was in them and sold grain to Egypt, and the famine strengthened in the land of Egypt.

57 And all the land came into Egypt to Joseph to buy grain, because the famine was severe throughout all the land.


## Chapter 42

1 Jacob saw that there was corn in Egypt, and Jacob said to his sons, "Why do you look at one another?"

2 And he said, Behold, I have heard that there is corn in Egypt; go down there and buy for us from there, so that we may live and not die.

3 And ten of Joseph's brothers went down to buy corn from Egypt.

4 And Jacob did not send Benjamin, Joseph's brother, with his brothers, for he said, "Lest mischief befall him."

5 The sons of Israel came to buy grain among those who came, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.

6 And Joseph, he was the ruler over the land, he was the provider for all the people of the land, and Joseph’s brothers came and bowed down before him, faces to the ground.

7 Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he acted like a stranger toward them and spoke harshly to them, saying, “Where have you come from?” And they said, “From the land of Canaan to buy food.”

8 Joseph recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him.

9 Joseph remembered the dreams he had dreamed for them, and said to them, "You are spies; you have come to see the nakedness of the land."

10 They said to him, No, my lord, your servants have come to buy food.
