11 We are all sons of one man; we are honest men; your servants have not been spies.

12 And he said to them, No, indeed, you have come to see the nakedness of the land.

13 We are twelve bondmen, brothers, sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is with our father today, and the one is not with us.

14 Joseph said to them, "It is what I spoke to you, saying, 'You are spies.'"

15 By this you shall be tested: as Pharaoh lives, you shall not leave this place unless your younger brother comes here.

16 Send one of you to fetch your brother, while you remain confined, so that your words may be tested to see if there is truth in you; otherwise, as Pharaoh lives, you are spies.

17 And he gathered them to a guard for three days.

18 Then Joseph said to them on the third day, "Do this and live: I fear God."

19 If you are honest, your brother will be kept under guard in your prison, but you go, bring grain for the famine in your households.

20 And your younger brother you shall bring to me, so that your words may be confirmed and you will not die. And they did so.

21 And one said to another, We are certainly guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul when he pleaded with us, and we did not listen; therefore this distress has come upon us.

22 Reuben answered them, saying, "Did I not speak to you, saying, 'Do not sin against the boy'? But you did not listen; and also, his blood—behold, it is being sought."

23 And they did not know that Joseph was listening, for there was an interpreter between them.

24 Then he turned away from them and wept; then he returned to them and spoke to them, and he took Simeon from among them and bound him before their eyes.

25 Joseph commanded, and they filled their bags with wheat and returned each man's silver to his sack, and provided them with provisions for the journey, and he did so for them.

26 And they lifted their victuals upon their asses and went from there.

27 And one of them opened his sack to give provender to his ass at the lodging place, and he saw his money, and there it was at the mouth of his sack.

28 And he said to his brothers, My money was returned, and also, behold, it is in my sack; and their heart failed them and they trembled, each to his brother, saying, What is this that God has done to us?

29 And they came to Jacob their father in the land of Canaan and told him all that had befallen them, saying:

30 The man, lord of the land, spoke harshly to us and treated us as if we were spies in the land.

31 And we said to him, We are honest men; we were not spies.

32 We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is not here, and the youngest is today with our father in the land of Canaan.

33 And the man, the lord of the land, said to us, "By this I will know that you are honest men: leave your one brother with me, and take grain for the famine of your houses, and go."

34 And bring your youngest brother to me, that I may know you are not spies, that you are honest; your brothers I will give to you, and the land you shall trade in.

35 And it happened, as they were emptying their sacks, that behold, each man's bundle of money was in his sack; and when they saw their bundles of money, they and their father, they were afraid.

36 Then Jacob their father said to them, I am bereaved of my children: Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and Benjamin you would take; upon me are all these things come.

37 Reuben said to his father, "Put my two children to death if I do not bring him back to you. Deliver him into my hand, and I will return him to you."

38 And he said, My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead and he alone is left; and if mischief meets him along the journey that you will take, then you will bring down my gray hairs with grief to the grave.


## Chapter 43

1 And the famine was heavy in the land.

2 And it came to pass, when they had finished eating the corn which they had brought from Egypt, that their father said to them, “Go back, buy for us a little food.”

3 Judah said to him, "The man solemnly warned us, saying, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.'"

4 If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy food for you.

5 But if you do not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.'

6 Israel said, Why have you treated me badly, to tell the man, Do you still have another brother?

7 And they said, A certain man questioned us and our kindred, saying, Is your father still alive? Is there yet another brother to you? And we told him according to these words. How could we possibly know that he would say, Bring down your brother?

8 Judah said to Israel his father, "Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, and we will live and not die, both we and you and our little children."

9 I will guarantee him; from my hand you shall demand him. If I do not bring him to you and set him before your face, then I will be guilty to you all the days.

10 For if we had not delayed, by now we would have returned this twice.

11 Israel their father said to them, If so, then do this: take the best fruit of the land in your vessels and bring down as a gift to the man a little balm and a little honey, spicery, myrrh, nuts, and almonds.

12 Take double money in your hand, and return the money that was replaced in the mouth of your sacks—perhaps it was an oversight.

13 And take your brothers and arise, return to the man.

14 And God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, so that he may send back to you your other brother and Benjamin; and I, as for me, if I am bereaved, I am bereaved.

15 The men took the gift and twice the amount of silver in their hands, and Benjamin, and they rose and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.

16 Joseph saw Benjamin with them, and said to his steward, "Bring the men into the house; slaughter an animal and prepare, for the men will eat with me at midday."

17 And the man did as Joseph had said, and the man brought the men to Joseph's house.

18 The men were afraid when they were brought to Joseph's house, and they said, "Because of the silver that was returned in our sacks at the beginning, we are being brought in so that he may fall upon us and seize us as bondmen, along with our donkeys."

19 And they drew near to the man who was over Joseph's house and spoke to him at the entrance of the house.

20 My lord, we went down at first to buy food.

21 When we came to the lodging place, we opened our sacks, and there was each man’s money in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight, and we returned it in our hands.

22 And money other we brought down in our hand to buy food; we did not know who put our money in our sacks.

23 Do not fear, may the peace be with you; your God and the God of your fathers has given you treasure in your sacks; your silver has come to me. And he brought out Simeon to them.

24 And the man brought the men into Joseph's house, and he gave water and they washed their feet, and he gave provender for their asses.

25 They prepared the gift until Joseph came at midday, for they had heard that there they would eat food.

26 Joseph came into the house, and they brought to him the gift that was in their hand into the house, and they bowed down to him to the ground.

27 He asked them about their welfare and said, “Is your old father, of whom you spoke, still alive and well?”

28 And they said, "Your servant our father is well; he is still alive." And they bowed their heads and prostrated themselves.

29 He lifted his eyes and saw Benjamin his brother, the son of his mother, and said, “Is this your younger brother of whom you told me?” And he said, “God grant you favor, my son.”

30 And Joseph made haste, for his bowels did yearn toward his brother; and he sought to weep, and went into the inner chamber and wept there.

31 And he washed his face and went out and restrained himself and said, Set bread.

32 They put for him alone, and for them alone, and for the Egyptians who ate with him alone, because the Egyptians could not eat bread with the Hebrews, for it was an abomination to the Egyptians.

33 And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the younger according to his youth; and the men looked at one another in astonishment.

34 He lifted the gifts from his presence to them, and Benjamin's gift was five times greater in quantity than the gifts of all the others; they drank and became drunk with him.


## Chapter 44

1 And he commanded the one in charge of his court, saying, Fill the sacks of the men with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man’s money in the mouth of his sack.

2 And put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the little sack, with the money for his corn; and he did as Joseph had spoken.

3 The morning was light, and the men were sent away, they and their donkeys.

4 They went out of the city but had not gone far, and Joseph said to the man in charge of his household, "Go, pursue after the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?'"

5 Is not this the thing in which my lord drinks and which he uses for divination? You have done evil in what you have done.

6 And he overtook them and spoke to them these words.

7 And they said to him, Why should my lord speak such words? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing.

8 Behold, the money which we found in the mouth of our sacks we returned to you from the land of Canaan; and how should we steal silver or gold from the house of your lord?

9 which may be found with your servants and die, and we ourselves also shall become servants to our lord.

10 And he said, Even now, according to your word, so it is: he with whom it is found shall be my servant, and you shall be blameless.

11 And they hurried, and each man lowered his sack to the ground, and each man opened his sack.

12 And he searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in the sack of Benjamin.

13 They tore their garments, and each man laid [his] garment on his donkey, and they returned to the city.

14 Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, and he was still there, and they fell before him to the ground.

15 And Joseph said to them, "What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that a man such as I will certainly practice divination?"

16 Judah said, "What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak or how shall we clear ourselves? God has found the iniquity of your servants. Here we are, servants to my lord, both we and also the one with whom the cup was found in his hand."

17 Far be it from me to do this thing; the man with whom the cup is found in his hand shall be my servant, but as for you, go up in peace to your father.

18 Then Judah drew near to him and said, "Please, my lord, let your servant speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant, for you are as Pharaoh."

19 My lord asked his servants, saying, Is there to you a father or a brother?

20 And we said to my lord There is to us an old father and a young child of his old age and his brother died and he alone was left to his mother and his father loves him

21 And she said to your servants, "Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes upon him."

22 And we said to my lord, The boy cannot leave his father, and if he leaves his father, he will die.

23 And she said to your bondmen, If your brother the younger does not come down with you, you shall not add to see my face.

24 And it came to pass, when we went up to your servant my father, that we told him the words of my lord.

25 And our father said, Return, buy for us a little food.

26 And we said, We cannot go down; if our younger brother is with us, then we will go down; for we cannot face the man unless our younger brother is with us.

27 And my father said to us, You know that two my wife bore to me.

28 And the one went out from me, and I said, "Surely torn he has been torn; and I have not seen him until now."

29 And you shall also take this one from before me, and mischief shall befall him, and you shall bring down my gray hairs in adversity to the grave.

30 And now, when I came to your servant my father, the boy was not with us, and his life was bound up in the life of the boy.

31 And it shall be when he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray head of your servant our father in sorrow to the grave.

32 For your servant became surety for the boy with my father, saying, If I do not bring him back to you, then I will have sinned against my father all my days.

33 Now please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go up with his brothers.

34 How can I go up to my father, when the boy is not with me, lest I see the evil that would find my father?


## Chapter 45

1 And Joseph could not restrain himself before all those standing before him, so he called out, “Remove every man from before me.” And no man remained with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.

2 And he gave his voice to weeping; and the Egyptians heard, and Pharaoh's household heard.

3 Joseph said to his brothers, "I am Joseph. Is my father still alive?" And his brothers could not answer him, for they were terrified before his presence.

4 Then Joseph said to his brothers, "Come near to me," and they came near. Then he said, "I am Joseph your brother, whom you sold to Egypt."

5 And now, do not grieve and do not be angry in your countenance because you sold me here, for to preserve life did God send me before you.

6 For this is the second year of the famine in the midst of the land, and there will be yet five years without sowing and harvest.

7 And God sent me before you to place a remnant in the land and to keep you alive by a great deliverance.

8 And now it was not you who sent me here, but God, and He has made me a father to Pharaoh and a lord over all his household and a ruler over all the land of Egypt.

9 Hurry and go up to my father and say to him, Thus says your son Joseph, God has made me lord of all Egypt; come down to me, do not stand still.

10 You shall dwell in the land of Goshen and be near me, you and your sons and your sons’ sons, and your flocks and your herds and all that you have.

11 I would have provided for you there, because there are still five years of famine, so that you and your household and all that you have would not be impoverished.

12 And behold, your eyes and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see that it is my mouth which speaks to you.

13 And you shall tell my father of all my glory in Egypt, and of all that you have seen, and you shall quickly bring my father down here.

14 And he fell on Benjamin’s neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck.

15 And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them, and after that his brothers spoke with him.

16 And the voice was heard in Pharaoh's court saying, Joseph's brothers have come, and it was good in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants.

17 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Say to your brothers, 'Do this: load your beasts and go, return to the land of Canaan.'"

18 And take your father and your households and come to me, and I will give you the good of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat the best of the land.

19 But you have been commanded this: take for yourselves wagons from the land of Egypt for your families and your wives, and carry your father and come.

20 Do not let your eyes spare your possessions, for the good of all the land of Egypt is yours.

21 So the children of Israel did thus: Joseph gave them wagons according to Pharaoh's command, and gave them provisions for the journey.

22 To all of them he gave each man changes of clothing, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred silver coins and five changes of clothing.

23 And to his father he sent in this manner: ten male donkeys carrying the goodness of Egypt, and ten female donkeys carrying wheat and bread and food for his father along the journey.

24 And he sent his brothers away and they went; and he said to them, Do not become agitated along the journey.

25 They went up from Egypt and came into the land of Canaan to Jacob their father.

26 They told him, saying, Joseph is still alive, and indeed he rules over all the land of Egypt; but his heart failed, for he did not believe them.

27 They spoke to him all the words of Joseph that he had spoken to them, and he saw the carts that Joseph had sent to carry him, and the spirit of Jacob their father revived.

28 Israel said, "It is enough; Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die."


## Chapter 46

1 Israel journeyed with all that he had and came to Beer-shebah and offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.

2 And God said to Israel in a night vision, and he said, Jacob, Jacob, and he said, Here I am.

3 And he said, I am God the God of your father: do not fear going down to Egypt, for there I will make you a great nation.

4 I will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again; and Joseph will put his hand upon your eyes.

5 Jacob rose up from Beer-shebah, and the sons of Israel carried Jacob their father, and their little children, and their wives, in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.

6 They took their cattle and the goods they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and Jacob and all his seed came to Egypt with him.

7 His sons and his sons' sons with him, his daughters and his daughters' daughters and all his offspring he brought with him to Egypt.

8 These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt, Jacob and his sons; Reuben was the eldest son of Jacob.

9 And the sons of Reuben: Enoch and Pallu and Hezron and Carmi.

10 The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Saul the son of the Canaanite woman.

11 The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

12 And the sons of Judah were Er and Onan and Shelah and Perez and Zerah; and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan; and the sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.

13 The sons of Issachar: Tola and Phuvah and Job and Shimron.

14 And the sons of Zebulun: Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.

15 These are the sons of Leah whom she bore to Jacob in Padan-aram, and Dinah his daughter; all the persons of his sons and his daughters were thirty-three.

16 The sons of Gad were Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.

17 The sons of Asher were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah, and their sister Serah; and the sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel.

18 These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to Leah his daughter, and she bore these to Jacob, sixteen persons.

19 The sons of Rachel, Jacob’s wife, were Joseph and Benjamin.

20 Manasseh and Ephraim were born to Joseph in the land of Egypt, whom Asenath daughter of Poti-pherah priest of On bore to him.

21 The sons of Benjamin: Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.

22 These are the children of Rachel who were born to Jacob, all persons fourteen.

23 The sons of Dan are Hushim.

24 The sons of Naphtali: Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.

25 These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter, and she bore these to Jacob, all souls seven.

26 All the persons who came to Jacob in Egypt, coming out of his loins, besides the wives of Jacob’s sons; every person, sixty and six.

27 And the sons of Joseph who were born to him in Egypt: two persons; all the persons of the house of Jacob who came into Egypt: seventy.

28 And Judah he sent before him to Joseph to prepare before him the way to Goshen, and they came to the land of Goshen.

29 Joseph yoked his chariot and went up to meet Israel his father in Goshen. When he appeared to him, he fell on his neck and wept on his neck still more.

30 Israel said to Joseph, I will die this time after seeing your face, for you are still alive.

31 Joseph said to his brothers and to his father’s family, I will go up and inform Pharaoh, and I will say to him, My brothers and my father’s household who are in the land of Canaan have come to me.

32 And the men were shepherds, for they were keepers of livestock; and their sheep, their cattle, and all that they had they brought.

33 And it shall be, when Pharaoh calls to you and says, "What is your business?"

34 And you shall say, “Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,” in order that you may dwell in the land of Goshen, because every shepherd of sheep is an abomination to the Egyptians.


## Chapter 47

1 Joseph came and told Pharaoh, and he said, My father and my brothers and their flock and their herd and all that they have have come from the land of Canaan, and behold, they are in the land of Goshen.

2 And from the edge of his brothers he took five men and set them before Pharaoh.

3 Pharaoh said to his kindred, "What is your occupation?" And they said to Pharaoh, "Your servants are shepherds, both we and our fathers."

4 They said to Pharaoh, We have come to sojourn in the land, for there is no pasture for the flock that belongs to your servants, because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan; now therefore, please let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen.

5 Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Your father and your brothers have come to you."

6 The land of Egypt is before you; in the best of the land settle your father and your brothers—they may dwell in the land of Goshen—and if you know that there are capable men among them, appoint them as overseers of my livestock over what is mine.

7 Joseph brought Jacob his father and presented him before Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.

8 Pharaoh said to Jacob, How many are the days of the years of your life?

9 Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The age of my pilgrimage is one hundred and thirty years; few and evil have been the days of the life of my years, and they have not reached the age of the life of my fathers in the days of their pilgrimage."

10 Jacob blessed Pharaoh and went out from the presence of Pharaoh.

11 Joseph settled his father and his brothers, and he gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.

12 Joseph provided for his father, his brothers, and all his household with food according to the number of the little children.

13 And there was no bread in all the land, for the famine was very severe indeed, and the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished by reason of the famine.

14 Joseph gathered all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan in exchange for the corn that they bought, and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh's house.

15 The money was gone from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan, and all the Egyptians came to Joseph, saying, "Give us food; why should we die before you? For the money has come to an end."

16 And Joseph said, Give your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock, if your money is gone.

17 They brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, and for the flocks, and for the herds of cattle, and for the donkeys; and he sustained them with food for all their livestock that year.

18 And that year ended, and in the second year they came to him and said to my lord, We will not hide from my lord that the silver is spent, and the cattle are my lord's; nothing remains before my lord except our bodies and our land.

19 Why are we and our land dying before your eyes? Buy us and our land for food, and we and our land will become slaves to Pharaoh; give seed, that we may live and not die, and the land may not become desolate.

20 Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, for the Egyptians sold each man his field because the famine had grown severe upon them, and the land became Pharaoh’s.

21 And he passed the folk through the cities, from the edge of the border of Egypt even to its uttermost end.

22 Only the country of the priests he did not buy, for a decree for the priests from Pharaoh was their portion; they ate the portion which Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their land.

23 Joseph said to the folk, "Here I have acquired you and your land today for Pharaoh; here is seed for you, and you shall sow the land."

24 And it shall be, when the produce comes, that you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and the four parts of the hand shall be yours for the seed of the field and for your food and for what is in your houses and for the eating of your little children.

25 They said, "You have saved our lives; let us find favour in the sight of my lord, and we will be servants to Pharaoh."

26 Joseph appointed it as a decree to this day concerning the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, the fifth; only the land of the priests alone was not for Pharaoh.

27 Israel settled in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen, and they held it and greatly increased and multiplied.

28 Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years, and the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were one hundred and forty-seven years.

29 The days of Israel's life drew near to dying, and he called to his son Joseph and said to him, "If now I have found favour in your eyes, please place your hand under my thigh: you will show me kindness and truth—do not please bury me in Egypt.

30 And I will lie down with my forefathers, and you shall bring me up from Egypt, and bury me in their burial place. And he said, I will do according to your word.

31 And he said, Swear to me; and he swore to him; and Israel bowed on the head of the bed.


## Chapter 48

1 After these things Joseph was told, Behold, your father is ill; so he took his two sons with him, Manasseh and Ephraim.

2 He told Jacob and said, "Behold, your son Joseph is coming to you"; and Israel strengthened himself and sat upon the bed.

3 Jacob said to Joseph, God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me.

4 And he said to me, behold, I make you fruitful and multiply you, and I set you for an assembly of peoples, and I give this land to your seed after you, an everlasting possession.

5 And now, your two sons who were born to you in the land of Egypt until I came to you in Egypt, they are mine: Ephraim and Manasseh; like Reuben and Simeon they shall be to me.

6 And your descendants whom you father after them shall be yours, called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.

7 And as for me, when I came from Padan, Rachel died on me in the land of Canaan on the way, while there was still a little land to go to Ephrath, and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, which is Bethlehem.

8 Israel saw the sons of Joseph and said, "Who are these?"

9 Joseph said to his father, "They are my children, whom God has given me in this place." And he said, "Please bring them to me, and I will bless them."

10 And the eyes of Israel were dim with age, unable to see; so he brought them near to him, and he kissed them and embraced them.

11 Israel said to Joseph, I did not expect to see your face, and behold, God has let me see even your offspring too.

12 And Joseph brought them out from before his knees, and he bowed himself with his face to the ground.

13 Joseph took the two of them, Ephraim to his right, on Israel’s left, and Manasseh to his left, on Israel’s right, and brought them near to him.

14 Israel stretched out his right hand and placed it on the head of Ephraim, though he was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh, arranging his hands deliberately because Manasseh was the firstborn.

15 And he blessed Joseph and said, The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has shepherded me from my youth to this day—

16 The ambassador who redeems me from every evil, bless the children, and let my name and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac be called on them, and let them increase greatly in the midst of the land.

17 Joseph saw that his father was placing his right hand on Ephraim’s head, and it displeased him in his eyes, so he grasped his father’s hand to move it from over Ephraim’s head to Manasseh’s head.

18 Joseph said to his father: Not so, my father, for this is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head.

19 And his father refused and said, I know, my son, I know; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; but truly, his younger brother shall become greater than he, and his offspring shall be the fullness of the nations.

20 And he blessed them on that day, saying, "By you shall Israel bless, saying, 'May God make you like Ephraim and like Manasseh'"; and he set Ephraim before Manasseh.

21 Then Israel said to Joseph, Behold, I am about to die; but God will be with you, and bring you back to the land of your fathers.

22 I have given you one portion more than your brothers, which I took from the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.


## Chapter 49

1 Jacob called to his sons and said, Gather and I will tell you what will befall you in the latter days.

2 Gather and hear, sons of Jacob, and hear Israel your father.

3 Reuben, you are my eldest son, my ability and the beginning of my strength, preeminent in dignity and preeminent in power.

4 Unstable as a flood, do not excel, because you went up to your father's bed, then you defiled my couch.

5 Simeon and Levi are brothers; their weapons are instruments of cruelty.

6 Do not let my soul enter their secret counsel; do not unite my glory with their assembly, for in their anger they slew a man, and in their self-will they houghed an ox.

7 Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel; I will divide them in Jacob and scatter them in Israel.

8 Judah, your kindred shall praise you; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father's sons shall bow down to you.

9 Judah is a young lion; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down, he crouched as a (young) lion, and as a (great) lion—who shall rouse him up?

10 The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his legs, until Shiloh come; and to him shall the peoples obey.

11 Binding his foal to the vine, his donkey's colt to the choice wine; he washed his garment in wine, his clothing in the blood of grapes.

12 His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

13 Zebulun to a coast of seas shall dwell and he to a coast of ships and his border on Sidon.

14 Issachar is a strong ass, crouching between the sheepfolds.

15 He saw that rest was good and the land that it was pleasant, so he bent his shoulder to bear a burden and became a servant to tribute.

16 Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.

17 Let Dan be a serpent along the path, an adder upon the way, biting the horse's heel so that his rider falls backward.

18 For your salvation I waited, O LORD.

19 Gad, a troop will raid him, but he will raid their heel.

20 From Asher, his bread is rich, and he will yield royal dainties.

21 Naphtali is a hind let loose that gives goodly words.

22 A fruitful son is Joseph, a fruitful son by a spring; daughters march upon the wall.

23 And they embittered him and mocked him; and those given to arrows shot at him.

24 His bow remained firm, and the arms of his hands grew strong from the Mighty One of Jacob, from there the shepherd, the stone of Israel.

25 May God your father help you and may the Almighty bless you, with the blessings of heaven above, with the blessings of the deep crouching beneath, with the blessings of the breasts and of the womb.

26 The blessings of your father have prevailed over the blessings of those before him, up to the utmost bound of the everlasting hills; they shall be on the head of Joseph and on the crown of the one separated from his brothers.

27 Benjamin is a wolf that seizes prey; in the morning he devours the prey, and in the evening he divides the spoil.

28 All these are the tribes of Israel, twelve; and this is what their father spoke to them, and he blessed them—each man according to his blessing he blessed them.

29 And he commanded them and said to them, I am being gathered to my folk; bury me with my forefathers, to the cave which is in the field of Ephron the Hittite.

30 In the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought as a possession for a burying place, the field from Ephron the Hittite.

31 There they buried Abraham and his wife Sarah; there they buried Isaac and his wife Rebekah; and there I buried Leah.

32 the cattle and the cave that are in the field from the sons of Heth

33 Jacob finished instructing his sons and drew his legs up onto the bed, then he breathed his last and was gathered to his people.


## Chapter 50

1 Joseph fell on the face of his father and wept over him and kissed him.

2 Joseph commanded his servants, the physicians, to embalm his father, and the physicians embalmed Israel.

3 And they filled for him forty days, for thus are filled the days of embalming, and the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.

4 The days of his mourning passed, and Joseph spoke to Pharaoh's court, saying, "If I have found favour in your eyes, please speak to Pharaoh's ears, saying:"

5 My father swore to me, saying, Behold, I am dying; in my burying place, which I dug for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me. Now therefore, let me go up, I pray, and bury my father, and I will return.

6 Pharaoh said, "Go up and bury your father as he made you swear."

7 Joseph went up to bury his father, and all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his court, and all the elders of the land of Egypt, went up with him.

8 And all the house of Joseph, and his brothers, and the house of his father went down, but their little ones, and their flocks, and their herds they left in the land of Goshen.

9 And he went up with him, both chariot and horsemen, and the camp was very heavy.

10 They came to the threshing floor beyond the Jordan and mourned there with a great and very heavy lamentation; and he made mourning for his father for seven days.

11 The Canaanite who lived in the land saw the mourning at the threshing place of Atad, and said, This is a heavy mourning for the Egyptians; therefore they called its name Abel-mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.

12 And his sons did for him so, just as he had commanded them.

13 And his sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham had bought as a possession for a burying place from Ephron the Hittite, opposite Mamre.

14 Then Joseph returned to Egypt, he and his brothers, and all who had gone up with him to bury his father, after he had buried his father.

15 And the brothers of Joseph saw that their father was dead, and Joseph might hate them and truly repay them all the evil that they had done to him.

16 They charged Joseph, saying, "Your father commanded before his death, saying:"

17 So you shall say to Joseph: Please forgive now the rebellion of your brothers and their sin, for they did evil to you; and now, please forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father. And Joseph wept when they spoke to him.

18 And his brothers also went and fell before his face and said, "Here we are, ready for you as servants."

19 And Joseph said to them, Do not be afraid, for am I instead of God?

20 But you intended against me for adversity, God intended it for good, in order to do as this day, to keep alive a numerous people.

21 And now do not fear, I will provide for you and your families, and he comforted them and spoke kindly to them.

22 Joseph settled in Egypt, he and his father's family, and Joseph lived one hundred and ten years.

23 Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation; even the children of Machir, son of Manasseh, were born upon Joseph’s knees.

24 Joseph said to his brothers, I am about to die, but God will surely remember you and bring you up from this land to the land which he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

25 And Joseph made the children of Israel swear, saying, God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.

26 Joseph died at the age of one hundred and ten years, and they embalmed him and placed him in a coffin in Egypt.


# EXODUS

## Chapter 1

1 These are the names of the children of Israel who came to Egypt, each man and his household with Jacob.

2 Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah.

3 Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin.

4 Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.

5 All the persons who went out from the loins of Jacob were seventy persons, and Joseph was in Egypt.

6 And Joseph died, and all his brothers, and all that generation.

7 The sons of Israel were fruitful and swarmed and multiplied and became very mighty; indeed, the land was filled with them.

8 A new king arose over Egypt who did not know Joseph.

9 And he said to his folk, Behold, the people of the children of Israel is numerous and mighty from us.

10 Come, let us deal wisely with him, lest he multiply, and when war occurs, he also joins our enemies, fights against us, and departs from the land.

11 They set taskmasters over them to afflict them with burdens, and they built for Pharaoh the store cities Pithom and Raamses.

12 And the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread; so the Egyptians came to loathe the presence of the people of Israel.

13 The Egyptian afflicted the children of Israel with cruelty.

14 And they embittered their lives with hard service in clay and in brick, and in every service in the field—all their service in which they served them with rigour.

15 And the king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives, the name of the one being Shiphrah and the name of the second Puah.

16 And he said, When you give birth to the Hebrew women, and you see upon the stools, if it is a son, then you shall kill him, but if it is a daughter, she shall live.

17 The midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt had spoken to them, and they kept the boys alive.

18 The king of Egypt called the midwives and said to them, "Why have you done this act, that you have kept the boys alive?"

19 And the midwives said to Pharaoh that not as the Egyptian women are the Hebrew women, for they are lively; before the midwife comes to them, they have given birth.

20 God dealt well with the midwives, and the people multiplied and grew very strong.

21 And it was because the midwives feared God that he made for them houses.

22 Pharaoh commanded all his people, saying, Every son who is born, you shall cast into the river, but every daughter you shall allow to live.


## Chapter 2

1 A man from the house of Levi went and took a daughter of Levi.

2 The woman conceived and bore a son, and she saw that he was good, and she hid him three months.

3 And she could no longer hide the boy, so she took for him an ark of bulrushes, and she sealed it with slime and with pitch, and she placed the boy in it, and she placed it in the weeds at the edge of the river.

4 And his sister stood at a distance to know what would be done to him.

5 Then the daughter of Pharaoh went down to bathe upon the river, and her maidens walked beside the river; and she saw the ark among the reeds, and she sent her handmaid and took it.

6 She opened and saw the boy, and there was a lad crying, and she had compassion on him and said, This is one of the Hebrew children.

7 And his sister said to Pharaoh’s daughter, Shall I go and call a nursing woman from the Hebrews, that she may nurse the child for you?

8 Then Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Go," and the virgin went and called the mother of the child.

9 Then Pharaoh's daughter said to her, "Take this child and nurse him for me, and I will give you your wages"; so the woman took the child and nursed him.

10 The boy grew, and she brought him to Pharaoh's daughter, and he became her son, and she called his name Moses, saying, "Because from the water I drew him out."

11 In those days Moses had grown up and went out to his brothers and saw their burdens, and he saw an Egyptian man striking a Hebrew man, one of his brothers.

12 He looked this way and that and saw that there was no man, so he struck the Egyptian and buried him in the sand.

13 On the second day he went out and saw two Hebrew men fighting, and he said to the wicked one, "Why do you strike your companion?"

14 Then he said, Who made you a man, a chief and judge over us? Will you kill me as you killed the Egyptian? And Moses feared and said, Surely the matter is known.

15 When Pharaoh heard this act, he sought to kill Moses, so Moses fled from Pharaoh's presence and settled in the land of Midian, and he sat down by the well.

16 And to a priest of Midian, seven daughters; and they came and drew water and filled the troughs to water their father's flock.

17 Then the shepherds came and drove them away, but Moses rose up and rescued them, and watered their flock.

18 And they came to Raguel their father, and he said, Why have you come back so soon today?

19 Then said one another: A certain Egyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds, and he also drew water for us, and watered the flock.

20 And he said to his daughters, "Why have you left the man? Call him so that he may eat bread."

21 Moses consented to dwell with the man, and he gave Zipporah his daughter to Moses.

22 She bore a son and he called his name Gershom, for he said, "I have been a sojourner in a foreign land."

23 And it came to pass in the many days that king of Egypt died and the sons of Israel groaned from the labour and they cried out and their crying went up to God from the labour.

24 And God heard their groaning, and God remembered his covenant, [the covenant] with Abraham, with Isaac, and with Jacob.

25 And God saw the sons of Israel, and God knew.


## Chapter 3

1 And Moses was shepherding the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian, and he led the flock beyond the wilderness and came to the mountain of God, to Horeb.

2 An ambassador of the LORD appeared to him in a flame of fire from the midst of the bush, and he saw and behold, the bush was burning in the fire, but the bush was not consumed.

3 Then Moses said, "Let me turn aside now and see the great sight, why the bush is not burned up."

4 And Jehovah saw that he turned aside to see, and God called to him from the midst of the bush, and said, Moses Moses, and he said, Here I am.

5 And he said, Do not draw near here; remove your sandals from upon your feet, for the place upon which you are standing is consecrated ground.

6 He said, I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. And Moses hid his face, for he was afraid to look upon God.

7 The LORD said, "I have surely seen the affliction of My people who are in Egypt, and I have heard their cry because of their taskmasters, for I know their sorrows."

8 I went down to deliver him from the hand of Egypt and to bring him up from that land to a good and broad land, to a land flowing with milk and honey, to the place of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

9 And now, behold, the cry of the children of Israel has come to me, and I have also seen the affliction with which the Egyptians oppress them.

10 And now go, and I will send you to Pharaoh, and bring out my folk the children of Israel from Egypt.

11 Moses said to God, Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh and that I should bring the children of Israel out of Egypt?

12 And he said, "Indeed, I will be with you, and this shall be the mark for you that I have sent you: when you bring the people out from Egypt, you will serve God upon this mountain."

13 Moses said to God, "Behold, I am coming to the children of Israel and I will say to them, 'The God of your fathers has sent me to you'; and they will say to me, 'What is his name?' What shall I say to them?"

14 God said to Moses, "I am who I am," and said, "Thus you shall say to the children of Israel, 'I am has sent me to you.'"

15 And God said again to Moses, Thus you shall say to the children of Israel, The LORD, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has sent me to you; this is my name forever, and this is my memorial to all generations.

16 Go and gather the elders of Israel and say to them, The LORD, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, appeared to me, saying, I have surely visited you and seen what is done to you in Egypt.

17 And I said, I will bring you up from the affliction of Egypt to the land of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, to a land flowing with milk and honey.

18 They will listen to your voice, and you and the elders of Israel shall go to the king of Egypt and say to him, “The LORD, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Now please let us go a three days’ journey into the wilderness, that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.”

19 And I knew that the king of Egypt would not let you go, nor by a strong hand.

20 I will stretch out my hand and strike Egypt with all the wonders that I will do among them, and afterward he will let you go.

21 And I will give this people's favour in the eyes of the Egyptians, and it will be that when you go, you will not go empty.

22 And a woman shall ask of her neighbor and of her who lives in her house, vessels of silver and vessels of gold and garments, and you shall put them upon your sons and upon your daughters, and you shall plunder the Egyptians.


## Chapter 4

1 Moses answered and said, "What if they do not believe me and do not heed my voice, but say, 'The LORD has not appeared to you'?"

2 And the LORD said to him, "What is that in your hand?" And he said, "A staff."

3 And he said, "Throw it to the ground," and he threw it to the ground, and it became a serpent, and Moses fled from its presence.

4 And the LORD said to Moses, Send your hand and take hold of its tail; so he sent his hand and held it, and it became a rod in his hand.

5 So that they may believe that the LORD has appeared to you, the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

6 And the LORD said to him further, "Put your hand into your bosom." And he put his hand into his bosom, and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous like snow.

7 He said, Put your hand into your bosom, and he put his hand into his bosom; when he took it out from his bosom, behold, it had returned as the flesh of his body.

8 And it shall be, if they do not believe you and do not heed the voice of the first sign, they will believe the voice of the latter sign.

9 And it will be, if they do not believe even these two signs and do not listen to your voice, then you will take from the waters of the river and pour out upon the dry ground; and the water that you take from the river will become blood upon the dry land.

10 Moses said to the LORD, "O my Lord, I am not a man of words, neither yesterday nor the day before, nor since you have spoken to your servant, for I am slow of speech and slow of tongue."

11 And the LORD said to him, "Who made the mouth of the person, or who makes [someone] mute or deaf or seeing or blind? Is it not I, the LORD?"

12 And now go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you are to say.

13 My Lord, I pray you, send by the hand you will send.

14 The LORD was angry with Moses and said, "Is not Aaron your brother, the Levite? I know that he will speak indeed. Behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you, he will rejoice in his heart."

15 And you shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth, and I will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and I will instruct you both in what you shall do.

16 And he shall speak to you to the folk, and he shall be to you for a mouth, and you shall be to him for God.

17 And take this rod in your hand with which you will perform the signs.

18 Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law and said to him, "Let me go, please, and return to my kindred who are in Egypt and see whether they are still alive." And Jethro said to Moses, "Go in peace."

19 The LORD said to Moses in Midian, Go, return to Egypt, for all the men who sought your life are dead.

20 Moses took his wife and his sons and mounted them on the ass and returned to the land of Egypt, and Moses took the staff of God in his hand.

21 The LORD said to Moses, "When you go back to Egypt, see, all the miracles that I have put in your hand—you will do them before Pharaoh, but I will harden his heart so that he will not let the people go."

22 And you shall say to Pharaoh, Thus says the LORD, Israel is my son, my firstborn.

23 I said to you, “Send my son that he may serve me,” but you refused to send him; now, I will kill your son, your firstborn.

24 And along the journey at the lodging place, the LORD met him and sought to put him to death.

25 Zipporah took a flint and cut off the foreskin of her son and touched it to his leg and said, "Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me."

26 Then she said, "A bridegroom of blood because of the circumcisions."

27 And Jehovah said to Aaron, Go meet Moses in the wilderness; and he went and met him at the mount of God, and kissed him.

28 Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD which he sent him and all the signs which he commanded him.

29 Moses and Aaron went and gathered all the elders of the people of Israel.

30 Aaron spoke in all the words that the LORD had spoken to Moses and he did the signs before the people.

31 The folk believed and listened when they heard that the LORD had visited the children of Israel and that he had seen their affliction, and they bowed low and prostrated themselves.


## Chapter 5

1 Afterward Moses and Aaron came and said to Pharaoh, Thus says the LORD, God of Israel, Let my people go that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.

2 Pharaoh said, Who is Jehovah that I should obey his voice to send Israel? I do not know Jehovah, neither will I send Israel.

3 The Hebrews' God has called upon us; let us go, please, a three days' journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God, lest He encounter us with pestilence or sword.

4 And the king of Egypt said to them, Why do you, Moses and Aaron, cause the folk to cease from their labors? Go to your burdens.

5 Pharaoh said, "Behold, the people of the land are now many; and you would stop them from their burdens."

6 Pharaoh commanded on that day the taskmasters of the people and their overseers, saying:

7 You shall not continue to provide the people with straw for making bricks as before; they themselves must go and gather straw.

8 And the composition of the brick altar they are making, as of yesterday and the day before, you shall set upon them; do not reduce from it, for they are languid; therefore they cry, saying, "Let us go sacrifice to our God."

9 Let the work weigh heavily upon the men, and let them labor in it, and let them not trust in lying words.

10 The task requires translating the Hebrew verse into English while strictly following the provided noun anchors and rules. Let's proceed step by step. **Hebrew verse:** וַיֵּ֨צְא֜וּ נֹגְשֵׂ֤י הָעָם֙ וְשֹׁ֣טְרָ֔יו וַיֹּאמְר֥וּ אֶל־ הָעָ֖ם לֵאמֹ֑ר כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר פַּרְעֹ֔ה אֵינֶ֛נִּי נֹתֵ֥ן לָכֶ֖ם תֶּֽבֶן׃ **Step 1: Identify the structure and components.** - וַיֵּצְאוּ – "And they went out" (third person masculine plural, past tense) - נֹגְשֵׂי – "taskmasters" (plural of נֹגֵשׂ), but not in the noun anchors. However, it's a noun derived from a root meaning "to oppress" or "to drive", so contextually "taskmasters" is appropriate. - הָעָם – "the people" (with article), and the noun anchor is "folk, men, nation, people". We must use one of these consistently. Since "people" is a common rendering in biblical context, we'll use "people". - וְשֹׁטְרָיו – "and his officers" (שֹׁטֵר, plural שֹׁטְרִים, meaning "officers" or "foremen"). Not in the anchors, but contextually clear. - וַיֹּאמְרוּ – "and they said" - אֶל־הָעָם – "to the people" - לֵאמֹר – "to say" - כֹּה אָמַר –

11 You go, get yourselves straw wherever you can find it, for nothing will be deducted from your work.

12 And the folk scattered throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.

13 And the taskmasters urged, saying, "Complete your labors, the task of each day in its day, as when there was straw."

14 The officers of the children of Israel, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were struck and said, "Why have you not completed your appointed task for making bricks both yesterday and today as before?"

15 The officials of the people of Israel came and cried out to Pharaoh, saying, "Why do you do this to your servants?"

16 Straw is not given to your bondmen, and bricks they say to us: “Do!” and behold, your bondmen are beaten, yet the fault is of your people.

17 And he said, You are idle, idle; therefore you say, Let us go sacrifice to the LORD.

18 And now go, serve; straw will not be given to you, and your measure of bricks you shall give.

19 The overseers of the people of Israel saw them in trouble, saying, You must not reduce from your brick quota the daily task.

20 And they encountered Moses and Aaron standing to meet them, when they came out from Pharaoh.

21 They said to them, “May the LORD see and judge, for you have made our savour abhorrent in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants, to place a sword in their hand to kill us.”

22 Moses returned to the LORD and said, my Lord, why have You dealt ill with this people? Why then did You send me?

23 And from the time I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has done evil to this people, and you have not delivered your folk.


## Chapter 6

1 And the LORD said to Moses, Now you will see what I will do to Pharaoh, for with a strong hand he will send them away, and with a strong hand he will drive them from his land.

2 God spoke to Moses and said to him, I am the LORD.

3 And I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as God Almighty, but by my name the LORD I was not known to them.

4 I also established my confederacy with them to give them the land of Canaan, the land of their dwellings in which they sojourned.

5 I also have heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom the Egyptians are afflicting, and I have remembered my covenant.

6 Therefore say to the sons of Israel I am the LORD and I will bring you out from under the burden of Egypt and I will deliver you from their bondage and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great judgments.

7 I will take you to me for a folk, and I will be to you for God, and you will know that I am the LORD your God, who brings you out from under the burden of Egypt.

8 I will bring you to the land that I lifted up my hand to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob; and I will give it to you as a possession; I am the LORD.

9 Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel, but they did not listen to Moses because of anguish of spirit and hard labor.

10 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

11 Go, speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he let go the children of Israel from his land.

12 Moses spoke before the LORD, saying, "Behold, the children of Israel have not listened to me; how then will Pharaoh listen to me, when I am of uncircumcised lips?"

13 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron and commanded them to the people of Israel and to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring the people of Israel out from the land of Egypt.

14 These are the chiefs of the fathers' houses, the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel: Enoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi; these are the families of Reuben.

15 And the sons of Simeon were Jemuel and Jamin and Ohad and Jachin and Zohar and Saul the son of the Canaanite: these were the families of Simeon.

16 And these are the names of the sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari; and the life of Levi was one hundred and thirty-seven years.

17 The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, by their families.

18 And the sons of Kohath: Amram and Izhar and Hebron and Uzziel; and the age of Kohath was a hundred and thirty-three years.

19 And the sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi; these are the families of Levi according to their generations.

20 Amram took Jochebed, his father's sister, as a wife, and she bore to him Aaron and Moses; and the lifetime of Amram was one hundred and thirty-seven years.

21 And the sons of Izhar: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.

22 The sons of Uzziel: Mishael and Elzaphan and Zithri.

23 Aaron took Elisheba daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon, as his wife, and she bore him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

24 And the sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are the families of the Korahites.

25 And Eleazar the son of Aaron took to himself one of the daughters of Putiel for a wife, and she bore him Phinehas; these were the heads of fathers among the Levites according to their families.

26 He is Aaron and Moses, whom the LORD said to, "Bring out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt according to their hosts."

27 They are the ones speaking to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt: he is Moses and Aaron.

28 And it came to pass on the day when the LORD spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt:

29 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying, I am the LORD; speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I am speaking to you.

30 Moses said before the LORD, "Behold, I am of uncircumcised lips; how then will Pharaoh listen to me?"


## Chapter 7

1 The Lord said to Moses, See, I have made you a god to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.

2 You shall speak all that I command you, and Aaron your brother shall speak to Pharaoh, and he shall send the children of Israel out of his land.

3 But I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and I will multiply my signs and my wonders in the land of Egypt.

4 But Pharaoh will not listen to you, and I will bring my power against Egypt and bring out my hosts, my people the children of Israel, from the land of Egypt by great judgments.

5 And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD when I stretch out my hand against Egypt and bring out the people of Israel from among them.

6 Moses and Aaron did as Jehovah, the Lord, commanded them; so they did.

7 Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three years old when they spoke to Pharaoh.

8 And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

9 When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, "Provide a miracle for yourselves," then say to Aaron, "Take your rod and cast it before Pharaoh—it will become a serpent."

10 Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and did so as Jehovah, the Lord commanded; Aaron cast down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants and it became a serpent.

11 Pharaoh also summoned the wise men and the sorcerers, and the magicians of Egypt also did according to their enchantments.

12 Then each man cast down his rod, and they became serpents; and Aaron’s rod swallowed up their rods.

13 And the heart of Pharaoh hardened, and he did not heed them, as the LORD had spoken.

14 The LORD said to Moses, "Pharaoh's heart is hardened; he refuses to let the people go."

15 Go to Pharaoh in the morning; behold, he is going out to the water, and you shall stand before him by the bank of the river, and the rod that became a serpent you shall take in your hand.

16 And you shall say to him, Jehovah, God of the Hebrews, has sent me to you, saying, Send my people, that they may serve me in the wilderness; and behold, until now you have not listened.

17 Thus says the LORD: By this you shall know that I am the LORD—behold, I will strike with the rod that is in my hand upon the waters that are in the river, and they shall be turned to blood.

18 And the fish that were in the river died, and the river stank, and the Egyptians loathed to drink water from the river.

19 The LORD said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Take your rod and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their rivers, over their streams, and over their ponds, and over every gathering of their waters, and they shall become blood, and there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, in wooden and in stone vessels.

20 Moses and Aaron did as Jehovah, the Lord commanded: he lifted up the rod and struck the water that was in the river before the eyes of Pharaoh and before the eyes of his servants, and all the water that was in the river turned to blood.

21 And the fish that was in the river died, and the river stank, and the Egyptians could not drink water from the river, and the blood was throughout all the land of Egypt.

22 And so did the magicians of Egypt with their enchantments, but Pharaoh's heart stiffened and he did not heed them, as the LORD had spoken.

23 Then Pharaoh turned and went into his court, and he did not take to heart even this.

24 And all the Egyptians dug around the river for water to drink, because they could not drink water from the river.

25 And seven days were fulfilled after the LORD had struck the river.


## Chapter 8

1 And the LORD said to Moses, Go to Pharaoh and say to him, Thus says the LORD, Let my people go that they may serve me.

2 But if you refuse to let go, behold, I will strike all your border with frogs.

3 And the river swarmed with frogs, and they came up and entered your home, and in the inner chamber of your bed, and on your bed, and in the house of your servants and in your people, and in your ovens and in your kneading troughs.

4 And on you, and on your folk, and on all your servants, frogs shall come up.

5 And the LORD said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Extend your hand with your staff over the rivers, over the canals, and over the ponds, and bring up the frogs over the land of Egypt.

6 Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the land of Egypt.

7 And so did the magicians with their enchantments, and they brought up the frogs upon the land of Egypt.

8 Pharaoh called to Moses and Aaron and said, Entreat Jehovah, the Lord, that he may take away the frogs from me and from my folk; and I will send away the folk, that they may sacrifice to Jehovah, the Lord.

9 Moses said to Pharaoh, "Set honor on me, when shall I entreat for you and for your bond-servants and for your people, to cut off the frogs from you and from your homes? Only in the river shall they remain."

10 Tomorrow he said, according to your word, so that you may know that there is none like the LORD our God.

11 And the frogs will depart from you and from your home and from your servants and from your people; they shall remain only in the river.

12 Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried to the LORD concerning the act of the frogs that he had brought upon Pharaoh.

13 And the LORD did according to the word of Moses, and the frogs died out from the houses, from the courts, and from the fields.

14 And they heaped heap upon them, and the land became corrupt.

15 And Pharaoh saw that there was respite, and he hardened his heart and did not heed them, as the LORD had spoken.

16 And the LORD said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Extend your rod and strike the dust of the land, and it will become lice throughout all the land of Egypt.

17 So they did thus: Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the land, and the dust became lice upon man and beast; all the dust of the land became lice throughout all the land of Egypt.

18 And so did the magicians with their enchantments to bring forth the lice, but they could not; and the lice were upon the person and upon the beast.

19 The magicians said to Pharaoh, "It is the finger of God." But Pharaoh's heart became hardened, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had spoken.

20 And the LORD said to Moses, Rise up early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh, behold, he goes out to the water, and you shall say to him, Thus says the LORD, Let my people go that they may serve me.

21 But if you will not let my people go, I am about to send swarms upon you and upon your servants and upon your people and upon your houses, so that the houses of Egypt will be filled with swarms, and also the land upon which they are.

22 And I will set apart on that day the land of Goshen, where my people dwell, so that no swarms of flies will be there, in order that you may know that I am the LORD in the midst of the earth.

23 I will set a division between my people and your people; tomorrow shall be the sign.

24 And the LORD did so, and there came a heavy swarm into the court of Pharaoh and into the court of his servants and into all the land of Egypt; the land was ruined because of the swarm.

25 Pharaoh called to Moses and Aaron and said, “Go, sacrifice to your God in the land.”

26 Moses said, It is not right to do so, for we sacrifice the abominable thing of the Egyptians to the LORD our God; behold, if we sacrifice the abominable thing of the Egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?

27 A three days' journey we will go into the wilderness, and we will sacrifice to the LORD our God as He shall say to us.

28 Pharaoh said, I will send you to sacrifice to the Lord your God in the wilderness, only do not go far; entreat for me.

29 Moses said, "Behold, I am going out from you, and I will entreat Jehovah, the Lord, and the swarm shall depart from Pharaoh, from his bondmen, and from his people tomorrow; only let not Pharaoh add further to deceive, so as not to send the people to sacrifice to Jehovah, the Lord."

30 Moses went out from Pharaoh and entreated Jehovah.

31 The LORD did as Moses had said, and removed the swarm from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people; not one remained.

32 Pharaoh hardened his heart also this time and did not let the people go.


## Chapter 9

1 The LORD said to Moses, "Go to Pharaoh and speak to him: 'Thus says the LORD, God of the Hebrews: Let my people go so that they may serve me.'"

2 But if you refuse to let them go, and you still hold them,

3 Behold, the hand of the LORD will be upon the cattle that is in the field, upon the horses, upon the asses, upon the camels, upon the beeves, and upon the sheep, a very grievous murrain.

4 And the LORD will make a distinction between the cattle of Israel and the cattle of Egypt, and nothing shall die of all the children of Israel.

5 The LORD appointed a time, saying, "Tomorrow the LORD will do this act in the land."

6 And the LORD did this act the next day, and all the cattle of Egypt died, but of the cattle of the children of Israel not one died.

7 Pharaoh sent and indeed not one of Israel's cattle had perished, but Pharaoh's heart was hardened and he did not let the people go.

8 And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron, Take for yourselves handfuls full of the ashes of the furnace, and let Moses throw them toward the heavens before the eyes of Pharaoh.

9 And it became (small) dust upon all the land of Egypt, and it came upon the man and upon the cattle as a boil breaking forth with blains throughout all the land of Egypt.

10 They took the ashes of the furnace and stood before Pharaoh, and Moses threw it toward the air, and it became boils and blains breaking out on the man and on the beast.

11 And the magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boil, for the boil was on the magicians and on all Egypt.

12 And the LORD strengthened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not listen to them, as the LORD had spoken to Moses.

13 The LORD said to Moses, Rise up early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh and say to him, Thus says the LORD, God of the Hebrews, Let my people go that they may serve me.

14 For this time I am sending all my plagues into your heart and into your servants and into your people, so that you may know that there is none like me in all the earth.

15 For now I have sent my hand and struck you and your people with the plague, so that you are removed from the earth.

16 But indeed, for this purpose I have raised you up, for the purpose of showing you my power, and so that my fame may be declared in all the earth.

17 Are you still oppressing my people, so as not to let them go?

18 I am about to cause heavy hail to fall at this time tomorrow, such as has never been in Egypt since the day it was founded until now.

19 And now send quickly your cattle and all that is yours in the field; every person and beast that is found in the field and is not gathered into the house, the hail will come down upon them and they will die.

20 The one who feared the word of the LORD among the servants of Pharaoh hurried his servants and his livestock into the houses.

21 And he who did not set his mind on the word of the LORD, he left his servants and his cattle in the field.

22 And the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand toward the heavens, so that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt, upon the people and upon the livestock and upon every herb of the field in the land of Egypt."

23 Moses stretched out his rod over the heavens, and the LORD gave thunder and hail, and fire went down to the earth; and the LORD rained hail upon the land of Egypt.

24 There was hail(stones) and burning fire flashing within the hail(stones), very heavy, such as had never been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.

25 The hail struck throughout the land of Egypt every person and every beast in the field, and the hail struck every herb of the field, and every tree of the field it broke.

26 Only in the land of Goshen where the children of Israel were there was no hail.

27 Pharaoh sent and called for Moses and Aaron, and said to them, "I have sinned this time; the LORD is righteous, and I and my people are wicked."

28 Entreat the LORD, for there is much thunder and hail from God; I will send you out, and you shall not have to stand any longer.

29 And Moses said to him, When I go out of the city, I will spread abroad the palms of my hands unto the LORD: the thunders shall cease, neither shall there be hail any more; that thou mayest know that the earth is the LORD's.

30 But you and your servants, I know that you have not yet feared before the LORD God.

31 And the flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was in the ear, and the flax was in bloom.

32 And the wheat and the fitches, rie were not struck, for they were hidden.

33 Moses went out from Pharaoh to the city and spread his hands to the Lord, and the thunder and hail ceased, and the rain did not pour upon the land.

34 Pharaoh saw that the rain and the hailstones and the thunder had ceased, and he sinned again, and he hardened his heart, he and his servants.

35 And Pharaoh's heart stiffened, and he did not let the people of Israel go, just as the LORD had spoken by the hand of Moses.


## Chapter 10

1 The LORD said to Moses, "Go to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart and the heart of his servants, in order that I may set my signs among him."

2 so that you may tell in the hearing of your son and your son's son what I did to Egypt and my signs that I set among them, and you may know that I am the LORD.

3 Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and said to him, Thus says the LORD, God of the Hebrews, How long will you refuse to humble yourself before me? Let my people go that they may serve me.

4 But if you refuse to let my people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring locusts into your border.

5 And he shall cover the outward appearance of the land, and he shall not be able to see the land, and he shall eat the remnant of the deliverance that is left for you from the hail, and he shall eat every tree which grows for you in the field.

6 And your houses and the houses of all your servants and the houses of all Egypt will be filled, which your fathers and the fathers of your fathers have not seen from the day they were upon the earth until this day, and he turned and went out from Pharaoh.

7 And the servants of Pharaoh said to him, "How long will this be a snare to us? Let the men go so that they may serve the LORD their God. Do you not yet know that Egypt is perishing?"

8 And Moses and Aaron were brought to Pharaoh, and he said to them, Go, serve Jehovah your God; who and who is going?

9 Moses said, "We will go with our babes and our elders, with our sons and our daughters, with our flocks and our herds, for a solemn feast of the LORD is for us."

10 And he said to them, May the LORD be with you, when I send you and your families; see, adversity is before your faces.

11 Not so; go now, you mighty men, and serve the LORD, for that is what you desire; and Pharaoh drove them out from his presence.

12 And the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt for the locust, so that it may come up upon the land of Egypt, and eat every herb of the land, all that the hail has left."

13 Moses stretched out his staff over the land of Egypt, and the LORD directed an east wind on the land all that day and all the night; when morning came, the east wind had brought up the locust.

14 The locust came up over all the land of Egypt and settled within every border of Egypt, very heavily; never before had there been such a locust as this, nor would there be such afterward.

15 And he covered the face of the whole land, and the land was darkened; and he ate all the grass of the land and all the fruit of the tree which the hail had left, and there remained no green thing on the tree or in the grass of the field in all the land of Egypt.

16 Pharaoh quickly summoned Moses and Aaron and said, "I have sinned against the LORD your God and against you."

17 And now, please bear my punishment this once; entreat the LORD your God, and let only this death be removed from me.

18 And he went out from with Pharaoh and entreated Jehovah.

19 Then the LORD turned a strong and mighty wind, and it lifted the locusts and cast them into the Red Sea; not a single locust remained in all the border of Egypt.

20 The LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not let the sons of Israel go.

21 The LORD said to Moses, "Stretch your hand over the heavens so that darkness spreads over the land of Egypt, a darkness that can be felt."

22 Moses stretched his hand toward the heavens, and there was thick darkness over all the land of Egypt for three days.

23 They did not see one another, nor did any rise from his place for three days, but for all the children of Israel there was light in their dwellings.

24 Pharaoh called to Moses and said, Go, serve Jehovah; only your flock and your herd will be detained; also your little children will go with you.

25 Moses said, "You must also provide sacrifices and burnt offerings into our hands, and we will perform them for the LORD our God."

26 And also our cattle shall go with us; not a hoof shall be left behind, for from them we must take to serve the LORD our God, and we shall not know how we shall serve the LORD until we arrive there.

27 The LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he was not willing to let them go.

28 Pharaoh said to him, Go away from my presence; take heed to yourself, do not add again to see my face, for in the day of your seeing my face you will die.

29 Moses said, You have spoken rightly; I will not again see your face.


## Chapter 11

1 And the LORD said to Moses, "One more plague I will bring upon Pharaoh and upon Egypt; after that he will let you go from here; when he lets you go, altogether he will drive you out from here."

2 Speak now in the hearing of the people, and let each man ask from his companion and each woman from her companion articles of silver and articles of gold.

3 And the LORD gave the favour of the people in the eyes of the Egyptians, and the man Moses was very great in the land of Egypt in the eyes of the servants of Pharaoh and in the eyes of the people.

4 Moses said, "Thus says the LORD, 'At midnight I am going among Egypt.'"

5 And every firstborn in the land of Egypt died, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the bondwoman who was behind the millstones, and every firstborn of cattle.

6 And there will be a great cry in all the land of Egypt, such as which has not happened and such as which will not happen again.

7 And to all the children of Israel no dog shall sharpen its tongue, from man even to beast, so that you may know that the LORD makes a distinction between Egypt and Israel.

8 Then all your servants shall come down to me and bow before me, saying, "Go out, you and all the people who follow you," and after that I will go out; and he went out from Pharaoh in fierce anger.

9 And the LORD said to Moses, Pharaoh will not listen to you, in order that my wonders may multiply in the land of Egypt.

10 Moses and Aaron performed all the miracles before Pharaoh, but the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not let the children of Israel go from his land.


## Chapter 12

1 And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying:

2 This month is for you the beginning of months, it is the first for you of the months of the year.

3 Speak to the whole congregation of Israel, saying: On the tenth day of this month, let each man take a lamb for his father's house, a lamb for the household.

4 And if the household is too small for a sheep, then he and his neighbor near his house shall take it according to the number of persons, each according to his eating; you shall make a reckoning for the lamb.

5 A lamb without blemish, a male one year old, shall be yours; from the sheep and from the goats you shall take it.

6 It shall be kept in your charge until the fourteenth day of this month, and all the congregation of Israel shall slaughter it between the evenings.

7 They shall take some of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and on the lintel of the houses in which they will eat it.

8 They shall eat the flesh that night roasted with fire and unleavened bread; with bitter herbs they shall eat it.

9 Do not eat any of it raw or boiled in water, but only roasted by fire, its head upon its legs and upon its inner parts.

10 Do not leave any of it until morning, and what remains of it until morning you shall burn with fire.

11 Thus you shall eat it: your loins girded, your shoes on your feet, and your staff in your hand; and you shall eat it in haste—it is the Passover offering to the LORD.

12 And I will pass through the land of Egypt on this night, and I will strike every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from man to beast, and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgment: I am the LORD.

13 And the blood shall be for you a sign on the houses where you are, and I will see the blood and pass over you, and there shall not be among you a plague for destruction when I strike in the land of Egypt.

14 This day shall be for you a memorial, and you shall celebrate it as a feast to the LORD; throughout your generations you shall observe it as an ordinance forever.

15 Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, but on the first day you shall remove leaven from your houses, for whoever eats leavened bread, that soul shall be cut off from Israel, from the first day to the seventh day.

16 On the first day a holy convocation shall be for you, and on the seventh day a holy convocation shall be for you; no work of any kind shall be done on them, only what every person must eat, that alone may be prepared for you.
