17 You shall keep the unleavened bread, because on the very day I brought your hosts out of the land of Egypt; you shall keep this day throughout your generations as a perpetual statute.

18 In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, until the one and twentieth day of the month at evening.

19 Seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, for whoever eats leavened food—that person shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, both the sojourner and the home-born of the land.

20 You shall not eat any leavened bread; in all your dwelling places you shall eat unleavened bread.

21 Moses called to all the elders of Israel and said to them, Draw out and take for yourselves sheep according to your families and sacrifice the passover offering.

22 You shall take a bunch of hyssop and dip it in the blood that is in the threshold, and touch the lintel and the two doorposts with the blood that is in the threshold, and you shall not go out, any man from the entrance of his house until morning.

23 And the LORD passed over to strike Egypt, and saw the blood upon the lintel and upon the two doorposts, and the LORD protected the entrance, and did not permit the destroyer to enter your homes to strike.

24 You shall keep this act as a law for you and your sons forever.

25 And it shall be when you come to the land which the LORD will give you, as He has promised, that you shall keep this service.

26 And it shall come to pass when your children say to you, "What is this service to you?"

27 And you shall say, "It is a passover offering to the LORD, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when he struck the Egyptians, and our houses he delivered." And the people bowed their heads and worshiped.

28 And the sons of Israel went out just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.

29 And it came to pass at midnight: the LORD struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive who was in the dungeon, and every firstborn of cattle.

30 Pharaoh rose in night, he and all his bondmen and all Egypt, and there was a great cry in Egypt, for there was not a home where there was not a dead.

31 And he called to Moses and to Aaron in the night and said Rise up go out from among my people both you and the children of Israel and go serve the Lord as you have said

32 Take both your (small) cattle and your beeve as you have requested and go, and also bless me.

33 The Egyptian folk grew urgent with the people to send them quickly out of the land, for they said, "We are all dying."

34 And the folk lifted their unleavened dough, their stores bound in their garments upon their shoulders.

35 The Israelites did as Moses had said, and asked the Egyptians for silver vessels, gold vessels, and clothing.

36 And the LORD gave the favour of the people in the eyes of the Egyptians, and they lent to them; and they despoiled the Egyptians.

37 The people of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides children.

38 And also a mixed multitude went up with them, and very many sheep and beeve, a very heavy herd.

39 They baked the dough that they had brought out of Egypt into cakes of unleavened bread, because it had not leavened; for they were driven out of Egypt and could not delay, nor had they prepared any provision for themselves.

40 The dwelling of the children of Israel, wherein they dwelt in Egypt, was four hundred and thirty years.

41 After thirty years and four hundred years, on the very day, all the hosts of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.

42 It is a night of being much observed to the LORD for bringing them out of the land of Egypt; this night is to the LORD a night of being much observed for all the children of Israel throughout their generations.

43 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron, This is the ordinance of the passover: no alien shall eat of it.

44 Every male servant purchased with money, when you have circumcised him, then he may eat of it.

45 A foreigner and a hired servant must not eat of it.

46 It shall be eaten in one house; you shall not bring out from the house any of the flesh outside, and you shall not break a bone in it.

47 All the congregation of Israel shall do it.

48 When a sojourner dwells with you and makes the passover offering to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him approach to make it; and he shall be as a home-born of the land; but no uncircumcised person shall eat of it.

49 One law shall be for the one born in the land and for the alien who sojourns among you.

50 And all the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.

51 On the very day the LORD brought the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their hosts.


## Chapter 13

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Consecrate to me every firstborn, whatever opens the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and of cattle; it is mine.

3 Moses said to the folk, Remember the day in which you went out from Egypt, from a house of bondage, for with strength of hand the LORD brought you out from this, and leavened bread shall not be eaten.

4 Today you are going out in the month of Abib.

5 And it shall be, when Jehovah brings you to the land of the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite, which he sware unto your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, that you shall serve this service in the month.

6 Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a feast to the LORD.

7 Unleavened bread shall be eaten with the seven days, and no leaven shall be seen for you, and no leaven shall be seen for you in all your borders.

8 And you shall tell your son on that day, saying, Because of this the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.

9 It will be for you as a mark on your hand and as a memorial between your eyes, so that the law of the LORD may be in your mouth, for with a strong hand the LORD brought you out of Egypt.

10 You shall keep this appointed custom at its appointed time continually.

11 And it shall come to pass when Jehovah brings you to the land of the Canaanite, as He swore to you and to your fathers, and gives it to you.

12 And you shall set apart unto the LORD every firstling that openeth the womb, and every firstling of the increase of cattle that is yours: the males shall be the LORD’s.

13 Every firstling of a donkey you shall redeem with a sheep, and if you do not redeem it, then you shall break its neck; and every firstborn among your children you shall redeem.

14 And it shall be when your child asks you tomorrow, saying, "What is this?" that you shall say to him, "With strength of hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt, from the house of slaves."

15 When Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the LORD killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of man to the firstborn of beast; therefore I sacrifice to the LORD every firstling that opens the womb, the males, but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.

16 And it will be for a mark on your hand and frontlets between your eyes, for with a strong hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt.

17 When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although it was near, for God said, "Lest the people change their mind when they see war and return to Egypt."

18 God turned the people toward the wilderness way of the Red Sea; the children of Israel went up armed from the land of Egypt.

19 Moses took Joseph's bones with him, for Joseph had made the sons of Israel swear, saying, "God will surely take notice of you, and you shall carry my bones up from here with you."

20 They journeyed from Succoth and camped in Etham, at the edge of the desert.

21 The LORD went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them on the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, to go day and night.

22 The pillar of cloud did not depart by day, nor the pillar of fire by night, from before the people.


## Chapter 14

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the children of Israel and let them turn back and encamp before Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, before Baal-zephon; opposite to it you shall encamp by the sea.

3 And Pharaoh will say concerning the children of Israel, They are confused in the land; the wilderness has shut them in.

4 And I will harden Pharaoh's heart, and he will pursue after them, and I will be honored in Pharaoh and in all his strength, and the Egyptians will know that I am the LORD; and they did so.

5 Then the king of Egypt was told that the people had fled, and the heart of Pharaoh and his servants turned against the people, and they said, "What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?"

6 And he saddled his chariot and took his people with him.

7 And he took six hundred chosen chariots and all the chariots of Egypt, and captains over all of them.

8 The LORD strengthened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the sons of Israel, and the sons of Israel went out with a high hand.

9 The Egyptians pursued them and overtook them encamped by the sea, all Pharaoh’s horses, chariots, horsemen, and his army, at Pi-hahiroth, before Baal-zephon.

10 Pharaoh approached, and the people of Israel lifted their eyes and saw the Egyptians marching after them; and they were very afraid and cried out to the LORD.

11 They said to Moses, "Is it because there are no graves in Egypt that you have taken us to die in the wilderness? What is this that you have done to us, to bring us out of Egypt?"

12 Is this not the very word we spoke to you in Egypt, saying, Let us alone, and we will serve the Egyptians, because it is better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness?

13 Moses said to the folk, Do not fear, stand firm and see the deliverance which the LORD will work for you today, for as you have seen the Egyptians today you shall not continue to see them again ever.

14 The LORD will fight for you, and you shall be silent.

15 And the LORD said to Moses, "Why do you cry to me? Speak to the sons of Israel and let them go forward."

16 But you, lift up your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, and the children of Israel shall go into the midst of the sea on dry ground.

17 And I, behold, I will harden the heart of the Egyptians, and they will pursue after them, and I will be honored through Pharaoh and through all his power, in his chariot and in his horsemen.

18 And the Egyptian will know that I am the LORD when I am honored at the expense of Pharaoh, his chariot, and his horsemen.

19 The messenger of God who was going before the camp of Israel moved and went behind them, and the pillar of cloud moved from before them and stood behind them.

20 And he came between the army of Egypt and the army of Israel, and the cloud and the darkness were there, yet it gave light to the night; and one did not come near the other all through the night.

21 Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the LORD drove the sea back by a strong east wind all night and made the sea dry land, and the waters were divided.

22 The sons of Israel came into the midst of the sea on dry ground, and the waters were a wall to them on their right and on their left.

23 The Egyptians pursued and went in after them—every horse of Pharaoh, his chariots and his horsemen—into the midst of the sea.

24 And it came to pass in the morning watch, that the LORD looked down upon the Egyptian army from a pillar of fire and cloud, and troubled the Egyptian army.

25 And he removed the wheel of his chariot and drove it heavily, and Egypt said, "I will flee from before Israel, for the LORD fights for them against Egypt."

26 And Jehovah said to Moses, Stretch out your hand over the sea, that the waters may return upon the Egyptians, upon their chariot, and upon their horsemen.

27 Moses stretched his hand over the sea, and the sea returned to its strength toward morning, and the Egyptians fled toward it, and the LORD shook off the Egyptians into the midst of the sea.

28 Then the waters returned and covered the chariot and the horsemen, all the army of Pharaoh that had gone in after them into the sea; not even one of them remained.

29 The children of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst of the sea, and the waters were a wall to them on their right and on their left.

30 And the LORD saved Israel from the hand of Egypt on that day, and Israel saw the Egyptians dead upon the edge of the sea.

31 Israel saw the great power that the LORD did in Egypt, and the people feared the LORD and believed in the LORD and in Moses his servant.


## Chapter 15

1 Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song to the LORD, and they said, saying: I will sing to the LORD, for he has triumphed gloriously; the horse and its rider he has thrown into the sea.

2 My strength and the song of the LORD became my deliverance; this is my God and I will glorify him, the God of my father and I will exalt him.

3 The LORD is a man of war, the LORD is his name.

4 The chariot of Pharaoh and his army the LORD cast into the sea, and the choice of his captains he drowned in the Red Sea.

5 The depths cover them; they go down to the bottoms like a stone.

6 Your right hand, O the LORD, is majestic in power; your right hand, O the LORD, shatters the enemy.

7 And in the abundance of Your pride You overthrow Your adversaries; You send forth Your fierce wrath, it consumes them like stubble.

8 By the blast of your anger the waters were heaped up; they stood like a heap, the depths congealed in the midst of the sea.

9 The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the prey; my soul shall be filled with spoil; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.

10 You blew with your breath, the sea covered them; they sank like lead in the mighty waters.

11 Who is like you among the gods, O the LORD? Who is like you, majestic in holiness, awesome in praises, worker of wonders?

12 You stretched out your right hand; the earth swallowed them.

13 You led in Your favour the people whom You redeemed; You guided in Your strength to the dwelling of Your holiness.

14 Hear, O nations, and tremble; pangs have seized the inhabitants of Philistia.

15 Then the captains of Edom were dismayed; the mighty men of Moab seized by trembling; all the inhabitants of Canaan melted away.

16 Dread and fear will fall upon them because of the greatness of Your strength; they will be as stone until Your people pass through, O LORD, until the people whom You have acquired pass through.

17 You will bring them in and plant them on the mount of your inheritance, a place for your dwelling which you have made, O LORD, a sanctuary, O Lord, which your hand has established.

18 The LORD will reign alway and for ever.

19 When Pharaoh's horse with his chariot and horsemen went into the sea, the LORD turned back upon them the waters of the sea, but the children of Israel walked on dry ground in the midst of the sea.

20 Miriam the prophetess, sister of Aaron, took the timbrel in her hand, and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.

21 Then Miriam answered them, Sing to the Lord, for he has triumphed gloriously; the horse and his rider he has thrown into the sea.

22 Moses caused Israel to journey from the Red Sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; they traveled three days in the wilderness and found no water.

23 They came to Marah and could not drink water from Marah because it was bitter; therefore he called its name Marah.

24 The folk murmured against Moses, saying, "What shall we drink?"

25 And he cried to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a stick, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters were sweetened; there he made for him a statute and judgment, and there he tested him.

26 If you diligently heed the voice of the LORD your God, and do what is right in his eyes, and pay attention to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, then I will not put on you any of the diseases I put on the Egyptians, for I am the LORD your healer.

27 They came to Elim, and there were twelve fountains of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there by the water.


## Chapter 16

1 They journeyed from Elim and all the assembly of the people of Israel came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their departure from the land of Egypt.

2 All the congregation of the people of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron in the wilderness.

3 The sons of Israel said to them, Would that we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt, as we sat by the fleshpots, eating bread to the full, for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill all this congregation with famine.

4 And Jehovah said to Moses, Behold, I will cause bread to rain down upon you from the heavens, and the people shall go out and gather a day's portion each day, in order to test whether they will walk in my law or not.

5 And it shall be on the sixth day, they shall prepare what they bring, and it shall be twice as much as they gather day by day.

6 Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, "At evening you shall know that the LORD has brought you out from the land of Egypt."

7 And in the morning you shall see the glory of the LORD, when he hears your murmurings against the LORD, and what are we, that you murmur against us?

8 Moses said, When Jehovah gives you in the evening flesh to eat and in the morning bread to be satisfied, because Jehovah hears your murmuring which you murmur against him, and what are we? your murmuring is not against us but against Jehovah.

9 Moses said to Aaron, Say to all the assembly of Israel, Come near before the LORD, for he has heard your murmurings.

10 And it happened, when Aaron spoke to all the assembly of the children of Israel, they turned toward the desert, and behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.

11 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

12 I have heard the murmuring of the sons of Israel; speak to them, saying, Between the evenings you shall eat flesh, and in the morning you shall be filled with food, and you shall know that I am the LORD your God.

13 And it came to pass in the evening that the quails came up and covered the camp, and in the morning there was a layer of dew round about the camp.

14 And when the layer of dew lifted, behold, upon the face of the wilderness was a fine flaky substance, fine as hoar frost on the ground.

15 The Israelites saw, and said each man to his brother, "What is it?" for they did not know what it was; and Moses said to them, "It is the bread that the LORD has given you to eat."

16 This is the act that the LORD has commanded: gather it, each man according to the eating of him, an omer per head, according to the number of your souls, each man for whom is in his tent you shall take.

17 And the sons of Israel did so, and they gathered—some more, some less.

18 They measured it by the omer, and the one who gathered much had no excess, and the one who gathered little lacked nothing; each according to his eating portion they gathered.

19 Moses said to them, "Let no man leave over more than it until morning."

20 They did not listen to Moses, and some of the people held back from it until morning, and the worms came up and it stank, and Moses became angry with them.

21 They gathered it every morning, each according to his eating, and when the sun grew hot, it melted.

22 On the sixth day they gathered double bread, two omers for each person, and all the leaders of the congregation came and reported it to Moses.

23 And he said to them, It is that which Jehovah spoke: a sabbath of rest, a holy sabbath to Jehovah; tomorrow you shall bake what you will bake, and boil what you will boil, and all the remainder that remains put aside for you to be kept until the morning.

24 They left it until morning as Moses had commanded, and it did not become foul, and there was no worm in it.

25 Moses said, Eat it today, for today is a sabbath to the LORD; today you will not find it in the field.

26 Six days you shall gather it, but on the seventh day, the sabbath, there shall be none in it.

27 On the seventh day, the people went out to gather, but they found nothing.

28 The LORD said to Moses, "How long will you refuse to keep my commandments and my laws?"

29 See that the LORD has given you the sabbath; therefore he gives you on the sixth day the bread of two days; abide each man in his place; let no man go out from his place on the seventh day.

30 And the folk rested on the day, the seventh.

31 The house of Israel called its name manna, and it was like coriander seed, white, and its taste like a wafer in honey.

32 Moses said, This is the act which the LORD commanded, Fill an omer from it to be kept for your generations, so that they may see the bread which I fed you in the wilderness when I brought you out from the land of Egypt.

33 Moses said to Aaron, Take one pot and put in it a full omer of manna, and place it before the LORD to be kept for your generations.

34 As the LORD commanded Moses, so Aaron placed it before the testimony to be kept.

35 And the sons of Israel ate manna forty years until they came to a land inhabited; they ate manna until they came to the edge of the land of Canaan.

36 And the omer is the tenth part of an ephah.


## Chapter 17

1 All the assembly of the children of Israel journeyed from the wilderness of Sin according to the command of the LORD and camped in Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.

2 The folk quarreled with Moses and said, Give us water that we may drink. And Moses said to them, Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test the LORD?

3 And the folk thirsted there for water, and the folk murmured against Moses, and said, Why is this you have brought us up from Egypt, to kill me and my children and my cattle with thirst?

4 Moses cried to the LORD, saying, What shall I do to this people? Yet a very little and they stone me.

5 And the LORD said to Moses, Pass before the people and take with you elders of Israel, and your rod with which you struck the river take in your hand and go.

6 Behold, I am standing before you there upon the rock at Horeb, and you shall strike the rock, and water shall come forth from it, and the people shall drink; and Moses did so before the eyes of the elders of Israel.

7 He named the place Massah and Meribah because of the strife of the children of Israel and because they tested the LORD, saying, Is the LORD among us or not?

8 Then Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.

9 Moses said to Joshua, Choose men for us and go out, fight against Amalek; tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill, and the staff of God in my hand.

10 Jehoshua did as Moses had spoken to him, to fight against Amalek, and Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.

11 And it was, when Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; but when he let down his hand, Amalek prevailed.

12 And the hands of Moses were heavy, so they took a stone and placed it under him, and he sat on it; and Aaron and Hur supported his hands, one on one side and one on the other, and his hands were steady until sunset.

13 And Jehoshua weakened Amalek and his folk by the mouth of the sword.

14 And the LORD said to Moses, Write this as a memorial in the book and place it in Joshua's ears, for I will utterly blot out the memory of Amalek from under the heavens.

15 Moses built an altar and named its name Jehovah-nissi.

16 And he said, A hand upon the throne of the LORD: the LORD will have war with Amalek from generation to generation.


## Chapter 18

1 Jethro, priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard all that God had done for Moses and for Israel, his people, that the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.

2 Jethro took Zipporah, Moses's wife, after he had sent her back.

3 and his two sons, the name of the first being Gershom, for he said, "I have been a stranger in a foreign land."

4 And the name of the one was Eliezer, because the God of my father helped me, and delivered me from the sword of Pharaoh.

5 Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to Moses, to the wilderness where he was camping, the mountain of God.

6 And he said to Moses, "I am Jethro your father-in-law, coming to you, and your wife and her two sons with her."

7 Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and he bowed and kissed him, and each man inquired of his fellow for welfare, and they entered the tent.

8 Moses told his father-in-law all that Jehovah had done to Pharaoh and to Egypt on account of Israel, all the trouble that had befallen them along the way, and how Jehovah had delivered them.

9 Jethro rejoiced over all the good that the LORD had done for Israel, that he had delivered them from the hand of Egypt.

10 Jethro said, "Blessed be the LORD, who has delivered you from the hand of Egypt and from the hand of Pharaoh, who has delivered the people from under the hand of Egypt."

11 Now I know that the LORD is greater than all gods, because of the thing in which they dealt arrogantly against them.

12 Jethro took a burnt offering and sacrifices for God, and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.

13 And it came to pass on the next day, that Moses sat to judge the people; and the people stood before Moses from the morning until the evening.

14 Moses's father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, and he said, "What is this matter that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, and all the people stand by you from morning until evening?"

15 Moses said to his father-in-law, "Because the folk come to me to seek God."

16 For if any matter comes to them, they come to me, and I judge between a man and his neighbour, and I make known to them the statutes of God and his laws.

17 And Moses's father-in-law said to him, Not good is the act that you are doing.

18 You will surely wear yourself out, both you and this people with you, for the matter is too heavy for you; you cannot do it alone.

19 Now listen to my voice, I will counsel you, and may God be with you; you shall represent the people before God, and you shall bring the matters to God.

20 And you shall warn them concerning the statutes and the laws, and make known to them the way in which they shall walk and the work that they shall do.

21 You shall select from all the people able men who fear God, trustworthy men who hate dishonest gain, and appoint them as rulers over thousands, rulers over hundreds, rulers over fifties, and rulers over tens.

22 And they shall judge the folk at all times, and it shall be that every great matter they shall bring to you, but every small matter they shall judge themselves, and they shall lighten the burden from upon you, and bear it with you.

23 If you do this act, God will command you, and you will be able to endure, and also all the people will come in peace to their place.

24 Moses listened to the voice of his father-in-law and did all that he said.

25 Moses chose able men from all Israel and set them as rulers over the people, captains of thousands, captains of hundreds, captains of fifties, and captains of tens.

26 And they shall judge the folk at every time: the hard matter they shall bring to Moses, and every minor matter they shall judge themselves.

27 Moses sent off his father-in-law, and he went to his land.


## Chapter 19

1 In the third month after the people of Israel left the land of Egypt, on this day they came to the wilderness of Sinai.

2 They journeyed from Rephidim and arrived at the desert of Sinai, and they camped in the wilderness; and Israel camped there opposite the mountain.

3 Moses went up to God, and the LORD called to him from the mountain, saying, Thus you shall say to the house of Jacob and declare to the children of Israel.

4 You have seen what I did to Egypt, and I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.

5 Now therefore, if you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, then you shall be my treasured possession among all peoples, for all the earth is mine.

6 You shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation; these are the words that you shall speak to the children of Israel.

7 Moses came and called the elders of the folk, and he set before them all the words that the LORD had commanded him.

8 And all the people answered together and said, All that the LORD has spoken we will do. And Moses returned the words of the people to the LORD.

9 And the LORD said to Moses, Behold, I am coming to you in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with you and also in you they may believe forever; and Moses told the words of the people to the LORD.

10 And the LORD said to Moses, Go to the people and sanctify them today and tomorrow, and they shall wash their garments.

11 And they shall be ready by the third day, for on the third day the LORD will come down before the eyes of all the people upon Mount Sinai.

12 And you shall set bounds to the folk round about, saying, Take heed to yourselves, do not go up on the mount, nor touch the edge of it; whoever touches the mount shall surely die.

13 Do not touch him with a hand, for he is to be stoned or shot; whether beast or man, he shall not live; when the ram’s horn sounds long, they shall go up on the mountain.

14 Moses went down from the mount to the people and he consecrated the people and they washed their garments.

15 And he said to the folk, Be ready by the third day; do not go near a wife.

16 And it happened on the third day, as morning came, that there were sounds and lightning and a heavy cloud upon the mountain and a very loud trumpet voice, and all the people in the camp trembled.

17 Moses brought the folk out from the camp to meet God, and they stood at the lower part of the mountain.

18 And the mount Sinai was smoking wholly because the LORD descended upon it in fire, and its smoke ascended like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mount quaked greatly.

19 The sound of the trumpet went on and grew exceedingly loud; Moses spoke and God answered him in a voice.

20 And the LORD went down upon mount Sinai to the top of the mount, and the LORD called to Moses to the top of the mount; and Moses went up.

21 And Jehovah said to Moses, "Go down, warn the people, lest they break through to Jehovah to see, and many of them fall."

22 And also the priests who approach the LORD must sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break out against them.

23 Moses said to the LORD, The people cannot go up to Mount Sinai, for you have testified to us, saying, Set bounds about the mount and hallow it.

24 And the LORD said to him, Go down, you and Aaron with you, and the priests and the people must not break through to go up to the LORD, lest he break out against them.

25 Moses went down to the people and said to them.


## Chapter 20

1 And God spoke all these words, saying:

2 I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

3 You shall not have other gods before me.

4 You shall not make for yourself a carved (graven) image, nor any likeness of anything that is in the heavens above, or on the earth beneath, or in the waters under the earth.

5 You shall not bow down to them or serve them, for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, to the third and to the fourth generation of those who hate me.

6 and showing favour to thousands, to those who love me and keep my commandments.

7 You shall not lift up the name of the LORD your God to vanity, for the LORD will not clear the one who lifts up his name to falsehood.

8 Remember the sabbath day to sanctify it.

9 Six days you shall labor and do all your work.

10 The seventh day is a sabbath to the LORD your God: you shall not do any work, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, your cattle, and your sojourner who is in your gates.

11 For six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them, and He rested on the seventh day; therefore the LORD blessed the sabbath day and set it apart.

12 Honor your father and your mother so that your days may be prolonged upon the land which the LORD your God gives you.

13 You shall not murder.

14 You shall not commit adultery.

15 You shall not steal.

16 You shall not bear false witness against your fellow.

17 You shall not covet your neighbour's house; you shall not covet your neighbour's wife, or his man-servant, or his maid-servant, or his ox, or his he-ass, or anything that belongs to your neighbour.

18 And all the people saw the sounds and the torches and the voice of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, and the people saw and trembled and stood far off.

19 They said to Moses, "You speak with us, and we will listen, but let not God speak with us, lest we die."

20 Moses said to the folk, Do not fear, for in order to test you the God has come, and in order that His fearfulness may be upon your faces so that you do not sin.

21 The folk stood from afar, and Moses drew near to the gross dark where God was.

22 And the LORD said to Moses, "Thus you shall say to the children of Israel, 'You have seen that I spoke with you from the heavens.'"

23 You shall not make gods of silver or gods of gold for yourselves.

24 An altar of earth you shall make for me, and on it you shall sacrifice your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen; in every place where I cause my name to be remembered, I will come to you and bless you.

25 And if an altar of stone you make for me, you shall not build it of hewn stone, for your tool has struck upon it and profaned it.

26 You shall not go up by steps to my altar, so that your nakedness may not be exposed upon it.


## Chapter 21

1 And these are the judgments that you shall set before them.

2 When you acquire a Hebrew bondman, six years he shall serve, and in the seventh he shall go free, for nothing.

3 If he comes in by himself, by himself he shall go out; if he is a husband, his wife shall go out with him.

4 If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters, the wife and her children shall belong to her master, and he shall go out by himself.

5 But if the servant says, "I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free,"

6 And his master shall bring him to God, and shall bring him to the door or to the doorpost, and his master shall pierce his ear with the awl, and he shall serve him forever.

7 If a man sells his daughter as a bondmaid, she shall not go out as the menservants do.

8 If she is displeasing in the sight of her master who designated her, then he shall let her be redeemed; he shall have no authority to sell her to a foreign people, since he has dealt treacherously with her.

9 But if she is given to her son, he shall deal with her according to the manner of daughters.

10 If he takes another wife, he shall not diminish her food, her clothing, or her marital duty.

11 But if he does not do these three things for her, she shall go out for nothing, no money.

12 Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall surely die.

13 and the one who did not act intentionally, yet God delivered him into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place to which he may flee.

14 But if any man schemes against his neighbor to kill him with guile, you shall take him even from my altar to die.

15 And whoever strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.

16 Whoever steals a man and sells him, and he is found in his hand, shall surely die.

17 And he who curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.

18 When men strive together and one man strikes his companion with a stone or with a fist, and he does not die but takes to his bed,

19 If he rises and walks abroad on his staff, then the one who struck him is clear, only his loss of time must be paid, and he shall be thoroughly healed.

20 If a man strikes his male or female servant with a rod and the servant dies under his hand, he shall surely be punished.

21 Only if it survives one day or two days, it shall not be avenged, for it is his money.

22 When certain men strive together and hurt a pregnant woman so that her children come out, but there is no mischief, he shall surely be punished according to what the husband of the woman imposes upon him, and he shall give it by the judges.

23 But if mischief happens, then you shall give life for life.

24 An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, a hand for a hand, a foot for a foot.

25 burning for burning, wound for wound, bruise for bruise.

26 When a man strikes the eye of his male servant or the eye of his female servant and destroys it, he shall let him go free on account of his eye.

27 But if he knocks out a tooth of his male servant or a tooth of his female servant, he shall let him go free for the sake of his tooth.

28 If a bull strikes a man or a woman and they die, the bull shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten; the owner of the bull is innocent.

29 And if a bull has been accustomed to gore from yesterday and three days ago, and its owner was warned, yet does not guard it, and it kills a man or a woman, the bull shall be stoned, and even its owner shall be put to death.

30 If a bribe is set upon him, then he shall give a ransom for his soul according to all that is set upon him.

31 Or a son is gored, or a daughter is gored; according to this judgment it shall be done to him.

32 If a bull strikes a bondman or a bondwoman, he shall give to their master thirty shekels of silver, and the bull shall be stoned.

33 And if a man opens a pit or a man digs a pit and does not cover it, and an ox or a donkey falls into it:

34 The owner of the pit shall pay silver to those to whom it is due, and the dead shall be his.

35 When a man's bull strikes the bull of another and it dies, they shall sell the living bull and divide its silver; they shall also divide the dead one.

36 Or if it is known that the ox was a gorer from yesterday or three days ago, and its owner did not keep it guarded, he shall surely pay ox for the ox, and the dead one shall be his.


## Chapter 22

1 If a man steals an ox or a sheep and slaughters it or sells it, he shall repay five oxen for the ox and four sheep for the sheep.

2 If the thief is caught breaking up and is struck so that he dies, there is no bloodguilt on account of him.

3 If the sun has risen upon him, bloodguiltiness is upon him; he shall surely pay; if he has nothing, he shall be sold for his theft.

4 If the theft is found in his hand, from a bull to an ass to a sheep, alive, he shall pay two.

5 If any man causes a field or vineyard to be grazed and sends his beast to graze in the field of another, he shall make restitution from the best of his field and from the best of his vineyard.

6 If a fire breaks out and spreads to thorns, so that a shock of corn or the standing corn or the field is consumed, the one who started the fire shall make restitution.

7 If a man gives to his neighbour money or vessels to keep, and theft is committed from the man's house, if the thief is found, he shall repay double.

8 If the thief is not found, then the master of the house shall be brought near to God, to see whether he has not laid his hand on his neighbor's work.

9 For any matter of transgression, for an ox, for a donkey, for a sheep, for a garment, for any lost thing, which he may claim, saying, "This is it," the matter of both of them shall come before God; whom God shall condemn, he shall pay double to his neighbour.

10 If a man gives to his neighbor a donkey, or an ox, or a sheep, or any beast to keep, and it dies or is broken or carried away, no one seeing,

11 The sworn oath of the LORD shall be between them, if he did not put his hand to the work of his neighbour; and the owner shall take it, and he shall not repay.

12 But if it is stolen from him, he shall repay those to whom it is due.

13 If a beast has torn in pieces, let him bring a witness; he shall not pay for the torn one.

14 When a man borrows from his companion and it is broken or dies, if its owner is not with him, he shall surely repay.

15 If its owner was with him, he shall not make restitution; if it was hired, he came for his wages.

16 If another man entices a virgin who is not betrothed and lies with her, he shall pay a bride-price for her to be his wife.

17 If her father refuses to give her to him, he shall pay money, according to the dowry of the virgins.

18 You shall not let a sorceress live.

19 Every one who lies with a beast shall be put to death.

20 Whoever sacrifices to the gods will be put under the ban, except to the LORD alone.

21 You shall not wrong or oppress the alien, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt.

22 You shall not oppress any widow or fatherless child.

23 For if you afflict him at all, and he cries out to me, I will surely hear his cry.

24 My anger will burn and I will kill you with the sword, and your wives will become widows and your children orphans.

25 If you lend money to my people, to the poor among you, you shall not be to him as a creditor; you shall not impose on him usury.

26 If you take your neighbor’s garment as a pledge, you must return it to him before the sun sets.

27 For it alone is his covering, it is his garment for his skin; in what shall he lie down? And when he cries to me, I will hear, for I am gracious.

28 You shall not curse God, nor curse a ruler among your people.

29 Do not delay giving to me the first of your fruit and your liquor; give me the firstborn of your sons.

30 So you shall do to your bull, to your flock: seven days it shall be with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.

31 You shall be holy men to me; you shall not eat any flesh torn in the field; you shall cast it to the dog.


## Chapter 23

1 You shall not carry false bruit; you shall not put your hand with a wicked man to be a witness of violence.

2 You shall not go after many to adversity, nor shall you answer upon a controversy to incline after many to make a wrong turn.

3 You shall not favor a poor person in his contention.

4 If you meet your enemy's bull or his ass going astray, you shall surely return it to him.

5 When you see the ass of your enemy lying under its burden, and you would refrain from helping him, you shall surely help him with it.

6 You shall not pervert the judgment of your poor in his cause.

7 Keep far from a false matter; do not kill the innocent and righteous, for I will not justify the guilty.

8 You shall not take a bribe, for a bribe blinds the clear-sighted and distorts the words of the righteous.

9 You shall not oppress the alien, for you know the soul of the alien, since you were aliens in the land of Egypt.

10 Six years you shall sow your land and gather its produce.

11 In the seventh year you shall let it rest and shall abandon its yield; the destitute of your folk shall eat, and their residue shall the wild beasts of the field eat; so you shall do to your vineyard, to your olive grove.

12 Six days you shall do your labors, and on the seventh day you shall rest, so that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and the son of your female servant and the sojourner may be refreshed.

13 And in all that I have commanded you, you shall keep; and the name of other gods you shall not mention; it shall not be heard upon your mouth.

14 Three times in the year you shall keep festival to me.

15 Observe the solemn feast of unleavened bread: seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in it you went out from Egypt, and they shall not appear before my face empty.

16 And a solemn feast of the harvest, the first fruit of your labors which you sow in the field, and a solemn feast of the ingathering at the going out of the year, when you gather your labors from the field.

17 Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the face of the Lord the LORD.

18 You shall not sacrifice the blood of my offering with leavened bread, and the fat of my solemn feast shall not remain until morning.

19 The beginning of the first fruit of your land you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God; you shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

20 Behold, I am sending a messenger before you to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place that I have prepared.

21 Beware of his presence and obey his voice; do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression, because my name is within him.

22 If you diligently obey his voice and do all that I speak, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.

23 For my ambassador will go before you and bring you to the Amorite and the Hittite and the Perizzite and the Canaanite and the Hivite and the Jebusite, and I will destroy them.

24 You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor do as their deeds; for you shall utterly destroy them and break down their pillars.

25 You will serve the LORD your God, and he will bless your food and your water, and I will remove sickness from among you.

26 You shall not be childless or barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.

27 I will send My terror before you and throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will give all your enemies into your hand to turn their backs to you.

28 I will send the hornet before you, and it will drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite from before you.

29 I will not drive him out before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the wild beast of the field increase against you.

30 Very little by little I will drive him out from before your face until you are fruitful and inherit the land.

31 I will set your border from the Sea of Reeds to the Sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the river, for I will deliver into your hand the inhabitants of the land, and you shall drive them out from before you.

32 You shall not cut a covenant with them nor with their gods.

33 They shall not dwell in your land, lest they cause you to sin against me, for you would serve their gods, and it would become a snare to you.


## Chapter 24

1 And to Moses he said, "Go up to the LORD, you and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel, and worship from afar."

2 Then Moses drew near alone to the LORD, but they did not draw near, and the people did not go up with him.

3 Moses came and told the people all the words of the LORD and all the judgments, and all the people answered with one voice and said, All the words which the LORD has spoken we will do.

4 Moses wrote down all the words of the LORD, and he rose early in the morning and built an altar at the foot of the mountain, and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.

5 He sent the young men of the sons of Israel, and they offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings to the LORD of bulls.

6 Moses took half the blood and put it in basins, and half the blood he splashed against the altar.

7 And he took the bill of the covenant and read it in the hearing of the people, and they said, All that the LORD has spoken we will do and we will hear.

8 Moses took the blood and sprinkled it on the people and said, Behold, the blood of the covenant which the LORD has cut with you upon all these words.

9 Moses and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up.

10 They saw the God of Israel, and under their feet something like a pavement of sapphire, like the very clearness of heaven.

11 And to the chief men of the sons of Israel he did not stretch out his hand, and they saw God, and they ate and they drank.

12 And the LORD said to Moses, Go up to me into the mountain and be there, and I will give you the stone tablets and the law and the commandment which I have written to teach them.

13 Moses rose up, and his attendant Joshua, and Moses went up to the mountain of God.

14 And to the elders he said, Wait for us in this place until we return to you, and behold, Aaron and Hur are with you; whoever has a matter shall approach them.

15 Moses went up the mount and the cloud covered the mount.

16 The glorious honour of the LORD settled on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it for six days; and he called to Moses from within the cloud on the seventh day.

17 The appearance of the glory of the LORD was like a consuming fire on the top of the mountain in the sight of the children of Israel.

18 Moses entered into the midst of the cloud and went up the mountain, and Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.


## Chapter 25

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel and have them take for me a gift; from every man whose heart willingly consents, you shall take my offering.

3 And this is the gift that you shall take from them: gold, silver, and copper.

4 blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine linen, and goats' hair,

5 and rams' skins dyed red, and badgers' skins, and shittim wood.

6 oil for light, spices for anointing oil and for sweet incense.

7 onyx stones and stones of consecration for the ephod and for the breastplate

8 And they shall make for me a sanctuary, and I will dwell among them.

9 According to all that I show you, the pattern of the dwelling place and the pattern of all its furnishings, so shall you do.

10 They shall make an ark of shittim wood: two and a half cubits its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

11 You shall overlay it with pure gold, both inside and outside you shall overlay it, and you shall make for it a gold crown all around.

12 And you shall cast for it four gold rings and place them on its four corners, and two rings on one side of it and two rings on its other side.

13 You shall make bars of shittim wood and overlay them with gold.

14 And you shall bring the bars into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark with them.

15 The bars shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be removed from it.

16 And you shall place the testimony that I will give you into the ark.

17 You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold, two cubits and a half its length, and a cubit and a half its breadth.

18 And you shall make two golden cherubims; of beaten work shall you make them, from the two uttermost edges of the mercy seat.

19 And make one cherub from the edge of this side and one cherub from the edge of that side; from the mercy seat you shall make the cherubims at its two edges.

20 The cherubims shall have their wings spread upward, covering over the mercy seat with their wings, and the faces of the cherubims shall be toward one another; toward the mercy seat shall be the faces of the cherubims.

21 You shall place the mercy seat on top of the ark, and into the ark you shall put the testimony that I will give you.

22 And I will meet with you there and speak to you from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubims, which is upon the ark of the testimony, all that I command you unto the children of Israel.

23 You shall make a table of shittim wood: two cubits its length, one cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

24 You shall overlay it with pure gold and make for it a golden crown all around.

25 You shall make for it a border of a hand-breadth round about, and you shall make a golden crown for its border round about.

26 You shall make for it four rings of gold and attach the rings at the four corners that belong to its four legs.

27 At the border the rings shall be, for the boards each apart to bear the table.

28 You shall make the bars of acacia wood and overlay them with gold, and they shall carry the table with them.

29 You shall make its chargers, its spoons, its covers, and its bowls with which they shall pour out; of pure gold you shall make them.

30 And you shall set upon the table the bread of the presence before me always.

31 And you shall make a candlestick of pure gold: the candlestick shall be made of beaten work; its shaft, its branches, its cups, its knops, and its blossoms shall be of one piece with it.

32 And six branches coming out from its side: three candlestick branches from one side and three candlestick branches from the second side.

33 Three cups shaped like almond blossoms on one branch, a knop and a flower, and three cups shaped like almond blossoms on one branch, a knop and a flower; so for the six branches that extend from the candlestick.

34 And on the candlestick four cups, shaped like almonds, its knops and its blossoms.

35 and a knop under two branches of it, and a knop under two branches of it, and a knop under two branches of it, for the six branches going out from the candlestick.

36 Their knops and their branches shall be of it; the whole shall be one beaten work of pure gold.

37 You shall make its seven lamps and set up its lamps so that they shine toward the opposite side of its face.

38 Its snuffers and its firepans were pure gold.

39 A talent of pure gold shall he make it, with all these vessels.

40 And see, and do according to their pattern which you are shown on the mount.


## Chapter 26

1 And the tabernacle thou shalt make ten curtains of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet: cherubims of skilful work shall ye make them.

2 The length of one curtain was twenty-eight cubits and the breadth four cubits; one measure for all the curtains.

3 Five curtains shall be joined each to the other, and five curtains shall be joined each to the other.

4 You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the curtain at the end of the first coupling, and so shall you do on the edge of the curtain at the end of the second coupling.

5 Make fifty loops on the one curtain, and make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is in the second coupling, so that the loops correspond one to another.

6 And you shall make fifty golden taches, and couple the curtains together with the taches, and the tabernacle shall be one.

7 And thou shalt make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle: eleven curtains shalt thou make them.

8 The length of one curtain was thirty cubits and the breadth four cubits; one measure for the eleven curtains.

9 You shall join five of the curtains by themselves and the six curtains by themselves, and you shall fold the sixth curtain to the front of the covering.

10 You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is the outer one in the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain that is the second in the coupling.

11 You shall make fifty brass taches and bring the taches into the loops and couple the tent so that it may be one.

12 And the remnant of the curtain excess in the tabernacle coverings, half of the excess curtain shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.

13 And the cubit on this side and the cubit on that side of the length of the curtains of the tabernacle shall extend on the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side to cover it.

14 You shall make a covering for the tabernacle of ram skins dyed red, and a covering of badger skins above.

15 And you shall make the boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing upright.

16 Ten cubits the length of the board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of the one board.

17 Two hands for one board, interlocking each with the other; so you shall make for all the boards of the tabernacle.

18 And thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle twenty boards for the south side southward.

19 And you shall make forty silver foundations under the twenty boards: two foundations under one board for its two tenons, and two foundations under another board for its two tenons.

20 And for the side of the tabernacle, the second, toward the north, twenty boards.

21 And their forty sockets were silver, two sockets under one board, and two sockets under one board.

22 And for the western side of the tabernacle you shall make six boards.

23 And two boards you shall make for the ends of the tabernacle at the sides.

24 They shall be coupled together below, and together they shall be complete at their top, to the one ring; so shall it be for the two of them, for the two corners.

25 And there were eight boards, and their sockets of silver: sixteen sockets, two sockets under one board, and two sockets under one board.

26 And you shall make bars of shittim wood: five for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, the one.

27 Five bars for the boards of the side of the second tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle for the western sides.

28 And the middle bar in the midst of the boards, extending from end to end.

29 And overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold, sockets for the bars, and overlay the bars with gold.

30 And thou shalt set up the tabernacle according to the manner which thou wast shown in the mount.

31 You shall make a vail of blue, purple, and scarlet, and fine twisted linen; the vail shall be made with an act of art, with cherubims.

32 And you shall place it on four shittim-wood pillars overlaid with gold, their hooks of gold, on four silver sockets.

33 You shall place the vail under the taches, and bring there within from behind the vail the ark of the testimony, and the vail shall separate for you between the holy and the holiness of the consecrated.

34 And you shall put the mercy seat upon the ark of the testimony in the most holy sanctuary.

35 And thou shalt set the table without the vail, and the candlestick over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south, and the table thou shalt place on the side toward the north.

36 You shall make a curtain for the entrance of the tabernacle of blue and purple and scarlet and twisted linen, the work of a weaver.

37 Make for the covering five pillars of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold; their hooks also shall be of gold, and you shall cast for them five brasen sockets.


## Chapter 27

1 You shall make the altar of shittim wood: five cubits long, five cubits wide, square shall be the altar, and three cubits its height.

2 You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of it, and you shall overlay it with copper.

3 You shall make its pots for removing its ashes, its shovels, its basins, its fleshhooks, and its firepans; for all its vessels you shall make bronze.

4 And you shall make for it a grate, a work of bronze net-work, and you shall make on the net-work four bronze rings on its four corners.

5 And you shall place it beneath the compass of the altar from underneath, and the net-work shall be to the middle of the altar.

6 Make bars for the altar, bars of acacia wood, and overlay them with brass.

7 And he shall bring its poles into the rings, and the poles shall be upon the two sides of the altar when carrying it.

8 Hollow with boards you shall make it, as he showed you on the mount, so shall they make it.

9 And thou shalt make the court of the tabernacle: on the south side southward, hangings for the court of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits long for one side.

10 And their pillars twenty, and their sockets twenty, brasen; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

11 And so for the northern side, in length, hangings a hundred long, and they stood, their twenty foundations copper, the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

12 The breadth of the court toward the west was fifty cubits, its pillars ten and their sockets ten.

13 The breadth of the court toward the east, fifty cubits.

14 Fifteen cubits the hanging for the shoulder-piece pillar their pillars three and their sockets three.

15 And for the second arm fifteen hangings, their pillars three, and their foundations three.

16 The gate of the court had a twenty-cubit curtain of blue, purple, and crimson yarn, and twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer; its pillars were four, and their foundations were four.

17 All the pillars of the court round about were overlaid with silver, their hooks silver, and their sockets bronze.

18 The length of the court was a hundred cubits, its breadth fifty by fifty, its height five cubits, of twisted linen, and their foundations of copper.

19 For all the vessels of the tabernacle in all its service, and all its pins, and all the pins of the court, copper.

20 You shall command the children of Israel, and they shall bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause the lamp to burn continually.

21 In the tabernacle of appointed assembly outside the vail which is over the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall set it in order from evening to morning before the LORD; an ordinance for the ages to the generations from the children of Israel.


## Chapter 28

1 And you, bring near to yourself Aaron your brother and his sons with him from among the sons of Israel, to minister as priests to me—Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, sons of Aaron.

2 Make sacred garments for your brother Aaron for glory and beauty.

3 And you, speak to all the skilled of heart, whom I have filled with a spirit of wisdom, that they may make the garments of Aaron for sanctifying him to serve me as priest.

4 And these are the garments they shall make: a breastplate and an ephod, and a robe, and a broidered coat, a mitre and a girdle; and they shall make consecrated apparel for Aaron your brother and his sons, to serve me as priests.

5 And they shall take the gold and the blue and the purple and the scarlet thread and the fine linen.

6 They shall make the ephod of gold, blue, purple, crimson, and twisted fine linen, the work of a skilled craftsman.

7 Two joining shoulder-pieces shall be on its two ends; they shall be joined together.

8 And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is on it, shall be of the same art as it, made of gold, blue, purple, crimson, and fine twined linen.

9 You shall take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel.

10 Six of their names on the first stone, and the names of the six remaining on the second stone, according to their generations.

11 An act of an engraver's art, like the engravings of a signet, you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel, enclosed in settings of gold you shall make them.

12 You shall place the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, memorial stones for the children of Israel, and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders for a memorial.

13 And you shall make ouches of gold.

14 And two chains of pure gold, made with ends, you shall make them, the work of woven bands; and you shall put the chains of woven bands on the ouches.

15 You shall make the breastplate of judgment, the work of a skilled craftsman, like the work of the ephod you shall make it: of gold, blue, purple, and crimson yarn, and finely twisted linen, you shall make it.

16 It shall be square, doubled, a cubit its length and a cubit its breadth.

17 And you shall fill it with an enclosing of stone, four rows of stone: a row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle shall be the first row.

18 and the second row, emerald, sapphire, and diamond.

19 The third row: ligure, agate, and amethyst.

20 The fourth row shall be beryl, onyx, and jasper, and they shall be set in golden settings.

21 The stones shall bear the names of the sons of Israel, twelve according to their names, engraved like a signet, each with his name, for the twelve tribes.

22 You shall make on the breastplate chains of twisted cords, a work of fine gold.

23 You shall make on the breastplate two rings of gold and you shall place the two rings on the two edges of the breastplate.

24 And you shall place the two twisted cords of gold on the two rings, at the two ends of the breastplate.

25 And you shall take the two ends of the two cords and give them on the two ouches, and put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, opposite its face.

26 You shall make two golden rings and place them on the two edges of the breastplate, on its border at the side of the ephod next to the inside.

27 You shall make two gold rings and attach them to the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod below its facing, opposite its coupling, above the curious girdle of the ephod.

28 They shall bind the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue thread, to be upon the curious girdle of the ephod, so that the breastplate will not come loose from upon the ephod.

29 Aaron shall bear the names of the Israelites on the breastplate of judgment over his heart when he goes into the sanctuary, for a memorial before the LORD continually.

30 You shall place into the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim, and they shall be upon Aaron’s heart when he comes before the LORD, and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the people of Israel upon his heart before the LORD always.

31 And you shall make the robe of the ephod all blue.

32 And his mouth at the top of him shall be in the midst of him; a lip shall be to his mouth all around, a work of weaving, according to the pattern of a habergeon it shall be for him; it shall not be torn.

33 You shall make pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson on its hems, round about the hems, and golden bells among them, round about.

34 A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, on the skirts of the robe all around.

35 And it shall be upon Aaron when he ministers, and his voice shall be heard when he enters the holy place before the LORD and when he goes out, so that he does not die.

36 You shall make a pure gold plate and engrave on it the engravings of a seal: Holy to the LORD.

37 And you shall fasten it with a blue thread, and it shall be on the front of the diadem; opposite the front of the diadem it shall be.

38 It shall be on Aaron's brow, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the consecrated things which the children of Israel shall sanctify in all their holy gifts; it shall be on his forehead continually, for acceptance for them before the LORD.

39 And you shall weave the linen coat, and make a linen diadem, and you shall make a girdle of woven work.

40 And for the sons of Aaron you shall make coats, and you shall make for them girdles, and bonnets you shall make for them for glory and for beauty.

41 And you shall clothe Aaron your brother and his sons with him, and you shall anoint them and consecrate their hands and sanctify them, that they may serve me.

42 Make for them linen breeches to cover the nakedness of the body; they shall reach from the loins to the thighs.

43 And they shall be upon Aaron and upon his sons when they come into the tent of meeting or when they approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary, so that they do not bear iniquity and die; a perpetual statute for him and for his seed after him.


## Chapter 29

1 This is the act that you shall perform for them to consecrate them to serve me: take one young bull from the herd and two unblemished rams.

2 Unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil: fine flour of wheat you shall make them.

3 And you shall place them on one basket and bring them near in the basket, and the young bull and the two rams.

4 And you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and you shall wash them with water.

5 And you shall take the apparel and put on Aaron the coat and the robe of the ephod and the ephod and the breastplate and fasten it to him with the curious girdle of the ephod.

6 And you shall set the mitre upon his head, and you shall put the crown of holiness upon the mitre.

7 And you shall take the anointing oil and pour it upon his head and anoint him.

8 And you shall bring near his sons and clothe them with garments.

9 You shall gird Aaron and his sons with a girdle and bind bonnets on them, and they shall have the priesthood as an everlasting statute; and you shall consecrate the hand of Aaron and the hand of his sons.

10 And you shall bring the young bull before the tent of meeting, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the young bull.

11 You shall slaughter the young bull before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

12 And you shall take from the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger, and all the blood you shall pour out at the base of the altar.

13 And you shall take all the fat covering the entrails, and the caul upon the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, and you shall burn them on the altar.

14 And the body of the bull and its hide and its dung he shall burn with fire outside the camp: it is a sin offering.

15 And you shall take the one ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram.

16 You shall slaughter the ram and take its blood and sprinkle it around on the altar.

17 And the ram you shall cut into pieces, and you shall wash its innards and its legs, and you shall place them on its pieces and on its head.

18 You shall burn the whole ram on the altar as a burnt offering to the LORD, a sweet savour, an offering by fire to the LORD.

19 And you shall take the second ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram.

20 You shall slaughter the ram, and take some of its blood and put it on the tip of Aaron's right ear and on the tip of the right ear of his sons, and on the right thumb of their right hand and on the right great toe of their right foot, and you shall sprinkle the blood around on the altar.

21 And you shall take from the blood that is on the altar and from the anointing oil, and sprinkle on Aaron and on his garments and on his sons and on the garments of his sons with him; and he and his garments and his sons and the garments of his sons shall be sanctified with him.

22 And you shall take from the ram the fat and the rump, and the fat that covers the entrails, and the lobe of the liver, and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, and the right thigh, for it is a ram of consecration.

23 and one loaf of bread, and one cake of oil bread, and one wafer from the basket of unleavened bread that is before the LORD

24 And you shall place all upon the palms of Aaron and upon the palms of his sons, and you shall wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

25 And you shall take them from their hand and burn them on the altar as a burnt offering, for a sweet savour before the LORD; it is an offering made by fire to the LORD.

26 And you shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron's consecration and wave it as a wave offering before the LORD, and it shall be your portion.

27 You shall sanctify the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering, which was waved and which was offered up from the ram of consecration, from that which is for Aaron and from that which is for his sons.

28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual statute from the people of Israel, for it is a heave offering; and it shall be a heave offering from the people of Israel from their sacrifices of peace offerings, their heave offering to the LORD.

29 The garments of holiness belonging to Aaron shall belong to his sons after him, to be anointed with and to consecrate their hands through them.

30 Seven days shall the priest, who is his son, wear them when he enters the tent of meeting to minister in the sanctuary.

31 And you shall take the ram of consecration and boil its flesh in a holy place.

32 And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

33 And they shall eat those things with which atonement was made, to fill their hands and consecrate them, but a stranger shall not eat them, for they are holy.

34 But if any of the flesh of the consecration and of the bread remains until the morning, then you shall burn the remainder in the fire; it shall not be eaten, for it is holy.

35 You shall do to Aaron and to his sons thus, according to all that I have commanded you: seven days you shall fill their hands.

36 And a purifying bull you shall make daily for the atonements, and you shall purify the altar by making atonement upon it, and you shall anoint it to sanctify it.

37 For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it, and the altar shall be most holy; whoever touches the altar shall be consecrated.

38 And this is what you shall do on the altar: two lambs each year old every day continually.

39 The lamb one you shall make in the morning and the lamb second you shall make between the evenings.

40 a tenth deal of fine flour mixed with a fourth part of a hin of oil of olive, and a fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink offering, for the lamb one.

41 And the second lamb you shall prepare between the evenings, as the morning gift and as its drink offering, to make a sweet savour, a fire offering to the LORD.

42 a continual ascent offering for your generations at the entrance of the tent of appointed assembly before the LORD where I will meet with you there to speak to you there

43 And I will meet there with the children of Israel, and I will be sanctified in my glory.

44 I will set apart the tabernacle of meeting and the altar, and I will consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve me as priests.

45 And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and I will be their God.

46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt to dwell in their midst; I am the LORD their God.


## Chapter 30

1 And you shall make an altar to burn incense upon, of shittim wood you shall make it.

2 A cubit its length and a cubit its breadth, square it shall be, and two cubits its height; from it its horns.

3 You shall overlay it with pure gold—its roof and its walls on all sides and its horns—and you shall make a gold crown around it.

4 And two gold rings you shall make for it beneath its crown, on its two sides you shall make them on its two beams, and they shall serve as housings for the poles to carry it with them.

5 You shall make the bars of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.

6 And you shall place it before the vail which is upon the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat which is upon the testimony, where I will meet with you.

7 Aaron shall burn sweet incense on it every morning when he trims the lamps he shall burn it.

8 When Aaron lights the lamps between the evenings, he shall burn the incense; a continual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.

9 You shall not offer up on it strange (sweet) incense, nor a burnt offering, nor a gift, nor shall you pour out a drink offering on it.

10 Aaron shall make atonement upon its horns once in the year with the blood of the sin purification offering for atonement; once in the year he shall make atonement upon it throughout your generations; it is most holy to the LORD.

11 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

12 When you take a census of the Israelites by their number, each man shall give a ransom for his life to the LORD when you number them, so that there may be no plague among them when you number them.

13 This is what everyone who passes over to those who are numbered shall give: half a shekel according to the shekel of the consecrated; twenty gerahs to the shekel; half a shekel shall be an offering to the LORD.

14 Every one who passes over among those numbered, from twenty years old and upward, shall give the offering to the LORD.

15 The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, to give the gift to the LORD, to atone for your souls.

16 You shall take the silver of the atonements from the sons of Israel and give it for the service of the tent of meeting, so that it may be for the sons of Israel a memorial before the LORD to make atonement for your souls.

17 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

18 You shall make a laver of brass and its base of brass for washing, and you shall place it between the tabernacle of assembly and the altar, and you shall put water there.

19 And Aaron and his sons shall wash from it their hands and their feet.

20 When they enter the tent of meeting, they shall wash with water, so that they do not die; or when they approach the altar to minister, to offer a fire offering to the LORD.

21 They shall wash their hands and their feet, and they shall not die; and it shall be a perpetual statute for him and for his descendants throughout their generations.

22 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

23 And you take for yourself spices, the chief balm, pure myrrh five hundred, and cinnamon of spice, half of it, two hundred and fifty, and calamus of spice two hundred and fifty.

24 and cassia five hundred shekels by the shekel of the consecrated thing, and olive oil a hin.

25 You shall make it a holy anointing oil, a compound of ointments, the work of a perfumer, a holy anointing oil it shall be.

26 and you shall anoint with it the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony.

27 and the table and all its vessels, and the candlestick and its vessels, and the altar of incense.

28 and the altar of ascent and all its vessels and the laver and its base

29 And you shall consecrate them, and they shall be most holy; whoever touches them shall be consecrated.

30 And anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them to serve me as priests.

31 And to the Israelites you shall speak, saying, This oil of anointing shall be holy to me throughout your generations.

32 On the body of a person you shall not mark, and according to its composition you shall not make yourselves like it: holy is it, holy shall it be for you.

33 Whoever compounds any like it, or puts any of it on an outsider, shall be cut off from his people.

34 And the LORD said to Moses, Take for yourself sweet spices, stacte, onycha, and galbanum, and pure frankincense; each spice shall be equal in weight.

35 You shall make it (sweet) incense, a confection, the art of a perfumer, seasoned, pure, a consecrated (thing).

36 You shall grind some of it finely and scatter it before the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with you; it shall be most holy to you.

37 And the (sweet) incense that you shall make, according to its composition you shall not make for yourselves; a consecrated (thing) it shall be for you to the LORD.

38 Any man who does likewise to smell of it shall be cut off from his people.


## Chapter 31

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 See, I have called by name Bezaleel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

3 And I filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, with knowledge, and with every kind of workmanship.

4 to devise cunning work, to work in gold and in silver and in brass.

5 and in carving stone to fill in, and in carving wood, to do in all manner of work.

6 And I, behold, I have appointed with him Aholiab the son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan, and in the heart of everyone who is wise-hearted I have put wisdom, and they shall make all that I have commanded you.

7 the tabernacle of meeting and the ark of the testimony and the mercy seat that is on it and all the vessels of the tabernacle

8 and the table and its vessels, and the pure candlestick and all its vessels, and the altar of incense.
