9 and the altar of ascent and all its vessels, and the laver and its base.

10 and the apparel of the service, and the apparel of the consecrated for Aaron the priest, and the apparel of his sons to serve as priests.

11 And the anointing oil and the incense of sweet spices for the holy place they shall make according to all that I have commanded you.

12 And the LORD said to Moses, saying:

13 But you, speak to the children of Israel, saying, You shall surely keep my sabbaths; for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I am the LORD who sanctifies you.

14 You shall keep every sabbath, for it is consecrated to you; whoever profanes it shall surely die, for every one doing any manner of work in it, that person shall be cut off from among his people.

15 Six days work shall be done, and on the seventh day a sabbath of rest, holy to the LORD; whoever does work on the sabbath day shall surely die.

16 And the children of Israel shall keep the sabbath, to observe the sabbath throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant.

17 Between me and the children of Israel it is a sign forever, for in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day he rested and was refreshed.

18 And he gave unto Moses, when he had made an end of speaking with him upon mount Sinai, two tables of the testimony, tables of stone, written with the finger of God.


## Chapter 32

1 The folk saw that Moses delayed to come down from the mount, and the folk gathered themselves together against Aaron and said to him, Rise up, make us gods which shall go before us; for as for Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.

2 Aaron said to them, "Break off the gold earrings from your wives’ ears, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me."

3 And all the people took off the gold earrings that were in their ears and brought them to Aaron.

4 He took it from their hand and fashioned it with a graving tool and made a molten calf; and they said, "These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt."

5 Aaron saw and built an altar before him, and Aaron called and said, "A solemn feast for the LORD tomorrow."

6 They rose early on the next day and offered burnt offerings and brought peace offerings, and the people sat to eat and drink, and they rose up to revel.

7 The LORD spoke to Moses, "Go down, for your people whom you brought up from the land of Egypt have corrupted."

8 They quickly turned away from the way that I commanded them; they made for themselves a molten calf, and they bowed down to it and sacrificed to it, and said, “These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.”

9 And the LORD said to Moses, I have seen this people, and indeed the folk is stiff-necked.

10 Now therefore let me alone, that my anger may burn against them and I may consume them, and I will make you into a great nation.

11 Moses entreated the face of the LORD his God, and said, Why doth thine anger burn against thy people, whom thou hast brought forth out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a strong hand?

12 Why should the Egyptians say, “He has done evil to bring them out to kill them in the mountains and to destroy them from the face of the earth”? Turn from the fierceness of Your anger and relent concerning the evil against Your people.

13 Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, your servants, to whom you swore by yourself, saying, I will greatly multiply your seed as the stars of the heavens, and all this land that I said I would give to your seed, they shall inherit it forever.

14 And the LORD relented from the adversity that he spoke of doing to his people.

15 Then Moses turned and went down from the mount, with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand; tablets written on both their sides; on this side and on that side they were written.

16 And the tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved upon the tablets.

17 Jehoshua heard the sound of the people in their shouting, and he said to Moses, "The sound of battle is in the camp."

18 And he said, There is no sound of strength, and there is no sound of weakness; a sound of distress—I am hearing it.

19 When he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dances, Moses became angry and threw down the tablets, breaking them at the foot of the mountain.

20 And he took the calf which they had made, and burned it in the fire, and ground it until it was fine, and scattered it upon the surface of the water, and made the children of Israel drink it.

21 Moses said to Aaron, "What have you done to this people, that you have brought upon it a great sin?"

22 And Aaron said, Do not let the anger of my lord burn; you know this people, that it is in evil.

23 They said to me, Make us gods who will go before us, for this Moses, the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt—we do not know what happened to him.

24 And I said to them, “Whose gold is this?” and they gave it to me, and I cast it into the fire, and there came out this calf.

25 And Moses saw the folk that they were out of control, for Aaron had let them get out of control to shame them in their enemies’ sight.

26 Moses stood at the gate of the camp and said, "Who is for the LORD? Come to me." And all the sons of Levi gathered to him.

27 He said to them, Thus says the LORD God of Israel, Put each man his sword on his thigh, pass back and forth from gate to gate in the camp, and each man slay his brother, each man his companion, and each man his neighbor.

28 The Levites did as Moses had said, and about three thousand men fell from the people that day.

29 Moses said, fill your hands today to the LORD, for each man has been consecrated today with his son and with his brother, and to place upon you today a blessing.

30 And it came to pass on the morrow, that Moses said unto the folk, Ye have sinned a great sin; and now I will go up unto the LORD, perhaps I may make atonement for your sin.

31 Then Moses returned to the LORD and said, "Alas, this people has committed a great sin, and they have made for themselves gods of gold."

32 And now, if You bear their sin-punishment, good; but if not, please blot me out of Your scroll that You have written.

33 And the LORD said to Moses, "Whoever has sinned against me, I will blot out from my book."

34 And now go, lead the folk to the place of which I have spoken to you: behold, my messenger will go before your face; and in the day of my taking account, I will indeed account for their sin upon them.

35 And the LORD smote the people because of what they did with the calf that Aaron made.


## Chapter 33

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, “Go up from here, you and the people whom you brought up from the land of Egypt, to the land which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, ‘To your seed I will give it.’”

2 I will send a messenger before you and drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

3 to a land flowing with milk and honey, for I will not go up among you, because you are a stiff-necked people, lest I consume you along the way.

4 The folk heard the evil word and mourned, and no man put his ornament on himself.

5 And the LORD said to Moses, Say to the children of Israel, You are a stiff-necked people: in one moment I will go up among you and consume you; therefore now put off your ornaments from upon you, that I may know what to do to you.

6 The Israelites stripped off their ornaments from Mount Horeb.

7 And Moses took the tent and pitched it outside the camp, far from the camp, and called it the tent of meeting, and it happened: every one who sought the LORD went out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.

8 And it was, when Moses went to the covering, all the people rose up, each one standing at the entrance of his tent, and they looked after Moses until he entered the covering.

9 When Moses came to the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the LORD would speak with Moses.

10 And all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, and all the people rose up and bowed down, each at the entrance of his tent.

11 And the LORD spoke to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his companion, and he returned to the camp, and his attendant Joshua the son of Nun, the young man, did not depart from the midst of the tent.

12 Moses said to the LORD, See, you say to me, Bring up this people, but you have not let me know whom you will send with me, and yet you have said, I know you by name, and you have also found favour in my sight.

13 And now, if I have found favour in your eyes, please make known to me your way, that I may know you, so that I may find favour in your eyes; and consider, this nation is your people.

14 My face will go and I will give you rest.

15 And he said to him, If your presence does not go, do not bring us up from this place.

16 How then can it be known that I have found favour in your eyes, I and your people? Is it not by your going with us, so that I and your people are distinguished from all the peoples who are on the face of the earth?

17 And the LORD said to Moses, I will also do this act which you have spoken, for you have found favour in my sight, and I have known you by name.

18 And he said, Please show me your glory.

19 And I will cause all my goodness to pass before you, and I will proclaim the name of the LORD before you, and I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and I will show mercy to whom I will show mercy.

20 He said, You cannot see my face, because no person can see me and live.

21 And the LORD said, Behold, there is a place with me, and you shall stand upon the rock.

22 And it shall come to pass, when my glorious presence passes by, that I will put you in the cleft of the rock, and I will cover you with my hand until I have passed by.

23 And I will remove my hand, and you will see my back, but my face shall not be seen.


## Chapter 34

1 The LORD said to Moses, "Carve for yourself two stone tablets like the first ones, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets that you broke."

2 Be ready by morning, and you shall go up in the morning to Mount Sinai, and you shall stand there for me at the top of the mountain.

3 No man shall go up with you, nor shall any man be seen in all the mountain; neither shall the flock nor the herd graze opposite that mountain.

4 And Moses cut two stone tablets like the former ones, and he rose early in the morning and went up to Mount Sinai, as the LORD had commanded him, and took in his hand two stone tablets.

5 And the LORD came down in a cloud and stood there with him, and called by the name of the LORD.

6 And the LORD passed before him and proclaimed, "Jehovah, Jehovah, God, compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness."

7 Preserver of favour for the thousands, bearer of fault and transgression and sin, yet not clearing utterly, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children and upon the children's children, upon the third generation and upon the fourth.

8 Moses quickly bowed to the ground and worshiped.

9 And he said, If now I have found favour in your eyes, my Lord, let my Lord go, I pray, in the midst of us, for it is a stiff-necked people, and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.

10 And he said, Behold, I am making a covenant; before all your people I will do marvels, such as have not been created in all the earth and among all the nations; and all the people in whose midst you are shall see the act of the LORD, for it is a terrible thing that I am doing with you.

11 Keep for yourself the thing which I command you today: behold, I am driving out before you the Amorite and the Canaanite and the Hittite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite.

12 Take care to yourself lest you make a confederacy with the inhabitant of the land, which you are coming upon, lest it become a snare in your midst.

13 For you shall tear down their altars, and break their pillars, and cut down their groves.

14 for you shall not bow down to another god, for the LORD is a jealous God, and his name is Jealous.

15 Lest you make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they play the harlot after their gods, and sacrifice to their gods, and someone invites you, and you eat of his sacrifice.

16 You shall take their daughters for your sons, and their daughters shall play the harlot after their gods, and make your sons to play the harlot after their gods.

17 You shall not make for yourself a molten image of God.

18 Observe the solemn feast of unleavened bread: seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you at the appointed season in the month of Abib, for in the month of Abib you went out from Egypt.

19 Every firstling of the womb is mine, and all your cattle, every firstling of bull and sheep.

20 The firstling of an ass thou shalt redeem with a sheep, and if thou do not redeem it, then thou shalt break its neck; every firstborn of thy sons thou shalt redeem, and they shall not appear before the LORD empty.

21 Six days you shall labor, and on the seventh day you shall rest; in plowing and in harvest you shall rest.

22 And a feast of weeks you shall make for yourself, [with] the first fruits of the harvest of wheat, and a feast of ingathering at the end of the year.

23 Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the face of the Lord the LORD God of Israel.

24 For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders, and no man shall covet your land when you go up to appear before the face of the LORD your God three times in the year.

25 You shall not sacrifice the blood of my offering on leavened bread, and the sacrifice of the passover feast shall not remain until the morning.

26 The beginning of the first fruits of your land you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God; you shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

27 The LORD said to Moses, Write for yourself these words, for on the basis of these words I have made with you a covenant and with Israel.

28 And he was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread and he did not drink water, and he wrote upon the tablets the words of the covenant, the ten commandments.

29 And it happened when Moses came down from Mount Sinai, with the two tablets of the testimony in Moses’ hand when he came down from the mountain, and Moses did not know that the skin of his face was radiant when he spoke with him.

30 And Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, and behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.

31 Moses called to them, and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned to him, and Moses spoke to them.

32 Afterward all the people of Israel drew near, and he commanded them all that the LORD had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.

33 Moses finished speaking with them and put a vail upon his face.

34 When Moses went before the LORD to speak with him, he would remove the vail until he came out; and he came out and spoke to the children of Israel all that he was commanded.

35 And the sons of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of the face of Moses shone, and Moses returned the vail upon his face until he went in to speak with him.


## Chapter 35

1 Moses assembled all the congregation of the people of Israel and said to them, These are the words which the LORD has commanded to do them.

2 Six days work shall be done, and on the seventh day shall be for you holy, a sabbath of rest to the LORD; every one who does work in it shall be put to death.

3 You shall not kindle a fire in any of your dwelling places on the sabbath day.

4 Moses said to all the assembly of the children of Israel, saying, This is the word that the LORD has commanded, saying:

5 Take from yourselves a gift for the LORD; everyone whose heart so moves him shall bring the LORD’s offering: gold, silver, and copper.

6 Blue, purple, crimson scarlet, and linen, and goats.

7 and red-dyed ram skins and badger skins and shittim wood

8 oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil, and sweet incense.

9 onyx stones and setting stones for the ephod and for the breastplate.

10 And every wise-hearted one among you shall come and do all that the LORD has commanded.

11 the tabernacle, its tent and its covering, its taches, its boards, its bars, its pillars and their foundations

12 the ark and its bars, the mercy seat, and the vail of the covering

13 the table and its bars and all its utensils and the bread of the Presence

14 and the candlestick of light and its vessels and its lamps and the oil for light.

15 and the altar of incense and its staffs and the anointing oil and the incense of sweet spices and the covering for the entrance to the entrance of the tabernacle

16 the altar of the ascent and the grate of brass that belongs to it, its bars and all its utensils, the laver and its base.

17 The hangings of the court, their pillars and their sockets, and the curtain of the gate of the court.

18 the nails of the tabernacle and the nails of the court and their cords

19 the apparel for service, the consecrated apparel for Aaron the priest, and the apparel of his sons to serve as priests

20 And all the congregation of the children of Israel departed from the presence of Moses.

21 And came every man whose heart lifted him up, and all whose spirit moved him willingly brought the offering of the LORD for the work of the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the holy garments.

22 And the men came with the women, every willing-hearted person bringing a bracelet, an earring, a ring, a pendant, every vessel of gold, and every man who waved a wave offering of gold to the LORD.

23 And every man with whom was found blue, purple, crimson yarn, fine linen, goat hair, ram skins dyed red, and badger skins brought them.

24 All who contributed a gift of silver and brass brought the offering to the LORD, and everyone who had shittim wood for any manner of work of the service, they brought it.

25 And every woman wise of heart spun with her hands, and brought spun the blue, the purple, the scarlet thread, and the fine linen.

26 And all the women whose heart had stirred them with wisdom spun the goats.

27 And the princes brought the onyx stones and the stones for setting, for the ephod and for the breastplate.

28 and the spice and the oil for light and for anointing oil and for sweet incense.

29 All the men and women whose hearts moved them to bring anything for the work that the LORD had commanded to be done by the hand of Moses, the people of Israel brought a freewill offering to the LORD.

30 Moses said to the sons of Israel, See, the LORD has called by name Bezaleel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.

31 And he filled him with the spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, and with knowledge, and with all manner of work.

32 and to devise cunning work, to work in gold and in silver and in brass.

33 and in the cutting of stone for inlaying, and in the cutting of wood, to work in every kind of workmanship.

34 And he has put in his heart that he may teach, both he and Aholiab son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan.

35 Fill them with wisdom of heart to perform all manner of work: artificer, designer, and weaver in blue, and in purple, in crimson yarn and in fine linen, and weaver; makers of all manner of work and designers of cunning work.


## Chapter 36

1 And Bezaleel and Aholiab and every man wise of heart to whom the LORD gave wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work of the service of the holy, for all that the LORD commanded.

2 Moses called to Bezaleel and to Aholiab and to every man wise of heart to whom the LORD had given wisdom and whose heart had lifted him up to come near to the work to do it.

3 They took from before Moses all the offering that the people of Israel had brought for the work of the service of the sanctuary to make it, and they continued to bring him a freewill offering every morning morning.

4 Then came all the skilled men who were doing all the work of the sanctuary, each man from his work which they were doing.

5 And they said to Moses, saying, The folk are bringing more than enough for the work, the occupation which the LORD commanded to make.

6 Moses commanded, and they caused a proclamation to pass throughout the camp, saying, "Let no man or woman do any more work for the holy offering," and the people were restrained from bringing.

7 And the work was sufficient for all the work to do it, and there was excess.

8 All the skilled-hearted among the workers of the work made the curtains for the tabernacle: ten curtains of fine linen, blue, purple, and crimson, with cherubim of artistic workmanship.

9 The length of one curtain was twenty-eight cubits and the breadth four cubits; one measure for all the curtains.

10 And he joined five curtains one to another, and the other five curtains he joined one to another.

11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the curtain at the end of the coupling; so he made on the edge of the curtain, the outer one, at the second coupling.

12 Fifty loops he made in the one curtain, and fifty loops he made at the edge of the curtain that is in the second coupling, corresponding loop to loop.

13 And he made fifty golden taches, and joined the curtains one to another with the taches, and the tabernacle became one.

14 And he made curtains of goats for a covering upon the tabernacle: eleven curtains he made them.

15 The length of one curtain was thirty cubits, and the breadth of one curtain was four cubits; one measure for eleven curtains.

16 And he coupled the five curtains alone, and the six curtains apart.

17 He made fifty loops on the edge of the curtain at the coupling, and fifty loops he made on the edge of the curtain of the second coupling.

18 And he made fifty brasen taches to couple the tent together that it might be one.

19 And he made a covering for the tabernacle of rams' skins red-dyed, and a covering of badgers' skins above.

20 And he made the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing upright.

21 Ten cubits the length of the board, and a cubit and a half the breadth of the one board.

22 Two boards for one bench, coupled together, each to each; so he made for all the boards of the tabernacle.

23 And he made the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side southward.

24 And he made forty silver sockets: two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.

25 And for the side of the second tabernacle toward the north, he made twenty boards.

26 And their forty sockets were silver, two sockets under one board, and two sockets under one board.

27 And for the western side of the tabernacle he made six boards.

28 And he made two boards for the sides of the tabernacle at the corners.

29 They were coupled together below, and together they were complete to their top to the one ring; so he made for the two of them, for the two corners.

30 And there were eight boards, and their sockets of silver: sixteen sockets, two sockets under one board.

31 And he made bars of shittim wood, five for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, the one.

32 And five bars for the boards of the side of the second tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the western sides.

33 And he made the middle bar to pass through among the boards from end to end.

34 And he overlaid the boards with gold, and made their rings of gold, houses for the bars, and overlaid the bars with gold.

35 He made the vail of blue, purple, crimson, and scarlet, and fine twined linen: with a design of woven work he made it, with cherubims.

36 He made for it four pillars of shittim, and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold, and he cast for them four silver sockets.

37 He made a hanging for the entrance of the tent of blue and purple and scarlet thread and twisted linen, the work of a weaver.

38 and its five pillars and their hooks, and he overlaid their bands and their fillets with gold, and their five foundations were of copper.


## Chapter 37

1 Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood; two cubits and a half its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

2 He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a gold crown all around it.

3 And he cast for it four golden rings upon its four corners, and two rings upon its one side, and two rings upon its second side.

4 He made bars of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.

5 He brought the bars into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark.

6 He made a mercy seat of pure gold: two and a half cubits long and one and a half cubits wide.

7 And he made two cherubims of gold; beaten out of one piece he made them, from the two corners of the mercy seat.

8 One cherub from this end and one cherub from that end; from the mercy seat he made the cherubims of the two ends.

9 The cherubims had their wings spread upward, covering over the mercy seat with their wings, and their faces were each toward his brother; toward the mercy seat were the faces of the cherubims.

10 He made the table of shittim wood: two cubits its length, one cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

11 And he overlaid it with pure gold; and he made for it a crown of gold round about.

12 He made for it a border, a hand-breadth broad, round about, and he made a crown of gold for its border round about.

13 He poured four golden rings for it and put the rings on the four corners which belonged to its four legs.

14 At the border the rings were housings for the poles to carry the table.

15 He made the bars of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold to carry the table.

16 He made the vessels for the table: its dishes, its spoons, its bowls, and the covers for pouring out drink offerings, all of pure gold.

17 He made the candlestick of pure gold, beaten out of one piece he made the candlestick, its shaft and its branch, its cups, knops, and blossoms were from it.

18 And six branches coming out from its side: three branches of the candlestick from its one side and three branches of the candlestick from its second side.

19 Three cups, knop and flower, on one branch; and three cups, knop and flower, on one branch; so for the six branches going out from the candlestick.

20 And on the candlestick four cups, shaped like almond blossoms, its knops and its flowers.

21 a knop under two branches from it, and a knop under two branches from it, and a knop under two branches from it, for the six branches going out from it.

22 Their knops and their branches were of it; the whole was one beaten piece of pure gold.

23 He made its seven lamps, and its snuffers and its firepans of pure gold.

24 a talent of pure gold he made it, and all its vessels

25 He made the altar of incense of shittim wood; its length was a cubit and its breadth a cubit, square, and its height two cubits; its horns were of one piece with it.

26 He overlaid it with pure gold, its roof and its walls all around and its horns, and made a golden crown around it.

27 And two golden rings he made for it under its crown, on its two sides, on both its beams, as courts for the bars to carry it with them.

28 He made the bars of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold.

29 He made the holy anointing oil and the pure incense of sweet spices, the work of a perfumer.


## Chapter 38

1 He made the altar of ascent of shittah wood, five cubits its length and five cubits its breadth, square, and three cubits its height.

2 He made its horns on the four corners of it; its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with brass.

3 He made all the vessels of the altar, the caldrons, the shovels, the basins, the fleshhooks, and the firepans; all its vessels he made of bronze.

4 He made a brasen net-work grate for the altar, a work beneath its compass from below up to its middle.

5 He cast four rings at the four ends of the brasen grate, homes for the bars.

6 He made the bars of acacia wood and overlaid them with brass.

7 He brought the bars into the rings on the sides of the altar to carry it with them; he made it hollow with boards.

8 He made the laver of brass and its base of brass from the mirrors of the women who assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

9 He made the court on the south side, hangings of the court of twisted linen, a hundred cubits.

10 Their pillars were twenty with twenty sockets of copper; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were silver.

11 On the north side, one hundred cubits, their pillars twenty, their foundations twenty, copper; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

12 And for the western side there were fifty cubits of hangings, their pillars ten, their sockets ten, and the hooks of the pillars and their fillets silver.

13 and toward the east side fifty cubits

14 Fifteen cubits were the hangings for the corner; their pillars three and their sockets three.

15 And for the second arm, on this side and that of the gate of the court, hangings fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.

16 All the hangings of the court round about were of fine twisted linen.

17 The foundations for the pillars were of copper, the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals of silver; all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.

18 And the covering of the gate of the court was a woven work of blue, purple, crimson, and twisted fine linen, twenty cubits long and five cubits high according to the breadth of the hangings of the court.

19 Their pillars were four, their sockets four, of brass; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their tops and their fillets of silver.

20 All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the surrounding court were bronze.

21 These are the accounts of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, which were numbered according to the command of Moses, the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.

22 Bezaleel the son of Uri the son of Hur of the tribe of Judah made all that the LORD commanded Moses.

23 And with him Aholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, a craftsman and designer, and one who works in blue and purple and crimson and fine linen.

24 All the gold used for the work, for all the work of the sanctuary, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary.

25 The silver of the census of the congregation was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary.

26 Half a shekel, the bekah per head, half a shekel by the consecrated shekel, for every man who passes over among those numbered, from the age of twenty years and upward, six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

27 And there was one hundred talents of silver for casting the bases, the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the vail: one hundred bases for one hundred talents, a talent for a base.

28 And he made the thousand seven hundred and seventy-five hooks for the pillars apiece, and overlaid their chapiters, and coupled them.

29 And the brass of the wave offering was seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.

30 He made from it the sockets for the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the bronze altar, and its bronze grating, and all the altar's vessels.

31 and the sockets of the court all around, and the sockets of the gate of the court, and all the stakes of the tabernacle and all the stakes of the court all around.


## Chapter 39

1 And from the blue, purple, and crimson worm, they made garments of service for ministering in the holy place, and they made the holy garments that belonged to Aaron, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

2 He made the ephod of gold, blue, purple, and crimson, and fine linen.

3 They hammered the golden gins and cut threads, to make among the blue and among the purple and among the crimson worm and among the fine linen, the work of a skilled designer.

4 They made shoulder-pieces, joined together at the two ends.

5 And the curious girdle of the ephod, which is upon it, was of the same piece, woven with the same design: gold, blue, purple, and crimson worm, scarlet thread, and fine linen, as the LORD commanded Moses.

6 They made the onyx stones, mounted in gold settings, engraved like the engravings of a signet, with the names of the children of Israel.

7 He placed them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, stones of memorial for the children of Israel, as the LORD commanded Moses.

8 He made the breastplate, the work of a skilled craftsman, like the work of the ephod: gold, blue, purple, crimson, and twisted fine linen.

9 They made the breastplate square and double, a span its length and a span its breadth, being double.

10 They filled it with four rows of stone: a row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle—the first row.

11 And the second row, emerald, sapphire, and diamond.

12 The third row: ligure, agate, and amethyst.

13 And the fourth row a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper; enclosed in gold settings with their fillings.

14 The stones shall be twelve, corresponding to the names of the sons of Israel, engraved with the names of their respective tribes, each stone with its name, as inscribed on a seal.

15 They made chains of twisted cords as ends for the breastplate, made of pure gold.

16 They made two ouches of gold and two rings of gold and placed the two rings on the two edges of the breastplate.

17 They gave the two wreathen chains of gold upon the two rings, upon the edges of the breastplate.

18 And they gave the two ends of the two braided chains upon the two ouches, and they put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, opposite its face.

19 They made two gold rings and placed them on the two edges of the breastplate, on its border next to the side of the ephod on the inside.

20 They made two golden rings and placed them on the two shoulder-pieces of the ephod beneath its front edge, opposite its coupling, above the curious girdle of the ephod.

21 They joined the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a blue lace, so that it would be on the curious girdle of the ephod, and the breastplate would not come loose from the ephod, as the LORD commanded Moses.

22 He made the robe of the ephod, a work of weaving, all blue.

23 And the mouth of the robe within it shall be like the mouth of a habergeon, a border round about it shall not be torn.

24 They made pomegranates of blue, purple, and crimson yarn upon the skirts of the robe.

25 They made golden bells and put the bells among the pomegranates on the skirts of the robe all around among the pomegranates.

26 Bell and pomegranate, bell and pomegranate upon the hems of the robe round about, to serve as the LORD commanded Moses.

27 They made the linen garments, the work of weaving, for Aaron and for his sons.

28 and the linen diadem, and the ornaments of the linen bonnets, and the linen breeches, finely worked:

29 and the girdle of twisted fine linen with blue and purple and crimson, the work of a weaver, as the LORD commanded Moses.

30 They made the gold plate, the crown of holiness, pure gold, and they wrote on it, engraved like the engraving of a signet: Holy to the LORD.

31 They tied to it a blue cord, to fasten it on the turban above, as the LORD commanded Moses.

32 So all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was completed; the children of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so they did.

33 And they brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent and all its furniture, its taches, its boards, and its bars, its pillars and its sockets.

34 And the covering of rams' skins dyed red, and the covering of badgers' skins, and the vail of the covering.

35 the ark of the testimony and its bars and the mercy seat

36 the table with all its utensils and the bread of the Presence

37 the pure candlestick, its lamps, the lamps of the ordered place, and all its vessels, and the oil for the light.

38 and the golden altar, and the anointing oil, and the sweet incense, and the curtain of the entrance to the tabernacle.

39 the brasen altar and its brasen grate with its bars and all its vessels the laver and its base

40 the hangings of the court, its pillars and their sockets, the hanging for the gate of the court, its cords and its stakes, and all the vessels for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting

41 the apparel for ministering in the sanctuary, the consecrated garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments for his sons to serve as priests

42 According to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did all the work.

43 Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it according to the way the LORD had commanded; so Moses blessed them.


## Chapter 40

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

3 And you shall place there the ark of the testimony, and you shall screen over the ark with the vail.

4 And you shall bring in the table and set in order its arrangement, and you shall bring in the candlestick and light its lamps.

5 You shall set the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony, and you shall place the screen at the entrance of the tabernacle.

6 And you shall set the altar of the burnt offering before the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

7 And you shall place the laver between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water there.

8 And you shall set the court all around, and you shall give the covering of the court's gate.

9 Take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and consecrate it and all its vessels, and it shall be holy.

10 You shall anoint the altar of ascent and all its vessels, and you shall consecrate the altar, and the altar shall be most holy.

11 And you shall anoint the laver and its base, and consecrate them.

12 And you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and you shall wash them with water.

13 And you shall clothe Aaron with the holy garments, and anoint him and consecrate him, that he may serve me.

14 And you shall bring near his sons and clothe them with coats.

15 And you shall anoint them as you anointed their father, and they shall be priests to me, and their anointing shall be for a perpetual priesthood throughout their generations.

16 Moses did as all that the LORD commanded him, so he did.

17 And it came to pass in the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, that the tabernacle was erected.

18 Moses set up the tabernacle and placed its sockets, set up its boards, inserted its bars, and erected its pillars.

19 And he spread the tent over the dwelling place, and he put the covering of the tent on it from above, as the LORD commanded Moses.

20 He took and put the testimony into the ark, placed the bars on the ark, and put the mercy seat on the ark above.

21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle and set up the vail of the covering and screened over the ark of the testimony as the LORD commanded Moses.

22 And he placed the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tabernacle outside the vail.

23 And he arranged in order before the LORD the arrangement of bread, as the LORD commanded Moses.

24 And he set the candlestick in the tabernacle of assembly opposite the table, on the side of the dwelling toward the south.

25 The lamps burned before the LORD as the LORD had commanded Moses.

26 He set the golden altar in the tabernacle of assembly before the vail.

27 And he made incense go up in smoke on it, sweet spices, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

28 And he set the covering of the entrance for the tabernacle.

29 And he placed the altar of ascent at the entrance of the dwelling of the tent of appointed assembly, and he offered upon it the ascent and the gift as the LORD commanded Moses.

30 He placed the laver between the tabernacle of meeting and the altar, and put water there for washing.

31 And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet from it.

32 When they enter the tent of meeting or approach the altar, they shall wash, as the LORD commanded Moses.

33 Moses set up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and he placed the hanging of the court gate, and Moses finished the work.

34 And the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

35 Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud had settled upon it and the glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.

36 When the cloudy pillar was taken up from above the tabernacle, the children of Israel would journey in all their journeys.

37 And if the cloud does not rise up, they do not set out until the day the cloud rises.

38 For the LORD's cloud was upon the tabernacle by day, and fire would be in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel throughout all their journeyings.


# LEVITICUS

## Chapter 1

1 And he called to Moses, and Jehovah spoke to him from the tent of meeting, saying:

2 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: when a person from among you brings an offering to the LORD, from the cattle, from the beeves or from the sheep, you shall bring your offering.

3 If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd, he shall offer it, a male without blemish; he shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, that it may be acceptable before the LORD.

4 And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the burnt offering, and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement for him.

5 And he shall slaughter the young bull before the LORD, and Aaron’s sons the priests shall present the blood and sprinkle the blood against the altar all around, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

6 And he shall strip the ascent, and cut it into its parts.

7 And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar and lay wood upon the fire.

8 And the sons of Aaron the priests shall arrange the pieces, the head and the fat, upon the wood that is on the fire which is upon the altar.

9 And his bowels and his legs he shall wash with water, and the priest shall burn all on the altar as a burnt offering, an offering made by fire, for a sweet savour to the LORD.

10 And if his offering is from the flock, from lambs or from goats, for a burnt offering, he shall offer it, a male without blemish.

11 He shall slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the LORD, and Aaron’s sons the priests shall dash its blood against the altar all around.

12 And he shall cut it into parts, and its head and its fat, and the priest shall arrange them on the wood upon the fire upon the altar.

13 The entrails and the legs he shall wash with water, and the priest shall bring all and burn it upon the altar: a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

14 But if his offering be a burnt offering of fowl unto the LORD, then he shall bring his oblation of turtle doves or of young pigeons.

15 And the priest shall bring it to the altar and remove its head, and burn it on the altar, and its blood shall be drained against the wall of the altar.

16 And he shall remove her crop when she is plucked, and shall cast her beside the altar eastward, to the place of the ashes.

17 and he shall split it with its wings, not dividing it; and the priest shall burn it on the altar upon the wood that is upon the fire; it is a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.


## Chapter 2

1 And any person who brings an offering of a grain offering to the LORD shall present fine flour; and they shall pour oil on it and put frankincense on it.

2 And she shall bring it to the sons of Aaron the priests; and he shall take from it a handful of the fine flour from her meal offering and from its oil, and all the frankincense that is on it; and the priest shall burn the memorial of it on the altar, an offering made by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

3 And the remainder of the oblation, for Aaron and his sons, is a most holy thing from the offerings made by fire to the LORD.

4 And when you bring an offering of a grain offering baked in an oven, fine flour cakes without leaven mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil.

5 And if your offering is a gift cooked in a pan, it shall be fine flour mixed with oil, unleavened.

6 Break it into pieces, pour oil upon it; it is an offering.

7 And if your offering is an oblation of a fryingpan, it shall be fine flour with oil made.

8 And you shall bring the oblation that is made from these to the LORD, and bring it near to the priest, and he shall present it to the altar.

9 And the priest shall lift the memorial portion from the gift and burn it on the altar, a sacrifice, a sweet savour to the Lord.

10 And the remainder of the gift for Aaron and his sons is most holy from the offerings made by fire to the LORD.

11 Every oblation which you shall offer to the LORD shall not be made with leaven, for any leaven and any honey you shall not burn from it as an offering by fire to the LORD.

12 You shall present the offering of the firstfruits to the LORD, but they shall not go up to the altar for a sweet savour.

13 Every offering of your grain offering you shall season with salt; you shall not omit the salt of your God's covenant from your grain offering; with all your offering you shall offer salt.

14 And if you bring a gift of first fruits to the LORD, you shall bring a present of fresh ears of corn, roasted with fire, of beaten corn from a fruitful field, even your gift of first fruits.

15 And you shall put oil upon it and place upon it frankincense; it is an offering.

16 The priest shall make smoke the memorial from its beaten corn and from its oil, with all its frankincense, an offering by fire to the LORD.


## Chapter 3

1 And if his offering is an offering of peace offerings, from the herd he shall offer it, whether male or female; he shall offer it unblemished before the LORD.

2 He shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the sons of Aaron the priests shall dash its blood against the altar all around.

3 He shall present the peace offering as an offering by fire to the LORD: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails.

4 And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is on the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, he shall remove.

5 And the sons of Aaron shall burn it on the altar upon the ascent, which is upon the wood that is upon the fire, a sacrifice, a sweet savour by fire to the LORD.

6 And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD is from the flock, male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.

7 If a lamb is offering his oblation, then he shall present it before the LORD.

8 He shall lay his hand upon the head of the offering and slaughter it before the tent of meeting, and Aaron’s sons shall dash its blood against the altar all around.

9 And from the peace offering he shall present an offering by fire to the LORD: the fat of the rump, the whole rump opposite the backbone, and the fat covering the inner parts, and all the fat that is upon the inner parts.

10 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, which is on the flanks, and the caul above the liver, upon the kidneys, he shall remove.

11 And the priest shall burn it on the altar, a food of fire for the LORD.

12 And if a goat is his offering, he shall present it before the LORD.

13 He placed his hand upon its head and slaughtered it before the tent of meeting, and Aaron’s sons dashed its blood against the altar all around.

14 And he shall bring from it his offering, a sacrifice by fire to the LORD: the fat that covers the inward parts, and all the fat that is upon the inward parts.

15 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, which is on the flanks, and the caul above the liver, which is on the kidneys, he shall remove.

16 And the priest shall burn them on the altar as food, a sacrifice made by fire, for a sweet savour: all the fat is the LORD's.

17 An appointed statute for your generations in all your dwelling places: all fat and all blood you shall not eat.


## Chapter 4

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: If a person sins unwittingly from any of the commandments of the LORD which should not be done, and does any one of these things,

3 If the anointed priest sins, causing the people to commit a trespass, then he shall bring a sinless young bull from the herd as a purification offering to the LORD for his sin.

4 And he shall bring the young bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD, and shall lay his hand upon the head of the young bull, and shall slaughter the young bull before the LORD.

5 And the anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it to the tent of meeting.

6 The priest dipped his finger in the blood and sprinkled some of the blood seven times before the LORD, at the face of the vail of the holy.

7 The priest shall take some of the blood and put it on the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD, which is in the tent of meeting, and all the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of ascent, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

8 And all the fat of the bull of the sin offering he shall remove from it, the fat that covers the inner parts, and all the fat that is on the inner parts.

9 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, which is on the flanks, and the caul above the liver, upon the kidneys, he shall remove.

10 As the ox is raised from the peace offering sacrifice, the priest shall burn them on the altar of ascent.

11 And the hide of the young bull and all its flesh, with its head and its legs, and its inward parts and its dung.

12 He shall bring out the entire bull outside the camp to a clean place, to the place where the ashes are poured out, and he shall burn it with wood on the fire, at the place where the ashes are poured out it shall be burned.

13 But if the whole congregation of Israel shall err and the thing be hidden from the eyes of the assembly, and they do any one of all the commandments of the LORD which should not be done, then they shall be guilty.

14 And the sin offering for which they sinned shall be known, and the congregation shall bring a young bull as a sin offering and bring it before the tent of meeting.

15 The elders of the assembly shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD, and they shall slaughter the bull before the LORD.

16 And the anointed priest shall bring from the blood of the bull to the tent of appointed assembly.

17 The priest dipped his finger from the blood and sprinkled seven times before the LORD the face of the vail.

18 And from the blood he shall put upon the horns of the altar that is before the LORD, which is in the tent of meeting, and all the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of ascent, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

19 And all its fat he shall remove from it and burn on the altar.

20 And he shall do to the young bull as he did to the young bull of the sin offering; the priest shall make atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven to them.

21 And he shall bring the bull outside the camp and burn it, as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering of the congregation.

22 When a ruler sins and does one of all the commandments of the LORD his God which should not be done unwittingly, and is guilty,

23 or the punishment for his sin is made known to him, and he brings his offering: a male goat, a flawless kid of the goats.

24 He shall lay his hand on the head of the goat and slaughter it in the place where they slaughter the burnt offering before the LORD; it is a sin offering.

25 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin-purification with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering, and pour out its blood at the base of the altar of the burnt offering.

26 And all its fat he shall burn on the altar, like the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.

27 And if any one person unwittingly sins from among the people of the land by doing any one of the commandments of the LORD which shall not be done, then he is guilty.

28 Or if he is made aware of his sin that he has sinned, then he shall bring his offering: a flawless female kid of the goats for the purifying offering for the sin that he has sinned.

29 He shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and kill the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering.

30 The priest shall take from her blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering, and all her blood he shall pour out at the foundation of the altar.

31 And he shall remove from it all its fat, just as the fat is removed from above the peace offering; and the priest shall burn it on the altar for a sweet savour to the LORD, and the priest shall make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.

32 And if a lamb is brought as his offering for purification from sin, a flawless female shall be brought.

33 He shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slaughter it as a sin offering in the place where they slaughter the burnt offering.

34 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering from the altar of the burnt offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of the burnt offering, and all her blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

35 And all its fat he shall remove, as the fat of the lamb is removed from the peace offering; and the priest shall burn them on the altar as an offering by fire to the LORD, and the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin that he has committed, and it shall be forgiven him.


## Chapter 5

1 If any person sins and hears the sound of a curse, and he is a witness, or has seen, or knows, if he does not report it, he shall bear his iniquity.

2 or any creature that touches any unclean thing, or the carcass of an unclean beast, or the carcass of unclean cattle, or the carcass of an unclean creeping thing, and it is hidden from him, and he is unclean and guilty.

3 Or when he touches the filthiness of any person, according to all the uncleanness by which he is defiled, and it is hidden from him, yet he comes to know, he is guilty.

4 Or any person who swears thoughtlessly with the lips to do evil or to do good, whatever the person may swear by oath, and the matter is hidden from them, though they know of it, they shall be guilty concerning one of these things.

5 And it shall be, when he is guilty concerning one of these, he shall confess the sin that he has committed upon it.

6 And he shall bring his guilt offering to the LORD for his sin which he committed: a female lamb from the flock or a female kid of the goats as a sin offering, and the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin.

7 But if his hand cannot reach the value of a lamb, then he shall bring his guilt offering for what he has sinned: two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the LORD, one for a purification offering and one for a burnt offering.

8 And he shall bring them to the priest, and shall present what is for the purifying first, and then pinch its head from above its back, but not sever.

9 And he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the wall of the altar, and the blood that remains shall be drained to the base of the altar; it is a purification offering.

10 And the second he shall make a burnt offering according to the ordinance, and the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin which he sinned, and it shall be forgiven him.

11 But if his hand cannot reach two turtle doves or two young pigeons, then he shall bring as his offering for that which he sinned a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a sin offering; he shall not put oil on it, nor shall he put frankincense on it, for it is a sin offering.

12 And he shall bring her to the priest, and the priest shall take from it a handful of its memorial and burn it on the altar by fire of the LORD; it is a purifying.

13 And the priest shall make atonement for him concerning the sin he sinned from one of these, and it shall be forgiven him; and the gift shall be the priest's, as the meal offering.

14 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

15 Any person who commits a trespass by unwitting error in any of the holy things of the LORD shall bring as his trespass offering to the LORD a ram without blemish from the flock, according to your valuation, in silver shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, for a guilt offering.

16 And the one who has sinned against the consecrated thing shall repay it, and add its fifth part to it, and give it to the priest; and the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering, and it shall be forgiven him.

17 And if any person sins and does any one of the LORD's commandments which ought not to be done, though unaware, then he is guilty and shall bear his iniquity.

18 And he shall bring a blameless ram from the flock, according to your valuation, for a guilt offering, to the priest; and the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his unwitting error, though he did not know, and it shall be forgiven him.

19 He is guilty, he has incurred guilt before the LORD.


## Chapter 6

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying

2 Any person who sins and commits a trespass against the LORD, and deals falsely with another in that which was delivered to keep, or in fellowship, or in robbery, or oppresses another.

3 or he finds a lost thing and deals with it deceitfully, and swears falsely regarding any one of all the things that a person may do to sin thereby.

4 And it shall be, if he sins and becomes guilty, that he shall restore the spoil which he took violently away, or the thing deceitfully gotten which he oppressed, or the store which was delivered to keep with him, or the lost thing which he found.

5 or for anything about which he swore falsely, he shall repay it in full and add a fifth to it; he shall give it to the one to whom it belongs on the day of his guilt.

6 and his guilt offering he shall bring to the LORD: a flawless ram from the flock, according to your valuation, for a guilt offering, to the priest.

7 And the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD, and it shall be forgiven him for any of the things whereby he might sin.

8 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

9 Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the law of the burnt offering: it is the burnt offering upon the burning upon the altar all night until the morning, and the fire upon the altar shall be kept burning in it.

10 The priest shall wear a linen garment and linen breeches on his body, and he shall lift the ashes from the burnt offering that the fire consumed upon the altar and place them beside the altar.

11 And he shall take off his garments and put on other garments, and carry out the ashes to a clean place outside the camp.

12 The burning fire shall be kept burning on the altar; it shall not go out. The priest shall burn wood on it morning by morning, and shall arrange the burnt offering on it, and shall make smoke the fat of the peace offerings on it.

13 A burning fire shall be alway burning upon the altar; it shall not be quenched.

14 And this is the law of the oblation: the sons of Aaron shall present it before the LORD before the face of the altar.

15 And he shall lift from it a handful of the fine flour of the gift and of its oil, and all the frankincense that is on the gift, and shall burn it on the altar as a sweet savour, its memorial, to the LORD.

16 And the remainder of it shall be eaten by Aaron and his sons as unleavened bread in a holy place; in the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.

17 You shall not bake leaven in the portion I have given of my fire offering; it is most holy, like the sin offering and like the guilt offering.

18 Every male among the sons of Aaron may eat it, a perpetual statute for your generations from the offerings made by fire to the LORD; whatever touches them shall be holy.

19 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

20 This is the oblation of Aaron and his sons which they shall offer to the LORD on the day he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a continual offering, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.

21 On a pan with oil it shall be made, a baked piece of the oblation of fine flour you shall bring, a sweet savour, a gift to the LORD.

22 And the anointed priest in his place from among his sons shall make it: a statute forever for the LORD, wholly consumed by fire.

23 And every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned; it shall not be eaten.

24 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

25 Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: This is the law of the sin offering: in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, there shall the sin offering be slaughtered before the LORD; it is most holy.

26 The priest who causes her to offend shall eat it; it shall be eaten in a holy place, in the court of the tent of meeting.

27 All that touches her body shall be holy, and whatever is sprinkled with her blood upon a garment, the garment upon which it is sprinkled must be washed in a holy place.

28 And a vessel of earthenware in which it is cooked shall be broken, but if it was cooked in a vessel of copper, it shall be scoured and rinsed in water.

29 Every male among the priests may eat it; it is a most holy thing.

30 Every sin offering that is brought in from its blood into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place shall not be eaten; it shall be burned in the fire.


## Chapter 7

1 And this is the law of the guilt offering: it is most holy.

2 In the place where they slaughter the burnt offering they shall slaughter the trespass offering, and its blood he shall dash against the altar all around.

3 And all the fat of it he shall offer from it: the rump and the fat that covers the inward parts.

4 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, which is on the flanks, and the caul above the liver, upon the kidneys, he shall remove.

5 And the priest shall burn them on the altar as an offering by fire to the Lord; it is a trespass offering.

6 Every male among the priests may eat it; it shall be eaten in a holy place; it is a most holy thing.

7 As the punishment for sin, so the guilt offering: one law applies to them; the priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.

8 And the hide of the burnt offering that a man presents shall belong to the priest who offers it.

9 Every oblation baked in an oven and every one prepared in a fryingpan or on a pan, to the priest who presents it shall belong.

10 Every grain offering mixed with oil and baked shall be for all the sons of Aaron, each like his brother.

11 And this is the law of the peace offering which he shall offer to the LORD.

12 If he brings it for a sacrifice of praise, then he shall offer cakes of unleavened bread mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and fine flour mixed into cakes soaked in oil.

13 With cakes of leavened bread he shall present his offering, upon the sacrifice of his thanksgiving peace offering.

14 And he shall offer from it one of every offering, a gift to the LORD; to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offerings it shall belong.

15 And the flesh of his thanksgiving peace offering shall be eaten on the day of his offering; they shall not leave any of it until morning.

16 And if a vow or a free offering is his oblation, on the day that he offers his sacrifice it shall be eaten, and on the morrow, and what remains of it shall be eaten.

17 And the flesh of the offering that remains on the third day shall be burned with fire.

18 But if the flesh of his sacrifice of peace offerings is eaten on the third day, it will not be accepted; he who offers it will not be credited with it; it will be an abomination, and the person who eats of it will bear his iniquity.

19 And the body that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burned with fire; and the body, everyone who is clean may eat body.

20 Any person who eats flesh from the peace offering sacrifice belonging to the LORD while having uncleanness upon him, that person shall be cut off from his people.

21 And any person who touches any uncleanness, whether human impurity, or unclean cattle, or any abominable unclean thing, and then eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings belonging to the LORD, that person shall be cut off from his people.

22 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

23 Speak to the children of Israel, saying: Every fat of ox, lamb, and goat you shall not eat.

24 The fat of a carcass and the fat of that which was torn by beasts shall be used for any manner of work, but as for eating, you shall not eat it.

25 For whoever eats any fat from the beast that one presents as an offering by fire to the LORD, that person who eats shall be cut off from among his people.

26 You shall not eat any blood, in all your dwelling places, whether it is from birds or from beasts.

27 Every person who eats any blood, that person shall be cut off from among his people.

28 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

29 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying, The one bringing his peace offering sacrifice to the Lord shall bring his offering to the Lord from the peace offering sacrifice.

30 Their hands shall bring the offerings made by fire to the LORD, the fat with the breast; they shall bring the breast to wave it as a wave offering before the LORD.

31 The priest shall burn the fat on the altar, and the breast shall be for Aaron and for his sons.

32 And the right thigh you shall give as an offering to the priest from your peace offerings.

33 The one presenting the blood of the peace offering and the fat from the sons of Aaron—he shall have the right thigh as his portion.

34 For the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering I have taken from the Israelites, from their peace offerings, and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons as a due for all time from the Israelites.

35 This is the anointing of Aaron and the anointing of his sons from the offerings by fire of the LORD on the day he brought them near to serve as priests to the LORD.

36 which the LORD commanded to give them on the day he anointed them from the children of Israel as a perpetual statute for their generations

37 This is the law for the burnt offering, the gift, the sin offering, the guilt offering, the consecration offering, and the sacrifice of peace offerings.

38 what the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai when he commanded the children of Israel to present their offerings to the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai


## Chapter 8

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and the bull of the sin offering, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread.

3 And all the congregation he assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

4 Moses did as the LORD commanded him, and the assembly gathered at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

5 Moses said to the assembly, This is the act which the LORD has commanded to do.

6 Moses brought forward Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.

7 And he put the coat upon him, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him, and girded him with the curious girdle of the ephod, and fastened it to him with it.

8 And he placed upon him the breastplate, and he put into the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim.

9 He placed the diadem on his head and placed on the diadem, opposite his face, the golden plate, the crown of holiness, as the LORD commanded Moses.

10 Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it, and consecrated them.

11 He sprinkled from it on the altar seven times and anointed the altar and all its vessels and the laver and its base to consecrate them.

12 He poured oil from the anointing ointment upon the head of Aaron and anointed him to consecrate him.

13 Moses brought near the sons of Aaron and clothed them with coats and girded them with a girdle and set bonnets upon them as the LORD commanded Moses.

14 And he brought near the bull of the purifying offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bull of the purifying offering.

15 Moses slaughtered it and took the blood and put it on the horns of the altar all around with his finger and purified the altar and atoned for it with the blood which he poured at the base of the altar.

16 And Moses took all the fat that was on the inward parts, and the caul upon the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that was on them, and Moses burned them on the altar.

17 And the young bull with its hide and its flesh and its dung he burned in the fire outside the camp as the LORD commanded Moses.

18 He brought near the ram of the burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.

19 Moses slaughtered it and Moses sprinkled the blood on the altar round about.

20 And the ram he cut into pieces and Moses burned the head and the pieces and the fat.

21 And he washed the inward parts and the legs with water, and Moses offered the whole ram upon the altar as a burnt offering, a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD, as the LORD commanded Moses.

22 And he brought near the second ram, the ram of consecration, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.

23 Moses slaughtered it and took some of its blood and put it on the tip of Aaron's right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the great toe of his right foot.

24 And Moses brought near the sons of Aaron and took blood and put it on the tip of their right ear and on the thumb of their right hand and on the great toe of their right foot and Moses sprinkled the blood on every side of the altar.

25 He took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that was on the inner parts, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat, and the right thigh.

26 From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD, he took one cake of unleavened bread, one cake of bread anointed with oil, and one wafer, and placed them upon the fat portions and upon the right thigh.

27 And he gave all of it upon the palms of Aaron and upon the palms of his sons, and he waved them as a wave offering before the LORD.

28 Moses took them from upon their hands and burned them on the altar with the burnt offering; they were a consecration as a savour of sweet odour, an offering by fire to the LORD.

29 Moses took the breast and waved it as a wave offering before the LORD from the ram of consecration; it was Moses’ portion, as the LORD commanded Moses.

30 Moses took oil of anointing and from the blood that was upon the altar and sprinkled upon Aaron, upon his apparel, and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him; and he consecrated Aaron, his apparel, and his sons, and the garments of his sons with him.

31 Moses said to Aaron and to his sons, Remove the flesh to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there you shall eat it, and the bread that is in the basket of consecration, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.

32 And the remainder of the flesh and the bread you shall burn with fire.

33 From the entrance of the tent of appointed meeting you shall not go out seven days until the day of the completion of your days of consecration, for seven days he shall fill your hand.

34 As He did on this day, so the LORD has commanded to do, to make atonement for you.

35 At the entrance of the tent of meeting you shall dwell day and night seven days and you shall keep the charge of the LORD so that you do not die for so I have been commanded

36 Aaron and his sons did all the words which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses.


## Chapter 9

1 On the eighth day Moses called to Aaron and to his sons and to the elders of Israel.

2 And he said to Aaron, Take for yourself a calf, a young bullock for a sin offering, and a ram without blemish for a burnt offering, and bring them before the LORD.

3 And to the children of Israel you shall speak, saying, Take a male goat for a sin offering, and a calf and a lamb, each of one year old, without blemish, for a burnt offering.

4 a bull and a ram for peace offerings to sacrifice before the LORD and a grain offering mixed with oil for today the LORD will appear to you

5 They took what Moses had commanded to the presence of the tent of meeting, and all the congregation approached and stood before the LORD.

6 Moses said, This is the act that the LORD has commanded you to do, and the glory of the LORD will appear to you.

7 Moses said to Aaron, "Come near to the altar and perform your purifying offering and your burnt offering, and make atonement for yourself and for the people; then make the offering of the people and make atonement for them, as the LORD has commanded."

8 Then Aaron came near to the altar and slaughtered the calf of the purifying offering that belonged to him.

9 Aaron's sons brought the blood to him, and he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar, and poured the blood at the base of the altar.

10 And the fat and the kidneys and the caul above the liver from the sin offering he burned on the altar, as the LORD commanded Moses.

11 And the body and the skin he burned in the fire outside the camp.

12 He slaughtered the ascent offering, and Aaron's sons brought the blood to him, and he sprinkled it on the altar all around.

13 And they brought near the ascent offering to him, its parts and its head, and he made smoke rise upon the altar.

14 And he washed the inward parts and the legs, and he burned the burnt offering on the altar.

15 And he brought near the offering of the people, and took the goat of the purifying offering that was for the people, and he slaughtered it and made atonement with it as the first.

16 And he brought near the burnt offering and did it according to the ordinance.

17 He brought near the oblation and filled his palm from it, and burned it on the altar, apart from the burnt offering of the morning.

18 He slaughtered the bull and the ram, the people's sacrifice of peace offerings, and Aaron's sons took the blood to him, and he sprinkled it around on the altar.

19 and the finest parts from the bull and from the ram: the rump, and that which covereth, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver.

20 They placed the best parts on the breasts and burned the fat portions on the altar.

21 And Aaron waved the breast and the right thigh as a wave offering before the LORD, as Moses had commanded.

22 Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them; then he came down after finishing the sin offering, the burnt offering, and the peace offering.

23 Moses and Aaron came into the tent of meeting, and they went out and blessed the people, and the glory of the LORD appeared to all the people.

24 Fire came forth from the presence of the LORD and consumed the ascent and the fat on the altar, and all the people saw and shouted for joy and fell upon their faces.


## Chapter 10

1 And the sons of Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, each took his censer and put fire in them and placed on it incense, and they brought near before the LORD strange fire, which he had not commanded them.

2 And fire came out from the presence of the LORD and consumed them, and they died before the presence of the LORD.

3 Moses said to Aaron, "It is that which Jehovah spoke, saying, 'I will be sanctified in My near ones, and before all the people I will be honored.' " And Aaron was silent.

4 Moses called to Mishael and Elizaphan, the sons of Uzziel, Aaron's uncle, and said to them, "Come near, carry your brothers from before the sanctuary to outside the camp."

5 They approached and carried them out in their garments to outside the camp as Moses had spoken.

6 Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and to Ithamar his sons, Do not let your heads be disheveled nor tear your garments, lest you die and wrath come upon all the congregation; but your brothers, all the house of Israel, shall mourn the burning that the LORD has kindled.

7 Do not go out from the entrance of the tent of appointed assembly, lest you die, for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.

8 The LORD spoke to Aaron, saying:

9 Do not drink wine or strong drink, you or your children with you, when you enter the tent of meeting, so that you do not die; a perpetual statute for your generations.

10 and to distinguish between the consecrated and the common, and between the unclean and the clean.

11 To teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the LORD spoke to them by the hand of Moses.

12 Moses spoke to Aaron and to Eleazar and to Ithamar his sons that were left: Take the gift that is left from the offerings made by fire to the LORD, and eat it unleavened beside the altar, for it is most holy.

13 You shall eat it in a holy place, for it is your due and the due of your children, from the offerings made by fire to the LORD, as I have commanded you.

14 And the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering you shall eat in a holy place, you and your sons and your daughters with you, for they are your due and your sons’ due, given from the sacrifices of the peace offerings of the people of Israel.

15 The hip of the heave offering and the breast of the wave offering they shall bring, with the fat portions burned by fire, to wave as a wave offering before the LORD; and it shall be for you and your sons with you as a perpetual statute, as the LORD has commanded.

16 And the goat of the sin offering was sought out by Moses, but behold, it had been burned up; so he was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, the sons of Aaron, saying:

17 Why did you not eat the punishment for sin in the place of the sanctuary, since it is a consecrated thing, a most holy offering? He gave it to you to bear the fault of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD.

18 Since its blood was not brought inside the holy place, you shall eat it in the sanctuary as I have commanded.

19 Then Aaron said to Moses, Behold, this day they presented their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, and such things have befallen me; would it be acceptable to the LORD if I ate the sin offering today?

20 And Moses heard, and it seemed good in his eyes.


## Chapter 11

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying to them:

2 Speak to the children of Israel, saying: This is the beast you may eat from all the cattle upon the land.

3 Every beast that parts the hoof and divides the cleft among the hooves and chews the cud—you may eat it.
