4 Yet this you shall not eat among those that bring up the cud or among those that divide the hoof: the camel, because it brings up the cud but does not divide the hoof—it is unclean to you.

5 And the coney, because it brings up cud but does not divide the hoof: it is unclean to you.

6 And the hare, because it brings up cud but does not have a divided hoof: it is unclean to you.

7 And the swine, because it parts the hoof and is clovenfooted, but does not chew the cud, it is unclean to you.

8 You shall not eat of their flesh and you shall not touch their dead body; they are unclean to you.

9 You shall eat this from all that is in the waters: everything that has fins and scales in the waters, in the seas and in the rivers, you may eat.

10 All that have no fins and scales in the seas and in the rivers, every creeping thing that moves in the waters and every living creature that is in the waters, they are an abomination to you.

11 And they shall be abominable to you; you shall not eat of their flesh, and their dead bodies you shall abominate.

12 Any creature in the waters that has neither fins nor scales is an abomination to you.

13 And these you shall detest from among the birds; they are an abomination: the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the ospray.

14 and the vulture after its kind, and the kite after its kind

15 every raven after its kind

16 and the daughter of the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckoo, and the hawk according to its kind.

17 and the cup and the cormorant and the (great) owl.

18 and the swan, and the cormorant, and the gier-eagle.

19 And the stork, the heron according to its kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.

20 Every creeping thing that flies, walking on four, is an abomination to you.

21 Only this you may eat of every creeping thing that flies, which walks upon four legs, whose legs above its feet are jointed to leap with upon the ground.

22 These you may eat from them: the locust according to its kind, and the bald locust according to its kind, and the beetle according to its kind, and the locust according to its kind.

23 And every creeping thing that flieth, which hath four legs, is an abomination unto you.

24 And by these you shall become defiled: whoever touches their dead body shall be unclean until evening.

25 And everyone who carries any of their dead bodies shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.

26 Every beast that parts the hoof but does not split the hoof and does not chew the cud is unclean to you; whoever touches them shall become unclean.

27 And every thing that goes on its paws, among all the living creatures that go on four, they are unclean to you: whatsoever touches their dead body shall be unclean until the evening.

28 And the one who carries their dead body shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.

29 And this is the unclean one for you among the creeping things that move upon the ground: the weasel, the mouse, and the tortoise according to its kind.

30 And the ferret and the chameleon and the lizard and the snail and the mole.

31 These are the unclean to you among all the creeping things: any that touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.

32 Everything that falls upon them when they die becomes unclean, whether any wooden vessel or garment or leather or sackcloth—all articles of workmanship—shall be immersed in water and remain unclean until evening, then become clean.

33 Every vessel of earth into which any of them falls shall be unclean, and it you shall break.

34 Every food that may be eaten, which water comes upon, shall be defiled; and every drink that may be drunk in any vessel shall be defiled.

35 And every thing that falls from their dead body upon it shall be unclean; an oven and ranges for pots shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and they shall be unclean to you.

36 Only a fountain and a cistern, a gathering of water, shall be clean, but whoever touches their dead body shall be unclean.

37 And if any of their carcasses falls upon any sown seed, the seed is clean.

38 And if water be poured upon seed, and any of their dead bodies fall upon it, it is unclean to you.

39 And if any of the cattle which are yours for food should die, the one who touches its dead body shall be unclean until evening.

40 And the one who eats from a body that died of itself shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening, and the one who carries the dead body shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening.

41 And every creeping thing that moves upon the ground is an abomination; it shall not be eaten.

42 Every creature that goes on its belly, and every creature that goes on all fours, and all that multiply legs among all the creeping things that creep upon the earth—you shall not eat them, for they are an abomination.

43 Do not make your souls abhorrent with any creeping thing that creeps, and do not become unclean through them so that you are defiled by them.

44 For I am the LORD your God, so you shall sanctify yourselves and be holy, for I am holy; and you shall not defile your souls with any creeping thing that moves upon the earth.

45 For I am the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God, and you shall be holy, for I am holy.

46 This is the law concerning the beast, and the bird, and every living creature that swarms in the waters, and every creature that swarms upon the land.

47 to distinguish between the unclean and the clean, and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that is not to be eaten.


## Chapter 12

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: When a woman conceives and bears a male, she shall be unclean seven days; like the days of the separation of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.

3 On the eighth day his foreskin shall be circumcised.

4 And thirty days and three days you shall dwell in the blood of purification; every holy thing you shall not touch, and to the sanctuary you shall not come until the days of her purification are fulfilled.

5 And if a female is born, she shall be unclean two weeks as during her menstruation; and she shall remain sixty-six days in the blood of her purification.

6 When the period of her purification is complete, for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of one year for a burnt offering, and a pigeon or a turtle dove for a sin offering, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to the priest.

7 And he shall bring her before the LORD, and make atonement for her; and she shall be clean from the fountain of her blood. This is the law for her who bears a male or a female.

8 But if her hand cannot find sufficient sheep, then she shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons, one for a burnt offering and one for a sin offering, and the priest shall make atonement for her, and she shall be clean.


## Chapter 13

1 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

2 When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling, a scab, or a bright spot, and it becomes on the skin of his body a plague of leprosy, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.

3 And the priest shall see the plague in the skin of the body, and hair in the plague has turned white, and the appearance of the plague is deeper than the skin of his flesh: it is a plague of leprosy; and the priest shall see it and pronounce him unclean.

4 But if the bright spot is white in the skin of his flesh, and its appearance is not deeper than the skin, and the hair has not turned white, then the priest shall isolate the sore for seven days.

5 And the priest shall see him on the seventh day, and behold, the sore remains the same in his eyes, the sore has not spread in the skin, and the priest shall confine him seven days a second time.

6 And the priest shall look at it again on the seventh day, and behold, the plague has become dim and the plague has not spread in the skin; then the priest shall cleanse him: it is a scab; and he shall wash his garments and be clean.

7 And if the scab spreads on the skin after it has been shown to the priest for its cleansing, it shall be shown again to the priest.

8 And the priest shall see, and behold, the scab has spread on the skin, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is leprosy.

9 When a leprous plague is upon a person, he shall be brought to the priest.

10 Then the priest shall look, and behold, a rising of white in the skin, and the hair in it has turned white, and the quick of living flesh is in the rising.

11 Leprosy it is in the skin of his body and the priest shall pronounce him unclean; he shall not shut him up, for unclean he is.

12 And if the leprosy breaks out abundantly on the skin, and the leprosy covers the whole skin of the plague from its head to its feet, as far as the eye of the priest can see,

13 The priest shall see that the leprosy has covered his whole body, and he shall cleanse the plague; when it all turns white, he is clean.

14 And on the day that living flesh appears in it, it shall become unclean.

15 The priest sees the living flesh and declares it unclean; the living flesh is unclean; it is leprosy.

16 Or if the living body should turn white and go to the priest,

17 And the priest shall see him, and behold, the sore has turned white, and the priest shall purify the sore: it is pure.

18 And if there is a boil in the flesh of his skin, it shall be healed.

19 And there was in the place of the boil a rising of white or a bright spot white reddish, and it appeared to the priest.

20 And the priest shall see, and behold, the appearance thereof is lower than the skin, and the hair thereof is turned white; and the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a plague of leprosy in the boil that has broken out.

21 And if the priest sees that there is no white hair in it and the swelling is not lower than the skin and it is not bright, then the priest shall shut him up seven days.

22 But if the sore spreads in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a plague.

23 But if the bright spot remains in its place and has not spread, it is an inflammation of the boil; the priest shall pronounce him clean.

24 Or flesh, if there is in his skin a burning by fire, and the healing of the burn becomes a bright spot, white reddish or white.

25 And the priest shall see it, and behold, the hair has turned white in the bright spot and its appearance is deep from the skin, it is leprosy in the burning that has broken out; and the priest shall pronounce him unclean, it is a plague of leprosy.

26 But if the priest sees that there is no white hair in the bright spot, and the bright spot is not lower than the skin, and it is dim, then the priest shall shut him up seven days.

27 And the priest shall see him on the seventh day; if the sore has spread in the skin, then the priest shall defile him: it is a plague of leprosy.

28 And if the bright spot be healed beneath it, has not spread in the skin, and it be dim, a rising of the burning, then the priest shall pronounce him clean, for it is an inflammation of the burning.

29 Any man or woman who has a sore on the head or on the beard.

30 And the priest shall see the sore, and behold, its appearance is lower than the skin, and therein is thin yellow hair; then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a dry scall, a leprosy of the head or of the beard.

31 And when the priest sees the scall plague, if indeed its appearance is not deeper than the skin and there is no black hair in it, then the priest shall confine the scall plague for seven days.

32 And the priest shall see the sore on the seventh day, and behold, the dry scall has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and the appearance of the dry scall is not deeper than the skin.

33 And he shall shave himself, but the (dry) scall he shall not shave; and the priest shall confine the (dry) scall seven days a second time.

34 Then the priest shall see the scall on the day, and indeed, the scall has not spread in the skin, and its appearance is not deeper than the skin; then the priest shall cleanse him, and he shall wash his garments, and he shall be clean.

35 But if the (dry) scall spreads on the skin after its cleansing:

36 And the priest shall see him, and behold, the scall has spread in the skin; the priest shall not seek the yellow hair; he is unclean.

37 But if in the view of the one afflicted the scall has remained and black hair has grown in it, the scall is healed; he is clean, and the priest shall cleanse him.

38 Any man or woman who has a bright spot on the skin of their flesh, a bright spot, white in color.

39 And the priest shall see, and behold, in the skin of their flesh bright spots, whitish freckled spots; it is a freckled spot that has broken out in the skin; he is pure.

40 And any man whose head is shaved is bald, he is pure.

41 But if bald at the front of his head, bald is he, clean is he.

42 And if in a bald head or in a bald forehead there is a reddish-white sore, it is spreading leprosy on his bald head or on his forehead.

43 And the priest shall see it, and behold, the rising of the plague a white reddish spot upon his bald head or upon his bald forehead, like the appearance of leprosy upon the skin of the body.

44 He is a leprous man, he is unclean; the priest shall surely pronounce him unclean; his plague is on his head.

45 And the person stricken with the plague, their garments shall be torn, and their head shall be disheveled, and over their beard they shall cover, and unclean, unclean they shall cry.

46 All the days that the plague is in him he shall be unclean; unclean he is; alone he shall dwell; outside the camp shall be his dwelling.

47 And if a plague of leprosy is in the apparel, in a woolen garment or in a linen garment,

48 or in warp or in woof, for linen or for wool, or in leather or in any manner of work of leather.

49 And if the sore is greenish or reddish in the garment, or in the leather, or in the warp, or in the woof, or in any vessel of leather, it is a plague of leprosy; and he shall show it to the priest.

50 And the priest shall see the sore, and he shall shut up the sore seven days.

51 And on the seventh day he shall see the sore, and indeed the sore has spread in the garment, or in the warp, or in the woof, or in the leather, in any work made of leather, it is a leprous plague; unclean it is.

52 And he shall burn the garment or the warp or the woof in wool or in linen or any vessel of leather in which is the plague, for it is a consuming leprosy; in the fire it shall be burned.

53 And if the priest sees that behold the plague has not spread in the garment or in the warp or in the woof or in any leather vessel

54 And the priest shall command that they wash the thing in which the plague is, and he shall confine it seven days again.

55 And the priest shall look after the sore has been washed, and behold, the sore has not changed its outward appearance, and the sore has not spread; unclean it is; in the fire you shall burn it; it is fret inward, whether on his bald head or on his bald forehead.

56 And if the priest sees that the plague has whitened after it was washed, he shall tear it out from the garment or from the leather or from the warp or from the woof.

57 And if the plague appears again in the apparel or in the warp or in the woof or in any leather vessel, it is a spreading plague; you shall burn in the fire that in which it is found.

58 And the apparel or the warp or the woof or any leather vessel that you wash, and the plague has departed from them, shall be washed again and be clean.

59 This is the law concerning the plague of leprosy in a garment of wool or linen or in a warp or in a woof or in any article of leather, for purifying it or for pronouncing it unclean.


## Chapter 14

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 This shall be the law of the leper at the time of his cleansing: he shall be brought to the priest.

3 Then the priest shall go out to the outside of the camp, and the priest shall look, and behold, the plague of leprosy has healed from the stricken one.

4 The priest shall command, and for the one purified they shall take two living clean birds, cedar wood, scarlet worm, and hyssop.

5 Then the priest shall command, and he shall slaughter the sparrow, one, into a vessel of earth upon living waters.

6 He shall take the living bird, the cedar wood, the crimson scarlet thread, and the hyssop, and dip them and the living bird in the blood of the slaughtered bird upon the living water.

7 And he shall sprinkle upon the one cleansed from the leprosy seven times, and shall cleanse him; and he shall release the living bird upon the face of the field.

8 And he that is cleansed shall wash his garments and shave off all his hair and bathe in the water and be clean; afterward he shall come into the camp, and shall stay abroad outside his tent seven days.

9 And on the seventh day he shall shave all his hair, his head and his beard and his eyebrows and all his hair; and he shall wash his garments and bathe his body in the water and be clean.

10 On the eighth day he shall take two unblemished lambs, one unblemished year-old ewe lamb, three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering mixed with oil, and one log of oil.

11 And the priest who purifies shall present the man who is to be purified and them before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

12 The priest shall take the lamb and present it as a trespass offering, along with the log of oil, and shall wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

13 He shall slaughter the lamb in the place where they slaughter the sin offering and the burnt offering, in the place of the sanctuary, for like the sin offering, the guilt offering belongs to the priest; it is most holy.

14 And the priest shall take of the blood of the trespass offering, and the priest shall put upon the tip of the right ear of the one being cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.

15 The priest shall take a log of oil and pour it on the priest’s left palm.

16 The priest dipped his right finger into the oil that was on the palm of his left hand and sprinkled the oil with his finger seven times before the LORD.

17 And from the rest of the oil that is on the palm of the priest, he shall put some on the tip of the right ear of the one being cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the great toe of his right foot, upon the blood of the guilt offering.

18 And the remainder of the oil that is on the hand of the priest shall be put on the head of the one being cleansed, and the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD.

19 And the priest shall perform the purifying for sin and make atonement for the purified one from his uncleanness, and afterward he shall slaughter the burnt offering.

20 And the priest shall bring up the burnt offering and the gift upon the altar, and the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be clean.

21 But if he is poor and his hand cannot reach [the required offering], then he shall take one lamb as a guilt offering for a wave offering to make atonement for him, and one-tenth deal of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering, and a log of oil.

22 and two turtle doves or two young pigeons, which his hand can reach, and one shall be for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.

23 And he shall bring them on the eighth day for their purification to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the LORD.

24 The priest took the guilt offering lamb and the log of oil and waved them as a wave offering before the LORD.

25 Then the priest shall slaughter the sheep for the guilt offering, take some of the blood of the guilt offering, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of the one being purified, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.

26 And from the oil the priest shall pour upon the left palm of the priest.

27 And the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is on his left hand's palm seven times before the LORD.

28 And the priest shall take some of the oil that is in his palm and put it on the tip of the right ear of the one being purified, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the great toe of his right foot, upon the place of the guilt offering's blood.

29 And the rest of the oil that is on the palm of the priest shall be put on the head of the one being cleansed, to make atonement for him before the LORD.

30 He shall make one of the turtle doves or one of the young doves, such as his hand can reach.

31 Whatever his hand can reach, one sin offering and one burnt offering, along with the grain offering; and the priest shall make atonement for the purified person before the LORD.

32 This is the law for the one who has the plague of leprosy that is in him, when his hand is not able to reach in regard to his cleansing.

33 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

34 When you come into the land of Canaan, which I give to you for a possession, and I will put a plague of leprosy in a house of the land of your possession,

35 Then the one to whom the house belonged came and told the priest, saying, "A plague has appeared to me in the house."

36 The priest shall command that they clear the house before the priest enters to examine the plague, so that all which is in the house may not become unclean; afterward the priest shall enter to inspect the house.

37 And he shall see the plague, and behold, the plague is in the walls of the house, hollow strakes greenish or reddish, and their appearance is lower than the wall.

38 Then the priest shall go out from the house to the entrance of the house, and shall shut up the house seven days.

39 Then the priest shall return on the seventh day and see that the plague has spread along the walls of the house.

40 And the priest shall command, and they shall take out the stones in which is the plague, and cast them outside the city, to an unclean place.

41 And the house he shall cut from house round about, and they shall pour out the dust which they scraped off outside the city to an unclean place.

42 And they shall take other stones and bring them to replace the stones, and other mortar shall he take and plaster the house.

43 And if the plague returns and breaks out in the house after they have removed the stones and after scraping the house and after plastering it,

44 Then the priest shall come and see, and behold, the plague has spread in the home: leprosy that spreads in the home—he is unclean.

45 And he shall tear down the house, its stones and its timber and all the dust of the house, and carry them outside the city to an unclean place.

46 And whoever enters the house all the days of his confinement shall be unclean until evening.

47 And the one who lies in the house shall wash his garments, and the one who eats in the house shall wash his garments.

48 And if the priest comes and sees that the plague has not spread in the house after the house has been replastered, then the priest shall cleanse the house, because the plague has healed.

49 And he shall take two birds for cleansing the house, and cedar wood, and crimson scarlet, and hyssop.

50 and he shall slaughter the bird upon a clay vessel over living water.

51 He shall take the cedar wood, hyssop, scarlet thread, and the living bird, and dip them in the blood of the slaughtered bird and in living water, and sprinkle the house seven times.

52 And he shall purify the temple with the blood of the bird and with the living water and with the living bird and with the cedar wood and with hyssop and with crimson wool.

53 He shall send the living bird out into the open country outside the city, and he shall make atonement for the house, and it shall be clean.

54 This is the law concerning any plague of leprosy and (dry) scall.

55 and for leprosy of apparel and of a house:

56 for the rising, for the scab, and for the bright spot.

57 To teach on the day of uncleanness and on the day of cleanness, this is the law of leprosy.


## Chapter 15

1 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When any man has an issue from his body, that issue is unclean.

3 And this shall be his uncleanness in his issue: when his body issues his issue, or when his body stops from his issue, his uncleanness is this.

4 Every bed on which the one who has a discharge lies shall be unclean, and every vessel on which he sits shall be unclean.

5 Any man who touches his bed shall wash his clothes and bathe in water and remain unclean until evening.

6 And the one who sits on the vessel on which the one with a discharge has sat shall wash his clothes and bathe in the water and remain unclean until evening.

7 And whoever touches the flesh of the man with a discharge shall wash his garments and bathe in the water and be unclean until evening.

8 And if the one with a discharge spits on the one who is clean, then he shall wash his garments and bathe in the water and be unclean until evening.

9 And every chariot that the man having a discharge rides on shall be unclean.

10 And anything that touches anything that is under it shall be unclean until evening, and the one who carries them shall wash his garments and bathe in water and remain unclean until evening.

11 And whatever the one with a discharge touches, when his hands have not washed in water, he shall wash his garments and bathe in water and remain unclean until evening.

12 And any earthen vessel that the one with a discharge touches shall be broken, and every wooden vessel shall be rinsed with water.

13 And when he that has an issue is cleansed of his issue, then he shall count seven days for his purification, and wash his garments, and bathe his body in living water, and shall be clean.

14 And on the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two young doves, and he shall come before the LORD to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and give them to the priest.

15 And the priest shall prepare one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD because of his discharge.

16 And if any man has a seminal emission, then he shall bathe with water all his body and be unclean until the evening.

17 Every garment and every leather on which there is a seminal emission shall be washed in water and shall be unclean until the evening.

18 And a woman with whom a man lies carnally, they shall bathe in water and remain unclean until evening.

19 When a woman has a discharge, and the discharge in her body is blood, she shall be in her menstrual impurity seven days; whoever touches her shall be unclean until evening.

20 And all that she lies upon during her impurity shall be unclean, and all that she sits upon shall be unclean.

21 And whoever touches her bed shall wash his garments and bathe in water and be unclean until evening.

22 And any that toucheth any vessel whereon she sitteth shall wash his garments and bathe in water and be unclean until the evening.

23 And if he touches it while she is lying on a bed or on any vessel she sits upon, it shall be unclean until evening.

24 But if a man also lies with her, and her separation is upon him, he shall be unclean seven days, and every bed on which he lies shall be unclean.

25 And if a woman has a discharge of blood many days not at the time of her separation, or if she discharges during her separation, all the days of her discharge of uncleanness shall be as the days of her separation; she is unclean.

26 Every bed on which the woman with an issue lies all the days of her issue shall be to her as the bed of her menstrual impurity, and every vessel on which she sits shall be unclean, as the uncleanness of her menstrual impurity.

27 And whatsoever toucheth them shall be unclean; and the person that toucheth them shall wash his apparel, and bathe in the water, and be unclean until the evening.

28 But if she is cleansed from her issue, then she shall count for herself seven days, and afterward she shall be clean.

29 On the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtle doves or two young pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

30 The priest shall make one a purification offering and the other a burnt offering, and the priest shall atone for her before the LORD from the issue of her filthiness.

31 And you shall keep the people of Israel separate from their filthiness, so that they do not die in their filthiness by defiling my tabernacle that is among them.

32 This is the law of the man with a discharge, and of him who has a seminal emission, so that he becomes unclean by it.

33 And the woman with her menstrual impurity, and the man with his issue, both male and female, and the man who lies with a menstruous woman.


## Chapter 16

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses after the death of the two sons of Aaron, when they drew near before the LORD and died.

2 And Jehovah said to Moses, Speak to Aaron your brother, that he not come at all times into the sanctuary within the vail, before the mercy seat which is upon the ark, lest he die; for in the cloud I will appear upon the mercy seat.

3 Aaron shall enter the consecrated place with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.

4 He shall wear a linen coat and linen breeches shall be upon his body, and he shall gird with a linen girdle, and with a linen diadem he shall be turbaned; they are consecrated garments. And he shall bathe his body in water and put them on.

5 From the assembly of the people of Israel, he shall take two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.

6 And Aaron shall bring near the young bull of the sin-purifying offering that belongs to him, and shall make atonement on his behalf and on behalf of his household.

7 He shall take the two goats and present them before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

8 And Aaron shall place lots upon the two goats: one lot for the LORD, and one lot for the scapegoat.

9 And Aaron shall bring near the goat over which the lot fell for the LORD, and make it a sin offering.

10 And the goat on which the lot fell for the scapegoat shall stand alive before the LORD to make atonement with it, to send it away for the scapegoat into the wilderness.

11 And Aaron shall bring near the bull of the sin offering that is for him, and make atonement on his behalf and on behalf of his household; and he shall slaughter the bull of the sin offering that is for him.

12 And he shall take a full firepan of burning coals from upon the altar before the LORD and two handfuls of finely beaten sweet incense, and bring it inside the vail.

13 And he shall put the incense on the fire before the LORD, and the cloud of incense shall cover the mercy seat that is upon the testimony, so that he does not die.

14 And he shall take some of the bull's blood and sprinkle with his finger upon the front of the mercy seat to the east, and before the mercy seat he shall sprinkle seven times with his finger from the blood.

15 and he shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering which is for the people and bring its blood inside the vail and do with its blood as he did with the blood of the bull and sprinkle it upon the mercy seat and before the mercy seat

16 And he shall atone for the consecrated place because of the filthinesses of the children of Israel and because of their transgressions for all their sins, and so he shall do for the tent of meeting that dwells among them in the midst of their filthinesses.

17 No person shall be in the tent of meeting when he enters to make atonement in the sanctuary, until he comes out; and he shall make atonement for himself, for his household, and for all the congregation of Israel.

18 He shall go out to the altar that is before the LORD and make atonement upon it, and shall take some of the blood of the bull and some of the blood of the goat and put it upon its horns all around the altar.

19 And he shall sprinkle blood on it with his finger seven times, and purify it and consecrate it from the impurities of the children of Israel.

20 When he has finished atoning for the consecrated place, the tent of meeting, and the altar, he shall present the live goat.

21 And Aaron shall lay both his hands upon the head of the live goat, and confess over it all the iniquities of the children of Israel, and all their transgressions in all their sins, and put them upon the head of the goat, and send it away by the hand of a man who is designated into the wilderness.

22 And the goat shall bear upon it all their iniquities to a land of separation, and he shall send the goat into the wilderness.

23 And Aaron entered the tent of meeting and removed the linen garments that he wore when he entered the holy place, and left them there.

24 He shall wash his body in water in a holy place and put on his garments and go out and prepare his burnt offering and the burnt offering of the people and make atonement for himself and for the people.

25 And the best of the purifying offering he shall burn on the altar.

26 And the one sending away the goat for the scapegoat shall wash his garments and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may come into the camp.

27 And the young bull of the purifying offering and the goat of the purifying offering whose blood was brought to make atonement in the sanctuary shall be brought outside the camp, and they shall burn in fire their hides and their flesh and their dung.

28 And the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may come into the camp.

29 It shall be a statute forever for you: in the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, you shall afflict your souls, and all work you shall not do, the native-born and the sojourner who sojourns among you.

30 For on this day atonement shall be made for you to purify you from all your sins; before the LORD you shall be purified.

31 It is a sabbath of solemn rest for you, and you shall afflict your souls; a perpetual statute.

32 And the priest who is anointed and ordained to minister in his father's place shall make atonement, and he shall wear the linen garments, the holy garments.

33 And he shall make atonement for the chapel, the hallowed part, and for the tent of meeting, and for the altar; and he shall make atonement for the priests and for all the people of the congregation.

34 This shall be an appointed statute for you forever, to make atonement for the children of Israel from all their sins once in the year; and he did as the LORD commanded Moses.


## Chapter 17

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the children of Israel and say to them This is the word which the LORD has commanded saying

3 Any man from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox, or a lamb, or a goat in the camp, or who slaughters outside the camp,

4 And to the entrance of the tent of meeting they did not bring, to present an offering to the LORD before the tabernacle of the LORD; blood shall be reckoned to that man, he has shed blood, and that man shall be cut off from among his people.

5 so that the sons of Israel may bring their sacrifices which they sacrifice in the open field and bring them to the LORD, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to the priest, and sacrifice their peace offerings to the LORD.

6 And the priest shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shall burn the fat for a sweet savour to the LORD.

7 They shall no longer sacrifice their offerings to the satyrs after which they have gone astray; this statute shall be a perpetual ordinance for them throughout their generations.

8 And to them you shall say: Any man, each man of the house of Israel and of the sojourners who dwell among them, who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice.

9 He shall not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to present it to the LORD, and that man shall be cut off from his people.

10 Any man, every man of the house of Israel and of the alien sojourning among them who eats any blood—I will set my face against the person who eats the blood, and I will cut off that one from the midst of his people.

11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it to you upon the altar to make atonement for your lives; for the blood is what atones by means of the life.

12 Therefore I said to the children of Israel, every person among you shall not eat blood, and the stranger who resides among you shall not eat blood.

13 Any man, both of the people of Israel and of the sojourner who sojourns among them, who by hunting takes a beast or bird that may be eaten, he shall pour out its blood and cover it with dust.

14 For the life of every body is its blood: I have said to the people of Israel, You shall not eat the blood of any body, for the life of every body is its blood; whoever eats it shall be cut off.

15 Every person, whether a native-born citizen or a resident alien, who eats a carcass or an animal torn by beasts, shall wash their garments, bathe in water, and remain unclean until evening; then they shall be clean.

16 But if he does not wash his clothes and does not bathe his body, he shall bear his fault.


## Chapter 18

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them I am the LORD your God

3 You shall not do according to the acts of the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do according to the acts of the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you, and you shall not walk in their statutes.

4 You shall do my judgments and keep my statutes, to walk in them: I am the LORD your God.

5 You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances, which a person shall do and live by them; I am the LORD.

6 Every man shall not approach any near of kin to uncover nakedness; I am the LORD.

7 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father and the nakedness of your mother: she is your mother, you shall not uncover her nakedness.

8 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife; it is the nakedness of your father.

9 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, whether the daughter of your father or the daughter of your mother, born in the house or born outside.

10 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter’s daughter or your son’s daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.

11 The nakedness of the daughter of your father's wife, born to your father, is your sister; you shall not uncover her nakedness.

12 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's sister; she is your father's own flesh.

13 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s flesh.

14 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father's brother; to his wife you shall not come near: she is your aunt.

15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law; she is your son's wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.

16 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.

17 You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter; you shall not take the daughter of her son or the daughter of her daughter to uncover her nakedness—they are near kinswomen; it is a heinous crime.

18 A woman shall not take her sister as a rival to uncover her nakedness while she is alive.

19 You shall not approach a woman during the impurity of her menstrual uncleanness to uncover her nakedness.

20 You shall not give your lying down to the seed of a foreigner with the wife of your fellow, to defile yourself with her.

21 From your seed you shall not give to pass to Molech, and you shall not profane the name of your God, I am the LORD.

22 You shall not lie with a male as one lies with a woman; it is an abomination.

23 With any beast you shall not lie to defile yourself with it, and a woman shall not stand before a beast to lie with it; it is confusion.

24 Do not defile yourselves in any of these, for in all of these the nations whom I am dispossessing before you have become defiled.

25 And the land was defiled, so I visited its iniquity upon it, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.

26 You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances, and you shall not do any of the abominable customs, both the home-born among you and the sojourner who dwells among you.

27 For they have done all these abominations, the men of the land who were before you, and the land was defiled.

28 The land will not vomit you out for defiling it, as it vomited out the nation before you.

29 For whoever does any of these abominations, the persons who do them shall be cut off from among their people.

30 You shall keep my charge, so as not to do the abominable statutes that were done before you, and you shall not defile yourselves by them; I am the LORD your God.


## Chapter 19

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying.

2 Speak to the whole congregation of the children of Israel and say to them You shall be holy for holy am I the LORD your God

3 Every man shall revere his mother and his father, and you shall keep my sabbaths; I am the LORD your God.

4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods; I am the LORD your God.

5 And when you offer a peace offering to the LORD, you shall offer it for your acceptance.

6 On the day of your sacrifice it shall be eaten, and on the next day; and whatever remains until the third day shall be burned with fire.

7 But if it is eaten on the third day, it is an abomination; it shall not be accepted.

8 And he shall bear his iniquity because he has profaned the consecrated thing of the LORD, and that person shall be cut off from among his people.

9 When you reap the harvest of your land, do not wholly reap the corner of your field, and do not glean the gleaning of your harvest.

10 You shall not gather the late growth of your vineyard, and the grapes of your vineyard you shall not glean; you shall leave them for the poor and for the alien; I am the LORD your God.

11 You shall not steal, and you shall not deny falsely, and no man shall lie to his fellow.

12 You shall not swear by my name falsely, so that you profane the name of your God: I am the LORD.

13 You shall not oppress your fellow and you shall not steal; you shall not withhold the wages of a hired worker with you until morning.

14 You shall not curse the deaf, nor place a stumbling-block before the blind, and you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.

15 You shall not do iniquity in judgment; you shall not show partiality to the poor, nor honor the face of the great; but you shall judge your neighbor in righteousness.

16 You shall not carry tales among your folk; you shall not stand against the blood of your fellow; I am the LORD.

17 You shall not hate your brother in your mind; you shall surely rebuke your fellow, and you shall not bear guilt on his account.

18 You shall not take vengeance and you shall not bear a grudge against the children of your people, and you shall love your companion as yourself; I am the LORD.

19 You shall keep my statutes: you shall not crossbreed your cattle with diverse kinds, you shall not sow your field with mingled seed, and a garment of linen and wool shall not come upon you.

20 If a man lies with a woman in seed-emission and she is a female slave pledged to a man, and she has not been freed or given her freedom, they shall be flogged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not freed.

21 And he shall bring his trespass offering to the LORD to the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram for a guilt offering.

22 And the priest shall make atonement for him with a ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin which he sinned, and it shall be forgiven him for the sin which he sinned.

23 When you come into the land and plant any tree of food, you shall keep its fruit uncircumcised for three years; it shall be for you as uncircumcised, not to be eaten.

24 In the fourth year all its fruit shall be consecrated for praise to the LORD.

25 In the fifth year you shall eat its fruit, to increase your harvest; I am the LORD your God.

26 You shall not eat with blood; you shall not practice divination or soothsaying.

27 You shall not cut off the corner of your head, and you shall not destroy the corner of your beard.

28 You shall not make any cutting on your body for the appetite, nor shall you put a mark of tattooing on yourselves: I am the LORD.

29 Do not profane your daughter by causing her to play the harlot, lest the land go whoring and the land be filled with heinous crime.

30 You shall keep every sabbath, and my sanctuary you shall revere; I am the LORD.

31 Do not turn to the familiar spirits and the wizards; do not seek them to become defiled by them. I am the LORD your God.

32 You shall rise up before gray hair and honor the face of an old person, and you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.

33 When an alien sojourner lives with you in your land, you shall not oppress him.

34 The resident alien who dwells with you shall be like the home-born among you, and you shall love him as yourself, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.

35 You shall not do iniquity in judgment, in measurement, in weight, or in measure.

36 Balances of evenness, stones of evenness, an ephah of evenness, and a hin of evenness shall be yours; I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.

37 You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances and do them; I am the LORD.


## Chapter 20

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 And to the children of Israel you shall say, each man, any one of the children of Israel or of the strangers who sojourn in Israel, who gives any of his seed to Molech, he shall surely be put to death; the people of the land shall stone him with stones.

3 But as for me, I will set my face against that man and cut him off from the midst of his people, because he has given of his seed to Molech, so as to defile my sanctuary and profane my holy name.

4 But if the folk of the land avert their eyes from that man when he gives of his seed to Molech, so as not to put him to death,

5 I will set my face against that man and against his family, and I will cut off him and all who go whoring after the Molech from among the midst of their people.

6 Any person who turns to the familiar spirits and the wizards to go whoring after them, I will set my face against that person and cut them off from the midst of their people.

7 You shall sanctify yourselves and be holy, for I, the LORD your God, am holy.

8 You shall keep my statutes and do them. I am the LORD who sanctifies you.

9 Every person who curses their father or their mother shall surely be put to death; he has cursed his father and his mother—his bloodguilt is upon him.

10 Also, any man who commits adultery with the wife of another man, who commits adultery with the wife of his neighbour, the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.

11 Any man who lies with his father’s wife has uncovered his father’s nakedness; both of them shall be put to death, their bloodguilt is upon them.

12 Any man who lies with his daughter-in-law shall both of them be put to death; they have committed confusion; their blood is upon them.

13 Any man who lies with a male as with a woman commits an abomination; both of them shall be put to death, their bloodguilt is upon them.

14 Any man who takes a woman and her mother commits a heinous crime; they shall be burned in the fire, both he and they, so that no heinous crime may remain among you.

15 Any man who lies with an animal shall be put to death, and the beast you shall kill.

16 And a woman who approaches any beast to lie with it, you shall kill the woman and the beast; they shall be put to death, their bloodguilt is upon them.

17 Also, a man who takes his sister, a daughter of his father or a daughter of his mother, and sees her nakedness, and she sees his nakedness, it is a disgrace; they shall be cut off before the eyes of the children of their people; he has uncovered the nakedness of his sister; his iniquity he shall bear.

18 If any man lies with a woman during her menstrual impurity and uncovers her nakedness, so that he exposes her fountain of blood and she reveals the fountain of her blood, both of them shall be cut off from among the people.

19 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister or your father’s sister, for they have uncovered their own kin’s body; their iniquity they shall bear.

20 Any man who lies with his aunt uncovers the nakedness of his uncle; they shall bear their guilt—childless they shall die.

21 Also, any man who takes the wife of his brother—it is uncleanness: he has uncovered the nakedness of his brother—childless they will be.

22 You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances and do them, so that the land where I am bringing you to dwell will not vomit you out.

23 You shall not walk in the appointed customs of the nation that I am casting out from before you, for all these things they have done, and I have rejected them.

24 I said to you, You shall possess their land, and I will give it to you to inherit it, a land flowing with milk and honey; I am the LORD your God, who has separated you from the peoples.

25 You shall distinguish between the clean beast and the unclean, and between the unclean bird and the clean, and you shall not make your souls abhorrent with beast, and with bird, and with any thing that creeps upon the ground, which I have separated for you to defile.

26 You shall be holy to me, for holy am I, the LORD, and I have separated you from the folk to be mine.

27 When a man or woman has a familiar spirit or a wizard among them, they shall be put to death; with stones they shall be stoned, their bloodguiltiness is upon them.


## Chapter 21

1 The LORD said to Moses, "Say to the priests, the sons of Aaron: No one shall defile himself for any person among his people."

2 but to his flesh, his nearest kin, to his mother, to his father, to his son, to his daughter, and to his brother.

3 But for his virgin sister who is near to him, who has not been with any man, he may become unclean.

4 A husband shall not defile himself among his people to profane himself.

5 They shall not make bald any part of their heads, nor shave the corners of their beards, nor make cutting in their bodies.

6 They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God, for they offer the fire offerings of the LORD, the bread of their God, and they shall be holy.

7 They shall not take a woman who plays the harlot or is profaned, nor shall they take a woman divorced from her husband, for he is holy to his God.

8 You shall sanctify him, for he offers the bread of your God; he shall be holy to you, for I the LORD am holy, and I make you holy.

9 And when a daughter of a priest profanes her father by playing the harlot, she shall be burned in the fire.

10 The high priest among his brothers, on whose head the anointing oil was poured and whose hand was filled to wear the garments, shall not let his head go bare, nor tear his garments.

11 He shall not come in contact with any dead person, not even for his father or his mother shall he become unclean.

12 And from the sanctuary he shall not go out, nor profane the sanctuary of his God, for the crown of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the LORD.

13 But he shall take a woman in her virginity.

14 He shall not take a widow, a divorced woman, a profaned woman, or one who commits harlotry; only a virgin from his own people may he take as wife.

15 He shall not profane his seed among his people, for I the LORD sanctify him.

16 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

17 Speak to Aaron, saying: Any man from your descendants throughout their generations who has a blemish shall not approach to offer the bread of his God.

18 No man who has a blemish may come near: a man blind or lame, or one who is hunchbacked or deformed.

19 or any man who has a broken foot or a broken hand

20 or a hunchback, or a dwarf, or a man with a blemish in his eye, or scab, or scurvy, or crushed testicles,

21 Every man who has a blemish from the seed of Aaron the priest shall not come near to offer the offerings by fire to the LORD; having a blemish, he shall not come near to present the bread of his God.

22 The bread of his God from the holy of holies, and from the holies he shall eat.

23 But to the vail he shall not come, nor shall he approach the altar, for a blemish is in him, that he profane not my sanctuary, for I am the LORD who sanctify them.

24 Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel.


## Chapter 22

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to Aaron and to his sons, that they keep themselves separate from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name, which they sanctify to me: I am the LORD.

3 Say to them, throughout your generations: any man from all your seed who approaches the holy things that the children of Israel consecrate to the LORD while his uncleanness is upon him, that person shall be cut off from before my presence: I am the LORD.

4 Any man from the offspring of Aaron who is leprous or has a discharge shall not eat of the consecrated things until he becomes clean; and the one who touches any corpse, or any man who has had a seminal emission, shall not eat of the consecrated things.

5 or any person who touches any creeping thing that makes him unclean, or any person who makes him unclean by any uncleanness of his.

6 Any person who touches it shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat any of the holy things unless he has washed his body in water.

7 When the sun sets he is clean, and afterward he may eat of the consecrated things, for it is his food.

8 A (dead) carcase or (that which was) torn (of beasts) shall he not eat, to defile himself therewith: I am the LORD.

9 They shall keep my charge, so that they do not bear punishment for trespassing against it, and die because they profane it: I am the LORD who sanctifies them.

10 No outsider may eat a consecrated thing; a priest’s resident foreigner or a hired servant may not eat a consecrated thing.

11 And if a priest acquires a person by means of money, that one may eat of his food, and those born in his household shall eat of his food.

12 And if a daughter of a priest becomes a wife of another man, she shall not eat of the heave offering of the holy things.

13 And if a priest's daughter becomes a widow or divorced and has no child, and returns to her father's house as in her youth, she may eat of her father's bread; no outsider shall eat of it.

14 If any man eats a consecrated thing unwittingly, then he shall add a fifth part of it to it and give the consecrated thing to the priest.

15 They shall not profane the consecrated things of the children of Israel, which they dedicate to the LORD.

16 And they shall bear the punishment of guilt for eating their consecrated things, for I the LORD sanctify them.

17 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

18 Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the children of Israel and say to them: Any man, each one from the house of Israel and from the stranger in Israel, who will present his offering to the LORD for a burnt offering, whether for a vow or for a free-will offering, must present it.

19 According to your will, a perfect male from the beeve, from the lambs, or from the goats.

20 You shall not offer up any offering that has a blemish, for it shall not be acceptable to you.

21 Any man who offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a free will offering, whether from the herd or from the flock, it shall be without blemish to be acceptable; there shall be no blemish in it.

22 Blindness or broken or bruised or wen or scab or scabbed you shall not offer these to the LORD, and a fire offering you shall not give from them upon the altar to the LORD.

23 And a bull or a sheep that is a runt or stunted shall not be offered as a free will offering; for a vow it shall not be accepted.

24 And crushed or maimed or broken or castrated you shall not offer to the LORD, and in your land you shall not do so.

25 And from the hand of a foreigner you shall not bring the bread of your God from any of these, because their corruption is in them; they have a blemish; they shall not be accepted for you.

26 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

27 A bullock, lamb, or (she) goat, when it is born, shall be seven days under its dam; and from the eighth day and onward it shall be accepted for an offering made by fire to the LORD.

28 You shall not slaughter a bull or a sheep and its young on the same day.

29 And when you offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD, you shall offer it to gain acceptance for yourselves.

30 On that day it shall be eaten; you shall not leave any of it until morning; I am the LORD.

31 You shall keep my commandments and do them; I am the LORD.

32 You shall not profane my holy name, and I will be hallowed among the people of Israel; I am the LORD who consecrates you.

33 the one bringing you out of the land of Egypt to be your God I am the LORD


## Chapter 23

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, The appointed feasts of the LORD, which you shall proclaim to them as holy convocations, these are my appointed times.

3 Six days work may be done, but on the seventh day there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest, a holy convocation; no manner of work shall you do; it is a sabbath to the LORD in all your dwellings.

4 These are the appointed feasts of the LORD, holy convocations, which you shall proclaim in their appointed times.

5 In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, between the evenings, a passover offering for the LORD.

6 And on the fifteenth day of this month a solemn feast to the LORD: seven days unleavened bread you shall eat.

7 On the first day a holy convocation shall be for you; no manner of work of service shall you do.

8 You shall present a sacrifice by fire to the LORD for seven days; on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation; you shall do no work of service.

9 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

10 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, when you come to the land which I am giving to you and you reap its harvest, then you shall bring the sheaf of the beginning of your harvest to the priest.

11 And the priest shall wave the sheaf before the LORD for your acceptance; on the morrow of the sabbath he shall wave it.

12 On the day you wave the sheaf, you shall offer a lamb, a year-old male sheep, as a burnt offering to the LORD.

13 His oblation shall be two-tenth deals of fine flour mixed with oil, an offering by fire to the LORD, a sweet savour; and his drink offering shall be a fourth of the hin of wine.

14 You shall not eat bread or parched corn or full ears of corn until the selfsame day that you bring the offering of your God; it is a perpetual statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings.

15 And from the morrow after the sabbath, from the day that you bring the sheaf of the wave offering, you shall count off seven sabbaths; they shall be complete.

16 From the morrow of the seventh sabbath you shall count fifty days, and you shall present a new gift offering to the LORD.

17 From your dwellings you shall bring a bread offering of two tenth deals of fine flour, leavened shall it be baked, as first fruits to the LORD.

18 You shall present upon the bread seven lambs, sons of a year, one young bull, and two rams; they shall be a burnt offering to the LORD, their grain offering and their drink offerings, an offering made by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

19 You shall prepare one goat for a sin offering and two year-old lambs for a peace offering.

20 The priest shall wave them as a wave offering of the bread of the first fruits before the LORD, along with the two sheep; they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.

21 And you shall proclaim on the very day a holy convocation shall be for you, any manner of work you shall not do, a perpetual statute in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.

22 When you reap the harvest of your land, do not wholly reap the corner of your field, and do not glean your harvest; leave the gleaning of your harvest for the poor and for the sojourner; I am the LORD your God.

23 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

24 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, it shall be a sabbath for you, a memorial of loud noise, a holy convocation.

25 No manner of work shall you do, and you shall present an offering by fire to the LORD.

26 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

27 But on the tenth day of this seventh month it is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you, and you shall afflict your souls, and you shall present an offering by fire to the LORD.

28 You shall do no manner of work in the very day itself, for it is a day of atonement, to make atonement for you before the LORD your God.

29 For any person who does not afflict himself on the very day itself shall be cut off from his people.

30 And any person who does any manner of work in the very day itself, that person I will destroy from among his people.

31 No work of any kind shall you do throughout your generations in all your dwelling places as a perpetual statute.

32 It is a sabbath of solemn rest for you, and you shall afflict your souls; on the ninth of the month in the evening, from evening to evening, you shall keep your sabbath.

33 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

34 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying, On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, a feast of booths for seven days to the LORD.

35 On the first day a holy convocation: no work of service shall you do.

36 For seven days you shall present a fire offering to the LORD; on the eighth day there shall be a holy convocation for you, and you shall present a fire offering to the LORD; it is a solemn assembly; you shall do no manner of laborious work.

37 These are the appointed times of the LORD, which you shall proclaim as holy assemblies, to present a fire offering to the LORD: a burnt offering and a grain offering, a sacrifice and drink offerings, each on its day.

38 besides the sabbaths of the LORD, and besides your gifts, and besides all your vows, and besides all your free will offerings which you give to the LORD.

39 But on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you gather the fruit of the land, you shall celebrate the feast of the LORD for seven days; on the first day shall be a sabbath, and on the eighth day shall be a sabbath.

40 And you shall take for yourselves on the first day fruit of the tree of glory, branches of palm trees, and bough of the tree of thick growth, and willows of the brook, and you shall rejoice before the LORD your God seven days.

41 And you shall celebrate it as a solemn feast to the LORD: seven days in the year you shall observe it; it is a perpetual statute for your generations; in the seventh month you shall celebrate it.

42 In the booths you shall dwell seven days; every native-born in Israel shall dwell in the booths.

43 so that your generations may know that I caused the children of Israel to dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.

44 Moses spoke the appointed times of the LORD to the children of Israel.


## Chapter 24

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Charge the sons of Israel to bring you pure olive oil for lighting, to keep the lamp burning continually.

3 Outside the vail of the testimony, in the tent of meeting, Aaron shall arrange it from evening to morning before the LORD continually, a perpetual statute for your generations.

4 Upon the pure candlestick shall he arrange the lamps before the LORD continually.

5 And you shall take (fine) flour and bake it into twelve cakes; two tenth deals shall be the one cake.

6 And you shall set them in two rows, six in a row, on the pure table before the LORD.

7 And you shall put frank-incense on the row of shewbread, and it shall be for the bread as a memorial, an offering by fire to the LORD.

8 On the sabbath day, every sabbath day, they shall set it before the LORD continually, from the children of Israel, an everlasting covenant.

9 It shall be for Aaron and his sons, and they shall eat it in a holy place, for it is a most holy portion for him from the offerings made by fire to the LORD, a perpetual statute.

10 A son of an Israelitish woman, who was the son of an Egyptian man, went out among the children of Israel, and the son of the Israelitish woman and an Israelite man fought in the camp.

11 The son of the Israelitish woman blasphemed the name and cursed, and they brought him to Moses; and the name of his mother was Shelomith daughter of Dibri of the tribe of Dan.

12 And they left him in custody, to keep watch over them by the command of the LORD.

13 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

14 Bring the one who cursed outside the camp, and all who heard shall lay their hands upon his head, and all the congregation shall stone him.

15 And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: any man, each man who curses his God shall bear his offence.

16 And one who blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death; all the congregation shall certainly stone him, as with the alien so with the native-born, when he blasphemes the name, he shall be put to death.

17 And any man who strikes any person mortally shall surely die.

18 And one who strikes any beast shall pay for it: a life for a life.

19 And if any man gives a blemish in his fellow, as he has done so shall it be done to him.

20 affliction for affliction eye for eye tooth for tooth as he gives a blemish in a person so shall it be given in him

21 But one who strikes a beast shall make restitution; but one who strikes a man shall be put to death.

22 There shall be one judgment for you, as the alien, so the home-born; for I am the LORD your God.

23 Moses spoke to the sons of Israel and they brought the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him with stones and the sons of Israel did as Jehovah commanded Moses.


## Chapter 25

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying:

2 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them, when you come into the land which I give to you, and the land shall keep a sabbath to the LORD.

3 Six years you shall sow your field and six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather its increase.

4 In the seventh year a sabbath of solemn rest shall be for the land, a sabbath for the LORD: your field you shall not sow, and your vineyard you shall not prune.

5 That which groweth of itself of thy harvest thou shalt not reap, neither shalt thou gather the (ripe) grapes of thy vine undressed; a year of rest shall it be unto the land.

6 And the sabbath of the land shall be for you to eat, for you and for your bond-servant and for your bondwoman and for your hired worker and for your sojourner who dwells with you.

7 And for your cattle and for the beast that is in your land all its increase shall be for food.

8 You shall count for yourself seven times every sabbath of years, seven years seven times, and the days of seven times every sabbath of years shall be for you nine and forty years.

9 And you shall blow the trumpet with a blast in the seventh month on the tenth day of the month, on the day of atonement; you shall blow the trumpet throughout all your land.

10 You shall sanctify the year of fifty years, and proclaim liberty in the land to all its inhabitants; a jubilee it shall be for you, and each man shall return to his possession, and each man shall return to his family.

11 It shall be a jubile for you, the fiftieth year; you shall not sow, nor reap what grows of itself in it, nor gather its undressed vines.

12 For it is a jubile; it shall be consecrated for you; from the field you shall eat its increase.

13 In the jubile year each man shall return to his possession.

14 When you sell ware to your fellow or buy from your fellow’s hand, no man shall wrong his brother.

15 According to the number of years after the jubile, you shall buy from your neighbour; according to the number of harvests he shall sell to you.

16 According to the abundance of the years you shall increase his purchase, and according to the fewness of the years you shall diminish his purchase, for according to the number of the crops he sells to you.

17 You shall not wrong one another, and you shall fear your God, for I am the LORD your God.

18 You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and do them, and you shall dwell in the land securely.

19 And the land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill, and dwell securely upon it.

20 And when you say, "What shall we eat in the seventh year? Behold, we will not sow and we will not gather our increase,"

21 And I will appoint my blessing for you in the sixth year, and it will produce the increase for three years.

22 And you shall sow in the eighth year, and eat of the old fruit until the ninth year; when its fruit comes in, you shall eat the old.

23 The land must not be sold beyond reclaim, for the land is mine; you are aliens and sojourners with me.

24 And in every land of your possession, you shall grant redemption to the land.

25 If your brother becomes poor and sells part of his possession, then his nearest kinsman shall come and redeem what his brother sold.

26 If a man has no redeemer, but his hand reaches and he finds sufficient means for his redemption,

27 And he reckoned the two wares, and restored the excess to the man to whom he had sold, and he returned to his possession.

28 But if his hand is not able to restore to him, then his sold property shall remain in the hand of the buyer until the year of the jubile; in the jubile he shall go out, and he shall return to his possession.

29 Also, if a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, its right of redemption shall last until the completion of a year from its sale; during that time the right of redemption shall remain.

30 But if it is not redeemed by the time a full year has passed, then the house in the walled city shall belong in perpetuity to the one who bought it, throughout his generations; it shall not go out in the jubilee.

31 And the homes of the villages that have no wall around about shall be reckoned as country land; it may be redeemed, and in the jubilee it shall go out.

32 And the cities of the Leviites, the homes of their possession, shall have perpetual redemption for the Leviites.

33 And if one of the Levites redeems, then the sale of the house and the city of his possession shall go out in the jubilee, for the houses of the cities of the Levites are their possession among the children of Israel.

34 And the field of the suburbs of their cities shall not be sold, for it is a perpetual possession for them.

35 And if your brother becomes poor and his hand falters beside you, then you shall uphold him, the sojourner and the resident alien, and he shall live with you.

36 Do not take usury or unjust gain from him, and you shall fear your God, so that your brother may live with you.

37 Your silver you shall not give him at usury, nor shall you give your food at increase.

38 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan to be your God.

39 If your brother becomes destitute and is sold to you, you shall not impose on him the labour of a bondservant.

40 As a hired worker, as a resident foreigner, he shall be with you; until the year of the jubilee he shall serve with you.

41 and he shall go out from you, he and his sons with him, and he shall return to his family and to the possession of his fathers.

42 For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as a servant is sold.

43 You shall not rule over him with cruelty, and you shall fear your God.

44 Your male and female servants who will belong to you from the nations around you, from them you may acquire a male servant and a female servant.

45 And from the sojourners who reside among you, you shall also acquire both from them and from their families who are among you, who have borne children in your land, and they shall be your possession.

46 You shall inherit them as a possession for your children after you, as a perpetual possession; you shall make them serve you forever, but among your brothers the children of Israel, man shall not rule over his brother with cruelty.

47 If a sojourner or resident alien among you grows rich while your brother grows poor beside him and sells himself to a resident alien or to a member of an alien’s family,

48 After he is sold, a right of redemption shall belong to him; one of his kindred shall redeem him.

49 or his beloved or a son of his beloved shall redeem him, or one of his flesh from his family shall redeem him, or his hand reaches and he is redeemed.

50 He shall compute with the one who bought him from the year of his sale to him until the year of the jubile, and the price of his sale shall be according to the number of years, as the wages of a hired servant shall be with him.

51 If many years remain, according to them he shall repay his redemption money for his purchase.

52 But if there remain but few years until the year of jubilee, then he shall reckon for him, and according to his years he shall refund his redemption.

53 Like a hired worker for a year shall he be with him; you shall not rule over him with rigour before your eyes.

54 But if he is not redeemed by these, then he shall go out in the year of the trumpet, he and his sons with him.

55 For the Israelites are my servants, my servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.


## Chapter 26

1 You shall not make for yourselves idols or a carved image or a standing pillar; you shall not set up for yourselves a stone image to worship upon it, for I am the LORD your God.

2 You shall keep my every sabbath and revere my sanctuary: I am the LORD.

3 If you walk in my statutes and keep my commandments and do them.

4 I will give your rains in their season, and the land will yield its increase, and the tree of the field will give its fruit.

5 Then the threshing shall reach you until the vintage, and the vintage shall reach until the sowing-time; you shall eat your food to satisfaction, and you shall dwell securely in your land.

6 I will give peace in the land, and you will lie down with no one causing terror, and I will remove wild beast from the land, and sword will not pass through your land.

7 You shall pursue your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.

8 And five of you shall pursue a hundred, and a hundred of you shall pursue ten thousand, and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

9 And I will turn to you and make you fruitful and multiply you and establish my covenant with you.

10 You shall eat old stored grain, and you shall clear out the old because of the new.

11 And I will set my dwelling among you, and my soul shall not abhor you.

12 And I will walk among you, and I will be to you for God, and you will be to me for a people.

13 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt from being their slaves; I broke the bands of your yoke and made you walk upright.

14 And if you do not listen to me and do not do all these commandments,

15 But if you spurn my statutes and your soul rejects my judgments, so as not to do all my commandments, thereby breaking my covenant,

16 I also will do this to you: I will appoint over you terror, consumption, and burning fever that consume the eyes and waste the soul; you will sow your seed to no purpose, and your enemies will eat it.

17 I will turn my face against you, and you will be struck down before your enemies; your haters will rule over you, and you will flee though no one is pursuing you.

18 But if even to these things you will not listen to me, I will continue to punish you sevenfold for your sins.

19 I will break your pride of strength and make your heavens like iron and your land like bronze.

20 And your strength will be spent in vain, and your land will not yield its increase, and the tree of the land will not yield its fruit.

21 And if you walk contrary with me, and will not hearken to me, then I will add to you a plague sevenfold according to your sins.

22 And I will send among you the wild beasts of the field, and they will bereave you, and cut off your cattle, and make you few, and your ways will become desolate.

23 But if you will not be disciplined by these things, then I will walk with you in contrary manner.

24 I will also walk contrary with you, and I will strike you also seven times for your sins.

25 I will bring against you a sword avenging the vengeance of the covenant, and you will be gathered into your cities; and I will send pestilence among you, and you will be delivered into the hand of the enemy.

26 When I break for you the staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall return your bread by weight, and you shall eat and not be satisfied.

27 But if you will not listen to me in this, but you also walk contrary to me in hostility.

28 I will walk with you in furious anger, and I myself will discipline you—sevenfold for your sins.

29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters you shall eat.

30 I will destroy your high places and cut off your idols, and I will lay your dead bodies upon the dead bodies of your idols, and my soul will abhor you.

31 I will make your cities a desolation and lay waste your sanctuaries, and I will not smell the savour of your sweet odour.

32 And I will make desolate the land, and your enemies who dwell in it shall be appalled by it.

33 And I will scatter you among the nations, and I will draw the sword after you; and your land shall become desolation, and your cities shall become waste.

34 Then the land will keep its sabbaths all the days of its desolation, while you are in the land of your enemies; then the land will rest and keep its sabbaths.

35 All the days that it lies desolate it shall rest, the rest it did not have in your sabbaths when you dwelt upon it.

36 And those of you who remain, I will bring faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a driven leaf will make them flee, a flight of sword, and they will fall though no one pursues.

37 And each man shall stumble against another, as before a sword, though none is pursuing, and there shall be no power to stand before your enemies.

38 And you will perish among the nations, and the land of your enemies will devour you.

39 And those of you who remain shall waste away in their iniquity in the lands of your enemies, and even in the iniquities of their fathers with them they shall waste away.

40 They shall confess their sin and the sin of their ancestors, in their unfaithfulness which they committed against me, and also for walking contrary to me.

41 I also will walk contrary with them and bring them into the land of their enemies; or then will their uncircumcised heart humble itself, and then they will accept their iniquity.
