42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob, and indeed my covenant with Isaac, and indeed my covenant with Abraham I will remember, and the land I will remember.

43 The land will be forsaken of them and will observe its sabbaths in desolation apart from them, and they will accept the punishment of their sin, because they rejected my judgments and their soul abhorred my statutes.

44 Even then, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not reject them or abhor them to destroy them utterly, breaking my covenant with them, for I am the LORD their God.

45 I will remember for them the first covenant, by which I brought them out of the land of Egypt before the eyes of the nations, to be their God; I am the LORD.

46 These are the statutes, and the judgments, and the law, which the LORD gave between him and the children of Israel on mount Sinai by the hand of Moses.


## Chapter 27

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: when a man vows a vow, according to your valuation of persons to the LORD.

3 And your valuation shall be for a male from the age of twenty years even to the age of sixty years; then your valuation shall be fifty shekels of silver by the shekel of the sanctuary.

4 And if she is a female, then your estimation shall be thirty shekels.

5 And if from a son of five years and up to a son of twenty years, then your estimation for the male shall be twenty shekels, and for the female ten shekels.

6 If from a child of one month up to a child of five years, then your valuation for a male shall be five shekels of silver, and for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver.

7 And if from a son of sixty years and upward, if male, then your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for a female, ten shekels.

8 But if he is less than your valuation, then he shall present him before the priest, and the priest shall value him; according to what the hand of the one who vowed can afford, the priest shall value him.

9 But if a beast of which they bring an offering to the LORD, whatever is given of it to the LORD shall be holy.

10 He shall not exchange it or change it, good for bad or bad for good; and if he should exchange beast for beast, then both it and its change shall become consecrated.

11 And if any unclean beast, of which they do not offer an oblation to the LORD, then he shall present the beast before the priest.

12 And the priest shall value her, between good and bad; as thou, O priest, valuest her, so shall it be.

13 But if a redeemer will redeem him, then he shall add a fifth part to your valuation.

14 If a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD, the priest shall appraise it, whether good or bad; as the priest appraises it, so it shall stand.

15 But if the one who consecrates redeems his house, he shall add a fifth of the silver of your estimation to it, and it shall be his.

16 If a man consecrates a field of his possession to the LORD, then your estimation shall be according to its seed: a homer of barley seed at fifty shekels of silver.

17 If in the year of the jubile he sanctifies his field, according to your valuation it shall stand.

18 But if after the jubile he consecrates his field, then the priest shall reckon the money to him according to the years that remain until the year of the jubile, and it shall be deducted from your valuation.

19 But if a kinsman redeems the field that he consecrated, he shall add a fifth of the silver of your estimation upon it, and it shall be his.

20 But if he does not redeem the field, and if he sells the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed again.

21 When the field goes out in the jubilee, it shall be holy to the LORD like the field of the devoted; the priest’s possession shall be its possession.

22 But if he sanctifies to the LORD a field of his purchase that is not from the field of his possession,

23 Then the priest shall reckon for him the worth of thy valuation from that year unto the year of the jubile, and he shall give thy valuation in that day as a holy thing unto the LORD.

24 In the jubile year the field shall return to the one who bought it from him, to the one to whom the possession of the land belongs.

25 All your estimation shall be according to the shekel of the consecrated: twenty gerah shall be the shekel.

26 Yet the firstborn that is born to the LORD of cattle, no man shall sanctify it, whether ox or sheep, it belongs to the LORD.

27 But if it is an unclean beast, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation, and shall add its fifth part to it; but if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.

28 But any devoted thing that a man devotes to the LORD, from all that is his, from human to beast and from the field of his possession, shall not be sold nor redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy to the LORD.

29 Every devoted thing that is devoted from among men shall not be redeemed; he shall surely be put to death.

30 And all the tenth of the land, from the seed of the land and from the fruit of the tree, belongs to the LORD; it is holy to the LORD.

31 But if a man redeems any of his tithe, he shall add a fifth part to it.

32 Every tenth beeve and sheep, all that passes under the staff, the tenth shall be holy to the LORD.

33 It will not distinguish between good and bad, and it will not be exchanged; if it is exchanged at all, then both it and its recompense shall be consecrated; it shall not be redeemed.

34 These are the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses to the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.


# NUMBERS

## Chapter 1

1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai in the tent of meeting on the first day of the second month in the second year after they had left the land of Egypt, saying:

2 Lift up the head of all the congregation of the sons of Israel by their families, by the house of their fathers, in the number of names, every male by their skulls.

3 From the age of twenty years and upward, all who are appointed to serve in the army of Israel, you shall number them by their divisions, you and Aaron.

4 And with you there shall be a man, a man for each tribe, a man who is head of his father's house.

5 And these are the names of the men who will stand with you: for Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur.

6 For Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

7 Nahshon son of Amminadab from Judah.

8 To Issachar: Nethaneel the son of Zuar.

9 Zebulun: Eliab son of Helon.

10 For the sons of Joseph: for Ephraim, Elishama the son of Ammihud; for Manasseh, Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.

11 Abidan son of Gideoni, of Benjamin.

12 For Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

13 For Asher, Pagiel the son of Ocran.

14 For Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.

15 Naphtali: Ahira son of Enan.

16 These are the assembly, the tribal chiefs of their ancestral fathers, the leading men of Israel.

17 Moses and Aaron took these men who were specified by name.

18 And they assembled all the congregation on the first day of the second month, and they registered by their families, according to their ancestral houses, by number of names, from twenty years old and upward, head by head.

19 As Jehovah commanded Moses, so he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

20 The sons of Reuben, the eldest son of Israel, their generations by their families according to their fathers’ houses, counted by name head by head, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone able to go on military service.

21 Their appointed number for the tribe of Reuben was forty-six thousand and five hundred.

22 According to their generations, by their families, by the house of their fathers, their number according to names by their heads, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the army.

23 Their appointed number by the tribe of Simeon: fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

24 For the sons of Gad, their generations by their families, by their fathers' houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, everyone who goes out to the army.

25 Their assigned number for the tribe of Gad: forty-five thousand six hundred fifty.

26 The generations of Judah according to their families by the house of their fathers, numbered by names, every one born of twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the army.

27 Their appointed number from the tribe of Judah was seventy-four thousand six hundred.

28 The descendants of Issachar according to their families by their ancestral houses according to the number of names every male twenty years old and upward all who were able to go out to war.

29 Their divisions by the tribe of Issachar: fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

30 For the sons of Zebulun, their generations by their families according to their father's houses, by number of names, every one from twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the army.

31 Their enrollment by the tribe of Zebulun: fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

32 To the sons of Joseph, to the sons of Ephraim, their generations by their families according to their fathers' houses, by number of names, every one from twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the army.

33 Their enrollment by the tribe of Ephraim: forty thousand five hundred.

34 According to the generations of the sons of Manasseh by their families according to the house of their fathers, numbered by name, every one from twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the army.

35 Their divisions by the tribe of Manasseh: thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

36 The generations of Benjamin according to their families, by the house of their fathers, counted by name, every one from the age of twenty years and upward, all who went forth to military service.

37 Their enrolled men from the tribe of Benjamin: thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

38 For the sons of Dan according to their generations by their families according to their fathers' houses counted by name from twenty years old and upward all who go out to the army.

39 Their enrollment by the tribe of Dan: sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

40 For the sons of Asher, their generations by their families, by the house of their fathers, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to war.

41 Their enrollment by the tribe of Asher: one and forty thousand and five hundred.

42 The sons of Naphtali according to their generations by their families according to their fathers' houses in number by names from twenty years old and upward all who go out to the army.

43 Their count by the tribe of Naphtali: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

44 These are the accounts that Moses and Aaron and the chiefs of Israel, twelve men, one man for each ancestral house, kept.

45 All the enrolled men of the people of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, everyone who goes out to the army in Israel.

46 All the numbered men were six hundred and three thousand five hundred and fifty.

47 And the Leviites according to the tribe of their forefathers were not numbered among them.

48 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

49 Only the tribe of Levi you shall not register, and you shall not take the sum of them among the children of Israel.

50 And you, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony and over all its furniture and over all that belongs to it: they shall bear the tabernacle and all its furniture, and they shall minister to it, and around the tabernacle they shall encamp.

51 And when the tabernacle sets out, the Leviites shall take it down, and when the tabernacle encamps, the Leviites shall set it up; but the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.

52 And the sons of Israel camped, each man by his camp and each man by his banner according to their armies.

53 And the Leviites shall camp round about the tabernacle of the testimony, that there be no wrath upon the congregation of the children of Israel, and the Leviites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of the testimony.

54 The sons of Israel did just as the LORD had commanded Moses; so they did.


## Chapter 2

1 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

2 Each man shall camp by his own banner according to the signs of their ancestral houses, the Israelites; opposite, around the Tent of Meeting they shall camp.

3 And those camping on the east side, the standard of the camp of Judah according to their armies, and the prince of the sons of Judah: Naashon son of Amminadab.

4 and his army and their appointed officers, seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

5 And those camping by him were the tribe of Issachar, and the prince of the people of Issachar was Nethaneel son of Zuar.

6 and his army and his numbered men, fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

7 The tribe of Zebulun, and the prince of the children of Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon.

8 and his army and their muster, seventy-five thousand and four hundred.

9 All the enrolled troops of the army of Judah, one hundred eighty-six thousand four hundred according to their hosts, shall set out first.

10 The banner of Reuben's camp was southward according to their armies; and the prince of the people of Reuben was Elizur son of Shedeur.

11 and his army and his muster: forty-six thousand and five hundred.

12 And over him the tribe of Simeon, and the chief of the people of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

13 and his army and their appointed officers, fifty-nine thousand three hundred.

14 and the tribe of Gad, and the prince of the people of Gad was Eliasaph son of Reuel.

15 and his army and their appointed officers, forty-five thousand six hundred and fifty

16 All the enrolled troops of the army of Reuben, one hundred one thousand five hundred forty-five by their hosts, shall set out second.

17 Then the tent of meeting shall set out, with the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps; as they camp, so they will travel, each man at his standard according to their banners.

18 The banner of the camp of the Ephraimites according to their hosts was on the west, and the leader of the Ephraimites was Elishama son of Ammihud.

19 and his army and their appointed officers, forty thousand and five hundred.

20 And over him the tribe of Manasseh, and a prince of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

21 And his army and their appointed officers, thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

22 and the tribe of Benjamin; and the captain for the children of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni.

23 and his army and their appointed officers, thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

24 All the mustered troops for the camp of Ephraim, one hundred eight thousand one hundred according to their divisions, shall set out.

25 The banner of the camp of Dan to the north according to their hosts, and the prince of the sons of Dan was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

26 and his army and their numbered ones, two and sixty thousand and seven hundred

27 And those encamped by him, the tribe of Asher, a prince of the sons of Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran.

28 And his army and their appointed officers: one and forty thousand and five hundred.

29 and the tribe of Naphtali, and the prince of the children of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.

30 and his army and their appointed number: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

31 All the encampments of Dan’s army, one hundred seventy-six thousand six hundred, shall march last by their banners.

32 These are the enrollments of the children of Israel by their ancestral houses: all the enrollments of the camps by their divisions, six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

33 And the Levites were not numbered among the sons of Israel as the LORD commanded Moses.

34 The sons of Israel did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses: they camped by their banners and they traveled each by his families according to the house of their fathers.


## Chapter 3

1 These are the generations of Aaron and Moses on the day that the LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.

2 These are the names of Aaron's sons: the eldest, Nadab; and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

3 These are the names of Aaron's sons, the anointed priests, whose hands were filled for priesthood.

4 Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered strange fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai, and they had no children, so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests before Aaron their father.

5 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

6 Bring near the tribe of Levi and set them before Aaron the priest, and they shall serve him.

7 They shall keep his charge and the charge of the whole congregation before the tent of meeting, to perform the service of the tabernacle.

8 And they shall keep all the vessels of the tent of meeting and the charge of the sons of Israel, to perform the service of the tabernacle.

9 You shall give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the people of Israel.

10 And you shall appoint Aaron and his sons, and they shall keep their priesthood; but the outsider who draws near shall be put to death.

11 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

12 And I, behold, I have taken the Leviite from among the sons of Israel instead of every firstborn that openeth the womb among the sons of Israel, and they shall be mine, the Leviite.

13 For to me belongs every firstborn: on the day that I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated to me every firstborn in Israel, from human to beast; they shall be mine. I am the LORD.

14 The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying:

15 Take a census of the sons of Levi by their families, by their fathers' houses; every male from a month old and upward you shall count them.

16 Moses took account of them according to the command of the LORD, just as he was commanded.

17 These were the sons of Levi by name: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.

19 The sons of Kohath by their families: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

20 The sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.

21 For Gershon: the family of the Libnites and the family of the Shimites; these are the families of the Gershonites.

22 Their enrollment by number, every male from the age of one month and upward, their enrollment: seven thousand five hundred.

23 The families of the Gershonites shall camp behind the tabernacle on the west.

24 The chief of the family of the Gershonites was Eliasaph son of Lael.

25 The charge of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was the tabernacle and the tent, its covering, and the curtain for the entrance of the tent of meeting.

26 And the hangings of the court, and the covering for the entrance of the court, which is by the tabernacle and by the altar around, and its cords, for all its service.

27 And the family of the Amramites, and the family of the Izeharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites: these are the families of the Kohathites.

28 According to the number of every male from a month old and upward, eight thousand six hundred keepers of the charge of the holy.

29 The families of the sons of Kohath shall encamp on the side of the tabernacle toward the south.

30 And a chief of the fathers' houses of the Kohathites, Elizaphan son of Uzziel.

31 Keep charge of the ark, the table, the candlestick, the altars, the vessels of the sanctuary with which they minister, the curtain, and all its service.

32 And the chief of the chiefs of the Levites was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, who had charge over the guardians of the sanctuary.

33 The families of the Mahlites and the families of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari.

34 Their enrollment by number of every male from one month and upward was six thousand two hundred.

35 And a chief of a father's house among the families of Merari, Zuriel son of Abihail, on the side of the tabernacle northward.

36 And the account of the charge of the sons of Merari: the boards of the tabernacle, its bars, its pillars, its sockets, and all its utensils, and all that pertains to its service.

37 The pillars of the court round about, their sockets, their pins, and their cords.

38 And those camping before the tabernacle toward the east, before the tent of meeting, are Moses and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the charge of the people of Israel; but the outsider who comes near shall be put to death.

39 All the numbered Leviites whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the word of the LORD by their families, every male from a month old and upward, were twenty-two thousand.

40 The LORD said to Moses, Take a census of every firstborn male among the sons of Israel from the age of one month and upward, and you shall list the number of their names.

41 And you shall take the Leviite to me, I am the LORD, in place of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the cattle of the Leviite in place of every firstborn among the cattle of the sons of Israel.

42 Moses recorded, as Jehovah had commanded him, every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

43 Every firstborn male, registered by name from one month old and upward, according to their enrollment, was twenty-two thousand three hundred seventy.

44 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

45 Take the Leviites instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the cattle of the Leviites instead of their cattle, and the Leviites shall be mine: I am the LORD.

46 And those to be redeemed of the three, and the seventy and two hundred, the excess over the Levites, from the firstborn of the sons of Israel.

47 You shall take five shekels per head, according to the shekel of the consecrated; you shall take twenty gerahs for the shekel.

48 You shall give the silver to Aaron and to his sons as the redemption of those who are in excess among them.

49 Moses took the money of the ransom from those remaining over from the price of those redeemed, from the redemption of the Levite.

50 From the firstborn among the children of Israel, he took the silver: a thousand three hundred and sixty-five shekels, according to the shekel of the consecrated thing.

51 Moses gave the money of those who were redeemed to Aaron and to his sons according to the command of the LORD, as the LORD commanded Moses.


## Chapter 4

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

2 Take a census of the Kohathites from among the Levites by their families, by their ancestral houses.

3 From the age of thirty years and upward even to the age of fifty years, every one who comes to serve in the work at the tent of meeting.

4 This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting, most holy things.

5 Then Aaron and his sons shall go in when the camp sets out, and they shall take down the covering vail and cover with it the ark of the testimony.

6 They shall place over it a covering of badger hide, spread a garment of blue above, and put its bars in place.

7 On the table of the presence they shall spread a blue cloth, and upon it they shall place the dishes, and the spoons, and the bowls, and the cups of the drink offering, and the continual bread upon it shall be.

8 They shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet, cover it with a badger-skin covering, and place its bars.

9 They shall take a blue garment and cover the lampstand of the light and its lamps and its snuffers and its firepans and all the vessels of its oil that they use to serve it.

10 And they shall place it and all its utensils on a covering of badger skin, and they shall put it on the bar.

11 And upon the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth, and cover it with a covering of badger skin, and they shall place its bars.

12 They shall take all the ministering vessels used in the sanctuary and put them in a blue cloth, cover them with a covering of badger skin, and place them on the staff.

13 And they shall cleanse the altar and spread over it a purple garment.

14 And they shall place upon it all its vessels with which they ministered, the firepans, the fleshhooks, the shovels, and the basins—all the vessels of the altar—and they shall spread over it a covering of badger skin and insert its poles.

15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the holy vessels at the breaking of camp, then the sons of Kohath shall come to carry them; but they shall not touch the sanctuary or they will die. These are the burdens of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting.

16 The account of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest was the oil for the light, the sweet incense, the continual grain offering, and the oil for anointing, the oversight of all the tabernacle, and all that was in it, the sanctuary and its vessels.

17 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

18 Do not cut off the tribe of families of the Kohathites from among the Leviites.

19 And this do to them so that they may live and not die when they approach the consecrated of the consecrated: Aaron and his sons shall come near and appoint them, each man to his service and to his burden.

20 They shall not come to see the consecrated thing being swallowed, lest they die.

21 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

22 Take a census of the Gershonites also, by their clans and by their ancestral houses.

23 From the age of thirty years and upward, even to the age of fifty years, you shall appoint them, every one who enters to perform service, to do work in the tent of meeting.

24 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites, for ministering and for bearing burdens.

25 And they shall bear the curtains of the tabernacle and the tent of the appointed assembly, its covering and the covering of badgers' skins which is above it, and the curtain of the entrance of the tent of the appointed assembly.

26 and the hangings of the court and the curtain of the entrance of the gate of the court, which is upon the tabernacle and upon the altar round about, and their cords and all the vessels of their service, and all that is made for them, and they shall serve.

27 According to the command of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the Gershonites, for every burden and for all their work, and you shall appoint over them charge for all their burdens.

28 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting, and their charge under the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.

29 The sons of Merari, according to their families, by the house of their fathers, you shall number them.

30 From the age of thirty years and upward, even to the age of fifty years, you shall appoint them, every one coming to the service, to perform the labor of the tent of meeting.

31 And this is the charge of their burden for all their service in the tent of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle, its bars, its pillars, and its sockets.

32 And the pillars of the court round about, and their sockets, and their pegs, and their cords, for all their vessels, and for all their service, and by name you shall reckon the vessels of their charge, their burden.

33 This is the service of the families of the Merarites, for all their service in the tent of meeting, under the charge of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

34 Moses and Aaron and the captains of the congregation visited the sons of the Kohathites by their families and by their fathers' houses.

35 From the age of thirty years and upward, even to the age of fifty years, every one appointed to the service for ministering in the tent of meeting.

36 Their enrollment by their families was two thousand seven hundred and fifty.

37 These are the accounts of the families of the Kohathites, all who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron registered by the command of the LORD through Moses.

38 The appointed duties of the sons of Gershon according to their families and their ancestral houses.

39 From the age of thirty years and upward even to the age of fifty years, every one who comes to serve in the service at the tent of meeting.

40 Their enrollment by their families according to their fathers' houses was two thousand six hundred and thirty.

41 These are the enrollments of the families of the sons of Gershon, all who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the word of the LORD.

42 and the registration of the families of the Merarites, by their families, by their fathers’ houses:

43 From the age of thirty years and upward even to the age of fifty years, every one coming for service to perform work in the tent of meeting.

44 Their enrollment by their families was three thousand and two hundred.

45 These are the appointments of the families of the Merarites, whom Moses and Aaron registered according to the command of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

46 All the appointed ones whom Moses and Aaron and the chiefs of Israel appointed the Levites according to their families and their fathers’ houses.

47 From the age of thirty years and upward even to the age of fifty years, all who enter to perform the service of service and the service of bearing burden in the tent of meeting.

48 Their enrollment was eight thousand five hundred and eighty.

49 According to the command of the LORD they were appointed by the hand of Moses, each man according to his service and according to his burden, even the appointments which the LORD commanded Moses.


## Chapter 5

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Command the sons of Israel that they send out of the camp every leprous person and everyone having a discharge and everyone unclean because of a person.

3 From male to female you shall send them outside the camp; you shall send them forth, so that they do not defile the camp of theirs in which I dwell.

4 The sons of Israel did so, and they sent them outside the camp; just as the LORD had spoken to Moses, so the sons of Israel did.

5 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

6 Speak to the children of Israel: when a man or woman commits any sin of humankind, acting unfaithfully against the LORD, that person shall bear guilt.

7 They shall confess their sin which they committed, and he shall restore the guilt offering in full and add its fifth to it, and give it to the one against whom he was guilty.

8 But if there is no man to whom the trespass offering may be returned, the returned trespass offering shall go to the LORD for the priest, apart from the ram of atonement with which atonement is made on his behalf.

9 And every offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel which they bring near to the priest shall belong to him.

10 Also, each man’s consecrated things shall be his; whatever a man gives to the priest shall be his.

11 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

12 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, when a man betrays his wife and commits a trespass against her.

13 And a man lies with her in seed-embrace, hidden from the eyes of her husband, and she is concealed, and she becomes defiled, and there is no witness against her, and she is not caught.

14 and a spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife, and she has been defiled, or a spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not been defiled.

15 And the man shall bring his wife to the priest, and he shall bring her offering for her, the tenth of an ephah of barley flour; he shall not pour oil upon it, nor put frankincense on it, for it is a meal offering of jealousy, a meal offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.

16 And the priest shall bring her near and set her before the LORD.

17 The priest shall take water of purification in a vessel of earth and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the dwelling place and put it into the water.

18 The priest shall present the woman before the presence of the LORD, and he shall unbind the woman’s head, and he shall place upon her hands the offering of memorial, the offering of jealousy, which is the offering of bitterness, and in the hand of the priest shall be the waters of bitterness that bring curse.

19 The priest shall make her take an oath and say to the woman, if no man has lain with you, and if you have not gone astray into uncleanness under your husband, be free from these bitter waters of cursing.

20 And you, because you have gone astray from your husband and have become defiled, and a man has lain with you besides your husband.

21 The priest shall make the woman swear an oath of cursing, and the priest shall say to the woman, “May the LORD make you a curse and an oath among your people, by making your thigh rot and your belly swell.”

22 And these waters of cursing shall enter your belly to swell the belly and cause the thigh to waste, and the woman shall say, Amen, amen.

23 And the priest shall write these curses in the scroll and erase them into the waters of bitterness.

24 and he shall make the woman drink the waters of bitterness that bring a curse, and the waters that bring a curse shall enter into her to cause bitterness:

25 The priest took from the woman’s hand the oblation of jealousy, waved the oblation before the LORD, and brought it near to the altar.

26 The priest shall take from the gift its memorial portion and burn it on the altar, and afterward he shall make the woman drink the water.

27 And he shall make her drink the water; and it shall be, if she has been defiled and has committed falsehood, transgressing against her husband, then the water that brings bitterness and cursing shall enter into her, and her belly shall swell and her thigh shall fall; and the woman shall become a curse among her people.

28 But if the wife has not been defiled and is pure, she shall be cleared and conceive seed.

29 This is the law of jealousy when a wife goes astray from her husband and becomes defiled.

30 or any man over whom a spirit of jealousy comes and he is jealous of his wife, then he shall bring the woman before the LORD, and the priest shall perform for her all this law.

31 And the man shall be clear of iniquity, but that woman shall bear her sin.


## Chapter 6

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them, when any man or woman shall separate himself to make a vow of a Nazarite, to separate himself to the LORD.

3 From wine and strong drink he shall abstain; vinegar of wine and vinegar of strong drink he shall not drink; and every liquor of grapes he shall not drink; nor fresh grapes nor dried grapes shall he eat.

4 All the days of his separation, from everything made from the vine of the wine, from the kernel even to the husk, he shall not eat.

5 All the days of his vow of separation no razor shall pass over his head until the days are fulfilled that he consecrates to the LORD he shall be holy, growing the locks of the hair of his head.

6 All the days of his separation to the LORD, for the sake of a dead person he shall not come near.

7 He shall not defile himself for his father, his mother, his brother, or his sister when they die, because the consecration of his God is upon his head.

8 All the days of his consecration he is holy to the LORD.

9 If a person should suddenly die upon him, defiling the head of his consecration, then he shall shave his head on the day of his purification; on the seventh day he shall shave it.

10 On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting.

11 And the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and one for a burnt offering, and shall make atonement for him concerning which he sinned against the person, and shall sanctify his head on that day.

12 And he shall consecrate to the LORD the days of his separation and bring a lamb of one year old for a guilt offering, but the former days shall be void because his separation was defiled.

13 And this is the law of the Nazarite: on the day when the days of his separation are fulfilled, he shall bring him to the entrance of the tent of meeting.

14 He shall bring his offering to the LORD: one lamb in its first year without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish for a sin offering, and one ram without blemish for a peace offering.

15 a basket of unleavened cakes of fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, their grain offering and their drink offerings.

16 The priest shall present himself before the LORD and shall make his purifying for sin and his burnt offering.

17 And the ram shall be offered as a peace offering to the LORD along with the basket of unleavened bread, and the priest shall present his offering and his drink offering.

18 And the Nazarite shall shave his consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and take the hair of his consecrated head and put it upon the fire which is under the peace offering.

19 The priest shall take the shoulder of boiled ram, one unleavened cake from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and place them on the hands of the Nazarite after he has shaved his consecration.

20 And the priest shall wave the offering before the LORD; it is holy to the priest, along with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering; and afterward the Nazirite may drink wine.

21 This is the law for the Nazarite who offers an oblation to the LORD for his consecration, besides what his hand is able to provide; according to his vow which he vows, so he shall do in accordance with the law of his consecration.

22 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

23 Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: Thus you shall bless the sons of Israel, saying to them:

24 May the LORD bless you and keep you.

25 May the LORD make his face shine upon you and grant you favor.

26 May the LORD lift his face toward you and grant you peace.

27 They shall place my name upon the sons of Israel, and I will bless them.


## Chapter 7

1 And it came to pass on the day that Moses finished setting up the tabernacle, he anointed it and consecrated it and all its vessels, and the altar and all its vessels; he anointed them and consecrated them.

2 And the chiefs of Israel, heads of their fathers' houses, these chiefs of the tribes, they who stood over the numbered, drew near.

3 They brought their offering before the LORD: six carts and twelve beeves, a cart for every two of the princes and an ox for each one; and they presented them before the tabernacle.

4 The LORD said to Moses, saying:

5 Take from them, and they shall be for the service of the service of the tent of meeting, and you shall give them to the Levites, each according to his service.

6 Moses took the wagons and the beeves and gave them to the Levites.

7 The two wagons and the four beeves he gave to the sons of Gershon, according to their service.

8 And the four carts and the eight beeves he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their service, by the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

9 But to the sons of Kohath he did not give any, because the service of the holy thing was upon them; on the shoulder they shall carry.

10 The captains brought the dedicating of the altar on the day of anointing it, and the captains presented their offering before the altar.

11 The LORD said to Moses, one prince for a day, one prince for a day shall present their offering for the dedicating of the altar.

12 On the first day, the one who brought his offering was Nahshon son of Amminadab, from the tribe of Judah.

13 His offering was one silver dish, its weight three hundred shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them filled with fine flour mixed with oil, for a grain offering.

14 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

15 One young bull, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering.

16 one goat for a sin offering

17 And for the peace offering, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of a year old—this was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.

18 On the second day Nethaneel son of Zuar, prince of Issachar, presented his offering.

19 He shall present his offering: one silver dish, its weight three hundred and ten; one silver bowl, seventy shekels by weight of the holy shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

20 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

21 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of its year for a burnt offering

22 one goat for a sin offering

23 And for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of one year old; this was the offering of Nethaneel son of Zuar.

24 On the third day a prince of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon.

25 Its offering was one silver dish, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the holy shekel, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

26 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

27 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of its year for a burnt offering

28 one he-goat of the she-goats for a sin offering.

29 And for the peace offering, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of one year old; this was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.

30 On the fourth day a prince of the sons of Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur.

31 His offering was one silver charger, its weight one hundred and thirty shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

32 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

33 one young bull, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering.

34 one goat for a sin offering

35 And for the peace offering, two beeves, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of a year old: this was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.

36 On the fifth day a prince of the children of Simeon: Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

37 Its offering was one silver dish, its weight one hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the holy shekel, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

38 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

39 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of the first year for a burnt offering

40 one hairy goat for a purifying offering

41 For the peace offering, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of a year old: this was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

42 On the sixth day a prince of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.

43 Its offering was one silver dish, its weight one hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl seventy shekels by the weight of the holy sanctuary, both of them filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering.

44 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

45 One young bull, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering.

46 one goat for a sin offering

47 For the peace offering: two beeves, five rams, five goats, five lambs of a year old; this was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.

48 On the seventh day a prince of the sons of Ephraim: Elishama son of Ammihud.

49 His offering was one silver charger, its weight three hundred shekels, one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the weight of the holy sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

50 a gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

51 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of one year for a burnt offering

52 one hairy goat for purification for sin

53 The offering of peace offerings: two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of one year old; this was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.

54 On the eighth day the prince of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

55 His offering was one silver charger, its weight three hundred, one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meat offering.

56 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

57 one young bull, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering.

58 one goat of the she goats for a sin offering

59 For the sacrifice of peace offerings, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of one year old; this was the oblation of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

60 On the ninth day a prince of the people of Benjamin: Abidan son of Gideoni.

61 Its offering was one silver dish weighing one hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the weight of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

62 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

63 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of the first year for a burnt offering

64 one goat for a sin offering

65 For the peace offering, two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of the first year; this was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.

66 On the tenth day a prince of the sons of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

67 His offering was one silver dish, its weight three hundred, and one silver bowl, seventy shekels by the shekel of the consecrated; both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a gift.

68 One gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense.

69 one young bull of the herd one ram one lamb of one year for a burnt offering

70 one goat for a sin offering

71 And for the sacrifice of the peace offering: two beeves, five rams, five he-goats, five lambs of one year old; this was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

72 On the twelfth day a prince of the sons of Asher: Pagiel son of Ocran.

73 Its offering was one silver dish, its weight a hundred and thirty [shekels], one silver bowl of seventy shekels by the holy shekel, both of them filled with fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.

74 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

75 one young bull of herd cattle one ram one lamb of one year old for a burnt offering

76 one goat for a sin offering

77 For the peace offering: two beeves, five rams, five he goats, five lambs of a year old; this was the offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.

78 On the twelfth day a prince from the children of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.

79 His offering was one silver charger, its weight three hundred and thirty (shekels), one silver bowl, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of fine flour mixed with oil for a meal offering.

80 one gold spoon of ten shekels, full of incense

81 one young bull, one ram, one lamb of the first year, for a burnt offering.

82 one he-goat for a sin offering

83 And for the sacrifice of peace offerings, two beeves, five rams, five goats, five sheep lambs of a year old; this was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.

84 This was the dedicating of the altar on the day it was anointed from the princes of Israel: twelve silver chargers, twelve silver bowls, twelve golden spoons.

85 Three hundred silver for one charger, and seventy silver for one bowl; altogether the silver of the vessels was two thousand four hundred shekels of the sanctuary.

86 Twelve gold spoons filled with incense, ten shekels of the holy sanctuary per spoon, all the gold of the spoons two hundred.

87 All the beeve for the burnt offering: twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve lambs of a year old, and their oblations, and twelve goats for a sin offering.

88 And all the beeves for the sacrifice of peace offerings were twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty chief goats, sixty lambs of one year old; this was the dedicating of the altar after it was anointed.

89 And when Moses came into the tent of meeting to speak with him, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was upon the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim; and he spoke to him.


## Chapter 8

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to Aaron and say to him, When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light opposite the face of the candlestick.

3 Aaron did so; he lit the lamps of the candlestick opposite the face of the candlestick, as the LORD commanded Moses.

4 This is the act of the candlestick, beaten out of one piece of gold, from its shaft to its blossom it is a whole piece; according to the pattern which the LORD showed Moses, so he made the candlestick.

5 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

6 Take the Leviite from among the children of Israel and purify them.

7 And thus you shall do to them to purify them: sprinkle over them waters of purification, and they shall cause a razor to pass over all their flesh, and they shall wash their garments and be purified.

8 And they shall take a young bull from the herd and its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and a second young bull from the herd you shall take for a sin offering.

9 And you shall bring the Leviite before the tent of appointed assembly, and you shall gather all the congregation of the children of Israel.

10 And you shall bring the Leviites before the LORD, and the sons of Israel shall lay their hands upon the Leviites.

11 And Aaron shall wave the Leviite as a wave offering before the LORD from among the children of Israel, that they may minister the service of the LORD.

12 And the Leviites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bulls, and you shall offer the one bull as a sin offering and the other bull as a burnt offering to the LORD, to make atonement for the Leviites.

13 You shall present the Levites before Aaron and before his sons, and you shall wave them as a wave offering to the LORD.

14 And you shall separate the Leviite from among the sons of Israel, and the Leviite shall be mine.

15 After this the Leviites shall come to serve at the covering of assembly, and you shall purify them and wave them as a wave offering.

16 For they are given, given to me from among the sons of Israel; in place of the firstling of every womb, the firstborn of all the sons of Israel, I have taken them for me.

17 For I have set apart to Myself every firstborn among the children of Israel, both of man and of beast; on the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated them to Myself.

18 And I took the Leviite instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

19 I gave the Levite as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the children of Israel to perform the service of the children of Israel in the tent of meeting and to make atonement for the children of Israel so that no plague would be among the children of Israel when the children of Israel come near to the sanctuary.

20 Moses and Aaron and all the assembly of the sons of Israel did to the Levites according to all that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Levites; so the sons of Israel did to them.

21 The Levites purified themselves and washed their garments; then Aaron waved them as a wave offering before the LORD, and Aaron made atonement for them to purify them.

22 Afterward the Leviites came to perform their service at the tent of meeting before Aaron and before his sons, just as the LORD commanded Moses concerning the Leviites, so they did to them.

23 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

24 This is what the Leviite is to do: from twenty-five years old and upward he shall come to serve in the service of the tent of meeting.

25 And from the age of fifty years he shall return from the service of the work and shall serve no longer.

26 And he shall serve his brothers in the tent of meeting, to keep charge, but he shall not do service; thus you shall do for the Levites in their charges.


## Chapter 9

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai in the second year after they had gone out from the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying:

2 And the children of Israel shall keep the passover offering at its appointed time.

3 On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall make it at its appointed time, according to all its statutes and according to all its judgments.

4 Moses spoke to the children of Israel to perform the passover offering.

5 They made the passover offering on the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at evening, in the wilderness of Sinai, according to all that the LORD commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.

6 There were certain men who were unclean because of the death of a person and could not keep the passover offering on that day, so they came near before Moses and before Aaron on that day.

7 And the men said to him, "We are defiled by reason of a person's soul; why should we be kept from bringing near the offering of the LORD at its appointed time among the people of Israel?"

8 Moses said to them, Stand and I will hear what Jehovah, the Lord, will command you.

9 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

10 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: Any man who is unclean because of a person’s soul or on a distant journey, whether you or your posterity, and he shall make the passover offering to the LORD.

11 In the second month on the fourteenth day between the evenings they shall make it; with unleavened bread and bitterness they shall eat it.

12 They shall leave none of it until morning, nor break any bone of it; according to all the ordinance of the passover offering, they shall do to it.

13 And the man who is clean and was not on a journey, yet refrained from making the passover offering, that person shall be cut off from his people; because he did not present the offering of the LORD at its appointed time, that man shall bear his fault.

14 And when a sojourner dwells with you and performs the passover offering to the LORD, according to the ordinance of the passover and its judgment, so shall he do; one statute shall be for you and for the sojourner and for the home-born of the land.

15 And on the day that the tabernacle was raised up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the testimony, and in the evening it was upon the tabernacle as the appearance of fire until morning.

16 So shall the cloud continually cover it, and the appearance of fire by night.

17 According to the rising of the cloud from over the tent, afterward the children of Israel would journey, and in the place where the cloud abode, there the children of Israel would encamp.

18 According to the LORD's command the people of Israel set out, and according to the LORD's command they encamped; all the time that the cloud remained over the tabernacle they remained encamped.

19 And when the cloud abode long upon the tabernacle many days, the children of Israel kept the charge of the LORD and did not journey.

20 And sometimes the cloud was a certain number of days upon the tabernacle; at the command of the LORD they remained camped, and at the command of the LORD they set out.

21 And sometimes the cloudy presence was there from evening until morning, and when the cloud lifted in the morning, they would set out; or it would be by day and by night, and when the cloud lifted, they would set out.

22 or two days or a month or days, when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle to remain upon it, the children of Israel would camp and not travel, and when it was taken up, they would travel.

23 At the command of the LORD they camped, and at the command of the LORD they journeyed; they kept the charge of the LORD at the command of the LORD by the hand of Moses.


## Chapter 10

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Make for yourself two silver trumpets; of beaten work you shall make them, and they shall be for you for calling the assembly and for journeying the camps.

3 And blow upon them, and all the congregation shall gather to you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

4 And if they blow with one trumpet, then the captains, the chiefs of the thousands of Israel, shall gather to you.

5 And you shall blow an alarm, and the camps that are encamped toward the east shall set out.

6 And you shall blow an alarm a second time, and the camps that are encamped on the south shall set out, an alarm they shall blow for their journeys.

7 When you assemble the congregation, blow but do not sound a loud alarm.

8 And the sons of Aaron the priests shall blow the trumpets, and it shall be to you for a statute forever throughout your generations.

9 When you go to war in your land against the enemy who oppresses you, then you shall sound the trumpets, and you shall be remembered before the LORD your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.

10 On your day of gladness, and at your appointed feasts, and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over your peace offerings; and they shall be a memorial for you before your God; I am the LORD your God.

11 In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth of the month, the cloud was lifted from above the tabernacle of the testimony.

12 The people of Israel journeyed according to their journeys from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud settled in the wilderness of Paran.

13 They journeyed from the first place at the command of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

14 The banner of the army of the sons of Judah set out first according to their divisions, and over his army was Nahshon son of Amminadab.

15 And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Issachar was Nethaneel the son of Zuar.

16 And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab the son of Helon.

17 And the tabernacle shall be taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari shall set forward, bearing the tabernacle.

18 Then the banner of the camp of Reuben set out according to their hosts, and over his host was Elizur the son of Shedeur.

19 Over the army of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

20 And over the army of the tribe of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.

21 Then the Kohathites set out, bearing the sanctuary, and they set up the tabernacle until their arrival.

22 And the banner of the army of the sons of Ephraim set out according to their divisions, and over his army was Elishama the son of Ammihud.

23 And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.

24 And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni.

25 Then the standard of the camp of the people of Dan set out, gathering all the camps according to their hosts, and over his host was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

26 And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Asher, Pagiel the son of Ocran.

27 And over the army of the tribe of the people of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.

28 These are the journeys of the people of Israel by their hosts, and they journeyed.

29 Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, "We are setting out for the place of which the Lord said, 'I will give it to you.' Come with us, and we will treat you well, for the Lord has spoken good concerning Israel."

30 And he said to him, I will not go, unless I go to my country and to my kindred.

31 And he said, Please do not forsake us, for you have known our affliction in the wilderness, and you have been to us for eyes.

32 And it shall be, if you go with us, then the good that Jehovah does for us, we will do for you.

33 They set out from the mount of the LORD, a journey of three days, and the ark of the covenant of the LORD journeyed before them, a journey of three days, to seek out for them a resting place.

34 And the LORD's cloud upon them by day, when they journeyed from the camp.

35 When the ark set out, Moses said, "Rise up, the LORD, and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee before you."

36 When it rested, he said, Return, O Jehovah, to the ten thousands of Israel.


## Chapter 11

1 The folk were as those who openly grieve evil in the hearing of the LORD, and the LORD heard and His anger burned, so the fire of the LORD blazed among them and consumed at the edge of the camp.

2 The folk cried out to Moses and Moses prayed to the LORD and the fire abated.

3 He called the name of that place Taberah because the fire of the LORD had burned among them.

4 The mixt multitude that was among them lusted a lust, and the children of Israel also wept again and said, Who shall give us flesh to eat?

5 We remember the fish we used to eat in Egypt for free, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic.

6 And now our appetite is dried up: there is nothing at all except the manna before our eyes.

7 The manna was like coriander seed; its outward appearance was like the appearance of bdellium.

8 The folk gathered and collected it, ground it with millstones or crushed it in a mortar, cooked it in a pot, and made it into cakes, and its taste was like the taste of fresh oil.

9 When the dew falls on the camp at night, the manna will come down upon it.

10 Moses heard the folk weeping, each man at the entrance of his tent, by their families, and the LORD’s anger burned greatly, and in the eyes of Moses it was evil.

11 Moses said to the Lord, Why have you dealt ill with your servant, and why have I not found favour in your eyes, to lay the burden of all this people upon me?

12 Have I borne all this people? Have I begotten them, that you should say to me, Carry them in your bosom, as a nursing father carries the sucking child, to the land which you swore to their fathers?

13 Whence is there flesh for me to give to all this people, that they should weep upon me saying, Give us flesh that we may eat?

14 I am not able to bear this people alone, for they are too heavy for me.

15 And if thus you deal with me, kill me, I pray you, kill, if I have found favour in your eyes, and let me not see my affliction.

16 And the LORD said to Moses, Gather for me seventy men from the elders of Israel, whom you know to be elders of the people and their officers; and you shall take them to the tent of meeting, and they shall stand there with you.

17 I will go down and speak with you there, and I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them, and they will bear the burden of the people with you, so that you do not bear it alone.

18 And to the folk you shall say: sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat flesh; because you have wept in the hearing of the LORD, saying, “Who will cause us to eat flesh? For it was better for us in Egypt.” And the LORD will give you flesh, and you shall eat.

19 It shall not be one day that you eat, nor two days, nor five days, nor ten days, nor twenty days.

20 Until a month of days, until anger departs from your face, and it becomes loathsome to you, because you have rejected the LORD who is in your midst, and you weep before him, saying, "Why have we come out of Egypt?"

21 And Moses said, Six hundred thousand footmen of the folk who are with me; yet you have said, Flesh I will give them, and they shall eat for a month of days.

22 Will (small) cattle and herd be slaughtered for them, and will fish of the sea be gathered for them and found for them?

23 And the LORD said to Moses, Is the hand of the LORD too short? Now you shall see whether my word will come true for you or not.

24 Moses went out and spoke to the people the words of the LORD, and he gathered seventy men of the elders of the people and stationed them around the tent.

25 Then the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took of the spirit that was upon him and placed upon seventy men, the elders; and when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied but did not cease.

26 Two men remained in the camp: the name of the one was Eldad and the name of the second was Medad, and the spirit rested upon them, and they were among the recorded, but they did not go out from the tent; and they prophesied in the camp.

27 The lad ran and told Moses, saying, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."

28 Then Jehoshua son of Nun, Moses’ assistant from among the young men, answered and said, “My lord Moses, stop them!”

29 Moses said to him, "Are you zealous for my sake? Would that all the people of the LORD were prophets, that the LORD would put his spirit upon them!"

30 Moses was gathered to the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

31 And a wind from the LORD went forth and brought quails from the sea and let them fall upon the camp about a day's journey here and a day's journey there around the camp and about two cubits above the surface of the earth.

32 The folk rose up that day, all the day, and all the night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: the one who gathered least collected ten heaps, and they spread them out for themselves, a stretch all around the camp.

33 The body was still between their teeth, not yet consumed, when the anger of the LORD burned against the people, and the LORD struck the people with a very great plague.

34 And he called the name of that place Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had craved.

35 From Kibroth-hattaavah the people journeyed to Hazeroth, and they were in Hazeroth.


## Chapter 12

1 Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses concerning the sake of the Ethiopian woman whom he had married, for he had married an Ethiopian woman.

2 They said, Did Jehovah speak only with Moses? Did he not also speak with us? And Jehovah heard.

3 And the man Moses was very humble, more than any man who was upon the face of the ground.

4 The LORD suddenly said to Moses and to Aaron and to Miriam, "Come out, the three of you, to the tent of meeting," and the three of them went out.

5 And the LORD descended in a pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance of the tent and called Aaron and Miriam, and the two of them came out.

6 Hear now my words: if there be a prophet among you, the LORD makes himself known to him in a vision; in a dream I speak with him.

7 Not so my servant Moses, in all my house he is faithful.

8 mouth to mouth I will speak with him, and not in riddles, and an image of the LORD he shall behold; and why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant in Moses?

9 And the LORD became angry with them and went away.

10 And the cloud withdrew from above the tabernacle, and behold, Miriam was leprous, snow-white; and Aaron turned toward Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.

11 Then Aaron said to Moses, "Please, my lord, do not place upon us the punishment for sin, for we have acted foolishly and have sinned."

12 Do not let him be like a stillborn child, who when he comes forth from his mother's womb has half his body eaten.

13 Moses cried out to the LORD, saying, "O God, please heal her, I pray."

14 The LORD said to Moses, “If her father had only spit in her face, would she not be shamed for seven days? Let her be shut outside the camp for seven days, and afterward she shall be brought back.”

15 And Miriam was shut outside the camp seven days, and the people did not journey onward until Miriam was gathered in again.

16 Afterward the folk journeyed from Hazeroth and camped in the wilderness of Paran.


## Chapter 13

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Send you men that they may spy out the land of Canaan which I am giving to the children of Israel one man one man to the tribe of their fathers you shall send every leader among them.

3 Moses sent them from the desert of Paran according to the word of the LORD; all of them were men, heads of the sons of Israel.

4 And these are the names: for the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur.

5 For the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.

6 According to the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.

7 For the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.

8 For the tribe of Ephraim, Hosea son of Nun.

9 For the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.

10 For the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi.

11 for the tribe of Joseph, for the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi

12 For the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.

13 For the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.

14 For the tribe of Naphtali, Nakbi son of Vophsi.

15 For the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.

16 These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, and Moses called Hosea the son of Nun Joshua.

17 Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan and said to them, Go up here into the south and go up into the hill country.

18 And you shall see what the land is, and what the people who dwell in it: are they strong or weak, are they few or many.

19 And what of the land where he dwells, is it good or bad, and what of the cities where he dwells, are they in camps or in fortresses?

20 And what is the land, is it fat or lean? Is there tree or not? And strengthen yourselves and take from the fruit of the land, and the days, the days of first ripe grapes.

21 They went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, entering Hamath.

22 They went up into the south country and came to Hebron, and there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak; and Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.

23 They came to the brook of Eshcol and cut down from there a vine and one cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a staff with two of them, and some pomegranates and some figs.

24 To that place he gave the name Nahal Eshcol because of the cluster of grapes that the children of Israel cut down from there.

25 They returned from touring the land at the end of forty days.

26 They went and came to Moses and to Aaron and to all the assembly of the people of Israel at Kadesh in the wilderness of Paran, and they brought back word to them and to all the assembly, and they showed them the fruit of the land.

27 They reported to him and said, We came to the land where you sent us, and indeed it flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.

28 Nevertheless, the people who dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified with very great walls, and also there we saw the sons of Anak.

29 Amalek dwells in the land of the south, and the Hittite and the Jebusite and the Amorite dwell in the hill country, and the Canaanite dwells by the sea and along the side of the Jordan.

30 And Caleb silenced the folk toward Moses and said, Go up, let us go up and possess it, for we are well able to it.

31 And the men who went up with him said, We are not able to go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are.

32 They brought out a slanderous report about the land that they had explored to the people of Israel, saying, “The land that we passed through to explore it is a land that devours its inhabitants, and all the people that we saw within it were of great stature.”

33 And there we saw the giants, the sons of Anak from among the giants, and we were in our own eyes like locusts, and so we were in their eyes.


## Chapter 14

1 And the whole assembly lifted up their voices and the people wept that night.

2 All the congregation of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron, and all the assembly said to them, "If only we had died in the land of Egypt or in this wilderness!"

3 Why is the LORD bringing us to this land to fall by the sword, our wives and our little ones becoming spoil? Would it not be better for us to return to Egypt?

4 And they said, each man to his brother, Let us appoint a leader and return to Egypt.

5 Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before the whole assembly of the congregation of the people of Israel.

6 And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had gone to spy out the land, tore their garments.

7 And they said to all the congregation of the children of Israel, saying, The land which we passed through to spy it out is a good land, very, very good.

8 If Jehovah is pleased with us, then he will bring us into this land and give it to us—a land flowing with milk and honey.

9 But in the LORD do not rebel, and you, do not fear the folk of the land, for they are our food; their defence is removed from over them, and the LORD is with us—do not fear them.

10 All the congregation said to stone them with stones, and the glory of the LORD appeared in the tent of meeting to all the children of Israel.

11 And the LORD said to Moses, Until when will this people despise me? And until when will they not believe in me, for all the signs which I have done among them?

12 I will strike him with pestilence and cut him off, and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than he.

13 Moses said to the Lord, "And the Egyptians will hear that you have brought up this people by your power out of their midst."

14 And they will say to the inhabitant of this land, “Have you not heard that you, O LORD, are in the midst of this people, that eye to eye you, O LORD, are seen, and your cloud stands over them, and by day you go before them in a pillar of cloud, and by night in a pillar of fire”?

15 You will kill this people as one man, and the nations who have heard your fame will say:

16 Because Jehovah was not able to bring this people into the land that he had sworn to them, he slaughtered them in the wilderness.

17 And now may the power of my Lord be magnified as You have spoken, saying:

18 The LORD is long-suffering and abundant in lovingkindness, bearing iniquity and transgression, yet not entirely clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children, upon the third generation and upon the fourth.

19 Please pardon the fault of this people according to the greatness of your merciful kindness, just as you have borne with this people from Egypt until now.

20 The LORD said, I have forgiven according to your word.

21 But as I live, the glory of the LORD shall fill the whole earth.

22 For all the men who have seen my glory and my signs which I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have tested me these ten times, and have not obeyed my voice,

23 If they shall see the land which I swore to their fathers, then all who despise me shall not see it.

24 But my servant Caleb, because he had another spirit with him and followed me fully, I will bring into the land into which he came, and his seed shall inherit it.

25 The Amalekite and the Canaanite dwell in the valley; tomorrow turn away and journey for yourselves to the wilderness by the way of the Red Sea.

26 The LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

27 How long will this wicked assembly continue to bring their murmuring of the children of Israel before me? I have heard their murmuring that they bring against me.

28 Say to them, As I live, saith the LORD, surely according to what you have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you.

29 In this wilderness your corpses shall fall, and all your numbered ones according to all your number, everyone from the age of twenty years and upward, who have murmured against me.

30 Surely you shall not come into the land which I swore to settle you in, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

31 And your little children, whom you said would be spoil, I will bring them, and they shall know the land that you despised.

32 And your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.

33 Your children will pasture in the wilderness forty years and bear your whoredom until the end of your dead bodies in the wilderness.

34 According to the number of days that you scouted the land, forty days, a day for each year, a day for each year, you shall bear your iniquities, forty years, and you shall know my breach of promise.

35 I am the LORD; I have spoken: surely this evil congregation that has conspired against me shall be consumed in this wilderness and there they shall die.

36 The men whom Moses sent to spy out the land returned and made all the congregation murmur against him by bringing up a slander against the land.

37 And the men who brought the evil report of the land died by the plague before the LORD.

38 And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh lived of those men who walked to spy out the land.

39 Moses spoke these words to all the sons of Israel and the people mourned greatly.

40 They rose early in the morning and went up to the top of the mountain, saying, "Here we are, and we will go up to the place that the LORD said, for we have sinned."

41 And Moses said, Why do you transgress the command of the LORD, and it shall not prosper?

42 Do not go up, for the LORD is not among you, lest you be struck down before your enemies.

43 For the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there before you, and you shall fall by the sword, because you have turned back from following the LORD, and the LORD will not be with you.

44 They rushed to go up to the top of the hill, but the ark of the covenant of the LORD and Moses did not move from the midst of the camp.

45 The Amalekites and the Canaanite who lived in that hill came down and attacked them and defeated them as far as Hormah.


## Chapter 15

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, when you come into the land of your dwelling places, which I give to you.

3 You shall make an offering by fire to the LORD, a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a free-will offering, or at your appointed feasts, to present as a sweet savour to the LORD, from the herd or from the flock.

4 And the one who brings his offering to the LORD shall bring a gift of fine flour, a tenth deal mixed with a quarter of a hin of oil.

5 And wine for the drink offering, a quarter of a hin, you shall make upon the burnt offering or for the sacrifice, for the one lamb.

6 Or for a ram you shall make an offering: fine flour, two tenths deal, mixed with oil, a third hin.

7 Wine for the drink offering, one-third of a hin, you shall offer as a sweet savour to the LORD.

8 When you offer a young bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice to fulfill a vow or as a peace offering to the LORD,

9 And he shall offer with the young bull a grain offering: three tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, half a hin.

10 and wine you shall offer for the drink offering, half a hin, an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

11 Thus shall be done to the bull or to the ram or to the lamb among the sheep or among the goats.

12 According to the number that you make, so shall you do to one according to their number.

13 Every home-born shall do this: offer by fire a sweet savour to the LORD.

14 When an alien sojourns among you throughout your generations and offers a fire offering, a sweet savour to the LORD, as you do, so shall he do.

15 The assembly shall have one statute for you and for the alien who sojourns, a perpetual statute throughout your generations; as you, so shall the sojourner be before the LORD.

16 There shall be one law and one judgment for you and for the alien who sojourns with you.

17 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

18 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them, when you come to the land that I am bringing you to there.

19 When you eat the bread of the land, you shall offer a gift to the LORD.

20 From the beginning of your dough, you shall lift up a cake as a heave offering; as the heave offering of the threshing floor, so shall you lift it up.

21 From the beginning of your dough you shall give the LORD a heave offering for your generations.

22 And if you err and do not do all the commandments which the LORD spoke to Moses,

23 All that the LORD commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that the LORD commanded [it] and onward throughout your generations.

24 If the thing was done unwittingly before the eyes of the assembly, then all the congregation shall offer one bull, a son of the herd, as a burnt offering for a sweet savour to the LORD, with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the ordinance, and one male goat of the goats for a sin offering.
