25 Then the priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the people of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them, because it was an error; and they have brought their offering, an offering by fire to the LORD, for their errors before the LORD.

26 And it shall be forgiven all the congregation of the people of Israel and the alien who sojourns among them, because all the people were unwitting.

27 But if any one person sins unwittingly, then she shall bring a she-goat, a daughter of one year, for a sin offering.

28 And the priest shall make atonement for the person who sinned unwittingly, through error before the LORD, to make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.

29 The native among the children of Israel and the stranger who sojourns among them shall have one law, for whoever does unwittingly.

30 Any person who blasphemes the LORD, whether native or sojourner, having done so defiantly, that person shall be cut off from among his people.

31 Because the word of the LORD he despised and his commandment he broke, cut off, cut off shall that person be, his iniquity is upon him.

32 The sons of Israel were in the wilderness and they found a certain man gathering sticks on the sabbath day.

33 And those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses and to Aaron and to the whole assembly.

34 And they left him in custody, because it had not been declared what should be done to him.

35 The LORD said to Moses, the man shall surely die, all the congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.

36 And the assembly brought him out of the camp, and they stoned him with stones, and he died, as the LORD commanded Moses.

37 The LORD said to Moses, saying:

38 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them that they shall make for themselves fringes on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and they shall place on the fringe of the corner a thread of blue.

39 It shall be for you as a fringe, and when you see it, you shall remember all the commandments of the LORD and perform them, and you shall not follow after your own heart and your own eyes, after which you are led astray.

40 so that you may remember and do all my commandments, and be holy to your God.

41 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD your God.


## Chapter 16

1 Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi took Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab and On son of Peleth sons of Reuben.

2 They rose up before Moses, men from the children of Israel, two hundred and fifty, leaders of the assembly, summoned to the appointed meeting, men of renown.

3 They assembled against Moses and against Aaron and said to them, It is too much for you, for all the assembly is holy, and among them is the LORD; and why do you lift yourselves up above the congregation of the LORD?

4 Moses heard, and he fell on his face.

5 And he spoke to Korah and to all his congregation, saying, "In the morning the LORD will make known who is his and who is holy, and will cause him to come near to him; and the one whom he chooses, he will cause to come near to him."

6 Do this: take for yourselves censers, Korah and all his assembly.

7 Put burning fire in them and place sweet incense before the LORD tomorrow, and the man whom the LORD will choose, he is the holy one; you have gone too far, sons of Levi.

8 Moses said to Korah, "Hear, please, you sons of Levi."

9 Is it not a very small matter to you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel to bring you near to Himself, to do the service of the tabernacle of the LORD and to stand before the congregation to minister to them?

10 He brought you near, and all your brothers, the sons of Levi, with you, and you have sought also the priesthood.

11 Therefore you and all your assembly who gather against the LORD, and Aaron, what is he that you murmur against him?

12 Moses sent to summon Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, but they said, We will not come up.

13 Is it a very small thing that you have brought us up from a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness? You have indeed ruled over us, and would even rule more?

14 Not even to a land flowing with milk and honey have you brought us, nor given us an inheritance of field and vineyard; the eyes of these men you would blind—no, we will not go up.

15 Moses was very angry and said to the Lord, "Do not respect their offering. Not one donkey of theirs have I taken, nor have I harmed any one of them."

16 Moses said to Korah, You and your entire assembly shall present yourselves before the LORD, you and they and Aaron, tomorrow.

17 And take each his censer and put incense on them and bring each his censer before the LORD, two hundred and fifty censers, and you and Aaron each with your censer.

18 So each man took his firepan, put fire in it, and laid incense on it; they stood at the entrance of the tent of meeting, Moses and Aaron also.

19 And Korah assembled against them the whole assembly at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD appeared to all the assembly.

20 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

21 Separate yourselves from among this assembly, that I may consume them in an instant.

22 They fell on their faces and said, God, God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one man sin and upon all the congregation will You be angry?

23 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

24 Speak to the assembly, saying, Go up from around the dwelling of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.

25 Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram, and the elders of Israel followed him.

26 He spoke to the assembly, saying, “Move away now from around the coverings of these wicked men, and do not touch anything that belongs to them, lest you be swept away with all their sin.”

27 They moved away from above the dwelling of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram, all around, and Dathan and Abiram came out standing at the entrance of their tents, with their wives, their children, and their little ones.

28 Moses said, By this you shall know that the LORD has sent me to do all these deeds, because they are not from my own mind.

29 If these men die as all persons die, and the account of all persons is laid upon them, the LORD has not sent me.

30 But if the LORD creates a new thing, and the earth opens its mouth and swallows them and all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into the grave, then you shall know that these men have spurned the LORD.

31 When he had finished speaking all these words, the ground beneath them split open.

32 The earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their houses, and all the people who belonged to Korah, and all their wealth.

33 They and all that belonged to them went down alive into the grave, and the earth covered them, and they perished from among the congregation.

34 All Israel round about them fled at the sound of their voices, for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up.

35 And fire came forth from the LORD and consumed the two hundred and fifty men offering the incense.

36 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

37 Say to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, and he shall lift up the firepans from between the burning, and scatter the fire far away, for they are holy.

38 The censers of these men who sinned with their souls—make them broad plates, a covering for the altar, because they presented them before the LORD and they became holy, and they shall be a sign to the people of Israel.

39 And Eleazar the priest took the brass firepans that the burned men had brought near, and they were beaten out for a covering of the altar.

40 a memorial for the children of Israel so that no outsider who is not of the seed of Aaron should draw near to offer incense before the LORD and not be as Korah and as his company as the LORD spoke by the hand of Moses to him

41 All the congregation of the people of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron on the next day, saying, You have killed the people of the LORD.

42 And it came to pass, when the assembly gathered together against Moses and against Aaron, that they turned toward the tent of meeting, and behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of the LORD appeared.

43 Moses and Aaron came before the tent of meeting.

44 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

45 Rise up from among this congregation, that I may consume them in an instant; but they fell upon their faces.

46 Moses said to Aaron, Take the censer and put fire from upon the altar upon it and put (sweet) incense and go quickly to the assembly and make atonement upon them because wrath has gone out from the presence of the Lord, the plague has begun.

47 And Aaron took as Moses had spoken, and he ran among the assembly, and behold, the plague had begun among the people, and he gave the incense and made atonement upon the people.

48 And he stood between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped.

49 The dead by the plague were fourteen thousand and seven hundred, besides the dead on account of the affair of Korah.

50 Aaron returned to Moses at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and the plague had been stopped.


## Chapter 17

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying

2 Speak to the children of Israel and take from them a rod, a rod for a father's house, from each of their princes according to their fathers' houses, twelve rods; each man's name you shall write on his rod.

3 And the name of Aaron you shall write upon the tribe of Levi, for one tribe for the chief of their fathers' house.

4 And you shall place them in the tent of meeting, before the testimony, where I will meet with you there.

5 And the man whom I choose, his tribe shall sprout, and I will remove from upon me the murmuring of the children of Israel, who are murmuring against you.

6 Moses spoke to the people of Israel and they gave to him all their princes a staff for each prince a staff for each chief according to their ancestral houses twelve tribes and the staff of Aaron among their tribes

7 Moses placed the rods before the LORD in the tent of the testimony.

8 And it came to be on the next day that Moses entered the tent of the testimony, and behold, the rod of Aaron for the house of Levi had sprouted, and it brought forth a blossom, and it produced almonds.

9 Moses brought out all the rods from before the LORD to all the children of Israel, and they saw and took each man his rod.

10 The LORD said to Moses, Return the rod of Aaron before the testimony for a charge, as a sign against the rebellious children, that their murmuring may cease from me, so that they do not die.

11 Moses did as Jehovah, the Lord, commanded him; thus he did.

12 The sons of Israel said to Moses, saying, Behold, we are perishing, we are all undone, we are all undone.

13 Every one who comes near to the dwelling place of the LORD shall die; will you indeed be consumed?


## Chapter 18

1 The LORD said to Aaron, you and your sons and your father's house with you shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary, and you and your sons with you shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood.

2 And also bring near your brothers, the tribe of Levi, your father’s tribe, to join you and assist you, and you and your sons with you shall stand before the tent of the testimony.

3 They shall keep your charge and the charge of all the tent, but they shall not come near to the vessels of the sanctuary or to the altar, lest they die, both they and you.

4 They shall join themselves to you and keep the charge of the tent of meeting for all the service of the tent, but a stranger shall not come near you.

5 You shall keep the charge of the consecrated and the charge of the altar, so that there may be no more wrath upon the sons of Israel.

6 And I, behold, I have taken your brothers the Levites from among the children of Israel for you, a gift given to the LORD, to perform the service of the tent of meeting.

7 You and your sons with you shall keep your priesthood for all that pertains to the altar and for all within the vail, and you shall serve; I give you the priesthood as a gift; and the outsider who comes near shall be put to death.

8 And the LORD spoke to Aaron: Behold, I have given to you the charge of my offerings, all the holy gifts of the people of Israel; to you I have given them and to your sons as a perpetual statute by anointing.

9 This shall be your portion from the most holy things, from the fire: all their offerings, every grain offering, every sin offering, and every guilt offering that they return to me—most holy things shall be yours and your sons’.

10 In the most holy place you shall eat it; every male may eat it; it shall be consecrated for you.

11 This is the gift of their offerings from all the wave offerings of the children of Israel: I have given them to you and to your sons and to your daughters with you as a perpetual statute; every one who is clean in your house may eat it.

12 All the finest of the anointed oil, and all the finest of the wine and wheat, the first of them which they shall give to the LORD, I have given to you.

13 The first fruit of all that is in their land, which they bring to the LORD, shall be yours; every clean thing in your house may eat it.

14 All devoted things in Israel shall be yours.

15 All firstling of the womb of every body which they bring near to the LORD, whether of man or of beast, shall be yours, but you shall surely redeem the firstborn of man and the firstborn of the unclean beast.

16 And its redemption from a month old you shall redeem according to your valuation, silver five shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, twenty gerahs it is.

17 But you shall not redeem the firstborn of a bull or the firstborn of a lamb or the firstborn of a goat: they are holy; their blood you shall pour out on the altar and their fat you shall burn as an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

18 And their flesh shall be yours, like the breast of the wave offering and like the right thigh it shall be yours.

19 All the heave offerings of the most holy things which the children of Israel offer to the LORD I have given to you and to your sons and to your daughters with you as a perpetual statute; it is a covenant of salt forever before the LORD, to you and to your seed with you.

20 The LORD said to Aaron: In their land you shall not inherit, and a portion shall not be yours among them; I am your portion and your inheritance among the children of Israel.

21 To the Levites I have given every tithe in Israel as an inheritance, in place of their service that they perform, the service of the tent of meeting.

22 And the sons of Israel shall no longer approach the tent of meeting to bear sin resulting in death.

23 The Leviite shall serve the service of the tent of meeting, and they shall bear their iniquity; it is a perpetual statute throughout your generations; among the children of Israel they shall not inherit an inheritance.

24 For the tithe of the children of Israel, which they raise up as a gift to the LORD, I have given to the Levites for an inheritance; therefore I said to them among the children of Israel they shall not inherit a possession.

25 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

26 And to the Leviite you shall speak, saying, When you take from the children of Israel the tithe which I have given you from them as your inheritance, then you shall lift up from it the LORD's heave offering, a tithe from the tithe.

27 And your heave offering shall be counted for you as the wheat from the threshing floor and as the first of ripe fruit from the winepress.

28 Thus you also shall offer the LORD’s heave offering from all your tithes, which you receive from the children of Israel; and from it you shall give the LORD’s heave offering to Aaron the priest.

29 From all your gifts, you shall set apart every heave offering due to the LORD, the best of it, its holy portion from it.

30 And you shall say to them, when you lift up its best from it, it shall be counted to the Levites as the fruit of the threshing floor and as the fruit of the winepress.

31 And you shall eat it in every place, you and your household, because it is your reward in place of your service at the tent of meeting.

32 You shall not bear fault when you lift up its fat from it, and you shall not profane the saints of Israel, and you shall not die.


## Chapter 19

1 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:

2 This is the appointed ordinance of the law which the LORD has commanded, saying: Speak to the children of Israel, that they take for you a red heifer without blemish, in which there is no spot and upon which no yoke has come.

3 And you shall give her to Eleazar the priest, and he shall bring her outside the camp, and he shall slaughter her before him.

4 Eleazar the priest took some of her blood with his finger and sprinkled it toward the front of the tent of assembly seven times.

5 And he shall burn the heifer before his eyes, her hide, her flesh, and her blood with her dung.

6 The priest shall take a cedar tree, hyssop, and scarlet of worm, and cast it into the midst of the burning of the heifer.

7 The priest shall wash his garments and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may enter the camp; but the priest shall be unclean until evening.

8 And the one who burns it shall wash his garments in water and bathe his body in water and remain unclean until evening.

9 And a clean man shall gather the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place, and it shall be kept by the congregation of the children of Israel as a charge for water of separation; it is a sin offering.

10 And he that gathereth the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening: and it shall be a statute forever unto the children of Israel, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among them.

11 Whoever touches a dead person, any human being, becomes unclean for seven days.

12 He shall purify himself with it on the third day and on the seventh day he shall become clean, but if he does not purify himself on the third day and on the seventh day he shall not become clean.

13 Everyone who touches a dead person, any human being who has died, and does not purify himself, defiles the tabernacle of the LORD, and that person shall be cut off from Israel; because the water of purification was not sprinkled on him, he remains unclean; his uncleanness is still upon him.

14 This is the law: when a person dies in a tent, everyone who enters the tent and everything that is in the tent shall be unclean seven days.

15 And every vessel that is open, which has no covering of thread upon it, is unclean.

16 Everything that touches the surface of the field on a slain by sword or on a dead person or on a human bone or on a grave shall be unclean seven days.

17 They shall take for the unclean person the ashes of the burning of the sin-purification, and put fresh water upon them into a vessel.

18 And a man who is clean shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and sprinkle it upon the tent and upon all the vessels and upon the persons who were there and upon the one who touched the bone or the corpse or the dead or the grave.

19 The one who is clean shall sprinkle on the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day, and he shall cleanse him on the seventh day, and he shall wash his garments and bathe in the water and be clean in the evening.

20 And any man who is unclean and does not purify himself, that person shall be cut off from among the assembly, because he has defiled the sanctuary of the LORD; the water of purification was not sprinkled on him; he is unclean.

21 It shall be an appointed statute for them forever, and the one who sprinkles the water of separation shall wash his garments, and whoever touches the water of separation shall be unclean until evening.

22 And whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean, and the person who touches it shall be unclean until evening.


## Chapter 20

1 The sons of Israel came, the whole assembly, into the wilderness of Zin in the first month, and the people stayed in Kadesh, and Miriam died there and was buried there.

2 And there was no water for the assembly, so they gathered against Moses and against Aaron.

3 The folk quarreled with Moses and said, “If only we had perished in the death of our kindred before the LORD.”

4 Why have you brought the assembly of the LORD into this wilderness to die there, we and our cattle?

5 Why have you brought us up from Egypt, to lead us to this wretched place, no place of seed, fig tree, vine, or pomegranate, and no water to drink?

6 Moses and Aaron came from before the assembly to the entrance of the tent of meeting, and they fell on their faces, and the glory of the LORD appeared to them.

7 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

8 Take the rod and assemble the congregation, you and your brother Aaron, and speak to the rock before their eyes so that it gives its water; then bring forth water for them from the rock and give drink to the congregation and their cattle.

9 Moses took the rod from before the LORD as he had commanded him.

10 Moses and Aaron gathered the assembly before the face of the rock, and he said to them, "Listen now, you rebels, shall we bring forth water for you from this rock?"

11 Moses raised his hand and struck the rock twice with his rod, and much water came out, and the congregation and their cattle drank.

12 And the LORD said to Moses and to Aaron, Because you have not believed in me, to sanctify me before the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore you shall not bring this congregation into the land which I have given them.

13 These are the waters of Meribah, where the children of Israel quarreled with the LORD, and he was sanctified among them.

14 Moses sent ambassadors from Kadesh to the king of Edom: Thus says your brother Israel: You know all the trouble that met us.

15 Our forefathers went down to Egypt, and we dwelled in Egypt many days, and the Egyptians dealt harshly with us and with our forefathers.

16 We cried aloud to the LORD, and he heard our voice; he sent a messenger and brought us out from Egypt, and now here we are at Kadesh, a city at the edge of your border.

17 Please let us pass through your land; we will not pass through field or vineyard, nor will we drink water from a well; we will go along the king's way; we will not turn to the right or to the left until we have passed your border.

18 Then Edom said to him, You shall not pass through me, lest with the sword I come out against you.

19 And the sons of Israel said to him, "We will go up by the causeway; if we drink your water, I and my cattle, I will pay its price; only without a thing at my feet, I will pass through."

20 He said, "You shall not pass," and Edom marched out against him with a heavy people and with a strong hand.

21 Edom refused to let Israel pass through his border, so Israel turned away from him.

22 They journeyed from Kadesh and all the congregation of the children of Israel came to Mount Hor.

23 The LORD said to Moses and to Aaron in Mount Hor at the border of the land of Edom:

24 Aaron will be gathered to his people; he shall not enter the land that I have given to the children of Israel, because you rebelled against my word at the waters of Meribah.

25 Take Aaron and his son Eleazar, and bring them up to Mount Hor.

26 Strip Aaron of his apparel and clothe Eleazar his son with them, and Aaron shall be gathered and die there.

27 Moses did as Jehovah commanded, and they went up to Mount Hor before the eyes of all the congregation.

28 Moses stripped Aaron of his apparel and put them on Eleazar his son, and Aaron died there on the top of the mountain, and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.

29 All the assembly saw that Aaron had died, and they wept for Aaron thirty days, all the house of Israel.


## Chapter 21

1 The Canaanite king of Arad, who lived in the south, heard that Israel had come along the way of the spies, so he attacked Israel and took captives from him.

2 Israel vowed a vow to the LORD and said if you will indeed give this people into my hand I will utterly destroy their cities.

3 And the LORD heard the voice of Israel and delivered the Canaanite and his cities into their hand, and they devoted him and his cities to destruction, and called the name of the place Hormah.

4 They journeyed from Mount Hor along the way of the Red Sea to go around the land of Edom, and the people's soul was shortened on the journey.

5 The folk spoke to God and to Moses, Why have you brought us up from Egypt to die in the wilderness? For there is no food and there is no water, and our soul loathes this light bread.

6 Then the LORD sent fiery serpents among the people, and they bit the people, so that a great multitude of the people of Israel died.

7 The folk came to Moses and said, We have sinned because we spoke against the Lord and against you; pray to the Lord and let him remove the serpent from upon us. Then Moses prayed on behalf of the folk.

8 And Jehovah said to Moses, "Make for yourself a fiery serpent and set it on a pole, and it shall be that everyone who is bitten, when he sees it, shall live."

9 Moses made a brasen serpent and set it upon the pole and it was that if the serpent bit any man when he looked upon the brasen serpent he would live.

10 The people of Israel set out and camped at Oboth.

11 They journeyed from Oboth and camped at Ije-abarim in the wilderness which is opposite Moab toward the east side of the sun.

12 From there they journeyed and encamped in the brook of Zared.

13 From there they journeyed and camped beyond the Arnon, which is in the wilderness, extending from the border of the Amorite, for the Arnon is the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorite.

14 Therefore it is said in the book of the wars of the LORD: Waheb in Suphah, and the valleys of the Arnon.

15 And a stream of the brooks that stretched out toward the seat of Ar, and leaned upon the border of Moab.

16 From there Beer, the pit, the well which the LORD said to Moses, Gather the people, and I will give them water.

17 Then Israel sang this song: Ascend, O well, sing to her.

18 A well which the captains dug, which the nobles of the people excavated, with a staff by their supports, and from the wilderness Mattanah.

19 From Mattanah to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth.

20 And from the high places of the valley in the field of Moab, the top of Pisgah, she looked toward the face of the desert.

21 Israel sent ambassadors to Sihon king of the Amorite, saying:

22 I will pass through your land; I will not turn aside into field or vineyard, nor drink water from a well; along the king's highway we will go until we cross your border.

23 But Sihon did not allow Israel to pass through his border, so Sihon gathered all his people and went out against Israel into the wilderness, coming to Jahazah, and he fought against Israel.

24 Israel struck him with the edge of the sword and took possession of his land from the Arnon to the Jabbok, as far as the Ammonites, because the border of the Ammonites was strong.

25 Israel took all these cities and Israel lived in all the cities of the Amorite, in Heshbon and in all its towns.

26 Heshbon is the city of Sihon king of the Amorite, and he fought against the king of Moab and took all his land from his hand to Arnon.

27 Therefore the poets will say: Come to Heshbon; let it be built, and let the city of Sihon be established.

28 For a burning has gone out from Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon, it has consumed Ar of Moab, the lords of the heights of Arnon.

29 Alas for you, Moab! You are destroyed, O folk of Chemosh; he has given his sons who escaped and his daughters as captives to the king of the Amorites, Sihon.

30 We destroyed Heshbon as far as Dibon, and we devastated even as far as Nophah, which reaches to Medeba.

31 Israel settled in the land of the Amorite.

32 Moses sent to spy out Jaazer, and they captured its villages and dispossessed the Amorites who were there.

33 Then they turned and went up by way of Bashan, and Og king of Bashan went out against them, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.

34 And the LORD said to Moses, Do not fear him, for into your hand I have delivered him, and all his people, and his land; and you shall do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorite, who lived in Heshbon.

35 They struck him and his sons and all his people until no remnant was left to him, and they took possession of his land.


## Chapter 22

1 The sons of Israel journeyed and camped in the plains of Moab beyond the Jordan at Jericho.

2 Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorite.

3 Moab was exceedingly afraid of the people, because they were numerous, and Moab was filled with dread because of the children of Israel.

4 Moab said to the elders of Midian, "Now the assembly will lick up all that is round about us, as the bull licks up the green of the field," and Balak the son of Zippor was king of Moab at that time.

5 He sent ambassadors to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor, which is by the river, land of the sons of his people, to summon him, saying: Behold, a people has come out of Egypt; behold, it covers the face of the land, and it is dwelling opposite me.

6 Now therefore come, curse this folk for me, for it is too mighty for me; perhaps I may strike it and drive it from the land; for I know that whomever you bless is blessed, and whomever you curse is cursed.

7 The elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed, with the reward of divination in their hand, and they came to Balaam and spoke to him the words of Balak.

8 He said to them, Spend the night here, and I will bring back word to you according to what the LORD speaks to me. So the officials of Moab stayed with Balaam.

9 God came to Balaam and said, "Who are these men with you?"

10 Balaam said to God, Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent for me.

11 Behold, the folk that came out of Egypt now covers the outward appearance of the land; come, devise for me a curse against him, perhaps I may be able to fight him and drive him out.

12 And God said to Balaam, You shall not go with them; you shall not curse the people, for they are blessed.

13 Balaam arose in the morning and said to the chiefs of Balak, "Go to your land, for the LORD has refused to let me go with you."

14 The captains of Moab arose and came to Balak and said, "Surely Balaam will not come with us."

15 And again Balak sent more captains, many and honorable.

16 They came to Balaam and said to him, "Thus says Balak son of Zippor: Please do not refuse to come to me."

17 For I will greatly honor you, and whatever you say to me I will do; now please go and curse this people for me.

18 Balaam answered and said to the servants of Balak, If Balak were to give me all that is in his house, silver and gold, I am not able to transgress the word of the LORD my God, to do either little or great.

19 Now therefore, stay here tonight, both you and I, and I will know what Jehovah will yet speak with me.

20 God came to Balaam at night and said to him, "If the men have come to summon you, rise and go with them, but only the word that I speak to you, that you shall do."

21 And Balaam rose up in the morning and saddled his she ass and went with the princes of Moab.

22 The anger of God burned because he was going, and the messenger of the LORD took his stand in the way as an adversary to him; and he was riding on his she-ass, and his two servants were with him.

23 The ass saw the messenger of the LORD standing in the way with his sword drawn in his hand, and the ass turned aside from the way and went into the field, and Balaam struck the ass to turn her back to the way.

24 The LORD's messenger stood in the path of the vineyards, a fence on this side and a fence on that side.

25 The ass saw the messenger of the LORD and pressed against the wall, crushing Balaam’s leg against the wall, so he struck her again.

26 And the ambassador of the LORD continued to pass on, and he stood in a place narrow where there was no way to turn right or left.

27 The she ass saw the messenger of the LORD and lay down under Balaam, and the anger of Balaam burned and he struck the she ass with the rod.

28 Then the LORD opened the mouth of the she ass, and she said to Balaam, "What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?"

29 Balaam said to the ass, "Because you have mocked me, if only there were a sword in my hand, I would kill you now."

30 Then said the (she) ass to Balaam, Is not I (she) ass on which you have ridden from the time of your earlier days until this day? Did I ever have the habit of doing so to you? And he said, No.

31 Then the LORD uncovered the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the messenger of the LORD standing in the way, with his sword drawn in his hand; and Balaam bowed down and fell on his face.

32 The ambassador of the Lord said to him, "Why have you struck your she-ass? This is the third time: I have come out as an adversary because the way has been contrary to me."

33 The ass saw me and turned aside before me these three times; perhaps she turned away from me, but now I would have killed you and spared her.

34 Balaam said to the messenger of the LORD, "I have sinned, for I did not know that you stood against me on the journey; now, if it is evil in your sight, I will return to me."

35 The LORD's messenger said to Balaam, "Go with the men, but only the word which I speak to you—that you shall speak." So Balaam went with Balak's officials.

36 Balak heard that Balaam had come, and he went out to meet him to the city of Moab, which is on the border of Arnon, at the edge of the border.

37 Balak said to Balaam, "Did I not send to you to summon you? Why did you not come to me? Am I not indeed able to honor you?"

38 Balaam said to Balak, "Behold, I have come to you; now, am I able to speak any-thing? The word that God puts in my mouth—that I will speak."

39 Balaam went with Balak and they came to Kirjath-huzoth.

40 Balak sacrificed beeve and sheep, and sent to Balaam and to the princes who were with him.

41 And it came to pass in the morning that Balak took Balaam and brought him up to Bamoth-baal, and he saw from there the edge of the people.


## Chapter 23

1 Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me here seven altars, and prepare for me here seven young bulls and seven rams.”

2 Balak did as Balaam had spoken, and Balak and Bileam offered up a young bull and a ram on the altar.

3 Balaam said to Balak, "Stand by your ascent, and I will go; perhaps the LORD will meet me; whatever He shows me I will report to you." So he went to the high place.

4 God came to Balaam and said to him, "Seven altars I have arranged, and a bull and a ram I have offered on each altar."

5 And the LORD put a word in the mouth of Balaam, and said, Return to Balak, and thus you shall speak.

6 And he returned to him, and behold, he was standing on his burnt offering, he and all the princes of Moab.

7 He took up his parable and said, From Aram Balak king of Moab has brought me, from the mountains of the east: Come, curse Jacob for me, come, denounce Israel.

8 How can I curse whom God has not cursed? How can I denounce whom the LORD has not denounced?

9 For from the top of the rocks I see him, and from the hills I behold him: behold, a people dwelling alone, and among the nations not reckoned.

10 Who can count the dust of Jacob, or number the fourth of Israel? Let my soul die the death of the upright, and let my latter end be like his.

11 Balak said to Balaam, "What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, but look, you have blessed with blessing."

12 And he answered and said, Will I not keep the word that Jehovah puts in my mouth to speak?

13 Balak said to him, Please come with me to another place from where you can see him; you will see only the edge of it, but not the whole; and curse it for me from there.

14 He took him into the field of the Zophim to the top of Pisgah, and he built seven altars and offered up a bull and a ram on the altar.

15 And he said to Balak, "Stand here by your ascent, and I will meet the LORD over there."

16 The LORD met Balaam and put a word in his mouth, and said, Return to Balak and thus you shall speak.

17 And he came to him, and behold, he was standing by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him, and Balak said to him, “What has the LORD spoken?”

18 He took up his parable and said, Rise up, Balak, and hear; give ear, O son of Zippor.

19 Not a man is God, that he should lie, nor a human being, that he should repent; has he spoken, and will he not do it? Has he spoken, and will he not fulfill it?

20 Behold, a blessing I have taken, and blessed, and I will not turn back.

21 There is no affliction seen in Jacob and no grievance observed in Israel; the LORD their God is with them, and the shout of a king is among them.

22 God who brought them out of Egypt is as strong as the strength of a unicorn.

23 For there is no enchantment in Jacob and no divination in Israel; at this time it is said to Jacob and to Israel, What has God done?

24 Behold, a folk like a great lion rises, and as a young lion it lifts itself up; it will not lie down until it eats prey, and drinks the blood of the slain.

25 And Balak said to Balaam, "You shall not curse them at all, and you shall not bless them at all."

26 Then Balaam answered and said to Balak, "Did I not speak to you, saying, Whatever the LORD speaks, that I must do"?

27 Balak said to Balaam, "Come now, let me take you to another place; perhaps it will be pleasing in the sight of the gods, and you can curse him for me from there."

28 Balak took Balaam to the summit of Peor, overlooking the face of the wilderness.

29 Balaam said to Balak, "Build for me seven altars here, and prepare for me here seven young bulls and seven rams."

30 Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered up a bull and a ram upon the altar.


## Chapter 24

1 Balaam saw that it was the LORD's will to bless Israel, so he did not go as before to seek enchantments, but set his face toward the wilderness.

2 And Balaam lifted up his eyes and saw Israel dwelling according to their tribes, and the Spirit of God came upon him.

3 He took up his parable and said: "The saying of Balaam son of Beor, and the saying of the man whose eye is open."

4 The saying of him who hears the words of God, the seer of the vision of the Almighty, who saw the one fallen and the open of eyes:

5 How good are your coverings, Jacob, your dwelling places, Israel.

6 Like streams planted, like gardens beside a river, like aloes that the LORD has planted, like cedars beside waters.

7 Water shall flow from his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters, and he shall be exalted above Agag his king, and his kingdom shall be raised up.

8 God brings him out of Egypt; he has strength like a unicorn; he shall devour the nations, his adversaries, and crush their bones, and his arrows shall shatter them.

9 He crouched, he lay down like a young lion, and like a lioness; who shall rouse him? Those who bless you are blessed, and those who curse you are cursed.

10 Balak’s anger burned against Balaam, and he clapped his hands; and Balak said to Balaam, “I summoned you to curse my enemies, but indeed you have blessed, blessed thrice!”

11 Now flee to your country; I said, "I will honor you greatly," but now the LORD has prevented you from glory.

12 Balaam said to Balak, "Did I not also speak to your messengers whom you sent to me, saying:"

13 If Balak were to give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not transgress the mouth of the LORD to do good or evil from my own mind; what the LORD speaks, that will I speak.

14 And now, behold, I am going to my people; come, let me advise you what this people will do to your people in the latter days.

15 He took up his parable and said, "The saying of Balaam son of Beor, the saying of the man whose eye is open."

16 The one who hath said, saith the hearer of the words of God, and who knoweth the knowledge of the Most High, seeth the vision of the Almighty, fallen, and the open eyes.

17 I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not near: a star shall come out of Jacob, and a sceptre shall rise out of Israel; and it shall crush the corners of Moab, and shall destroy all the sons of Sheth.

18 And Edom shall be a possession; Seir shall be a possession of his enemies; but Israel shall do valiantly.

19 And he will come down from Jacob and destroy the remnant of the city.

20 And he saw Amalek and took up his parable and said: "The chief of the nations is Amalek, but its latter end shall be utter perishing."

21 He saw the Kenite and took up his parable and said, "Strong is your dwelling-place, and your nest is set in the rocky stronghold."

22 But if Cain is for the burning, how long will Assyria hold you?

23 He took up his parable and said, Alas, who shall live when God destroys?

24 And ships from the coast of Kittim, and they will afflict Asshur and will afflict Eber, and he also will be utterly destroyed.

25 And Balaam rose and went to his home, and Balak also went and returned to his way.


## Chapter 25

1 Israel settled in Shittim, and the folk began to commit harlotry with the daughters of Moab.

2 And they called to the folk for sacrifices to their gods, and the folk ate and bowed down to their gods.

3 And Israel was joined to Baal-peor, and the anger of the LORD burned against Israel.

4 And the LORD said to Moses, Take all the chiefs of the people and set them up before the sun for the LORD, that the fierce wrath of the LORD may turn away from Israel.

5 Moses said to the judges of Israel, "Each man kill his people who are joined to Baal-peor."

6 And behold, a certain man from the people of Israel came and brought the Midianite woman before his brother, in the sight of Moses and in the sight of all the congregation of the people of Israel, while they were weeping at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

7 Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest saw, and he rose up from among the assembly and took a spear in his hand.

8 Then a certain man of Israel came and pierced both the man of Israel and the woman through her belly, and the plague was stopped from upon the children of Israel.

9 And those who died in the plague were twenty-four thousand.

10 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

11 Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest turned back my wrath from upon the children of Israel by his zeal with which he was zealous among them so that I did not consume the children of Israel in my zeal.

12 Therefore say: I am giving him my covenant of peace.

13 And it shall be to him and to his seed after him a covenant of perpetual priesthood, because he was zealous for his God and made atonement for the children of Israel.

14 The name of the Israelite man who was struck, the one struck down with the Midianite woman, was Zimri son of Salu, a prince of a father's house among the Simeonites.

15 The name of the woman who was struck down, the Midianite, was Cozbi daughter of Zur, a leader of the peoples, head of a father's house in Midian.

16 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

17 Strike the Midianites and attack them.

18 For they are adversaries to you through the wiles they devised against you concerning the matter of Peor and concerning the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of a Midianite prince, their sister, who was struck down on the day of the plague on account of the affair of Peor.


## Chapter 26

1 And it came to pass after the plague. The LORD said to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, saying:

2 Take a census of all the congregation of the people of Israel from twenty years old and upward, by their fathers' houses, all who are able to go to war in Israel.

3 Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke to them in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying:

4 From the age of twenty years and upward, as the LORD commanded Moses and the people of Israel, those coming out of the land of Egypt.

5 Reuben the firstborn of Israel: the sons of Reuben were Enoch, the family of the Hanochites; Pallu, the family of the Palluites.

6 To Hezron, the family of the Hezronites; to Carmi, the family of the Carmites.

7 These are the families of the children of Reuben, and their enrolled ones were forty-three thousand seven hundred and thirty.

8 and the sons of Pallu: Eliab.

9 The sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram; these are the Dathan and Abiram, called of the congregation, who strove against Moses and against Aaron in the assembly of Korah, when they opposed the LORD.

10 The ground opened its mouth and swallowed them and Korah when the assembly died, and the fire consumed two hundred and fifty men, and they became a sign.

11 But the sons of Korah did not die.

12 The sons of Simeon by their families: to Nemuel, the family of the Nemuelites; to Jamin, the family of the Jaminites; to Jachin, the family of the Jachinites.

13 For Zerah, the family of the Zarchite; for Saul, the family of the Shaulites.

14 These are the families of the Simeonites: twenty-two thousand and two hundred.

15 The sons of Gad according to their families: of Zephon, the family of the Zephonites; of Haggi, the family of the Haggites; of Shuni, the family of the Shunites.

16 For Ozni, the family of the Oznites; for Eri, the family of the Erites.

17 Of Arod, the family of the Arodites; of Areli, the family of the Arelites.

18 These are the families of the sons of Gad according to their enrollment: forty thousand and five hundred.

19 The sons of Judah were Er and Onan, and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.

20 The sons of Judah were by their families: to Shelah, the family of the Shelanites; to Perez, the family of the Pharzites; to Zerah, the family of the Zarchites.

21 The sons of Perez were Hezron, the family of the Hezronites; Hamul, the family of the Hamulites.

22 These are the families of Judah according to their enrollment: seventy-six thousand and five hundred.

23 The sons of Issachar by their families: Tola, the family of the Tolaites; Puah, the family of the Punites.

24 The family of the Jashubites, of Jashub; the family of the Shimronites, of Shimron.

25 These are the families of Issachar according to their enrollments: sixty-four thousand and three hundred.

26 The sons of Zebulun by their families: of Sered, the family of the Sardites; of Elon, the family of the Elonites; of Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites.

27 These are the families of the Zebulonite according to their enrollments: sixty thousand and five hundred.

28 The sons of Joseph by their families: Manasseh and Ephraim.

29 The sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the family of the Machirite; and Machir fathered Gilead: of Gilead, the family of the Gileadite.

30 These are the sons of Gilead: Jeezer, the family of the Jezerite; Helek, the family of the Helkites.

31 and Ashriel the family of the Asrielites and Shechem the family of the Shichemites.

32 and Shemida the family of the Shemidaites and Hepher the family of the Hepherites.

33 Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons, only daughters; and the names of Zelophehad's daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

34 These are the families of Manasseh, and their enrollments: fifty-two thousand and seven hundred.

35 These are the sons of Ephraim according to their families: of Shuthelah, the family of the Shuthalhites; of Becher, the family of the Bachrites; of Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.

36 And these are the sons of Shuthelah, to Eran, the family of the Eranites.

37 These are the families of the sons of Ephraim according to their enrollment: thirty-two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph according to their families.

38 The sons of Benjamin by their families: of Bela, the family of the Belaites; of Ashbel, the family of the Ashbelites; of Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites.

39 According to their families the Shuphamite family; according to their families the Huphamite family.

40 The sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman; the family of the Ardites, the family of the Naamites.

41 These are the sons of Benjamin by their families, and their enrolled men: forty-five thousand six hundred.

42 These are the sons of Dan by their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites; these are the families of Dan by their families.

43 All the families of the Shuhamites according to their enrollments: sixty and four thousand and four hundred.

44 The sons of Asher by their families: of Imnah, the family of the Imnites; of Ishvi, the family of the Ishvites; of Beriah, the family of the Beerites.

45 To the sons of Beriah, to Heber, the Heberites family; to Malchiel, the Malchielite family.

46 And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah.

47 These are the families of the sons of Asher according to their enrollments: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

48 The children of Naphtali by their families: of Jahzeel, the family of the Jahzeelites; of Guni, the family of the Gunites.

49 For Jezer, the family of the Jezerites; for Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.

50 These are the families of Naphtali according to their families, and their enrolled ones: forty-five thousand and four hundred.

51 These are the numbered ones of the children of Israel: six hundred thousand and one thousand, seven hundred and thirty.

52 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

53 To these the land shall be divided as an inheritance according to the number of names.

54 To the many increase his inheritance, and to the few diminish his inheritance; each man according to his census shall be given his inheritance.

55 Only by lot shall the land be divided, according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.

56 According to the lot shall his inheritance be divided, between the many and the few.

57 These are the families of the Leviite: for Gershon, the family of the Gershonites; for Kohath, the family of the Kohathites; for Merari, the family of the Merarites.

58 These are the families of the Leviite: the family of the Libnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Mahlites, the family of the Mushites, the family of the Korahite; and Kohath begot Amram.

59 The name of Amram's wife was Jochebed, daughter of Levi, who bore her to Levi in Egypt; and she bore to Amram Aaron and Moses and their sister Miriam.

60 Aaron became the father of Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

61 Nadab and Abihu died when they offered a fiery offering before the LORD.

62 Their numbered men were twenty-three thousand, every male from the age of one month and upward, because they were not numbered among the people of Israel, since no inheritance was given to them among the people of Israel.

63 These are the instructions of Moses and Eleazar the priest, who took charge of the children of Israel in the Arabah of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho.

64 And in these there was no man from those numbered by Moses and Aaron the priest, who had numbered the children of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.

65 For the LORD said to them, They shall surely die in the wilderness, and not a man of them shall remain, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.


## Chapter 27

1 The daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher son of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh son of Joseph, came near, and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

2 And they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before the leaders and before all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying:

3 Our chief died in the wilderness, and he was not among the assembly that gathered against the LORD in the company of Korah, for he died because of his fault, and sons were not to him.

4 Why should the name of our father be cut off from his family because he has no son? Give us a possession among the brothers of our father.

5 Moses brought the matter of judgment before the LORD.

6 And the LORD said to Moses, saying:

7 Indeed, the daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly; you shall give them a possession of inheritance among the brothers of their father, and you shall transfer the inheritance of their father to them.

8 And to the children of Israel you shall speak, saying, When a man dies and has no son, then you shall transfer his inheritance to his daughter.

9 But if he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.

10 But if he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the brothers of his father.

11 And if there are no brothers to their father, then you shall give his inheritance to his near kin from his family, and he shall inherit it; and it shall be for the children of Israel a statute of judgment, as the LORD commanded Moses.

12 The LORD said to Moses, "Go up to this mountain of the Abarim and see the land that I have given to the children of Israel."

13 And you shall see it and be gathered to your folk, even you, as Aaron your brother was gathered.

14 According to your rebellion at the waters of Meribah in the wilderness of Zin, when you provoked my word before their eyes, to sanctify me in the waters—those are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.

15 Moses spoke to the LORD, saying:

16 May the LORD, God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation.

17 who shall go out before them and who shall come in before them, and who shall lead them out and who shall bring them in, so that the congregation of the LORD may not be as sheep that have no shepherd.

18 And the LORD said to Moses, Take Joshua the son of Nun, a man in whom is spirit, and lay your hand upon him.

19 And you shall present him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and you shall command him in their sight.

20 And you shall give of your honor to him, so that all the congregation of the children of Israel may obey him.

21 Before Eleazar the priest shall he stand, and inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before the LORD; at his word they shall go out, and at his word they shall come in, he and all the children of Israel with him, even all the congregation.

22 Moses did as Jehovah the Lord commanded him and took Joshua and presented him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation.

23 And he laid his hands upon him and charged him, as the LORD had spoken by the hand of Moses.


## Chapter 28

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Command the children of Israel and say to them My offering, the food of my offering by fire, a sweet savour to me, you shall keep to present to me in its appointed season.

3 And you shall say to them This is the offering by fire which you shall offer to the LORD two lambs each year without blemish daily continual burnt offering

4 One lamb you shall prepare in the morning, and the second lamb you shall prepare between the evenings.

5 and a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a gift, mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil.

6 a continual ascent offering made on mount Sinai for a sweet savour an offering by fire to the LORD

7 Its drink offering shall be a quarter of a hin of wine for each lamb, offered as a drink offering of strong drink to the LORD in the sanctuary.

8 And the second lamb you shall prepare between the evenings, as the morning gift and as its drink offering, an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD.

9 And on the sabbath day, two sheep of one year old without blemish, and two tenth deals of fine flour for a meal offering mixed with oil, and its drink offering.

10 The sabbath ascent offering every sabbath, in addition to the continual ascent offering and its drink offering.

11 At the beginnings of your months you shall present a burnt offering to the LORD: two young bulls, one ram, seven lambs of the first year, all without blemish.

12 Three tenth deals of fine flour for a gift, mixed with oil, for one bull; and two tenth deals of fine flour for a gift, mixed with oil, for one ram.

13 a tenth deal of fine flour as an oblation, mixed with oil, for the lamb, the one burnt offering, a sweet savour, an offering by fire to the LORD.

14 Their drink offerings shall be: half a hin of wine for the bull, a third of a hin for the ram, and a quarter of a hin for the lamb; this is the monthly burnt offering for each month of the year.

15 and one goat for a purifying offering for the LORD, for the daily burnt offering it shall be prepared, and its drink offering

16 In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, a Passover offering to the LORD.

17 And on the fifteenth day of this month a feast: seven days unleavened bread shall be eaten.

18 On the first day a holy convocation, any work of service you shall not do.

19 You shall present a fire offering, a burnt offering to the LORD: two young bulls, one ram, and seven year-old male lambs; they shall be without blemish for you.

20 Their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for the bull and two-tenths of an ephah for the ram you shall prepare.

21 A tenth deal for the one lamb, for the seven sheep.

22 and one goat for purifying-offering to make atonement for you

23 Besides the morning burnt offering that is for the continual burnt offering, you shall prepare these.

24 These things you shall do daily for seven days: food made by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD; upon the continual burnt offering it shall be made, and its drink offering.

25 On the seventh day a holy convocation shall be for you; no work of service shall you do.

26 On the day of the first fruits, when you present a new grain offering to the LORD during your week of weeks, there shall be a holy convocation for you; you shall do no laborious work.

27 You shall present a burnt offering as a sweet savour to the LORD: two young bulls, one ram, seven lambs, each a year old.

28 Their oblation was fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths deal for each bull, two-tenths deal for each ram.

29 A tenth deal for each lamb, for the seven sheep.

30 one goat of the goats for a sin offering on your behalf

31 Apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering, you shall make them—your sacrifices and their drink offerings—without blemish for yourselves.


## Chapter 29

1 In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, a holy convocation shall be for you; no manner of work of service shall you do; a day of loud shouting shall be for you.

2 You shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet savour to the LORD: one young bull from the herd, one ram, seven lambs of the first year, perfect ones.

3 Their offering of fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the ram.

4 a tenth deal for each lamb, for the seven lambs

5 and one goat of the goats as a sin offering to make atonement for you.

6 besides the burnt offering of the month and its gift, and the continual burnt offering and its gift, and their drink offerings, according to their due ordinance, for a sweet savour, an offering by fire to the LORD.

7 On the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be a holy convocation for you, and you shall afflict your souls; no manner of work shall you do.

8 You shall present a burnt offering to the LORD, a sweet savour: one young bull, one ram, seven lambs of the first year; they shall be without blemish for you.

9 Their grain offering was fine flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah for each bull, two-tenths of an ephah for each ram.

10 A tenth deal, a tenth deal for the one lamb, for the seven lambs.

11 one male goat for a sin offering, besides the sin offering for atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and its gift, and their drink offerings

12 And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month there shall be a holy convocation for you; every work of service you shall not do; and you shall celebrate a feast to the LORD seven days.

13 You shall present a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a sweet savour to the LORD: thirteen young bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year, all without blemish.

14 Their oblation was fine flour mixed with oil: three tenth deals for each bull, for the thirteen bulls, two tenth deals for each ram, for the two rams.

15 and a tenth deal for each lamb, for fourteen lambs.

16 and one hairy goat for purification for sin, apart from the daily burnt offering with its grain offering and its drink offering

17 On the second day bulls of the herd twelve, rams two, lambs of the year fourteen, all without blemish.

18 Their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, according to their number, as prescribed.

19 one goat for a sin offering, apart from the continual burnt offering and its grain offering and their drink offerings

20 On the third day twelve bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish.

21 Their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, according to their number, as prescribed.

22 and one goat for purifying from sin besides the daily burnt offering and its gift and its drink offering

23 On the fourth day ten bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year, all without blemish.

24 Their oblation and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, according to their number, as prescribed by ordinance.

25 and one hairy goat for purification for sin, apart from the daily burnt offering with its gift and its drink offering

26 On the fifth day nine bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year, all without blemish.

27 Their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, according to their number, as prescribed.

28 and one goat for purification for sin, apart from the daily burnt offering and its gift and its drink offering

29 On the sixth day eight bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year, all without blemish.

30 Their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, in the prescribed number according to the ordinance.

31 and one purifying offering of a goat besides the daily burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.

32 On the seventh day seven bulls, two rams, fourteen lambs of one year old, all without blemish.

33 Their grain offering and their drink offering for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, according to their number, according to their ordinance.

34 and one goat for purification for sin besides the daily burnt offering with its gift and its drink offering

35 On the eighth day there shall be a solemn assembly for you; no manner of work shall you do.

36 You shall present a burnt offering, a fire offering, a sweet savour to the LORD: one bull, one ram, seven lambs, each a year old, without blemish.

37 Their grain offerings and their drink offerings for the bull, for the ram, and for the lambs, according to their number, as prescribed by ordinance.

38 and one goat for purification for sin, apart from the daily burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.

39 These you shall offer to the LORD at your appointed times, apart from your vows and your free will offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meal offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.

40 Moses said to the children of Israel according to all that the LORD commanded Moses.


## Chapter 30

1 Moses spoke to the chiefs of the tribes of the people of Israel, saying, This is the word that the LORD has commanded.

2 Any man who vows a vow to the LORD or swears an oath to bind a bond upon himself must not break his word; according to all that proceeds from his mouth he shall do.

3 And if a woman vow a vow to the LORD, binding herself under a bond in her father’s house in her youth,

4 And if her father hears of her vow or her binding obligation which she has bound upon herself and says nothing to her, then all her vows shall stand and every bond she has imposed upon herself shall remain in force.

5 But if her father forbade her on the day he heard, none of her vows or the bindings by which she bound herself shall stand, and the LORD will forgive her, because her father forbade her.

6 But if a man has a vow on her, or an utterance of her lips by which she has bound herself,

7 And if her husband hears and says nothing to her on the day he hears, then her vows and the bindings by which she bound herself shall stand.

8 But if on the day her husband hears, he causes her to stop and nullifies her vow that is on her and the utterance of her lips which she bound on herself, then the LORD will forgive her.

9 And a widow's or a divorced woman's vow, whatever she has bound upon herself, shall stand against her.

10 And if a woman takes a vow or binds herself by a bond upon her person with an oath, in her husband's household,

11 And if her husband hears and says nothing to her, does not reject her, then all her vows shall stand, and every bond she imposed on herself shall remain.

12 But if her husband nullifies them on the day he hears them, none of the utterances of her lips concerning her vows or the bond of her soul shall stand; her husband has annulled them, and the LORD will forgive her.

13 Every vow and every binding oath to afflict the soul—her husband shall establish it, and her husband shall annul it.

14 But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, he thereby confirms all her vows or all her bonds that are upon her; he upholds them, because he remained silent to her on the day when he heard.

15 But if he nullifies them after he has heard, then he shall bear her iniquity.

16 These are the statutes that the LORD commanded Moses between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter in her youth in her father's house.


## Chapter 31

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Avenge the vengeance of the children of Israel from the Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people.

3 Moses spoke to the folk, saying, "Set apart men from among you for the host, and let them be against Midian to execute the vengeance of the LORD on Midian."

4 A thousand from each tribe, a thousand from each tribe, from all the tribes of Israel, you shall send to the army.

5 They were mustered from the thousands of Israel, one thousand per tribe, twelve thousand equipped for battle.

6 Moses sent them, a thousand from each tribe for the army, them and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest for the army, and the holy vessels and the trumpets for alarm in his hand.

7 They encamped against Midian as the LORD had commanded Moses, and they killed every male.

8 They killed the kings of Midian on top of their slain: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba, the five kings of Midian, and Balaam son of Beor they killed with the sword.

9 The sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little children, and they plundered all their cattle, all their herds, and all their wealth.

10 And all their cities in their dwelling places, and all their palaces, they burned with fire.

11 And they took all the spoil and all the prey, both the people and the cattle.

12 They came to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the assembly of the people of Israel, bringing the prisoners and the booty and the spoil, to the camp at the plain of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.

13 Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the rulers of the assembly went out to meet them outside the camp.

14 Moses was angry with the commanders of the army, the captains of thousands and the captains of hundreds, who came from the service of the battle.

15 Moses said to them, "Have you spared every female?"

16 Behold, they caused the sons of Israel through the word of Balaam to commit a trespass against the LORD on account of the matter of Peor, and the plague came upon the congregation of the LORD.

17 Now kill every male among the little children and every woman who has known a man by lying with a man.

18 And all the little children among the women who have not known the lying with a male, keep alive for yourselves.

19 And you shall stay outside the camp seven days; everyone who has killed any person, and everyone who has touched a dead body, shall purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, you and your captives.

20 And every garment, and every vessel of leather, and every work of goats, and every vessel of wood, shall be purified.

21 And Eleazar the priest said to the men of the host who came to the battle: This is the statute of the law which the LORD commanded Moses.

22 Only the gold and the silver, the copper, the iron, the tin, and the lead.

23 Every thing that may go into fire you shall pass through fire and it shall be clean, only with waters of separation you shall purify it, and every thing that may not go into fire you shall pass through water.

24 And you shall wash your garments on the seventh day and be clean, and afterward you may come into the camp.

25 And the LORD said to Moses, saying:

26 Take the sum of the spoil taken from the people and from the cattle, you and Eleazar the priest and the chiefs of the fathers of the congregation.

27 And you shall divide the booty between those who went out to war, the fighters in the battle, and all the congregation.

28 And you shall take a tribute to the LORD from the men of war who went out to battle: one person from every five hundred, from the people, from the livestock, from the donkeys, and from the sheep.

29 From half of it you shall take, and give to Eleazar the priest the LORD's heave offering.

30 And from the half of the children of Israel you shall take one from every fifty, from the man, from the beeve, from the (he) ass, and from the flock, from all the cattle, and you shall give them to the Levites, keepers of the charge of the tabernacle of the LORD.

31 Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the LORD commanded Moses.

32 And the booty, the excess of the spoil which the army had plundered, was sheep, six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand.

33 and seventy-two thousand beeves

34 and one hundred and sixty asses

35 And any person, among the women who had not known lying with a male, every person thirty-two thousand.

36 And the half of the portion of those who went out to the army was in number of the sheep three hundred thirty-seven thousand and five hundred.

37 The tribute to the LORD from the flock was six hundred seventy-five.

38 And the beeve, thirty and six thousand, and their tribute to the LORD, two and seventy.

39 And thirty thousand and five hundred asses, and tribute for the LORD, one and sixty.

40 And the persons of man were sixteen thousand, and the tribute for the LORD was thirty-two persons.

41 Moses gave the tribute, the gift of the LORD, to Eleazar the priest, as the LORD commanded Moses.

42 and from half of the sons of Israel, whom Moses had divided from the men of the army,

43 And half of the assembly from the flock was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred.

44 and beeve thirty and six thousand

45 And three thousand and five hundred asses.

46 and any soul of man sixteen thousand

47 Moses took from half the children of Israel one out of every fifty, of man and of beast and gave them to the Levites who kept charge of the tabernacle of the LORD as the LORD commanded Moses.

48 The officers who were over the thousands of the army, the captains of the thousands and the captains of the hundreds, came near to Moses.

49 They said to Moses, Your servants have taken a count of the men of war who are in our hand, and not a man has been missed from among them.

50 And we brought near the offering of the LORD, each man who found a gold item: a bracelet, a covering, a ring, an earring, and a pendant, to atone for our souls before the LORD.

51 Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from them, all articles of workmanship.

52 All the gold of the offered gift that they lifted up for the LORD was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels, from the captains of thousands and from the captains of hundreds.

53 The men of the host plundered, each man for himself.

54 Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from the captains of the thousands and the hundreds, and brought it to the tent of meeting as a memorial for the children of Israel before the LORD.


## Chapter 32

1 And abundant cattle belonged to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad, very mighty; and they saw the land of Jaazer and the land of Gilead, and behold, the place was a place for cattle.

2 And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came and said to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the rulers of the assembly, saying:

3 Ataroth and Dibon and Jaazer and Nimrah and Heshbon and Elealeh and Shebam and Nebo and Beon.

4 The land which the LORD struck before the congregation of Israel is a land for cattle, a possession for your servants.

5 And they said, If we have found favour in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as a possession; do not bring us across the Jordan.

6 Moses said to the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben, "Shall your brothers go to the battle and you sit here?"

7 Why do you discourage the heart of the children of Israel from crossing over to the land that the LORD has given them?

8 Thus your forefathers did, when I sent them from Kadeshbarnea to see the land.

9 They went up to the brook Eshcol and saw the land, and they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel from entering the land which the LORD had given them.

10 The LORD became angry on that day and swore, saying:

11 If the men who went up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, see the land which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, because they did not follow me fully.

12 except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenezite and Joshua son of Nun, for they followed after the LORD.

13 The LORD was angry with Israel and made them wander in the wilderness forty years until the end of the generation that had done evil in the eyes of the LORD.

14 Look, you have risen up in place of your ancestors, a brood of sinful men, to add still more to the fierce wrath of the LORD against Israel.

15 If you turn back from following him, he will again leave him in the wilderness, and you will destroy this whole people.

16 They approached him and said, Let us build sheep-cotes for our cattle here, and cities for our little children.

17 And we will go up hastily before the children of Israel until we bring them to their place, and our little children will dwell in the fortified cities because of the inhabitants of the land.

18 We will not return to our homes until every man of Israel has taken possession of his inheritance.

19 For we did not inherit with them beyond the Jordan and onward, for our inheritance came to us beyond the Jordan eastward.

20 Then Moses said to them, If you will do this act, if you will go to war before the LORD,

21 And every warrior among you shall cross the Jordan before the LORD, until he has dispossessed his enemies from before him.

22 The land will be subdued before the LORD, and afterward you shall return, and you will be blameless before the LORD and before Israel; and this land will be yours as a possession before the LORD.

23 But if you do not do so, behold, you have sinned against the LORD, and your sin will find you.

24 Build towns for your little children and sheep-cotes for your sheep, and what has gone out from your mouth you shall do.

25 The sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben said to Moses, "Your servants will do as my lord commands."

26 Our little children, our wives, our cattle, and all our beasts will be there in the cities of Gilead.

27 And your servants will pass over, everyone a warrior of the army, before the LORD for battle, as my lord speaks.

28 Moses commanded Eleazar the priest and Jehoshua son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes to the children of Israel.

29 Moses said to them, If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben pass with you over the Jordan, every armed man for battle before the LORD, and the land is subdued before you, then you shall give them the land of Gilead as a possession.

30 But if the vanguard does not pass over with you, then they will take hold among you in the land of Canaan.

31 The sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying, "What the LORD has spoken to your servants, that we will do."

32 We will pass over armed before the LORD into the land of Canaan, and our possession of inheritance shall remain with us beyond the Jordan.

33 Moses gave to them, to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben and to the half tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorite and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land with its cities in their borders, the cities of the land round about.

34 The sons of Gad built Dibon and Ataroth and Aroer.

35 Atroth and Shophan and Jaazer and Jogbehah

36 Beth-Nimrah and Beth-haran, fortified cities and sheepfolds.

37 And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon and Elealeh and Kiriathaim.

38 And Nebo and Baal-meon, named for a name, and Shebam; they called the names of the towns which they built by names.

39 The sons of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead and captured it, and dispossessed the Amorite who was in it.

40 Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he lived there.

41 Jair the son of Manasseh went and captured their towns, and he called them Havoth-jair.

42 And Nobah went and captured Kenath and her daughters, and he called Kenath Nobah, after his own name.


## Chapter 33

1 These are the journeys of the children of Israel who went out from the land of Egypt according to their hosts by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

2 Moses wrote their goings out according to their journeys at the command of the LORD, and these are their journeys according to their goings out.

3 They journeyed from Raamses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the month, on the morrow of the passover offering, the sons of Israel went out by a mighty hand, before the eyes of all Egypt.

4 And the Egyptians buried all the firstborn whom the LORD had struck down among them, and against their gods the LORD executed judgment.

5 The people of Israel journeyed from Rameses and encamped at Succoth.

6 They journeyed from Succoth and camped in Etham, which is at the edge of the wilderness.

7 They journeyed from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which is before Baal-zephon, and they camped before Migdol.

8 They journeyed from before Pi-hahiroth and crossed in the midst of the sea into the wilderness; they went a way of three days in the wilderness of Etham and encamped at Marah.

9 They journeyed from Marah and came to Elim, and at Elim were twelve fountains of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.

10 They set out from Elim and camped by the Sea of Reeds.

11 They set out from the Sea of Reeds and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.

12 They journeyed from the desert of Sin and camped at Dophkah.

13 They journeyed from Dophkah and camped in Alush.

14 They journeyed from Alush and encamped at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.

15 They journeyed from Rephidim and camped in the desert of Sinai.

16 They journeyed from the desert of Sinai and camped in Kibroth-hattaavah.

17 They journeyed from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped in Hazeroth.

18 They journeyed from Hazeroth and camped in Rithmah.

19 They journeyed from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-parez.

20 They journeyed from Rimmon-parez and camped at Libnah.

21 They journeyed from Libnah and camped at Rissah.
