22 They journeyed from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.

23 They journeyed from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shapher.

24 They journeyed from Mount Shapher and camped at Haradah.

25 They journeyed from Haradah and encamped in Makheloth.

26 They journeyed from Makheloth and camped in Tahath.

27 They journeyed from Tahath and camped at Tarah.

28 They journeyed from Tarah and camped in Mithcah.

29 They journeyed from Mithcah and camped in Hashmonah.

30 They journeyed from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.

31 They journeyed from Moseroth and camped in Bene-jaakan.

32 They journeyed from Bene-jaakan and camped in Hor-hagidgad.

33 They journeyed from Hor-hagidgad and camped in Jotbathah.

34 They journeyed from Jotbathah and camped in Ebronah.

35 They journeyed from Ebronah and camped in Ezion-geber.

36 They journeyed from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin, which is Kadesh.

37 They journeyed from Kadesh and camped at mount Hor at the edge of the land of Edom.

38 Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor by the command of the LORD and died there in the fortieth year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the month.

39 Aaron was 123 years old at his death in Hor the mountain.

40 The Canaanite king Arad, who lived in the south of the land of Canaan, heard when the sons of Israel came.

41 They journeyed from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.

42 They set out from Zalmonah and camped at Punon.

43 They set out from Punon and camped at Oboth.

44 They journeyed from Oboth and camped in Ije-abarim, on the border of Moab.

45 They journeyed from Iim and camped in Dibon Gad.

46 They journeyed from Dibon-gad and camped at Almondilathaim.

47 They journeyed from Almondilathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim before Nebo.

48 They journeyed from the mountains of the Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.

49 They camped along the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth to Abelshittim in the Arabah of Moab.

50 The LORD spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying:

51 Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, when you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan.

52 You shall dispossess all the inhabitants of the land from before you and destroy all their images and all their molten images you shall make to perish and all their high places you shall demolish.

53 You shall inherit the land and settle in it, for I have given the land to you to take possession of it.

54 You shall inherit the land by lot according to your families: to the many you shall increase his inheritance, and to the few you shall diminish his inheritance; to whomever the lot goes out, to him it shall be; by the tribes of your fathers you shall inherit.

55 But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those whom you let remain shall be pricks in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they shall trouble you in the land where you dwell.

56 And as I have purposed to do to them, so will I do to you.


## Chapter 34

1 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

2 Command the sons of Israel and say to them When you come into the land of Canaan this is the land that shall fall to you for an inheritance the land of Canaan according to its borders

3 Your southern side shall be from the wilderness of Zin alongside Edom, and your southern border shall be from the eastern end of the Salt Sea.

4 And the border shall turn for you from the south at the ascent of Akrabbim, and cross Zin, and its outgoings shall be from the south to Kadeshbarnea, and it shall go out to Hazar-addar and cross Azmon.

5 And the border shall turn from Azmon to the brook of Egypt, and its outgoings shall be at the sea.

6 And a border the sea, and it shall be for you the great sea and a border; this shall be for you a border the sea.

7 This shall be your northern border: from the Great Sea you shall assign to yourselves Mount Hor.

8 From Mount Hor you shall go to Hamath, and the border shall go out to Zedad.

9 Then the border shall go out from Ziphron, and its goings forth shall be to Hazar-enan; this shall be your northern border.

10 And you shall desire for yourselves a border eastward from Hazar-enan to Shepham.

11 The border went down from Shepham to Riblah east of Ain, and the border went down and touched the shoulder of the sea of Chinnereth on the east.

12 The border shall descend to the Jordan, and its outgoings shall be at the Salt Sea; this shall be your land according to its borders all around.

13 Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, This is the land that you shall inherit by lot, which the LORD commanded to give to the nine tribes and the half tribe.

14 For the tribe of the Reubenites took according to their families, and the tribe of the Gadites according to their families, and half the tribe of Manasseh took their inheritance.

15 Two tribes and a half tribe took their inheritance beyond the Jordan, eastward toward Jericho.

16 The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

17 These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land to you: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun.

18 And one prince, one prince from each tribe, shall you take to apportion the land.

19 And these are the names of the men for the tribe of Judah: Caleb son of Jephunneh.

20 and for the tribe of the people of Simeon Samuel son of Ammihud

21 by the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon.

22 And for the tribe of the Danites, the prince was Bukki son of Jogli.

23 For the sons of Joseph, for the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, a prince: Hanniel son of Ephod.

24 And for the tribe of the Ephraimites a prince, Kemuel son of Shiphtan.

25 And for the tribe of the children of Zebulun, the prince was Elizaphan son of Parnach.

26 And for the tribe of the children of Issachar a prince, Paltiel son of Azzan.

27 And for the tribe of the people of Asher, a prince, Ahihud son of Shelomi.

28 and for the tribe of the children of Naphtali a prince, Pedahel son of Ammihud.

29 These are what the LORD commanded to inherit to the sons of Israel in the land of Canaan.


## Chapter 35

1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the Arabah of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho, saying:

2 Command the sons of Israel to give to the Leviites from the inheritance of their possession cities for dwelling, and suburbs for the cities around them shall you give to the Leviites.

3 And the towns shall be for them to dwell in, and their suburbs shall be for their cattle, for their goods, and for all their living creatures.

4 And from the suburbs of the cities that you will give to the Levites, from the wall of the city outward, a thousand cubits round about.

5 And you shall measure off two thousand cubits beyond the city toward the east side, and two thousand cubits toward the south side, and two thousand cubits toward the west side, and two thousand cubits toward the north side, and the city shall be in the middle; this shall belong to them as open land of the cities.

6 The cities that you shall give to the Leviites include the six cities of refuge to which a manslayer may flee, and in addition you shall give forty-two cities.

7 All the cities which you shall give to the Levites, forty and eight, them and their suburbs.

8 The towns that you give from the possession of the sons of Israel, from the many you shall increase, and from the few you shall diminish; each according to the inheritance that he shall inherit shall give of his towns to the Levites.

9 And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:

10 Speak to the children of Israel and say to them, when you pass over the Jordan into the land of Canaan.

11 And you shall prepare for yourselves cities, cities of refuge shall be for you, and there shall flee the manslayer who strikes a person unwittingly.

12 These cities shall be for you as places of refuge from the avenger, so that the manslayer may not die until he stands before the assembly for judgment.

13 And the cities that you shall give shall be six cities of refuge for you.

14 The three cities you shall appoint beyond the Jordan and the three cities you shall appoint in the land of Canaan shall be cities of refuge.

15 For the children of Israel and for the alien and for the inhabitant among them shall be these six cities of refuge to flee there, every one who strikes a person unwittingly.

16 And if with an iron weapon he struck him so that he died, the murderer shall surely be put to death.

17 But if with a stone, such as one may die by, he struck him and he died, the striker is a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death.

18 Or with a wooden instrument in hand, with which a man is struck and dies, the striker is a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death.

19 The avenger of blood shall kill the murderer; when he meets him, he shall kill him.

20 But if in hatred he pushes him, or hurls at him lying in wait, and he dies,

21 Or if in hatred he struck him with his hand so that he died, the striker shall surely be put to death; he is a murderer; the blood-avenger shall put the murderer to death when he meets him.

22 But if suddenly, without hatred, he pushed him, or threw any weapon at him without lying in wait,

23 or by any stone, which if he dies by it without seeing, and he falls upon it and dies, and he was not his enemy, nor seeking his harm.

24 The assembly shall decide between the one who struck and the blood-avenger, according to these judgments.

25 Then the assembly shall rescue the murderer from the hand of the blood-avenger, and the assembly shall restore him to his city of refuge where he had fled, and he shall dwell there until the death of the high priest who had anointed him with the holy oil.

26 But if the murderer should ever go beyond the border of the city of refuge to which he had fled,

27 And if the blood-avenger finds him outside the border of his city of refuge, and the blood-avenger kills the manslayer, there is no bloodguiltiness for him.

28 For in the city of his refuge he shall dwell until the death of the chief priest; but after the death of the chief priest the manslayer shall return to the land of his possession.

29 These shall be an appointed statute for you for judgment throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.

30 Every one who strikes a person shall be put to death on the testimony of witnesses, but a single witness shall not testify against any person for death.

31 You shall not accept a ransom for the life of a murderer who is guilty of death, for he shall surely be put to death.

32 And you shall not accept a bribe for the one who flees to his city of refuge, to return to dwell in the land until the death of the priest.

33 Do not defile the land in which you are, for blood defiles the land, and the land cannot be expiated for the blood that is shed in it except by the blood of the one who shed it.

34 You shall not defile the land in which you dwell, where I reside among the children of Israel, for I the LORD dwell in the midst of the children of Israel.


## Chapter 36

1 The chiefs of the fathers of the clans of the sons of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, of the clans of the sons of Joseph, came forward and spoke before Moses and before the princes, the chiefs of the fathers of the sons of Israel.

2 They said, “The LORD commanded our lord to give the land as an inheritance by lot to the children of Israel, and our lord was commanded by the LORD to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother to his daughters.”

3 And they shall be to one of the tribes of the sons of Israel for wives, and their inheritance shall be taken from the inheritance of our fathers and added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they belong, so that our inheritance will be diminished from the lot of our inheritance.

4 And if a jubile shall be to the sons of Israel, then their inheritance shall be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they are joined, and from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers shall their inheritance be subtracted.

5 Moses commanded the sons of Israel according to the word of the LORD, saying, "Thus speak the tribe of the sons of Joseph."

6 This is the act that the LORD commanded concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying, “To those who are good in their eyes they shall be married, but only into the family of the tribe of their father shall they be married.”

7 The inheritance must not pass from tribe to tribe among the Israelites, for each man of the Israelites shall remain bound to the inheritance of his ancestral tribe.

8 Every daughter who inherits a heritage among the tribes of the children of Israel shall be married to one of the family of the tribe of her father, so that the children of Israel may each possess the inheritance of their fathers.

9 A tribe must not transfer its heritage to another tribe, for each man shall remain bound to the heritage of his tribe, the tribes of the people of Israel.

10 As Jehovah commanded Moses, so did the daughters of Zelophehad.

11 Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, married their uncles’ sons.

12 From the families of the sons of Manasseh son of Joseph there were women, and their inheritance was on the tribe of the family of their father.

13 These are the commandments and the judgments which the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses to the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.


# DEUTERONOMY

## Chapter 1

1 These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah opposite the Red Sea, between Paran and Tophel and Laban and Hazeroth and Dizahab.

2 Eleven days from Horeb by the way of mount Seir to Kadeshbarnea.

3 In the fortieth year, on the eleventh month, on the first day of the month, Moses spoke to the children of Israel according to all that the LORD had commanded him concerning them.

4 After he had struck Sihon king of the Amorites who lived in Heshbon, and Og king of Bashan who lived in Asharoth at Edrei.

5 Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to expound this law, saying:

6 The LORD our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying, You have stayed long enough at this mountain.

7 Turn and go up for yourselves, and come to the hill country of the Amorite and to all his neighbors, to the Arabah, to the hill country, to the low plain, to the south country, and to the seacoast, to the land of the Canaanite and to Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.

8 See, I have set the land before you; go in and possess the land that the LORD swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their seed after them.

9 And I said to you at that time, saying, I am not able alone to bear you.

10 The Lord your God has greatly multiplied you, and behold, you today are as numerous as the stars of the heavens.

11 The LORD God of your fathers may increase you a thousand times and bless you as He has promised.

12 How can I alone bear your cumbrance, your burden, and your strife?

13 Give yourselves wise and discerning men, known among your tribes, and I will appoint them at your head.

14 You answered me and said, "Good is the advice that you have spoken to do."

15 I took your tribal chiefs, wise and experienced men, and I appointed them as leaders over you—commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officers for your tribes.

16 I commanded your judges at that time, saying, Hear between your brothers and judge righteously between a man and his brother and his sojourner.

17 You shall not show partiality in judgment; you shall hear the small and the great alike; you shall not fear the face of any man, for the judgment belongs to God; and any matter that is too difficult for you, you shall bring to me, and I will hear it.

18 At that time I commanded you all the things you were to do.

19 We journeyed from Horeb and went through all the great and terrible wilderness that you saw, along the way of the hill country of the Amorites, as the LORD our God commanded us, and we came to Kadeshbarnea.

20 And I said to you, "You have come to the hill country of the Amorite that the LORD our God is giving us."

21 See, the LORD has given the land before you; go up, take possession, as the LORD your God promised you; do not fear, and do not be dismayed.

22 You came to me, all of you, and said, “Let us send men before us to search out the land for us and bring back word to us about the way by which we shall go up and the cities into which we shall come.”

23 The matter seemed good in my sight, so I took from you twelve men, one man for each tribe.

24 They turned and went up to the hill country and came to the valley of Eshcol and spied it out.

25 They took in their hands some of the fruit of the land and brought it down to us, and they brought back word to us and said, "Good is the land which the LORD our God gives us."

26 But you were unwilling to go up, and you rebelled against the word of the LORD your God.

27 And you murmured in your tents and said, "Because of the LORD's hatred for us, he has brought us out of the land of Egypt to deliver us into the hand of the Amorite to destroy us."

28 Here we are going up; our brothers have discouraged our hearts, saying, a people great and tall, greater than we, with cities great and walled up to the heavens, and also we saw the Anakim there.

29 And I said to you, “You shall not be terrified, nor shall you fear them.”

30 The LORD your God who goes before you will fight for you, as everything that he did with you in Egypt before your eyes.

31 In the wilderness where you saw how the LORD your God carried you as a man carries his son, throughout the entire way that you walked until coming to this place.

32 In this act you are not believing in the LORD your God.

33 who goes before you on the way to seek out a place for you to camp, in fire by night to show you the way you should walk, and in a cloud by day.

34 And the LORD heard the voice of your words, and he was angry, and swore, saying:

35 If any man among these people sees the good land that I swore to give to your fathers, that evil generation,

36 Except for Caleb son of Jephunneh, he shall see it, and to him I will give the land that he has trodden, and to his sons, because he has wholly followed the LORD.

37 Even against me did the LORD become angry because of you, saying, You also shall not go there.

38 Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you, he shall enter there; strengthen him, for he shall cause Israel to inherit it.

39 Your little children, whom you said would be spoil, and your sons who do not today know good and evil, they shall enter there, and to them I will give it, and they shall inherit it.

40 But you, turn for yourselves, and set out for the desert by way of the Red Sea.

41 And you answered and said to me, We have sinned against the LORD; we will go up and fight, according to all that the LORD our God commanded us. And each man girded his weapons of war, and you were eager to go up into the hill country.

42 And the LORD said to me, Say to them, You shall not go up, neither shall you fight, for I am not among you, lest you be struck down before your enemies.

43 I spoke to you, but you did not listen, and you rebelled against the word of the LORD, and you acted defiantly and went up the mountain.

44 The Amorite who lived in that hill country came out against you and pursued you as bees do, and they struck you down in Seir as far as Hormah.

45 You returned and wept before the LORD, but the LORD did not hear your voice, nor listen to you.

46 You remained in Kadesh many days, the days that you remained.


## Chapter 2

1 So we turned and set out into the wilderness by the way of the Sea of Reeds, as the LORD commanded me, and we circled around Mount Seir a long time.

2 And the LORD said to me, saying:

3 You have circled this hill country long enough; turn for yourselves northward.

4 Command the folk, saying, You are passing through the border of your brothers, the sons of Esau, who live in Seir; they will fear you, so be very careful.

5 Do not contend with them, for I will not give you any of their land, not even the breadth of a footstep, because I have given the hill country of Seir as a possession to Esau.

6 Food you will buy from them for money and you will eat, and also water you will buy from them for money and you will drink.

7 For the LORD your God has blessed you in all the work of your hands; he has known your going through the great wilderness; these forty years the LORD your God has been with you—you have lacked no thing.

8 We passed on from our kindred, the sons of Esau, who live in Seir, along the way of the Arabah, from Elath and from Ezion-geber, and we turned and passed on the way of the wilderness of Moab.

9 The LORD said to me, Do not harass Moab and do not provoke them to war, for I will not give you any of their land as a possession, because I have given Ar as a possession to the children of Lot.

10 The Emims formerly dwelt therein, a people great and many and tall as the Anakim.

11 The Rephaim will be considered even they as Anakim, and the Moabites will call them Emims.

12 In Seir the Horites dwelt formerly, but the sons of Esau dispossessed them and settled in their place, just as Israel did in the land of their possession which the LORD gave them.

13 Now rise up and cross over for yourselves the Zared brook and we will pass over the Zared brook.

14 And the days that we journeyed from Kadeshbarnea until we crossed the brook Zered were thirty-eight years, until the end of all the generation of the men of war perished from within the camp, as the LORD swore to them.

15 And also the hand of the LORD was upon them to destroy them from within the camp until they were finished.

16 And it came to pass, when all the men of war had finished dying from among the people.

17 And the LORD spoke to me, saying:

18 You are passing today the border of Moab, [to] Ar.

19 When you approach the Ammonites, do not harass them or provoke them, for I will not give you any of the land of the Ammonites as an inheritance, because I have given it as an inheritance to the sons of Lot.

20 The land is counted a land of Rephaim; even they were Rephaim who lived there formerly, and the Ammonites call them Zamzummim.

21 A great and numerous and tall folk, like the Anakim; and the LORD destroyed them from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their stead.

22 As he did for the sons of Esau who live in Seir, who destroyed the Horites from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their stead, even to this day.

23 And the Avim who lived in Hazerim as far as Azzah, the Caphthorim who came from Caphtor destroyed them and lived in their place.

24 Rise, go forward, and cross the Arnon brook; see, I have given Sihon king of Heshbon the Amorite into your hand, and his land; begin to possess it, and confront him in battle.

25 This day I begin to put your dread and your fear upon the faces of the peoples under all the heavens, who will hear your fame and tremble and be in anguish because of you.

26 I sent ambassadors from the wilderness of Kedemoth to Sihon king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying:

27 I will pass through your land along the way, along the way I will go; I will not turn to the right or to the left.

28 Food for money you will break me, and I will eat; and water for money you will give me, and I will drink; only let me pass by foot.

29 As the Edomites who live in Seir and the Moabites who live in Ar have done to me, until I cross the Jordan to the land that the LORD our God gives us.

30 But King Sihon of Heshbon was unwilling to let us pass through his territory; for the LORD your God stiffened his spirit and hardened his heart, in order to deliver him into your hand, as at this day.

31 And the LORD said to me, See, I have begun to deliver Sihon and his land before you; begin to possess his land.

32 Then Sihon came out against us, he and all his folk, to battle at Jahaz.

33 The LORD our God gave him over to us, and we struck him and his sons and all his people.

34 And we captured all his cities at that time and devoted to destruction every city of men, women, and children; we left no remnant.

35 Only the cattle we took for ourselves, and the spoil of the towns which we captured.

36 From Aroer, which is on the bank of the brook Arnon, and the city which is in the valley, even to Gilead, there was no city too high for us; the LORD our God delivered all into our presence.

37 Only the land of the Ammonites you did not approach, all the hand of the river Jabbok and the cities of the hill and all that the LORD our God commanded.


## Chapter 3

1 We turned and went up the way to Bashan, and Og king of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.

2 And the LORD said to me, Do not fear him, for I have delivered him and all his people and his land into your hand, and you shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorites who lived in Heshbon.

3 The LORD our God delivered into our hand also Og king of Bashan and all his people, and we struck him until no remnant was left to him.

4 And we captured all his cities at that time; there was no city that we did not take from them: sixty cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

5 All these cities were walled with high walls, double doors, and bars, except many unwalled villages.

6 We devoted them to destruction as we had done to Sihon king of Heshbon, devoting to destruction every city with its men, women, and children.

7 And all the cattle and the spoil of the cities we took for ourselves.

8 At that time we took the land from the hand of the two Amorite kings who were beyond the Jordan, from the Arnon river to Mount Hermon.

9 The Sidonians will call Hermon Sirion, and the Amorite will call it Senir.

10 All the cities of the plain and all Gilead and all Bashan as far as Salcah and Edrei, the cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

11 Only Og king of Bashan remained of the remnant of the Rephaim. Behold, his bedstead was a bedstead of iron. Is it not in Rabbah of the Ammonites? Nine cubits was its length, and four cubits its breadth, by the cubit of a man.

12 And we took possession of the land at that time, from Aroer which is on the river Arnon, and half the hill country of Gilead with its cities; I gave it to the Reubenites and to the Gadites.

13 The rest of Gilead and all Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh, all the region of Argob in all Bashan, which is called the land of the Rephaim.

14 Jair son of Manasseh took all the region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and called them by his name, Havoth-jair in Bashan, to this day.

15 And to Machir I gave Gilead.

16 And to the Reubenites and to the Gadites I gave from Gilead to the Arnon river, the middle of the river as a border, and up to the Jabbok river, the border of the children of Ammon.

17 and the Arabah and the Jordan and the border, from Chinnereth even to the sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, under the springs of Pisgah eastward.

18 At that time I commanded you, saying, the LORD your God has given you this land to possess it; all you men of valor shall cross over before your brothers, the sons of Israel.

19 Only your wives, your little ones, and your cattle—I know that you have abundant livestock—shall remain in the cities that I have given you.

20 until the LORD gives your brothers rest like you, and they also inherit the land that the LORD your God gives them beyond the Jordan; then you shall return, each to his inheritance that I have given you.

21 And I commanded Joshua at that time, saying, Your eyes have seen all that the LORD your God did to these two kings; so will the LORD do to all the kingdoms over which you are passing.

22 You shall not fear them, for the LORD your God is the one who fights for you.

23 And I pleaded with the LORD at that time, saying:

24 (my) Lord God, you have begun to show to your servant your greatness and your strong hand, for which God in the heavens or on the earth is able to do according to your deeds and your mighty acts?

25 Let me pass now, and see the good land beyond the Jordan, that good hill country and Lebanon.

26 The LORD was angry with me because of you and would not listen to me; and the LORD said to me, "Enough! Do not speak to me again about this matter."

27 Go up to the top of Pisgah and lift up your eyes west and north and south and east, and see with your own eyes, for you shall not cross over this Jordan.

28 And charge Joshua, and strengthen him, and cause him to persevere, for he shall go before this people, and he shall cause them to inherit the land which you shall see.

29 And we dwelt in the valley opposite Bethpeor.


## Chapter 4

1 And now, Israel, listen to the commandments and the judgments that I am teaching you to do, so that you may live and enter and possess the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

2 You shall not add to the word which I command you, nor take from it, so that you keep the commandments of the LORD your God which I command you.

3 Your eyes have seen what the LORD did at Baal-peor, for every man who followed Baal-peor, the LORD your God destroyed him from among you.

4 But you who cling to the LORD your God are alive, all of you, this day.

5 See, I have taught you statutes and ordinances as the LORD my God commanded me, to do thus in the midst of the land where you are entering to possess it.

6 You shall keep and do this, for it is your wisdom and understanding in the sight of the peoples, who will hear all these statutes and say, “Only a wise and discerning people is this great nation.”

7 For what great nation is there whose gods are near to them as the LORD our God is to us whenever we call upon him?

8 And which nation is great that has statutes and judgments as righteous as all this law which I set before you today?

9 Only take utmost care for your soul, lest you forget the words which your eyes have seen, and lest they depart from your heart all the days of your life; but make them known to your sons and to the sons of your sons.

10 The day that you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb, when the LORD said to me, Gather the people to me, that I may make them hear my words, so that they may learn to fear me all the days that they live upon the land, and they may teach their children.

11 You came near and stood beneath the mount, and the mount burned with fire reaching to the midst of the heavens, darkness, cloud, and thick darkness.

12 The LORD spoke to you from the midst of the fire; you heard the sound of words, but no image did you see—only a voice.

13 And he declared to you his covenant which he commanded you to perform, the ten words, and he wrote them on two stone tablets.

14 And the LORD commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances, to do them in the land which you are passing over there to possess.

15 You must keep your souls exceedingly, for you did not see any image on the day the LORD spoke to you at Horeb out of the midst of the fire.

16 Lest you corrupt and make for yourselves a carved (graven) image, any image, figure, likeness, or similitude of any idol, the figure of a male or a female.

17 the figure of every beast that is on the earth, the figure of every winged bird that flies in the heavens.

18 the figure of every creeping thing upon the ground, the figure of every fish which is in the waters under the earth.

19 Lest you lift up your eyes to the heavens and see the sun and the moon and the stars, all the host of the heavens, and you be led astray and bow down to them and serve them, which the LORD your God has allotted to all the peoples under all the heavens.

20 And the LORD took you and brought you out of the iron furnace, out of Egypt, to be for him a people of inheritance, as at this day.

21 And the LORD was angry with me because of your words, and swore that I would not cross the Jordan or enter the good land that the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance.

22 For I am dying in this land; I am not crossing the Jordan, but you are crossing and inheriting this good land.

23 Take care for yourselves lest you forget the covenant that the LORD your God made with you, and make for yourselves a carved image or any likeness of anything that the LORD your God has forbidden you.

24 For the LORD your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.

25 When you father children and children's children, and grow old in the land, and then corrupt yourselves and make a carved image in the form of anything, and do the evil in the eyes of the LORD your God, provoking him to anger.

26 I call the heavens and the earth to witness against you this day: you will surely perish quickly from the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess— you will not prolong your days upon it, for you will be utterly destroyed.

27 And the LORD will scatter you among the nations, and you will be few in number among the peoples where the LORD will drive you.

28 And there you will serve gods, the work of human hands, wood and stone, that do not see, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.

29 And you shall seek from there Jehovah your God, and you shall find him when you search for him with all your mind and with all your being.

30 When distress overtakes you and all these things befall you in the latter days, you shall return to the LORD your God and listen to his voice.

31 For the LORD your God is a merciful God; he will not let you go nor destroy you nor forget the covenant of your ancestors to which he swore.

32 Ask now of the days that were before you, from the day when God created man upon the earth, and from one end of the heavens to the other end of the heavens: has there ever been such a great thing as this, or has there ever been heard anything like it?

33 Has any folk heard the voice of gods speaking from within the fire as you have heard and lived?

34 Or has God ever tried to take for Himself a nation from the midst of another nation, by trials, by signs, by wonders, by war, by a mighty hand, by an outstretched arm, and by great terrors, according to all that the LORD your God did for you in Egypt before your eyes?

35 You have been shown to know that Jehovah, the Lord, is God; there is no other besides him.

36 From the heavens he made you hear his voice to discipline you, and on the earth he showed you his great fire, and his words you heard from within the fire.

37 And because He loved your ancestors and chose their offspring after them, the LORD brought you out with His presence by His great power from Egypt.

38 to dispossess nations greater and mightier than you from before you, to bring you in, to give you their land as an inheritance, as at this day.

39 Today you shall know and set upon your heart that the LORD is God in the heavens above and upon the earth beneath—there is no other.

40 You shall keep his statutes and the commandments that I am commanding you today, so that it may go well with you and your children after you, and so that you may prolong your days upon the land that the LORD your God is giving you all the days.

41 Then Moses set apart three cities beyond the Jordan to the east.

42 to flee there the one who kills his fellow unawares, having not hated him heretofore, and he flees to one of the cities and lives.

43 Bezer in the wilderness, in the land of the plain, to the Reubenites; and Ramoth in Gilead, to the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan, to the Manassites.

44 And this is the law that Moses set before the children of Israel.

45 These are the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which Moses spoke to the children of Israel when they came forth from Egypt.

46 Beyond the Jordan in the valley opposite Bethpeor in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, whom Moses and the people of Israel defeated when they came out of Egypt.

47 They took possession of his land and the land of Og, king of Bashan, the two Amorite kings beyond the Jordan, eastward of the sun.

48 From Aroer, which is on the bank of the Arnon brook, even to Mount Sion, that is Hermon.

49 All the Arabah beyond the Jordan eastward, as far as the Sea of the Arabah beneath the springs of Pisgah.


## Chapter 5

1 Moses called to all Israel and said to them, Hear, O Israel, the statutes and the judgments which I speak in your ears today, and learn them and keep them to do.

2 The LORD our God made a covenant with us at Horeb.

3 Not with our forefathers did the LORD make this covenant, but with us, we ourselves, all of us who are here today, alive.

4 Face to face the LORD spoke with you on the mountain from the midst of the fire.

5 I stood between the LORD and you at that time, to convey to you the word of the LORD, because you were afraid of the presence of the fire and did not go up the mountain, saying:

6 I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

7 You shall not have other gods before my face.

8 You shall not make for yourself a carved (graven) image, any likeness of anything which is in the heavens above, or which is on the earth beneath, or which is in the waters under the earth.

9 You shall not bow down to them or serve them, for I am the LORD your God, a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children and upon the third and upon the fourth generation of those who hate me.

10 and doing favour to the thousands, to those who love me and to those who keep my commandments.

11 You shall not lift up the name of the LORD your God to vanity, for the LORD will not clear the one who lifts up his name to vanity.

12 Observe the sabbath day to keep it holy, as the LORD your God commanded you.

13 Six days you shall labor and do all your work.

14 The seventh day is a sabbath to the LORD your God: you shall not do any work, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, your ox and your donkey, and all your cattle, and the sojourner who is within your gates, so that your male servant and your female servant may rest as you do.

15 You shall remember that you were a bondman in the land of Egypt, and the LORD your God brought you out from there with a strong hand and with an outstretched arm; therefore the LORD your God has commanded you to do the sabbath day.

16 Honor your father and your mother as the LORD your God has commanded you, so that your days may be prolonged and so that it may go well with you upon the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

17 You shall not murder.

18 You shall not commit adultery.

19 You shall not steal.

20 You shall not testify against your fellow a false witness.

21 You shall not covet your fellow's wife, nor desire your fellow's house, his field, his male servant, his female servant, his ox, his donkey, or anything that belongs to your fellow.

22 These acts the LORD spoke to all your assembly from the midst of the fire the cloud and the thick darkness with a great voice and no more, and he wrote them on two stone tablets and gave them to me.

23 When you heard the voice from the midst of the darkness, and the mountain burning with fire, you came near me, all the heads of your tribes and your elders.

24 And you said, Behold, the LORD our God has shown us his glory and his greatness, and we have heard his voice from the midst of the fire; this day we have seen that God may speak with a man and he live.

25 And now, why should we die, for this fiery fire will consume us; if we again hear the voice of the LORD our God, we will die.

26 For who is any flesh, that has heard the voice of the living God speaking from the midst of the fire, as we have, and lived?

27 Come thou near, and hear thou all that the LORD our God shall say, and thou shalt speak unto us all that the LORD our God shall speak unto thee, and we will hear and do.

28 The LORD heard the sound of your words when you spoke to me, and the LORD said to me, I have heard the sound of the words of this people which they have spoken to you; they have done well in all that they have spoken.

29 Who will make them bethink themselves to fear me and to keep all my commandments always, that it may go well with them and with their children forever?

30 Go, say to them, Return to your tents.

31 And you, stand here with me, and I will speak to you all the commandments and statutes and judgments which you shall teach them, so that they may do them in the land which I am giving them to possess.

32 You shall keep to do as Jehovah, the Lord your God has commanded you; you shall not turn aside to the right hand or to the left hand.

33 In all the way that the LORD your God has commanded you, you shall walk, that you may live and it may be well with you, and that you may prolong days in the land which you shall possess.


## Chapter 6

1 And this is the commandment, the statutes, and the ordinances which the LORD your God commanded to teach you, to do in the land which you are crossing over there to possess.

2 so that you may fear the LORD your God, to keep all his statutes and commandments that I command you, you and your son and your son's son, all the days of your life, and that your days may be prolonged.

3 Israel shall hear and keep to do so that it will be well with you and that you will greatly increase, just as the LORD your God promised your ancestors, a land flowing with milk and honey.

4 Hear, O Israel: the LORD our God, the LORD is one.

5 You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your might.

6 And these words which I command you today shall be upon your heart.

7 You shall diligently teach them to your children, and you shall speak of them when you sit in your house, when you walk along the way, when you lie down, and when you rise up.

8 and bind them as a mark upon your hand, and they shall be frontlets between your eyes.

9 And you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates.

10 And it shall be, when the LORD your God brings you into the land that he swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give you, great and good cities that you did not build,

11 and houses full of all good that you did not fill, and hewn cisterns that you did not hew, vineyards and olive trees that you did not plant, and you shall eat and be satisfied.

12 Beware lest you forget the LORD who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

13 You shall fear the LORD your God, and Him shall you serve, and by His name shall you swear.

14 You shall not go after other gods, of the nations that are around you.

15 For a jealous God is the LORD your God in your midst, lest the anger of the LORD your God burn against you and destroy you from upon the face of the ground.

16 You shall not test the LORD your God as you tested at Massah.

17 You shall surely keep the commandments of the LORD your God, and his testimonies, and his statutes, which he has commanded you.

18 Do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD, so that it may go well with you and you may come and possess the good land that the LORD swore to your fathers.

19 to drive out all your enemies from before you, as the LORD has spoken.

20 When your child asks you in time to come, saying, "What are the testimonies, statutes, and judgments which the LORD our God has commanded you?"

21 And you shall say to your son, We were slaves to Pharaoh in Egypt, and the LORD brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand.

22 The LORD gave great and terrible marks and wonders in Egypt to Pharaoh and to all his household before our eyes.

23 and he brought us out from there in order to bring us in, to give us the land that he swore to our fathers.

24 And Jehovah commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear Jehovah our God, for our good always, to keep us alive as on this day.

25 Justice will be ours if we carefully obey all this commandment before the LORD our God as He commanded us.


## Chapter 7

1 When the LORD your God brings you into the land that you are entering to possess, and he drives out before you many nations, the Hittite and the Girgashite and the Amorite and the Canaanite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite, seven nations greater and mightier than you,

2 And the LORD your God will deliver them over to you and you shall strike them; you shall devote them utterly—do not make a covenant with them or show them mercy.

3 You shall not intermarry with them: do not give your daughter to his son, and do not take his daughter for your son.

4 For he will turn your child away from following me, and they will serve other gods, and the LORD’s anger will burn against you, and he will swiftly destroy you.

5 But thus you shall do to them: their altars you shall break down, their garrisons you shall destroy, their groves you shall cut down, and their carved images you shall burn in the fire.

6 For you are a holy folk to the LORD your God, the LORD your God has chosen you to be for him a special people from all the peoples who are on the face of the land.

7 The LORD did not set his affection on you nor choose you because you were more numerous than all the nations, for you were the fewest of all the nations.

8 Because of the LORD’s love for you and because of his keeping the oath that he swore to your forefathers, the LORD brought you out with a strong hand and redeemed you from the house of bondage, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

9 You shall know that Jehovah, your God, he is God, the faithful God, keeping the covenant and the kindness for those who love him and for those who keep his commandments to a thousand generations.

10 and he repays his enemies to their face to destroy them; he will not delay for his enemy, to their face he repays him.

11 You shall keep the commandment, the statutes, and the ordinances that I am commanding you today to do them.

12 Because you will listen to these judgments and keep and do them, the LORD your God will keep for you the covenant and the steadfast love that he swore to your fathers.

13 I will love you and bless you and multiply you, and bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your land, your wheat and your wine and your anointed oil, the increase of your kine and the flocks of your sheep, on the land which the LORD swore to your fathers to give to you.

14 You shall be blessed above all peoples; there shall be no barren man or barren woman among you, nor in your cattle.

15 And the LORD will remove from you all disease and every evil sickness of Egypt that you know, not placing it upon you, but He will put it upon all your enemies.

16 You shall consume all the peoples whom the LORD your God gives over to you; your eye shall not pity them, and you shall not serve their gods, for that would be a snare to you.

17 When you bethink yourselves in your heart, The nations are more numerous than I; how can I dispossess them?

18 You shall not fear them; remember, you must remember what the LORD your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt.

19 The great trials that your eyes have seen, and the signs and wonders, and the strong hand and outstretched arm by which the LORD your God brought you out—so will the LORD your God do to all the peoples whom you fear before them.

20 Moreover, the LORD your God will send the hornet against them until the remnant and the hidden ones perish from before you.

21 Do not fear their presence, for the LORD your God is in your midst, a great and awesome God.

22 And the LORD your God will drive out those nations before you little by little; you will not be able to destroy them quickly, lest the wild animals of the field increase against you.

23 And the LORD your God will deliver them over to you and throw them into great discomfiture until they are destroyed.

24 And you will give their kings into your hand, and you will destroy their name from under the heavens; no man will stand before your face until you have destroyed them.

25 You shall burn their carved images in the fire; you shall not desire the silver or gold on them, nor take it for yourself, lest you be ensnared by it, for it is an abomination to the LORD your God.

26 You shall not bring an abominable thing into your household, and you shall not be under the same curse as it; you shall utterly detest it and utterly abhor it, for it is under utter destruction.


## Chapter 8

1 You shall keep to do all the commandment which I command you today, so that you may live and multiply and go in and possess the land which the LORD swore to your fathers.

2 You shall remember all the way that the LORD your God has led you these forty years in the wilderness, in order to humble you, to test you, to know what is in your heart, whether you would keep the commandment or not.

3 And he humbled you and made you hungry and fed you with manna, which you did not know, nor did your fathers know, so that he might make known to you that not by bread alone does a person live, but by every thing that proceeds from the mouth of the LORD does a person live.

4 Your apparel did not wear out from upon you, and your foot did not swell these forty years.

5 You shall know in your heart that as a man disciplines his son, so the LORD your God disciplines you.

6 You shall keep the ordinances which were commanded by the LORD your God, to walk in his ways and to fear him.

7 For the LORD your God is bringing you into a good land, a land of streams of water, springs and deeps, emerging in the valley and in the hill country.

8 A land of wheat and barley, vine and fig tree and pomegranate, a land of olive oil and honey.

9 a land where you will not eat bread in scarceness, nothing will be lacking in it, a land whose stones are iron and from whose mountains you will mine copper

10 You shall eat and be satisfied, and bless the LORD your God for the good land that he has given you.

11 Take care, lest you forget the LORD your God, so as not to keep his commandments, his judgments, and his statutes, which I am commanding you today.

12 Lest you eat and be satisfied, and build good houses and dwell in them.

13 And your beeve and your flock shall multiply, and silver and gold shall increase for you, and everything that is yours shall increase.

14 and your heart becomes lifted up and you forget the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slaves.

15 who led you through the great and terrible wilderness, the fiery serpent and scorpion, the thirsty land where there is no water, who brought forth water for you from the flinty rock.

16 who makes you eat manna in the desert, a thing your fathers did not know, in order to humble you and in order to test you, to do good to you in the end.

17 And you shall say in your heart, My power and the might of my hand have gained me this wealth.

18 You shall remember the LORD your God, for it is He who gives you the power to make wealth, in order to confirm His covenant that He swore to your fathers, as at this day.

19 If you ever forget the LORD your God and follow other gods to serve them and bow down to them, I testify against you today that you will surely perish.

20 Like the nations whom the LORD destroys before you, so you shall be destroyed, because you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God.


## Chapter 9

1 Hear, Israel: you are crossing today over the Jordan to go in to dispossess nations greater and mightier than yourself, great and fortified cities reaching to the heavens.

2 a great and tall folk, the sons of Anak, whom you know and of whom you have heard: who can stand before the sons of Anak?

3 And you shall know this day that it is the LORD your God who is crossing over before you as a consuming fire; He will destroy them and He will subdue them before you, and you shall dispossess and destroy them quickly, just as the LORD has spoken to you.

4 Do not bethink yourselves in your heart, when the LORD your God drives them out from before you, saying, By my righteousness the LORD has brought me in to possess this land; but by the wickedness of these nations the LORD is dispossessing them from before you.

5 Not by your justice and the equity of your heart are you coming to dispossess them of their land, but because of the wickedness of these nations the LORD your God is dispossessing them before you, in order to establish the word which the LORD swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

6 You will know that it is not for your justice the LORD your God gives you this good land to possess, for a stiff-necked people you are.

7 Remember, do not forget, how you provoked the LORD your God in the wilderness, from the day you left the land of Egypt until you came to this place, you have been rebellious against the LORD.

8 And at Horeb you provoked the LORD to anger, so the LORD was angry with you, to destroy you.

9 When I went up the mountain to receive the stone tablets, the tablets of the covenant that the LORD had made with you, I stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights; I did not eat bread nor drink water.

10 The LORD gave me the two stone tablets, written with the finger of God, and on them all the words that the LORD spoke with you on the mountain from the midst of the fire on the day of the assembly.

11 After forty days and forty nights, the LORD gave me the two stone tablets, the tablets of the covenant.

12 And Jehovah said to me, Arise, go down quickly from here, for your people whom you brought out of Egypt have corrupted themselves; they have quickly turned aside from the way which I commanded them; they have made themselves a molten image.

13 And the LORD said to me, saying, I have seen this people, and indeed, it is a stiff-necked folk.

14 Leave Me alone, that I may destroy them and blot out their name from under the heavens, and I will make you into a great and mighty nation instead of them.

15 Then I turned and went down from the mount, and the mount burned with fire, and the two tables of the covenant were upon my two hands.

16 And I saw, and behold, you had sinned against the LORD your God: you had made for yourselves a molten calf; you had quickly turned aside from the way that the LORD commanded you.

17 I seized the two boards and threw them down from my two hands and broke them before your eyes.

18 I fell down before the LORD as at the first, forty days and forty nights; I did not eat bread and I did not drink water because of all your sin that you sinned to do evil in the sight of the LORD to provoke him.

19 For I was afraid because of the anger and wrath that the LORD was furious with you to destroy you, and the LORD listened to me also at that time.

20 And against Aaron was Jehovah exceedingly angry to destroy him, and I also prayed for Aaron at that time.

21 And the punishment of your sin which you committed with the calf I took and burned it in the fire and crushed it finely until it became dust and cast its dust into the brook descending from the mount.

22 At Taberah, and at Massah, and at Kibroth-hattaavah, you angered Jehovah, the Lord.

23 When Jehovah sent you from Kadeshbarnea to say, “Go up and possess the land which I have given you,” you rebelled against the word of Jehovah your God, and you did not believe him, and you did not obey his voice.

24 You have been rebelling against the LORD ever since the day I knew you.

25 And I fell down before the LORD the forty days and the forty nights that I fell down, because the LORD said to destroy you.

26 And I prayed to the LORD and said, O Lord God, do not destroy Your people and Your inheritance, whom You have redeemed in Your greatness, whom You have brought out of Egypt with a strong hand.

27 Remember your bondmen, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Do not turn to the stubbornness of this people, and to their iniquity, and to their sin.

28 Lest the nations say, “Because of the LORD’s inability to bring them into the land that he promised them, and because of his hatred for them, he has brought them out to kill them in the wilderness.”

29 And they are your folk and your inheritance, whom you brought out with your great power and with your outstretched arm.


## Chapter 10

1 At that time the LORD said to me, Carve for yourself two stone tablets like the first ones, and come up to me to the mountain, and make for yourself a wooden ark.

2 I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets which you broke, and you shall place them in the ark.

3 And I made an ark of shittim wood, and I fashioned two stone tablets like the first ones, and I went up the mountain with the two tablets in my hand.

4 And he wrote on the tablets the writing of the first, the ten commandments, which the LORD spoke to you on the mountain from the midst of the fire in the day of the assembly, and the LORD gave them to me.

5 And I turned and went down from the mount, and I put the tables into the ark which I had made, and they were there, as the LORD commanded me.

6 The Israelites journeyed from Beeroth of the children of Jaakan to Mosera, there Aaron died and was buried, and Eleazar his son ministered in his stead.

7 From there they journeyed to Gudgodah, and from Gudgodah to Jotbath, a land of streams of water.

8 At that time the LORD set apart the tribe of Levi to carry the ark of the covenant of the LORD, to stand before the LORD to minister to him and to bless in his name, until this day.

9 Therefore Levi did not have a portion or inheritance with his brothers; the LORD is his inheritance, as the LORD your God spoke to him.

10 And I stood on the mount as at other times, forty days and forty nights, and the LORD heeded me also that time; the LORD was not willing to destroy you.

11 The LORD said to me, "Arise, go journeying before the people, that they may come and possess the land which I swore to their fathers to give them."

12 And now Israel what doth the LORD thy God require of thee but to revere the LORD thy God to walk in all his ways and to love him and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul

13 To keep the commandments of the LORD and his statutes which I am commanding you today, for your good.

14 Behold, to the LORD your God belong the heavens and the heavens of heavens, the earth and all that is in it.

15 Only in your fathers did the LORD delight, to love them, and he chose their offspring after them, you, from among all the peoples, as of this day.

16 And you shall circumcise the foreskin of your heart, and your neck you shall no longer stiffen.

17 For the LORD your God is God of gods and Lord of lords, the great, mighty, and awesome God who does not show partiality and does not accept a bribe.

18 He executes justice for the orphan and the widow, and loves the sojourner, giving him bread and clothing.

19 You shall love the stranger, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.

20 You shall fear the LORD your God, him you shall serve, to him you shall hold fast, and by his name you shall swear.

21 He is your praise and he is your God, who has done with you these great and awesome things that your eyes have seen.

22 Your fathers went down to Egypt with seventy persons, and now the LORD your God has made you as numerous as the stars of the heavens.


## Chapter 11

1 You shall love the LORD your God and keep his charge, his statutes, his judgments, and his commandments all the days.

2 And you shall know today that it was not your children, who did not know and who did not see the discipline of the LORD your God, His greatness, His strong hand, and His outstretched arm.

3 and his marks and his deeds that he did among Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt and to all its land.

4 And what he did to the host of Egypt, to their horses and chariots, how he poured the waters of the Red Sea over their face when they pursued after you, and how the LORD destroyed them to this day.

5 and what he did for you in the wilderness until your coming to this place.

6 And what he did to Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab son of Reuben, when the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their households and their tents and all the living substance that was at their feet, in the midst of all Israel.

7 For your eyes have seen all the work of the LORD, the great thing that He did.

8 You shall keep in all manner the commandment which I command you today, so that you may grow strong and come and possess the land which you are crossing over there to possess.

9 and so that you may prolong your life in the land that the LORD swore to your fathers to give to them and to their offspring, a land flowing with milk and honey.

10 For the land that you are entering to possess is not like the land of Egypt from which you came, where you sowed your seed and watered with your foot like a garden of herbs.

11 And the land that you are crossing over to possess is a land of hills and valleys, drinking water from the rain of the heavens.

12 a land which the LORD your God seeks, always the eyes of the LORD your God are on it from the beginning of the year until the end of the year.

13 It shall be that if you diligently obey the commandments which I am commanding you today, to love the LORD your God and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul.

14 I will give rain for your land in its due season, the former rain and the latter rain, and you shall gather your wheat, your wine, and your anointed oil.

15 And I will give grass in your field for your cattle, and you will eat and be satisfied.

16 Take good heed to yourselves, lest your heart become complacent and you turn aside and serve other gods and bow down to them.

17 And the anger of the LORD will burn against you, and he will restrain the heavens so that there will be no rain, and the land will not give its fruit, and you will perish quickly from upon the good land which the LORD gives you.

18 You shall set these acts upon your mind and upon your life and bind them as a sign upon your hand and they shall be frontlets between your eyes.

19 You shall teach them to your children, speaking of them when you sit in your house, when you walk along the way, when you lie down, and when you rise up.

20 And you shall write them upon the doorposts of your house and upon your gates.

21 so that your days and the days of your children may be prolonged on the land that the LORD swore to your fathers to give them, as long as the heavens are above the earth.

22 For if you diligently keep all this commandment that I command you to do, to love the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways, and to hold fast to him.

23 And the LORD will drive out all these nations from before you, and you shall dispossess nations greater and mightier than yourselves.

24 Every place that the sole of your foot shall tread upon shall be yours, from the wilderness and Lebanon, from the river, even the river Euphrates, even to the western sea shall be your border.

25 No man shall stand against you; the LORD your God will put your dread and fear upon the face of all the land wherever you go, as He has spoken to you.

26 See, I am setting before you today a blessing and a curse.

27 the blessing, if you obey the commandments of the LORD your God which I command you today;

28 And the curse, if you do not listen to the commandments of the LORD your God, and turn aside from the way which I command you today, to go after other gods which you have not known.

29 And it shall be, when the LORD your God brings you into the land that you are entering to possess, that you shall set the blessing upon Mount Gerizim and the curse upon Mount Ebal.

30 Are they not beyond the Jordan, toward the west, in the land of the Canaanite who dwells in the Arabah, opposite Gilgal, by the terebinths of Moreh?

31 For you are crossing the Jordan to come to take possession of the land that the LORD your God is giving you, and you shall possess it and dwell in it.

32 You shall keep to do all the statutes and the judgments which I am giving before you today.


## Chapter 12

1 These are the appointed laws and judgments which you shall keep to do in the land which the LORD your fathers' God has given you to possess, all the days that you live upon the ground.

2 You shall utterly destroy all the places where the nations whom you are dispossessing served their gods, upon the high mountains and upon the hills and under every green tree.

3 You shall break down their altars, and shatter their pillars, and burn their groves in fire, and cut down the carved images of their gods, and destroy their name from that place.

4 You shall not do so to the LORD your God.

5 But to the place that the LORD your God will choose from all your tribes, to put his name there, you shall seek his presence and come there.

6 You shall bring there your burnt offerings and your sacrifices, your tithes and your heave offerings, your vowed offerings and your freewill offerings, and the firstborn of your beeves and your sheep.

7 And you shall eat there before the LORD your God and rejoice in all the work of your hands, you and your households, which the LORD your God has blessed.

8 You shall not do according to all that we are doing here today, every man whatever is right in his own eyes.

9 For you have not yet come to the rest and to the inheritance that the LORD your God is giving you.

10 And you shall cross the Jordan and dwell in the land that the LORD your God gives you, and he will give you rest from all your enemies around you, and you shall dwell securely.

11 The place that the LORD your God will choose to establish his name there, there you shall bring all that I command you: your burnt offerings, your sacrifices, your tithes, the offering of your hand, and all the choice of your vows that you vow to the LORD.

12 You shall rejoice before the LORD your God, you and your sons and your daughters, your male servants and your female servants, and the Levite who is within your gates, for he has no portion or inheritance with you.

13 Be careful not to offer your burnt offerings in any place that you may see.

14 But in the place that the LORD chooses from among your tribes, there you shall bring up your burnt offerings, and there you shall do all that I command you.

15 Only according to the desire of your soul may you slaughter and eat flesh, within any gate that the LORD your God gives you, the unclean and the clean alike may eat it, like the roe and like the hart.

16 Only the blood you shall not eat; upon the ground you shall pour it out like water.

17 You shall not be able to eat within your gates the tithe of your wheat and your new wine and your anointed oil, nor the birthright of your beeve and your sheep, nor any of your vows that you vow, nor your free offerings, nor the gift of your hand.

18 But you shall eat it before the LORD your God in the place that the LORD your God will choose—you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, and the Levite who is in your gates—and you shall rejoice before the LORD your God in everything that your hand sends forth.

19 Be careful not to forsake the Levite, in all your days, upon your land.

20 When the LORD your God enlarges your territory, as He promised you, and you say, "I will eat flesh, for my soul desires to eat flesh," you may eat flesh, according to all the desire of your soul.

21 When the place where the LORD your God chooses to put his name is far from you, you may slaughter from your herd and from your flock, which the LORD gives you, as I have commanded you, and you may eat in your gates according to all the desire of your soul.

22 Only as the roebuck and the hart are eaten, so you may eat it; the unclean and the clean may eat it together.

23 Only be strong not to eat the blood, for the blood is the life; you shall not eat the life with the flesh.

24 You shall not eat it; pour it out upon the ground like water.

25 You shall not eat it, so that it may go well with you and your children after you, because you do what is right in the eyes of the LORD.

26 Only your consecrated things which you will have and your vowed things shall you take and go to the place which the LORD will choose.

27 You shall make your burnt offerings, the flesh and the blood, on the altar of the LORD your God; and the blood of your sacrifices shall be poured out on the altar of the LORD your God, but the flesh you shall eat.

28 Keep and obey all these words which I command you, so that it may go well with you and your children after you forever, when you do what is good and right in the eyes of the LORD your God.

29 When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land you are entering to possess, driving them out before you, and you inherit them and dwell in their land,

30 Take heed to yourself, lest you be ensnared by following them after they are destroyed before you, and lest you inquire after their gods, saying, "How did these nations serve their gods? I will do the same."

31 You shall not do so to the LORD your God, for they have done to their gods every abomination that the LORD hates, even burning their sons and their daughters in the fire to their gods.

32 All the word that I command you, that you shall keep to do; you shall not add to it, nor take away from it.


## Chapter 13

1 For if there arise among you a prophet or a dreamer of dreams and he gives you a sign or a wonder,

2 And the mark and the miracle that he spoke to you, saying, "Let us go after other gods whom you have not known, and let us serve them," come to pass.

3 You shall not listen to the advice of that prophet or to the dream of that dreamer, for the LORD your God is testing you to know whether you love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

4 After the LORD your God you shall walk and him you shall fear and his commandments you shall keep and his voice you shall hear and him you shall serve and to him you shall cling

5 And that prophet or dreamer of the dream shall die because he has spoken rebellion against the LORD your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of bondage to turn you away from the way which the LORD your God commanded you to walk in and you shall burn the evil from your midst.

6 If your brother, your mother’s son, or your own son or daughter, or the wife of your bosom, or your companion who is as your own soul, secretly entices you, saying, “Let us go and serve other gods, which neither you nor your ancestors have known,”

7 from the gods of the peoples who are about you, near you or far from you, from the end of the land to the end of the land.

8 You shall not consent to him, nor listen to him; your eye shall not pity him, nor shall you spare him or shield him.

9 For you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be first upon him to put him to death, and afterward the hand of all the people.

10 They shall stone him with stones, and he shall die, because he sought to drive you away from the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage.

11 All Israel shall hear and fear, and never again do such an evil thing among you.

12 When you hear in one of your towns, which the LORD your God gives you to dwell in, saying:

13 Men, sons of Belial, came out from among you and incited the inhabitants of their city, saying, “Let us go and serve other gods, which you have not known.”

14 And you shall inquire, investigate, and thoroughly examine, and if the matter is true and confirmed, that this abomination has been committed among you,

15 Strike the city with the sword; utterly destroy it and all that is in it and its cattle with the sword.

16 And all her spoil you shall gather into the midst of her broad place, and you shall burn with fire the city and all her spoil altogether to the LORD your God, and she shall be a heap forever; she shall not be built again.

17 Nothing devoted to destruction shall remain in your hand, so that the LORD may turn from His fierce anger and show you mercy, have compassion on you, and multiply you, as He swore to your ancestors.

18 If you listen to the voice of the LORD your God to keep all his commandments that I am commanding you today to do what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God


## Chapter 14

1 You are sons to the LORD your God; you shall not cut yourselves nor put baldness between your eyes for the dead.

2 For you are a holy people to the LORD your God, and the LORD has chosen you to be for himself a special people from all the peoples who are on the face of the earth.

3 You shall not eat any abomination.

4 This is the cattle which you may eat: ox, sheep of the lambs, and sheep of the goats.

5 The hart, the roe, the fallow deer, the wild goat, the pygarg, the wild bull, and the chamois.

6 And every beast that parts the hoof and is clovenfooted, chewing the cud, among the beasts, that you may eat.

7 But these you shall not eat among those that bring up the cud and have a divided hoof: the camel, because it brings up the cud but does not have a divided hoof, it is unclean to you; the hare, because it brings up the cud but does not have a divided hoof, it is unclean to you; the coney, because it brings up the cud but does not have a divided hoof, it is unclean to you.

8 And the swine, because it parts the hoof and does not chew the cud, it is unclean to you; you shall not eat of its flesh, and you shall not touch its dead body.

9 You shall eat this from all that is in the water: everything that has fins and scales you may eat.

10 Whatever has no fins and scales you shall not eat; it is unclean to you.

11 Every clean bird you may eat.

12 And this is what you shall not eat of them: the eagle, and the vulture, and the ospray.

13 and the glede, and the vulture, and the kite after its kind

14 and every raven after its kind.

15 and the daughter of the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckoo, and the hawk according to its kind.

16 the cup and the (great) owl and the mole

17 and the pelican and the gier-eagle and the cormorant

18 And the stork, and the heron after its kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.

19 And every flying creature that creeps shall be unclean to you; you shall not eat it.

20 Every clean bird you may eat.

21 You shall not eat any carcass; to the alien who is within your gates you shall give it, that he may eat it, or you may sell it to a foreigner, for you are a holy people to the LORD your God; you shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.

22 You shall surely tithe all the increase of your seed, that which comes forth from the field year by year.

23 And you shall eat before the LORD your God in the place that he will choose to establish his name, the tithe of your wheat, your wine, and your anointed oil, and the firstborn of your beeve and your sheep, so that you may learn to fear the LORD your God all the days.

24 And when the journey is too far for you, because you are not able to carry it, since the place is far from you, the place that the LORD your God will choose to establish his name there, because the LORD your God will bless you:

25 You shall give the silver, and you shall bind the silver in your hand, and you shall go to the place that the LORD your God will choose.

26 You shall spend the money on anything your soul desires—beef, sheep, wine, strong drink, and all that your soul asks for—and you shall eat there before the LORD your God, and you and your household shall rejoice.

27 You shall not neglect the Leviite who is in your gates, for he has no portion or inheritance with you.

28 After three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in that year and shall leave it within your gates.

29 When the Levite comes, for he has no portion or inheritance with you, and the sojourner, the orphan, and the widow who are within your gates, they shall come and eat and be satisfied, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands that you do.


## Chapter 15

1 After seven years you shall make release.

2 This is the act of release: every creditor shall release what he has loaned to his fellow and to his brother, for a release has been proclaimed to the LORD.

3 You shall exact from the foreigner, but your hand shall release your brother.

4 Nevertheless, there will be no destitute among you, for the LORD your God will surely bless you in the land that the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance to possess.

5 Only if you will indeed obey the voice of the LORD your God to keep to do all the commandment which I command you today.

6 For the LORD your God will bless you as He promised, and you will lend to many nations, but you will not borrow, and you will rule over many nations, but they will not rule over you.

7 If there is a poor person among your brothers in one of your gates in your land that the LORD your God is giving you, do not harden your heart or close your hand against your brother who is poor.

8 For you shall surely open your hand to him, and freely lend him sufficient for his lack which he lacks.

9 Be careful not to let an evil act take root in your mind, saying, “The year of release is near,” and you look grudgingly at your brother the poor and do not give to him, for he may cry to the LORD against you, and it will be a fault against you.

10 You shall surely give to him, and your heart shall not be grieved when you give to him, because for this thing the LORD your God will bless you in all your deeds and in all your undertakings.

11 For the needy will never cease from among the land, therefore I command you, saying, You shall surely open your hand to your brother, to your poor and to your needy in your land.

12 If your Hebrew brother or Hebrew woman is sold to you, and serves you six years, then in the seventh year you shall send him or her free from you.

13 When you send him away free from you, you must not send him away empty-handed.

14 You shall surely give him of your flock, your threshing floor, and your wine press, that which the LORD your God has blessed you with you shall give him.

15 You shall remember that you were a bondman in the land of Egypt and that the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you this thing today.

16 And it shall be, if he says to you, “I will not go out from you,” because he loves you and your household, for it is good for him with you.

17 And you shall take the aul and put it in his ear and in the door, and he shall be a bondman to you forever; and also to your bondwoman you shall do likewise.

18 Do not regard it as a hardship in your sight when you release him free from you, for double the wages of a hired servant he has served you six years; and the LORD your God will bless you in all that you do.

19 Every firstborn male that is born of your beeve and of your flock you shall sanctify to the LORD your God; you shall not work with the firstborn of your ox nor shear the firstborn of your flock.

20 Before the LORD your God you shall eat it year by year in the place that the LORD will choose, you and your household.

21 If there is a blemish in it, lame or blind, any evil blemish, you shall not sacrifice it to the LORD your God.

22 Within your gates you shall eat it, the unclean and the clean together, like the roe and the hart.

23 Only his blood you shall not eat; upon the ground you shall pour it out like water.


## Chapter 16

1 Observe the month of Abib and keep the passover for the LORD your God, for in the month of Abib the LORD your God brought you out of Egypt by night.
