2 You shall sacrifice the passover offering to the LORD your God, sheep and cattle, in the place that the LORD will choose to cause his name to dwell there.

3 You shall not eat leavened bread with it; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread with it, bread of affliction, for in haste you went out from the land of Egypt, so that you may remember the day of your going out from the land of Egypt all the days of your life.

4 No leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory for seven days, and none of the flesh that you sacrifice in the evening on the first day shall remain until morning.

5 You must not sacrifice the passover offering in any one of your gates which the LORD your God gives you.

6 But to the place that the LORD your God will choose to establish his name there, there you shall sacrifice the passover offering in the evening, at sunset, the appointed time of your going out from Egypt.

7 And you shall boil and eat in the place that the LORD your God will choose; and you shall turn in the morning and go to your tents.

8 Six days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day a solemn assembly to the LORD your God; you shall do no work.

9 Seven weeks you shall count for yourself, from the time the sickle is put to the standing corn; you shall begin to count seven weeks.

10 And you shall make a solemn feast of weeks to the LORD your God, a tribute of a free-will offering from your hand, which you shall give according to how the LORD your God blesses you.

11 You shall rejoice before the LORD your God, you and your son and your daughter, your male servant and your female servant, the Levite who is within your gates, and the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow who are among you, in the place that the LORD your God will choose to establish His name there.

12 You shall remember that you were a bondman in Egypt, and you shall keep and do the appointed statutes.

13 You shall make for yourself the feast of booths seven days when you gather in from your threshing-floor and from your winepress.

14 You shall rejoice in your (solemn) feast day, you and your son and your daughter and your man-servant and your maid-servant and the Leviite and the stranger and the fatherless (child) and the widow who are within your gates.

15 Seven days you shall celebrate to the LORD your God in the place that the LORD will choose, because the LORD your God will bless you in all your produce and in all the work of your hands, and you shall be altogether joyful.

16 Three times in the year shall all your males appear before the face of the LORD your God in the place that he will choose: in the feast of unleavened bread, in the feast of weeks, and in the feast of booths. And none shall appear before the face of the LORD empty-handed.

17 Every man according to the gift of his hand, according to the blessing of the LORD your God which he has given you.

18 You shall appoint judges and officers in all your gates that the LORD your God gives you according to your tribes, and they shall judge the people with righteous judgment.

19 Do not pervert judgment, do not show partiality, and do not accept a bribe, for the bribe blinds the eyes of the wise and twists the words of the righteous.

20 Justice, justice shall you pursue, that you may live and possess the land which the LORD your God gives you.

21 You shall not plant for yourself a grove of any kind of tree near the altar of the LORD your God which you shall make for yourself.

22 You shall not set up for yourself a pillar, which the LORD your God hates.


## Chapter 17

1 You shall not sacrifice to the LORD your God an ox or a sheep that has a blemish or any evil thing, for that is an abomination to the LORD your God.

2 If among your cities, which the LORD your God gives you, a man or woman is found who does evil in the eyes of the LORD your God by transgressing his covenant,

3 And he went and served other gods and bowed down to them, or to the sun or to the moon or to all the host of heaven, which I did not command.

4 And it was declared to you and you heard, and you diligently inquired, and behold, it is true: the matter is confirmed, the abomination has been committed in Israel.

5 You shall bring out from your gates the man or the woman who has done this evil thing, the man or the woman, and you shall stone them with stones, and they shall die.

6 At the word of two witnesses or three witnesses shall the dead person be put to death; he shall not be put to death at the word of one witness.

7 The hand of the witnesses shall be upon him first to put him to death, and afterward the hand of all the people, and you shall burn away the evil from within you.

8 If any matter of judgment is too difficult for you, between blood and blood, between judgment and judgment, or between plague and plague, matters of controversy in your gates, then you shall rise up and go to the place that the LORD your God will choose.

9 You shall come to the priests, the Levites, and to the judge who will be in those days, and you shall inquire, and they shall declare to you the word of the judgment.

10 You shall act according to the word that they declare to you from the place that the LORD will choose, and you must take care to do according to all that they instruct you.

11 According to the law that they instruct you and the judgment that they declare to you, you shall do; do not turn aside from the word that they announce to you, neither to the right nor to the left.

12 And any person who acts presumptuously by not listening to the priest standing there to serve the LORD your God, or to the judge, that person shall die, and you shall remove the evil from Israel.

13 And all the people shall hear and fear, and they shall not dare again.

14 When you enter the land that the LORD your God is giving you, and you possess it and settle there, and you say, “I will set a king over myself, like all the nations that are around me,”

15 You shall set a king over you whom the LORD your God chooses, one from among your brothers; you shall not be able to put a foreign man over you, who is not your brother.

16 Only do not multiply horses for himself, and do not cause the people to return to Egypt to multiply horses, for the LORD has said to you, You shall not return again by this way.

17 He shall not multiply wives for himself, nor shall his heart turn aside, nor shall he greatly multiply silver and gold for himself.

18 When he sits on the throne of his kingdom, he shall write for himself a copy of this law on a scroll from before the priests, the Levites.

19 And it shall be with him, and he shall read it all the days of his life, so that he may learn to fear the LORD his God, to keep all the words of this law and the statutes that are these, to do them.

20 so that his heart does not become lifted up above his brothers, and so that he does not turn aside from the commandment to the right or to the left, in order that he may prolong his days in his kingdom, he and his sons, among Israel.


## Chapter 18

1 The Levitical priests, all the tribe of Levi, shall have no inheritance or portion with Israel; they shall eat the offerings by fire to the LORD, and of his inheritance.

2 And an inheritance shall not be his among his brothers; the LORD is his inheritance, as the LORD spoke to him.

3 This shall be the due of the priests from the people, from those offering the sacrifice, whether ox or sheep: they shall give to the priest the shoulder, the cheeks, and the maw.

4 The beginning of your wheat, your wine, and your anointed oil, and the beginning of the fleece of your flock, you shall give to him.

5 For in him has the LORD your God chosen from all your tribes to stand and serve in the name of the LORD—he and his sons—all the days.

6 When a Levite comes from any one of your gates out of all Israel, where he resides, and comes in the full desire of his soul to the place that the LORD will choose,

7 He shall serve in the name of the LORD his God like all his brother Levites who stand there before the LORD.

8 They shall eat a portion like a portion, only apart from what is sold by the fathers.

9 For you are coming into the land that the LORD your God gives you; you shall not learn to do according to the abominations of those nations.

10 There shall not be found among you anyone who burns his son or his daughter in the fire, a soothsayer, an enchanter, a diviner, a sorcerer, or a practitioner of magic.

11 and one who joins a charm, and one who asks of a familiar spirit, and a wizard who seeks the dead.

12 For the LORD abhors all who do these abominations, and because of these abominable things the LORD your God is driving them out before you.

13 You shall be blameless with the LORD your God.

14 For the nations that you are dispossessing listen to diviners and fortune-tellers, but you—no such thing has been allotted to you by the LORD your God.

15 A prophet from among you, one of your brothers, will the LORD your God raise up for you; to him you shall listen.

16 According to all that you asked from the LORD your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly, saying, I will not again hear the voice of the LORD my God, and this great fire I will not see again, lest I die.

17 And the LORD said to me, "They have done well what they have spoken."

18 I will raise up a prophet for them from among their brothers like you, and I will put my words in his mouth, and he will speak to them all that I command him.

19 And the man who does not listen to my words, which I will speak in my name, I myself will require it from him.

20 But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in my name that I did not command him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods—that prophet shall die.

21 And when you bethink yourselves in your heart, How shall we know the word which the LORD has not spoken?

22 When a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD and the word does not come about or happen, that is the word which the LORD did not speak; the prophet has spoken it presumptuously; you shall not fear him.


## Chapter 19

1 When the LORD your God cuts off the nations whose land the LORD your God is giving you, and you inherit them and dwell in their cities and in their houses,

2 Three cities you shall set apart for yourself in the midst of your land which the LORD your God gives you to possess.

3 You will prepare the way and triple the border of your land that the LORD your God gives you, so that every murderer may flee there.

4 This is the act of the manslayer who flees there and lives: the one who strikes his neighbor without knowledge, and he was not hating him heretofore.

5 When a man comes into the forest to cut wood, and his hand swings the ax to cut down the tree, and the iron head slips from the handle and strikes his fellow and he dies, then he shall flee to one of these cities and live.

6 Lest the blood-guilty avenger pursue the slayer in the heat of his heart and overtake him, because the way is long, and strike him mortally—though he is not deserving of death, for he was not an enemy to him heretofore.

7 Therefore I command you, saying, You shall set apart three towns.

8 And if the LORD your God enlarges your border, as he swore to your fathers, and gives you all the land which he promised to give to your fathers,

9 If you keep all this commandment to do it, which I command you today, to love the LORD your God and to walk in his ways all the days, then you shall add for yourself three more cities besides these three.

10 Innocent blood shall not be shed among your land which the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance, lest bloodguiltiness be upon you.

11 If any man hates his neighbour and lies in wait for him and rises against him and strikes him a mortal blow so that he dies, and he flees to one of these cities,

12 Then the elders of his city shall send and take him from there, and deliver him into the hand of the avenger of blood, and he shall die.

13 You shall not let your eye pity him, and you shall purge the blood of the innocent from Israel, and it will be good for you.

14 You shall not move your fellow's landmark, which the former ones set up in your inheritance, in the land that the LORD your God gives you to possess.

15 A single witness may not rise up against any person concerning any iniquity or any sin for any offence that one may commit; by the mouth of two witnesses or by the mouth of three witnesses a matter shall stand.

16 For if a cruel witness rises against a man to accuse him of rebellion.

17 Then both parties to the dispute shall stand before the LORD, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days.

18 The judges shall investigate thoroughly, and if the witness is a false witness, having testified falsely against another,

19 You shall do to him as he schemed to do to his brother, and you shall burn away the evil from your midst.

20 And those who remain shall hear and fear, and shall no longer continue to do such an evil act among you.

21 You shall not show pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.


## Chapter 20

1 When you go out to war against your enemies and see horse and chariot, a people more numerous than you, do not fear them, for the LORD your God is with you, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.

2 When you draw near to the battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the people.

3 Hear, Israel: you are coming near to battle against your enemies today; do not let your heart be faint, do not fear, and do not panic or be terrified before them.

4 For the LORD your God is the one who goes with you to fight for you against your enemies, to save you.

5 And the officers shall speak to the folk saying Who is the man that has built a new house and has not dedicated it let him go and return to his house lest he die in the battle and another man dedicate it

6 And who is the man who planted a vineyard and has not used its fruit? He shall go and return to his home, lest he die in the battle and another man use it.

7 And which man is there who has betrothed a woman and has not taken her? He shall go and return to his home, lest he die in the battle and another man take her.

8 Then the officers shall continue to speak to the people and say, Who is the man afraid and tender of heart? Let him go and return to his home, so that he does not melt the heart of his brothers like his own heart.

9 And it was, when the officers had finished speaking to the folk, that they appointed captains of the hosts at the head of the folk.

10 When you approach a city to fight against it, you shall proclaim to it peace.

11 And it shall be, if peace answers you, and opens to you, then all the people found in it shall be tribute to you and shall serve you.

12 But if she does not make peace with you, and she fights against you, then you shall besiege her.

13 And the LORD your God will give her into your hand, and you shall strike every male among them with the sword.

14 Only the women, the little ones, the cattle, and all that is in the city—its entire spoil—shall be your spoil, and you shall eat the spoil of your enemies, which the LORD your God gives you.

15 Thus you shall do to all the cities that are very far from you, which are not of the cities of these nations.

16 Only from the cities of these nations that the LORD your God gives you as a possession, you shall not keep alive any breath.

17 You shall utterly destroy them—the Hittites, the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites—as the LORD your God has commanded you.

18 so that they may not teach you to do in all the abominable things they have done for their gods, and you sin against the LORD your God.

19 When you shall besiege a city many days to fight against it, you shall not destroy its trees by wielding an ax against them, for you may eat from them, and you shall not cut them down, since man is not the tree of the field to come before you in the siege.

20 Only a tree that you know is not a food tree—you may destroy and cut down—so as to build a siege against the city that makes war with you, until you capture it.


## Chapter 21

1 If a slain person is found fallen in the field in the land that the LORD your God gives you to possess, and it is not known who struck him,

2 Then your elders and your judges shall go out and measure the distance to the cities round about the slain person.

3 And it shall be the town nearest to the slain man, and the elders of that town shall take a heifer from the herd that has not been worked with and that has not borne a yoke.

4 Then the elders of that city shall bring down the heifer to a perennial stream, which has not been tilled or sown, and there in the stream they shall break the heifer's neck.

5 Then the priests, the sons of Levi, shall come near, for the LORD your God has chosen them to minister to him and to bless in the name of the LORD, and by their word every controversy and every wound shall be settled.

6 And all the elders of that city, the ones near to the slain man, shall wash their hands over the calf whose neck was broken in the valley.

7 They answered and said, Our hands have not shed this blood, neither have our eyes seen it.

8 Atone for your people Israel whom you have redeemed, O LORD, and do not lay innocent blood among your people Israel; and the blood shall be atoned for them.

9 But you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from among you, for doing what is right in the eyes of the LORD.

10 When you go out to war against your enemies, and the LORD your God delivers them into your hand, and you take captives,

11 And you see among the captives a woman of beautiful form, and you desire her, and you take her to yourself as wife.

12 And you shall bring her into the midst of your home, and she shall shave the hair of her head and trim her nails.

13 And she shall remove the apparel of her captivity from upon her, and dwell in your house, and mourn her father and her mother a full month; afterward you may come to her and be her husband, and she shall be your wife.

14 But if you do not delight in her, you must send her away according to her own desire; you must not sell her for silver, nor deal harshly with her, having humbled her.

15 If a man has two wives, one loved and the other unloved, and both bear him sons, the firstborn son being the son of the unloved wife,

16 And it shall be on the day when he causes his sons to inherit what he has, that he is not able to treat as firstborn the son of the loved wife in place of the son of the unloved wife who is the firstborn.

17 For he shall acknowledge the firstborn, the son of the one unloved, by giving him a double portion of all that is found to him, for he is the beginning of his strength; to him belongs the right of the birthright.

18 If there is a man whose son is rebellious and defiant, not heeding the voice of his father and the voice of his mother, and they discipline him but he does not listen to them,

19 And his father and mother shall seize him and bring him out to the elders of his city and to the gate of his place.

20 They shall say to the elders of their city, Our son is rebellious and stubborn; he does not listen to our voice; he is a glutton and a drunkard.

21 And all the men of his city shall stone him with stones, so that he dies, and you shall burn away the evil from your midst, and all Israel shall hear and fear.

22 And if a man has a sentence of death upon him for a sin and he is put to death, you shall hang him on a tree.

23 You shall not let the body of him who is hanged remain on the tree overnight, but you shall surely bury him the same day, for a hanged man is a curse of God; you shall not defile the land that the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance.


## Chapter 22

1 You shall not see your brother's bull or his small cattle driven away and hide yourself from them; you shall surely return them to your brother.

2 But if your brother is not near you and you do not know him, then gather it inside your house, and it shall be with you until your brother seeks it, and then you shall return it to him.

3 You shall do the same for (his) ass, and you shall do the same for his clothing, and you shall do the same for any lost thing of your brother’s that is lost from him and you have found; you must not be able to hide yourself.

4 You shall not see another's ass or his ox fallen along the way and ignore them; you shall surely help raise them up with him.

5 There shall not be a man's weapon upon a woman, and a man shall not put on a woman's garment, for whoever does these things is an abomination to the LORD your God.

6 If a bird's nest happens before you along the way, in any tree or on the ground, with young ones or eggs, and the mother sitting on the young or on the eggs, you shall not take the mother with the children.

7 Send away the dam, but the children you shall take for yourself, so that it may go well with you and you may prolong your days.

8 When you build a new house, you shall make a battlement for your roof; otherwise you will bring blood-guiltiness upon your house if the one who falls should fall from it.

9 You shall not sow your vineyard with mingled seed, lest the fullness of the seed that is sown and the increase of the vineyard be forfeited.

10 You shall not plow with an ox and with an ass together.

11 You shall not wear a garment of divers sorts, linen and woolen, together.

12 Make for yourself fringes on the four corners of your covering with which you cover yourself.

13 When a man takes a wife and goes in to her and hates her,

14 and invents malicious deeds against her and brings a bad name upon her, saying, “I took this woman and drew near to her, but I did not find signs of virginity in her.”

15 Then the father of the young woman and her mother shall take her, and bring out the tokens of the young woman's virginity to the elders of the city at the gate.

16 And the father of the young woman said to the elders, "My daughter I gave to this man as wife, but he hated her."

17 And behold, he has invented acts of words, saying, “I did not find your daughter having virginity, but these are the virginities of my daughter”; and they shall spread the garment before the elders of the city.

18 And the elders of that city shall admonish that man.

19 They shall punish him with a hundred silver coins and give them to the damsel's father, because he has defamed the name of an Israelite virgin; and she shall remain his wife; he shall not be permitted to divorce her all the days of his life.

20 But if this matter was true, no tokens of virginity were found in her.

21 And they shall bring forth the damsel to the door of her father's house, and the men of her city shall stone her with stones, and she shall die, because she has wrought folly in Israel, to play the harlot in her father's house; so shalt thou put away the evil from thy midst.

22 If a man is found lying with a woman married to a husband, then both the man who lay with the woman and the woman shall die, and you shall burn out the evil from Israel.

23 If a virgin is engaged to a man, and another man meets her in the city and lies with her,

24 You shall bring the two of them out to the gate of that city and stone them with stones, and they shall die: the young woman because she did not cry out in the city, and the man because he violated his neighbour’s wife; so you shall purge the evil from among you.

25 But if in the field a man meets a betrothed young woman and seizes her and lies with her, then only the man who lay with her shall die.

26 But to the young woman you shall do nothing; the young woman has committed no sin worthy of death, for this matter is just as when a man rises against his neighbour and slays him.

27 For in the field he found her; the betrothed woman cried out, but there was no one to save her.

28 If a man is found with a virgin who is not betrothed, and he seizes her and lies with her, and they are discovered,

29 The man who lies with her shall pay to the father fifty silver coins, and she shall be his wife because he has humbled her; he cannot divorce her all his days.

30 No man shall take his father’s wife, nor uncover his father’s skirt.


## Chapter 23

1 A wounded man or one whose privy member is cut off shall not enter the assembly of the LORD.

2 A bastard shall not enter into the assembly of the LORD; even to the tenth generation shall none of his enter into the assembly of the LORD.

3 No Ammonite or Moabite shall enter the assembly of the LORD; even to the tenth generation, none of them shall enter the assembly of the LORD forever.

4 Because they did not meet you with bread and with water on the journey when you went out from Egypt, and because they hired against you Balaam son of Beor from Pethor of Aram Naharaim to curse you.

5 But the LORD your God was not willing to listen to Balaam, and the LORD your God turned the curse into a blessing for you, because the LORD your God loves you.

6 You shall not seek their peace nor their prosperity all your days forever.

7 You shall not abhor an Edomite, for he is your brother; you shall not abhor an Egyptian, because you were an alien in his land.

8 Children who will be born to them, a third generation, shall enter the assembly of the LORD.

9 When you go out as an army against your enemies, you must keep yourselves from every evil matter.

10 If any man among you is unclean by reason of a nocturnal occurrence, he shall go outside the camp; he shall not enter within the camp.

11 And when evening comes, he shall wash in water, and at the going down of the sun he may come into the midst of the camp.

12 You shall have a designated place outside the camp, and you shall go out there.

13 And you shall have a nail upon your belt, and when you sit abroad, you shall dig with it and turn back and cover that which cometh from you.

14 For the LORD your God walks in the midst of your camp to deliver you and to give your enemies before you, and your camp must be holy, that he may not see in you a nakedness of anything and turn away from behind you.

15 You shall not deliver to his lord the bondman who has escaped to you from his master.

16 He shall dwell among you, in the place that the LORD will choose, within one of your gates, where it is good for him; you shall not oppress him.

17 There shall be no prostitute among the daughters of Israel, and there shall be no male prostitute among the sons of Israel.

18 You shall not bring the hire of a prostitute or the price of a dog into the house of the LORD your God for any vow, for both are an abomination to the LORD your God.

19 You shall not lend to your brother usury of money, usury of food, usury of anything that is lent.

20 You shall lend to the foreigner, but to your brother you shall not lend; so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hand in the land that you are entering to possess.

21 If you vow a vow to the LORD your God, you shall not delay to fulfill it, for the LORD your God will surely require it of you, and it will be a sin against you.

22 But if you refrain from vowing, there will be no fault in you.

23 You shall guard the thing that has gone out from your lips and shall do as you have freely vowed to the LORD your God, the word that you have spoken in your mouth.

24 When you enter your neighbour’s vineyard, you may eat grapes to your heart’s desire, but you must not put any in your vessel.

25 When you come into your fellow’s standing corn, you may pluck the ears with your hand, but you shall not wave a sickle over your fellow’s standing corn.


## Chapter 24

1 If a man takes a wife and marries her, and it happens that she does not find favour in his eyes because he has found in her a matter of nakedness, then he shall write her a bill of divorce and put it in her hand and send her away from his house.

2 and she shall go out from his house and walk and be for another man.

3 And if the latter man hates her, he writes her a bill of divorce and puts it in her hand and sends her from his house, or if the latter man who took her as wife dies,

4 The first husband who divorced her may not return to take her again as his wife after she has been defiled, for that is an abomination before the LORD, and you shall not cause the land that the LORD your God gives you as an inheritance to sin.

5 When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out to the warfare nor be charged with any matter, being free for his household one year, and shall cheer his wife whom he has taken.

6 You shall not take a mill or an upper millstone as a pledge, for it is a person’s life.

7 If a man is found stealing a person among his brothers from the children of Israel and deals falsely with him or sells him, then that thief shall die, and you shall purge the evil from among you.

8 Take care concerning the plague of leprosy, to observe diligently and to do according to all that the priests the Levites shall instruct you; as I have commanded them, so you shall observe to do.

9 Remember what the LORD your God did to Miriam on the journey when you went out from Egypt.

10 If you lend your fellow any kind of debt, you shall not enter his house to take his pledge.

11 You shall stand outside, and the man to whom you are indebted shall bring the pledge out to you.

12 But if a certain man is poor, you shall not lie down upon his pledge.

13 You shall surely return the pledge to him when the sun sets, and he will lie down in his garment and bless you, and for you it will be righteousness before the LORD your God.

14 You shall not oppress a poor and needy brother or the alien who is in your land within your gates.

15 On the day you shall give his wages, and the sun shall not go down on it, for he is poor, and on it he sets his heart; lest he cry against you to the LORD, and it become a sin in you.

16 Fathers must not be put to death for children, and children must not be put to death for fathers; a man must be put to death for his own offence.

17 Do not pervert the judgment of an alien, orphan, and do not take a widow's garment in pledge.

18 You shall remember that you were a bondman in Egypt and the LORD your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this act.

19 When you reap the harvest of your field and forget a sheaf in the field, you shall not return to take it; it shall be for the sojourner, for the orphan, and for the widow, so that the LORD your God may bless you in all the work of your hands.

20 When you beat your olive, do not glean after you; it shall be for the alien, the orphan, and the widow.

21 When you gather the grapes of your vineyard, do not glean after you; it shall be for the alien, the orphan, and the widow.

22 You shall remember that you were a bondman in the land of Egypt; therefore I command you to do this act.


## Chapter 25

1 When there is a controversy between people and they come to judgment, and the judges decide between them, justifying the innocent and condemning the guilty.

2 And if the offender is the guilty one, then the judge shall lay him down and strike him in his presence, according to the measure of his wickedness, a numbered punishment.

3 He shall be beaten with forty stripes; he must not exceed, lest he continue to beat him beyond these with a severe beating, and your brother be humbled before your eyes.

4 You shall not muzzle an ox when it is treading out the grain.

5 When brothers dwell together and one of them dies and has no son, the wife of the dead shall not be married outside to another man; her husband's brother shall come to her, take her as his wife, and perform the duty of a husband's brother.

6 And it shall be, the firstborn that you bear shall stand in the name of his brother who died, and his name shall not be blotted out from Israel.

7 But if the man does not desire to take his brother's wife, then his brother's wife shall go up to the gate before the elders and say, "My husband's brother refuses to raise up a name for his brother in Israel; he is not willing to perform my duty as brother-in-law."

8 And the elders of his town shall summon him and speak to him; and he shall stand and say, I did not desire to take her.

9 Then his brother's wife shall come near to him before the elders, and remove his shoe from upon his foot, and spit in his face, and answer and say, "Thus shall it be done to the man who will not build the house of his brother."

10 and his name will be called in Israel, the house of him whose sandal was removed.

11 When men strive together, one against another, and the wife of one comes near to rescue her husband from the hand of the one striking him, and she reaches out her hand and seizes his private parts,

12 Cut off her hand; do not let your eye pity her.

13 You shall not have in your bag a large and a small stone.

14 You shall not have in your house an ephah and an ephah, a large and a small.

15 A just stone and just shall be yours; a complete ephah and just shall be yours; so that your days may be prolonged upon the land which the LORD your God gives you.

16 For the LORD your God detests every abominable thing, all who do iniquity.

17 Remember what Amalek did to you along the journey when you went out from Egypt.

18 who met you along the way and attacked all who were weary behind you, though you were faint and exhausted, and did not fear God.

19 When the LORD your God gives you rest from all your enemies around, in the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance to possess, you shall blot out the memory of Amalek from under heaven; you must not forget.


## Chapter 26

1 When you come to the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance, and you possess it and dwell in it,

2 You shall take from the beginning of all the fruit of the ground which the LORD your God gives you, and put it in a basket, and go to the place which the LORD your God chooses to make his name dwell there.

3 You shall come to the priest who is in those days and say to him, I declare today to the LORD your God that I have come into the land which the LORD swore to our forefathers to give us.

4 And the priest shall take the basket from your hand and set it down before the altar of the LORD your God.

5 You shall answer and say before the LORD your God: "A wandering Aramean was my father, and he went down to Egypt and sojourned there, few in number, and became there a great, mighty, and numerous people."

6 The Egyptians treated us harshly and oppressed us, and laid upon us hard service.

7 We cried out to the LORD, the God of our fathers, and the LORD heard our voice and saw our affliction, our toil, and our oppression.

8 The LORD brought us out of Egypt with a strong hand, and with an outstretched arm, and with great dread, and with signs and with wonders.

9 And he brought us to this place and gave us this land, a land flowing with milk and honey.

10 And now, behold, I have brought the beginning of the fruit of the ground which you gave me, O the LORD; and I have set it before the LORD your God, and you shall prostrate yourselves before the LORD your God.

11 You shall rejoice in all the good that the LORD your God has given you, your household, the Levite, and the sojourner within your midst.

12 When you have finished tithing all the tithe of your produce in the third year, the year of the tithe, you shall give it to the Levite, to the sojourner, to the orphan, and to the widow, and they shall eat within your gates and be satisfied.

13 And you shall say before the LORD your God: I have removed the consecrated thing from the house and also have given it to the Levite and to the stranger to the orphan and to the widow according to all your commandments which you commanded me I have not transgressed any of your commandments nor have I forgotten.

14 I did not eat from it in affliction, nor did I consume any of it while unclean, nor did I give any of it to the dead; I listened to the voice of the LORD my God, I did according to all that You commanded me.

15 Look down from your holy dwelling place, from heaven, and bless your people Israel and the land that you gave us, as you swore to our ancestors, a land flowing with milk and honey.

16 This day the LORD your God commands you to do these statutes and these judgments, and you shall keep and do them with all your heart and with all your soul.

17 Today you have declared the LORD to be your God, to walk in His ways, to keep His statutes, His commandments, and His judgments, and to obey His voice.

18 Today the LORD has declared you to be his special people, as he promised you, and to keep all his commandments.

19 and to set you on high above all the nations he has made, for praise and for fame and for glory, and that you may be a people holy to the LORD your God, as he has spoken.


## Chapter 27

1 Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the folk, saying, Keep the whole commandment which I am commanding you today.

2 And it shall be on the day when you cross over the Jordan into the land that the LORD your God gives you, you shall set up for yourself great stones and plaster them with lime.

3 And you shall write on them the whole of the words of this law, when you cross over, so that you may enter the land which the LORD your God gives you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has spoken to you.

4 And it shall be when you cross the Jordan, you shall set up these stones that I command you this day on Mount Ebal, and you shall coat them with lime.

5 And you shall build there an altar to the LORD your God, an altar of stones; you shall not wave iron upon them.

6 You shall build the altar of the LORD your God of whole stones, and you shall offer on it burnt offerings to the LORD your God.

7 You shall sacrifice peace offerings and eat them there, and you shall rejoice before the LORD your God.

8 You shall write upon the stones all the words of this law very plainly.

9 Moses and the priests, the Levites, spoke to all Israel, saying: Be silent and listen, Israel: today you have become a people to the LORD your God.

10 You shall hear aloud the voice of the LORD your God and do the commandments and the statutes which I command you today.

11 Moses charged the folk on that day, saying:

12 These shall stand to bless the folk upon Mount Gerizim when you cross the Jordan: Simeon and Levi and Judah and Issachar and Joseph and Benjamin.

13 And these shall stand for the curse on Mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad, and Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.

14 And the Levites answered, and said to every man of Israel with a loud voice.

15 Cursed is the man who makes a carved image or a molten image, an abomination to the LORD, the work of a craftsman, and sets it up in secret; and all the people shall answer and say, Amen.

16 Cursed is the one who dishonors his father and his mother, and all the people shall say, Amen.

17 Cursed is the one who moves his neighbor’s boundary; and all the people shall say, Amen.

18 Cursed is the one who misleads a blind person along the way, and all the people shall say, Amen.

19 Cursed is the tribe that perverts the judgment of the sojourner, the fatherless child, and the widow; and all the people shall say, Amen.

20 Cursed be he who lies with the wife of his father, for he has uncovered the skirt of his father; and all the people shall say, Amen.

21 Cursed be whoever lies with any beast; and all the people shall say, Amen.

22 Cursed is he who lies with his sister, the daughter of his father or daughter of his mother, and all the people shall say, Amen.

23 Cursed be he who lies with his daughter-in-law; and all the people shall say, Amen.

24 Cursed is the one who strikes his fellow in secret, and all the people shall say, Amen.

25 Cursed be he that takes a bribe to slay the life of an innocent: and all the people shall say, Amen.

26 Cursed be anyone who does not uphold the words of this law to carry them out, and let all the people say, Amen.


## Chapter 28

1 And it shall be, if you diligently obey the voice of the LORD your God, to keep all the commandments that I command you today, then the LORD your God will set you on high above all the nations of the earth.

2 All these blessings will come upon you and overtake you if you obey the voice of the LORD your God.

3 You are blessed in the city and you are blessed in the country.

4 Blessed be the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground and the fruit of your cattle, the increase of your kine and your flocks.

5 Blessed be your basket and your kneading trough.

6 Blessed are you when you come in, and blessed are you when you go out.

7 May the LORD deliver your enemies who rise against you, struck down before you; by one way they shall come out against you, but by seven ways they shall flee before you.

8 May the LORD command the blessing upon your barns and upon all the sending forth of your hand, and may the LORD your God bless you in the land which the LORD your God gives you.

9 The LORD will establish you as a holy people to himself, as he swore to you, if you keep the commandments of the LORD your God and walk in his ways.

10 And all the peoples of the earth will see that the name of the LORD is called upon you, and they will fear you.

11 And the LORD will make abundant for you good things, in the fruit of your womb, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your land, upon the ground that the LORD swore to your ancestors to give you.

12 The LORD will open for you His good storehouse, the heavens, to give rain to your land in its due season and to bless all the work of your hands, and you will lend to many nations, but you will not borrow.

13 The LORD will make you the head and not the tail, and you will be only above and not below, if you listen to the commandments of the LORD your God, which I am commanding you today, to keep and to do.

14 Do not turn aside from all the things which I command you today, to the right or to the left, to go after other gods to serve them.

15 And it shall come to pass if you do not listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep and to do all his commandments and his statutes which I command you today, that all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you.

16 Cursed are you in the city and cursed are you in the country.

17 Cursed be your basket and your kneading trough.

18 Cursed be the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, the increase of your kine and the flock of your sheep.

19 Cursed are you when you come in, and cursed are you when you go out.

20 The LORD will send upon you the curse, the discomfiture, and the rebuke in all the work of your hand that you undertake, until you are utterly destroyed and until you perish quickly, because of the wickedness of your doings, for you have forsaken me.

21 May the LORD attach to you the pestilence until He has finished you off from upon the land, to which you are coming in order to possess it.

22 The LORD will strike you with consumption, with burning ague, with inflammation, with extreme burning, with sword, with blasting, and with yellow, and they shall pursue you until you perish.

23 Your heavens above you will be copper, and the land beneath you will be iron.

24 May the LORD give the rain of your land as dust and ashes; from the heavens it shall descend upon you until you are destroyed.

25 May the LORD deliver you defeated before your enemies; by one way you shall go out against them, but by seven ways you shall flee before them, and you shall become a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

26 And your dead body will become food for every bird of the air and for the beasts of the earth, and there will be no one to frighten them away.

27 The LORD will strike you with the boils of Egypt and with scabs and with itch, which you cannot heal.

28 The LORD will strike you with madness, blindness, and confusion of heart.

29 You will grope at midday as the blind grope in darkness, and you will not prosper in your ways; you will only be oppressed and plundered all the days, and there will be no one to save.

30 You shall betroth a wife, and another man shall lie with her; you shall build a house, but you shall not dwell in it; you shall plant a vineyard, but you shall not use its fruit.

31 Your bull is slaughtered before your eyes, yet you do not eat from it; your donkey is stolen from before you, yet it does not return to you; your sheep are given to your enemies, and there is no deliverer for you.

32 Your sons and daughters given to another people, and your eyes see, and they are consumed for them all the day; and there is no power in your hand.

33 The fruit of your land and all your labor shall be eaten by a people you do not know, and you shall only be oppressed and crushed all your days.

34 And you shall be driven mad by the sight of your eyes which you will see.

35 The LORD will strike you with a malignant boil on your knees and on your thighs, that cannot be healed, from the sole of your foot to the crown of your head.

36 The LORD will lead you and the king whom you will set over you to a nation that you and your fathers have not known, and there you will serve other gods, wood and stone.

37 You will become an astonishment, a byword, and a taunt, in all the nations where the LORD will drive you.

38 Much seed you will bring out to the field, but little you will gather, for the locust will consume it.

39 You shall plant vines and tend them, but you shall not drink wine nor gather it, for the worm shall eat it.

40 Olive trees shall be yours in all your borders, but you shall not anoint yourself with the oil, for your olive trees shall yield fruit.

41 You will bear sons and daughters, but they will not remain yours, for they will go into captivity.

42 All your trees and the fruit of your land the locust shall inherit.

43 The sojourner who is among you shall rise above you higher and higher, while you shall sink lower and lower.

44 He shall lend to you, but you shall not lend to him; he shall be the head, and you shall be the tail.

45 And all the curses shall come upon you and pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed, because you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God, to keep his commandments and his statutes which he commanded you.

46 And they shall be in you for a sign and a wonder, and in your seed forever.

47 Because you did not serve the LORD your God with joy and with gladness of heart, from the abundance of all.

48 You shall serve your enemies whom Jehovah the Lord will send against you, in famine and in thirst, in nakedness and in want of all things; and he will put an iron yoke upon your neck until he has destroyed you.

49 The LORD will raise up against you a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, like the eagle swoops, a nation whose language you do not understand.

50 a nation impudent of face, which will not regard the person of an elder, nor have pity for the young.

51 And you shall eat the fruit of your cattle and the fruit of your land until you are destroyed, for he will leave you neither wheat nor wine nor anointed oil, nor the increase of your oxen nor the flocks of your sheep, until they have caused you to perish.

52 And he shall distress you in every city and in every land, until your high and fortified walls in which you trust come down, and he shall distress you in every city and in all your land which the LORD your God has given you.

53 You shall eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your sons and your daughters whom the LORD your God gives you, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy will straiten you.

54 The man who is delicate among you and very sensitive will glare with his eye at his brother, at the wife of his bosom, and at the remnant of his children whom he leaves.

55 from giving any one of them a portion of the flesh of his children to eat, leaving him no remnant, because of the siege and the distress with which your enemy will oppress you at all your gates.

56 The delicate and tender woman among you, who has never ventured to set the sole of her foot upon the ground because of her delicacy and tenderness, will look with affliction upon the husband of her bosom and upon her son and upon her daughter.

57 And in the young one that comes out from between her legs, and in her children whom she bears, for she will eat them in want of everything in secret, in siege and in distress, which your enemy will bring on you in your gates.

58 If you do not carefully observe to do all the words of this law written in this scroll, to fear the great and awesome name, the LORD your God,

59 And the LORD will wonderfully increase your plagues and the plagues of your offspring—great and sure plagues, and grievous and sure diseases.

60 He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt that you dreaded because of their presence, and they will cling to you.

61 Even every disease and every plague which is not written in this book of the law, the LORD will bring upon you until you are destroyed.

62 And you shall be left few in number, compared to what you were, like the stars of the heavens in multitude, because you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God.

63 And it will be, as Jehovah rejoiced over you to do good to you and to multiply you, so will Jehovah rejoice over you to destroy you and to annihilate you, and you will be plucked from upon the land, which you are entering there to possess.

64 And the LORD will scatter you among all peoples, from one end of the earth to the other end of the earth, and there you will serve other gods, which you have not known, you and your fathers, wood and stone.

65 And among those nations you shall find no rest, nor shall there be a place of rest for the sole of your foot; and there the LORD will give you a trembling heart, failing of the eyes, and sorrow of soul.

66 Your life shall hang in suspense before you; you shall fear night and day, and you shall not believe in your life.

67 In the morning you will say, Who will give evening? and in the evening you will say, Who will give morning? because of the dread of your heart that you dread and because of the sight of your eyes that you see.

68 And the LORD will bring you back to Egypt in ships, by the way of which I said to you, “You shall never again see it,” and there you will be sold to your enemies as male and female slaves, and there will be no buyer.


## Chapter 29

1 These are the words of the covenant that the LORD commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, besides the covenant that he made with them in Horeb.

2 Moses called to all Israel and said to them, You have seen with your own eyes all that the LORD did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land.

3 the great temptations which your eyes have seen, the signs and those great miracles.

4 and the LORD has not given you a heart to understand, or eyes to see, or ears to hear, up to this day.

5 I led you forty years in the wilderness; your clothes did not wear out from upon you, and your shoe did not wear out from upon your leg.

6 You did not eat bread, and you did not drink wine or strong drink, so that you might know that I am the LORD your God.

7 You came to this place, and Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan came out against us to battle, and we struck them.

8 We took their land and gave it as an inheritance to the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh.

9 You shall keep the acts of this covenant and do them, so that you may succeed in all that you do.

10 You are standing today, all of you, before the LORD your God—your chiefs, your tribes, your elders, and your officers, every man of Israel—

11 Your little ones, your wives, the alien in your midst, and everyone from the one who cuts your wood to the one who draws your water.

12 to pass into the covenant of the LORD your God and into his curse which the LORD your God is making with you today

13 so that he may establish you today as a people for himself, and he will be to you as God, as he spoke to you and as he swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

14 Not with you alone do I make this covenant and this curse.

15 For the one who is here with us today standing before the LORD our God, and the one who is not here with us today.

16 For you know the manner in which we dwelled in the land of Egypt, and the manner in which we passed through the midst of the nations through which you passed.

17 And you saw their abominable filth and their idols, wood and stone, silver and gold which were with them.

18 Lest there be among you any man or woman, family or tribe whose heart turns away today from the LORD our God to go serve the gods of those nations; lest there be among you a root bearing poison and wormwood.

19 And when he hears the words of this curse, he will bless himself in his heart, saying, "I will have peace, even though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart"—so that the well-watered may sweep away the parched.

20 The LORD is not willing to forgive him, for then the anger and jealousy of the LORD will burn against that man, and all the curse written in this book will lie upon him, and the LORD will blot out his name from under the heavens.

21 And the LORD set him apart for adversity from all the tribes of Israel, according to all the curses of the covenant written in the book of this law.

22 And the latter generation, your children who will arise after you, and the foreigner who comes from a distant land, will say, when they see the plagues of that land and its diseases with which the LORD has made it sick.

23 Brimstone and salt-pit, a burning overthrew all her land; it shall not be sown nor cause to grow, nor shall any grass rise up in it, like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which the LORD overthrew in His anger and in His wrath.

24 And all the nations will say, "Why has the LORD done thus to this land? What is this great outburst of wrath?"

25 They will say, Because they abandoned the covenant of the LORD their fathers’ God, which he made with them when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.

26 They went and served other gods and bowed down to them, gods which they had not known and which I had not allotted to them.

27 The anger of the LORD burned against that land, to bring upon it every curse written in this book.

28 And the LORD cast them from upon their country in anger and in wrath and in great fury, and hurled them to another land, as at this day.

29 The secret things belong to the LORD our God, but the revealed things are for us and our children forever, to do all the words of this law.


## Chapter 30

1 And it shall be, when all these things come upon you, the blessing and the curse, which I have set before you, and you return to your heart among all the nations where the LORD your God has driven you,

2 And you shall return to the LORD your God and obey his voice according to all that I command you today, you and your children, with all your heart and with all your soul.

3 And the LORD your God will restore your captivity and have compassion on you, and He will return and gather you from all the peoples where the LORD your God has scattered you.

4 If you are driven to the edge of the heavens, from there the LORD your God will gather you and from there He will take you.

5 And the LORD your God will bring you to the land that your fathers inherited, and you will inherit it; and he will do good to you and multiply you more than your fathers.

6 And the LORD your God will circumcise your heart and the heart of your seed, to love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul, that you may live.

7 And the LORD your God will put all these curses on your enemies and on those who hate you, who have pursued you.

8 You shall return and listen to the voice of the LORD and do all the commandments that I command you today.

9 And the LORD your God will make you abound in all the work of your hands, in the fruit of your womb, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your land for good, for the LORD will again rejoice over you for good, as He rejoiced over your fathers.

10 If you obey the LORD your God by keeping his commandments and statutes written in this book of the law, when you return to the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.

11 For this commandment that I am commanding you today is not too extraordinary for you, nor is it far away.

12 It is not in the heavens, saying, "Who will go up for us to the heavens and take it for us and make it heard to us, that we may do it?"

13 But it is not beyond the sea, saying, Who will cross over for us to the other side of the sea and bring it to us, that we may hear it and do it?

14 For the matter is very near to you, in your mouth and in your heart to do it.

15 See, I have set before you today life and good, and death and evil.

16 which I command you today to love the LORD your God, to walk in his ways, and to keep his commandments, his statutes, and his judgments, so that you may live and multiply, and the LORD your God may bless you in the land to which you are coming to possess it.

17 But if your heart turns away and you will not listen, and you are drawn away and bow down to other gods and serve them,

18 I have declared to you today that you will surely perish; you shall not prolong your days upon the land, which you are passing over the Jordan to enter and possess.

19 I call the heavens and the earth this day to witness against you: I have set before you life and death, the blessing and the curse; therefore choose life, that you and your seed may live.

20 To love the LORD your God, to listen to his voice, and to cling to him, for he is your life and the length of your days, to dwell upon the land that the LORD swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them.


## Chapter 31

1 Moses went and spoke these words to all Israel.

2 And he said to them I am a hundred and twenty years old today I am not able any longer to go out and come in and the LORD said to me you shall not cross this Jordan

3 The LORD your God is the one who will pass before you; he will destroy these nations before you, and you will dispossess them. Joshua is the one who will pass before you, as the LORD has spoken.

4 And the LORD did to them as he did to Sihon and to Og, the kings of the Amorites, and to their land: he destroyed them.

5 And the LORD delivered them into your hands, and you were to do to them according to all the commandment which I commanded you.

6 Be strong and courageous, do not fear nor be in dread because of them, for the LORD your God is the one who goes with you; he will not fail you nor forsake you.

7 Moses called to Joshua and said to him before the eyes of all Israel, Be strong and courageous, for you will bring this people to the land that the LORD swore to their fathers to give them, and you will cause them to inherit it.

8 The LORD is the one who goes before you; he will be with you; he will neither let go of you nor forsake you; do not fear, and do not be dismayed.

9 Moses wrote this law and gave it to the priests, the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and to all the elders of Israel.

10 Moses commanded them, saying, after the end of seven years, at the appointed time of the year of release, during the feast of booths.

11 When all Israel comes to appear before the LORD your God in the place that he will choose, you shall read this law before all Israel in their hearing.

12 Gather the people, the men and the women and the little children and the sojourner who is in your gates, so that they may hear and so that they may learn and fear the LORD your God, and keep all the words of this law.

13 Their children who do not know shall hear and learn to fear the LORD your God all the days that you live on the land which you are crossing over the Jordan to possess.

14 The LORD said to Moses, "Indeed, your days are drawing near to die. Summon Joshua and present yourselves at the tent of meeting, so that I may command him." And Moses went, and Joshua, and they presented themselves at the tent of meeting.

15 And the LORD appeared in the covering by a pillar of cloud, and the pillar of cloud stood at the entrance of the covering.

16 And the LORD said to Moses, Behold, you shall lie down with your fathers; and this people shall rise up and go a whoring after the strange gods of the land, into the midst of which they go, and they shall forsake me and break my covenant which I have made with them.

17 and my anger will burn against him that day and I will forsake them and hide my face from them and he will be for devouring and many evils and troubles will find him and he will say that day is it not because my God is not in my midst that these evils have found me

18 But I will hide My face in that day because of all the evil he has done, for he has turned to other gods.

19 Now therefore write down for yourselves this song and teach it to the children of Israel, put it in their mouth, so that this song may be a witness for me against the children of Israel.

20 For I will bring him to the land that I swore to his fathers, a land flowing with milk and honey; and he will eat and be satisfied and grow fat, then turn to other gods and serve them, and spurn me and break my covenant.

21 And it shall come to pass, when he finds among them many adversities and distresses, that this song shall answer before him as a witness, because it shall not be forgotten from the mouth of his seed; for I know the imagination which he is forming today, before I bring him into the land which I swore.

22 Moses wrote this song on that day and taught it to the children of Israel.

23 And he commanded Joshua son of Nun and said, Be strong and courageous, for you shall bring the children of Israel to the land which I swore to them, and I will be with you.

24 And it came to pass when Moses finished writing the words of this law in a scroll completely.

25 And Moses commanded the Levites, bearers of the ark of the covenant of the LORD, saying:

26 to take this book of the law and set it beside the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God and it shall be there among you for a witness.

27 For I know your rebellion and your stiff neck: behold, while I am still alive with you today, you have been rebellious against the LORD; how much more after my death.

28 Gather to me all your tribal elders and officials, that I may speak these words in their hearing and call heaven and earth to witness against them.

29 For I know that after my death you will surely act corruptly and turn aside from the way that I have commanded you, and disaster will befall you in the latter days because you will do evil in the sight of the LORD to provoke Him to anger by the work of your hands.

30 Moses spoke in the ears of the whole congregation of Israel all the words of this song, until they were complete.


## Chapter 32

1 Hear, O heavens, and I will speak; and let the earth hear the words of my mouth.

2 May my teaching drop as the rain, my speech distill as the dew, like small rain upon the tender grass, and like showers upon the herb.

3 For I will call upon the name of the LORD: give greatness to our God.

4 The Rock—perfect is His work, for all His ways are justice; a God of faithfulness with no iniquity, righteous and upright is He.

5 They have acted corruptly toward Him; they are not His children, but their blemish; a crooked and perverse generation.

6 Will you repay such a thing to the LORD, O foolish and unwise people? Is He not your father who acquired you? He made you and established you.

7 Remember the days of old, consider the years of many generations; ask your father, and he will show you; your elders, and they will tell you.

8 When the Most High apportioned the Gentiles, when he separated the sons of man, he fixed the borders of the nations according to the number of the sons of Israel.

9 For the portion of the LORD is his people, Jacob the portion of his inheritance.

10 He found him in the wilderness, in a desolate, howling wasteland; He encompassed him, He cared for him, He guarded him as the apple of His eye.

11 Like an eagle that stirs up its nest, hovering over its young, spreading its wings, taking them, carrying them on its wings.

12 The LORD alone will lead him, and there is no strange god with him.

13 He made him ride on the land, and he ate the produce of the field, and He fed him honey from the rocky crag, and oil from the flinty rock.

14 Butter of beeve and milk with the fat of rams and rams, sons of Bashan, and he-goats with the fat of kidneys of wheat, and blood of the grape you shall drink red wine.

15 Jeshurun grew fat and kicked; you grew fat, thick, and gross—you forsook God who made you, and scorned the Rock of his deliverance.

16 They will make him jealous with foreign things; they will provoke him to anger with abominable things.

17 They sacrifice to devils, not to God, to gods they do not know, new ones who came recently; your fathers did not fear them.

18 You forsake the Rock who begot you, and forget the God who formed you.

19 The LORD saw and spurned them because of the anger of his sons and his daughters.

20 I will hide my face from them; I will see what their end will be, for they are a generation of perversity, children in whom is no faithfulness.

21 They have made me jealous with no-god; they have angered me with their vanities. So I will make them jealous with a no-people; with a base nation I will anger them.

22 For a burning fire is in my anger, kindled even to the grave beneath, and it consumes the earth and its fruit, and sets ablaze the foundations of the mountains.

23 I will gather adversity against them; my arrows I will spend upon them.

24 They shall be wasted with hunger, devoured by burning heat and bitter destruction; I will send the teeth of beasts upon them, with the venom of creatures of the dust.

25 Abroad the sword shall bereave, and in the inner chambers terror; both young man and virgin, infant with gray-haired man shall fall.

26 I said, "I will make them perish; I will blot out their memory from among mankind."

27 If not for the anger of the adversary I would have been cut off, lest his enemies misunderstand, lest they say, "Our hand is high, and the LORD has not done all this."

28 For the heathen is a people of lost counsel, and there is no wisdom in them.

29 If only they were wise, they would understand the latter end for them.

30 How can one pursue a thousand, and two put ten thousand to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, and the LORD delivered them up?

31 For not like our Rock is their rock, and our enemies are judges.

32 For their vine is from the vine of Sodom and from the blasted fields of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of gall; their clusters are clusters of bitterness.

33 The anger of their wine is poison, the poison of dragons, and the cruel venom of asps.

34 Is not wrath shut up with me, sealed in my storehouses?

35 Vengeance is mine, and recompense, when their foot shall slip; for the day of their destruction is near, and the time set for them hastens.

36 For the LORD will judge his people and upon his servants have compassion, for he sees that their hand is failed, neither is there any restrained or left.

37 And he will say, "Where are their gods, the rock in whom they trusted?"

38 whose sacrifices' fat they eat, whose drink offering wine they drink—let them rise up and help you, may protection be upon you.

39 See now that I, I am he, and there is no God with me; I kill and I make alive, I wound and I heal, and there is none from my hand that can deliver.

40 For I will raise my hand to heaven and swear, I live forever.

41 If I flash the brightness of my sword and my hand takes hold of judgment, I will repay vengeance to my adversaries and recompense those who hate me.

42 I will make my arrows drunk with blood, and my sword will devour flesh from the blood of the slain and captives, from the heads of the leaders of the enemy.

43 Sing out, O nations, his people, for he will avenge the blood of his servants, and render vengeance to his adversaries, and atone for his land, his people.

44 Moses came and spoke in the ears of the people all the words of this song, he and Hosea the son of Nun.

45 Moses finished speaking all these words to all Israel.

46 Set your hearts to all the words that I testify among you today, that you may command your children to keep and do all the words of this law.

47 For it is not an empty act from you, for it is your life, and by this word you will prolong days upon the land which you are crossing over the Jordan to possess.

48 And the LORD spoke to Moses on the selfsame day, saying:

49 Go up to the mountain of the Abarim, that mountain, Mount Nebo, which is in the land of Moab, opposite Jericho, and see the land of Canaan that I am giving to the children of Israel for a possession.

50 And you shall die on the mount that you are ascending, and be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people.

51 Because you rebelled against me among the children of Israel at the waters of Meribah Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin, because you did not sanctify me among the children of Israel.

52 For you shall see the land from afar, but you shall not come there, to the land which I give to the children of Israel.


## Chapter 33

1 And this is the blessing that Moses, the man of God, bestowed on the children of Israel before his death.

2 The LORD came from Sinai and dawned from Seir for them; He shone forth from Mount Paran and came from the myriads of holy ones; at His right hand was a fiery law for them.

3 Indeed, he loves the people; all his holy ones are in your power; they prostrate themselves at your feet; he accepts instruction from your words.

4 The law that Moses commanded us is an inheritance for the assembly of Jacob.

5 And there was a king in Jeshurun, when the chiefs of the people gathered, the tribes of Israel together.

6 Let Reuben live and not die, and let his men be numbered.

7 And this for Judah: he said, “Hear, O the LORD, the voice of Judah, and bring him to his people. May his hands be sufficient for him, and may you be a help from his adversaries.”

8 And to Levi he said: Let your Thummim and your Urim be for your faithful man, whom you tested at Massah, with whom you contended at the waters of Meribah.

9 who says to his father and his mother, "I have not seen him," and does not recognize his brothers or acknowledge his children, for they have kept your word and guard your covenant.

10 They shall teach Your judgments to Jacob and Your law to Israel; they shall place incense before Your face and a whole offering on Your altar.

11 Bless, O LORD, his substance, and accept the work of his hands; may those who rise against him be struck in the loins, and may his enemies not rise up.

12 To Benjamin he said, "Beloved of the LORD, he shall dwell in assurance; he shall be protected all the day, and between his shoulders he shall dwell."

13 And to Joseph he said, Blessed of the LORD be his land, for the precious fruit of the heavens and for the dew, and for the deep lying beneath.

14 And from the precious fruit of the sun’s increase, and from the precious fruit of the put forth of the months.

15 and with the finest of the ancient mountains, and with the precious things of the everlasting hills,

16 And from the precious fruit of the land and its fullness, and from the favor of the one who dwells in the bush, may it come upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the one set apart from his brothers.

17 The firstborn of his bull is his majesty, and the horns of a unicorn are his horns; with them he shall gore the peoples together to the ends of the earth; they are the ten thousands of Ephraim, and they are the thousands of Manasseh.

18 And to Zebulun he said, Rejoice, Zebulun, in your going out, and Issachar in your homes.

19 Nations will call the mountain there, they will sacrifice offerings of justice, for they will suck abundance of the seas and the uttermost parts of the sand.

20 And to Gad he said: Blessed is Gad; he expands his territory; like a lion he lies in wait, and he tears the arm and the crown of the head.

21 He saw the first part, that his portion was set apart; he came with the chiefs of the people; the justice of the LORD he did, and his judgments with Israel.

22 And to Dan he said, Dan is a young lion; he leaps from Bashan.

23 And to Naphtali he said: Naphtali is satisfied with favour, and full with the blessing of the LORD; west and south he shall possess.

24 And for Asher he said, "Blessed more than sons is Asher; let him be favored by his brothers, and let him dip his foot in oil."

25 Iron and brass are your shoes, and your strength is as your days.

26 There is no God like Jeshurun, who rides upon the heavens in your help and in his excellency upon the sky.

27 The habitation of God of old, and beneath the everlasting arms; he drove the enemy from before you, and said, Destroy.

28 Israel dwelt in assurance, alone, the fountain of Jacob, upon a land of wheat and wine; even his heavens dripped dew.

29 Blessed are you, Israel, who is like you, a people saved by the LORD, the shield of your help and the sword of your pride, and your enemies shall be subdued before you, and you shall tread upon their high places.


## Chapter 34

1 Moses went up from the Arabah of Moab to Mount Nebo, the top of Pisgah, which is opposite Jericho, and the LORD showed him all the land, as far as Gilead to Dan.

2 and all Naphtali, and the land of Ephraim and Manasseh, and all the land of Judah as far as the western sea

3 and the south country and the plain of the valley of Jericho, the city of palm trees, as far as Zoar.

4 The Lord said to him, This is the land that I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, To your seed I will give it; I have let you see it with your own eyes, but you shall not cross over there.

5 Moses the servant of the LORD died there in the land of Moab according to the word of the LORD.

6 And he buried him in the valley in the land of Moab, opposite Bethpeor, and no man has known his burial place to this day.

7 Moses was one hundred and twenty years old at his death, his eyes had not dimmed nor had his natural force abated.

8 The sons of Israel wept for Moses in the Arabah of Moab thirty days and the days of weeping in mourning for Moses were completed.

9 Jehoshua son of Nun was full of the spirit of wisdom because Moses had laid his hands on him, and the children of Israel listened to him and did as the LORD commanded Moses.

10 And there has not arisen a prophet since in Israel like Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face.

11 For all the signs and wonders that the LORD sent to do in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land.

12 and for all the mighty hand and for all the great terror that Moses did before the eyes of all Israel.


# JOSHUA

## Chapter 1

1 After the death of Moses the servant of the LORD, the LORD said to Joshua the son of Nun, servant of Moses, saying:

2 Moses my servant is dead, and now rise, pass over this Jordan, you and all this people, into the land which I am giving to them, to the children of Israel.

3 Every place where the sole of your foot treads shall be given to you, as I have spoken to Moses.

4 From the wilderness and this Lebanon even to the great river, the river Euphrates, all the land of the Hittites, and to the great sea, even the going down of the sun, shall be your border.

5 No man shall stand against you all the days of your life; as I was with Moses, so I will be with you. I will not leave you or forsake you.

6 Be strong and courageous, for you will cause this folk to inherit the land that I swore to their forefathers to give them.

7 Only be strong and very courageous to keep and do according to all the law that Moses my servant commanded you; do not turn from it to the right hand or to the left, so that you may have success in all that you do.

8 The book of this law shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to act according to all that is written in it; for then you will prosper in your way and then you will succeed.

9 Have I not commanded you: be strong and courageous? Do not tremble or be dismayed, for with you is the LORD your God wherever you go.

10 Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people, saying:

11 Pass through the midst of the camp and command the people, saying, Prepare for yourselves provisions, for within three days you are crossing the Jordan here to come and take possession of the land that the LORD your God is giving you to possess.

12 And to the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh, Joshua said, saying:

13 Remember the command that Moses, the servant of the LORD, gave you, saying, “The LORD your God gives you rest and will give you this land.”

14 Your wives, your little ones, and your cattle shall remain in the land that Moses gave you beyond the Jordan, but you shall cross over armed, all you warriors of the army, before your brothers, to help them.

15 until the LORD gives your brothers rest, as you, and they also inherit the land that the LORD your God is giving them, then you shall return to the land of your inheritance across the Jordan toward the sunrise, which Moses, the servant of the LORD, gave you.

16 And they answered Joshua, saying, All that you have commanded us we will do, and to all where you send us we will go.

17 According to all that we have heard unto Moses, so will we hear unto thee; only may the LORD thy God be with thee, as he was with Moses.

18 Every man who disobeys your mouth and does not heed your words in all that you command him shall be put to death; only be strong and courageous.


## Chapter 2

1 Jehoshua son of Nun sent from Shittim two men secretly to spy out the land and Jericho, and they went and came into the house of a woman who was a prostitute, and her name was Rahab, and they lay down there.

2 It was reported to the king of Jericho, saying, "Behold, men came here tonight from the children of Israel to search out the land."

3 The king of Jericho sent to Rahab, saying, "Bring out the men who came to you, who entered your house, for they came to search out the whole land."

4 The woman took the two men and hid them, and she said, "Indeed, the men came to me, and I did not know where they were from."

5 The gate was shut in the darkness and the men had gone out; I do not know where the men went; pursue them quickly, for you will overtake them.

6 And she brought them up to the roof and hid them among the stalks of flax that she had laid out on the roof.

7 And the men pursued them along the Jordan at the fords, and the gate was shut after the pursuers had gone out after them.

8 But they had not yet lain down when she went up to them on the roof.

9 And she said to the men, I know that the LORD has given the land to you, and that the fear of you has fallen upon us, and that all the inhabitants of the land have melted away before you.

10 For we have heard how the LORD dried up the waters of the Red Sea before you when you came out of Egypt, and what you did to the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Jordan, to Sihon and to Og, whom you utterly destroyed.
