11 We heard and our hearts melted; no courage rose again in any man before you, for the LORD your God is God in the heavens above and on the earth beneath.

12 Now therefore, swear now to me by the LORD, since I have dealt kindly with you, that you also will show kindness to the house of my father, and give me a true sign.

13 And you shall keep alive my father, and my mother, and my brothers, and my sisters, and all that belongs to them, and you shall deliver our persons from death.

14 The men said to her, Our lives for yours, if you do not reveal our business; and when the LORD gives us the land, we will show you kindness and truth.

15 They let her down by a cord through the window, for her house was in the wall, and she lived in the wall.

16 And she said to them, "Go to the hill country, lest the pursuers meet you, and hide yourselves there three days until the pursuers return, and afterward go on your way."

17 The men said to her, We are innocent of this oath which you have made us swear.

18 Look, we are coming into the land: tie the cord of scarlet thread in the window through which you let us down, and gather your father and your mother and your brothers and all your father’s household to your house.

19 And it shall be, whoever goes out from the doors of your house into the street, his blood shall be upon his head, and we shall be guiltless; but whoever is with you in the house, his blood shall be upon our heads, if a hand is laid upon him.

20 But if you report our business, then we will be blameless concerning your oath that you have made us swear.

21 She said, "According to your advice so it is," and she sent them away, and they went; and she tied the scarlet thread in the window.

22 They went and came to the hill country and stayed there three days until the pursuers returned, and the pursuers searched along every way but found nothing.

23 And the two men returned and came down from the mountain and passed on and came to Joshua son of Nun and reported to him all that had happened to them.

24 They said to Joshua, Because the LORD has given the whole land into our hand, and also all the inhabitants of the land have melted away before us.


## Chapter 3

1 Jehoshua rose early in the morning, and they set out from Shittim and came to the Jordan, he and all the children of Israel, and they lodged there before they passed over.

2 After three days the officers passed through the midst of the camp.

3 And they commanded the people, saying, When ye see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the Levite priests bearing it, then ye shall depart from your places and follow after it.

4 But at a distance of about two thousand cubits from you, you shall keep far from him, so that you may know the way by which you will go, for you have not passed along this way before.

5 Jehoshua said to the people, "Sanctify yourselves, for tomorrow the LORD will do wonders among you."

6 Jehoshua said to the priests, saying, Take up the ark of the covenant and pass on before the people, and they took up the ark of the covenant and went on before the people.

7 And the LORD said to Joshua, This day I will begin to magnify you in the sight of all Israel, that they may know that as I was with Moses, so I will be with you.

8 And you shall command the priests who bear the ark of the covenant, saying, when you come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, in the Jordan you shall stand.

9 Jehoshua said to the children of Israel, Come here and hear the words of the LORD your God.

10 And Joshua said, By this you shall know that the living God is among you, and He will drive out from before you the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Hivite, the Perizzite, the Girgashite, the Amorite, and the Jebusite.

11 Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the land is passing over before you into the Jordan.

12 And now take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel, one man, one man for each tribe.

13 And when the soles of the feet of the priests bearing the ark of the LORD, Lord of all the earth, rest in the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan shall be cut off, the waters coming down from above, and they shall stand in a single heap.

14 And it happened, when the people set out from their tents to pass over the Jordan, and the priests bearing the ark of the covenant were before the people.

15 When the bearers of the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests who carried the ark dipped into the water’s edge, the Jordan being full upon all its banks all the days of harvest.

16 The waters flowing downstream piled up in a single heap far from the city of Zarthan, while the waters flowing down to the Arabah, the Salt Sea, were completely cut off, and the people crossed opposite Jericho.

17 The priests who bore the ark of the covenant of the Lord stood on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan, and all Israel passed over on dry ground, until all the nation had finished crossing over the Jordan.


## Chapter 4

1 And it came to pass, when all the people had finished passing over the Jordan, that the LORD said to Joshua, saying:

2 Take for yourselves twelve men from the people, one man from each tribe.

3 And command them, saying, Take for yourselves from this place among the Jordan, from the place where the feet of the priests are standing, twelve stones, and carry them with you and set them down in the lodging place where you will lodge tonight.

4 Jehoshua called to the twelve men whom he had appointed from the children of Israel, one man, one man from each tribe.

5 Jehoshua said to them, Pass before the ark of the LORD your God into the midst of the Jordan, and let every man take up a stone upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel.

6 So that this may be a mark among you, when your children ask tomorrow, saying, What are these stones to you?

7 And you shall say to them, "The waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of the LORD when it crossed the Jordan; the waters of the Jordan were cut off, and these stones shall be a memorial to the children of Israel forever."

8 So the children of Israel did as Joshua commanded and took twelve stones from the midst of the Jordan as the LORD had spoken to Joshua according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel and carried them over with them to the lodging place and set them down there.

9 And twelve stones Joshua set up in the midst of the Jordan, under the place where the feet of the priests stood, bearing the ark of the covenant, and they are there unto this day.

10 And the priests bearing the ark stood in the midst of the Jordan until all the commandment which the LORD had commanded Joshua to speak to the people according to all that Moses had commanded Joshua was completely fulfilled and the people hurried and passed over.

11 And it came to pass, when all the people had completely crossed over, that the ark of the LORD crossed over, and the priests in front of the people.

12 The sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh crossed over, armed, before the sons of Israel, just as Moses had spoken to them.

13 About forty thousand warriors ready for war crossed before the LORD to battle, toward the plains of Jericho.

14 On that day the LORD magnified Joshua in the sight of all Israel, and they stood in awe of him as they had stood in awe of Moses all the days of his life.

15 The LORD said to Joshua, saying:

16 Command the priests who bear the ark of the testimony to go up from the Jordan.

17 Jehoshua commanded the priests, saying, "Go up from the Jordan."

18 And it came to pass, when the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of the LORD came up out of the midst of the Jordan, and the soles of the priests' feet were lifted up onto the dry ground, that the waters of the Jordan returned to their place and went on as before, over all its banks.

19 And the folk went up from the Jordan on the tenth of the month, the first month, and they encamped at Gilgal, at the edge of the east of Jericho.

20 And the twelve stones which they took from the Jordan, Joshua set up in Gilgal.

21 And he said to the children of Israel, saying, When your children shall ask their fathers in time to come, saying, What mean these stones?

22 And you shall make known to your children, saying, "On dry ground Israel crossed this Jordan."

23 the LORD your God dried up the waters of the Jordan before you until you passed over, just as the LORD your God did to the Red Sea, which he dried up before us until we passed over.

24 so that all the peoples of the earth may know the hand of the LORD, that it is mighty, so that you may fear the LORD your God all the days.


## Chapter 5

1 When all the kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Jordan to the west and all the kings of the Canaanites who were by the sea heard how the LORD had dried up the waters of the Jordan before the people of Israel until we crossed over, their hearts melted and there was no spirit left in them because of the people of Israel.

2 At that time the LORD said to Joshua, Make for yourself knives of stone and again circumcise the sons of Israel.

3 Then Joshua made flint knives and circumcised the sons of Israel at Gibeath-ha-Arloth.

4 And this is the act that Joshua did: all the people who came out of Egypt, the males, all the men of war, had died in the wilderness on the journey when they went out from Egypt.

5 For all the people who had come out were circumcised, and all the people born in the wilderness during the journey after their departure from Egypt had not been circumcised.

6 For forty years the children of Israel walked in the wilderness until the end of all the generation of the men of war who came out of Egypt, who did not listen to the voice of the LORD, whom the LORD swore to them not to let them see the land which the LORD swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.

7 And their children he raised up in their place; them did Joshua circumcise, for they were uncircumcised, because they had not circumcised them along the journey.

8 And it came to pass, when all the people had finished being circumcised, that they remained in their places in the camp until they were healed.

9 And the LORD said to Joshua, This day I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from off you; and he called the name of that place Gilgal unto this day.

10 The people of Israel camped at Gilgal and kept the passover offering on the fourteenth day of the month at evening in the plains of Jericho.

11 They ate of the old corn of the land, on the morrow of the passover offering, unleavened bread and roasted grain, in the very day itself.

12 The manna ceased on the morrow after they ate of the old corn of the land, and there was no more manna for the children of Israel; and they ate of the fruit of the land of Canaan that year.

13 And it happened, when Joshua was by Jericho, that he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, a man stood before him, and his sword drawn in his hand; and Joshua went to him and said to him, "Are you for us, or for our adversaries?"

14 And he said, "No, for I am the captain of the host of the LORD; now I have come." And Joshua fell on his face to the ground and worshiped, and said to him, "What does my lord say to his servant?"

15 The captain of the LORD’s host said to Joshua, Remove your shoes from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy. And Joshua did so.


## Chapter 6

1 Jericho was shut up and barred because of the children of Israel; no one went out and no one came in.

2 The LORD said to Joshua, See, I have given into your hand Jericho and its king, the warriors of strength.

3 And you shall compass the city, all the men of war marching around the city once; thus you shall do for six days.

4 And seven priests will carry seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark; and on the seventh day you shall march around the city seven times, and the priests shall blow the trumpets.

5 And when the ram's horn sounds aloud and the trumpet blast is heard, all the people shall shout with a great shout, and the wall of the city shall collapse beneath itself, and the people shall advance, every man straight ahead.

6 Jehoshua son of Nun called to the priests and said to them, Take up the ark of the covenant, and seven priests shall carry seven ram's horn trumpets before the ark of the LORD.

7 To the folk: pass through and circle the town, and let the vanguard pass before the ark of the LORD.

8 And it was as Joshua said to the people: and the seven priests bearing seven trumpets of rams' horns before the LORD passed over and blew the trumpets, and the ark of the covenant of the LORD followed after them.

9 And the armed man went before the priests who had the trumpets, and the one who gathered followed after the ark, going and blowing the trumpets.

10 And Joshua charged the folk, saying, Do not cry aloud, and do not let your voice be heard, and no word shall come out of your mouth, until the day when I say to you, Cry aloud, and you shall cry aloud.

11 The ark of the LORD turned the city around once, and the camp came and lodged in the camp.

12 And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests took up the ark of the LORD.

13 And seven priests bearing seven trumpets of ram's horns before the ark of the LORD were walking continually, and they blew the trumpets, and the vanguard went before them, and the rearguard walked after the ark of the LORD, and they blew the trumpets.

14 They marched around the city once on the second day, then returned to the camp; thus they did for six days.

15 And it came to pass on the seventh day that they rose early at the ascent of the morning, and they circled the city in the manner of this judgment seven times; only on that day did they circle the city seven times.

16 And it came to pass, at the seventh time, the priests blew the trumpets; and Joshua said to the people, Shout, for the LORD has given you the city.

17 The city shall be under the ban, it and all that is in it, to the LORD; only Rahab the prostitute shall live, she and all who are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers whom we sent.

18 But you must keep yourselves from the cursed thing, so that you do not take from the devoted thing and bring disaster upon the camp of Israel by making it accursed.

19 And all silver and gold, and bronze and iron vessels, are holy to the LORD; they shall go to the treasury of the LORD.

20 So the people shouted when the trumpets sounded, and it was, when the folk heard the voice of the trumpet, the folk shouted a great shout, and the wall fell beneath itself, and the folk went up into the city, each man against his fellow, and they captured the city.

21 They devoted to destruction all who were in the city, from man to woman, from youth to old man, and also the oxen, the sheep, and the donkeys, by the edge of the sword.

22 And to the two men who had spied out the land, Joshua said, “Come into the house of the prostitute and bring out from there the woman and all that is hers, as you swore to her.”

23 The young men who had spied out the land came and brought out Rahab, her father, her mother, her brothers, all who belonged to her, and all her families, and they set them outside the camp of Israel.

24 And the city they burned with fire, and all that was in it; only the silver and the gold and the vessels of bronze and iron they put into the treasury of the house of the LORD.

25 And Joshua spared Rahab the prostitute, her father’s family, and all that belonged to her; she has lived in the midst of Israel to this day, because she hid the messengers whom Joshua sent to spy out Jericho.

26 At that time Joshua pronounced an oath, saying, “Cursed before the LORD be the man who rises up and rebuilds this city, Jericho: at the cost of his firstborn he shall lay its foundation, and at the cost of his youngest son he shall set up its gates.”

27 And the LORD was with Joshua, and his fame was in all the land.


## Chapter 7

1 The sons of Israel committed a trespass in the devoted things, for Achan son of Carmi son of Zabdi son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, took some of the devoted things, and the anger of the LORD burned against the sons of Israel.

2 Jehoshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is near Beth-aven, east of Beth-el, and said to them, "Go up and spy out the land." So the men went up and spied out Ai.

3 They returned to Joshua and said to him, Do not let all the people go up; about two thousand men or three thousand men should go up and attack Ai; do not weary all the people there, for they are few.

4 And they went up from the folk there, about three thousand men, and fled before the men of Ai.

5 Then the men of Ai struck some of them, about thirty-six men, and pursued them from before the gate as far as Shebarim, and struck them down on the descent; and the heart of the people melted and became like water.

6 Jehoshua tore his garments and fell upon the earth before the ark of the LORD until the evening, he and the elders of Israel, and they put dust upon their heads.

7 And Joshua said, "Ah, Lord God, why have You brought this people across the Jordan to deliver us into the hand of the Amorite to destroy us? Would that we had been willing and remained beyond the Jordan!"

8 By me, my Lord, what shall I say, after Israel has turned back before their enemies?

9 And the Canaanite and all the inhabitants of the land will hear, and they will surround us and destroy our fame from the earth; what will you do about your great renown?

10 The LORD said to Joshua, "Get up, why are you falling on your face?"

11 Israel has sinned and also transgressed the covenant which I commanded them, and also taken from the devoted things, and also stolen, and also lied, and also put in their vessels.

12 And the children of Israel could not stand before their enemies, turning their backs before their enemies, because they had become devoted to destruction; I will not continue to be with you unless you destroy the devoted things from among you.

13 Rise, consecrate the folk, and say, “Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, for thus says the LORD, God of Israel: a devoted thing is in your midst, O Israel; you will not be able to stand before your enemies until you remove the devoted thing from among you.”

14 You shall come near in the morning by your tribes, and the tribe that the LORD takes shall come near by families, and the family that the LORD takes shall come near by households, and the household that the LORD takes shall come near by men.

15 And the one taken from under the curse shall be burned with fire, he and all that belongs to him, because he has broken the covenant of the LORD and committed a vile act in Israel.

16 Jehoshua rose early in the morning and brought forward Israel by their tribes, and the tribe of Judah was taken.

17 And he brought near the family of Judah, and he took the Zarchite family; and he brought near the Zarchite family among the mighty, and Zabdi was taken.

18 And he brought near his family to the men, and Achan son of Carmi son of Zabdi son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, was taken.

19 Jehoshua said to Achan, my son, give now honour to the LORD, God of Israel, and render thanks to him; now declare to me what you have done; do not conceal it from me.

20 And Achan answered Joshua and said, Indeed, I have sinned against the LORD, the God of Israel, and thus and thus I have done.

21 In the spoil a goodly garment from Shinar one and two hundred shekels of silver and a wedge of gold one fifty shekels its weight and I coveted them and I took them and behold they are hidden in the ground in the midst of my tent and the silver under it.

22 Then Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent, and behold, it was hidden in his tent, and the silver under it.

23 They took them from among the tent and brought them to Joshua and to all the sons of Israel and poured them out before the LORD.

24 Then Joshua took Achan son of Zerah, and the silver, and the garment, and the wedge of gold, and his sons, and his daughters, and his ox, and his donkey, and his sheep, and his tent, and all that he had, and all Israel with him, and they brought them up to the Valley of Achor.

25 Then Joshua said, "Why have you troubled us? May the LORD trouble you today." And all Israel stoned him with stones, and they burned them with fire, and they stoned them with stones.

26 They raised over him a great heap of stones to this day, and the LORD turned from the fierceness of his anger; therefore the name of that place has been called Achor Valley to this day.


## Chapter 8

1 The LORD said to Joshua, Do not fear and do not be dismayed: take with you all the people of the battle and rise up against Ai. Behold, I have given into your hand the king of Ai, and his people, and his city, and his land.

2 And do to Ai and her king as you did to Jericho and her king, only plunder her spoil and her cattle for yourselves; set an ambush for the city behind her.

3 Jehoshua rose, and all the people of the battle, to go up against Ai, and Jehoshua chose thirty thousand men, mighty men of valour, and sent them by night.

4 And he commanded them, saying, See, you are to lie in ambush against the city; do not be far from the city; and all of you be ready.

5 And I and all the people who are with me will approach the city, and it shall be that when they come out against us as at the first, then we will flee before them.

6 And they will go out after us until we have destroyed them from the city, for they will say, "They flee before us as at the first," and we will flee before them.

7 And you shall rise up from the ambush and destroy the city, and the LORD your God will deliver it into your hand.

8 When you seize the city, you shall burn the city with fire, according to the commandment of the LORD; behold, I have commanded you.

9 Joshua sent them and they went to the lying in wait and remained between Beth-el and Ai, west of Ai, and Joshua spent that night among the people.

10 And Joshua rose early in the morning and mustered the people, and he and the elders of Israel went up before the people to Ai.

11 And all the fighting men who were with him went up and drew near and came opposite the city and encamped north of Ai, with the valley between them and Ai.

12 And he took about five thousand men and set them in ambush between Beth-el and Ai, west of the city.

13 The folk set the whole army north of the city and its ambush from the sea, west of the city, and Joshua went that night into the midst of the valley.

14 When the king of Ai saw, they hurried and rose early and the men of the city went out against Israel to battle, he and all his people, at the appointed time before the Arabah, and he did not know that an ambush was against him behind the city.

15 And Jehoshua and all Israel let themselves be beaten before them and fled by way of the wilderness.

16 And all the people who were in the city were called together to pursue them, and they pursued after Joshua, and were drawn away from the city.

17 And no one remained in Ai and Beth-el who did not go out after Israel; so they abandoned the city open, and pursued after Israel.

18 And the LORD said to Joshua, "Extend the spear that is in your hand toward Ai, for into your hand I will deliver it." And Joshua extended the spear that was in his hand toward the city.

19 And the ambush rose quickly from their place and ran as soon as they saw it, entered the city, captured it, and swiftly set the city on fire.

20 Then the men of Ai turned back after them and saw, and behold, the smoke of the city ascended to the heavens, and they had no power to flee this way or that, and the people who fled to the wilderness turned back against the pursuers.

21 And Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had captured the city and that the smoke of the city ascended, so they turned back and struck the men of Ai.

22 And these went out from the city to meet them, and they were in the midst of Israel, some from this side and some from that side, and they struck them down until they left no remnant or fugitive.

23 And they took the king of Ai alive, and they brought him to Joshua.

24 When Israel had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the field, in the wilderness where they had pursued them, and all of them had fallen by the sword until they were utterly destroyed, then all Israel returned to Ai and struck it with the sword.

25 And all who fell that day, from man to woman, were twelve thousand, all the men of Ai.

26 And Joshua did not draw back his hand that he had stretched out with the lance until he had utterly destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.

27 Only the cattle and the spoil of the city did Israel take for themselves, according to the word of the LORD that he commanded Joshua.

28 Then Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap forever, a desolation, until this day.

29 And the king of Ai they hanged on the tree until the time of evening, and at the coming of the sun Joshua commanded, and they took down his dead body from the tree and cast it at the entrance of the gate of the city, and they raised over him a great heap of stones, to this day.

30 Then Joshua built an altar to the LORD, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal.

31 As Moses the servant of the LORD had commanded the people of Israel, according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, an altar of whole stones upon which no iron tool had been lifted; and they offered upon it burnt offerings to the LORD, and sacrificed peace offerings.

32 And he wrote there on the stones a copy of the law of Moses, which he had written before the people of Israel.

33 All Israel, with its elders, officials, and judges, stood on both sides of the ark, facing the Levitical priests who carried the ark of the covenant of the LORD, both the stranger and the native-born, half of them opposite Mount Gerizim and half of them opposite Mount Ebal, just as Moses, the servant of the LORD, had commanded, to bless the people of Israel at the beginning.

34 And afterward he read all the words of the law, the blessing and the curse, according to all that is written in the book of the law.

35 There was no act of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read before all the congregation of Israel, the women, the children, and the sojourner who walked among them.


## Chapter 9

1 When all the kings who were beyond the Jordan, in the hill country and in the low plain and on all the coast of the sea, opposite Lebanon—the Hittite, the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite—heard,

2 They gathered together to fight against Joshua and against Israel, with one accord.

3 And the inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and to Ai.

4 And they also acted with subtilty, and went and made themselves out to be envoys, and took old sacks upon their asses, and old wine bottles, broken and bound up.

5 Their sandals were old and patched, their clothes worn out upon them, and all their provision of food had become mouldy.

6 They went to Joshua at the camp at Gilgal and said to him and to the men of Israel, "From a distant land we have come; now make a covenant with us."

7 And the men of Israel said to the Hivite, "Perhaps you dwell among us; how then can we make a covenant with you?"

8 They said to Joshua, "We are your servants," and Joshua said to them, "Who are you, and where do you come from?"

9 Your servants came from a very distant land on account of the fame of the LORD your God, because we heard the report and all that he did in Egypt.

10 and all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Jordan, to Sihon king of Heshbon and to Og king of Bashan who was at Ashtaroth.

11 Our elders and all the inhabitants of our land said to us, “Take provisions in your hand and go to meet them. You shall say to them, ‘We are your servants; now therefore, make a covenant with us.’”

12 This is the bread we kept warm, which we prepared from our homes on the day we left to come to you; and now, look, it is moldy.

13 And these are the bottles of the wine which we filled with new wine, and behold, they have burst; and these are our garments and our shoes, worn out from the great journey.

14 The men took from their hunting, and they did not inquire at the mouth of the LORD.

15 And Joshua made peace with them and cut a covenant with them to let them live, and the rulers of the assembly swore to them.

16 After three days from their having made a covenant with them, they heard that they were near neighbors and dwelling in their midst.

17 The people of Israel set out and came to their cities on the third day, and their cities were Gibeon, Chephirah, Beeroth, and Kirjath-jearim.

18 The sons of Israel did not strike them because the rulers of the congregation had sworn to them by the LORD, the God of Israel; and the whole congregation complained against the rulers.

19 And all the captains said to all the congregation, We have sworn to them by the LORD, the God of Israel; now we are not able to touch them.

20 This we will do to them and let them live, so that no wrath come upon us for the oath that we swore to them.

21 And the rulers said to them, Live; and they became woodcutters and water carriers for all the congregation, as the rulers had spoken to them.

22 Jehoshua called to them and spoke to them, saying, Why have you deceived us, saying, We are very far from you, when you are dwelling among us?

23 And now you are cursed, and from among you there shall not be cut off a bondman, woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.

24 They answered Joshua and said, "Indeed, it was reported to your servants what the LORD your God commanded Moses, his servant, to give you all the land and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land before you; and we greatly feared for our lives because of you, and so we have done this thing."

25 And now, behold, we are in your hand; do with us as is good and right in your sight.

26 And he did so for them, and delivered them from the hand of the children of Israel, and they did not kill them.

27 Joshua assigned them on that day to be woodcutters and water carriers for the assembly and for the altar of the LORD at the place that he would choose.


## Chapter 10

1 And it came to pass, when Adonizedec king of Jerusalem heard how Joshua had captured Ai and had utterly destroyed it, as he had done to Jericho and its king, so he had done to Ai and its king, and how the inhabitants of Gibeon had made peace with Israel and were among them,

2 They greatly feared, for Gibeon was a great city, like one of the royal cities, and greater than Ai, and all its men were warriors.

3 Adonizedec king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron, and to Piram king of Jarmuth, and to Japhia king of Lachish, and to Debir king of Eglon, saying:

4 Come up to me and help me, and let us strike Gibeon, for it has made peace with Joshua and with the people of Israel.

5 So gathered and went up the five Amorite kings, the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon, they and all their armies, and encamped against Gibeon and fought against it.

6 The men of Gibeon sent to Joshua at the camp at Gilgal, saying, Do not withhold your hand from your servants; come up to us quickly and save us and help us, for all the kings of the Amorites who live in the hill country have gathered together against us.

7 Jehoshua went up from Gilgal, he and all the people of the battle with him, and all the warriors of the army.

8 The LORD said to Joshua, Do not fear them, for I have given them into your hand; not a man of them shall stand before you.

9 Then Jehoshua came upon them suddenly, all night he had gone up from Gilgal.

10 And the LORD confounded them before Israel and struck them with a great slaughter at Gibeon and pursued them by the way of the ascent of Beth-horon and struck them to Azekah and to Makkedah.

11 And it happened, as they fled before Israel, while they were on the descent of Beth-horon, that the LORD cast great stones from heaven upon them as far as Azekah, and they died; many perished from the hailstones more than those whom the sons of Israel slew with the sword.

12 Then Joshua spoke to the LORD on the day the LORD gave the Amorite before the children of Israel, and he said before the eyes of Israel, Sun, stand still in Gibeon, and Moon, in the valley of Aijalon.

13 And the sun stood still, and the moon stayed, until the nation had avenged itself on its enemies; is this not written in the book of the upright? And the sun stood in the midst of the heavens, and did not hasten to go, like a whole day.

14 There was no age like that day before it or after it, to hear the LORD in the voice of a man, because the LORD fought for Israel.

15 And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.

16 So the five kings fled and hid in the cave at Makkedah.

17 And it was told to Joshua, saying, "The five kings have been found, hidden in the cave at Makkedah."

18 Then Joshua said, Roll large stones to the mouth of the cave and appoint men to guard them.

19 But you shall not stand still; pursue your enemies and cut them down, and do not let them enter their city, for the LORD your God has delivered them into your hand.

20 And it came to pass, when Joshua and the sons of Israel had finished striking them with a very great slaughter, until they were utterly destroyed, that those who remained of them escaped and entered into the fortified cities.

21 All the people returned to the camp at Makkedah to Joshua in peace; not a man of the children of Israel moved his tongue against any.

22 Then Joshua said, Open the mouth of the cave, and bring out to me the five kings from the cave.

23 They did so and brought out to him those five kings from the cave: the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon.

24 When they brought out those kings to Joshua, Joshua called to all the men of Israel and said to the captains of the men of war with him, “Come near, put your feet upon the necks of these kings.” So they came near and put their feet upon their necks.

25 Then Joshua said to them, Do not fear and do not be dismayed; be strong and firm, for thus the LORD will do to all your enemies whom you are fighting against them.

26 Then Joshua struck them down and killed them, and he hanged them on five trees, and they remained hanging on the trees until evening.

27 After the sun went down, Joshua commanded, and they brought them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had hidden, and he placed great stones at the mouth of the cave until this very day.

28 And Joshua captured Makkedah on that day and struck it down with the sword and its king he devoted to destruction along with every person who was in it and he left no remnant and he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.

29 Jehoshua and all Israel with him passed from Makkedah to Libnah, and fought against Libnah.

30 And the LORD also delivered that city into the hand of Israel, and its king; and he struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person who was in it; he left no remnant in it; and he did to its king as he had done to the king of Jericho.

31 Then Joshua passed on and all Israel with him from Libnah to Lachish and encamped against it and fought against it.

32 And the LORD gave Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he captured it on the second day, and struck it with the edge of the sword, and every person who was in it, according to all that he had done to Libnah.

33 Then Horam king of Gazer went up to help Lachish, and Joshua struck him and his folk until he left him no remnant.

34 Then Joshua passed on and all Israel with him from Lachish to Eglon, and they encamped against it and fought against it.

35 On that day they captured her and struck her down with the sword, and every person who was in her they devoted to destruction on that day, just as they had done to Lachish.

36 Jehoshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron and fought against it.

37 They captured her and struck her with the sword, and her king and all her cities and all the people who were in her they left no remnant, according to all that they had done to Eglon, and they devoted her and all the people who were in her.

38 And Jehoshua returned, and all Israel with him, to Debir; and they fought against it.

39 He captured it and its king and all its cities, and they struck them with the edge of the sword, and they devoted to destruction every person who was in it; he left no remnant, just as he had done to Hebron, so he did to Debir and its king, and as he had done to Libnah and its king.

40 Jehoshua struck all the land, the hill country, and the south, and the low plain, and the springs, and all their kings; he left no remnant, and he devoted to destruction every spirit, as the LORD God of Israel commanded.

41 Joshua struck them from Kadeshbarnea to Azzah and all the land of Goshen to Gibeon.

42 Joshua captured all these kings and their lands at one time, because the LORD, the God of Israel, fought for Israel.

43 And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.


## Chapter 11

1 When Jabin king of Hazor heard, he sent to Jobab king of Madon, and to the king of Shimron, and to the king of Achshaph.

2 and to the kings who are north of the hill country and in the Arabah and southward to Kinneroth and in the lowlands and in the regions of Dor westward

3 The Canaanite from the east and the sea, and the Amorite and the Hittite and the Perizzite and the Jebusite in the hill country, and the Hivite under Hermon in the land of Mizpah.

4 They marched out, they and all their army with them, a people as numerous as the sand on the seashore, in great abundance, and with many chariots and horses.

5 All the kings assembled and came and camped together by the waters of Merom to fight against Israel.

6 And the LORD said to Joshua, Do not fear them, for tomorrow at this time I will deliver them all slain before Israel; their horses you shall hamstring, and their chariots you shall burn with fire.

7 Then Joshua and all the fighting men with him came suddenly against them at the waters of Merom and fell upon them.

8 And the LORD gave them into the hand of Israel, and they struck them and pursued them to Zidon, to Misrephoth-mayim, and to the plain of Mizpeh eastward, and struck them until they left no remnant among them.

9 Joshua did to them as the LORD had said to him: he hamstrung their horses and burned their chariots with fire.

10 At that time Joshua returned and captured Hazor, and struck its king with the sword, for Hazor formerly was the head of all these kingdoms.

11 They struck every person who was in it with the edge of the sword, devoted to destruction; no living being was left. Hazor they burned with fire.

12 And Joshua captured all the cities of these kings and all their kings, and he struck them down by the sword; he devoted them to destruction, just as Moses the servant of the LORD commanded.

13 Only all the cities that stood on their mounds did not Israel burn, except Hazor alone—Joshua burned it.

14 And all the spoil of the cities and the cattle the sons of Israel took as prey for themselves, but every person they struck with the edge of the sword until they had destroyed them; they left no soul remaining.

15 Just as the LORD had commanded Moses his servant, so Moses commanded Joshua, and thus Joshua did; he did not omit anything from all that the LORD had commanded Moses.

16 Then Joshua took all this land, the hill country, and all the southern region, and all the land of Goshen, and the low country, and the wilderness plain, and the mountain of Israel with its lowland.

17 From Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir, as far as Baal-gad in the valley of Lebanon, under Mount Hermon, and all their kings—he captured them and struck them down and put them to death.

18 Joshua waged war a long time against all those kings.

19 There was no city that made peace with the Israelites except the Hivite who dwelt in Gibeon; all they took by war.

20 For from the LORD it was to harden their hearts for battle against Israel, in order to devote them to destruction, so that they would find no favour, in accordance with what the LORD commanded Moses.

21 At that time Joshua came and cut off the Anakim from the hill country, from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah and from all the hill country of Israel; Joshua devoted their cities to destruction.

22 No Anakim were left in the land of the children of Israel; only in Azzah, in Gath, and in Ahdod were some left.

23 Jehoshua took the whole land just as the LORD had spoken to Moses, and Jehoshua gave it to Israel as an inheritance according to their divisions by their tribes, and the land was quiet from war.


## Chapter 12

1 These are the kings of the land whom the sons of Israel defeated and whose land they possessed beyond the Jordan toward the sunrise, from the valley of Arnon to Mount Hermon, and all the wilderness eastward.

2 Sihon king of the Amorite who lived in Heshbon, ruling from Aroer which is on the edge of the Arnon river and the middle of the river and half of Gilead as far as the Jabbok river, the border of the children of Ammon.

3 The Arabah as far as the Sea of Chinnereth eastward, and the Arabah as far as the Salt Sea eastward, the way of Beth-jeshimoth, and from the south beneath the slopes of Pisgah.

4 And the border of Og king of Bashan, of the remnant of the Rephaim, who lived in Astaroth and in Edrei.

5 and ruling over Mount Hermon and Salcah and all Bashan, to the border of the Geshuri and the Maachathite, and half Gilead, the border of Sihon king of Heshbon.

6 Moses the servant of the LORD and the children of Israel struck them, and Moses the servant of the LORD gave them as an inheritance to the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh.

7 These are the kings of the land whom Joshua and the children of Israel struck beyond the Jordan westward, from Baal-gad in the valley of Lebanon to the mount Halak that rises toward Seir, and Joshua gave it to the tribes of Israel as a heritage according to their divisions.

8 in the hill and in the low country and in the Arabah and in the springs and in the wilderness and in the south the Hittite the Amorite and the Canaanite the Perizzite the Hivite and the Jebusite

9 one king of Jericho one king of Ai which is beside Beth-el

10 one king of Jerusalem, one king of Hebron.

11 one king of Jarmuth, one king of Lachish.

12 one king of Eglon, one king of Gazer.

13 One king of Debir, one king of Geder.

14 One king of Hormah, one king of Arad.

15 one king of Libnah, one king of Adullam.

16 one king of Makkedah, one king of Beth-el.

17 One king of Tappuah, one king of Hepher.

18 One king of Aphek, one king of Lasharon.

19 one king of Madon, one king of Hazor.

20 The king of Shimon-meron is one, the king of Achshaph is one.

21 a king of Taanach one a king of Megiddo one

22 one king of Kedesh, one king of Jokneam of Carmel

23 A king of Dor for the region of Dor, a king of the nations for Gilgal.

24 One king, Tirzah, of all kings thirty-one.


## Chapter 13

1 And Joshua was old, advanced in age, and the LORD said to him, "You are old, advanced in age, and much land remains to be possessed."

2 This is the land that remains: all the border of the Philistines and all the Geshurites.

3 From the Shihor which is before Egypt even unto the border of Ekron northward, it shall be accounted to the Canaanite: the five lords of the Philistines, the Gazathite, and the Ashdodites, the Eshkalonites, the Gittite, and the Ekronite; and the Avims.

4 From the south side all the land of the Canaanite and Mearah which belongs to the Sidonians as far as Aphek, to the border of the Amorite.

5 The common land of the Giblites, and all Lebanon, from the east of the sun even from Baal-gad under mount Hermon to the entering of Hamath.

6 All the inhabitants of the mountain from Lebanon to Misrephoth-mayim, all the Sidonians, I myself will drive out from before the children of Israel; only allot the land to Israel for inheritance as I have commanded you.

7 Now therefore divide this land as inheritance for nine tribes and the half tribe of Manasseh.

8 The children of Reuben and the Gadites took their inheritance, which Moses gave them, beyond the Jordan eastward, as Moses the servant of the LORD gave them.

9 From Aroer, which is on the bank of the Arnon brook, and the city that is in the midst of the brook, and all the plain from Medeba to Dibon.

10 All the cities of Sihon king of the Amorite who reigned in Heshbon as far as the border of the sons of Ammon.

11 Gilead and the border of the Geshurites and the Maachathite, and all Mount Hermon and all Bashan as far as Salcah.

12 All the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei, he remained from the remnant of the Rephaim; Moses struck him and they dispossessed him.

13 The sons of Israel did not dispossess the Geshurites and the Maachathites, and Geshur and Maachah have dwelt in the midst of Israel to this day.

14 Only to the tribe of Levi he did not give an inheritance; the LORD God of Israel is his inheritance, as He spoke to him.

15 Moses gave to the tribe of the sons of Reuben according to their families.

16 Their border was from Aroer, which is on the bank of the brook Arnon, and the city that is in the midst of the brook, and all the plain to Medeba.

17 Heshbon and all its cities which are in the plain, Dibon and Bamoth-baal and Beth-baal-meon.

18 Jahaz, Kedemoth, and Mephaath.

19 Kiriathaim and Shebam and Zareth-shahar on the hill of the valley.

20 Bethpeor, the springs of Pisgah, and Beth-jeshimoth.

21 All the cities of the plain and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorite, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses struck, and the chiefs of Midian, Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, princes of Sihon, inhabitants of the land.

22 And Balaam the son of Beor, the diviner, they struck down with the sword among their slain.

23 The border of the sons of Reuben was the Jordan and its border; this was the inheritance of the sons of Reuben according to their families, the cities and their villages.

24 Moses gave to the tribe of Gad, to the children of Gad, according to their families.

25 Their border was Jaazer and all the cities of Gilead and half the land of the Ammonites as far as Aroer, which is before Rabbah.

26 From Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to the border of Debir.

27 In the valley Beth-aram and Beth-Nimrah and Succoth and Zaphon the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon the Jordan and its border to the edge of the sea of Chinnereth beyond the Jordan eastward.

28 This is the inheritance of the children of Gad according to their families, the cities and their villages.

29 Moses gave to the half tribe of Manasseh, and it happened to the half tribe of the sons of Manasseh according to their families.

30 Their border was from Mahanaim, all Bashan, all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, and all the towns of Jair that are in Bashan, sixty cities.

31 Half of Gilead and Astaroth and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan, were for the sons of Machir son of Manasseh, for half of the sons of Machir according to their families.

32 These are the things that Moses initiated in the Arabah of Moab, beyond the Jordan at Jericho, on the east side.

33 And to the tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance; the LORD, the God of Israel, is their inheritance, as He spoke to them.


## Chapter 14

1 These are the inheritances that the children of Israel received in the land of Canaan, which Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of Israel allotted to them.

2 By lot their inheritance, as the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses, for nine tribes and a half tribe.

3 For Moses had given an inheritance to the two tribes and to half the tribe beyond the Jordan, but to the Levites he did not give an inheritance among them.

4 For the sons of Joseph were two tribes, Manasseh and Ephraim, and they did not give a portion to the Levites in the land, only cities to dwell in and their suburbs for their cattle and for their substance.

5 As Jehovah commanded Moses, so the sons of Israel did, and they divided the land.

6 Then the people of Judah came to Joshua at Gilgal, and Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenezite said to him, “You know the word that the LORD spoke to Moses the man of God concerning me and concerning you at Kadeshbarnea.”

7 I am forty years old when Moses the servant of the LORD sent me from Kadeshbarnea to spy out the land, and I brought him back word according to my heart.

8 And my brothers who went up with me caused the heart of the people to melt, but I followed fully after the LORD my God.

9 Moses swore on that day, saying, "If not, the land which your foot has trodden shall be yours for a possession and for your children's forever, because you have fully followed the LORD my God."

10 And now indeed, the LORD has kept me alive as He promised; this is forty-five years since the LORD spoke this word to Moses, when Israel walked in the wilderness, and now indeed, today I am eighty-five years old.

11 I am still strong this day as I was in the day Moses sent me, my strength then is my strength now, for battle and for going out and coming in.

12 Now therefore give me this hill country which the LORD spoke of on that day, for you heard on that day that Anakim were there and cities great and walled, perhaps the LORD will be with me and I will drive them out as the LORD said.

13 Then Joshua blessed him and gave Hebron to Caleb the son of Jephunneh as an inheritance.

14 Therefore Hebron became Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite's inheritance to this day because he fully followed the LORD God of Israel.

15 and the name of Hebron formerly was Kirjath-arba, the man great among the Anakim, and the land was quiet from war.


## Chapter 15

1 The lot for the tribe of Judah’s families was to the border of Edom, the wilderness of Zin southward, at the uttermost part of the south.

2 Their border was southward from the end of the Salt Sea, from the tongue that faces southward.

3 He went out toward the south to the ascent of Akrabbim and passed Zin and went up from the south to Kadeshbarnea and passed Hezron and went up to Addar and turned about the Karkaa.

4 And it passes Azmon and goes out to the brook of Egypt; the border's outgoings are westward; this shall be for you the southern border.

5 The border on the east side was the Salt Sea as far as the end of the Jordan, and the border on the north side was from the bay of the sea from the end of the Jordan.

6 Then the border went up at Beth-hoglah and passed northward to Beth-arabah, and the border went up to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.

7 The border went up from the valley of Achor northward to Gilgal, which is opposite the ascent of Adummim south of the river; and the border passed on to the waters of Enshemesh, and its outgoings were at En-rogel.

8 The border went up by the valley of the son of Hinnom, on the south side of the Jebusite, the same is Jerusalem; and the border went up to the top of the mountain that lies before the valley of Hinnom westward, which is at the end of the valley of the Rephaim northward.

9 The border ascends from the top of the hill to the spring of the waters of Neptoah, and proceeds to the cities of the mountain of Ephron; the border turns about to Baalah, which is Kirjath-jearim.

10 The border curved westward from Baalah, passed into the hill country of Seir, crossed to the northern shoulder of Mount Jearim, that is, Chesalon, and descended to Beth-shemesh, passing on to Timnah.

11 The border went out to the shoulder of Ekron northward; the border turned toward Shicron and passed Mount Baalah, going out to Jabneel, and the goings out of the border were toward the sea.

12 and the border [was] the Great Sea and its border; this [was] the border of the children of Judah according to their families, round about.

13 And to Caleb son of Jephunneh he gave a portion among the children of Judah, according to the word of the LORD to Joshua: Kirjath-arba, the father of Anak, that is, Hebron.

14 Caleb drove out from there the three sons of Anak: Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai, the children of Anak.

15 He went up from there to the inhabitants of Debir, and Debir was formerly named Kirjath-sepher.

16 And Caleb said, Whoever attacks Kirjath-sepher and captures it, I will give him my daughter Achsah as wife.

17 Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's brother, captured it, and he gave to him Achsah his daughter as wife.

18 And when she came, she urged him to ask her father for a field, and she dismounted from the ass, and Caleb said to her, "What is it to you?"

19 And she said, "Grant me a blessing, for you have given me the land of the Negeb; give me also springs of water"; and he gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

20 This is the heritage of the tribe of the children of Judah according to their families.

21 The cities were from the edge of the tribe of Judah to the border of Edom in the south: Kabzeel and Eder and Jagur.

22 Kinah and Dimonah and Adadah.

23 Kedesh, Hazor, and Ithnan.

24 Ziph and Telem and Bealoth.

25 Hazor, Hadattah, and Kerioth—Hezron is Hazor.

26 Amam, Shema, and Moladah.

27 Hazar-gaddah, Heshmon, and Beth-palet.

28 Hazar-shual and Beer-shebah and Bizjothjah.

29 Baalah and Iim and Azem.

30 and to Eltolad and to Chesil and to Hormah.

31 Ziklag and Madmannah and Sansannah.

32 Lebaoth, Shilhim, Ain, and Rimmon: twenty-nine towns and their villages.

33 In the low country, Eshtaol and Zorah and Ashnah.

34 Zanoah and En-gannim, Tappuah and Enaim.

35 Jarmuth and Adullam, Shochoh and Azekah.

36 Shaaraim and Adithaim and Gederah and Gederothaim, fourteen towns and their villages.

37 Zenan and Hadashah and Migdal-gad.

38 Dilean and Mizpeh and Joktheel.

39 Lachish and Bozcath and Eglon.

40 and Cabbon and Lahmam and Kithlish.

41 Gederoth, Beth-dagon, Naamah, and Makkedah, towns sixteen with their villages.

42 Libnah, Ether, and Ashan.

43 Jephthah, Ashnah, and Nezib.

44 Keilah and Achzib and Mareshah, nine towns and their villages.

45 Ekron and its villages and its towers.

46 From Ekron and westward, all that lay near Ashdod, with its villages.

47 Ahdod with her daughters and her villages, Azzah with her daughters and her villages, as far as the river of Egypt, and the sea with its border.

48 And in the hill, Shamir and Jattir and Shochoh.

49 And Dannah and Kirjath-sannah, she is Debir.

50 Anab, Eshtemoa, and Anim.

51 Goshen, Holon, and Giloh, eleven towns and their villages.

52 Arab and Dumah and Eshean.

53 Janim, and Beth-tappuah, and Aphekah.

54 Humtah and Kirjath-arba, which is Hebron, and Zior; nine cities and their villages.

55 Maon, Carmel, Ziph, and Juttah.

56 Jezreel, Jokdeam, and Zanoah.

57 Cain, Gibeah, and Timnah, ten cities and their villages.

58 Halahul, Beth-zur, and Gedor.

59 And Maarath, and Beth-anoth, and Eltekon, six cities with their villages.

60 Kirjathbaal, that is, Kirjath-jearim, and Rabbah: two cities and their villages.

61 In the wilderness, Beth-arabah, Middin, and Secacah.

62 And Nibshan, and the city of salt, and En-gedi; six cities and their villages.

63 The Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem were not driven out by the people of Judah, so the Jebusites lived with the people of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.


## Chapter 16

1 The lot went out for the sons of Joseph from the Jordan at Jericho, from the waters of Jericho eastward, the wilderness going up from Jericho into the hill country to Beth-el.

2 And he went out from Beth-el to Luz, and passed over to the border of the Archite, Ataroth.

3 And it went down westward to the border of the Japhleti as far as the border of Beth-horon below, even to Gazer, and ended westward.

4 The sons of Joseph, Manasseh and Ephraim, inherited.

5 The border of the Ephraimites according to their families was the border of their inheritance eastward, Ataroth-addar to Beth-horon upper.

6 And the border went out westward from Michmethath northward, and the border turned eastward to Taanath-shiloh, and passed it on the east of Janoah.

7 And he went down from Janoah to Ataroth and Naarath, and came to Jericho, and crossed the Jordan.

8 From Tappuah the border goes westward to the brook Kanah, and its outgoings are at the sea; this is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Ephraim according to their families.

9 The cities separated for the Ephraimites within the inheritance of the Manassites, all the cities and their villages.

10 They did not dispossess the Canaanite who dwelt in Gazer, and the Canaanite dwelt among Ephraim to this day, and became a task-tax.


## Chapter 17

1 The lot was for the tribe of Manasseh, for he was the firstborn of Joseph; to Machir the firstborn of Manasseh, the chief of Gilead, for he was a man of war, and there was for him Gilead and Bashan.

2 And to the sons of Manasseh according to their families remained: to the sons of Abiezer, and to the sons of Helek, and to the sons of Ashriel, and to the sons of Shechem, and to the sons of Hepher, and to the sons of Shemida; these are the sons of Manasseh son of Joseph, the males according to their families.

3 And Zelophehad the son of Hepher the son of Gilead the son of Machir the son of Manasseh had no sons, only daughters; and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

4 They came forward before Eleazar the priest and before Joshua son of Nun and before the rulers, saying, “The LORD commanded Moses to give us an inheritance among our brothers.” So he gave them an inheritance by the command of the LORD among their father’s brothers.

5 The portions of Manasseh were ten regions, apart from the land of Gilead and Bashan which were beyond the Jordan.

6 For the daughters of Manasseh received an inheritance among his sons, and the land of Gilead belonged to the remaining sons of Manasseh.

7 The border of Manasseh went from Asher to Michmethath, which is before Shechem, and the border went to the right, to the inhabitants of En-tappuah.

8 Tappuah was the land of Manasseh, and Tappuah was on the border of Manasseh toward the children of Ephraim.

9 The border went down to the brook Kanah southward to the brook; these cities were for Ephraim among the cities of Manasseh, and the border of Manasseh was from the north of the brook, and its outgoings were toward the sea.

10 To the south Ephraim and to the north Manasseh, and the sea was its border, and they shall border on Asher from the north and on Issachar from the east.

11 Manasseh had Beth-shean and its towns, and Ibleam and its towns, and the inhabitants of Dor and its towns, and the inhabitants of En-dor and its towns, and the inhabitants of Taanach and its towns, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its towns, three countries.

12 The sons of Manasseh could not dispossess the inhabitants of those cities, and the Canaanite was determined to dwell in this land.

13 When the sons of Israel grew strong, they placed the Canaanite under tribute, but did not dispossess him.

14 And the sons of Joseph spoke to Joshua, saying, Why have you given me one lot and one portion as inheritance, while I am a numerous people, and yet the LORD has blessed me until now?

15 Jehoshua said to them, "If you are a numerous folk, go up for yourself to the forest and clear for yourself there in the land of the Perizzites and the Rephaim, because the hill country of Ephraim is too confined for you."

16 The sons of Joseph said, "The hill country is not enough for us, yet all the Canaanites who dwell in the valley land have chariots of iron, both those in Beth-shean and its villages and those in the Valley of Jezreel."

17 Jehoshua said to the house of Joseph, to Ephraim and to Manasseh, saying: "You are a numerous people and have great strength; you shall not have one lot alone."

18 For the hill country will be yours, though it is a forest, and you will clear it, and its borders will be yours, when you drive out the Canaanite, though he has an iron chariot, though he is strong.


## Chapter 18

1 All the assembly of the children of Israel assembled at Shiloh and set up there the tent of meeting, and the land was subdued before them.

2 And there remained among the sons of Israel who had not received their inheritance, seven tribes.

3 Jehoshua said to the children of Israel, "How long will you delay coming to take possession of the land that the LORD, the God of your fathers, has given you?"

4 Take for yourselves three men from each tribe, and I will send them to go through the land and write down its portions according to their inheritance, and they shall come back to me.

5 They shall divide it into seven portions; Judah shall remain on his border toward the south, and the house of Joseph shall remain on their border toward the north.

6 But you shall divide the land into seven portions and bring them here to me, and I will cast lots for you before the LORD our God.

7 For there is no portion for the Levites among you, for the priesthood of the LORD is their inheritance; and Gad and Reuben and half the tribe of Manasseh have taken their inheritance beyond the Jordan eastward, which Moses the servant of the LORD gave to them.

8 The men rose and went, and Joshua commanded those who went to write down the land, saying, Go and traverse the land and write it down, then return to me; here I will cast a lot for you before the LORD at Shiloh.

9 And the men went and passed through the land and wrote it down for the cities in seven portions on a scroll and came to Joshua at the camp in Shiloh.

10 Joshua cast a lot before the LORD at Shiloh, and there Joshua divided the land among the children of Israel according to their divisions.

11 The lot came up for the tribe of Benjamin by its families, and the border of their lot came out between the children of Judah and the children of Joseph.

12 Their border was on the north side from the Jordan; the border went up to the shoulder of Jericho northward, went up through the hill country westward, and its outgoings to the wilderness of Beth-aven.

13 Then the border passed on from Luz to the side south of Luz, the same being Beth-el; and the border went down to Ataroth-adar, to the hill that lies south of Beth-horon lower.

14 And the border shall turn and go around toward the western side southward from the hill that is before Beth-horon southward; its goings out shall be to Kirjathbaal, that is, Kirjath-jearim, a city of the children of Judah; this is the western side.

15 And the southern quarter from the end of Kirjath-jearim, and the border went out westward, and went out to the spring of the waters of Neptoah.

16 The border descended to the edge of the hill facing the valley of Hinnom, which is in the valley of the Rephaim northward; then the valley of Hinnom descended southward to the shoulder of the Jebusite, and descended to En-rogel.

17 From the north it turns to Enshemesh, and goes out to Geliloth opposite the ascent of Adummim, and descends to the stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.

18 And he passed over to the shoulder side opposite the wilderness of the north, and descended to the Arabah.

19 Then the border extends from the shoulder of Beth-hoglah northward; the border goes to the tongue of the Salt Sea northward, to the southern end of the Jordan; this is the southern border.

20 And the Jordan shall border it on the east side; this is the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin according to their borders all around by their families.

21 The cities were allotted to the tribe of Benjamin by their families: Jericho, Beth-hoglah, and the Valley of Keziz.

22 Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, and Beth-el.

23 And the Avim and Parah and Ophrah.

24 Chefarhaamonai, Ophni, and Gaba: twelve towns and their villages.

25 Gibeon and Ramah and Beeroth.

26 Mizpeh, Chephirah, and Mozah.

27 Rekem and Irpeel and Taralah.

28 And Zelah and Eleph and the Jebusite, that is Jerusalem, Gibeath and Kirjath, fourteen towns and their villages; this is the inheritance of the children of Benjamin according to their families.


## Chapter 19

1 The second lot came out for Simeon, for the tribe of the children of Simeon according to their families, and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the children of Judah.

2 They had inheritance in their heritage, in Beer-sheba, and Sheba, and Moladah.

3 Hazar-shual and Balah and Azem.

4 And Eltolad and Bethuel and Hormah.

5 Ziklag, Bethmarcaboth, and Hazar-susah.

6 Beth-lebaoth and Sharuhen, thirteen towns and their villages.

7 Ain, Remmon, Ether, and Ashan; four towns and their villages.

8 And all the villages that were around the cities as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negev—this was the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Simeon according to their families.

9 From the territory of the people of Judah was the inheritance of the people of Simeon, for the portion of the people of Judah was too large for them, and the people of Simeon inherited within the inheritance of the people of Judah.

10 The lot came up for the sons of Zebulun according to their families, and the border of their inheritance reached to Sarid.

11 Their border went up to the west and Maralah, touched Dabbesheth, and reached the brook that is before Jokneam.

12 And a Sarid shall return from the east, from the rising of the sun, to the border of Chisloth-tabor; and it shall go out to Dabareh and go up to Japhia.

13 From there he passed on eastward to Gittah-hepher, to Ittah-kazin, and went out to Remmon-methoar, to Neah.

14 Then the border turned from the north at Hannathon, and the goings out thereof were toward the valley of Jiphthah-el.

15 Kattath, Nahalal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem, twelve towns and their villages.

16 This is the inheritance of the sons of Zebulun according to their families: the cities and their villages.

17 The lot came out for Issachar, the fourth, for the sons of Issachar according to their families.

18 Their border was Jezreel, Chesulloth, and Shunem.

19 Haphraim and Shihon and Anaharath.

20 Rabbith, Kishion, and Abez.

21 Remeth, En-gannim, En-haddah, and Beth-pazzez.

22 And the border touched Tabor, and Shahazimah, and Beth-shemesh, and their border outgoings were at the Jordan; sixteen cities and their villages.

23 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Issachar according to their families: the cities and their villages.

24 The lot came out for the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families.

25 Their border was Helkath, Hali, Beten, and Achshaph.

26 And to Alammelech and to Amad and to Misheal, and it met with Carmel of the west and with Shihor-libnath.

27 From the east side, Beth-dagon, and it touched Zebulun and the valley of Jiphthah-el northward, Beth-emek and Neiel, and it went out to Cabul on the left side.

28 Hebron, Rehob, Hammon, and Kanah, as far as great Sidon.

29 Then the border turned from Ramah to the city of the fortress of Tyre, and the border turned to Hosah, its outgoings toward the sea from the region of Achzib.

30 Ummah, Aphek, and Rehob, twenty-two cities and their villages.

31 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families, these cities and their villages.

32 The lot went out for the sons of Naphtali, the sixth, for the sons of Naphtali according to their families.

33 Their border was from Heleph, from Allon at Zaanaim, and Adami to the Nekeb, and Jabneel as far as Lakum, and their outgoings were at the Jordan.

34 The border turns westward to Aznoth-tabor and proceeds from there to Hukkokah, touching Zebulun on the south and Asher on the west, and Judah at the Jordan toward the sunrise.

35 and the fortified cities, Ziddim, Zer, Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth.

36 Adamah, Ramah, and Hazor.

37 Kedesh and Edrei and En-hazor.

38 Iron, Migdal-el, Horem, Beth-anath, Beth-shemesh, nine and one cities, and their villages.

39 This is the heritage of the tribe of the children of Naphtali according to their families: the cities and their villages.

40 For the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their families the lot came forth, the seventh.

41 The border of their inheritance was Zorah and Eshtaol and Irshemesh.

42 and Shaalabbin and Aijalon and Jethlah.

43 Elon, Timnah, and Ekron.

44 Eltekeh, Gibbethon, and Baalath.

45 Jehud and Bene-barak and Gath-rimmon.

46 And the waters of Me-jarkon and Rakkon with the border opposite Joppa.

47 The border of the sons of Dan went out from them, and the sons of Dan went up and fought against Leshem, and they captured it and struck it with the edge of the sword, and they possessed it and dwelled in it, and they called Leshem Dan, according to the name of Dan their father.

48 This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Dan according to their families: the cities these and their villages.

49 They finished apportioning the land according to its borders, and the children of Israel gave an inheritance among them to Joshua son of Nun.

50 At the command of the LORD they gave him the city which he requested, Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim, and he built the city and lived in it.

51 These are the inheritances which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua son of Nun, and the chiefs of the fathers’ tribes of the people of Israel divided by lot before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting in Shiloh; and they finished dividing the land.


## Chapter 20

1 And the LORD spoke to Joshua, saying:

2 Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: Appoint for yourselves the cities of refuge which I spoke to you about by Moses.

3 so that the manslayer who kills any person unwittingly without knowledge may flee there, and they shall be for you as a refuge from the blood-avenger.

4 Then he shall flee to one of these cities and stand at the entrance of the gate of that city and speak his words in the ears of the elders of that city, and they shall gather him to themselves and give him a place, and he shall dwell with them.

5 But if the blood-avenger pursues him, they shall not deliver the murderer into his hand, because unintentionally he struck his neighbour, and previously he was not his enemy.

6 He shall dwell in that city until he stands before the assembly for judgment, until the death of the chief priest who shall be in those days; then the manslayer may return and come to his city and to his house, even to the city from which he fled.

7 And they sanctified Kedesh in Galilee on the hill country of Naphtali, and Shechem on the hill country of Ephraim, and Kirjath-arba, which is Hebron, on the hill country of Judah.

8 Beyond the Jordan eastward from Jericho they gave Bezer in the wilderness in the plain from the tribe of Reuben and Ramoth in Gilead from the tribe of Gad and Golan in Bashan from the tribe of Manasseh.

9 These were the cities of refuge for all the children of Israel and for the stranger who sojourned among them, that anyone who killed a person unwittingly might flee there, so that he would not die by the hand of the blood-avenger until he stood before the assembly.


## Chapter 21

1 The chiefs of the fathers of the Levites approached Eleazar the priest and to Joshua son of Nun and to the chiefs of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel.

2 They spoke to them in Shiloh in the land of Canaan, saying, "The LORD commanded through Moses to give us cities to dwell in and their suburbs for our cattle."

3 The sons of Israel gave the Levites from their inheritance the cities and their suburbs according to the word of the LORD.

4 The lot went out for the families of the Kohathites, and it was for the sons of Aaron the priest from the tribe of Judah and from the tribe of Simeon and from the tribe of Benjamin by lot, thirteen cities.

5 And to the remaining sons of Kohath, from the families of the tribe of Ephraim and from the tribe of Dan and from half the tribe of Manasseh, by lot, ten cities.

6 To the sons of Gershon, by families of the tribe of Issachar and by the tribe of Asher and by the tribe of Naphtali and by half the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities by lot.

7 To the sons of Merari, according to their families, from the tribe of Reuben and from the tribe of Gad and from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve cities.

8 The sons of Israel gave to the Levites these cities and their suburbs by lot, as the LORD commanded through the hand of Moses.

9 They gave from the tribe of Judah and from the tribe of Simeon these cities which he called by name.

10 And to the sons of Aaron, of the families of the Kohathites, of the sons of Levi, was the lot first.

11 They gave them Kirjath-arba, the chief of Anak—that is Hebron—in the hill country of Judah, and its suburbs around it.

12 And the field of the city and its villages he gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh in his possession.

13 To the sons of Aaron the priest they gave the city of refuge, Hebron, with its suburbs, and Libnah with its suburbs.

14 and Jattir with its suburb, and Eshtemoa with its suburb.

15 and Holon with its suburbs, and Debir with its suburbs.

16 Ain and its suburbs, Juttah and its suburbs, Beth-shemesh and its suburbs: nine towns from these two tribes.

17 and from the tribe of Benjamin, Gibeon with its suburb, Geba with its suburb.

18 Anathoth and its suburb, and Almon and its suburb: four towns.

19 All the cities of the sons of Aaron the priests, thirteen cities and their suburbs.

20 And to the families of the Levite Kohathites, the remainder of the sons of Kohath, their cities by lot were from the tribe of Ephraim.

21 They gave them the city of refuge for the manslayer: Shechem with its suburbs in the hill country of Ephraim, and Gezer with its suburbs.

22 Kibzaim with its suburbs, and Beth-horon with its suburbs, four towns.

23 and from the tribe of Dan, Eltekeh and its suburbs, Gibbethon and its suburbs.

24 Aijalon and its suburb, Gath-rimmon and its suburb, four cities.

25 and from half the tribe of Manasseh, Taanach and its suburb, and Gath-rimmon and its suburb, two cities.

26 All ten cities and their suburbs, for the families of the sons of Kohath who remained.

27 And to the sons of Gershon, from the families of the Levites, from half the tribe of Manasseh, the city of refuge for the manslayer, Golan in Bashan and its suburb, and Beeshterah and its suburb—two cities.

28 From the tribe of Issachar, Kishion and its suburb, Dabareh and its suburb.

29 Jarmuth and its suburb, En-gannim and its suburb, four towns.

30 From the tribe of Asher, Mishal and its suburb, Abdon and its suburb.

31 Helkath and its suburb, and Rehob and its suburb, four towns.

32 From the tribe of Naphtali, the city of refuge for the manslayer: Kedesh in Galilee, with its suburbs; Hamath-Dor, with its suburbs; and Kartan, with its suburbs—three cities.

33 All the cities of the Gershonites according to their families, thirteen cities and their suburbs.

34 And to the families of the remaining sons of Merari the Leviites, from the tribe of Zebulun, [were given] Jokneam and its suburb, Kartah and its suburb.

35 Dimnah and its suburb, Nahalal and its suburb, four towns.

36 From the tribe of Reuben, Bezer and its suburbs, and Jahazah and its suburbs.

37 Kedemoth and its suburbs, and Mephaath and its suburbs, four towns.

38 From the tribe of Gad, the city of refuge for the manslayer: Ramoth in Gilead with its suburbs, and Mahanaim with its suburbs.

39 Heshbon and its suburb, Jaazer and its suburb; four cities in all.

40 All the cities for the sons of Merari according to their families, the remainder from the families of the Levites, and their lot was twelve cities.

41 All the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty-eight cities with their suburbs.

42 Let these cities be city for city, and their suburbs around them; so for all these cities.

43 And the LORD gave to Israel all the land that he had sworn to give to their fathers, and they took possession of it and dwelt in it.

44 And the LORD gave them rest on every side according to all that he swore to their forefathers, and no man stood against them in their presence; all their enemies the LORD delivered into their hand.

45 Not one act of all the good acts that the LORD spoke to the house of Israel failed; all came to pass.


## Chapter 22

1 Then Joshua called to the Reubenites and to the Gadites and to the half tribe of Manasseh.

2 And he said to them, You have kept all that Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, and you have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you.

3 You have not forsaken your brothers this long time until this day, and you have kept the charge of the commandment of the LORD your God.

4 Now the LORD your God has given your brothers rest, as He promised them; now therefore turn and go to your tents, to the land of your possession which Moses, the servant of the LORD, gave you beyond the Jordan.

5 Only take great care to do the commandment and the law which Moses my servant commanded you, to love the LORD your God, and to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, and to cleave unto him, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul.

6 Then Joshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their homes.

7 And to half the tribe of Manasseh Moses gave in Bashan, and to the other half Joshua gave with their brothers beyond the Jordan westward; and indeed, when Joshua sent them to their tents, he blessed them.

8 And he said to them, saying, With much wealth return to your tents, and with very many cattle, with much silver and with gold, and with brass and with iron, and with very many garments; divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers.

9 Then the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh returned and went from the sons of Israel at Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan, to go to the land of Gilead, to their own land of possession, which they had possessed, according to the command of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

10 They came to the borders of the Jordan in the land of Canaan and built there an altar on the Jordan, the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manasseh, a great altar to be seen.

11 The people of Israel heard, saying, Behold, the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built an altar opposite the land of Canaan, at the Jordan's border, on the side of the people of Israel.

12 The sons of Israel heard, and all the congregation of the sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh to go up with the army.

13 The people of Israel sent Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest to the people of Reuben and to the people of Gad and to the half tribe of Manasseh in the land of Gilead.

14 And ten chiefs with him, one prince for each ancestral house of all the tribes of Israel, each the head of their ancestral house among the thousands of Israel.

15 They came to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh, to the land of Gilead, and they spoke with them, saying:
