16 Thus said all the congregation of the LORD, What trespass is this that you have committed against the God of Israel, to turn back today from following the LORD, building yourselves an altar to rebel this day against the LORD?

17 Is the fault of Peor too little for us, that we have not been cleansed from it to this day, and that the plague came upon the assembly of the LORD?

18 But you today will turn back from following the LORD, and you yourselves will rebel today against the LORD, and tomorrow wrath will come upon all the congregation of Israel.

19 But if the land of your possession is unclean, pass over to you into the land of the possession of the LORD, where the tabernacle of the LORD dwells among us, and let them possess in our midst, and rebel not against the LORD, nor rebel against us, by setting up for yourselves an altar besides the altar of the LORD our God.

20 Did not Achan son of Zerah commit a trespass by taking from the devoted things, bringing the wrath of the LORD upon all the congregation of Israel, yet he was but one man and did not perish in his sin?

21 The sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh spoke to the chiefs of Israel.

22 God, God the LORD, God, God the LORD—he knows, and Israel he knows—whether in rebellion or in trespass against the LORD, do not save us this day.

23 To build us an altar to turn from following after the Lord, or to offer upon it a burnt offering and a gift, or to make upon it sacrifices of peace offerings, the Lord is the one who will require.

24 But if not from care concerning the matter, we have done this, saying, Tomorrow your children will say to our children, What to you and to the LORD, God of Israel?

25 And the LORD has set a border between us and you, Reuben and Gad, by the Jordan; you have no part with the LORD, and you would turn back our children from following the LORD.

26 And we said, “Let us now build an altar, not for ascent and not for sacrifice.”

27 For it is a witness between us and you and our generations after us, to perform the service of the LORD before Him with our burnt offerings and with our sacrifices and with our peace offerings, so that your children may not say to our children tomorrow, "You have no portion in the LORD."

28 And we said, If they say this to us or to our generations tomorrow, then we shall say, See the pattern of the altar of the LORD which our fathers made, not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice, but as a witness between us and you.

29 Far be it from us to rebel against the LORD and to turn back this day from following the LORD by building an altar for a burnt offering, for a gift, and for a sacrifice, apart from the altar of the LORD our God that is before his dwelling.

30 Phinehas the priest and the rulers of the assembly and the chiefs of the thousands of Israel who were with him heard the words that the children of Reuben and the children of Gad and the children of Manasseh spoke, and it seemed good in their eyes.

31 Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest said to the children of Reuben and to the children of Gad and to the children of Manasseh, Today we know that the LORD is among us, because you have not trespassed against the LORD with this trespass; then you have delivered the children of Israel from the hand of the LORD.

32 Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest and the leaders returned from the Reubenites and the Gadites from the land of Gilead to the land of Canaan to the people of Israel and brought them back word.

33 The advice pleased the sons of Israel, and God blessed the sons of Israel, and they did not say to go up against them to war to destroy the land where the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad dwelt.

34 The sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad called the altar a witness between us, for the LORD is God.


## Chapter 23

1 And it came to pass after many days, when the LORD had given Israel rest from all their enemies round about, and Joshua was old and advanced in years.

2 Jehoshua called to all Israel, to its elders and to its chiefs and to its judges and to its officers, and said to them, I am grown old, I have come into my days.

3 And you have seen all that the LORD your God did to all these nations before you, for it is the LORD your God who fought for you.

4 See, I have allotted to you as inheritance these remaining nations, among the tribes of you, from the Jordan and all the nations that I have cut off, even to the great sea toward the west.

5 The LORD your God will drive them out before you and dispossess them from before you, and you shall inherit their land, as the LORD your God promised you.

6 You shall therefore carefully keep the whole law written in the book of the law of Moses, not turning aside from it to the right or to the left.

7 You shall not come into the nations remaining with you, nor mention the name of their gods, nor cause to swear by them, nor serve them, nor bow down to them.

8 But you shall hold fast to the LORD your God, as you have done to this day.

9 And the LORD has driven out before you nations great and strong, and no man has stood before you until this day.

10 a certain man of you will pursue a thousand, for the LORD your God is the one who fights for you, as he promised you.

11 You must diligently keep your souls by loving the LORD your God.

12 But if you ever turn back and cling to the remaining Gentiles who are left among you, and make marriages with them and you come into them and they into you,

13 You shall surely know that the LORD your God will no longer drive out these nations from before you, and they shall be a snare and a trap to you, and a scourge on your sides, and thorns in your eyes, until you perish from off the good land which the LORD your God has given you.

14 And behold I am going today the way of all the land and you shall know with all your heart and with all your soul that not one thing of all the good words which the LORD your God spoke concerning you has failed; all of them have come to you; not one thing of them has failed.

15 And it shall come to pass, as all the good that the LORD your God promised you has come upon you, so the LORD will bring upon you all the evil, until he has destroyed you from off this good land that the LORD your God has given you.

16 When you cross over the covenant of the LORD your God which he commanded you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them, then the anger of the LORD will burn against you, and you will quickly perish from upon the good land which he gave to you.


## Chapter 24

1 Then Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel to Shechem and called for the elders of Israel and for its chiefs and for its judges and for its officers, and they presented themselves before God.

2 Jehoshua said to all the people, Thus says the LORD God of Israel, Beyond the river your fathers lived of old, Terah the father of Abraham and the father of Nahor, and they served other gods.

3 And I took your father Abraham from beyond the river and led him throughout all the land of Canaan and multiplied his seed, and gave him Isaac.

4 I gave to Isaac Jacob and Esau, and I gave to Esau the hill country of Seir to possess it, but Jacob and his children went down to Egypt.

5 And I sent Moses and Aaron, and I struck Egypt as I did among him, and afterward I brought you out.

6 I brought your forefathers out of Egypt, and you came to the sea; and the Egyptians pursued after your forefathers with chariot and horseman to the Red Sea.

7 And they cried to the LORD, and he put darkness between you and the Egyptians, and brought the sea upon them and covered them; and your eyes saw what I did to the Egyptians, and you dwelt in the wilderness many days.

8 I gave you the Amorite who lived beyond the Jordan, and they fought against you, and I delivered them into your hands, and you took possession of their land, and I destroyed them before you.

9 Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, rose up against Israel and fought with Israel, and he sent and called Balaam son of Beor to curse you.

10 I was not willing to listen to Balaam, so he blessed you, and I delivered you from his hand.

11 You crossed the Jordan and came to Jericho, and the lords of Jericho, the Amorite, the Perizzite, the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Girgashite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, fought against you, and I delivered them into your hand.

12 I sent the hornet before you, and it drove them out from before you—two kings of the Amorites—not by your sword and not by your bow.

13 And I gave you a land on which you had not labored, and cities which you had not built, and you dwell in them; vineyards and olive trees which you had not planted—you eat of them.

14 And now fear the LORD and serve him faithfully and truly, and remove the gods whom your fathers served beyond the river and in Egypt, and serve the LORD.

15 If it seems wrong in your sight to serve the LORD, choose for yourselves today whom you will serve, whether the gods whom your fathers served beyond the river, or the gods of the Amorites in whose land you dwell; but I and my house will serve the LORD.

16 And the people answered and said, It is unthinkable for us to forsake the LORD to serve other gods.

17 For the LORD our God is the one who brought us and our ancestors up from the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage, and who did before our eyes these great signs, and protected us throughout the entire way that we traveled, and among all the peoples through whose midst we passed.

18 And the LORD drove out all the nations, even the Amorite who lived in the land, from before us; we will also serve the LORD, for he is our God.

19 Joshua said to the people, You will not be able to serve the LORD, for he is a holy God, a jealous God, who will not bear with your rebellions and your sins.

20 When you forsake the LORD and serve gods of strange, then he will turn and do evil to you and consume you after he has done good to you.

21 And the folk said to Joshua, No, but the LORD we will serve.

22 Jehoshua said to the folk You are witnesses against yourselves that you have chosen for yourselves the LORD to serve him and they said Witness

23 And now put away the strange gods that are among you, and incline your hearts to the LORD, the God of Israel.

24 And the folk said to Joshua, "The LORD our God we will serve, and his voice we will obey."

25 Jehoshua made a covenant with the people on that day and set for them a statute and judgment at Shechem.

26 Joshua wrote these words in the book of the law of God and took a large stone and set it up there under the oak that was in the sanctuary of the LORD.

27 Joshua said to all the people, Behold, this stone shall be a witness for us, for it has heard all the words of the LORD which he spoke with us, and it shall be a witness among you, lest you deny your God.

28 Then Joshua sent the people, each to his inheritance.

29 And it came to pass after these acts that Joshua son of Nun, servant of the LORD, died, being one hundred and ten years old.

30 They buried him in the border of his inheritance at Timnath-serah, which is in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.

31 Israel served the LORD all the days of Joshua and all the days of the elders who prolonged days after Joshua and who knew all the work that the LORD did for Israel.

32 And the bones of Joseph, which the people of Israel brought up out of Egypt, they buried at Shechem, in the parcel of ground that Jacob bought from the sons of Hamor, the father of Shechem, for a hundred pieces of silver; and it became an inheritance for the children of Joseph.

33 Eleazar son of Aaron died, and they buried him in the hill of Phinehas his son, which was given to him in the hill country of Ephraim.


# JUDGES

## Chapter 1

1 After the death of Joshua, the children of Israel inquired of the LORD, saying, "Who shall go up for us against the Canaanite at the beginning, to fight against him?"

2 The LORD said, Judah shall go up; behold, I have given the land into his hand.

3 Judah said to his brother Simeon, "Come up with me into my lot, and let us fight against the Canaanite, and I also will go with you into your lot," and Simeon went with him.

4 Judah went up and Jehovah gave the Canaanite and the Perizzite into their hand, and they struck them in Bezek ten thousand men.

5 They found Adonibezek in Bezek and fought against him, and they struck the Canaanite and the Perizzite.

6 Adonibezek fled, and they pursued him; they caught him and cut off his thumbs and his great toes.

7 Adonibezek said, "Seventy kings, their thumbs and great toes cut off, used to gather under my table; as I have done, so God has repaid me." They brought him to Jerusalem, and there he died.

8 The sons of Judah fought against Jerusalem and captured it, and struck it with the sword, and burned the city with fire.

9 Afterward the people of Judah went down to fight against the Canaanite who lived in the hill country, and in the south, and in the low country.

10 Judah went to the Canaanite who lived in Hebron, and the name of Hebron formerly was Kirjath-arba, and they struck Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai.

11 And he went from there to the inhabitants of Debir, and the name of Debir formerly was Kirjath-sepher.

12 And Caleb said, Whoever attacks Kirjath-sepher and captures it, I will give him my daughter Achsah as wife.

13 Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother, captured it, and he gave to him Achsah his daughter as a wife.

14 When she arrived, she urged him to ask her father for the land, and she dismounted from the ass, and Caleb said to her, "What is it to you?"

15 And she said to him, Give me a blessing, for you have given me the land of the Negev, and you have given me water springs; so Caleb gave her the upper spring and the lower spring.

16 The Kenite, Moses’ father-in-law, went up from the city of palm trees with the people of Judah into the wilderness of Judah, which is in the south of Arad, and he went and settled among the people.

17 Judah went with Simeon his brother and they struck the Canaanite inhabitant of Zephath, and they devoted it, and he called the name of the city Hormah.

18 Judah captured Azzah and its border, and Ashkelon and its border, and Ekron and its border.

19 And the LORD was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country, but he could not drive out the inhabitants of the valley because they had iron chariots.

20 They gave to Caleb Hebron as Moses had spoken, and he drove out from there the three sons of Anak.

21 The Jebusites who dwelt in Jerusalem were not driven out by the sons of Benjamin, and the Jebusite dwelt with the sons of Benjamin in Jerusalem to this day.

22 And the court of Joseph also went up to Beth-el and the LORD was with them.

23 The house of Joseph advanced to Beth-el, and the name of the city formerly was Luz.

24 The guards saw a man coming out from the city, and they said to him, "Show us now the entrance to the city, and we will do with you a kindness."

25 And he saw them the entrance to the city, and they struck the city with the sword, but the man and all his family they sent away.

26 And the man went to the land of the Hittites and built a city and called its name Luz; that is its name to this day.

27 And Manasseh did not dispossess the inhabitants of Beth-shean and its villages, nor of Taanach and its villages, nor of Dor and its villages, nor of the inhabitants of Ibleam and its villages, nor of the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages, and the Canaanite was willing to dwell in this land.

28 And it came to pass, when Israel was strong, that they put the Canaanite to tribute, but did not discomfit him.

29 And Ephraim did not dispossess the Canaanite who dwelt in Gazer, so the Canaanite dwelt among him in Gazer.

30 Zebulun did not dispossess the inhabitants of Kitron and the inhabitants of Nahalal, and the Canaanite dwelt among him, and they became tribute.

31 Asher did not dispossess the inhabitants of Accho, and the inhabitants of Sidon, and of Ahlab, and of Achzib, and of Helbah, and of Aphek, and of Rehob.

32 The Asherites lived among the Canaanites, the inhabitants of the land, for they did not drive them out.

33 Naphtali did not dispossess the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and the inhabitants of Beth-anath, so they dwelt among the Canaanite inhabitants of the land, and the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath became tribute to them.

34 The Amorite afflicted the sons of Dan in the hill country, because he did not allow them to go down to the valley.

35 The Amorite was willing to dwell in Mount Heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalabbin, but the hand of the house of Joseph prevailed, and they became tribute.

36 The border of the Amorite from the ascent of Akrabbim, from the (ragged) rock and upward.


## Chapter 2

1 An ambassador of the LORD went up from Gilgal to Bochim and said, I brought you up from Egypt and I brought you to the land which I swore to your fathers, and I said, I will not break my covenant with you forever.

2 But you shall not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land; their altars you shall tear down. You have not obeyed my voice. What is this you have done?

3 I also said, I will not drive them out before you; but they shall be as thorns in your side, and their gods shall be a snare to you.

4 And it came to pass, when the messenger of the LORD spoke these words to all the children of Israel, that the people lifted up their voice and wept.

5 They called the name of that place Bochim, and they sacrificed there to the LORD.

6 Jehoshua sent the people away, and the children of Israel went, each man to his inheritance, to possess the land.

7 The folk served the LORD all the days of Joshua and all the days of the elders who prolonged days after Joshua who saw all the act of the LORD the great which he did for Israel.

8 Jehoshua son of Nun the servant of the LORD died, being one hundred and ten years old.

9 They buried him in the border of his inheritance at Timnath-serah in the hill country of Ephraim north of Mount Gaash.

10 And also all that generation were gathered to their fathers, and there arose another generation after them who did not know the LORD nor the work which he had done for Israel.

11 The sons of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD and served the Baalim.

12 They abandoned the LORD, the God of their ancestors, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt, and they went after other gods of the nations that were around them, and they bowed down to them, and they provoked the LORD to anger.

13 They abandoned the LORD and served Baal and the Asharoth.

14 The LORD was angry with Israel and delivered them into the hands of raiders, who plundered them; and he sold them into the hands of their enemies all around, so that they were no longer able to stand before their enemies.

15 In all that they undertook, the hand of the LORD was against them for evil, as the LORD had spoken and as the LORD had sworn to them, and it distressed them exceedingly.

16 The LORD raised up judges who delivered them from the power of their oppressors.

17 Yet they did not listen even to their judges, for they prostituted themselves after other gods and bowed down to them; they quickly turned aside from the way their fathers had walked, obeying the commandments of the LORD; they did not do so.

18 And whenever the LORD raised up judges for them, the LORD was with the judge and delivered them from the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge, for the LORD relented concerning their groaning because of those who oppressed and afflicted them.

19 And it was, when the judge died, they would return and act corruptly after their fathers, to go after other gods, to serve them and to bow down to them; they did not cease from their deeds nor from their stubborn way.

20 The LORD became angry with Israel and said, "Because this nation has transgressed my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and has not obeyed my voice,"

21 I also will no longer drive out any man from before them, out of the nations that Joshua left when he died.

22 in order to test Israel by them, whether they will keep the way of the LORD by walking in it as their fathers kept it or not.

23 The LORD allowed those nations to remain, not dispossessing them quickly, nor delivering them into the hand of Joshua.


## Chapter 3

1 These are the nations that the LORD left to test Israel, all who had not known any of the wars of Canaan.

2 Only so that the generations of the children of Israel might know, to teach them war, those who previously had not known it.

3 Five lords of the Philistines, and all the Canaanite, and the Sidonian, and the Hivite dwelling in Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon to the entering of Hamath.

4 And they were to test Israel, to know whether they would obey the ordinances of the LORD which He commanded their forefathers by the hand of Moses.

5 The sons of Israel dwelt among the Canaanite, the Hittite, and the Amorite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.

6 They took their daughters as wives for themselves, and gave their daughters to their sons, and served their gods.

7 The sons of Israel did evil in the eyes of the LORD and forgot the LORD their God and served the Baalim and the groves.

8 The LORD was angry with Israel and sold them into the hand of Chushan-rishathayim king of Aram-naharaim, and the sons of Israel served Chushan-rishathayim eight years.

9 The sons of Israel cried to the LORD, and the LORD raised up a deliverer for the sons of Israel, and he delivered them: Othniel the son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.

10 And the air of the LORD came upon him, and he judged Israel; and he went out to battle, and the LORD delivered into his hand Chushan-rishathayim king of Aram, and his power prevailed over Chushan-rishathayim.

11 The land was quiet for forty years, and Othniel son of Kenaz died.

12 The sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD strengthened Eglon king of Moab against Israel because they had done evil in the sight of the LORD.

13 He gathered to him the sons of Ammon and Amalek, and went and struck Israel, and they took possession of the city of the palm trees.

14 The sons of Israel served Eglon king of Moab eighteen years.

15 The sons of Israel cried to the LORD, and the LORD raised up for them a savior, Ehud the son of Gera, son of the Benjamite, a man left-handed; and the sons of Israel sent by his hand a present to Eglon king of Moab.

16 Ehud made for himself a dagger, and it had two edges, a cubit long, and he girded it beneath his garment on his right thigh.

17 He brought the offering to Eglon king of Moab, and Eglon was a very fat man.

18 And when he had finished presenting the gift, he sent away the people, the bearers of the gift.

19 And he returned from the quarries which were at Gilgal, and said, "A private matter concerns me with you, O king," and he said, "Keep silence," and all who stood about him withdrew from him.

20 Ehud came to him while he was sitting alone in his summer chamber, and Ehud said, "I have a message from God for you," and he arose from his throne.

21 Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and thrust it into his belly.

22 Then the officer came also after the flame and enclosed the fat within the flame, for he had not drawn the sword from his belly, and the dirt came out.

23 Ehud went out to the porch and closed the doors of the upper chamber behind him and locked them.

24 And he went out, and his servants came and saw that the doors of the upper chamber were locked, and they said, "Surely he is relieving himself in the inner chamber of the summer house."

25 They waited until they were ashamed, but there was no one opening the doors of the upper chamber, so they took the key and opened, and behold, their lord lay fallen to the ground, dead.

26 And Ehud escaped while they delayed; he passed beyond the carved images and escaped to Seirath.

27 When he arrived, he blew the trumpet on Mount Ephraim, and the sons of Israel came down with him from the hill, he before their face.

28 He said to them, "Pursue after me, for the LORD has delivered your enemy Moab into your hand." So they went down after him and seized the fords of the Jordan against Moab, and did not let any man cross.

29 And they struck Moab at that time ten thousand men, every one a warrior and every man of strength, and no man escaped.

30 Moab was subdued on that day under the hand of Israel and the land was at rest eighty years.

31 After him was Shamgar son of Anath, who struck down six hundred Philistines with an ox goad, and he also saved Israel.


## Chapter 4

1 The sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, and Ehud died.

2 And the LORD delivered them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who reigned in Hazor; and the captain of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth of the Gentiles.

3 The sons of Israel cried to the LORD because he had nine hundred chariots of iron and oppressed the sons of Israel severely for twenty years.

4 And Deborah, a prophetess, wife of Lappidoth, she judged Israel at that time.

5 And she was sitting under the palm tree of Deborah between Ramah and Beth-el on the hill of Ephraim, and the children of Israel went up to her for judgment.

6 She sent and summoned Barak the son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali, and said to him, “Has not the LORD, the God of Israel, commanded you: ‘Go, march on Mount Tabor, and take with you ten thousand men from the children of Naphtali and from the children of Zebulun’?”

7 I will draw Sisera, the captain of the army of Jabin, with his chariot and his multitude, to you at the brook Kishon, and I will deliver him into your hand.

8 Barak said to her, "If you go with me, I will go; but if you do not go with me, I will not go."

9 But she said, I will go with you, yet there will be no glory for you on the journey that you take, for by the hand of a woman will the LORD sell Sisera. And Deborah arose and went with Barak to Kedesh.

10 Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh; ten thousand men went up on foot with him, and Deborah went up with him.

11 Heber the Kenite separated from the Kenites, from the sons of Hobab, Moses' father-in-law, and pitched his tent as far as the oak in Zaanannim, which is by Kedesh.

12 They reported to Sisera that Barak son of Abinoam had gone up to Mount Tabor.

13 Sisera summoned all his chariots, nine hundred iron chariots, and all the people who were with him, from Harosheth of the nations to the river Kishon.

14 Deborah said to Barak, "Arise, for this day is when the LORD has given Sisera into your hand; has not the LORD gone out before you?" And Barak went down from Mount Tabor, and ten thousand men after him.

15 And the LORD routed Sisera and all his chariots and all his army before Barak by the edge of the sword; and Sisera alighted from his chariot and fled away on foot.

16 And Barak pursued after the chariot and after the army to Harosheth of the Gentiles, and all the army of Sisera fell by the sword; not one was left.

17 Sisera fled on foot to the tent of Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite, for there was peace between Jabin king of Hazor and the house of Heber the Kenite.

18 Jael went out to meet Sisera, and she said to him, Turn aside, my lord, turn aside to me; do not be afraid. So he turned aside to her into the tent, and she covered him with the mantle.

19 And he said to her, “Please give me a little water to drink, for I am thirsty.” So she opened the milk bottle, gave him a drink, and covered him.

20 He said to her, Stand at the entrance of the tent, and if any man comes and asks you, Is there a man here? say, There is none.

21 Jael the wife of Heber took the tent peg and seized the hammer in her hand, then came to him softly and drove the peg into his temple, and it went down into the ground; he was fast asleep, exhausted, and died.

22 And behold, Barak was pursuing Sisera, and Jael went out to meet him and said to him, “Come, and I will show you the man whom you seek.” And he went in with her, and behold, Sisera fell dead, and the nail was in his temple.

23 On that day God subdued Jabin king of Canaan before the children of Israel.

24 The power of the people of Israel continued to grow stronger against Jabin king of Canaan until they destroyed Jabin king of Canaan.


## Chapter 5

1 Then Deborah and Barak son of Abinoam sang on that day, saying:

2 When avenging in Israel, when the people offer themselves willingly, bless the LORD.

3 Hear, kings; listen, rulers; I, even I, will sing to the LORD, I will sing praise to the LORD, the God of Israel.

4 When the LORD went out from Seir, when he marched from the field of Edom, the land trembled; indeed, the heavens also dripped, indeed, the clouds also dripped water.

5 The hills flowed from before the LORD; this Sinai, from before the LORD God of Israel.

6 In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the highways ceased and travelers went by winding paths.

7 The village ceased in Israel, they ceased, until I, Deborah, arose, until I arose, a mother in Israel.

8 God chose new rulers; then war was at the gates; neither shield nor spear was seen among forty thousand in Israel.

9 My heart is for the leaders of Israel, who willingly offered themselves among the people. Bless the LORD.

10 Riders of white she-asses, sitters on white donkeys, and walkers on the way of conversation.

11 At the sound of archers between watering places, there they recited the righteous acts of the LORD, the righteous deeds of his village in Israel; then the people of the LORD went down to the gates.

12 Awake, awake, Deborah! Awake, awake, sing a song! Arise, Barak, and take away your captives, son of Abinoam!

13 Then the remnant descended to the mighty folk; the LORD descended with me among the mighty.

14 From Ephraim their root is in Amalek; after you, Benjamin, among your peoples; from Machir came down lawgivers, and from Zebulun those who bear the staff of the scribe.

15 And the captain was with Issachar, and Issachar was with Deborah; even Barak into the valley at his feet was sent; in the divisions of Reuben great were the resolutions of heart.

16 Why do you sit between the burdens, to hear the bleating of the flocks? For the divisions of Reuben, great were the searchings of heart.

17 Gilead abides beyond the Jordan and why does Dan dwell in ships Asher sits by the coast of the sea and on his breaches he dwells

18 Zebulun is a folk who jeopards his life unto death, and Naphtali on the heights of the field.

19 Kings came, they fought; then the kings of Canaan fought at Taanach, by the waters of Megiddo; they took no profit of silver.

20 From the heavens they fought; the stars from their courses fought against Sisera.

21 The brook Kishon swept them away, the ancient brook, the brook Kishon; tread down strength, O my soul.

22 Then the hoofs of the horse pranced from the prancing of his mighty ones.

23 Cry aloud, Meroz, said the LORD's messenger; cry aloud against its inhabitants, because they came not to the help of the LORD, to the help of the LORD against the mighty.

24 Jael wife of Heber the Kenite is blessed among women; among women in the tent she is blessed.

25 Water he asked, milk she gave, in a lordly bowl she offered butter.

26 Her hand reached for the tent peg, and her right hand for the workman's hammer; and she struck Sisera, she crushed his head, she shattered and pierced his temple.

27 Between her legs he fell, he lay, between her legs he sank, where he sank there he fell, plundered.

28 Through the window peered Sisera's mother, through the lattice she fretted: Why is his chariot delaying to come? Why are the wheels of his chariots late?

29 The wisdom of her princesses answers her; indeed, she returns her speech to her.

30 Will they not find and divide the prey: a damsel, two damsels for each man, the diverse colours of needlework, the raiment of needlework on both sides for Sisera, the diverse colours of needlework, for the necks of the prey?

31 So may all your enemies perish, O LORD, and those who love him like the going forth of the sun in its might; and the land had rest forty years.


## Chapter 6

1 The sons of Israel did evil in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD delivered them into the hand of Midian for seven years.

2 And the hand of Midian prevailed against Israel; because of Midian the people of Israel made for themselves the dens which are in the hills, and the caves, and the strongholds.

3 And it would happen, if Israel sowed, that Midian and Amalek and the people of the east would come up against him.

4 They encamped against them and destroyed the increase of the land as far as Gaza; they left no sustenance in Israel, neither sheep nor ox nor he-ass.

5 For they and their livestock would go up, and their tents with them, like locusts in abundance, and their camels were without number, and they came into the land to destroy it.

6 And Israel was greatly impoverished because of Midian, and the people of Israel cried out to the LORD.

7 When the people of Israel cried to the LORD on account of the cause of Midian,

8 The LORD sent a certain prophet to the children of Israel and said to them, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, I brought you up out of Egypt and brought you out of the house of bondage.

9 I delivered you from the power of Egypt and from the power of all your oppressors, and I drove them out from before you and gave you their land.

10 And I said to you, I am the LORD your God: you shall not fear the gods of the Amorite in whose land you dwell; but you did not obey my voice.

11 An ambassador of the LORD came and sat under the elm which was in Ophrah, belonging to Joash the Abiezrite, and Gideon his son was beating wheat in the winepress to hide it from the Midianites.

12 An ambassador of the LORD appeared to him, and said to him, The LORD is with you, O mighty man of valour.

13 And Gideon said to him, "Please, my lord, if the LORD is with us, why has all this overtaken us? And where are all his wonders that our ancestors recounted to us, saying, 'Did not the LORD bring us up from Egypt?' But now the LORD has forsaken us and delivered us into the hand of Midian."

14 Then the LORD turned to him and said, "Go in your strength and save Israel from the hand of Midian. Have I not sent you?"

15 And he said to him, "Please, my Lord, with what shall I save Israel? Behold, my family is the least in Manasseh, and I am the youngest in my father's house."

16 And the LORD said to him, "Because I will be with you, you shall strike Midian as one man."

17 And he said to him, If now I have found favour in your eyes, then do a sign for me that it is you speaking with me.

18 Please do not move from here until I come to you, and I will bring out my gift and set it before your face; and he said, I myself will remain until you return.

19 Gideon came and prepared a kid and an ephah of unleavened flour; the flesh he placed in a basket and the broth he put in a pan, and he brought them out to him under the elm and presented them.

20 And the ambassador of God said to him, Take the flesh and the unleavened bread and place upon the rocky stone, and pour out the broth, and he did so.

21 The LORD's messenger sent the tip of the staff that was in his hand and touched the flesh and the unleavened bread, and fire went up from the rock and consumed the flesh and the unleavened bread, and the LORD's messenger departed from his sight.

22 Gideon saw that he was the angel of the LORD, and Gideon said, Ah, Lord GOD, for indeed I have seen the angel of the LORD face to face.

23 The LORD said to him, "Peace to you; do not fear; you will not die."

24 There Gideon built an altar to the LORD and called its name Jehovah-shalom; to this day it is still in Ophrah of the Abiezrite.

25 And it came to pass that night, and the LORD said to him, Take the young bull that belongs to your father and the second bull of seven years, and pull down the altar of Baal that belongs to your father, and cut down the grove that is upon it.

26 You will build an altar for the LORD your God on the top of this stronghold in the ordered place, and you will take the second young bull and offer it as a burnt offering on the wood of the grove which you cut down.

27 Gideon took ten men from his servants and did as the LORD had spoken to him, but because he feared his father’s household and the men of the city, he did it by night.

28 The men of the city rose early in the morning and saw that the altar of Baal was broken down, and the grove that was on it cut down, and the second young bull offered upon the built altar.

29 And each man said to his companion, What deed is this that has been done? And they inquired and searched and said, Gideon the son of Joash has done this deed.

30 The men of the city said to Joash, "Bring out your son, that he may die, for he has torn down the altar of Baal and cut down the grove that was on it."

31 Joash said to all who were with him, "Will you contend for Baal? If you would save him, whoever contends for him shall be put to death by morning; if he is God, let him contend for himself, for his altar has been torn down."

32 On that day Jerubbaal said to him, "Let Baal contend with him, because he has torn down his altar."

33 And all Midian and Amalek and the children of the east were gathered together, and they crossed over and camped in the valley of Jezreel.

34 The spirit of the LORD clothed Gideon, and he blew the trumpet, and Abiezer rallied behind him.

35 And he sent ambassadors into all Manasseh, and he also cried after them; and he sent ambassadors into Asher and into Zebulun and into Naphtali, and they came up to meet them.

36 Gideon said to God, If you will indeed save Israel by my hand as you have spoken.

37 Behold, I am placing the fleece of wool upon the threshing floor: if dew shall be upon the fleece alone and upon all the land drought, then I will know that by my hand You save Israel as You have spoken.

38 And it was so: he rose early on the next day and wrung the fleece, and he squeezed from the fleece all the water in a bowl.

39 Gideon said to God: Do not let your anger burn against me, and I will speak only this time: let me test just once with the fleece: let there be dryness on the fleece alone, and on all the earth let there be dew.

40 God did so on that night: there was desolation upon the fleece alone, and on all the earth there was dew.


## Chapter 7

1 Jerubbaal, he Gideon, and all the people who were with him, rose early and camped by the well of Harod; and the camp of Midian was to the north of them, beyond the hill of Moreh in the valley.

2 And the LORD said to Gideon, Too many are the folk that are with you for me to give Midian into their hand, lest Israel boast against me, saying, My hand has saved me.

3 Now therefore proclaim in the hearing of the people, saying, Whoever is afraid and trembling, let him return and depart from Mount Gilead; and twenty-two thousand of the people returned, and ten thousand remained.

4 And Jehovah said to Gideon, The folk are still too many; bring them down to the watering; and there I will purge them for thee. He that I say to thee, This one shall go with thee, the same shall go with thee; and every one that I say to thee, This one shall not go with thee, the same shall not go.

5 And he brought down the folk to the watering, and the LORD said to Gideon, Every one who laps with his tongue from the water, as a dog laps, set apart by himself, and every one who kneels on his knees to drink.

6 And the number of those who lapped from their hands to their mouths was three hundred men, but all the rest of the people kneeled to drink water.

7 And the LORD said to Gideon, By the three hundred men that lap [water] I will save you and deliver Midian into your hand, and let all the people go every man to his place.

8 They took the people's provision and their trumpets, and every man of Israel went each to his tent, and three hundred men held firm, and the camp of Midian was below in the valley.

9 And it came to pass that night, that the LORD said to him, "Arise, go down into the camp; for I have given it into your hand."

10 But if you are afraid to go down, go down, you and your servant Phurah, to the camp.

11 And you shall hear what they say, and afterward your hand shall be strengthened, and you shall go down to the camp, and he went down, he and Phurah his servant, to the edge of the companies that were in the camp.

12 And Midian and Amalek and all the people of the east fell into the valley like locusts in multitude, and their camels were without number, like the sand on the shore of the sea in abundance.

13 Gideon came and behold a certain man was telling his companion a dream and he said behold a dream I dreamed and behold a barley bread tumbled into the camp of Midian and came to the tent and struck it and it fell and turned it upside down and the tent fell.

14 And his companion answered and said, This is nothing other than the sword of Gideon son of Joash, the man of Israel, which God has delivered into his hand, along with Midian and all the camp.

15 When Gideon heard the account of the dream and its interpretation, he bowed down and returned to the camp of Israel. He said, "Arise, for the LORD has given the camp of Midian into your hand."

16 And he divided the three hundred men into three companies, and he gave trumpets into the hands of all of them, and empty pitchers, and torches inside the pitchers.

17 And he said to them, From me you shall see, and so you shall do: Behold, I am coming to the edge of the camp, and as I do, so you shall do.

18 I will blow the trumpet, and all who are with me; and you also blow the trumpets all around the whole camp, and say, "For the LORD and for Gideon."

19 Gideon came with a hundred men who were with him to the edge of the camp, at the beginning of the middle watch, just as they had posted the guards; and they blew the trumpets and broke the pitchers that were in their hands.

20 They blew the three bands with the trumpets and broke the pitchers, and they held in their left hand the torches, and in their right hand the trumpets to blow, and they cried, "A sword for the LORD and for Gideon."

21 So each man stood in his place around the camp, and all the camp ran, and cried out, and fled.

22 They blew three hundred cornets, and the LORD set every man's sword against his fellow and against all the host; and the army fled as far as Beth-shittah, Zererath, and as far as the brink of Abel-meholah, Tabbath.

23 A certain man of Israel cried out from Naphtali and from Asher and from all Manasseh, and they pursued after Midian.

24 And Gideon sent ambassadors throughout all the hill country of Ephraim, saying, "Come down against Midian and seize the waters for yourselves as far as Beth-barah and the Jordan." So all the men of Ephraim cried out and seized the waters for themselves as far as Beth-barah and the Jordan.

25 They captured two Midianite captains, Oreb and Zeeb; they killed Oreb at Oreb's rock and Zeeb at Zeeb's winepress; they pursued Midian and brought the heads of Oreb and Zeeb to Gideon beyond the Jordan.


## Chapter 8

1 And the men of Ephraim said to him, "What is this thing that you have done to us, that you did not call us when you went to fight against Midian?" and they contended with him sharply.

2 Then he said to them, “What have I done now, in comparison of you, O Abiezer? Is not the gleaning of the grapes of Ephraim better than the vintage of Abiezer?”

3 By your hand God has delivered into your power the princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; and what could I do compared to you? Then their spirit sank from them when he spoke this word.

4 Gideon crossed the Jordan, he and the three hundred men who were with him, all weary and pursuing.

5 He said to the men of Succoth, “Please give loaves of bread to the people who are with me, for they are weary, and I am pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.”

6 The captains of Succoth said, "Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your power, that we should give bread to your army?"

7 Gideon said, "Therefore, when the LORD gives Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will thresh your bodies with the thorns of the wilderness and with briers."

8 He went up from there to Penuel and spoke to the men of Penuel in the same way, and the men of Penuel answered him as the men of Succoth had answered him.

9 And he said also to the men of Penuel, saying, When I return in peace, I will tear down this tower.

10 And Zebah and Zalmunna were at Karkor, and their armies with them, about fifteen thousand, all that were left of the army of the people of the east; and the slain were one hundred and twenty thousand men who drew sword.

11 Gideon went along the way of the tent dwellings eastward to Nobah and Jogbehah and struck the camp while the camp was secure.

12 And Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued after them; and he captured the two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and the entire army he routed.

13 Gideon son of Joash returned from the battle, from the ascent of Heres.

14 And a lad from the men of Succoth was captured, and he questioned him; and he wrote down for him the princes of Succoth and its elders, seventy-seven men.

15 He came to the men of Succoth and said, "Behold, Zebah and Zalmunna, whom you taunted me about, saying, 'Are the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna now in your power, that we should give bread to your men who are weary?"

16 And he took the elders of the city and the thorns of the wilderness and the briers, and he made known to them the men of Succoth.

17 And he tore down the tower of Penuel and struck down the men of the city.

18 And he said to Zebah and to Zalmunna, "Where are the men whom you killed at Tabor?" and they said, "As you are, so were they; each one resembled the form of the sons of the king."

19 He said, My brothers are my mother's sons; as the LORD lives, if you had let them live, I would not have killed you.

20 And he said to Jether, his firstborn, "Rise, kill them," but the lad did not draw his sword, for he was afraid because he was still a young man.

21 Then Zebah and Zalmunna said, “Rise up, you yourself, and attack us, for as the man, so is his might.” So Gideon rose up and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and he took the moon-shaped ornaments that were on the necks of their camels.

22 And each man of Israel said to Gideon, Rule over us, even you and your son and your son's son also, for you have saved us from the hand of Midian.

23 Gideon said to them, I will not rule over you, nor will my son rule over you; the LORD will rule over you.

24 Gideon said to them, "I would like to make a request of you: each man give me an earring from his spoil, for they had gold earrings, because they were Ishmaelites."

25 They said, "Give! We will give!" and they spread out the garment and each man threw his spoil jewel upon it.

26 The full weight of the golden earrings that he requested was one thousand seven hundred shekels of gold, apart from the ornaments and collars and purple apparel that were on the kings of Midian, and apart from the chains that were on the necks of their camels.

27 And Gideon made it into an ephod and set it up in his town at Ophrah, and all Israel played the harlot after it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and to his household.

28 Midian was subdued before the sons of Israel, and they did not continue to lift up their head; and the land was quiet forty years in the days of Gideon.

29 Jerubbaal the son of Joash went and lived in his home.

30 And to Gideon there were seventy sons born of his loins, for he had many wives.

31 And his concubine who was in Shechem also bore him a son, and he set his name as Abimelech.

32 Gideon the son of Joash died in a good old age and was buried in the grave of Joash his father in Ophrah of the Abiezrite.

33 After Gideon died, the sons of Israel turned back and played the harlot after the Baalim, and they set up Baal-berith as their god.

34 The sons of Israel did not remember the LORD their God, who delivered them from the hand of all their enemies round about.

35 They did not show favour to the house of Jerubbaal Gideon, in accordance with all the good that he had done for Israel.


## Chapter 9

1 Abimelech the son of Jerubbaal went to Shechem to his mother's brothers and spoke to them and to all the family of his mother's father, saying:

2 Speak now in the hearing of all the men of Shechem: Which is better for you, that seventy men, all the sons of Jerubbaal, rule over you, or that one man rule over you? Remember that I am your own flesh and bone.

3 And his mother's brothers spoke to him these words in the hearing of all the lords of Shechem, and the heart of them inclined after Abimelech, for they said, He is our brother.

4 They gave him seventy silver coins from the court of Baal-berith, and Abimelech hired with them worthless and reckless men, and they went after him.

5 He went to his father's house at Ophrah and killed his brothers the sons of Jerubbaal, seventy men, on one stone, but Jotham the youngest son of Jerubbaal was left, for he had hidden himself.

6 All the people of Shechem and all the household of Millo gathered together and went and made Abimelech king, at the oak that stands at Shechem.

7 They told Jotham, and he went and stood at the top of Mount Gerizim and lifted his voice and cried out and said to them, "Hear me, lords of Shechem, and God will hear you."

8 The trees went to anoint a king over them, and they said to the olive tree, "Rule over us."

9 Then said the olive tree to them, I cease from my fatness, with which they honor God and also men; and indeed I will go to be moved upon the trees.

10 And the trees said to the fig tree, Go, reign over us.

11 And the fig tree said to them, I have ceased my sweetness and my good fruit, and I will go to wave over the trees.

12 Then the trees said to the vine, Go, you, be our king.

13 And the vine said to them, I have ceased from my (new, sweet) wine, which brings joy to God (gods) and people, and I will go to wave over the trees.

14 And all the trees said to the bramble, "Come, you be king over us."

15 The bramble said to the trees, If truly you anoint me king over you, come, take refuge in my shade; but if not, fire will come out from the bramble and consume the cedars of Lebanon.

16 Now therefore, if in truth and integrity you have acted, and have made Abimelech king, and if good you have done with Jerubbaal and with his house, and if according to the recompense of his hands you have dealt with him.

17 The chief fought for you and risked his life before you and delivered you from the power of Midian.

18 But you have risen up today against the household of my father and have killed his seventy sons, men, on one stone, and have made Abimelech son of your female servant king over the lords of Shechem because he is your brother.

19 And if you have truly and uprightly dealt with Jerubbaal and with his household this day, then rejoice in Abimelech, and may he also rejoice in you.

20 But if fire does not come out from Abimelech and consume the lords of Shechem and the house of Millo, then let fire come out from the lords of Shechem and from the house of Millo and consume Abimelech.

21 Jotham fled and escaped and went to Beer and lived there because of Abimelech his brother.

22 Abimelech ruled over Israel three years.

23 God sent a spirit of evil between Abimelech and the men of Shechem, and the men of Shechem dealt treacherously with Abimelech.

24 The coming of the violence against the seventy sons of Jerubbaal and their blood to be laid upon Abimelech their brother who killed them, and upon the lords of Shechem who strengthened his hand to kill his brothers.

25 The lords of Shechem set up an ambush for him on the hilltops and they plundered everyone who passed by them along the way and it was reported to Abimelech.

26 Gaal son of Ebed came with his brothers and passed through Shechem, and the men of Shechem put their trust in him.

27 They went out into the field and gathered the increase of their vineyards and trod it and made merry and came to the temple of their gods and ate and drank and cursed Abimelech.

28 Gaal son of Ebed said, "Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal, and Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor, the father of Shechem; why should we serve him?"

29 Who will put this folk into my hand, that I may seize Abimelech? And he said to Abimelech, "Great is your host, go out!"

30 Zebul, the captain of the city, heard the words of Gaal son of Ebed, and his anger was kindled.

31 He sent messengers to Abimelech at Shechem, saying, "Behold, Gaal son of Ebed and his brothers have come to Shechem and are stirring up the city against you."

32 Now arise by night, you and the folk who are with you, and lie in wait in the field.

33 And it shall be in the morning at the rising of the sun, you shall rise early and rush upon the city; and behold, he and the people who are with him will come out to you, and you shall do to him according to what your hand shall find.

34 And Abimelech arose, and all the people that were with him, by night, and they lay in wait against Shechem, four bands.

35 Gaal son of Ebed went out and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city, and Abimelech and the people who were with him rose up from the lurking place.

36 Gaal saw the folk and said to Zebul, "Behold, folk are coming down from the tops of the mountains." Zebul said to him, "You are seeing the shade of the mountains; you take them for men."

37 And Gaal spoke again, saying, “Look, folk are coming down from the midst of the land, and one band is advancing by the way of Elon Meonenim.”

38 Then Zebul said to him, "Where now is your mouth, according to which you said, 'Who is Abimelech that we should serve him?' Is not this the people you despised? Go out now and fight with them."

39 Gaal went out before the lords of Shechem and fought with Abimelech.

40 Abimelech pursued him, and he fled from his presence, and many fell slain as far as the entrance of the gate.

41 Abimelech settled in Arumah, and Zebul drove out Gaal and his kindred from dwelling in Shechem.

42 And it came to pass on the morrow, that the folk went out into the field, and it was told to Abimelech.

43 He took the folk and divided them into three bands and lay in wait in the country and saw, and behold, the folk coming out from the city, and he rose against them and struck them.

44 Abimelech and the chiefs who were with him advanced and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city, and two of the chiefs advanced against all who were in the field and struck them.

45 And Abimelech fought against the city all the day and captured the city and struck down the people who were in it and tore down the city and sowed it with salt.

46 And all the lords of the tower of Shechem heard, and they came to the hold of the house of Berith.

47 It was told to Abimelech that all the lords of the tower of Shechem had gathered together.

48 Abimelech went up to Mount Zalmon, he and all the people who were with him, and Abimelech took the axes in his hand and cut down a bough of trees and lifted it and placed it on his shoulder, and said to the people who were with him, "What you have seen me do, quickly do likewise."

49 And all the people also each a bough took and went after Abimelech and put upon the hold and set on fire the hold with the fire and died also all the men of the tower of Shechem, a thousand men and women.

50 Abimelech went to Thebez, encamped against Thebez, and captured it.

51 And a tower of strength was in the middle of the city, and all the men and the women and all the owners of the city fled there, and they shut themselves in behind it, and they went up upon the roof of the tower.

52 Abimelech came to the tower and fought against it; he approached the entrance of the tower to burn it with fire.

53 Then a certain woman cast a piece of an upper millstone on Abimelech's head and crushed his skull.

54 And he hastily called to the young man, his armour-bearer, and said to him, “Draw your sword and kill me, lest they say to me, ‘A woman killed him.’” And his servant pierced him, and he died.

55 The men of Israel saw that Abimelech was dead, and each man went to his place.

56 And God returned the adversity that Abimelech had done to his father in killing seventy of his brothers.

57 And all the evil of the men of Shechem did God repay upon their own heads, and upon them came the curse of Jotham son of Jerubbaal.


## Chapter 10

1 After Abimelech died, Tola son of Puah son of Dodo, a man of Issachar, arose to save Israel; and he lived in Shamir in the hill country of Ephraim.

2 He judged Israel for twenty-three years, and he died and was buried in Shamir.

3 Then Jair the Gileadite arose after him and judged Israel twenty-two years.

4 He had thirty sons who rode on thirty donkeys, and they had thirty donkeys for themselves; they were called Havoth-jair until this day, which is in the land of Gilead.

5 Jair died and was buried in Camon.

6 The sons of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD and served Baalim and Ashtaroth and the gods of Aram and the gods of Sidon and the gods of Moab and the gods of the sons of Ammon and the gods of the Philistines, and they abandoned the LORD and did not serve him.

7 The LORD became angry with Israel and sold them into the hand of the Philistines and into the hand of the sons of Ammon.

8 And they oppressed and crushed the people of Israel that year, eighteen years, all the people of Israel who were beyond the Jordan in the land of the Amorites, which is in Gilead.

9 The Ammonites crossed the Jordan to fight also in Judah and Benjamin and in the house of Ephraim, and Israel was greatly afflicted.

10 The sons of Israel cried out to the LORD, saying, We have sinned against you, for we have forsaken our God and served the Baalim.

11 The LORD said to the children of Israel, Did I not deliver you from the Egyptians, and from the Amorites, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines?

12 The Sidonians, Amalek, and the Maonites harassed you, and you cried out to me, and I delivered you from their power.

13 But you have forsaken me and served other gods, therefore I will no longer save you.

14 Go, cry out to the gods whom you have chosen; let them save you in your time of distress.

15 The sons of Israel said to the LORD, We have sinned; do to us whatever seems best in your eyes; only deliver us, we pray, this day.

16 They removed the foreign gods from among them and served the LORD, and his soul became impatient with the toil of Israel.

17 The Ammonites cried out and encamped in Gilead, and the people of Israel gathered and encamped in Mizpah.

18 And the folk, the captains of Gilead, said each man to his companion: Who is the man who will begin to fight against the sons of Ammon? He shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.


## Chapter 11

1 Jephthah the Gileadite was a mighty warrior, but he was the son of a prostitute, and Gilead became the father of Jephthah.

2 The wife of Gilead bore him sons, and the sons of the woman grew up and drove out Jephthah and said to him, You shall not inherit in the house of our father, for a son of another woman are you.

3 Jephthah fled from the face of his brothers and dwelt in the land of Tob, and certain men gathered to Jephthah and went out with him.

4 And it came to pass after a while that the sons of Ammon fought against Israel.

5 When the Ammonites fought against Israel, the elders of Gilead went to take Jephthah from the land of Tob.

6 They said to Jiphtah, Come and be our ruler, and we will fight against the Ammonites.

7 Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, "Did you not hate me and drive me away from my father's house? Why then have you come to me now that you are in distress?"

8 The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, Therefore now we have turned to you, that you may go with us and fight against the Ammonites, and you shall be head over all the inhabitants of Gilead.

9 Jephthah said to the elders of Gilead, "If you bring me back to fight against the Ammonites and the LORD delivers them before me, then I will be your ruler."

10 The elders of Gilead said to Jephthah, "The LORD will be the one to hear between us, if not according to your word so we do."

11 Jephthah went with the elders of Gilead, and the people made him head and ruler over them, and Jephthah spoke all his words before the LORD at Mizpah.

12 Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites and said, "What do you have against me that you have come to fight me in my land?"

13 The king of the Ammonites said to the messengers of Jephthah, "Because Israel took my land when he came up from Egypt, from the Arnon to the Jabbok and to the Jordan. Now therefore, restore them to me in peace."

14 And again Jephthah sent messengers to the king of the Ammonites.

15 He said to him, Thus says Jephthah, Israel did not take the land of Moab nor the land of the Ammonites.

16 When they went up from Egypt, Israel went through the wilderness to the Sea of Reeds and came to Kadesh.

17 Israel sent ambassadors to the king of Edom, saying, "Please let me pass through your land," but the king of Edom would not listen; and he also sent to the king of Moab, but he too refused; so Israel stayed at Kadesh.

18 He went through the wilderness and turned around the land of Edom and the land of Moab, and came from the east side of the sun to the land of Moab and camped beyond the Arnon; but they did not come into the border of Moab, for the Arnon is the border of Moab.

19 Israel sent ambassadors to Sihon king of the Amorites, king of Heshbon, and Israel said to him, "We will pass through your land to our place."

20 But Sihon did not believe Israel to pass through his border, so Sihon gathered all his people and encamped at Jahaz, and fought against Israel.

21 And the LORD, God of Israel, gave Sihon and all his people into the hand of Israel, and they struck them down; and Israel took possession of all the land of the Amorite who lived in that land.

22 They took possession of all the territory of the Amorite from the Arnon to the Jabbok and from the wilderness to the Jordan.

23 And now, O Jehovah, God of Israel, Thou hast driven out the Amorite from before Thy people Israel, and Thou shalt possess it.

24 Is it not what Chemosh your god gives you that you will possess, and all that the LORD our God has driven out before us, that we possess?

25 And now, are you any better than Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab? Did he ever strive with Israel, or did he ever fight against them?

26 While Israel was dwelling in Heshbon and in its villages, and in Aroer and in its villages, and in all the towns that are along the banks of the Arnon, three hundred years—why did you not deliver them at that time?

27 Yet I have not sinned against you, but you are doing adversity to me by fighting against me; may the LORD judge this day between the children of Israel and the children of Ammon.

28 The king of the Ammonites did not listen to the words of Jephthah that he sent to him.

29 And the spirit of the LORD came upon Jephthah, and he passed through Gilead and Manasseh, and passed on to Mizpeh of Gilead, and from Mizpeh of Gilead he crossed over against the children of Ammon.

30 Jephthah vowed a vow to the LORD and said, If You will indeed give the sons of Ammon into my hand,

31 And it shall be, the one who comes out from the doors of my house to meet me when I return in peace from the Ammonites shall belong to the LORD, and I will offer him as a burnt offering.

32 Jephthah went over to the sons of Ammon to fight against them and the LORD gave them into his hand.

33 They struck them from Aroer to Minnith, twenty towns, as far as the plain of the vineyards, with a very great slaughter, and the Ammonites were subdued before the Israelites.

34 Jephthah came to his home in Mitspah, and suddenly his daughter was coming out to meet him with timbrels and dancing; she was his only child; he had no son or daughter besides her.

35 When he saw her, he tore his clothes and said, “Alas, my daughter! You have brought me very low; you are among my most bitter enemies. I have opened my mouth to the LORD, and I cannot take back my vow.

36 And she said to him, My chief, you have opened your mouth to the LORD; do to me according to what has come from your mouth, after the LORD has taken vengeance for you on the Ammonites.

37 And she said to her father, Let this matter be done for me: stay away from me two months, and I will go and go down to the mountains and weep for my virginity, I.

38 And he said, Go; and he sent her away for two months, and she went with her companions, and she wept on the mountains for her virginity.

39 After two months she returned to her father, who carried out the vow he had made concerning her; she had not known any man, and it became an ordinance in Israel.

40 From age to age the daughters of Israel went to lament the daughter of Jephthah the Gileadite four days in the year.


## Chapter 12

1 Then a certain man of Ephraim cried out and passed northward, and they said to Jephthah, "Why have you crossed to fight against the Ammonites, and to us you did not call to go with you? With fire we will burn your house over you."

2 Jephthah said to them, "I and my people had a great controversy with the people of Ammon, and I cried out to you, but you did not deliver me from their hand."

3 I saw that you were not delivering, so I put my life in my hand and went over to the Ammonites, and the LORD delivered them into my hand; and why do you come up against me today to fight with me?

4 Jephthah gathered all the people of Gilead and fought against Ephraim, and the people of Gilead struck down Ephraim because the fugitives of Ephraim said, "You are fugitives of Gilead, in the midst of Ephraim, in the midst of Manasseh."

5 Gilead captured the fords of the Jordan from the Ephraimites; and when fugitives of Ephraim said, "Let me pass," the men of Gilead said to him, "Are you an Ephraimite?" and he said, "No."

6 They said to him, “Say now ‘Shibboleth.’” But he said “Sibboleth” and could not speak it correctly. So they seized him and slaughtered him at the fords of the Jordan; and at that time forty-two thousand Ephraimites fell.

7 Jephthah judged Israel six years and Jephthah the Gileadite died and was buried in the towns of Gilead.

8 Ibzan from Bethlehem judged Israel after him.

9 He had thirty sons and thirty daughters; he sent them outside, and thirty daughters he brought in for his sons from abroad, and he judged Israel seven years.

10 Ibzan died and was buried in Bethlehem.

11 Then Elon the Zebulonite judged Israel after him, and he judged Israel ten years.

12 Elon the Zebulonite died and was buried in Aijalon in the land of Zebulun.

13 After him Abdon son of Hillel the Pirathonite judged Israel.

14 He had forty sons and thirty grandsons riding on seventy young asses, and he judged Israel eight years.

15 Abdon son of Hillel the Pirathonite died and was buried in Pirathon in the land of Ephraim, on the hill of the Amalekite.


## Chapter 13

1 The sons of Israel continued to do evil in the sight of the LORD, and the LORD delivered them into the hand of the Philistines for forty years.

2 A certain man from Zorah, of the family of the Danites, and his name was Manoah, and his wife was barren and had not borne a child.

3 An ambassador of the LORD appeared to the woman and said to her, "Behold, you are childless and have not borne a child, but you will conceive and bear a son."

4 And now, please be careful and do not drink wine or strong drink, and do not eat any unclean thing.

5 For behold, you are with child and shall bear a son, and no razor shall come upon his head, for the boy shall be a separate one of God from the womb; and he shall begin to deliver Israel from the hand of the Philistines.

6 The woman came and said to her husband, “The man of God came to me, and the appearance of him was like the appearance of an angel of God, very awesome; but I did not ask him where he was from, and his name he did not tell me.”

7 He said to me, "You are with child and shall bear a son; now do not drink wine or strong drink, and do not eat any unclean thing, for the boy shall be a Nazarite to God from the womb until the day of his death."

8 Manoah entreated Jehovah and said, "Oh (my) Lord, let the man of God who You did send come again to us and instruct us what we shall do with the child to be born."

9 God heard the voice of Manoah, and the angel of God came again to the woman while she was sitting in the field, and Manoah her husband was not with her.

10 And the woman hastened and ran and told her husband, and said to him, Behold, the man who came to me on that day has appeared to me.

11 Manoah rose and went after his wife and came to the man and said to him, "Are you the man who spoke to the woman?" And he said, "I am."

12 Manoah said, "Now let your word come true: what will be the condition of the boy and his work?"

13 The LORD's messenger said to Manoah, "In regard to all that I said to the woman, she must take care."

14 From all that proceeds from the vine of wine you shall not eat, and wine and strong drink you shall not drink, and any filthiness you shall not eat; whatsoever I have commanded her, she shall keep.

15 Manoah said to the ambassador of the LORD, Please wait, and let us prepare a kid of (she) goats before you.

16 The LORD's messenger said to Manoah, If you detain me, I will not eat your bread; and if you prepare a burnt offering to the LORD, you must present it. For Manoah did not know that he was the LORD's messenger.

17 Manoah said to the messenger of the LORD, "What is your name, so that we may honor you when your word comes to pass?"

18 The ambassador of the LORD said to him, "Why do you ask for my name? It is wonderful."

19 Manoah took the kid of the goats and the gift, and offered it upon the rock to the LORD, who performed wonders in doing so, and Manoah and his wife looked on.

20 And it happened, when the flame went up toward the heavens from the altar, the angel of the LORD went up in the flame of the altar; and Manoah and his wife saw, and they fell on their faces to the ground.

21 And the ambassador of the LORD did not appear again to Manoah and to his wife; then Manoah knew that he was the ambassador of the LORD.

22 Manoah said to his wife, "We will surely die, for we have seen God."

23 And his wife said to him, "If the LORD had desired to kill us, he would not have taken from our hand a burnt offering and a gift, nor shown us all these things, and now he would not have told us such a thing."

24 The woman bore a son and called his name Samson. The boy grew, and the LORD blessed him.

25 And the spirit of the LORD began to move him in the camp of Dan, between Zorah and Eshtaol.


## Chapter 14

1 Samson went down to Timnah and saw a woman in Timnah from the daughters of the Philistines.

2 He went up and told his father and mother, saying, “I saw a woman in Timnah, one of the daughters of the Philistines; now take her for me as a wife.”

3 His father and his mother said to him, Is there not a woman among the daughters of your brothers and among all my people, that you must go to take a woman from the uncircumcised Philistines? And Samson said to his father, Take her for me, for she is right in my eyes.

4 His father and his mother did not know that it was from the LORD, for an occasion he was seeking from the Philistines; and at that time the Philistines were ruling over Israel.

5 Samson went down, and his father and his mother, to Timnath, and they came to the vineyards of Timnath, and behold, a young lion of lions roared against him.

6 The LORD's spirit came upon him, and he tore the kid apart as one tears a young goat, though nothing was in his hand; yet he did not tell his father or his mother what he had done.

7 And he went down and spoke to the woman, and she was right in Samson's eyes.

8 He returned from the journey to take her, and turned aside to see the carcass of the lion, and behold, a swarm of bees in the body of the lion and honey.

9 He handed it over in his palm and went on his way to eat, then went to his father and mother, gave it to them, and they ate; but he did not tell them that the honey came from the carcass of the lion.

10 Then her father went down to the woman, and Samson made a feast there, for thus the young men used to do.

11 And it was, when they saw him, that they took thirty companions, and they were with him.

12 Then Samson said to them, Let me propose a dark saying to you: if you can tell me the answer within the seven days of the feast, and solve it, then I will give you thirty fine linen sheets and thirty changes of apparel.

13 But if you cannot tell me, then you shall give me thirty sheets of fine linen and thirty changes of apparel; and they said to him, "Put forth your riddle, that we may hear it."

14 He said to them, Out of the eater came forth food, and out of the strong came forth sweetness; and they could not declare the riddle for three days.

15 On the seventh day they said to the wife of Samson, "Entice your husband so that he may tell us the riddle, or else we will burn you and your father's house with fire. Have you brought us here to impoverish us?"

16 And the wife of Samson wept over him and said, "You surely hate me and do not love me; you have put forth a riddle to the sons of my people, but to me you have not revealed it." And he said to her, "Behold, I have not revealed it to my father or my mother—should I reveal it to you?"

17 And she wept for him seven days, the days of their feast; and it came to pass on the seventh day, that he told her that she distressed him, and she told the riddle to the sons of her people.

18 And the men of the city said to him on the seventh day, before the sun came, What is sweeter than honey, and what is stronger than a lion? And he said to them, If you had not plowed with my heifer, you would not have found my riddle.

19 The air of the LORD came upon him and he went down to Ashkelon and struck thirty of them, took their armour, and gave the change to those who told the riddle; and his anger burned and he went up to his father's house.

20 And the wife of Samson became to him a companion.


## Chapter 15

1 And it came to pass in the days of wheat harvest that Samson visited his wife with a kid, and said, “I will go in to my wife in the inner chamber,” but her father would not let him come in.

2 And her father said, I said surely you hate her; therefore I gave her to your companion: is not the younger sister better than she? Let her now be to you instead of her.

3 Then Samson said to them, "This time I shall be blameless toward the Philistines when I do them harm."

4 Samson went and captured three hundred foxes and took torches and turned tail to tail and put one torch in the middle between the two tails.

5 He set fire to the torches and sent it into the standing corn of the Philistines, and he burned up the shocks and the standing corn and the vineyards of olives.

6 The Philistines said, Who has done this? And they said, Samson the husband, the Timnite, because he took his wife and gave her to his companion. And the Philistines went up and burned her and her father with fire.

7 Samson said to them, If you do this, I will surely take vengeance on you, and afterward I will stop.

8 He struck them on the hip and thigh with a great blow, and he went down and dwelt in the top of the rocky stronghold of Etam.

9 The Philistines went up and camped in Judah, and were spread out at Lehi.

10 And the men of Judah said to them, "Why have you come up against us?" And they said, "We have come up to bind Samson, to do to him as he did to us."

11 Three thousand men of Judah went down to the top of the rocky cliff of Etam, and said to Samson, "Do you not know that the Philistines rule over us? What then is this that you have done to us?" And he said to them, "As they did to me, so have I done to them."

12 They said to him, "We have come down to bind you, to deliver you into the hands of the Philistines." And Samson said to them, "Swear to me, lest you attack me yourselves."

13 They said to him, saying, "No, surely you are bound and we will deliver you into their hand, and you will not die." So they bound him with two new ropes and brought him up from the rocky stronghold.

14 He came to Lehi, and the Philistines came shouting to meet him; then the spirit of the LORD rushed upon him, and the ropes that were on his arms became like flax that burns in the fire, and his bindings melted from upon his hands.

15 He found a fresh donkey's jawbone, stretched out his hand and took it, and struck a thousand men with it.

16 Samson said, "With the jawbone of an ass, heap upon heap, with the jawbone of an ass I have struck down a thousand men."

17 When he had finished speaking, he threw the jawbone from his hand and called that place Ramath-lehi.

18 And he was exceedingly thirsty and called to the LORD and said, You have given this great deliverance into the hand of Your servant, and now I will die of thirst and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised.

19 And God split the hollow place which was at Lehi, and water came out of it, and he drank, and his spirit returned, and he lived; therefore he called its name En-hakhore which is at Lehi to this day.

20 He judged Israel in the age of the Philistines twenty years.


## Chapter 16

1 Samson went to Gaza and saw there a woman, a harlot, and went in to her.

2 To the Gazites, saying, "Samson has come here"; so they surrounded and lay in wait for him all night at the gate of the city, and were silent all night, saying, "Until the light of the morning—then we will kill him."

3 Samson lay until the middle of the night, then arose in the middle of the night and seized the doors of the city gate and the two doorposts, pulled them out along with the bar, placed them on his shoulders, and carried them up to the top of the hill that is before Hebron.

4 Afterward he loved a woman in the valley of Sorek, and her name was Delilah.

5 The lords of the Philistines went up to her and said to her, "Entice him and see wherein his great strength lies, and by what means we may overpower him, so that we can bind him to afflict him; and each of us will give you eleven hundred pieces of silver."

6 Then Delilah said to Samson, "Please tell me how great your strength is, and by what means you might be bound to afflict you."

7 Samson said to her, If they bind me with seven fresh cords that have not been dried, then I will become weak and be like one of the common sort of men.

8 The lords of the Philistines brought up to her seven fresh cords that had not been dried, and she bound him with them.

9 And the ambush sat for her in the inner chamber, and she said to him, "Philistines upon you, Samson!" and he tore the cords like the tearing of the thread of the tow when it smells of fire, and his strength was not known.

10 Then Delilah said to Samson, "Behold, you have mocked me and spoken to me lies; now tell me, I pray you, by what means you might be bound."

11 And he said to her, If they bind me with new cords that have not been used for work, then I will become weak and be like any other person.
