12 Then Delilah took new ropes and bound him with them, and said to him, "Philistines upon you, Samson!" while the ambush lay waiting in the inner chamber; but he snapped them from upon his arms like a thread.

13 Then Delilah said to Samson, "How long will you mock me and tell me lies? Tell me how you can be bound." And he said to her, "If you weave the seven locks of my head with the web."

14 And she drove the pin into the beam and said to him, "The Philistines are upon you, Samson!" And he awoke from his sleep and pulled out the pin of the beam and the web.

15 And she said to him, How can you say, I love you, when your heart is not with me? These three times you have mocked me, and you have not told me wherein your great strength lies.

16 And it was, because she pressed him with her words all the days, that she forced him, and his soul was shortened to die.

17 He told her all his heart and said to her, "A razor has not come upon my head, for I am a Nazarite of God from my mother's womb; if I am shaved, my strength will leave me and I will grow weak and be like any other man."

18 And Delilah saw that he had revealed to her all his mind, so she sent and called the lords of the Philistines, saying, "Come up this once, for he has revealed to me all his mind." Then the lords of the Philistines came up to her, and the silver was in their hands.

19 She made him sleep on her knees, and called to a man, and shaved off seven locks of his head, and began to afflict him, and his strength departed from him.

20 The Philistine said to Samson, “Awake from your sleep,” And he awoke from his sleep and said, “I will go out as at other times and shake myself free,” but he did not know that the Lord had departed from him.

21 The Philistine took him and gouged out his eyes and brought him down to Gaza and bound him with brass fetters and he was grinding in the prison house.

22 The hair of his head began to grow again as it had been when he was shaved.

23 The lords of the Philistines gathered to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god and to rejoice, and they said, "Our god has given into our hand Samson, our enemy."

24 The folk saw him and praised their God, saying, "Our God has delivered into our hand our enemy and the one who devastated our land and greatly multiplied our slain."

25 And when their hearts were merry, they said, "Call Samson that he may entertain us." So they called Samson from the dungeon, and he performed for them; and they positioned him between the pillars.

26 Samson said to the boy who was holding him, Let me touch the pillars on which the house stands, that I may lean on them.

27 The house was full of men and women, and there were all the lords of the Philistines, and on the roof about three thousand men and women were watching Samson's sport.

28 Samson called to Jehovah and said, "O Lord God, remember me, I pray; strengthen me, I pray, only this once, O God, and let me be avenged with one vengeance for my two eyes upon the Philistines."

29 Then Samson grasped the two pillars, the middle ones upon which the house rested, and leaned upon them, one upon his right hand and one upon his left hand.

30 Samson said, "Let my life perish with the Philistines." Then he bent with all his strength, and the house fell upon the lords and upon all the people who were in it. The dead whom he killed at his death were more than those whom he killed in his life.

31 His brothers and all the house of his father came down and took him and brought him up and buried him in the burying place between Zorah and Eshtaol, in the grave of Manoah his father, and he judged Israel twenty years.


## Chapter 17

1 And there was a certain man from the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Micaiah.

2 And he said to his mother, "The thousand and hundred pieces of silver that were taken for you, I have not lied, and you also said in my hearing, 'Behold, the silver is with me; I have taken it,' and his mother said, "Blessed be my son by the LORD."

3 He returned the one thousand and one hundred silver coins to his mother, and his mother said, “I have consecrated that silver to the LORD from my hand for my son, to make a carved image and a molten image; now I return it to you.”

4 He returned the money to his mother, and his mother took two hundred silver coins and gave them to the silversmith, who made from it a carved image and a molten image, and it was in the house of Micaiah.

5 And the man Micah had a house of gods, and he made an ephod and teraphim, and he filled the hand of one of his sons, and he became to him a priest.

6 In those days there was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes.

7 And a boy was from Bethlehem Judah, of the family of Judah, and he was a Levite, and he was a sojourner there.

8 And the man went from the city, from Bethlehem Judah, to sojourn wherever he might find, and he came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, to do his journey.

9 Micah said to him, "Where are you from?" And the Levite said to him, "I am from Bethlehem in Judah, and I am going to live wherever I can find a place."

10 Micah said to him, "Stay with me, and be to me a chief and a priest; I will give you ten pieces of silver per year, and clothing, and your sustenance." So the Levite went.

11 The Leviite consented to dwell with the man, and the boy became to him like one of his sons.

12 And Micah filled the hand of the Levite, and the young man became his priest, and he was in the house of Micah.

13 Then said Micah, Now I know that the LORD will do me good, because the Leviite has become my own priest.


## Chapter 18

1 In those days there was no king in Israel and in those days the tribe of the Danites was seeking for itself an inheritance to dwell in for their inheritance had not fallen to them until that day among the tribes of Israel.

2 The Danites sent from their family five men, capable men from Zorah and Eshtaol, to spy out the land and to search it; and they said to them, "Go, search out the land." So they came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, and lodged there.

3 They came to the house of Micah and recognized the voice of the young Levite, so they turned aside there and said to him, Who brought you here, and what are you doing here, and what is your business here?

4 And he said to them, Thus and so has Micah done to me; he hired me, and I have been his priest.

5 And they said to him, “Please inquire of God, that we may know whether the way on which we are going will succeed.”

6 And the priest said to them, Go in peace; your way, which you take, is before the LORD.

7 And five of the men went and came to Laish, and saw the people who were in its midst dwelling in security, in the manner of the Sidonians, quiet and secure, and there was no one in the land who oppressed anything, nor was there any ruler; they were far from the Sidonians, and had no dealings with any person.

8 Then they came to their brothers in Zareah and Eshtaol, and their brothers said to them, "What are you?"

9 They said, "Come, let us go up against them, for we have seen the land and indeed it is very good; and you must not be sluggish, to go in and take possession of the land."

10 When you come, you shall come to a folk trusting, and the land a broad place of hands, for God has given it into your hand, a place where there is no lack of anything that is in the land.

11 They departed from there, from the family of the Danites, from Zorah and from Eshtaol, six hundred men girded with weapons of war.

12 They went up and camped in Kirjath-jearim in Judah; therefore they called that place Mahaneh-dan to this day; behold, behind Kirjath-jearim.

13 They passed from there to the hill country of Ephraim and came to the house of Micah.

14 And five of the men who had gone to scout out the land toward Laish answered their brothers: Do you know that in those houses there is an ephod, teraphim, a carved image, and a molten image? Now consider what you should do.

15 They turned aside there and came to the house of the Levite youth, the house of Micah, and asked him about his welfare.

16 and six hundred men girded with weapons of war stationed at the entrance of the gate who were from the sons of Dan

17 The five men who had gone to spy out the land went there and took the carved image, the ephod, the teraphim, and the molten image, while the priest stood at the entrance of the gate, and the six hundred men with weapons of war remained outside.

18 And these came to the house of Micah and took the carved image, the ephod, the teraphim, and the molten image, and the priest said to them, "What are you doing?"

19 They said to him, Be silent; put your hand over your mouth and go with us, and be a father and a priest to us; is it better for you to be priest to the house of one man, or to be priest to a tribe and family in Israel?

20 The priest's heart was glad, and he took the ephod and the teraphim and the carved image, and he went within the midst of the people.

21 Then they turned and departed, and placed the little children, the cattle, and the stately carriage ahead of them.

22 When they had gone far from the house of Micah, the men in the houses near Micah's house were called together, and they overtook the sons of Dan.

23 And they called to the sons of Dan, and they turned their faces, and said to Micah, What ails you that you cry out?

24 And he said, What gods have you taken for yourselves, and the priest? And you have gone away; what more is there for me? And what is this that you say to me? What is it to you?

25 The sons of Dan said to him, Do not let your voice be heard among us, lest men of bitter life attack you and you gather your life and the life of your household.

26 The sons of Dan went on their journey, and Micah saw that they were stronger than he; so he turned and returned to his home.

27 They took what Micah had made and the priest who belonged to him, and they came against Laish, against a quiet and unsuspecting people, and they struck them with the sword, and they burned the city with fire.

28 And there is no deliverer because it is far from Sidon, and they have no advice with any person, and it is in the valley which belongs to Beth-rehob; so they built the city and lived in it.

29 They named the city Dan, by the name of Dan their father, who was born to Israel, but formerly the city's name was Laish.

30 The sons of Dan established for themselves the carved image; Jonathan son of Gershom son of Manasseh and his sons were priests for the tribe of the Danites until the day of the land's exile.

31 They set up for themselves the carved image of Micah that he had made, all the days that the house of God was in Shiloh.


## Chapter 19

1 In those days there was no king in Israel, and a certain Levite was staying in the remote parts of the hill country of Ephraim, who took for himself a concubine from Bethlehem in Judah.

2 And his concubine played the harlot against him, and she departed from him to her father's house, to Bethlehem in Judah, and she was there four months.

3 Then her husband arose and went after her to speak to her heart, and his young man was with him and a couple of donkeys, and she brought him to her father's house, and the father of the young woman saw him and rejoiced at his coming.

4 And his father-in-law, the father of the young woman, held him, and he stayed with him three days; they ate and drank and lodged there.

5 On the fourth day, they rose early in the morning to depart, and the father of the young woman said to his son-in-law, "Strengthen your heart with a piece of bread, and then you may go."

6 So the father of the young woman said to the man, "Be pleased to stay the night and let your heart be glad."

7 Then the man rose to go, but his father-in-law urged him, so he turned back and spent the night there.

8 He rose early in the morning on the fifth day to depart, and the father of the young woman said, “Be comforted, I pray, in your heart, and tarry until the day declines”; so both of them ate.

9 Then the man rose to go, he and his concubine and his servant, and his father-in-law, the girl's father, said to him, "Behold now, the day has declined toward evening; stay the night, I pray you; behold, the day has drawn to a close—stay here and let your heart be comforted; and you shall rise early tomorrow for your journey, and go on to your tent."

10 But the man would not consent to spend the night there, so he rose up and went, and came opposite Jebus, that is, Jerusalem, and with him a couple of asses loaded, and his concubine with him.

11 They are with Jebus, and the day is very far spent, and the young man said to his master, Let us turn aside now to this Jebusite city and spend the night in it.

12 And his lord said to him, Do not turn aside to the foreign city which is not of the children of Israel, and we will pass on to Gibeah.

13 And he said to his servant, Go, let us go to one of the places, and we will lodge in Gibeah or in Ramah.

14 And they passed over and went on, and the sun came upon them at the hill which belonged to Benjamin.

15 And they turned aside there to enter Gibeah, the hill, and he came and sat in the broad place of the city, and there was no man taking them into the house to lodge.

16 And behold, a certain man came from his work from the field at evening, and the man was from the hill of Ephraim, and he was a sojourner in Gibeah, and the men of the place were Benjamites.

17 He lifted up his eyes and saw the man who was staying in the city at the broad place of the town, and the old man said, Where are you going and from where have you come?

18 And he said to him, We are passing from Bethlehem in Judah to the parts of the hill country of Ephraim; from there I am, and I am going to Bethlehem in Judah, and I am going to the house of the LORD, but there is no man gathering me to the house.

19 And also straw and provender are available for our asses, and bread and wine are mine and for your female servant and for the boy with your servants; there is no lack of anything.

20 And the old man said, Peace be with you, I will cover all your needs, only do not spend the night in the broad place.

21 And he brought him to his house and gave the donkeys fodder, and they washed their feet, and they ate and drank.

22 They had considered their hearts, but behold, certain men of the city, wicked men, surrounded the house, beating on the door, and said to the man, the master of the house, the old man, saying, “Bring out the man who came into your house, that we may know him.”

23 Then the man, the master of the house, came out to them and said to them, “No, my brothers, do not act vilely now; since this man has come into my house, do not do this vile thing.”

24 Behold, my daughter who is a virgin and her concubine, I will bring them out now; afflict them and do to them what seems good in your eyes, but to this man do not do the act of folly like this.

25 And the men did not consent to listen to him, so the man seized his concubine and brought her out to them abroad; they knew her and abused her all night long until the morning, and they let her go at daybreak.

26 The woman came early in the morning and fell at the entrance of the house of the man whose lord she was, until daylight.

27 His master rose in the morning, opened the doors of the house, and went out to go on his way, and behold, the woman, his concubine, lay fallen at the threshold of the house, her hands upon the doorpost.

28 He said to her, “Get up and let us go,” but there was no answer; so he took her up on the ass, and the man rose and went to his place.

29 He went to his home and took the knife and seized his concubine and dismembered her body into twelve parts and sent her throughout all the border of Israel.

30 And it shall be, everyone who sees shall say, It has not happened nor has it been seen, such a thing as this, from the day the children of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt until this day. Set yourselves to it, take counsel, and speak.


## Chapter 20

1 And all the people of Israel came out, and the whole assembly gathered together as one man, from Dan even to Beer-shebah and the land of Gilead, to the LORD at Mizpah.

2 And the bulwarks of all the people, all the tribes of Israel, stood in the assembly of the people of God, four hundred thousand foot soldiers, every man with drawn sword.

3 The sons of Benjamin heard that the sons of Israel had gone up to Mitspah, and the sons of Israel said, Tell us how this adversity happened.

4 And the man, the Levite, the husband of the murdered woman, answered and said, I came, I and my concubine, to Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin, to lodge.

5 The men of Gibeah rose against me and surrounded my house at night, intending to kill me; they abused my concubine and she died.

6 I seized my concubine and dismembered her, and I sent her throughout all the land of Israel’s inheritance, for they committed a heinous crime and folly in Israel.

7 Behold, all of you sons of Israel, bring forth a matter and counsel now.

8 And all the people rose as one man, saying, We will not go, each man to his tent, and each man will not turn aside to his house.

9 And now this is the act that we will do to Gibeah: upon her by lot.

10 And we will take ten men from every tribe of Israel and a hundred from the thousand and a thousand from the ten thousand to take provision for the people to go to Gibeah in Benjamin to do all the vile thing that he did in Israel.

11 All the men of Israel gathered to the city, as one man, companions.

12 The tribes of Israel sent men throughout all the tribes of Benjamin, saying, “What is this evil that has occurred among you?”

13 Now therefore give up the men, the sons of Belial who are in Gibeah, that we may put them to death and remove the evil from Israel; but Benjamin would not listen to the voice of their brothers, the sons of Israel.

14 The sons of Benjamin were gathered from the cities to Gibeah to go out to battle against the sons of Israel.

15 The sons of Benjamin were numbered on that day from the towns twenty-six thousand men who drew sword, besides the inhabitants of Gibeah who were numbered seven hundred chosen men.

16 From all this people, seven hundred chosen men, every one of them left-handed, all of whom could sling a stone at a hair and not miss.

17 Also, the men of Israel were mustered apart from Benjamin, four hundred thousand men who drew the sword, every one of them a warrior.

18 They rose and went up to Beth-el, and inquired of God; the people of Israel said, “Who shall go up for us first to battle against the people of Benjamin?” and the LORD said, “Judah first.”

19 The sons of Israel rose early in the morning and camped against the hill.

20 A certain man of Israel went out to battle against Benjamin, and the men of Israel arrayed themselves against them for battle at Gibeah.

21 The sons of Benjamin came out from Gibeah and struck down in Israel on that day twenty-two thousand men to the ground.

22 The folk, the men of Israel, strengthened themselves and again prepared for battle at the place where they had previously arrayed themselves on the first day.

23 The sons of Israel went up and wept before the LORD until evening, and they inquired of the LORD, saying, "Shall we again go out to battle against Benjamin our brother?" And the LORD said, "Go up against him."

24 The sons of Israel approached the sons of Benjamin on the second day.

25 Benjamin came out against them from Gibeah on the second day, and they struck down more eighteen thousand men of Israel to the ground, all of them sword-drawers.

26 All the people of Israel and all the folk came up to Beth-el, and they wept and sat there before the LORD; they fasted on that day until evening, and they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before the LORD.

27 The sons of Israel inquired of the LORD, and there the ark of the covenant of God was in those days.

28 Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron stood before him in those days, saying, "Shall I again go out to war against the sons of Benjamin my brother?" And the LORD said, "Go up, for tomorrow I will give him into your hand."

29 Israel set an ambush against Gibeah, the hill, on every side.

30 The sons of Israel went up against the sons of Benjamin on the third day and deployed themselves against Gibeah as at other times.

31 The sons of Benjamin went out against the people and drew away from the city, and began to strike down men from the people two by two on the highways, one of which goes up to Beth-el and one to Gibeah in the country, about thirty men of Israel.

32 The sons of Benjamin said, "They are struck down before us as at the first"; and the men of Israel said, "Let us flee and draw them away from the city to the highways."

33 And all the men of Israel rose up from their place and arrayed themselves at Baal-tamar, and the ambush of Israel rushed forth from its place out of the meadows of Gibeah.

34 They came from the direction of Gibeah, ten thousand chosen men from all Israel; and the battle was severe, but they did not know that adversity had come upon them.

35 And the LORD struck Benjamin before Israel, and the sons of Israel destroyed of Benjamin that day twenty-five thousand and a hundred men, all these drawing sword.

36 The sons of Benjamin saw that they were afflicted, and the men of Israel gave place to Benjamin, because they trusted in the ambush which they had set against Gibeah.

37 And the ambush hurried and rushed to Gibeah, the hill, and the ambush deployed and struck all the city with the edge of the sword.

38 And the appointed time was for a man of Israel with the ambush, a signal to raise up the sign of fire from the city.

39 Then the men of Israel turned in the battle, and Benjamin began to strike down men of Israel, about thirty men; for they said, "Surely he is struck down before us, as in the former battle."

40 And the sign of fire began to ascend from the city in a pillar of smoke, and Benjamin turned after him; and behold, the smoke of the city went up into the air.

41 And the men of Israel turned, and the men of Benjamin were dismayed, for they saw that evil had come upon them.

42 They turned before the men of Israel by the way of the wilderness, and the battle clung to them, and those from the cities were destroying them within.

43 They surrounded Benjamin; they pursued him; they trod him down at their resting place, as far as opposite Gibeah toward the sunrise.

44 And there fell from Benjamin eighteen thousand men, all of them valiant warriors.

45 They turned and fled toward the wilderness to the rocky stronghold of Rimmon, and they cut them down along the highways—five thousand men—and pursued them as far as Gidom and struck from them two thousand men.

46 And all who fell of Benjamin that day, twenty-five thousand men who drew the sword, all these were men of valor.

47 They turned and fled to the wilderness, six hundred men, and they fled to the rocky stronghold of Rimmon, and they stayed in the rocky stronghold of Rimmon four months.

48 Also, any man of Israel turned back to the sons of Benjamin and struck them with the edge of the sword, from city to cattle, to all that was found, and also all the cities that were found they sent out in fire.


## Chapter 21

1 And also, Israel swore at Mitspah, saying, No man among us shall give his daughter to Benjamin as wife.

2 The folk came to Beth-el and sat there until the evening before God and raised their voices and wept a great weeping.

3 They said, Why has this happened in Israel, O Jehovah, God of Israel, that one tribe of Israel should be missing today?

4 And it came to pass on the next day that the people rose early and built an altar there, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings.

5 The sons of Israel said, Who of all the tribes of Israel did not come up to the assembly before the LORD? For the great oath at Mizpah was against whoever did not come up to the LORD at Mizpah, saying, He shall surely be put to death.

6 The sons of Israel grieved for Benjamin their brother and said, Today one tribe has been cut off from Israel.

7 What shall we do for them concerning wives, since we have sworn by the LORD not to give them any of our daughters as wives?

8 And they said, Which one of the tribes of Israel did not come up to the LORD at Mizpah? And behold, no man came from Jabesh-gilead to the assembly.

9 And the people were mustered, but behold, there was no man among the inhabitants of Jabesh Gilead.

10 Then the assembly sent there twelve thousand men, mighty men among the sons of valor, and charged them, saying, Go and strike the inhabitants of Jabesh Gilead with the edge of the sword, both the women and the little children.

11 And this is the act that you shall do: every male and every woman who has known lying with a male you shall utterly destroy.

12 And they found among the inhabitants of Jobesh-Gilead four hundred maidens, virgins who had not known a man in lying with him; and they brought them to the camp at Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan.

13 And all the assembly sent word to the Benjaminites who were at the rocky stronghold of Rimmon and called to them peace.

14 Then Benjamin returned at that time, and they gave them the women whom they had spared from the women of Jabesh Gilead, but even so they did not find wives for them.

15 And the folk regretted Benjamin, because the LORD had made a breach among the tribes of Israel.

16 The elders of the assembly said, "What shall we do for wives for those who are left, since women have been cut off from Benjamin?"

17 They said, "Let there be an inheritance of the escaped remnant for Benjamin, and let not the tribe perish from Israel."

18 We are not able to give them wives from our daughters, because the sons of Israel have sworn, saying, “Cursed be anyone who gives a wife to Benjamin.”

19 They said, "Look, the solemn feast of the LORD at Shiloh recurs regularly year by year, north of Beth-el, east of the highway that goes up from Beth-el to Shechem, and south of Lebonah."

20 Go and lie in wait in the vineyards, you sons of Benjamin.

21 And you shall see, and behold, if the daughters of Shiloh come out to dance in the dances, then you shall come out from the vineyards and seize for yourselves each man his wife from the daughters of Shiloh, and you shall go to the land of Benjamin.

22 And it will be, when their chiefs or their brothers come to us and say to us, “Have pity on them,” we will say to them, “Grant them to us, for we did not take for each man his wife in the war; and you did not give them to them, so you are not guilty.”

23 So the sons of Benjamin did so, and took wives according to their number from the dancers whom they had seized, and they went and returned to their inheritance, and they built the cities and lived in them.

24 The children of Israel went from there at that time, each to his tribe and to his family, and they departed from there, each to his inheritance.

25 In those days there was no king in Israel; every man did what was right in his own eyes.


# RUTH

## Chapter 1

1 In the age of the judging of the judges there was a famine in the land and a certain man from Bethlehem Judah went to sojourn in the country of Moab he and his wife and his two sons.

2 The man’s name was Elimelech, and his wife’s name was Naomi, and the names of his two sons were Mahlon and Chilion, Ephrathites from Bethlehem in Judah; they came to the country of Moab and remained there.

3 Elimelech, Naomi's husband, died, and she was left with her two sons.

4 They took for themselves Moabite women as wives: the name of the one was Orpah and the name of the second was Ruth, and they lived there about ten years.

5 And both Mahlon and Chilion died, and the woman was left without her two sons and without her husband.

6 Then she and her daughters-in-law got up and returned from the country of Moab, for she had heard in the country of Moab that the LORD had visited his people to give them food.

7 And she departed from the place where she had been, and her two daughters-in-law with her; and they went on the way to return to the land of Judah.

8 Naomi said to her two daughters-in-law, "Go, return each woman to her mother's house. May the LORD show you kindness as you have dealt kindly with the dead and with me."

9 May the LORD grant you that each find rest, a wife in her husband's home; then she kissed them, and they raised their voices and wept.

10 And they said to her, "Indeed, we will return with you to your people."

11 Naomi said, “Return, my daughters! Why will you go with me? Are there still sons in my womb who could become husbands for you?”

12 Return, my daughters; go, for I am too old to have a husband, for I said I still have hope, even spent the night with a husband, and also bore sons.

13 Will you wait for them to grow up? Will you keep yourselves from having a husband? No, my daughters—for it is very bitter for me because the hand of the LORD has gone out against me.

14 And they raised their voices and wept again; Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth clung to her.

15 Then she said, "Behold, your sister-in-law has returned to her people and to her gods; return after your sister-in-law."

16 And Ruth said, "Do not urge me to leave you, to turn back from following you, for where you go I will go, and where you lodge I will lodge; your folk are my folk, and your God is my God."

17 Wherever I die, there I will be buried; thus may the LORD do to me, and thus may he add, for death will separate between me and you.

18 And she saw that she was strongly determined to go with her, so she stopped speaking to her.

19 And they went on, the two of them, until they came to Bethlehem; and it happened that when they came to Bethlehem, the whole city was astir over them, and they said, Is this Naomi?

20 And she said to them, Do not call me Naomi; call me Mara, for the Almighty has dealt very bitterly with me.

21 I went away full, and the LORD has brought me back empty; why do you call me Naomi? the LORD has testified against me, and the Almighty has afflicted with me.

22 Naomi returned, and Ruth the Moabite daughter-in-law with her, returning from the country of Moab, and they came to Bethlehem at the beginning of the barley harvest.


## Chapter 2

1 Naomi had a relative on her husband's side, a man of great wealth, from the family of Elimelech, and his name was Boaz.

2 Ruth the Moabite said to Naomi, "Let me go now to the field and glean ears of grain after the one in whose eyes I may find favour." And she said to her, "Go, my daughter."

3 And she went and came and gleaned in the field after the reapers; and her chance chanced upon the portion of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.

4 And behold, Boaz came from Bethlehem, and he said to the reapers, "The Lord be with you," and they answered him, "May the Lord bless you."

5 Boaz said to the servant who was in charge of the reapers, "To whom does this young woman belong?"

6 And the young man who was standing over the reapers answered and said, She is a Moabitess, the woman who returned with Naomi from the country of Moab.

7 And she said, Let me now glean and gather sheaves after the reapers; and she came and has stood from then, this morning, until now, this being a little time that she was in the house.

8 Boaz said to Ruth, "Have you not heard, my daughter? Do not go to glean in another field, and also do not pass from this one; instead, keep close to my young women."

9 Your eyes are on the field where they reap, and you shall go after them; have I not commanded the young men not to touch you? And you shall drink from the vessels from which the young men draw water.

10 She fell on her face, bowed to the ground, and said to him, "Why have I found favour in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, when I am a foreigner?"

11 Boaz answered her, saying, "It has been fully reported to me all that you have done for your mother-in-law after the death of your husband: how you left your father and your mother and your native land and came to a people you did not know three days ago."

12 May the LORD repay you for your deeds, and may your reward be complete from the LORD, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge.

13 And she said, I find favour in your eyes, my lord, for you have comforted me, and for you have spoken to the heart of your handmaid, though I am not as one of your female servants.

14 Boaz said to her, "At the time of eating, come here and eat from the bread and dip your morsel in the vinegar." She sat beside the reapers, and he passed her parched corn; she ate and was satisfied and had some left over.

15 And she rose up to glean, and Boaz commanded his servants, saying, "Even among the sheaves you shall let her glean, and you shall not shame her."

16 Also pull out some from the bundles for her, and leave it for her to glean; and you shall not rebuke her.

17 And she gleaned in the field until evening and beat out what she had gleaned, and it was an ephah of barley.

18 She rose and went to the city and her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned, and she brought out and gave her what she had left over from satisfying herself.

19 And her mother in law said to her, Where did you glean today? Where did you work? Blessed be the one who took notice of you. And she told her mother in law with whom she had worked, saying, The man's name with whom I worked today is Boaz.

20 Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, “Blessed be he to the LORD, who has not withdrawn his kindness from the living and from the dead”; and Naomi said to her, “The man is a near kinsman to us, one of our redeemers.”

21 And Ruth the Moabitess said, “Even he said to me, ‘You shall keep close to my young men until they have finished all the harvest that is mine.’”

22 Naomi said to her daughter-in-law Ruth, "It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, so that you will not be met in another field."

23 And she clave unto the damsels of Boaz to glean, until the harvest of barley and the harvest of wheat was ended; and she dwelt with her mother in law.


## Chapter 3

1 And Naomi her mother in law said to her, my daughter, shall I not seek for you a place of rest where it will be well for you?

2 And now, is not Boaz our kindred, with whose damsels you were? Behold, he is winnowing barley on the threshing floor tonight.

3 And you shall wash and anoint yourself, and put on your apparel, and go down to the threshing floor; do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.

4 And when he lies down, observe the place where he will lie, then go and uncover his feet and lie down, and he will tell you what you should do.

5 And she said to her, "All that you say I will do."

6 And she went down to the threshing floor and did according to all that her mother-in-law had commanded her.

7 Boaz ate and drank and and his heart was glad, and he went and lay down at the edge of the heap, and she came secretly and uncovered his feet and lay down.

8 And it came to pass at midnight that the man was startled and turned, and behold, a woman lay at his feet.

9 And he said, Who are you? And she said, I am Ruth your handmaid; spread your wing over your handmaid, for you are a redeemer.

10 Blessed are you by the LORD, my daughter; your latter kindness is better than the former, in that you did not go after the young men, whether poor or rich.

11 Now, my daughter, do not fear; all that you say I will do for you, for all the gate of my people knows that you are a woman of valor.

12 Now indeed, I am a redeemer, but there is also a redeemer closer than I.

13 Lodge here through the night, and it shall be in the morning, if he is good to redeem, he will redeem; but if he does not desire to redeem you, then I will redeem you, as the LORD lives; lie down until morning.

14 She lay at his feet until morning, and she arose before one could recognize another, and he said, "Do not let it be known that the woman came to the threshing floor."

15 And he said, Bring the vail that is on you and hold it, and she held it, and he measured six barley and put it on her, and he went to the town.

16 She came to her mother in law and said, “Who are you, my daughter?” and she told her all that the man had done to her.

17 And she said, The six barley he gave me, for he said, Do not go empty to your mother in law.

18 Stay, my daughter, until you know how the matter will turn out, for the man will not rest until the matter is settled today.


## Chapter 4

1 Boaz went up to the city gate and sat there, and behold, the redeemer passed by whom Boaz had spoken; and he said, "Turn aside, sit here, so-and-so," and he turned aside and sat down.

2 And he took ten men from the elders of the city and said, Sit here; so they sat.

3 Then he said to the redeemer, "The portion of the field that belonged to our brother Elimelech, Naomi has sold—the one who has come back from the fields of Moab."

4 And I said, I will advertise your ear, saying, Buy in the presence of the people and in the presence of the elders of my people: if you will redeem, redeem; but if you will not redeem, then declare to me, for there is no one besides you to redeem, and I am after you. And he said, I will redeem.

5 Boaz said, "On the day you buy the field from Naomi, you must also acquire Ruth the Moabite, the wife of the dead, to restore the name of the dead to his inheritance."

6 Then the redeemer said, I cannot redeem it for myself, lest I ruin my inheritance; you redeem my right of redemption, for I cannot redeem.

7 And this was formerly done in Israel concerning redemption and exchange: a man would remove his sandal and give it to his companion, and this was the testimony in Israel.

8 Then the redeemer said to Boaz, "Buy it for yourself," and he removed his sandal.

9 Boaz said to the elders and all the people, "You are witnesses today that I have acquired all that belonged to Elimelech and all that belonged to Chilion and Mahlon from the hand of Naomi."

10 And also Ruth the Moabitess, Mahlon’s wife, I have acquired for myself as wife, to raise up the name of the dead upon his inheritance, and that the name of the dead may not be cut off from among his brothers and from the gate of his place; you are witnesses today.

11 And all the people who were at the gate and the elders said, We are witnesses: may the LORD make the woman who is coming into your house like Rachel and Leah, who built the house of Israel; and may you do valour in Ephrathah and call a name in Bethlehem.

12 May your family be like the family of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah, through the offspring that the LORD will give you from this young woman.

13 Boaz took Ruth and she became his wife, and he came to her, and the LORD granted her conception, and she bore a son.

14 The women said to Naomi, Blessed be the LORD who has not left you today without a redeemer, and may his name become renowned in Israel.

15 It shall be for you a refreshment of spirit and a comfort to your old age, for the daughter-in-law who loves you has borne him; she who is better to you than seven sons.

16 Naomi took the boy and placed him in her lap, and she became his nurse.

17 The neighbors gave him a name, saying, A son is born to Naomi, and they called his name Obed, he is the father of Jesse, the father of David.

18 These are the generations of Perez: Perez fathered Hezron.

19 Hezron fathered Ram, and Ram fathered Amminadab.

20 Amminadab fathered Nahshon and Nahshon fathered Salmon.

21 Salmon fathered Boaz, and Boaz fathered Obed.

22 Obed fathered Jesse, and Jesse fathered David.


# 1 SAMUEL

## Chapter 1

1 There was a certain man from Ramathaimzophim of the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah son of Jeroham son of Elihu son of Tohu son of Zuph, an Ephrathite.

2 He had two wives; the name of one was Hannah and the name of the second was Peninnah; and Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.

3 And that man went up from his city from day to day to worship and to sacrifice to the LORD of hosts in Shiloh, and there were the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, priests to the LORD.

4 And it came to pass on that day, Elkanah sacrificed, and he gave portions to Peninnah his wife and to all her sons and her daughters.

5 And to Hannah he gave one portion before [others], for he loved Hannah, but the LORD had closed her womb.

6 And her adversary provoked her also to anger for the sake of provoking her, because the LORD had closed her womb.

7 And thus he did year by year, whenever she went up to the house of the LORD, so he provoked her to bitterness, and she wept and would not eat.

8 Elkanah her husband said to her, "Hannah, why do you weep? Why do you not eat? Why is your heart troubled? Am I not better to you than ten sons?"

9 Then Hannah rose up after eating in Shiloh and after drinking, and Eli the priest was sitting upon the seat by the doorpost of the temple of the LORD.

10 And she was bitter of soul, and she prayed to the LORD and wept bitterly.

11 She vowed a vow and said, "O the Lord of hosts, if You will indeed look upon the affliction of Your handmaid, and remember me and not forget Your handmaid, but will give to Your handmaid a child of men, then I will give him to the Lord all the days of his life, and a razor shall not come upon his head."

12 And it came to pass, when she continued praying before the LORD, that Eli was watching her mouth.

13 And Hannah was speaking upon her heart; only her lips moved, and her voice was not heard; and Eli considered her to be drunk.

14 Then Eli said to her, "How long will you be drunk? Put away your wine from upon you."

15 Then Hannah answered and said, No, my lord, I am a woman of troubled spirit; I have drunk neither wine nor strong drink, but have poured out my soul before the LORD.

16 Do not deliver your handmaid into the hand of the ungodly, for out of the abundance of my meditation and my anger I have spoken until now.

17 Then Eli answered and said, Go in peace, and may the God of Israel grant your request that you have asked of him.

18 And she said, May your handmaid find favour in your sight; and the woman went on her way, and ate, and her face was no longer sad.

19 They rose early in the morning and bowed down before the LORD, then returned and came to their home in Ramah. Elkanah knew Hannah his wife, and the LORD remembered her.

20 And it came to be at the end of days that Hannah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Samuel, saying, For from the LORD I have asked him.

21 And the man Elkanah went up, and all his household, to offer to the LORD the sacrifice of the days and his vow.

22 And Hannah did not go up, for she said to her husband, Until the boy is weaned, then I will bring him, that he may appear before the face of the LORD and remain there forever.

23 Elkanah said to her, his wife, "Do what is good in your eyes; remain until you wean him; only, may the LORD establish his word." And the woman remained and nursed her son until she weaned him.

24 And when she had weaned him, she brought him up with her, with three bulls, one ephah of flour, and a skin of wine, and she brought him to the house of the LORD at Shiloh; and the child was young.

25 They slaughtered the young bull and brought the lad to Eli.

26 My lord, I beseech you, by your life, my lord, I am the woman who stood beside you here to pray to the LORD.

27 I prayed concerning this child, and the LORD granted me the request that I asked of Him.

28 And indeed, I also dedicated him to the LORD for all the days of his life during which he was dedicated to the LORD, and there they bowed down to the LORD.


## Chapter 2

1 And Hannah prayed and said, My heart exults in the LORD, my horn is exalted in the LORD, my mouth is enlarged upon my enemies, for I rejoice in Your deliverance.

2 There is no holy one like the LORD, for there is none besides you, and there is no rock like our God.

3 Do not speak haughtily, let not arrogant words come from your mouth, for the LORD is a God of knowledge, and by him deeds are weighed.

4 The bow of archers is broken, and the stumbling gird themselves with strength.

5 The well-fed were hired for bread, and the hungry ceased; the barren bore seven, and she who had many children languished.

6 The LORD puts to death and makes alive; he brings down to the grave and raises up.

7 The LORD makes poor and makes rich; he brings low, he also exalts.

8 He raises the poor from the dust, from the dung hill lifts the needy, to seat with nobles, and a glorious throne He bequeaths them; for the LORD owns the pillars of the earth, and He has established the habitable part upon them.

9 He guards the feet of His faithful ones, but the wicked will be silenced in darkness, for no man prevails by strength.

10 The Lord will thunder from the heavens; the Lord will judge the ends of the earth; and he will give might to his king, and exalt the horn of his anointed.

11 Elkanah went to his home at Ramah, and the boy was serving the LORD before Eli the priest.

12 And the sons of Eli were sons of Belial; they did not know the LORD.

13 And the judgment of the priests was this: the people would bring every man a sacrifice, and when the young priest came as the flesh was boiling, the fleshhook with three teeth was in his hand.

14 And he shall strike with the hearth or with the kettle or with the caldron or with the pan: all that the fleshhook brings up, the priest shall take with it; thus they shall do to all Israel who come there to Shiloh.

15 Even before they burned the fat, the priest’s boy came and said to the man sacrificing, “Give flesh for the priest to roast, for he will not take boiled flesh from you, only raw.”

16 And the man said to him, "Let them offer fat today as they do daily; take for yourself as much as your soul desires. But he said, "No, I will give it; otherwise, I will take it by force."

17 The punishment for the sin of the young men was very great before the LORD, for the men had rejected the offering of the LORD.

18 And Samuel served before the face of the LORD, a boy girded with a linen ephod.

19 And a coat shall make for him his mother, and she shall bring it to him from day to day, when she comes up with her husband to offer the sacrifice of the days.

20 Then Eli blessed Elkanah and his wife, and said, “May the LORD grant you seed from this woman in place of the petition she requested from the LORD.” Then they went to their home.

21 For the LORD had remembered Hannah, and she conceived and bore three sons and two daughters, and the boy Samuel grew up with the LORD.

22 And Eli was very old, and he heard all that his sons were doing to all Israel, and how they lay with the women who assembled at the door of the tent of meeting.

23 And he said to them, Why are you doing such things? Behold, I hear your evil words from all this people.

24 My sons, not good is the report that I hear; you are causing the people of the LORD to transgress.

25 If a man sins against another, God may be entreated for him, but if a man sins against the LORD, who can entreat for him? They did not listen to the voice of their father, for the LORD desired to kill them.

26 And the boy Samuel grew on and increased, and it was good both with the LORD and with people.

27 A certain man of God came to Eli and said to him, “Thus says the LORD: Was I not clearly revealed to the house of your father when he was in Egypt at the house of Pharaoh?”

28 And I chose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priest, to go up to my altar, to burn incense, to bear the ephod before me; and I gave to the house of your father all the offerings by fire of the people of Israel.

29 Why do you kick at my sacrifice and my offering, which I commanded at my dwelling place, and honor your sons more than me, to fatten them with the beginning of all the offerings of my people Israel?

30 Therefore (hath) said the Lord God of Israel, I said indeed your family and the family of your father should walk before me forever, but now (hath) said the Lord, far be it from me, for those who honor me I will honor, and those who despise me shall be held in contempt.

31 Behold, days are coming when I will cut off your arm and the arm of your father's house from being old in your house.

32 Look with hostility upon the dwelling place in all that which benefits Israel, and there shall never be an elder in your household all the days.

33 Yet I will not cut off every man of yours from my altar, but they shall consume your eyes and grieve your soul, and all the increase of your house shall die in the prime of life.

34 This shall be the mark for you that will come upon your two sons, Hophni and Phinehas: on one day both of them shall die.

35 And I will raise up for myself a faithful priest who will act according to my heart and my soul; and I will build him a faithful house, and he will walk before my anointed all the days.

36 And everyone left in your household will come and bow down to him for a piece of silver and a loaf of bread, saying, "Please assign me to one of the priesthoods so I may eat a piece of bread."


## Chapter 3

1 And the boy Samuel served the LORD before Eli, and the word of the LORD was rare in those days, no vision was widespread.

2 On that day Eli was lying in his place; his eyes had begun to grow dim and he could not see.

3 And the lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying down in the temple of the LORD, where the ark of God was.

4 And the LORD called to Samuel, and Samuel said, Here I am.

5 He ran to Eli and said, "Here I am, for you called me." But he said, "I did not call; return, lie down." So he went and lay down.

6 And the LORD called Samuel again; and Samuel rose up and went to Eli, and said, "Here I am, for you called me." And he said, "No, my son, I did not call you; return, lie down."

7 And Samuel had not yet known the LORD, nor had the word of the LORD yet been revealed to him.

8 And Jehovah added to call Samuel the third time, and he arose and went to Eli, and said, Here I am, for you called me; and Eli perceived that Jehovah was calling the boy.

9 Eli said to Samuel, "Go, lie down; and it shall be, if he calls to you, then you shall say, 'Speak, the Lord, for your servant is listening.' " So Samuel went and lay down in his place.

10 Then the LORD came and stood, calling as at other times, “Samuel, Samuel!” And Samuel said, “Speak, for your servant is listening.”

11 And Jehovah said to Samuel, Behold, I am doing an act in Israel, such that whoever hears it, both his ears will tingle.

12 On that day I will carry out for Eli all that I have spoken concerning his house, from beginning to end.

13 And I declared to him that I judge his household forever because of the iniquity, for he knew that his sons were cursing, and he did not restrain them.

14 Therefore I have sworn concerning the house of Eli: his family's iniquity shall not be atoned for by sacrifice or offering forever.

15 Samuel lay until morning and opened the doors of the house of the LORD, and Samuel was afraid to report the vision to Eli.

16 Eli called Samuel, and said, "Samuel, my son," and he said, "Here I am."

17 And he said, What is the word that he spoke to you? Do not, I beg you, hide any word from me. Thus may God do to you, and thus may he add, if you hide from me any word of all the word that he spoke to you.

18 Samuel told him all the words and withheld nothing from him, and he said, "the LORD is He; let Him do what is good in His eyes."

19 And Samuel grew, and the LORD was with him and did not let any of his words fall to the ground.

20 All Israel from Dan to Beer-shebah knew that Samuel was established as a prophet to the LORD.

21 And again Jehovah appeared in Shiloh, for Jehovah was revealed to Samuel in Shiloh by the word of Jehovah.


## Chapter 4

1 The word of Samuel came to all Israel, and Israel went out to meet the Philistines in battle and camped beside Ebenezer, while the Philistines camped in Aphek.

2 The Philistines drew up in battle array against Israel, and the battle was joined, and Israel was defeated before the Philistines, and they struck down in the fighting in the field about four thousand men.

3 The folk came into the camp and the elders of Israel said, Why has the LORD struck us today before the Philistines? Let us bring to ourselves from Shiloh the ark of the covenant of the LORD, that it may come among us and save us from the power of our enemies.

4 The folk sent to Shiloh and brought from there the ark of the covenant of the LORD of hosts who sits upon the cherubim, and there with the ark of the covenant of God were the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas.

5 And it happened, when the ark of the covenant of the LORD came into the camp, that all Israel gave a great shout of joy, and the earth trembled.

6 The Philistines heard the sound of the shouting, and they said, "What is this great shouting in the camp of the Hebrews?" And they learned that the ark of the LORD had come into the camp.

7 The Philistines saw and said, God has come into the camp; and they said, Alas for us! For there has not been the like of this heretofore.

8 Woe to us! Who will deliver us from the hand of these mighty gods? These are the gods who struck Egypt with every plague in the wilderness.

9 Be strong and become men, you Philistines, or you will serve the Hebrews as they have served you; then you will become men and fight.

10 The Philistine fought and Israel was struck, and each man fled to his tent, and the slaughter was very great indeed, and from Israel fell thirty thousand footmen.

11 And the ark of God was taken, and the two sons of Eli died, Hophni and Phinehas.

12 A certain man of Benjamin ran from the army and came to Shiloh on that day, and his garments were torn and earth was on his head.

13 Then he came, and Eli was sitting upon the throne by the way, for his heart trembled for the ark of God; and the man came to tell in the city, and all the city cried out.

14 Eli heard the sound of the cry and said, "What is this noise of such a tumult?" and the man hurried and came and reported to Eli.

15 And Eli was ninety-eight years old, and his eyes were fixed so that he could not see.

16 And the man said to Eli, I am the one who came from the army, and I escaped from the army today; and Eli said, What was the matter, my son?

17 The messenger answered and said, Israel has fled before the Philistines, and there has also been a great slaughter among the people; moreover your two sons are dead, Hophni and Phinehas, and the ark of God has been captured.

18 When they mentioned the ark of God, he fell backward off the throne and broke his neck and died, for the man was old and heavy, and he had judged Israel forty years.

19 And his daughter-in-law, the wife of Phinehas, who was with child, heard the report of the ark of God being taken, and of her father-in-law and her husband, and she collapsed and gave birth, for her pains came upon her.

20 After her time of death, the women standing over her said to her, "Do not fear, for you have given birth to a son," but she did not answer and did not pay attention to her heart.

21 And she called the babe I-chabod, saying, The glory has departed from Israel: for the ark of God was taken, and for her father in law, and for her husband.

22 And she said, "The glory has departed from Israel, for the ark of God has been captured."


## Chapter 5

1 And the Philistines took the ark of God and brought it from Ebenezer to Ahdod.

2 The Philistine took the ark of God and brought it into the house of Dagon and set it beside Dagon.

3 The Ashdodites rose early the next day and saw that Dagon had fallen on his face to the ground before the ark of the LORD, so they took Dagon and returned him to his place.

4 And they rose early in the morning on the next day and saw Dagon fallen on his face to the ground before the ark of the LORD and the head of Dagon and the two palms of his hands cut off on the threshold only Dagon's torso remained with him.

5 Therefore the priests of Dagon and all who enter the house of Dagon do not tread on the threshold of Dagon in Ashdod to this day.

6 The hand of the LORD weighed heavily upon the Ashdodites and he devastated them and struck Ashdod and its territory with tumors.

7 The men of Ahdod saw that it was so, and said, The ark of the God of Israel must not stay with us, for his hand has been heavy upon us and upon Dagon our god.

8 They sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines to them, and said, "What shall we do about the ark of the God of Israel?" And they said, "Let the ark of the God of Israel be carried off to Gath." So they carried off the ark of the God of Israel.

9 After they had turned him around, the hand of the LORD was upon the city with great and severe discomfiture, and he struck the men of the city, both small and great, and tumors broke out on them.

10 They sent the ark of God to Ekron, and when the ark of God came to Ekron, the Ekronites cried out, saying, "They have brought the ark of the God of Israel to me to kill me and my people."

11 They sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines and said, Send back the ark of the God of Israel to its place, so that it may not kill me and my people, for there was a very heavy death-tumult in all the city; the hand of God was there with heavy force.

12 And the men who had not died were struck with tumors, and the cry of the city went up to the heavens.


## Chapter 6

1 The ark of the LORD was in the land of the Philistines seven months.

2 The Philistine called to the priests and the diviners, saying, What shall we do about the ark of the Lord? Tell us how we should send it back to its place.

3 They said, "If you send back the ark of the God of Israel, do not send it away empty; for as a guilt offering you must return to him a guilt offering. Then you will be healed, and it will be known to you why his hand does not turn away from you."

4 What guilt offering shall we return to him? And they said: a number of Philistine lords, five golden tumors and five golden mice, for one plague has struck all of you and your lords.

5 You shall make images of your tumors and images of your mice that are destroying the land, and you shall give glory to the God of Israel; perhaps he will lighten his hand from upon you, and from upon your gods, and from upon your land.

6 Why do you harden your hearts as the Egyptians and Pharaoh hardened their hearts? Did the LORD not deal mightily with them, so that they let them go and they departed?

7 Now therefore take and prepare one new cart and two milch cows on which there has come no yoke, and hitch the cows to the cart and bring back their children into the house.

8 Take the ark of the LORD and put it on the cart, and put the gold vessels that you returned to him as a guilt offering into a coffer beside it, and send it away that it may go.

9 And you shall see if along the border's way it goes up to Beth-shemesh, he has done this great calamity to us; but if not, then we shall know that his hand did not touch us; it was a chance event that happened to us.

10 The men did so, and took two heifers and harnessed them to the cart, and left their children at home.

11 They placed the ark of the LORD on the cart, and the coffer, and the mice of gold, and the images of their emerods.

12 And the cows went straight in the way by the way of Beth-shemesh, along one causeway they went, lowing as they went, and did not turn aside to the right or to the left, and the lords of the Philistines went after them to the border of Beth-shemesh.

13 And Beth-shemesh, they were harvesting wheat in the valley, and they lifted their eyes and saw the ark, and rejoiced at the sight.

14 And the cart came to the field of Joshua the Bethshemite and stood there, and there a great stone, and they split the wood of the cart and offered the cows as a burnt offering to the LORD.

15 The Leviites brought down the ark of the LORD and the coffer that was with it, in which were the gold vessels, and set them upon the great stone; and the men of Beth-shemesh offered burnt offerings and sacrificed sacrifices on that day to the LORD.

16 And the five lords of the Philistines saw, and they returned that day to Ekron.

17 These are the emerods of gold which the Philistines returned as a guilt offering to the LORD: one for Ahdod, one for Gaza, one for Ashkelon, one for Gath, one for Ekron.

18 And five golden mice, according to the number of all the cities of the Philistines belonging to the five lords, from the fortified city to the unwalled village, as far as Abel the great, where they set down the ark of the LORD, to this day, in the field of Joshua the Bethshemite.

19 He struck the people of Beth-shemesh because they looked upon the ark of the LORD, and he struck the people, seventy men and fifty thousand men, and the people mourned because the LORD had struck the people with a great blow.

20 The men of Beth-shemesh said, Who can stand before the LORD, the holy God? And to whom shall he go up from us?

21 They sent ambassadors to the inhabitants of Kirjath-jearim, saying, Return the ark of the LORD; come down, bring it up to you.


## Chapter 7

1 And the men of Kirjath-jearim came and carried up the ark of the LORD, and brought it into the house of Abinadab on the hill; and they consecrated Eleazar his son to keep the ark of the LORD.

2 And it came to pass from the day that the ark abode in Kirjath-jearim, that the days were multiplied and they were twenty years, and all the house of Israel lamented after the LORD.

3 Samuel said to all the house of Israel, "If with all your mind you are returning to the LORD, remove the foreign gods and the Asharoth from among you, and prepare your mind toward the LORD and serve him alone, and he will deliver you from the hand of the Philistines."

4 The sons of Israel removed the Baalim and the Astaroth, and served the LORD alone.

5 Samuel said, Gather (in) all Israel to Mitspah, and I will pray on your behalf to the LORD.

6 They gathered at Mitspah, and drew water and poured it out before the LORD, and fasted on that day, and said there, "We have sinned against the LORD." And Samuel judged the sons of Israel at Mitspah.

7 The Philistines heard that the sons of Israel had gathered at Mitspah, and the lords of the Philistines went up against Israel; and when the sons of Israel heard, they were afraid because of the Philistines.

8 The sons of Israel said to Samuel, Do not hold your peace from us; cry out to the LORD our God, that he may save us from the hand of the Philistines.

9 Samuel took one sucking lamb as a whole burnt offering to the LORD, and Samuel cried to the LORD on behalf of Israel, and the LORD answered him.

10 Samuel was offering the burnt offering when the Philistines drew near to fight against Israel, and the LORD thundered with a great voice that day against the Philistines and terrified them, and they were defeated before Israel.

11 The men of Israel went out from Mizpah and pursued the Philistines and struck them down as far as below Beth-car.

12 Samuel took a stone and set it between Mitspah and Shen and called its name Ebenezer and said, Thus far the LORD has helped us.

13 The Philistines were subdued and did not continue anymore to come into the border of Israel, and the hand of the LORD was against the Philistines all the days of Samuel.

14 The cities that the Philistines had taken from Israel were restored to Israel, from Ekron to Gath, and the border thereof Israel delivered out of the hand of the Philistines; and there was peace between Israel and the Amorite.

15 Samuel judged Israel all the days of his life.

16 And he went according to year in year and went around Beth-el and Gilgal and Mitspah and judged Israel in all those places.

17 And his return was to Ramah, for there was his home, and there he judged Israel, and there he built an altar to the LORD.


## Chapter 8

1 And it came to pass, when Samuel was old, that he appointed his sons judges over Israel.

2 The name of his firstborn son was Joel, and the name of his second was Abijah; they judged in Beer-shebah.

3 Their sons did not walk in his ways but turned after dishonest gain and took bribes and perverted justice.

4 And all the elders of Israel gathered together and came to Samuel at Ramah.

5 They said to him, "Behold, you have grown old, and your sons do not walk in your ways; now appoint for us a king to judge us like all the nations."

6 And the act was evil in the eyes of Samuel, when they said, “Give us a king to judge us”; and Samuel prayed to the LORD.

7 And the LORD said to Samuel, "Obey the voice of the people in all that they say to you, for they have not rejected you, but they have rejected me from ruling over them."

8 According to all the deeds they have done from the day I brought them up out of Egypt until this day, and they have forsaken me and served other gods, so they are doing also to you.

9 Now listen to their voice, but you shall surely warn them and explain to them the manner of the king who will reign over them.

10 Samuel told all the words of the LORD to the people who were asking a king from him.

11 And he said, This shall be the charge of the king who will reign over you: he will take your sons and appoint them to his chariots and to his horsemen, and they will run before his chariot.

12 and to set for him captains of thousands and captains of fifties, and to plow his plowing and to reap his harvest, and to make the weapons of his war and the chariots of his war.

13 And he will take your daughters as confectioners, cooks, and bakers.

14 And your fields and your vineyards and your choice olive trees he will take and give to his servants.

15 Your seed and your vineyards shall be taxed, and given to his eunuchs and to his servants.

16 And he will take your bondmen and your bondwomen and your choice young men and your he-asses, and he will use them for his work.

17 Your flock will be multiplied, and you will become servants to him.

18 And you will cry out in that day because of the king whom you have chosen for yourselves, but the LORD will not answer you in that day.

19 And the folk refused to listen to the voice of Samuel, and they said, No, but a king shall be over us.

20 And we also would be like all the nations, and our kings would judge us and go out before us, and fight our battles.

21 Samuel heard all the words of the people and spoke them in the ears of the LORD.

22 And the LORD said to Samuel, "Listen to their voice and you shall set over them a king"; and Samuel said to the men of Israel, "Go, each man to his city."


## Chapter 9

1 There was a certain man whose name was Kish, son of Abiel, son of Zeror, son of Bechorath, son of Aphiah, a Benjaminite, a mighty man of power.

2 And he had a son whose name was Saul, a choice young man and handsome, and there was no man among the people of Israel better than he; from his shoulders and upward he was taller than all the people.

3 The she-asses of Kish, Saul’s father, were lost, and Kish said to his son Saul, “Please take with you one of the young men and arise, go, seek the she-asses.”

4 And he passed through the hill country of Ephraim and passed through the land of Shalisha and did not find them; and they passed through the land of Shalim and there was none, and he passed through the land on the right hand and did not find them.

5 They came into the land of Zuph, and Saul said to the servant who was with him, “Come, let us return, lest my father cease concerning the donkeys and become anxious for us.”

6 And he said to him, behold now a certain man of God is in this city, and the man is honored; all that he speaks comes to pass. Now let us go there; perhaps he will tell us about our way, which we have taken.

7 Saul said to his servant, "Behold, we are going, but what shall we bring to the man? For the food has failed from our vessels, and there is no present to bring to the man of God. What do we have?"

8 The lad continued to answer Saul, saying, Behold, a fourth part of a shekel of silver has been found in my hand; I will give it to the man of God, that he may tell us our way.

9 In earlier times in Israel, when someone went to inquire of God, they would say, “Come, let us go to the seer,” for the prophet of today was formerly called the seer.

10 Saul said to his servant, "Your advice is good; come, let us go." So they went to the city where the man of God was.

11 They went up by the ascent of the city and found young women coming out to draw water, and they said to them, Is the seer here?

12 They answered them and said, "Yes, look, here it is before you—hurry now, for the day has come when the sacrifice of the day is for the people at the city's high place."

13 When you come to the city, you will find him before he goes up to the high place to eat, for the people will not eat until he comes, since he must bless the sacrifice; afterward the invited guests may eat. Now go up, for you will surely find him today.

14 So they went up to the city; they came into the midst of the city, and behold, Samuel was coming out to meet them, to go up to the high place.

15 And the LORD revealed to Samuel the ear, one day before Saul came, saying:

16 At this time tomorrow I will send to you a man from the land of Benjamin and appoint him leader over my people Israel, and he will save my people from the hand of the Philistines; for I have seen the cry of my people, because their cry has come to me.

17 And Samuel saw Saul, and the LORD answered him, "Behold, the man of whom I spoke to you—this one will restrain my people."

18 Saul approached Samuel in the middle of the gate and said, Please tell me, where is the house of the seer?

19 Samuel answered Saul and said, I am the seer; go up before me to the high place, and you shall eat with me today; and in the morning I will send you away, and all that is in your heart I will tell you.

20 Do not set your heart on the donkeys that were lost to you today, for they have been found; and to whom does all the preciousness of Israel belong if not to you and to all your father's house?

21 Saul answered and said, "Am I not a Benjamite, of Benjamin, from the smallest of the tribes of Israel, and is my family the least of all the families of the tribe of Benjamin? Why then have you spoken to me such a word as this?"

22 And Samuel took Saul and his servant and brought them into the chamber and gave them a place at the head of the ones called, about thirty men.

23 Samuel said to the cook, Give the portion that I gave you, of which I said to you, Set it by you.

24 The cook lifted the leg and the fat tail, and placed them before Saul, and said, "Here is what remains; set it before you and eat, for it has been kept for you at the appointed time, saying, 'The people I have called.' " So Saul ate with Samuel that day.

25 They went down from the height to the city and spoke with Saul on the roof.

26 They rose early, and it came to be at the rising of the morning, and Samuel called to Saul, saying, "Rise, and I will send you forth." So Saul arose, and the two of them, he and Samuel, went out abroad.

27 They are going to the edge of the city, and Samuel said to Saul, “Tell the servant to go on ahead of us.” So he went on ahead, but you stand still for a while, and I will tell you the word of God.


## Chapter 10

1 Samuel took the vial of oil and poured it on his head and kissed him and said, "Behold, has not the LORD anointed you over his inheritance for leader?"

2 When you go today from me, you will meet two men by Rachel's burial at the border of Benjamin in Zelzah, and they will say to you, The donkeys that you went to seek have been found, and now your father has abandoned the matter of the donkeys and is anxious for you, saying, What shall I do for my son?

3 And you shall depart from there onward to the plain of Tabor, and there you shall meet three men going up to God at Beth-el: one carrying three kids, another carrying three loaves of bread, and another carrying a bottle of wine.

4 They will ask after your welfare and give you two loaves of bread, and you shall take them from their hand.

5 After that you shall come to the hill of God where the Philistine garrison is; and when you come there to the city, you shall meet a company of prophets coming down from the high place, with a psaltery, a tabret, a pipe, and a harp before them, and they shall be prophesying.

6 And the spirit of the LORD will rush upon you, and you will prophesy with them, and you will be turned into another man.

7 And it shall be, when these signs are for you, do for yourself whatever your hand finds, for God is with you.

8 Go down before me to Gilgal, and behold, I will come down to you there to offer up burnt offerings and to sacrifice peace offerings; for seven days you shall wait until I come to you, and I will tell you what you shall do.

9 And when he turned his back to go from Samuel, God turned for him another heart; and all these signs came to pass on that day.

10 And they came there to the little hill, and behold, a company of prophets met him, and the Spirit of God rushed upon him, and he prophesied among them.

11 And all who had known him from the day before and three days past saw, and behold, he was with the prophets, prophesying; and the people said, each man to his companion, What is this that has happened to the son of Kish? Is even Saul among the prophets?

12 Then a certain man answered and said, “Who are their fathers?” Therefore it became a proverb, “Is even Saul among the prophets?”

13 And he finished prophesying and came to the height.

14 And Saul's uncle said to him and to his servant, "Where have you been?" And he said, "To seek the asses; and when we saw that they were not there, we came to Samuel."

15 And Saul's uncle said, Tell me, I pray thee, what Samuel said unto you.

16 Saul said to his uncle, "He told us plainly that the donkeys had been found." But about the matter of the kingship, of which Samuel had spoken, he told him nothing.

17 And Samuel cried to the LORD for the people at Mizpah.

18 And he said to the children of Israel, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, I brought up Israel from Egypt, and I delivered you from the hand of Egypt and from the hand of all kingdoms that oppressed you.

19 But today you are rejecting your God, who delivers you from all your evils and your distresses, and you have said to him, “Set a king over us!” Now therefore, take your stand before the LORD by your tribes and your thousands.

20 Samuel brought near all the tribes of Israel, and the tribe of Benjamin was taken.

21 And he brought near the tribe of Benjamin, and the family of the Matri was taken; and Saul son of Kish was taken, but when they sought him, he was not found.

22 And they inquired again of the LORD, "Is the man come here yet?" And the LORD said, “Behold, he is hidden beside the vessels.”

23 They ran and took him from there and he stood among the people, and he was taller than all the people from his shoulders upward.

24 Samuel said to all the people, "Have you seen whom the LORD has chosen? For there is none like him among all the people." And all the people shouted and said, "Long live the king!"

25 Samuel told the people the manner of the kingship, and he wrote it in a scroll and placed it before the LORD; then Samuel sent all the people, each man to his house.

26 And also Saul went to his home in Gibeah, and there went with him the band of men whose heart God had touched.

27 And the sons of Belial said, What shall this man save us? and they despised him, and brought him no present; but he held his peace.


## Chapter 11

1 And Nahash the Ammonite advanced and encamped against Jabesh-gilead, and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash, "Make a covenant with us and we will serve you."

2 Nahash the Ammonite said to them, "This is how I will make a covenant with you: by gouging out every right eye of you, and setting it as a reproach upon all Israel."

3 And the elders of Jobesh said to him, Wait for us seven days, that we may send messengers throughout all the border of Israel; and if there is no one who will save us, then we will go out to you.

4 The ambassadors came to Gibeah of Saul and spoke the words in the ears of the people and all the people lifted up their voice and wept.

5 And behold, Saul came after the herd from the field, and Saul said, "What is the matter with the people, that they weep?" And they told him the words of the men of Jabesh.

6 And the spirit of God came mightily upon Saul when he heard these words, and his anger burned greatly.

7 He took a yoke of beeves and cut them up and sent them throughout all the border of Israel by the hand of the messengers, saying, Whoever does not come out after Saul and after Samuel, so shall it be done to his beeves. And the dread of the LORD fell upon the people, and they came out as one man.

8 And he mustered them at Bezek, and the people of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.

9 The ambassadors said to the messengers who were coming, “Thus you shall say to the men of Jabesh-gilead: ‘Tomorrow you will have deliverance by the sun.’” And the messengers came and declared it to the men of Jabesh, and they rejoiced.

10 The men of Jabesh said, Tomorrow we will go out to you, and you may do to us whatever seems good in your eyes.

11 And it came to pass on the morrow that Saul set the folk in three bands and they came into the midst of the camp in the morning watch and smote the Ammonites until the heat of the day and it came to pass that those who remained were scattered and there remained not two together.

12 And the folk said to Samuel, Who said Saul will reign over us? Give us the men, that we may put them to death.

13 Saul said, no man shall die on this day, for today the LORD has accomplished deliverance in Israel.

14 And Samuel said to the folk, "Come, let us go to Gilgal and renew the kingship there."

15 All the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul king before the LORD at Gilgal; and there they offered peace offerings before the LORD; and there Saul and all the men of Israel greatly rejoiced.


## Chapter 12

1 Samuel said to all Israel, "Behold, I have listened to your voice in all that you have said to me and have made a king over you."

2 And now, behold, the king walks before you, and I am grown old and gray, and my sons are with you; and I have walked before you from my youth even to this day.

3 Here I am, answer me before the LORD and before his anointed: whose bull have I taken, or whose ass have I taken, or whom have I oppressed, or whom have I bribed to overlook with my eyes? And I will restore it to you.

4 They said, You have not defrauded us or oppressed us, nor have you taken anything from any man’s hand.

5 The witness is Jehovah against you and the witness of His anointed this day, that you have found no fault in my hand. And he said, Witness.

6 Samuel said to the people, The Lord who did Moses and Aaron and who brought up your fathers from the land of Egypt.

7 And now stand before the LORD, that I may plead with you concerning all the righteous acts of the LORD which He has done with you and with your fathers.

8 As Jacob came to Egypt, your fathers cried to the LORD, and the LORD sent Moses and Aaron, and brought your fathers out of Egypt, and settled them in this place.
