9 They forgot the LORD their God, and He delivered them into the hand of Sisera, captain of the army of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them.

10 They cried out to the LORD, "We have sinned, for we have forsaken the LORD and served the Baalim and the Asharoth; now deliver us from the hand of our enemies, and we will serve you."

11 And the LORD sent Jerubbaal and Bedan and Jephthah and Samuel, and delivered you from the power of your enemies all around, and you dwelt securely.

12 You saw that Nahash the king of the Ammonites came up against you, and you said to me, “No, but a king shall reign over us,” when the LORD your God is your king.

13 And now behold the king whom you chose, whom you asked for, and behold the LORD has given you a king.

14 If you fear the LORD, and serve him and obey his voice, and do not rebel against the word of the LORD, then both you and the king who reigns over you will follow the LORD your God.

15 But if you do not listen to the voice of the LORD and you rebel against the word of the LORD, then the hand of the LORD will be against you and against your fathers.

16 Even now, stand and see the great act that the LORD is doing before your eyes.

17 Is not the harvest of wheat today? I will call to the LORD, and he will send thunder and rain; and know and see that your great evil which you have done in the eyes of the LORD is to ask for yourselves a king.

18 And Samuel called to the LORD, and the LORD gave thunder and rain that day, and all the people greatly feared the LORD and Samuel.

19 And all the people said to Samuel, "Pray for your servants to the LORD your God, and do not let us die, for we have added to all our sins evil in asking a king for ourselves."

20 And Samuel said to the people, "Do not fear; you have done all this evil, yet do not turn aside from following the LORD, but serve the LORD with all your heart."

21 Do not turn aside after the confusion which does not profit nor deliver, for they are a vain thing.

22 For the LORD will not forsake his people because of his great name, since the LORD has resolved to make you into a people for himself.

23 Even I myself—may it be far from me to sin against the LORD by ceasing to pray for you; and I will instruct you in the good and right way.

24 Only fear the LORD and serve him faithfully with all your heart; for see what great things he has done for you.

25 But if you act wickedly, both you and your king will be swept away.


## Chapter 13

1 Saul was [the age of] one year in his reigning, and two years he reigned over Israel.

2 Saul chose for himself three thousand men of Israel, and two thousand were with Saul in Mikmash and in the hill country of Beth-el, and a thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin, and the rest of the people he sent each to his tent.

3 Jonathan struck the Philistine garrison that was in Geba, and the Philistines heard; then Saul blew the trumpet throughout the land, saying, "Let the Hebrews hear."

4 And all Israel heard, saying, Saul has struck down the Philistine garrison, and also Israel has become despised by the Philistines, and the people cried after Saul at Gilgal.

5 The Philistines gathered to fight against Israel, thirty thousand chariots and six thousand horsemen and a people like the sand that is on the seashore in abundance, and they went up and encamped in Mikmash, east of Beth-aven.

6 And the people of Israel saw that they were in distress, for the people were pressed hard, and the people hid themselves in the caves, in the thickets, in the rocky strongholds, in the high places, and in the pits.

7 And the Hebrews crossed the Jordan into the land of Gad and Gilead, while Saul was still at Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.

8 And he waited seven days, the appointed season that Samuel set, and Samuel did not come to Gilgal, and the people scattered from him.

9 Saul said, "Bring the burnt offering and the peace offering to me," and he offered the burnt offering.

10 And it happened, when he had finished offering the burnt offering, that Samuel arrived; and Saul went out to meet him in order to greet him.

11 Samuel said, "What have you done?" And Saul said, "When I saw that the folk drew back from me, and you did not come at the appointed time of the days, and the Philistines were gathered at Mikmash,"

12 And I said, Now the Philistines will come down against me to Gilgal, and I have not waited before the LORD; so I restrained myself and went up the burnt offering.

13 Samuel said to Saul, "You have acted foolishly; you did not keep the commandment which the LORD your God commanded you, for now the LORD would have established your kingdom over Israel forever."

14 And now your kingdom shall not endure: the LORD has sought a man after his heart, and the LORD has commanded him to be prince over his people, because you have not kept what the LORD commanded you.

15 Samuel rose up and went up from Gilgal to Gibeah of Benjamin, and Saul mustered the people who were with him, about six hundred men.

16 Saul and Jonathan his son and the people found with them were sitting in Geba of Benjamin, and the Philistines encamped in Mikmash.

17 The spoiler went out from the camp of the Philistines, three bands; the first band turned toward the way of Ophrah, to the land of Shual.

18 One band turns toward the way of Beth-horon, and one band turns toward the way of the border overlooking the valley of Zeboim toward the wilderness.

19 And no craftsman was found in all the land of Israel, because the Philistines feared that the Hebrews might make sword or spear.

20 All Israel went down to the Philistines to sharpen each man his share, and his coulter, and his ax, and his mattock.

21 The file shall be for the mattocks and for the plowshares and for the three forks and for the axes and for the sharpening of the goad.

22 And it came to pass on the day of battle that no sword or spear was found in the hand of any of the people who were with Saul and Jonathan, but swords were found for Saul and for Jonathan his son.

23 A Philistine garrison went out to the passage of Mikmash.


## Chapter 14

1 And it came to pass on the day that Jonathan son of Saul said to the young man who carried his weapons, “Let us go over to the Philistine garrison which is on the other side”; but to his father he did not tell.

2 And Saul was sitting at the edge of the hill in Migron under the pomegran�t, and the people who were with him were about six hundred men.

3 Ahiah son of Ahitub, brother of Ichabod, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, priest of the LORD at Shiloh, was wearing the ephod, but the people did not know that Jonathan had gone.

4 And between the fords where Jonathan sought to pass over against the Philistine garrison, a crag of the (ragged) rock on this side and a crag of the (ragged) rock on that side, and the name of the one was Bozez and the name of the other was Seneh.

5 The one crag was situated northward opposite Michmash, and the other southward opposite Geba.

6 Then Jonathan said to the lad who carried his weapons, “Come, let us cross over to the garrison of these uncircumcised; perhaps the LORD will act for us, for nothing restrains the LORD from saving by many or by few.”

7 And his armour-bearer said to him, "Do all that is in your heart; turn, behold, I am with you according to your heart."

8 Then Jonathan said, "Behold, we are crossing over to the men, and we will reveal ourselves to them."

9 If they say to us, "Wait until we come to you," then we will stand in our place and not go up to them.

10 And if they say, "Come up against us," then we will go up against them, because the LORD has given them into our hand, and this shall be the sign to us.

11 Then both of them were uncovered to the Philistine garrison, and the Philistines said, "Here are Hebrews coming out from the caves where they had hidden themselves."

12 The men of the garrison answered Jonathan and his armour-bearer, and said to them, “Come up to us, and we will show you a thing.” And Jonathan said to his armour-bearer, “Come up after me, for the LORD has given them into the hand of Israel.”

13 Jonathan climbed up on his hands and on his feet, and his armour-bearer after him; and they fell before Jonathan, and his armour-bearer put them to death after him.

14 The first blow that Jonathan struck, and his armor-bearer with him, killed about twenty men in one half-furrow of an acre of field.

15 There was trembling in the camp in the field and among all the people—the garrison and the spoiler were afraid, even they; and the earth trembled with a trembling of God.

16 The lookouts for Saul saw at Gibeah the hill of Benjamin, and behold, the multitude was stirring and going here and there.

17 Saul said to the folk who were with him, "Count now and see who has gone from us." And they counted, and behold, Jonathan and his armour-bearer were not there.

18 Saul said to Ahijah, Bring near the ark of God, for the ark of God was there that day with the sons of Israel.

19 And it was while Saul was still speaking to the priest, that the tumult in the Philistine camp kept growing louder and greater; so Saul said to the priest, "Withdraw your hand."

20 And Saul and all the people who were with him cried out and came to the battle, and behold, every man's sword was against his companion, a very great tumult.

21 And the Hebrews were with the Philistine as of yesterday and the day before, those who had gone up with them into the camp all around, and also they were to be with Israel, those with Saul and Jonathan.

22 And all the men of Israel who were hiding in the hill country of Ephraim heard that the Philistines had fled, and they also pursued after them into battle.

23 And the LORD saved Israel on that day, and the battle passed beyond Beth-aven.

24 Also, a certain man of Israel drew near on that day, and Saul pledged the people, saying, “Cursed be the man who eats food until evening, before I take vengeance on my enemies.” So none of the people tasted bread.

25 And all the land came into the forest, and there was honey upon the surface of the field.

26 The folk came into the wood, and behold, honey had dropped, but no man put his hand to his mouth, for the folk feared the oath.

27 Jonathan did not hear the chief binding the people, so he extended the end of the rod that was in his hand and dipped it into the honeycomb, and he returned his hand to his mouth, and his eyes brightened.

28 A certain man from the people answered and said, "Your father made the people swear, saying, 'Cursed be the man who eats bread today,'" and the people were faint.

29 Then Jonathan said, "My father has troubled the land. See now, my eyes are bright because I tasted a little of this honey."

30 Indeed, if only the people had eaten today from the spoil of their enemies which they found, for now the slaughter would not have been so great among the Philistines.

31 On that day they struck the Philistines from Michmas to Aijalon, and the people were very faint.

32 The folk took sheep and beeve and young of the herd and slaughtered upon the ground and the folk ate on the blood.

33 They told Saul, saying, "Behold, the people are sinning against the LORD by eating with the blood." And he said, "You have broken faith; roll to me today a great stone."

34 Saul said, "Scatter among the people and say to them, 'Bring near me each man his bull and each man his sheep, and slaughter them here and eat, so that you do not sin against the LORD by eating blood.'" Then all the people brought each man his bull in his hand that night, and they slaughtered them there.

35 And Saul built an altar to the LORD; he began to build the altar to the LORD.

36 Saul said, "Go down after the Philistines by night and plunder them until morning light, and let no man be left among them." And they said, "Do whatever is good in your eyes." Then the priest said, "Come near here to God."

37 Saul inquired of God, "Shall I go down after the Philistines? Will you deliver them into the hand of Israel?" But he did not answer him that day.

38 Saul said, "Come here, all you chief men of the people, and know and see how this sin has arisen today."

39 For the LORD who saves Israel lives, except Jonathan my son dies—he shall surely die—and no one of the people answers him.

40 And he said to all Israel, you shall be on one side and I and Jonathan my son will be on one side; and the people said to Saul, do what is good in your eyes.

41 Saul said to the LORD God of Israel, "Show the blameless," and Jonathan and Saul were taken, but the people went out.

42 Then Saul said, Cast the lot between me and Jonathan my son, and Jonathan was taken.

43 Saul said to Jonathan, "Tell me what you have done." And Jonathan told him, saying, "I tasted a little honey with the end of the staff that was in my hand. Here I am; I must die."

44 Then Saul said, Thus may God do, and thus may he add, that Jonathan must die.

45 Then the people said to Saul, Shall Jonathan die, who wrought this great deliverance in Israel? Far be it! As the LORD liveth, not one hair of his head shall fall to the ground; for with God he did this day. So the people rescued Jonathan, and he died not.

46 Saul went up from after the Philistines, and the Philistines went to their place.

47 And Saul took the kingship over Israel, and he fought against all his enemies, in Moab, and in the sons of Ammon, and in Edom, and in the kings of Zoba, and in the Philistines, and in all to whom he turned his face he did evil.

48 He performed valour and struck Amalek, and delivered Israel from the hand of its oppressor.

49 The sons of Saul were Jonathan and Ishvi and Malchishua, and the name of his two daughters was Merab the firstborn and Michal the younger.

50 The name of Saul's wife was Ahinoam daughter of Ahimaaz, and the name of the captain of his army was Abner son of Ner, Saul's uncle.

51 Kish the chief of Saul and Ner the chief of Abner son of Abiel.

52 And the war was severe against the Philistines all the days of Saul, and Saul saw every mighty man and every man of valor, and he gathered him to himself.


## Chapter 15

1 Samuel said to Saul, "The LORD sent me to anoint you king over his people over Israel; now therefore, listen to the voice of the words of the LORD."

2 Thus says the LORD of hosts: I have remembered what Amalek did to Israel, how he lay in wait for him along the way when he came up from Egypt.

3 Go now and strike Amalek and utterly destroy all that is his; do not spare him, but kill both man and woman, infant and nursing child, ox and sheep, camel and donkey.

4 And Saul summoned the people and numbered them at Telaim: two hundred thousand footmen and ten thousand men of Judah.

5 Then Saul came to the city of Amalek and disputed by the stream.

6 Saul said to the Kenite, "Go, depart from among the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them, for you showed favour to all the children of Israel when they came up from Egypt." So the Kenite departed from among Amalek.

7 Saul struck Amalek from Havilah as you go to Shur, which is opposite Egypt.

8 He took Agag king of Amalek alive, and all the people he utterly destroyed with the edge of the sword.

9 And Saul and the folk had pity on Agag and on the best of the sheep and the cattle and the fatlings and the rams and on all the good, and they were not willing to devote them to destruction, but every work that was vile and abhorrent, that they devoted to destruction.

10 The word of the LORD came to Samuel, saying:

11 I regret that I made Saul king, for he has turned back from following me and has not carried out my commands; and Samuel was angry, and he cried out to the LORD all night.

12 Samuel rose early in the morning to meet Saul, and it was told to Samuel, saying, "Saul has come to the Carmel and behold, he is setting up a monument for himself; then he turned and passed on and went down to Gilgal."

13 Samuel came to Saul, and Saul said to him, "Blessed are you to the LORD; I have fulfilled the word of the LORD."

14 Samuel said, "What is this sound of the sheep in my ears, and the sound of the cattle that I hear?"

15 Saul said, "From the Amalekites they brought them; the people spared the best of the sheep and the cattle, to sacrifice to the Lord your God, and the rest we devoted."

16 Samuel said to Saul, "Wait, and I will tell you what Jehovah spoke to me at night."

17 Samuel said, "Is it not that you are small in your own sight, chief of the tribes of Israel? And the LORD has anointed you to be king over Israel."

18 And the LORD sent you on a journey and said, Go and utterly destroy the sinners, the Amalekites; fight against them until you have finished them.

19 Why did you not obey the voice of the LORD, but rush upon the spoil, and do evil in the sight of the LORD?

20 Saul said to Samuel, "I have obeyed the voice of the LORD and have gone the way by which the LORD sent me, and I have brought Agag the king of Amalek, and Amalek I have utterly destroyed."

21 And the folk took from the spoil sheep and beeve, the chief of the things which should have been utterly destroyed, to sacrifice to the LORD your God at Gilgal.

22 And Samuel said, Is it acceptable to the Lord with burnt offerings and sacrifices as with obeying the voice of the Lord? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, to listen than the fat of rams.

23 For the punishment of divination and rebellion, the iniquity of idolatry, therefore you have rejected the word of the LORD, so he has rejected you from being king.

24 Saul said to Samuel, I have sinned, for I have transgressed the command of the LORD and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.

25 Now please take away my punishment for sin, and return with me, that I may bow down to the LORD.

26 Samuel said to Saul, "I will not return with you, because you have rejected the word of the LORD, and the LORD has rejected you from being king over Israel."

27 Samuel turned to go, and he caught hold of the corner of his robe, and it tore.

28 And Samuel said to him, "The LORD has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this day and given it to your fellow who is better than you."

29 Moreover, the alway of Israel will neither deceive nor relent, for He is not a man to relent.

30 I have sinned; now honor me, I pray, before the elders of my people and before Israel; return with me, that I may bow down to the LORD your God.

31 Then Samuel turned back after Saul, and Saul bowed to the LORD.

32 Then Samuel said, "Bring me Agag king of Amalek." And Agag came to him delicately, and Agag said, "Surely the bitterness of death is past."

33 And Samuel said, "As your sword has made women childless, so shall your mother be childless among women." And Samuel hewed Agag to pieces before the LORD at Gilgal.

34 Samuel went to Ramah, and Saul went up to his home at Gibeah of Saul.

35 And Samuel did not see Saul again until the day of his death, for Samuel grieved over Saul, and the LORD regretted that he had made Saul king over Israel.


## Chapter 16

1 And the LORD said to Samuel, How long will you mourn for Saul, seeing I have rejected him from reigning over Israel? Fill your horn with oil and go; I am sending you to Jesse the Bethlehemite, for I have seen among his sons a king for myself.

2 Samuel said, How can I go? For Saul will hear and kill me. And Jehovah said, Take a heifer in your hand and say, I have come to sacrifice to the Lord.

3 And call Jesse to the sacrifice, and I will tell you what you shall do, and you shall anoint for me the one I name to you.

4 Samuel did what Jehovah spoke, and he came to Bethlehem; the elders of the city trembled at meeting him, and said, "Do you come in peace?"

5 Peace, I have come to offer sacrifice to the LORD; sanctify yourselves and come with me to the sacrifice. So he sanctified Jesse and his sons, and called them to the sacrifice.

6 When they came, he saw Eliab and said, "Surely the LORD's anointed stands before the LORD."

7 And the LORD said to Samuel, Do not look upon his appearance or to the height of his stature, because I have rejected him, for not as man sees does man see, because man sees to the eyes, but the LORD sees to the heart.

8 Then Jesse called Abinadab and brought him before Samuel, and he said, "Also in this one the LORD has not chosen."

9 And Jesse passed Shammah, and he said, "Also in this one Jehovah did not choose."

10 Jesse passed seven of his sons before Samuel, and Samuel said to Jesse, The LORD has not chosen these.

11 Samuel said to Jesse, "Have the boys all come?" And he said, "The youngest one remains, and behold, he is tending the flock." And Samuel said to Jesse, "Send and bring him here, for we will not sit down until he comes here."

12 And he sent and brought him, and he was ruddy, with beautiful eyes and goodly to look upon; and the LORD said, "Arise, anoint him, for this is he."

13 Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him among his brothers, and the spirit of the LORD rushed upon David from that day forward; then Samuel arose and went to Ramah.

14 And the spirit of the LORD turned from Saul, and terrified him with a spirit of evil from the LORD.

15 And the servants of Saul said to him, Behold now, an evil spirit from God troubles you.

16 Let our lord now command his servants before you to seek a man who knows how to play the harp, and it shall be that when the spirit of God is upon you in distress, he will play with his hand and you will be refreshed.

17 Saul said to his servants, "Look now for me for a man who is good at playing the lyre, and bring him to me."

18 One of the lads answered and said, Behold, I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite, skilled in playing, and a mighty man of valour, and a man of war, and prudent in speech, and a man of form, and the LORD is with him.

19 Saul sent messengers to Jesse and said, Send to me David your son who is with the flock.

20 And Jesse took an ass, bread, a bottle of wine, and one kid of the goats, and sent them by the hand of David his son to Saul.

21 David came to Saul and stood before him; and he loved him greatly, and he became his armour-bearer.

22 Saul sent to Jesse saying Let David stand before me for David has found favour in my eyes

23 And it came to pass, when the spirit of God came upon Saul, that David took the harp and played with his hand; and Saul was refreshed, and it was well with him, and the evil spirit departed from him.


## Chapter 17

1 The Philistine gathered their armies for battle and assembled at Shochoh, which belongs to Judah, and camped between Shochoh and Azekah, at Ephes-dammim.

2 Saul and the men of Israel gathered and camped in the valley of Elah, and they arrayed for battle against the Philistines.

3 And the Philistines are standing on the hill over here, and Israel is standing on the hill over there, and the valley between them.

4 And there came out a certain champion from the army camps of the Philistines, Goliath was his name, from Gath; his height was six cubits and a span.

5 And a brass helmet upon his head, and a coat of mail he wears, and the full weight of the coat of mail is five thousand shekels of brass.

6 brasen greaves upon his legs, and a brasen spear between his shoulders

7 His spear was like a weaver's beam, and the head of his spear weighed six hundred shekels of iron, and he who bore the shield went before him.

8 He stood and called out to the armies of Israel, and said to them, Why do you come out to set yourselves in battle order? Am I not the Philistine, and are you not servants to Saul? Choose for yourselves a man, and let him come down to me.

9 If he is able to fight with me and strike me down, then we will be your servants; but if I am able to prevail over you and strike you down, then you shall be to us for servants and shall serve us.

10 And the Philistine said, I have defied the armies of Israel this day; give me a man, that we may fight together.

11 Saul and all Israel heard the words of the Philistine, and they were dismayed and greatly afraid.

12 David, this son of an Ephrathite from Bethlehem in Judah, whose name was Jesse, had eight sons; and the man was old and advanced in years during the days of Saul.

13 Three sons of Jesse, the firstborn and his brothers, went after Saul to the battle, and the names of the three sons who went to the battle were Eliab the firstborn, and his second Abinadab, and the third Shammah.

14 David was the youngest, and the three older ones went after Saul.

15 And David went and returned from before Saul to pasture the flock of his father in Bethlehem.

16 And the Philistine drew near morning and evening, and stationed himself for forty days.

17 Jesse said to his son David, "Take now for your brothers an ephah of this parched corn and ten loaves of this bread, and run to the camp to your brothers."

18 And you shall bring these ten portions of cheese to the captain of a thousand, and you shall visit your brothers for welfare, and you shall take their pledge.

19 And Saul, and they, and every man of Israel, were in the valley of Elah, fighting with the Philistines.

20 David rose early in the morning and left the flock with the keeper, then took up his things and went as Jesse had commanded him; he came to the trench, and the army was going out to the battle line and drawing up for battle.

21 And Israel was set in order and the Philistine in rank opposite rank.

22 David left the armour on the ground beside the armour-bearer, ran to the army, and came and asked his brothers how they fared.

23 And he was speaking with them when, behold, the champion Goliath the Philistine from Gath, his name, came up from the army of the Philistines and spoke according to these words, and David heard him.

24 And every man of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from his presence and were greatly afraid.

25 And a certain man of Israel said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? He comes up to defy Israel. The king will enrich with great riches the man who strikes him down, and will give him his daughter, and will make his father’s house free in Israel.”

26 David said to the men standing with him, "What will be done for the man who strikes down this Philistine and removes the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should defy the armies of the living God?"

27 And the people said to him according to this word, saying, Thus shall it be done to the man whom he defeats.

28 Eliab, David's older brother, heard him speaking to the men, and Eliab's anger burned against David, and he said, "Why have you come down here? And whom have you left with the few sheep in the wilderness? I know your pride and the evil of your heart, for you have come down only to see the battle."

29 David said, "What have I done now? Is there not an act?"

30 He turned from him and approached another, and said the same thing, and the people answered him with the same answer as at first.

31 And the acts that David had done were reported before Saul, and he took him.

32 David said to Saul, Let no man's heart fail him; your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.

33 Saul said to David, You are not able to go to this Philistine to fight with him, for you are a lad and he has been a man of war from his youth.

34 David said to Saul, Your servant was a shepherd for his father over the flock, and the young lion and the bear came and carried off a lamb from the drove.

35 And I went out after him and struck him and delivered from his mouth, and he rose up against me, and I held fast to his beard and struck him and killed him.

36 Your servant has also struck the young lion and the bear, and so shall the uncircumcised Philistine be like one of them, for he has defied the armies of the living God.

37 David said, "The LORD who delivered me from the paw of the lion and from the paw of the bear, he will deliver me from the hand of the Philistine." And Saul said to David, "Go, and the LORD be with you."

38 And Saul clothed David with his garments, and put a brasen helmet upon his head, and clothed him with a coat of mail.

39 David girded his sword upon his garments and tried to walk, for he had not tested them; and David said to Saul, "I cannot walk with these, for I have not tested them"; and David removed them from upon himself.

40 He took his rod in his hand and chose for himself five sling stones from the brook and put them in the shepherd's bag which he had and in the scrip, and his sling was in his hand, and he approached the Philistine.

41 The Philistine went forward and came near David, and the man bearing the shield was before him.

42 The Philistine looked and saw David, and he despised him, for he was a lad, ruddy and with a fair appearance.

43 The Philistine said to David, “Am I a dog, that you come to me with rods?” And the Philistine cursed David by his gods.

44 The Philistine said to David, Come to me, and I will give your body to the birds of the air and to the beasts of the field.

45 David said to the Philistine, You come to me with sword, and with spear, and with javelin, but I come to you in the name of the LORD of armies, the God of the armies of Israel, whom you have defied.

46 This day the LORD will deliver you into my hand, and I will strike you and remove your head from you; and I will give the corpse of the camp of the Philistines this day to the birds of the heavens and to the beasts of the earth, so that all the earth may know that there is God in Israel.

47 And all the assembly shall know that it is not by sword or spear that the LORD saves, for the battle belongs to the LORD, and he will give you into our hand.

48 And it happened, when the Philistine arose and came and approached David, David hurried and ran from the army to meet the Philistine.

49 David sent his hand into the bag and took a stone from there and slung it and struck the Philistine on his forehead and the stone sank into his forehead and he fell on his face to the ground.

50 So David prevailed over the Philistine with the sling and the stone, and he struck the Philistine and killed him, and there was no sword in the hand of David.

51 David ran and stood over the Philistine, took his sword, drew it from its sheath, and killed him; he struck off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.

52 The men of Israel and Judah rose up and shouted and pursued the Philistines as far as the valley of Gath and to the gates of Ekron, and the slain of the Philistines fell along the way of Shaaraim and as far as Gath and Ekron.

53 The sons of Israel turned back from pursuing the Philistines and plundered their camp.

54 David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem, and his weapons he placed in his tent.

55 When Saul saw David going out to meet the Philistine, he said to Abner the captain of the host, "Whose son is this young man, Abner?" And Abner said, "As the life of the king, I do not know."

56 The king said, "Ask whose son this young man is."

57 When David returned from striking down the Philistine, Abner brought him before Saul, and the head of the Philistine was in his hand.

58 Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” And David said, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”


## Chapter 18

1 And it came to pass, when he had finished speaking to Saul, that the soul of Jonathan was knit to the soul of David; Jonathan loved him as his own soul.

2 Saul took him that day and did not let him return to his father's house.

3 Jonathan and David made a confederacy, for he loved him as himself.

4 Jonathan stripped off the robe that was on him and gave it to David, and his armor, even to his sword and to his bow and to his belt.

5 David prospered in all that Saul sent him to do, and Saul set him over the men of war; he was pleasing in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Saul's servants.

6 When David returned from striking down the Philistine, the women came out from all the cities of Israel, dancing companies to meet King Saul, with tabrets, with joy, and with instruments of music.

7 And the women answered, saying, "Saul has struck down his thousands, and David his ten thousands."

8 Saul was very angry, and this matter greatly displeased him, and he said, "They have given David ten thousands, and to me they have given the thousands; he lacks only the kingship."

9 Saul held a grudge against David from that day forward.

10 And it came to pass on the morrow that an evil spirit from God came mightily upon Saul, and he prophesied in the midst of the court, and David played with his hand as at other times, and the javelin was in Saul's hand.

11 Saul cast the javelin and said, "I will strike David and the wall," but David escaped from his presence twice.

12 Saul feared from the face of David, for the LORD was with him, but had departed from Saul.

13 And Saul removed him from with him and appointed him for himself a captain of a thousand, and he went out and came in before the people.

14 David was successful in all his endeavors, and the LORD was with him.

15 Saul saw that he was very successful, and Saul feared from his presence.

16 All Israel and Judah loved David, because he went out and came in before them.

17 Saul said to David, "Here is my daughter the elder, Merab; her I will give to you as wife, only be to me a valiant son and fight the wars of the LORD." But Saul said, "Let not my hand be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him."

18 David said to Saul, Who am I, and what is my life, my father's family in Israel, that I should be son in law to the king?

19 After Merab the daughter of Saul was given to David, she was given to Adriel the Mecholathite as wife.

20 Michal daughter of Saul loved David, and they told Saul, and the matter pleased him.

21 Saul said, "I will give her to him, and she will be a trap for him, and by her the hand of the Philistines will be against him." Then Saul said to David, "Today you shall be son-in-law to me with two hundred foreskins of the Philistines."

22 Saul commanded his servants to speak to David secretly, saying, "Behold, the king takes delight in you, and all his servants love you; now become the king's son-in-law."

23 And the servants of Saul spoke these words in the ears of David, and David said, Is this a light thing in your eyes, to be son-in-law to the king, when I am a poor and lightly esteemed man?

24 And the servants of Saul told him, saying, "According to these words spoke David."

25 Saul said: Thus you shall say to David: The king desires no dowry, but a hundred Philistine foreskins, to take vengeance on the king's enemies. Now Saul intended to cause David to fall by the hand of the Philistines.

26 His servants reported these matters to David, and the matter was pleasing in David's eyes, to enter into alliance with the king; but the days had not been completed.

27 David rose and went with his men and struck down two hundred men of the Philistines, and David brought their foreskins and filled them to the king to become the king's son-in-law; so Saul gave him Michal his daughter as a wife.

28 And Saul saw and knew that the LORD was with David, and Michal daughter of Saul loved him.

29 Saul added yet again to fear from the face of David, and Saul was David's enemy all the days.

30 The captains of the Philistines went out, and it came to pass, as often as David went out, he was more prudent than all the servants of Saul, and his fame was greatly increased.


## Chapter 19

1 Saul spoke to Jonathan his son and to all his servants to kill David, but Jonathan son of Saul greatly delighted in David.

2 Jonathan told David, saying, "Saul my father seeks to kill you; now therefore, please be on guard in the morning, and sit in hiding and conceal yourself."

3 And I will go out and stand beside my father in the field where you are, and I will speak to him about you, and I will see what he says and tell you.

4 Jonathan spoke well of David to Saul his father and said to him, Let not the king sin against his servant David, for David has not sinned against you, and for David's deeds have been very good toward you.

5 And he put his life in his hand and struck the Philistine, and the LORD made great deliverance for all Israel; you saw and rejoiced—why then will you sin with innocent blood to put David to death for nothing?

6 Saul listened to the voice of Jonathan, and Saul swore, "As the LORD lives, he shall not die."

7 Jonathan called David and reported to him all these words, and Jonathan brought David to Saul, and he was before him as formerly.

8 The battle continued, and David went out and fought against the Philistines, and he struck them with a great slaughter, and they fled from his presence.

9 And the spirit of the LORD was bad upon Saul, and he was sitting in his house with his spear in his hand, and David was playing with a hand.

10 Saul sought to strike David with the javelin against the wall, but David escaped from before Saul, and the javelin struck the wall; David fled and escaped by night.

11 Saul sent messengers to David's house to guard him and to kill him in the morning, and Michal his wife told David, saying, If you do not save your life tonight, tomorrow you will be killed.

12 Michal let David down through the window, and he went and fled and escaped.

13 Michal took the idols and put them on the bed, and she placed the skin of goats at its head and covered it with the garment.

14 Saul sent messengers to take David, but she said, "He is ill."

15 Saul sent the messengers to see David, saying, Bring him up to me on the bed to kill him.

16 The messengers came and there were the idols upon the bed and a goat's hair pillow at its head.

17 Saul said to Michal, "Why have you deceived me like this and sent away my enemy, so that he escaped?" And Michal said to Saul, "He said to me, 'Send me away, why should I kill you?'"

18 David fled and escaped and came to Samuel at Ramah and told him all that Saul had done to him and he and Samuel went and stayed in Naioth.

19 And it was told to Saul, saying, "Behold, David is in Ramah, at Naioth."

20 Saul sent messengers to seize David, and he saw the company of prophets prophesying, with Samuel standing stationed over them; and the spirit of God came upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.

21 They reported to Saul, and he sent other messengers, and those messengers also prophesied; then Saul again sent messengers a third time, and those messengers also prophesied.

22 He went also to Ramah and came to the great well that is in Sechu and inquired and said, Where are Samuel and David? And one said, Behold, they are at Ramah.

23 He went there to Naioth in Ramah, and the spirit of God came even upon him, and he went on prophesying until he came to Naioth in Ramah.

24 He also stripped off his apparel and prophesied before Samuel, and lay naked all that day and all the night; therefore they say, Is Saul also among the prophets?


## Chapter 20

1 David fled from Naioth in Ramah and came and said before Jonathan what have I done what is my fault and what is my sin before your father that he seeks my life

2 He said to him, Far be it, you shall not die; behold, my father, any great matter or small matter, he will not conceal from my hearing; why then should my father hide this matter from me? It is not so.

3 David swore again and said, “Your father certainly knows that I have found favour in your eyes, and he said, ‘Let not Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved’—but as the LORD lives and as your life is precious, there is but a step between me and death.”

4 Jonathan said to David, "What is your desire, and I will do it for you."

5 David said to Jonathan, "Look, tomorrow is the new moon, and I should return to sit with the king to eat, but send me away so that I may hide in the country until the evening of the third day."

6 If your father should inquire after me, then say, David earnestly asked leave of me to run to Bethlehem, his city, because there is a sacrifice of days there for every family.

7 If he says, "Good, your servant prospers," or if he is angry, know that the calamity has come upon him.

8 And you shall show favour to your servant, for in the covenant of the LORD you brought your servant with you; but if there is iniquity in me, you yourself should put me to death—why then would you bring me to your father?

9 Then Jonathan said, "Far be it from you, for if I knew that adversity were coming upon you from my father, I would surely tell you."

10 David said to Jonathan, "Who will tell me, or what will your father answer you harshly?"

11 Jonathan said to David, Go, let us go out to the country, and the two of them went out to the country.

12 Jonathan said to David, "Jehovah, God of Israel: tomorrow at this time I will find out about my father; if good is shown toward David, then I will not send to you and inform you; but if not, I will tell you and uncover your ear."

13 Thus will the LORD do to Jonathan, and more also, if adversity toward my father comes upon you; and I disclosed to your ear, and sent you, and you went in peace; and may the LORD be with you, as He was with my father.

14 but not if I am still alive will you do with me a favour of the LORD, nor will I die.

15 and do not cut off your favour from my household forever, even when the LORD cuts off every man of the enemies of David from upon the face of the land.

16 Jonathan made a covenant with the house of David, and the LORD sought from the hand of David's enemies.

17 Then Jonathan made David swear again by his love for him, because he loved him as the love of his own soul.

18 Jonathan said to him, tomorrow is the new moon, and your dwelling place will be missed, for it will be observed that you are not there.

19 And you shall go down very far, and come to the place where you hid on the day of the deed, and you shall sit by the stone Ezel.

20 But I will shoot three arrows to the side as a signal to him.

21 And behold, I will send the lad, saying, “Go, find the arrows”; if I say to the lad, “Behold, the arrows are beyond you and here—take them and come,” then it shall be peace for you, and there is no matter; as the LORD lives.

22 And if thus I say to the young man, Behold, the arrows are beyond you; turn back and go, for the LORD has sent you.

23 And the act that I and you have spoken, behold, the LORD is between me and you forever.

24 David hid in the field, and it came to be the new moon, and the king sat to eat food.

25 The king sat in his seat as was customary, opposite the seat by the wall; Jonathan rose, and Abner sat at Saul's side, and David's place was empty.

26 And Saul spoke nothing that day, for he said, "It is a chance occurrence; he is not clean, for he is not clean."

27 And it came to pass on the morrow of the new moon that David's place was empty, and Saul said to Jonathan his son, Why has not the son of Jesse come hither, neither yesterday nor today, to the food?

28 Jonathan answered Saul, David has certainly been sent away from me to Bethlehem.

29 And he said, “Please let me go, for a family sacrifice is ours in the city, and my brother commanded me; now, if I have found favour in your eyes, let me escape, I pray, and see my brothers.” Therefore I did not come to the king’s table.

30 Saul's anger burned against Jonathan, and he said to him, "Son of a rebellious wretch, did you not know that you have chosen the son of Jesse to your shame and to the shame of your mother's nakedness?"

31 For as long as Jesse's son lives on the earth, you and your kingdom will not be established; now therefore send and bring him to me, for he is a son of death.

32 Jonathan answered Saul his father and said to him, Why should he be put to death? What has he done?

33 Then Saul cast the javelin at him to strike him; and Jonathan knew that it was altogether determined with his father to kill David.

34 Jonathan rose from the table in fierce anger and did not eat food on the second day of the month, for he was grieved over David because his father had humiliated him.

35 And it came to pass in the morning, that Jonathan went out into the field to meet with David, and a little boy was with him.

36 He said to his boy, “Run, please, find the arrows that I shoot.” The boy ran, and he shot the arrow past him.

37 The lad came to the place of the arrow that Jonathan had shot, and Jonathan called after the lad and said, "Is not the arrow beyond you?"

38 And Jonathan called to his lad, Quickly, swiftly do not delay! And the lad of Jonathan gathered up the arrow and came to his master.

39 And the boy knew nothing, only Jonathan and David knew the thing.

40 Jonathan gave his artillery to the lad who was with him and said to him, Go, carry them to the city.

41 The lad came, and David rose from beside the south, and fell on his face to the ground and prostrated himself three times, and each man kissed his companion, and each man wept for his companion, until David made great.

42 Jonathan said to David, "Go in peace, since we both swore in the name of the LORD, saying, 'The LORD will be between me and you, and between my seed and your seed forever.'" And he arose and went, and Jonathan came to the city.


## Chapter 21

1 David came to Nob, to Ahimelech the priest; and Ahimelech trembled when he met David, and said to him, "Why are you alone, and no man is with you?"

2 David said to Ahimelech the priest, "The king has given me a matter, saying, 'Let no man know anything of the matter that I am sending you and that I have commanded you, and with regard to the young men, I have directed them to a certain place.'"

3 And now, what do you have on hand? Give five loaves of food into my power, or whatever is found.

4 The priest answered David and said, There is no common bread on hand, only consecrated bread; if the young men have kept themselves from women.

5 David answered the priest and said to him, "Surely if women have been withheld from us as heretofore, when I went out, then the vessels of the young men were holy, even though it was a common way; how much more today, when the vessel will be sanctified?"

6 The priest gave him holy food because there was no bread there except the bread of the Presence that had been removed from before the LORD, to set out hot bread on the day it was taken up.

7 And there a certain man from the servants of Saul was detained before the LORD that day, and his name was Doeg the Edomite, the chief of the shepherds who belonged to Saul.

8 David said to Ahimelech, Is there not here under your authority a spear or a sword? For neither my sword nor my weapons did I take in my hand, because the king’s business was urgent.

9 The priest said, "The sword of Goliath the Philistine whom you struck down in the valley of Elah—look, it is wrapped in a garment behind the ephod. If you want it, take it, for there is no other like it here." And David said, "There is none like it; give it to me."

10 David arose and fled that day from the presence of Saul and went to Achish king of Gath.

11 And the servants of Achish said to him, Is not this David the king of the land? Is not this the one to whom they sing in dances, saying, Saul has slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands?

12 David took these acts to heart and greatly feared King Achish of Gath.

13 And he changed his behaviour before them, and feigned madness in their hands, and scratched marks on the doors of the gate, and let his spittle fall upon his beard.

14 Achish said to his servants, "Behold, you see a man who is raving mad; why have you brought him to me?"

15 I am without the mad ones, for you have brought this one to drive me mad; will this one enter my household?


## Chapter 22

1 David went from there and escaped to the cave of Adullam, and his brothers and all his father's house heard and went down to him there.

2 And all the men of anguish, and all the men who had a grievance, and all the men of bitter spirit gathered to him, and he became their captain, and there were with him about four hundred men.

3 David went from there to Mizpeh of Moab and said to the king of Moab, “Let my father and my mother please stay with you until I know what God will do for me.”

4 And he was accepted before the king of Moab, and they dwelt with him all the days of David's being in the stronghold.

5 Gad the prophet said to David, “Do not stay in the stronghold; go, and enter the land of Judah.” And David went and came to the forest of Hereth.

6 Saul heard that David and the men who were with him had been reported, and Saul was sitting in Gibeah under the grove in the high place, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing beside him.

7 Saul said to his servants who stood by him, "Hear now, you men of the right hand: will the son of Jesse also give to all of you fields and vineyards? To all of you will he appoint captains of thousands and captains of hundreds?"

8 For all of you have conspired against me, and there is no one who uncovers my ear when my son makes a covenant with the son of Jesse, and none of you is grieved for me or uncovers my ear that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait this day.

9 Doeg the Edomite answered, and he was standing among the bondmen of Saul, and said, I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech son of Ahitub.

10 And he asked of the LORD, and provision was given to him, and also the sword of Goliath the Philistine was given to him.

11 Then the king sent to summon Ahimelech son of Ahitub the priest and all his father's house, the priests who were in Nob; and they all came to the king.

12 Saul said, "Please listen, son of Ahitub." And he said, "Here I am, my lord."

13 Saul said, Why have you conspired against me, you and the son of Jesse, by giving him bread and sword and inquiring of God for him, to rise up against me with ambush today?

14 Ahimelech answered the king and said, Who among all your servants is as faithful as David, the king's son-in-law, who attends to your bidding and is honored in your house?

15 Is this the first time I have inquired of God for him? Far be it from me! Let not the king impute any matter to your servant throughout all the house of my father, for your servant knew nothing of this, neither small nor great.

16 The king said, You will surely die, Ahimelech, you and all your father's house.

17 The king said to the runners who stood before him, "Surround and put to death the priests of the LORD, for indeed their hand is with David, and indeed they knew that he was fleeing and did not disclose it." But the servants of the king were not willing to put forth their hands to attack the priests of the LORD.

18 The king said, "Turn, Doeg, and attack the priests." So Doeg the Edomite turned and attacked the priests, and he killed on that day eighty-five men who bore the linen ephod.

19 And Nob, the city of the priests, he struck with the edge of the sword, from man to woman, from infant to suckling, and ox, and donkey, and sheep, with the edge of the sword.

20 One son of Ahimelech son of Ahitub escaped, and his name was Abiathar, and he fled after David.

21 Abiathar told David that Saul had slain the priests of the LORD.

22 David said to Abiathar, I knew on that day that the Edomite was there because Doeg would surely report to Saul; I myself have brought about the death of every person of your father's house.

23 Stay with me, do not fear, for whoever seeks my life will seek your life, for you are under my protection.


## Chapter 23

1 They told David, saying, Behold, the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and they are seizing the threshing-floors.

2 David inquired of the LORD, saying, Shall I go and strike the Philistines? And the LORD said to David, Go and strike the Philistines and save Keilah.

3 And the men of David said to him, Behold, we are here in Judah afraid, and how much more if we go to Keilah against the army of the Philistines.

4 David again inquired of the LORD, and the LORD answered him and said, "Arise, go down to Keilah, for I will deliver the Philistines into your hand."

5 David went to Keilah and fought against the Philistines and drove away their cattle and struck them with a great blow, and David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.

6 When Abiathar son of Ahimelech fled to David at Keilah, an ephod came down in his hand.

7 And it was told to Saul that David had come to Keilah, and Saul said, "God has delivered him into my hand, for he has shut himself in by entering a city with two doors and a bar."

8 And Saul summoned all the people to the war, to go down to Keilah against David and against his men.

9 David knew that Saul was plotting evil against him, and he said to Abiathar the priest, “Bring me the ephod.”

10 David said, "O Jehovah, God of Israel, your servant has heard that Saul is seeking to come to Keilah to destroy the city because of me."

11 Will the men of Keilah deliver me into his hand? Will Saul come down as Your servant has heard? I pray You, tell Your servant. And the LORD said, He will come down.

12 David said, "Will the men of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul?" And Jehovah said, "They will deliver."

13 David rose up, and his men, six hundred men, and they went out from Keilah and walked wherever they could walk, and it was told to Saul that David had escaped from Keilah, so he stopped going out.

14 David stayed in the strongholds of the desert and remained on the hill in the wilderness of Ziph, and Saul sought him every day, but God did not give him into his hand.

15 David saw that Saul had come out to seek his life, and David was in the wilderness of Ziph in the forest.

16 Jonathan the son of Saul arose and went to David in the forest and strengthened his hand in God.

17 And he said to him, Do not fear, for the hand of Saul my father will not find you, and you shall reign over Israel, and I will be next to you, and even Saul my father knows this.

18 And the two of them made a confederacy before the LORD, and David stayed in the forest, and Jonathan went to his home.

19 The Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah, saying, "Is not David hiding with us in the strongholds in the wood, on the hill of Hachilah, which is south of the wilderness?"

20 Now therefore, as for all the desires of your soul, let the king go down; and we will deliver him into the hand of the king.

21 Saul said, "You are blessed to the LORD, for you have shown mercy to me."

22 Go now, make ready again, and know and see the place where his foot will be; who has seen him there? For he said to me, "He will surely hide himself."

23 And see and know from all the hiding places where he may hide, and you shall return to me in truth, and I will walk with you, and if he remains in the land, I will search him out in all the thousands of Judah.

24 So the Ziphites arose and went before Saul, while David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon in the Arabah, to the south of Jeshimon.

25 Saul and his men went to search, and they reported to David; then David descended to the rocky stronghold and stayed in the wilderness of Maon, and Saul heard, and he pursued David in the wilderness of Maon.

26 Saul went on one side of the hill and David and his men on the other side of the hill, and David was hurrying to escape from Saul's presence, while Saul and his men were encircling David and his men to seize them.

27 A messenger came to Saul, saying, "Hurry and go, for the Philistines have raided the land."

28 Then Saul returned from pursuing David and went to meet the Philistines; therefore they called that place Sela-hammalekoth.

29 David went up from there and stayed in the fortresses of En-gedi.


## Chapter 24

1 When Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, they told him saying, "Behold, David is in the wilderness of En-gedi."

2 Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel and went to seek David and his men on the face of the wild goat rocks.

3 And he came to the sheep-cotes along the way, and there was a cave; and Saul went in to cover his feet, but David and his men were sitting in the sides of the cave.

4 The men of David said to him, Behold, this day the LORD said to you, Behold, I give Saul into your hand, and you shall do to him as it seems good in your eyes. Then David arose and cut off the edge of the robe that was on Saul secretly.

5 Afterward, David's heart struck him for having cut off the corner of the garment that belonged to Saul.

6 Then he said to his men, Far be it from me because of the LORD that I should do this act to the lord, to the anointed of the LORD, by sending my hand against him, for he is the anointed of the LORD.

7 David divided his men according to the advice and did not let them rise up against Saul, but Saul rose from the cave and went on the journey.

8 Then David rose up afterward and went out and called after Saul, saying, "My lord, the king!" And Saul looked behind him, and David bowed with his face to the ground and prostrated himself.

9 David said to Saul, Why do you listen to the words of a man, saying, David seeks your harm?

10 Behold, this day your eyes have seen how the LORD delivered you today into my hand in the cave, and one said to kill you, but I had compassion on you, and I said, “I will not put forth my hand against my lord, for he is the anointed of the LORD.”

11 My chief, see indeed, look at the corner of your robe in my hand: because I cut off the corner of your robe and did not kill you, know and see that there is no evil and transgression in my hand; I have not sinned against you, but you are hunting my life to take it.

12 The LORD will judge between me and you, and the LORD will avenge me against you, but my hand shall not be against you.

13 As the byword of the ancients says, "From the wicked comes wickedness," my hand shall not be against you.

14 After whom has the king of Israel gone out? After whom do you pursue? After a dead dog, after a single flea.

15 And the LORD will be judge, and will judge between me and you, and see, and plead my cause, and deliver me from your hand.

16 And it came to pass, when David had finished speaking these words to Saul, that Saul said, Is this your voice, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice and wept.

17 And he said to David, You are righteous more than me, for you have dealt well with me, but I have repaid you with adversity.

18 You have shown today the good you did to me, that the LORD delivered me into your hand and you did not kill me.

19 If a man finds his enemy, yet sends him away in a good way, the LORD will repay you with good for this day that you have done to me.

20 And now, behold, I know that you will surely reign, and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hand.

21 Now therefore swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my seed after me, nor destroy my name from the house of my father.

22 David swore to Saul, and Saul went to his home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.


## Chapter 25

1 Samuel died, and all Israel gathered and mourned for him, and they buried him in his home in Ramah. David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.

2 Also, a certain man from Maon, and his business was in Carmel; the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep and one thousand goats; and it came to pass, when shearing his sheep in Carmel,

3 The man's name was Nabal and his wife's name was Abigal, and the woman was of good understanding and beautiful appearance, but the man was harsh and evil in his deeds and churlish.

4 David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.

5 David sent ten lads, and David said to the lads, "Go up to Carmel and come to Nabal, and inquire for him in my name concerning peace."

6 And you shall say thus to the living: you are well, and your household is well, and all that is yours is well.

7 Now I have heard that you have sheared those who were yours; the shepherds who were with us did not shame us, nor was there any fault found with them all the time they were at Carmel.

8 Ask your servants and they shall tell you, and the servants shall find favour in your eyes, for we came on account of a feast day; please give what your hand finds to your servants and to your son David.

9 And the servants of David came and spoke to Nabal all these words in the name of David, and they waited.

10 Nabal answered David's servants and said, "Who is David and who is the son of Jesse? Today there are many servants fleeing each man from his master."

11 I will take my bread and my water and the flesh I slaughtered for my shearers and give it to men who do not know from where they come.

12 The lads of David turned back along their way and returned and came and told him all these things.

13 David said to his men, "Each man gird on his sword," and each man girded on his sword, and David also girded on his sword, and four hundred men went up after David, and two hundred stayed by the baggage.

14 And a young man told Abigal, Nabal's wife, saying, Behold, David sent messengers from the wilderness to greet our lord, but he rebuked them.

15 And the men were very good to us, and we were not shamed nor lacked anything all the days we were with them while we were in the field.

16 We were as a walled wall, both by night and by day, all the days of our dwelling with them, shepherding the flock.

17 Now therefore know and consider what you will do, because adversity has come upon our lord and upon all his house, and he is a son of Belial in speaking to him.

18 And Abigal hurried and took two hundred loaves of bread and two bottles of wine and five sheep and five measures of parched corn and a hundred bunches of raisins and two hundred cakes of figs and placed them upon the asses.

19 And she said to her servant, "Go before me; behold, I am coming after you," but to her husband Nabal she did not tell.

20 And it was, as she was riding on the ass, going down by the hiding place of the hill, that behold, David and his men were coming down to meet her, and she met them.

21 And David said, Surely without a cause I kept watch over all that belonged to this man in the wilderness, and nothing was missed of all that was his, yet he repays me evil for good.

22 Thus may God do to the enemies of David and more may He add: if I leave of all that belongs to him until the morning, one who urinates against a wall.

23 Abigal saw David and hurried down from the ass and fell before David on her face and prostrated herself to the ground.

24 She fell at his feet and said, "My lord, let me bear the iniquity; please let your handmaid speak in your hearing, and hear the words of your handmaid."

25 Please do not let my lord pay attention to this worthless man Nabal, for as his name is, so is he: Nabal is his name, and folly is with him; but I, your servant, did not see my lord's young men whom you sent.

26 Now, my lord, as the LORD lives, and as your life is dear, the LORD has kept you this day from coming to bloodshed and from avenging your hand upon yourself; now therefore let your enemies be as Nabal, and those who seek harm against my lord.

27 And now this present which your handmaid has brought to my lord, let it be given to the young men who walk at the feet of my lord.

28 Please lift up the rebellion of your handmaid, for the LORD will make for my lord a faithful house, because my lord has fought the battles of the LORD, and no calamity will be found in you all your days.

29 Another will rise up to pursue you and seek your life, but the life of my lord will be bound in the bundle of life with the LORD your God, and the lives of your enemies he will hurl in the middle of the sling.

30 And it shall be, when Jehovah does for my lord according to all the good that he has spoken concerning you, and commands you to be leader over Israel,

31 And let this not become grief and a stumbling-block to your heart, my lord, nor the shedding of blood without cause, nor my lord saving himself; but the LORD will do good to my lord, and you will remember your handmaid.

32 David said to Abigal, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who sent you this day to meet me."

33 And blessed be your advice and blessed are you, who today have kept me from entering into bloodguiltiness and preserved my hand from doing violence to me.

34 But as surely as the LORD, the God of Israel lives, who has restrained me from doing you harm, if you had not hurried to meet me, by morning light there would not have been left unto Nabal so much as one that pisseth against the wall.

35 David took from her hand what she had brought to him, and said to her, Go up in peace to your house; behold, I have heeded your voice and lifted up your face.

36 Abigal came to Nabal, and there was a banquet in his house like the king's feast, and Nabal's heart was good upon him, and he was drunk exceedingly; and she told him nothing small or great until the morning light.

37 And it came to pass in the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, that his wife told him these words, and his heart died within him, and he became as a stone.

38 And it came to pass, after ten days, that the LORD struck Nabal and he died.

39 David heard that Nabal was dead, and said, Blessed be the LORD who has pleaded my cause of reproach from the hand of Nabal and has kept back his servant from evil; and the evil of Nabal the LORD has returned upon his own head. Then David sent and spoke to Abigail to take her to himself as wife.

40 David's servants came to Abigal at Carmel and spoke to her, saying, "David has sent us to you to take you to him as wife."

41 She rose and fell facedown to the ground and said, "Behold, your female servant is a maidservant to wash the feet of my lord's servants."

42 And Abigal hurried and arose and rode on the ass, with her five damsels following her on foot; she went after David's messengers and became his wife.

43 And David took Ahinoam from Jezreel, and both of them became his wives.

44 Saul gave Michal his daughter, the wife of David, to Palti son of Laish, who was from Gallim.


## Chapter 26

1 The Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah, saying, "Is not David hiding at the hill of Hachilah, opposite the wilderness?"

2 Saul arose and went down to the wilderness of Ziph, and with him were three thousand chosen men of Israel to seek David in the wilderness of Ziph.

3 Saul encamped at the hill of Hachilah, which is opposite the wilderness by the way, and David remained in the wilderness; and David saw that Saul had come after him into the wilderness.

4 David sent spies and learned that Saul had come to Nachon.

5 David rose and went to the place where Saul had camped and saw the place where Saul and Abner son of Ner, the captain of his army, had lain, with Saul lying in the trench and the people encamped around him.

6 David answered and said to Ahimelech the Hittite and to Abishai son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother, "Who will go down with me to Saul at the camp?" And Abishai said, "I will go down with you."

7 David and Abishai came to the folk by night, and there was Saul lying asleep in the trench, and his javelin was stuck in the ground, and Abner and the men were lying around him.

8 Abishai said to David, "God has delivered your enemy into your hand today; now let me strike him once with the javelin to the ground, and I will not do it a second time."

9 David said to Abishai, Do not destroy him, for who has raised his hand against the anointed of the LORD and remained innocent?

10 David said, "As the LORD lives, surely the LORD will strike him, or his day will come and he will die, or he will go down into battle and perish."

11 Far be it from me before the LORD to stretch out my hand against the anointed of the LORD; now, please, take the spear and the cruse of water and let us go.

12 David took the spear and the cruse of water from the bolster by Saul's head, and they went away; and there was none seeing, none knowing, none awakening, for all of them were asleep, because a deep sleep from the LORD had fallen upon them.

13 David passed over against and stood on the top of the hill afar off, a great space between them.

14 David called to the people and to Abner son of Ner, saying, "Will you not answer, Abner?" And Abner answered and said, "Who are you who calls to the king?"

15 David said to Abner, "Are you not a man, and who is like you in Israel? Why did you not guard your lord the king? For one of the people came to destroy your lord the king."

16 The act is not good that you have done: as the LORD lives, you are men of death, because you did not guard your lord, the anointed of the LORD. Now see, where is the king’s spear and the cruse of water that was at his head?

17 Saul recognized the voice of David and said, Is this your voice, my son David? And David said, It is my voice, my lord, the king.

18 And he said, Why does my lord pursue after his servant? For what have I done, and what evil is in my hand?

19 Now please hear, my lord the king, the words of your servant: if the LORD has incited you against me, may He accept an offering; but if it is the sons of men, accursed be they before the LORD, for they have driven me out today from joining the inheritance of the LORD, saying, “Go, serve other gods.”

20 And now let not my blood fall to the ground before the face of the LORD, for the king of Israel has come out to seek a single flea, as one might pursue a partridge in the mountains.

21 Saul said, Return, my son David, for I have sinned; I will harm you no more, since my life was precious in your eyes this day; behold, I have acted foolishly, and I have erred greatly.

22 Then David answered and said, "Here is the king's spear; let one of the boys go over and take it."

23 And the LORD will return to the man his righteousness and his faithfulness, which the LORD gave you today into your hand, and I was not willing to stretch out my hand against the anointed of the LORD.

24 And behold, as your soul has grown today in my eyes, so may my soul grow in the eyes of the LORD, and may he deliver me from every trouble.

25 Saul said to David, "You are blessed, my son David; indeed you will succeed, and indeed you will prevail." Then David went on his way, and Saul returned to his place.


## Chapter 27

1 David said in his heart, Now shall I be taken one day by the hand of Saul; there is no good for me but to escape, to escape to the land of the Philistines; and Saul will cease from me to seek me any more throughout all the border of Israel, and I will escape from his hand.

2 David rose and passed on, he and six hundred men who were with him, to Achish son of Maoch, king of Gath.

3 David stayed with Achish in Gath, he and his men, each man with his household; David and his two wives, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess and Abigal the wife of Nabal the Carmelitess.

4 And it was told to Saul that David had fled to Gath, and he no longer sought him.

5 David said to Achish, If I have found favour in your eyes, let them give me a place in one of the cities of the field, and I will dwell there; why should your servant dwell in the royal city with you?

6 And Achish gave him Ziklag on that day, therefore Ziklag belonged to the kings of Judah to this day.

7 And it came to pass, the number of days that David dwelt in the country of the Philistines, days and four months.

8 David and his men went up and raided the Geshurites, the Girzites, and the Amalekites, for they were the inhabitants of the land from ancient times, from Shur as far as the land of Egypt.

9 David struck the land and left no man or woman alive, and he took sheep, cattle, donkeys, camels, and garments, and returned and came to Achish.

10 Achish said, Do not act foolishly today; and David said, Against the south of Judah, and against the south of the Jerahmeelites, and against the south of the Kenite.

11 David would let neither man nor woman remain alive to bring to Gath, saying, "Lest they report about us, saying, 'Thus David has done,' and thus his custom all the days that he dwelt in the land of the Philistines."

12 Achish believed David, saying, "Surely in making himself abhorrent to his folk, even to Israel, he has made himself my servant forever."


## Chapter 28

1 In those days the Philistines gathered their armies for war against Israel, and Achish said to David, "You surely know that you and your men will go out with me to the army."

2 David said to Achish, "Know therefore what your servant will do"; and Achish said to David, "Therefore I will appoint you keeper of my head all the days."

3 Samuel died and all Israel mourned him and buried him in Ramah and in his city and Saul removed the familiar spirits and the wizards from the land.

4 The Philistine gathered and came and encamped in Shunem, and Saul gathered all Israel and they encamped in Gilboa.

5 Saul saw the Philistine army and was afraid, and his heart was greatly frightened.

6 Saul inquired of the LORD but the LORD did not answer him, neither by dreams nor by Urim nor by prophets.

7 Saul said to his servants, "Seek me a woman who has a familiar spirit, that I may go to her and inquire of her." And his servants said to him, "Behold, there is a woman who has a familiar spirit at En-dor."

8 Saul disguised himself and put on different apparel, and he went, he and two men with him, and they came to the woman by night, and he said, Please, divine for me by the familiar spirit, and bring up for me the one whom I shall name to you.

9 And the woman said to him, Behold, you know what Saul has done, who has cut off the familiar spirits and the wizards from the land; why then do you set a snare for my soul to put me to death?

10 Saul swore to her by the LORD, saying, "As the LORD lives, no fault shall come upon you in this matter."

11 And the woman said, To whom shall I bring up? And he said, Bring up Samuel to me.

12 The woman saw Samuel and cried out in a loud voice, and the woman said to Saul, “Why have you deceived me? You are Saul!”

13 The king said to her, Do not fear, for what have you seen? And the woman said to Saul, I have seen God coming up out of the earth.

14 And he said to her, What is his form? And she said, An old man is coming up, and he is wrapped in a robe. And Saul knew that it was Samuel, and he bowed with his face to the ground and prostrated himself.

15 Samuel said to Saul, "Why have you disturbed me by bringing me up?" And Saul said, "I am in great distress, for the Philistines are fighting against me, and God has turned from me and no longer answers me, not even through the prophets nor through dreams; so I have called on you to tell me what I should do."

16 Samuel said, Why do you ask me? The LORD has turned from you and become your enemy.

17 And the LORD did to him as he spoke by my hand, and the LORD tore the kingdom from your hand and gave it to your companion, to David.

18 Because you did not listen to the voice of the LORD and did not do the fierce wrath of his anger against Amalek, therefore this act has the LORD done to you today.

19 And the LORD will also give Israel with you into the hand of the Philistines; tomorrow you and your sons will be with me; the LORD will also give the army of Israel into the hand of the Philistines.

20 Saul quickly fell full length to the ground and was greatly afraid because of the words of Samuel, and there was no strength in him because he had eaten no bread all day and all night.

21 The woman came to Saul and saw that he was greatly terrified, and she said to him, "Behold, your handmaid obeyed your voice, and I put my life in my hand, and I listened to your words that you spoke to me."

22 Now therefore please listen also to the voice of your maidservant; I will set a piece of bread before you—eat, that you may have strength when you go on the way.

23 But he refused and said, I will not eat, and his servants urged him, and also the woman, and he heeded their voice, and he rose from the ground and sat upon the bed.

24 And to the woman a stall-fed calf in the house, and she hurried and slaughtered it, and she took flour and kneaded it and baked unleavened bread.

25 And she brought it before Saul and before his servants, and they ate; and they rose up and went that night.


## Chapter 29

1 The Philistine gathered all their army at Aphek, and Israel was encamped by the fountain which is in Jezreel.

2 The lords of the Philistines passed on by hundreds and by thousands, and David and his men passed on in the rear with Achish.

3 The Philistine captains said, "What are these Hebrews doing here?" And Achish said to the Philistine captains, "Is this not David, the servant of Saul king of Israel, who has been with me these days or these years, and I have found no fault in him from the day he defected until this day?"

4 The Philistine captains became angry at him and said to him, the captains of the Philistines, "Return the man; let him go back to the place where you stationed him, and let him not go down with us into battle, lest he become our adversary in the battle. By what means should this one gain favor with his lord? Is it not by the heads of those men?"

5 Is this not David, of whom they sang in dances, saying, "Saul has slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands"?

6 Achish called to David and said to him, As the LORD lives, you are upright, and your coming and going with me in the camp is pleasing in my sight, for I have found no evil in you from the day of your coming to me until this day; but in the eyes of the lords you are not pleasing.

7 Now therefore return and go in peace, and do no evil in the sight of the lords of the Philistines.

8 David said to Achish, What have I done, and what have you found in your servant from the day that I came before you until this day, that I may not go and fight against the enemies of my lord the king?

9 Achish answered and said to David, I know that you are good in my sight as an angel of God, but the lords of the Philistines have said, He shall not go up with us to the battle.

10 Now therefore, rise early in the morning, you and the bondmen of your master who came with you, and rise early in the morning, and when light comes, go your way.

11 David rose early, he and his men, to go in the morning to return to the land of the Philistines, and the Philistines went up to Jezreel.


## Chapter 30

1 And it came to pass, when David and his men came to Ziklag on the third day, that the Amalekites had raided the south and Ziklag, and had struck Ziklag and burned it with fire.

2 They took the women who were in it, from the least to the greatest; they did not kill any man; and they departed and went on their way.

3 David and his men came to the city and saw that it was burned with fire, and their wives and their sons and their daughters had been taken captive.

4 David and the folk with him raised their voices and wept until they had no strength left to weep.

5 And two wives of David were captured: Ahinoam the Jezreelitess and Abigal the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

6 David was greatly distressed because the people spoke of stoning him, for the life of every man’s sons and daughters weighed heavily on the people, but David strengthened himself in the LORD his God.

7 David said to Abiathar the priest, son of Ahimelech, Bring me the ephod here, and Abiathar brought the ephod to David.

8 David inquired of the LORD, saying, "Shall I pursue this band? Shall I overtake it?" And he said to him, "Pursue, for you will surely overtake and surely rescue."
