9 David went, he and six hundred men who were with him, and they came to the brook Besor, and the rest stood still.

10 David pursued with four hundred men, while two hundred men remained behind who were too exhausted to cross the brook Besor.

11 They found a certain Egyptian in the field and brought him to David, and they gave him bread and he ate, and they gave him water to drink.

12 They gave him a piece of cake of figs and two bunches of raisins, and he ate; and his spirit returned to him, for he had not eaten bread nor drunk water for three days and three nights.

13 David said to him, "To whom do you belong, and where are you from?" And the lad said, "I am an Egyptian, a servant to an Amalekite man; my master abandoned me because I fell sick three days ago."

14 We devastated the south of the Cherethites, and the south of Judah, and the south of Caleb, and we burned Ziklag with fire.

15 David said to him, "Will you hand me over to this army?" And he said, "Swear to me by God that you will not kill me or deliver me into the hand of my master, and I will bring you to this army."

16 He brought him down, and behold, they were scattered over the face of all the land, eating and drinking and celebrating with all the great spoil that they had taken from the land of the Philistines and from the land of Judah.

17 David struck them from the dawning of the day until the following evening of the next day; not a man escaped from them except four hundred young men who rode on camels and fled.

18 David rescued all that Amalek had taken, and David rescued his two wives.

19 And nothing was lacking for them from the least to the greatest, and from the children and the daughters, and from the spoil, even to all that they had taken for themselves; the whole David restored.

20 David took all the sheep and the cattle, driving them before that herd, and they said, "This is David's spoil."

21 David came to the two hundred men who had been left behind with the supplies because they were too exhausted to go on with David; he came to them at the brook Besor. They went out to meet David and the people who were with him. Then David approached the people and greeted them in peace.

22 And all the wicked and base men among the men who had gone with David answered, saying, Since they did not go with me, nothing of the spoil that we rescued shall be given to them, only each man his wife and his children, and let them take them and go.

23 David said, Do not do so, my brothers; consider what the LORD has given us and how he has protected us and delivered the raiding band that came against us into our hand.

24 Who will listen to you concerning this matter, since the portion of the one who goes down to the battle is equal to the portion of the one who stays with the supplies—they will share alike?

25 From that day onward it became a statute and judgment for Israel to this day.

26 David came to Ziklag and sent from the spoil to the elders of Judah, his companions, saying, "Behold, a blessing for you from the spoil of the enemies of the LORD."

27 To the one in Beth-el, and to the one in south Ramoth, and to the one in Jattir.

28 And to the one in Aroer and to the one in Siphmoth and to the one in Eshtemoa.

29 And to the one in Rachal, and to the one in the cities of the Jerahmeelites, and to the one in the cities of the Kenite.

30 and to the one in Hormah, and to the one in Chorashan, and to the one in Athach

31 and to Hebron, and to all the places where David and his men had gone.


## Chapter 31

1 And the Philistines fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.

2 The Philistines clung to Saul and to his sons, and the Philistines struck down Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchishua, sons of Saul.

3 And the battle pressed hard against Saul, and the archers found him, men with the bow, and he was greatly distressed by the archers.

4 Saul said to his armour-bearer, "Draw your sword and thrust me through with it, lest these uncircumcised come and thrust me through and abuse me." But the armour-bearer was unwilling, for he feared greatly. So Saul took the sword and fell upon it.

5 And the armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, and he fell likewise upon his sword and died with him.

6 Saul died and his three sons and the bearer of his weapons and all his men on that day together.

7 The men of Israel who were across the valley and those across the Jordan saw that the men of Israel had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, so they abandoned the cities and fled, and the Philistines came and lived in them.

8 And it came to pass on the morrow, that the Philistines came to strip the slain, and they found Saul and his three sons fallen upon mount Gilboa.

9 And they cut off his head and stripped off his weapons and sent into the land of the Philistines all around to proclaim in the houses of their idols and among the people.

10 They placed his armour in the house of Astaroth, and they impaled his corpse on the wall of Beth-shan.

11 And the inhabitants of Jobesh Gilead heard what the Philistines had done to Saul.

12 And every warrior rose up and went all the night and took the corpse of Saul and the corpses of his sons from the wall of Beth-shan and came to Jabesh and burned them there.

13 And they took her bones and buried them under the terebinth in Jabesh and fasted seven days.


# 2 SAMUEL

## Chapter 1

1 After Saul's death, David returned from defeating the Amalekites and David stayed in Ziklag two days.

2 On the third day, behold, a man came from the camp of Saul, his clothes torn and earth upon his head; and when he came to David, he fell to the ground and bowed.

3 David said to him, "Where have you come from?" and he said to him, "I have escaped from the camp of Israel."

4 David said to him, "What was the act? Please tell me." And he said, "The people fled from the battle, and many of the people also fell and died, and also Saul and Jonathan his son are dead."

5 David said to the lad who reported to him, "How do you know that Saul and Jonathan his son are dead?"

6 And the boy who told him said, I was called on Mount Gilboa, and behold, Saul was leaning on his spear, and behold, the chariot and the horsemen were pressing hard after him.

7 Then he turned around and saw me, and he called to me, and I said, Here I am.

8 And he said to me, "Who are you?" and I to him, "An Amalekite I am."

9 He said to me, "Please stand beside me and support me, for anguish has seized me, because my life is still within me."

10 I stood over him and killed him, for I knew that he would not live after he had fallen; and I took the crown that was on his head and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them here to my lord.

11 David seized the apparel and tore it, and so did all the men who were with him.

12 And they mourned and wept and fasted until evening for Saul and for Jonathan his son and for the people of the LORD and for the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.

13 David said to the boy who was reporting to him, "Where are you from?" And he said, "I am the son of a foreign man, an Amalekite."

14 David said to him, How could you not fear to raise your hand to destroy the anointed of the LORD?

15 David called to one of the boys and said, "Go, strike him," and he struck him, and he died.

16 David said to him, "Your blood be on your head, for your mouth has testified against you, saying, 'I have killed the anointed of the LORD.'"

17 David chanted the lamentation concerning Saul and concerning Jonathan his son.

18 And he said to teach the sons of Judah archery; behold, it is written in the book of the upright.

19 How the beautiful of Israel is slain on your heights! How the mighty have fallen!

20 Do not proclaim it in Gath, do not bear news abroad in Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised exult.

21 Mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain upon you, nor fields of offerings; for there the shield of the mighty was defiled, the shield of Saul, without anointing in oil.

22 From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, Jonathan’s bow did not turn back, and Saul’s sword did not return empty.

23 Saul and Jonathan, beloved and delightful in their lives, and in their deaths they were not parted; swifter than eagles, stronger than lions.

24 Daughters of Israel, weep for Saul, who clothed you in scarlet with delights, who made ornaments of gold go up upon your apparel.

25 How the mighty have fallen in the midst of the battle! Jonathan lies slain upon your heights.

26 I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan; you were very pleasant to me; your love was extraordinary to me, more than the love of women.

27 How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war have perished.


## Chapter 2

1 Afterward David inquired of the Lord, saying, Shall I go up to one of the towns of Judah? And the Lord said to him, Go up. And David said, Where shall I go up? And he said, Hebron.

2 David went up there, and also his two wives: Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, and Abigal the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

3 And David brought up his men who were with him, each man and his household, and they lived in the towns of Hebron.

4 Then came the men of Judah and anointed David there as king over the house of Judah, and they told David, saying, "The men of Jabesh Gilead who buried Saul."

5 David sent ambassadors to the men of Jobesh Gilead and said to them, "Blessed are you to the LORD for doing this act of kindness with your lord, with Saul, and for burying him."

6 And now may the LORD deal with you in kindness and truth, and indeed I myself will also do with you this good deed that you have done.

7 Now strengthen your hands and be valiant men, for your lord Saul is dead, and the house of Judah has also anointed me king over them.

8 And Abner the son of Ner, captain of the host that belonged to Saul, took Ish-bosheth the son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.

9 He made him king over Gilead and over the Ashurites and over Jezreel and over Ephraim and over Benjamin and over all Israel.

10 Ish-bosheth son of Saul was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years, but the house of Judah followed David.

11 The number of days that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.

12 Abner son of Ner and the servants of Ish-bosheth son of Saul went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.

13 Joab and the servants of David came out, and they met them by the pool of Gibeon; these sat down by the pool on this side, and those by the pool on that side.

14 Abner said to Joab, Let the lads please arise and play before us. And Joab said, Let them arise.

15 They rose and crossed over by number, twelve for Benjamin and for Ish-bosheth son of Saul, and twelve from the servants of David.

16 Each man seized the other by the head and thrust his sword against the other’s side, and they fell together; and the place was called Helkath-hazzurim, which is in Gibeon.

17 The battle was severe, exceedingly so, that day, and Abner and the men of Israel were defeated before the servants of David.

18 There were there three sons of Zeruiah: Joab, Abishai, and Asahel; and Asahel was swift of foot as one of the roe that are in the field.

19 Asahel pursued Abner and did not turn to the right or to the left from following after Abner.

20 Abner turned back and said, "Is it you, Asahel?" and he said, "I am."

21 And Abner said to him, Turn aside to your right or to your left and seize one of the young men and take his armour for yourself but Asahel was not willing to turn aside from following him

22 Abner again said to Asahel, Turn aside from following me; why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I face Joab your brother?

23 And he refused to turn aside, so Abner struck him in the fifth rib with the javelin, and the javelin came out behind him; and he fell there and died. And every one who came to the place where Asahel fell, he struck down and they died.

24 Joab and Abishai pursued Abner, and the sun was going down when they arrived at the hill of Ammah, which is on the face of Giah, along the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.

25 The sons of Benjamin gathered after Abner and became one troop and stood on top of a hill.

26 Abner called to Joab and said, "Shall the sword devour alway? knowest thou not that it shall be bitter in the latter end? and until when wilt thou not bid the folk to return from following after their brethren?"

27 Joab said, "As surely as God lives, if you had not spoken, already from morning the people would have gone up, each after his brother."

28 And Joab blew the cornet, and all the people stood still and pursued Israel no longer, nor did they fight anymore.

29 Abner and his men marched through the wilderness all that night, crossed the Jordan, and went all the way through Bithron, arriving at Mahanaim.

30 And Joab returned from following Abner and gathered all the people, and nineteen of David's servants were missed, and Asahel.

31 And the servants of David struck from Benjamin and from the men of Abner three hundred and sixty men.

32 They took Asahel and buried him in the grave of his father who was in Bethlehem, and Joab and his men walked all night and came to Hebron at dawn.


## Chapter 3

1 The battle was long between the house of Saul and the house of David, and David continued growing stronger, while the house of Saul continued weakening.

2 David's sons in Hebron: his firstborn was Amnon, by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess.

3 and his second was Abigail the Carmelite, wife of Nabal; and the third was Absalom son of Maachah, daughter of Talmai king of Geshur.

4 And the fourth was Adonijah the son of Haggith, and the fifth was Shephatiah the son of Abital.

5 And the sixth, Ithream, by Eglah, David's wife, these were born to David in Hebron.

6 And it came to pass, while there was war between the house of Saul and the house of David, that Abner was strengthening the house of Saul.

7 Saul had a concubine, and her name was Rizpah daughter of Aiah, and he said to Abner, Why have you come to the concubine of my father?

8 Abner was exceedingly angry at the words of Ish-bosheth, and he said, "Am I a dog's head who belongs to Judah? This day I show loyalty to the house of Saul your father, to your brothers and to your companions, and have not delivered you into David's hand; and yet you charge me with the iniquity of this woman today."

9 Thus may God do to Abner and thus may he add to him, for as Jehovah swore to David, so will I do to him.

10 To transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul and to establish the throne of David over Israel and over Judah from Dan to Beer-shebah.

11 And he could no longer answer Abner a word because of his fear of him.

12 Abner sent ambassadors to David, saying, To whom is the land? saying, You made a covenant with me, and behold, my hand is with you, to bring over to you all Israel.

13 And he said, It is good; I will make with you a confederacy: only one thing I require of you, saying, You shall not see my face, except when you bring Michal daughter of Saul; only then may you see my face.

14 David sent messengers to Ish-bosheth son of Saul, saying, “Give me my wife Michal, whom I betrothed to me with a hundred Philistine foreskins.”

15 Ish-bosheth sent a man and took her from another man, from Paltiel son of Laish.

16 And that man went with her, walking and weeping behind her until Bahurim, and Abner said to him, “Go back!” and he returned.

17 And the advice of Abner was with the elders of Israel, saying, "Even yesterday and the day before, you were seeking David to be king over you."

18 Now therefore do it, for the LORD has said to David, "By the hand of David my servant I will save my people Israel from the hand of the Philistines and from the hand of all their enemies."

19 Abner also spoke in the ears of Benjamin, and Abner went also to speak in the ears of David at Hebron all that was good in the eyes of Israel and in the eyes of all the house of Benjamin.

20 Abner came to David at Hebron, and twenty men with him, and David made a banquet for Abner and the men who were with him.

21 Abner said to David, I will rise and go and gather all Israel to my lord the king, and they will make a covenant with you, and you will reign over all that your soul desires, and David sent Abner away, and he went in peace.

22 And behold, the bond-servants of David and Joab came from the army, bringing a great spoil with them; but Abner was not with David in Hebron, for he had sent him and he went in peace.

23 And Joab and all the host that was with him came, and they told Joab, saying, Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he let him go, and he departed in peace.

24 Joab came to the king and said, "What have you done? Abner came to you; why have you sent him away, and he has gone?"

25 You know Abner son of Ner came to deceive you and to learn your going forth and your coming, and to know all that you are doing.

26 Joab went out from with David and sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah, but David did not know.

27 Abner returned to Hebron, and Joab took him aside into the middle of the gate to speak with him quietly, and he struck him there in the fifth rib, and he died in blood, because of the blood of his brother Asahel.

28 David heard later and said, I am innocent and my kingdom is innocent before the LORD forever from the blood of Abner son of Ner.

29 Let there be a band upon the head of Joab and upon all the house of his father, and let not the house of Joab be cut off, [but let there be] one with a discharge, or a leper, or one who leans on a staff, and one who falls by the sword, and one who lacks bread.

30 Joab and Abishai his brother killed Abner because he had struck down Asahel their brother in battle at Gibeon.

31 David said to Joab and to all the people who were with him, "Tear your garments and gird on sackcloth and mourn before Abner," and King David walked after the bier.

32 They buried Abner in Hebron and the king raised his voice and wept at the grave of Abner and all the people wept.

33 The king lamented for Abner and said, “As the death of a fool dies, so dies Abner.”

34 Your hand is not bound, nor have your feet been brought near with chains of brass; you have fallen like one slain before the sons of iniquity, and all the people added to weep over him.

35 All the people came to provide David with food while yet the day lasted, and David swore, saying, "Thus may God do to me and thus may he add, if before the sun comes I taste food or anything at all."

36 And all the people took note and were pleased in their sight, according to all that the king had done, good in the sight of all the people.

37 And all the people and all Israel knew on that day that it was not from the king to kill Abner son of Ner.

38 The king said to his servants, Do you not know that a prince and great one has fallen this day in Israel?

39 And I today am weak though anointed king, and these men, the sons of Zeruiah, are too harsh for me; may the LORD repay the evildoer according to his wickedness.


## Chapter 4

1 The son of Saul heard that Abner had died in Hebron, and his hands became feeble, and all Israel was dismayed.

2 And two men, captains of bands, were sons of Saul; the name of one was Baanah and the name of the second was Rechab, sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, of the sons of Benjamin, for indeed Beeroth also is reckoned with Benjamin.

3 The Beerothites fled to Gittaim and have been sojourners there to this day.

4 And Jonathan the son of Saul was the son of five years when the report of Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel, and his nurse took him and fled; and it happened as she hurried to flee, he fell and became lame, and his name was Mephibosheth.

5 The sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, went and came at the heat of the day to the house of Ish-bosheth, and he was lying on his bed at midday.

6 And behold, they came among the house, taking wheat; and they struck him to the fifth rib, but Rechab and Baanah his brother escaped.

7 They came to the house, and he was lying on his bed in the inner chamber of his sleeping room, so they struck him and killed him, and they removed his head and took his head and went the way of the wilderness all night.

8 They brought the head of Ish-bosheth to David at Hebron and said to the king, "Behold, the head of Ish-bosheth, son of Saul, your enemy, who sought your life; the LORD has given the king vengeance this day from Saul and from his offspring."

9 David answered Rechab and Baanah his brother, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, and said to them, "As the LORD lives, who has delivered my life from all adversity."

10 For he who told me, "Behold, Saul is dead," thinking he was bringing good news, I seized him and killed him in Ziklag, to give him reward for tidings.

11 Even though wicked men have murdered a righteous man in his own house on his bed, shall I not now require his blood from your hand and destroy you from the earth?

12 David commanded the young men, and they killed them, and cut off their hands and their feet, and hung them over the fish-pool at Hebron; and they took the head of Ish-bosheth, and buried it in the grave of Abner at Hebron.


## Chapter 5

1 All the tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron and said to him, saying, "Behold, we are your bone and your flesh."

2 Even yesterday and the day before, when Saul was king over us, you were the one who led Israel out and brought them in, and the LORD said to you, “You will shepherd my people Israel, and you will be leader over Israel.”

3 All the elders of Israel came to King David at Hebron, and King David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the LORD, and they anointed David king over Israel.

4 David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned for forty years.

5 In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years over all Israel and Judah.

6 The king and his men went to Jerusalem against the Jebusite, the inhabitant of the land; and he said to David, "You shall not come in here, for if the blind and the lame will drive you away," saying, "David shall not come in here."

7 David captured the castle of Zion; it is the city of David.

8 David said on that day, Whoever strikes a Jebusite, let him reach the water-spout and the lame and the blind, who are hateful to David; therefore it is said, The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.

9 David settled in the fortress and named it the city of David, and David built around from the Millo and inward.

10 And David went on, growing greater, and the LORD, God of hosts, was with him.

11 Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and cedar wood, and carpenters of wood, and masons of stone; and they built a house for David.

12 David knew that Jehovah had established him as king over Israel and that his kingdom was exalted for the sake of his people Israel.

13 David took more concubines and wives from Jerusalem after he came from Hebron, and more sons and daughters were born to David.

14 These are the names of those born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon.

15 Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, and Japhia were chosen.

16 Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphalet.

17 The Philistines heard that they had anointed David king over Israel, and all the Philistines came up to seek David; and David heard, and went down to the fortress.

18 Then the Philistines came and abandoned themselves in the dale of Rephaim.

19 David inquired of the LORD, saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you deliver them into my hand? And the LORD said to David, Go up, for I will surely deliver the Philistines into your hand.

20 David came to Baal-perazim and struck them there, and David said, "Jehovah has made a breach upon my enemies before me like the breach of waters"; therefore he called the name of that place Baal-perazim.

21 They abandoned their idols there, and David and his men took them up.

22 The Philistines added yet again to go up, and they were scattered in the dale of Rephaim.

23 David inquired of the LORD, and he said, Do not go up; turn aside behind them and come upon them opposite the mulberry trees.

24 When you hear the sound of marching at the tops of the mulberry trees, then you shall act swiftly, for then the LORD will go out before you to strike the camp of the Philistines.

25 David did so just as the LORD had commanded him, and he struck the Philistines from Geba to the approach of Gezer.


## Chapter 6

1 And David added again all thirty thousand young men of Israel.

2 David rose and went with all the people who were with him from Baale of Judah to bring up from there the ark of God, over which the name called is the name of the LORD of hosts, who sits enthroned above the cherubim.

3 They placed the ark of God on a new cart and carried it from the house of Abinadab, who was on the hill, and Uzza and Ahio, sons of Abinadab, were driving the new cart.

4 from the house of Abinadab that was on the hill with the ark of God and Ahio walking before the ark

5 David and all the house of Israel were dancing before the LORD with all kinds of cypress wood, with harps, with psalteries, with tabrets, with cornets, and with cymbals.

6 They came to the threshing floor of Nachon, and Uzza reached out to the ark of God and took hold of it because the beeve had stumbled.

7 The LORD was angry with Uzza, and there God struck him for the error, and he died there with the ark of God.

8 David was angry because Jehovah had made a breach in Uzza, and he called that place Perezuzza to this day.

9 David feared the LORD on that day and said, "How can the ark of the LORD come to me?"

10 But David was unwilling to move the ark of the LORD to the city of David, so David diverted it to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.

11 The ark of the LORD remained in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months, and the LORD blessed Obed-edom and all his household.

12 It was reported to King David saying, "The LORD has blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that belongs to him because of the ark of God"; so David went and brought up the ark of God from the house of Obed-edom to the city of David with joy.

13 And it happened that when the bearers of the ark of the LORD had taken six steps, he sacrificed an ox and a fatling.

14 David danced with all his might before the LORD, and David was girded with a linen ephod.

15 And David and all the house of Israel brought up the ark of the LORD with shouting and with the sound of the trumpet.

16 When the ark of the LORD came into the city of David, Michal the daughter of Saul looked through the window and saw King David leaping and whirling before the LORD, and she despised him in her heart.

17 They brought the ark of the LORD and set it in its place inside the tent which David had pitched for it, and David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before the LORD.

18 And David finished from ascending the burnt offering and the peace offering, and he blessed the people in the name of the LORD of hosts.

19 He divided among all the people, the whole multitude of Israel, to every man and woman, to each man a cake of bread, one good piece of flesh, and one flagon of wine; and all the people went, each to his house.

20 David returned to bless his household, and Michal daughter of Saul came out to meet David, and she said, "How glorious was the king of Israel today, who uncovered himself today before the eyes of the female servants of his servants, like one of the base fellows shamelessly uncovers himself!"

21 David said to Michal, before the LORD who chose me from your father and from all his house to appoint me as leader over the people of the LORD, over Israel, that I would dance before the LORD.

22 And I shall be more lightly esteemed than this, and I shall be humble in my own eyes, and with the handmaids whom you said I would be honored with, I will be honored.

23 And Michal the daughter of Saul had no child until the day of her death.


## Chapter 7

1 And it came to pass, when the king sat in his house, that the LORD gave him rest round about from all his enemies.

2 The king said to Nathan the prophet, See now, I am dwelling in a house of cedar, but the ark of God dwells within the curtain.

3 Then Nathan said to the king, All that is in your mind, go and do, for the LORD is with you.

4 And it came to pass that night, that the word of the LORD came to Nathan, saying:

5 Go and say to my servant David, Thus says the LORD, Will you build me a house for my dwelling?

6 For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought up the children of Israel out of Egypt even to this day, but I have been moving in a tent and in a dwelling place.

7 In all where I have walked among all the people of Israel, was there ever a word that I spoke to one of the tribes of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people Israel, saying, Why have you not built me a house of cedar?

8 Now thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the LORD of hosts: I took you from the sheepcote, from after the flock, to be ruler over my people, over Israel.

9 I was with you in all that you did, and I cut off all your enemies from before you, and I made for you a great name, like the name of the great ones in the land.

10 I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and I will plant him, and he shall dwell securely therein; he shall not be disturbed again, and the children of wickedness shall not oppress him as at first.

11 From the age when I commanded judges over my people Israel, and I gave you rest from all your enemies; and the LORD declared to you that the LORD would make you a house.

12 When your days are fulfilled and you lie down with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, who shall come from your womb, and I will establish his kingdom.

13 He will build a temple for my name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever.

14 I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son; when he does wrong, I will correct him with the rod of men and with the stripes of the children of man.

15 My favour will not turn away from him, as I turned it away from Saul, whom I removed from before you.

16 And your court and your reign shall be established before you, your throne shall remain steadfast forever.

17 According to all these words and according to all this vision so Nathan spoke to David.

18 King David came and sat before the LORD and said, "Who am I, my Lord the LORD, and who is my house, that you have brought me thus far?"

19 And this thing is yet small in your eyes, my Lord God, and you have spoken also concerning the house of your servant for a great while to come; and this is the law of man, my Lord God.

20 What more can David say to you, since you know your servant, my Lord God?

21 Because of your word and your purpose, you have done all this greatness, to make known to your servant.

22 Therefore you are great, (my) Lord God, for there is none like you, and there is no God besides you, in all that we have heard with our ears.

23 And who is like your folk, like Israel, a nation unique on the earth, whom God went to redeem for himself as a people, to make himself a name, and to do for you deeds of greatness and awe, in the presence of your people whom you redeemed for yourself from Egypt, from nations and their gods?

24 You established for yourself your people Israel to be your people forever, and you, the LORD, have become their God.

25 And now, O Lord God, establish the word that You spoke concerning Your servant and concerning his house, forever, and do as You have spoken.

26 May your name be renowned forever, saying, “The LORD of hosts is God over Israel,” and may the house of your servant David remain established before you.

27 For you, O LORD of hosts, God of Israel, have revealed to your servant’s ear: “I will build you a house”; therefore your servant has found in his heart to pray this prayer to you.

28 And now, (my) Lord God, you are the one who is God, and your words shall be true, and you have spoken this good thing to your servant.

29 And now, please bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you, for you, my Lord God, have spoken; and by your blessing, let the house of your servant be blessed forever.


## Chapter 8

1 Afterward David struck the Philistines and subdued them and David took Metheg-ammah from the hand of the Philistines.

2 And he struck Moab and measured them with the line, casting them to the ground; he measured two bands for destruction, and the fullness of the line for life; and Moab became to David for servants bearing tribute.

3 David struck Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zoba, when he went to restore his power by the river.

4 David captured from him one thousand and seven hundred horsemen and twenty thousand foot soldiers, and David hamstrung all the chariots, sparing only one hundred chariots.

5 Then Aram of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, and David struck the Arameans twenty-two thousand men.

6 David placed garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and Aram became to David for bondmen bringing a gift, and the LORD saved David in all that he went.

7 David took the gold shields that had belonged to the servants of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.

8 From Betah and from Berothai, the cities of Hadadezer, King David took very much copper.

9 And Toi, king of Hamath, heard that David had struck all the army of Hadadezer.

10 Toi sent his son Joram to King David to ask for his welfare and to bless him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him, for Hadadezer had been a man of wars to Toi, and in his hand were vessels of silver, vessels of gold, and vessels of bronze.

11 Even them did King David consecrate to the LORD, along with the silver and the gold that he had consecrated from all the nations he had subdued.

12 from Aram and from Moab and from the sons of Ammon and from Philistine and from Amalek and from the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob king of Zobah.

13 David made a name for himself when he returned from defeating Aram in the Valley of Salt, eighteen thousand.

14 And he placed garrisons in Edom; in all Edom he placed garrisons, and all Edom became servants to David; and the LORD preserved David wherever he went.

15 David reigned over all Israel, and David was doing justice and right for all his people.

16 Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder.

17 Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests, and Seraiah was the scribe.

18 Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and the sons of David were priests.


## Chapter 9

1 David said, "Is there still anyone left of the house of Saul, that I may show him favour for Jonathan's sake?"

2 And to the house of Saul belonged a servant whose name was Ziba, and they summoned him to David, and the king said to him, "Are you Ziba?" and he said, "Your servant."

3 Then said the king, Is there yet any man of the house of Saul, that I may show him kindness for the sake of God? And Ziba said to the king, There is yet a son of Jonathan, lame of feet.

4 The king said to him, "Where is he?" And Ziba said to the king, "Behold, he is at the house of Machir son of Ammiel in Lo-debar."

5 King David sent and took him from the house of Machir son of Ammiel from Lodebar.

6 Mephibosheth son of Jonathan son of Saul came to David and fell on his face and bowed himself and David said Mephibosheth and Mephibosheth said behold your servant

7 David said to him, Do not fear, for I will show you favour because of Jonathan your father, and I will restore to you all the land of Saul your father, and you shall eat bread at my table always.

8 And he bowed down and said, What is your servant, that you have turned to your dog who is like me?

9 The king summoned Ziba, Saul's servant, and said to him, All that belonged to Saul and to all his house I have given to your master's son.

10 You shall till the land for him, you and your sons and your servants, and bring in the produce, and it shall be food for your master’s son; and Mephibosheth, your master’s son, shall eat food continually at my table; and for Ziba, fifteen sons and twenty servants.

11 Then said Ziba to the king, According to all that my lord the king may command his servant, so shall your servant do. And Mephibosheth is eating at my table like one of the sons of the king.

12 And to Mephibosheth belonged a son whose name was Micha, and all the dwelling of Ziba's household were servants to Mephibosheth.

13 And Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem, for he always ate at the king's table, and he was lame in both legs.


## Chapter 10

1 After this, the king of the Ammonites died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.

2 David said, I will show favour to Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father showed me favour; so David sent his servants to comfort him concerning his father. David's servants came into the land of the Ammonites.

3 The captains of the Ammonites said to Hanun their lord, "Do you think David honors your father by sending comforters to you? Is it not because he sent his servants to you to search out the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?"

4 Then Hanun took David's bondmen, shaved off half their beards, cut their garments in half to their buttocks, and sent them away.

5 They informed David, and he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed; and the king said, "Stay in Jericho until your beards have grown, then return."

6 The Ammonites saw that they were despised by David, so the Ammonites sent and hired Aram of Beth-rehob and Aram of Zobah, twenty thousand foot soldiers, and the king of Maachah, one thousand men, and the men of Tob twelve thousand men.

7 David heard and sent Joab and all the warrior host.

8 The Ammonites marched out and drew up for battle at the entrance of the gate, while Aram of Zobah, Rehob, and Tob, and Maachah were by themselves in the field.

9 Joab saw that the battle was against him in front and in back, so he selected from all Israel some of the choice men and deployed them against Aram.

10 And the rest of the people he gave into the hand of Abishai his brother, and he deployed to meet the Ammonites.

11 He said, If Aram prevails over me, then I will be to you for deliverance, but if the sons of Ammon prevail over you, then I will go to deliver you.

12 Be strong and strengthen one another for the sake of our people and for the sake of the cities of our God, and the LORD will do what is good in his sight.

13 Joab and the folk who were with him went to battle against the Aram, and they fled from his face.

14 When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they fled before Abishai and entered the city, and Joab returned from fighting the Ammonites and came to Jerusalem.

15 And Aram saw that it was defeated before Israel, and they gathered together.

16 Then Hadadezer sent and brought out Aram which was beyond the river, and they came to Helam, and Shobach the captain of Hadadezer's army was before them.

17 It was reported to David, and he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan, came to Helam, and Aram deployed against David and fought with him.

18 And Aram fled before Israel, and David killed from Aram seven hundred chariots and forty thousand horsemen, and he struck Shobach, the captain of their army, and he died there.

19 All the kings, servants of Hadadezer, saw that they were defeated before Israel, so they made peace with Israel and served them; and Aram was afraid to save the sons of Ammon anymore.


## Chapter 11

1 At the turn of the year, when kings go out, David sent Joab and his servants with him and all Israel, and they destroyed the sons of Ammon and besieged Rabbah, while David remained in Jerusalem.

2 And it came to pass at evening time that David arose from his bed and walked upon the roof of the king's house, and he saw a woman bathing from upon the roof, and the woman was very goodly of appearance.

3 David sent and inquired about the woman, and said, "Is not this Bath-sheba, daughter of Eliam, wife of Uriah the Hittite?"

4 David sent messengers and took her, and she came to him and he lay with her, and she was purified from her filthiness, and she returned to her home.

5 The woman conceived and sent and informed David, and said, "I am pregnant."

6 David sent to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” So Joab sent Uriah to David.

7 Uriah came to him, and David asked after the welfare of Joab, and after the welfare of the people, and after the welfare of the war.

8 David said to Uriah, "Go down to your home and wash your feet." Uriah went out from the king's house, and the king's gift went out after him.

9 Uriah slept at the entrance of the king's palace with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.

10 They told David, saying, Uriah did not go down to his home; and David said to Uriah, Are you not come from the way? Why did you not go down to your house?

11 Uriah said to David, "The ark and Israel and Judah are dwelling in booths, and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord are camping in the open field, and shall I go to my house to eat and to drink and to lie with my wife? As you live, and as the life of your soul lives, I will not do this thing."

12 David said to Uriah, "Stay here today, and tomorrow I will send you away." So Uriah remained in Jerusalem that day and the next day.

13 David summoned him, and he ate before him, drank, and became drunk; then he went out in the evening to lie down on his bed with the servants of his lord, but did not go down to his house.

14 And it came to pass in the morning that David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it by the hand of Uriah.

15 And he wrote in the scroll, saying, Give Uriah to the front of the battle, the strong battle, and you shall withdraw from behind him, and he shall be struck and die.

16 And it came to pass, when Joab was keeping watch over the city, that he sent Uriah to the place where he knew the men of valour were.

17 Then the men of the city went out and fought against Joab, and some of the people of David’s servants fell, and Uriah the Hittite also died.

18 Joab sent and reported to David all the events of the battle.

19 And he commanded the messenger, saying, When you have finished all the words of the war, to speak to the king.

20 And if the king's anger should rise and he says to you, "Why have you approached the city to fight? Did you not know what they shoot from upon the wall?"

21 Who struck Abimelech son of Jerubbesheth? Was it not a woman who threw an upper millstone on him from upon the wall so that he died in Thebez? Why have you drawn near to the wall? And say, “Your servant also, Uriah the Hittite, is dead.”

22 The messenger went and came and told David everything that Joab had sent him to do.

23 And the angel said to David, "The men have prevailed against us, and they came out to us into the field, and we were upon them until the entrance of the gate."

24 and the archers looked down upon your servants from upon the wall, and some of the king's servants died, and your servant Uriah the Hittite also died

25 David said to the messenger, Say to Joab, Do not let this matter displease you, for the sword devours one as well as another; strengthen your battle against the city and destroy it, and encourage him.

26 And the wife of Uriah heard that Uriah her husband was dead, and she mourned for her husband.

27 After the mourning had passed, David sent and brought her to his house, and she became his wife and bore him a son, but the act that David had done was evil in the sight of the LORD.


## Chapter 12

1 The Lord sent Nathan to David, and he came to him and said to him, Two men were in one city, one rich and one poor.

2 A rich man had very many sheep and cattle.

3 And the poor man had nothing at all except one little ewe lamb that he had bought, and it grew up with him and with his children; from his morsel it would eat, from his cup it would drink, in his bosom it would lie, and it was like a daughter to him.

4 And a traveller came to the rich man, and the rich man spared taking from his own sheep or his own cattle to prepare for the guest who had come to him; so he took the ewe lamb of the poor man and prepared it for the man who had come to him.

5 David was very angry with the man and said to Nathan, "As surely as the LORD lives, the man who did this is worthy of death."

6 And the lamb he shall repay fourfold, because of the act he did this and because he had no pity.

7 Nathan said to David, "You are the man. Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, 'I anointed you king over Israel, and I delivered you from the hand of Saul.'"

8 I gave you your master’s house and your master’s wives into your bosom, and I gave you the house of Israel and Judah; and if that was too little, I would have added for you even more and more.

9 Why have you despised the word of the LORD to do evil in his sight by striking Uriah the Hittite with the sword and taking his wife as your wife, and him you killed with the sword of the Ammonites?

10 And now the sword will not depart from your household forever, because you despised me and took Uriah the Hittite’s wife to be your wife.

11 Thus says the LORD: I am raising up adversity against you from within your household; I will take your wives before your eyes and give them to your companion, and he will lie with your wives before the eyes of this sun.

12 For you acted in secret, but I will do this thing before all Israel and before the sun.

13 David said to Nathan, "I have sinned against the Lord." Nathan said to David, "The Lord has also passed over your sin; you shall not die."

14 Nevertheless, you have utterly rejected the enemies of the LORD by this word; also the son born to you shall surely die.

15 Nathan went to his home, and the LORD struck the child whom Uriah’s wife bore to David, and he became ill.

16 David sought God on behalf of the boy, and David fasted, went in, and lodged and lay on the ground.

17 The elders of his court rose up against him to raise him from the ground, but he was unwilling and refused to eat food with them.

18 On the seventh day the child died, and David's servants were afraid to tell him that the child was dead, for they said, "Look, while the child was still alive, we spoke to him and he would not listen to our voice; how then can we say to him 'The child is dead'—he might do evil?"

19 David saw that his servants were whispering, and David perceived that the boy was dead; so David said to his servants, "Is the boy dead?" and they said, "He is dead."

20 David rose from the ground and washed and anointed himself and put on apparel and changed and went into the house of the LORD and prostrated himself and went into his house and asked and they set for him food and he ate.

21 His servants said to him, What is this matter which you have done? For the sake of the child, while he was still alive, you fasted and wept; but when the child died, you rose up and ate food.

22 And he said, While the child was yet alive, I fasted and wept, for I said, Who knows whether the LORD will be gracious to me and the child will live?

23 Now that he has died, why should I fast? Can I bring him back again? I am going to him, but he will not return to me.

24 David comforted Bath-sheba his wife and went to her and lay with her, and she bore a son whose name was Solomon, and the LORD loved him.

25 He sent by the hand of Nathan the prophet and called his name Jedidiah for the Lord's sake.

26 Joab fought against Rabbah of the children of Ammon and captured the city of the royal.

27 Joab sent messengers to David and said, I have fought against Rabbah and also captured the city of waters.

28 Now therefore gather the remnant of the people, and encamp against the city, and capture it, lest I capture the city and my renown be called upon it.

29 David gathered all the people and went to Rabbah and fought against it and captured it.

30 He took the crown from the head of their king, and its weight was a talent of gold and a precious stone, and it was placed on David's head; and the spoil of the city was very great.

31 And the people who were in it he brought out and put them to the brickkiln and to the iron saws and to the iron axes and made them pass through; thus he did to all the cities of the Ammonites. Then David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.


## Chapter 13

1 After this thing, Absalom the son of David had a beautiful sister whose name was Tamar, and Amnon the son of David loved her.

2 And a craving came upon Amnon for Tamar his sister because she was a virgin, and it seemed impossible in the eyes of Amnon to do her any harm.

3 Amnon had a companion whose name was Jonadab, son of Shimeah, David’s brother, and Jonadab was a very wise man.

4 And he said to him, Why are you so afflicted, son of the king, morning after morning? Will you not tell me? And Amnon said to him, I love Tamar, my brother Absalom's sister.

5 Then Jehonadab said to him, "Lie down on your bed and pretend to be ill; when your father comes to see you, say to him, 'Please let Tamar my sister come and prepare bread for me, and make the meat before my eyes, so that I may see and eat from her hand.'"

6 Amnon lay down and feigned illness, and the king came to see him. Amnon said to the king, "Let my sister Tamar please come and make two cakes before me, that I may eat from her hand."

7 David sent to Tamar at home, saying, "Go now to the house of Amnon your brother and prepare the meal for him."

8 Tamar went to the court of Amnon her brother while he was lying down and took the dough and kneaded cakes before his eyes and cooked the cakes.

9 Then she took the pan and poured it out before him, but he refused to eat; and Amnon said, "Remove every man from me," so every man went out from him.

10 Amnon said to Tamar, Bring the meat into the inner chamber and I will eat from your hand; so Tamar took the cakes that she had made and brought them to her brother Amnon in the inner chamber.

11 She approached him to eat, and he caught hold of her and said to her, “Come, lie with me, my sister.”

12 And she said to him, “No, my brother, do not humiliate me, for such a thing is not done in Israel; do not commit this vile act.”

13 But I, where shall I carry my shame? And you will be like one of the fools in Israel; now, please speak to the king, for he will not withhold me from you.

14 But he would not listen to her voice, and being stronger than she, he forced her and lay with her.

15 Amnon hated her with exceedingly great hate, for the great hate with which he hated her was greater than the love with which he had loved her, and Amnon said to her, "Get up, go!"

16 And she said to him, Do not cause the great adversity any longer after what you have done to me by sending me away, but he was not willing to listen to her.

17 And he called to his servant boy and said, Send now this one forth outside from me, and bolt the door after her.

18 And upon her was a robe of divers colours, for thus do the daughters of the king who are virgins wear robes; and her attendant brought her out outside and shut the door after her.

19 Tamar took ashes and put them on her head, tore the coat of divers colours that was on her, put her hand on her head, and went on her way, crying aloud.

20 Abishalom said to her, “Was Amnon your brother with you? Now, my sister, be still; your brother is this matter; do not take to heart this word.” And Tamar remained, desolate, in the house of Abishalom her brother.

21 And King David heard all these words, and he was very angry.

22 And Absalom spoke with Amnon neither good nor bad, because Absalom hated Amnon for the act that he had humbled Tamar his sister.

23 It happened at the end of two years, days, that sheepshearers were with Absalom in Baal-hazor which is with Ephraim, and Absalom called to all the sons of the king.

24 Then Abishalom came to the king and said, "Behold now, your servant has sheepshearers; please let the king and his servants come with your servant."

25 The king said to Abishalom, "No, my son, do not go with us, lest it become a burden to you." But he insisted and would not go, so the king blessed him.

26 And Absalom said, "Let not Amnon, my brother, go with us." But the king said to him, "Why should he go with you?"

27 Then Absalom struck him, and sent with him Amnon and all the king's sons.

28 And Absalom commanded his servants, saying, "See now, when Amnon's heart is merry with wine, and I say to you, 'Strike Amnon,' then kill him. Do not fear. Have I not commanded you? Be courageous and be sons of valor."

29 The lads of Absalom did to Amnon as Absalom had commanded, and all the sons of the king rose up and each rode upon his mule and fled.

30 And they were on the way when the report came to David, saying, Absalom has struck all the sons of the king, and not one of them has been left.

31 The king arose and tore his garments and lay on the ground, and all his servants stood by with torn garments.

32 Then Jonadab the son of Shimeah, David's brother, answered and said, "Let not my lord say, 'All the young men, the king's sons, are killed,' for only Amnon is dead; indeed, this was by the command of Absalom from the day he defiled Tamar his sister."

33 Now then, let not my lord the king take to heart the matter of saying, “All the king’s sons are dead”; for Amnon alone is dead.

34 Abishalom fled, and the lad who was watching lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, many people were coming along a path from the side of the mountain.

35 Jonadab said to the king, "Look, the sons of the king are coming; according to the word of your servant, so it is."

36 And it came to pass, when he had finished speaking, that behold, the sons of the king came, and they lifted up their voice and wept; and also the king and all his servants wept with a great weeping exceedingly.

37 And Absalom fled and went to Talmai the son of Ammihud, king of Geshur, and he mourned for his son all the days.

38 And Absalom fled and went to Geshur, and he was there three years.

39 And King David ceased going out to Absalom, for he had comforted himself concerning Amnon, since he was dead.


## Chapter 14

1 Joab knew that the king's heart was toward Absalom.

2 Joab sent to Tekoa and took from there a wise woman and said to her, "Please mourn; put on garments of mourning and do not anoint yourself with oil, but be like a woman who for many days has been mourning the dead."

3 And you shall go to the king and speak to him about this matter, and Joab placed the words in her mouth.

4 And the Tekoite woman said to the king, and she fell on her face to the ground and prostrated herself, and said, "May the king save."

5 Then the king said to her, "What ails you?" And she said, "Indeed, I am a widow woman; my husband died."

6 And to your handmaid two sons; and the two of them strove in the field, and there was none to deliver between them; and the one struck the other and he died.

7 And behold, all the family has risen up against your handmaid, saying, “Give up the one who struck his brother, that we may put him to death in place of his brother whom he killed, and also destroy the heir,” so they would extinguish my coal that remains, leaving my husband neither name nor remnant on the face of the earth.

8 The king said to the woman, Go to your home, and I will command concerning you.

9 And the Tekoite woman said to the king, Upon me, my lord the king, be the fault, and upon my father's house; but the king and his throne are guiltless.

10 The king said, "Whoever speaks to you about this matter, bring him to me, and he shall never touch you again."

11 And she said, Let the king remember the LORD your God, that the avenger of blood destroy not, and they not destroy my son. And he said, As the LORD lives, not a hair of your son shall fall to the ground.

12 Then the woman said, "Please let your maidservant speak a word to my lord the king." And he said, "Speak."

13 And the woman said, Why have you thought thus concerning the people of God? And in speaking this word, the king would be guilty, for not returning the banished one.

14 For we are surely to die, and like waters spilt upon the ground that cannot be gathered again, and God does not take back a soul, yet he devises means so that the banished one not be cast out from him.

15 And now that I have come to speak this matter to my lord the king, the people will see me; so let your handmaid speak, I pray, to the king, perhaps the king will do the request of his maidservant.

16 For if the king hears to deliver his handmaid from the hand of the man, to destroy me and my son together from the inheritance of God.

17 Your handmaid, let my lord the king's word be for rest, for my lord the king is like a messenger of God, to hear good and evil; and the LORD your God be with you.

18 The king answered and said to the woman, Do not please hide any thing from me, which I am asking you. And the woman said, Let my lord the king please speak.

19 The king said, Is Joab with you in all this? And the woman answered and said, As your soul lives, my lord the king, if one speaks or is silent, it is only what my lord the king has said; for your servant Joab commanded me, and he put into the mouth of your maidservant all these words.

20 To turn your face from this matter, your servant Joab did this thing; and my lord is wise, like the wisdom of the angel of God, to know all that is in the land.

21 The king said to Joab, "Behold, I have done this act; go, bring back the young man Absalom."

22 Joab fell on his face to the ground and prostrated himself and blessed the king, and Joab said, "This day your servant knows that I have found favour in your eyes, my lord the king, in that the king has carried out the word of his servant."

23 Then Joab rose up and went to Geshur and brought Absalom to Jerusalem.

24 The king said, Turn to your home, and my face you shall not see; so Absalom turned to his home, and the face of the king he did not see.

25 Like Absalom, there was no man in all Israel so exceedingly praised for beauty; from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head there was no blemish in him.

26 When he shaves his head, and at the end of the days for which he was to let his hair grow because it had become heavy upon him, he shall shave it and weigh the hair of his head: two hundred shekels by the king's stone.

27 Three sons were born to Absalom, and a daughter whose name was Tamar; she was a woman of beautiful appearance.

28 Abishalom dwelt in Jerusalem two whole years and did not see the face of the king.

29 Absalom sent to Joab to send him to the king, but he was unwilling to come to him; so he sent again a second time, but he was unwilling to come.

30 And he said to his servants, "See, Joab's field is near my hand, and there he has barley; go, set fire to the field," so the servants of Absalom set the field on fire.

31 Joab rose and came to Absalom at home and said to him, Why have your servants set my field on fire?

32 Abishalom said to Joab, "Look, I sent to you, saying, 'Come here, and I will send you to the king, to say, "Why have I come from Geshur? It would be better for me if I were still there. Now let me see the face of the king; and if there is fault in me, let him put me to death."'”

33 Joab came to the king and reported to him; the king summoned Abishalom, and Abishalom came to the king, and he prostrated himself on the ground before the king's face; and the king kissed Abishalom.


## Chapter 15

1 After this, Absalom made for himself a chariot and horses, and fifty men to run before him.

2 And Absalom rose early and stood beside the way of the gate, and it was, when any man had a controversy to come to the king for judgment, that Absalom called to him and said, “From what city are you?” And he said, “Your servant is from one of the tribes of Israel.”

3 And Absalom said to him, See, your advice is good and right, but there is no hearing for you from the king.

4 Absalom said, Who will set me as judge in the land, that every man who has a controversy and a cause may come to me, and I will give him justice?

5 And when another man was near to bow down to him, he would stretch out his hand, take hold of him, and kiss him.

6 And Absalom did this thing to all Israel who came to the king for judgment, and Absalom stole the heart of the men of Israel.

7 After forty years Absalom said to the king Let me go now and fulfill the vow that I vowed to the LORD in Hebron

8 For your servant made a vow while I was in Geshur in Aram, saying, If the LORD brings me back to Jerusalem, then I will serve the LORD.

9 And the king said to him, "Go in peace," and he arose and went to Hebron.

10 And Absalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “As soon as you hear the sound of the trumpet, then you shall say, ‘Absalom reigns in Hebron.’”

11 And two hundred men from Jerusalem went with Abishalom, called and walking in integrity, and they did not know any thing.

12 And Absalom sent Ahithophel the Gilonite, David's counselor, from his city Giloh while he was sacrificing the offerings, and the conspiracy became strong, and the people with Absalom kept increasing.

13 And the messenger came to David, saying, The care of the people of Israel has turned to Absalom.

14 David said to all his servants who were with him in Jerusalem, "Rise up, and let us flee, for there will be no deliverance for us from the face of Absalom; let us hurry to depart, lest he quickly overtake us and bring disaster upon us, and strike the city with the sword."

15 And the servants of the king said to the king, "Behold, your servants are ready for whatever choice my lord the king may select."

16 The king went out, and all his household with him on foot; and the king left ten women, concubines, to keep the house.

17 The king went out and all the people on foot and they stood at the place that was far off.

18 And all his servants passed by his hand, and all the Cherethites and all the Pelethites, and all the Gittites, six hundred men who came after him from Gath, passing before the king.

19 The king said to Ithai the Gittite, Why will you go with us also? Return and stay with the king, for you are a foreigner and also a refugee in your country.

20 Yesterday you came, and today you are with us to go; while I go where I may go? Return, go back with your brothers, kindness and truth.

21 Ithai answered the king and said, As the LORD lives, and as my lord the king lives, wherever my lord the king shall be, whether to death or to life, there will be your servant.

22 David said to Ithai, "Go and pass over," and Ithai the Gittite passed over, and all his men, and all the families who were with him.

23 And all the land wept with a loud voice, and all the people passed by, and the king crossing the brook Kidron, and all the people passing over before the way toward the wilderness.

24 And behold, Zadok and all the Levites with him were carrying the ark of the covenant of God, and they set down the ark of God, and Abiathar went up, until all the people had finished passing out of the city.

25 The king said to Zadok, Return the ark of God to the city. If I find favour in the eyes of the LORD, then he will bring me back and let me see it and its dwelling place.

26 But if he says, "I have no delight in you," then I will do to him as seems good in his eyes.

27 The king said to Zadok the priest, "Are you a seer? Return to the city in peace, and let your son Ahimaaz and Jonathan the son of Abiathar, your two sons, go with you."

28 See, I am waiting in the wilderness for a word from you to tell me.

29 Zadok and Abiathar returned the ark of God to Jerusalem and they remained there.

30 David went up by the ascent of the Olives, ascending and weeping, with his head covered and walking barefoot; and all the people who were with him each had his head covered, and they went up, weeping as they went.

31 And David said, saying, Ahithophel is conspiring with Abishalom; and David said, O the LORD, make void the counsel of Ahithophel.

32 David came to the summit where he was to worship God, and there met him Hushai the Archite with his robe torn and earth upon his head.

33 David said to him, "If you cross over with me, you will become a burden to me."

34 But if the city returns and you say to Absalom, Your servant I will be, O king; your father's servant I was from the beginning, and now I am your servant—then you will reject the counsel of Ahithophel.

35 And is not Zadok and Abiathar the priests with you there, and it shall be that all the word which you hear from the house of the king you shall report to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests.

36 Behold, there with them are their two sons, Ahimaaz with Zadok and Jonathan with Abiathar; and you shall send by their hand to me all the word that you hear.

37 Hushai David's friend came into the city, and Absalom came into Jerusalem.


## Chapter 16

1 David had gone just a little past the summit when Ziba, Mephibosheth’s servant, met him with a pair of donkeys saddled, bearing two hundred loaves of bread, one hundred bunches of raisins, one hundred summer fruits, and a wineskin.

2 The king said to Ziba, "To whom do these belong?" And Ziba said, "The asses are for the king's household to ride, the summer fruit for the young men to eat, and the wine for the weary to drink in the wilderness."

3 The king said, "And where is your lord's son?" Ziba said to the king, "Behold, he is sitting in Jerusalem, for he said, 'Today the house of Israel will restore to me the kingdom of my father.'"

4 The king said to Ziba, "Behold, all that belongs to Mephibosheth is yours." And Ziba said, "I bow low before you; may I find favour in your eyes, my lord, O king."

5 King David came to Bahurim, and there came out from there a certain man of the family of the house of Saul, and his name was Shimei son of Gera; he came out, cursing.

6 They pelted David and all the servants of King David with stones, and all the people and all the warriors on his right and on his left.

7 Thus said Shimei in his cursing, "Go, go, you man of blood and man of Belial."

8 May the LORD repay you for all the blood of the house of Saul, because you reigned and the LORD gave the kingship into the hand of Absalom your son; and behold, you are in your own calamity, for you are a man of blood.

9 Abishai son of Zeruiah said to the king, "Why should this dead dog curse my lord the king? Please let me go over and take off his head."

10 The king said, What have I to do with you, sons of Zeruiah? If the LORD said to him, Curse David, who then shall say, Why have you done so?

11 David said to Abishai and to all his servants, Behold, my son who came forth from my womb seeks my life; how much more now may the Benjamite be let alone, that he may curse, since the LORD has said to him?

12 Perhaps the LORD will see that I am cursed this day, and the LORD will repay me with good for his cursing this day.

13 David and his men went along the way, while Shimei went on the hillside opposite him, hurling and casting stones at him, and throwing dust.

14 The king came, and all the people with him, weary, and there they refreshed themselves.

15 And Abishalom and all the people, every man of Israel, came to Jerusalem, and Ahithophel with him.

16 When Hushai the Archite, David's friend, came to Absalom, Hushai said to Absalom, "Long live the king! Long live the king!"

17 And Abishalom said to Hushai, Is this your favour to your friend? Why did you not go with your friend?

18 Hushai said to Absalom, "Not so; but the one whom the LORD and this people and all the men of Israel have chosen, with him I will be and with him I will stay."

19 And whom else shall I serve, if not before your son, as I have served before your father, so will I be before you?

20 Abishalom said to Ahithophel, "Give us advice, what shall we do?"

21 Ahithophel said to Abishalom, "Go to the concubines of your father, whom he left to guard the house, and all Israel will hear that you have defied your father, and the hands of all who are with you will be strengthened."

22 They pitched the tent for Absalom on the roof, and Absalom went in to the concubines of his father before the eyes of all Israel.

23 And the advice of Ahithophel that he advised in those days was like an inquiry of the word of God; so was all the advice of Ahithophel, both to David and to Absalom.


## Chapter 17

1 Ahithophel said to Abishalom, "Let me choose now twelve thousand men, and I will arise and pursue David tonight."

2 I will come against him when he is weary and powerless, and I will terrify him so that all the people with him will flee, and I will strike the king alone.

3 And I will bring back all the people to you, when that man whom you seek returns, all the people will be in peace.

4 The act pleased Absalom and all the elders of Israel.

5 And Absalom said, Call now also Hushai the Archite, and let us hear also his word.

6 Hushai came to Absalom, and Absalom said to him, "Shall we do the advice of Ahithophel? If not, you speak."

7 Hushai said to Absalom, The advice that Ahithophel has advised is not good this time.

8 Hushai said, You know your father and his men, that they are mighty men, and their souls are fierce as a bear robbed in the wild; and your father is a man of war, and will not lodge with the people.

9 Behold, now he is hidden in one of the pits or in one of the places, and it will be that when they fall upon them at the beginning, the one who hears will hear and say, "There has been a plague among the people after Absalom."

10 And he also is a valiant son whose heart is like the courage of a lion, who has made many to melt, for all Israel knows that your father is a mighty man and his sons who are with him are valiant men.

11 For I have commanded: Let all Israel be gathered to you, from Dan even to Beer-shebah, as the sand that is upon the sea in abundance, and your face going into battle.

12 We came to him in the places where he was found, and we lay upon him as the dew falls upon the ground, and there remained not in him nor in any of the men who were with him even one.

13 And if to a city they gather, all Israel will bring ropes to that city, and we will drag it to the river until not even a small stone is found there.

14 And Absalom and every man of Israel said, The counsel of Hushai the Archite is good rather than the counsel of Ahithophel. And the LORD commanded to defeat the good counsel of Ahithophel, in order to bring upon Absalom the calamity by the LORD.

15 Hushai said to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests, "Thus and thus did Ahithophel counsel Absalom and the elders of Israel, and thus and thus did I counsel."

16 And now send hastily and tell David, “Do not stay overnight in the Arabah wilderness, but also cross over swiftly, lest the king and all the people with him be consumed.”

17 And Jonathan and Ahimaaz were standing at En-rogel, and the maid went and told them, and they went and told King David, for they could not be seen entering the city.

18 A lad saw them and told Absalom; the two of them went quickly and came to the house of a certain man at Bahurim, who had a well in his courtyard, and they went down there.

19 The woman took the covering and spread it over the face of the well, and laid the ground corn upon it, and no word was known.

20 The servants of Absalom came to the woman at the house and said, Where are Ahimaaz and Jonathan? And the woman said to them, They have crossed over the water-brook; and they sought but did not find, so they returned to Jerusalem.

21 After they had gone, they came up from the well and went and told King David, and said to David, “Rise up and cross the water quickly, for thus has Ahithophel counseled against you.”

22 David rose, and all the people who were with him, and they crossed the Jordan until morning light; by dawn, not one remained who had not crossed the Jordan.

23 Ahithophel saw that his counsel was not followed, so he saddled his ass, arose, and went to his home, to his city; he gave instructions concerning his household, then hanged himself and died; and he was buried in the grave of his father.

24 David came to Mahanaim, and Absalom crossed the Jordan, he and every man of Israel with him.

25 And Absalom appointed Amasa over the army instead of Joab, and Amasa was the son of a man whose name was Ithra the Israelite, who had gone in to Abigal the daughter of Nahash, sister of Zeruiah, mother of Joab.

26 Israel and Absalom encamped in the land of Gilead.

27 When David came to Mahanaim, Shobi the son of Nahash from Rabbah of the Ammonites, and Machir the son of Ammiel from Lodebar, and Barzillai the Gileadite from Rogelim, came to meet him.

28 beds, bason, and potter's tool, and wheat and barley and flour and parched corn, and beans and lentile and parched corn.

29 Honey-comb, butter, sheep, and cheese from beeves they brought to David and to the folk who were with him to eat, for they said, the folk are hungry, weary, and thirsty in the wilderness.


## Chapter 18

1 David reviewed the folk who were with him and set over them captains of thousands and captains of hundreds.

2 David sent the people out, one third under Joab’s hand, one third under Abishai son of Zeruiah, Joab’s brother, and one third under Ithai the Gittite; and the king said to the people, “I myself will surely go out with you.”

3 The folk said, No, you shall not go out; for if we flee, they will not care for us, and if half of us die, they will not care for us; for now we are as ten thousand compared to their city, and now it is better that you be ready to help us from the city.

4 The king said to them, "Whatever seems best in your sight, I will do." And the king stood beside the gate, and all the people went out by hundreds and by thousands.

5 The king commanded Joab and Abishai and Ithai, saying, Deal gently for me with the lad Absalom. And all the people heard the command of the king to all the captains concerning Absalom.

6 The folk went out into the field to meet Israel and the battle was in the forest of Ephraim.

7 The folk of Israel were struck there before the servants of David, and a great plague was there that day—twenty thousand.

8 And the battle was there over all the face of the land, and the forest devoured more people than the sword devoured of the people on that day.

9 Abishalom came before David's servants, and Abishalom was riding on the mule; the mule came under the great thick boughs of the oak, and his head caught fast in the oak, and he was left hanging between the heavens and the earth, and the mule that was under him passed on.

10 And a certain man saw and told Joab, and he said, "Behold, I saw Absalom hanging in the elm."

11 And Joab said to the man who told him, "Behold, you saw him, so why did you not strike him down there to the ground? And I would have had to give you ten pieces of silver and one girdle."

12 And the man said to Joab, Behold, I would take in my hands a thousand silver coins, yet I would not stretch out my hand against the king's son, for in our hearing the king charged you and Abishai and Ithai, saying, Keep watch over the young man Absalom.

13 Or have I done deceitfully, and is any word hidden from the king? then you yourself would have stood aloof.

14 Joab said, I will not wait thus before you; and he took three rods in his hand and thrust them into the heart of Absalom while he was still alive in the midst of the oak.

15 Ten lads bearing Joab's weapons turned and struck Absalom and killed him.

16 And Joab did blow the cornet; and the folk returned from pursuing after Israel; for Joab did hold back the folk.

17 They took Abishalom, Absalom and threw him into the forest, into the great pit, and they set over him a great very heap of stones, and all Israel fled, every man to his tent.

18 And Absalom took and set up for himself a pillar in the king's valley, for he said, There is no son of mine to keep my name in remembrance, and he called the pillar by his name, and it is called Absalom's Hand to this day.

19 Ahimaaz son of Zadok said, "Let me run now, and I will bring the king news that the LORD has delivered him from the hand of his enemies."

20 And Joab said to him, You are not a man of reward for tidings today, but you shall bring tidings on another day; however, today you shall not bring tidings, for the son of the king has died.

21 Joab said to the Cushite, “Go, tell the king what you have seen.” And the Cushite bowed to Joab and ran.

22 Then Ahimaaz son of Zadok said again to Joab, “Let me also run after the Cushite.” And Joab said, “Why will you run, my son? There is no reward for tidings.”

23 And he said, "What is your urgent matter?" and he said to him, "Run," and Ahimaaz ran along the plain and passed the Cushite.

24 David was sitting between the two gates, and the watchman went up to the roof of the house, to the wall above the gate; he lifted his eyes and saw a man running alone.

25 The watchman called and reported to the king, and the king said, “If he is alone, there is reward for tidings in his mouth,” and he kept going and approaching.

26 And the watchman saw another man running; and the watchman called to the doorkeeper and said, “Behold, a man is running alone”; and the king said, “He also is a messenger.”

27 The lookout said, "I see the course of the first runner resembling the course of Ahimaaz son of Zadok." And the king said, "He is a good man, and he comes with good news."

28 Ahimaaz called and said to the king, "May you fare well," and he bowed before the king with his face to the ground, and said, "Blessed be the LORD your God, who has delivered the men who raised their hands against my lord the king."

29 The king said, "Is the young man Absalom safe?" And Ahimaaz answered, "When I saw the great tumult, I did not know what had happened."

30 The king said, "Turn, stand here." So he turned and stood.

31 And behold, the Cushite came, and the Cushite said, "Let my lord the king rejoice, for today the LORD has delivered you from the hand of all who rose against you."

32 The king said to the Cushite, "Is the young man Absalom safe?" And the Cushite replied, "May the enemies of my lord the king and all who rise against you for harm be like the young man."

33 The king was deeply agitated and went up to the chamber over the gate, and as he went he wept, saying, "My son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! I wish I had died instead of you, Absalom, my son, my son!"


## Chapter 19

1 And it was told to Joab, Behold, the king is weeping and mourning for Absalom.

2 And the deliverance became mourning for all the people on that day, for the people heard on that day, saying, the king is grieved for his son.

3 The folk stole into the city that day, as the people, being ashamed, steal away when fleeing from battle.

4 And the king turned his face slowly, and the king cried with a loud voice, O my son Absalom, Absalom, my son, my son.

5 Joab came into the royal palace and said, You have today put to shame the faces of all your bond servants who saved your life today and the life of your sons and daughters and the life of your wives and the life of your concubines.
