6 To love those who hate you and to hate those who love you, for today you have declared that you have no captains or servants, for today I know that Absalom is alive and we are all dead today; then it would be right in your eyes.

7 Now therefore arise, go and speak to the mind of your servants, for by the LORD I have sworn that you will not go out, if any man stays with you this night; and this will be worse for you than all the evil that has come upon you from your youth until now.

8 The king rose and sat in the gate, and to all the people they reported, saying, "Behold, the king is sitting in the gate," and all the people came before the king, but Israel fled, each man to his tent.

9 And all the people were in an uproar throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, The king delivered us from the power of our enemies and rescued us from the power of the Philistines, but now he has fled from the land because of Absalom.

10 And Abishalom whom we anointed over us has died in the battle, so why are you silent about restoring the king?

11 King David sent to Zadok and Abiathar the priests, saying, “Speak to the elders of Judah, saying, ‘Why are you last to bring back the king to his home? For the word of all Israel has come to the king, to bring him to his home.’”

12 You are my brothers, my very body and flesh; why then will you be last to return the king?

13 And to Amasa you shall say, Is not my bone and my flesh you? Thus may God do to me and so may he add, if you are not captain of the army before me all the days instead of Joab.

14 And he turned the heart of every man of Judah as one man, and they sent to the king, saying, Return, you and all your servants.

15 Then the king returned and came to the Jordan, and Judah came to Gilgal to go to meet the king, to bring the king across the Jordan.

16 Shimei the Bahurite, son of Gera, son of Benjamin, hurried down with a man of Judah to meet King David.

17 And a thousand men with him from Benjamin, and Ziba the servant of Saul, and his fifteen sons and twenty servants with him, and they crossed the Jordan ahead of the king.

18 And the ferry passed over to cross the king's court and to do what was good in his eyes; and Shimei son of Gera fell before the king as he crossed at the Jordan.

19 And he said to the king, Do not let my lord impute fault to me, and do not remember what your servant did on the day my lord the king went out from Jerusalem, so as to lay it upon the king's mind.

20 For your servant knows that I have sinned, and here I have come today, the first of all the house of Joseph, to go down to meet my lord the king.

21 Abishai son of Zeruiah answered and said, Shall Shimei not be put to death for this, because he cursed the anointed of the LORD?

22 David said, "What have I to do with you, you sons of Zeruiah, that you should be an adversary to me today? Today an innocent man is being killed in Israel, for indeed I know that today I am king over Israel."

23 The king said to Shimei, You shall not die, and the king swore to him.

24 And Mephibosheth the son of Saul went down to meet the king, and he had not tended his legs nor trimmed his beard nor washed his garments from the day the king departed until the day he returned in peace.

25 When Mephibosheth came to Jerusalem to meet the king, the king said to him, "Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?"

26 And my lord the king said, "My servant Rimmoni, for your servant said, 'Saddle the donkey for me, that I may ride on it and go to the king, because your servant is lame.'"

27 And he slandered your servant before my lord the king, and my lord the king is like a messenger of God; do therefore what is good in your eyes.

28 For there was not any family of my father's house that remained alive to my lord the king, yet you placed your servant among those who eat at your table; what right do I still have, that I should cry out again to the king?

29 Then said the king to him, Why speakest thou any more of thy words? I have said, Thou and Ziba divide the land.

30 Mephibosheth said to the king, "Let the king take it all, since my lord the king has come home in peace."

31 And Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim and crossed over the Jordan with the king to send him off.

32 Barzillai was very old, an aged man of eighty years, and he had supported the king during his stay in Mahanaim, for he was a very great man.

33 The king said to Barzillai, "You come with me, and I will provide for you with me in Jerusalem."

34 Barzillai said to the king, how many are the days of the age of my life, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?

35 I am eighty years old today; can I still discern between good and evil? Can your servant taste what he eats and what he drinks? Can I still hear the voice of singers and singers? Why should your servant still be a burden to my lord the king?

36 Almost, your servant would cross the Jordan with the king—why then does the king reward me with such a reward?

37 Let your servant now return and die in my city, with the grave of my father and my mother; but let Chimham, your servant, go with my lord the king, and do to him what seems good to you.

38 The king said, Let Chimham go over with me; I will do for him what is good in your sight; and whatever you choose from me, I will do for you.

39 Then all the people crossed the Jordan, and the king crossed; and the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his place.

40 The king passed on to Gilgal, and Chimham passed on with him; and all the people of Judah and also half the people of Israel passed on with the king.

41 And behold, every man of Israel came to the king, and they said to the king, "Why have your brothers, the men of Judah, stolen you away? They have passed over the king and his household across the Jordan, and all the men of David with him."

42 All the men of Judah answered the man of Israel, "Because the king is near to us; why should you be angry about this matter? Have we eaten anything from the king, or has any favor been shown to us?"

43 Then the man of Israel answered the man of Judah and said, Ten parts have I in the king, and also in David I am more than you; why then have you despised me, that my word was not first to restore my king? And the words of the man of Judah were harsher than the words of the man of Israel.


## Chapter 20

1 And there came a certain ungodly man whose name was Sheba son of Bichri a Jaminite and he blew the trumpet and said We have no part in David neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse every man to his tent O Israel

2 All the men of Israel went up after David, after Sheba son of Bichri, but all the men of Judah clung to their king from the Jordan to Jerusalem.

3 David came to his court in Jerusalem, and the king took the ten women, concubines, whom he had left to keep the palace; he placed them in a house of custody and provided for them, but did not go in to them; and they remained confined until the day of their death, living as widows.

4 The king said to Amasa, Summon for me the men of Judah within three days, and you stand here.

5 Amasa went to summon Judah, but he delayed beyond the appointed time that had been set for him.

6 David said to Abishai, Now will Sheba the son of Bichri do us more harm than did Absalom; take you the servants of your lord, and pursue after him, lest he find for himself fortified cities, and escape out of our sight.

7 The men of Joab went out after him, the Cherethites, the Pelethites, and all the mighty men, and they went out from Jerusalem to pursue Sheba son of Bichri.

8 They were by the great stone which is in Gibeon, and Amasa came before them, and Joab was girded with his garment and over it a girdle of sword fastened upon his loins in its sheath, and he went out and it fell.

9 Joab said to Amasa, "Are you well, my brother?" and Joab took Amasa's beard with his right hand to kiss him.

10 Amasa did not guard against the sword that was in Joab's hand, and Joab struck him with it in the fifth rib, so his bowels poured out on the ground; he did not strike again, and he died; and Joab and Abishai his brother pursued Sheba son of Bichri.

11 Also, a certain man stood over him from the young men of Joab and said, “Whoever desires Joab and whoever is for David, after Joab!”

12 And Amasa was wallowing in the blood in the midst of the highway, and the man saw that all the people stood still, so he turned Amasa from the highway into the field and threw a garment over him when all who came upon him stood still.

13 As he turned from the causeway, every man passed after Joab to pursue after Sheba the son of Bichri.

14 He passed through all the tribes of Israel, Abel and Beth-maachah and all the Berites; and they assembled and also went after him.

15 They came and besieged him at Abel Beth-maachah, and they cast up a mount against the city, and it stood by the rampart, and all the people who were with Joab battered it to overthrow the wall.

16 And a wise woman called from the city, “Hear, hear, I beg you, tell Joab, ‘Come near here, and I will speak to you.’”

17 And he approached her, and the woman said, "Are you Joab?" And he said, "I am." Then she said to him, "Hear the words of your maidservant." And he said, "I am listening."

18 And she said, saying, "Speak! They shall speak in the first place, saying, 'Shall they ask at Abel?' and thus they ceased."

19 I am among the peaceful and faithful in Israel; you seek to kill a city and a mother in Israel. Why swallow up the inheritance of the LORD?

20 Joab answered and said, Far be it, far be it from me to swallow or to destroy.

21 Not so is the matter; rather, a man from Mount Ephraim, Sheba the son of Bichri, has raised his hand against the king David. Deliver him alone, and I will withdraw from the city. Then the woman said to Joab, Behold, his head is thrown to you over the wall.

22 Then the woman came to all the people with her wisdom, and they cut off the head of Sheba son of Bichri and threw it to Joab; and he blew the trumpet, and they dispersed from upon the city, every man to his tent; and Joab returned to Jerusalem to the king.

23 Joab was over all the army of Israel, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and over the Pelethites.

24 Adoram was over the levy, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder.

25 scribe and Zadok and Abiathar priests

26 Ira the Jairite was also a priest to David.


## Chapter 21

1 There was a famine in David's time for three years, year after year, and David sought the face of the LORD, and the LORD said, "Because of Saul and his blood-guilty house, for having put to death the Gibeonites."

2 The king summoned the Gibeonites and said to them; now the Gibeonites were not of the people of Israel but were the remnant of the Amorites, and the people of Israel had sworn to them, but Saul had sought to strike them down in his zeal for the people of Israel and Judah.

3 David said to the Gibeonites, "What shall I do for you, and how shall I make atonement, that you may bless the heritage of the LORD?"

4 The Gibeonites said to him, There is no silver or gold with Saul and with his house, and we have no man to put to death in Israel, and he said, What do you say I should do for you?

5 They said to the king, "The man who has wearied us and compared himself to us—we are worn out from standing against him in all the border of Israel."

6 Let seven men from his sons be given to us, and we will impale them before the LORD at Gibeah of Saul, the chosen of the LORD; and the king said, I will give them.

7 The king showed mercy to Mephibosheth son of Jonathan son of Saul because of the oath of the LORD that was between them, between David and Jonathan son of Saul.

8 The king took the two sons of Rizpah daughter of Aiah, whom she bore to Saul, Armoni and Mephibosheth, and the five sons of Michal daughter of Saul, whom she bore to Adriel son of Barzillai the Mecholathite.

9 And he gave them into the hand of the Gibeonites, and they hanged them on the hill before the LORD, and the seven of them fell together; they were put to death in the days of harvest, in the first boughs, the harvest of barley.

10 Rizpah daughter of Aiah took the sackcloth and spread it for herself upon the rock from the beginning of harvest until water poured upon them from the heavens, and she did not allow the birds of the heavens to rest upon them by day nor the beasts of the field by night.

11 It was told to David what Rizpah daughter of Aiah the concubine of Saul had done.

12 David went and took the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son from the men of Jabesh-gilead who had stolen them from the broad place of Beth-shan, where the Philistines had hanged Saul on the day they defeated him in Gilboa.

13 He went up from there with the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son, and they gathered the bones of those who had been hanged.

14 They buried the bones of Saul and Jonathan his son in the land of Benjamin at Zelah in the grave of Kish his father, and they did all that the king commanded, and God was entreated for the land afterward.

15 There was again fighting between the Philistines and Israel, and David went down with his servants and they fought against the Philistines, and David grew weary.

16 At Nob, the home-born of the giant, whose spear weighed three hundred [units of] brass by weight, and he was girded with a new sword, he said to strike David.

17 Then Abishai son of Zeruiah came to his aid and struck the Philistine and killed him. Then the men of David swore to him, saying, You shall not go out with us again to battle, lest you quench the lamp of Israel.

18 Afterward there was again war at Gob with the Philistines, and Sibbecai the Hushathite struck down Saph, who was one of the descendants of the giants.

19 There was again fighting at Gob with the Philistines, and Elkanan son of Jaare-oregim the Bethlehemite struck down Goliath the Gittite, and the shaft of his javelin was like a weaver’s beam.

20 There was again fighting in Gath, and there was a man of great stature whose hands and feet each had six fingers and six toes, twenty-four in number, was born to the giant.

21 He taunted Israel, and Jonathan the son of Shimei, David's brother, struck him down.

22 These four were born to the giant in Gath, and they fell by the hand of David and by the hand of his servants.


## Chapter 22

1 David spoke to the LORD the words of this song on the day the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.

2 The LORD is my rock, and my rocky stronghold, and my castle, my refuge.

3 My God, the Rock in whom I take refuge, my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold and my refuge, who delivers me from violence.

4 I will call upon Jehovah the Lord, and I will be saved from my enemies.

5 For the waves of death have surrounded me; the torrents of Belial have terrified me.

6 The pangs of the grave have encompassed me; the snares of death have overtaken me.

7 In my distress I call to the LORD, and to my God I cry; and from his temple he hears my voice, and my cry enters his ears.

8 The earth trembled, the foundations of the heavens shook and quaked because he was angry.

9 Smoke rose in his nostrils, and fire from his mouth devoured; burning coals blazed forth from him.

10 He bent the heavens and came down, and thick darkness was under his feet.

11 And he rode upon a cherub and flew, and he appeared upon the wings of the wind.

12 He set darkness about him as a booth, dark waters, thick clouds of the sky.

13 From the brightness before him, burning coals of fire blazed.

14 The LORD thunders from the heavens; the Most High utters his voice.

15 He sent out arrows and scattered them, lightning, and routed them.

16 The channels of the sea were seen, the foundations of the world were laid bare by the rebuke of the LORD, by the blast of the breath of his anger.

17 He sent from on high to take me; he drew me out of many waters.

18 Deliver me from my mighty enemy, from those who hate me, for they are too strong for me.

19 They confront me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD is my stay.

20 He brought me out to a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.

21 May the LORD repay me according to my justice, according to the cleanness of my hands he will reward me.

22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not turned wickedly from my God.

23 For all his ordinances are before me, and his statutes I do not set aside.

24 And I was blameless toward him, and I guarded myself from my fault.

25 And the LORD returned to me according to my justice, according to my cleanness before his eyes.

26 With the faithful you show faithfulness, with the blameless warrior you are blameless.

27 With the pure you show yourself pure, and with the crooked you show yourself shrewd.

28 and folk in affliction you will save, but your eyes upon the proud you will bring low.

29 For you are my light, O the LORD, and the LORD illumines my darkness.

30 For in God I will pass beyond a troop; by my God I will leap over a wall.

31 God is blameless in his way; the word of the LORD is refined; he is a shield to all who take refuge in him.

32 For who is God besides the LORD, and who is a rock besides our God?

33 God is my strong fortress, and he makes the path of the blameless complete.

34 Making my legs like hinds, and on my high places he sets me.

35 Teaching my hands for war, and my arms bend a bow of brass.

36 And you have given me the buckler of your salvation, and your answer has made me great.

37 You enlarged my steps beneath me, and my feet did not slip.

38 I will pursue my enemies and destroy them, and I will not turn back until they are consumed.

39 And I consumed them and shattered them, and they were not able to rise; they fell under my feet.

40 You armed me with strength for battle; You subdued my adversaries beneath me.

41 You made my enemies turn their backs to me, my adversaries, and I destroyed them.

42 They cry for help, but there is no savior; they cry to the LORD, and he does not answer them.

43 I beat them as fine as the dust of the earth, I crushed them and trampled them like mire of the streets.

44 And you delivered me from the strivings of my people; you preserved me as head of the nations; a people I did not know shall serve me.

45 The sons of the alien will disown me; by hearing of the ear they will obey me.

46 The sons of the alien shall wither and gird themselves from their close places.

47 As the LORD lives, blessed be my rock, and exalted be the God of my salvation.

48 God who gives me vengeance and brings nations under me.

49 and bringing me out from my enemies; you lifted me up above those who rose against me, and delivering me from the man of violence.

50 Therefore I will praise you, O the LORD, among the nations, and to your name I will sing praises.

51 He gives great deliverance to His king and shows steadfast love to His anointed, to David and to his seed forever.


## Chapter 23

1 These are the last words of David, says David son of Jesse, and says the man raised above, anointed of God of Jacob, and sweet psalms of Israel.

2 The spirit of the LORD spoke through me, and his word was upon my tongue.

3 The God of Israel said to me, the Rock of Israel: a ruler over humanity, a righteous one, rules with the fear of God.

4 And as the clear light of morning the sun shines, a morning without clouds, from the brightness after rain, grass from the earth.

5 For is not my house so with God? For He has made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things and secure. For all my salvation and all my desire, will He not make it grow?

6 But the worthless, all of them, are like thorns thrust away, for they are not taken by hand.

7 And if any man touches them, he shall be filled with iron and a wooden spear, and with fire they shall be utterly burned at the place.

8 These are the names of the mighty men who were with David: he that sat in the seat, the Tachmonite chief of the captains, he Adino the Eznite, who with one step struck down eight hundred.

9 Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the three with David, who joined him in the battle against the Philistines when they gathered there for war, and the men of Israel went up.

10 He rose and struck down the Philistines until his hand grew weary and clung to the sword, and the LORD brought about a great victory that day, and the people returned after him only to strip the slain.

11 After him was Shamma son of Agee the Hararite, and the Philistines were gathered to the life, and there the portion of the field was full of lentiles, and the people fled from the presence of the Philistines.

12 He stood in the midst of the field and delivered it, and struck the Philistines, and the LORD made great deliverance.

13 Then thirty of the band came down to David at the harvest time to the cave of Adullam, while a troop of Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.

14 And David was then in the stronghold, while the Philistine garrison was then in Bethlehem.

15 David longed and said, Who will give me water to drink from the cistern of Bethlehem that is by the gate?

16 Three of the warriors broke through the Philistine army's camp and drew water from the cistern that is at the gate of Bethlehem, and they carried it and brought it to David, but he refused to drink it; instead, he poured it out to the LORD.

17 And he said, "Far be it from me, O LORD, to do such a thing: this is the blood of the men who went in jeopardy of their lives!" and he would not drink it; these things did the three mighty men.

18 Abishai, the brother of Joab son of Zeruiah, was chief; he wielded his spear against three hundred slain, and had a name among the three.

19 Of the three, he was most honored, and he became their captain, but he did not come to the three.

20 Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, a valiant man of Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds, struck down two of the lionlike men of Moab; and he went down and struck in the midst of the pit on a snowy day.

21 And he struck a certain Egyptian whom he saw, and in the hand of the Egyptian was a spear, and he went down to him with a staff, and he took the spear from the hand of the Egyptian and killed him with his own spear.

22 These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and he had a name among the three mighty ones.

23 From thirty he was honored, but to the thirty he did not come, and David set him over his guard.

24 Asahel, brother of Joab, was in the Thirty; Elkanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem.

25 Shammah the Harodite, Elika the Harodite.

26 Helez the Paltite, Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite.

27 Abiezer the Anethothite, Mebunnai the Hushathite.

28 Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite.

29 Heleb Baanah the Netophathite, Ithai son of Ribai, from Gibeah of the sons of Benjamin.

30 Benaiah the Pirathonite, Hiddai of the brooks of Gaash.

31 Abialbon the Arbahite, Azmaveth the Barhumite.

32 Eliahbah the Shaalbonite, sons of Jashen, Jonathan.

33 Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam son of Sharar the Hararite.

34 Eliphalet son of Ahasbai the Maachathite, Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite.

35 Hezrai the Carmelite, Paarai the Arbite.

36 Igal son of Nathan from Zobah, Bani the Gadite.

37 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite, the armour-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah.

38 Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite.

39 Uriah the Hittite, altogether thirty and seven.


## Chapter 24

1 And the LORD added to be angry with Israel, and He incited David against them, saying, “Go, number Israel and Judah.”

2 The king said to Joab the captain of the army who was with him, Go now through all the tribes of Israel from Dan even to Beer-shebah and count the people, that I may know the number of the people.

3 Joab said to the king, "May the LORD your God add to the people a hundred times as many as they are, and may the eyes of my lord the king see it; but why does my lord the king desire this thing?"

4 The king's command prevailed over Joab and the captains of the army, so Joab and the captains of the army went out before the king to number the people of Israel.

5 They crossed the Jordan and camped at Aroer, south of the city that is in the middle of the valley of Gad, toward Jaazer.

6 They came to Gilead and to the land of Tahtim-hodshi, and they came to Dan-jaan and around to Sidon.

7 They came to the fortress of Tyre and all the cities of the Hivites and the Canaanites and went to the south of Judah at Beer-shebah.

8 They spread throughout all the land and came to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.

9 Joab gave the account of the number of the people to the king, and Israel was eight hundred thousand men of valor drawing sword, and the men of Judah five hundred thousand.

10 David's heart struck him after this, so he numbered the people; and David said to the LORD, "I have sinned greatly in what I have done; now, O LORD, please remove the iniquity of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly."

11 David rose early in the morning, and the word of the LORD came to Gad the prophet, seer of David, saying:

12 Go and say to David, ‘Thus says the LORD: Three I will bring upon you; choose for yourself one of them, and I will do it to you.’

13 Gad came to David and reported to him, and said to him, "Will you have seven years of famine in your land, or three months of fleeing before your enemies who pursue you, or three days of pestilence in your land? Now consider and see what I shall return to the one who sent me the word."

14 David said to Gad, I am very distressed; let me fall now into the hand of the LORD, for his mercies are great, but into the hand of man let me not fall.

15 The LORD sent a pestilence upon Israel from morning until the appointed time, and among the people seventy thousand men died, from Dan to Beer-sheba.

16 The angel sent his hand against Jerusalem to destroy it, but the LORD relented concerning the evil and said to the destroying angel in the midst of the people, "Now stay your hand." And the angel of the LORD was by the threshing floor of the Jebusite Araunah.

17 David said to the LORD when he saw the angel who was striking the people, "Behold, I have sinned and I have done wickedly; but these sheep—what have they done? Let your hand be against me and against my father's house."

18 Gad came to David that day and said to him, "Go up, build an altar to the LORD at the threshing floor of the Jebusite."

19 David went up according to the advice of Gad, as the LORD had commanded.

20 And Araunah looked and saw the king and his servants passing by him, and Araunah went out and bowed before the king, his face to the ground.

21 And Araunah said, Why has my lord the king come to his servant? And David said, To buy from you the threshing floor in order to build an altar to the LORD, so that the plague may be restrained from upon the people.

22 Then said Araunah to David, Let my lord the king take and offer up that which is good in his sight; behold, the beeve for a burnt offering, and the threshing instruments and vessels of the beeve for wood.

23 Araunah said to the king, "All that is needed, Araunah gives to the king; may the LORD your God accept you."

24 The king said to Araunah, No, but I will buy from you at a price, and I will not offer up burnt offerings to the LORD my God without cost. And David bought the threshing floor and the cattle for fifty shekels of silver.

25 Then David built an altar to the LORD there and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings; and the LORD was entreated toward the land, and the plague was stayed from over Israel.


# 1 KINGS

## Chapter 1

1 And King David was old, advanced in age, and they covered him with garments, but he was not warmed.

2 His servants said to him, Let a young virgin be sought for my lord the king, and let her stand before the king and be his attendant, and let her lie in your bosom, that warmth may come to my lord the king.

3 And they sought a fair damsel in all the borders of Israel, and found Abishag the Shunamite, and brought her to the king.

4 And the damsel was very fair; and she became a keeper for the king and served him; but the king did not know her.

5 And Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, "I will reign," and he prepared for himself a chariot and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.

6 And his father never grieved him all his days by saying, "Why have you done thus?" and he was also very good in form, and his mother bore him after Absalom.

7 His advice was with Joab the son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest, and they supported Adonijah.

8 Zadok the priest, Benaiah son of Jehoiada, Nathan the prophet, Shimei, Rei, and the mighty men who were with David were not with Adonijah.

9 Adonijah sacrificed sheep and beeve and fat cattle at the stone of Zoheleth which is by En-rogel, and he called all his brothers the sons of the king and all the men of Judah the servants of the king.

10 And Nathan the prophet and Benaiah and the mighty men and Solomon his brother he did not call.

11 Nathan said to Bath-sheba the mother of Solomon, saying, "Have you not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith has become king, and our lord David does not know?"

12 Now go, I will give you advice: save your life and the life of your son Solomon.

13 Go and enter to King David and say to him, "My lord, O king, did you not swear to your bondwoman, saying, 'Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he will sit on my throne'? Then why has Adonijah become king?"

14 Look, you are still speaking there with the king, and I will come after you and fulfill your words.

15 Bath-sheba came to the king in the inner chamber, and the king was very old, and Abishag the Shunamite served the king.

16 Bath-sheba bowed down and prostrated herself before the king, and the king said, "What is the matter with you?"

17 And she said to him, my lord, you swore by the LORD your God to your servant, “Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.”

18 And now Adonijah has become king, yet my lord the king did not know.

19 He sacrificed a bull and a fatling and sheep in abundance, and he called all the sons of the king and Abiathar the priest and Joab the captain of the host, but Solomon your servant he did not call.

20 But you, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are on you, to tell them who will sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.

21 And it shall be, when my lord the king lies with his fathers, I and my son Solomon will be counted as offenders.

22 And behold, she is still speaking with the king, and Nathan the prophet came.

23 They informed the king, saying, "Behold, Nathan the prophet has come before the king," and he bowed before the king, falling to the ground on his face.

24 Nathan said, "My lord the king, you said, 'Adonijah shall reign after me,' and he has sat on my throne."

25 When the day was descending, he sacrificed a bull and a fattened animal and sheep in abundance, and he summoned all the sons of the king and the commanders of the army and Abiathar the priest, and behold, they were eating and drinking before him, and they said, “Long live King Adonijah!”

26 But me, your servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Solomon your servant—he did not call.

27 If this word has come forth from my lord the king, why have you not declared who will sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?

28 King David answered and said, Call me Bath-sheba. So she came before the king and stood before the king.

29 The king swore and said, "As the LORD lives, who has delivered my life from all distress."

30 For as I swore to you by the LORD, the God of Israel, saying, 'Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne,' so will I do this day.

31 Bath-sheba bowed with her face to the ground before the king and said, "May my lord king David live forever."

32 Then King David said, Call for me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada. And they came before the king.

33 The king said to them, Take with you the servants of your lord, and mount Solomon my son on my mule, and bring him down to Gihon.

34 Then Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed Solomon king over Israel, and they blew the trumpet and said, "Long live King Solomon!"

35 You shall go up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne, and he shall reign in my place, and I have appointed him to be leader over Israel and Judah.

36 Benaiah son of Jehoiada answered the king and said, "Amen, so says the LORD, the God of my lord the king."

37 As the LORD was with my lord the king, so may he be with Solomon, and may he make his throne greater than the throne of my lord King David.

38 Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet and Benaiah son of Jehoiada and the Cherethites and the Pelethites went down and had Solomon ride upon the mule of King David and they led him to Gihon.

39 Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon, and they blew the trumpet, and all the people said, "Long live King Solomon!"

40 And all the people went up after him, the people playing on pipes and rejoicing with great joy, and the earth split apart at their sound.

41 Adonijah and all the guests who were with him had just finished eating when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet and said, "Why is the sound of the city in an uproar?"

42 While he was still speaking, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came, and Adonijah said, "Come in, for you are a man of worth and bring good news."

43 Then Jonathan answered Adonijah, saying, "But our lord the king David has made Solomon king."

44 Then the king sent Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet and Benaiah son of Jehoiada and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and they caused him to ride on the king's mule.

45 Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed him king at Gihon, and they went up from there rejoicing, and the sound of the city was the noise you heard.

46 Solomon also sat upon the royal throne.

47 And also the servants of the king came to bless our lord King David, saying, May the name of Solomon be better than your name, and may his throne be greater than your throne, and the king bowed on the bed.

48 And thus also said the king, Blessed be the LORD, God of Israel, who has given one to sit on the throne today while my eyes see it.

49 They trembled and all the men who belonged to Adonijah got up and each went his own way.

50 Adonijah feared because of Solomon, so he rose up and went and grasped the horns of the altar.

51 It was reported to Solomon, saying, "Behold, Adonijah fears King Solomon, and indeed, he has seized the horns of the altar, saying, 'Let King Solomon swear to me today that he will not put his servant to death with the sword.'"

52 Solomon said, if he proves to be a worthy man, not a hair of his shall fall to the ground; but if evil be found in him, he shall die.

53 Then King Solomon sent and brought him down from the altar, and he came and bowed down to King Solomon, and Solomon said to him, Go to your house.


## Chapter 2

1 The days of David drew near to die, and he charged Solomon his son, saying:

2 I am going the way of all the earth; you shall be strong and become a man.

3 You shall keep the charge of the LORD your God, to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, his commandments, his judgments, and his testimonies, as it is written in the law of Moses, so that you may prosper in all that you do and wherever you turn.

4 So that the LORD may establish his word which he spoke concerning me, saying, If your sons will keep their way to walk before me in truth, with all their heart and with all their soul, then he said, There shall not be cut off from you a man from the throne of Israel.

5 You also know what Joab the son of Zeruiah did to me, how he killed the two captains of the armies of Israel, Abner the son of Ner and Amasa the son of Jether, and put the blood of war in peace, and placed the blood of war in his belt which was at his loins and in the shoes which were on his feet.

6 Act wisely, and do not let his gray head go down to the grave in peace.

7 And to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite you shall show favour, and they shall eat at your table, for thus they drew near to me when I fled from the presence of Absalom your brother.

8 And behold, Shimei the son of Gera, a Benjamite from Bahurim, is with you; he cursed me with a grievous curse on the day I went to Mahanaim, and he came down to meet me at the Jordan, and I swore to him by the LORD, saying, If I shall put you to death with the sword.

9 And now do not let him go unpunished, for you are a wise man and you know what you must do to him; you shall bring his gray hairs down with blood to the grave.

10 David slept with the forefathers and was buried in the city of David.

11 The age that David reigned over Israel was forty years; in Hebron he reigned seven years, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years.

12 Solomon sat upon the throne of David his father, and his kingdom was established exceedingly.

13 Adonijah son of Haggith came to Bath-sheba mother of Solomon, and she said, "Do you come in peace?" And he said, "In peace."

14 He said, "I have a word for you," and she said, "Speak."

15 You know that kingship belonged to me, and all Israel turned their faces to me to reign, but the kingship turned and became my brother's, for it was from the LORD to him.

16 And now, one request I ask of you: do not refuse my face. And he said to her, Speak.

17 And he said, I pray you, to Solomon the king, Let him not turn his face from me, and give Abishag the Shunamite to me for a wife.

18 Bath-sheba said, It is good; I will speak to the king on your behalf.

19 Bath-sheba came to King Solomon to speak with him about Adonijah, and the king rose to meet her, bowed to her, sat on his throne, and set a seat for the mother of the king; she sat at his right hand.

20 And she said, "One small loan, petition, request I am asking from you: do not turn your face from me." And the king said to her, "Ask, my mother, for I will not turn your face from me."

21 Then she said, Let Abishag the Shunamite be given to Adonijah your brother as wife.

22 King Solomon answered his mother, Why do you ask Abishag the Shunamite for Adonijah? Ask also for him the kingship, since he is my elder brother, and for him and for Abiathar the priest and for Joab son of Zeruiah.

23 King Solomon swore by the LORD, saying, "May God do so to me and more also, for Adonijah has spoken this word with his life."

24 Now as surely as the LORD lives, who has established me on the throne of David my father and has made for me a house as he promised, today Adonijah shall be put to death.

25 King Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and he encountered him and he died.

26 And to Abiathar the priest the king said, Return to Anathoth; for you are a man of death: but I will not put you to death this day, because you bore the ark of the LORD my God before David my father, and because you were afflicted in all the affliction with which my father was afflicted.

27 Solomon banished Abiathar from being priest to the Lord to fulfill the word of the Lord which he spoke concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.

28 And the report came to Joab, for Joab had sided with Adonijah but not with Absalom; so Joab fled to the tent of the LORD and seized the horns of the altar.

29 Then it was reported to King Solomon that Joab had fled to the tent of the LORD and was now beside the altar, so Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada, saying, "Go, strike him down."

30 Benaiah came into the tent of the LORD and said to him, Thus says the king, Come out. And he said, No, for here I will die. And Benaiah returned to the king the answer, saying, Thus Joab spoke, and thus he answered me.

31 The king said to him, "Do as he has spoken; strike him and bury him, and you shall remove from me and from my father's house the blood-guiltiness of Joab who shed innocent blood."

32 And the LORD will return his blood upon his own head, who struck down two righteous and good men more than he, and killed them with the sword, while David my father did not know Abner the son of Ner, captain of the army of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether, captain of the army of Judah.

33 And the bloodguilt of them shall return upon the head of Joab and upon the head of his seed forever, but to David and to his seed and to his house and to his throne shall there be peace from the LORD forever.

34 Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up and struck him and killed him, and he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.

35 The king appointed Benaiah son of Jehoiada over the army in his place, and the king appointed Zadok the priest in place of Abiathar.

36 Then the king sent and called for Shimei and said to him, Build yourself a house in Jerusalem and dwell there, and you shall not go out from there here and there.

37 And it shall be on the day you go out and cross the brook Kidron, you shall surely know that you must surely die; your blood shall be on your own head.

38 Shimei said to the king, "The word is good, as my lord the king has said, so will your servant do." And Shimei lived in Jerusalem many days.

39 After three years, two of Shimei's servants fled to Achish son of Maacah, king of Gath, and it was reported to Shimei, saying, "Behold, your servants are in Gath."

40 Shimei rose and saddled his ass and went to Gath to Achish to seek his servants, and Shimei went and brought his servants from Gath.

41 It was reported to Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and had returned.

42 The king sent and summoned Shimei, and said to him, Did I not make you swear by the LORD, and warn you, saying, On the day you go out and walk about, know for certain that you will surely die? And you said to me, The word is good; I have heard.

43 And why have you not kept the sworn oath of the LORD and the commandment that I commanded you?

44 The king said to Shimei, You know all the evil that your heart is aware of, which you did to David my father; and the LORD will bring your evil back upon your own head.

45 And King Solomon be blessed, and may David's throne be established before the LORD forever.

46 Then the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who went out and met him and put him to death, and the kingdom was established in Solomon's hand.


## Chapter 3

1 Solomon made a marriage alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he took Pharaoh's daughter and brought her into the city of David, until he had finished building his own house and the house of the LORD and the wall of Jerusalem all around.

2 Only the folk were sacrificing at the high places, because a temple had not been built for the name of the LORD until those days.

3 Solomon loved the Lord to walk in the statutes of David his father, only in the high places he sacrificed and made offerings.

4 The king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for it was the great high place; Solomon would offer a thousand burnt offerings on that altar.

5 At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream at night, and God said, "Ask what I shall give you."

6 Solomon said, You have shown great favour to David your servant, my father, as he walked before you in truth and in righteousness and in uprightness of heart with you, and You have kept for him this great favour, and You have given him a son to sit upon his throne, as at this day.

7 And now, O LORD my God, you have made your servant succeed David my father as king, but I am a young boy who does not know how to go out or come in.

8 And your servant, among your people whom you have chosen, a people too numerous to be counted or numbered for their abundance.

9 And give to your servant a hearing heart to judge your people, to discern between good and evil, for who is able to judge your people, this heavy burden?

10 And the thing was good in the eyes of the LORD, that Solomon had asked this thing.

11 And God said to him, Because you have asked this thing, and have not asked for yourself many days, and have not asked for yourself riches, and have not asked for yourself the life of your enemy, but have asked for yourself understanding to hear judgment,

12 Behold I have done according to your word behold I have given to you a heart of wisdom and discernment such as you none has been before you and after you none will arise like you

13 And also what you did not ask I have given you—both riches and glory—so that there has not been any man like you among the kings all your life.

14 If you walk in my ways, to keep my statutes and my commandments, as David your father walked, then I will lengthen your days.

15 Solomon awoke and behold it was a dream, and he went to Jerusalem and stood before the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and he offered burnt offerings and made peace offerings, and made a feast for all his servants.

16 Then two women who were harlots came before the king and stood in his presence.

17 Then the woman said, “Please, my lord, I and this woman dwell in one house, and I gave birth with her in the house.”

18 On the third day after I gave birth, this woman also gave birth, and we were together with no stranger in the house; only the two of us were in the house.

19 And the son of the woman died that night on which she lay upon him.

20 And she arose in the midst of the night and took my son from beside me while your female servant was asleep and laid him in her bosom and laid her dead son in my bosom.

21 I rose early in the morning to nurse my son, and there he was—dead; and I looked at him closely in the morning, and indeed, my son whom I had borne was not alive.

22 Then the other woman said, No, for my son is the living one and your son is the dead one, but the other said, No, for your son is the dead one and my son is the living one, and they spoke before the king.

23 The king said, "This one says, 'My son is the living one and your son is the dead one,' and this one says, 'No, your son is the dead one and my son is the living one.'"

24 The king said, "Bring me a sword," and they brought the sword before the king.

25 Then the king said, Cut the living child into two parts, and give half to one woman and half to the other.

26 The woman whose son was alive said to the king, for her compassion for her child was stirred, and she said, "Please, my lord, give her the living child and do not put him to death," but the other woman said, "It shall be neither mine nor yours; divide it."

27 Then the king answered and said, Give the living child to her, but the dead one do not put to death; she is his mother.

28 All Israel heard the judgment that the king had rendered, and they stood in awe of the king, for they saw that the wisdom of God was within him to do justice.


## Chapter 4

1 King Solomon was king over all Israel.

2 And these are the captains who belonged to him: Azariah son of Zadok the priest.

3 Elihoreph and Ahiah the sons of Shisha were scribes, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder.

4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the host, and Zadok and Abiathar were priests.

5 Azariah son of Nathan was over the officers, and Zabud son of Nathan was priest, the king's friend.

6 Ahishar was over the palace, and Adoniram son of Abda was over the tribute.

7 Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel, and they provided for the king and his court; each one had to provide supplies for one month of the year.

8 And these are the names of them: the son of Hur in the hill country of Ephraim.

9 The son of Dekar, Makaz, Shaalabbin, Beth-shemesh, and Elon-bethhanan.

10 The son of Hesed in Aruboth: to him belonged Shochoh and all the land of Hepher.

11 The son of Abinadab, who was in all the region of Dor, married Taphath, the daughter of Solomon, as his wife.

12 Baana son of Ahilud [was in] Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shan, which [is] beside Zarthan, below Jezreel, from Beth-shan to Abel-meholah, as far as beyond Jokmeam.

13 The son of Geber in Ramoth-gilead; to him the (small) town of Jair the son of Manasseh in Gilead; to him the region of Argob in Bashan, sixty great walled cities with bars of copper.

14 Ahinadab son of Iddo at Mahanaim.

15 Ahimaaz the Naphtalite also took Bashemath the daughter of Solomon as wife.

16 Baana son of Hushai in Asher and in Bealoth.

17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah of Issachar.

18 Shimei son of Elah of Benjamin.

19 Geber, a son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the land of Sihon king of the Amorites and Og king of Bashan, and one garrison that was in the land.

20 Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand on the sea, abundant in number, eating and drinking and rejoicing.

21 Solomon was ruling over all the kingdoms from the river, the land of the Philistines, and to the border of Egypt; they brought gifts and served Solomon all the days of his life.

22 The bread of Solomon for one day was thirty cors of fine flour and sixty cors of flour.

23 Ten beeves of the herd, and twenty beeves of the pasture, and a hundred sheep, besides the hart and the roe and the fallow deer and fattened fowl.

24 For he ruled over all the region beyond the river from Tipsah to Gaza, over all the kings of the region beyond the river, and peace was with him from all his surroundings.

25 Judah and Israel lived in assurance, each under his vine and under his fig tree, from Dan to Beer-shebah, all the days of Solomon.

26 Solomon had forty thousand stalls for horses of his chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.

27 And these officers provided for King Solomon and for all who came to the table of King Solomon, each in his month; they did not lack any thing.

28 And the barley and straw for the horses and for the swift beasts shall be brought to the place where each man is, according to his custom.

29 God gave Solomon wisdom and very great understanding, and breadth of mind like the sand on the seashore.

30 And Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of former times and all the wisdom of Egypt.

31 He was wiser than any person, than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, and Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol; and his fame was in all nations round about.

32 He spoke three thousand proverbs, and his song was a thousand and five.

33 He spoke of the trees, from the cedar in Lebanon to the hyssop that grows on the wall, and he spoke of the beasts, and of the birds, and of the creeping things, and of the fish.

34 And they came from all the peoples to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from every king of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.


## Chapter 5

1 Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon, for he had heard that they had anointed him king in place of his father, because Hiram had been a lover of David all the days.

2 Solomon sent to Hiram, saying:

3 You knew David my father, that he was not able to build a house for the name of the LORD his God because of the war which surrounded him, until the LORD had given them under the palms of his hands.

4 And now the LORD my God has given me rest all around; there is no adversary and no harmful occurrence.

5 And behold, I say to build a temple for the name of the LORD my God, as the LORD spoke to David my father, saying, “Your son whom I will set in your place upon your throne, he shall build the temple for my name.”

6 Now therefore command, and they shall cut down for me cedar trees from Lebanon, and my servants shall be with your servants, and I will give you wages for your servants, according to all that you say; for you know that there is no one among us who knows how to cut timber like the Sidonians.

7 When Hiram heard the words of Solomon, he rejoiced greatly and said, "Blessed be the LORD this day, who has given to David a wise son over this great people."

8 Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, I have heard what you sent to me; I will do for you all that you desire in timber of cedar and in timber of fir.

9 My servants will go down from Lebanon to the west and I will appoint them floats upon the sea to the place where you will send me, and I will break them there, and you will take up; and you will do my desire to give food for my household.

10 Hiram supplied Solomon with cedar wood and fir wood according to all his desire.

11 Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household and twenty cors of oil of olives; so Solomon gave to Hiram year by year.

12 The LORD gave wisdom to Solomon as He had spoken to him, and there was peace between Hiram and Solomon, and they made a covenant between them.

13 King Solomon imposed a levy on all Israel, and the levy was thirty thousand men.

14 He sent them to Lebanon: ten thousand per month in rotation; a month they would be in Lebanon, two months at home; and Adoniram was over the levy.

15 Solomon had seventy thousand burden bearers and eighty thousand mountain cutters.

16 Except the rulers who were over Solomon's work, three thousand and three hundred who ruled the people doing the work.

17 The king commanded, and they transported great costly stones, hewn stones, to lay the foundation of the temple.

18 The builders of Solomon and the builders of Hiram and the Giblites prepared the timber and the stones to build the temple.


## Chapter 6

1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the people of Israel came out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month Ziv, which is the second month, he built the temple for the LORD.

2 The temple that King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits in length, twenty cubits in breadth, and thirty cubits in height.

3 And the porch before the temple of the house was twenty cubits long according to the width of the house, and ten cubits was its breadth before the house.

4 He made the temple with shuttered windows.

5 He built a wall against the wall of the house all around, the walls of the house all around, for the temple and for the oracle, and he made side chambers all around.

6 The lower chamber was five cubits wide, the middle chamber six cubits wide, and the upper chamber seven cubits wide; for he made narrowed rests around the house on the outside, so that they would not hold to the walls of the house.

7 And the temple in its building was constructed with stone finished before it was brought, and any tool, hammer, or ax, every iron implement, was not heard in the temple in its building.

8 The entrance of the middle side chamber, at the shoulder of the house on the right, and winding stairs go up from the middle to the third.

9 And he built the temple and finished it, and he ceiled the temple with beams and boards of cedar.

10 He built a chamber for the whole temple, its height five cubits, and he attached the temple with cedar timbers.

11 The word of the LORD came to Solomon, saying:

12 This temple that you are building—if you walk in my statutes, and keep my judgments, and observe all my commandments by walking in them, then I will establish my word with you, which I spoke to David your father.

13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and I will not forsake my people Israel.

14 Solomon built the temple and finished it.

15 He built the temple walls inside with cedar planks from the floor of the temple up to the ceiling walls; he paneled the temple with wood and overlaid the temple floor with fir planks.

16 He built the temple twenty cubits long with cedar beams from the floor to the walls, and built within the temple an inner sanctuary, the most holy place.

17 And forty cubits was the temple, that is, the hall, before him.

18 And cedar for the inside of the temple, carved knops and blossoms, all cedar, no stone seen.

19 And within the temple he prepared the oracle in the innermost part, to place there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.

20 Before the oracle was twenty cubits in length, twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in height, and he overlaid it with solid gold; he also overlaid the altar of cedar.

21 Solomon overlaid the temple from inside with pure gold, and he drew chains of gold across the front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.

22 And he overlaid the entire house with gold, even the whole of the entire house; and he overlaid with gold the entire altar that belonged to the oracle.

23 He made in the oracle two cherubims of olive wood; ten cubits was their height.

24 And five cubits the wing of the first cherub, and five cubits the wing of the second cherub: ten cubits from the ends of one wing to the ends of the other wing.

25 And ten cubits the second cherub; one measure and one size for the two cherubims.

26 The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was the second cherub.

27 He set the cherubims in the midst of the inner house, and they spread forth the wings of the cherubims; one wing touched the wall, and the wing of the second cherub touched the second wall, and their wings in the midst of the house touched wing to wing.

28 And he overlaid the cherubims with gold.

29 And all the walls of the house were surrounded with carved figures of cherubs, palm trees, and blossoms, on the inner and outer parts.

30 And the bottom of the house he overlaid with gold, inside and out.

31 And for the entrance of the oracle he made two doors of olive wood, their lintels and posts being a fifth part.

32 And two doors of olive wood, and carvings on them: carved cherubim, palm trees, and blossoms, and gold overlay; and the gold extended to the cherubim and to the palm trees.

33 And so he did for the entrance of the temple: olive-wood doorposts, from the fourth part.

34 And the two doors of fir wood: the first door with two side beams and folding rings, and the second door with two hangings and folding rings.

35 And he carved cherubims and palm trees and flowers, and he overlaid gold upon the hammered work.

36 He built the inner court with three rows of hewn stone and a row of cedar beams.

37 In the fourth year the temple of the LORD was founded, in the month of Ziv.

38 In the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the temple was finished according to all its specifications and according to all that he had built, in seven years.


## Chapter 7

1 And Solomon built his palace in thirteen years, and he finished all his palace.

2 He built the forest house of Lebanon: one hundred cubits its length, fifty cubits its breadth, and thirty cubits its height, on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams upon the pillars.

3 And overlaid with cedar above on the beams that were on the pillars, forty-five, fifteen per row.

4 And windows in three rows, light opposite light in three times.

5 All the doorways and posts were square with frames, and window was opposite window in three ranks.

6 He made the porch of the pillars: its length fifty cubits and its breadth thirty cubits, and a porch before them, with pillars and a thick beam before them.

7 And the porch of the throne where he was to judge, the porch of judgment, he made, and it was covered with cedar from floor to ceiling.

8 His house in which he dwelt, the other court within from the porch, was of the same kind of work; and a house he made for Pharaoh's daughter, whom Solomon had taken, like this porch.

9 All these stones were costly, hewn to measure with the saw, both inside and outside, from the foundation to the coping, and from the outside to the great court.

10 and founded with costly stones, stones, great stones, stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits.

11 And above, precious stones like hewn stone, and cedar.

12 The great court all around had three rows of hewn stone, and a row of cedar beams, and for the inner court of the house of the LORD and for the porch of the house.

13 King Solomon sent and took Huram from Tyre.

14 He was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze; he was filled with wisdom, understanding, and knowledge to do every work in bronze, and he came to King Solomon and did all his work.

15 He cast the two pillars of bronze; the height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, and a line of twelve cubits encircled each pillar.

16 And he made two chapiters to set upon the tops of the pillars, cast in brass; five cubits was the height of the one chapiter, and five cubits was the height of the second chapiter.

17 Netted network work of wreaths, chainwork for the chapiters that were on the heads of the pillars: seven for one chapiter and seven for the second chapiter.

18 He made the pillars and two rows around the one lattice to cover the chapiters that were on the top of the pomegranates, and so he did for the second chapiter.

19 The capitals that were on the top of the pillars, the work of lilies in the porch, were four cubits.

20 And chapiters upon the two pillars, also above, at the belly which is on the other side of the network, and two hundred pomegranates in rows round about upon the second chapiter.

21 He set up the pillars for the porch of the temple, and he set up the right pillar and named its name Jachin, and he set up the left pillar and named its name Boaz.

22 And on the top of the pillars was the work of a lily; and the work of the pillars was completed.

23 He made the sea of cast metal, ten cubits from one brim to the other, round all around, and five cubits its height; a line of thirty cubits encircled it all around.

24 Knops beneath its brim all around it, ten cubits, encircling the sea all around; two rows of knops cast when it was cast.

25 Standing upon twelve beeves: three facing northward, three facing westward, three facing southward, and three facing eastward; and the sea was upon them above, and all their hinder parts were within.

26 Its thickness was a hand-breadth, and its brim was made like the brim of a lily cup; it held two thousand baths.

27 He made the ten bases of bronze; four cubits was the length of each base, and four cubits its breadth, and three cubits its height.

28 And this was the act of the base: its borders for them, and borders between the ledges.

29 On the borders between the ledges were lions, beeves, and cherubims; and on the ledges likewise above, and beneath the lions and the beeves, additions of thin work.

30 And four brasen wheels for the one base, and brasen plates; and four feet thereof with shoulders; the shoulders are cast under the laver, on the side of each addition.

31 Its mouth from the house of the chapiter and upward was one cubit, and the mouth of it round, work of a base, a cubit and a half; and also upon its mouth were carvings, and their borders square, not round.

32 And the four wheels were under the borders, and the axletrees of the wheels were in the base, and the height of one wheel was one cubit and a half cubit.

33 And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their rims and their naves and their felloes and their spokes, all of it was cast.

34 And four arms to the four corners of the one base, the arms of the base.

35 On top of the base, half a cubit high, was a rounded rim all around, and on top of the base were its handles and its borders.

36 And on the plates of its stays and on its panels he engraved cherubim, lions, and palm trees, according to the space of each, with wreaths all around.

37 Thus he made the ten bases: one casting, one measure, one size for all of them.

38 He made ten bronze lavers: each laver held forty baths and was four cubits; one laver was for one base, for the ten bases.

39 He set the bases: five on the right side of the temple and five on the left side of the temple, and he set the sea on the right side of the temple toward the east, opposite the south.

40 Hiram made the hearths, and the shovels, and the bowls; and Hiram finished all the work that he made for King Solomon in the house of the LORD.

41 Two pillars, and two bowls of the chapiters that were upon the heads of the pillars, and two checkerworks to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were upon the heads of the pillars.

42 And four hundred pomegranates for the two wreath-enworks: two rows of pomegranates for one wreath-enwork, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were upon the faces of the pillars.

43 and the ten bases, and the ten lavers upon the bases.

44 and the one sea and the twelve beeves under the sea.

45 And the caldrons, and the shovels, and the basons, and all the vessels that Hiram made for King Solomon for the house of the LORD, were of molten brass.

46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them in clay ground between Succoth and Zarthan.

47 Solomon altogether set aside all the brass vessels because of the very great abundance; the full weight of the brass was not investigated.

48 Solomon made all the vessels for the temple of the LORD: the golden altar and the table of the presence bread of gold.

49 And the five candlesticks on the right and the five on the left before the oracle, gold closed up, and the blossom and the lamps and the snuffers of gold.

50 The basins, snuffers, bowls, spoons, and firepans of pure gold, and the hinges for the doors of the inner house, the Most Holy Place, and the doors of the temple, gold.

51 All the work that King Solomon had done for the temple of the LORD was completed, and Solomon brought in the holy things of his father David, the silver and the gold and the vessels, and placed them in the treasuries of the house of the LORD.


## Chapter 8

1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel, all the tribal leaders, the heads of the fathers of the children of Israel, to King Solomon in Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the city of David, that is, Zion.

2 All the men of Israel gathered to King Solomon in the month of Ethanim, at the feast—this was the seventh month.

3 Then all the elders of Israel came, and the priests lifted up the ark.

4 They brought up the ark of the LORD and the tent of meeting and all the vessels of the sanctuary that were in the tent; the priests and the Levites brought them up.

5 And King Solomon, and all the assembly of Israel that were assembled to him, were with him before the ark, sacrificing sheep and oxen, which could not be counted nor numbered for the abundance.

6 The priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place, into the oracle of the temple, the most holy sanctuary, beneath the wings of the cherubim.

7 For the cherubims spread their wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubims covered the ark and its bars above.

8 The bars were long, and the ends of the bars were seen from the holy place in front of the oracle, but the bars were not seen outside, and they have been there until this day.

9 There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed there at Horeb, which the LORD made with the children of Israel when they came out of the land of Egypt.

10 And when the priests went out from the sanctuary, the cloud filled the house of the LORD.

11 And the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud, for the glory of the LORD filled the house of the LORD.

12 Then Solomon said, "Jehovah has said that he will dwell in the thick darkness."

13 I have built you a princely house, a dwelling place for your habitation forever.

14 The king turned his face and blessed all the assembly of Israel, and all the assembly of Israel was standing.

15 Blessed be Jehovah, the Lord God of Israel, who spoke with his mouth to David my father and with his hand fulfilled to say.

16 From the age when I brought my people Israel out of Egypt, I did not choose a city from all the tribes of Israel to build a house for my name to be there; but I chose David to be over my people Israel.

17 And it came to pass that David my father had it in mind to build a house for the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.

18 And Jehovah said to David my father, Because it was with your mind to build a temple for my name, you have done well, for it was with your mind.

19 Only you shall not build the temple, but your son who comes from your loins—he shall build the temple for my name.

20 The LORD fulfilled his word that he spoke, and I have risen in place of David my father and sit on the throne of Israel, as the LORD promised, and I have built the temple for the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.

21 And I have placed there a place for the ark, where is the covenant of the LORD which he made with our fathers when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.

22 Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD opposite all the assembly of Israel and spread his hands toward the heavens.

23 The Lord God of Israel said: There is no god like you in the heavens above or on the earth beneath, keeping covenant and steadfast love with your servants who walk before you with all their heart.

24 which you have kept for your servant David, my father, what you spoke to him, and with your mouth and with your hand you have fulfilled this day.

25 And now, O LORD God of Israel, keep for Your servant David my father the promise You made him, saying, "There shall never fail you a man before Me to sit upon the throne of Israel, if only your sons take care to walk in My ways, as you have walked before Me."

26 And now, O God of Israel, let what you have said to your servant David my father be confirmed.

27 Indeed, does God dwell on the earth? Behold, the heavens and the heavens of the heavens cannot contain you, how much less this temple that I have built.

28 And you will turn to the prayer of your servant and to his supplication, O LORD my God, to hear the cry and the prayer that your servant prays before you this day.

29 Keep your eyes open toward this house night and day, toward the place of which you said, “My name will be there,” to listen to the prayer that your servant prays toward this place.

30 And you shall hear the supplication of your servant and of your people Israel, when they pray toward this place, and you yourself shall hear from the place of your enthronement, from the heavens, and you shall hear and forgive.

31 Whatever a man sins against his neighbor and an oath is laid upon him to make him swear, and he comes with that oath before your altar in this house.

32 But you, hear from the heavens and act and judge your servants, to declare the wicked guilty, to place his way upon his own head, and to declare the righteous one righteous, to give to him according to his righteousness.

33 When your people Israel are struck down before an enemy because they have sinned against you, and they return to you and acknowledge your name and pray and plead with you in this temple,

34 But you, you will hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel and restore them to the land that you gave to their fathers.

35 When the heavens are shut and there is no rain because they have sinned against you, and they pray toward this place and confess your name and return from their sin because you afflict them,

36 But you, O heavens, will hear and pardon the purifying of your bond-servants and your people Israel, for you will teach them the good way in which they should walk, and you will give rain upon your land that you gave to your people as an inheritance.

37 If there is dearth in the land, if there is pestilence, blasting, yellowing, locust, caterpillar; if his enemy lays siege to him in the gates of his land, every plague, every sickness;

38 Every prayer, every supplication, of any person, of all your people Israel, each knowing the plague of his own heart, and spreading his hands toward this house.

39 But you, O LORD, hear from the heavens, the place of your dwelling; forgive, act, and render to each person according to all his ways, for you alone know the heart of every human being.

40 so that they may fear you all the days that they live upon the face of the land which you gave to our fathers

41 Even to the foreigner who is not of your people Israel, but comes from a distant land because of your name.

42 When they hear of your great fame, your strong hand, and your outstretched arm, and come and pray toward this temple.

43 You will hear from heaven, the place of your dwelling, and act according to all that the foreigner calls to you, so that all the peoples of the earth may know your name to fear you, like your people Israel, and may know that your name is called upon this house that I have built.

44 When your people go out to war against their enemies along the way that you will send them, they shall pray to the LORD toward the city that you have chosen and the temple that I have built for your name.

45 And the heavens will hear their prayer and their supplication, and you will execute their judgment.

46 When they sin against you—for there is no person who does not sin—and you are angry with them and give them into the hand of the enemy, and their captors take them to an enemy land, far or near—

47 They will consider in their heart in the land where they were exiled, and they will return and plead to you in the land of their captivity, saying, We have sinned, we have acted wickedly, we have done wrong.

48 And they shall return to you with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies, where they were taken captive, and they shall pray to you toward the land which you gave to their fathers, the city which you chose, and the house which you built and named for your name.

49 And the heavens, the place of your dwelling, shall hear their prayer and their supplication, and you shall execute their judgment.

50 And you shall forgive your people who have sinned against you, and all their rebellions with which they have rebelled against you, and you shall grant them compassion before those who took them captive, so that they may have mercy on them.

51 For they are your people and your possession, whom you brought out of Egypt, from the midst of the furnace of iron.

52 May your eyes be open to the supplication of your servant and to the supplication of your people Israel, to hear them whenever they call to you.

53 For you have set them apart as your inheritance from all the peoples of the land, as you spoke by the hand of Moses your servant when you brought our fathers out of Egypt, O Lord God.

54 When Solomon finished praying to the Lord with all this prayer and supplication, he rose from before the altar of the Lord, from kneeling on his knees with his hands spread toward the heavens.

55 And he stood and blessed all the congregation of Israel with a loud voice, saying:

56 Blessed be the LORD who has given rest to his people Israel according to all that he spoke; not one word has failed of all the good promise that he spoke by the hand of Moses his servant.

57 May the LORD our God be with us as He was with our fathers; may He not leave us nor forsake us.

58 to turn our heart unto him, to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, his statutes, and his judgments which he commanded our fathers.

59 Let these words which I have pleaded before the LORD be near to the LORD our God day and night, to render judgment for his servant and for his people Israel, the duty of each day in its day.

60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God; there is no other.

61 And your bethink themselves shall be whole with the LORD our God, to walk in his statutes and to keep his commandments, as at this day.

62 And the king and all Israel with him sacrificed an offering before the LORD.

63 Solomon offered the peace offering that he sacrificed to the LORD: beeves twenty-two thousand and sheep one hundred and twenty thousand, and the king and all the children of Israel dedicated the house of the LORD.

64 On that day the king consecrated the middle of the court which was before the house of the LORD, because he made there the burnt offering and the grain offering and the fat of the peace offerings, since the bronze altar which was before the LORD was too small to contain the burnt offering and the grain offering and the fat of the peace offerings.

65 Solomon kept the feast at that time, and all Israel with him, a great assembly, from the entrance of Hamath to the brook of Egypt, before the LORD our God, seven days and seven days, fourteen days.

66 On the eighth day he sent the people away, and they blessed the king, and went to their tents joyful and good of heart for all the good that the LORD had done for David his servant and for Israel his people.


## Chapter 9

1 And it happened when Solomon had finished building the temple of the LORD and the king's palace and all the desire that Solomon desired to do.

2 Then the LORD appeared to Solomon a second time, as he had appeared to him at Gibeon.

3 The LORD said to him, I have heard your prayer and your supplication that you made before me. I have consecrated this temple that you built to put my name there forever, and my eyes and my heart will be there all the days.

4 And you, if you walk before me as David your father walked, with integrity of heart and in uprightness, to do according to all that I have commanded you, my statutes and my judgments, you shall keep.

5 And I will establish the throne of your kingdom over Israel forever, as I promised to David your father, saying, "There shall not be cut off from you a man over the throne of Israel."

6 If you indeed turn back, you and your children, from following me and do not keep my commandments and my statutes which I have set before you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them,

7 I will cut off Israel from upon the face of the land that I gave to them, and I will send away the temple that I consecrated for my name from my presence, and Israel will become a byword and a taunt among all the nations.

8 And this temple shall be exalted; everyone passing by it shall be appalled and shall whistle, and they shall say, "Why has the Lord done thus to this land and to this temple?"

9 And they will say, Because they have forsaken Jehovah their God, who brought their fathers out of the land of Egypt, and have held fast to other gods, and bowed down to them and served them; therefore Jehovah has brought upon them all this evil.

10 After twenty years, during which Solomon built the two houses, the house of the LORD and the house of the king.

11 Hiram king of Tyre went up to help Solomon with cedar wood and pine wood and gold for all his desire; then King Solomon gave to Hiram twenty cities in the land of Galilee.

12 Hiram came from Tyre to see the towns that Solomon had given him, and they were not pleasing in his eyes.

13 And he said, “What are these cities that you have given me, my brother?” and he called them the land of Cabul to this day.

14 Hiram sent to the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold.

15 And this is the account of the levy that King Solomon raised to build the house of the LORD and his own house and the Millo and the wall of Jerusalem and Hazor and Megiddo and Gazer.

16 Pharaoh king of Egypt went up and captured Gazer and burned it with fire and struck down the Canaanite who dwelt in the city and gave it as presents to his daughter wife of Solomon.

17 Solomon built Gezer and Beth-horon lower.

18 And Baalath, and Tamar in the wilderness, in the land.

19 and all the cities of storehouses that belonged to Solomon and the cities of the chariots and the cities of the horsemen and the desire of Solomon that he desired to build in Jerusalem and in Lebanon and in all the land of his dominion
