20 All the people remaining from the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite, who were not of the children of Israel.

21 The sons of them that were left after them in the land, whom the sons of Israel could not utterly destroy, Solomon did lift up for a levy of slave labor to this day.

22 Solomon gave no Israelite to be a bondservant, for they were men of the war, and his servants, and his captains, and his commanders, and captains of his chariots, and his horsemen.

23 These are the captains who were over the work for Solomon: five hundred and fifty, ruling over the people who labored in the work.

24 But Pharaoh's daughter went up from the city of David to her house which he had built for her, then he built the Millo.

25 Solomon offered burnt offerings and peace offerings three times a year on the altar that he built for the LORD, and he burned incense with them before the presence of the LORD, and he completed the temple.

26 Solomon made a galley navy at Ezion-geber, which is by Elath on the shore of the Red Sea in the land of Edom.

27 Hiram sent by the galley his servants, shipmen who knew the sea, with the servants of Solomon.

28 They came to Ophir and took from there gold, four hundred and twenty talents, and brought it to King Solomon.


## Chapter 10

1 And the queen of Sheba heard the fame of Solomon by the name of the LORD and came to test him with riddles.

2 And she came to Jerusalem with a very great retinue, camels bearing spices and much gold and precious stones, and she spoke to him all that was on her mind.

3 Solomon told her all his matters; there was no word hidden from the king that he did not tell her.

4 The queen of Sheba saw all the wisdom of Solomon, and the house that he built.

5 And the food of his table, and the dwelling of his servants, and his attendance and the apparel of them, and his drink, and his burnt offering that he would offer in the house of the LORD; and there was no spirit in her anymore.

6 And she said to the king, "The report that I heard in my land about your words and your wisdom was true."

7 I did not believe the reports until I came and my eyes saw, and indeed, half was not told me: you have added wisdom and goodness to the report that I heard.

8 Blessed are your men, blessed are your servants, those who continually stand before you, who hear your wisdom.

9 May the LORD your God be blessed who has delighted in you to set you upon the throne of Israel forever because of the LORD’s love for Israel and has made you king to execute justice and righteousness.

10 And she gave the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold, and a very great abundance of spices, and precious stones; never again did such an abundance of spices come in great quantity as that which the queen of Sheba gave to king Solomon.

11 And Huram also, the galley, who brought gold from Ophir, brought from Ophir very much timber of almug trees and precious stone.

12 The king made the almug trees into pillars for the house of the LORD and for the house of the king, and harps and psalteries for the singers; such almug trees had not come and had not been seen until this day.

13 And King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire which she asked, besides what Solomon gave her according to the king's hand; and she turned and went to her land, she and her servants.

14 The weight of the gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold.

15 apart from the men of the Tarim and the traders of traffic and all the kings of Arabia and the governors of the land

16 King Solomon made two hundred shields of beaten gold; six hundred [shekels] of gold went up for each shield.

17 And three hundred shields of beaten gold, three pounds of gold going up for each shield, and the king put them in the house of the forest of Lebanon.

18 The king made a great ivory throne and overlaid it with fine gold.

19 Six steps led up to the throne, and a rounded top was above the throne at its back, with arms on either side of the throne’s seat, and two lions were standing beside the arms.

20 And twelve young lions were standing there on the six steps, six on this side and six on that side; nothing like this was made for any kingdom.

21 All the drinking vessels of King Solomon were gold, and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were gold; silver was not counted for anything in the days of Solomon.

22 For my navy is Tarshish for the king, with the navy of Hiram; every three years a navy of Tarshish comes, bearing gold and silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks.

23 King Solomon became greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.

24 And all the earth sought the face of Solomon to hear his wisdom that God had given in his heart.

25 And they brought every man his gift, vessels of silver and vessels of gold and garments and weapons and spices, horses and mules, year by year.

26 Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen, and there were for him one thousand and four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen, and he stationed them in the chariot towns and with the king in Jerusalem.

27 The king gave the silver in Jerusalem like stones, and he gave the cedars like sycamores in the low country in abundance.

28 The going forth of the horses that belonged to Solomon was from Egypt, and the king's merchants received a drove at a price.

29 And she brought out a chariot from Egypt for six hundred silver-shekels and a horse for a hundred and fifty, and thus to all the kings of the Hittites and to the kings of Aram, by their hand they brought them out.


## Chapter 11

1 King Solomon loved many foreign women, including Pharaoh’s daughter, Moabite women, Ammonite women, Edomite women, Sidonian women, and Hittite women.

2 From the Gentiles, of whom the LORD said to the sons of Israel, “You shall not come among them, and they shall not come among you,” indeed they turned your hearts after their gods; among them did Solomon cling in love.

3 He had wives, princesses, seven hundred, and concubines, three hundred, and his wives turned his heart away.

4 And it came to pass at the time of Solomon's old age, his wives turned his heart after other gods; and his heart was not whole with the LORD his God, as was the heart of David his father.

5 Solomon went after Ashtoreth the gods of the Sidonians and after Milcom the abominable filth of the Ammonites.

6 Solomon did the evil thing in the eyes of the LORD, and he did not fully follow the LORD as David his father did.

7 Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh the abominable filth of Moab on the hill opposite Jerusalem and for Molech the abominable filth of the Ammonites.

8 And he did likewise for all his foreign wives, who made offerings and sacrifices to their gods.

9 The LORD was angry with Solomon because his heart turned away from the LORD, the God of Israel, who had appeared to him twice.

10 and he commanded him concerning this act not to go after other gods, but he did not keep what the LORD commanded.

11 And the LORD said to Solomon, Because this has been with you, and you have not kept my covenant and my statutes which I commanded you, I will surely tear the kingdom from you and give it to your servant.

12 But in your lifetime I will not do it, for the sake of David your father; from the hand of your son I will tear it away.

13 Only the kingdom as a whole I will not tear apart; one tribe I will give to your son, for David my servant's sake, and for Jerusalem which I have chosen.

14 The Lord raised up an adversary against Solomon: Hadad the Edomite, of the king's seed in Edom.

15 And it came to pass, when David was in Edom, that Joab the captain of the host went up to bury the slain, and he struck every male in Edom.

16 For six months Joab and all Israel remained there until they had cut off every male in Edom.

17 Hadad fled, and certain Edomite men, servants of his father, with him to go to Egypt, and Hadad was a young boy.

18 They departed from Midian and came to Paran, and they took men with them from Paran and came to Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he gave him a house and food and spoke to him, and land he gave him.

19 Hadad found favour in the eyes of Pharaoh exceedingly, and he gave him a wife, the sister of Pharaoh's wife, the sister of Tahpenes the queen.

20 An other sister of Tahpenes bore Genubath, her son, and Tahpenes weaned him in the house of Pharaoh, and Genubath was in Pharaoh's house among the sons of Pharaoh.

21 When Hadad heard in Egypt that David had lain down with his fathers and that Joab the captain of the host was dead, Hadad said to Pharaoh, "Send me away, that I may go to my own land."

22 Pharaoh said to him, "What do you lack with me, that you are requesting to go to your country?" And he said, "No, but send, you must send me."

23 God raised up an adversary against him, Rezon the son of Eliada, who had fled from Hadadezer king of Zobah, his lord.

24 And he gathered men to him and became captain of a band when David killed them, and they went to Damascus and dwelt there, and they reigned in Damascus.

25 And there was an adversary against Israel all the days of Solomon, and Hadad caused adversity in Israel, and he reigned over Aram.

26 Jeroboam the son of Nebat, an Ephrathite from Zereda, whose mother's name was Zeruah, a widow woman, a servant of Solomon, and he lifted up his hand against the king.

27 This is the act that Solomon undertook by royal authority: he built the Millo, closed the breach in the city of David his father.

28 And the man Jeroboam, a valiant man of strength, and Solomon saw the young man, that he was industrious in his work, and he appointed him to oversee all the burden of the house of Joseph.

29 At that time Jeroboam went out from Jerusalem, and the prophet Ahiah the Shilonite found him on the way, and he was wrapped in a new garment, and the two of them were alone in the field.

30 Ahijah seized the garment that was on him, the new one, and tore it into twelve rags.

31 He said to Jeroboam, "Take for yourself ten pieces, for thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, 'Behold, I am tearing the kingdom from the hand of Solomon and will give to you ten tribes.'"

32 And the one tribe shall belong to him, for the sake of my servant David and for the sake of Jerusalem, the city that I chose out of all the tribes of Israel.

33 Because they have forsaken me and bowed down to Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians, to Chemosh the god of Moab, and to Milcom the god of the Ammonites, and have not walked in my ways to do what is right in my eyes and my statutes and my judgments as David my father did.

34 But I will not take the entire kingdom from his hand, for he shall be prince all the days of his life, for the sake of David my servant, whom I have chosen, who kept my commandments and my statutes.

35 I will take the kingship from his son’s hand and give it to you—the ten tribes.

36 And to his son I will give one tribe, that My lamp may burn before David My servant continually in Jerusalem, the city that I have chosen for Myself, to place My name there.

37 And you, I will take, and you shall reign over all that your soul desires, and you shall be king over Israel.

38 And it shall be, if you listen to all that I command and walk in my ways and do what is right in my eyes, to keep my statutes and my commandments as my servant David did, then I will be with you and build you a faithful house as I built for David, and I will give you Israel.

39 And I will afflict the seed of David for this, but not forever.

40 Solomon sought to kill Jeroboam, and Jeroboam arose and fled to Egypt to Shishak king of Egypt, and he was in Egypt until the death of Solomon.

41 And the rest of the acts of Solomon and all that he did and his wisdom, are they not written in the book of the acts of Solomon?

42 The years that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel were forty years.

43 Solomon slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of David his father, and Rehoboam his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 12

1 Rehoboam went to Shechem, for in Shechem all Israel had come to make him king.

2 When Jeroboam the son of Nebat heard, while he was still in Egypt, where he had fled from the presence of King Solomon, then Jeroboam remained in Egypt.

3 And they sent and called for him—Jeroboam and all the assembly of Israel—and they spoke to Rehoboam, saying:

4 Your father has made our yoke heavy; now you, lighten the hard service and the heavy yoke your father placed upon us, and we will serve you.

5 He said to them, Go yet three days and return to me. And the people went.

6 Then King Rehoboam consulted the elders who had stood before Solomon his father while he was still alive, saying, How do you advise me to respond to this people with a word?

7 To him, saying, If today you will be a servant to this people, and serve them and answer them and speak to them good words, then they will be your servants all the days.

8 And he abandoned the counsel of the elders who advised him, and consulted with the young men who grew up with him, those who stood before him.

9 He said to them, "What advice do you give? We must give an answer to this people, who said to me, 'Lighten the yoke that your father laid on us.'"

10 The young men who had grown up with him spoke to him, saying, “Thus you shall say to the people who have spoken to you, ‘Your father made our yoke heavy; now you, lighten it from upon us’—thus you shall speak to them: ‘My little finger is thicker than my father’s loins.’”

11 Now my father laid a heavy yoke on you, and I will add to your yoke; my father disciplined you with whips, but I will discipline you with scorpions.

12 Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day, as the king had directed, saying, “Return to me on the third day.”

13 And the king answered the folk harshly, and he abandoned the counsel that the elders had advised him.

14 He spoke to them according to the advice of the young men, saying, My father made your yoke heavy, and I will add to your yoke; my father disciplined you with whips, but I will discipline you with scorpions.

15 And the king did not listen to the people, for the cause was from the LORD, in order to establish his word which the LORD spoke by the hand of Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam son of Nebat.

16 All Israel saw that the king did not listen to them, and the people answered the king, saying, “What portion do we have in David, and no inheritance in the son of Jesse? To your tents, O Israel! Now see, David, to your house!” So Israel departed to their tents.

17 And the sons of Israel dwelling in the towns of Judah were ruled over by Rehoboam.

18 Then King Rehoboam sent Adoram, who was over the levy, and all Israel stoned him with a stone, so that he died; then King Rehoboam strengthened himself to mount the chariot to flee to Jerusalem.

19 Israel has transgressed against the house of David to this day.

20 When all Israel heard that Jeroboam had returned, they sent and called him to the assembly, and they made him king over all Israel, and no one followed the house of David except the tribe of Judah alone.

21 Rehoboam gathered in Jerusalem all the house of Judah and the tribe of Benjamin, one hundred and eighty thousand chosen young men who made war, to fight against the house of Israel, to restore the kingship to Rehoboam son of Solomon.

22 The word of God came to Shemaiah, the man of God, saying:

23 Say to Rehoboam son of Solomon king of Judah and to all the house of Judah and Benjamin and the rest of the people, saying:

24 Thus says the LORD, Do not go up and do not fight against your brothers the children of Israel; return each man to his house, for from me this word has come; and they listened to the word of the LORD and returned from going according to the word of the LORD.

25 And Jeroboam built Shechem on the hill country of Ephraim and lived there; and he went out from there and built Peniel.

26 Then Jeroboam considered in his mind, Now the kingdom will return to the house of David.

27 If this people goes up to offer sacrifices in the temple of the LORD at Jerusalem, then the heart of this people will turn back to their lords, to Rehoboam king of Judah, and they will kill me and return to Rehoboam king of Judah.

28 Then the king took counsel and made two golden calves and said to them, It is too much for you to go up to Jerusalem; behold, your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.

29 And he set one in Beth-el and the other he set in Dan.

30 And this act became a sin, and the people went to worship before the one, as far as Dan.

31 He made a court of high places and appointed priests from the edges of the people who were not from the sons of Levi.

32 Jeroboam observed a solemn feast on the fifteenth day of the eighth month, like the feast in Judah, and he went up to the altar at Beth-el to sacrifice to the bullocks he had made, and he stationed at Beth-el the priests of the high places whom he had appointed.

33 He went up to the altar that he had made in Beth-el on the fifteenth day of the eighth month, in the month that he had devised, and he made a feast for the children of Israel, and he went up to the altar to burn incense.


## Chapter 13

1 And behold, a man of God came from Judah by the word of the LORD to Beth-el, and Jeroboam was standing by the altar to burn incense.

2 He called upon the altar by the word of the LORD and said, O altar, altar, thus says the LORD, Behold, a son shall be born to the house of David, Josiah is his name, and he shall sacrifice upon you the priests of the high places who make offerings upon you, and human bones shall be burned upon you.

3 And on that day he shall give a sign, saying, This is the miracle that the LORD spoke: behold, the altar is split apart and the ashes that are upon it are poured out.

4 When the king heard the word of the man of God who had cried out against the altar at Beth-el, Jeroboam stretched out his hand from upon the altar, saying, "Seize him!" But his hand that he had stretched out against him dried up, and he could not draw it back to himself.

5 And the altar was torn apart and the ashes were poured out from the altar like the miracle that the man of God had given by the word of the LORD.

6 The king answered and said to the man of God, “Please intercede before the LORD your God and pray for me, that the hand of the LORD may turn from me.” So the man of God interceded before the LORD, and the hand of the king returned to him, and it was as at first.

7 The king said to the man of God, "Come home with me and refresh yourself, and I will give you a gift."

8 The man of God said to the king, "If you give me half of your household, I will not go with you, nor will I eat food or drink water in this place."

9 For so the LORD has commanded me by word, saying, You shall not eat bread or drink water, and you shall not return by the way that you came.

10 And he went along another way and did not return by the way by which he came to Beth-el.

11 And an old prophet was dwelling in Beth-el and his son came and told him all the things that the man of God had done that day in Beth-el, the words that he had spoken to the king, and they told them to their father.

12 Their father spoke to them, "Which way did he go?" And his sons saw the way that the man of God who had come from Judah had walked.

13 And he said to his sons, Bind me the ass; and they bound him the ass, and he rode on it.

14 He went after the man of God and found him sitting under the elm, and he said to him, "Are you the man of God who came from Judah?" And he said, "I am."

15 He said to him, "Go with me to the house and eat bread."

16 He said, I am not able to return with you and come with you, and I will not eat bread or drink water with you in this place.

17 For a word came to me by the word of the LORD: do not eat bread or drink water there, and do not return by the way you came.

18 He said to him, "I too am a prophet like you, and an angel spoke to me by the word of the LORD, saying, 'Bring him back with you to your house, and he will eat bread and drink water'; but you have lied to him."

19 And he returned with him, and he ate bread in his house, and he drank water.

20 They were sitting at the table when the word of the LORD came to the prophet who had brought him back.

21 So the LORD said to the man of God who came from Judah, "Because you have disobeyed the word of the LORD and have not kept the commandment which the LORD your God commanded you,"

22 So she returned and ate bread and drank water in the place where he had spoken to you, “Do not eat bread or drink water,” lest your dead body come to the grave of your fathers.

23 And it came to pass after he had eaten bread and after he had drunk, that he saddled the ass for the prophet whom he had brought back.

24 And he went and a (young) lion found him along the way and killed him, and his (dead) body was lying thrown along the way, and the (he) ass was standing beside it, and the (young) lion was standing beside the (dead) body.

25 And behold, men passing by saw the dead body cast along the way and the young lion standing beside the dead body, and they came and told in the city where the old prophet dwelt.

26 The prophet heard, who had turned him from the way, and said, "It is the man of God who disobeyed the mouth of the LORD; and the LORD delivered him to the lion, and the lion tore him and killed him, according to the word of the LORD which he spoke to him."

27 And he spoke to his sons, saying, Saddle me the ass; and they saddled it.

28 And he went and found his dead donkey thrown along the way, and the lion and the ass standing beside the carcass: the lion had not eaten the carcass, nor had it broken the ass.

29 And the prophet took up the dead body of the man of God and laid it on the donkey and brought it back, and he came to the city of the old prophet to mourn and to bury him.

30 And he laid his dead body in his grave, and they lamented over him, saying, "Alas, my brother!"

31 And it came to pass after they buried him, that he said to his sons, saying, When I die, you shall bury me in the grave where the man of God is buried; beside his bones you shall lay my bones.

32 For the act that he proclaimed by the word of the LORD concerning the altar at Beth-el and concerning all the houses of the high places that are in the towns of Samaria will surely come to pass.

33 After this act Jeroboam did not turn from his evil way, but again he made from the extremities of the people priests of high places; whoever was willing filled his hand, and became priests of high places.

34 And it came to pass in this act that it became a sin offering for the house of Jeroboam, and for destruction and to be blotted out from upon the face of the land.


## Chapter 14

1 At that time Abijah son of Jeroboam became ill.

2 Then Jeroboam said to his wife, "Arise now, and disguise yourself so that no one will know that you are the wife of Jeroboam, and go to Shiloh. Behold, there is Ahijah the prophet; he it was who spoke concerning me to become king over this people."

3 Take in your hand ten loaves of bread, some cracknels, a cruse of honey, and go to him; he will tell you what will happen to the boy.

4 And Jeroboam's wife did so: she arose and went to Shiloh and came to the house of Ahijah. Now Ahijah could not see, for his eyes were dim from old age.

5 And the LORD said to Ahijah, Behold, the wife of Jeroboam comes to inquire of you concerning her son, for he is sick; thus and thus you shall speak to her, and when she comes, she will be disguised.

6 When Ahijah heard the sound of her footsteps entering at the door, he said, "Come in, wife of Jeroboam; why do you come disguised? I am sent to you with a hard message."

7 Go, say to Jeroboam, Thus says the LORD, God of Israel: Because I raised you up from among the people and made you leader over my people Israel,

8 I tore the kingdom from the house of David and gave it to you, but you were not like my servant David, who kept my ordinances and walked after me with all his heart, to do only what was right in my eyes.

9 You have done evil in all that was before you, and you have gone and made for yourself other gods and molten images to anger me, and you have cast me behind your back.

10 Therefore, behold, I will bring adversity upon the house of Jeroboam, and I will cut off from Jeroboam every one who urinates against the wall, both the restrained and the abandoned in Israel; and I will burn up the house of Jeroboam after the manner in which dung is burned, until it is consumed.

11 The one who dies to Jeroboam in the city, the dogs will eat; and the one who dies in the field, the birds of the air will eat, for the LORD has spoken.

12 But you, rise up, go to your home; when your feet enter the city, the child will die.

13 And all Israel mourned for him and buried him, for only he of the house of Jeroboam would come to the grave, because there was found in him a good thing toward the LORD, the God of Israel.

14 And the LORD will raise up a king over Israel who will cut off the house of Jeroboam this day, and indeed, what is more, now.

15 And the LORD will strike Israel as the reed is shaken in the water, and he will uproot Israel from upon this good land that he gave to their fathers, and he will scatter them beyond the river, because they have made their groves, provoking the LORD.

16 And he gave over Israel because of the sins of Jeroboam which he sinned and by which he caused Israel to sin.

17 Then the wife of Jeroboam arose and went and came to Tirzah; she came to the threshold of the house and the young man died.

18 And they buried him, and all Israel mourned for him, according to the word of the LORD which he spoke by the hand of his servant Ahijah the prophet.

19 And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam which he did and how he reigned, behold they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

20 The time that Jeroboam reigned was twenty-two years, and he lay down with his fathers, and Nadab his son reigned in his place.

21 Rehoboam son of Solomon reigned in Judah. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city that the LORD chose to establish his name there out of all the tribes of Israel. The name of his mother was Naamah the Ammonitess.

22 Judah did evil in the sight of the LORD and provoked him to jealousy more than all that their fathers had done by their sins which they had sinned.

23 They also built for themselves high places and standing images and groves on every high hill and under every green tree.

24 And also there was a sodomite in the land, and they committed all the abominations of the nations whom the LORD drove out from before the children of Israel.

25 In the fifth year of King Rehoboam, King Shishak of Egypt went up against Jerusalem.

26 He took the treasures of the house of the LORD and the treasures of the house of the king, and he took all; he also took all the golden shields that Solomon had made.

27 King Rehoboam made bronze shields in place of them, and committed them to the hand of the captains of the runners who kept the entrance of the king's palace.

28 Whenever the king entered the house of the LORD, the runners would take them up and return them to the little chamber.

29 And the rest of the acts of Rehoboam, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

30 And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days.

31 Rehoboam lay down with his forefathers and was buried with his forefathers in the city of David, and the name of his mother was Naamah the Ammonitess, and Abijam his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 15

1 In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam son of Nebat, Abijam became king over Judah.

2 Three years he reigned in Jerusalem and the name of his mother was Maachah daughter of Abishalom.

3 He walked in all the sins his father had done before him, and his heart was not whole with the LORD his God, as the heart of David his father.

4 For David's sake, the LORD his God gave him a lamp in Jerusalem, to raise up his son after him, and to establish Jerusalem.

5 David did what was right in the eyes of the LORD and did not turn aside from all that he commanded him all the days of his life, except in the matter of Uriah the Hittite.

6 And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days of his life.

7 And the rest of the acts of Abijam and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah? And there was war between Abijam and Jeroboam.

8 Abijam slept with his forefathers and they buried him in the city of David, and Asa his son reigned in his place.

9 In the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Asa became king of Judah.

10 And forty-one year he reigned in Jerusalem and the name of his mother Maachah daughter of Abishalom.

11 Asa did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, like David his father.

12 He removed the shrine from the land and took away all the idols that his ancestors had made.

13 And also Maachah, his mother, he removed from being queen because she had made an idol for the grove; and Asa cut down her idol and burned it in the brook Kidron.

14 The high places were not removed, but Asa's heart was whole with the LORD all his days.

15 He brought the consecrated things of his father into the house of the LORD: silver, gold, and vessels.

16 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.

17 Then Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and built Ramah, so that no one might go out or come in to Asa king of Judah.

18 Asa took all the silver and gold that remained in the treasuries of the house of the LORD and in the treasuries of the king’s house, and he gave them into the hand of his servants; and King Asa sent them by the hand of his servants to Benhadad son of Tabrimmon son of Hezion, king of Aram who lived in Damascus, saying:

19 My confederacy and your confederacy, between my father and between your father: behold, I have sent to you a bribe of silver and gold; go, annul your covenant with Baasha king of Israel, that he may withdraw from me.

20 Benhadad listened to King Asa and sent the captains of his forces against the cities of Israel, and he struck Ijon, Dan, Abel-beth-maachah, and all the towns of Chinnereth throughout the whole land of Naphtali.

21 When Baasha heard, he stopped building Ramah and remained in Tirzah.

22 And King Asa called out to all Judah, no one exempt, and they carried off the stones of Ramah and its timber that Baasha had used to build with, and King Asa built with them Geba of Benjamin and Mizpah.

23 And the rest of Asa's acts and all his might and all that he did and the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah? Only in the time of his old age he was diseased in his feet.

24 Asa slept with his forefathers and was buried with his forefathers in the city of David his father, and Jehoshaphat his son reigned after him.

25 And Nadab son of Jeroboam became king over Israel in the second year of Asa king of Judah, and he reigned over Israel two years.

26 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and walked in the way of his father and in his sin which he made Israel to sin.

27 Baasha the son of Ahijah, of the house of Issachar, conspired against him; and Baasha struck him at Gibbethon, which belonged to the Philistines, while Nadab and all Israel were besieging Gibbethon.

28 Baasha killed him in the third year of Asa king of Judah and reigned in his place.

29 When he became king, he struck down all the household of Jeroboam; he left no living person to Jeroboam until he destroyed him, according to the word of the LORD which he spoke by the hand of his servant Ahijah the Shilonite.

30 Because of the sins of Jeroboam which he sinned and with which he caused Israel to sin, in the anger with which he provoked the LORD God of Israel.

31 And the rest of the acts of Nadab, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

32 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.

33 In the third year of Asa king of Judah, Baasha son of Ahijah began to reign over all Israel in Tirzah for twenty-four years.

34 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and walked in the way of Jeroboam and in his sin which he caused Israel to commit.


## Chapter 16

1 The word of the LORD came to Jehu son of Hanani against Baasha, saying:

2 Because I raised you from the dust and made you leader over my people Israel, and you walked in the way of Jeroboam and caused my people Israel to sin, provoking me with their sins.

3 I am about to destroy Baasha and his family, and I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat.

4 The one who dies of Baasha in the city, the dogs will eat; and the one who dies of him in the field, the birds of the air will eat.

5 And the rest of the acts of Baasha and the might he displayed, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

6 Baasha slept with his fathers and was buried in Tirzah, and Elah his son reigned in his place.

7 And also by the hand of Jehu son of Hanani the prophet, the word of the LORD came against Baasha and against his house, and on account of all the evil that he did in the eyes of the LORD to provoke him to anger with the works of his hands, to be like the house of Jeroboam, and because he struck him.

8 In the twenty-sixth year of Asa king of Judah, Elah son of Baasha began to reign over Israel in Tirzah, two years.

9 And his servant Zimri, captain of half the chariots, conspired against him while he was in Tirzah drinking wine in the house of Arza, who was steward of the palace in Tirzah.

10 Then Zimri came and struck him and killed him in the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah, and he reigned in his place.

11 When he reigned, as he sat on his throne, he struck all the house of Baasha; he left not to him one who urinates against a wall, nor his relatives, nor his friends.

12 Zimri destroyed all the household of Baasha according to the word of the LORD which he spoke against Baasha by the hand of Jehu the prophet.

13 For all the sins of Baasha and the sins of Elah his son, which they sinned and with which they caused Israel to sin, provoking the LORD, the God of Israel, to anger with their vanities.

14 And the rest of the acts of Elah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

15 In the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah, Zimri reigned seven days in Tirzah, and the people were encamped against Gibbethon which belonged to the Philistines.

16 And the folk heard in the camp, saying, Zimri has indeed killed the king; so all Israel made Omri, the captain of the host, king over Israel that day in the camp.

17 Omri went up, and all Israel with him, from Gibbethon, and they besieged Tirzah.

18 When Zimri saw that the city was captured, he entered the royal palace and set the king's house on fire with fire, and he died.

19 because he sinned to do evil in the sight of the LORD, walking in the way of Jeroboam and in his sin which he committed, causing Israel to sin.

20 And the rest of the acts of Zimri and the conspiracy that he made, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

21 Then the people of Israel was divided in half: half of the people were after Tibni son of Ginath, to make him king, and half after Omri.

22 The folk who were after Omri strengthened themselves against the folk who were after Tibni the son of Ginath, and Tibni died, and Omri reigned.

23 In the thirty-first year of Asa king of Judah, Omri reigned over Israel twelve years; in Tirzah he reigned six years.

24 He bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver, and he built on the hill; and he called the name of the city which he built after the name of Shemer, the owner of the hill, Samaria.

25 Omri did evil in the sight of the LORD and was worse than all who were before him.

26 He walked in all the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and in his sins by which he made Israel to sin, provoking the LORD God of Israel with their vanities.

27 And the rest of the acts of Omri which he did, and his mighty deeds which he accomplished, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

28 Omri lay down with his fathers and was buried in Samaria, and Ahab his son reigned in his place.

29 Ahab son of Omri became king over Israel in the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah, and Ahab son of Omri reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty-two years.

30 Ahab son of Omri did evil in the sight of the LORD more than all who were before him.

31 And the punishment of Jeroboam son of Nebat was light to him, so he took a wife, even Jezebel daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians, and he went and served Baal and bowed down to him.

32 He set up an altar for Baal, the temple of Baal that he built in Samaria.

33 Ahab made the grove; and Ahab continued to do in order to provoke the anger of the LORD, God of Israel, more than all the kings of Israel who had been before him.

34 In his days Hiel the Bethelite built Jericho; at the cost of his firstborn son Abiram he laid its foundation, and at the cost of his youngest son Segub he set its doors, according to the word of the LORD that he spoke by the hand of Joshua the son of Nun.


## Chapter 17

1 Elijah the Tishbite, of the foreigners of Gilead, said to Ahab: As the LORD God of Israel lives, before whom I stand, there shall be neither dew nor rain in these years except according to my word.

2 The word of the LORD came to him, saying:

3 Go from here and turn eastward and hide yourself by the brook Cherith which is before the Jordan.

4 And it shall be from the stream you will drink, and the ravens I have commanded to feed you there.

5 He went and did according to the word of the LORD, and he went and dwelt in the brook Cherith which is before the Jordan.

6 And the raven brings him bread and flesh in the morning and bread and flesh in the evening, and from the stream he drinks.

7 After days had passed, the stream dried up because there was no rain in the land.

8 And the word of the LORD came to him, saying:

9 Arise, go to Zarephath which belongs to Sidon, and dwell there; behold, I have commanded there a woman, a widow, to provide for you.

10 He rose and went to Zarephath, and when he came to the entrance of the town, there was a widow woman gathering sticks; he called to her and said, "Please bring me a little water in a vessel, and I will drink."

11 And she went to take, and he called to her and said, “Please take for me a piece of bread in your hand.”

12 She said, "As the LORD your God lives, I have no cake; there is only a handful of flour in the jar and a little oil in the cruse; and now I am gathering sticks, two of them, and will come and prepare it for me and my son, and we will eat it and die."

13 Elijah said to her, "Do not fear; go, do according to your word, but first make me a small cake from what is there and bring it out to me, and afterward make for yourself and for your son."

14 For thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: The barrel of flour shall not be emptied, and the cruse of oil shall not fail, until the day when the LORD sends rain upon the face of the land.

15 She went and did according to the word of Elijah; she and he and her household ate for many days.

16 The barrel of flour did not fail and the cruse of oil did not lack, according to the word of the LORD that he spoke by the hand of Elijah.

17 And it came to pass after these things that the son of the woman the mistress of the house fell sick and his sickness was very severe until there was no breath left in him.

18 She said to Elijah, "What have you to do with me, man of God? Have you come to me to bring my sin to remembrance and to kill my son?"

19 And he said to her, Give me your child, and he took him from her bosom and carried him up to the upper chamber where he was staying, and laid him on his bed.

20 He called to the LORD and said, O LORD my God, have You also done evil to the widow with whom I am staying, to kill her son?

21 He stretched himself upon the boy three times and cried to the LORD and said, O LORD my God, let the soul of this boy come back into his body.

22 And the LORD heard the voice of Elijah, and the soul of the boy returned to his inward parts, and he lived.

23 Elijah took the boy and brought him down from the upper chamber into the house and gave him to his mother and Elijah said see your son lives

24 The woman said to Elijah, Now I know that you are a man of God and the word of the LORD in your mouth is true.


## Chapter 18

1 Many days passed, and the word of the LORD came to Elijah in the third year, saying, Go, show yourself to Ahab; I will send rain upon the face of the land.

2 Elijah went to appear to Ahab, and the famine was severe in Samaria.

3 Ahab called Obadiah who was over the household, and Obadiah greatly feared the LORD.

4 When Jezebel destroyed the prophets of the Lord, Obadiah took one hundred prophets and hid them, fifty men in a cave, and provided them with bread and water.

5 Ahab said to Obadiah, Go into the land to every spring of water and to every brook, perhaps we may find grass and keep alive horse and mule, and not cut off the cattle.

6 They divided the land among them to pass through it: Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went one way by himself.

7 And Obadiah was along the way, and behold, Elijah was coming toward him, and he recognized him and fell on his face and said, "Is it you, my lord Elijah?"

8 And he said to him, Go, tell your lord, Behold, Elijah.

9 And he said, What have I sinned, that you give your bond-servant into the hand of Ahab to kill me?

10 As the LORD your God lives, is there a nation or kingdom to which my lord has not sent to seek you? And they said, He is not there. And he made the kingdom and the nation swear, that you would not be found.

11 And now you say, Go, tell your lord, Behold, Elijah is here.

12 And I— I will go from you, and the LORD’s spirit will take you to a place I do not know, and I will come to tell Ahab, but he will not find you and will kill me, though your servant has feared the LORD since my youth.

13 Has not my lord been told of what I did when Jezebel killed the prophets of the LORD, that I hid one hundred men of the prophets of the LORD, fifty men in a cave, and provided them bread and water?

14 And now you say, Go, say to your lord: Here is Elijah; and he will kill me.

15 Elijah said, As the LORD of hosts lives, before whom I stand, today I will see him.

16 Obadiah went to meet Ahab and told him, and Ahab went to meet Elijah.

17 When Ahab saw Elijah, Ahab said to him, Is it you who trouble Israel?

18 He said, I have not troubled Israel, but you and your father's family, by forsaking the commandments of the Lord and following after the Baalim.

19 Now therefore send and gather to me all Israel to Mount Carmel, and the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal, and the four hundred prophets of the grove who eat at the table of Jezebel.

20 Ahab sent throughout all Israel and gathered the prophets to Mount Carmel.

21 Then Elijah drew near to all the people and said, How long will you limp upon two opinions? If the LORD is God, go after him; but if Baal, go after him. And the people did not answer him a word.

22 Elijah said to the people, I alone remain a prophet to the LORD, but the prophets of Baal are four hundred and fifty men.

23 Let them give us two young bulls, and let them choose for themselves one of the bulls and cut it into pieces and lay it on the wood, but fire they shall not lay; and I will prepare the other bull and lay it on the wood, but fire I will not lay.

24 You shall call in the name of your gods, but I will call in the name of the LORD; and the God who answers by fire, He is God. And all the people answered and said, The word is good.

25 Then Elijah said to the prophets of Baal, "Choose for yourselves the one young bull and prepare it first, for you are the many; and call on the name of your gods, but do not set the fire."

26 They took the young bull that he had given them, made it ready, and called on the name of Baal from morning until midday, saying, "Answer us, Baal!" But there was no voice, and no one answered; and they limped about the altar that he had made.

27 At noon Elijah mocked them and said, “Cry aloud, for he is a god; perhaps he is praying, or pursuing, or on a journey—maybe he is asleep and will wake up.”

28 And they cried aloud with a great voice and cut themselves after their custom with swords and spears until blood came upon them.

29 When noon had passed, they prophesied until the time of the offering of the oblation, but there was no voice, no answer, and no one who paid heed.

30 Then Elijah said to all the people, "Come near to me"; and all the people came near to him, and he repaired the altar of the LORD that had been torn down.

31 Elijah took twelve stones according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the word of the LORD had come, saying, "Israel shall be your name."

32 He built the stones into an altar by the name of the LORD and made a watercourse like a house measure of seed all around the altar.

33 He arranged the wood and cut up the bull and laid it on the wood.

34 And he said, Fill four pitchers with water and pour it on the burnt offering and on the wood; and he said, Do it again, and they did it again; and he said, Do it a third time, and they did it a third time.

35 The water went around about the altar and also filled the conduit with water.

36 When the gift was brought up, the prophet Elijah approached and said, the LORD God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, today let it be known that you are God in Israel and I am your servant; I have done all these deeds at your word.

37 Answer me, O LORD, answer me, that this people may know that You, O LORD, are God, and that You have turned their heart backward.

38 And fire from the LORD fell and consumed the burnt offering and the wood and the stones and the dust and the water that was in the trench.

39 All the people saw and fell on their faces and said, the LORD indeed is God, the LORD indeed is God.

40 Then Elijah said to them, Seize the prophets of Baal; let not a man escape from among them. So they seized them, and Elijah brought them down to the Kishon river and slaughtered them there.

41 Elijah said to Ahab, "Go up, eat and drink, for the sound of a noise of rain."

42 Ahab went up to eat and to drink, and Elijah went up to the top of Carmel and bowed himself to the ground and put his face between his knees.

43 And he said to his servant, "Go up now, look toward the sea," and he went up and looked and said, "There is nothing," and he said, "Return seven times."

44 And in the seventh it happened: he said, "Behold, a small cloud like the palm of a man rises from the sea." Then he said, "Go up, say to Ahab, 'Harness and go down, so that the rain does not stop you.'"

45 And it was so from morning until the time of evening sacrifice, and the air became thick with clouds and wind, and there was heavy rain, and Ahab rode and went to Jezreel.

46 And the power of the LORD was upon Elijah, and he girded up his loins and ran before Ahab to the entrance of Jezreel.


## Chapter 19

1 Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done, and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.

2 Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah, saying, "May the gods do so to you and more also, for tomorrow I will make your life like the life of one of them."

3 And he saw and rose and went to his own self and came to Beer-shebah which belongs to Judah and left his servant there.

4 He went a day’s journey into the wilderness and came and sat down under a juniper tree and asked his soul to die and said, “It is enough; now, O the LORD, take my soul, for I am not better than my fathers.”

5 And he lay down and slept under one juniper tree, and behold, an ambassador touched him and said to him, "Rise, eat."

6 He looked, and behold, his bolsters were a cake baked on coals and a cruse of water; and he ate and drank, and lay down again.

7 And the ambassador of the LORD returned a second time and touched him and said, "Rise, eat, for the journey is too great for you."

8 He rose and ate and drank, and went in the strength of that meat forty days and forty nights to the mount of God, Horeb.

9 He entered the cave and spent the night there, and behold, the word of the LORD came to him, and said to him, "What are you doing here, Elijah?"

10 And he said, I have been very jealous for the LORD, the God of hosts, for the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I, even I, am left alone, and they seek my life to take it away.

11 And he said, Go out and stand on the mount before the LORD, and behold, the LORD passes by with a great and strong wind, splitting the mountains and breaking the rocks before the LORD; not in the wind is the LORD, and after the wind a commotion; not in the commotion is the LORD.

12 After the commotion a fire; the LORD was not in the fire; and after the fire a sound of still silence.

13 When Elijah heard, he wrapped his face in his mantle and went out and stood at the entrance of the cave, and behold, a voice to him saying, What to you here, Elijah?

14 And he said, I have been zealous for Jehovah, the Lord God of hosts, for the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I am left alone, and they seek to take my life.

15 And Jehovah said to him, Go, return along your journey by the wilderness of Damascus, and you shall come and anoint Hazael king over Aram.

16 And Jehu son of Nimshi you shall anoint to be king over Israel, and Elisha son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah you shall anoint to be prophet in your place.

17 And he who flees from the sword of Hazael will be killed by Jehu, and he who flees from the sword of Jehu will be killed by Elisha.

18 I will leave in Israel seven thousand, all the knees that have not bowed to Baal and all the mouths that have not kissed him.

19 He went from there and found Elisha the son of Shaphat, and he was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen before him, and he was with the twelfth; and Elijah passed by him and cast his mantle upon him.

20 And he left the beeve and ran after Elijah, and said, Let me, I pray, kiss my father and my mother, and I will go after thee. And he said to him, Go back, for what have I done to thee?

21 He returned from following him and took the yoke of oxen and sacrificed them and with the vessel of the oxen boiled the flesh and gave it to the people and they ate and he arose and went after Elijah and served him.


## Chapter 20

1 Benhadad king of Aram gathered all his forces and thirty-two kings with him, horses and chariots, and went up and laid siege against Samaria and fought against her.

2 He sent messengers to Ahab king of Israel, into the city,

3 He said to him, Thus says Benhadad, Your silver and your gold are mine, and your wives and your good sons are mine.

4 The king of Israel answered and said, According to your word, my lord, O king: I am yours, and all that I have.

5 The ambassadors returned and said, “Thus says Benhadad: ‘You shall give me your silver and your gold, your wives and your sons.’”

6 If at this time tomorrow I send my servants to you, and they search your house and the houses of your servants, then everything your eyes desire they will put in their hand and take away.

7 The king of Israel summoned all the elders of the land and said, "Know now and see, this one seeks evil, for he has sent to me for my wives, my sons, my silver, and my gold, and I have not withheld anything from him."

8 And all the elders and all the people said to him, Do not hearken, neither consent.

9 He said to the ambassadors of Benhadad, "Tell my lord the king, 'All that you sent to your servant the first time I will do, but this matter I am not able to do.'" So the ambassadors went and brought back this answer.

10 Benhadad sent to him, saying, "Thus may the gods do to me and thus may they add: if the dust of Samaria suffices for handfuls to all the people who are at my feet."

11 The king of Israel answered and said, "Say, 'Let not the one who girds boast like the one who removes the garment.'"

12 When he heard this act, while he was drinking with the kings in the pavilion, he said to his servants, “Set (a watch)!”, and they set (a watch) upon the city.

13 And behold, a prophet approached Ahab king of Israel and said, Thus says the LORD, Have you seen all this great multitude? Behold, I will deliver it into your hand today, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

14 And Ahab said, "By whom?" And he said, "Thus says the LORD: By the servants of the rulers of the provinces." And he said, "Who will take hold of the battle?" And he said, "You."

15 He visited the lads of the governors of the provinces, and they were two hundred and thirty, and after them he visited all the people, all the sons of Israel, seven thousand.

16 They marched out at noon, while Benhadad was drinking wine, drunk in the booths, he and the thirty-two kings who were helping him.

17 The lads of the governors of the provinces went out first, and Benhadad sent and they reported to him, saying, "Men have come out from Samaria."

18 And he said, If they have gone in peace, then capture them alive; but if they have gone to battle, then capture them alive.

19 And these went out from the city, young men who were rulers of the provinces, and the army that followed after them.

20 They struck each other, and the Arameans fled; Israel pursued them, and Ben-hadad king of Aram escaped on a horse with horsemen.

21 The king of Israel went out and struck the horse and the chariot, and he struck Aram with a great slaughter.

22 The prophet came near to the king of Israel and said to him, Go, strengthen yourself and know and see what you will do, for at the return of the year the king of Aram will come up against you.

23 And the servants of the king of Aram said to him, "Their gods are gods of the hills; therefore they were stronger than we. But now, let us fight against them in the plain; will we not be stronger than they?"

24 And do this act: remove the kings, each one from his place, and set deputies in their stead.

25 And you shall appoint for yourself an army like the army that fell from you, and horse like the horse, and chariot like the chariot, and fight against them in the plain; if not, we will be stronger than them. And he listened to their voice and did so.

26 At the return of the year, Benhadad king of Aram marched against Aphek to fight against Israel.

27 And the sons of Israel were mustered and provisioned, and they went to meet them; and the sons of Israel encamped opposite them like two little flocks of goats, and Aram filled the land.

28 Then the man of God drew near and said to the king of Israel, “Thus says the LORD: ‘Because Aram has said, “The LORD is a god of the mountains, but he is not a god of the valleys,” therefore I will deliver all this great multitude into your hand, and you shall know that I am the LORD.’”

29 So they camped, these opposite these, for seven days, and it happened on the day, the seventh, that the battle drew near, and the sons of Israel struck Aram, a hundred thousand footmen in one day.

30 The rest fled to Aphek, and the wall fell upon twenty-seven thousand of the rest, and Benhadad fled and came to the city, chamber within chamber.

31 And his servants said to him, behold now we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings; let us now put sackcloth around our loins and cords upon our heads, and go out to the king of Israel, perhaps he will spare your life.

32 They girded sackclothes on their loins and cords on their heads, and came to the king of Israel and said, “Your servant Benhadad says, ‘Please let me live, my life.’” The king said, “Is he still alive? He is my brother.”

33 And the men divined and hurried and decided from him, and said, "Your brother Benhadad," and he said, "Come, take him." And Benhadad went out to him, and he helped him up into the chariot.

34 And he said to him, The cities which my father took from your father I will restore; you shall have streets in Damascus as my father had in Samaria; and I will send you away in the covenant. So he made a covenant with him and sent him away.

35 And also, a certain prophet said to his companion in the word of the LORD, "Strike me, please," but the man refused to strike him.

36 And he said to him, Because you have not obeyed the voice of the LORD, behold, you shall go from me and the lion shall strike you down; and he went from him, and the lion found him and struck him.

37 And he found another man, and said, "Please be ready," but the man struck him, striking and wounding.

38 The prophet went and stood before the king on the way and disguised himself with ashes over his eyes.

39 And it happened, the king passed by, and he cried out to the king, and he said, Your servant went among the battle, and behold, a certain man came out and brought me a certain man, and he said, Guard this man; if he is missed, then shall your life be for his life, or a talent of silver you shall weigh.

40 And your servant was doing this and that, and he was not there, and the king of Israel said to him, "Your judgment is thus: you have decided."

41 And he quickly removed the ashes from his face, and the king of Israel recognized him, for he was one of the prophets.

42 So he said to him, Thus says the LORD, Because you have let go the man of my devoted curse from your hand, your life shall be for his life, and your people for his people.

43 The king of Israel went to his home in Samaria, angry and resentful.


## Chapter 21

1 After these affairs, there was a vineyard belonging to Naboth the Jezreelite in Jezreel, beside the palace of Ahab king of Samaria.

2 Ahab spoke to Naboth, saying, “Give me your vineyard, that it may become a garden of herbs for me, since it is close beside my house; and I will give you in exchange a better vineyard than it, or if it seems good in your eyes, I will give you its price in silver.”

3 Then Naboth said to Ahab, Far be it from me before the LORD that I should give you my ancestral inheritance.

4 Ahab went to his palace, sullen and angry because of the word that Naboth the Jezreelite had spoken to him, saying, "I will not give you the inheritance of my fathers." And he lay down upon his bed, turned his face away, and did not eat bread.

5 Then Jezebel his wife came to him, and she said to him, "Why is your spirit disheartened, and why are you not eating food?"

6 And he said to her, I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite, saying, Give me your vineyard for money, or if you prefer, I will give you a vineyard in exchange for it; but he said, I will not give you my vineyard.

7 Then said Jezebel his wife to him, Do you now exercise kingship over Israel; arise, eat bread, and let your heart be merry: I will give you the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite.

8 She wrote letters in the name of Ahab and sealed them with his seal, and sent them to the elders and the nobles who were in his city, the ones living with Naboth.

9 And she wrote in the bills, saying, Proclaim a fast and seat Naboth at the head of the people.

10 And they shall seat two men, sons of Belial, opposite him, and they shall testify against him, saying, “You have cursed God and the king”; and they shall bring him out and stone him, and he shall die.

11 The elders and the nobles who lived in his city did as Jezebel had sent to them, according to what was written in the letters she had sent to them.

12 Proclaim a fast and seat Naboth at the head of the people.

13 Then came two men, sons of Belial, and sat opposite him; and the men of Belial brought false charges against Naboth before the people, saying, "Naboth has cursed God and the king!" So they took him out of the city, and stoned him with stones, and he died.

14 They sent to Jezebel, saying, "Naboth has been stoned and is dead."

15 When Jezebel heard that Naboth had been stoned and was dead, Jezebel said to Ahab, "Get up, take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give to you for money, for Naboth is not alive but dead."

16 When Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, Ahab rose up to go down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite to take possession of it.

17 The word of the LORD came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying:

18 Rise, go down to meet Ahab king of Israel in Samaria; behold, he is in the vineyard of Naboth, where he has gone down to take possession.

19 You shall say to him, ‘Thus says the LORD: “Have you murdered and also taken possession?” And you shall say to him, “Thus says the LORD: ‘In the place where the dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, the dogs will also lick up your blood.’”’

20 Ahab said to Elijah, "Have you found me, my enemy?" And he said, "I have found you, because you have sold yourself to do what is evil in the sight of the LORD."

21 I am bringing adversity to you, and I will consume your followers; I will cut off from Ahab every one bound to the wall, both the restrained and the forsaken in Israel.

22 I will give your household like the household of Jeroboam son of Nebat and like the household of Baasha son of Ahijah because of the anger with which you have angered me and have caused Israel to sin.

23 And also to Jezebel spoke Jehovah, saying, The dogs will eat Jezebel in the bulwark of Jezreel.

24 He who dies to Ahab in the city, the dogs will eat; and he who dies in the field, the birds of the air will eat.

25 Yet there was no one like Ahab, who sold himself to do what was evil in the sight of the LORD, whom Jezebel his wife incited.

26 And he was exceedingly disgusted with walking after the idol, according to all that the Amorite did, whom the LORD drove out from before the children of Israel.

27 When Ahab heard these acts, he tore his garments, put sackcloth on his body, fasted, lay in sackcloth, and walked slowly.

28 The word of the LORD came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying:

29 Have you seen how Ahab has humbled himself before me? Because he has humbled himself before me, I will not bring the adversity in his days; in the days of his son I will bring the adversity upon his house.


## Chapter 22

1 They lived for three years; there was no war between Aram and Israel.

2 In the third year, Jehoshaphat king of Judah went down to the king of Israel.

3 The king of Israel said to his servants, Do you know that Ramoth in Gilead belongs to us, and we are idly sitting, not taking it from the hand of the king of Aram?

4 He said to Jehoshaphat, Shall you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead? And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, I am as you, my folk as your folk, my horses as your horses.

5 Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, "Please inquire now of the word of the LORD."

6 The king of Israel gathered the prophets, about four hundred men, and said to them, "Shall I go to Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I refrain?" And they said, "Go up, and may the LORD deliver into the hand of the king."

7 Jehoshaphat said, Is there not yet a prophet of the LORD, that we may inquire of him?

8 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "There is yet another man by whom we may inquire of the LORD: Micaiah son of Imlah," but Jehoshaphat said, "Let not the king say such a thing."

9 The king of Israel called to a certain chamberlain and said, "Quickly, bring Micah son of Imla."

10 The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah were sitting each upon his throne, dressed in their garments, at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the prophets were prophesying before them.

11 Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made for him horns of iron and said, "Thus says the LORD: 'With these you shall gore Aram until they are consumed.'"

12 And all the prophets prophesied thus, saying, Go up to Ramoth-gilead, and prosper; and the LORD will give it into the hand of the king.

13 And the ambassador who had gone to summon Micah said to him, "Behold now, the words of the prophets are unanimously favorable to the king; let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak favorably."

14 Micah said, "As the Lord lives, the word that the Lord speaks to me—that I will speak."

15 Then he came to the king, and the king said to him, "Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth in Gilead to battle, or shall we refrain?" And he said to him, "Go up, and prosper, for the LORD will give into the hand of the king."

16 Then the king said to him, "How many times must I adjure you to speak to me nothing but the truth in the name of the LORD?"

17 He said, I have seen all Israel scattered on the hills, like sheep that have no shepherd, and the LORD said, These have no masters; they shall return each man to his house in peace.

18 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Did I not say to you that he would not prophesy good concerning me, but only evil?"

19 Therefore hear the word of the LORD: I saw the LORD sitting on his throne, and all the host of heaven standing beside him, on his right hand and on his left.

20 And Jehovah said, Who will entice Ahab, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth in Gilead? And one said in this way, and another said in that way.

21 And a spirit came out and stood before the LORD and said, I will entice him.

22 And the LORD said to him, How? And he said, I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, You shall entice and also prevail; go out and do so.

23 And now, behold, the LORD has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all your prophets, and the LORD has spoken disaster concerning you.

24 Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah approached and struck Micah on the cheek and said, Where has the spirit of the LORD passed from me to speak to you?

25 Micah said, "You are seeing on that day when you will go from chamber to chamber to hide yourself."

26 The king of Israel said, Take Micah and bring him back to Amon the captain of the city and to Joash son of the king.

27 Then you shall say, Thus says the king, Place this man in the prison and feed him with bread of affliction and water of affliction until I return in peace.

28 Micah said, "If you return in peace, the LORD has not spoken by me." And he said, "Hear, all you peoples."

29 The king of Israel went up, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, to Ramoth in Gilead.

30 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Disguise yourself and go into battle, but you wear your own garments." So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into the battle.

31 And the king of Aram commanded his captains who had charge of the chariotry, thirty-two of them, saying, "Do not fight against small or great, but only the king of Israel alone."

32 When the captains of the chariot saw Jehoshaphat, they said, "Surely this is the king of Israel," and they turned toward him to fight, but Jehoshaphat cried out.

33 When the captains of the chariot saw that he was not the king of Israel, they turned back from following him.

34 And another drew his bow at a venture and struck the king of Israel between the joints of the breastplate and brought him down; and he said to his chariot driver, "Turn your hand and carry me out of the camp, for I am mortally wounded."

35 The battle intensified that day, and the king stood in his chariot facing Aram; he died in the evening, and the blood from the wound drained into the chariot's lap.

36 The cry of rejoicing passed through the camp at the coming of the sun, saying, “Every man to his city and every man to his land.”

37 The king died and came to Samaria, and they buried the king in Samaria.

38 The chariot was swept into the pool of Samaria, and the dogs licked up his blood, and the prostitutes washed themselves, according to the word of the LORD that he spoke.

39 And the rest of the acts of Ahab and all that he did and the ivory house that he built and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

40 Ahab lay down with the fathers and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.

41 Jehoshaphat son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.

42 Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.

43 He went in all the ways of Asa his father; he did not turn from him, to do what was right in the eyes of the LORD. But the high places were not removed; the people continued to offer sacrifices and burn incense at the high places.

44 Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.

45 And the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat and his might that he did and how he fought, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

46 The remnant of the sanctuary that remained from the days of Asa his father he destroyed from the land.

47 and there was no king standing in Edom, a king.

48 Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold, but they did not go, because the ships were wrecked at Ezion-geber.

49 Then Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat, "Let my servants go with your servants in the ships," but Jehoshaphat refused.

50 Jehoshaphat lay down with the forefathers and was buried with the forefathers in the city of David his father, and Jehoram his son reigned in his place.

51 Ahaziah the son of Ahab reigned over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and he reigned over Israel two years.

52 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and walked in the way of his father and in the way of his mother and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat who caused Israel to sin.

53 And he served the Baalim and bowed down to them, and he angered the LORD, God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.


# 2 KINGS

## Chapter 1

1 Moab rebelled against Israel after the death of Ahab.

2 Ahaziah fell through the lattice of his upper chamber which was in Samaria and was injured, so he sent messengers and said to them, "Go, inquire of Baal-zebub, the gods of Ekron, whether I will recover from this sickness."

3 And the angel of the LORD spoke to Elijah the Tishbite, "Arise, go up to meet the messengers of the king of Samaria, and say to them, 'Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are going to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron?'"

4 Therefore, thus says the LORD, "The bed-chamber on which you have gone up you shall not come down from, for you shall surely die." And Elijah went on.

5 The messengers returned to him, and he said to them, "Why have you returned?"

6 They said to him, “A certain man came up to meet us and said to us, ‘Go, return to the king who sent you and say to him, “Thus says the LORD: Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are sending to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? Therefore, the bed on which you have gone up you shall not come down from, for you shall surely die.”’”

7 And he said unto them, What manner of man was he that came up to meet you, and spoke to you these words?

8 They said to him, a certain man with hairy clothing and a leather girdle bound around his loins, and he said, It is Elijah the Tishbite.

9 And he sent to him a captain of fifty with his fifty, and he went up to him, and behold, he was sitting on the top of the mount; and the man of God said to him, The king has spoken: Come down.

10 Elijah answered and spoke to the captain of fifty, If I am a man of God, let fire come down from heaven and consume you and your fifty. Then fire came down from heaven and consumed him and his fifty.

11 Then he returned and sent to him another captain of fifty with his fifty, and the man of God answered and said to him, Thus says the king, Come down quickly.

12 Then Elijah answered and spoke to them, If I am a man of God, may fire come down from heaven and consume you and your fifty; and fire of God came down from heaven and consumed him and his fifty.

13 Then he returned and sent the third captain of fifty with his fifty, and he went up and came to Elijah, and fell on his knees before Elijah and entreated him, saying, O man of God, please let my life and the lives of these fifty of your servants be precious in your sight.

14 Behold, fire came down from heaven and consumed the two captains of the fifties who were first and their fifties; now let my life be precious in your sight.

15 The ambassador of the Lord spoke to Elijah, "Go down with him, do not fear his presence," so he arose and went down with him to the king.

16 And he said to him, Thus says the LORD, Because you have sent messengers to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron, as though there were no God in Israel to inquire of his word, therefore the bed you have gone up on you shall not come down from, for you shall surely die.

17 He died according to the word of the LORD that Elijah had spoken, and Jehoram reigned in his place in the second year of Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, for he had no son.

18 And the rest of the acts of Ahaziah which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?


## Chapter 2

1 And it came to pass, when the LORD took up Elijah by a whirlwind into heaven, that Elijah and Elisha went from Gilgal.

2 Elijah said to Elisha, "Stay here, please, for the LORD has sent me to Beth-el." And Elisha said, "As the LORD lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you." So they went down to Beth-el.

3 The sons of the prophets who were in Beth-el went out to Elisha, and said to him, Do you know that today the LORD will take away your master from over your head? And he said, Yes, I know; hold your peace.

4 Elijah said to Elisha, "Stay here, please, for the LORD has sent me to Jericho." And he said, "As the LORD lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you." And they came to Jericho.

5 And the sons of the prophets who were in Jericho drew near to Elisha and said to him, "Do you know that today the LORD will take your master from over your head?" And he said, "Yes, I also know; be silent."

6 Then Elijah said to him, "Stay here, I pray, for the LORD has sent me to the Jordan." But he said, "As the LORD lives, and as your soul lives, I will not leave you." So the two of them went on together.

7 And fifty men from the sons of the prophets went and stood opposite at a distance, and the two of them stood by the Jordan.

8 Elijah took his mantle and wrapped it and struck the water, and it split apart to one side and to the other, and the two of them crossed over on dry ground.

9 As they were passing on, Elijah said to Elisha, "Ask what I shall do for you before I am taken from you." And Elisha said, "Please let a double portion of your spirit be upon me."

10 And he said, "You have strongly requested; if you see me taken from you, it shall be so for you, but if not, it shall not be."

11 And as they were going on, walking and talking, suddenly a fiery chariot with fiery horses appeared and separated the two of them, and Elijah ascended in the stormy whirlwind into the heavens.

12 And Elisha saw and he cried, My father, my father, chariot of Israel and its horseman; and he saw him no more. And he seized his apparel and tore it into two pieces.

13 He took up the mantle of Elijah that had fallen from him, and he returned and stood by the bank of the Jordan.

14 He took the mantle of Elijah that had fallen from him and struck the waters, saying, "Where is the LORD, the God of Elijah?" Then he also struck the waters, and they divided to the one side and to the other, and Elisha crossed over.

15 The sons of the prophets who were in Jericho saw him from afar, and they said, "The spirit of Elijah rests on Elisha." Then they went to meet him and bowed to the ground before him.

16 They said to him, "Behold now, there are fifty men, valiant ones, among your servants; let them please go and search for your master, lest the Spirit of the LORD has taken him and cast him on one of the mountains or into one of the valleys." But he said, "You shall not send."

17 They pressed him until he was ashamed, and he said, “Send!” So they sent fifty men, and they searched for three days but did not find him.

18 They returned to him, and he was sitting in Jericho, and he said to them, "Did I not say to you, 'Do not go'?"

19 The men of the city said to Elisha, "Please, the dwelling of the city is good, as my lord sees, but the water is bad and the land is barren."

20 And he said, Take for me a new cruse and put salt there; and they took it to him.

21 He went out to the source of the waters and threw salt there, and said, Thus says the LORD, I have healed these waters; there shall no longer be from there death and bereavement.

22 The water was healed until that day, according to the word of Elisha that he spoke.

23 He went up from there to Beth-el, and he was going along the way when young men came out from the city and mocked him and said to him, "Go up, baldy, go up, baldy."

24 Then he turned around and saw them, and he cursed them in the name of the LORD; so two bears came out from the forest and tore forty-two children from them.

25 He went from there to the mount of Carmel, and from there he returned to Samaria.


## Chapter 3

1 Jehoram son of Ahab became king over Israel in Samaria in the eighteenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, and he reigned twelve years.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, but not like his father and like his mother; he removed the standing image of Baal that his father had made.

3 Only in the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin, did he persist; he did not turn away from them.

4 And Mesha king of Moab was a sheepmaster, and he paid to the king of Israel one hundred thousand lambs and one hundred thousand rams of wool.

5 After the death of Ahab, the king of Moab rebelled against the king of Israel.

6 King Jehoram went out from Samaria on that day and inspected all Israel.

7 He went and sent to Jehoshaphat king of Judah, saying, The king of Moab has rebelled against me; will you go with me to Moab to battle? And Jehoshaphat said, I will go up; as I am, so are you; as my folk, so are your folk; as my horses, so are your horses.

8 He said, "Which way shall we go up?" and he said, "The way of the wilderness of Edom."

9 The king of Israel went, and the king of Judah, and the king of Edom, and they turned a journey of seven days, and there was no water for the army and for the cattle that were in their charge.

10 The king of Israel said, Alas, that the LORD has called these three kings to deliver them into the hand of Moab.

11 Jehoshaphat said, "Is there not a prophet of the Lord here, that we may inquire of the Lord by him?" And one of the bondmen of the king of Israel answered and said, "Here is Elisha son of Shaphat, who poured water on the hands of Elijah."

12 Jehoshaphat said, "There is still an act of the LORD," and the king of Israel, Jehoshaphat, and the king of Edom went down to him.
