13 Elisha said to the king of Israel, "What have I to do with you? Go to the prophets of your father and to the prophets of your mother." Then the king of Israel said to him, "No, for it is the LORD who has called these three kings together to give them into the hand of Moab."

14 Elisha said, As the Lord of hosts lives, before whom I stand, I would not look at you nor regard you, were it not for the presence of Jehoshaphat king of Judah.

15 And now, bring me a musician; and when the musician played, the hand of the LORD would come upon him.

16 And he said, Thus says the LORD, Shall the brook of this place make ditches, ditches?

17 For thus says the LORD: You will not see wind and you will not see rain, but that brook will be filled with water, and you will drink, both you and your cattle and your beasts.

18 And this matter was regarded as insignificant in the eyes of the LORD, and he gave Moab into your hand.

19 And you shall strike every fortified city and every choice city, and every good tree you shall cut down, and all water springs you shall stop up, and all the good land you shall ruin with stones.

20 And it came to pass in the morning, at the time of the offering, that behold, waters were coming from the direction of Edom, and the land was filled with water.

21 And all Moab heard that the kings had marched up to fight against them, and they cried out from every girded one and above, and they took their stand upon the border.

22 They rose early in the morning and the sun shone upon the waters, and Moab saw from afar the waters red as blood.

23 They said, "This is blood-guiltiness; the kings have destroyed one another; each man has struck his fellow; now to the spoil, Moab!"

24 They came to the camp of Israel and Israel rose up and struck Moab and they fled from their presence there and struck Moab.

25 And they shall lay waste the cities and cast every good field with a man's stone and fill it; and every spring of water they shall stop, and every good tree they shall fell, until they leave her stones in the wall of Kir-haraseth; and the slingers shall surround it and strike it.

26 The king of Moab saw that the battle was too strong for him, so he took seven hundred swordsmen to break through to the king of Edom, but they could not.

27 He took the son, the firstborn who was to reign after him, and offered him as a burnt offering on the wall; and there was great wrath against Israel, and they withdrew from him and returned to the land.


## Chapter 4

1 And a certain woman of the wives of the sons of the prophets cried to Elisha, saying, My husband your servant is dead, and you know that your servant feared the LORD, and the creditor has come to take my two children to be bondmen.

2 Elisha said to her, "What shall I do for you? Tell me, what do you have in the house?" And she replied, "There is nothing for your handmaid in the house except a pot of oil."

3 And he said, Go, ask for yourself vessels from abroad, from every man his empty vessels; do not diminish.

4 And you shall come and shut the door behind you and your children, and pour out on all the vessels; and when a vessel is full, move it away.

5 And she went from him and shut the door behind her and her children; they kept bringing the vessels to her, and she kept pouring.

6 When the vessels were full, she said to her son, "Bring me another vessel," and he said to her, "There is no more vessel," and the oil stopped.

7 She came and told the man of God, and he said, “Go, sell the oil, and pay your debt; and you and your children shall live on the rest.”

8 And it came to pass one day that Elisha passed to Shunem, where there was a woman of great means, and she detained him to eat bread; and it came to pass that whenever he passed by, he would turn aside there to eat bread.

9 And she said to her husband, "Behold now, I know that he is a holy man of God; he passes by us continually."

10 Let us make a small upper chamber with a wall, and there we will place for him a bed, a table, a seat, and a candlestick; whenever he comes to us, he will turn aside there.

11 And it came to pass at the time that he came there, and he turned aside to the upper chamber and lay down there.

12 And he said to Gehazi his servant, Call this Shunamite woman; and he called her, and she stood before him.

13 And he said to her, “Please say to her, ‘Behold, you have shown us such great care; what is to be done for you? Is there anything to speak for you to the king or to the captain of the host?’” And she said, “I am dwelling among my people.”

14 And he said, "What then is to be done for her?" And Gehazi said, "But she has no son, and her husband is old."

15 And he said, Call to her; and he called to her, and she stood at the entrance.

16 And he said, At this appointed time, about this season, you shall embrace a son. And she said, No, my lord, O man of God, do not lie to your handmaid.

17 The woman conceived and bore a son at the appointed time, in due season, as Elisha had spoken to her.

18 And the boy grew, and the day came, and he went out to his father to the reapers.

19 And he said to his father, My head, my head! And he said to the young man, Carry him to his mother.

20 And he lifted him and brought him to his mother, and he sat upon her knees until midday, and he died.

21 And she went up and laid him on the bed of the man of God, and shut it after him and went out.

22 Then she called to her husband and said, “Please send me one of the boys and one of the donkeys, that I may hurry to the man of God and return.”

23 And he said, Why do you go to him today? It is neither new moon nor sabbath. And she said, It is well.

24 And she saddled the ass, and said to her servant, "Go on and walk; do not stop for me to ride, unless I say to you."

25 And she went and came to the man of God at Mount Carmel, and when the man of God saw her coming toward him, he said to his servant Gehazi, "Look, there is the Shunamite woman."

26 Now run, I pray thee, to meet her, and say to her, Do you fare well? Do you fare well? Do you fare well? And she said, I fare well.

27 She came to the man of God at the mountain and took hold of his feet, but Gehazi approached to push her away, and the man of God said, “Let her alone, for her soul is bitter to her; and the LORD has hidden it from me and did not tell me.”

28 And she said, "Did I ask a son from my lord? Did I not say, 'Do not afflict me'?"

29 He said to Gehazi, "Gird your loins and take my staff in your hand and go; if you meet any man, do not greet him, and if any man greets you, do not answer him; and place my staff on the face of the lad."

30 And the boy's mother said, As the LORD lives and as your soul lives, I will not leave you; and he rose up and went after her.

31 And Gehazi passed before them and laid the staff upon the face of the lad, but there was no voice and no hearing; so he returned to meet him and told him, saying, The boy has not awakened.

32 Elisha came into the house and saw the boy dead, lying on his bed.

33 And he came and closed the door behind the two of them, and prayed to the LORD.

34 He went up and lay upon the child, putting his mouth upon the child's mouth, his eyes upon the child's eyes, and his hands upon the child's hands; he stretched himself upon him, and the child's body grew warm.

35 He returned and went back and forth in the house, once and again, then went up, and bowed himself over the boy, and the boy sneezed seven times, and the boy opened his eyes.

36 He called to Gehazi and said, "Call this Shunamite woman," so he called her and she came to him. Then he said, "Take your son."

37 Then she came and fell at his feet, bowed to the ground, took up her child, and went out.

38 Elisha returned to Gilgal and the dearth was in the land and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him and he said to his servant set the great pot on and boil pottage for the sons of the prophets

39 One went out into the field to gather herbs and found a wild vine; he gathered from it wild gourds, filling his garment; and he came and put them into the caldron of pottage, for they did not know.

40 They poured pottage for the men to eat, and when they ate from the pottage, they cried out and said, "Death in the caldron, man of God!" and they could not eat.

41 And he said, Take flour, and he threw it into the pot, and he said, Bake for the people, and they shall eat, and there was no bad thing in the pot.

42 And a certain man came from Baal-shalisha, bringing to the man of God bread of first fruits, twenty loaves of barley and full ears in his husk; and he said, "Give to the people and let them eat."

43 His servant said, What shall I give this to before a hundred men? And he said, Give it to the people and they shall eat, for thus says the LORD: They shall eat and have leftover.

44 And he gave it before them, and they ate and had left over according to the word of the LORD.


## Chapter 5

1 And Naaman, captain of the army of the king of Aram, was a great man with his lord and in high favour, because by him the LORD gave deliverance to Aram; and the man was a mighty man of valour, but he was a leper.

2 And bands of Aram went out and raided from the land of Israel, taking captive a young woman, who came to be before the wife of Naaman.

3 And she said to her lady, "If only my lord were before the prophet who is in Samaria, then he would cure him of his leprosy."

4 And he came and told his master, saying, Thus and thus spoke the young woman from the land of Israel.

5 Then the king of Aram said, "Go, I will send a letter to the king of Israel." So he went and took in his hand ten talents of silver, six thousand of gold, and ten changes of apparel.

6 The letter was brought to the king of Israel saying, And now, when this letter comes to you, behold, I have sent to you my servant Naaman; and you shall cure him of his leprosy.

7 When the king of Israel read the letter, he tore his garments and said, "Am I God, to kill and to keep alive, that this one sends to me to recover a man from his leprosy? Indeed, only perceive and see that he seeks to provoke me."

8 When Elisha the man of God heard that the king of Israel had torn his clothes, he sent to the king, saying, Why have you torn your clothes? Let the king come now to me, and he shall know that there is a prophet in Israel.

9 And Naaman came with his chariot and stood at the entrance of Elisha's house.

10 Elisha sent a messenger to him, saying, Go, wash seven times in the Jordan, and your body will return to you and you will be clean.

11 Naaman was angry and went away, and he said, "Look, I said to myself, 'Surely he will come out to me and stand and call upon the name of the LORD his God and wave his hand over the place and remove the leprosy.'"

12 Are not the flood of Abana and the river Pharpar of Damascus better than all the waters of Israel? Shall I not wash in them and be clean? But he turned away and went off in anger.

13 And his servants approached him and spoke to him, saying, "My father, a great act has the prophet spoken to you—will you not do it? Indeed, he said to you, 'Wash and be clean.'"

14 He went down and dipped in the Jordan seven times, according to the word of the man of God, and his flesh returned like the flesh of a little boy, and he was cleansed.

15 Then he returned to the man of God, he and all his army, and came and stood before him, and said, "Now indeed I know that there is no God in all the earth except in Israel; now therefore please accept a present from your servant."

16 As the LORD lives, before whom I stand, I will not take it; but he urged him to accept, and he refused.

17 And Naaman said, Let now a burden of two mules’ earth be given to your servant, for your servant will no longer make burnt offering or sacrifice to other gods, but only to the LORD.

18 For this act may the LORD pardon Your servant: when my lord enters the house of Rimmon to prostrate himself there, and I prostrate myself in the house of Rimmon—when I bow down in the house of Rimmon—may the LORD pardon Your Servant for this matter.

19 Go in peace and he went from him about a short distance of land.

20 Gehazi, Elisha's servant, said, "Look, my lord has let this Syrian Naaman off lightly by not accepting from his hand what he brought; as the LORD lives, I will run after him and take something from him."

21 Gehazi pursued after Naaman, and Naaman saw him running after him, so he alighted from the chariot to meet him, and said, "Is all well?"

22 The man said, "Peace, my lord, your servant has sent me to say: Now two young men from the hill country of Ephraim, sons of the prophets, have come to me; please give them a talent of silver and two changes of clothing."

23 Naaman said, "Please accept two talents of silver," and he pressed him; so he weighed out two talents of silver into two bags, and two changes of clothing, and gave them to two of his servants, and they carried them before him.

24 He went to the emerod and took from their hand and appointed in the court and sent the men and they went.

25 And he came and stood by his master, and Elisha said to him, "Gehazi!" And he said, "Your servant did not go anywhere here or there."

26 He said to him, "My heart did not consent, as a man turns from his chariot to meet you—should the time now be to take silver and garments and olive trees and vineyards and sheep and cattle and male servants and female servants?"

27 And the leprosy of Naaman shall cling to you and to your descendants forever, and he went out from his presence leprous as snow.


## Chapter 6

1 The sons of the prophets said to Elisha, Behold now, the place where we dwell before you is too small for us.

2 Let us go now to the Jordan and each take from there a beam, and let us make for ourselves a place there to dwell; and he said, Go.

3 And the one said, Please agree and go with your servants, and he said, I will go.

4 He went with them, and they came to the Jordan and cut down the trees.

5 And it happened that one was felling the beam, and the (ax) head fell into the water; and he cried out and said, “Alas, lord, and it was borrowed!”

6 And the man of God said, "Where did it fall?" and he saw the place, and he cut a stick and threw it there, and the iron sank.

7 And he said, "Lift up your hand," and he stretched out his hand and took it.

8 And a king of Aram was at war with Israel, and he consulted with his servants, saying, "Set up my camp at such-and-such a place."

9 A certain man of God sent to the king of Israel, saying, Beware of passing through this place, for Aram has lain in wait there.

10 The king of Israel sent to the place that the man of God had said to him, and guarded it there, not once or twice.

11 The care for this matter stirred the king of Aram, and he called to his servants and said to them, Will you not show me who among us is for the king of Israel?

12 And one of his servants said, "Not so, my lord, O king: Elisha the prophet who is in Israel tells the king of Israel the words that you speak in your bedchamber."

13 And he said, Go and see how he is, and I will send and bring him; and it was told him, saying, Behold, he is in Dothan.

14 And he sent there horses, chariots, and a heavy host, and they came by night, and surrounded the city.

15 And the servant of the man of God rose early in the morning and went out, and behold, an army with horses and chariots surrounded the city; and his servant said to him, "Alas, my lord, what shall we do?"

16 He said, Do not fear, for many are with us more than with them.

17 Elisha prayed and said, O Jehovah, please open the eyes of the lad that he may see; and Jehovah opened the eyes of the young man, and he saw, and behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire round about Elisha.

18 So they went down to him, and Elisha prayed to the LORD and said, “Strike this people with blindness,” and he struck them with blindness according to the word of Elisha.

19 Then Elisha said to them, This is not the way, and this is not the city; follow after me, and I will lead you to the man whom you seek; and he led them to Samaria.

20 When they came to Samaria, Elisha said, "O the LORD, open the eyes of these that they may see." So the LORD opened their eyes, and they saw, and behold, they were in the midst of Samaria.

21 The king of Israel said to Elisha, when he saw them, "Shall I strike? Shall I strike, my father?"

22 Would you strike those whom you took captive with your sword and your bow? Set bread and water before them, let them eat and drink, and go to their masters.

23 He made a great provision for them, and they ate and drank, and he sent them away, and they went to their masters, and the bands of Aram did not add anymore to come into the land of Israel.

24 Some time later, Benhadad king of Aram gathered all his army and went up and laid siege to Samaria.

25 There was a severe famine in Samaria, and indeed, siege was laid against it, until a donkey's head was worth eighty pieces of silver and a quarter of a cab of dove's dung was worth five pieces of silver.

26 And a king of Israel passed by on the wall, and a woman cried out to him, saying, "Save, my lord, O king!"

27 And he said, "If the LORD does not save you, from where shall I save you? From the threshing floor or from the winepress?"

28 Then the king said to her, "What is the matter with you?" And the woman said, "This woman said to me, 'Give me your son that we may eat him today, and my son we will eat tomorrow.'"

29 We boiled my son and ate him, and I said to her the next day, “Give your son, that we may eat him,” but she hid her son.

30 When the king heard the woman's words, he tore his clothes, though he was passing by the wall; and the people looked, and there was the sackcloth on his body from inside the house.

31 And he said, Thus may God do to me and thus may Joseph, if the head of Elisha son of Shaphat stands over him today.

32 Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him, when a man sent ahead from his presence arrived, before the messenger came to him; and he said to the elders, "Have you seen how this son of a murderer has sent to take away my head? Look, when the messenger comes, shut the door and hold him back at the door; is not the sound of the feet of his master behind him?"

33 While he was still speaking with them, behold, the messenger came down to him, and said, "Behold, this adversity is from the LORD; how long shall I wait for the LORD still?"


## Chapter 7

1 And Elisha said, Hear the word of the LORD: Thus says the LORD, Tomorrow at this time, a measure of fine flour for a shekel, and two measures of barley for a shekel, at the gate of Samaria.

2 The captain who was the king's aide and leaned on his hand answered the man of God, saying, "If the LORD should make windows in the heavens, could this word come to pass?" And he said, "You will see it with your own eyes, but you will not eat from there."

3 And four men were lepers at the entrance of the gate, and they said one to another, Why are we sitting here until we die?

4 If we say, Let us enter the city, and the famine is in the city, then we will die there; and if we remain here, we will die; now therefore come, let us surrender to the camp of Aram; if they let us live, we will live; and if they kill us, we will die.

5 They rose at the dark of the morning to enter the camp of Aram, and they came to the edge of the camp of Aram, and behold, there was no man there.

6 The LORD made the army of Aram hear the sound of chariots, the sound of horses, the sound of a great host, and one man said to another, "Look, the king of Israel has hired the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Egypt to come against us."

7 They rose and fled at the dawning of the day, and they abandoned their tents and their horses and their donkeys, the camp just as it was, and they fled for their lives.

8 The lepers came to the edge of the camp and entered one tent and ate and drank and took from there silver and gold and garments and went and hid them and returned and entered another tent and took from there and went and hid it.

9 And each man said to his companion, Not so will we do this day, for this day is a day of reward for tidings; and we are hiding, waiting until the light of morning, and we have found fault; now therefore come, let us go and report to the court of the king.

10 They came and called to the doorkeeper of the town, and they told them, saying, "We came to the camp of Aram, and behold, there was no man there and no voice of a person, only the horse was tied and the donkey was tied, and the tents as they were."

11 The doorkeepers called and reported inside the royal palace.

12 The king arose at night and said to his servants, Let me tell you what Aram has done to us: they know that we are hungry, so they have left the camp to hide in the field, saying, When they come out from the city, we will seize them alive and enter the city.

13 Then one of his servants answered and said, Let them take now five of the horses that remain, which are left here; behold, they are like all the multitude of Israel that remain; behold, they are like all the abundance of Israel that are consumed; so let us send and see.

14 They took two chariots of horses, and the king sent after the Syrian army, saying, "Go and see."

15 They went after them to the Jordan, and behold, the whole way was full of garments and vessels that Aram had cast away, and the messengers returned and reported to the king.

16 The folk went out and plundered the camp of Aram, and a measure of fine flour was [sold] for a shekel and two measures of barley for a shekel, according to the word of the LORD.

17 And the king appointed the captain who was supported by his hand to be in charge of the gate, but the people trampled him in the gate and he died, according to the word that the man of God had spoken when the king came down to him.

18 And it came to pass, when the man of God spoke to the king, saying, Two measures of barley for a shekel, and a measure of fine flour for a shekel, shall be tomorrow at this time in the gate of Samaria.

19 The captain answered the man of God and said, "Behold, if the LORD makes windows in the heavens, could such a word be?" And he said, "You will see it with your own eyes, but you will not eat from it."

20 It was so for him, and the folk trampled him in the gate, and he died.


## Chapter 8

1 Elisha spoke to the woman whose son he had brought back to life, saying, "Get up and go, you and your household, and live wherever you may live, for the LORD has called for a famine, and it has also come upon the land for seven years."

2 The woman arose and did according to the word of the man of God; she went with her household and sojourned in the land of the Philistines for seven years.

3 After seven years the woman returned from the land of the Philistines and went to cry out to the king for her house and for her field.

4 And the king spoke to Gehazi, the servant of the man of God, saying, Tell now to me all the great things that Elisha has done.

5 And he was telling the king about the things by which Elisha had revived the dead, when suddenly the woman whose son he had revived was crying out to the king about her house and her field; and Gehazi said, "My lord the king, this is the woman and this is her son whom Elisha revived."

6 The king asked the woman, and she told him; so the king gave her a chamberlain, saying, "Restore to her all that is hers, and all the revenue from the field from the day she left the land until now."

7 Elisha came to Damascus, and Benhadad king of Aram was ill; and it was told to him, saying, "The man of God has come even here."

8 The king said to Hazael, Take a gift in your hand and go to meet the man of God, and inquire of the LORD through him, saying, Shall I recover from this sickness?

9 Hazael went to meet him, taking a gift in his hand: all the good of Damascus, a tribute of forty camels; he came and stood before him and said, “Your son Benhadad king of Aram has sent me to you, asking, ‘Will I live from this sickness?’”

10 Then Elisha said to him, "Go, say, 'You will surely live'; but Jehovah, the Lord, has shown me that he will surely die."

11 And he fixed his gaze steadily on him until he was ashamed, and the man of God wept.

12 And Hazael said, Why does my lord weep? And he said, Because I know the adversity that you will bring upon the sons of Israel: their fortresses you will set on fire, and their young men you will kill with the sword, and their infants you will dash in pieces, and their pregnant women you will rip open.

13 And Hazael said, "What is your servant, a dog, that he should do this great act?" And Elisha said, "The LORD has shown me that you will be king over Aram."

14 He went from Elisha and came to his master, and said to him, "What did Elisha say to you?" And he said, "He said to me, 'You will surely live.'"

15 And on the morrow he took the thick cloth and dipped it in the water and spread it over his face and he died and Hazael reigned in his stead.

16 In the fifth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat began to reign as king of Judah.

17 He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

18 He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as the house of Ahab had done, for the daughter of Ahab was his wife, and he did evil in the sight of the LORD.

19 But the LORD was not willing to destroy Judah for David his servant's sake, as he had promised him, to give him a lamp among his sons forever.

20 In his days Edom rebelled from under the hand of Judah and they set a king over themselves.

21 Joram passed through Zair with all the chariot force with him, and it came to pass that he arose at night and struck Edom who was surrounding him and the captains of the chariots, and the people fled to their tents.

22 Edom rebelled against the dominion of Judah to this day; then Libnah rebelled at that time.

23 And the rest of the acts of Joram, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

24 Joram slept with his forefathers and was buried with his forefathers in the city of David, and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.

25 In the twelfth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah began to reign.

26 Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Athaliah, daughter of Omri king of Israel.

27 He went along the way of the house of Ahab and did the evil in the eyes of the LORD like the house of Ahab, for he was son in law to the house of Ahab.

28 He went with Joram son of Ahab to battle against Hazael king of Aram at Ramoth-gilead, and the Syrians struck Joram.

29 Joram the king returned to recover at Jezreel from the wounds that the Syrians had inflicted on him at Ramah when he fought against Hazael king of Aram, and Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to visit Joram son of Ahab at Jezreel because he was ill.


## Chapter 9

1 Elisha the prophet called one of the sons of the prophets and said to him, "Gird up your loins, take this vial of oil in your hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead."

2 And you shall come there and see Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi; then you shall go in and raise him up from among his brothers, and you shall bring him to an inner chamber.

3 Take the vial of oil and pour it on his head, and say, “Thus says the LORD: I have anointed you king over Israel,” then open the door and flee, and do not wait.

4 So the young man, the young man the prophet, went to Ramoth-gilead.

5 He came, and behold, the captains of the army were sitting, and he said, "I have a matter with you, O captain," and he said, "To which of us?" and he said, "To you, O captain, Jehu."

6 He rose and entered the house, poured the oil upon his head, and said to him, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, I have anointed you king over the people of the LORD, over Israel.

7 And you shall strike the house of Ahab your lord, and I will avenge the blood of my servants the prophets and the blood of all the servants of the LORD from the hand of Jezebel.

8 And all the house of Ahab shall perish, and I will cut off from Ahab every one who urinates against the wall, both the bound and the free in Israel.

9 I will give the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah.

10 And the dogs will eat Jezebel in the portion of Jezreel, and there will be no one to bury her; and he opened the door and fled.

11 Then Jehu went out to the servants of his lord and said to them, Why does this madman come to you? And he said to them, You know the man and his talk.

12 They said, "It is a lie!" Tell us now, and he said, "Thus and thus he said to me, saying, 'Thus says the LORD, I have anointed you a king over Israel.'"

13 They quickly took each man his garment and put it under him at the top of the steps, and blew the trumpet and said, "Jehu has become king!"

14 Jehu son of Jehoshaphat son of Nimshi conspired against Joram; and Joram king of Israel was guarding Ramoth-gilead, he and all Israel, from Hazael king of Aram.

15 Then Jehoram returned to recover in Jezreel from the wounds which the Syrians had inflicted on him when he fought against Hazael king of Aram, and Jehu said, "If you desire life, let no fugitive escape from the city to go to Jezreel."

16 Then Jehu rode and went to Jezreel, for Joram was lying there, and Ahaziah king of Judah had gone down to see Joram.

17 And the watchman stood on the tower in Jezreel and saw the company of Jehu as he approached, and he said, “I see a multitude.” Then Jehoram said, “Take the chariot man and go to meet them and say, ‘Do you come in peace?’”

18 A horseman went to meet him, and he said, “Thus says the king, ‘Is it well?’” And Jehu replied, “What have you to do with peace? Turn behind me.” Then the lookout reported, saying, “The messenger has come to them, but he has not returned.”

19 He sent a second horseman, who came to them and said, “Thus says the king: ‘Is it peace?’” And Jehu replied, “What have you to do with peace? Come behind me.”

20 The lookout reported, saying, "He has reached them but has not returned; the driving is like the driving of Jehu son of Nimshi, for he drives furiously."

21 Jehoram said, "Hitch the chariot," and he hitched his chariot. Jehoram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went out, each in his chariot, and they went out to meet Jehu at the field of Naboth the Jezreelite.

22 When Jehoram saw Jehu, he said, "Do you come in peace?" And Jehu replied, "How can there be peace, so long as the whoredom of your mother Jezebel and her many sorceries continues?"

23 Jehoram turned his hands and fled, and he said to Ahaziah, "There is treachery, Ahaziah."

24 Jehu drew his bow and shot Jehoram between the arms, and the arrow went out through his heart, so he sank down in his chariot.

25 Then he said to Bidkar, "Take him and throw him into the field of Naboth the Jezreelite. For I remember, when you and I were riding together behind Ahab his father, that the LORD laid this burden upon him."

26 If not the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons I saw yesterday, says the LORD, then in this field I will repay you, says the LORD; now take and throw him into this field according to the word of the LORD.

27 Ahaziah king of Judah saw and fled along the garden path, and Jehu pursued after him and said also him strike into the chariot at the ascent of Gur which is by Ibleam, and he fled to Megiddo and died there.

28 His servants carried him to Jerusalem and buried him in his grave with his fathers in the city of David.

29 In the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab, Ahaziah became king over Judah.

30 Jehu came to Jezreel, and Jezebel heard; she put paint on her eyes, arranged her head, and looked out the window.

31 And Jehu came into the gate, and the one there said, "Is it peace, Zimri, murderer of his lord?"

32 He lifted up his face toward the window and said, "Who is with me?" And two or three chamberlains looked out at him.

33 And he said, Throw her down. So they threw her down, and he sprinkled her blood upon the wall and upon the horses, and they trampled her.

34 And he came and ate and drank, and said, "Please care for this cursed woman and bury her, for she is a daughter of a king."

35 They went to bury her but found nothing on her except the skull and the legs and the palms of the hands.

36 They returned and reported to him, and he said, "It is the act of the LORD which he spoke by the hand of his servant Elijah the Tishbite, saying, 'In the portion of Jezreel the dogs will eat the flesh of Jezebel.'"

37 The dead body of Jezebel will be like dung upon the face of the field in the portion of Jezreel, so that no one will say, This is Jezebel.


## Chapter 10

1 Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria, and Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the rulers of Jezreel and to the elders, and to the guardians of Ahab's children, saying:

2 And now, when this bill comes to you, and with you the sons of your masters, and with you the chariot and the horses, and a fortified city and the weapons,

3 And you shall see the good and the right from among the sons of your lords, and you shall set him over the throne of his father, and they shall fight for the house of your lords.

4 They greatly, greatly feared and said, "Behold, the two kings did not stand before him; how then can we stand?"

5 He who was over the court and he who was over the city, and the elders and the artisans, sent to Jehu, saying, We are your servants; all that you say to us we will do; we will not make any man king; what is good in your eyes, do.

6 He wrote them a second letter, saying: If you are for me and will listen to my voice, take the heads of the men, the sons of your lords, and come to me at this time tomorrow in Jezreel. Now the king’s sons were seventy men, and the great men of the city were raising them.

7 When the letter came to them, they took the sons of the king and slaughtered seventy men, and they put their heads in the baskets and sent them to him at Jezreel.

8 The messenger came and reported to him, saying, "They have brought the heads of the king's sons"; and he said, "Put them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate until morning."

9 And it was in the morning, and he went out and stood and said to all the people, "You are righteous; behold, I have taken hold of my lord and killed him, but who struck all these?"

10 Know now that no word of the LORD has fallen to the ground which the LORD spoke concerning the house of Ahab, and the LORD has done what he spoke by the hand of his servant Elijah.

11 Jehu struck all the remainder of Ahab's court in Jezreel, and all his nobles and close associates and his priests, until he left no remnant for him.

12 He rose and went and came to Samaria, that shearing house of the shepherds along the way.

13 Jehu came upon the brothers of Ahaziah king of Judah and said, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are the brothers of Ahaziah, and we have come down to greet the sons of the king and the sons of the queen."

14 He said, "Seize them alive," and they seized them alive, and they slaughtered them by the cistern at the shearing house, forty-two men, and no man of them was left.

15 He went from there and found Jehonadab the son of Rechab coming to meet him, and he blessed him and said to him, "Is your heart upright with my heart as my heart is with your heart?" And Jehonadab said, "It is." And he said, "Give me your hand," and he gave him his hand, and he took him up to himself into the chariot.

16 And he said to him, Come with me, and see for the sake of my zeal for the LORD; and they made him ride in his chariot.

17 And he came to Samaria and struck all who were left of Ahab in Samaria, until he destroyed them, according to the word of the LORD which Elijah spoke.

18 Jehu gathered all the people and said to them, Ahab served Baal a little; Jehu will serve him much.

19 Now therefore call to me all the prophets of Baal, all his servants, and all his priests; let no man be absent, for there is a great sacrifice to Baal; whoever is absent shall not live. And Jehu did so with subtilty, in order to destroy the worshippers of Baal.

20 And Jehu said, "Consecrate a solemn assembly for Baal," and they proclaimed it.

21 Jehu sent throughout all Israel, and all the worshippers of Baal came, and not a single man was left who did not come; and they entered the house of Baal, and the house of Baal was filled mouth to mouth.

22 And he said to the one over the vestry, "Bring out apparel for all who serve Baal," and he brought out vestments for them.

23 Jehu and Jehonadab son of Rechab entered the temple of Baal, and he said to the servants of Baal, "Search and see that there is not among you some of the servants of the LORD; only servants of Baal are here."

24 They came to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings, and Jehu was there outside with eighty men, and the man said, "Whoever escapes from the men I bring into your hands, his life for his life."

25 When he had finished making the ascent offering, Jehu said to the runners and the captains, “Come, let no man go out,” and they struck them down by the sword, and the runners and the captains were thrown out, and they went to the city of the house of Baal.

26 They brought out the pillars of the house of Baal and burned them.

27 They broke down the pillar of Baal and destroyed the temple of Baal, and they have left it so to this day.

28 And Jehu destroyed Baal from Israel.

29 Only the faults of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin, did Jehu not turn away from, namely the golden calves that were in Beth-el and that were in Dan.

30 And the LORD said to Jehu, Because you have done what is right in my sight, according to all that was in my mind, to the house of Ahab, sons of the fourth generation shall sit for you upon the throne of Israel.

31 Jehu did not keep the law of the LORD God of Israel with all his heart, nor did he turn from the sins of Jeroboam, who caused Israel to sin.

32 In those days the LORD began to cut Israel short, and Hazael defeated them throughout all the border of Israel.

33 From the Jordan eastward, all the land of Gilead, the Gadites, the Reubenites, and the Manassites, from Aroer which is by the valley of Arnon, and Gilead and Bashan.

34 And the rest of the acts of Jehu, and all that he did, and all his might, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

35 Jehu lay down with his fathers and was buried in Samaria, and Jehoahaz his son reigned in his place.

36 The days that Jehu reigned over Israel was twenty-eight years in Samaria.


## Chapter 11

1 Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah, when the son of her had died, arose and destroyed all the seed of the kingdom.

2 Jehosheba daughter of king Joram, sister of Ahaziah, took Joash son of Ahaziah and stole him from among the sons of the king, him and his nurse, and they hid him in the chamber of the beds from the face of Athaliah, so that he was not put to death.

3 And she was with him in the temple of the LORD, hidden for six years, while Athaliah reigned over the land.

4 In the seventh year Jehoiada summoned the captains of the Cherethites and of the runners and brought them to him into the house of the LORD and made a covenant with them and made them swear in the house of the LORD and showed them the son of the king.

5 And he commanded them, saying, "This is the act that you shall do: one-third of you coming in on the sabbath shall keep the watch of the king's palace."

6 And a third shall be at the gate of Sur, and a third at the gate behind the runners; so you shall keep watch over the house as a guard.

7 and all of you who go out on the sabbath shall be on duty, keeping the watch of the temple for the king.

8 And you shall surround the king, each man with his weapons in hand, and whoever approaches the ranges shall be put to death, and you shall be with the king when he goes out and when he comes in.

9 The captains did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded, and each man took his men, those coming in on the sabbath and those going out on the sabbath, and they came to Jehoiada the priest.

10 The priest gave to the captains the spears and the shields that belonged to King David, which were in the temple of the LORD.

11 The runners stood, each man with his weapon in his hand, from the right side of the temple to the left side of the temple, around the king.

12 They brought out the king's son and set upon him the crown and the testimony; they made him king and anointed him, and they clapped their hands and said, "Long live the king!"

13 And Athaliah heard the sound of the people, and she came to the people at the house of the LORD.

14 And she looked, and behold, the king standing by the pillar after the custom, and the captains and the trumpeters by the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets; and Athaliah tore her garments and cried, Treason, treason!

15 Then Jehoiada the priest commanded the captains of the host and said to them, Bring her forth outside the temple courts, and let him who follows her be put to the sword. For the priest said, Do not let her be slain in the house of the LORD.

16 They laid hands upon her, and she entered by the way of the entry of the horses into the king's house, and she was put to death there.

17 Jehoiada made the confederacy between the LORD and between the king and between the people, to be people to the LORD, and between the king and between the people.

18 Then all the people of the land entered the house of Baal and tore it down; they broke in pieces his pillars and his images, and they thoroughly destroyed them. And Mattan, the priest of Baal, they killed before the altars. Then the priest appointed officers over the house of the LORD.

19 And he took the captains of hundreds, and the Cherethites, and the guards, and all the people of the land, and they brought down the king from the house of the LORD, and came through the gate of the guards to the king's house; and he sat on the throne of the kings.

20 And all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet; and Athaliah they put to death with the sword at the house of the king.

21 Jehoash was seven years old when he began to reign.


## Chapter 12

1 In the year of seven of Jehu, Jehoash reigned, and forty years reigned in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Zibiah of Beer-shebah.

2 Jehoash did what was right in the eyes of the LORD all his days, during which Jehoiada the priest instructed him.

3 Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and making offerings at the high places.

4 Jehoash said to the priests, all the money of the consecrated things that comes into the house of the LORD, money from every man, money for the souls of their estimation, every money that anyone's heart moves him to bring into the house of the LORD,

5 Let the priests, each from his acquaintance, take money and they shall reinforce the breach of the house for all the breaches found there.

6 In the twenty-third year of King Jehoash, the priests had not repaired the breaches of the temple.

7 King Jehoash summoned Jehoiada the priest and the priests and said to them, Why do you not repair the breach of the temple? And now, take no money from your acquaintances, for to the repair of the temple you shall give it.

8 The priests did not take money from the people, nor repair the breach of the temple.

9 Then Jehoiada the priest took a chest and bored a hole in its lid and placed it beside the altar at the entrance into the house of the LORD and the priests who kept the threshold put there all the silver brought into the house of the LORD.

10 When they saw that there was much money in the chest, the king’s scribe and the chief priest went up and bound and counted the money found in the house of the LORD.

11 And they gave the consecrated money to the workers of the work of the house of the LORD, and they brought it out to the carpenters of the wood and to the masons doing the work of the house of the LORD.

12 For the stonecutters and the hewers of stone, and to buy timber and hewn stones to strengthen the breach of the house of the LORD, and for all that would go out for the house for strengthening.

13 Only silver and gold vessels, basins, snuffers, sprinkling bowls, and trumpets shall not be made for the temple of the LORD from the silver brought into the temple of the LORD.

14 For those doing the work it shall be given, and they shall strengthen in the temple of the LORD.

15 They shall not reckon the men who receive the silver on their hand to give to those doing the work, for they are acting faithfully.

16 Silver of guilt offerings and silver of sin offerings shall not be brought into the house of the LORD; it shall belong to the priests.

17 Then Hazael king of Aram went up and fought against Gath and captured it, and Hazael set his face to go up against Jerusalem.

18 Jehoash king of Judah took all the consecrated things that Jehoshaphat and Jehoram and Ahaziah his fathers, kings of Judah, had dedicated, and his own consecrated things, and all the gold found in the treasuries of the house of the LORD and the house of the king, and sent it to Hazael king of Aram, and he withdrew from Jerusalem.

19 And the rest of the acts of Joash, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

20 His servants rose up in conspiracy and struck Joash in the house of Millo, which goes down to Silla.

21 Jozachar son of Shimath and Jehozabad son of Shomer, his servants, struck him and he died, and they buried him with his fathers in the city of David, and Amaziah his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 13

1 In the twenty-third year of Joash son of Ahaziah king of Judah, Jehoahaz son of Jehu became king over Israel in Samaria, and reigned seventeen years.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and walked after the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin; he did not turn from them.

3 The LORD was angry with Israel and delivered them into the hand of Hazael king of Aram and into the hand of Benhadad son of Hazael all those days.

4 Jehoahaz besought the face of the LORD, and the LORD accepted him, because he saw the affliction of Israel, how the king of Aram oppressed them.

5 The LORD gave Israel a deliverer, and they went out from under the power of Aram, and the children of Israel dwelt in their tents as in days of old.

6 Yet they did not turn from the sins of the house of Jeroboam, which he made Israel to commit; also the grove remained standing in Samaria.

7 For there was left to Jehoahaz of the army only fifty horsemen and ten chariots and ten thousand footmen, for the king of Aram destroyed them and made them like dust for trampling.

8 And the rest of the acts of Jehoahaz and all that he did and his might, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

9 Jehoahaz lay down with the forefathers and they buried him in Samaria, and Joash his son reigned in his place.

10 In the thirty-seventh year of Joash king of Judah, Jehoash son of Jehoahaz began to reign over Israel in Samaria for sixteen years.

11 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, and did not turn from any of the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin; he walked in them.

12 And the rest of the acts of Joash and all that he did and his might with which he fought against Amaziah king of Judah are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

13 Joash slept with his fathers, and Jeroboam sat on his throne, and Joash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.

14 And Elisha was sick with the disease by which he would die, and Joash king of Israel went down to him and wept over his face and said, My father, my father, chariot of Israel and horseman thereof.

15 Elisha said to him, Take bow and arrows, and he took to him bow and arrows.

16 He said to the king of Israel, "Put your hand on the bow"; and he put his hand on it, and Elisha put his hands on the hands of the king.

17 Then he said, Open the eastern window; and he opened it. Elisha said, Shoot; and he shot. Then he said, An arrow of deliverance from the LORD, even an arrow of deliverance against Syria; you will strike Syria at Aphek until you have finished them.

18 And he said, Take the arrow; and he took it. And he said to the king of Israel, Strike the ground; and he struck three times and stopped.

19 Then the man of God was angry with him and said, If you had struck the Syrians five or six times, then you would have destroyed Aram completely, but now you will strike the Syrians three times.

20 Elisha died and they buried him, and bands of Moab came into the land in that year.

21 And it came to pass, as they were burying a certain man, that behold, they saw the band; and they cast the man into the sepulchre of Elisha. And when the man touched the bones of Elisha, he lived and rose up on his feet.

22 And Hazael king of Aram oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.

23 And the LORD had compassion on them and relented toward them because of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and was not willing to destroy them, nor to cast them from before his presence until now.

24 Hazael king of Aram died, and Benhadad his son reigned in his place.

25 Jehoash the son of Jehoahaz returned and took the cities from the hand of Benhadad the son of Hazael, which he had taken from the hand of Jehoahaz his father in war; three times Joash struck him and returned the cities of Israel.


## Chapter 14

1 In the year two of Joash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel, Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah began to reign.

2 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned for twenty-nine years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.

3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, only not like David his father; according to all that Joash his father did, he did.

4 Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and making smoke at the high places.

5 When the kingdom was firmly established in his hand, he struck the servants who had struck the king, his father.

6 Yet the sons of the murderers he did not put to death, according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, which the LORD commanded, saying, Fathers shall not be put to death for children, nor children be put to death for fathers, but each man shall die for his own sin.

7 He struck Edom in the valley of salt, ten thousand, and captured the rock in battle, and he named its name Joktheel, as at this day.

8 Amaziah sent ambassadors to Jehoash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu king of Israel, saying, Come, let us face one another.

9 Jehoash king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, "The bramble that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that is in Lebanon, saying, 'Give your daughter to my son as wife,' but the wild animals of the field that were in Lebanon trampled the bramble."

10 Did you strike Edom and then your heart consider you exalted? Stay comfortably in your house. Why should you provoke adversity? Then you and Judah will fall.

11 And Amaziah did not listen, so Jehoash king of Israel went up, and he and Amaziah king of Judah faced one another at Beth-shemesh which belongs to Judah.

12 Judah was struck down before Israel, and each man fled to his tent.

13 And Jehoash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah, at Beth-shemesh, and brought him to Jerusalem; and he broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the gate of Ephraim to the corner gate, four hundred cubits.

14 And he took all the gold and silver and all the vessels found in the house of the LORD and in the storehouses of the king's house and the sons of the hostages; and he returned to Samaria.

15 And the rest of the acts of Jehoash which he did and his might with which he fought against Amaziah king of Judah, are they not written in the book of the words of the days for the kings of Israel?

16 Jehoash slept with his fathers and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel, and Jeroboam his son reigned in his place.

17 Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah lived after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel fifteen years.

18 And the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

19 They formed a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem and he fled to Lachish; they sent after him to Lachish and killed him there.

20 They carried him on the horses and he was buried in Jerusalem with his fathers in the city of David.

21 All the people of Judah took Azariah, and he was sixteen years old, and they made him king instead of his father Amaziah.

22 He built Elath and restored it to Judah after the king lay with his fathers.

23 In the fifteenth year of Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah, Jeroboam son of Joash king of Israel reigned in Samaria forty-one years.

24 He did evil in the sight of the LORD; he did not turn from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel to sin.

25 He restored the border of Israel from the entrance of Hamath to the sea of the Arabah, according to the word of the LORD, the God of Israel, which he spoke by the hand of his servant Jonah the son of Amittai the prophet, who was from Gath-hepher.

26 For the LORD saw the affliction of Israel, very severe indeed, there was none shut up, none left, and no helper for Israel.

27 Yet the LORD did not speak to blot out the name of Israel from under heaven, but saved them by the hand of Jeroboam the son of Joash.

28 And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might with which he fought, and how he brought back Damascus and Hamath to Judah in Israel, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

29 Jeroboam slept with his forefathers, with the kings of Israel, and Zachariah his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 15

1 In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Azariah son of Amaziah king of Judah began to reign.

2 He was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jecholiah of Jerusalem.

3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, according to all that Amaziah his father had done.

4 Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and making offerings at the high places.

5 The LORD struck the king so that he remained a leper until the day of his death, and he lived in a separate house, while Jotham the son of the king was over the palace, ruling the people of the land.

6 And the rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

7 Azariah slept with his fathers and they buried him with his fathers in the city of David and Jotham his son reigned in his place.

8 In the year thirty-eight of Azariah king of Judah, Zachariah son of Jeroboam reigned over Israel in Samaria six months.

9 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like all that his fathers had done; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.

10 And Shallum son of Jobesh struck him before the people and killed him, and reigned in his place.

11 And the rest of the words of Zechariah are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

12 It was the act of the LORD that he spoke to Jehu, saying, "Sons of the fourth generation shall sit for you upon the throne of Israel," and so it came to pass.

13 Shallum son of Jobesh reigned in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah king of Judah, and he reigned one month in Samaria.

14 Menahem the son of Gadi went up from Tirzah and came to Samaria and struck Shallum the son of Jobesh in Samaria and killed him and reigned in his place.

15 The rest of the acts of Shallum and his conspiracy that he conspired are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

16 Then Menahem struck Tipsah and all that was in it and its territory from Tirzah because he did not open to him, and he struck all its pregnant women, splitting them open.

17 In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah king of Judah, Menahem son of Gadi reigned over Israel ten years in Samaria.

18 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, not turning from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin, all his days.

19 Pul king of Assyria came against the land, and Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver to establish his hand with him to strengthen the kingdom in his hand.

20 Menahem exacted the money from Israel upon all the mighty men of the army, to give to the king of Assyria fifty shekels of silver per man, and the king of Assyria turned back and did not remain in the land.

21 And the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

22 Menahem slept with his fathers, and Pekahiah his son reigned in his place.

23 In the year fifty of Azariah king of Judah, Pekahiah son of Menahem reigned over Israel in Samaria two years.

24 He did evil in the sight of the LORD; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.

25 Pekah son of Remaliah, his officer, conspired against him with Argob and Arieh, captains, and fifty men from the Gileadites, and struck him in Samaria in the palace of the king, and killed him, and reigned in his place.

26 And the rest of the acts of Pekahiah and all that he did are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

27 In the fifty-second year of Azariah king of Judah, Pekah son of Remaliah reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty years.

28 He did evil in the sight of the LORD and did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat who caused Israel to sin.

29 In the days of Pekah king of Israel, Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria came and captured Ijon and Abel-beth-maachah and Janoah and Kedesh and Hazor and Gilead and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali, and he carried them to Assyria.

30 Hosea son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah son of Remaliah and struck him and killed him and reigned in his place in the twentieth year of Jotham son of Uzziah.

31 And the rest of the acts of Pekah, and all that he did, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

32 In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah, king of Israel, Jotham son of Uzziah, king of Judah, reigned.

33 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jerusha the daughter of Zadok.

34 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, according to all that Uzziah his father had done.

35 The high places were not removed; the people continued to offer sacrifices and burn incense at the high places. He built the gate of the house of the LORD, the upper one.

36 And the rest of the acts of Jotham which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

37 In those days the LORD began to send Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah against Judah.

38 Jotham slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father, and Ahaz his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 16

1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, Ahaz son of Jotham became king of Judah.

2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem; and he did not do what was right in the eyes of the LORD his God like David his father.

3 He walked along the way of the kings of Israel, and he also made his son pass through the fire, according to the abominations of the nations whom the LORD dispossessed from before the children of Israel.

4 And he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places and on the hills and under every green tree.

5 Then Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel went up to Jerusalem to fight, and they besieged Ahaz but could not fight.

6 At that time Rezin king of Aram restored Elath to Aram and drove the Jews from Elath; the Syrians came to Elath and have lived there to this day.

7 Ahaz sent ambassadors to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, saying, "Your servant and son am I; come up and save me from the power of the king of Aram and from the power of the king of Israel, who rise against me."

8 Ahaz took the silver and the gold that were found in the house of the LORD and in the storehouses of the king's house, and he sent them as a bribe to the king of Assyria.

9 The king of Assyria listened to him, and the king of Assyria marched against Damascus, captured it, deported its people to Kir, and killed Rezin.

10 King Ahaz went to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria at Damascus, and he saw the altar that was at Damascus; and King Ahaz sent to Uriah the priest the fashion of the altar and its likeness according to all its work.

11 Uriah the priest built the altar, according to all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus; so Uriah the priest did, until King Ahaz came from Damascus.

12 The king came from Damascus and the king saw the altar and the king drew near to the altar and went up upon it.

13 He offered up his ascent and his gift, poured out his drink offering, and dashed the blood of his peace offering against the altar.

14 And the brasen altar that was before the LORD, he brought from before the house, from between the altar and the house of the LORD, and placed it on the north side of the altar.

15 King Ahaz said to Uriah the priest, Offer on the great altar the burnt offering of the morning and the grain offering of the evening, and the burnt offering of the king and his grain offering, and the burnt offering of all the people of the land and their grain offerings and their drink offerings; all the blood of burnt offerings and all the blood of sacrifices you shall pour out on it; but the bronze altar shall be for me to inquire.

16 Uriah the priest did just as King Ahaz had commanded.

17 King Ahaz cut up the border bases, removed the laver from them, brought down the sea from upon the bronze bulls which were under it, and set it upon a pavement of stones.

18 And the covered way for the sabbath that had been built in the house, and the entrance of the king which was outside, he removed from the house of the LORD because of the king of Assyria.

19 And the rest of the acts of Ahaz which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

20 Ahaz slept with the fathers and was buried with the fathers in the city of David, and Hezekiah his son reigned in his stead.


## Chapter 17

1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah, Hoshea son of Elah reigned in Samaria over Israel nine years.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, but not like the kings of Israel who were before him.

3 Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria, and Hosea became his servant and brought him tribute.

4 The king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hosea, who had sent messengers to So, king of Egypt, and had not brought gift to the king of Assyria year by year; so the king of Assyria seized him and imprisoned him in a house of confinement.

5 The king of Assyria went up against all the land and went up against Samaria and besieged it for three years.

6 In the ninth year of Hosea, the king of Assyria captured Samaria and exiled Israel to Assyria, and he settled them in Halah and in Habor, river of Gozan, and the cities of Media.

7 The people of Israel sinned against the LORD their God, who had brought them up from the land of Egypt, from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and they feared other gods.

8 They walked in the statutes of the nations whom the LORD dispossessed from before the children of Israel, and in the customs of the kings of Israel.

9 The sons of Israel committed acts that were not right before the LORD their God, and they built for themselves high places in every one of their cities, from the tower of the watchmen to the fortified city.

10 They set up for them pillars and Asherim on every high hill and under every green tree.

11 They sacrificed on every high place like the nations whom the LORD had driven out from before them, and they did evil things to provoke the LORD to anger.

12 They served the idols, concerning which the LORD said to them, "You shall not do this act."

13 The LORD testified against Israel and Judah by the hand of every prophet and seer, saying, "Turn from your evil ways, and keep my commandments and statutes, according to all the law that I commanded your fathers, and that I sent to you by the hand of my servants the prophets."

14 They did not listen, and stiffened their necks like the neck of their forefathers, who did not believe in the LORD their God.

15 They rejected his statutes and the covenant which he made with their fathers, and the testimonies which he testified to them, and they went after vanity and became vain, and after the nations which were around them, whom the LORD had commanded them not to do as they did.

16 They abandoned all the commandments of the LORD their God, and made for themselves a molten calf, and made an Asherah, and worshipped all the host of heaven, and served Baal.

17 They passed their sons and their daughters through the fire, practiced divination, practiced witchcraft, and sold themselves to do evil in the eyes of the LORD to provoke him.

18 The LORD was very angry with Israel and turned them away from his presence; only the tribe of Judah remained alone.

19 Also Judah did not keep the commandments of the LORD their God, and they walked in the statutes of Israel which they did.

20 Jehovah rejected all the seed of Israel and afflicted them and delivered them into the hand of spoilers until He cast them from His presence.

21 For Israel had revolted from the house of David and made Jeroboam son of Nebat king, and Jeroboam led Israel away from following the LORD and caused them to commit a great sin.

22 The sons of Israel walked in all the sins of Jeroboam which he did; they did not turn from them.

23 Until the LORD removed Israel from before his face, as he spoke by the hand of all his servant prophets, and Israel was exiled from their land to Assyria, until this day.

24 The king of Assyria brought [people] from Babylon, and from Cuthah, and from Ava, and from Hamath, and from Sepharvaim, and he settled [them] in the cities of Samaria instead of the children of Israel; and they took possession of Samaria, and dwelt in its cities.

25 At the beginning of their dwelling there, they did not fear the LORD, so the LORD sent against them the lions, and they were killing among them.

26 They said to the king of Assyria, saying, The nations whom you deported and settled in the cities of Samaria do not know the law of the God of the land, so he has sent lions among them, and behold, they are killing them because they do not know the law of the God of the land.

27 Then the king of Assyria commanded, saying: Bring one of the priests whom you deported from there; let him go and dwell there, and let him teach the law of the God of the land.

28 One of the priests who had been exiled from Samaria came and lived in Beth-el and became a teacher to them in how to fear the Lord.

29 Each nation made its own god, and they placed them in the houses of the high places which the Samaritans had made, each nation in the cities where they dwelt.

30 And the men of Babylon made Succothbenoth, and the men of Cuth made Nergal, and the men of Hamath made Ashima.

31 And the Avims made Nibhaz and Tartak, and the Sepharvites burned their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech.

32 They feared the LORD, and the priests of the high places made offerings for them according to their own practices at the shrines.

33 They feared Jehovah, the Lord, and served their gods according to the custom of the nations from which they had been exiled.

34 To this day they do after the former customs, they do not fear the LORD, nor do they after their statutes and their judgments, nor after the law and the commandment which the LORD commanded the sons of Jacob, whose name he called Israel.

35 And the LORD made a covenant with them and charged them, saying, You shall not fear other gods, nor bow down to them, nor serve them, nor sacrifice to them.

36 But you shall fear the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a stretched-out arm, and to him you shall bow down and to him you shall sacrifice.

37 You shall observe the statutes and the ordinances, the law and the commandment that he wrote for you, to do them all the days; you shall not fear other gods.

38 And the covenant that I made with you, you shall not forget, nor shall you fear other gods.

39 But you shall fear the LORD your God, and He will deliver you from the hand of all your enemies.

40 And they did not listen, but they acted according to their former custom.

41 The Gentiles feared the LORD but served their carved images, and so did their children and their children’s children, just as their fathers did, even to this day.


## Chapter 18

1 In the third year of Hosea son of Elah king of Israel, Hezekiah son of Ahaz king of Judah began to reign.

2 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Abi the daughter of Zechariah.

3 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, according to all that David his father had done.

4 He removed the high places, broke the standing images, cut down the grove, and crushed the bronze serpent that Moses had made, for until those days the people of Israel had been burning incense to it, and he called it Nehushtan.

5 In the LORD God of Israel he trusted, and after him there was none like him among all the kings of Judah, nor among those who were before him.

6 He clung to the LORD, did not turn away from following him, and kept the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses.

7 And the LORD was with him, in all that he went out he prospered, and he rebelled against the king of Assyria and did not serve him.

8 He struck the Philistine as far as Gaza and its borders, from the tower of the watchmen to the fortified city.

9 In the fourth year of King Hezekiah, which was the seventh year of Hoshea son of Elah, king of Israel, Shalmaneser king of Assyria went up against Samaria and laid siege to it.

10 And they captured her after three years, in the sixth year of Hezekiah; she was the ninth year of Hoshea king of Israel—Samaria was captured.

11 The king of Assyria exiled Israel to Assyria and settled them in Halah and in Habor, river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.

12 Because they did not listen to the voice of the LORD their God, and they transgressed his covenant, all that Moses the servant of the LORD commanded, and they did not listen nor do.

13 In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and seized them.

14 Hezekiah king of Judah sent to the king of Assyria at Lachish, saying, I have sinned; turn back from me; whatever you impose on me I will bear. And the king of Assyria imposed on Hezekiah king of Judah three hundred talents of silver and thirty talents of gold.

15 Hezekiah gave all the silver found in the house of the LORD and in the storehouses of the house of the king.

16 At that time Hezekiah cut up the doors of the temple of the LORD and the doorposts that Hezekiah king of Judah had overlaid, and gave them to the king of Assyria.

17 The king of Assyria sent Tartan, Rab-saris, and Rabshakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah with a heavy army to Jerusalem; they went up and came to Jerusalem, went up and came and stood at the conduit of the upper fish-pool, which is on the causeway of the fuller's field.

18 They called to the king, and Eliakim son of Hillkiah came out to them, who was over the house, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph the recorder.

19 Then Rabshakeh said to them, "Tell Hezekiah, 'Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria, "What is this confidence in which you trust?"'"

20 You said, "Only the act of my lips—advice and might for war"—now on whom have you relied that you have rebelled against me?

21 Now look, you have relied on the staff of this broken reed, Egypt, which if a man leans on it, it will go into his hand and pierce him; so is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who rely on him.

22 But if you say to me, “We have trusted in the LORD our God,” is it not he who removed the high places and the altars of Hezekiah, and who said to Judah and to Jerusalem, “Before this altar in Jerusalem you shall prostrate yourselves”?

23 And now, please mediate, my lord, with King Assyria, and I will give you two thousand horses, if you are able to provide for yourself riders for them.

24 How can you turn back the face of one governor, the least of my lord's servants, and rely on Egypt for chariots and horsemen?

25 Now have I come against the command of the Lord to destroy this place? The Lord said to me, Go up against this land and destroy it.

26 Eliakim son of Hillkiah, and Shebna, and Joah said to the Rabshakeh, "Speak, please, to your servants in the Syrian language, for we understand; but do not speak to us in the Jews' language in the hearing of the people who are on the wall."

27 Rabshakeh said to them, "Is it not to your lord and to you that my lord has sent me to speak these words? Are they not for the men sitting on the wall, to eat their own dung and drink their own piss with you?"

28 Then Rabshakeh stood and called aloud in the Jews' language, and he spoke and said, "Hear the word of the great king, the king of Assyria."

29 Thus says the king: Do not let Hezekiah mislead you, for he will not be able to deliver you from his hand.

30 Do not let Hezekiah make you trust in the Lord by saying, The Lord will surely deliver us, and this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.

31 Do not listen to Hezekiah, for thus says the king of Assyria: make peace with me and come out to me, and each man will eat from his vine and from his fig tree, and each man will drink water from his cistern.

32 Until I come and take you to a land like your own land, a land of wheat and new wine, a land of bread and vineyards, a land of olive trees and anointed oil, and you live and do not die; and do not listen to Hezekiah, for he will mislead you, saying, "The LORD will deliver us."

33 Has any of the gods of the nations ever delivered his land from the hand of the king of Assyria?

34 Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivah? Have they delivered Samaria from my hand?

35 Who among all the gods of the lands has delivered his land from my hand, that the LORD should deliver Jerusalem from my hand?

36 And the folk held their peace and answered him not a word, for the commandment of the king was to say "Do not answer him."

37 Eliakim son of Hillkiah, who was over the house, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their garments torn and told him the words of the Rabshakeh.


## Chapter 19

1 When King Hezekiah heard, he tore his garments, put on sackcloth, and went into the house of the LORD.

2 And he sent Eliakim who was over the court, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, wearing sackcloth, to the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz.

3 They said to him, "Thus says Hezekiah, 'This day is a day of distress, of rebuke, and of blasphemy, for children have come to the point of birth, but there is no strength for delivery.'"

4 Perhaps the LORD your God will hear all the words of Rabshakeh, whom the king of Assyria his lord has sent to taunt the living God, and will rebuke him for the words that the LORD your God has heard; therefore offer prayer for the remnant that is left.

5 And the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah.

6 Isaiah said to them, "Thus you shall say to your lord: Thus says the LORD, Do not fear the words that you have heard, which the young men of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me."

7 I am giving in him air, and he will hear news and return to his land, and I will strike him down with the sword in his land.

8 Then Rabshakeh returned and found the king of Assyria fighting against Libnah, for he had heard that he had departed from Lachish.

9 Then Tirhakah king of Chush heard, saying, Behold, He has gone out to war against you; so he returned and sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying:

10 Thus you shall say to Hezekiah king of Judah: Do not let your God in whom you trust deceive you by saying, Jerusalem will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.

11 Behold, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, to destroy them utterly; will you indeed be delivered?

12 Did the gods of the nations whom my fathers served—Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the children of Eden who are in Telassar—deliver them?

13 Where is the king of Hamath and the king of Arpad and the king of the town Sepharvaim, Hena and Ivah?

14 Hezekiah took the letters from the hand of the messengers and read them, and Hezekiah went up to the house of the LORD and spread the letters before the LORD.

15 Hezekiah prayed before the LORD and said, the LORD God of Israel, enthroned above the cherubim, you are the God alone of all the kingdoms of the earth, you made the heavens and the earth.

16 Incline Your ear, O LORD, and hear; open Your eyes, O LORD, and see; and hear the words of Sennacherib which he sent to taunt the living God.

17 Of a truth, O LORD, the kings of Assyria have destroyed the nations and their land.

18 They will give their gods into the fire, for they are not gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone, and they will destroy them.

19 And now, O LORD our God, save us, we pray, from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that You, the LORD, are God alone.

20 Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying: Thus says the LORD, God of Israel, to whom you have prayed concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria: I have heard.

21 This is the act that the LORD has spoken concerning him: She has despised you, she has mocked you, the virgin daughter of Zion. Behind you the daughter of Jerusalem has shaken her head.

22 Whom have you defied and blasphemed, and against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes in pride against the Holy One of Israel?

23 By your messengers you have taunted my Lord, and said, “With my chariot I have ascended the heights of the mountains, the farthest sides of Lebanon; I will cut down the height of its cedars, the choicest of its firs; I will enter the lodging of its farthest end, the forest of its fruitful field.”

24 I have called and drunk foreign water and I dried up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.

25 Have I not long ago decreed it, from ancient times I planned it? Now I have brought it to pass, and it shall become waves of destruction, fenced cities laid waste.

26 Their inhabitants, though they seemed strong, were powerless and shamed; they became like grass of the field, and green herbs, like grass on the housetops, scorched before the standing corn.
