27 I have known your sitting down and your going out and your coming in, and your rage against me.

28 Because you have raged against me and your arrogance has come up into my hearing, I will put my hook in your nose and my bridle on your lips, and I will turn you back by the way you came.

29 This shall be the mark for you: you will eat this year what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs of the same; but in the third year, sow and reap, plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.

30 And the remnant of the house of Judah that is left shall take root below and bear fruit above.

31 For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant, and a band of survivors from the mount of Zion; the zeal of the LORD shall do this.

32 Therefore, thus says the LORD to the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come against it with shield, nor cast up a bank against it.

33 Along the way by which he came, he shall return, and to this city he shall not come, says the LORD.

34 And I will defend this city to deliver it, for my sake and for the sake of David my servant.

35 And it came to pass that night, that an ambassador of the LORD went out and struck in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred eighty-five thousand, and they arose in the morning and behold all of them were dead bodies.

36 So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed and went and returned and dwelt in Nineveh.

37 And he was worshipping in the court of Nisroch his gods, and Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him with the sword, and they fled into the land of Ararat, and Esar-haddon his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 20

1 In those days Hezekiah became mortally ill, and Isaiah son of Amoz the prophet came to him and said to him, “Thus says the LORD, ‘Set your house in order, for you shall die and not live.’”

2 He turned his face toward the wall and prayed to the LORD, saying:

3 O Jehovah, I beseech thee, remember now how I have walked before thee in truth and with an upright heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Hezekiah wept with a great weeping.

4 And it came to pass, when Isaiah son of Amoz had not yet gone out into the city, that the word of the LORD came to him, saying:

5 Return and say to Hezekiah prince of my people Thus says the LORD God of David your father I have heard your prayer I have seen your tears Behold I will heal you on the third day you will go up to the house of the LORD

6 I will add fifteen years to your life and deliver you and this city from the power of the king of Assyria, and I will defend this city for my sake and for the sake of David my servant.

7 Isaiah said, "Take a cake of figs," and they took and laid upon the boil, and he lived.

8 Hezekiah said to Isaiah, "What is the sign that the LORD will heal me, and that I will go up to the house of the LORD on the third day?"

9 Isaiah said, This is the sign for you from the LORD, that the LORD will do the act which he has spoken: the shadow will go forward ten steps or return ten steps.

10 Hezekiah said, "It is a light thing for the shade to go forward ten steps; but let the shade return backward ten steps."

11 Isaiah the prophet called to the LORD, and he brought back the shade on the steps, where it had gone down on the steps of Ahaz, ten steps backward.

12 At that time Berodach-baladan son of Baladan king of Babylon sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.

13 Hezekiah heard them and saw their whole house of precious things—the silver, the gold, the spices, the fine oil, the house of his vessels, and all that was found in his storehouses—there was no thing that Hezekiah did not show them in his house and in all his dominion.

14 Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah, and said to him, "What did these men say, and from where did they come to you?" And Hezekiah said, "They came to me from a distant land, from Babylon."

15 He said, "What have they seen in your house?" And Hezekiah said, "All that is in my house they have seen; there was no thing that I did not show them among my treasures."

16 Isaiah said to Hezekiah, "Hear the word of the LORD."

17 Behold, days are coming when all that is in your house and all that your fathers have stored up until this day will be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the LORD.

18 And from your children who will come forth from you, whom you will father, he will take, and they will be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

19 Hezekiah said to Isaiah, "Good is the word of the LORD which you have spoken," and he said, "Is it not good if peace and truth will be in my days?"

20 And the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and all his might and what he did with the fish-pool and the conduit and how he brought the water into the city, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

21 Hezekiah slept with the fathers, and Manasseh his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 21

1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Hephzi-bah.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like the abominations of the nations whom the LORD dispossessed from before the children of Israel.

3 He returned and rebuilt the high places that Hezekiah his father had destroyed, and he set up altars for Baal and made a grove, as Ahab king of Israel had done, and he bowed down to all the host of the heavens and served them.

4 And he built altars in the temple of the LORD, of whom it was said, "In Jerusalem I will set my name."

5 And he built altars for all the host of the heavens in the two courts of the house of the LORD.

6 and passed his child through the fire and practiced divination and sorcery and consulted a familiar spirit and wizards, greatly increasing to do evil in the sight of the LORD to provoke him.

7 And he set the carved (graven) image, which he had made, in the temple, of which the LORD said to David and to Solomon his son, "In this temple and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will put my name forever."

8 I will no longer cause the foot of Israel to wander from the land that I gave to their fathers, only if they will keep to do according to all that I commanded them and to all the law that my servant Moses commanded them.

9 And they did not listen, and Manasseh led them to do evil more than the nations whom the LORD destroyed from before the children of Israel.

10 The LORD spoke by means of his servants the prophets, saying:

11 Because Manasseh king of Judah committed these abominable things, he did evil more than all that the Amorites had done who were before him, and he also caused Judah to sin with his idols.

12 Therefore, thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Behold, I am bringing adversity upon Jerusalem and Judah, so that the hearing of both ears will tingle.

13 I will stretch over Jerusalem the line of Samaria and the plummet of the house of Ahab, and I will wipe Jerusalem as one wipes a dish, wiping and overturning it upon its face.

14 I will forsake the remnant of my heritage and deliver them into the hand of their enemies, and they shall become spoil and plunder to all their enemies.

15 Because they have done evil in my sight and have provoked me to anger, from the day their fathers came out of Egypt even to this day.

16 And also innocent blood did Manasseh shed very much, until he filled Jerusalem mouth to mouth, besides his sin whereby he caused Judah to sin, to do evil in the eyes of the LORD.

17 And the rest of the acts of Manasseh and all that he did and his sin that he sinned, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

18 Manasseh slept with his forefathers and was buried in the garden of his house, in the garden of Uzza, and Amon his son reigned in his place.

19 Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem; the name of his mother was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.

20 He did evil in the eyes of the LORD, as Manasseh his father had done.

21 He walked in all the way that his father walked, and served the idols that his father served, and bowed down to them.

22 He abandoned Jehovah, the God of his fathers, and did not walk in the way of the LORD.

23 And the bondmen of Amon conspired against him, and killed the king in his house.

24 The folk of the land struck all the conspirators against King Amon, and the folk of the land made Josiah, his son, king in his stead.

25 And the rest of the acts of Amon which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

26 And they buried him in his burial place in the garden of Uzza, and Josiah his son reigned in his stead.


## Chapter 22

1 Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem; and the name of his mother was Jedidah the daughter of Adaiah of Bozcath.

2 He did what was right in the sight of the LORD and walked in all the way of David his father, and did not turn aside to the right or to the left.

3 In the eighteenth year of King Josiah, the king sent the scribe Shaphan son of Azaliah son of Meshullam to the house of the LORD, saying:

4 Go up to Hilkiah the high priest and have him count the money brought into the temple of the Lord, which the gatekeepers have collected from the people.

5 by the hand of the workers of the work appointed by the LORD and they shall give it to the workers who are in the house of the LORD to repair the breach of the house

6 For the artificers and for the craftsmen and for the masons, and to buy timber and hewed stone to strengthen the house.

7 Only the money given into their hand shall not be reckoned with them, for they work in faithfulness.

8 Hillkiah the high priest said to the scribe Shaphan, "The book of the law I have found in the house of the LORD"; and Hillkiah gave the book to Shaphan, and he read it.

9 Then Shaphan the scribe came to the king and returned to the king word and said, Your bondmen have emptied the silver found in the house and have delivered it into the hand of the doers of the work, the overseers of the house of the LORD.

10 Shaphan the scribe declared to the king, saying, A book has been given to me by Hillkiah the priest, and Shaphan read it before the king.

11 When the king heard the words of the book of the law, he tore his garments.

12 The king commanded Hillkiah the priest, and Ahikam son of Shaphan, and Achbor son of Micah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah servant of the king, saying:

13 Go, inquire for the LORD on behalf of the people and on behalf of all Judah concerning the words of the book that has been found, for great is the anger of the LORD that has been kindled against us because our ancestors did not obey the words of this book to do according to all that is written upon us.

14 Hillkiah the priest, Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah went to Huldah the prophetess, wife of Shallum son of Tikvah son of Harhas, keeper of the wardrobe, who lived in Jerusalem in the college, and they spoke to her.

15 And she said to them, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, Say to the man who sent you to me:

16 Thus says the LORD, Behold, I am bringing adversity upon this place and upon its inhabitants, even all the words of the book which the king of Judah has read.

17 Because they have forsaken me and burned incense to other gods, to provoke me by all the works of their hands, my wrath will burn in this place and not be quenched.

18 To the king of Judah who sent you to inquire of the LORD, thus shall you say to him: Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: As for the words which you have heard,

19 Because your heart was tender and you humbled yourself before the LORD when you heard what I said concerning this place and concerning its inhabitants, that it should become desolation and a curse, and you tore your garments and wept before me, I also have heard, declares the LORD.

20 Therefore, behold, I will gather you to your forefathers, and you shall be gathered to your graves in peace; and your eyes shall not see all the evil that I will bring upon this place. So they returned the answer to the king.


## Chapter 23

1 Then the king sent and gathered to him all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

2 The king went up to the temple of the LORD, and all the people of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem with him, and the priests and the prophets and all the people, both small and great; and he read in their hearing all the words of the book of the covenant that was found in the temple of the LORD.

3 The king stood by the pillar and made the covenant before the LORD to follow the LORD and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes with all heart and with all soul, to establish the words of this covenant written in this book, and all the people stood by the covenant.

4 The king commanded Hilkiah the chief priest and the second priests and the keepers of the threshold to bring out of the temple of the LORD all the vessels made for Baal and for the grove and for all the host of heaven, and he burned them outside Jerusalem in the fields of Kidron, and carried the ashes to Beth-el.

5 And he removed the Chemarims (idolatrous) priests whom the kings of Judah had appointed, who burned incense in the high places in the cities of Judah and in the places round about Jerusalem, and those who burned incense to Baal, to the sun and to the moon and to the planets and to all the host of heaven.

6 He brought out the grove from the temple of the LORD outside Jerusalem to the brook Kidron, and he burned it at the brook Kidron and beat it to dust, and he scattered its dust upon the grave of the common people.

7 And he tore down the houses of the shrines which were in the house of the LORD, which the women wove there for the grove.

8 He brought all the priests from the cities of Judah and defiled the high places where the priests made offerings, from Geba to Beer-shebah, and he tore down the high places at the gates that were at the entrance of the gate of Joshua, captain of the city, which was on the left side of a man at the gate of the city.

9 Only the priests of the high places shall not ascend to the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem, but shall eat unleavened bread among their brothers.

10 And he defiled Topheth, which is in the valley of Hinnom, so that no man might make his son or his daughter pass through the fire to Molech.

11 He removed the horses that the kings of Judah had given to the sun from the entrance of the house of the LORD, to the chamber of Nathanmelech the king, the chamberlain who was in Parbar, and he burned the chariots of the sun with fire.

12 And the altars which were on the roof, the upper chamber of Ahaz, which the kings of Judah had made, and the altars which Manasseh had made in the two courts of the house of the LORD, the king tore down; and he went from there and threw their dust into the brook Kidron.

13 And the high places that were before Jerusalem, which were on the right side of the mount of destruction, that Solomon king of Israel had built for Ashtoreth the abomination of the Sidonians, and for Chemosh the abomination of Moab, and for Milcom the abomination of the children of Ammon—these the king defiled.

14 and he broke in pieces the standing images, and cut down the groves, and filled their places with human bones.

15 And also the altar which was at Beth-el and the high place that Jeroboam son of Nebat had made, who caused Israel to sin—he both destroyed that altar and the high place, burned the high place to ashes, and burned the grove.

16 Then Josiah turned and saw the burying places that were there on the hill, and he sent and took the bones from the graves, and burned them on the altar and defiled it, according to the word of the LORD which the man of God proclaimed, who proclaimed these words.

17 Then he said, "What is the sign that I see?" And the men of the city said to him, "The grave of the man of God who came from Judah; he proclaimed the words that you have done upon the altar at Beth-el."

18 So he said, “Let him alone; let no man trouble his bones.” So they let his bones alone, those bones of the prophet who had come from Samaria.

19 And also the high places in the cities of Samaria, which the kings of Israel had made to provoke, Josiah removed, and did to them just as he had done to Beth-el.

20 He sacrificed all the priests of the high places who were there upon the altars, and he burned human bones upon them, and returned to Jerusalem.

21 The king commanded all the people, saying: Keep the passover offering for the LORD your God, as it is written in this book of the covenant.

22 For we did not make the passover offering like this passover offering from the days of the judges who judged Israel and all the days of the kings of Israel and the kings of Judah.

23 But in the eighteenth year of King Josiah's reign, this Passover offering was made to the LORD in Jerusalem.

24 And also the familiar spirits, and the wizards, and the images, and the idols, and all the abominable filth which were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem, Josiah burned, to fulfill the words of the law written in the book that Hillkiah the priest found in the house of the LORD.

25 And there was no king like him who turned to the LORD with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his might, according to all the law of Moses; and after him there arose no king like him.

26 Only Jehovah did not turn from the fierceness of His anger, with which His anger burned against Judah, because of all the provocations with which Manasseh provoked Him.

27 And the LORD said, I will also cast off Judah from before my face, just as I cast off Israel, and I reject the city Jerusalem which I chose and the temple where I said my name should be.

28 And the rest of the acts of Josiah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

29 In his days Pharaohnechoh king of Egypt went up against the king of Assyria to the river Euphrates and king Josiah went to meet him and he killed him at Megiddo when he saw him.

30 His servants carried him dead from Megiddo and brought him to Jerusalem and buried him in his grave and the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah and anointed him and made him king instead of his father.

31 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem; the name of his mother was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

32 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his fathers had done.

33 Pharaohnecho imprisoned him at Riblah in the land of Hamath and imposed on the land a tribute of one hundred talents of silver and one talent of gold.

34 Pharaohnecho made Eliakim son of Josiah reign instead of Josiah his father, and he changed his name to Jehoiakim; and he took Jehoahaz and went to Egypt, and he died there.

35 Jehoiakim gave money and gold to Pharaoh, but he taxed the land to give the money according to Pharaoh's command; each man according to his taxation he imposed on the people of the land to give to Pharaoh Necho.

36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Zebudah daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah.

37 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his fathers had done.


## Chapter 24

1 In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up, and Jehoiakim became his servant for three years; then he turned and rebelled against him.

2 The LORD sent against him bands of Chaldeans and bands of Arameans and bands of Moabites and bands of Ammonites, and dispatched them against Judah to destroy it, according to the word of the LORD spoken by the hand of his prophets.

3 Only according to the command of the LORD did this happen in Judah, to remove from his presence because of the sins of Manasseh, according to all that he had done.

4 And also the blood of the innocent which he shed, and filled Jerusalem with innocent blood, and the LORD was not willing to forgive.

5 And the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

6 Jehoiakim slept with the fathers and Jehoiachin his son reigned in his place.

7 And the king of Egypt did not add again to go out from his land, for the king of Babylon had taken from the brook of Egypt to the river Euphrates, all that had been to the king of Egypt.

8 Jehoiachin was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem; and the name of his mother was Nehushta, daughter of Elnathan of Jerusalem.

9 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his father had done.

10 At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came against Jerusalem, and the city was besieged.

11 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came against the city and his servants laid siege to it.

12 Jehoiachin king of Judah went out to the king of Babylon, he and his mother and his servants and his captains and his officers, and the king of Babylon took him in the eighth year of his reign.

13 And he brought out from there all the treasures of the house of the LORD and the treasures of the house of the king, and he cut in pieces all the vessels of gold that Solomon king of Israel had made in the temple of the LORD, as the LORD had spoken.

14 And he exiled all Jerusalem and all the captains and all the mighty men of valour, ten thousand exiles, and all the craftsmen and the smiths; none remained except the poorest of the people of the land.

15 And he exiled Jehoiachin to Babylon, and the king's mother, and the king's wives, and his chamberlains, and the nobles of the land he carried away into captivity from Jerusalem to Babylon.

16 And all the men of war, seven thousand, and the carpenters and the smiths, one thousand, all of them mighty warriors skilled in battle, the king of Babylon brought into captivity to Babylon.

17 The king of Babylon made Mattaniah, his uncle, king in his place, and changed his name to Zedekiah.

18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; the name of his mother was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

19 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.

20 Because of the anger of the LORD this happened in Jerusalem and in Judah, until he cast them from his presence; and Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.


## Chapter 25

1 In the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came with all his army against Jerusalem, and he camped against it; and they built a fort all around it.

2 The city was in the siege until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.

3 In the ninth month the famine was severe in the city and there was no food for the people of the land.

4 The city was broken into, and all the men of war fled by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, which was by the king's garden, and the Chaldeans were around the city, and they went by the way of the Arabah.

5 The army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king and overtook him in the plains of Jericho, and all his army was scattered from about him.

6 They seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah, and they spoke with him in judgment.

7 And they slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and they blinded the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him in bronze fetters, and brought him to Babylon.

8 In the fifth month, on the seventh day of the month, in the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan captain of the guards, a servant of the king of Babylon, came to Jerusalem.

9 He burned the temple of the LORD and the royal palace, and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every great house he burned with fire.

10 And all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guards broke down the walls of Jerusalem all around.

11 And the remnant of the people who were left in the city and those who had defected to the king of Babylon and the rest of the multitude—Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard exiled.

12 And the poorest of the land the captain of the guard left to be vinedressers and plowmen.

13 And the Chaldeans broke the brasen pillars that were in the temple of the LORD, and the bases and the brasen sea that was in the temple of the LORD; and they carried the brass of them to Babylon.

14 And the pots, and the shovels, and the snuffers, and the spoons, and all the vessels of bronze with which they ministered, they took.

15 And the censers and the basins, those of gold he took as gold, and those of silver as silver—the captain of the guards took.

16 The two pillars, the one sea, and the bases that Solomon made for the temple of the LORD: the weight of the brass for all these vessels was not measured.

17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, and a brass chapiter was upon it, and the height of the chapiter was three cubits, with a wreathenwork, and pomegranates upon the chapiter all around, all of brass; and so for the second pillar, according to the wreathenwork.

18 The captain of the guards took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the threshold.

19 From the city he took one chamberlain who had charge over the men of the war, and five men from among the princes of the king who were found in the city, and the scribe, the chief of the army, who mustered the people of the land, and sixty men of the people of the land who were found in the city.

20 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guards took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.

21 The king of Babylon struck them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and Judah was exiled from his land.

22 And the folk who remained in the land of Judah, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had left, he set over them Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan.

23 All the captains of the forces and the men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah, and they came to Gedaliah at Mizpah—Ishmael son of Nethaniah, Johanan son of Careah, Seraiah son of Tanhumeth the Netophathite, and Jaazaniah son of the Maachathite, they and their men.

24 Gedaliah swore to them and to their men, and said to them, "Do not fear the servants of the Chaldeans; dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it will go well with you."

25 In the seventh month, Ishmael son of Nethaniah son of Elishama, of the royal seed, came with ten men, and they struck Gedaliah and he died, and the Jews and the Chaldeans who were with him at Mitspah.

26 And all the people, from the least to the greatest, and the captains of the forces, arose and went down to Egypt, for they were afraid because of the presence of the Chaldeans.

27 In the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the year of his reign, lifted up the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah out of the house of prison.

28 And he spoke good things to him and gave his seat above the seat of the kings who were with him in Babylon.

29 and he shall despise the apparel of prison, and shall eat bread continually before him all the days of his life.

30 His allowance was a continual allowance given to him from the king, a daily provision every day of his life.


# 1 CHRONICLES

## Chapter 1

1 Adam Seth Enos.

2 Cainan was the son of Mahalaleel, who was the son of Jared.

3 Enoch begot Methuselah; Methuselah begot Lamech.

4 Noah, Sem, Ham, and Japheth.

5 The sons of Japheth: Gomer and Magog and Madai and Javan and Tubal and Meshech and Tiras.

6 The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Diphath, and Togarmah.

7 The sons of Javan were Elishah, Tarshish, Chittim, and Rodanim.

8 The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.

9 The sons of Cush were Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raamah, and Sabtechah; and the sons of Raamah were Sheba and Dedan.

10 And Cush became the father of Nimrod; he began to be a mighty one in the land.

11 Egypt became the father of Ludim and of Anamim and of Lehabim and of Naptuhim.

12 And the Pathrusim and the Casluhim, from whom the Philistine came forth, and the Caphthorim.

13 Canaan fathered Sidon his eldest son and Heth.

14 and the Jebusite and the Amorite and the Girgashite

15 and the Hivite and the Arkite and the Sinite

16 and the Arvadite and the Zemarite and the Hamathite.

17 The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram; and Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.

18 Arphaxad fathered Salah, and Salah fathered Eber.

19 To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and the name of his brother was Joktan.

20 Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, and Jerah.

21 and Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah.

22 and Ebal and Abimael and Sheba.

23 And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these are sons of Joktan.

24 Shem, Arphaxad, Shelah.

25 Eber, Peleg, Reu.

26 Serug, Nahor, Terah,

27 Abram, he is Abraham.

28 The sons of Abraham: Isaac and Ishmael.

29 These are the generations of them: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth, and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mibsam.

30 Mishma, and Dumah, Massa, Hadad, and Tema,

31 Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah: these are the sons of Ishmael.

32 The sons of Keturah, Abraham’s concubine, were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah; and the sons of Jokshan were Sheba and Dedan.

33 The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Enoch, Abida, and Eldaah; all these were sons of Keturah.

34 Abraham became the father of Isaac; the sons of Isaac were Esau and Israel.

35 The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

36 The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, Kenaz, Timnah, and Amalek.

37 The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.

38 The sons of Seir were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.

39 And the sons of Lotan were Hori and Homam, and Lotan's sister was Timna.

40 The sons of Shobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam; and the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.

41 The sons of Anah were Dishon, and the sons of Dishon were Amran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.

42 The sons of Ezer: Bilhan and Zaavan and Jaakan; the sons of Dishon: Uz and Aran.

43 These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before a king reigned over the children of Israel: Bela son of Beor, and the name of his city was Dinhaban.

44 Bela died, and Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.

45 And Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

46 Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad reigned in his place, he who struck Midian in the field of Moab, and the name of his city was Avith.

47 Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.

48 And Samlah died, and Saul reigned in his place, from Rehoboth of the river.

49 Saul died, and Baal-hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.

50 Baal-hanan died, and Hadad reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Pai, and the name of his wife was Mehetabeel daughter of Matred daughter of Mezahab.

51 Hadad died, and the captains of Edom became captain Timna, captain Alvah, captain Jetheth.

52 Duke Aholibamah, duke Elah, duke Pinon.

53 captain Kenaz, captain Teman, captain Mibzar.

54 Captain Magdiel, captain Iram—these are the captains of Edom.


## Chapter 2

1 These are the sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah, Issachar and Zebulun.

2 Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.

3 The sons of Judah were Er and Onan and Shelah, three born to him from Shua the Canaanite, and Er the firstborn of Judah was evil in the sight of the LORD, so he killed him.

4 Tamar his daughter-in-law bore to him Perez and Zerah, all the sons of Judah five.

5 The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul.

6 The sons of Zerah: Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara; all of them five.

7 And the sons of Carmi: Achar, who troubled Israel, he who trespassed against the devoted thing.

8 And the sons of Ethan were Azariah.

9 And the sons of Hezron who were born to him were Jerahmeel, Ram, and Chelubai.

10 Ram became the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab became the father of Nahshon, prince of the people of Judah.

11 Naashon fathered Salma, and Salma fathered Boaz.

12 Boaz fathered Obed, and Obed fathered Jesse.

13 And Jesse fathered his firstborn, Eliab, and Abinadab the second, and Shimea the third.

14 Nethaneel the fourth, Raddai the fifth.

15 Ozem the sixth, David the seventh.

16 Zeruiah and Abigal, and the sons of Zeruiah: Abishai, Joab, and Asahel—three.

17 Abigal bore Amasa, and the father of Amasa was Jether the Ishmaelite.

18 Caleb son of Hezron fathered Azubah the wife and Jerioth, and these were her sons: Jesher and Shobab and Ardon.

19 And Azubah died, so Caleb took Ephrath as wife, and she bore to him Hur.

20 Hur became the father of Uri, and Uri became the father of Bezaleel.

21 Afterward Hezron went in to the daughter of Machir, the father of Gilead, and he took her, and he was sixty years old, and she bore to him Segub.

22 Segub fathered Jair, and he had twenty-three towns in the land of Gilead.

23 Geshur and Aram took the towns of Jair from them, Kenath and its daughters, sixty towns; all these were sons of Machir, father of Gilead.

24 After Hezron died in Caleb-ephrathah, his wife Abijah bore to him Ashur, father of Tekoa.

25 The sons of Jerahmeel, the firstborn, were Ram the firstborn, and Bunah, and Oren, and Ozem, and Ahiah.

26 Another wife of Jerahmeel was named Atarah; she was the mother of Onam.

27 The sons of Ram, the firstborn of Jerahmeel, were Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.

28 The sons of Onam were Shammai and Jada, and the sons of Shammai were Nadab and Abishur.

29 The name of Abishur’s wife was Abihail, and she bore to him Ahban and Molid.

30 And the sons of Nadab were Seled and Appaim; and Seled died without sons.

31 The sons of Appaim were Ishi, and the sons of Ishi were Sheshan, and the sons of Sheshan were Ahlai.

32 The sons of Jada, brother of Shammai, were Jether and Jonathan; Jether died without sons.

33 The sons of Jonathan were Peleth and Zaza; these were sons of Jerahmeel.

34 Sheshan had no sons, only daughters, and Sheshan had an Egyptian servant whose name was Jarha.

35 Sheshan gave his daughter to Jarha his servant as a wife, and she bore him Attai.

36 And Attai fathered Nathan, and Nathan fathered Zabad.

37 Zabad fathered Ephlal, and Ephlal fathered Obed.

38 Obed fathered Jehu, and Jehu fathered Azariah.

39 Azariah fathered Helez, and Helez fathered Elasah.

40 Elasah begot Sisamai, and Sisamai begot Shallum.

41 Shallum fathered Jekamiah, and Jekamiah fathered Elishama.

42 And the sons of Caleb, brother of Jerahmeel, were Mesha his firstborn, who was father of Ziph, and the sons of Mareshah, father of Hebron.

43 And the sons of Hebron: Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.

44 Shema fathered Raham, father of Jorkeam, and Rekem fathered Shammai.

45 And the son of Shammai was Maon, and Maon was the father of Beth-zur.

46 Ephah, the concubine of Caleb, bore Haran, Moza, and Gazez; and Haran became the father of Gazez.

47 And the sons of Jehdai: Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.

48 The concubine of Caleb, Maacah, bore Sheber and Tirhanah.

49 And Shaaph fathered Madmannah; Sheva fathered Machbenah and was father of Gibeah, and the daughter of Caleb was Achsah.

50 These were the sons of Caleb, son of Hur, firstborn of Ephrathah: Shobal, father of Kirjath-jearim.

51 Salma father of Bethlehem, Hareph father of Beth-gader.

52 Sons were born to Shobal, the chief of Kirjath-jearim, Haroeh: half of the Manahethites.

53 And families of Kirjath-jearim: the Ithrite, and the Puhites, and the Shumathites, and the Mishraites; from these came forth the Zorathites and the Eshtaulites.

54 The sons of Salma: Bethlehem and Netophathite, Ataroth the house of Joab, and half of the Manahethites, the Zorathites.

55 The families of the scribes: Jabez, the Tirathites, the Shimeathites, the Suchathites. These are the Kenites who came from Hammath, the chief of the house of Rechab.


## Chapter 3

1 These were the sons born to David in Hebron: the firstborn Amnon by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, the second Daniel by Abigal the Carmelitess.

2 The third was Abishalom the son of Maacah, daughter of Talmai king of Geshur; the fourth was Adonijah the son of Haggith.

3 The fifth was Shephatiah for Abital; the sixth Ithream for Eglah his wife.

4 Six were born to him in Hebron, and he reigned there seven years and six months, and thirty and three years he reigned in Jerusalem.

5 These were born to him in Jerusalem: Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon, four, to Bath-shua the daughter of Ammiel.

6 and Ibhar and Elishama and Eliphelet.

7 Nogah, Nepheg, and Japhia.

8 Elishama and Eliada and Eliphelet, nine.

9 All the sons of David, apart from the sons of the concubines and Tamar their sister.

10 And the son of Solomon was Rehoboam, Abijah his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son.

11 Joram son of Ahaziah son of Joash son.

12 Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son,

13 Ahaz his son Hezekiah his son Manasseh his son.

14 Amon son of Josiah son.

15 The sons of Josiah: the firstborn Johanan, second Jehoiakim, third Zedekiah, fourth Shallum.

16 And the sons of Jehoiakim: Jeconiah his son, Zedekiah his son.

17 The sons of Jeconiah the prisoner were Shealtiel his son.

18 Malchiram, Pedaiah, Senazar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.

19 The sons of Pedaiah were Zerubbabel and Shimei, and the son of Zerubbabel was Meshullam, and Hananiah, and Shelomith their sister.

20 Hashubah, Ohel, Berachiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-hesed, five.

21 And the son of Hananiah was Pelatiah and Jesaiah, the sons of Rephaiah, the sons of Arnan, the sons of Obadiah, the sons of Shecaniah.

22 The sons of Shecaniah were Shemaiah, and the sons of Shemaiah were Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat—six in total.

23 The son of Neariah, Elihoenai, Hezekiah, and Azrikam, three.

24 The sons of Elihoenai were Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Dalaiah, and Anani—seven.


## Chapter 4

1 The sons of Judah: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.

2 Reaiah son of Shobal fathered Jahath, and Jahath fathered Ahumai and Lahad; these are the families of the Zorathites.

3 These were the chiefs of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, Idbash, and the name of their sister Hazelelponi.

4 Peniel father of Gedor and Ezer father of Hushah, these are the sons of Hur, firstborn of Ephrath, father of Bethlehem.

5 Ashur, the father of Tekoa, had two wives, Helah and Naarah.

6 A servant woman bore him Ahuzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haakashtari; these were the sons of Naarah.

7 The sons of Helah were Zereth, Izhar, and Ethnan.

8 Koz fathered Anub and Zobebah, and the families of Aharhel son of Harum.

9 Jabez was more honorable than his brothers, and his mother called his name Jabez, saying, "Because I bore him in sorrow."

10 Jabez called to the God of Israel, saying, "If You will indeed bless me and enlarge my border, and Your hand be with me, and You keep me from adversity so that it does not grieve me," then God granted what he had requested.

11 Chelub, brother of Shuah, fathered Mehir, who was father of Eshton.

12 Eshton fathered Beth-rapha, and Paseah, and Tehinnah the chief of Irnahash; these were the men of Rechah.

13 And the sons of Kenaz: Othniel and Seraiah; and the sons of Othniel: Cathath.

14 Meonothai fathered Ophrah, and Seraiah fathered Joab, the chief of the valley of the craftsmen, for they were craftsmen.

15 The sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh: Iru and Naam; and the sons of Elah and Kenaz.

16 And the sons of Jehalelel: Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asareel.

17 and the son of Ezrah: Jether and Mered and Epher and Jalon; and she bore Miriam and Shammai and Ishbah the chief of Eshtemoa.

18 His wife Jehudijah bore Jared, father of Gedor, and Heber, father of Shocho, and Jekuthiel, father of Zanoah; these are the sons of Bithiah, daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered took.

19 The sons of the wife of Hodiah, the sister of Naham, the father of Keilah the Garmite, and Eshtemoa the Maachathite.

20 The sons of Shimon: Amnon and Rinnah, Ben-hanan and the sons of Ishi: Zoheth and Ben-zoketh.

21 The sons of Shelah son of Judah: Er father of Lecah, and Laadah father of Mareshah, and the families of the house of the workers of fine linen of the house of Ashbea.

22 Jokim and the men of Choseba and Joash and Saraph who belonged to Moab and Jashubi-lehem, and the words are ancient.

23 They are the potters and inhabitants of plants and a sheep-cote with the king in his work; they dwelt there.

24 The sons of Simeon: Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Saul.

25 Shallum, his son, Mibsam, his son, Mishma, his son.

26 The sons of Mishma: Hamuel his son, Zaccur his son, Shimei his son.

27 And to Shimei were sixteen sons and six daughters, but his brothers had few sons, and all their families did not multiply as the sons of Judah.

28 They lived in Beer-shebah, and in Moladah, and in Hazar-shual.

29 and with Bilhah and with Azem and with Tolad.

30 and in Bethuel, and in Hormah, and in Ziklag.

31 Bethmarcaboth and Hazar-susim and Bethbirei and Shaaraim: these were their towns until David reigned.

32 Their villages were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan, five towns.

33 And all their villages that were around these cities as far as Baal. These were their dwelling places, and they had their genealogy.

34 And Meshobab and Jamlech and Joshah son of Amaziah.

35 and Joel, and Jehu the son of Josibiah, son of Seraiah, son of Asiel.

36 Elihoenai, Jaakobah, Jeshoaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimael, and Benaiah.

37 Ziza son of Shiphi son of Allon son of Jedaiah son of Shimri son of Shemaiah.

38 These are the ones who came by name, leaders by their families, and by the house of their fathers, they multiplied greatly.

39 And they went to the entrance of Gedor, eastward of the valley, to seek pasture for their sheep.

40 They found a rich and good pasture, and the land spacious and quiet, for it was from Ham’s descendants who had lived there previously.

41 These listed by name came during the days of Hezekiah king of Judah and they struck their dwellings and all that was found there, and they devoted them to destruction until this day, and they dwelt in their place because pasture for their flock was there.

42 And from them, of the sons of Simeon, went to Mount Seir five hundred men, and Pelatiah and Neariah and Rephaiah and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi, were at their head.

43 And they struck down the remnant of the escaped at Amalek, and they stayed there until this day.


## Chapter 5

1 And the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel—for he was the firstborn, but by his defiling his father’s bed his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph the son of Israel, yet not to be reckoned according to the birthright.

2 For Judah prevailed over his brothers, and from him came a leader, but the birthright belonged to Joseph.

3 The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel: Enoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.

4 The sons of Joel: Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son.

5 Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son.

6 Beerah his son, whom Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria carried away; he was a prince of the Reubenites.

7 And his brothers according to their families in their genealogies: the chief Jeiel, and Zechariah,

8 And Bela son of Azaz son of Shema son of Joel, he dwelt in Aroer and as far as Nebo and Baal-meon.

9 And eastward he settled from the wilderness to the river Euphrates, for their cattle had multiplied in the land of Gilead.

10 In the days of Saul, they fought a battle against the Hagarenes, and the Hagarenes fell by their hand; they dwelt in their tents across all the face of the east beyond Gilead.

11 The sons of Gad dwelled in the land of Bashan opposite them, as far as Salcah.

12 Joel the chief and Shapham the second, and Jaanai and Shaphat in Bashan.

13 And their brothers according to the houses of their fathers: Michael and Meshullam and Sheba and Jorai and Jachan and Zia and Eber, seven.

14 These are the sons of Abihail son of Huri son of Jaroah son of Gilead son of Michael son of Jeshishai son of Jahdo son of Buz.

15 Ahi son of Abdiel son of Guni, chief of their fathers' houses.

16 They settled in Gilead, in Bashan, and in its villages, and in all the suburbs of Sharon according to their borders.

17 All of them were enrolled in the days of Jotham king of Judah and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.

18 The sons of Reuben and the Gadites and the half-tribe of Manasseh, warriors, men bearing shield and sword and drawing bow, trained for war, forty-four thousand seven hundred and sixty going forth to the host.

19 They fought a battle against the Hagarenes, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.

20 They were helped against them and they were given into their hand, the Hagarenes and all who were with them, because they cried to God in the battle, and he gave them relief because they trusted in him.

21 They took their cattle, camels fifty thousand, and sheep two hundred and fifty thousand, and donkeys two thousand, and persons one hundred thousand.

22 For many warriors have fallen, for the battle is from the angels, and they have taken their place until the captivity.

23 The sons of half the tribe of Manasseh lived in the land from Bashan to Baal-hermon and Senir and Mount Hermon; they were many.

24 These are the chiefs of their fathers' houses: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel, mighty men of valor, men of renown, leaders of their fathers' houses.

25 They trespassed against the God of their fathers and played the harlot after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed from before them.

26 The God of Israel stirred the spirit of Pul king of Assyria and the spirit of Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, and he exiled the Reubenites and the Gadites and half the tribe of Manasseh, and brought them to Halah and Habor and Hara and the river Gozan, to this day.


## Chapter 6

1 The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

2 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

3 The sons of Amram were Aaron, Moses, and Miriam; and the sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

4 Eleazar fathered Phinehas; Phinehas fathered Abishua.

5 Abishua fathered Bukki, and Bukki fathered Uzzi.

6 Uzzi fathered Zerahiah, and Zerahiah fathered Meraioth.

7 Meraioth begot Amariah, and Amariah begot Ahitub.

8 Ahitub fathered Zadok, and Zadok fathered Ahimaaz.

9 Ahimaaz begot Azariah, and Azariah begot Johanan.

10 And Johanan fathered Azariah, who was priest in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem.

11 Azariah fathered Amariah, and Amariah fathered Ahitub.

12 Ahitub fathered Zadok, and Zadok fathered Shallum.

13 And Shallum fathered Hillkiah, and Hillkiah fathered Azariah.

14 Azariah begot Seraiah, and Seraiah begot Jehozadek.

15 And Jehozadek went when the LORD exiled Judah and Jerusalem by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.

16 The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

17 And these are the names of the sons of Gershom: Libni and Shimei.

18 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

19 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their ancestors.

20 Gershom, Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son.

21 Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, Jeaterai his son.

22 The sons of Kohath: Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son.

23 Elkanah his son and Ebiasaph his son and Assir his son.

24 under his son Uriel, his son Uzziah, his son Shaul.

25 The sons of Elkanah were Amasai and Ahimoth.

26 Elkanah, Elkanah, Zophai his son and Nahath his son.

27 Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.

28 The sons of Samuel: the firstborn and Vashni and Abijah.

29 The sons of Merari: Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzza his son.

30 Shimea his son Haggiah his son Asaiah his son.

31 And these are the ones whom David appointed over the service of song in the house of the LORD, from the time the ark was established.

32 They served before the tabernacle of the tent of meeting with song until Solomon's time, when the house of the LORD was built in Jerusalem, and they stood in their appointed order for their service.

33 And these are the ones standing and their sons, from the sons of the Kohathites: Heman the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel.

34 son of Elkanah son of Jeroham son of Eliel son of Toah

35 son of Zif son of Elkanah son of Mahath son of Amasai

36 the son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah

37 Son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Ebiasaph, son of Korah.

38 son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi son of Israel

39 His brother Asaph stood on his right, Asaph son of Berachiah son of Shimea.

40 son of Michael son of Baaseiah son of Malchijah

41 Ethni son of Zerah son of Adaiah.

42 son of Ethan son of Zimmah son of Shimei

43 the son of Jahath the son of Gershom the son of Levi

44 The sons of Merari, their brothers, were on the left: Ethan son of Kishi, son of Abdi, son of Malluch.

45 son of Hashabiah son of Amaziah son of Hillkiah

46 Amzi son of Bani son of Shamer.

47 son of Mahli son of Mushi son of Merari son of Levi

48 And their fellow Levites were assigned to all the service of the tabernacle of the house of God.

49 Aaron and his sons shall offer on the altar of the burnt offering and on the altar of the incense for all manner of work of the most holy things and for making atonement on behalf of Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God commanded.

50 And these are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son.

51 Bukki son of Uzzi son of Zerahiah son.

52 Meraioth, son of Amariah, son of Ahitub.

53 Zadok son of Ahimaaz son.

54 And these were their dwelling-places, their habitations within their borders, for the sons of Aaron, of the family of the Kohathites, for to them came the lot.

55 They gave them Hebron in the land of Judah and its suburbs round about it.

56 And the field of the city and its courts they gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh.

57 And to the sons of Aaron they gave the cities of refuge: Hebron, with its suburbs; Libnah, with its suburbs; Jattir, and Eshtemoa, with their suburbs.

58 And Hilen and its suburbs, and Debir and its suburbs.

59 and Ashan with its suburbs, and Beth-shemesh with its suburbs.

60 From the tribe of Benjamin, Geba with its suburbs, and Almuth with its suburbs, and Anathoth with its suburbs; all their cities, thirteen in all, according to their families.

61 And to the remaining sons of Kohath, from the families of the tribe, half the tribe of Manasseh, ten cities by lot.

62 To the sons of Gershom, according to their families, from the tribe of Issachar and from the tribe of Asher and from the tribe of Naphtali and from the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities.

63 To the sons of Merari, according to their families, from the tribe of Reuben and from the tribe of Gad and from the tribe of Zebulun, by lot, twelve cities.

64 The sons of Israel gave to the Leviites the cities and their suburbs.

65 They gave by lot to the tribe of the children of Judah, and to the tribe of the children of Simeon, and to the tribe of the children of Benjamin, these cities which they called by name.

66 And from the families of the sons of Kohath, their cities of border were from the tribe of Ephraim.

67 They gave them the cities of refuge: Shechem with its suburbs in the hill country of Ephraim, and Gezer with its suburbs.

68 and Jokmeam with its suburbs, and Beth-horon with its suburbs.

69 and Aijalon and its suburbs and Gath-rimmon and its suburbs

70 And from half the tribe of Manasseh, Aner and its suburbs, and Balaam and its suburbs, for the family of the sons of Kohath who remained.

71 To the sons of Gershom, according to the family of half the tribe of Manasseh: Golan in Bashan and its suburbs, and Ashtaroth and its suburbs.

72 From the tribe of Issachar, Kedesh and its suburbs, Dabareh and its suburbs.

73 and Ramoth with its suburbs and Anem with its suburbs.

74 and from the tribe of Asher, Mashal and its suburbs, and Abdon and its suburbs.

75 And Hukkok and its suburbs, and Rehob and its suburbs.

76 From the tribe of Naphtali, Kedesh in Galilee with its suburbs, and Hammon with its suburbs, and Kiriathaim with its suburbs.

77 To the sons of Merari the rest, from the tribe of Zebulun, Remmon and its suburbs, Tabor and its suburbs.

78 Beyond the Jordan, east of the Jordan, from the tribe of Reuben, Bezer in the wilderness with its suburbs, and Jahazah with its suburbs.

79 Kedemoth and its suburbs, and Mephaath and its suburbs.

80 And from the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Gilead and its suburbs, and Mahanaim and its suburbs.

81 And Heshbon and its suburbs, and Jaazer and its suburbs.


## Chapter 7

1 And the sons of Issachar: Tola, and Puah, Jashub, and Shimron, four.

2 The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Jibsam, and Samuel—chiefs of their ancestral houses for Tola, mighty warriors according to their generations; their number in the days of David was twenty-two thousand six hundred.

3 And the sons of Uzzi were Izrahiah, and the sons of Izrahiah were Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah, five in all, all chiefs.

4 Their generations according to their fathers' houses, bands of troops for war, thirty-six thousand, for they multiplied wives and sons.

5 Their brothers according to all families of Issachar, mighty men of valor, eighty-seven thousand, were genealogically registered to all.

6 Benjamin: Bela, Becher, and Jediael—three.

7 And the sons of Bela: Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri, five chiefs of their father's houses, mighty warriors, and they were enrolled, twenty-two thousand and thirty-four.

8 And the sons of Becher were Zemira, Joash, Eliezer, Elihoenai, Omri, Jermoth, Abiah, Anathoth, and Alameth; all these were sons of Becher.

9 And they were enrolled by their generations, chiefs of their fathers' houses, mighty men of valor, twenty thousand and two hundred.

10 The sons of Jediael were Bilhan, and the sons of Bilhan were Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishar.

11 All these were sons of Jediael, chiefs of their fathers, mighty men of valor, seventeen thousand two hundred going out to war.

12 Shuppim and Huppim, sons of Ir, Hushim, sons of Aher.

13 The sons of Naphtali: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, the sons of Bilhah.

14 The sons of Manasseh were Ashriel, whom his concubine the Aramitess bore: she bore Machir, father of Gilead.

15 Machir took a wife from Huppim and from Shuppim, and the name of his sister was Maachah, and the name of the second was Zelophehad; and Zelophehad had daughters.

16 Maachah, wife of Machir, bore a son and called his name Peresh, and the name of his brother was Sharesh, and their sons were Ulam and Rekem.

17 The sons of Ulam were Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh.

18 And his sister Hammoleketh bore Ishod and Abiezer and Mahlah.

19 The children of Shemida were Ahian and Shechem and Likhi and Aniam.

20 The sons of Ephraim: Shuthelah, and Bered his son, and Tahath his son, and Eladah his son, and Tahath his son.

21 Zabad his son and Shuthelah his son and Ezer and Elead, and the men of Gath who were born in the land struck them down when they went down to take their cattle.

22 Ephraim their father mourned many days, and his brothers came to comfort him.

23 He entered his wife and she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Beriah because adversity had been in his house.

24 and her daughter was Sherah, who built both Beth-horon the lower and Beth-horon the upper, and Uzzen-sherah.

25 Rephah and Resheph and Telah his son and Tahan his son.

26 Laadan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son.

27 Non, his son, Joshua, his son.

28 Their possession and their dwellings were Beth-el and its villages, and on the east Naaran, and on the west Gezer and its villages, and Shechem and its villages, as far as Ayyah and its villages.

29 And by the hand of the sons of Manasseh, Beth-shean and its daughters, Taanach and its daughters, Megiddo and its daughters, Dor and its daughters, there the sons of Joseph son of Israel dwelt.

30 The sons of Asher: Imna, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah, and Serah their sister.

31 And the sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel; he was the father of Birzoth.

32 Heber fathered Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and Shua their sister.

33 The sons of Japhlet: Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath; these are the sons of Japhlet.

34 And the sons of Shamer: Ahi, and Rohgah, Jehubbah, and Aram.

35 And the sons of his brother Helem: Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.

36 The sons of Zophah: Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, and Imrah.

37 Bezer and Hod and Shamma and Shilshah and Ithran and Beera.

38 And the sons of Jether: Jephunneh and Pispah and Ara.

39 The sons of Ulla: Arah, Hanniel, and Rezia.

40 All these were sons of Asher, heads of fathers' houses, chosen and mighty men of valor, leaders among the princes, and they were enrolled in the army for war; their number of men was twenty-six thousand.


## Chapter 8

1 Benjamin fathered Bela, his firstborn, Ashbel the second, and Aharah the third.

2 Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.

3 Sons were born to Bela: Addar, Gera, and Abihud.

4 Abishua, Naaman, and Ahoah.

5 Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.

6 And these are the sons of Ehud: they were heads of fathers' houses of the inhabitants of Geba, and they were exiled to Manahath.

7 Naaman and Ahiah and Gera—he it was who carried them away—and he begot Uzza and Ahihud.

8 Shaharaim begot children in the country of Moab, after he had sent them away; Hushim and Baara were his wives.

9 He bore from Hodesh his wife, Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, and Malcham.

10 And Jeuz and Shachia and Mirma; these are his sons, heads of fathers.

11 Hushim fathered Abitub and Elpaal.

12 The sons of Elpaal were Eber, Misham, and Shamer; he built Ono and Lod and its towns.

13 Beriah and Shema were chiefs of the inhabitants of Aijalon; they drove out the inhabitants of Gath.

14 Ahio, Shashak, and Jermoth.

15 Zebadiah, Arad, and Ader.

16 Michael and Ispah and Joha, sons of Beriah.

17 Zebadiah and Meshullam and Hezeki and Heber.

18 Ishmerai, Jezliah, and Jobab, sons of Elpaal.

19 and Jakim and Zichri and Zabdi

20 Elienai and Zilthai and Eliel.

21 Adaiah and Beraiah and Shimrath, sons of Shimei.

22 And Ishpan and Eber and Eliel.

23 Abdon, Zichri, and Canan.

24 Hananiah and Elam and Antothijah.

25 Iphedeiah, and Penuel, the sons of Shashak.

26 Shamsherai and Shehariah and Athaliah.

27 Jaresiah and Elijah and Zichri, sons of Jeroham.

28 These are the chiefs of their fathers by their generations; these chiefs lived in Jerusalem.

29 And at Gibeon dwelt Abi Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.

30 His sons were Abdon the firstborn, and Zur, Kish, Baal, and Nadab.

31 Gedor and Ahio and Zeker.

32 Mikloth fathered Shimah, and they also dwelt in Jerusalem opposite their kindred.

33 Ner begot Kish, and Kish begot Saul, and Saul begot Jonathan and Malchishua and Abinadab and Eshbaal.

34 And the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal, and Merib-baal fathered Micah.

35 The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.

36 Ahaz begot Jehoada, and Jehoada begot Alameth and Azmaveth and Zimri, and Zimri begot Moza.

37 Moza fathered Binea; Rapha was his son, Elasah his son, Azel his son.

38 And to Azal six sons, and these are the names of them: Azrikam, Bocheru, and Ishmael, and Sheariah, and Obadiah, and Canan; all these are sons of Azal.

39 The sons of Eshek his brother: Ulam the firstborn, Jehush the second, and Eliphalet the third.

40 The sons of Ulam were mighty men, archers, and had many sons and sons’ sons, one hundred and fifty; all these were from the sons of Benjamin.


## Chapter 9

1 And all Israel were recorded, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Israel, and Judah was exiled to Babylon because of their transgression.

2 And the earlier residents who were in their possessions in their cities: Israel, the priests, the Leviites, and the Nethinims.

3 And in Jerusalem dwelt some of the sons of Judah and some of the sons of Benjamin and some of the sons of Ephraim and Manasseh.

4 Uthai son of Ammihud son of Omri son of Imri son of Benjamin son of Perez son of Judah.

5 and of the Shilonite, Asaiah the eldest and his sons.

6 And from the sons of Zerah, Jehiel and his brothers, six hundred and ninety.

7 From the sons of Benjamin, Sallai son of Meshullam son of Hodaviah son of Hasenuah.

8 Ibneiah son of Jeroham, and Elah son of Uzzi son of Michri, and Meshullam son of Shephatiah son of Raguel son of Ibnijah.

9 Their brothers, according to their generations, nine hundred fifty and six, every one of these men, heads of fathers, for their fathers’ houses.

10 and from the priests, Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, and Jachin.

11 Azariah son of Hillkiah son of Meshullam son of Zadok son of Meraioth son of Ahitub, leader of the house of God.

12 Adaiah son of Jeroham son of Pashur son of Malchiah, and Maasiai son of Adiel son of Jahzerah son of Meshullam son of Meshillemith son of Immer.

13 And their brothers, heads of fathers' houses, one thousand seven hundred and sixty mighty men of valor, for the work of the service of the house of God.

14 And of the Leviites: Shemaiah son of Hashub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari.

15 Bakbakkar and Heresh and Galal and Mattaniah son of Micha son of Zichri son of Asaph.

16 Obadiah son of Shemaiah son of Galal son of Jeduthun, and Berachiah son of Asa son of Elkanah who lived in the villages of the Netophathite.

17 And the doorkeepers were Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their brother Shallum the chief.

18 Even to here, at the king's gate on the east side, they, the porters, were in charge of the camps of the sons of Levi.

19 Shallum son of Kore son of Ebiasaph son of Korah and his brothers from their ancestral house the Korahites were over the work of the service, keepers of the thresholds of the tent, and their ancestors were over the camp of the LORD, keepers of the entrance.

20 Phinehas son of Eleazar was their leader formerly, and the LORD was with him.

21 Zachariah son of Meshelemiah, doorkeeper of the entrance to the tent of meeting.

22 All of them, the doorkeepers at the gates, two hundred and twelve, were enrolled by their genealogies in their courts; David and Samuel the seer established them in their faithfulness.

23 And they and their children were at the gates of the house of the LORD, the house of the tent, for keeping watch.

24 To the four sides shall be the porters, eastward, westward, northward, and southward.

25 And their brothers in their villages came to them at appointed times, from time to time, with these.

26 For by faith they were four mighty doorkeepers, they were the Levites, and they were over the chambers and over the storehouses of the house of God.

27 And round about the house of God they lodge, for upon them is the watch, and they have charge of the doors, morning by morning.

28 And from them, concerning the vessels of the service, because by number they shall bring them in, and by number they shall take them out.

29 And from them were appointed over the vessels and all the utensils of the sanctuary, and over the fine flour, and the wine, and the oil, and the frank-incense, and the spices.

30 And from the sons of the priests, the compounders of the ointment for the spices.

31 And Mattithiah of the Levites, who was the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with the work of the things baked in pans.

32 And from the sons of the Kohathites, from their brothers, for the bread of the row, to prepare [every] sabbath [on the] sabbath.

33 These are the singers, heads of fathers' houses of the Levites, who were free from other service in the chambers, for they were employed in that work day and night.

34 These are the chiefs of the fathers of the Levites according to their generations, chiefs who lived in Jerusalem.

35 And at Gibeon dwelt the father of Gibeon, and the name of his wife was Maacah.

36 His son the firstborn was Abdon, and Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, and Nadab.

37 Gedor and Ahio and Zechariah and Mikloth.

38 Mikloth fathered Shimeam, and they also dwelled in Jerusalem opposite their kindred, with their kindred.

39 Ner fathered Kish, and Kish fathered Saul, and Saul fathered Jonathan and Malchishua and Abinadab and Eshbaal.

40 And the son of Jonathan was Merib-baal, and Meri-baal fathered Micah.

41 The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, and Tahrea.

42 Ahaz fathered Jarah, and Jarah fathered Alameth and Azmaveth and Zimri, and Zimri fathered Moza.

43 Moza fathered Binea, and Rephaiah his son, Elasah his son, Azel his son.

44 And to Azal six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Canan; these are the sons of Azal.


## Chapter 10

1 Then the Philistines fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled before the Philistines, and they fell slain on Mount Gilboa.

2 The Philistines pursued Saul and his sons, and the Philistines struck down Jonathan and Abinadab and Malchishua, sons of Saul.

3 The battle pressed hard against Saul, and the archers found him with the arrows, and he was wounded by the archers.

4 Then Saul said to his armour-bearer, "Draw your sword and thrust me through with it, lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me"; but his armour-bearer was very afraid and would not do it. So Saul took the sword and fell upon it.

5 And the armour-bearer saw that Saul was dead, and he fell likewise upon the sword and died.

6 Saul died and his three sons, and all his household, all of them died together.

7 And all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that they fled, and that Saul and his sons were dead, so they abandoned their cities and fled, and the Philistines came and lived in them.

8 And it came to pass on the morrow, that the Philistines came to strip the slain, and they found Saul and his sons fallen upon mount Gilboa.

9 They stripped him, and took his head and his weapons, and sent them around the land of the Philistines to proclaim the news to their idols and to the people.

10 They placed his armour in the temple of their god, and his skull they set up in the house of Dagon.

11 And all the people of Jabesh-gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul.

12 Then all the fighting men rose up and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons and brought them to Jabesh and buried their bones beneath the oak in Jabesh and fasted seven days.

13 Saul died because of the transgression with which he trespassed against the LORD, in that he did not keep the word of the LORD, and also consulted a familiar spirit to inquire.

14 And he did not inquire of the LORD, so he put him to death, and turned the kingship to David son of Jesse.


## Chapter 11

1 All Israel gathered to David at Hebron, saying, "Behold, we are your bone and your body."

2 Even yesterday and heretofore, even when Saul was king, you were the one bringing out and bringing in Israel; and the LORD your God said to you, You will shepherd my people Israel, and you will be leader over my people Israel.

3 All the elders of Israel came to the king in Hebron, and David made a covenant with them in Hebron before the LORD, and they anointed David to be king over Israel, according to the word of the LORD by the hand of Samuel.

4 David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, which is Jebus, and the Jebusites were the inhabitants of the land.

5 The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, You shall not come in here; but David captured the castle of Zion; it is the city of David.

6 David said, Whoever strikes down a Jebusite first shall become chief and commander, so Joab the son of Zeruiah went up first and became chief.

7 David settled in the stronghold; therefore they called it the city of David.

8 And he built the city from the Millo round about, and Joab repaired the rest of the city.

9 And David went on, growing greater, and Jehovah the Lord of hosts was with him.

10 These are the chiefs of the mighty men who were with David, who strengthened him in his reign, together with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the LORD concerning Israel.

11 These are the number of David's mighty men: Jashobeam son of Hachmoni, chief of the thirty; he raised his spear against three hundred, and slew them, all at one time.

12 After him was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite, who was among the three mighty ones.

13 He was with David at Pas-dammim, and the Philistines were gathered there for battle, and the field was full of barley, and the people fled before the Philistines.

14 They took their stand in the midst of the field and delivered it, and struck the Philistines, and the LORD brought about a great deliverance.

15 Three of the thirty chiefs went down to the rock to David, to the cave of Adullam, while the army of the Philistines was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.

16 David was then in the stronghold, and the Philistine garrison was then in Bethlehem.

17 David said, Who will give me to drink water from the well of Bethlehem which is by the gate?

18 The three broke through the Philistine army and drew water from the well that was at the gate of Bethlehem, and they carried it and brought it to David, but David was unwilling to drink it, so he poured it out to the Lord.

19 Far be it from me by my God to do this thing—shall I drink the blood of these men at the risk of their lives? For at the risk of their lives they brought it. And he would not drink it. These things did the three mighty men.

20 Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the three; he wielded his spear against three hundred slain; he had a name among the three.

21 Of the three he was more honored than the two, and he became their commander, but he did not attain to the first three.

22 Benaiah son of Jehoiada, son of a valiant man of might, mighty in deeds from Kabzeel, he struck down the two lionlike men of Moab, and he went down and struck the lion in the midst of the pit on the day of snow.

23 And he struck the Egyptian man, a champion five cubits tall, and in the hand of the Egyptian was a spear like a weaver's beam, and he went down to him with a staff, and snatched the javelin from the hand of the Egyptian and killed him with his own spear.

24 These things did Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and he had a name among the three mighty men.

25 From the thirty he was honored, but to the three he did not come, and David placed him over his guard.

26 The mighty men of the armies were Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,

27 Shamoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite.

28 Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abiezer of Anathoth.

29 Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,

30 Maharai the Netophathite, Heled the son of Baanah the Netophathite.

31 Ithai the son of Ribai from Gibeah of the sons of Benjamin, Benaiah the Pirathonite.

32 Hurai from the brooks of Gaash, Abiel the Arbahite.

33 Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahbah the Shaalbonite.

34 The sons of Hashem the Gizonite: Jonathan son of Shage the Hararite.

35 Ahiam son of Sacar the Hararite Eliphal son of Ur.

36 Hepher the Macherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite.

37 Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai son of Ezbai.

38 Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar the son of the Hagarene.

39 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite, the armour-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah.

40 Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite.

41 Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai.

42 Adina son of Shiza, chief of the Reubenites, and thirty with him.

43 Canan son of Maachah and Joshaphat the Mithnite.

44 Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel the sons of Hotham the Aroerite,

45 Jediael son of Shimri and Joha his brother the Tizite.

46 Eliel the Mahavite, Jeribai, and Joshaviah the sons of Elnaam, and Ithmah the Moabite.

47 Eliel and Obed and Jaasiel the Mesobaite.


## Chapter 12

1 These are the ones who came to David at Ziklag while he was still confined because of Saul son of Kish, and they were among the warriors, helpers in battle.

2 Archers who shoot with the right hand and the left, with stones and with arrows and with the bow, from Saul’s brothers, from Benjamin.

3 The chief Ahiezer and Joash the sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite, Jeziel, Pelet the sons of Azmaveth, Berachah and Jehu the Anethothite.

4 Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, a mighty man among the thirty, was over the thirty. Jeremiah and Jahaziel and Jehohanan and Josabad the Gederathite.

5 Eluzai, Jermoth, Bealiah, Shamariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite,

6 Elkanah, Ishiah, Azarael, Joezer, and Jashobeam, the Korahites.

7 Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham from Gedor.

8 And from the Gadites, mighty warriors and men of war, skilled in battle, who bore shield and spear, whose faces were like the faces of lions, and as swift as the gazelles on the mountains, separated themselves to David at the wilderness stronghold.

9 Ezer the chief, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third.

10 Mishmannah the fourth, Jeremiah the fifth.

11 Attai the sixth, Eliel the seventh.

12 Jehohanan the eighth, Elzabad the ninth.

13 Jeremiah the tenth, Machbanai the eleventh.

14 These are from the sons of Gad, chiefs of the army: the least one over a hundred, and the greatest over a thousand.

15 These are the ones who crossed the Jordan in the first month, which was overflowing all its banks; they drove out all the inhabitants of the valleys toward the east and toward the west.

16 And there came of the sons of Benjamin and Judah to David at the castle.

17 David went out before them and answered and said to them, If you have come to me in peace to help me, may the heart be with us together; but if you have come to betray me to my adversary without violence in my hands, may the God of our fathers see and judge.

18 A spirit came upon Amasai, saying, "We are yours, O David, and with you, O son of Jesse; peace, peace to you, and peace to your helper, for your God helps you." Then David received them and appointed them over the chief of the band.

19 And from Manasseh fell to David when he came with the Philistines against Saul to battle, but they did not help him, for the lords of the Philistines sent him away by counsel, saying, He will fall to his lord Saul.

20 When he went to Ziklag, there fell to him from Manasseh: Adnah, Josabad, Jediael, Michael, Josabad, Elihu, and Zilthai, chiefs of the thousands of Manasseh.

21 And they helped David against the band, for they were all mighty men of valour, and they became captains in the army.

22 For at a certain time day by day they came to David to help him, until there was a great army like the army of God.

23 And these are the number of the chief men of the army who came to David at Hebron to transfer the kingdom of Saul to him according to the word of the LORD.

24 The sons of Judah bearing shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred armed for battle.

25 From the sons of Simeon, mighty men of strength for the army, seven thousand and one hundred.

26 from the Levite sons, four thousand and six hundred

27 Jehoiada the leader with Aaron and with him three thousand and seven hundred.

28 Zadok was a young man of great strength, and his father's house were twenty-two rulers.

29 And from the sons of Benjamin, brothers of Saul, three thousand, and until now their multitude has increased, keepers of the charge of the house of Saul.

30 And from the sons of Ephraim, twenty-eight thousand valiant warriors, men of renown, by their fathers' houses.

31 And from the half tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, those who were named, came to make David king.

32 And from the sons of Issachar, men who had understanding of the times, to know what Israel should do; their chiefs were two hundred, and all their brothers were under their word.

33 From Zebulun those coming forth to battle, arrayed for war with every kind of weapon of war, fifty thousand, who were not of double heart.

34 From Naphtali one thousand captains, and with them thirty-seven thousand armed with buckler and spear.

35 From the Danites, skilled in warfare, twenty-eight thousand six hundred.

36 And from Asher, those going out with the army to arrange the battle line, forty thousand.

37 And beyond the Jordan, from the Reubenites and the Gadites and half the tribe of Manasseh, all equipped for war, one hundred and twenty thousand.

38 All these were fighting men, skilled in battle order, with wholehearted resolve came to Hebron to make David king over all Israel, and also all the remnant of Israel were of one mind to make David king.

39 And they were there with David three days, eating and drinking, for their brothers had prepared for them.

40 Even their neighbors, Issachar, Zebulun, and Naphtali, brought food on asses, camels, mules, and beeves: provisions of flour, cakes of figs, bunches of raisins, wine, oil, beeves, and sheep in great abundance, for there was joy in Israel.


## Chapter 13

1 David consulted with the captains of thousands and hundreds, every leader.

2 David said to all the assembly of Israel, if it seems good to you and from the LORD our God, let us send to our brothers who remain in all the lands of Israel, and with them the priests and the Levites in the suburbs of their cities, that they may gather to us.

3 And let us bring back the ark of our God to us, for we did not seek it in the days of Saul.

4 And all the assembly said to do so, for the matter was right in the eyes of all the people.

5 David assembled all Israel from Shihor in Egypt to Hamath to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.

6 David and all Israel went up to Baalah, that is, to Kirjath-jearim which belongs to Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, the LORD who dwells among the cherubim, whose name is called.

7 They placed the ark of God on a new cart from the house of Abinadab, and Uzza and Ahio were driving the cart.

8 David and all Israel were playing before God with all their might, with songs and harps and psalteries and timbrels and cymbals and trumpets.

9 They came to the threshing floor of Chidon, and Uzza reached out his hand to steady the ark because the beeve stumbled.

10 And the anger of the LORD burned against Uzza, and He struck him down for reaching with his hand to the ark, and he died there before God.

11 David was angry because Jehovah made a breach upon Uzza, and he called that place Perezuzza to this day.

12 David feared God on that day, saying, "How can I bring the ark of God to me?"

13 David did not take the ark to himself into David's city, but turned it aside to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.

14 The ark of God remained in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite for three months, and the LORD blessed the house of Obed-edom and all that belonged to him.


## Chapter 14

1 Then the king of Tyre sent ambassadors to David, with cedar timbers, stone masons, and carpenters to build him a house.

2 David knew that Jehovah had established him as king over Israel, because his reign had been exalted on high for the sake of his people Israel.

3 David took more wives in Jerusalem and David fathered more sons and daughters.

4 And these are the names of the sons born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon.

5 Ibhar and Elishua and Eliphalet were chosen.
