6 Nogah, Nepheg, and Japhia.

7 Elishama and Beeliada and Eliphalet.

8 The Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, and all the Philistines went up to seek David; and David heard, and went out before them.

9 Then the Philistines came and stripped [the land] in the valley of the Rephaim.

10 David inquired of God, saying, "Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you give them into my hand?" And the LORD said to him, "Go up, for I will give them into your hand."

11 David went up to Baal-perazim, and David defeated them there; and David said, "God has broken through my enemies by my hand like the breakthrough of waters"; therefore he called the name of that place Baal-perazim.

12 They abandoned there their gods, and David said, and they burned them in the fire.

13 The Philistines added yet again and raided the valley.

14 Then David inquired again of God, and He said to him, "You shall not go up after them; turn away from them and come upon them opposite the mulberry trees."

15 Then it shall be, when you hear the sound of the going in the tops of the mulberry trees, you shall go out to the war, for God has gone forth before you to strike the camp of the Philistines.

16 David did as God had commanded him, and he struck the camp of the Philistines from Gibeon to Gazer.

17 David's fame went out through all the lands, and the LORD gave the dread of him upon all the nations.


## Chapter 15

1 He made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the ark of God, and pitched a tent for it.

2 Then David said, Leviites alone shall bear the ark of God, for the LORD has chosen them to bear the ark of the LORD and to minister to him always.

3 David assembled all Israel to Jerusalem to bring up the ark of the LORD to its place which he had prepared for it.

4 David gathered the sons of Aaron and the Levites.

5 To the sons of Kohath: Uriel the chief, and his brothers one hundred and twenty.

6 To the sons of Merari, Asaiah the captain and his brothers two hundred and twenty.

7 For the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brothers one hundred and thirty.

8 To the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the ruler and his brothers, two hundred.

9 To the sons of Hebron, Eliel the captain and his brothers eighty.

10 To the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief and his brothers, one hundred and twelve.

11 David summoned Zad0k and Abiathar the priests, and to the Levites, to Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.

12 And he said to them, You are the chief fathers of the Levites: sanctify yourselves, both you and your brothers, and bring up the ark of the LORD, the God of Israel, to the place I have prepared for him.

13 For at first it was not you who broke through, but the LORD our God who broke through for us, because we did not seek him according to the judgment.

14 The priests and the Levites consecrated themselves to carry up the ark of the LORD God of Israel.

15 The sons of Levi carried the ark of God as Moses had commanded according to the word of the LORD on their shoulders by its staves.

16 David said to the captains of the Leviites, to appoint their brothers the singers with instruments of song, psalteries and harps and cymbals, making a loud sound with the voice for joy.

17 The Leviites stationed Heman son of Joel, and from his brothers, Asaph son of Berechiah, and from the sons of Merari their brothers, Ethan son of Kushaiah.

18 And with them their brothers the second order: Zachariah, Ben, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Elipheleh, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, the gatekeepers.

19 And the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, with brasen cymbals for sounding.

20 Zachariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah, on psalteries for Alamoth.

21 And Mattithiah and Elipheleh and Mikneiah and Obed-edom and Jeiel and Azaziah, on harps set to the Sheminith, to lead.

22 And Chenaniah was appointed captain over the Leviites in the burden; he was to direct the burden, for he was skilled.

23 And Berachiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.

24 Shebaniah and Joshaphat and Nethaneel and Amasai and Zechariah and Benaiah and Eliezer, the priests with trumpets, before the ark of God, and Obed-edom and Jehiah, doorkeepers to the ark.

25 David and the elders of Israel and the captains of thousands went to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the house of Obed-edom with joy.

26 And it came to pass, when God helped the Levites who bore the ark of the covenant of the LORD, that they sacrificed seven bulls and seven rams.

27 And David was wearing a robe of fine linen, and all the Levites who bore the ark and the singers, and Chenaniah the chief of the singers was over the burden, and David had an ephod of linen.

28 And all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouting and with the voice of the trumpet and with trumpeters and with cymbals, making a loud noise with psalteries and harps.

29 The ark of the covenant of the LORD came to the city of David, and Michal daughter of Saul looked out through the window and saw King David leaping and dancing, and she despised him in her heart.


## Chapter 16

1 They brought the ark of God and set it inside the tent that David had pitched for it, and they presented burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.

2 David finished offering the burnt offering and the peace offering and blessed the people in the name of the LORD.

3 And he distributed to every man of Israel, from man even to woman, to each man a loaf of bread, a good piece of flesh, and a flagon.

4 And he set before the ark of the LORD from the Levites ministering, to cause to remember and to give thanks and to praise the LORD God of Israel.

5 Asaph the chief and his second, Zechariah, Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel, with instruments of psalteries and harps and Asaph with the loud-sounding cymbals.

6 Benaiah and Jahaziel the priests continually with trumpets before the ark of the covenant of God.

7 On that day David first gave thanks to the LORD through Asaph and his brothers.

8 Give praise to the LORD, call on his name, make known among the peoples his deeds.

9 Sing to him, make music to him; speak of all his wonders.

10 Boast in his holy name; let the heart of those who seek the LORD rejoice.

11 Seek Jehovah and his strength, seek his face continually.

12 Remember the wonders he has done, his miracles and the judgments from his mouth.

13 Seed of Israel his servant, children of Jacob his chosen ones.

14 He is the LORD our God; in all the earth are his judgments.

15 Remember his covenant always, the word that he commanded to a thousand generations.

16 which he made with Abraham, and his oath to Isaac,

17 And he appointed it to Jacob for a statute, a perpetual covenant to Israel.

18 Saying, To you I will give the land of Canaan, the portion of your inheritance.

19 when you were few in number and strangers in it

20 They went from nation to nation and from kingdom to another people.

21 He did not allow any man to oppress them, and He rebuked kings on their account.

22 Do not touch my anointed one, and do not harm my prophet.

23 Sing to the LORD, all the earth; proclaim his deliverance from day to day.

24 Declare among the nations his glory, among all the peoples his wondrous deeds.

25 For great is the LORD and highly praised, and terrible is he above all gods.

26 For all the gods of the nations are idols, but the LORD made the heavens.

27 Beauty and majesty are before him, strength and joy are in his place.

28 Give to the LORD, O families of the peoples, give to the LORD glory and strength.

29 Give to the LORD glory to his name; bring an offering and come before his presence; bow down to the LORD in holy beauty.

30 Tremble before his presence, all the earth; indeed, he has established the world; it shall not be moved.

31 Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad, and let the nations say, "The LORD reigns."

32 Let the sea and all that is in it roar; let the country and all that is in it exult.

33 Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the LORD, for he has come to judge the earth.

34 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness is alway.

35 And they shall say, "Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us and deliver us from the nations, to give thanks to your renowned name, to glory in your praise."

36 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting, and all the people said, Amen, and praise to the LORD.

37 So he left Asaph and his brothers before the ark of the covenant of the LORD to minister continually before the ark, attending to the duties of each day in its day.

38 Obed-edom and his kindred, sixty-eight; and Obed-edom the son of Jeduthun, and Hosah, to the doorkeepers.

39 and Zadok the priest and his brothers the priests before the tabernacle of the LORD at the high place that was in Gibeon

40 to offer burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of ascent continually morning and evening and for all that is written in the law of the LORD which he commanded concerning Israel

41 And with them were Heman and Jeduthun and the rest who were designated by name to give thanks to the LORD, for his mercy endures forever.

42 And with them were Heman and Jeduthun, trumpets and cymbals for sounding, and instruments for the song of God, and the sons of Jeduthun at the gate.

43 Then all the people went every man to his house, and David returned to bless his household.


## Chapter 17

1 When David was settled in his home, David said to the prophet Nathan, "Here I am, dwelling in a house of cedar, while the ark of the covenant of the LORD is under curtains."

2 Nathan said to David, "Do all that is in your heart, for God is with you."

3 And it came to pass that night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying:

4 Go and say to David my servant, Thus says the LORD, You shall not build me a house to dwell in.

5 For I have not dwelt in a house from the day that I brought up Israel to this day, but have been from tent to tent and from dwelling place.

6 In all where I have walked with all Israel, was there a word that I spoke to any of the judges of Israel whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, Why have you not built me a house of cedar?

7 Now therefore thus you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the LORD of hosts: I took you from the sheepcote, from after the flock, to be ruler over my people Israel.

8 I have been with you in all that you have done, and I have cut off all your enemies from before you, and I have made for you a name like the name of the great ones in the land.

9 I will appoint a country for my people Israel and plant them, and they will dwell in their place and not be disturbed again, and the sons of wickedness will no longer oppress them as at first.

10 And in the days when I commanded judges over my people Israel, and I subdued all your enemies, I declared to you that the LORD would build you a house.

11 And it shall come to pass, when your days are fulfilled to go to your fathers, that I will raise up your offspring after you, who shall come from your sons, and I will establish his kingdom.

12 He will build me a temple, and I will establish his throne forever.

13 I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son, and my kindness I will not remove from him, as I removed it from him who was before you.

14 And I will set him in my house and in my kingdom forever, and his throne shall be established forever.

15 According to all these words and according to all this vision, so Nathan spoke to David.

16 King David came and sat before the LORD and said, Who am I, O Lord God, and what is my house, that You have brought me thus far?

17 And this affliction seemed small in your eyes, O God, and you spoke concerning the house of your servant from afar, and you have regarded me as the estate of man who rises up, O LORD God.

18 What more can David add to you to honour your servant, when you yourself know your servant?

19 the LORD has done all this greatness for the sake of your servant and according to your will, to make known all the greatnesses.

20 The LORD there is none like you and there is no God besides you in all that we have heard with our ears.

21 And who is like your people Israel, a unique nation on earth, whom God went to redeem for himself as a people, to make for you a renowned name of greatness and awesome deeds, driving out from before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt, the nations?

22 You gave Your folk Israel to You as a people forever, and You, the LORD, were to them as God.

23 And now, O Lord, let the word that you spoke concerning your servant and his house be established forever, and do as you have spoken.

24 And may your name be established and grow famous forever, saying, "The LORD of armies, God of Israel, is God to Israel," and may the house of David your servant remain established before you.

25 For you are my God; you have opened the ear of your servant to build a house for him; therefore your servant has found [courage] to pray before you.

26 And now, O Jehovah, Thou art God, and Thou hast spoken this good thing concerning Thy servant.

27 And now you have blessed the house of your servant to be before you forever, for you, O LORD, have blessed, and it is blessed forever.


## Chapter 18

1 After this David struck the Philistines and subdued them and took Gath and its towns from the hand of the Philistines.

2 He struck Moab, and Moab became servants to David, bringing a gift.

3 David struck Hadadezer king of Zoba, Hamath, when he went to establish his dominion at the River Euphrates.

4 David captured from him one thousand chariots and seven thousand horsemen and twenty thousand foot soldiers, and David hamstrung all the chariots but left one hundred chariots.

5 Then Aram of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, and David struck Aram, killing twenty-two thousand men.

6 David placed garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and Aram became to David servants bringing a gift, and the LORD saved David in all that he went.

7 David took the shields of gold that were on the bondmen of Hadadezer and brought them to Jerusalem.

8 From Tibhath and from Chun, cities of Hadadezer, David took very great copper; from it Solomon made the Sea of brass and the pillars and the bronze vessels.

9 Toi the king of Hamath heard that David had defeated the entire army of Hadadezer king of Zobah.

10 He sent Hadoram his son to King David to greet him and bless him because he had fought against Hadadezer and defeated him for Hadadezer had been a man of wars to Toi and all his vessels of gold and silver and bronze.

11 Even them did King David consecrate to the LORD, with the silver and the gold that he took from all the nations, from Edom and from Moab and from the sons of Ammon and from the Philistines and from Amalek.

12 Abishai son of Zeruiah struck Edom in the valley of salt, eighteen thousand.

13 He set garrisons in Edom, and all Edom became servants to David, and the LORD saved David wherever he went.

14 David reigned over all Israel and he executed justice and righteousness for all his people.

15 Joab son of Zeruiah was over the host, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder.

16 Zadok son of Ahitub and Abimelech son of Abiathar were priests, and Shavsha was the scribe.

17 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and the sons of David were chief officials near the king.


## Chapter 19

1 After these things, Nahash the king of the Ammonites died, and his son reigned in his place.

2 David said, I will show favour to Hanun son of Nahash, because his father showed favour to me; so David sent messengers to console him concerning his father. Then David's servants came to the land of the Ammonites, to Hanun, to console him.

3 The captains of the Ammonites said to Hanun, "Does David truly honor your father in your eyes by sending his servants to comfort you? Are not his servants come to you only because of intent to spy and overthrow and to scout out the land?"

4 Then Hanun took David's bondmen, shaved them, cut their garments halfway to the buttocks, and sent them away.

5 They went and reported to David about the men, and he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed, and the king said, “Stay in Jericho until your beards have grown, then return.”

6 The Ammonites saw that they had become odious to David, so Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and horsemen from Aram-naharaim, from Aram-maacah, and from Zobah.

7 They hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots and the king of Maachah with his people, and they came and encamped before Medeba, and the sons of Ammon were gathered from their cities and came to battle.

8 David heard and sent Joab and all the host of the warriors.

9 The sons of Ammon went out and drew up for battle at the entrance of the city, while the kings who had come remained by themselves in the field.

10 Joab saw that the battle was against him both in front and behind, so he chose from all the choice men of Israel and deployed them to face Aram.

11 And the rest of the people he gave into the hand of Abishai his brother, and they deployed against the Ammonites.

12 He said, "If Aram is too strong for me, then you shall help me, but if the sons of Ammon are too strong for you, then I will help you."

13 Be strong, and let us strengthen ourselves for the sake of our people and for the sake of the cities of our God; and may the LORD do what is good in his sight.

14 Joab and the folk who were with him drew near before Aram for battle, and they fled from his presence.

15 When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they also fled from Abishai, the brother of Joab, and entered the city, and Joab returned to Jerusalem.

16 Aram saw that they were defeated before Israel, so they sent messengers and brought Aram that was beyond the river, and Shophach the captain of the army of Hadadezer before them.

17 It was reported to David, and he gathered all Israel, crossed the Jordan, came against them, and deployed against them; David also drew up in battle formation against Aram to meet them in war, and they fought with him.

18 Aram fled from before Israel, and David killed from Aram seven thousand chariots and forty thousand foot soldiers, and Shophach, the captain of the host, he killed.

19 The bondmen of Hadadezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, so they made peace with David and served him; and Aram was no longer willing to help the sons of Ammon.


## Chapter 20

1 After the return of the year, at the time when kings go out, Joab led the army and destroyed the land of the Ammonites; he came and besieged Rabbah, while David remained in Jerusalem. Joab struck Rabbah and overthrew it.

2 David took the crown of their king from upon his head and found its weight to be a talent of gold, and it had a precious stone; and it was placed upon David's head. The spoil of the city was very great.

3 And the people who were in it he brought out and subjected them to the saw, and to iron axes, and to harrows of iron; and so David did to all the cities of the Ammonites; and David returned, and all the people, to Jerusalem.

4 Afterward there was war at Gazer with the Philistines; then Sibbecai the Hushathite struck down Sippai, one of the sons of the giants, and they were subdued.

5 There was again fighting with the Philistines, and Elkanan son of Jaare–Oregim of Bethlehem struck down Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite, whose spear’s shaft was like a weaver’s beam.

6 There was again fighting in Gath, and a certain man of great stature was there, whose fingers and toes were six and six, twenty-four in all, and he also was born to the giant.

7 He cursed Israel, and Jonathan the son of Shimea, David's brother, struck him.

8 The sons of the giant were born in Gath, and they fell by the hand of David and by the hand of his servants.


## Chapter 21

1 The adversary stood against Israel and incited David to count Israel.

2 David said to Joab and to the captains of the people, Go, number Israel from Beer-shebah even to Dan, and bring me word, that I may know their number.

3 And Joab said, May Jehovah add to His people a hundredfold more than this; are they not all my lord the king’s, my lord’s servants? Why does my lord seek this? Why should it become a cause of trespass to Israel?

4 And the act of the king was strong upon Joab, and Joab went out and walked throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.

5 Joab gave the account of the number of the people to David, and all Israel was one thousand thousand and one hundred thousand men drawing sword, and Judah was four hundred seventy thousand men drawing sword.

6 Levi and Benjamin he did not count among them, because the word of the king was abhorrent concerning Joab.

7 And it was evil in the sight of God concerning this matter, and he struck Israel.

8 David said to God, I have greatly sinned in doing this act; now, please remove the iniquity of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.

9 And the LORD spoke to Gad, David's seer, saying:

10 Go and speak to David, saying, Thus says the LORD, I am bringing three things upon you; choose for yourself one of them, and I will do it to you.

11 Gad came to David and said to him, Thus says the LORD, Take for yourself.

12 If three years of famine, or three months being consumed before your distress and sword of your enemy to destroy, or three days the sword of the LORD and pestilence in the land and the messenger of the LORD destroying in all the border of Israel—now see, what shall I return to him who sent me the word?

13 David said to Gad, I am very distressed; let me fall now into the hand of the LORD, for his mercies are great, but let me not fall into the hand of man.

14 And Jehovah gave a plague in Israel, and there fell from Israel seventy thousand men.

15 God sent a messenger to Jerusalem to destroy it, and as the destroyer was about to act, the LORD saw and relented from the calamity; so he said to the messenger who was destroying, "Enough! Now stop!" And the messenger of the LORD was standing at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

16 David lifted his eyes and saw the messenger of the LORD standing between the earth and the heavens, with his sword drawn in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem; then David and the elders fell facedown, covered with sackcloth.

17 David said to God, "Did I not say to count the people? I am the one who sinned and did very wickedly, but these sheep—what have they done? May the LORD my God please let Your hand be against me and against my father's house, but not against Your people, that there be no plague."

18 And the messenger of the LORD said to Gad, to say to David, that David should go up and raise an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

19 David went up at the word of Gad, which he spoke in the name of the LORD.

20 Ornan turned and saw the angel, and his four sons with him were hiding, and Ornan was threshing wheat.

21 David came to Ornan, and Ornan looked and saw David; and Ornan went out from the threshing-floor and bowed down before David with his face to the ground.

22 David said to Ornan, Give me the place of the threshing floor, that I may build an altar to the LORD there; for the full price of silver give it to me, that the plague may be restrained from upon the people.

23 Then Ornan said to David, Take for yourself, and let my lord the king do what is good in his eyes. Behold, I give the beeve for burnt offerings and the threshing instruments for wood and the wheat for the offering; the whole I have given.

24 King David said to Ornan, No, but I will surely buy it from you for full silver, for I will not take what is yours for the LORD, nor offer a burnt offering without price.

25 David gave to Ornan in the place shekels of gold, the full weight six hundred.

26 David built an altar there to the Lord and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings; he called to the Lord, and he answered him with fire from heaven upon the altar of the burnt offering.

27 And the LORD said to the messenger, and he returned his sword to its sheath.

28 At that time, when David saw that the LORD had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he sacrificed there.

29 And the tabernacle of the LORD which Moses made in the wilderness, and the altar of the ascent in that time, were at the high place in Gibeon.

30 And David could not go before him to inquire of God, for he was afraid because of the sword of the angel of the LORD.


## Chapter 22

1 David said, This is the house of the Lord God, and this is the altar for burnt offering to Israel.

2 David said to gather the aliens who were in the land of Israel, and he stationed stonecutters to hew hewn stones to build the house of God.

3 And iron in abundance for the nails of the gates of the cities and for the couplings did David prepare, and copper in abundance without weight.

4 And cedar trees without number, for the Sidonians and the men of Tyre brought much cedar wood for David.

5 David said, "Solomon my son is young and tender, and the house to be built for the LORD must be exceedingly great in fame and glory for all the lands; therefore I will now prepare for him." So David made abundant preparations before his death.

6 And he called for Solomon his son and charged him to build a house for the LORD God of Israel.

7 David said to Solomon, I had it in my heart to build a house for the name of the LORD my God.

8 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying, "You have shed much blood and waged great wars; you shall not build a house for my name, because you have shed much blood upon the earth before me."

9 Behold, a son will be born to you; he will be a man of rest, and I will give him rest from all his enemies round about, for Solomon will be his name, and I will give peace and quiet to Israel in his days.

10 He will build a temple for my name, and he will be a son to me, and I will be a father to him, and I will establish his throne over Israel forever.

11 Now, my son, may the LORD be with you and grant you success, and may you build the temple of the LORD your God as he has spoken concerning you.

12 But may the LORD grant you discretion and understanding, and appoint you over Israel, and may you keep the law of the LORD your God.

13 Then you will succeed if you keep to do the statutes and the judgments which the LORD commanded Moses concerning Israel; be strong and courageous, do not fear and do not be dismayed.

14 And behold, in my affliction, I have prepared for the temple of the LORD one hundred thousand talents of gold and ten thousand talents of silver, and for copper and iron there is no weighing, for it is abundant; and timber and stones I have prepared, and you shall add to them.

15 And with you in abundance are those who do work, hewers and artificers of stone and wood, and every skilled person in every manner of work.

16 There is no number to the gold, to the silver, to the copper, and to the iron; arise and do it, and may the LORD be with you.

17 David commanded all the captains of Israel to help Solomon his son.

18 Is not the LORD your God with you and has he not given you rest on every side? For he has delivered into my hand the inhabitants of the land, and the land has been subdued before the LORD and before his people.

19 Now bethink yourselves and your souls to seek the LORD your God, and arise and build the sanctuary of the LORD the God, to bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD and the holy vessels of God into the house that is built for the name of the LORD.


## Chapter 23

1 David was old and advanced in age, and he made Solomon his son king over Israel.

2 He gathered together all the princes of Israel and the priests and the Levites.

3 The Levites were counted from the age of thirty years and upward, and their number by heads, as men, was thirty-eight thousand.

4 From these, twenty-four thousand for the business of the house of the LORD, and six thousand officers and judges.

5 and four thousand doorkeepers, and four thousand who praise the LORD with the instruments which I have made for praise.

6 David divided them into divisions according to the sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

7 To the Gershonite, to Laadan and to Shimei.

8 The sons of Laadan, the chief: Jehiel, Zetham, and Joel, three.

9 The sons of Shimei were Shelomoth, and Haziel, and Haran, three. These were chiefs of the fathers for Laadan.

10 And the sons of Shimei were Jahath, Zina, Jehush, and Beriah; these were the sons of Shimei, four.

11 Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second; Jehush and Beriah had few sons; and they became a family household under one oversight.

12 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.

13 The sons of Amram were Aaron and Moses, and Aaron was set apart to consecrate him as most holy, he and his sons forever, to burn incense before the LORD, to minister to him, and to bless in his name forever.

14 Moses the man of God—his sons shall be called by the tribe of Levi.

15 The sons of Moses were Gershom and Eliezer.

16 The sons of Gershom were Shebuel the chief.

17 The sons of Eliezer were Rehabiah the chief, and Eliezer had no other sons, and the sons of Rehabiah were many in number.

18 The sons of Izhar: Shelomith the chief.

19 The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

20 The sons of Uzziel: Micah the chief and Ishiah the second.

21 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.

22 Eleazar died and he had no sons, only daughters, so the sons of Kish his brothers took them.

23 The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Edar, and Jerimoth—three.

24 These are the sons of Levi by their fathers' houses, the chiefs of the fathers numbered by name according to their heads, doing the work of the service of the house of the LORD, from twenty years of age and upward.

25 For David said, Jehovah the Lord God of Israel has given rest to His people, and dwells in Jerusalem forever.

26 And also to the Leviite, there is no bearing the tabernacle and all its furniture for the service thereof.

27 For by the last words of David they counted the Levites from twenty years old and upward.

28 For their attendance beside the sons of Aaron for the service of the house of the LORD, concerning the courts and concerning the chambers and concerning the purification of every holy thing, and the work of the service of the house of God.

29 and for the bread of the row and for fine flour for a grain offering and for unleavened cakes and for a pan and for a baking tray and for every measure and for every measuring.

30 To stand in the morning, morning after morning, to give thanks and to praise the LORD, and likewise in the evening.

31 And for all burnt offerings ascending to the LORD, for the sabbaths, for the new moons, and for the appointed feasts, according to their number, as prescribed by ordinance, continually before the LORD.

32 They shall keep the charge of the tent of meeting and the charge of the sanctuary and the charge of the sons of Aaron their brother for the service of the house of the LORD.


## Chapter 24

1 And for the sons of Aaron, their divisions: the sons of Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

2 Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and they had no children, so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests.

3 David and Zadok from the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech from the sons of Ithamar, divided them according to their offices in their service.

4 The sons of Eleazar were found to be many in number among the leaders of the men, from the sons of Ithamar, and they divided them: for the sons of Eleazar, heads of fathers' houses, sixteen; and for the sons of Ithamar, according to their fathers' houses, eight.

5 They divided them by lot, these with these, for they were holy captains and captains of God, from the sons of Eleazar and from the sons of Ithamar.

6 Shemaiah son of Nethaneel the scribe, a Levite, wrote them down before the king and the officials and Zadok the priest and Ahimelech son of Abiathar and the chiefs of the fathers of the priests and the Levites: one father's house allotted to Eleazar and an allotted father's house to Ithamar.

7 The lot came out for Jehoiarib, the first, and for Jedaiah, the second.

8 The third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim.

9 To Malchiah the fifth, to Miamin the sixth.

10 For Hakkoz the seventh, for Abijah the eighth.

11 Jeshua the ninth, Shecaniah the tenth.

12 to Eliashib eleven, to Jakim twelve.

13 On the thirteenth day for Huppah, the fourteenth for Jeshebeab.

14 On the fifteenth to Bilgah, on the sixteenth to Immer.

15 Hezir, the seventeenth; Happizzez, the eighteenth.

16 In the nineteenth year of Pethakiah, in the twentieth of Ezekiel.

17 Jachin one and twenty, Gamul two and twenty.

18 Delaiah three and twenty, Maaziah four and twenty.

19 These are their accounts for their service, to enter the house of the LORD according to their ordinance, by the hand of Aaron their father, as the LORD God of Israel commanded him.

20 And to the remaining Levites, to the sons of Amram, Shebuel; to the sons of Shebuel, Jehdeiah.

21 For Rehabiah, the sons of Rehabiah, the chief was Ishiah.

22 To the Izharites: Shelomoth, to the sons of Shelomoth: Jahath.

23 The sons of Jeriah: Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.

24 The sons of Uzziel: Micah, and the sons of Micah.

25 The brother of Micah was Ishiah; of the sons of Ishiah, Zechariah.

26 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi; the sons of Jaaziah, his son.

27 The sons of Merari: Jaaziah his son, and Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.

28 To Mahli Eleazar, and he had no sons.

29 Kish, sons of Kish, Jerahmeel.

30 The sons of Mushi were Mahli, Edar, and Jerimoth; these were the sons of the Levites by their families.

31 They cast lots as well, they and their brothers the sons of Aaron, before David the king and Zadok and Ahimelech and the heads of the fathers among the priests and the Levites—fathers of the heads against their younger brothers.


## Chapter 25

1 David and the captains of the host set apart for service the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun, who were to prophesy with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals; and the number of those who were counted for their work was according to the service they performed.

2 The sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph, who prophesied under the authority of the king.

3 The sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jesaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six in all; they were under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the harp in giving thanks and praise to the LORD.

4 The sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jermoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.

5 All these were sons of Heman the king's seer, in the words of God, to exalt the horn; and God gave to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

6 All these under their father's charge in song for the house of the LORD, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps, for the service of the house of God, by the king's order, Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.

7 Their number together with their brothers, trained in song for the LORD, all who were skilled, was two hundred eighty-eight.

8 They cast lots, charge against charge, from the small to the great, scholar with scholar.

9 The lot came out for Asaph, for Joseph; the second was for Gedaliah, he and his brothers and his sons, twelve.

10 The third was Zaccur, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

11 The fourth to Isri: his sons and his brothers, twelve.

12 The fifth was Nethaniah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

13 The sixth was Bukkiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

14 The seventh, to Jesharelah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

15 The eighth was Jesaiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

16 The ninth was Mattaniah; his sons and his brothers were twelve.

17 Shimei the tenth, his sons and his brothers twelve.

18 Twelve, Azarael; his sons and his brothers, twelve.

19 The twelve for Hashabiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

20 Shebuel, his sons, and his brothers, twelve.

21 To Mattithiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

22 For fifteen Jermoth, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

23 For Hananiah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

24 Seventeen to Joshbekashah, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

25 Eighteen to Hanani: his sons and his brothers, twelve.

26 To the nineteenth, Mallothi: his sons and his brothers, twelve.

27 To Eliathah twenty, his sons and his brothers twelve.

28 To Hothir, one and twenty, his sons and his brothers, twelve.

29 To Giddalti, twenty-two: his sons and his brothers, twelve.

30 For Mahazioth, twenty-three reckonings of his sons and his brothers, twelve.

31 For twenty-four to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his brothers, twelve.


## Chapter 26

1 For the divisions of the doorkeepers, the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.

2 Meshelemiah's sons were Zachariah the eldest, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth.

3 Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elihoenai the seventh.

4 The sons of Obed-edom: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth.

5 Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth, for God blessed him.

6 And to Shemaiah his son were born sons who ruled in the house of their father, for they were men of might.

7 The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, and Obed; Elzabad their brother, and Elihu and Semachiah, mighty men.

8 All these were sons of Obed-edom, they and their sons and their brothers, every man mighty in strength for service; sixty-two were for Obed-edom.

9 And to Meshelemiah [there were] sons and brothers, mighty men, eighteen.

10 And as for Hosah, the sons from Merari: Shimri was the chief, although he was not the firstborn, and his father made him chief.

11 Hillkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zachariah the fourth; all the sons and brothers of Hosah, thirteen.

12 These are the divisions of the doorkeepers, for the chief men in their offices, opposite their brothers, to serve in the house of the LORD.

13 They cast lots, small and great, by their ancestral houses, for each gate and gate.

14 The lot fell to Shelemiah toward the east, and his son Zachariah, a counselor in wisdom, cast lots, and his lot came out toward the north.

15 To Obed-Edom southward, and to his sons the storehouse.

16 Shuppim and Hosah to the west, at the gate of Shalleketh on the causeway, the watch opposite the watch.

17 To the east side, six Levites; to the north side daily four; to the south side daily four; and for the Asuppim, two and two.

18 To Parbar, westward: four to the causeway, two to Parbar.

19 These are the divisions of the gatekeepers, for the sons of Korah and for the sons of Merari.

20 The Leviites: Ahiah was over the treasuries of the house of God and over the treasuries of the holy things.

21 The sons of Laadan, the sons of the Gershonite: the heads of the fathers of Laadan, the Gershonite, were Jehieli.

22 The sons of Jehieli: Zetham and Joel his brother, over the storehouses of the house of the LORD.

23 Amramites, Izeharites, Hebronites, Uzzielites.

24 Shebuel son of Gershom son of Moses was overseer of the treasuries.

25 And his brothers of Eliezer were Rehabiah his son, and Jesaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.

26 Shelomith and his brothers were over all the treasuries of the consecrated things that King David and the heads of fathers, the captains of thousands and hundreds, and the commanders of the army had dedicated.

27 From the battles and from the spoil they dedicated to strengthen the house of the LORD.

28 All that Samuel the seer and Saul the son of Kish and Abner the son of Ner and Joab the son of Zeruiah had consecrated was under the authority of Shelomith and his brothers.

29 The Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons, were appointed over the external work for Israel, as officers and judges.

30 The Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kindred, mighty men, one thousand seven hundred, were appointed over the affairs of Israel beyond the Jordan westward, for all the work of the LORD and for the service of the king.

31 The Hebronites, Jeriah the chief; the Hebronites according to their generations, by fathers: in the year forty of David's reign they were sought, and there were found among them mighty men of valor at Jaazer in Gilead.

32 And his brothers, valiant men, two thousand seven hundred chief fathers, King David appointed over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh for every matter of God and the king's word.


## Chapter 27

1 And the sons of Israel according to their number, the chiefs of the fathers and the princes of the thousands and the hundreds and their officers who served the king in every matter of the divisions that came in and went out month by month throughout all the months of the year, the division that came in being twenty-four thousand.

2 Over the first division of the month, Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge, and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

3 From the sons of Perez, the chief over all the captains of the host for the first month.

4 For the division of the second month, Dodai the Ahohite and his division, and Mikloth the chief and his division, twenty-four thousand.

5 The captain of the host for the third month, Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest, was chief; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

6 He was Benaiah, a mighty man of the thirty, and over the thirty; his course was Ammizabad, his son.

7 In the fourth month, Asahel the brother of Joab served, and after him Zebadiah his son, and over his division twenty-four thousand.

8 The fifth for the month, the captain Shamhuth the Izrahite, and over his division twenty-four thousand.

9 In the sixth month, the sixth, Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, and over his division twenty-four thousand.

10 In the seventh month, Helez the Pelonite, of the sons of Ephraim, and his division, twenty-four thousand.

11 In the eighth month, Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarchite clan, over his division, twenty-four thousand.

12 On the ninth day of the ninth month, Abiezer the Anethothite by his division, twenty-four thousand.

13 On the tenth day of the month, for the tenth month, Maharai the Netophathite, for the Zarchite, and for his division twenty-four thousand.

14 In the eleventh month of the year, Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the sons of Ephraim, was over the division; and he had twenty-four thousand.

15 The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, for Othniel, and over his division twenty-four thousand.

16 Over the tribes of Israel: for the Reubenites, Eliezer son of Zichri was their leader; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah son of Maacah.

17 For Levi, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;

18 For Judah, Elihu, a brother of David; for Issachar, Omri the son of Michael.

19 For Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jermoth son of Azriel.

20 For the sons of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Azaziah; for half the tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah.

21 For half the Manasseh, Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner.

22 For Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham; these were the captains of the tribes of Israel.

23 David did not number them from the age of twenty years and under, because the LORD had said he would multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.

24 Joab son of Zeruiah began to count, but did not finish, and there was wrath on Israel because of this, and the number was not entered in the account of the chronicles for King David.

25 Over the king's storehouses was Azmaveth son of Adiel, and over the storehouses in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers was Jonathan son of Uzziah.

26 and over those doing business in the field for the service of the ground, Ezri son of Chelub.

27 And over the vineyards: Shimei the Ramathite; and over what was in the vineyards for the storehouses of wine: Zabdi the Shiphmite.

28 Over the olive trees and the sycamores that were in the low country, Baal-hanan the Gederite; and over the storehouses of oil, Joash.

29 And over the cattle that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite, and over the cattle in the valleys was Shaphat son of Adlai.

30 And over the camel. Obil the Ishmaelite. and over the (she) ass. Jehdeiah the Meronothite.

31 And over the (small) cattle Jaziz the Hagarene was chief; all these were stewards of the substance that belonged to king David.

32 Jonathan, David's uncle, a man of understanding and a scribe, was counselor, and Jehiel son of Hachmoni was with the king's sons.

33 Ahithophel was advising the king, and Hushai the Archite was the king's friend.

34 After Ahithophel was Jehoiada son of Benaiah and Abiathar, and Joab was captain of the king’s army.


## Chapter 28

1 David assembled all the captains of Israel, the captains of the tribes, and the captains of the divisions that ministered to the king, and the captains of thousands and the captains of hundreds, and the captains of all riches and cattle for the king and for his sons, with the officers and the mighty men, and to every valiant warrior in Jerusalem.

2 David the king arose upon his feet and said, Hear me, my brothers and my people; it was in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the LORD and for the footstool of our God, and I prepared to build it.

3 And God said to me, You shall not build a temple for my name, because you are a man of wars and have shed blood.

4 The LORD God of Israel chose me from all the house of my father to be king over Israel forever, for in Judah He chose for leader, and in the house of Judah, the house of my father, and among the sons of my father He was pleased with me to make king over all Israel.

5 And from all my sons, for the LORD has given me many sons, he chose Solomon my son to sit on the throne of the kingdom of the LORD over Israel.

6 And he said to me, Solomon your son—he will build my temple and my courts, for I have chosen him for me as a son, and I will be to him as a father.

7 I will establish his kingdom forever, if he strengthens himself to keep my commandments and my ordinances as on this day.

8 And now, before the eyes of all Israel, the assembly of the LORD, and in the hearing of our God, keep and seek all the commandments of the LORD your God, so that you may possess the good land and inherit it for your children after you forever.

9 And you, Solomon my son, know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and a willing soul, for the LORD searches all hearts and understands every purpose and thought; if you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever.

10 See now that the LORD has chosen you to build a temple for the sanctuary; be strong and do it.

11 David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch and of its courts and of its treasuries and of its upper chambers and of its inner chambers and of the house of the mercy seat.

12 And the pattern of all that was in his spirit for the courts of the house of the LORD and for all the chambers around, for the storehouses of the house of God and for the storehouses of the holy things.

13 and for the divisions of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of the LORD, and for all the vessels of the service of the house of the LORD.

14 for gold by weight, for gold, for all vessels of service and service, for all silver vessels by weight, for all vessels of service and service.

15 and the weight for the golden candlesticks, their lamps of gold, by weight candlestick by candlestick and their lamps, and for the silver candlesticks by weight, candlestick and their lamps, according to the service of each candlestick and candlestick.

16 and the gold, its weight for the tables of the shewbread, for one table and another, and silver for the tables of silver.

17 And the fleshhooks and the basins and the cups of pure gold, and for the gold basins by weight, basin by basin, and for the silver basins by weight, basin by basin.

18 and for the altar of (sweet) incense, gold refined by (full) weight, and for the figure of the chariot, the cherubims of gold, spreading out and covering over the ark of the covenant of the LORD.

19 All the writing from the hand of the LORD has made me understand, all the manner of work of the pattern.

20 David said to Solomon his son, Be strong and courageous, do not fear and do not be dismayed, for the LORD my God is with you, he will not fail you nor forsake you until all the work of the service of the house of the LORD is finished.

21 And behold, the divisions of the priests and the Levites, for all service of the house of God, and with you in every work every skilled person in wisdom for all service, and the rulers and all the people for all your word.


## Chapter 29

1 David the king said to all the assembly, "Solomon my son is young and untested, and the work is great, for the palace is not for man but for the LORD God."

2 And according to all my ability, I have prepared for the house of my God: gold for the gold, silver for the silver, brass for the brass, iron for the iron, and wood for the woods; onyx stones, and stones for inlaying, and glistering stones, and every precious stone, and marble in abundance.

3 Moreover, in my delight in the house of God, I have a special treasure of gold and silver; I have given to the house of my God above all that I have prepared for the holy temple.

4 three thousand talents of gold, even gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver for overlaying the walls of the houses.

5 For gold, for silver, and for every work by the hand of artificers; and who will dedicate his hand today to the LORD?

6 The captains of the fathers and the chiefs of the tribes of Israel and the captains of the thousands and the hundreds, and to the captains of the king's work, freely offered.

7 They gave for the service of the house of God five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold, and ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of brass, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.

8 And the stones found with him they gave to the treasury of the house of the LORD, by the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.

9 And the folk rejoiced over their willing contributions, because with full consent they had contributed to the LORD, and also David the king rejoiced with great joy.

10 David blessed the LORD before all the assembly, and David said, "Blessed are you, the LORD, God of Israel our father, from everlasting to everlasting."

11 To you, O LORD, belong the greatness, the might, the glory, the victory, and the majesty, for all that is in the heavens and on the earth belongs to you, O LORD; yours is the kingdom, and you are exalted as head over all.

12 And riches and glory are before you, and you rule over all, and in your hand is power and might, and in your hand to increase and strengthen all.

13 And now, our God, we give thanks to you and praise the name of your glory.

14 But who am I and who are my folk, that we should have the strength to offer this freely? For from you is all, and from your hand we have given to you.

15 For we are aliens and sojourners before you, as all our fathers were; the days of our life on the earth are like a shadow, and there is no abiding.

16 the LORD our God, all this abundance that we have prepared to build you a temple for the name of your holiness comes from your hand and to you is the whole.

17 And I know, O God, that You test the heart and delight in integrity; I have given freely with an upright heart, and now seeing Your people here, I rejoice with joy in their free offerings to You.

18 O the LORD, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers, keep this forever in the purpose of the thoughts of the heart of Your people, and prepare their heart toward You.

19 And for Solomon my son, give a whole heart to keep your commandments, your testimonies, and your statutes, and to do all, and to build the palace that I have prepared.

20 Then David said to all the assembly, Bless now the LORD your God. And all the assembly blessed the LORD, the God of their fathers, and bowed down and prostrated themselves before the LORD and the king.

21 They sacrificed offerings to the LORD and offered burnt offerings to the LORD on the next day of that day: one thousand bulls, one thousand rams, one thousand sheep, and their drink offerings, and great sacrifices for all Israel.

22 They ate and drank before the LORD on that day with great joy, and they made Solomon son of David king a second time, and they anointed him to the LORD as leader and Zadok as priest.

23 Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king instead of David his father and prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.

24 And all the captains and the mighty men, and also all the sons of King David, gave their hand to Solomon the king.

25 The LORD magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel and gave him royal glory such as had not been on any king before him over Israel.

26 David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.

27 The years that he reigned over Israel were forty years; in Hebron he reigned seven years, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three.

28 He died in good old age, full of days, riches and honour, and Solomon his son reigned in his place.

29 And the acts of David the king, the first and the last, behold they are written on the acts of Samuel the seer, and on the acts of Nathan the prophet, and on the acts of Gad the seer.

30 with all his kingdom and his might, and the times that passed over him and over Israel and over all the kingdoms of the lands


# 2 CHRONICLES

## Chapter 1

1 Solomon the son of David strengthened himself upon his kingdom, and the LORD his God was with him and made him exceedingly great.

2 Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and to the judges, and to every leader of all Israel, the heads of the fathers.

3 Solomon and all the assembly went together to the high place that was in Gibeon, for there was the tent of meeting of God, which Moses the servant of the LORD had made in the wilderness.

4 But the ark of God David brought up from Kirjath-jearim and prepared for him, for he pitched for him a tent in Jerusalem.

5 And the brasen altar which Bezalel the son of Uri the son of Hur made, he set before the tabernacle of the LORD, and Solomon and the congregation sought it.

6 Solomon went up to the brasen altar before the LORD, which was by the tent of meeting, and offered burnt offerings on it, a thousand.

7 That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him, Ask what I should give you.

8 Solomon said to God, You have done great kindness with David my father, and You have made me king in his place.

9 Now, O Lord God, may your word be confirmed with David my father, for you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.

10 Now give me wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out before this people and come in before them, for who can judge this great people of yours?

11 And God said to Solomon, Because this was with your heart, and you did not ask for riches, wealth, and honour, nor the life of those who hate you, and neither many days did you ask, but you asked for yourself wisdom and knowledge, that you may judge my people over whom I have made you king.

12 Wisdom and knowledge are given to you, and I will give you riches and wealth and honour such as has not been for the kings before you, and after you there will not be such.

13 Solomon came to the high place which was in Gibeon, Jerusalem, before the tent of meeting, and he reigned over Israel.

14 Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen, and he had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen, and he stationed them in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.

15 The king gave the silver and the gold in Jerusalem like stones, and he gave the cedars like sycamores that are in the low country for abundance.

16 And the going forth of the horses that belonged to Solomon was from Egypt, and the gathering place of the king's traders, from a gathering place they took at a price.

17 They imported a chariot from Egypt for six hundred pieces of silver and a horse for fifty and one hundred, and thus to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram, by their hand they brought out.


## Chapter 2

1 Solomon said to build a temple for the name of the LORD and a palace for his kingdom.

2 Solomon counted seventy thousand men bearing burden, and eighty thousand men hewing in the mountain, and overseers over them, three thousand and six hundred.

3 Solomon sent to Huram king of Tyre, saying, As you dealt with David my father, and sent him cedars to build him a house to dwell in,

4 Behold, I am building a temple for the name of the LORD my God, to sanctify it for Him, to burn before Him sweet incense, and continual rows of shewbread, and burnt offerings for the morning and for the evening, for every sabbath and for the new moons, and for the appointed feasts of the LORD our God, forever—this for Israel.

5 And the temple that I am building is great, for great is our God above all gods.

6 And who is able to muster the strength to build him a house, since the heavens and the highest heavens cannot contain him? And who am I, that I should build him a house, except to make offerings before him?

7 Now send me a skilled man to work in gold and silver, in bronze and iron, in purple, crimson, and blue, and who knows how to engrave engravings, along with the skilled men who are with me in Judah and Jerusalem, whom my father David provided.

8 And send me cedar trees, fir trees, and algum trees from Lebanon, for I know that your servants know how to cut timber from Lebanon, and behold, my servants will be with your servants.

9 and to prepare for me timber in abundance, for the temple that I am building is great and magnificent.

10 And behold, to the woodcutters for cutting timber I have given wheat twenty cors and beaten barley twenty cors for your servants, and wine twenty baths and oil twenty baths.

11 Huram king of Tyre wrote a letter to Solomon saying, because of the love of the LORD for his people he has made you king over them.

12 Huram said, "Blessed be the LORD, God of Israel, who made the heavens and the earth, who gave to David the king a son wise, having understanding and discernment, to build a temple for the LORD and a royal palace for his reign."

13 And now I have sent a man who is wise, having knowledge, to Huram my father.

14 A son of a woman, of the daughters of Dan, and his father a man of Tyre, skilled to work in gold and in silver, in brass, in iron, in stones, and in woods, in purple, in blue, and in fine linen, and in crimson, and to grave any graving, and to devise every device which may be given him, with the wise men of my lord David your father.

15 And now the wheat and barley, the oil and wine that my lord said he would send to his servants.

16 And we will cut wood from Lebanon according to all your need, and bring it to you in floats by sea to Joppa, and you will carry it up to Jerusalem.

17 Solomon counted all the aliens who were in the land of Israel, after the numbering that David his father had numbered, and they were found to be one hundred fifty-three thousand six hundred.

18 He made from them seventy thousand burden bearers and eighty thousand mountain stonecutters and three thousand and six hundred overseers to supervise the people.


## Chapter 3

1 Solomon began to build the temple of the Lord in Jerusalem on the mountain of Moriah, where the Lord appeared to David his father, the place that David had prepared on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

2 And he began to build in the second month, in the second year of his reign.

3 And these were the foundations Solomon laid for building the temple of God: the length by the former measure was sixty cubits, and the breadth twenty cubits.

4 And the porch before the hall was twenty cubits broad according to the breadth of the house, and the height thereof was a hundred and twenty cubits, and he overlaid it within with pure gold.

5 And the great house he covered with fir wood, and he overlaid it with fine gold, and he added upon it palm trees and chains.

6 And he overlaid the temple with precious stone for beauty, and the gold was gold from Parvaim.

7 And he overlaid the house, the roof, the posts, and its walls and its doors with gold, and carved cherubim on the walls.

8 He made the most holy sanctuary, its length twenty cubits corresponding to the breadth of the temple, and its breadth twenty cubits, and he overlaid it with good gold amounting to six hundred talents.

9 And the weight for the nails, fifty shekels of gold, and the upper chambers overlaid with gold.

10 He made in the house of the holy of holies two cherubim, the work of images, and he overlaid them with gold.

11 And the wings of the cherubims were twenty cubits in length; one wing of the one was five cubits reaching to the wall of the house, and the other wing of the other was five cubits reaching to the wing of the other cherub.

12 And the wing of the one cherub was five cubits reaching to the wall of the house, and the other wing was five cubits joined to the wing of the other cherub.

13 The wings of these cherubim were each twenty cubits, and they stood on their legs with their faces toward the temple.

14 And he made the vail of blue, purple, crimson, and fine white linen, and he worked upon it cherubims.

15 He made before the temple two pillars, thirty-five cubits in length, and the chapiter upon the top of each was five cubits.

16 He made chains in the oracle and placed a band on the heads of the pillars, and he made one hundred pomegranates and put them on the chains.

17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple, one on the right and one on the left, and he called the name of the right one Jachin and the name of the left one Boaz.


## Chapter 4

1 He made a brasen altar, twenty cubits its length, and twenty cubits its breadth, and ten cubits its height.

2 He made the sea of cast metal, ten cubits from its brim to its brim, round all about, and five cubits its height, and a line of thirty cubits encircled it all around.

3 And the fashion of beeves was under it, round about, ten cubits, encircling the sea all around; two rows of beeves were cast in its casting.

4 Standing upon twelve beeves: three facing northward, three facing westward, three facing southward, and three facing eastward; and the sea was upon them above, and all their hinder parts were within.

5 Its thickness was a hand-breadth, and its brim like the work of a lily blossom, a bath holding three thousand.

6 He made ten basins and placed five on the right and five on the left to wash in them the items of the burnt offering; the sea was for the priests to wash in.

7 He made the ten golden candlesticks according to their charge, and set them in the temple, five on the right and five on the left.

8 He made ten tables and placed them in the temple, five on the right and five on the left, and he made one hundred golden bowls.

9 He made the court of the priests and the great court, and the gates for the court and their doors were overlaid with brass.

10 And the sea he gave from the right shoulder, eastward, opposite the south.

11 Huram made the caldrons, the shovels, and the basins, and finished the work that he had done for King Solomon in the temple of God.

12 two pillars, and the bowls and the chapiters upon the top of the pillars, two; and the wreathen works to cover the two bowls of the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars.

13 And four hundred pomegranates for the two checkerwork wreaths: two rows of pomegranates for one checkerwork wreath, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were on the faces of the pillars.

14 And he made the bases, and he made the lavers upon the bases.

15 one sea and twelve beeves under it

16 And the pans, the shovels, the fleshhooks, and all their vessels, Huram his father made for King Solomon for the house of the LORD, burnished bronze.

17 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them in the thick of the ground between Succoth and Zeredathah.

18 Solomon made all the vessels in great abundance, for the weight of the brass was not measured.

19 Solomon made all the vessels for the house of God, and the golden altar, and the tables with their bread of the Presence.

20 And the candlesticks and their lamps, to burn according to the ordinance before the oracle, solid gold, closed.

21 And the blossom, and the lamps, and the tongs, of gold—those are golden vessels.

22 And the snuffers and the bowls and the spoons and the firepans of pure gold, and the entrance of the temple, its inner doors to the most holy place, and the doors of the temple to the sanctuary, of gold.


## Chapter 5

1 And all the work that Solomon had done for the house of the LORD was finished, and Solomon brought in the holy things of David his father, and the silver and the gold and all the vessels, and put them in the treasuries of the house of God.

2 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the tribal leaders, the heads of the fathers of the people of Israel, to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the city of David, that is, Zion.

3 All the men of Israel gathered to the king at the feast in the seventh month.

4 And all the elders of Israel came, and the Levites lifted the ark.

5 They carried up the ark and the tent of meeting and all the vessels of the sanctuary that were in the tent; the priests, the Levites, carried them up.

6 King Solomon and all the assembly of Israel gathered before him at the ark were sacrificing sheep and cattle that could not be counted or numbered for their abundance.

7 The priests brought the ark of the covenant of the LORD to its place, into the inner sanctuary of the house, to the most holy place, beneath the wings of the cherubim.

8 The cherubims had their wings spread out over the place of the ark, and the cherubims covered the ark and its poles from above.

9 And they extended the bars, and the tops of the bars were seen from the ark toward the face of the oracle, but the outside was not seen, and it was there until this day.

10 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets which Moses placed at Horeb, which the LORD made with the children of Israel when they came out from Egypt.

11 And it happened when the priests went out from the sanctuary, for all the priests who were found had sanctified themselves, there was no need to divide by courses.

12 And the Leviites the singers, all of them, for Asaph, for Heman, for Jeduthun, and for their sons and for their brothers, clothed in fine linen, with cymbals and psalteries and harps, standing east of the altar, and with them priests, one hundred and twenty, with trumpets.

13 And it was, when the trumpeters and singers were as one, to make a sound with one voice, to praise and to give thanks to the LORD, and when the voice rose with the trumpets and cymbals and musical instruments in praise to the LORD, for good, for his mercy is everlasting, that the house, the temple of the LORD, was filled with a cloud.

14 And the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud, for the glory of the LORD filled the house of God.


## Chapter 6

1 Then Solomon said, "Jehovah, the Lord, said to dwell in the thick dark."

2 But I have built a house of dwelling for you, a settled place for your habitation forever.

3 Then the king turned his face and blessed all the congregation of Israel, and all the congregation of Israel was standing.

4 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel who spoke by his mouth to David my father and has fulfilled with his hand what he promised.

5 From the day I brought my people out of the land of Egypt, I did not choose a city from all the tribes of Israel to build a house for my name to be there, nor did I choose a man to be leader over my people Israel.

6 And I chose Jerusalem to be the place of my name, and I chose David to be over my people Israel.

7 And David my father had it in mind to build a house for the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.

8 And Jehovah said to David my father, Because it was with your mind to build a temple for my name, you have done well that it was with your mind.

9 Only you shall not build the temple, for your son who comes from your loins shall build the temple for my name.

10 The Lord upheld his word that he spoke, and I have risen in place of David my father and sit on the throne of Israel as the Lord promised, and I have built the temple for the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.

11 And I placed there the ark, in which is the covenant of the LORD that he made with the children of Israel.

12 He stood before the altar of the LORD opposite all the congregation of Israel and spread his hands.

13 Solomon made a brasen laver and placed it among the court; its length was five cubits and its breadth five cubits and its height three cubits; and he stood upon it and blessed upon his knees before all the congregation of Israel and spread his hands toward the heavens.

14 The Lord God of Israel said: There is no one like you, O God, in heaven and on the earth, keeping covenant and steadfast love with your servants who walk before you with all their heart.

15 which you have kept for your servant David my father, what you spoke to him, and with your mouth and with your hand you have fulfilled as this day.

16 And now, O LORD God of Israel, keep for Your servant David my father the promise You made him, saying, There shall not be cut off from you a man before Me to sit upon the throne of Israel; only if your sons take heed to their way, to walk in My law, as you have walked before Me.

17 And now, O LORD, God of Israel, let your promise that you spoke to your servant David be established.

18 Indeed, would God dwell with humanity on the land? Behold, the heavens and the heavens of heavens cannot contain you—how much less this temple that I have built.

19 And you will turn to the prayer of your servant and to his supplication, O LORD my God, to listen to the cry and to the prayer that your servant prays before you.

20 May your eyes be open toward this house day and night, toward the place of which you said to put your name there, to listen to the prayer that your servant shall pray toward this place.

21 And you shall hear the supplication of your servant and of your people Israel, when they pray toward this place; and you, from the place of your dwelling in the heavens, shall hear and forgive.

22 If a man sins against his neighbour and an oath is laid upon him to make him swear, and he comes with that oath before your altar in this temple.

23 But you, hear from the heavens and act, and judge your servants, to return to the wicked, to give his way upon his head, and to justify the righteous, to give to him according to his righteousness.

24 If your people Israel are struck down before the enemy because they have sinned against you, and they return and acknowledge your name and pray and plead for mercy before you in this temple,

25 But you, hear from the heavens, and pardon the sin of your people Israel, and bring them back to the land that you gave to them and to their fathers.

26 When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against you, and they pray toward this place and confess your name, returning from their sin because you answer them.

27 But you, hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants and your people Israel, for you will instruct them in the good way that they may walk in it, and give rain upon your land that you gave to your people for an inheritance.

28 If there is dearth in the land, pestilence, blasting or yellowing, locust or caterpillar, if their enemies besiege them in the land of their gates, every plague and every sickness.

29 all prayer and all supplication that may be from any person and from all your people Israel, each knowing his plague and his sorrow, spreading his hands toward this house.

30 But you will hear from the heavens, the place of your dwelling, and pardon, and render to each person according to all their ways, for you alone know the mind of every human being.

31 so that they may fear you to walk along your ways all the days that they live upon the face of the land which you gave to our fathers

32 Even for the foreigner who is not of your people Israel, who comes from a distant land because of your great name and your mighty hand and your outstretched arm, when they come and pray toward this temple.

33 But you are to hear from the heavens, from the place of your dwelling, and act according to all that the foreigner calls to you, so that all the peoples of the earth may know your name and fear you, like your people Israel, and may know that your name is called upon this house that I have built.

34 When your people go out to war against their enemies along the way that you send them, and they pray to you toward this city that you have chosen and the temple that I have built for your name,

35 And you shall hear from the heavens their prayer and their supplication, and you shall execute their judgment.

36 When they sin against you—for there is no person who does not sin—and you are angry with them and give them into the hand of the enemy, so that their captors carry them away to a land far away or near—

37 and they bethink themselves in the land where they were carried captive, and they return and make supplication to you in the land of their captivity, saying, We have sinned, we have done perversely, we have acted wickedly.

38 And they shall return to you with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their captivity, where you have led them captive, and they shall pray toward their land which you gave to their fathers, and toward the city which you have chosen, and toward the house which I have built for your name.

39 And you shall hear from the heavens, from the place of your dwelling, their prayer and their supplication, and execute judgment for them, and forgive your people who have sinned against you.

40 Now, O God, let your eyes be open and your ears attentive to the prayer at this place.

41 Now therefore arise, O the LORD, O God, for your resting place; you and the ark of your strength; let your priests, O the LORD, O God, be clothed with deliverance, and let your faithful rejoice in good.

42 O LORD God, do not turn away the face of your anointed; remember the mercies of David your servant.


## Chapter 7

1 When Solomon finished praying, fire came down from the heavens and consumed the burnt offering and the sacrifices, and the glory of the LORD filled the temple.

2 The priests could not enter the temple of the LORD because the glory of the LORD filled the temple of the LORD.

3 All the people of Israel saw the fire descending and the glory of the LORD upon the temple, and they bowed their faces to the ground on the pavement and prostrated themselves, and gave thanks to the LORD, for good, for everlasting is his mercy.

4 And the king and all the people offered sacrifice before the LORD.

5 Then King Solomon offered as a sacrifice twenty-two thousand beeves and one hundred and twenty thousand sheep, and the king and all the people dedicated the house of God.

6 And the priests stood in their offices, and the Levites with instruments of song to the LORD, which David the king had made to give thanks to the LORD, for his kindness endures forever, with the praises of David in their hands; and the priests blowing trumpets opposite them, and all Israel standing.

7 Solomon consecrated the middle of the court which was before the temple of the LORD, because there he offered the burnt offerings and the fat of the peace offerings, since the brasen altar that Solomon had made could not contain the burnt offering, the grain offering, and the fat.

8 Solomon observed the feast at that time for seven days, and all Israel with him, a very great assembly, from the entrance of Hamath to the river of Egypt.

9 On the eighth day they held a solemn assembly, for the dedicating of the altar they had made seven days, and the feast seven days.

10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month he sent the people to their tents, joyful and good of heart for the good that the LORD had done for David and for Solomon and for Israel his people.

11 Solomon finished the temple of the LORD and the king's palace and all that came to Solomon's mind to make in the temple of the LORD and in his house he prospered.

12 The Lord appeared to Solomon at night and said to him, I have heard your prayer and have chosen this place for me as a house of sacrifice.

13 Behold, I will shut up the heavens so that there will be no rain, and I will command the locust to devour the land, and if I send pestilence among my people—

14 And My folk who are called by My name shall humble themselves and pray and seek My face and turn from their evil ways; then I will hear from heaven and forgive their sin and heal their land.

15 Now my eyes will be open and my ears attentive to the prayer of this place.

16 And now I have chosen and consecrated this temple, that my name may be there forever; and my eyes and my heart shall be there continually.

17 But you, if you will walk before me as David my father walked, and do according to all that I have commanded you, and keep my statutes and my judgments,

18 I will establish your throne as I promised to David your father, saying, "There shall not be cut off from you a man ruling over Israel."

19 But if you indeed turn away and abandon my statutes and the ordinances that I have set before you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them,

20 And I will pluck them from off the land that I gave them, and I will cast this temple, which I sanctified for my name, from before my face, and I will give it for a byword and a taunt among all the nations.

21 And this temple that was exalted, everyone passing by it will be appalled, and will say, "Why has the LORD done thus to this land and to this temple?"

22 They will say, Because they abandoned the LORD their fathers' God who brought them out of the land of Egypt, and they held fast to other gods, and they bowed down to them and served them, therefore he has brought upon them all this evil.


## Chapter 8

1 After twenty years, during which Solomon built the temple of the LORD and his palace, it happened.

2 The cities that Huram gave to Solomon, Solomon built them, and he settled there the sons of Israel.

3 Solomon went to Hamath-Zobah and strengthened his hold over it.

4 And he built Tadmor in the wilderness and all the store cities that he built in Hamath.

5 And he built Beth-horon the upper and Beth-horon the lower, fortified cities with walls, two doors and a bar.
