6 and Baalath and all the towns of storehouses that belonged to Solomon and all the towns of the chariots and the towns of the horsemen and all the desire of Solomon that he desired to build in Jerusalem and in Lebanon and in all the land of his dominion.

7 All the people remaining of the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, who were not of Israel.

8 From their children who remained after them in the land, whom the children of Israel did not destroy, Solomon conscripted for tribute to this day.

9 From the people of Israel whom Solomon did not assign to serve in his work, for they were men of war, his captains, commanders of his chariots, and his horsemen.

10 And these were the captains who were over the officers for King Solomon: two hundred and fifty, who ruled over the people.

11 Solomon brought up Pharaoh's daughter from the city of David to the house that he had built for her, for he said, "My wife shall not dwell in the house of David king of Israel, because the places are holy where the ark of the LORD has come."

12 Then Solomon ascended burnt offerings to the LORD upon the altar of the LORD which he built before the porch.

13 According to the commandment of Moses, day by day continually, for the sabbaths and for the new moons and for the appointed seasons, three times in the year, at the feast of unleavened bread, and at the feast of weeks, and at the feast of booths.

14 He established according to the custom of David his father the divisions of the priests for their service and the Levites for their watches to praise and to minister before the priests, day by day, and the gatekeepers in their divisions for gate and gate, for so was the commandment of David the man of God.

15 They did not turn aside from the commandment of the king concerning the priests and the Levites in all matters and the storehouses.

16 All the work of Solomon was completed from the day of the foundation of the house of the LORD to its completion; the house of the LORD was finished.

17 Then Solomon went to Ezion-geber and to Elath on the shore of the sea in the land of Edom.

18 Huram sent him by the hands of his servants ships and servants skilled with the sea, and they came with the servants of Solomon to Ophir, and they took from there four hundred and fifty talents of gold, and brought it to King Solomon.


## Chapter 9

1 The queen of Sheba heard the report about Solomon and came to test Solomon with hard questions in Jerusalem, with a very great retinue, and camels bearing spices, and a great abundance of gold and precious stones; and she came to Solomon and spoke with him about all that was on her heart.

2 Solomon told her all his affairs, and there was not a thing of Solomon's that he did not tell her.

3 The queen of Sheba saw the wisdom of Solomon and the palace which he built.

4 The food of his table, and the dwelling of his servants, and the attendance of his attendants, and their apparel, and his drink, and their apparel, and his upper chamber, which he went up into the house of the LORD, and there was no spirit any more in her.

5 She said to the king, "True is the word that I heard in my land concerning your words and your wisdom."

6 I did not believe their reports until I came and my eyes saw, and indeed, they did not tell me half the greatness of your wisdom; you have surpassed the report that I heard.

7 Blessed are your people and blessed are your servants who stand before you continually and hear your wisdom.

8 Blessed be the LORD your God who delighted in you to set you upon the throne of the king for the LORD your God by your God’s love for Israel to establish it forever and who set you over them as king to execute justice and righteousness.

9 She gave the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold, and very great abundance of spices, and precious stones, and there was no spice like the spice that the queen of Sheba gave King Solomon.

10 And also the servants of Hiram and the servants of Solomon who brought gold from Ophir brought algum trees and precious stones.

11 The king made the algum trees into terraces for the house of the LORD and for the house of the king, and into harps and psalteries for the singers; and such as these had not been seen before in the land of Judah.

12 And King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all her desires which she asked, besides what she had brought to the king; and she turned and went to her land, she and her servants.

13 The full weight of the gold that came to Solomon in a year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold.

14 except for traders and merchants, all the kings of Arabia and governors of the land brought gold and silver to Solomon

15 King Solomon made two hundred gold shields, each shield requiring six hundred gold pieces of gold.

16 And three hundred shields of beaten gold, three hundred shekels of gold went to the one shield, and the king placed them in the house of the forest of Lebanon.

17 The king made a great ivory throne and overlaid it with pure gold.

18 And six steps to the throne, and a golden footstool for the throne, with armrests on either side of the place of the seat, and two lions standing beside the armrests.

19 And twelve (young) lions were standing there, six on this side and six on that side of the steps; nothing like this had been made for any kingdom.

20 All the drinking vessels of King Solomon were gold, and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were gold; silver was not counted for anything in the days of Solomon.

21 For ships of the king went to Tarshish with servants of Huram; once every three years came Tarshish ships bringing gold and silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks.

22 King Solomon became greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.

23 And all the kings of the earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom that God had given in his heart.

24 And they brought every man his gift, vessels of silver and vessels of gold, garments, weapons, spices, horses and mules, each year in its year.

25 Solomon had four thousand stalls for horses and chariots and twelve thousand horsemen, and he stationed them in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.

26 He ruled over all the kings from the River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt.

27 The king gave the silver in Jerusalem as stones, and the cedars he gave as sycamores in the low country for abundance.

28 And they brought horses out of Egypt for Solomon and from all the lands.

29 And the rest of the words of Solomon, the earlier and the later, are they not written in the chronicles of Nathan the prophet, and in the prophecy of Ahijah the Shilonite, and in the visions of the seer concerning Jeroboam son of Nebat?

30 Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel for forty years.

31 Solomon slept with his forefathers and they buried him in the city of David his father, and Rehoboam his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 10

1 Rehoboam went to Shechem, for all Israel had come to Shechem to make him king.

2 When Jeroboam son of Nebat heard in Egypt, where he had fled from the presence of King Solomon, then Jeroboam returned from Egypt.

3 They sent and summoned him, and Jeroboam came with all Israel, and they spoke to Rehoboam, saying:

4 Your father has made our yoke heavy; now lighten the hard service and the heavy yoke that your father imposed on us, and we will serve you.

5 And he said to them, Yet three days and return to me; and the people went.

6 Then King Rehoboam consulted the elders who had attended his father Solomon during his lifetime, saying, "How do you advise me to answer this people's word?"

7 And they spoke to him, saying, If it is good in the eyes of this folk, and you are pleased with them, and you speak to them good words, then they will be your servants all the days.

8 He abandoned the advice of the elders that they advised him, and consulted the young men who had grown up with him, those standing before him.

9 He said to them, "What advice do you give? Let us return an answer to this people, who said to me, 'Lighten the yoke that your father laid upon us.'"

10 The boys who had grown up with him spoke to him, saying, “Thus you shall say to the people who spoke to you, saying, ‘Your father made heavy our yoke; now you, lighten it from upon us’—thus you shall say to them, ‘My little finger is thicker than my father’s loins.’”

11 Now my father imposed a heavy yoke on you, and I will add to your yoke; my father disciplined you with the scourge, but I with scorpions.

12 And Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day, as the king had directed, saying, "Return to me on the third day."

13 And the king answered them harshly, and King Rehoboam abandoned the counsel of the elders.

14 He spoke to them according to the advice of the young men, saying, "I will make heavier your yoke; and I will add to it; my father disciplined you with whips, but I with scorpions."

15 And the king did not listen to the people, for the cause was from with God, so that the LORD might confirm his word which he spoke by the hand of Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam son of Nebat.

16 All Israel turned away from the king when they saw that the king would not listen to them, and the people answered the king, saying, “What portion do we have in David, and what inheritance in the son of Jesse? To your tents, O Israel! Look now to your own house, O David!” So all Israel went to their tents.

17 And the sons of Israel who dwelt in the cities of Judah, Rehoboam reigned over them.

18 Then King Rehoboam sent Hadoram, who was over the levy, but the sons of Israel stoned him with a stone and he died; and King Rehoboam strengthened himself to mount the chariot to flee to Jerusalem.

19 Israel has transgressed against the house of David to this day.


## Chapter 11

1 Rehoboam came to Jerusalem and gathered the court of Judah and Benjamin: one hundred and eighty thousand chosen fighting men, to fight against Israel, to restore the kingdom to Rehoboam.

2 And the word of the LORD came to Shemaiah the man of God, saying:

3 Say to Rehoboam son of Solomon king of Judah and to all Israel in Judah and Benjamin, saying:

4 Thus says the LORD: Do not go up and do not fight against your brothers; return, each man to his house, for from me this thing has happened. And they listened to the words of the LORD and turned back from going to battle against Jeroboam.

5 Rehoboam lived in Jerusalem and built towns for defence in Judah.

6 He built Bethlehem, Etam, and Tekoa.

7 and Beth-zur, and Soco, and Adullam,

8 and Gath and Mareshah and Ziph.

9 And Adoraim and Lachish and Azekah.

10 Zoreah and Aijalon, and Hebron, which is in Judah and Benjamin, fortified cities.

11 He strengthened the fortified cities and appointed captains in them, and put in them storehouses of food, oil, and wine.

12 In every city and town shields and spears, and he strengthened them exceedingly, and he had Judah and Benjamin.

13 The priests and the Levites who were in all Israel stood before him from every one of their borders.

14 Because the Levites abandoned their suburbs and their possessions, and went to Judah and to Jerusalem, because Jeroboam and his sons had rejected them from serving the LORD.

15 And the priests stood for the high places and for the satyrs and for the calves which he made.

16 And after them, of all the tribes of Israel, those who set their heart to seek the LORD, the God of Israel, came to Jerusalem to sacrifice to the LORD, the God of their fathers.

17 They strengthened the kingdom of Judah and made Rehoboam son of Solomon strong for three years, for they walked in the way of David and Solomon for three years.

18 Rehoboam took for himself as wife Mahalath, the daughter of David's son Jermoth and Abihail the daughter of Eliab son of Jesse.

19 She bore him sons: Jehush and Shemariah and Zaham.

20 Afterward he took Maachah daughter of Absalom, and she bore to him Abijah, Attai, Ziza, and Shelomith.

21 Rehoboam loved Maachah daughter of Abishalom more than all his wives and concubines, for he took eighteen wives and sixty concubines, and he fathered twenty-eight sons and sixty daughters.

22 Rehoboam made Abijah the son of Maacah chief over his brothers to establish him as ruler.

23 And he built and broke out with all his sons into all the lands of Judah and Benjamin, to all the fortified cities, and he gave them abundant food, and sought many wives.


## Chapter 12

1 When Rehoboam’s reign was established and strengthened, he abandoned the law of the LORD, and all Israel with him.

2 In the fifth year of King Rehoboam, Shishak king of Egypt went up against Jerusalem because they had rebelled against the LORD.

3 With a thousand and two hundred chariots and with sixty thousand horsemen, and no number for the people who came with him from Egypt: Libyans, Sukkiims, and Ethiopians.

4 He captured the fortified cities of Judah and advanced as far as Jerusalem.

5 Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam and the chiefs of Judah who had gathered in Jerusalem because of Shishak, and he said to them, Thus says the LORD, You have forsaken me, and so I have forsaken you into the hand of Shishak.

6 The captains of Israel and the king were humbled and said, "Righteous is the LORD."

7 When Jehovah saw that they had humbled themselves, the word of Jehovah came to Shemaiah, saying, "They have humbled themselves; I will not destroy them. I will grant them a small deliverance, and my wrath will not be poured out against Jerusalem by the hand of Shishak."

8 For they shall be servants to him, and they shall know my service, and the service of the kingdoms of the lands.

9 Shishak king of Egypt went up against Jerusalem and took the treasures of the house of the LORD and the treasures of the house of the king; he took everything. He also took the golden shields that Solomon had made.

10 King Rehoboam made bronze shields in place of them and placed them in the hands of the captains of the runners who guarded the entrance of the royal house.

11 Whenever the king came to the house of the LORD, the runners came and took them and returned them to the little chamber.

12 When he humbled himself, the anger of the LORD turned from him so as not to consume him altogether, and also in Judah there were good things.

13 King Rehoboam strengthened himself in Jerusalem and reigned; Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city that the LORD chose to place his name there, out of all the tribes of Israel; and the name of his mother was Naamah the Ammonitess.

14 He did evil, for he did not set his mind to seek the LORD.

15 And the acts of Rehoboam, the first and the last, are they not written in the chronicles of the prophet Shemaiah and Iddo the seer, and the wars of Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days?

16 Rehoboam slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of David and Abijah his son reigned after him.


## Chapter 13

1 In the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam, Abijah reigned over Judah.

2 Abijah reigned three years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Michaiah daughter of Uriel of Gibeah, and there was war between Abijah and Jeroboam.

3 Abijah joined battle with mighty warriors, four hundred thousand chosen men, and Jeroboam drew up against him for battle with eight hundred thousand chosen valiant men.

4 Then Abijah rose up from upon the hill of Zemaraim which is in the hill of Ephraim and said, Hear me, Jeroboam and all Israel.

5 Is it not known to you that the LORD, the God of Israel, gave the kingdom to David over Israel forever, to him and to his sons, a covenant of salt?

6 Jeroboam the son of Nebat, a servant of Solomon the son of David, arose and rebelled against his lord.

7 And there gathered against him worthless men, sons of Belial, and they grew strong against Rehoboam son of Solomon; and Rehoboam was a youth and tender of heart, and he did not assert himself before them.

8 And now you say to strengthen yourselves before the kingdom of the LORD by the hand of the sons of David, yet you are a great multitude and with you are golden calves which Jeroboam made for you for gods.

9 Have you not rejected the priests of the LORD, the sons of Aaron and the Levites, and made for yourselves priests like the peoples of the lands, anyone who comes to fill his hand with a young bull and seven rams, and becomes a priest to gods that are no gods?

10 We are the LORD our God, and we have not forsaken him; and the priests, the sons of Aaron, serve the LORD, and the Levites are in their work.

11 And they offer up to the LORD burnt offerings every morning and every evening, and sweet incense of spices, and the row of bread on the table of pure gold, and the lampstand of gold with its lamps to be kindled every evening; for we keep the charge of the LORD our God, but you have forsaken him.

12 And behold, with us is the chief of the gods and his priests and the trumpets for the alarm to shout against you, O children of Israel: do not fight with the LORD, the God of your fathers, for you shall not succeed.

13 Jeroboam took the ambush and went behind them, and they were before Judah, and the ambush behind them.

14 Then Judah turned, and behold, they had the battle before them and behind them, so they cried out to the LORD, and the priests blew the trumpets.

15 And the man of Judah shouted, and when the man of Judah shouted, God struck Jeroboam and all Israel before Abijah and Judah.

16 The sons of Israel fled from the presence of Judah, and God gave them into their hand.

17 And Abijah and his people smote them with a great slaughter, so that there fell slain of Israel five hundred thousand chosen men.

18 The people of Israel were subdued at that time, but the people of Judah grew strong because they relied on the LORD their God.

19 Abiah pursued Jeroboam and captured towns from him: Beth-el and its towns, Jeshanah and its towns, and Ephron and its towns.

20 And Jeroboam did not retain his strength any longer in the days of Abijah; the LORD struck him and he died.

21 Abijah grew strong and took fourteen wives, and he fathered twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.

22 And the rest of the words of Abijah, and his ways and his deeds, are written in the story of the prophet Iddo.


## Chapter 14

1 Abijah slept with his fathers and they buried him in the city of David and Asa his son reigned in his place in his days the land was at rest ten years

2 Asa did what was good and right in the sight of the LORD his God.

3 He removed the altars of the alien and the high places, and broke the standing images and cut down the groves.

4 And he said to Judah, to seek Jehovah, the Lord, God of their fathers, and to do the law and the commandment.

5 He removed from all the cities of Judah the high places and the idols, and the kingdom was at peace before him.

6 He built fortified cities in Judah, for the land was quiet and there was no war with him in those years because the LORD gave him rest.

7 And he said to Judah, Let us build these cities and surround them with a wall and towers, with gates and bars; still we have the land before us, because we have sought the LORD our God; we have sought him, and he has given us rest on every side. So they built and prospered.

8 Asa had an army of three hundred thousand men from Judah bearing shields and spears, and two hundred and eighty thousand men from Benjamin bearing bucklers and drawing bows; all these were mighty warriors.

9 Zerah the Cushite came out against them with an army of a thousand thousand and three hundred chariots, and he came to Mareshah.

10 Asa went out before him and deployed for battle in the valley of Zephathah toward Mareshah.

11 Asa called to the LORD his God and said, O LORD, there is no one with You to help between the mighty and the one who has no power; help us, O LORD our God, for we rely on You, and in Your name we have come against this multitude; O LORD our God, do not let mere mortals hinder You.

12 The LORD struck the Ethiopians before Asa and before Judah, and the Ethiopians fled.

13 Asa and the folk with him pursued them to Gerar, and many of the Cushites fell without recovering themselves, for they were broken before the LORD and before his army, and they took very much spoil.

14 They struck all the cities around Gerar, for the fear of the LORD was upon them, and they plundered all the cities, because there was great spoil in them.

15 They also struck the tents of the herd, and carried off very many sheep and camels, and returned to Jerusalem.


## Chapter 15

1 And the spirit of God came upon Azariah the son of Oded.

2 He went out before Asa and said to him, Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: the LORD is with you when you are with him, and if you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.

3 And many days for Israel without a true God and without a teaching priest and without law.

4 Then he returned in his distress to the LORD, the God of Israel, and sought him and was found by them.

5 And in those times there was no peace for the one going out or the one coming in, for many troubles were upon all the inhabitants of the lands.

6 Nations will be shattered by nations and city by city, for God has confounded them in every trouble.

7 But you, be strong and do not let your hands hang down, for there is reward for your work.

8 When Asa heard these words and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he was strengthened and removed the detestable things from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities which he had captured from the hill country of Ephraim, and he renewed the altar of the LORD that was before the porch of the LORD.

9 He gathered Judah and Benjamin and the resident aliens with them from Ephraim and Manasseh and Simeon, for many from Israel had joined him when they saw that the LORD his God was with him.

10 They gathered in Jerusalem in the third month, in the fifteenth year of Asa's reign.

11 And they sacrificed to the LORD on that day from the spoil they brought: seven hundred beeves and seven thousand sheep.

12 And they entered into a covenant to seek the LORD, the God of their fathers, with all their heart and with all their soul.

13 And all who do not seek the LORD, the God of Israel, shall be put to death, from the least to the greatest, from man to woman.

14 And they swore to the LORD with a loud voice and with shouting and with trumpets and with cornets.

15 All Judah rejoiced over the oath, because they had sworn with all their heart and sought him with all their will, and he was found by them, and the LORD gave them rest on every side.

16 And also Maachah the mother of King Asa he removed from being queen because she had made an idol for the grove, and Asa cut down her idol, crushed it to powder, and burned it at the brook Kidron.

17 And the high places were not removed from Israel, but Asa’s heart was whole all his days.

18 He brought in the consecrated things of his father and his own dedicated items into the house of God—silver, gold, and vessels.

19 And there was no battle or fighting until the year thirty-five of Asa's reign.


## Chapter 16

1 In the thirty-sixth year of Asa’s reign, Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and built Ramah, so as not to let anyone go out or come in to Asa king of Judah.

2 Asa brought out silver and gold from the storehouses of the temple of the LORD and from the storehouses of the royal palace, and sent to Benhadad king of Aram who lived in Damascus, saying:

3 My confederacy and my father’s confederacy with your father: behold, I have sent to you silver and gold; go, break your confederacy with Baasha king of Israel, that he may withdraw from me.

4 Benhadad listened to King Asa and sent the captains of his forces against the cities of Israel, and they struck Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, and all the store-cities of Naphtali.

5 When Baasha heard, he stopped building Ramah and abandoned his work.

6 And King Asa took all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Ramah and its timber that Baasha had used to build with, and he built with them Gaba and Mizpah.

7 At that time Hanani the seer came to Asa king of Judah and said to him, "Because you relied on the king of Aram and did not rely on the LORD your God, therefore the army of the king of Aram has escaped from your hand."

8 Were not the Cushites and the Libyans a great army with many chariots and horsemen, exceedingly numerous? Yet when you relied on the LORD, he delivered them into your hand.

9 For the LORD's eyes range over all the earth to strengthen those whose heart is wholly toward him; you have acted foolishly in this, for from now on there will be wars with you.

10 Asa became angry with the one who had advised him and put him in prison because of his rage over this matter, and Asa oppressed some of the people at that time.

11 And behold, the acts of Asa, the former and the latter, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

12 In the thirty-ninth year of his reign Asa became diseased in his feet, and his disease was severe; yet in his disease he did not seek the LORD, but only the physicians.

13 Asa lay down with his fathers and died in the forty-first year of his reign.

14 And they buried him in his own sepulchres, which he had hewn for himself in the city of David; and they laid him in the bed which he had filled with spices and divers kinds of ointments prepared by the apothecaries' art; and they made for him a very great burning.


## Chapter 17

1 Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his place and strengthened himself against Israel.

2 He gave able men in all the fortified cities of Judah and appointed officers in the land of Judah and in the cities of Ephraim that Asa his father had captured.

3 And the LORD was with Jehoshaphat because he walked in the ways of David his father and did not seek the Baals.

4 For he sought the LORD, the God of his father, and walked in His commandments, not according to the deeds of Israel.

5 The LORD established the kingdom in his hand, and all Judah brought gifts to Jehoshaphat, and he had great wealth and honor.

6 and his heart was proud in the ways of the LORD and still he removed the high places and the groves from Judah

7 In the third year of his reign he sent his officials, Ben-hail and Obadiah and Zachariah and Nethaneel and Michaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah.

8 And with them the Leviites: Shemaiah and Nethaniah and Zebadiah and Asahel and Shemiramoth and Jonathan and Adonijah and Tobiah and Tob-adonijah the Leviites, and with them Elishama and Jehoram the priests.

9 They taught in Judah, and with them a scroll of the law of the LORD, and they went around in all the cities of Judah and taught among the people.

10 There was dread of the LORD over all the kingdoms of the lands surrounding Judah, and they did not make war with Jehoshaphat.

11 And from the Philistines they brought to Jehoshaphat a gift and silver as tribute, also the Arabians brought to him sheep: seven thousand rams and seven hundred he goats.

12 And Jehoshaphat went on and increased in power, and he built fortresses in Judah and store cities.

13 And great business was his in the cities of Judah, and men of war, mighty men of valor, in Jerusalem.

14 And these are their accounts according to the houses of their fathers: for Judah, captains of thousands, Adnah the ruler, and with him mighty men of valor, three hundred thousand.

15 And Jehohanan the captain was with him, and two hundred and eighty thousand.

16 And over him was Amasiah son of Zichri, who volunteered to the LORD, and with him two hundred thousand mighty men of valour.

17 From Benjamin, a man of might, Eliada, and with him two hundred thousand archers and shield bearers.

18 and Jehozabad by his side, and with him one hundred and eighty thousand men of the army.

19 These were the ones who served the king besides what the king gave in the fortified cities throughout all Judah.


## Chapter 18

1 Jehoshaphat had riches and glory in abundance, and he made a marriage alliance with Ahab.

2 After some years he went down to Ahab at Samaria, and Ahab sacrificed sheep and cattle in abundance for him and for the people who were with him, and he persuaded him to go up to Ramoth-gilead.

3 Ahab king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat king of Judah, "Will you go with me to Ramoth-gilead?" And he said to him, "I am as you are, and my people as your people; we will be with you in the battle."

4 Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, "Please consult today the word of the LORD."

5 The king of Israel gathered the prophets, four hundred men, and said to them, Shall I go to Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I refrain? And they said, Go up, and may God give into the hand of the king.

6 Jehoshaphat said, Is there not yet a prophet of the LORD that we may inquire of him?

7 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, There is yet one man by whom we may inquire of the LORD, but I hate him, because he does not prophesy good concerning me, but all his days evil: he is Micaiah son of Imla. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.

8 The king of Israel called to a certain chamberlain and said, "Hurry, bring me Ben-Imla."

9 The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah sat each on his throne, dressed in their garments, at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the prophets were prophesying before them.

10 Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made for him horns of iron and said, Thus says the LORD, With these you shall gore Aram until they are consumed.

11 And all the prophets prophesied thus, saying: Go up to Ramoth-gilead, and prosper; and the LORD will give it into the hand of the king.

12 And the messenger who had gone to summon Micah said to him, Behold, the words of the prophets are with one mouth good to the king; let now your word be like one of theirs and speak good.

13 Micah said, "As the LORD lives, what my God says to me, that I will speak."

14 Then he came to the king, and the king said to him, “Micah, shall we go to Ramoth in Gilead to battle, or shall I refrain?” He answered, “Go up, and may you succeed; they will be delivered into your hand.”

15 Then the king said to him, "How many times must I adjure you to speak nothing but the truth in the name of the LORD?"

16 He said, I have seen all Israel scattered on the hills like sheep that have no shepherd; and the LORD said, These have no masters: they shall return each man to his house in peace.

17 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy good concerning me, but only evil?"

18 Therefore hear the word of the LORD: I saw the LORD sitting on his throne, and all the host of heaven standing beside him, on his right hand and on his left.

19 And Jehovah said, Who shall entice Ahab king of Israel, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth in Gilead? And one said after this manner, and another said after that manner.

20 The air went out and stood before the presence of the LORD, and said, I will entice him. And the LORD said to him, In what?

21 And he said, I will go forth and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, You shall entice and also prevail; go forth and do so.

22 Now therefore, behold, the LORD has put a lying spirit in the mouth of your prophets, and the LORD has spoken disaster concerning you.

23 Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah drew near and struck Micah on the cheek and said, "Which way did the spirit of the LORD pass from me to speak to you?"

24 Micah said, Behold, you will see on that day, when you go from chamber to chamber to hide.

25 The king of Israel said, Take Micah and bring him back to Amon the city captain and to Joash son of the king.

26 And you shall say, “Thus says the king, ‘Place this man in the prison, and feed him bread of affliction and water of affliction until I return in peace.’”

27 Micah said, "If you return in peace, the LORD has not spoken by me." And he said, "Hear, all you peoples."

28 The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth in Gilead.

29 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Disguise yourself and go into battle, but you wear your garments." So the king of Israel disguised himself, and they went into battle.

30 And the king of Aram commanded the captains of his chariots, saying, Do not fight against the small or against the great, but only against the king of Israel alone.

31 When the captains of the chariot saw Jehoshaphat, they said, He is the king of Israel, and they turned aside toward him to fight, and Jehoshaphat cried out, and the LORD helped him, and God turned them away from him.

32 When the captains of the chariot saw that there was no king of Israel, they turned back from following him.

33 And another drew his bow at random and struck the king of Israel between the joints and the breastplate, so he said to the chariot driver, "Turn around and take me out of the camp, for I am wounded."

34 The battle raged that day, and the king of Israel propped himself up in the chariot facing Aram until evening; and he died at the time of the going down of the sun.


## Chapter 19

1 Jehoshaphat king of Judah returned to his home in peace to Jerusalem.

2 Then Jehu son of Hanani the seer went out to meet King Jehoshaphat and said to him, Is it to help the wicked that you have labored, and to love those who hate the LORD? Because of this, wrath is upon you from the presence of the LORD.

3 But good things are found with you, in that you have removed the groves from the land and have set your heart to seek God.

4 Jehoshaphat lived in Jerusalem and turned and went out among the people from Beer-shebah to the hill country of Ephraim and brought them back to the LORD the God of their ancestors.

5 Judges were appointed in the land, in every fortified city of Judah, city by city.

6 See what you are doing, for you do not judge on behalf of another but on behalf of the LORD, and with you is the matter of judgment.

7 Now let the dread of the LORD be upon you; keep and do, for with the LORD our God there is no iniquity, respect of persons, or taking of bribes.

8 And also in Jerusalem Jehoshaphat appointed from among the Levites and the priests and the heads of the fathers of Israel for the judgment of the LORD and for the controversy, and they returned to Jerusalem.

9 And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall you do in the fear of the LORD, in faithfulness and with a perfect heart.

10 And every controversy that comes upon you from your brothers who dwell in their cities, between blood and blood, between law and commandment, statutes and judgments, you shall warn them, so that they may not be guilty toward the LORD, lest wrath arise upon you and upon your brothers; thus you shall do, and you shall not be guilty.

11 And behold, Amariah the chief ruler is over you in all the word of the LORD, and Zebadiah son of Ishmael is leader of the house of Judah in all the word of the king, and the Levites are before you; strengthen yourselves and do it, and may the LORD be with the good.


## Chapter 20

1 Afterward the Moabites and the Ammonites came, and with them some of the Meunites, against Jehoshaphat to battle.

2 They came and reported to Jehoshaphat, saying, "A great multitude is coming against you from beyond the sea, from Aram, and now they are in Hazezon-tamar, which is En-gedi."

3 Jehoshaphat was afraid and turned his face to seek Jehovah, and he proclaimed a fast over all Judah.

4 Then Judah gathered to seek the Lord; even from all the cities of Judah they came to seek the Lord.

5 Jehoshaphat stood in the assembly of Judah and Jerusalem in the house of the LORD before the new court.

6 And Jehovah, God of our fathers, said, Is not He the God in the heavens? And You rule over all the kingdoms of the nations, and in Your hand is power and might, and there is none with You to withstand.

7 Are not you our God who dispossessed the inhabitants of this land before your people Israel, and gave it to the seed of Abraham your lover forever?

8 And they settled in it and built for you there a sanctuary to your name, saying:

9 If adversity, sword, judgment, pestilence, or famine comes upon us, we will stand before this temple and before you, for your name is in this temple, and we will cry to you in our distress, and you will hear and save.

10 Now behold, the Ammonites and Moab and the hill country of Seir, whom you did not allow Israel to enter when they came from the land of Egypt, for they turned aside from them and did not destroy them.

11 And behold, they are repaying us by coming to drive us out of your inheritance which you caused us to inherit.

12 Our God, will You not judge them? For we have no power before this great multitude that is coming against us, and we do not know what to do, for our eyes are on You.

13 And all Judah stood before the LORD, even their little children, their wives, and their sons.

14 Jahaziel son of Zechariah son of Benaiah son of Jeiel son of Mattaniah the Leviite from the sons of Asaph, upon him was the spirit of the LORD in the midst of the assembly.

15 Hear, all Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, and King Jehoshaphat: thus says the LORD to you, Do not fear and do not be dismayed because of this great multitude, for the battle is not yours but God's.

16 Tomorrow go down against them behold they are coming up by the ascent of Ziz and you will find them at the end of the brook before the wilderness of Jeruel.

17 Not for you to fight in this; take your stand, hold firm, and see the deliverance of the LORD with you, Judah and Jerusalem; do not fear, do not be dismayed; tomorrow go out against them, and the LORD will be with you.

18 Jehoshaphat bowed his face to the ground and all Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem fell down before the LORD to worship the LORD.

19 The Leviites, from the sons of the Kohathites and from the sons of the Korahites, arose to praise the LORD, God of Israel, with a loud voice on high.

20 They rose early in the morning and went out to the wilderness of Tekoa, and as they went out, Jehoshaphat stood and said, Hear me, Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem: believe in the LORD your God and you shall be established; believe in his prophets and you shall succeed.

21 And he took counsel with the folk and appointed singers to the LORD, praising in the beauty of holiness, as they went out before the vanguard, saying, "Give thanks to the LORD, for his kindness is everlasting."

22 And when they began to sing and praise, the LORD set ambushments against the sons of Ammon, Moab, and Mount Seir, who had come against Judah, and they were defeated.

23 The Ammonites and Moab stood against the inhabitants of Mount Seir to destroy and annihilate, and when they had finished with the inhabitants of Seir, each man helped his fellow to corrupt.

24 Judah went up to the watch tower in the wilderness and turned toward the abundance, and behold, carcases falling to the ground and no deliverance.

25 Jehoshaphat and his folk came to strip the spoil and found among them abundance and riches and dead bodies and desirable vessels, and they stripped for themselves to the point of no burden, and they were three days taking the spoil because it was so great.

26 And on the fourth day they assembled in the Valley of Berachah, for there they blessed the LORD; therefore they called the name of that place Berachah Valley until the day.

27 And all the men of Judah and Jerusalem returned with Jehoshaphat at their head to Jerusalem with joy, for the LORD had made them joyful over their enemies.

28 They came to Jerusalem with psalteries, with harps, and with trumpets, to the temple of the LORD.

29 There was a dread of God upon all the kingdoms of the lands when they heard that the LORD had fought against the enemies of Israel.

30 And the realm of Jehoshaphat was quiet, and his God gave him rest on every side.

31 Jehoshaphat reigned over Judah; he was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. The name of his mother was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.

32 He walked along the way of his father Asa and did not turn from it, to do what was right in the eyes of the LORD.

33 Yet the high places were not removed, and the people still had not bethought themselves unto the God of their fathers.

34 And the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, the former and the latter, behold they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel by Jehu son of Hanani.

35 And after this Jehoshaphat king of Judah joined himself with Ahaziah king of Israel, who acted wickedly.

36 And he joined with him to make ships to go to Tarshish, and they made ships at Ezion-geber.

37 Eliezer the son of Dodavah from Mareshah prophesied against Jehoshaphat, saying, "Because you have allied yourself with Ahaziah, the LORD has destroyed your works; and the ships have been broken and have not reached Tarshish."


## Chapter 21

1 Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of David, and Jehoram his son reigned in his place.

2 And to him were brothers, sons of Jehoshaphat: Azariah, Jehiel, Zechariah, Azariah, Michael, and Shephatiah; all these were sons of Jehoshaphat king of Israel.

3 Their chief gave them many gifts of silver and gold and precious things, along with fortified cities in Judah, but the kingdom he gave to Jehoram, for he was the eldest.

4 Jehoram rose up over the kingdom of his father and strengthened himself and struck all his brothers with the sword and also some of the rulers of Israel.

5 Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

6 He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as the house of Ahab had done, for Ahab’s daughter was his wife, and he did evil in the eyes of the LORD.

7 But Jehovah was not willing to destroy the house of David, because of the covenant that he had made with David, and as he had promised to give to him a light and to his sons forever.

8 In his days Edom revolted from under Judah's hand and they set a king over themselves.

9 Jehoram passed with his captains and all the chariotry with him, and he arose at night and struck the Edomites surrounding him and the captains of the chariotry.

10 Edom rebelled against the hand of Judah to this day; then Libnah rebelled in that time against his hand, because he abandoned the LORD, the God of his fathers.

11 He also made high places in the hills of Judah and caused the inhabitants of Jerusalem to commit acts of prostitution, and he led Judah astray.

12 A writing came to him from Elijah the prophet, saying: Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: Because you have not walked in the ways of Jehoshaphat your father, nor in the ways of Asa king of Judah,

13 She walked in the way of the kings of Israel and prostituted Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, like the prostitution of the house of Ahab, and also the brothers of your father’s house, who were better than you, she murdered.

14 Behold, the LORD will strike a great plague upon your people, and upon your children, and upon your wives, and upon all your substance.

15 And you in many diseases, in sickness of your bowels, until your bowels come out from the disease, day upon day.

16 The LORD stirred up against Jehoram the anger of the Philistines and the Arabians who were by the side of the Ethiopians.

17 They went up against Judah and plundered it, and they took all the riches found in the king's court and also his sons and his wives, and there was no son left to him except Jehoahaz, the youngest of his sons.

18 And after all this the LORD struck him in his bowels with a sickness that had no remedy.

19 And it came to pass, as time passed, that at the end of two years his bowels came out because of his sickness, and he died in grievous diseases, and his people did not make for him a burning as the burning of his fathers.

20 He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem, and he walked without desire, and they buried him in the city of David, but not in the burying places of the kings.


## Chapter 22

1 The inhabitants of Jerusalem made Ahaziah his son, the younger, king in his place, for all the older ones were killed by the raiding band that came with the Arabians to the camp, and Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah reigned.

2 Ahaziah was forty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Athaliah daughter of Omri.

3 He also walked along the way of the house of Ahab, for his mother was urging him to do evil.

4 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like the house of Ahab, for they were his advisers after the death of his father to destroy him.

5 He also followed their counsel and went with Jehoram son of Ahab king of Israel to battle against Hazael king of Aram at Ramoth-gilead, and the Syrians struck Joram.

6 He returned to recover at Jezreel because of the wounds he had received at Ramah when he fought against Hazael king of Aram, and Azariah son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to visit Jehoram son of Ahab at Jezreel, since he was ill.

7 And from God came the destruction of Ahaziah to come to Joram; and when he came, he went out with Jehoram to Jehu son of Nimshi, whom the LORD had anointed to cut off the house of Ahab.

8 And it came to pass, when Jehu was judging the court of Ahab, that he found the captains of Judah and the sons of the brothers of Ahaziah who were serving Ahaziah, and he killed them.

9 They sought Ahaziah and captured him while he was hiding in Samaria; they brought him to Jehu and put him to death. They buried him, for they said, "He is the son of Jehoshaphat, who sought Jehovah with all his heart." But there was no strength in the house of Ahaziah to retain the kingdom.

10 And Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah saw that her son was dead, and she arose and destroyed all the royal offspring of the house of Judah.

11 Jehoshabeath daughter of the king took Joash son of Ahaziah and stole him from among the children of the king who were being murdered, and she put him and his nurse in the chamber of the beds, and Jehoshabeath daughter of the king Jehoram wife of Jehoiada the priest hid him, for she was sister of Ahaziah, because of Athaliah, and she did not let him be killed.

12 He was with them in the house of God, hidden six years, while Athaliah reigned as queen over the land.


## Chapter 23

1 In the seventh year Jehoiada strengthened himself and took the captains of hundreds, Azariah son of Jeroham, Ishmael son of Jehohanan, Azariah son of Obed, Maaseiah son of Adaiah, and Elishaphat son of Zichri, into the covenant with him.

2 They turned about in Judah and gathered the Leviites from all the cities of Judah and the chief fathers of Israel, and they came to Jerusalem.

3 All the assembly made a covenant with the king in the house of God, and he said to them, "Behold, the son of the king shall reign, as the LORD has spoken concerning the sons of David."

4 This is the act that you shall do: one-third of you coming on the sabbath shall be for the priests and for the Levites, and for the doorkeepers of the gates.

5 and the third in the king's palace and the third at the foundation gate and all the people in the courts of the temple of the LORD

6 Let no one enter the temple of the LORD except the priests and the Levite ministers; they shall enter, for they are holy; but all the people shall keep the charge of the LORD.

7 Then the Leviites surrounded the king, each with his weapons in hand; and whoever approached the temple was to be put to death, and they were to attend the king when he came and when he went.

8 The Leviites and all Judah did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded, and each man took his men, those coming in on the sabbath with those going out on the sabbath, for Jehoiada the priest did not dismiss the divisions.

9 Then Jehoiada the priest gave to the captains the spears and the shields and the shields that had been King David’s, which were in the house of God.

10 And he stationed all the people, and each man his weapon in his hand, from the right side of the house to the left side of the house, by the altar and by the temple, around the king.

11 They brought out the king's son and set upon him the crown and the testimony, and they made him king; Jehoiada and his sons anointed him and said, "Long live the king!"

12 And Athaliah heard the sound of the people running and praising the king, and she came to the people into the house of the LORD.

13 And she looked, and behold, the king stood by his pillar at the entrance, and the captains and the trumpeters were by the king, and all the people of the land rejoiced and blew trumpets, and the singers with musical instruments were making proclamation to praise. Then Athaliah tore her garments and cried, "Conspiracy, conspiracy!"

14 Then Jehoiada the priest brought out the captains of the hundreds, the officers of the army, and said to them, "Bring her out from between the ranges, and let him who follows her be put to the sword," for the priest had said, "Do not put her to death in the house of the LORD."

15 They laid hands on her, and she entered the entrance of the horse gate of the king's house, and they killed her there.

16 And Jehoiada made a confederacy between himself and all the people and the king, to be a people to the LORD.

17 Then all the people came to the temple of Baal and tore it down; they broke in pieces his altars and his images, and they killed Mattan, the priest of Baal, in front of the altars.

18 Jehoiada placed the oversight of the house of the LORD in the hands of the priests, the Levites, whom David had appointed over the house of the LORD, to offer burnt offerings to the LORD as written in the law of Moses, with joy and with singing, by the hands of David.

19 The doorkeepers stood at the gates of the house of the LORD, and no one unclean was allowed to enter for any matter.

20 He took the captains, the nobles, the rulers of the people, and all the people of the land, and brought down the king from the house of the LORD; they came into the inner gate of the king's house, and they set the king upon the throne of the kingdom.

21 And all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet, and Athaliah they put to death with the sword.


## Chapter 24

1 Joash was seven years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Zibiah of Beer-shebah.

2 Joash did what was right in the sight of the LORD all the days of Jehoiada the priest.

3 Jehoiada took for himself two wives and he fathered sons and daughters.

4 Later on, Joash was with full consent to renovate the temple of the LORD.

5 He gathered the priests and the Levites and said to them, Go out to the cities of Judah and gather from all Israel silver to strengthen the house of your God every year, and you must hasten the matter, but the Levites did not hasten.

6 The king called Jehoiada the chief and said to him, Why have you not required the Levites to bring from Judah and from Jerusalem the gift of Moses the servant of the LORD and the assembly of Israel for the tent of the testimony?

7 For Athaliah the wicked woman, her sons broke into the house of God, and they also used all the holy things of the house of the LORD for the Baalim.

8 The king said, and they made one ark, and they placed it at the gate of the house of the LORD outside.

9 They proclaimed aloud in Judah and Jerusalem to bring to the LORD the tribute of Moses, the servant of God, from Israel in the wilderness.

10 And all the captains and all the people rejoiced, and they brought and cast into the ark until it was finished.

11 And it came to pass at the time when the chest was brought to the king's account by the hand of the Levites, and when they saw that there was much silver, that the scribe of the king came, and the overseer of the priest who was chief, and they emptied the chest and took it up and returned it to its place; so they did day by day, and they gathered much silver.

12 The king and Jehoiada gave him to the one doing the work of the service of the house of the LORD, and there were hired hewers and craftsmen to renew the house of the LORD, and also smiths of iron and copper to strengthen the house of the LORD.

13 The workers carried out the work, and the work was perfected in their hands, and they set up the house of God on its foundation and strengthened it.

14 And when they had finished, they brought the rest of the silver before the king and Jehoiada, and he made it into vessels for the house of the LORD, vessels for ministering and for burnt offerings, and spoons, and vessels of gold and silver; and they continued to offer burnt offerings in the house of the LORD continually all the days of Jehoiada.

15 Jehoiada grew old and full of years and died, being a hundred and thirty years old at his death.

16 And they buried him in the city of David with the kings because he had done good in Israel with God and his house.

17 After the death of Jehoiada, the captains of Judah came and bowed to the king; then the king listened to them.

18 They abandoned the temple of the LORD, the God of their ancestors, and served the groves and the idols, and wrath came upon Judah and Jerusalem because of their sin.

19 And he sent among them prophets to turn them back to the LORD, and testified to them, but they did not listen.

20 And the spirit of God clothed Zachariah son of Jehoiada the priest, and he stood above the people and said to them, Thus says God, Why do you transgress the commandments of the LORD so that you do not prosper? Because you have forsaken the LORD, he has forsaken you.

21 They conspired against him and stoned him with a stone by the king's commandment in the court of the house of the LORD.

22 Joash the king did not remember the favour that Jehoiada his father had done with him, and he slew his son; and as he died, he said, "May the LORD see and require."

23 At the circuit of the year the army of Aram came up against him and came to Judah and Jerusalem and destroyed all the princes of the people from among them and all their spoil they sent to the king of Damascus.

24 Although few men came from Syria, the LORD delivered a great army into their hand because they had forsaken the LORD, the God of their fathers; and they executed judgments against Joash.

25 And when they departed from him, for many had forsaken him, his servants conspired against him because of the blood of the sons of Jehoiada the priest, and they murdered him on his bed, and he died; and they buried him in the city of David, but they did not bury him in the tombs of the kings.

26 And these are the ones who rebelled against him: Zabad son of Shimath the Ammonitess, and Jehozabad son of Shimrith the Moabitess.

27 And his sons bore the great burden upon him, and they established the house of God; behold, they are written in the story of the book of the kings; and Amaziah his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 25

1 Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.

2 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, but not with a whole heart.

3 And it came to pass, when the kingdom became strong upon him, that he killed his servants who had struck the king, his father.

4 Yet their children he did not put to death, according to what is written in the law, in the book of Moses, which the LORD commanded, saying, Fathers shall not die for their children, nor children be put to death for their fathers; each man shall die for his own sin.

5 Amaziah gathered Judah and organized them by their ancestral families, appointing over them commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds throughout all Judah and Benjamin; he registered them from the age of twenty years and upward, and found them to be three hundred thousand choice young men fit for military service, bearing spear and shield.

6 And he hired from Israel a hundred thousand mighty warriors for a hundred talent of silver.

7 And the man of God came to him, saying, "The king, do not let the army of Israel go with you, for the LORD is not with Israel, all the sons of Ephraim."

8 But if you come to fight, God will cause you to be defeated before your enemy, for power to help or to cause defeat belongs to God.

9 Amaziah said to the man of God, "But what shall we do about the hundred talents which I have given to the army of Israel?" And the man of God answered, "There is to the LORD to give you much more than this."

10 Amaziah separated the army that had come to him from Ephraim to return to their place, and the anger of those men burned fiercely against Judah, so they returned to their place in fierce anger.

11 And Amaziah strengthened himself and led his people and went to the Valley of Salt and struck the sons of Seir ten thousand.

12 And ten thousand living ones returned, the sons of Judah, and they brought them to the top of the rock, and they cast them down from the top of the rock, and all of them were dashed to pieces.

13 And the men of the troop whom Amaziah had sent back from fighting with him in the war attacked the cities of Judah from Samaria to Beth-horon and struck down from them three thousand, and took great spoil.

14 After Amaziah came from defeating the Edomites, he brought the gods of the people of Seir, set them up as gods for himself, and bowed down before them and burned incense to them.

15 The anger of the LORD burned against Amaziah, and he sent a prophet to him, who said to him, "Why have you sought the gods of the people who did not deliver their folk from your hand?"

16 And it came to pass, when he spoke to him, that the prophet said to him, "O counselor of the king, why should they strike you?" And the prophet ceased. Then he said, "I know that God has purposed to destroy you, because you have done this and have not heeded my counsel."

17 Amaziah king of Judah took counsel and sent to Joash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu king of Israel, saying, Let us meet face to face.

18 Then Joash king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, The bramble that is in Lebanon sent to the cedar that is in Lebanon, saying, Give your daughter to my son for a wife; but the wild beast that is in Lebanon passed by and trampled the bramble.

19 You said, “Behold, I have struck Edom,” and your heart has lifted you up to act proudly; now stay in your house; why should you provoke evil, so that you and Judah fall with you?

20 And Amaziah did not listen, for it was from God, to deliver them into the hand, because they had sought the gods of Edom.

21 Joash king of Israel went up, and he and Amaziah king of Judah faced one another at Beth-shemesh which belongs to Judah.

22 Judah was struck down before Israel, and each man fled to his tent.

23 And Joash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Joash son of Jehoahaz, at Beth-shemesh, and brought him to Jerusalem, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the gate of Ephraim to the gate of the corner, four hundred cubits.

24 All the gold and silver and all the vessels found in the house of God, with Obed-edom and the treasures of the house of the king, and the sons of the hostages, and he returned to Samaria.

25 Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah lived fifteen years after the death of Joash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel.

26 And the rest of the acts of Amaziah, the former and the latter, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah and Israel?

27 After Amaziah turned away from following the LORD, a conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, so he fled to Lachish; but they sent men after him to Lachish and killed him there.

28 They lifted him onto the horses and buried him with his fathers in the city of Judah.


## Chapter 26

1 All the people of Judah took Uzziah, and he was sixteen years old, and they made him king instead of his father Amaziah.

2 He built Elath and restored it to Judah after the king lay with his forefathers.

3 Uzziah was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jecholiah of Jerusalem.

4 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, according to all that Amaziah his father had done.

5 It happened in the days of Zechariah, who had insight in the vision of God; and in the days that he sought the LORD, God prospered him.

6 He went out and fought against the Philistine, and he breached the wall of Gath and the wall of Jabneh and the wall of Ahdod, and he built cities in Ahdod and in the Philistine.

7 And God helped him against the Philistines and against the Arabians that dwelt in Gur-baal and the Mehunim.

8 The Ammonites gave a gift to Uzziah, and his fame went as far as Egypt, for he grew strong unto greatness.

9 Uzziah built towers in Jerusalem at the Gate of the Corner and at the Gate of the Valley and at the Corner, and he strengthened them.

10 He built towers in the wilderness and hewed many cisterns, for he had much cattle, and in the low country and in the plain husbandmen and vine dressers, in the mountains and in Carmel, for he loved the land.

11 Uzziah had an army for warfare, troops going out to battle, counted by number according to their muster, by the hand of Jeiel the scribe and Maaseiah, by the authority of Hananiah, one of the king's captains.

12 All the number of the heads of fathers, mighty men of valor, two thousand six hundred.

13 By their hand was an army of three hundred thousand seven thousand and five hundred warriors, mighty in strength, to assist the king against the enemy.

14 Uzziah prepared for them in all the army shields and spears and helmets and coats of mail and bows and sling stones.

15 He made in Jerusalem engines, a cunning work, to be upon the towers and upon the bulwarks, to shoot arrows and great stones; and his renown went forth to far countries, for he was marvelously strengthened until he became mighty.

16 And as his strength increased, his heart was lifted up to destroy, and he sinned against the LORD his God by entering the temple of the LORD to burn incense on the altar of incense.

17 Then Azariah the priest followed him, and with him eighty priestly warriors of the LORD.

18 So they confronted King Uzziah and said to him, It is not for you, Uzziah, to burn incense to the LORD, but for the consecrated priests, the sons of Aaron, who are appointed to burn incense. Leave the sanctuary, for you have trespassed, and you shall have no honour from the LORD God.

19 Uzziah became enraged, and in his hand was a censer for burning incense; and while he was raging against the priests, leprosy broke out on his forehead before the priests in the house of the LORD, from beside the incense altar.

20 Then Azariah the chief priest and all the priests turned toward him, and behold, he was leprous in his brow, so they hurried him away; indeed, he himself hurried to exit, because the LORD had struck him.

21 Uzziah the king was leprous until the day of his death and he lived in a leper house because he was excluded from the house of the LORD and Jotham his son was over the royal palace judging the people of the land.

22 And the rest of the acts of Uzziah, first and last, were written by Isaiah son of Amoz the prophet.

23 Uzziah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the field of the royal burial, because they said, "He is a leper"; and Jotham his son reigned in his stead.


## Chapter 27

1 Jotham was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem, and the name of his mother was Jerushah daughter of Zadok.

2 He did what was right in the sight of the LORD, according to all that Uzziah his father had done, only he did not go to the temple of the LORD, and moreover the people continued to act corruptly.

3 He built the gate of the house of the LORD, the upper one, and at the wall of Ophel he built much.

4 And he built cities in the hill country of Judah, and in the forests he built castles and towers.

5 And he fought against the king of the Ammonites, and prevailed over them; and the Ammonites gave him that year a hundred talents of silver, and ten thousand cors of wheat, and ten thousand of barley; this the Ammonites gave him; and in the second year and the third year.

6 Jotham grew strong because he directed his ways before the LORD his God.

7 The rest of the acts of Jotham and all his wars and his ways are written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah.

8 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem.

9 Jotham slept with his forefathers and they buried him in the city of David and Ahaz his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 28

1 Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem, and he did not do what was right in the eyes of the LORD like David his father.

2 He walked along the customs of the kings of Israel and also made molten images for the Baalim.

3 And he burned incense in the valley of the son of Hinnom, and burned his children in the fire, according to the abominations of the nations whom the LORD drove out from before the children of Israel.

4 He sacrificed and burned incense on the heights, on the hills, and under every green tree.

5 The LORD his God gave him into the hand of the king of Aram, and he struck him and took from him a great captiveship, and brought it to Damascus; and also he was given into the hand of the king of Israel, and he struck him with a great blow.

6 Pekah son of Remaliah killed one hundred twenty thousand men of Judah in one day, all valiant men of might, because they had forsaken the LORD, the God of their fathers.

7 Then Zichri the Ephraimite killed Maaseiah the son of the king, and Azrikam the captain of the house, and Elkanah the second to the king.

8 The sons of Israel took from their fellow Israelites two hundred thousand women, sons, and daughters as spoil, along with much plunder; and they brought the spoil to Samaria.

9 And there was a prophet of the LORD whose name was Oded, and he went out before the army that came to Samaria and said to them, Behold, in the fury of the LORD the God of your fathers upon Judah he delivered them into your hand, and you have slain them with rage, even to heaven it has ascended.

10 And now, you people of Judah and Jerusalem, you say to enslave as bondmen and bondwomen to you—will you not surely yourselves be guilty of sin before the LORD your God?

11 Now therefore hear me and restore the captives you took from your kindred, for the LORD’s fierce wrath is upon you.

12 And certain men, heads of the Ephraimites, Azariah son of Jehohanan, Berechiah son of Meshillemoth, Hezekiah son of Shallum, and Amasa son of Hadlai, arose over those coming from the army.

13 They said to them, Do not bring the captives here, for you are adding to our sin and to our guilt; indeed, the cause of our trespass is great, and the fierce wrath of the LORD is upon Israel.

14 The armed men abandoned the captives and the spoil before the captains and all the assembly.

15 The men who were named arose and took hold of the captives, and all their naked ones they clothed from the spoil, and they clothed them and shod them and fed them and gave them drink and anointed them and led them on donkeys, every one who stumbled, and brought them to Jericho, the city of palm trees, to their brothers, and they returned to Samaria.

16 At that time King Ahaz sent to the kings of Assyria for help.

17 Edomite people came again and struck Judah and took captive.

18 The Philistines advanced into the cities of the low country and the south of Judah, and captured Beth-shemesh, Aijalon, Gederoth, Shocho and its villages, Timnah and its villages, and Gimzo and its villages, and they settled there.

19 Because the LORD humbled Judah for Ahaz king of Israel's sake, for he had acted perversely in Judah and trespassed grievously against the LORD.

20 Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria came against him and oppressed him, and did not strengthen him.

21 For Ahaz took the temple of the LORD and the palace of the king and the princes, and gave it to the king of Assyria, but they did not help him.

22 And in the time of his distress he added to rebel against the LORD, he the king Ahaz.

23 He sacrificed to the gods of Damascus who defeated him, and said, "Because the gods of the kings of Aram are helping them, I will sacrifice to them that they may help me." But they became a snare to him and to all Israel.

24 Ahaz gathered the vessels of the house of God and cut up the vessels of the house of God and shut the doors of the house of the LORD and made altars for himself in every corner in Jerusalem.

25 And in every city of Judah he made high places to offer sacrifice to other gods, and he angered the LORD, the God of his ancestors.

26 And the rest of his acts and all his ways, the former and the latter, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

27 Ahaz was laid to rest with his fathers, and they buried him in the city, in Jerusalem, because they had not brought him to the burial places of the kings of Israel, and Hezekiah his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 29

1 Hezekiah was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem, and his mother's name was Abijah the daughter of Zechariah.

2 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, according to all that David his father had done.

3 In the first year of his reign, in the first month, he opened the doors of the house of the LORD and strengthened them.

4 And he brought the priests and the Levites, and gathered them to the broad place of the east.

5 He said to them: "Listen to me, you Leviites: now consecrate yourselves and consecrate the temple of the LORD, the God of your ancestors, and remove the unclean from the sanctuary."

6 For our fathers have sinned and done evil in the sight of the LORD our God, and they abandoned him, and turned their faces from the dwelling of the LORD, and turned their backs.

7 They also shut the doors of the porch and extinguished the lamps and did not burn incense and did not offer a burnt offering in the sanctuary to the God of Israel.

8 The wrath of the LORD was kindled against Judah and Jerusalem, and He gave them to desolation and hissing, as you see with your own eyes.

9 And behold, our forefathers have fallen by the sword, and our sons and our daughters and our wives are in captivity because of this.

10 Now with heart committed, to make a covenant with the LORD, the God of Israel, that the fierce wrath of his anger may turn away from him.

11 My sons, do not now become discouraged, for the LORD has chosen you to stand before him to serve and to be his ministers and offerers of incense.

12 The Levites rose up—Mahath son of Amasai and Joel son of Azariah from the sons of the Kohathites; and from the sons of Merari: Kish son of Abdi and Azariah son of Jehalelel; and from the Gershonites: Joah son of Zimmah and Eden son of Joah.

13 and from the sons of Elizaphan, Shimri, and from the sons of Asaph, Zachariah and Mattaniah.

14 From the sons of Heman, Jehuel and Shimei, and from the sons of Jeduthun, Shemaiah and Uzziel.

15 They gathered their brothers and consecrated themselves, and came according to the king's commandment by the word of the LORD to purify the house of the LORD.

16 The priests entered the inner part of the temple of the LORD to purify it, and they brought out all the uncleanness that they found in the temple of the LORD to the court of the house of the LORD, and the Levites received it to carry outside to the Kidron valley.

17 They began on the first day of the first month to sanctify, and on the eighth day of the month they came to the porch of the LORD, and they sanctified the temple of the LORD for eight days, and on the sixteenth day of the first month they finished.

18 They came inside to King Hezekiah and said, We have purified all the house of the LORD, the altar of the burnt offering and all its vessels, and the table of the shewbread and all its vessels.

19 And all the vessels that King Ahaz had cast aside during his reign because of his transgression they set in order and consecrated, and behold, they are before the altar of the LORD.

20 And Hezekiah the king arose early and gathered the captains of the city and went up to the house of the LORD.

21 They brought seven young bulls, seven rams, seven sheep, and seven male goats for purification for sin, for the kingdom, for the sanctuary, and for Judah; and he said to the sons of Aaron the priests to offer them up on the altar of the LORD.

22 They slaughtered the bulls, and the priests received the blood and dashed it against the altar; and they slaughtered the rams, and they dashed the blood against the altar; and they slaughtered the sheep, and they dashed the blood against the altar.

23 They brought the goats of the sin offering before the king and the assembly, and they laid their hands on them.

24 The priests slaughtered them and sprinkled their blood on the altar to make atonement for all Israel, for the king had said that the burnt offering and the sin offering were for all Israel.

25 He stationed the Leviite in the temple of the Lord with cymbals, with psalteries, and with harps, according to the ordinance of David and Gad the seer of the king and Nathan the prophet, for the ordinance was by the hand of the Lord through the hand of his prophets.

26 The Leviites stood with the instruments of David and the priests with the trumpets.

27 Hezekiah commanded the burnt offering to be brought up to the altar, and when the burnt offering began, the singing to the LORD and the trumpets began, accompanied by the instruments of David king of Israel.

28 And all the assembly prostrating, and the singers singing, and the trumpets sounding, all until the completion of the burnt offering.

29 When he finished offering, the king and all who were with him bowed down.

30 Hezekiah the king and the chiefs said to the Leviites to praise the LORD with the words of David and Asaph the seer, and they praised until there was joy, and they bowed and prostrated themselves.

31 Then Hezekiah answered and said, Now you are able for the LORD; come and bring sacrifices and thanksgiving offerings to the house of the LORD. And the assembly brought sacrifices and thanksgiving offerings, and every willing heart brought burnt offerings.

32 And the number of the burnt offering that the assembly brought was seventy beeves, one hundred rams, two hundred lambs, all as a burnt offering to the LORD.

33 and the consecrated things: beeve six hundred and sheep three thousand.

34 Only the priests were few and could not handle all the burnt offerings, so their brothers the Levites helped them until the work was finished and until the priests were consecrated, for the Levites were sincere of heart in consecrating themselves apart from the priests.

35 And also the burnt offering in abundance, with the fat of the peace offerings and with the drink offerings for the burnt offering, and the service of the house of the LORD was established.

36 Hezekiah and all the people rejoiced over what God had prepared for the people, for the matter had come about suddenly.


## Chapter 30

1 Hezekiah sent to all Israel and Judah, and he also wrote letters to Ephraim and Manasseh, urging them to come to the house of the LORD in Jerusalem to celebrate the passover offering to the LORD, the God of Israel.

2 The king and his captains and all the assembly in Jerusalem took counsel to keep the passover in the second month.

3 For they could not carry it out at that time, because the priests had not consecrated themselves according to sufficiency, and the people had not been gathered to Jerusalem.

4 The act was pleasing in the eyes of the king and in the eyes of all the assembly.

5 They established an act to send a proclamation throughout all Israel, from Beer-shebah to Dan, summoning them to come and observe the passover offering to the LORD, the God of Israel, in Jerusalem; for not in great number had they performed it as prescribed.

6 The runners went with the letters from the hand of the king and his captains throughout all Israel and Judah, according to the commandment of the king, saying, "O children of Israel, return to the LORD, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, that the LORD may return to the remnant that remains to you from the hand of the kings of Assyria."

7 Do not be like your forefathers and your brothers who trespassed against the LORD, the God of their forefathers, and He delivered them to desolation, as you see.

8 Now do not stiffen your necks as your fathers did; give your hand to the LORD and come to his sanctuary which he has sanctified forever, and serve the LORD your God, that his fierce wrath may turn from you.

9 When you return to the LORD, your brothers and your children will find compassion before those who carried them away, and they will return to this land, for gracious and merciful is the LORD your God, and he will not turn his face from you if you return to him.

10 The runners passed from city to city in the land of Ephraim and Manasseh and as far as Zebulun, and they became a laughingstock to them and were mocked.

11 Only men from Asher, Manasseh, and Zebulun came to Jerusalem.

12 Also in Judah the hand of God was upon them to give them one heart to do the commandment of the king and the officials by the word of the LORD.

13 And the folk gathered in Jerusalem to observe the solemn feast of unleavened bread in the second month, a multitude exceedingly great.

14 They rose up and removed the altars that were in Jerusalem, and they removed all the places of burning incense, and they cast them into the brook Kidron.

15 They slaughtered the passover offering on the fourteenth day of the second month, and the priests and the Levites were ashamed, so they consecrated themselves and brought burnt offerings to the temple of the LORD.

16 They stood in their places according to their custom, by the law of Moses the man of God, the priests receiving the blood from the hand of the Levites.

17 For the assembly was large, those who had not sanctified themselves, and the Leviites were in charge of the killing of the passover offerings for everyone who was not clean, to sanctify to the LORD.

18 For the greatest part of the people, Ephraimites and Manasseh, Issachar and Zebulun, did not purify themselves, for they ate the passover not according to the writing; therefore Hezekiah prayed for them, saying, The LORD, who is good, pardon every one.

19 With all his heart he set himself to seek God, the LORD, the God of his fathers, but not according to the cleanness of the holy thing.

20 And the LORD listened to Hezekiah and healed the people.

21 The sons of Israel who were found in Jerusalem kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with great joy, and the Levites and the priests praised the LORD day by day with instruments of strength to the LORD.

22 Hezekiah spoke to the heart of all the Levites who had wisdom and good understanding for the Lord, and they ate the feast for seven days, sacrificing peace offerings and giving thanks to the Lord, the God of their ancestors.
