23 And all the assembly consulted to observe another seven days, and they observed seven days of joy.

24 For Hezekiah king of Judah provided for the assembly one thousand bulls and seven thousand sheep, and the princes provided for the assembly one thousand bulls and ten thousand sheep, and the priests sanctified themselves in great number.

25 And all the congregation of Judah rejoiced, and the priests and the Levites, and all the assembly who came from Israel, and the strangers who came from the land of Israel, and those who dwelt in Judah.

26 There was exceedingly great joy in Jerusalem, for since the days of Solomon son of David king of Israel, there had been nothing like it in Jerusalem.

27 The priests and the Levites rose up and blessed the people, and their voice was heard, and their prayer came into his holy dwelling place to the heavens.


## Chapter 31

1 When all these things were finished, all Israel who were found went out from the cities of Judah and broke down the pillars, cut down the groves, and destroyed the high places and the altars throughout all Judah and Benjamin, and in Ephraim and Manasseh, until they had finished; then all the people of Israel returned, each man to his possession, to their cities.

2 Hezekiah appointed the divisions of the priests and the Leviites according to their divisions, each according to his service, for the priests and for the Leviites, for burnt offering and for peace offering, to serve and to give thanks and to praise at the gates of the camps of the LORD.

3 And the portion of the king from his substance for the burnt offerings, for the burnt offerings of the morning and the evening, and for the burnt offerings on the sabbaths, and on the new moons, and on the appointed feasts, as written in the law of the LORD.

4 And he said to the folk, to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, to give the portion of the priests and the Leviites, so that they might strengthen themselves in the law of the LORD.

5 And as for the act, the sons of Israel greatly increased the firstfruits of wheat, sweet wine, anointed oil, and honey, and all the produce of the field, and brought in abundance the tithe of all.

6 And the sons of Israel and Judah living in the cities of Judah also brought the tithe of beeves and sheep, and the tithe of holy things consecrated to the LORD their God, and they brought them and laid them in heaps, heaps.

7 In the third month they began the heaps for the foundation, and in the seventh month they finished.

8 Hezekiah and the rulers came and saw the heaps, and they blessed the LORD and his people Israel.

9 Hezekiah inquired of the priests and the Levites concerning the heaps.

10 Then Azariah the chief priest, head of the house of Zadok, said to him, "Since the offering was brought to the house of the LORD, the people have eaten and been satisfied, and there has remained an abundance, because the LORD has blessed his people; and what remains is this great multitude."

11 Hezekiah said to prepare a chamber in the temple of the LORD and they prepared it.

12 They brought the gift, the tithing, and the consecrated things in faithfulness, and over them was a chief Levite, Conaniah, and his brother Shimei as second.

13 Jehiel, Azaziah, Nahath, Asahel, Jerimoth, Josabad, Eliel, Ismachiah, Mahath, and Benaiah were overseers under the hand of Conaniah and Shimei his brother, by the appointment of King Hezekiah and Azariah, ruler of the house of God.

14 And Kore the son of Imna the Levite, the doorkeeper for the east, was in charge of the free will offerings of God, to give the heave offering of the LORD and the most holy things.

15 And over them, Eden, Miniamin, Jeshua, Shemaiah, Amariah, and Shecaniah, in the cities of the priests, faithfully to distribute to their brothers by divisions, according to the great and the small.

16 apart from being enrolled among males from the age of three years and upward, for all who come to the house of the LORD, to perform duties day by day, according to their divisions in their courses.

17 and the priests according to their ancestral houses and the Levites from twenty years of age and upward, by their divisions in their courses.

18 and to be joined with all their little children, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, to all the congregation; for by their faithfulness they shall be consecrated as holy.

19 To the sons of Aaron the priests in the fields of the suburbs of their cities, in every city and city, men who are named by name, to give portions to every male among the priests and to every Levite who is enrolled.

20 Hezekiah did this in all Judah, and he did the good and the right and the true before the LORD his God.

21 And in every work that he began for the service of the house of God and in the law and in the commandment, to seek his God, with all his heart he did and prospered.


## Chapter 32

1 After these acts and the truth, Sennacherib king of Assyria came and entered into Judah and encamped against the fortified cities and spoke to take them for himself.

2 Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come, and his face was toward the battle against Jerusalem.

3 And he took counsel with his captains and his mighty men to stop the waters of the fountains which were outside the city, and they helped him.

4 A great folk gathered and stopped up all the springs and the river flowing in the midst of the land, saying, Why should the kings of Assyria come and find abundant waters?

5 He strengthened himself and built up all the broken wall and raised it up to the towers, and another wall outside, and strengthened the Millo, the city of David, and made darts in abundance and shields.

6 And he appointed captains of warfare over the people, and he gathered them to himself at the broad place of the city gate, and he spoke to their hearts, saying:

7 Be strong and courageous, do not fear and do not be dismayed because of the king of Assyria and because of all the multitude that is with him, for the one with us is greater than the one with him.

8 With him is an arm of flesh, but with us is the LORD our God to help us and to fight our battles; and the people relied on the words of Hezekiah king of Judah.

9 After this, Sennacherib king of Assyria sent his servants to Jerusalem, while he himself was at Lachish, with all his power with him, against Hezekiah king of Judah and against all Judah who were in Jerusalem, saying:

10 Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria: On what do you rely, that you sit besieged in Jerusalem?

11 Is not Hezekiah misleading you, to deliver you up to die by famine and by thirst, saying: "The LORD our God will deliver us from the hand of the king of Assyria"?

12 Is it not Hezekiah who removed his high places and his altars and said to Judah and to Jerusalem to worship before one altar and to burn incense upon it?

13 Do you not know what I and my forefathers have done to all the peoples of the lands? Could the gods of the nations of the lands deliver their land from my hand?

14 Who among all the gods of the nations whom my fathers utterly destroyed was able to deliver his people from my hand? How much less can your gods deliver you from my hand?

15 Now therefore do not let Hezekiah deceive you or mislead you thus, and do not believe him, for no god of any nation or kingdom has been able to deliver his people from my hand or from the hand of my ancestors; how much less will your gods deliver you from my hand?

16 And again his servants spoke against the LORD God and against Hezekiah his servant.

17 And he wrote letters to taunt the LORD God of Israel, and to speak against him, saying, "Just as the gods of the nations of the lands have not delivered their people from my hand, so too the God of Hezekiah will not deliver his people from my hand."

18 And the Jews cried aloud in the Jews' language to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall, to frighten them and to terrify them, in order to capture the city.

19 They spoke against the God of Jerusalem as against the gods of the peoples of the land, the work of the hands of man.

20 King Hezekiah and Isaiah the prophet, son of Amoz, prayed about this matter and cried out to heaven.

21 And Jehovah sent a messenger and cut off every mighty warrior, commander, and leader in the camp of the king of Assyria, so he returned in shame of face to his land; and when he came into the house of his gods, from his own body they struck him down with the sword.

22 And the LORD saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib king of Assyria and from the hand of all, and He dealt with them on every side.

23 And many brought a gift to the LORD to Jerusalem and precious things to Hezekiah king of Judah, and he was exalted in the eyes of all the nations thereafter.

24 In those days Hezekiah became ill to the point of death, and he prayed to the LORD, and he spoke to him, and a sign he gave to him.

25 But Hezekiah did not render according to the benefit done to him, for his heart was lifted up; therefore wrath was upon him and upon Judah and Jerusalem.

26 And Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart, he and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so that the wrath of the LORD did not come upon them in the days of Hezekiah.

27 And Hezekiah had exceedingly great riches and glory, and he made himself storehouses for silver and for gold and for precious stones and for spices and for shields and for all manner of desirable vessels.

28 and storehouses for the fruit of grain and wine and oil, and stalls for every beast and cattle, and folds for the flocks.

29 And he made towns for himself and livestock of sheep and cattle in great abundance, for God had given him very great substance.

30 Hezekiah blocked the upper outlet of the waters of Gihon and directed them to the west side of the city of David, and Hezekiah prospered in all his works.

31 And so the captains of Babylon who sent for him to inquire about the miracle that had occurred in the land said, "His God has forsaken him, to test him, that all his heart may be known."

32 And the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and his mercies, behold, they are written in the vision of Isaiah son of Amoz the prophet, in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

33 Hezekiah slept with his fathers and they buried him in the ascent of the graves of the sons of David, and all Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem made honour for him at his death, and Manasseh his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 33

1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like the abominations of the nations whom the LORD dispossessed from before the children of Israel.

3 He returned and rebuilt the high places that Hezekiah his father had torn down, and he set up altars for the Baalim and made groves, and he bowed down to all the host of heaven and served them.

4 And he built altars in the temple of the LORD, of which the LORD had said, In Jerusalem shall my name be forever.

5 He built altars to all the host of the heavens in the two courts of the house of the LORD.

6 And he caused his sons to pass through the fire in the Valley of Hinnom, and practiced divination, and used enchantments, and practiced sorcery, and consulted a familiar spirit and a wizard; he greatly increased to do evil in the sight of the LORD to provoke him.

7 And he set the carved image, the idol which he had made, in the house of God, of which God said to David and to Solomon his son, In this temple and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will set my name forever.

8 I will no longer remove Israel’s foot from the land which I established for your fathers, only if they keep to do all that I commanded them, according to all the law, the statutes, and the judgments by the hand of Moses.

9 Manasseh caused Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem to stray, so that they did evil more than the nations whom the LORD destroyed from before the face of Israel.

10 The LORD spoke to Manasseh and to his people, but they did not listen.

11 Then the LORD brought against them the captains of the army of the king of Assyria, and they captured Manasseh with thorns and bound him with fetters of brass and took him to Babylon.

12 And when he was in distress, he entreated the LORD his God and humbled himself greatly just as his ancestors had done.

13 And he prayed to him, and he was granted favour, and he heard his supplication, and he returned him to Jerusalem to his kingdom, and Manasseh knew that the LORD is God.

14 Afterward he built an outer wall for David's city on the west, extending from the Gihon spring along the valley to the Fish Gate, then around the Ophel, and he raised it very high; and he stationed military commanders in all the fortified cities of Judah.

15 He removed the foreign gods and the idol from the temple of the LORD, and all the altars that he had built on the mountain of the house of the LORD and in Jerusalem, and he cast them outside the city.

16 And he built up the altar of the LORD and sacrificed on it peace offerings and offerings of thanksgiving and said to Judah to serve the LORD God of Israel.

17 But still the folk were sacrificing at the high places, only to the LORD their God.

18 And the rest of the acts of Manasseh, and his prayer to his God, and the words of the prophets who spoke to him in the name of the LORD, the God of Israel, behold, they are recorded in the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

19 And his prayer and how it was answered, and all his sin and transgression, and the places where he built high places and set up the groves and the carved images before he humbled himself—behold, they are written in the chronicles of the seers.

20 Manasseh slept with his forefathers and they buried him in his home, and Amon his son reigned in his place.

21 Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem.

22 Amon did evil in the sight of the LORD, as Manasseh his father had done; to all the carved images that Manasseh his father had made, Amon sacrificed and served them.

23 And he did not humble himself before the LORD as Manasseh his father had humbled himself, but Amon greatly increased guilt.

24 And his servants conspired against him and killed him in his house.

25 The folk of the land struck all the conspirators against King Amon, and the folk of the land made Josiah, his son, king in his place.


## Chapter 34

1 Josiah was eight years of age when he began to reign, and he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem.

2 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD and walked in the ways of David his father and did not turn aside to the right or to the left.

3 In the eighth year of his reign, while he was still a boy, he began to seek the God of David his father, and in the twelfth year he began to purge Judah and Jerusalem of the high places, the groves, the carved images, and the molten images.

4 They tore down the altars of the Baalim before him, and cut down the idol images that were above them; and he broke in pieces the groves, the carved images, the molten images, and the vails, and scattered them upon the faces of the sepulchres of those who had sacrificed to them.

5 And the bones of the priests he burned upon the altars; thus he purified Judah and Jerusalem.

6 and in the cities of Manasseh and Ephraim and Simeon as far as Naphtali, all around.

7 He tore down the altars, the groves, and the carved images, broke them in pieces, and cut down every idol throughout the land of Israel, then returned to Jerusalem.

8 In the eighteenth year of his reign, to cleanse the land and the temple, he sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah, and Maaseiah son of the city's governor, and Joah son of Joahaz the recorder, to strengthen the house of the LORD his God.

9 They came to Hilkiah the high priest and gave him the silver brought to the temple of God, which the Leviites who were keepers of the threshold had collected from the hand of Manasseh and Ephraim and from all the remnant of Israel and from every one of Judah and Benjamin, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

10 They gave it by the hand of the one doing the work, appointed in the house of the LORD, and they gave it to the workers doing the work in the house of the LORD, to repair and to strengthen the temple.

11 They gave to the artisans and the builders hewed stones and timber for joining, and to repair the houses which the kings of Judah had destroyed.

12 Also, the men were working faithfully in the work, and over them were appointed Jahath and Obadiah the Levites from the sons of Merari, and Zechariah and Meshullam from the sons of the Kohathites for the oversight; and all the Levites who had understanding in the instruments of song.

13 And over the burden-bearers and overseers of all workers in labor and service, and from the Levites, scribes, officers, and doorkeepers.

14 And when they were bringing out the silver that was brought into the temple of the LORD, Hilkiah the priest found the book of the law of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

15 Hillkiah answered and said to Shaphan the scribe, "I have found the book of the law in the house of the LORD," and Hillkiah gave the book to Shaphan.

16 Shaphan brought the scroll to the king and again returned word to the king, saying, All that was given into the hand of your servants they are doing.

17 They brought up the silver that was found in the house of the LORD and gave it into the charge of the overseers and into the hands of the workers of the work.

18 Shaphan the scribe declared to the king, saying, A book has been given to me by Hillkiah the priest, and Shaphan read it before the king.

19 When the king heard the words of the law, he tore his garments.

20 The king commanded Hilkiah, and Ahikam son of Shaphan, and Abdon son of Micah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the servant of the king, saying:

21 Go, inquire of the LORD for me and for those who are left in Israel and Judah concerning the words of the book that was found, for great is the wrath of the LORD which has been poured out on us because our fathers did not keep the word of the LORD to do according to all that is written in this book.

22 Hillkiah and the royal party went to Huldah the prophetess, wife of Shallum son of Hasrah, keeper of the wardrobe, who resided in Jerusalem in the college, and they spoke to her accordingly.

23 And she said to them, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, Say to the man who sent you to me:

24 Thus says the LORD: Behold, I am bringing adversity upon this place and upon its inhabitants, even all the curses written in the book which they read before the king of Judah.

25 Because they have forsaken me to serve other gods, provoking my anger with all the works of their hands, my wrath will burn in this place and not be quenched.

26 To the king of Judah who sent you to inquire of the LORD, thus shall you say to him: Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: As for the words that you have heard,

27 Because your heart is tender and you humbled yourself before God when you heard his words concerning this place and its inhabitants, and you humbled yourself before me and tore your garments and wept before me, I also have heard, declares the LORD.

28 I am about to gather you to your forefathers, and you shall be gathered to your burying places in peace, and your eyes shall not see all the calamity that I am bringing upon this place and upon its inhabitants; and they brought back the answer to the king.

29 The king sent and gathered all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

30 The king went up to the temple of the LORD, and all the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and the priests and the Levites, and all the people, from the greatest to the least; and he read in their hearing all the words of the book of the covenant that was found in the temple of the LORD.

31 The king stood in his place and made the covenant before the LORD to follow the LORD and to keep his commandments, testimonies, and statutes with all his heart and with all his soul, to carry out the words of the covenant written in this book.

32 And he set up all that were found in Jerusalem and in Benjamin, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem acted according to the covenant of the God of their fathers.

33 Josiah removed all the abominable things from all the lands that belonged to the children of Israel, and he made all who were found in Israel serve the LORD their God; all his days they did not turn away from following the LORD, the God of their ancestors.


## Chapter 35

1 Josiah made a passover (offering) to the Lord in Jerusalem, and they slaughtered the passover (offering) on the fourteenth of the first month.

2 He stationed the priests in their charges and encouraged them for the service of the house of the LORD.

3 He said to the Levites, the builders for all Israel, the holy ones of the Lord: give the ark of the holy into the house which Solomon the son of David king of Israel built; you have no burden upon the shoulder; now serve the Lord your God and his people Israel.

4 Prepare your families by your divisions, according to the writing of David king of Israel and according to the writing of Solomon his son.

5 And stand in the sanctuary for the division of the households of the fathers for your brothers, the sons of the people, and the division of the household of the father for the Levites.

6 And they shall slaughter the passover offering, and sanctify themselves, and prepare for their kindred, to do according to the word of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

7 Josiah raised for the people sheep and goats, all for the passover offerings, for everyone present, thirty thousand in number, and beeves three thousand, these from the substance of the king.

8 And their captains gave a freewill offering to the people and to the priests; the leaders of the house of God, Hilkiah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, gave to the priests for the passover offerings two thousand six hundred and for cattle three hundred.

9 Conaniah, and Shemaiah and Nethaneel his brothers, and Hashabiah, Jeiel, and Josabad, leaders of the Levites, provided for the Levites five thousand passover offerings and five hundred beeves.

10 The service was established, and the priests stood in their places and the Levites in their divisions according to the commandment of the king.

11 They slaughtered the passover offering, and the priests dashed the blood from their hand, while the Leviites flayed it.

12 They removed the burnt offering to give it for divisions to the families among the people, to offer to the LORD, as written in the book of Moses, and so also for the cattle.

13 They boiled the passover offering in fire according to the ceremony, and the consecrated things they boiled in caldrons, in kettles, and in pans, and they carried them to all the people.

14 And afterward they prepared for the priests and for the Levites, for the priests, the sons of Aaron, were busy with ascending the burnt offering and the fat until night, and the Levites prepared for them and for the priests, the sons of Aaron.

15 The singers, the sons of Asaph, were at their posts according to the commandment of David, Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun the king's seer, and the gatekeepers were at each gate; they could not depart from their service, for their brothers the Levites prepared for them.

16 All the service of the LORD was carried out on that day to make the passover offering and to offer burnt offerings on the altar of the LORD, according to the commandment of King Josiah.

17 The Israelites who were found kept the passover offering at that time and the feast of unleavened bread for seven days.

18 And no passover offering like it had been made in Israel since the days of the prophet Samuel, and all the kings of Israel did not make a passover offering such as Josiah made, along with the priests, the Levites, all Judah, Israel that was found, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

19 In the eighteenth year of Josiah’s reign, this passover offering was made.

20 After all this, when Josiah had prepared the temple, Necho king of Egypt went up to fight at Carchemish on the Euphrates, and Josiah went out to meet him.

21 He sent ambassadors to him, saying, What have I to do with you, king of Judah? I am not against you today, but against the house of my war; and God has commanded me to hurry. Cease from opposing God, who is with me, so that he does not destroy you.

22 And Josiah did not turn his face from him, for he had disguised himself to fight against him, and he did not listen to the words of Necho from the mouth of God, and he came to fight in the valley of Megiddo.

23 The archers shot at King Josiah, and the king said to his servants, “Take me away, for I am severely wounded.”

24 His servants removed him from the chariot and placed him on the second chariet that belonged to him, and they brought him to Jerusalem; and he died, and was buried in the sepulchres of his fathers; and all Judah and Jerusalem mourned for Josiah.

25 Jeremiah lamented for Josiah, and all the officials and the women uttered their lamentations for Josiah to this day, and established them as a decree for Israel; indeed, they are written in the lamentations.

26 And the rest of the acts of Josiah and his kindnesses, according to what is written in the law of the LORD,

27 And his earlier and later acts are indeed recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel and Judah.


## Chapter 36

1 The folk of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah and made him king in place of his father at Jerusalem.

2 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem.

3 And the king of Egypt removed him in Jerusalem and laid a fine on the land of one hundred talents of silver and one talent of gold.

4 The king of Egypt made Eliakim, his brother, king over Judah and Jerusalem, and changed his name to Jehoiakim; but Necho took Joahaz, his brother, and brought him to Egypt.

5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem, and he did evil in the sight of the LORD his God.

6 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against him and bound him in fetters of brass to take him to Babylon.

7 And from the vessels of the house of the LORD, Nebuchadnezzar brought to Babylon; and he put them in his temple at Babylon.

8 And the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim and his abominations that he did, which were found against him, indeed they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel and Judah, and Jehoiachin his son reigned in his place.

9 Jehoiachin was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months and ten days in Jerusalem, and he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD.

10 And at the return of the year king Nebuchadnezzar sent and brought him to Babylon, with the vessels of the precious things of the house of the LORD, and he made his brother Zedekiah king over Judah and Jerusalem.

11 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem.

12 He did evil in the sight of the LORD his God and did not humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet, from the mouth of the LORD.

13 He also rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar, who had made him swear by God, and he stiffened his neck and hardened his heart from turning to the LORD, the God of Israel.

14 Also all the leaders of the priests and the people multiplied their trespassing, following all the abominations of the nations, and they defiled the temple that the LORD had sanctified in Jerusalem.

15 The LORD, the God of their fathers, sent against them by the hand of his messengers, sending continually because he had compassion on his people and on his dwelling place.

16 They mocked the messengers of God, despised his word, and scoffed at his prophets, until the wrath of the LORD rose against his people until there was no healing.

17 He brought against them a king and slaughtered their young men with the sword in their temple, and he had no compassion on young man or virgin, old man or aged; all he delivered into his hand.

18 All the vessels of the house of God, both great and small, and the treasures of the house of the LORD, and the treasures of the king, and the treasures of his officers, all these did Babylon bring.

19 They burned the temple of God and broke down the wall of Jerusalem, and all its palaces they burned with fire, and all its lovely vessels they destroyed.

20 And the remnant that had escaped the sword were exiled to Babylon, and they became his and his sons’ servants until the kingdom of Persia reigned.

21 To fulfill the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah, until the land had enjoyed its sabbaths; all the days of the desolation it kept sabbath, to fulfill seventy years.

22 In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, to fulfill the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah, the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, and he issued a proclamation throughout all his kingdom and also by writing, saying:

23 Thus says Cyrus the king of Persia: All the kingdoms of the earth has the LORD, God of heaven, given to me; and He has charged me to build Him a house in Jerusalem, which is in Judah. Who among all His people is there? The LORD his God being with him, let him go up.


# EZRA

## Chapter 1

1 In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, to fulfill the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah, the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, and he issued a proclamation throughout all his kingdom and also in writing, saying:

2 Thus says Cyrus king of Persia: The LORD, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and He has charged me to build Him a house in Jerusalem, which is in Judah.

3 Who among all his people will have his God with him and go up to Jerusalem which is in Judah, and build the house of the LORD, the God of Israel—he is the God who is in Jerusalem?

4 And all who remain from every place where he has dwelt, the men of his place shall carry him with silver and gold and goods and cattle, with the free offering for the house of God which is in Jerusalem.

5 The chiefs of Judah and Benjamin, and the priests and the Levites, and all whose spirit God stirred, arose to go up to build the house of the LORD which is in Jerusalem.

6 And all their surroundings strengthened them with their hands, with silver vessels, with gold, with wealth, and with livestock and with presents, only apart from all freewill offering.

7 And King Cyrus brought out the vessels of the house of the LORD, which Nebuchadnezzar had brought out from Jerusalem, and he gave them to the house of his gods.

8 Cyrus the king of Persia brought them forth by the hand of Mithredath the treasurer, who delivered them to Sheshbazzar the prince of Judah.

9 And these are their number: thirty chargers of gold, one thousand chargers of silver, nine and twenty knives.

10 thirty basons of gold, four hundred and twenty-four basons of silver, a thousand other vessels

11 All vessels of gold and silver, five thousand and four hundred in total, Sheshbazzar brought up with the exiles who went up from Babylon to Jerusalem.


## Chapter 2

1 And these are the people of the province who came up from the captivity, the removal that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had removed to Babylon; and they returned to Jerusalem and Judah, each man to his city.

2 Those who came with Zerubbabel: Jeshua, Nehemiah, Seraiah, Reeliah, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mizpar, Bigvai, Rehum, Baanah, and the men of the people of Israel.

3 The sons of Parosh: two thousand one hundred seventy-two.

4 The sons of Shephatiah: three hundred seventy-two.

5 The sons of Arah: seven hundred five and seventy.

6 The sons of Pahathmoab, according to the sons of Jeshua and Joab, two thousand eight hundred twelve.

7 The sons of Elam: one thousand two hundred fifty-four.

8 The sons of Zattu: nine hundred and forty-five.

9 The sons of Zaccai: seven hundred and sixty.

10 The sons of Bani: six hundred forty-two.

11 The sons of Bebai, six hundred twenty-three.

12 The sons of Azgad: a thousand two hundred and twenty-two.

13 The sons of Adonikam: six hundred sixty-six.

14 The sons of Bigvai: two thousand fifty and six.

15 The sons of Adin: four hundred fifty-four.

16 The sons of Ater, Hezekiah: ninety-eight.

17 The sons of Bezai: three hundred and twenty-three.

18 The sons of Jorah: one hundred and twelve.

19 The sons of Hashum: two hundred twenty-three.

20 Ninety-five sons of Gibbar.

21 The sons of Bethlehem: one hundred twenty and three.

22 The men of Netophah, fifty-six.

23 The men of Anathoth: one hundred twenty-eight.

24 The sons of Azmaveth: forty-two.

25 The children of Kirjath, the cities Chephirah and Beeroth, seven hundred forty and three.

26 The sons of Ramah and Gaba, six hundred twenty-one.

27 The men of Michmas, one hundred twenty-two.

28 The men of Beth-el and Ai, two hundred and twenty-three.

29 The sons of Nebo, fifty-two.

30 The sons of Magbish: one hundred fifty-six.

31 The sons of Elam: one thousand two hundred fifty-four.

32 The sons of Harim: three hundred and thirty.

33 The sons of Lod: Hadid and Ono, seven hundred twenty-five.

34 The sons of Jericho, three hundred forty-five.

35 The children of Senaah: three thousand six hundred and thirty.

36 The priests, the sons of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua, nine hundred seventy-three.

37 The sons of Immer: one thousand and fifty-two.

38 The sons of Pashur: one thousand two hundred forty and seven.

39 The sons of Harim: one thousand and seventeen.

40 The Leviite, sons of Jeshua and Kadmiel, sons of Hodaviah, seventy-four.

41 The singers, the sons of Asaph, one hundred twenty-eight.

42 The sons of the doorkeepers: the sons of Shallum, the sons of Ater, the sons of Talmon, the sons of Akkub, the sons of Hatita, the sons of Shobai—all one hundred thirty-nine.

43 The Nethinims, the sons of Ziha, the sons of Hashupha, the sons of Tabaoth.

44 The sons of Keros, the sons of Siaha, the sons of Padon.

45 The sons of Lebanah, the sons of Hagabah, the sons of Akkub.

46 The sons of Hagab, the sons of Shalmai, the sons of Hanan,

47 The sons of Giddel, the sons of Gahar, the sons of Reaia.

48 The sons of Rezin, the sons of Nekoda, the sons of Gazzam.

49 The sons of Uzza, the sons of Paseah, the sons of Besai.

50 The sons of Asnah, the sons of Meunim, the sons of Nephisim,

51 The sons of Bakbuk, the sons of Hakupha, the sons of Harhur.

52 The sons of Bazluth, the sons of Mehida, the sons of Harsha.

53 The sons of Barkos, the sons of Sisera, the sons of Tamah.

54 The sons of Neziah, the sons of Hatipha.

55 The sons of the servants of Solomon, the sons of Sotai, the sons of Sophereth, the sons of Perida.

56 The sons of Jaala, the sons of Darkon, the sons of Giddel.

57 The sons of Shephatiah, the sons of Hattil, the sons of Pochereth of Zebaim, the sons of Ami.

58 All the Nethinims and the sons of the servants of Solomon, three hundred ninety and two.

59 These are the ones coming up from Tel-melah, Tel-haresha, Cherub, Addan, Immer; but they were unable to declare their family and their offspring, whether they were of Israel.

60 The sons of Dalaiah, the sons of Tobijah, the sons of Nekoda, six hundred fifty-two.

61 And of the sons of the priests, the sons of Habaiah, the sons of Hakkoz, the sons of Barzillai, who took a wife from the daughters of Barzillai the Gileadite, and was called by their name.

62 These sought their register among those who were enrolled, but it was not found, and they were excluded from the priesthood.

63 The Tirshatha said to them that they could not eat from the consecrated things, the holy ones, until a priest stood up for the Urim and Thummim.

64 All the congregation as one, forty-two thousand three hundred and sixty.

65 Besides their male servants and female servants, these were seven thousand three hundred thirty-seven; and they had two hundred male and female singers.

66 Their horses seven hundred thirty-six, their mules two hundred forty-five.

67 Their camels four hundred thirty-five, (he) asses six thousand seven hundred and twenty.

68 And from the chiefs of the fathers, when they came to the house of the LORD which is in Jerusalem, they contributed to the house of God to establish it on its foundation.

69 According to their ability they gave to the treasury of the work: gold, one hundred thousand drams, and silver, five thousand manehs, and priestly garments, one hundred.

70 The priests and the Levites and some of the people and the singers and the gatekeepers and the Nethinims lived in their towns, and all Israel in their towns.


## Chapter 3

1 And the seventh month came, and the children of Israel were in the cities, and the people gathered as one man to Jerusalem.

2 Jeshua son of Jozadak and his brothers the priests, and Zerubbabel son of Shalthiel and his brothers, arose and built the altar of the God of Israel, to offer burnt offerings on it according to the law of Moses the man of God.

3 They set up the altar on its bases, for dread was upon them because of the peoples of the lands, and they offered burnt offerings to the LORD, burnt offerings for the morning and for the evening.

4 They made the (solemn) feast of booths as it was written, and the burnt offering of the day by day according to number, by the judgment of the word of the day in its day.

5 and after the continual ascent offering, and for the monthly offerings, and for all the appointed solemnities of the LORD that are sanctified, and for every free-will offering to the LORD.

6 From one day of the seventh month they began to offer burnt offerings to the LORD, but the temple of the LORD was not founded.

7 They gave silver to the craftsmen and the artificers, and food, drink, and oil to the Sidonians and to the men of Tyre, to bring cedar timber from Lebanon to the sea of Joppa, according to the grant of Cyrus king of Persia to them.

8 In the second year of their coming to the house of God at Jerusalem, in the second month, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jozadak and the rest of their brothers, the priests and the Levites and all who came from the captivity to Jerusalem, began, and they appointed the Levites from twenty years old and upward to oversee the work of the house of the LORD.

9 Jeshua and his sons and his brothers, Kadmiel and his sons, the sons of Judah, united as one to oversee the work at the house of God, and the sons of Henadad, their sons and their brothers, the Levites.

10 And the builders laid the foundation of the temple of the LORD, and the priests stood clothed in their garments with trumpets, and the Levites, the sons of Asaph, with cymbals, to praise the LORD according to the direction of David king of Israel.

11 And they responded with praise and thanksgiving to the LORD, for He is good, for His kindness endures alway toward Israel; and all the people shouted with a great shout of rejoicing to the LORD at the foundation of the house of the LORD.

12 And many of the priests and Levites and chief rulers of the fathers who had seen the first temple when it was founded, this temple in their eyes, wept with loud voice, and many shouted with joy to raise a voice.

13 And the folk did not discern the voice of rejoicing in the voice of the folk's weeping, for the folk shouted a great shout of joy, and the voice was heard afar off.


## Chapter 4

1 The adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the children of the captivity were building the temple to the LORD, the God of Israel.

2 Then they approached Zerubbabel and the chiefs of the fathers and said to them, We will build with you, for we are seeking your God, we ourselves have been sacrificing since the days of Esar-haddon, king of Assyria, who brought us here.

3 Then Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the other chiefs of the fathers of Israel said to them, We will not let you build with us the temple of our God, for we ourselves alone will build to the LORD God of Israel, as King Cyrus king of Persia has commanded us.

4 The folk of the land weakened the hands of the people of Judah and terrified them in building,

5 and they hired counselors against them to frustrate their counsel, all the days of Cyrus king of Persia and until the reign of Darius king of Persia.

6 In the reign of Ahasuerus, at the beginning of his reign, an accusation was written against the inhabitants of Judah and Jerusalem.

7 In the age of Artaxerxes, Bishlam, Mithredath, Tabeel, and the rest wrote to Artaxerxes king of Persia; and the letter was written in the Syrian language and translated in the Syrian language.

8 Rehum the chancellor and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter together concerning Jerusalem to Artaxerxes the king, as follows:

9 Rehum chancellor, Shimshai scribe, and the rest of their companions, Dinaites, Apharsachites, Tarpelites, Apharsites, Babylonians, Susanchites, Elamites,

10 And the rest of the nations whom Asnapper the great and honorable had deported and settled in the city of Samaria, and the rest beyond the river and so forth.

11 This is a copy of the letter that his servants, men beyond the river, sent to him, King Artaxerxes.

12 Be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from you to Jerusalem, a rebellious city, are building and reinforcing wall and foundation.

13 So that it may be known to the king that your city is being rebuilt and its walls are being completed, with toll, tribute, and custom not being given, and by this the kings will suffer loss.

14 Because of the salt of the palace and its maintenance, and because the dishonor of the king cannot be endured, therefore I have sent and informed the king.

15 Search in the book of the records of your fathers and let it be recorded in the book of the records that your city is a rebellious city and causing harm to kings and provinces, and seditions have been done in its midst from ancient times; therefore your city has been destroyed.

16 We inform you, O king, that if your city is built and its walls are completed, then a portion beyond the river will not be yours.

17 The king sent an answer to Rehum the chancellor and Shimshai the scribe and the rest of their companions in Samaria and the rest beyond the river: peace.

18 The letter that you sent to me is declared unclean before me.

19 And a decree was made by me, and search has been made, and it was found that this city has risen against kings from ancient days, and rebellion and sedition have been made in it.

20 And mighty kings ruled over Jerusalem and held authority over all beyond the river, and toll, tribute, and custom were paid to them.

21 Now give a command to make these men cease, and your city shall not be rebuilt until my commandment is established.

22 Be on guard and do not fail, so that no damage may occur to bring harm to the king.

23 Then, as soon as the copy of the letter of King Artaxerxes was read before Rehum and Shimshai the scribe and their companions, they went in haste to Jerusalem to the Jews and stopped them by force and power.

24 Then the service of the house of God in Jerusalem came to a halt, and it remained stopped until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.


## Chapter 5

1 And the prophet Haggai and Zechariah son of Iddo, the prophets, prophesied to the Jews who were in Judea and Jerusalem, in the name of the God of Israel over them.

2 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua son of Jozadak arose, and the leaders with them, to build the house of God in Jerusalem, and the prophets of God were supporting them.

3 At that time there came to them Tatnai governor beyond the River and Shetharboznai and his companions and thus they said to them Who has given you a decree to build this house and to finish this wall

4 Then I said to them, What are the names of the men who are constructing this building?

5 And the eye of their God was upon the elders of the Jews, and they did not stop them until the command of Darius came, and then they returned answer by letter concerning this.

6 Copy of the letter which Tatnai governor beyond the river, Shetharboznai, and his companions the Apharsachites who are beyond the river sent to King Darius.

7 An answer was sent to him, and this was written in it: To Darius the king, peace to all.

8 It is known to the king that we went to the province of Judah, to the house of the great God, whose house is being built with great stones, and timber is set in its walls, and this work is being carried out and prospering in their hands.

9 Then I asked the elder, saying to them, "Who has set for you a decree concerning this house to build it, and this wall to finish it?"

10 And even their name I have asked of them to make you known, which is the name of the certain men who are their chiefs.

11 And according to this matter the answer was returned, saying, We are servants of the God of heaven and earth, and we are building a house which was built aforetime in many years, and a king of Israel built it and established it.

12 For this, from the time our fathers provoked the God of heaven, he gave them into the power of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and this house of his he destroyed, and their people he exiled to Babylon.

13 In the year one of Cyrus the king of Babylon, Cyrus the king issued a decree concerning this house of God to build it.

14 And also the vessels of the house of God, of gold and silver, which Nebuchadnezzar took out from the temple that was in Jerusalem and brought them to the temple in Babylon, Cyrus the king brought them out from the temple in Babylon and they were given to Sheshbazzar, whose name was governor.

15 And I said to him, Take these vessels, carry them to the temple which is in Jerusalem, and let the house of God be built on its place.

16 Then Sheshbazzar came and laid the foundation of the house of God in Jerusalem, and from that time until now it has been under construction and has not been completed.

17 Now therefore, since the king is good, let a search be made in the house of the treasures, which is with the king in Babylon; if it be so that a decree was given from Cyrus the king to build this house of God in Jerusalem, let the will of the king be sent to us.


## Chapter 6

1 Then King Darius issued a decree, and they searched in the house of the scrolls where the treasures were stored in Babylon.

2 And it was forgotten in Achmetha, in the palace which is in the province of the Medes, a single roll; and it was written in its midst a record.

3 In the first year of Cyrus the king, Cyrus the king issued a decree concerning the house of God in Jerusalem: the house is to be rebuilt, the place where sacrifices are offered and its foundation laid; its height sixty cubits and its breadth sixty cubits.

4 Three rows of great stones and one row of timber, and the expense from the house of the king shall be given.

5 And also the vessels of the house of God, which Nebuchadnezzar had taken from the temple in Jerusalem and brought to Babylon, let them be returned to the temple in Jerusalem, to its place, and placed in the house of God.

6 Then Tatnai, governor beyond the river, Shetharboznai, and his companions, the Apharsachites who were beyond the river, came up to them and said to them thus:

7 Leave off from the work of this house of God; let the governor of the Jews and the elders of the Jews build this house of God on its place.

8 And from me a decree is issued as to what you shall do for the elders of the Jews, to build the house of your God; and from the goods of the king beyond the river, from tribute, let expense be given to these men so that they not be hindered.

9 What need have the sons of oxen, rams, and lambs, for a burnt offering to God of heaven, of wheat, salt, wine, and oil, according to the appointment of the priests in Jerusalem, to be given to them day by day, when there is no failure?

10 who are to offer sweet odours to the God of heaven and pray for the life of the king and his children.

11 According to the decree, any man who disregards this word shall be torn down from his house, and a beam shall be pulled from his house, and he shall be impaled upon it, and his house shall be made a dunghill because of this.

12 May the God whose name is called there destroy every king and people who stretches out his hand to change or destroy this house of God in Jerusalem; I, Darius, have made a decree; let it be done.

13 Then Tatnai governor beyond the river, Shetharboznai and his companions, according to the decree that King Darius had sent, they made haste.

14 And the elders of Judah built and prospered by the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah son of Iddo, and they built and completed it according to the commandment of the God of Israel and according to the decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artaxerxes king of Persia.

15 and issue the decree from this house until the third day of the month of Adar, which is the sixth year of King Darius's reign.

16 And the children of Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the rest of the children of the captivity, celebrated the dedication of this house of God with joy.

17 And they offered for the dedication of the house of their God one hundred bullocks, two hundred rams, four hundred lambs, and twelve he-goats for all Israel, according to the number of the tribes of Israel.

18 And they established the priests in their divisions and the Levites in their courses over the service of God in Jerusalem, according to the writing of the book of Moses.

19 The returned exiles kept the passover on the fourteenth day of the first month.

20 For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves, all of them pure, and they slaughtered the passover offering for all the exiles and for their brothers the priests and for themselves.

21 The sons of Israel who returned from the captivity ate, and all who had separated themselves from the filthiness of the peoples of the land to seek the LORD, the God of Israel.

22 They held a solemn feast of unleavened bread for seven days with joy, for the LORD had filled them with joy and turned the heart of the king of Assyria toward them to strengthen their hands in the work of the house of the God of Israel.


## Chapter 7

1 After these acts in the reign of Artaxerxes king of Persia, Ezra son of Seraiah son of Azariah son of Hillkiah.

2 Shallum son of Zadok son of Ahitub.

3 son of Amariah son of Azariah son of Meraioth

4 son of Zerahiah son of Uzzi son of Bukki

5 son of Abishua son of Phinehas son of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest the chief

6 Ezra went up from Babylon, and he was a skilled scribe in the law of Moses which the LORD, the God of Israel, had given; and the king granted him all his request according to the hand of the LORD his God upon him.

7 Some of the children of Israel and some of the priests and the Levites and the singers and the doorkeepers and the Nethinims went up to Jerusalem in the seventh year of King Artaxerxes.

8 He came to Jerusalem in the fifth month, in the seventh year of the king.

9 In the first month the ascent began from Babylon, and on the first day of the fifth month he came to Jerusalem, according to the good hand of his God upon him.

10 For Ezra had prepared his heart to seek the law of the LORD, and to do it, and to teach in Israel statute and judgment.

11 This is the copy of the letter that King Artaxerxes gave to Ezra the priest, the scribe, a scribe of the commandments of the LORD and his laws concerning Israel.

12 Artaxerxes, king of kings, to Ezra the priest, scribe of the law of the God of heaven, perfect and complete.

13 I make a decree, that all of the people of Israel, the priests and the Levites, in my kingdom, who are minded of their own free will to go up to Jerusalem, go with you.

14 Because from before the king and his seven counselors a messenger was sent to investigate Judah and Jerusalem according to the law of your God which is in your hand.

15 And to Hebabel, silver and gold—what the king and his officials freely contributed for the God of Israel, whose habitation is in Jerusalem.

16 All silver and gold that is found in the whole province of Babylon, with the freewill offerings of the people and the priests, willingly given for the house of their God in Jerusalem.

17 Because of this I have appointed that from this money bulls, rams, lambs, their oblations, and their drink offerings be provided and brought near upon the altar of your God's house which is in Jerusalem.

18 And whatever seems good to you and your brothers to do with the rest of the silver and gold, do according to the will of your God.

19 And the vessels that are given to you for the service of your God, deliver in full before the God of Jerusalem.

20 And whatsoever more needful for the house of your God, which they shall have need to give, you shall give from the house of the king’s treasure.

21 And I, Artaxerxes the king, decree that all treasurers beyond the river, whoever they may be, shall diligently carry out any command requested by Ezra, the priestly scribe of the law of the God of heaven.

22 up to a hundred talents of silver and up to a hundred cor measures of wheat and up to a hundred baths of wine and up to a hundred baths of oil and salt without prescribing.

23 All, any matter that is of the God of heaven shall be done to the house of the God of heaven, forasmuch as there be wrath upon the kingdom of the king and his children.

24 And it is known to you that all the priests, the Levites, the singers, the porters, the Nethinims, and the servants of this house of God are exempt from toll, tribute, and custom.

25 But you, Ezra, according to the wisdom of your God which is in your power, appoint judges and magistrates who shall govern all the people who are beyond the river, all who know the laws of your God, and whoever does not know, you shall inform.

26 And every one who will not obey the law of your God and the law of the king will have judgment executed upon him, whether for death, or for banishment, or for confiscation of goods, or for imprisonment.

27 Blessed be the LORD, the God of our fathers, who put such a thing in the heart of the king to honor the house of the LORD which is in Jerusalem.

28 And he turned toward me kindness before the king and his counsellors, and before all the princes of the king, the mighty men; and I strengthened myself according to the hand of the LORD my God upon me, and I gathered from Israel heads to come up with me.


## Chapter 8

1 These are the chiefs of their fathers, who were enrolled with me, who came up with me in the reign of King Artaxerxes from Babylon.

2 From the sons of Phinehas, Gershom; from the sons of Ithamar, Daniel; from the sons of David, Hattush.

3 From the sons of Shecaniah, from the sons of Parosh, Zechariah and with him one hundred fifty men registered by name.

4 From the sons of Pahathmoab: Elihoenai son of Zerahiah, and with him two hundred males.

5 From the sons of Shecaniah son of Jahaziel, and with him three hundred males.

6 From the sons of Adin, Ebed the son of Jonathan, and with him fifty males.

7 And from the sons of Elam, Jesaiah son of Athaliah, and with him seventy males.

8 And of the sons of Shephatiah, Zebadiah son of Michael, and with him eighty males.

9 From the sons of Joab, Obadiah son of Jehiel, and with him two hundred eighteen males.

10 And of the sons of Shelomith, the son of Josiphiah, with him one hundred and sixty males.

11 And of the sons of Bebai, Zachariah the son of Bebai, and with him twenty-eight males.

12 And of the sons of Azgad, Johanan son of Hakkatan, and with him one hundred and ten males.

13 And from the sons of Adonikam, the last ones: these are their names: Eliphalet, Jeiel, and Shemaiah, and with them sixty males.

14 And from the sons of Bigvai, Uthai and his companions, seventy males.

15 I gathered them by the river that comes to Ahava, and we encamped there three days; and I searched among the people and the priests, but of the sons of Levi I found none there.

16 I sent to Eliezer, Ariel, Shemaiah, Elnathan, Jarib, Elnathan, Nathan, Zechariah, Meshullam, captains, and to Joiarib, Elnathan, the discerning.

17 I brought them out to Iddo the chief at the place Casiphia and put words in their mouths to speak to Iddo his brother at the place Casiphia, to bring us ministers for the house of our God.

18 They brought us a man of discretion, of the sons of Mahli, son of Levi, son of Israel, according to the good hand of our God upon us, and Sherebiah and his sons and his brothers, eighteen men.

19 and Hashabiah, and with him Jesaiah from the sons of Merari, their brothers and their sons, twenty.

20 From the Nethinims whom David and the officials had given for service to the Leviites, two hundred and twenty Nethinims, all of them recorded by name.

21 And I proclaimed there a fast by the river Ahava, to humble ourselves before our God, to seek from him a safe journey for us and our children and all our possessions.

22 For I was ashamed to ask the king for an army and horsemen to help us against the enemy on the journey, because we had said to the king, "The hand of our God is for all who seek him for good, and his strength and his anger are against all who forsake him."

23 And we fasted and sought God concerning this, and He was entreated by us.

24 And I separated from the captains of the priests twelve, to Sherebiah, to Hashabiah, and with them from their brothers ten.

25 Their silver and their gold and their vessels—the gift for the house of our God—King and his counselors and his princes and all Israel present there brought up.

26 And I weighed into their hand silver, six hundred and fifty talents, and vessels of silver, a hundred talents, and gold, one hundred talents.

27 Twenty basins of gold, one thousand drams, and two excellent copper vessels overlaid with gold, desirable as gold.

28 And I said to them, You are consecrated to the LORD and the vessels are holy, and the silver and the gold are a free offering to the LORD, the God of your fathers.

29 Be diligent and keep watch until you weigh before the chiefs of the priests and the Levites and the heads of the fathers of Israel in Jerusalem, the chambers of the house of the LORD.

30 And the priests and the Levites shall receive the weight of the silver and the gold and the vessels, to bring to Jerusalem for the house of our God.

31 We set out from the river Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month to go to Jerusalem, and the hand of our God was over us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy and from ambush on the way.

32 And we came to Jerusalem, and we stayed there three days.

33 On the fourth day the silver and the gold and the vessels were weighed in the house of our God by the hand of Meremoth son of Uriah the priest, and with him Eleazar son of Phinehas, and with them Josabad son of Jeshua and Noadiah son of Binnui the Levites.

34 with number, by weight, for everything, and all the weight was recorded at that time.

35 Those coming from the captivity, the children of the exile, offered burnt offerings to the God of Israel: twelve bulls for all Israel, ninety-six rams, seventy-seven sheep, and twelve male goats for sin offerings; all were burnt offerings to the LORD.

36 They gave the king's decrees to the king's lieutenants and governors beyond the river, and they raised up the people and the house of God.


## Chapter 9

1 When these things were finished, the leaders approached me and said, The people Israel and the priests and the Levites have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands according to their abominations, those of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Jebusite, the Ammonite, the Moabite, the Egyptian, and the Amorite.

2 For they had taken for themselves and for their sons from their daughters, and the holy seed had intermingled with the peoples of the lands, and the hand of the officials and the rulers was first in this trespass.

3 When I heard this act, I tore my clothes and my robe, and I plucked hair from my head and my beard, and I sat down appalled.

4 And to me shall gather all who tremble at the words of the God of Israel because of the transgression of the captivity, and I myself sitting in distress until the evening offering.

5 And at the evening oblation I rose from my heaviness, and having torn my garment and robe, I fell on my knees and spread my hands to the LORD my God.

6 And I said, "My God, I am ashamed and embarrassed to lift my face to you, my God, because our iniquities have increased above the head and our trespass has grown great to the heavens."

7 From the days of our fathers we have been in great trespass to this day, and for our iniquities we and our kings and our priests have been delivered into the hand of the kings of the lands by the sword and by captivity and by spoil and by shame of face, as at this day.

8 And now, almost in an instant, we received favour from the LORD our God, to leave us a remnant, to give us a nail in his holy place, to enlighten our eyes, our God, and to grant us a little sustenance in our bondage.

9 For we are servants, and in our bondage our God has not forsaken us, but has extended mercy upon us before the kings of Persia, to give us life, to raise up the house of our God and to restore its ruins, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.

10 And now, what shall we say, our God, after this, seeing we have forsaken your commandments?

11 which you commanded by the hand of your servants the prophets, saying, the land which you are entering to possess is a land of uncleanness, defiled by the uncleanness of the peoples of the lands, by their abominations with which they have filled it from mouth to mouth with their filthiness.

12 Now do not give your daughters to their sons, and do not take their daughters for your sons, and do not seek their peace or welfare forever, so that you may grow strong and eat the good of the land and leave it as an inheritance to your sons forever.

13 And after all the evil that has come upon us because of our wicked deeds and our great sin, for you, our God, have restrained from punishing our iniquities and have granted us a deliverance such as this.

14 Shall we return to break your laws and to intermarry with the peoples of these abominations? Would you not be exceedingly angry with us until there is no remnant and no escaped?

15 Jehovah, God of Israel, you are righteous, for we are left a remnant, as on this day; behold, we are before you in our guilt, for there is no standing before you because of this.


## Chapter 10

1 While Ezra was praying and confessing, weeping and prostrating himself before the temple of God, a large assembly of Israelites, men and women and children, gathered around him, for the people were weeping very bitterly.

2 Shecaniah son of Jehiel from the sons of Elam answered Ezra, "We have acted unfaithfully toward our God by taking foreign wives from the peoples of the land, but now there is hope for Israel concerning this matter."

3 And now let us cut a covenant with our God to send out all the women and the children born from them by the counsel of our Lord and those who tremble at the command of our God, and it shall be done according to the law.

4 Arise, for the matter concerns you, and we are with you; be strong and do.

5 Then Ezra arose and made the chief rulers, the priests, the Levites, and all Israel take oath to act according to this word, and they took oath.

6 Ezra rose up from before the temple of God and went to the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib, and he remained there without eating bread or drinking water, because he was mourning over the transgression of the exiles.

7 They proclaimed aloud in Judah and Jerusalem to all the exiles, to gather in Jerusalem.

8 And all who would not come within three days, according to the counsel of the officials and the elders, all his substance would be forfeited, and he himself would be separated from the assembly of the exiles.

9 All the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered in Jerusalem within three days; it was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month, and all the people sat in the open square before the house of God, trembling because of the matter and because of the rain.

10 Then Ezra the priest arose and said to them, "You have trespassed by taking foreign wives, adding to the sin of Israel."

11 And now give confession to the LORD God of your fathers and do his will and separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign women

12 And all the congregation answered and said with a loud voice, "Thus it is upon us to do."

13 But the folk are many and the season is rainy, and there is no strength to stand outside, and the work is not for one day or for two, because we have greatly transgressed in this matter.

14 Let our captains stand before all the assembly, and everyone among the cities where we dwell who has married foreign women shall come at appointed times, with the elders and judges of each city, until the fierce wrath of our God is turned away from us concerning this matter.

15 But Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahaziah son of Tikvah stood on this side, and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite supported them.

16 And the children of the captivity did so. Ezra the priest selected men, heads of fathers' houses according to their fathers' houses, each of them by name; and they sat down on the first day of the tenth month to examine the matter.

17 And they finished with all the men who had married foreign women, until one day of the first month.

18 And found among the sons of the priests, who had married foreign women, were from the sons of Jeshua son of Jozadak and his brothers: Maaseiah, Eliezer, Jarib, and Gedaliah.

19 They gave their hands to bring out their wives, and offered a ram of the flock for their offense.

20 And from the sons of Immer: Hanani and Zebadiah.

21 And from the sons of Harim: Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah.

22 And from the sons of Pashur: Elihoenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethaneel, Josabad, and Elasah.

23 And of the Leviites: Josabad, and Shimei, and Kelaiah—he is Kelita—Pethakiah, Judah, and Eliezer.

24 And of the singers: Eliashib. And of the gatekeepers: Shallum, Telem, and Uri.

25 And of Israel, of the sons of Parosh: Ramiah, Jeziah, Malchiah, Miamin, Eleazar, Malchiah, and Benaiah.

26 And from the sons of Elam: Mattaniah, Zachariah, Jehiel, Abdi, Jermoth, and Elijah.

27 From the sons of Zattu: Elihoenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, Jermoth, Zabad, and Aziza.

28 And of the sons of Bebai: Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, Athlai.

29 And from the sons of Bani: Meshullam, Malluch, Adaiah, Jashub, Sheal, and Jerimoth.

30 And from the sons of Pahathmoab, Adna and Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezaleel, Binnui, and Manasseh.

31 The sons of Harim: Eliezer, Ishiah, Malchiah, Shemaiah, Simeon.

32 Benjamin, Malluch, Shamariah.

33 From the sons of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattathah, Zabad, Eliphalet, Jeremai, Manasseh, Shimei.

34 From the sons of Bani, Maadai, Amram, and Uel.

35 Benaiah the son of Bedeiah.

36 Vaniah and Meremoth and Eliashib.

37 Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasau.

38 Bani and Binnui and Shimeah.

39 and Shelemiah and Nathan and Adaiah.

40 Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai.

41 Azarael and Shelemiah, Shamariah.

42 Shallum the son of Amariah, son of Joseph.

43 Of the sons of Nebo: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Joel, and Benaiah.

44 All these had taken foreign wives, and some of them had wives by whom they had children.


# NEHEMIAH

## Chapter 1

1 The acts of Nehemiah son of Hachaliah: in the month of Chislev, in the twentieth year, and I was in Shushan the palace.

2 Then Hanani, one of my brothers, came, he and men from Judah; and I asked them about the Jews who had escaped, the remnant who had survived from the captivity, and about Jerusalem.

3 And they said to me, those who remained of the captives who were left in the province there are in great adversity and reproach, and the wall of Jerusalem is broken down and its gates have been burned with fire.

4 When I heard these words, I sat down and wept and mourned for days, and I fasted and prayed before the God of the heavens.

5 And I said, "Please, the LORD God of the heavens, the great and awesome God, keeping the covenant and steadfast love for those who love Him and keep His commandments."

6 Let your ear be attentive and your eyes open to hear the prayer of your servant that I pray before you today, day and night, for the people of Israel, your servants, and to confess the sins of the people of Israel that we have sinned against you, and I and my father’s house have sinned.

7 We have indeed rebelled against You, and we have not kept the commandments, the statutes, and the ordinances which You commanded Moses Your servant.

8 Remember now the word that you commanded your servant Moses, saying, If you act unfaithfully, I will scatter you among the peoples;

9 And you shall return to me and keep the ordinances that were commanded and do them; if your outcasts are at the uttermost parts of the heavens, from there I will gather them and bring them to the place that I have chosen to set my name there.

10 And they are your servants and your people whom you have redeemed with your great power and with your strong hand.

11 Please, my Lord, let your ear be attentive to the prayer of your servant and to the prayer of your servants who delight to fear your name; and grant success to your servant today, and grant him mercy in the sight of this man. Now I was cupbearer to the king.


## Chapter 2

1 In the month of Nisan, in the twentieth year of King Artaxerxes, wine was before him; and I took the wine and gave it to the king, and I had not been sad before him.

2 And the king said to me, Why is your face sad, though you are not sick? This is nothing but sorrow of heart. Then I became very much afraid.

3 And I said to the king, "May the king live forever. Why should my face not be sad, when the city of the tombs of my fathers is a ruin, and its gates consumed by fire?"

4 And the king said to me, Why is this that you seek? And I prayed to God of the heavens.

5 And I said to the king, If it please the king, and if your servant is acceptable in your sight, that you send me to Judah, to the city of the sepulchres of my fathers, that I may build it.

6 And the king and the queen, who sat beside him, said to me, “How long will your journey be, and when will you return?” And it pleased the king before me, so he sent me, and I gave him a time.

7 And I said to the king, If it seems good to the king, let letters be given me for the governors beyond the river, that they may grant me passage until I come to Judah.

8 And a letter to Asaph, keeper of the king’s forest, that he might give me timber to make beams for the gates of the palace, for the wall of the city, and for the house that I would enter, and the king gave me, according to the good hand of my God upon me.

9 I came to the governors beyond the river and gave them the king's letters, and the king sent with me military captains and horsemen.

10 Sanballat the Horonite and Tobijah the Ammonite bondman heard, and it displeased them greatly that a man had come to seek good for the children of Israel.

11 I came to Jerusalem, and I was there for three days.

12 I arose at night, I and a few men with me, and I had not told any man what my God was putting into my heart to do for Jerusalem; and there was no cattle with me except the beast on which I rode.

13 And I went out by night to the valley gate, facing the dragon's fountain and to the dung hill gate, and I inspected the walls of Jerusalem which were broken down, and its gates which had been consumed by fire.

14 And I passed to the gate of the fountain and to the king's pool, and there was no place for the cattle to pass under me.

15 And I went up by night into the valley and I broke through the wall and I returned and I entered by the valley gate and I returned.

16 And the rulers did not know where I had gone or what I was doing, and to the Jews and to the priests and to the nobles and to the rulers and to the rest doing the work I had not disclosed thus far.

17 And I said to them, “You see the adversity in which we are, that Jerusalem lies in ruins and her gates have been consumed by fire. Come, let us rebuild the wall of Jerusalem, so that we will no longer be a reproach.”

18 I told them about the good hand of my God that was upon me, and also about the words of the king that he had spoken to me, and they said, "Let us rise up and build," and they strengthened their hands for the good work.

19 Sanballat the Horonite, Tobijah the Ammonite bondman, and Geshem the Arabian heard, and they mocked us and despised us, and said, "What is this act that you are doing? Are you rebelling against the king?"

20 I answered them and said, "The God of the heavens is the one who will prosper us; and we, his servants, will rise up and build; but you have no portion, no right, and no memorial in Jerusalem."


## Chapter 3

1 Then Eliashib the high priest arose, and his brothers the priests, and they built the Sheep Gate; they consecrated it and set up its doors, even to the Tower of the Hundred, and they consecrated it to the Tower of Hananeel.

2 And by his hand the men of Jericho built, and by his hand built Zaccur the son of Imri.

3 And the Fish Gate—Hassenaah’s sons built it; they overlaid it and set up its doors, its locks, and its bars.

4 And by their hand Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz held charge, and by their hand Meshullam son of Berachiah son of Meshezabeel held charge, and by their hand Zadok son of Baana held charge.

5 And by their hands the Tekoites held fast, but their nobles did not put their necks to the service of their masters.

6 And the old gate, Jehoiada son of Paseah and Meshullam son of Besodeiah repaired; they set up its doors, with their locks and its bars.

7 And supported by their hands were Melatiah the Gibeonite and Jadon the Meronothite, men of Gibeon and Mizpah, for the seat of the governor beyond the River.

8 Uzziel son of Harhaiah, the refiner, held by his hand and had charge of the work, and Hananiah son of the apothecary held by his hand and had charge of the work; they were over Jerusalem as far as the Broad Wall.

9 And Rephaiah son of Hur held charge over half the district of Jerusalem.

10 And next to them Jedaiah son of Harumaph made repairs opposite his house. And next to him Hattush son of Hashabniah held his post.

11 A second measure Malchijah son of Harim and Hashub son of Pahathmoab repaired, and the tower of the furnaces.

12 And Shallum son of Hallohesh, governor of half the district of Jerusalem, he and his daughters, held charge by him.

13 Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah strengthened the gate of the valley; they built it and set up its doors, its locks, and its bars, and a thousand cubits of the wall as far as the Dung Gate.

14 And Malchiah son of Rechab, ruler of Beth-haccerem, held the Dung Gate; he rebuilt it and set up its doors, with their locks and bars.

15 And Shallum son of Col-hozeh, captain of the district of Mitspah, he built and repaired the gate of the fountain; its doors, its bolts, and its bars; and the wall of the pool of Shiloah by the king's garden as far as the steps that go down from the city of David.

16 After him Nehemiah son of Azbuk, ruler of half Beth-zur, repaired as far as opposite the graves of David, and as far as the made fish-pool, and as far as the house of the mighty men.

17 After him stood the Leviite Rehum son of Bani, and by his hand stood Hashabiah, captain of half the district of Keilah, for his division.

18 After them, their brothers strengthened Bavai son of Henadad, captain of half the district of Keilah.

19 And he took hold upon his hand Ezer son of Jeshua, captain of Mizpah, a second garment, opposite the ascent of the armed men at the corner.

20 After him Baruch son of Zabbai secured a second section from the corner to the entrance of the house of Eliashib the great priest.

21 After him Meremoth son of Uriah son of Hakkoz repaired a second section from the entrance of the house of Eliashib to the end of the house of Eliashib.

22 After him the priests, men of the district, held fast to the work.

23 After him stood Benjamin and Hashub opposite their family; after him stood Azariah son of Maaseiah son of Ananiah beside his house.

24 After him Binnui the son of Henadad repaired a second section, from the house of Azariah to the corner and to the tower.

25 Palal son of Uzai was next to the corner tower that went out from the upper king's house which belonged to the court of the prison; after him was Pedaiah son of Parosh.

26 And the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel, opposite the Water Gate toward the east, and the tower that goes out.

27 After him the Tekoites repaired another section, from opposite the great tower that projects to the wall of Ophel.

28 From above the horse gate the priests held, each man opposite his house.

29 After him Zadok the son of Immer was in charge of his household, and after him Shemaiah the son of Shecaniah was guardian of the east gate.

30 Hananiah son of Shelemiah and Hanun son of Zalaph the sixth took charge of the second gate; after them Meshullam son of Berachiah took charge opposite his chamber.

31 Malchiah the son of the goldsmith held from the court of the Nethinims to the merchants' [area], opposite the Miphkad Gate and to the ascent of the corner.

32 Between the ascent of the tower and the sheep gate, the refiners and the merchants held.


## Chapter 4

1 When Sanballat heard that we were building the wall, he became angry and greatly incensed, and he mocked the Jews.

2 And he said before his brothers and the army of Samaria, What are the feeble Jews doing? Will they recover from them? Will they sacrifice? Have they made an end in one day? Will they revive the stones from the heaps of dust, though they are burned?

3 And Tobijah the Ammonite was beside him and said also, "What are they building? If a fox goes up, it will break down their stone wall."

4 Hear our God, for we have been despised; turn their reproach upon their own head, and give them as prey in the land of captivity.

5 Do not cover their iniquity, and do not erase their sin from your presence, for they have provoked you to anger before the builders.

6 And we built the wall, and the whole wall was joined together up to its middle, and the people had a mind to do the work.

7 When Sanballat, Tobiah, the Arabians, the Ammonites, and the Ashdodites heard that the health of the walls of Jerusalem had been made up and that the breaches had begun to be closed, they were very angry.

8 And they all joined together to come and fight against Jerusalem and to harm it.

9 We prayed to our God and set a guard against them by day and by night because of their presence.

10 Judah said, "The ability of the bearer of burdens is failing, and the dust is abundant, and we are not able to build the wall."

11 And our adversaries said, They shall not know nor see, until we come among them and strike them down, and cease the work.

12 And it happened when the Jews who lived near them came, they said to us ten times, From all the places where you return, they will be against us.

13 And I stood beneath the place behind the wall, and I stationed the people by their families with their swords, spears, and bows.
