14 I looked and rose up and said to the nobles, the rulers, and the rest of the people, "Do not fear their presence; remember the LORD your Lord, the great and awesome one, and fight for your brothers, your sons, your daughters, your wives, and your homes."

15 When our enemies heard that we knew, God frustrated their counsel, all of us returned to the wall, each to his work.

16 From that day half of my young men were doing the work in the business and half of them held the spears and the shields and the bows and the coats of mail and the captains behind all the house of Judah.

17 The builders on the wall and those carrying the burden were overburdened, each with one hand doing the work and one hand holding the weapon.

18 And the builders, each with his sword girded at his side, were building, and the one who sounded the trumpet was beside me.

19 And I said to the nobles and to the rulers and to the rest of the people, The work is great and extensive, and we are separated on the wall, each far from his brother.

20 In the place where you hear the sound of the trumpet, there you shall gather to us; our God will fight for us.

21 And we are doing the business, and half of them are holding the spears from the rising of the morning until the going out of the stars.

22 At that time I also said to the people, every man and his servant should lodge within Jerusalem, so that they would be our guard by night and the day for work.

23 But I and my brothers and my servants and the men of the guard who are with me are not stripping off our garments, each man his weapon is at the ready.


## Chapter 5

1 And the cry of the folk and their women was a great cry to their brothers the Jews.

2 And there are those who say, “We have many sons and daughters; let us take wheat and eat, that we may live.”

3 There are those who say, "Our fields, our vineyards, our houses—we are pledging them—so that we may obtain wheat during the famine."

4 And there are those who say, "We have borrowed money according to the king's measure; our fields and our vineyards."

5 And now, our own flesh is like the flesh of our brothers, their children are like our children, yet we are enslaving our sons and our daughters, and some of our daughters are already enslaved, with no power in our hand, and our fields and our vineyards belong to others.

6 I became very angry when I heard their cry and these words.

7 My heart consented within me, and I contended with the nobles and the rulers, and I said to them, "Each of you is exacting usury from his brother"; so I held a great assembly against them.

8 And I said to them, We have bought our Jewish kindred who were sold to the Gentiles; and even you would sell your kindred to us? But they were silent and found nothing to say.

9 The act you are doing is not good; will you not walk in the fear of our God, because of the reproach of the nations, our enemies?

10 And I also—I, my brothers, and my servants are lending them money and grain; let us leave now this exaction.

11 Restore to them, we pray, according to this age, their fields, their vineyards, their olive trees, and their houses, as well as the hundred of silver, the wheat, the sweet wine, and the anointed oil, which you are lending at interest.

12 And they said, Let us return, but of them we will not ask; so will we do as you say. And I called the chief rulers and made them swear to do this word.

13 Also I shook my lap as I said, Thus may God do to the man who does not uphold this word, from his house and from his labor; so may he be emptied and laid waste. And all the people said, Amen, and praised the LORD, and the people did according to this word.

14 Even from the day when he appointed me to be governor in the land of Judah, from the year twenty until the year thirty-two of King Artaxerxes, twelve years, I and my brothers did not eat the food of the governor.

15 And the former governors who were before me burdened the people and took from them in food and wine, besides forty shekels of silver; also their servants ruled over the people, but I did not do so because of the fear of God.

16 And also in the work of the wall I strengthened my hands, and we did not buy a field; and all my servants were gathered there for the work.

17 And the Jew and the ruler, a hundred and fifty men, and those who came to us from the Gentile people who are round about us, to my table.

18 And what was prepared for one day was one ox, six choice sheep, and birds, and wine in abundance every ten days; yet with this, I did not demand the food of the governor, because the service was heavy upon this people.

19 Remember for me, O God, for good, all that I have done for this people.


## Chapter 6

1 When Sanballat, Tobijah, Geshem the Arabian, and the rest of our enemies heard that I had built the wall and that no breach remained in it—though up to that time I had not set up doors in the gates—

2 Sanballat and Geshem sent to me, saying, "Come, let us meet together in the village of the plain of Ono," and they intended to do me harm.

3 And I sent messengers to them, saying, "I am doing a great work; I cannot come down. Why should the work cease while I leave it and come down to you?"

4 They sent to me according to this word four times, and I answered them according to this word.

5 Sanballat sent to me this answer five times, each time his servant with an open letter in his hand.

6 It is reported among the Gentiles that Geshem has said: "You and the Jews intend to rebel; therefore you are building the wall, and you are appointing them as king according to these words."

7 You also raised up prophets in Jerusalem to cry against you, saying, “King of Judah!” Now may the king hear these words. Come now, and let us take counsel together.

8 And I sent to him, saying, These things are not as you say, for you are imagining them from your own mind.

9 For they are all afraid of us, saying, Their hands will be weakened from the work so that it will not be done; but now strengthen my hands.

10 And I came to the house of Shemaiah son of Dalaiah son of Mehetabel, and he was confined; and he said, Let us go into the house of God, into the middle of the temple, and let us shut the doors of the temple, for they are coming by night to kill you.

11 And I said, A certain man like me will flee, and who is like me that will come into the temple and live? I will not come.

12 And I perceived that he was not sent by God, for he prophesied against me, and Tobiah and Sanballat had hired him.

13 Because he is hired, because of fear, I will do so, and I will sin, and it will be to them a name of evil, so that they may taunt me.

14 May my God remember Tobiah and Sanballat according to their deeds, and also Noadiah the prophetess and the rest of the prophets who were trying to make me afraid.

15 The wall was completed on the twenty-fifth of Elul, in fifty-two days.

16 And it came to pass, when all our enemies heard, and all the nations that were about us saw, that they fell greatly in their own eyes, and knew that this work had been done by our God.

17 Also in those days the nobles of Judah sent many letters to Tobiah, and Tobiah's letters came to them.

18 For many in Judah were sworn to him, for he was son-in-law to Shecaniah son of Arah, and Jehohanan his son took the daughter of Meshullam son of Berachiah.

19 Also my good deeds they reported before me, and my words they brought out to me; letters Tobijah sent to me.


## Chapter 7

1 And it happened, when the wall was built and I had set up its gates, that the doorkeepers, the singers, and the Leviites were appointed.

2 And I charged Hanani my brother, and Hananiah the captain of the palace in Jerusalem, for he was a man of truth, and one who feared God more than many.

3 They shall not open the gates of Jerusalem until the heat of the sun; and while they are standing, they shall shut its doors and hold fast; and the keepers who dwell in Jerusalem shall be stationed, each at his post and each facing his house.

4 And the city is wide of hand and great, and the people few in her midst, and there are no houses built.

5 And my God put into my heart to gather the nobles and the rulers and the people to assemble, and I found the book of the genealogy of those who came up at first, and I found it written in it.

6 These are the sons of the province who came up from the captivity of the exile that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had exiled, and they returned to Jerusalem and to Judah, each man to his city.

7 Those who came with Zerubbabel: Jeshua, Nehemiah, Azariah, Raamiah, Nahamani, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispereth, Bigvai, Nehum, Baanah, and the account of the men of the people of Israel.

8 The sons of Parosh: two thousand one hundred and seventy-two.

9 The sons of Shephatiah: three hundred seventy-two.

10 The sons of Arah: six hundred fifty-two.

11 The sons of Pahathmoab, from the sons of Jeshua and Joab, two thousand eight hundred eighteen.

12 The sons of Elam: one thousand two hundred fifty and four.

13 The sons of Zattu: eight hundred forty-five.

14 The sons of Zaccai: seven hundred and sixty.

15 The sons of Binnui: six hundred forty-eight.

16 The children of Bebai, six hundred twenty-eight.

17 The sons of Azgad: two thousand three hundred and twenty-two.

18 The sons of Adonikam: six hundred sixty and seven.

19 The sons of Bigvai: two thousand sixty and seven.

20 The sons of Adin: six hundred fifty-five.

21 The sons of Ater to Hezekiah: ninety-eight.

22 The sons of Hashum: three hundred twenty-eight.

23 The sons of Bezai: three hundred and twenty-four.

24 The sons of Hariph: one hundred twelve.

25 Ninety-five sons of Gibeon.

26 The men of Bethlehem and Netophah: one hundred and eighty-eight.

27 The men of Anathoth: one hundred twenty-eight.

28 The men of Bethazmaveth, forty-two.

29 The men of Kirjath-jearim, Chephirah, and Beeroth, seven hundred forty-three.

30 The men of Ramah and Gaba, six hundred and twenty-one.

31 The men of Mikmas: one hundred and twenty-two.

32 The men of Beth-el and Ai, one hundred and twenty-three.

33 Fifty-two men of Nebo.

34 The sons of Elam: one thousand two hundred fifty and four.

35 The sons of Harim: three hundred and thirty.

36 The sons of Jericho: three hundred forty-five.

37 The sons of Lod: Hadid and Ono, seven hundred and twenty-one.

38 The children of Senaah: three thousand nine hundred and thirty.

39 The priests, sons of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua, nine hundred seventy-three.

40 The sons of Immer: one thousand five hundred and two.

41 The sons of Pashur: one thousand two hundred forty and seven.

42 The sons of Harim: one thousand seventeen.

43 The Leviite, sons of Jeshua, to Kadmiel, to the sons of Hodevah, seventy-four.

44 The singers, the sons of Asaph, one hundred forty-eight.

45 The doorkeepers: the sons of Shallum, the sons of Ater, the sons of Talmon, the sons of Akkub, the sons of Hatita, the sons of Shobai— one hundred thirty-eight.

46 The Nethinims, the sons of Ziha, the sons of Hashupha, the sons of Tabaoth.

47 The sons of Keros, the sons of Siaha, the sons of Padon.

48 The sons of Lebanah, the sons of Hagabah, the sons of Shalmai.

49 The sons of Canan, the sons of Giddel, the sons of Gahar.

50 The sons of Reaiah, the sons of Rezin, the sons of Nekoda.

51 The sons of Gazzam, the sons of Uzza, the sons of Paseah.

52 The sons of Besai, the sons of the Mehunim, the sons of Nephisesim.

53 the sons of Bakbuk the sons of Hakupha the sons of Harhur

54 The sons of Bazlith, the sons of Mehida, the sons of Harsha.

55 The sons of Barkos, the sons of Sisera, the sons of Tamah.

56 The sons of Neziah, the sons of Hatipha.

57 The sons of the servants of Solomon: the sons of Sotai, the sons of Sophereth, the sons of Perida.

58 The sons of Jaala, the sons of Darkon, the sons of Giddel.

59 The sons of Shephatiah, the sons of Hattil, the sons of Pochereth of Zebaim, the sons of Amon.

60 All the Nethinims and the sons of the servants of Solomon, three hundred ninety and two.

61 These who came up from Tel-melah, Tel-haresha, Cherub, Addon, and Immer, and were not able to declare their family and their seed, whether they were of Israel.

62 The sons of Dalaiah, the sons of Tobiah, the sons of Nekoda, six hundred and forty-two.

63 And from the priests, the sons of Habaiah, the sons of Hakkoz, the sons of Barzillai, who took a wife from the daughters of Barzillai the Gileadite, and was called by their name.

64 These sought their register of those who were genealogically related, but it was not found, and they were excluded from the priesthood.

65 The Tirshatha said to them that they could not eat from the consecrated things, the most holy portion, until a priest stood up with Urim and Thummim.

66 All the congregation as one, forty-two thousand three hundred and sixty.

67 Besides their male servants and female servants, seven thousand three hundred thirty-seven, and they had singers, male and female, two hundred forty-five.

68 Their horses seven hundred thirty-six, their mules two hundred forty-five.

69 four hundred thirty-five camels, six thousand seven hundred and twenty (he) asses.

70 And some of the heads of the fathers gave for the work: the Tirshatha gave to the treasury a thousand gold drams, fifty bowls, and thirty-five thousand priestly garments.

71 And from the chiefs of the fathers they gave to the treasury of the work gold drams twenty thousand and silver pounds two thousand and two hundred.

72 And the remainder of the people gave twenty thousand darics of gold and two thousand manehs of silver and seventy-two priestly robes.

73 The priests, the Levites, the doorkeepers, the singers, some of the people, the Nethinims, and all Israel lived in their towns; and the seventh month came, and the children of Israel were in their towns.


## Chapter 8

1 All the people gathered as one man into the broad place before the water gate, and they said to Ezra the scribe, Bring the scroll of the law of Moses which the LORD commanded to Israel.

2 Ezra the priest brought the law before the assembly, from man to woman, and all who could understand what they heard, on one day of the seventh month.

3 And he read from it in the broad place before the Water Gate from daybreak until midday, in the presence of the men and the women and those who could understand, and the ears of all the people were attentive to the book of the law.

4 Ezra the scribe stood upon a wooden tower made for the purpose, and at his right stood Mattithiah, Shema, Anaiah, Uriah, Hillkiah, and Maaseiah, while at his left stood Pedaiah, Mishael, Malchiah, Hashum, Hasbadana, Zechariah, and Meshullam.

5 Ezra opened the bill before the eyes of all the people, for he was above all the people, and when he opened it, all the people stood.

6 Ezra blessed the LORD God the great, and all the people answered, Amen, Amen, with the lifting of their hands, and they bowed their faces to the ground in worship to the LORD.

7 Jeshua and Bani, Sherebiah, Jamin, Akkub, Shabbethai, Hodijah, Maaseiah, Kelita, Azariah, Josabad, Canan, and Pelaiah, and the Leviites, understanding the folk in the law, and the folk upon their place stood.

8 They read from the scroll the law of God clearly, gave understanding, and they comprehended the reading.

9 Nehemiah the Tirshatha, Ezra the priest the scribe, and the Levites who caused the people to understand said to all the people, This day is holy to the LORD your God: do not mourn and do not weep, for all the people were weeping when they heard the words of the law.

10 Go, eat fat, drink sweet, and send portions to those who have nothing prepared, for the day is holy to our Lord, and do not be grieved, for the joy of the LORD is your strength.

11 And the Leviites stilled all the folk, saying, “Cease, for the day is holy; do not be distressed.”

12 And all the people went to eat and to drink and to send portions and to make great joy, because they understood in the words which were made known to them.

13 On the second day, the heads of the fathers of all the people, the priests and the Levites, gathered to Ezra the scribe to give attention to the words of the law.

14 They found written in the law that Jehovah, the Lord commanded by Moses that the children of Israel should dwell in booths during the feast in the seventh month.

15 And they proclaimed aloud in all their cities and in Jerusalem, saying, “Go out to the hill and bring leafy branches of olive, leafy branches of oil tree, leafy branches of myrtle, leafy branches of palm, and leafy branches of leafy tree, to make booths as written.”

16 The folk went out and brought and made for themselves booths, each man on his roof and in their courts and in the courts of the house of God and in the broad place of the gate of the waters and in the broad place of the gate of Ephraim.

17 And all the congregation who returned from the captivity made booths and dwelt in the booths; for since the days of Jeshua son of Nun had not the children of Israel done so until that day, and there was very great joy.

18 He read from the scroll of the law of God daily, from the first day to the last day, and they made a solemn feast for seven days, and on the eighth day there was a solemn assembly according to the ordinance.


## Chapter 9

1 On the twenty-fourth day of this month the people of Israel were assembled with fasting and sackcloth, and earth upon them.

2 The offspring of Israel separated themselves from all foreign people, stood up, and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their ancestors.

3 They rose up on the place where they stood and called upon the scroll of the law of the LORD their God a fourth of the day, and a fourth they confessed and prostrated themselves to the LORD their God.

4 Then Jeshua and Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, and Chenani arose upon the ascent of the Levites and cried aloud to the LORD their God.

5 The Levite Jeshua and Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabniah, Sherebiah, Hodijah, Shebaniah, and Pethakiah said: "Arise, bless the LORD your God from of old even to everlasting, and let them bless the name of your glorious honor, exalted above all blessing and praise."

6 You are the LORD alone; you made the heavens, even the heaven of heavens, and all their host, the earth and all that is in it, the seas and all that is in them; and you give life to all of them; and the host of the heavens worships you.

7 You are the LORD God who chose Abram and brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans and gave him the name Abraham.

8 and you found his heart faithful before you, and made with him the covenant to give the land of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite, the Perizzite, the Jebusite, and the Girgashite, to give it to his seed, and you kept your words, for you are righteous

9 You saw the affliction of our forefathers in Egypt, and You heard their cry by the Red Sea.

10 And you gave marks and miracles in Pharaoh and in all his bondmen and in all the people of his land, for you knew that they acted haughtily toward them, and you made for yourself a name as of this day.

11 And you split the sea before them, and they crossed through the midst of the sea on dry ground, and you cast their pursuers into the depths like a stone in mighty waters.

12 And by a pillar of cloud you led them by day, and by a pillar of fire by night to light for them the way that they should go.

13 And you descended upon Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven and gave them upright judgments and true laws, statutes and good commandments.

14 And you made known to them your consecrated sabbath, and commandments and statutes and law you commanded them by the hand of Moses your servant.

15 You gave them bread from the heavens for their hunger and brought forth water for them from the rock for their thirst, and You said to them to enter to possess the land which You lifted up Your hand to give to them.

16 And they and our forefathers acted proudly, and stiffened their necks, and did not listen to your commandments.

17 They refused to listen and did not remember your wonders which you performed among them; they stiffened their necks and appointed a leader to return to bondage in rebellion, but you are a God of forgiveness, gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in kindness, and you did not forsake them.

18 Indeed, they made for themselves a molten calf and said, This is your God who brought you up from Egypt, and they committed great blasphemies.

19 But in your abundant compassion you did not abandon them in the wilderness, the pillar of cloud did not depart from over them by day to lead them along the way, nor the pillar of fire by night to illuminate for them the path on which they should walk.

20 And your good spirit you gave to instruct them, and manna you did not withhold from their mouths, and water you gave to them for their thirst.

21 For a whole age in the wilderness you were sustained; you did not lack, your clothes did not wear out, and your legs did not swell.

22 And you gave them kingdoms and peoples and divided them into quarters and they inherited the land of Sihon and the land of the king of Heshbon and the land of Og the king of Bashan.

23 And you multiplied their children as the stars of the heavens, and brought them into the land which you said to their fathers to enter and possess.

24 The sons came and inherited the land, and the Canaanites who lived in the land were subdued before them, and they were given into their hand, and their kings and the peoples of the land, to do with them according to their will.

25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land, and they inherited houses filled with every good thing, hewn cisterns, vineyards, olive trees, and fruit trees in great abundance; they ate and were satisfied, and they grew fat on your great goodness.

26 They rebelled and defied you, and cast your law behind their back, and killed your prophets who testified among them to turn them to you, and they committed great blasphemies.

27 You gave them into the hand of their adversaries, and they oppressed them; but in their time of distress they cried out to you, and you heard from heaven, and according to your great mercies you gave them deliverers who saved them from the hand of their adversaries.

28 But when they had rest, they again did evil before you, so you abandoned them into the hand of their enemies, who ruled over them; then they returned and cried out to you, and from the heavens you heard and delivered them many times according to your abundant mercies.

29 You charged them to return to your law, but they acted proudly and did not listen to your commandments, and they sinned against your judgments, by which a person does live, and they turned a stubborn shoulder and stiffened their neck and would not listen.

30 And you extended over them many long years and reproved them by your spirit through your prophets, but they did not listen, and you gave them into the hand of the peoples of the lands.

31 And in your abundant compassion you did not utterly consume them, nor did you forsake them, for you are a God, gracious and merciful.

32 And now our God the great God the mighty and awesome who keeps the covenant and steadfast love do not let before your face all the trouble that has come upon us and our kings and our rulers and our priests and our prophets and our ancestors and all your people from the days of the kings of Assyria until this day

33 But you are righteous concerning all that has come upon us, for you have dealt truly, and we have acted wickedly.

34 And our kings, our captains, our priests, and our fathers did not keep your law, and they did not obey your commandments and your testimonies which you testified against them.

35 But in their kingdom, and in your great goodness that you gave them, and in the broad and rich land that you gave them before them, they did not serve you, and they did not turn from their evil doings.

36 Behold, we are servants this day, and the land which you gave to our fathers to eat its fruit and its goodness—behold, we are servants upon it.

37 and its revenue greatly increases for the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins, and over our corpses they rule, and over our cattle as they please, and we are in great distress.

38 And in all this we are making a sure agreement, writing it, and our leaders, our Levites, and our priests are affixing their names to the seal.


## Chapter 10

1 And over the sealed was Nehemiah the Tirshatha son of Hachaliah and Zedekiah.

2 Seraiah, Azariah, Jeremiah.

3 Pashur, the son of Amariah, the son of Malchijah.

4 Hattush, Shebaniah, Malluch.

5 Harim, Meremoth, Obadiah.

6 Daniel, Ginnethon, Baruch.

7 Meshullam was Abiah's from Miamin.

8 Maaziah, Bilgai, Shemaiah—these were the priests.

9 and the Leviites: and Jeshua son of Azaniah, Binnui son of Henadad, Kadmiel.

10 Their brothers: Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Canan.

11 Micha of Rehob, Hashabiah.

12 Zaccur, Sherebiah, Shebaniah.

13 Hodiah, Bani, and Beninu.

14 The chiefs of the people: Parosh, Pahathmoab, Elam, Zattu, Bani.

15 Bunni, Azgad, Bebai.

16 Adonijah with Bigvai and Adin.

17 Ater fortified Hezekiah.

18 Hodiah, Hashum, and Bezai.

19 Hariph, Anathoth, Nobai.

20 Magpiash, Meshullam, Hezir.

21 Meshezabeel, Zadok, and Jaddua were known.

22 Pelatiah, Canan, and Anaiah.

23 Hoshea, Hananiah, Hashub.

24 Hallohesh seizes Pilcha; Shobek departs.

25 Rehum, Hashabnah, and Maaseiah considered the matter.

26 Ahijah, Canan, and Anan.

27 Malluch, Harim, Baanah,

28 And the rest of the people, the priests, the Levites, the porters, the singers, the Nethinims, and all those separated from the peoples of the lands to the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, everyone knowing understanding.

29 Holding fast to their brothers, the mighty ones, and coming with an oath and a curse to walk in the law of God, which was given by the hand of Moses, God's servant, and to keep and to do all the commandments of the LORD our Lord, and his judgments and his statutes.

30 And not to give our daughters to the peoples of the land, and their daughters not to take for our sons.

31 The folk of the land who bring wares and all victuals to sell on the sabbath day, we will not take from them on the sabbath or on a holy day; and we will let go the seventh year and all exaction of every hand.

32 And we established upon ourselves the obligation to contribute a third of the shekel yearly for the service of the house of our God.

33 for the bread of the row and for the gift that is continual and for the ascent of the continual daily burnt offering, for the sabbaths, for the new moons, for the appointed times, and for the consecrated things, and for the sin offerings, to make atonement for Israel, and for all manner of work of the house of our God.

34 And the lots we cast for the wood offering—upon the priests, the Levites, and the people—for bringing to the house of our God, to the houses of our fathers, at appointed times, year by year, to burn upon the altar of the LORD our God, as it is written in the law.

35 and to bring the first fruit of our land and the first fruit of all manner of fruit of every tree, year by year, to the house of the LORD

36 And the firstborn of our children and our cattle, as it is written in the law, and the firstborn of our herds and our flocks, to bring to the house of our God, to the priests who minister in the house of our God.

37 And the beginning of our dough, and our gifts, and the fruit of every tree, new wine and anointed oil, we bring to the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God; and the tithe of our land to the Levites, who are the Levites that collect the tithe in all the cities of our service.

38 And the priest, being of the sons of Aaron, shall be with the Levites when the Levites receive the tithe of the tithes, and the Levites shall bring the offering of the tithe to the house of our God, into the chambers of the storehouse.

39 For the sons of Israel and the sons of Levi shall bring the offering of the corn, the new wine, and the anointed oil to the chambers, and there shall be the vessels of the sanctuary, and the priests who minister, and the doorkeepers, and the singers; and we will not forsake the house of our God.


## Chapter 11

1 The captains of the people dwelt in Jerusalem, and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of every ten to dwell in Jerusalem, the holy city, and nine parts in the towns.

2 And they blessed the folk, all the men who volunteered to dwell in Jerusalem.

3 And these are the chiefs of the province who lived in Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah the men lived each in his possession: Israel, the priests, the Levites, the Nethinims, and the sons of the servants of Solomon.

4 And in Jerusalem dwelt some of the people of Judah and some of the people of Benjamin: of the people of Judah, Athaiah son of Uzziah son of Zachariah son of Amariah son of Shephatiah son of Mahalaleel, of the sons of Perez.

5 Maaseiah son of Baruch son of Col-hozeh son of Hazaiah son of Adaiah son of Joiarib son of Zachariah son of Shiloni.

6 All the sons of Perez who dwelt in Jerusalem: four hundred sixty and eight men of valor.

7 And these are the sons of Benjamin: Sallu, son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jesaiah.

8 After him Gabbai of Sallai nine hundred twenty-eight.

9 Joel son of Zichri was appointed over them, and Judah son of Hasenuah was over the city as second.

10 from the priests, Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin.

11 Seraiah son of Hillkiah son of Meshullam son of Zadok son of Meraioth son of Ahitub, leader of the house of God.

12 Their brothers who did the work for the temple were eight hundred twenty-two, and Adaiah son of Jeroham son of Pelaliah son of Amzi son of Zachariah son of Pashur son of Malchiah.

13 And his brothers, chiefs of fathers, two hundred forty-two, and Amashai son of Azarel son of Ahasai son of Meshillemoth son of Immer.

14 And their brothers, mighty men of valor, one hundred twenty-eight, and over them was set Zabdiel son of the great ones.

15 From the Leviite, Shemaiah son of Hashub son of Azrikam son of Hashabiah son of Bunni.

16 And Shabbethai and Josabad were over the outward business of the house of God, of the chief Levites.

17 Mattaniah son of Micah son of Zabdi son of Asaph was chief over the beginning of the prayer, and Bakbukiah second among his brothers, and Abda son of Shammua son of Galal son of Jeduthun.

18 All the Levites in the holy city were two hundred eighty and four.

19 The doorkeepers were Akkub, Talmon, and their kindred who kept watch at the gates, one hundred seventy-two.

20 And the rest of Israel, the priests, the Levites, in all the cities of Judah, every man in his inheritance.

21 And the Nethinims dwelt in Ophel; and Ziha and Gispa were over the Nethinims.

22 And appointed the Levites in Jerusalem, Uzzi son of Bani son of Hashabiah son of Mattaniah son of Micha, of the sons of Asaph, who had the charge over the work of the house of God.

23 For the king's commandment was upon them, and a certain portion was upon the overseers, the matter of each day in its day.

24 Pethakiah son of Meshezabeel, of the sons of Zerah son of Judah, was at the king's side for every matter concerning the people.

25 And in the villages with their fields, of the children of Judah, dwelt in Kirjath-arba and its daughters, and in Dibon and its daughters, and in Jekabzeel and its villages.

26 and in Jeshua and in Moladah and in Beth-palet.

27 Hazar-shual and Beer-shebah and its towns.

28 and in Ziklag and in Mekonah and in its towns.

29 En-rimmon, Zorah, and Jarmuth.

30 Zanoah, Adullam, and its villages, Lachish and its fields, Azekah and its towns, and they encamped from Beer-shebah to the valley of Hinnom.

31 And the sons of Benjamin from Geba, from Michmash and Ai, and from Beth-el and its towns.

32 Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah.

33 Hazor, Ramah, and Gittaim.

34 Hadid, Zeboim, and Neballat.

35 Lod and Ono, the valley of the craftsmen.

36 From the Leviites, divisions of Judah and Benjamin.


## Chapter 12

1 These are the priests and the Levites who went up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, and Ezra.

2 Amariah, Malluch, Hattush.

3 Shecaniah the son of Rehum of Meremoth.

4 Iddo Ginnetho Abiah.

5 From Miamin to Maadiah, Bilgah.

6 Shemaiah and Joiarib and Jedaiah.

7 Sallai, Amok, Hillkiah, Jedaiah—these were the chief priests and their brothers in the days of Jeshua.

8 And the Leviite Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, and Mattaniah, over the thanksgiving, he and his kindred.

9 And Bakbukiah's kindred were opposite them for the watch.

10 Jeshua fathered Joiakim, and Joiakim fathered Eliashib, and Eliashib fathered Jehoiada.

11 Jehoiada fathered Jonathan, and Jonathan fathered Jaddua.

12 In the age of Joiakim, the heads of the fathers of the priests were Seraiah son of Meraiah, Jeremiah son of Hananiah.

13 To Ezra, Meshullam, to Amariah, Jehohanan.

14 Jonathan, Shebaniah, Joseph.

15 Harim to Adna, Meraioth to Helkai.

16 Zachariah to Ginnetho, Meshullam.

17 Abijah of Zichri, Miniamin, Moadiah, Piltai.

18 Bilgah, Shammua, Shemaiah, Jonathan.

19 And to Joiarib, Mattenai; to Jedaiah, Uzzi.

20 To Sallai, Kallai, Amok, Eber.

21 Hillkiah, Hashabiah, Jedaiah, Nethaneel.

22 In the age of Eliashib, Joiada, and Johanan, and Jaddua, the Leviites and the priests were recorded as chief fathers, during the reign of Darius the Persian.

23 The sons of Levi, heads of the fathers, were written in the book of the words of the days, even until the days of Johanan son of Eliashib.

24 The chief Levites were Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, and their brothers opposite them, to praise and give thanks according to the commandment of David the man of God, watch facing watch.

25 Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub, doorkeepers, the guard at the thresholds, the gate.

26 These were in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor, and Ezra the priest, the scribe.

27 And at the dedicating of the wall of Jerusalem they sought the Leviites from all their places to bring them to Jerusalem, to perform the dedicating with gladness and with confessions and with singing, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps.

28 The sons of the singers gathered from the plain round about Jerusalem and from the villages of the Netophathite.

29 From the court of Gilgal and from the fields of Geba and Azmaveth, for courts they built for themselves around Jerusalem.

30 The priests and the Leviites purified themselves, and they purified the people and the gates and the wall.

31 And I brought up the captains of Judah upon the wall, and I stationed two great choirs giving thanks, and the one company went to the right upon the wall, toward the Dung Gate.

32 And Hoshaiah went after them, and half of the captains of Judah.

33 Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam.

34 Judah, Benjamin, Shemaiah, and Jeremiah.

35 And of the priests, those with the trumpets: Zechariah son of Jonathan son of Shemaiah son of Mattaniah son of Micah son of Zaccur son of Asaph.

36 And his brothers: Shemaiah, Azarael, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethaneel, Judah, and Hanani, with musical instruments of David the man of God; and Ezra the scribe before them.

37 And they went up at the gate of the fountain and over against them, they went up by the steps of the city of David, by the ascent to the wall, above the house of David, even to the gate of the water on the east.

38 And the second confession going to the left, and I after it, and half the people upon the wall above the tower of the ovens, and to the broad wall.

39 From above the gate of Ephraim and the Jeshanah Gate and the Fish Gate and the Tower of Hananeel and the Tower of the Hundred to the Sheep Gate they stood at the Mark Gate.

40 And the two sacrifices of praise stood in the temple of God, and I and half of the rulers were with me.

41 And the priests were Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Michaiah, Elihoenai, Zechariah, and Hananiah with the trumpets.

42 Maaseiah and Shemaiah and Eleazar and Uzzi and Jehohanan and Malchiah and Elam and Ezer made the singers heard, and Izrahiah was the overseer.

43 On that day they offered great sacrifices and rejoiced, for God had given them great joy, and the women and the children also rejoiced, and the joy of Jerusalem was heard from afar.

44 On that day men were appointed over the chambers for the storehouses, for the contributions, for the firstfruits, and for the tithes, to gather into them the portions of the law for the priests and for the Levites, for Judah rejoiced over the priests and over the Levites who were standing.

45 They kept the charge of their God and the charge of the purification, as did the singers and the gatekeepers, according to the commandment of David and Solomon his son.

46 For in the age of David and Asaph aforetime, there were leaders of the singers and songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.

47 And in all Israel during the days of Zerubbabel and during the days of Nehemiah, the portion of the singers and the doorkeepers was given day by day; and they consecrated portions for the Levites, and the Levites consecrated portions for the sons of Aaron.


## Chapter 13

1 On that day it was read in the book of Moses in the hearing of the people, and it was found written therein that an Ammonite or a Moabite should not enter the assembly of God forever.

2 For they did not meet the children of Israel with bread and with water, and they hired Balaam against him to curse him, but our God turned the curse into a blessing.

3 When they heard the law, they separated all the mixed multitude from Israel.

4 Before this, Eliashib the priest had been given a chamber in the house of our God, near Tobiah.

5 He made for him a large chamber, and there formerly they had put the grain offering, the frankincense, and the vessels, and the tithe of the grain, the new wine, and the anointed oil, the ordinance for the Levites, the singers, and the gatekeepers, and the heave offering of the priests.

6 And in all this I was not in Jerusalem, for in the year thirty-two of Artaxerxes king of Babylon I came to the king, and after a period of time I asked leave of the king.

7 I came to Jerusalem and perceived the adversity that Eliashib had done for Tobiah by preparing a chamber for him in the courts of the temple of God.

8 It became very bad for me, and I threw out all the vessels of Tobiah’s house to the outside of the chamber.

9 And I said, and they purified the chambers, and I returned there the vessels of the house of God, the offering and the frankincense.

10 I knew that no portion had been given to the Leviites, so each man fled to the country, the Leviites and the singers who did the work.

11 I contended with the rulers and said, Why is the temple of God forsaken? And I gathered them and set them in their place.

12 And all Judah brought the tithe of the wheat and the wine and the anointed oil to the storehouses.

13 I stored it in the storehouses of Shelemiah the priest, Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah of the Levites, and under their hand was Hanan son of Zaccur son of Mattaniah, for they were considered faithful, and over them was the duty to distribute to their brothers.

14 Remember me, O God, for this, and do not wipe out my kindness that I did in the house of my God and in his watches.

15 In those days I saw in Judah people treading wine presses on the sabbath, bringing heaps and loading them on asses, also wine, grapes, figs, and every burden, and bringing them into Jerusalem on the sabbath day; and I testified against them on the day they sold food.

16 And the men of Tyre dwelt therein, bringing fish and all manner of ware, and selling on every sabbath to the people of Judah in Jerusalem.

17 I contended with the nobles of Judah and said to them, "What is this evil act that you are doing, profaning the sabbath day?"

18 Did not your forefathers do so, and did not our God bring upon us all this evil and upon this city, and you are increasing fierce wrath upon Israel to profane the sabbath?

19 When the gates of Jerusalem were shut before the sabbath, I commanded, and the doors were shut; I commanded that they should not be opened until after the sabbath. I stationed my young men at the gates so that no burden would enter on the sabbath day.

20 And the merchants and all who sold wares stayed outside Jerusalem once and twice.

21 I charged them and said to them, Why do you linger beside the wall? If you do it again, I will lay hands on you. From that time on, they did not come on the sabbath.

22 I said to the Levite that they should be purified and come to guard the city gates in order to sanctify the sabbath day; also this remember for me, my God, and spare me according to the abundance of your mercy.

23 Also in those days I saw Jews who had married women of Ashdod, of Ammon, and of Moab.

24 And their children: half spoke in the speech of Ashdod, and they did not know how to speak in the Jews' language, but according to the language of one people and another.

25 I contended with them and cursed them and struck some of them and maimed them and made them swear by God: if you give your daughters to their sons or take their daughters for your sons or for yourselves.

26 Was not Solomon king of Israel guilty of these things, and yet there was no king like him among all the nations—he was loved by his gods, and God had made him king over all Israel—yet even he was led into sin by foreign women?

27 And shall we indeed listen to do all this great evil, to act unfaithfully against our God by returning to foreign wives?

28 And from the sons of Joiada, the son of Eliashib the high priest, who was son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite, I drove him away from me.

29 Remember them, O my God, because they defiled the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites.

30 And I will cleanse them from all strangers, and I will appoint for the priests and for the Levites each in his work.

31 And for the oblation of wood at its appointed times and for the first fruits, remember me, my God, for good.


# ESTHER

## Chapter 1

1 In the days of Ahasuerus—this Ahasuerus who reigned from India even to Cush, one hundred twenty-seven provinces—

2 In those days, when King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne in the palace of Shushan,

3 In the third year of his reign he made a feast for all his princes and servants, the army of Persia and Media, the nobles, and the governors of the provinces before him.

4 When he showed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the honour of his majestic splendour, many days, one hundred and eighty days.

5 And when the days were completed, the king made a feast for all the people found in Shushan the palace, from great to small, a banquet of seven days in the court of the garden of the king's palace.

6 White, green, and blue, held by cords of fine linen and purple, on pillars of silver and columns of marble; beds of gold and silver on a pavement of red marble, white, and black marble.

7 and let them drink from vessels of gold—vessels different from other vessels—and royal wine in great abundance, according to the king’s power.

8 And the drinking was according to the commandment: there was no compulsion, for thus had the king established for all the officers of his court to do according to the will of each man and every man.

9 Also Queen Vashti gave a women's banquet in the royal house of King Ahasuerus.

10 On the seventh day, when the king Ahasuerus was merry with wine, he said to Mehuman, Biztha, Harbona, Bigtha, Abagtha, Zethar, and Carcas, the seven chamberlains who attended the presence of king Ahasuerus.

11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king with the royal crown to display her beauty to the peoples and the officials for she was goodly of appearance

12 Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command conveyed by the chamberlains, and the king became very angry, and his wrath burned within him.

13 The king said to the wise men who knew the times, for such was the king's word before all who knew the law and judgment.

14 And the next to him was Carshena, Shethar, Admatha, Tarshish, Meres, Marsena, and Memucan, the seven princes of Persia and Media, who saw the face of the king, sitting first in the kingdom.

15 According to the commandment concerning Queen Vashti, because she did not obey the commandment of King Ahasuerus by the hand of the chamberlains.

16 Memucan spoke before the king and the officials: Vashti the queen has wronged not only the king alone, but all the officials and all the peoples who are in all the provinces of King Ahasuerus.

17 For the queen's act will spread among all women to despise their husbands in their sight, saying, "King Ahasuerus commanded Queen Vashti to come before him, but she did not come."

18 On this day the ladies of Persia and Media who have heard the word of the queen will say to all the princes of the king, and there will be enough contempt and wrath.

19 If it please the king, let a royal decree go out from his presence and be written among the laws of the Persians and the Medes, which shall not be altered, that Vashti come no more before King Ahasuerus, and that his royal realm be given to her companion who is better than she.

20 And the decree of the king, which he will make in all his realm, will be heard, for it is great, and all the women will give honour to their husbands, from the great to the small.

21 The advice pleased the king and the officials, and the king acted according to the word of Memucan.

22 He sent letters to all the provinces of the king, to every province according to its script and to every people according to its language, that every man should be master in his house and speak according to the language of his people.


## Chapter 2

1 After these acts, when the fury of King Ahasuerus had subsided, he remembered Vashti and what she had done and what had been decreed concerning her.

2 The king's servant lads said to seek for the king young women, good of appearance, virgins.

3 And the king shall appoint governors throughout all the provinces of his realm, and they shall gather every fair young virgin to Shushan the palace, to the house of the women, to the custody of Hegai, the king's chamberlain, keeper of the women, and let purification treatment be given to them.

4 And the damsel who pleases the king shall reign instead of Vashti, and the matter pleased the king, so he did accordingly.

5 A certain Jew was in Shushan the palace, and his name was Mordecai son of Jair son of Shimei son of Kish, a Benjamite.

6 who was carried away from Jerusalem with the captives who were carried away with Jeconiah king of Judah, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon carried away.

7 And he reared Hadassah, she is Esther, daughter of his uncle, for she had no father or mother; and the young woman was of beautiful form and goodly appearance; and at the death of her father and mother, Mordecai took her to himself as daughter.

8 And it came to pass, when the word of the king and his law were heard, and when the young women were gathered to Shushan the palace to the custody of Hegai, that Esther was taken to the house of the king, to the care of Hegai, keeper of the women.

9 And the damsel pleased him, and she obtained favour before him; so he hastened to give her her purification things and her portions, and the seven damsels who were fit to be given her from the house of the king; and he changed her and her damsels to the best place in the women's house.

10 Esther did not tell her folk nor her kindred, for Mordecai had charged her not to tell.

11 And every day continually Mordecai walked before the court of the house of the women, to know the welfare of Esther and what would be done to her.

12 And when the turn of a young woman and a young woman came to go in to King Ahasuerus after the end of the days of her purification according to the law of the women, twelve months—for so were the days of their purification completed: six months with oil of myrrh and six months with spices and the purifying things of the women—

13 And to this damsel coming to the king, let everything she says be given to her, to come with her from the court of the women to the court of the king.

14 In the evening she came, and in the morning she returned to the second house of the women, to the custody of Shaashgaz, the king’s eunuch who kept the concubines; she would not come again to the king unless the king delighted in her and she was called by name.

15 When Esther daughter of Abihail the uncle of Mordecai, whom he had taken to himself as daughter, came before the king to go in, she did not seek anything except what Hegai the eunuch of the king, keeper of the women, would say, and Esther obtained favour in the eyes of all who saw her.

16 Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus, to his royal court, in the tenth month, the month of Tebeth, in the seventh year of his reign.

17 The king loved Esther more than all the women and she obtained favour and kindness before him more than all the virgins and he set a crown of royalty on her head and made her queen instead of Vashti.

18 The king made a great banquet for all his captains and servants, the banquet of Esther, and granted release to the provinces, and gave a gift according to the hand of the king.

19 And when the maidens were gathered a second time, Mordecai was sitting at the king's gate.

20 Esther did not reveal her kindred and her people, as Mordecai had commanded her, and Esther carried out Mordecai's commandment as she had done when brought up by him.

21 In those days, while Mordecai was sitting at the gate of the king, Bigthan and Teresh, two of the king's chamberlains who were guards at the threshold, became angry and sought to lay a hand on King Ahasuerus.

22 The act became known to Mordecai, and he reported it to Queen Esther, and Esther spoke to the king in Mordecai's name.

23 The act was sought and found, and the two of them were hanged on a tree, and it was recorded in the book of the words of the days before the king.


## Chapter 3

1 After these acts, King Ahasuerus promoted Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite and advanced him, and set his throne above all the rulers who were with him.

2 And all the king's servants who were at the king's gate would kneel and bow down to Haman, for so the king had commanded concerning him, but Mordecai would not kneel nor bow down.

3 The king's servants at the king's gate said to Mordecai, "Why do you transgress the king's commandment?"

4 And it happened to him day by day, but he did not listen to them; so they reported to Haman, to see whether Mordecai's words would stand, because he had told them that he was a Jew.

5 And Haman saw that Mordecai neither bowed nor prostrated himself before him, and Haman was filled with anger.

6 He disdained in his sight to lay hands on Mordecai alone, for they had made known to him the people of Mordecai; therefore Haman sought to destroy all the Jews throughout the whole kingdom of Ahasuerus, the people of Mordecai.

7 In the month, that is, the month Nisan, in the second year twelve of King Ahasuerus, Pur, that is, the lot, was cast before Haman from day to day and from month to month, the twelfth, that is, the month Adar.

8 And Haman said to king Ahasuerus, There is a certain folk, scattered abroad and dispersed among the peoples in all the provinces of your kingdom, whose laws are different from those of any people, and they do not keep the laws of the king, so that it is not to the king's advantage to tolerate them.

9 If it please the king, let it be recorded to destroy them, and ten thousand talents of silver I will weigh out by the hand of those who do the work, to bring to the treasury of the king.

10 The king removed his ring from his hand and gave it to Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite, enemy of the Jews.

11 The king said to Haman, The money is given to you, and the people, to do with them as you think best.

12 The scribes of the king were summoned in the first month, on the thirteenth day thereof, and there was written according to all that Haman had commanded the lieutenants of the king and the governors who were over each province and the rulers of each people and province, each province according to its writing and each people according to its language, written in the name of King Ahasuerus and sealed with the king's ring.

13 And send letters by the hand of couriers to all the king's provinces, to destroy, to kill, and to annihilate all the Jews, from children and up to the aged, the little ones and women, on one day, on the thirteenth day of the twelfth month, which is the month Adar, and to plunder their spoil.

14 Copy of the register to give law in every province and province, made known to all peoples, to be ready for this day.

15 The couriers went out quickly by the king's command, and the decree was issued in Shushan the palace; and the king and Haman sat down to drink, while the city of Shushan was perplexed.


## Chapter 4

1 Mordecai learned of all that had been done, and Mordecai tore his apparel and put on sackcloth and ashes; he went out into the midst of the city and cried out with a great and bitter cry.

2 He came as far as the entrance of the king's gate, because no one could enter the king's gate wearing sackcloth.

3 In every province and province, in every place where the word of the king and his decree reached, there was great mourning for the Jews, and fasting and weeping and lamentation; sackcloth and ashes were laid upon many.

4 Then came the damsels of Esther and her chamberlains and told her, and the queen was exceedingly afraid; and she sent garments to clothe Mordecai and to remove his sackcloth from him, but he did not accept.

5 Esther called Hatach, one of the king's chamberlains whom he had appointed to attend her, and charged him to go to Mordecai to learn what this meant and why it was.

6 Hatach went out to Mordecai, to the broad place of the city which is before the gate of the king.

7 Mordecai told him all that had happened to him and the sum of money that Haman had promised to pay into the king's treasury to destroy them.

8 And he gave him the copy of the writing of the decree that was given in Shushan to destroy them, to show to Esther and to tell her, and to charge her to go in to the king, to entreat his favour and to petition before him for her people.

9 Hatach came and told Esther the advice of Mordecai.

10 And Esther said to Hatach and gave him orders concerning Mordecai.

11 All the servants of the king and the people of the king's provinces know that any man or woman who comes to the king in the inner court without being summoned has one law: to be put to death, except the one to whom the king holds out the golden sceptre, and then he shall live; but I have not been summoned to come to the king for these thirty days.

12 They reported to Mordecai Esther's advice.

13 Mordecai told Esther to respond: "Do not imagine in your own mind to escape in the king's court from all the Jews."

14 But if you altogether hold your peace at this time, relief and deliverance will arise for the Jews from another place, and you and your father's house will perish; and who knows whether you have come to the kingdom for such a time as this?

15 And Esther said to return to Mordecai.

16 Go, gather all the Jews who are present in Shushan, and fast on my account; do not eat and do not drink for three days, night and day. I also, with my damsels, will fast in this way; then I will go to the king, though it is contrary to the commandment, and if I perish, I will have perished.

17 And Mordecai passed and did according to all which Esther had commanded him.


## Chapter 5

1 On the third day Esther put on her royal attire and stood in the inner court of the king’s house opposite the king’s house while the king sat on his royal throne in the royal house opposite the entrance of the house.

2 When the king saw Queen Esther standing in the court, she obtained favour in his eyes, and the king extended to Esther the golden sceptre that was in his hand; so Esther approached and touched the top of the sceptre.

3 And the king said to her, "What is it to you, Esther the queen? And what is your request, even to half of the kingdom, it shall be given to you."

4 Esther said, "If it pleases the king, let the king and Haman come today to the banquet that I have prepared for him."

5 The king said, "Hurry Haman to carry out Esther's request." So the king and Haman came to the banquet that Esther had prepared.

6 The king said to Esther at the banquet of wine, "What is your petition, and it will be given to you, and what is your request? Even to half of the kingdom, it will be done."

7 Then Esther answered and said, "My petition and my request.

8 If I have found favour in the eyes of the king, and if it pleases the king to grant my petition and to fulfill my request, let the king and Haman come to the banquet that I will prepare for them, and tomorrow I will do according to the word of the king.

9 On that day Haman went out joyful and with a glad heart, but when Haman saw Mordecai at the king's gate and he did not rise or move from before him, Haman was filled with anger against Mordecai.

10 Haman restrained himself and went to his home, and he sent and brought his friends and Zeresh his wife.

11 Haman recounted to them the glory of his riches, the abundance of his sons, and all that the king had done to honour him and how he had advanced him above the royal officials and the king’s servants.

12 And Haman said, "Indeed, Queen Esther did not bring with the king to the banquet that she made anyone except me, and also tomorrow I am called to her with the king."

13 And all this is nothing to me whenever I see Mordecai the Jew sitting in the gate of the king.

14 Then said Zeresh his wife and all his friends to him, Let a gallows fifty cubits high be made, and in the morning speak to the king, and let Mordecai be hanged upon it, and go in with the king to the banquet joyful. And the thing pleased before Haman, and he made the gallows.


## Chapter 6

1 That night the king’s sleep was disturbed, and he said to bring the book of records, the chronicles of the days; and they were read before the king.

2 And it was found written that Mordecai had reported concerning Bigthana and Teresh, two chamberlains of the king, who guarded the threshold and had sought to lay hands on King Ahasuerus.

3 The king said, What honour and dignity has been done to Mordecai for this? And the king's servants, his attendants, said, Nothing has been done with him.

4 The king said, "Who is in the court?" and Haman entered the outer court of the king's palace to speak to the king about hanging Mordecai on the gallows he had prepared for him.

5 The king's servants said to him, "Behold, Haman is standing in the court." And the king said, "Let him come in."

6 Haman came in, and the king said to him, "What should be done for the man whom the king delights to honour?" Haman said in his heart, "To whom would the king delight to show honour more than me?"

7 Then Haman said to the king, "There is a certain man whom the king desires to honour."

8 They shall bring a royal garment with which the king was clothed, and a horse on which the king rode, and upon whose head a royal crown was set.

9 Let the apparel and the horse be given into the hand of one of the king's most noble princes, and let them array the man whom the king delights to honour, and cause him to ride on the horse in the city's broad place, and let them proclaim before him, Thus shall be done to the man whom the king delights to honour.

10 The king said to Haman, "Quickly, take the apparel and the horse as you have spoken, and do so for Mordecai the Jew who sits at the king's gate; do not fail one word from all that you have spoken."

11 Haman took the apparel and the horse, clothed Mordecai with it, and set him on horseback in the broad place of the city; and he proclaimed before him, "Thus shall be done to the man whom the king delights to honour."

12 Then Mordecai returned to the king's gate, but Haman was driven to his house mourning and with covered head.

13 Haman told Zeresh his wife and all his friends all that had happened to him, and his wise men and Zeresh his wife said to him, “If Mordecai, from the seed of the Jews, before whom you have begun to fall, is of the seed of the Jews, you will not be able to prevail against him, but will surely fall before him.”

14 While they were still speaking with him, the king's chamberlains arrived and hurried to bring Haman to the banquet that Esther had prepared.


## Chapter 7

1 The king and Haman came to drink with Queen Esther.

2 The king said to Esther, "Even on the second day, at the wine banquet, what is your petition, Queen Esther? It shall be given you. And what is your request? Even to half of the kingdom, it shall be done."

3 And Esther the queen answered and said, If I have found favour in your eyes, O king, and if it pleases the king, let my life be granted me by my petition, and my people by my request.

4 For we have been sold, I and my folk, to be destroyed, to be slain, and to be annihilated; but if we had been sold into bondage and into handmaidens, I would have remained silent, for the adversary is not worthy of the damage to the king.

5 And King Ahasuerus said, and spoke to Queen Esther, "Who is this, and where is he, who has filled his mind to do so?"

6 Esther said, "A certain adversary and enemy, this evil Haman," and Haman was afraid because of the presence of the king and the queen.

7 The king rose in his fury from the wine banquet to the garden of the palace, and Haman remained standing to beg for his life from Queen Esther, for he saw that evil had come upon him from the king.

8 The king returned from the garden of the palace to the house of the wine drinking, and Haman had fallen on the bed where Esther was; and the king said, “Will he even assault the queen with me in the house?” The word went out of the mouth of the king, and Haman’s face was covered.

9 Harbonah, one of the chamberlains before the king, said, "Also, the gallows that Haman made for Mordecai, who spoke good concerning the king, stands at the house of Haman, fifty cubits high." And the king said, "Hang him on it."

10 And they hanged Haman on the gallows that he had prepared for Mordecai, and the king's wrath subsided.


## Chapter 8

1 On that day King Ahasuerus gave to Queen Esther the house of Haman the adversary of the Jews and Mordecai came before the king for Esther had told what he was to her.

2 The king removed the ring that he had taken from Haman and gave it to Mordecai, and Esther set Mordecai over the house of Haman.

3 And Esther added and spoke before the king, and fell before his feet, and wept and entreated him to avert the evil of Haman the Agagite and his device that he devised against the Jews.

4 Then the king extended the golden sceptre to Esther, and Esther rose and stood before the king.

5 And she said, If it please the king, and if I have found favour in his sight, and if the matter seems right before the king, and if I am pleasing in his eyes, let it be written to reverse the letters of Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite, which he wrote to destroy the Jews who are in all the provinces of the king.

6 How can I bear to see the adversity that will find my people, and how can I bear to see the destruction of my kindred?

7 The king Ahasuerus said to Queen Esther and to Mordecai the Jew: Behold, the house of Haman I have given to Esther, and him they have hanged on the gallows because he laid his hand on the Jews.

8 And you, write concerning the Jews as seems best to you in the name of the king and seal with the ring of the king, for a writing which is written in the name of the king and sealed with the ring of the king cannot be revoked.

9 At that time, the king's scribes were summoned in the third month, which is the month of Sivan, on the twenty-third day thereof; and it was written according to all that Mordecai commanded unto the Jews and unto the lieutenants and governors and rulers of the provinces from India even unto Cush, one hundred twenty-seven provinces, each province according to its writing and each people according to their language, and unto the Jews according to their writing and according to their language.

10 And he wrote in the name of King Ahasuerus, and sealed with the king's ring, and sent letters by the hand of the couriers, riding on the swift horses, the mules, the dromedaries.

11 which the king had granted the Jews in every city and town to assemble and stand for their lives, to destroy, to kill, and to annihilate all the forces of the people and province that were hostile to them, their children and women, and to plunder their spoil.

12 On one day, in all the provinces of King Ahasuerus, on the thirteenth day of the twelfth month, that is, the month Adar.

13 Copy of the writing, to give the decree in every province, published to all peoples, and that the Jews should be ready on that day to avenge themselves on their enemies.

14 The couriers riding on swift horses went out hurriedly and pressed forward because of the king's commandment, and the decree was given in Shushan the palace.

15 And Mordecai went forth from the presence of the king in royal apparel of blue and white, with a great crown of gold, and a garment of fine linen and purple; and the city Shushan shouted and rejoiced.

16 The Jew. had light and joy and rejoicing and honour.

17 In every province and in every city, wherever the word of the king and his law reached, there was gladness and joy for the Jews, a banquet and a festive day, and many of the peoples of the land became Jews, because the fear of the Jews had fallen upon them.


## Chapter 9

1 In the twelfth month, which is the month Adar, on the thirteenth day thereof, when the word of the king and his law had come to be executed, on the day that the enemies of the Jews had expected to gain power over them, and it was reversed: that the Jews themselves should gain power over their haters.

2 The Jews gathered in their cities throughout all the provinces of King Ahasuerus to attack those who sought their adversity, and no man stood against them because the dread of them had fallen upon all the peoples.

3 And all the governors of the provinces and the lieutenants and the captains and the officers who served the king showed respect for the Jews, for the dread of Mordecai had fallen upon them.

4 For Mordecai was great in the king's court, and his fame spread throughout all the provinces, for the man Mordecai continued to grow in greatness.

5 The Jews struck all their enemies with the sword, slaughter, and destruction, and did to their adversaries as they pleased.

6 And in Shushan the palace the Jews killed and destroyed five hundred men.

7 and Parshandatha and Dalphon and Aspatha

8 And Poratha and Adalia and Aridatha.

9 And Parmasta, and Arisai, and Aridai, and Vajezatha.

10 Ten sons of Haman son of Hammedatha, the enemy of the Jew, they struck, and in the spoil they did not stretch out their hand.

11 On that day came the number of those slain in Shushan the palace before the king.

12 The king said to Queen Esther in Shushan the palace: "The Jews have killed and destroyed five hundred men and the ten sons of Haman; what have they done in the rest of the king's provinces? And what is your petition, that it may be granted to you? Or what further request will you make, that it may be done?"

13 Then Esther said, If it pleases the king, let it also be granted to the Jews who are in Shushan to act according to the decree of today tomorrow, and let the ten sons of Haman be hanged on the tree.

14 The king said to do so, and a decree was issued in Shushan, and the ten sons of Haman were hanged.

15 The Jews who were in Shushan gathered also on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and killed three hundred men in Shushan, but they did not stretch out their hands to the spoil.

16 The rest of the Jews who were in the provinces of the king assembled and stood for their lives, finding rest from their enemies, and killed seventy-five thousand of their haters, but they did not stretch their hands to the spoil.

17 On the thirteenth day of the month Adar, and rest on the fourteenth thereof, and make it a day of feasting and joy.

18 But the Jews who were in Shushan assembled on the thirteenth day and on the fourteenth day of it, and on the fifteenth day of it they rested, and made it a day of feasting and gladness.

19 Therefore the Jews who dwell in the unwalled towns make the fourteenth day of the month of Adar a day of joy and feasting, a good day, and of sending portions one to another.

20 Mordecai wrote down these acts and sent letters to all the Jews in every province of King Ahasuerus, both the near and the far.

21 to observe upon them to be keeping the day, fourteenth of the month Adar, and the day, fifteen of it, each year and year.

22 as the days on which the Jews had rest from their enemies, and the month which was turned to them from sorrow to joy and from mourning to a day, to make them days of feasting and joy and sending portions each man to his neighbour and gifts to the poor.

23 And the Jews accepted what they had begun to do and what Mordecai had written to them.

24 For Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite, enemy of all the Jews, plotted against the Jews to destroy them and cast Pur, that is, the lot, to crush and destroy them.

25 When she came before the king, he said: "Let his evil device that he devised against the Jews fall on his own head, and let him and his sons be hanged on the gallows."

26 Therefore they called these days Purim, on account of the name Pur; therefore, on account of all the words of this letter, and what they saw on this account, and what came upon them.

27 The Jews established for themselves, their descendants, and all who joined them, that they would not fail to observe these two days annually according to their writing and according to their time, every year and year.

28 And these days are remembered and observed in every generation, generation, family, family, province, province, and city, city, and the days of Purim shall not pass from among the Jews, nor shall their remembrance cease from their seed.

29 Queen Esther daughter of Abihail and Mordecai the Jew wrote with all authority to confirm this second letter of Purim.

30 And he sent letters to all the Jews, to the one hundred twenty-seven provinces of the empire of Ahasuerus, words of peace and truth.

31 To keep the days of Purim in their seasons, as Mordecai the Jew and Esther the queen established, and as they established for themselves and for their seed, the words of their fasting and their cry.

32 And the commandment of Esther confirmed the words of this Purim, and it was written in the book.


## Chapter 10

1 The king imposed a levy on the land and the islands of the sea.

2 And all the acts of his authority and his might, and the declaration of the greatness of Mordecai, whom the king honored—behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Media and Persia.

3 For Mordecai the Jew was next to King Ahasuerus and great among the Jews and acceptable to the multitude of his brothers, seeking good for his people and speaking peace to all his seed.


# JOB

## Chapter 1

1 There was a certain man in the land of Uz whose name was Job, and that man was blameless and upright, one who feared God and turned away from evil.

2 And there were born to him seven sons and three daughters.

3 His flock was seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred she-asses, and very great household store; and the man was exceedingly great among all the people of the east.

4 And his sons would go and hold a feast each in his own house on his day, and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.

5 When the days of the banquet were completed, Job would send and consecrate them, rising early in the morning to offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all, for Job said, “Perhaps my children have sinned and blessed God in their hearts.” Thus Job did continually.

6 And it came to pass on the day, that the sons of the gods came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came among them.

7 And the LORD said to the adversary, From where have you come? And the adversary answered the LORD and said, From roaming about in the land and walking around in it.

8 And the LORD said to the adversary, Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the land, a man of integrity and upright, fearing God and turning from evil?

9 The adversary answered the LORD and said, "Does Job fear God for nothing?"

10 Have you not made a hedge around him, his household, and all that is his, on every side? The work of his hands you have blessed, and his possessions have greatly increased in the land?

11 But now, please stretch out your hand and strike all that he has, if indeed he will not curse you to your face.

12 The LORD said to the adversary, "Behold, all that he has is in your power; only upon him do not lay your hand." Then the adversary went out from the presence of the LORD.

13 One day his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their eldest brother.

14 An ambassador came to Job and said, "The beeve were plowing and the she asses were grazing beside them."

15 And Sheba fell upon them and took them, and the lads they struck by the mouth of the sword, and I alone escaped to tell you.

16 While he was still speaking, another came and said, "A fiery divine fire fell from the heavens and burned up the sheep and the young men, consuming them; I alone escaped to tell you."

17 While he was still speaking, another came and said, “The Chaldeans formed three bands and swept down on the camels and carried them off, and the servants they struck with the sword; I alone have escaped to tell you.”

18 Until now he was speaking, and behold, one came and said, Your sons and your daughters are eating and drinking wine in the house of their brother the firstborn.

19 And behold, a great wind came from beyond the wilderness and struck the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men and they died, and I alone escaped to tell you.

20 Job arose and tore his robe, shaved his head, fell to the ground, and prostrated himself.

21 I was naked as I came from my mother's womb, and naked shall I return there; the LORD gave, and the LORD has taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.

22 Yet in all this Job did not sin nor utter folly against God.


## Chapter 2

1 One day the sons of the gods came to present themselves before the LORD, and the Satan also came among them to present himself before the LORD.

2 And the LORD said to the adversary, From where have you come? And the adversary answered the LORD and said, From going about in the land and from walking in it.

3 And the LORD said to the adversary, Have you considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a man of integrity, who is upright, one who fears God and turns from evil, and still he holds fast to his integrity, though you incited me against him to destroy him without cause?

4 Then the adversary answered the LORD and said, Skin for skin, and all that a man has he will give for his life.

5 But now, please stretch out your hand and strike his bones and his flesh; if not, to your face he will surely bless you.

6 The LORD said to the adversary, "Behold, he is in your hand, but you must preserve his life."

7 Then the adversary went forth from the presence of the LORD and struck Job with a malignant boil from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.

8 And he took for himself a pot sherd with which to scrape himself, and he sat among the ashes.

9 Then his wife said to him, "Are you still holding to your integrity? Curse God and die!"

10 He said to her, "You speak as one of the senseless women speaks; shall we accept good from God, and not accept evil?" In all this, Job did not sin with his speech.

11 And Job's three companions heard of all this adversity that had come upon him, and each man came from his place—Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite—so they agreed together to come and mourn with him and comfort him.

12 They lifted up their eyes from afar and did not recognize him; they raised their voices and wept; each man tore his robe and threw dust into the air upon their heads.

13 So they sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and no one spoke to him a word, for they saw that the grief was very great.


## Chapter 3

1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed his day.

2 Job answered and said:

3 May the day of my birth perish, and the night that said, "A man has been born."

4 That age will be darkness; God will not seek it from on high, nor will light shine upon it.

5 Darkness and the shadow of death shall dwell upon him; a cloud shall dwell upon it; the blackness of the day shall terrify it.

6 That night he shall be seized by darkness; let him not join the days of the year, nor enter by count of months.

7 Behold, that night shall be barren; let no singing come into it.

8 Let those who curse the age rouse Leviathan against him.

9 May the stars of the twilight grow dark; let them wait for light but find none, nor see the eyelids of the dawn.

10 For I did not close the doors of my belly, nor did I hide affliction from my eyes.

11 Why did I not die from the womb, come forth from the belly and perish?

12 Why have my knees preceded me, and what have my breasts, that I should nurse?

13 For now I will lie down and be quiet; I will sleep, then I will be at rest.

14 With kings and counselors of the land who built desolate places for themselves.

15 Or with captains whose gold is abundant, whose houses are filled with silver?

16 Or an untimely birth, I shall not be, like babes who do not see light.

17 There the wicked cease from troubling, and there the weary in strength find rest.

18 Together we have rested as prisoners; we did not heed the voice of the taskmaster.

19 There the small and the great are together, and a servant is free from his master.

20 Why does he give light to the toiler and life to the bitter of soul?

21 Those who wait for death, but it does not come, and they dig for it more than for hidden treasures.

22 Those who rejoice with gladness will rejoice greatly when they find a grave.

23 Every one whose way is hidden, and God has covered him.

24 Before my food my sighing comes, and my roaring pours out like water.

25 For dread I feared, and it came upon me, and that which I feared has come upon me.

26 I did not rest, nor was I quiet, nor did I cease, yet trouble came.


## Chapter 4

1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:

2 Will an act trouble you? Who can hold back words?

3 Behold, you have admonished many and strengthened feeble hands.

4 He who stumbles, your word will raise; and those who kneel, it will strengthen.

5 For now you will come to him and grow weary, you will reach him and be dismayed.

6 Is not your fear of him your confidence, your hope, and the integrity of your ways?
