7 Remember now, who ever perished being innocent? And where were the upright cut off?

8 As I have seen those who plow affliction and sow iniquity, they will reap it.

9 By the blast of God they perish, and by the spirit of his anger they are consumed.

10 The roaring of a (young) lion and the voice of a (fierce) lion, and the teeth of (young) lions are broken.

11 An (old) lion perishes for lack of prey, and the whelps of a (great, old, stout) lion are scattered.

12 But to me a thing is stolen, and my ear has taken a little hint of it.

13 In opinions from visions of night, when deep sleep falls upon men.

14 Dread called me and trembling, and the multitude of my bones was terrified.

15 And a blast passes over my face; the hair of my body bristles.

16 He will stand and I will not recognize his appearance; an image before my eyes—silence and a voice I hear.

17 Can a mortal be righteous before God? Can a man be pure before his Maker?

18 Behold, in His bondmen He does not put trust, and in His messengers He charges folly.

19 Even the inhabitants of clay houses, whose foundation is in the dust, are crushed before the moth.

20 From morning to night they are struck down, without ceasing they perish forever.

21 Will not their abundant rest depart from them? They will die, and not by wisdom.


## Chapter 5

1 Call now—Is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?

2 For anger kills the foolish, and jealousy slays the simple.

3 I saw a fool taking root, and I cursed his pleasant place suddenly.

4 Their children shall be far from safety, and crushed in the gate, with none to deliver.

5 whose harvest the hungry eats and into thorns they take it, and the robber devours their strength

6 For affliction does not come from ashes, nor does trouble grow from the ground.

7 For a person is born to trouble, and sons of the spark lift him up to fly.

8 But I will seek God, and to God I will commit my cause.

9 Doing great things beyond finding out, wonders without number.

10 the one who gives rain upon the face of the land and sends water upon the face of the out-of-doors.

11 to set the lowly on high, and those who are in distress exalted in safety.

12 He frustrates the cunning of the crafty, and the hands of the wicked cannot achieve wisdom.

13 The wise are snared by their own cunning, and the counsel of the froward is overthrown.

14 By day they meet with darkness, and at night they grope at midday.

15 He saved the poor from the sword by their mouth and from the hand of the strong.

16 So the poor has hope, and iniquity shuts its mouth.

17 Behold, happy is the man whom God reproves; do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.

18 For he wounds, but he binds up; he shatters, but his hands heal.

19 In six adversities he will deliver you, and in seven no evil will touch you.

20 From famine He will deliver you from death, and in war from the hand of the sword.

21 Under the scourge the tongue will be silenced, and you will not fear destruction when it comes.

22 You shall laugh at desolation and famine, and shall not fear the beasts of the earth.

23 For with the stones of the field is your covenant, and the wild beasts of the field are at peace with you.

24 And you will know that your tent is at peace, and you will inspect your habitation and not err.

25 And you shall know that your seed shall be numerous, and your offspring like the grass of the earth.

26 She shall come to the grave in full age, like a shock of corn in its season.

27 Behold, this we have searched out; so it is; hear it, and you, know it for yourself.


## Chapter 6

1 Job answered and said:

2 If only my anger and my calamity could be weighed together in balances.

3 Now from the sand of the seas it is heavier, therefore my words are despised.

4 For the arrows of the Almighty are with me, their poison drinks my spirit; the terrors of God array against me.

5 Will the wild ass graze on (tender) grass, or will the bull feed on fodder?

6 Can corruption be eaten without salt, or is there taste in the white of an egg?

7 My soul refuses to touch them; they are like loathsome food to me.

8 Who will grant that my petition may come, and that God may grant my hope?

9 But if God should be willing and crush me, let him let loose his hand and cut me off!

10 And still my comfort is pain, and I would exult in pain though he does not spare; for I have not rejected the speech of the Holy One.

11 What is my strength, that I should wait? And what is my end, that I should prolong my soul?

12 If the strength of stones is my strength, if my body is bronze,

13 Is not my help in me, and has sound wisdom been driven from me?

14 A companion shows kindness, but one who fears the Almighty will abandon it.

15 My brothers have dealt treacherously like a brook, like the channels of streams they pass away.

16 Which are dark with ice, and over which the snow hides itself.

17 At the time their blossoms were scattered, in his heat they were driven from their place.

18 Their paths will be entangled, they will go up into waste and perish.

19 They looked at the paths of Tema, the companies of Sheba awaited them.

20 They are put to shame because they trusted; they came there and were disappointed.

21 For now you did not see the casting down, and you were afraid.

22 Was I indeed saying, "Give to me, and from your power offer bribes for my sake"?

23 Deliver me from the hand of the adversary, and from the hand of violent men rescue me.

24 Instruct me, and I will be silent; make me understand what I have done wrong.

25 How fair are the words of uprightness, and how convincing is the rebuke of you!

26 Will you speak what is said for the wind?

27 You will surely oppress the fatherless and crush your fellow.

28 And now, be pleased to look at me; surely I will not lie to your face.

29 Return, I beg you, let there be no iniquity; my justice is still in it.

30 Is there iniquity in my tongue? Does my mouth not discern calamity?


## Chapter 7

1 Is not man's appointed time upon earth a service, and are not his days like the days of a hired laborer?

2 As a servant longs for shade, and as a hireling awaits his wages.

3 So I have inherited for myself months of futility, and toilsome nights have been apportioned to me.

4 If I lie down, I say, "When will I arise? And I am filled with tossing to and fro until the dawning of the day."

5 Worm has clothed my flesh; clods of dust my skin; my skin is shriveled and loathsome.

6 My days are consumed faster than a weaver's shuttle and are spent without any hope.

7 Remember that my spirit is air, my life not to return—my eyes will not see good again.

8 Your eye will not spare me; Your eyes are upon me, and I am not.

9 The cloud has passed away and is gone; so he who goes down to the grave does not come up.

10 He will not return again to his home, nor will his place know him anymore.

11 I also will not keep my mouth from speaking in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

12 Am I the sea or a sea-monster, that you set a watch over me?

13 For I said, my bed will comfort me, my babbling will lift my couch.

14 and he terrified me in his dreams, from his visions he terrified me.

15 My soul has chosen strangling, death rather than my bones.

16 I loathe it; I shall not live alway: cease from me, for my days are vanity.

17 What is man, that you should magnify him, and that you should set your care upon him?

18 You visit him in the morning, you test him in moments.

19 How long will you not leave me alone? Will you not let me be until I swallow my spit?

20 I have sinned; what shall I do to you, O preserver of man? Why have you set me as a mark for you, that I should be a burden to myself?

21 What if you do not take away my transgression and pass over my iniquity, for now I shall lie down in the dust and you will seek me, but I shall not be?


## Chapter 8

1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:

2 How long will you keep speaking these things, with a mighty wind in the words of your mouth?

3 Will God pervert judgment, or will the Almighty pervert justice?

4 If your sons have sinned against him, he has sent them away in the power of their transgression.

5 If you seek God and entreat the Almighty,

6 If you are pure and upright, then now he will awaken for you and repay the comely dwelling of your righteousness.

7 And your beginning was small, but your end will greatly increase.

8 Indeed, inquire now of a former generation and establish for the search of their ancestors.

9 For yesterday we were and knew not, for the shadow of our days is upon the earth.

10 Will they not instruct you and speak to you, and from their hearts utter words?

11 Will the bul-rush grow tall without a fen? Will the flag grow without water?

12 While it is still in its greenness, it is not plucked; before any grass it withers.

13 So are the ways of all who forget God, and the hope of the godless will perish.

14 who makes folly his confidence and the spider’s house his trust.

15 He will lean on his house, but it will not stand; he will hold fast to it, but it will not endure.

16 It is moist before the sun, and from his garden his young twig will emerge.

17 On a heap its roots are entwined; a house of stones it beholds.

18 If he swallows him up from his place, then let him deny, I have not seen you.

19 Behold, he is the joy of his way, and from another's dust they will sprout.

20 Behold, God does not reject the blameless, nor does he uphold the wicked by hand.

21 until your mouth is filled with derision and your lips with shouting

22 May those who hate you be clothed with shame, and may the dwelling place of the wicked not be.


## Chapter 9

1 Job answered and said:

2 Indeed, I know that it is so; but how can a mortal man be in the right before God?

3 If he wishes to contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.

4 Who is wise of heart and strong of power? Who has hardened himself against him, and prospered?

5 Who removes mountains, and they do not know; who overturns them in his wrath.

6 He who shakes the land from its place, and its pillars tremble.

7 who says to the sun, “Do not shine,” and to the stars, “Be sealed up.”

8 Stretching out the heavens alone, and treading upon the heights of the sea.

9 He who made Arcturus, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the innermost chambers of the south.

10 Doing great things beyond finding out, and wondrous things beyond number.

11 Behold, he passes over me and I do not see; he goes by, and I do not perceive him.

12 Behold, if he snatches, who can turn him back? Who will say to him, "What are you doing?"

13 God does not turn back his anger; the helpers of proud strength have crouched.

14 Indeed, I will answer him; I will choose my words with him.

15 If I am righteous, I will not answer my judge; I will plead with him.

16 If I call and he answers me, I will not believe that he hears my voice.

17 who in storm will crush me, and without cause greatly multiplied my woundings.

18 He will not let me catch my breath, but fills me with bitterness.

19 If to power, behold, I am able; but if to judgment, who will appoint me a time?

20 If I justify my speech, he condemns me; I am blameless, yet he deems me crooked.

21 I am weary, I loathe my life.

22 One thing has occurred, therefore I said: He destroys the blameless and the wicked.

23 If a scourge kills suddenly, it mocks the trial of the innocent.

24 The land is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges. If not, then who is he?

25 And my days have passed away faster than a runner; they have fled without seeing good.

26 They passed by with swift ships, like an eagle swooping upon its prey.

27 If I say, "I will forget my babbling, I will forsake my presence and mourn,"

28 All my sorrows I have endured; I know that you will not acquit me.

29 I am wicked; why then is it altogether vain?

30 If I washed myself with snow and purified my hands in the purest of powders,

31 Then in the pit you will immerse me, and my garments will abhor me.

32 For no man like me can answer him; let us come together in judgment.

33 There is no one between us to mediate; let him lay his hand upon both of us.

34 May his rod turn away from me, and may his dread not overwhelm me.

35 I will speak and not fear him, for it is not so with me; I am with him.


## Chapter 10

1 My soul is weary of my life; leave me alone that I may speak of the bitterness of my soul.

2 I say to God, Do not condemn me; make me know on what account you contend with me.

3 Is it good for you to oppress, to despise the labour of your hands, and and to shine upon the counsel of the wicked?

4 Are your eyes the eyes of flesh, or do you see as a mortal man sees?

5 Are your days the days of a mortal man, or your years like the years of a man?

6 for you seek my fault and inquire after my sin.

7 According to your cunning, I do not rebel, and there is no one from your power who delivers.

8 Your hands have afflicted me and made me; together they have encircled me.

9 Remember now, you made me like clay, and to dust you will return me.

10 Would you not pour me out like milk and cause me to curdle like cheese?

11 Skin and flesh you clothe me, and with bones and sinews you weave me.

12 Life and lovingkindness you have done with me, and your oversight preserved my spirit.

13 But these things you have hidden in your heart; I know that this is with you.

14 If I have sinned, then you watch me, and from my fault you do not clear me.

15 If I have sinned, woe to me; and if I am righteous, woe to me, I will not lift my head; full of reproach and beholding my affliction.

16 You hunt me like a fierce lion, and again you work wonders against me.

17 Renew your witness against me, and multiply your anger against me; change and host are with me.

18 And why did you bring me out of the womb, to perish, and the eye not see me?

19 As I have not been in the belly, so I will not be; from the womb to the grave, indeed.

20 Are not my days few enough? I will grieve a little.

21 Before I go and do not return to the land of darkness and the shadow of death.

22 A land of darkness, like thick darkness and the shadow of death, without any order, and shining as thick darkness.


## Chapter 11

1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:

2 Abundance of words does not go unanswered, and will a man of speech be justified?

3 In your lie the dead will be silent, and you mocked, but there is no one to rebuke.

4 And she said, "Take my fair speech, for I was pure in your sight."

5 But who can make God speak and open his lips with you?

6 And he will declare to you the secrets of wisdom, for it is double the substance; and know that God has removed from your sin.

7 Can you find out God? Can you reach the perfection of the Almighty?

8 How can you fathom the excellency of the heavens? How can you know the depth of the pit?

9 Its measure is longer than the land, and its width is greater than the sea.

10 If he passes by and seizes, and gathers, who will make him return?

11 For he knows men of falsehood, and sees iniquity, yet does not understand.

12 And a certain fool will be esteemed, and a wild donkey colt will be born a man.

13 If you have set your mind and stretched out your hand to him.

14 If there is iniquity in your hand, put it far away, and do not let unrighteousness dwell in your tents.

15 Then you will lift up your face without blemish, and you will be firm and not fear.

16 For you forget grievance; you remember it as waters that pass by.

17 Your life will rise brighter than midday; though it grows dim, it will be like the morning.

18 And you will be secure, because there is hope; yes, you will dig about you and lie down in safety.

19 You lay down without fear, and many will entreat your favor.

20 The eyes of the wicked will fail, and refuge will perish from them, and their hope is the giving up of the soul.


## Chapter 12

1 Job answered and said:

2 Indeed, you are folk, and with you wisdom will die.

3 Even I have a heart like you; I do not faint from you, and to whom is there none like these?

4 A derision to his fellow I will be, calling to God and He answers him; a derision the righteous innocent.

5 A brand of contempt for the thought of the proud, fixed for times of journeying.

6 The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure, who carry their god in their hand.

7 But ask now the beasts and they shall teach you, and the fowl of the air and they shall tell you.

8 Or speak to the land, and it will tell you, and the fish of the sea will relate to you.

9 Who does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?

10 In whose hand is the life of all the living, and the breath of every human being.

11 Does not the ear test words, and the palate taste its food?

12 With the aged is wisdom, and in length of days understanding.

13 With him are wisdom and power; to him are counsel and understanding.

14 Indeed, though he hews it, it will not be built; he shuts up against a certain man, and it will not be opened.

15 Behold, he withholds the waters and they dry up; he sends them forth and they overturn the earth.

16 With him are strength and sound wisdom; he who errs and causes error.

17 He leads counselors away plundered, and judges he makes to stagger.

18 He opens the chastisement of kings, and girds their loins with a girdle.

19 He leads astray the chief rulers and causes the trusted ones to stumble.

20 He removes the speech of the trusty and takes away the discernment of the elders.

21 He pours contempt on nobles and weakens the strength of the mighty.

22 Revealing deep things from darkness, and bringing the shadow of death to light.

23 He increases the nations and destroys them; He spreads out the nations and leads them back.

24 Removing care from the leaders of the people of the land, and He caused them to wander in a waste not way.

25 They grope in darkness and not light, and he makes them stagger like a drunkard.


## Chapter 13

1 Behold, mine eye hath seen all, mine ear hath heard and considered it.

2 According to your cunning I also have known; not do I fall from you.

3 But I will speak to the Almighty, and I will argue my case to God.

4 But you are forgers of lies; you are all worthless physicians.

5 Who will grant that you would be silent, and it would be wisdom for you?

6 Hear now my argument, and listen to the pleadings of my lips.

7 Will you speak iniquity to God, and speak deceit to him?

8 Will you accept his face? Will you contend with God?

9 Is it right that he should search you out, or will you deceive him as one deceives a man?

10 He will surely rebuke you if you show partiality in secret.

11 Will not his dignity reject you, and will not his dread fall upon you?

12 Your memorials are parables of ashes, in comparison to your backs of clay.

13 Be silent toward me, that I may speak; then let come upon me what may.

14 Why should I take my body in my teeth, and put my soul in the palm of my hand?

15 Behold, he will kill me; I will be impatient; but yet my ways toward his face I will prove.

16 He also is my salvation, for the impious shall not come before his presence.

17 Hear, indeed, my word, and give ear to my declaration.

18 Behold now I have ordered judgment; I know that I am righteous.

19 Who will contend with me? Now I will be silent and perish.

20 Only two things do not do with me, then from your presence I will not hide.

21 Remove your hand from upon me, and do not let your dread terrify me.

22 Call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and respond to me.

23 How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my transgression and sin.

24 Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?

25 You drive away the driven branch and pursue the dry stubble.

26 For you write against me bitter things and reproach me with the sins of my youth.

27 And you will place my feet in the stocks, and guard all my paths; you will press upon the roots of my feet.

28 And he wears out like rottenness; like a garment the moth consumes him.


## Chapter 14

1 Man born of woman, short of days and full of trouble.

2 Like a flower he comes forth and withers; he flees like a shadow and does not stand.

3 Even so, you have opened your eyes upon him, and me you will bring into judgment with you.

4 Who can bring a clean thing from the unclean? Not one.

5 If the number of his months is with you, their sum fixed, and you have determined his limit that he cannot pass,

6 Look away from him and let him cease, until he fulfills his day like a hired worker.

7 For there is hope for the tree, if it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its young twig will not cease.

8 If its root grows old in the ground, its stem will die in the dust.

9 By the scent of water it flourishes, and makes harvest like a plant.

10 And every one dies and weakens, and a man expires—where is he?

11 Then the water from the sea dried up, and the river was parched and dried out.

12 And another will lie down and not rise again until the heavens are no more; they will not awaken nor stir from their sleep.

13 Who will grant me to be hidden in the grave until Your anger turns from me, You would appoint me a set time and remember me?

14 If a man die, shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait, until my change should come.

15 You will call, and I will answer you; I will long for the work of your hands.

16 For now my steps are numbered; you do not keep watch over my sin.

17 Sealed in a bag my transgression, and thou dost fix attention upon my sin.

18 But a hill will fall and a rock will be moved from its place.

19 Stones have been worn away by waters; its offspring, the dust of the earth, is swept away; and the hope of mankind you have destroyed.

20 You will seize him for perpetuity and go on; changing his countenance, you will dismiss him.

21 Their children will be honored, but he will not know it; they will be afflicted, yet he will not understand it.

22 Only his body will suffer pain, and his soul will mourn over him.


## Chapter 15

1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said.

2 The wise one answers with the knowledge of the spirit, and fills his belly with the east wind.

3 Should he argue with words that do no good, or with speeches with which he can accomplish nothing?

4 You too break off fear and withdraw prayer before God.

5 For your mouth teaches your iniquity, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.

6 Your own mouth will condemn you, and not I; your own lips will testify against you.

7 The first man was born, and before the hills you were in travail.

8 You will hear counsel from God; wisdom will be granted to you.

9 What have you known that we do not know? You understand, but it is not with us.

10 The gray-headed and the aged are among us, older in days than your father.

11 Are God's consolations too few for you, and a word spoken gently with you?

12 What will your heart care for, and what will your eyes look upon?

13 If you return to God your spirit and remove from your mouth words,

14 What is a mortal man that he should be righteous, or one born of woman that he should be pure?

15 Behold, he puts no trust in his holy ones, and the heavens are not pure in his sight.

16 Even the man who drinks iniquity like water is abhorred and loathed.

17 I will declare to you; this I have seen, and I will recount.

18 which the wise will declare, and they did not hide from their forefathers.

19 To them alone was the land given, and no stranger passed among them.

20 All the days of the wicked he is in anguish, and the number of years stored up for the tyrant.

21 A sound of terror in his ears; in times of peace, the destroyer will come upon him.

22 He will not believe in returning from darkness; and he is marked for the sword.

23 He wanders for bread—where is it? He knows that a day of darkness is in his hand.

24 Distress and anguish will seize him; they will overpower him like a king ready for battle.

25 For he stretched out his hand toward God, and against the Almighty he grew strong.

26 He runs at him with outstretched neck, through the thick of the shields upon his body.

27 For he has covered his face with his fat and made collops over his loins.

28 And he dwelt in desolate cities, houses which no one inhabited, which had been prepared for waves.

29 He will not be rich, nor will his wealth endure, nor shall his strength arise; nor shall his perfection incline toward the land.

30 He will not turn from darkness; the flame will dry up his young shoot, and by the breath of his mouth he will go away.

31 Let not the wanderer trust, for his change will be false.

32 Before his day it shall be fulfilled, and his branch shall not be green.

33 He will wither like a vine its unripe grape, and he will cast off like an olive its flower.

34 For the assembly of the hypocrite is destroyed, and fire has consumed the tents of bribery.

35 They conceive mischief and bring forth iniquity, and their belly prepares deceit.


## Chapter 16

1 Job answered and said:

2 I have heard such things many times, all your consolations are grievous.

3 Shall windy words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?

4 Even I will speak to you as you do; if only your life were in place of my life, I could heap up words against you and shake my head at you.

5 I will strengthen you according to my mouth, and the moving of my lips will restrain.

6 If I speak, my grief will not be relieved; if I am silent, what relief will I have?

7 Yet now, ease me; have you destroyed all my assembly?

8 And he gathered leanness as a witness against me; he rises up to accuse me to my face.

9 His anger tears me, and he hates me; he gnashes at me with his teeth; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.

10 They opened their mouths against me in reproach; they struck my cheeks; together they will be filled.

11 God will deliver me to the ungodly, and by the hand of the wicked He will cast me away.

12 I was secure, but he shattered me; he seized my back and crushed me; he set me up as a target.

13 Archer surrounds me, pierces my kidneys, and does not pity; he pours out my gall upon the ground.

14 A breach breaks forth against a breach, trampling me like a mighty man.

15 I have torn sackcloth upon my skin, and in ashes I have brought low my horn.

16 My face is red with weeping, and on my eyelids is deep darkness,

17 Because of no violence in my hands and my prayer is pure.

18 Land, do not cover my blood, and let there be no place for my cry.

19 Even now, behold, my witness is in the heavens, and my record is on high.

20 My companions and friends are against God; my eye pours out tears to God.

21 And he will reprove every one with God, and the son of man with his fellow.

22 For numbered years are few and fleeting, and the way I shall not return, I will go.


## Chapter 17

1 My spirit is broken, my days are cut off, graves are mine.

2 If the mocker had not been with me, at their adversity my eye would have grieved.

3 Put me under pledge with you; who is he that will enter into my surety?

4 For you have hidden their heart from understanding; therefore you will not exalt them.

5 May he recount flattery for a portion among friends, and the eyes of his sons grow dim.

6 And he will set me to rule over folk, and I will become a byword before their faces.

7 My eyes grew weary from grief and my members were all like a shadow.

8 The upright will be appalled at this, and the innocent will rise up against the hypocrite.

9 And the righteous one holds to his way, and the one pure of hands adds strength.

10 But indeed, you shall all return and come now, yet I shall not find among you a wise man.

11 My days have passed; my heinous crimes are cut off; my possession is the anguish of my heart.

12 Night shall appoint light to age, for light is near before darkness.

13 If I expect the grave my dungeon, in darkness I have spread my bed.

14 To the pit I cried, You are my father; to the worm, My mother and my sister.

15 But where is my hope? And who will uphold my hope?

16 Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?


## Chapter 18

1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:

2 How long will you set ends to speaking? Will you ever understand, and then speak?

3 Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we considered stupid in your sight?

4 The beast tears apart its prey in its fury—shall the earth be forsaken for your sake, and shall the rock be removed from its place?

5 Even the light of the wicked will be put out, and the spark of his fire will not glow.

6 Darkness covers his tent, and his lamp will be extinguished above him.

7 His steps are straitened in his might, and his own counsel casts him down.

8 For a net has been spread at his feet, and he will walk upon a snare.

9 Holding by the heel a snare, he seizes upon him a robber.

10 His band is hidden in the land, and his trap upon the path.

11 About him were troubles of terror, and scatter him at his feet, and harry him at his heels.

12 Let his strength be hungry and destruction ready at his side.

13 He shall eat the strength of his skin, he shall eat his own strength, the firstborn of death.

14 May his confidence be cut off from his covering, and may it drive him to a king of terror.

15 He will dwell in his tent where no man is; brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.

16 Under its root it dries up, and above it its harvest is cut off.

17 His memorial is perished from the land, and he has no name abroad.

18 They will drive him from light into darkness, and from the world they will banish him.

19 No son or nephew among his people, and no survivor among his dwellings.

20 On their day the later ones are desolate and the earlier ones are seized by horror.

21 Only the dwellings of the unjust, and this place which knew no God.


## Chapter 19

1 Job answered and said:

2 How long will my soul be grieved, and how long will you oppress me with words?

3 This ten times you shall make me to tremble; you shall not be ashamed, you shall mock at me.

4 Indeed, I have surely erred; you charge me with my error.

5 If indeed you magnify yourselves against me, and reproach me with my disgrace.

6 Know then that God has brought me down, and his net he has spread around me.

7 Behold, I cry out against violence, but I am not answered; I shout, but there is no justice.

8 My path he has blocked and I cannot pass, and on my paths darkness he sets.

9 My glory he stripped from me, and removed the crown of my head.

10 They surrounded me on every side, and I went; and he removed my hope like a tree.

11 His anger burned against me, and he counted me as one of his adversaries.

12 Together his troops come and turn aside upon their journey against me; they encamp round about my tent.

13 My kinsmen have distanced me and my acquaintances are altogether estranged from me.

14 My close ones have ceased, and my acquaintances have forgotten me.

15 My household and my female servants regard me as a stranger; I am a foreigner in their eyes.

16 I called to my servant, but he did not answer; according to my word I will entreat for him.

17 My spirit is estranged from my wife, and my compassion is for the children of my womb.

18 Even young children despised me; I stood up, and they spoke against me.

19 All my intimate friends despise me, and he whom I loved has turned against me.

20 In my skin and in my flesh my bone clung, and I escaped by the hide of my tooth.

21 Be gracious, be gracious to me, my friends, for the hand of God has struck me.

22 Why do you pursue me like God, and not be satisfied with my flesh?

23 Who will grant that my words be written, who will grant that they be inscribed in a scroll?

24 With an iron pen and lead, they shall be engraved forever in the rock.

25 But I knew my Redeemer is alive, and lastly upon the dust he will arise.

26 After my skin this will be destroyed, and from my body I will see God.

27 which I myself see, and my eyes behold, and not a stranger: my kidneys are consumed within my bosom.

28 If you say, "Why are we pursued?" a root of the matter will be found in me.

29 Flee from the sword, for the anger of iniquities is a sword, so that you may know the Almighty.


## Chapter 20

1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:

2 Therefore my opinion returns to me, and because of my awareness of me:

3 I will hear the chastening of my shame, and the spirit of my understanding answers me.

4 Did you know from eternity, from old, when man was set over the earth?

5 For the singing of the wicked is from near, and the joy of the hypocrite is but for a moment.

6 If his excellency ascends to the heavens, his head reaches the thick cloud.

7 Like dung he shall perish forever; those who see him will say, Where is he?

8 He flies away like a dream and is not found; he is chased away like a vision of the night.

9 The eye that saw him will see him no more, nor will his place behold him any longer.

10 His children will satisfy the afflicted, and his hands will return his evil.

11 His bones are filled with strength, and with him upon dust it will lie.

12 If he draws evil into his mouth, he will conceal it under his tongue.

13 He will have mercy on her and will not forsake her, and he will protect her in the midst of his mouth.

14 His bread turns to gall in his belly, the venom of adders within him.

15 The mighty one swallowed riches, but God will make him vomit it up from his belly.

16 He will suck the poison of the adder; he will be killed by the tongue of the viper.

17 He will not look upon the rivers, the streams flowing with honey and curds.

18 He does not let that which he laboured for be swallowed up; according to the riches of his recompense, he does not oppress.

19 For he crushes and forsakes the poor, he seizes a house and does not build it.

20 For he knows no peace within his belly; in his luxury he cannot escape.

21 There is no remnant left to eat it; therefore its goodness will not go well.

22 At the fullness of strokes he has sufficiency; every hand of toil will come upon him.

23 To fill his belly to the full, God will send his fierce anger upon him, and may he rain down his anger upon them while they are eating.

24 He shall flee from the iron weapon; a bronze bow shall strike him.

25 He drew it out from his body, and the glittering sword went forth from his gall; upon him are terrors.

26 All darkness is stored up for his treasures; a fire not blown shall devour him; it shall consume what remains in his tent.

27 The heavens expose his iniquity, and the earth rises up against him.

28 His household’s increase shall be carried away, the stream on the day of his anger.

29 This is the portion of a low person from God, and the inheritance appointed unto him from the Mighty One.


## Chapter 21

1 Job answered and said:

2 Hear, yes, hear my word, and let this be your consolation.

3 Lift me up, and I will speak; after my speaking, scoff.

4 Am I not a man of low degree, my complaint? And why should my spirit not be impatient?

5 Turn to me and be ashamed, and put a hand upon the mouth.

6 But if I remember, I am terrified, and my body seizes with horror.

7 Why do the wicked live, advance, and grow in power?

8 Their seed is established before them, and their offspring in their sight.

9 Their houses are safe from fear, and the rod of God is not upon them.

10 His bull has crossed over and not refused; his cow delivers and does not miscarry.

11 They shall send out their young children like sheep, and their boys shall dance.

12 Lift up the tabret and the harp, and rejoice with the sound of the organ.

13 They spend their days in prosperity, and in a moment they go down to Sheol.

14 They said to God, "Depart from us, and knowledge of your ways we do not desire."

15 What profit is the Almighty to us, that we should serve Him, and what profit have we if we pray to Him?

16 Indeed, not in their power is their goodness; the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

17 How often will the lamp of the wicked know you? And calamity will come upon them; that God distributes pains in his anger?

18 Let them be like chaff before the wind, and like chaff the storm carries away.

19 God will store up affliction for His sons; He will repay it to them, and they will understand.

20 His eyes will see his own destruction, and from the wrath of the Almighty he will drink.

21 For what delight has he in his house after him, and the number of his months is cut off?

22 Shall God be taught knowledge, when He judges the lofty?

23 This one will die in the very strength of his integrity, his whole being at ease and secure.

24 His breasts are full of milk, and the marrow of his bones is refreshed.

25 And this one will die in a bitter appetite, and he has never tasted of good.

26 Together upon the dust they will lie down, and a worm will cover them.

27 Indeed, I know your cunning and your wicked devices against me.

28 When you say, "Where is the noble's court?" and "Where is the tent, the dwelling place of the wicked?"

29 Have you not asked those who travel along the way, and will you not recognize their signs?

30 For a day of calamity one is hidden from evil; on a day of wrath they are carried away.

31 Who can declare before his face his way? And who has acted that he should repay him?

32 And he is brought to his burying place, and upon his tomb he keeps watch.

33 His clods are the clods of a stream, and after him every person will be drawn; but before him there is no number.

34 How can your vain answers comfort me, when your answers remain full of faithlessness?


## Chapter 22

1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:

2 Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.

3 Is it pleasing to the Almighty that you are righteous, or is it gain that you make your ways complete?

4 Will he who fears you contend with you? Will he enter into judgment with you?

5 Is not your adversity great and your iniquities without end?

6 For you have taken pledges from your brothers for no reason and stripped the naked of their clothing.

7 You will not withhold water from the weary nor food from the hungry.

8 A man of strength to whom the land belongs, and one who is lifted up in countenance dwells in it.

9 You have sent the widows away empty, and the strength of the fatherless is crushed.

10 Therefore around your circuits are snares and sudden terror will terrify you.

11 or darkness you will not see, and an abundance of water will cover you.

12 Is not God the height of heaven? See how the stars are high.

13 And you shall say, How can God know, does He judge through thick darkness?

14 Thick clouds are His covering; He does not see, and He walks on the circle of the heavens.

15 The way of old you will keep, which the men of iniquity have trodden.

16 They have been gathered, but not at the right time; a flood will pour out their foundation.

17 Those who say to God, "Depart from us!" and to the Almighty, "What can the Mighty One do to them?"

18 But he filled their houses with good, and the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

19 The righteous will see and rejoice, and the pure will mock them.

20 If not, our substance would have been consumed; the remnant would have been devoured by fire.

21 Please dwell with him, and complete in them your good offering.

22 Take now the law from his mouth, and place his words in your heart.

23 If you return to the Almighty, you will be built up; you will remove iniquity from your tent.

24 And lay your gold in the dust, and the gold of Ophir among the stones of the brooks.

25 And the Almighty would be your gold defence, and silver your treasures.

26 Then you will delight in the Almighty, and you will lift your face to God.

27 You will cry to him, and he will hear you; your vows you will fulfill.

28 You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.

29 For they are brought low, and she says, "Pride"; but he saves the one whose eyes are downcast.

30 He will deliver even one who is not innocent; yes, he will be delivered by the cleanness of your hands.


## Chapter 23

1 Job answered and said:

2 Even now my rebellion is my complaint; my hand is heavy upon my groaning.

3 Who will give that I may know him and find him, that I may come to his seat?

4 I will set before him judgment, and my mouth will be filled with reproof.

5 I shall know what to say in answer to me, and I shall understand what he will speak to me.

6 With the abundant mighty one contending with me, not only does he overpower me.

7 There he will rightly contend with me, and I shall constantly escape from my judge.

8 Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I do not perceive him.

9 The left [hand] when he does it, and I do not hold; he fails the right [hand], and I do not see.

10 For he has known my path; he has tested me, and I shall emerge as gold.

11 In my going I have held fast to my steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.

12 The precept of his lips I have not removed; from my appointed decrees I have hidden no word of his mouth.

13 And he is alone, and who can turn him? And his soul has desired, and he has done it.

14 For he shall fulfill my appointed, and with him many shall be great.

15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence; I see him and I fear.

16 And God softened my heart, and Shaddai terrified me.

17 For I have not been overcome by darkness, and gloom has covered my presence.


## Chapter 24

1 Why are the times not kept from the Almighty, and why do those who know him not see his days?

2 Its borders will extend; a drove has taken it and they have pastured.

3 Orphaned children shall drive the ass; they shall defile the ox of the widow.

4 The poor turn aside from the way; together the needy of the land are hidden.

5 Behold, wild asses come out into the wilderness, in their act seeking prey; a desert is their food for the young men.

6 They reap their fodder in the field, and [they] gather the vintage of wickedness.

7 Naked they lie down, without clothing, and there is no covering against the cold.

8 From the storm of the mountains they were drenched, and from lack of refuge they embraced the rock.

9 They strip the breast of the orphan and take a pledge against the poor.

10 Naked they walked without clothing, and hungry people carried a sheaf.

11 Between their walls they will shine; they tread the winepresses, yet suffer thirst.

12 From the city the dead will cry out, and the soul of the slain will plead, but God will not regard folly.

13 They were rebels against the light; they did not know his ways, nor did they dwell in his paths.

14 At daybreak the murderer rises to kill the poor and needy, but at night he becomes like a thief.

15 An eye that commits adultery watches for twilight, saying, "No eye will see me," and puts a secret before the face.

16 They dig through the darkness, homes by day they seal for themselves; they do not know light.

17 For together morning is theirs, a shadow of death; because they recognize the terror of a shadow of death.

18 It is light upon the face of the waters; cursed is their portion in the land; they shall not turn the way of the vineyards.

19 The barren land also, the heat shall steal the waters of snow; so does Sheol snatch away those who have sinned.

20 Let the womb forget him, let the worm feed sweetly; he shall be remembered no more, and iniquity shall be broken like a tree.

21 A barren woman will not bear children, and does no good to the widow.

22 And he draws the mighty by his strength; he rises, yet does not believe in the living.

23 Grant him assurance and let him be supported, and let his eyes be upon their ways.

24 They are exalted a little while, and then they are gone; they are brought low, gathered up like all others, and cut off like the heads of grain.

25 But if not, then who will prove me false, and set my word as nothing?


## Chapter 25

1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:

2 Might and dread are with him; he makes peace in his heights.

3 Is there a number to his troops, and upon whom does his light not arise?

4 How can a mortal be righteous before God, or how can one born of woman be pure?

5 Behold, the moon does not endure and will not shine, and the stars do not prevail in His sight.

6 Yet indeed, a mortal man is a worm, and a son of humankind is a worm.


## Chapter 26

1 Job answered and said:

2 How have you helped the one without ability? You have saved the arm without strength.

3 What counsel have you given without wisdom, and what substance have you revealed in abundance?

4 Whose words have you declared, and whose breath has gone out from you?

5 The dead writhe beneath the water and its inhabitants.

6 Naked is the grave before him, and there is no covering for destruction.

7 Stretching the north over the empty place, suspending the earth over nothing.

8 Holding back water in its thick clouds, and the cloud beneath them does not burst.

9 Holding the face of the throne, he spread his cloud upon it.

10 An appointed custom upon the face of the waters until the end of light with darkness.

11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are astounded at his rebuking.

12 By his power he stilled the sea, and by his understanding he shattered Rahab.

13 By his spirit he garnished the heavens; his hand formed the fleeing serpent.

14 Behold, these are the outskirts of his ways, and how small a whisper of a word is heard of him; but the thunder of his power who can understand?


## Chapter 27

1 Then Job again took up his parable and said:

2 As God lives who removed my judgment, and the Almighty has embittered my soul.

3 For as long as my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils.

4 If my lips speak iniquity, and my tongue utter deceit.

5 Far be it from me to justify you; I will not let my integrity leave me.

6 By my justice I have held fast and will not let go; my heart shall not reproach me all my days.

7 Let my enemy be wicked, and the one who rises against me unrighteous.

8 For what is the hope of a hypocrite when he gains it, though God takes his soul?

9 God hears his crying when distress comes upon him.

10 If indeed one finds delight in the Almighty, let him call upon God at all times.

11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.

12 Behold, ye are all of you come together; why then do ye thus become vanity?

13 This is the portion of a man of low degree with God, and the inheritance of tyrants from the Almighty they shall receive.

14 If his children multiply, they will be for the sword, and his offspring will not be satisfied with food.

15 They will be buried in death, and his widows will not weep.

16 If he gathers silver like dust, and prepares raiment like clay,

17 A righteous and just man will be clothed, and pure silver he will divide.

18 He builds his house like a moth, like a booth that a watchman makes.

19 The rich man lies down but is not gathered; his eyes open and he is not.

20 May terror seize him like a flood of water; by night a storm has swept him away.

21 The east wind will carry him away and he will depart, and it will sweep him from his place.

22 He will cast upon him and will not spare; from his hand they will flee in terror.

23 He will clap his hand upon them, and whistle at him from his place.


## Chapter 28

1 For there is a place where silver is brought out, and a place where gold is refined.

2 Iron is taken from dust, and copper is smelted from the stone.

3 The end of darkness he appoints; every limit he searches out—the stone of darkness and the shadow of death.

4 He breaks open a shaft away from where people dwell; they are forgotten by the foot, they hang far from men, they swing to and fro.

5 A land from which food comes forth, and beneath it is turned as fire.

6 A place of sapphire is its stone, and dust of gold is for it.

7 A path no bird of prey has known, nor has the eye of the kite gazed upon it.

8 The proud beasts have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.

9 With flint he sent his hand, overturned the roots of the mountains.

10 In the rocks he split the streams; every precious thing his eye beheld.

11 From the overflowing of rivers he made it firm, and its secret he brings forth to light.

12 But where can wisdom be found, and what place is there for understanding?

13 No mortal man knows its equal, nor is it found in the land of the living.

14 The deep said, "Not with me is she"; and the sea said, "She is not with me."

15 No gold shall be given for its covering, nor shall silver be weighed for its price.

16 It cannot be valued with the fine gold of Ophir, with precious onyx, or sapphire.

17 It cannot be exchanged for gold or crystal, nor can its recompense be a vessel of fine gold.

18 Coral and pearl are not remembered, and the price of wisdom is above rubies.

19 Topaz of Ethiopia will not equal it; it cannot be weighed against pure gold.

20 But wisdom, from where does it come, and where is the place of understanding?

21 And it is hidden from the eyes of all living, and concealed from the birds of the heavens.

22 Destruction and death said, in our hearing we have heard her report.

23 God has understood the way, and He knows its place.

24 For he looks to the uttermost edges of the earth, and sees under all the heavens.

25 To assign the air a full weight and to measure the waters.

26 When he appoints a time for the rain, and a path for the lightning's thunder.

27 Then he saw it and declared it; he established it, yes, he searched it out.

28 Then he said to the man, Behold, the fear of the LORD—that is wisdom; and to turn from evil is understanding.


## Chapter 29

1 Then Job took up his parable again and said:

2 Who will grant me like the months of old, as the days when God watched over me?

3 By his light above my head I walk through darkness.

4 As in the days of my youth, when God's counsel was over my tent.

5 While the Almighty was still with me, young men were about me.

6 When my steps are washed in butter, and the Rock pours out streams of oil with me.

7 When I went out through the city gate, into the broad place, I prepared my dwelling.

8 Babes saw me and hid; old men rose and stood.

9 Captains stopped speaking, and laid a hand upon their mouths.

10 The voice of leaders is silenced and their tongue clings to their palate.

11 For an ear heard and approved me, and an eye saw and testified to me.

12 For I will deliver the afflicted who cries out, and the fatherless child who has no helper.

13 The blessing of the one perishing came upon me, and I made the heart of the widow sing for joy.

14 I put on righteousness and it clothed me; like a robe and a diadem was my judgment.

15 Eyes I was to the blind, and feet I was to the lame.

16 I am a chief to the poor, and a controversy I do not know—I will investigate it.

17 And I will break the jaw of the wicked, and from his teeth I will cast out the prey.

18 And I said, "With my nest I will die, and like the sand I will multiply days."

19 My root is open to the waters and dew lies on my harvest.

20 My glory is renewed with me, and my bow is renewed in my hand.

21 Listen to me and wait expectantly, and be silent before my counsel.

22 After my counsel they will not change, and upon them my word will distill.

23 And they waited for me as for the rain, and their mouths opened for the latter rain.

24 I will rejoice over them; they will not believe, and the light of my presence they will not behold.

25 I will choose their journey and sit as chief, and dwell as a king among the troops, like those who comfort mourners.


## Chapter 30

1 And now I am mocked by youths younger than I, whose fathers I disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.

2 Even the strength of their hands is of no use to me; upon them has perished full old age.

3 Through want and famine they are gaunt, gnawing the dry ground in gloom, desolation, and waste.

4 They who pluck mallow leaves from the bush, and eat juniper roots as their bread.

5 From the body they are driven; they cry out against them like a thief.

6 In the cliffs of brooks to dwell, caves of dust and rocks.

7 Among thorns they are entangled; under a nettle they are ensnared.

8 The sons of Nabal, even the sons of those without renown, were humbled from the land.

9 And now I have become their song; I have become a byword to them.

10 You have loathed me, you have kept far from me, and have not spared spitting before my face.

11 For he has loosened my cord and afflicted me, and they have cast off restraint before me.

12 On the right the youth will rise, my legs are sent forth; they have pressed upon me the paths of their destruction.

13 They have blocked my path to bring me calamity; no helper is for them.

14 Like a breach in the storm they are drawn forth; under desolation they roll.

15 Terrors are turned upon me; they chase my honor like the wind, and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.

16 And now my soul is poured out within me; the days of affliction hold me fast.

17 At night my body is chilled from me and my sinews do not rest.

18 With abundant strength he disguises my clothing; according to my tunic he girds me.

19 I am poured out like clay, and I become like dust and ashes.

20 I cry to you and you do not answer me; I stand, and you look at me.

21 Be turned to cruelty toward me; with the might of your hand you afflict me.

22 You lift me up to the wind; you make me ride on it, and you dissolve me in the storm.

23 For I know that death will return me to the house of appointed assembly for all living.

24 Yet not by the grave will he send a hand; if in his ruin, they cry out.

25 If I did not weep for the distress of the age, my soul was grieved for the poor.

26 For good I hoped, but evil came; and I waited for light, but darkness came.

27 My bowels are seething and have not ceased; the days of my affliction confront me.

28 I became dark; I was sunless; I rose in the congregation and cried out.

29 I was a brother to dragons and a companion to the daughters of the owl.

30 My skin turns black upon me, and my bones burn with heat.

31 My harp is for mourning, and my organ for the sound of weeping.


## Chapter 31

1 A covenant I made before my eyes; why then should I regard a virgin?

2 What portion has God from above, and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?

3 Is not calamity for the wicked, and a strange fate for workers of iniquity?

4 Will he not see my way and count all my steps?

5 If I have walked with lying, and my foot has followed after deceit,

6 Weigh me in even balances, that God may know my integrity.

7 If my step turns away from the way and my heart goes after the sight of my eyes, then let blemish cling to me in my hand.

8 I will sow, and another shall eat; and my offspring shall be rooted out.

9 If my heart has consented to a woman, or if I have lain in wait at the entrance of my friend,

10 Let another have intercourse with my wife, and upon her let others bow down.

11 For that is lewdness; yea, it is an iniquity to be judged.

12 For it is a fire that will consume even to destruction, and it will uproot all my increase.

13 If I despise the judgment of my male servant or my female servant, when they contend with me.

14 What shall I do, for God has risen, and if He should visit, what shall I answer Him?

15 Did not he who made me in the belly also make him? And did not one fashion him in the womb?

16 If I withhold the poor's desire, the widow's eyes I will consume.

17 And I ate my morsel alone, and the fatherless child did not eat from it.

18 For from my youth they raised me as a father, and from my mother's womb they have guided me.

19 If I see one perishing for lack of clothing, and there is no covering for the poor,

20 If his loins have not blessed me, and if he was not warmed from the fleece of my sheep.

21 If I have raised my hand against the fatherless, for I saw my help at the gate,

22 Upon my shoulder-blade you shall fall, and my arm shall be broken from the reed.

23 For dread of God a calamity, a mighty one, and from His rising up I am not able to bear.

24 If I have made gold my confidence, and to the fine gold I have said, "You are my trust."

25 If I rejoice because of the abundance of my strength, and because my hand has gained much.

26 If I see light, though it shines, and the moon walking in its brightness.

27 I made a secret agreement with my heart, and my hand swore to my mouth.

28 It is also my fault, for I have denied God above.

29 If I rejoice in the destruction of him that hates me, and rise up because evil has found him.

30 I did not give my mouth to sin, asking a curse upon his soul.

31 If only the few of my tent had said, Who will give us of his flesh that we may not be satisfied?

32 The alien shall not remain abroad; I will open my door to the traveler.

33 If I have concealed my transgression like Adam, hiding my iniquity in my bosom.

34 For I will crush the abundance of the multitude, and the contempt of families will dismay me; yet I will remain, I will not go out to the entrance.

35 Who will grant me that the Almighty may hear me, behold, my desire would answer me; and though a man of contention writes a bill against me,

36 If not upon my shoulder I will bear him, I will bind him as a crown to me.

37 I will tell of the number of my steps, as a prince I will approach him.

38 If my land should cry out against me, and its furrows weep together.

39 If I have eaten her strength without money, and the life of her owner has been diminished.

40 Instead of wheat shall come forth thistle, and instead of barley shall come forth cockle. The words of Job are ended.


## Chapter 32

1 Then those three men ceased to answer Job, for he was righteous in his own eyes.

2 Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, burned with anger against Job; his anger burned because he justified his soul before God.

3 And his anger burned against his three companions because they had found no answer, and so they condemned Job.

4 Elihu waited for Job in his words, for they were older than he in age.

5 Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men, and the anger of him was kindled.

6 Then Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young in age, and you are elders; therefore I was afraid and feared to show you my knowledge.

7 I said, "Time will speak, and a great many years will teach wisdom."

8 Indeed, air it is in a mortal, and the breath of the Almighty gives them understanding.

9 Not many are wise, and elders understand judgment.

10 Therefore I said, "Listen to me; I will declare my opinion; even I."

11 Indeed, I began to heed your advice, to listen until your wisdom, until you inquire by words.

12 And you, cease, consider and see: there is no one who can refute Job—no one among you answers his words.

13 Lest you say, "We have found wisdom; God will thrust him down, not man."

14 But he has not given me an answer, and in your sayings I will not return him.

15 They are dismayed; they answer no more; words have failed them.

16 And I waited because they would not speak, because they stood but answered no longer.

17 I also will answer my part; I also will declare my knowledge.

18 For I am weary of words; the spirit within my body troubles me.

19 Behold, my belly is like wine that will not be opened, like new bottles it will burst.

20 I will speak, and I will find relief; I will open my lips, and I will answer.

21 I will not accept a man's face, nor will I favor any person.

22 For I do not know how to flatter; otherwise my Maker would soon take me away.


## Chapter 33

1 But now, listen, Job, to my answer, and attend to all my words.

2 Behold now I have opened my mouth; my tongue speaks in my palate.

3 The equity of my heart, my words, and the knowledge of my lips, is pure.

4 The breath of God has made me, and the blast of the Almighty sustains me.

5 If you can, restore me to your presence; stand me before you.

6 Behold, I am as your mouth to God; I too was pinched off from clay.

7 Behold, my dread will not accuse you, and my hand upon you will not be heavy.

8 But you said in my ear, and I will hear the sound of words.

9 I am pure, without transgression; I am innocent, and there is no fault in me.

10 Behold, a breach of promise against me he will find; he will count me his enemy.

11 He set my feet in the stocks; he watches over all my ways.

12 Indeed, you have not been righteous; I will answer you: God is greater than a mortal.

13 Why do you contend with him, since in all his words he does not answer?

14 For God speaks once, and in a second instance they do not discern it.

15 In a dream vision of night, during deep sleep upon men, in their slumbers upon bed.

16 Then he will expose the ear of men, and by their instruction he will seal.

17 To remove a person from his act, and pride from a man he will conceal.

18 He keeps his soul from the pit and his life from perishing by the sword.

19 And he is reproved in pain upon his bed, and with continual strife in his bones.

20 So that his life loathes bread, and his soul loathes desirable food.

21 His body wasted away from sight; his bones were not seen.

22 His soul drew near to destruction, and his life to the dead.

23 If there is for him an ambassador, one messenger among a thousand, to declare to a person what is right,

24 And he had mercy on him and said, "Redeem him from the descent to the pit; I have found a ransom."

25 His body wasted from youth, he shall return to the days of his youth.

26 He will entreat God and He will accept him, and he will see His face with shouting, and he will restore to another his righteousness.

27 It is right upon also, and he said, I have sinned, and I have perverted right, and it does not profit me.

28 He has delivered my soul from passing into the pit; in the light I shall see.

29 Behold, all these things God does, two times, three times, with a man.

30 To restore his soul from the pit, to light in the light of life.

31 Job, listen to me; be silent, that I may speak.

32 If there is any word, answer me; speak, for I desire your righteousness.

33 If you will not listen to me, be silent and I will teach you wisdom.


## Chapter 34

1 Then Elihu answered and said:

2 Hear my word, you wise; and you knowledgeable, give ear to me.

3 For the ear tries words, and the mouth tastes food.

4 A judgment has been chosen for us; we will know among us what is good.

5 For Job said, I am righteous, and God has taken away my judgment.

6 I will deny my charge; I am punished, my arrow, though without transgression.

7 Who is there like Job, who drinks scorn as water?

8 and company with those who cause affliction, and to go with men of wickedness.

9 For he said, "Every one shall not dwell in his favor with God."

10 Therefore you men of understanding, hear me: far be it from God to do wickedness, and from the Almighty to commit iniquity.

11 For a person's deed he will repay him, and according to a man's manner he will find him.

12 Indeed, truly, God does not pervert justice, nor does the Almighty pervert judgment.

13 Who has appointed him over the earth, and who has set over the whole world?

14 If he should set his mind upon him, his spirit and his breath he would gather to himself.

15 All flesh shall perish together, and person shall return unto dust.

16 And if understanding hears this, let her listen to the sound of my words.

17 Shall one who hates justice indeed be clothed, or will you acquit a mighty man who is righteous?

18 Say to the king, O Belial: You are wicked among nobles.

19 who does not show partiality to rulers nor regard the rich before the poor, for all are the work of his hands.

20 Suddenly they die, at midnight the people tremble and pass on, and the mighty are removed without a hand.

21 For his eyes are upon the ways of a man, and he sees all his steps.

22 There is no darkness and no shadow of death where workers of iniquity may hide.

23 For no longer will He place another to go to God in judgment.

24 He shepherded the great ones without search, and set others in their place.

25 Therefore he recognizes their work, and turns to night and they are distressed.

26 Instead of the wicked, their place among those who see.

27 Therefore they turned away from following him, and in all his ways there was no success.

28 to bring upon him the cry of the poor, and the crying of the needy he shall hear.

29 And he shall cause to cease, and who shall condemn? And he shall hide faces, and who shall make him to appear? And over nation and over man alike.

30 From the reign of a hypocrite who ensnares the people.

31 For to God I said, "I have lifted up; I will not bear a burden."

32 Except that You see me, You cause me to see—have I done iniquity? I will not do it again.

33 Is it from you that they will repay me, since you have rejected me and you choose, not I? And what have you known that you speak?

34 Men of understanding will say to me, and a wise man will listen to me.

35 Job speaks without knowledge, and his words are without insight.

36 Job will be tested alway by my father, because of answers from men of iniquity.

37 For adding to his punishment rebellion, he provokes strife between us, and multiplies his speeches against God.


## Chapter 35

1 Then Elihu answered and said:

2 Is this the judgment you imagined? You said, "My justice is from God."

3 For you say, “What will I gain? What benefit from my punishment?”

4 I will answer you in matters, and your companions with you.

5 Look to the heavens and see, and gaze upon the skies, which are higher than you.

6 If you have sinned, what will you do to him, and if your transgressions are many, what can you do to him?

7 If you have been righteous, what will you give him, or what from your hand will he take?

8 To a man like you, your wickedness; and to a son of man, your righteousness.

9 Because of the abundance of oppression they cry out, they call for help from the arm of many.

10 but did not say, "Where is God my Maker, who gives songs in the night?"

11 He teaches us from the beasts of the land and makes us wise from the birds of the heavens.

12 There they will cry out, but he will not answer, because of the pride of the wicked.

13 But falsely God will not listen, and Almighty will not regard it.

14 Even if you say that you do not see him, judgment is before him, and you must wait for him.

15 And now, since His anger has not visited and He has not known extremity.

16 But Job will open his mouth in vanity, multiplying words without knowledge.


## Chapter 36

1 Then Elihu continued and said:

2 Show me a little, and I will answer you, for there is yet more to say to God.

3 I will carry my knowledge to a far distance, and to my work I will assign justice.

4 Indeed, my speaking is not without a cause; upright knowledge is with you.

5 Behold, God is great and does not reject; great is the power of heart.

6 The wicked will not be acquitted, and judgment will be rendered for the poor.

7 He will not remove his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne he has established them forever, and they are exalted.

8 And if they are bound in chains, they will be caught in the cords of affliction.

9 And he declared to them their acts, and their transgressions, because they rebelled.

10 He opens their ear to discipline, and commands that they return from iniquity.

11 If they obey and serve, their days will be good and their years pleasant.

12 And if they do not heed when sent forth with a dart, they shall perish for lack of knowledge.

13 But the stubborn of heart store up wrath; they do not cry for help when he binds them.

14 Their soul shall perish in youth, and their life among the consecrated.

15 He will deliver the afflicted in his affliction, and will open their ear in oppression.

16 And indeed, I delivered you from the mouth of distress into a broad place, not straitened; and your table's quietness was filled with fatness.

17 You have filled the wicked with legal strife; judgment and justice take hold of you.

18 Because there is wrath, beware lest he take you away with a stroke; then a great ransom cannot turn you aside.

19 Will your cry avail you against distress, and will all your forces and power deliver you?

20 Do not strive at night to go up against them, folk.

21 Take heed, do not turn to iniquity, for on account of this you were chosen from affliction.

22 Behold, God increases in strength; who is like him for teaching?

23 Who has appointed his way, and who has said, "You have done iniquity"?

24 Remember to magnify his work, which men have sung.

25 Every person sees him, a mortal beholds from afar.

26 Behold, God is great and unknowable, the number of whose years is beyond searching out.

27 For he draws up the drops of water; they distill rain from his mist.

28 when the skies drip and shower upon a common sort.

29 Even if he who spreadest forth the thick cloud understand the stir of his pavilion.

30 Behold, he spread his light upon it, and covered the roots of the sea.

31 For by them he judges nations, gives food to the increasing.

32 He covers his hands with lightning, and commands it to strike the mark.

33 His companion proclaims aloud concerning his cattle, even upon the ascending offering.


## Chapter 37

1 Even for this my heart trembles and is removed from its place.

2 Hear and hear with fear the sound of his voice, and the mourning from his mouth goes out.

3 under all the heavens he spreads his light, and his brightness upon the uttermost parts of the earth

4 After him the thunder roars with a voice of his majesty; he does not restrain them when his voice is heard.

5 God thunders with His voice, doing wonders, great deeds not understood.

6 For he says to the snow: Be upon the ground, and heavy rain, and rain of his might.

7 By the hand of every person he shall be confirmed, to know all the deeds of his work.

8 And the living creature came to its den, and in its dwelling places it dwells.

9 A storm comes from the inner chamber, and cold from the north.

10 By the blast of God frost is given, and the breadth of waters is constricted by anguish.

11 Even my watering troubles the thick cloud; it spreads the cloud of his light.

12 And it is turned around by his guidance, that they may do whatever he commands them upon the face of the inhabited earth.

13 If for correction, if for land, if for kindness, he will find it.

14 Job, listen to this; stand and consider the wonders of God.

15 Do you know when God appoints them, that He displays the light of His cloud?

16 Do you know by the balancing of the thick cloud, by the wondrous work of perfect knowledge?

17 whose garments are warm when there is peace in the land from the south

18 He spreads out the sky with him, strong as a molten looking glass.

19 Make known to us what we should say to him; we cannot stand before darkness.

20 Will it be told to you if I speak? Or if a certain man says, "He will be swallowed up"?

21 And now they do not see light, bright it is in the skies, and wind has passed and cleared them.

22 Gold comes from the north, and to God comes majesty, the awesome one.

23 The Almighty, we cannot find him; he is great in power, and in judgment and abundant justice he does not oppress.

24 Therefore people fear him; he does not regard any who are wise of heart.


## Chapter 38

1 Then the LORD answered Job and said:

2 Who is this who darkens counsel with words without knowledge?

3 Gird up thy loins now like a mighty man, and I will ask thee, and declare thou me.

4 Where were you when I founded the earth? Declare, if you have knowledge.

5 Who has set its measure, that you should know? Or who has stretched a line upon it?

6 Why have her foundations sunk? Or who has struck the cornerstone?

7 The morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy.

8 He shut the sea with its two gates, when it burst forth from the womb.

9 With a cloud as his garment and thick darkness his swaddling band.

10 I broke my appointed commandment for him, and I set the bar and the doors.

11 And I said, "Thus far shall you come, and no more; here shall your swelling waves be stopped."

12 Have you commanded the morning since your days began, and caused the dawn to know its place?

13 to seize the corners of the earth and the wicked be shaken out of it

14 You will be turned like clay to the seal, and they will stand as a garment.

15 And their light shall be withheld, and the strong arm shall be broken.

16 Have you journeyed to the springs of the sea, and in the searching of the deep have you walked?

17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you, and will you see the gates of the shadow of death?

18 Have you considered the breadth of the land? Declare if you know the whole of it.

19 Where is the way to the home of light, and where is the place of darkness?

20 When you take him to his border, and when you understand the pathways of his house.

21 You knew that then you would be born, and the number of your days is many.

22 Have you come to the storehouse of snow, and will you see the storehouse of hailstones?

23 for a time of distress, for a day of battle and war.

24 Where is the way that light is divided, or the east wind scattered across the earth?

25 Who cut a conduit for the overflowing flood and a path for the thunder of the lightning?

26 to pour rain upon a land without a man, a wilderness without a man in it.

27 to satisfy desolation and waste, and to cause the spring of tender grass to flourish.

28 Is there a chief for the rain, or who has fathered the drops of dew?

29 From the belly who brings forth the frost, and who fathers the hoary frost from the heavens?

30 Like a stone the waters are hidden, and the face of the deep is imprisoned.

31 Can you bind the influence of the Pleiades, or loosen the band of Orion?

32 Will you bring forth Mazzoroth in its season, or will you guide the Bear with its children?

33 Do you know the appointed customs of the heavens, or establish his dominion on the earth?

34 Will you lift up your voice to the cloud, so that an abundance of water may cover you?

35 Send forth lightning and they will go; they will say to you, Here we are.

36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts, or who has given understanding to the heart?

37 Who can recount the skies with wisdom, and who can lay the vessels of heaven?

38 In the mortar of dust for casting, and clods shall cleave.

39 Will you hunt prey for the lion, and fill the appetite of the young lions?

40 When they crouch in their dens, they sit in a booth to lie in wait.

41 Who provides food for the raven when it cries to God and its young wander without food?


## Chapter 39

1 Do you know the due season when the wild goats of the rock give birth? Do you observe the birth pangs of the hinds?

2 Count the months fulfilled, and you will know the time of her giving birth.

3 They bow themselves, they bring forth their young; they cast out their pangs.

4 Their young ones become strong; they grow up in the open field; they go out and do not return to them.

5 Who has let loose the wild ass, and who has opened the bands of the wild ass?

6 the Arabah of his home and his dwellings a salt land

7 He will laugh at the tumult of the city, the shouting of the taskmaster he will not hear.

8 The feeding place of the mountains, and after every green thing he shall seek.

9 Will the unicorn consent to serve you, or will it stay the night by your crib?

10 Will the unicorn be willing to be bound with his thick bough in the furrow? Or will he plow valleys after you?

11 Will you trust in him because his strength is great, and commit your labor to him?

12 Will you trust in him that your seed may come, and your threshing floor gather in?

13 The wing of the ostrich rejoices, but is it the pinion and feather of the stork?

14 If you abandon its eggs to the earth and let them be warmed on the dust.

15 And she forgets that a foot may crush them, or that a wild beast may trample them.

16 Her children have hardened against her; her labor is in vain, without fear.

17 For God has withheld wisdom and has not imparted understanding to her.

18 At that time in the heights he makes strong; he laughs at the horse and its rider.

19 Will you give strength to the horse? Will you clothe his neck with thunder?

20 You make him leap like a locust; the majesty of his snorting is terror.

21 They will dig in the valley and rejoice in strength, going out to meet the armed men.

22 He will laugh at dread and will not be afraid, and will not turn back from the presence of the sword.

23 Upon it the quiver rattles, the blade of the javelin and the lance.

24 In commotion and rage the earth trembles and does not believe because of the sound of trumpet.

25 According to the blast of the trumpet he shall say, "Aha!" and from afar he shall smell the battle, the thunder of captains and the shouting.

26 Can the hawk fly by your wisdom, spreading its wings toward the south?

27 Even if the eagle should exalt according to your mouth, and if it should lift up its nest.

28 A (ragged) rock will dwell and will complain upon the crag of a (ragged) rock and a castle.

29 From there he searches out food; his eyes look to the distance.

30 And the young ones shall lick blood, and where the slain are, there is he.


## Chapter 40

1 And the LORD answered Job and said:

2 Shall a multitude of the Almighty instruct? Will God answer?

3 Job answered the LORD and said:

4 Behold, I am vile; what shall I repay you? I will lay my hand upon my mouth.

5 One thing I have spoken, and I will not answer; even twice, and I will not proceed further.

6 The LORD answered Job and said:

7 Gird up now thy loins like a mighty man; I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

8 Will you indeed overturn my judgment? Will you condemn me so that you may be justified?

9 But if an arm like God is yours, and a voice like His, terrify them.

10 Adorn yourself now with majesty and height, and clothe yourself with honour and splendour.

11 Scatter the outbursts of your wrath, and see every haughty one and bring him low.

12 Look on every haughty one and humble him; crush the wicked in their place.

13 Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in a hidden place.

14 I will also praise you because your right hand saves you.

15 Behold now Behemoth, which I made with you: it eats grass like a beeve.

16 Behold now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the tendons of his belly.

17 It bends its tail like a cedar; the sinews of its thighs tremble with fear.

18 His bones are channels of brass; his frame like a bar of iron.

19 It is the beginning of God's ways, the one who made it brings near its sword.

20 For the hills' stock shall sustain him, and every wild beast of the field shall play there.

21 He will lie down under shady trees, in the hiding place of reed and mire.

22 Shady trees shelter his shadow; willows of the stream surround him.

23 Behold, if a river overflows, he does not tremble; he is confident though Jordan rushes to his mouth.

24 In his affliction he will take him; in snares his face will be pierced.


## Chapter 41

1 Can you draw out leviathan with a hook, or press down his tongue with a cord?
