2 Will you put a bulrush in his nose, and pierce his jaw with a thorn?

3 Will he multiply intreaties toward you, or speak to you with enticements?

4 Will he make a covenant with you? Will you take him as a bondservant forever?

5 Do you mock him like a bird, and bind him for your maidens?

6 Companions plot against him; they divide him among merchants.

7 Fill his hide with barbed iron and his head with fish spears.

8 Place your hand upon him; remember war no more.

9 Behold, his hope is in vain; shall it even be cast upon his appearance?

10 I will not spare, for who can stand before me?

11 Who has anticipated Me, that I should repay? Under all the heavens, I am He.

12 I will not be silent about his limbs, nor about his mighty strength and the comeliness of his form.

13 Who has uncovered the face of his apparel, with a double bridle who shall enter?

14 Who has opened the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.

15 The excellency of the streams of shields is shut up, a sealed decree, narrow.

16 One by one they draw near, and a breath does not come between them.

17 A certain man shall cleave to his brother; they shall be joined together and not be separated.

18 His sneezing flashes forth light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of morning.

19 From his mouth go forth torches, sparks of fire flee.

20 From his nostrils comes smoke, like a seething pot and a rush.

21 His soul is inflamed with burning coals and a flame comes forth from his mouth.

22 His neck endures might, and before him he crushes sorrow.

23 The flake of his flesh clings; it is poured upon him; he shall not totter.

24 His heart is poured out like a stone, and poured out like a piece of the nether millstone.

25 When he rises up, the mighty are afraid; because of terror they cleanse themselves.

26 A sword overtakes him so that he does not rise, a javelin, a dart, and a coat of mail.

27 He will regard iron as straw and wood as rotten brass.

28 His arrow will not drive him away; stubble is turned for him into sling stones.

29 Like stubble they are reckoned, darts; he laughs at the shaking of a lance.

30 Beneath him are sharp things of earth; he spreads out a threshing instrument upon mire.

31 Boil the caldron deep; set the sea like a pot of ointment.

32 After him the path will shine; he will regard the deep as gray hair.

33 There is nothing like him on the dust; they have made him without fear.

34 Everything high he sees; he is king over all the sons of pride.


## Chapter 42

1 Job answered the LORD and said:

2 For thou canst do everything, and no device is hidden from thee.

3 Who is this who gives counsel without knowledge, so that I have declared it and do not understand? Wonders too great for me, which I do not know.

4 Please hear me, and I will speak; I will question you, and you shall inform me.

5 To hear a report I heard of you, and now my eye has seen you.

6 Therefore I despise and am sorry for dust and ashes.

7 After the LORD had spoken these words to Job, he said to Eliphaz the Temanite, "My wrath is kindled against you and your two friends, because you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has."

8 Now therefore take for yourselves seven young bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves, and my servant Job will pray for you, for I will accept his face so as not to deal with you according to your folly, because you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.

9 Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as Jehovah had spoken to them, and Jehovah accepted Job's face.

10 And the LORD restored Job’s fortunes when he prayed on behalf of his friend, and the LORD increased all that Job had twofold.

11 All his brothers and all the sisters and all who had known him previously came to him, and they ate food with him in his house, and they lamented for him and comforted him concerning all the adversity that the LORD had brought upon him, and each man gave him a piece of silver money and each man a gold earring.

12 And the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning, and there came to him fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.

13 He had seven sons and three daughters.

14 He named the first Jemimah, and the name of the second Kezia, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.

15 And no women were found as beautiful as Job’s daughters in all the earth, and their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.

16 Job lived after this one hundred and forty years, and saw his children and his children's children, four generations.

17 Job died, an old man, full of days.


# PSALMS

## Chapter 1

1 Blessed is the man who does not walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand in the way of sinners, nor sit in the assembly of mockers.

2 but his delight is in the law of the LORD, and in his law he meditates day and night.

3 And he shall be like a tree planted by streams of water, whose fruit it brings forth in its season, and its leaf does not wither, and whatsoever he does prospers.

4 Not so the wicked, for they are like the chaff that a wind blows away.

5 Therefore the wicked will not stand in judgment, nor sinners in the assembly of the righteous.

6 For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.


## Chapter 2

1 Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot in vain?

2 Let the kings of the land take their stand and the rulers gather together against the LORD and against his anointed.

3 We will break their bands apart and cast their cords away from us.

4 The one sitting in the heavens laughs; (my) Lord mocks them.

5 Then he will speak to them in his anger, and in his fury he will terrify them.

6 But as for me, I have installed my king on Zion, my holy hill.

7 I will declare the decree: the LORD has said to me, "You are my son; today I have begotten you."

8 Ask of me, and I will give the Gentiles as your inheritance, and your possession the uttermost parts of the earth.

9 You will shatter them with an iron rod; like a potter's vessel you will break them in pieces.

10 And now, kings, be wise; be warned, judges of the earth.

11 Serve the LORD with fear and rejoice with trembling.

12 Kiss the son, lest he be angry and you perish from the way, for his anger will burn a little; blessed are all who take refuge in him.


## Chapter 3

1 A psalm of David when he fled from the presence of Absalom his son. O Jehovah, how many are my adversaries? Many are those who rise up against me.

2 Many say to my soul, There is no salvation for him in God. Selah.

3 But you, O Jehovah, are a shield for me, my glory, and the one lifting my head.

4 I call aloud to the LORD, and he answers me from his holy mountain. Selah.

5 I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the LORD sustains me.

6 I will not fear ten thousands of people who have set themselves against me on every side.

7 Arise, O Lord, save me, O God, for you have struck all my enemies on the cheek, you have broken the teeth of the wicked.

8 Deliverance belongs to the LORD; upon your people be your blessing, Selah.


## Chapter 4

1 For the music director, with stringed instruments, a psalm by David. When I called, answer me, O God of my righteousness; in distress You made room for me; be gracious to me and hear my prayer.

2 O children of man, how long will my glory be turned to shame? How long will you love emptiness and seek lies? Selah.

3 Know that Jehovah has set apart the godly for himself; Jehovah will hear when I call to him.

4 Tremble, and do not sin; bethink yourselves upon your bed-chambers and be still. Selah.

5 Offer sacrifices of justice and trust in the LORD.

6 Many are saying, “Who will show us good?” Lift up the light of your face, O LORD.

7 You have given gladness in my heart from the time their wheat and wine increased.

8 In peace I will both lie down and sleep, for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.


## Chapter 5

1 For the leader; according to Nehiloth: a psalm of David. Give ear to my speech, O LORD; consider my meditation.

2 Hear the sound of my cry, my King and my God, for to you I pray.

3 The LORD in the morning You will hear my voice, in the morning I will prepare [for] You and watch.

4 For you are not a God who delights in iniquity; evil will not dwell with you.

5 The wicked shall not stand before your presence; you hate all workers of iniquity.

6 The LORD abhors the man of blood and deceit who speaks words of falsehood.

7 But as for me, in the abundance of your mercy I will enter your house; I will bow down toward your holy temple in fear of you.

8 O the LORD, in your justice grant me favor for the sake of my enemies; make straight your way before me.

9 For there is no truth in his mouth; their inward part is wickedness; their throat is an open grave; their tongue speaks deceit.

10 O God, cast them down from their counsel; in the abundance of their rebellion, reject them, for they have rebelled against You.

11 And let all who take refuge in you rejoice forever; may they sing for joy, and may you shelter them, and may those who love your name exult in you.

12 For you will bless the righteous, O the LORD; with favour you will surround him as with a shield.


## Chapter 6

1 For the music director, on the sheminith, with stringed instruments, a psalm of David. O LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger, nor discipline me in your wrath.

2 Be gracious to me, O the LORD, for I am weak; heal me, O the LORD, for my bones are dismayed.

3 and my soul is greatly dismayed; O LORD, how long?

4 Return, O LORD, rescue my soul; save me for the sake of your mercy.

5 For there is no remembrance of you in death, in the grave who will give thanks to you?

6 I am weary with sighing; every night I drench my bed, with my tears I soak my couch.

7 My eye grows dim with grief because of all my adversaries.

8 Turn away from me, all workers of iniquity, for the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.

9 The LORD has heard my supplication; the LORD will accept my prayer.

10 Let them be greatly ashamed and confounded, all mine enemies; let them turn back, let them be suddenly ashamed.


## Chapter 7

1 A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning the words of Cush, a Benjamite. The LORD my God, in you I have taken refuge; save me from all who pursue me and deliver me.

2 Lest like a young lion he tear my soul apart, and there be none to deliver.

3 O LORD my God, if I have done this, if there is iniquity in my hand,

4 If I have repaid my ally with evil, or delivered up my enemy empty-handed,

5 Let the enemy pursue my life and overtake it, and trample my living being to the ground, and set my honour in the dust.

6 Rise up, O LORD, in Your anger; lift Yourself up against the rage of my adversaries; and awake for me to the judgment You have commanded.

7 An assembly of nations surrounds you; return upward to dignity.

8 The LORD will judge peoples; judge me, O LORD, according to my justice and according to my integrity upon me.

9 O let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous, for the righteous God tests hearts and kidneys.

10 My shield is God, the savior of the upright in heart.

11 God judges the righteous, and God is wrathful every day.

12 If one does not repent, he will sharpen his sword; he has bent his bow and made it ready.

13 And for him he prepared weapons of death; his arrows he made fiery.

14 Behold, he travails to form iniquity, conceives affliction, and gives birth to falsehood.

15 He dug a cistern, he hollowed it out, and he fell into the pit he made.

16 His grievance returns upon his own head; upon the crown of his head his injustice descends.

17 I will give thanks to the LORD for his righteousness, and sing praise to the name of the LORD on high.


## Chapter 8

1 For the music director, upon the Gittith: a psalm of David. O the LORD our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth, who have given Your glory above the heavens!

2 From the mouth of babes and infants You established strength, to silence the enemy and the avenger.

3 When I see the heavens, the work of Your fingers, the moon and the stars, which You established.

4 What is a certain man that you remember him, and a son of a person that you attend to him?

5 Yet you made him almost like divine beings, and crowned him with glory and majesty.

6 You make him rule over the works of your hands; all you have placed under his feet.

7 Sheep and kine, all of them, and also cattle of my field.

8 the bird of the heavens and the fish of the sea, the traveller of the ways of the seas

9 O the LORD our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth.


## Chapter 9

1 For the music director; upon Muthlabben, a psalm of David. I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart; I will recount all your wonders.

2 I will rejoice and be glad in you; I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.

3 When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish from your presence.

4 For you have done my judgment and my cause; you sit on a throne of justice.

5 You rebuked Gentiles, You destroyed the wicked; You blotted out their name forever and ever.

6 The enemy has utterly destroyed places of decay forever, and cities you have overthrown; their memory has perished, they themselves.

7 And the LORD will alway sit upon his throne, established for judgment.

8 And he will judge the habitable part with justice, will rule nations with equity.

9 Let the LORD be your Misgab, a Misgab at times of trouble.

10 And those who know your name will trust in you, for you have not abandoned those who seek you, the LORD.

11 Sing to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; declare among the peoples his acts.

12 For he remembers them, requiring blood-guiltiness; he does not forget the cry of the poor.

13 Be gracious to me, O the LORD; behold my affliction from those who hate me, O you who lift me up from the gates of death.

14 that I may recount all your praises in the gates of Daughter Zion; I will rejoice in your deliverance.

15 The heathen sank in the pit of corruption they made; in the net they hid their foot was caught.

16 The LORD is known by the judgment he has done; by the work of his hands he has made the wicked fall; meditation. Selah.

17 The wicked will return to the grave, all the nations that forget God.

18 For the needy will not be forgotten alway, the expectation of the poor will not perish for ever.

19 Rise up, O the LORD, let not a blood-thirsty man prevail; judge the nations before Your presence.

20 You have set terror, O the LORD, among them; let the nations know that they are but men, Selah.


## Chapter 10

1 Why do you stand afar off, O Jehovah, and hide yourself in times of distress?

2 In the pride of the wicked the poor is set on fire; they are caught in the devices they have imagined.

3 For the wicked praises the greed of his soul, and the greedy man blesses and scorns the LORD.

4 The wicked in his pride does not seek; there is no God in all his thoughts.

5 His ways prosper at all times; your judgments are high out of his sight; as for all his adversaries, he puffs at them.

6 He said in his heart, "I will not be moved, from generation to generation, without calamity."

7 His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and fraud; under his tongue is iniquity and trouble.

8 He lies in ambush in the courts, in secret he murders the poor; his eyes are set to spy out affliction.

9 He lies in wait in a secret place like a young lion in a covert; he lurks to seize a poor person; he seizes a poor person when he draws him into his net.

10 He crouches, he bows down, and the poor fall among his mighty ones.

11 He says in his heart, "God has forgotten; he hides his face, he will never see."

12 Rise up, O LORD; O God, lift up your hand; do not forget the afflicted.

13 Why does the wicked one scorn God? He says in his heart, "You will not require it."

14 You have seen it; for you behold grievance and anger, to take it into your hand: the poor commits himself to you; you have been the helper of the fatherless.

15 Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer; his wickedness you will seek but not find.

16 The LORD is king alway and for ever; the nations perish from his land.

17 The LORD has heard the desire of the humble; you will prepare their heart, you will attend to their ear.

18 To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, so that man will no longer oppress from the land.


## Chapter 11

1 To the conductor, by David. In the LORD I trust; how can you say to my soul, "Flee to your mountain like a bird"?

2 For behold the wicked bend the bow, they make ready their arrow on the string, to shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.

3 For the foundations are destroyed; what can the righteous do?

4 The LORD in his holy temple, the LORD has his throne in the heavens; his eyes behold, his eyelids test the children of men.

5 The LORD tests the righteous, but his soul hates the wicked and the one who loves injustice.

6 He will rain down on the wicked snares, fire and brimstone, a tempest of horrors, the portion of their cup.

7 For the LORD is righteous, he loves justice; the upright shall see his face.


## Chapter 12

1 For the choirmaster; upon the octave, a psalm of David. Save, O the LORD, for the faithful are no more, for the steadfast have vanished from among men.

2 They speak falsely, each one to his neighbour, with a smooth lip and a double heart they speak.

3 May the LORD cut off all prating lips, the tongue of the babbler that speaks great things.

4 who said, "We will make our tongue mighty; our lips are with us; who is lord over us?"

5 From the desolation of the poor, from the groaning of the needy, now I will rise, says the LORD; I will set in safety him who pants for it.

6 The commandments of the LORD are pure commandments, silver refined in a furnace of the earth, purified seven times.

7 You, the LORD, will keep them; You will preserve him from this generation forever.

8 About, the wicked walk; the vilest of mankind are exalted.


## Chapter 13

1 For the director: a psalm of David. How long, O the LORD, will you forget me always? How long will you hide your face from me?

2 How long will I take counsel in my soul, with sorrow in my heart daily? How long will my enemy triumph over me?

3 Look upon me, O LORD my God; enlighten my eyes, lest I sleep the death.

4 Lest my enemy say, "I have overcome him"; my adversaries rejoice when I am shaken.

5 But I have trusted in your favour; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation.

6 I will sing to the LORD, for he has dealt bountifully with me.


## Chapter 14

1 For the director: of David. A fool says in his mind: There is no God. They have corrupted, they have acted abominably; there is no one doing good.

2 The LORD from the heavens looked upon the sons of man to see if there is one who is wise, who seeks God.

3 All have turned aside, altogether corrupt; there is no one who does good, not even one.

4 Do not all workers of affliction, who devour my people as they eat bread, call upon the LORD?

5 There they felt dread, for God is in the generation of the righteous.

6 The counsel of the poor is despised, for the LORD is his refuge.

7 Who will give from Zion deliverance to Israel? When the LORD restores the captivity of his people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad.


## Chapter 15

1 A psalm of David. O the LORD, who may sojourn in your covering and dwell in your holy hill?

2 He walks uprightly and does justice, and speaks truth in his heart.

3 He does not go about as a slanderer with his tongue, does no harm to his fellow, and does not take up a reproach against his neighbour.

4 He is despised in his own eyes, held in low regard, yet he honors those who fear the LORD; he swears to his own hurt and does not change.

5 His silver he did not give at usury, and a bribe against the innocent he did not take; he who does these things will not falter alway.


## Chapter 16

1 A Michtam of David: Guard me, O God, for I have taken refuge in you.

2 You said to the LORD, “My Lord, you are my goodness; apart from you I have nothing.”

3 To the holy ones in the land, they are the majestic ones; all my delight is in them.

4 Their sorrows will multiply; they hasten after another: not shall I pour out their drink offerings from blood, nor will I take up their names upon my lips.

5 The Lord is the portion of my inheritance and my cup; you uphold my lot.

6 Portions fell to me in pleasant places; indeed, my inheritance is beautiful to me.

7 I will bless Jehovah, who has counseled me; even night seasons instruct me in my kidneys.

8 I have set the LORD always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

9 Therefore my heart is glad and my glory rejoices; indeed, my body shall dwell in safety.

10 For you will not abandon my soul to the grave, nor give your faithful one to see destruction.

11 Make me know the path of life: fullness of joys before Your face; pleasures at Your right hand forever.


## Chapter 17

1 A prayer of David. O Jehovah, hear thou my righteous cause; give ear to my cry; attend unto my prayer, which proceedeth not from lips of guile.

2 My judgment will proceed before your face; your eyes will behold what is upright.

3 You have tested my heart, visited me by night, refined me, and found nothing; I have purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.

4 According to the works of a person by the word of your lips I have guarded the paths of the destroyer.

5 You make firm my steps in your paths, so that my feet do not slip.

6 I have called upon you, for you answer me, O God; incline your ear to me, hear my word.

7 How great is your favour, saving those who seek refuge, from those who rise up against them at your right hand.

8 Keep me as the apple of the eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings.

9 From the wicked who oppress me, from my deadly enemies who surround me.

10 Their finest is shut up; their mouth speaks proudly.

11 Our steps now their eyes have set to turn upon the land.

12 Like a (young) lion he longs to pierce, and as a (young) lion sitting in secret places.

13 Arise, O Lord, confront him and bring him down; deliver my soul from the wicked with your sword.

14 From men, by Your hand, O LORD, from men of the world whose portion is in life; You fill their belly with what You have hidden; they are satisfied with children, and leave their excess to their infants.

15 I will behold Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness.


## Chapter 18

1 To the director, by the servant of the LORD, by David, who spoke to the LORD the words of this song, on the day the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. And he said, "You will have mercy on me, the LORD, my strength."

2 The LORD is my rocky stronghold, my castle and deliverer; my God is my rocky strength, in whom I take refuge; my shield and the horn of my salvation, my high fortress.

3 I will call upon Jehovah, who is worthy to be praised, and I shall be saved from my enemies.

4 The bands of death encompassed me, and the torrents of Belial terrified me.

5 The pangs of the grave surrounded me; the snares of death confronted me.

6 In my distress I called upon the LORD, and I cried out to my God; he heard my voice from his temple, and my cry came into his ears.

7 The earth trembled and shook, and the foundations of the mountains quaked and trembled because he was angry.

8 Smoke rose in his nostrils and fire from his mouth consumed; (burning) coals blazed forth from him.

9 He bent the heavens and came down, and thick darkness was under his feet.

10 And he rode upon a cherub and flew, and he was seen upon the wings of wind.

11 He set darkness as his hiding place, all around him his pavilion—dark waters, thick clouds of the sky.

12 From the brightness before him, his thick clouds passed on; hail and burning coals.

13 The LORD thundered from the heavens, and the Most High gave forth his voice, hailstones and burning coals of fire.

14 He sent his arrows and scattered them, and many lightnings, and routed them.

15 The channels of water were seen, the foundations of the habitable world were laid bare by your rebuke, O LORD, by the blast of the breath of your nostrils.

16 He sent from on high, he took me; he drew me out of many waters.

17 Deliver me from my mighty enemy and from those who hate me, for they are too strong for me.

18 They anticipate me in the day of my calamity, and the LORD becomes my stay.

19 He brought me out to a place of breadth; he delivered me because he delighted in me.

20 The LORD will repay me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands he will restore to me.

21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not acted wickedly from my God.

22 For all his judgments are before me, and his statutes I will not transgress.

23 I was blameless with him, and I kept myself from my fault.

24 And the LORD returned to me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands before his eyes.

25 With the faithful you show faithfulness; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless.

26 With the pure you show yourself pure, and with the crooked you deal deviously.

27 For you rescue a poor folk, and humble the outward appearance of the proud.

28 For you will light my lamp, the LORD my God will illumine my darkness.

29 For by you I can crush an army, and by my God I can leap over a wall.

30 God's way is perfect; the word of the LORD is refined; he is a shield to all who take refuge in him.

31 For who is God besides the LORD, and who is a rock if not our God?

32 God is the one girding me with strength and making my way blameless.

33 He makes my legs like hinds; on my high places He causes me to stand.

34 Teaching my hands for war, and my arms of steel do bend the bow.

35 And You have given me the shield of Your salvation, and Your right hand has held me up, and Your gentleness has made me great.

36 Enlarge my step beneath me, and my feet do not slip.

37 I will pursue my enemies and overtake them, and I will not return until they are destroyed.

38 I will crush them, and they will not be able to endure; they will fall under my feet.

39 You have girded me with strength for war; You make those who rise against me bow beneath me.

40 And my enemies you have given into my back (stiff-neck(ed), and those who hate me I have destroyed.

41 They cry out, but there is no savior; they cry to Jehovah, and he does not answer them.

42 I will scatter them like dust before the wind, like mud in the streets.

43 Deliver me from the strife of nations; make me the head of peoples— a people I have not known shall serve me.

44 To a hearing ear they will listen to me; sons of a foreigner will deny me.

45 The sons of the alien will be humbled and will flee from their fortified places.

46 As the LORD lives, and blessed be my rock, and exalted be the God of my salvation.

47 God who gives me vengeance and subdues peoples under me.

48 You deliver me from my enemies, even from those who rise against me; from a man of violence you rescue me.

49 Therefore I will praise you among the nations, O the LORD, and to your name I will sing.

50 the deliverance of his king and showing kindness to his anointed, to David and to his seed for alway.


## Chapter 19

1 For the music director, a psalm of David. The heavens declare the glory of God and the firmament proclaims the work of his hands.

2 Day to day utters speech, and night to night reveals knowledge.

3 There is no speech and no word without their voice being heard.

4 Their line has gone out through all the earth, and their words to the ends of the habitable world; the LORD has set a tent for the sun in them.

5 And he, like a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, rejoices as a mighty man to run the path.

6 From the end of the heavens is his going forth, and his circuit to their uttermost coasts, and there is nothing hidden from his heat.

7 The law of the LORD is perfect, restoring the soul; the testimony of the LORD is trustworthy, making wise the simple.

8 The commandments of the LORD are upright, rejoicing the heart; the precept of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.

9 The fear of the LORD is pure, enduring forever; the judgments of the LORD are true, altogether righteous.

10 More desirable than gold and than fine gold in abundance, and sweeter than honey and honeycomb.

11 Your servant is careful concerning them; in keeping them there is great reward.

12 Who can understand [their] errors? From hidden [sins] cleanse me.

13 Also from presumptuous men spare your servant; let them not rule over me. Then I will be blameless, and I shall be cleared from great rebellion.

14 May the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable before you, the LORD, my rock and my redeemer.


## Chapter 20

1 For the director: a psalm of David. The LORD will answer you in a day of distress, the name of the God of Jacob will protect you.

2 May he send you help from the sanctuary and support you from Zion.

3 May he remember all your gifts and may your burnt offering be accepted Selah.

4 May He grant you according to your heart's desire and fulfill all your counsel.

5 Let us rejoice in your salvation and in the name of our God let us triumph; may the LORD fulfill all your petitions.

6 Now I know that the LORD saves his anointed; he answers him from his holy heavens with the might of his saving right hand.

7 Some on chariots and some on horses do rely, but we by the name of the LORD our God will make mention.

8 They stumbled and fell, but we rose and were strengthened.

9 O the LORD, save! Let the king answer us on the day we call.


## Chapter 21

1 For the music director, a psalm of David. O LORD, the king will rejoice in your strength, and in your salvation how greatly will he rejoice.

2 You gave him the desire of his heart and did not withhold the request of his lips, selah.

3 For you will meet him with abundant blessings; you will set upon his head a crown of fine gold.

4 Life he asked of you; you gave it to him—length of days, forever and ever.

5 Great is his glory in your salvation; beauty and majesty you bestow upon him.

6 For you will set him with blessings forever; you will make him glad with joy in your presence.

7 For the king trusts in the LORD, and through the favour of the Most High he shall not be shaken.

8 Your hand will find all your enemies; your right hand will find those who hate you.

9 You will set them as a fiery furnace at the time of your presence, O LORD; in your anger you will devour them, and fire will consume them.

10 Their bough from the land you destroy, and their seed from among the children of man.

11 For they have plotted adversity against you; they devise a scheme, but will not succeed.

12 When you set them on their back, you will prepare your string against their face.

13 Exalt Jehovah in Your strength; we will sing and make music to Your might.


## Chapter 22

1 For the director, upon Aijeleth of the morning, a psalm of David. My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Far from my deliverance are the words of my roaring.

2 My God, I call by day and you do not answer, and by night, and there is no silence for me.

3 But you are holy, dwelling in the praise of Israel.

4 In you our forefathers trusted; they trusted, and you delivered them.

5 To you they cried and were delivered; in you they trusted and were not put to shame.

6 But I am a worm and not a man, the reproach of humankind and the scorn of the people.

7 All my watchers mock me, they speak with speech, they shake the head.

8 He trusts in the LORD; let him deliver him; let him rescue him, for he delights in him.

9 For you are my support from the womb, my confidence at my mother's breasts.

10 On you I have cast from the womb; from my mother's belly, you are my God.

11 Do not be far from me, for distress is near, for there is no helper.

12 Many young bulls surround me; mighty ones from Bashan encircle me.

13 They opened their mouths against me, a lion tearing and roaring.

14 Like water poured out is my life, and all my bones are disjointed; my mind is like wax, melting within my bowels.

15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd and my tongue sticks to my jaws, and you lay me in the dust of death.

16 For dogs have surrounded me; a company of evildoers has encircled me; like a lion they maul my hands and my feet.

17 I will recount all my bones; they look, they stare at me.

18 They divide my garments among them, and for my clothing they cast a lot.

19 But you, O LORD, do not be far off; my strength, make haste to help me.

20 Deliver my soul from the sword, my life from the power of the dog.

21 Save me from the mouth of the lion, and from the horns of the unicorns you answered me.

22 I will make your name known to my brothers; in the midst of the assembly I will praise you.

23 Fear the LORD, you of Jacob's seed, praise him, all you of Israel's offspring, revere him.

24 For he did not despise nor abhor the affliction of the afflicted, nor hide his face from him; when he cried to him, he heard.

25 From you comes my praise in the great assembly; I will pay my vows before those who fear him.

26 The humble shall eat and be satisfied; they shall praise Jehovah, those who seek him; may your hearts live forever.

27 Let all the ends of the earth remember and turn to the LORD, and let all the families of the nations bow down before your face.

28 For the LORD has the kingship, and rules over the nations.

29 They ate and bowed down, all the wealthy of the earth before him; all who go down to the dust will kneel—yet his soul is not kept alive.

30 Seed shall serve him; it shall be told of the LORD to the generation.

31 They shall come and declare his justice to a people yet to be born, for he has done it.


## Chapter 23

1 A psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

2 In pasture of tender grass he makes me lie down; by waters of resting he leads me.

3 My soul he restores; he guides me in paths of righteousness for the sake of his name.

4 Even though I walk in the valley of the shadow of death I will fear no evil because you are with me your rod and your staff they comfort me

5 You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; you anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows.

6 Only goodness and favour will pursue me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for the length of days.


## Chapter 24

1 A psalm of David. The earth and its fullness belong to the LORD, the habitable world and those who dwell in it.

2 For he founded it upon seas and established it upon rivers.

3 Who may ascend the mount of the LORD, and who may stand in his holy place?

4 With clean hands and a pure heart, who has not lifted up my soul to vanity, nor sworn deceitfully.

5 He will receive blessing from the LORD and justice from the God of his salvation.

6 This generation seeking your face, O Jacob, Selah.

7 Lift up your heads, O gates, and be lifted up, O ancient doors, that the King of glory may enter.

8 Who is this king of glory? The LORD, strong and mighty, the LORD, mighty in battle.

9 Lift up your heads, O gates, and lift up the doors of the world, that the King of glory may enter.

10 Who is this glorious king? The LORD of hosts—he is the glorious king. Selah.


## Chapter 25

1 To David: to you, O Jehovah, I lift up my soul.

2 My God, in you I have trusted; let me not be shamed; let my enemies not rejoice over me.

3 All who hope in him will not be put to shame, but those who deal faithlessly without cause will be put to shame.

4 the LORD, make known to me your ways; teach me your paths.

5 Direct me in your truth and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation; I have waited for you all the day.

6 Remember your compassion and your mercy, O LORD, for they are from of old.

7 Do not remember the sins of my youth and my transgressions; according to your mercy remember me, for the sake of your goodness, the LORD.

8 Good and upright is the LORD; therefore he instructs sinners along the way.

9 he will judge the humble with justice, and teach the humble his way.

10 All the ways of the LORD are steadfast love and faithfulness for those who keep his covenant and his testimonies.

11 For the sake of your name, O LORD, and forgive my sin, for it is great.

12 Who is the man who fears the LORD? He will instruct him in the way he should choose.

13 His soul rests in good, and his offspring will inherit the land.

14 The secret counsel of the LORD is for those who fear him, and his covenant is to make it known to them.

15 My eyes are continually toward the LORD, for he will bring my feet out of the net.

16 Turn to me and be gracious to me, for I am alone and afflicted.

17 The anguish of my heart has grown wide; from my distresses bring me out.

18 See my affliction and my grievance, and lift up all my sin.

19 See how my enemies are many and how hatefully they hate me with violence.

20 Keep my life and deliver me; let me not be put to shame, for I take refuge in you.

21 Integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I have waited for you.

22 Deliver, O God, Israel from all his distresses.


## Chapter 26

1 David. Judge me, O Jehovah, for I have walked in mine integrity; in the Lord have I trusted: I shall not slide.

2 Examine me, O Jehovah, and test me; refine my reins and my heart.

3 For your favour is before my eyes, and I walk in your truth.

4 I did not sit with men of falsehood, nor with those who conceal things will I go.

5 I hate the assembly of evildoers, and with the wicked I will not sit.

6 I will wash my hands in cleanness, and I will encircle your altar, the LORD.

7 to hear the sound of praise and to declare all your wonders.

8 The LORD, I love the dwelling place of your house and the place of the habitation of your glory.

9 Do not gather my soul with sinners, nor my life with men of blood.

10 whose hands are full of heinous crime and whose right hand is full of bribe.

11 But I in my integrity will walk; redeem me and show me favor.

12 My foot stands in the even place; in the congregation I will bless the LORD.


## Chapter 27

1 Of David. The LORD is my light and my salvation; from whom shall I fear? The LORD is the strength of my life; from whom shall I be afraid?

2 When enemies came near me to devour my body, my adversaries and foes stumbled and fell.

3 If an army encamps against me, my heart will not fear; if war rises against me, in this I trust.

4 One thing I have asked of the LORD, that I will seek: to dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the LORD, and to inquire in his temple.

5 For he will hide me in his covert on a day of adversity; he will conceal me in the hiding place of his tabernacle; in a rock he will lift me up.

6 Now my head will be lifted up above my enemies all around me; I will sacrifice sacrifices of shouting in his tent; I will sing and make music to the LORD.

7 Hear, O Lord, my voice when I call; be gracious to me and answer me.

8 To you my heart said: Seek my face; your face, O the LORD, I seek.

9 Do not hide your face from me, do not turn in anger from your servant; you have been my help, do not forsake me or abandon me, O God of my salvation.

10 For my father and my mother have forsaken me, but the LORD will gather me.

11 Teach me your way, O LORD, and lead me in a path of equity, for the sake of my enemies.

12 Do not give me into the hand of my adversary, for false witnesses have risen against me and breathe out violence.

13 If I had not believed to see the fair goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.

14 Wait for the LORD; be strong, and let your heart take courage; wait for the LORD.


## Chapter 28

1 To David. To you, O LORD, I call, O my rock; do not be deaf to me, lest you become silent toward me, and I be like those who go down to the pit.

2 Hear the sound of my supplication when I cry to you, when I lift my hands toward your holy sanctuary.

3 Do not draw me away with sinners or with workers of iniquity who speak peace with their companions, but have evil in their hearts.

4 Give them according to their deeds and according to the evil of their doings; according to the work of their hands give them; render to them their recompense.

5 For they do not understand the works of the LORD nor the deeds of his hands; he will overthrow them and not build them up.

6 Blessed be the LORD, for he has heard the voice of my supplication.

7 The LORD is my strength and my shield; in him my heart trusts, and I am helped; my heart exults, and with my song I will praise him.

8 The LORD is their strength, and the fortress of their salvation is His anointed.

9 Save your people and bless your inheritance, and shepherd them and carry them forever.


## Chapter 29

1 A psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O mighty ones, ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.

2 Give the LORD glory for his name; bow down to the LORD in holy beauty.

3 The voice of the LORD is upon the waters; the God of glory thunders—the LORD is upon many waters.

4 The voice of the LORD in power, the voice of the LORD in majesty.

5 The voice of the LORD shatters cedars; the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.

6 He made them skip like a calf, Lebanon and Sirion like a young bullock.

7 The voice of the LORD hews flames of fire.

8 The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.

9 The voice of the LORD makes the hinds to writhe and strips the forests bare; in his palace, all of it proclaims glory.

10 The LORD sat upon the flood; the LORD sits as king forever.

11 The LORD will give strength to His people; the LORD will bless His people with peace.


## Chapter 30

1 A psalm, a song of the dedicating of the house to David. I will extol you, O the LORD, for you have lifted me up, and have not let my enemies rejoice over me.

2 The LORD my God, I cried to you and you healed me.

3 The LORD has brought up my soul from the grave; you have kept me alive, from going down to the pit.

4 Sing to the LORD, O his faithful ones, and give thanks to the remembrance of his holiness.

5 For a moment in his anger, life in his favor; in the evening weeping stays, but in the morning joy.

6 But as for me, I said in my prosperity: I shall not be moved for ever.

7 The LORD, in your good pleasure, you had established strength for the mountain; you hid your face—I became afraid.

8 To you, O LORD, I call; to my Lord I plead.

9 What profit in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Will the dust praise you? Will it declare your truth?

10 Hear, O Jehovah, and be gracious to me; O Jehovah, be my helper.

11 Thou didst turn my lamentation into dancing for me; Thou didst open my sackcloth and gird me with gladness.

12 so that my glory may sing to you and not be silent, the LORD my God, forever I will praise you.


## Chapter 31

1 For the music director, a psalm of David. In you, the LORD, I have trusted; let me not be ashamed forever; in your justice, deliver me.

2 Incline your ear to me quickly; deliver me. Be for me a rock of strength, a house of fortresses, to save me.

3 For my (ragged) rock and my castle you are, and for the sake of your name you will lead me and guide me.

4 Bring me out of the net they have secretly set for me, for you are my strength.

5 Into your hand I commit my spirit; you have redeemed me, O LORD, faithful God.

6 I hate those who regard lying vanities, but I trust in the LORD.

7 I will rejoice and exult in your favour, who have seen my affliction, known the distresses of my soul.

8 And he did not deliver me into the hand of the enemy; you have set my feet in a broad place.

9 Be gracious to me, O the LORD, for I am distressed; my eye, my soul, and my belly are consumed in anger.

10 For my life is consumed by grief and my years by groaning; my strength fails because of my fault, and my bones waste away.

11 From all my adversaries I became a reproach and exceedingly to my neighbors, and a dread to those who knew me; those seeing me outside fled from me.

12 I am forgotten as a dead man out of mind; I am like a vessel that is broken.

13 For I have heard the defaming of many, terror all around, as they conspire together against me, plotting to take my life.

14 But I on you have relied, the LORD; I said, "My God, you are."

15 In your hand are my times; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from my pursuers.

16 Let your face shine upon your servant; save me in your mercy.

17 O LORD, let me not be put to shame, for I have called upon you; let the wicked be put to shame, let them be turned to the grave.

18 Let the lips of lying speech, speaking against the righteous with arrogance and contempt, be silenced.

19 How abundant is your goodness that you have stored up for those who fear you, which you have worked for those who take refuge in you, in the sight of the children of humankind.

20 You will hide them in the secret of your presence from the pride of man; you will conceal them in a booth from the strife of tongues.

21 Blessed be the LORD, for he has shown his wonderful lovingkindness to me in a besieged city.

22 But I said in my haste, I am cut off before your presence; surely you have heard the voice of my supplications when I cried to you.

23 Love the LORD, all his faithful ones; the LORD preserves the loyal, and repays abundantly the one who acts with pride.

24 Be strong and let your hearts take courage, all you who wait for the LORD.


## Chapter 32

1 A Maschil of David. Happy are those whose rebellion is forgiven, whose sin (offering) is covered.

2 Blessed is the person to whom the LORD does not reckon fault, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.

3 Because I kept silent, my body wasted away from roaring all day long.

4 For day and night your power weighs heavily upon me; my vigor is turned to weakness, in the drought of summer. Selah.

5 My sin I will declare to you and my iniquity I did not conceal. I said, I will confess concerning my transgressions to the LORD, and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

6 For this reason let every faithful one pray to you at a time of finding; when the great floodwaters come, they shall not reach him.

7 You are my covering from distress; you surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.

8 I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; my eye will watch over you.

9 Do not be like a horse or a mule without understanding, whose trappings are bit and bridle to restrain their mouth, otherwise they will not come near you.

10 Many are the griefs of the wicked, but the one who trusts in the LORD, mercy surrounds him.

11 Rejoice in the LORD and be glad, you righteous; and shout for joy, all you upright in heart.


## Chapter 33

1 Sing for joy, you righteous, in the LORD; praise is fitting for the upright.

2 Give praise to the LORD with the harp; with the psaltery of ten strings make music to him.

3 Sing to him a new song; skillfully make a loud joyful sound.

4 For the LORD's act is upright, and all his works are done in faithfulness.

5 Loving justice and judgment, the steadfast love of the LORD fills the earth.

6 By the act of the LORD the heavens were made, and by the breath of his mouth all their host.

7 Gathering the heap of the sea's water, putting [it] into storehouses of the depths.

8 All the earth shall fear from Jehovah; from him shall tremble all the inhabitants of the world.

9 For he spoke, and it came to be; he commanded, and it stood firm.

10 The LORD has frustrated the counsel of the nations; he has thwarted the devices of the peoples.

11 The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the purposes of his heart to all generations.

12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people he has chosen as his inheritance.

13 From the heavens the LORD looked down; he saw all the children of mankind.

14 From his settled place he looked out upon all the inhabitants of the earth.

15 the one who formed their heart as one, the one who understands every deed of theirs

16 A king is not saved by great abundance of might, nor is a mighty man delivered by great strength.

17 False is the horse for deliverance, and by its great strength it cannot save.

18 Behold, the eye of the LORD is toward those who fear him, to those who wait for his mercy.

19 To deliver their soul from death, and to sustain them in famine.

20 Our soul waits for the LORD; he is our help and our shield.

21 For in him our heart shall rejoice, because in his holy name we have trusted.

22 May your favour, O LORD, be upon us, as we have hoped for you.


## Chapter 34

1 Of David, when he changed his behavior before Abimelech, and he drove him away, and he went. I will bless the LORD at all times; his praise is continually in my mouth.

2 In the LORD shall my soul take pride; let the humble hear and rejoice.

3 Magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.

4 I sought the LORD and He answered me, and from all my fears He delivered me.

5 Look to him and be radiant, and let your faces not be ashamed.

6 This poor man cried and the LORD heard, and from all his troubles he saved him.

7 An ambassador of the LORD encamps about those who fear him and delivers them.

8 Taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.

9 Fear the LORD, his holy ones, for there is no lack to those who fear him.

10 Young lions may suffer want and hunger, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.

11 Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.

12 Who is the man who desires life, loving days to see good?

13 Guard your tongue from evil and your lips from speaking deceit.

14 Turn away from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.

15 The eyes of the LORD are toward the righteous, and his ears are toward their cry.

16 The face of the LORD is against those who do evil, to cut off from the land their memory.

17 They cried, and the LORD heard; from all their troubles he delivered them.

18 The LORD is near to the broken-hearted and saves those crushed in spirit.

19 Many are the adversities of the righteous, but from all of them the LORD delivers him.

20 He guards every bone of his body; not one of them is broken.

21 The death of the wicked is evil, and those who hate the righteous will be guilty.

22 The LORD redeems the soul of His servant, and all who take refuge in Him shall not be condemned.


## Chapter 35

1 For David. Jehovah, strive Thou with them that strive with me; fight Thou against them that fight against me.

2 Take firm hold of shield and buckler, and rise up to help me.

3 And brandish the javelin and close in against my pursuers, say to my soul, “I am your deliverance.”

4 Let those who seek my life be ashamed and humiliated; let those who devise evil against me turn back and be disgraced.

5 Let them be like chaff before the wind, and the angel of the LORD driving them on.

6 Let their way be dark and slippery, and the angel of the LORD pursuing them.

7 For without cause they have hidden for me their net in a pit; without cause they have dug for my soul.

8 Let destruction come upon him unknown, and the net he hid ensnare him; in destruction let him fall.

9 And my soul will rejoice in the LORD, will delight in his salvation.

10 All my bones say, "O LORD, who is like you, delivering the poor from one stronger than he, and the poor and needy from the oppressor?"

11 Let witnesses of injustice rise up; those whom I did not know will question me.

12 They repay me evil for good, to bereave my soul of children.

13 But as for me, in their time of distress I clothed myself with sackcloth; I afflicted my soul with fasting, and my prayer returned upon my bosom.

14 I walked about as for a friend, as for my brother; as one mourning for a mother, I bowed down in gloom.

15 In my adversity they rejoiced and gathered against me; enemies gathered without my knowledge; they tore at me and would not cease.

16 In the crookedness of scoffers' feast, they gnash their teeth at me.

17 (my) Lord how long will you look on? rescue my soul from their destruction, from (young) lions my self.

18 I will praise you in a great assembly; among a mighty people I will honor you.

19 Let not my deceitful enemies rejoice over me, and those who hate me wrongfully wink the eye in scorn.

20 For they do not speak peace, and against the quiet of the land, they devise words of treachery.

21 They opened their mouths wide against me, saying, “Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it.”

22 O Jehovah, see and do not be silent; O Lord, do not be far from me.

23 Stir up Yourself and awake to my judgment, my God and my Lord, even to my cause.

24 Judge me, O LORD my God, according to your justice, and let them not rejoice over me.

25 Let them not say in their heart, “We have devoured him”; let them not say, “We have swallowed him up.”

26 Let them be ashamed and confounded together, those who delight in my adversity; let them be clothed with shame and dishonour who magnify themselves against me.

27 Let them sing and rejoice, those who delight in justice; and let them say continually, "May the LORD be magnified, who delights in the peace of his servant."

28 And my tongue shall recite your righteousness, and all day long your praise.


## Chapter 36

1 For the choirmaster, a bondman of the LORD, to David. The saying concerning the transgression of the wicked is within my heart: there is no fear of God before his eyes.

2 For he flatters him in his own eyes, to find his sin and hate it.

3 The acts of his mouth are iniquity and deceit; he has ceased to act wisely or do good.

4 Affliction he devises upon his bed; he sets himself upon a way that is not good; evil he does not reject.

5 The LORD, your steadfast love is in the heavens, your faithfulness reaches the skies.

6 Your justice is like the mighty mountains, your judgments are a great deep; O Lord, you preserve both man and beast.

7 How precious is your favour, O God, and the sons of man take refuge in the shadow of your wings.

8 They will be satisfied with the fatness of your house; from your river of delights you will give them drink.

9 For with you is the fountain of life; in your light we see light.

10 Extend your favour to those who know you, and your justice to the upright in heart.

11 May the leg of pride not come against me, and the hand of the wicked not drive me away.

12 There the workers of iniquity fell, thrust down and unable to rise.


## Chapter 37

1 Of David. Do not be vexed by evildoers; do not envy those who practice wickedness.

2 For as grass quickly withers, and as the green grass fades.

3 Trust in the LORD and do good; dwell in the land and feed on faithfulness.

4 And delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.

5 Cast upon Jehovah your journey, and trust in him, and he will do.

6 And he will bring out your righteousness like bright light, and your judgment like midday.

7 Be silent before the LORD and wait for him; do not fret because of the one prospering in his way, because of the man carrying out wicked schemes.

8 Refrain from anger and forsake wrath; do not grow vexed, only to do evil.

9 For the wicked will be cut off, but those who wait for the LORD, they shall inherit the land.

10 Yet almost a very few and the wicked is not, and you will consider his place and he is not there.

11 And the meek shall inherit the land and delight in the abundance of peace.

12 The wicked plots against the righteous and gnashes at him with his teeth.

13 (my) Lord will laugh at him, for he sees that his day is coming.

14 The wicked have drawn the sword and strung their bow to cast down the poor and needy, to slaughter the upright in way.

15 Their sword will enter their own heart, and their bows will be broken.

16 A little of the righteous is better than the abundance of many wicked.

17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken, but the LORD upholds the righteous.

18 The LORD knows the days of the blameless; their inheritance will be forever.

19 They will not be ashamed in times of adversity; in days of famine they will be satisfied.

20 For the wicked will perish, and the enemies of the LORD will be like the glory of pastures; they vanish, in smoke they vanish.

21 The wicked borrows and does not repay, but the righteous is gracious and gives.

22 For his blessed ones shall inherit the land, but those who curse him shall be cut off.

23 From the LORD are the steps of a man established, and his path he desires.

24 For though he fall, he shall not be cast down, for the LORD upholds his hand.

25 A lad I was, and also I am old, and I have not seen a righteous one forsaken, and his seed seeking bread.

26 All the day he graciously gives and lends, and his offspring is for blessing.

27 Turn from evil and do good, and dwell alway.

28 For the LORD loves justice and will not abandon his faithful ones; they are preserved forever, but the seed of the wicked will be cut off.

29 The righteous will inherit the land and dwell on it forever.

30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.

31 The law of his God is in his mind; his steps will not falter.

32 The wicked watches the righteous and seeks to kill him.

33 The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

34 Wait for the LORD and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land; when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.

35 I saw a wicked man, powerful and spreading like a bay tree, flourishing in his native land.

36 And he passed on, and behold, he was not there; and I sought him, but he was not found.

37 Keep the blameless way and see the upright, for the latter end of a man of peace is peace.

38 And transgressors are destroyed together; the latter end of the wicked is cut off.

39 The deliverance of the righteous is from the LORD; their stronghold in time of trouble.

40 And the LORD helped them and delivered them and rescued them from the wicked and saved them because they trusted in him.


## Chapter 38

1 A psalm of David, for remembrance. O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your wrath, nor discipline me in Your fury.

2 For your arrows have pierced me, and your hand has fallen upon me.

3 There is no soundness in my body because of your indignation; there is no peace in my bones because of my sin.

4 For my iniquities have passed over my head; like a heavy burden they weigh heavily upon me.

5 My wounds are loathsome and corrupt because of my folly.

6 I am weary, I am worn out completely; all the day long I walk in gloom.

7 For my flanks are filled with folly, and there is no soundness in my body.

8 I am faint and sore crushed; I groan because of the disquietness of my heart.

9 (My) Lord, my desire is before You, and my sigh is not hidden from You.

10 My heart is dismayed; my strength has left me, and the light of my eyes is also not with me.

11 My loved ones and my friends stand opposite me because of my wounds, and my kinsmen stand far off.

12 And they that seek my life grew strong, and those that seek my adversity spoke mischiefs, and deceit all the day they devised.

13 But I am like a deaf man, I do not hear; and like a mute man, his mouth does not open.

14 And I became as a man who does not hear, and in whose mouth there are no rebukes.

15 For you, O LORD, I have waited; O my Lord, my God, you will answer.

16 For I said, Lest they rejoice over me; when my leg falters, they magnify themselves against me.

17 For I am a halt from adversity, and my pain is before me alway.

18 I will declare my fault; I will grieve over my sin.

19 But my enemies are strong and alive, and those who hate me without a cause are many.

20 And those who repay adversity for good hate me despite my doing good.

21 Do not forsake me, O LORD; O my God, do not be far from me.

22 Hasten to my help, O my Lord, my deliverance.


## Chapter 39

1 For the music director, a psalm of David. I said, I will guard my ways from sinning with my tongue; I will guard the opening of my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.

2 I was silent and still, silent even from good, and my grief grew worse.

3 My heart is hot within me, in my meditation the fire burns; I speak with my tongue.

4 Make known to me, O the LORD, the end, and the measure of my days what it is, that I may know how frail I am.

5 Behold, You have made my days as hand-breadths; my lifetime is as nothing before You; surely all vanity, every man stands.

6 Sure man walketh in a vain shew, surely he disquieteth himself in vain; he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.

7 And now, what have I hoped for, my Lord? My hope is in you.

8 From all my transgressions deliver me; do not set me the reproach of a fool.

9 I was silent; I did not open my mouth, for you have done.

10 Remove your plague from me; I am worn out by the blows of your hand.

11 With arguments about sin you chastened a man; you made his desirable thing melt like a moth; altogether vanity, every man. Selah.

12 Hear my prayer, O LORD, and attend to my crying; do not be silent at my tears, for I am a sojourner with you, a foreigner, like all my ancestors.

13 Turn from me, that I may take comfort, before I go and am no more.


## Chapter 40

1 For the choirmaster: a psalm of David. I waited long for the LORD; and he inclined to me, and heard my crying.

2 He lifted me up from a pit of tumult, from the mire of clay, and set my feet upon a rocky crag; he made firm my steps.

3 And he put a new song in my mouth, praise to our God; many will see and fear, and trust in the LORD.

4 Blessed is the man who makes the LORD his trust, and does not turn to the proud or to those who turn aside to falsehood.

5 Many are the wonders you have done, O LORD my God, and your purposes toward us; there is none like you; I would declare and speak of them; they surpass all reckoning.

6 Sacrifice and offering you did not desire; my ears you pierced; burnt offering and sin offering you did not require.

7 Then I said, "Here I come; in the roll of the scroll it is written concerning me."

8 To do your will, my God, I delighted, and your law is within my heart.

9 I have done justice in the great congregation; behold, my lips have not spoken falsely; the LORD, You know.

10 Your justice I have not hidden within my heart; your faithfulness and your salvation I have declared; your kindness and your truth I have not concealed from the great assembly.

11 You, the LORD, will not withhold your compassion from me; your kindness and your faithfulness will continually protect me.

12 For surrounding me are adversities beyond number; my iniquities have overtaken me and I cannot see—more than the hairs of my head; my heart has forsaken me.

13 Accept, O Jehovah, to deliver me; O Jehovah, hasten to my help.

14 Let those who seek my life to destroy it be ashamed and confounded; let those who delight in my adversity turn back and be disgraced.

15 They will be ashamed because of their confusion, those who say to me, "Aha, aha."

16 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation say continually, "The LORD be magnified."

17 But I am poor and needy; (my) Lord, think upon me: You are my help and my deliverer—God (gods), do not delay.


## Chapter 41

1 For the director: a psalm of David. Blessed is the one who considers the poor; in the day of adversity the LORD will deliver them.

2 The LORD will keep him and preserve him alive in the land, and will not give him over to the desire of his enemies.

3 The LORD will sustain him on the bed of languishing; all his couch has turned in his sickness.

4 I said, O LORD, be gracious to me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against you.

5 My enemies say evil about me: When will he die and his name perish?

6 But if he comes to see vanity, his heart speaks falsely; he gathers iniquity to himself; he goes out abroad and speaks.

7 All my enemies speak together against me and devise evil against me.

8 An act of wickedness is poured out upon him; he who has lain down will not rise again.

9 Even a certain one at peace with me, in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted up his heel against me.

10 But you, O Jehovah, favor me and raise me up, that I may repay them.

11 By this I knew that You favored me, for my enemy did not triumph over me.

12 But as for me, in my integrity you upheld me and set me before your face forever.

13 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from of old and to perpetuity; amen and amen.


## Chapter 42

1 For the music director, a Maschil of the sons of Korah. As a hart pants for channels of water, so my soul pants for you, God.

2 My soul thirsts for God, for the living God; when will I come and appear before the face of God?

3 My tears have been my food day and night, while they say to me all day long, “Where is your God?”

4 These things I recall, and pour out my soul within me: when I would go with the multitude, leading them in procession to the house of God, with voices of joy and thanksgiving, the great crowd keeping festival.

5 Why are you cast down, my soul, and why do you moan within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, the salvation of his presence.

6 My God, my soul is cast down upon me; therefore I remember you from the land of Jordan and the Hermonites, from Mount Mizar.

7 Deep calls to deep at the sound of your water-spouts; all your waves and billows have passed over me.

8 By day the LORD commands his steadfast love, and in the night his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.

9 Saying to God my rock: Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning in affliction from the enemy?

10 With sword in my bones my enemies reproach me, saying to me all day long, Where is your God?

11 Why are you cast down, my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance and my God.


## Chapter 43

1 Judge me, O God, and plead my cause from a nation not faithful; from a man of deceit and iniquity deliver me.

2 For you are God my fortress, why have you cast me off? Why must I walk in gloom because of the oppression of the enemy?

3 Send your light and your truth; they will guide me; they will bring me to your holy hill and to your dwelling places.

4 And I will come to the altar of God, to God, the exceeding joy of my rejoicing, and I will give thanks to you with the harp, O God, my God.

5 Why are you cast down, my soul, and why do you moan within me? Hope in God, for I will yet praise him, the salvation of my countenance and my God.


## Chapter 44

1 For the music director; by the sons of Korah; a Maschil. God, we have heard with our ears—our ancestors have told us—your deeds you did in their days, in days of old.

2 You with Your hand dispossessed nations and planted them, You afflicted peoples and sent them away.

3 For not by their sword did they inherit the land, nor did their arm save them, but your right hand and your arm and the light of your face, for you favored them.

4 You are he, my king, God; command deliverances for Jacob.

5 In you our adversaries we have crushed; in your name our enemies we trample.

6 For I did not seek deliverance by my bow, and my sword did not save me.

7 For you have saved us from our adversaries, and have put to shame those who hate us.

8 In God we praise all day long, and your name forever we acknowledge. Selah.

9 You have rejected us and disgraced us, and You do not go out with our hosts.

10 You turn us back from the adversary, and those who hate us have plundered for themselves.

11 You give us like sheep for food, and scatter us among the nations.

12 You will sell your folk for no wealth, and you did not profit from their worth.

13 You set us as a reproach to our neighbors, a derision and scorn to those round about us.

14 You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

15 All day long my reproach is before me, and the shame of my face has covered me.

16 From the sound of the taunter and blasphemer, from the presence of the enemy and the avenger.

17 All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten you, nor have we been false to your covenant.

18 Our heart does not draw back, nor do our steps turn from your way.

19 For you have cast us into the place of dragons, and covered us over with the shadow of death.

20 If we have forgotten the name of our God and spread out our hands to a foreign god,

21 Will not God search out this, for he knows the secrets of the heart?

22 For your sake we are killed all day long; we are counted as sheep for slaughter.

23 Awake, why do You sleep, my Lord? Rouse Yourself; do not reject us forever.

24 Why do you hide your face, forgetting our affliction and oppression?

25 For our soul has bowed down to the dust, our belly clings to the earth.

26 Rise up, our help, and redeem us for the sake of your mercy.


## Chapter 45

1 For the music director, upon Shoshannim, by the sons of Korah, a Maschil, a song of loves. My heart is stirred by a noble word; I speak of my works to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful scribe.

2 You are more beautiful than the sons of man; grace is poured upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.

3 Gird your sword upon your thigh, O mighty one; your glory and your majesty.

4 And in your majesty ride on triumphantly for the sake of truth, for humility and righteousness; and your right hand shall teach you awesome deeds.

5 Your arrows are sharp—peoples fall beneath you—wounded in the heart of the king's enemies.

6 Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; a sceptre of equity is the sceptre of your reign.

7 You have loved justice and hated wickedness, therefore your God has anointed you with oil of gladness beyond your companions.

8 Myrrh and aloes and cassia—your apparel is all from palaces of ivory, from the things that make you glad.

9 The daughters of kings were among your honored women; the queen stood at your right hand in fine gold of Ophir.

10 Hear, daughter, and see, and incline your ear, and forget your people and your father's house.

11 Let the king desire your beauty, for he is your lord, and bow to him.

12 And the daughter of Tyre will entreat your face with a gift, the rich of the people.

13 All glorious is the king's daughter within, her clothing is of golden wrought work.

14 For broidered work, she is brought to the king; her maids, her companions, are brought to you.

15 They shall be brought in with joy and gladness; they shall enter into the king's palace.

16 Instead of your fathers will be your sons; you shall appoint them as rulers throughout all the land.

17 I will remember your name throughout every generation; therefore the peoples will praise you forever and ever.


## Chapter 46

1 For the music director, by the sons of Korah, upon Alamoth, a song. God is our refuge and strength, a help in distresses found exceedingly.

2 Therefore we will not fear when the earth changes and the mountains shift in the midst of the seas.

3 Let his waters be troubled and grow hot; let the mountains tremble at his excellency. Selah.

4 A river and its streams make glad the city of God, the holy dwelling place of the Most High.

5 God in her midst—she shall not be moved; God shall help her at morning's dawn.

6 The Gentiles rage, kingdoms fall at the sound of his voice; the earth melts.

7 The LORD of hosts is with us, a stronghold for us is the God of Jacob. Selah.

8 Come, see the works of the LORD, what desolations he has made in the land.

9 He makes wars cease to the end of the land; he breaks the bow and shatters the spear; chariots he burns with fire.

10 Be still and know that I am God, exalted among the nations, exalted in the earth.

11 The LORD of hosts is with us, a stronghold for us is the God of Jacob. Selah.


## Chapter 47

1 For the music director, a psalm of the sons of Korah. All the peoples clap hands, shout to God with a voice of rejoicing.

2 For the LORD is most high and awesome, a great king over all the earth.

3 May He subdue folk under us, and nations under our feet.

4 He chooses our heritage, the excellency of Jacob whom He loves, Selah.

5 God ascended with a shout; the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.

6 Sing to God, sing! Sing to our King, sing!

7 For God is king over all the earth; sing a skillful song.

8 God reigns over the nations; God sits on the throne of his holiness.

9 The nobles of the folk are gathered, the people of the God of Abraham, for to the LORD belong the shields of the earth; he is greatly exalted.


## Chapter 48

1 A song, a psalm for the sons of Korah. Great is the LORD and highly praised in the city of our God, his holy mountain.

2 A beautiful situation, the joy of all the earth, Mount Zion, the sides of the north, the city of the great king.

3 God is known in her palaces for a stronghold.

4 For behold, the kings have gathered together, they have passed by in unity.

5 They saw, indeed they were amazed; they were astounded, they were seized with panic.

6 Trembling seized them there, pain like a woman in labor.

7 With an east wind you will shatter ships of Tarshish.

8 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish her forever. Selah.

9 Consider, O God, Your mercy in the midst of Your temple.

10 As your name, O God, so your praise reaches to the ends of the earth; your right hand is full of justice.

11 Let Mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad because of your judgments.

12 Circle Zion and encompass her; count her towers.

13 Set your minds on the bulwark; break down her palaces, so that you may recount it to a future generation.

14 For this is God, our God, forever and ever; he will guide us even unto death.


## Chapter 49

1 For the director of music, by the sons of Korah, a psalm. Hear this, all peoples; give ear, all inhabitants of the world.

2 Even the children of men, even the children of a person, together the rich and the poor.

3 My mouth will speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart will be understanding.

4 I will incline my ear to a byword; I will open my dark saying upon the harp.

5 Why should I fear in the time of evil, when the iniquity of my heel surrounds me?

6 Those who trust in their strength and in the abundance of their riches boast.

7 No brother can ransom a man; he cannot give to God his ransom.

8 And costly is the ransom of their soul; it ceases forever.

9 And he shall yet live alway; he shall not see destruction.

10 For the wise see that they die, the fool and the brute alike perish, and leave their wealth to others.

11 Their inward thought is that their houses are forever, their habitations for generation and generation; they call their lands by their names.

12 Yet a person of honour does not remain; they are like beasts that perish.

13 This is their way, folly to them; and after them, with their mouths, they approve. Selah.

14 Like sheep they are appointed for the grave; death shall feed them, and the upright shall rule over them in the morning; their form shall be consumed by the grave, away from their dwelling.

15 Only God will ransom my soul from the power of the grave, for he will take me. Selah.

16 Do not fear when a man becomes rich, when the glory of his house increases.

17 For not in his death will he take it all; his glory will not go down after him.

18 For his soul blesses himself in his life, and men will praise you when you do well to him.

19 He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.

20 Man, though honoured, does not understand; he is compared to the beasts that perish.


## Chapter 50

1 A psalm of Asaph. God, the LORD God, spoke and summoned the earth from the rising of the sun to its going down.

2 From Zion, the perfection of beauty, God has shone forth.

3 Our God comes and does not keep silence; fire devours before him, and around him a very great storm rages.

4 He will call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, to judge his people.

5 Gather for me my faithful ones, those who make a covenant over a sacrifice.

6 The heavens declare his righteousness, for God is the judge. Selah.

7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak, O Israel, and I will testify against you: I am God, your God.

8 I do not rebuke you for your offerings, nor are your burnt offerings continually before me.

9 I will not take a bull from your house, nor goats from your folds.

10 For mine is every wild beast of the forest, the cattle on a thousand hills.

11 I know every bird of the mountains, and the wild beasts of the field are mine.

12 If I were hungry, I would not say to you, for the habitable part and all that is in it is mine.

13 Shall I eat the flesh of bulls and drink the blood of goats?

14 Sacrifice a thanksgiving offering to God and fulfill your vows to the Most High.

15 Call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you will honor me.

16 And to the wicked one God said, "What have you to do to recite my statutes, and take up my covenant with your mouth?"

17 But you have hated instruction and cast my words behind you.

18 If you saw a thief and ran with him, your portion is with adulterers.

19 Your mouth has sent forth evil and your tongue frames deceit.

20 You sit and speak against your brother; you slander your mother's son.

21 These things you have done, and I kept silence; you thought I would be like you—but I will rebuke you and set before your eyes.

22 Understand this now, you who forget God, lest I tear you apart, and there be no deliverer.

23 He who sacrifices a thank offering glorifies me; and to him who orders his way, I will show the salvation of God.


## Chapter 51

1 For the choirmaster: a psalm of David. Be gracious to me, O God, according to your mercy; according to the abundance of your tender mercies, blot out my transgression.

2 When Nathan the prophet came to him, like as he came to Bath-sheba. Wash me from my fault, and from my sin purify me.

3 For my transgressions I know, and my sin is ever before me.

4 Against you alone have I sinned, and done what is evil in your sight, so that you may be justified in your word, vindicated in your judgment.

5 Behold, in iniquity I was brought forth, and in sin my mother conceived me.

6 Surely you desire true inward parts, and in the hidden place you will make wisdom known to me.

7 Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean; wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.

8 You will make me hear gladness and joy; my crushed bones will rejoice.

9 Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.

10 Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.

11 Do not cast me from your presence, and do not take your holy spirit from me.

12 Restore to me the joy of your salvation, and a willing spirit sustain me.

13 I will teach transgressors your ways, and sinners shall return to you.

14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, God of my salvation; my tongue shall sing aloud of Your righteousness.

15 (my) Lord, open my lips, and my mouth will declare your praise.

16 For you do not delight in sacrifice; if I offered a burnt offering, you would not accept.

17 The offerings of God are a broken spirit; a broken and crushed heart, O God, you will not despise.

18 Do with favor toward Zion; build the walls of Jerusalem.
