19 Then you will delight in righteous sacrifices, in burnt offerings and whole offerings; then bulls will go up on your altar.


## Chapter 52

1 For the director, a Maschil of David. Why do you boast in adversity, you mighty one? The steadfast love of God endures all day long.

2 When Doeg the Edomite came, he told Saul, and said to him, David came to Ahimelech's house. Mischief you plan with your tongue, like a sharpened razor working deceit.

3 You have loved evil rather than good, lying rather than speaking justice. Selah.

4 You have loved the words of a devourer, a deceitful tongue.

5 Indeed God will utterly destroy you forever; he will snatch you from your tent and uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.

6 And the righteous will see and fear, and at him they will laugh.

7 Behold, the man who does not make God his strength, but trusts in the abundance of his riches, grows strong in his very wickedness.

8 But I am like an olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the steadfast love of God forever and ever.

9 I will praise you alway for you have done it, and I will wait for your name because it is good before your faithful ones.


## Chapter 53

1 For the choirmaster; upon Mahalath; a Maschil of David. The fool said in his heart, "There is no God." They have corrupted, they are defiled with iniquity; there is no one who does good.

2 God looked down from heaven upon the children of man to see if there is one who is wise, seeking after God.

3 The whole of them altogether have perished; there is no doer of good, not even one.

4 Do not those who work affliction, who eat my people as food, eat bread? They do not call upon God.

5 There they were seized by a dread where no fear had been, for God scattered the bones of those who encamped against you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.

6 Who will give from Zion deliverance to Israel? When God restores the captivity of his people, Jacob will rejoice, Israel will be glad.


## Chapter 54

1 For the music director, with stringed instruments, a Maschil of David. God, by your name save me, and by your strength judge me.

2 When the Ziphites came, they said to Saul, Is not David hiding with us? God, hear my prayer; attend to the words of my mouth.

3 For strangers have risen against me, and tyrants have sought my life; they have not set God before them. Selah.

4 Behold, God is my helper; the LORD is the support of my soul.

5 He will return evil to my enemies; in your faithfulness destroy them.

6 With a free-will offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, the LORD, for it is good.

7 For in all adversity he delivered me, and my eye looked upon my enemies.


## Chapter 55

1 For the director, with stringed instruments, a Maschil of David. Give ear, O God, to my prayer and do not hide from my supplication.

2 Hear me and answer me; I am faint in my babbling, and I moan.

3 From the sound of the enemy, from the face of the oppression of the wicked, for they bring down affliction upon me, and in their anger they hate me.

4 My heart trembles within me and terrors of death fall upon me.

5 Dread and trembling came upon me, and horror overwhelmed me.

6 And I said, Who will give me a winged part like a dove? I would fly away and find rest.

7 Behold, I will go far in wandering, lodging in the wilderness, Selah.

8 I hasten my escape from the stormy wind.

9 O (my) Lord, divide their language, for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

10 By day and night they encircle her walls, and affliction and toil are within her.

11 Calamity within her, and deceit and treachery do not cease from her streets.

12 For it is not an enemy who taunts me—then I could bear it; it is not one who hates me who has grown insolent toward me—then I could hide from him.

13 But you, a mere mortal like me, my equal, my trusted friend.

14 where we together sweeten counsel, in the house of God we walk with fervor.

15 Let death come upon them; let them go down alive to Sheol, for evil is in their dwelling and among them.

16 I call to God for help, and the LORD will save me.

17 Evening, morning, and midday I will speak and cry out, and he will hear my voice.

18 He delivered my soul in peace from battle, for many were with me.

19 May God hear and answer them, even the One who sits in ancient times, Selah; those who have no changes and do not fear God.

20 He stretched out his hand against his peaceful ones; he profaned his covenant.

21 The smooth words of his mouth are like butter, yet war is in his heart; his words are softer than oil, but they are drawn swords.

22 Cast your burden on the LORD, and he will sustain you; he will never allow the righteous to be moved.

23 But you, O God, will bring them down into the pit of destruction—men of blood and deceit will not live out half their days—yet I will trust in you.


## Chapter 56

1 For the director, on a dove of silence afar off, a Michtam of David, when the Philistines held him in Gath. Have mercy on me, O God, for a man has overwhelmed me; all the day long he fights and oppresses me.

2 My enemies have been devouring me all day long, for many are exalting themselves against me on high.

3 In time of fear I will trust in you.

4 In God I will praise his word; in God I have trusted; I will not fear: what can flesh do to me?

5 All the day their words grieve me; against me are all their thoughts for evil.

6 They lurk at my heels; they watch my soul, as I have longed.

7 For iniquity, deliver them; in anger bring down the peoples, O God.

8 My wandering you have recorded; set my tears in your bottle; have you not learned them in your scroll?

9 Then my enemies will turn back on the day I call; this I know, that God is for me.

10 In God I will praise the word; in the LORD I will praise the word.

11 In God I have trusted; I will not fear—what can a mere person do to me?

12 To you, O God, are my vows; I will render thanksgiving to you.

13 For you have delivered my soul from death, have you not kept my feet from falling, to walk before God in the light of the living?


## Chapter 57

1 For the music director; do not destroy, a Michtam of David, when he fled from Saul's presence in the cave. Be gracious to me, O God, be gracious to me, for in you my soul has taken refuge; in the shadow of your wings I will take shelter until the calamity passes by.

2 I call to God Most High, to the God who fulfills his purpose for me.

3 He sends from heaven and saves me, he rebukes the one who tramples me; Selah. God sends his kindness and his truth.

4 My soul is among lions; I lie among the sons of men who are burning coals, their teeth are a spear and arrows, their tongue is a sharp sword.

5 Exalted be God over the heavens, your glory over all the earth.

6 They have prepared a net-work for my feet; they have brought down my life; they have dug a pit before me; they have fallen into the midst of it. Selah.

7 My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and make music.

8 Awake, my glory; awake, psaltery and harp; I will awaken the morning.

9 I will give thanks to you among the folk, my Lord; I will sing praise to you among the nations.

10 For great to the heavens is your kindness, and to the skies is your faithfulness.

11 Exalt over the heavens, O God; over all the earth your glory.


## Chapter 58

1 For the director; do not destroy; a Michtam of David. Will you indeed speak justice, O congregation? Will you judge uprightly, O sons of men?

2 Even with a heart of wickedness you devise violence on the earth; the cruelty of your hands you make to prevail.

3 The wicked are estranged from the womb; they go astray from the belly, speaking lies.

4 Their anger is like the venom of a serpent, like the deaf adder that stops its ear.

5 who does not listen to the sound of enchanters, of a company of enchanters.

6 God, break the teeth in their mouth; the LORD shatter the great teeth of the young lions.

7 Let them melt away like water that runs off; when he bends his arrows, let them be as if cut off.

8 Like a snail that melts, it will go away; like a stillborn child, it will not see the sun.

9 Before your pots feel the thorn, like the living, like the fierce wrath, he will sweep it away.

10 The righteous will rejoice when he sees vengeance; his feet will trample the blood of the wicked.

11 And the common sort shall say, Surely reward for the righteous! Indeed, there are divine judges on the earth!


## Chapter 59

1 For the director: do not destroy, a Michtam of David, when Saul sent men to watch his house to kill him. Deliver me from my enemies, O God; by my adversaries exalt me.

2 Deliver me from the workers of affliction and save me from men of blood.

3 For behold, they lie in wait for my soul; fierce men set upon me, though I have no rebellion and no sin, O the LORD.

4 Without fault they run and prepare themselves; awake to meet me and see.

5 But you, O LORD God of hosts, God of Israel, arise to punish all the nations; do not show favor to any wicked transgressor, Selah.

6 They return at evening, they howl like dogs, and they prowl about the city.

7 Behold, they spew swords from their mouths, with their lips, for who is listening.

8 But you, O LORD, laugh at them; you mock all the nations.

9 His strength I will keep for you, for God is my Misgab.

10 God goes before me; God will let me see the face of my enemy.

11 Do not kill them, lest my people forget; drive them away by your strength and bring them down, our shield, O Lord.

12 For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips, let them be caught in their pride, and for cursing and lying which they speak.

13 Consume them in wrath, consume them so that they are no more; and let them know that God rules over Jacob, to the ends of the earth. Selah.

14 They return at evening, they howl like a dog, and they prowl about the city.

15 They wander about for food; if they are not satisfied, they spend the night.

16 But I will sing of your strength and will rejoice in your mercy at morning, for you have been a fortress for me and a refuge in the day of distress.

17 My strength to you I will sing; for God is my stronghold, the God of my mercy.


## Chapter 60

1 For the director, upon Shushan-eduth, a Michtam of David, for teaching. God, you have rejected us, you have broken us open, you have been angry; turn back to us.

2 When he had routed Aham-naharaim and Aram-zobah, Joab returned and struck Edom in the valley of salt, twelve thousand. You shook the land and split it open; heal its breaches, for it totters.

3 You showed your people hardship; you made them drink a wine of trembling.

4 You have given a banner to those who fear you, to be displayed because of the truth. Selah.

5 So that your beloved ones may be delivered, save with your right hand and answer us.

6 God spoke in his holiness: I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and the valley of Succoth I will measure.

7 Mine is Gilead and mine is Manasseh, and Ephraim is the strength of my head; Judah is my ruler.

8 Moab is a wash-pot; upon Edom I will cast my shoe; over Philistia I will triumph.

9 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?

10 Is it not You, God, who has rejected us, and You, O God, do not go out with our armies?

11 Give us help from distress, for human deliverance is vain.

12 By God we shall do valiantly, and He shall trample our adversaries.


## Chapter 61

1 For the choirmaster, upon a song by David. Hear, O God, my cry; attend to my prayer.

2 From the end of the earth I call to you when my heart grows faint; lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

3 For you were my refuge, a tower of strength from the face of the enemy.

4 I will dwell in your covering alway(s); I will hide in the secret of your wing(s) selah.

5 For you, God, have heard my vows; you have given an inheritance to those who fear your name.

6 May days be added to the king's life; may his years extend generation after generation.

7 He sits before God; lovingkindness and truth will preserve him.

8 So I will continually sing of your name, to fulfill my vows day by day.


## Chapter 62

1 For the music director, on Jeduthun, a psalm of David. Only to God be silent, my soul; from him is my salvation.

2 But he is my rock and my deliverance, my Misgab; I shall not greatly falter.

3 How long will you keep attacking a certain man? Will you all murder him? Every one of you is like a leaning wall, a fence about to fall.

4 Only from his dignity do they plot to cast him down; they delight in falsehood; with his mouth they bless, but in their heart they curse. Selah.

5 Yet to God my soul waits in silence, for from him comes my hope.

6 Yet he is my rock and my deliverance, my Misgab; I shall not be moved.

7 On God is my salvation and my glory, the rock of my strength, my refuge in God.

8 Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out before him your hearts; God is our refuge. Selah.

9 Surely, the children of man are altogether vain; the children of a person are deceitful—they are weighed in balances; together they are altogether vanity.

10 Do not trust in oppression or rely on robbery; do not set your heart on riches even if they increase.

11 One thing God has spoken, two things have I heard: that power belongs to God.

12 But to you, my Lord, is mercy, for you repay each man according to his deeds.


## Chapter 63

1 A psalm of David when he was in the wilderness of Judah. God, You are my God, I thirst for You, my soul longs for You, my body yearns for You in a dry and weary land without water.

2 So in the sanctuary I beheld you, to see your might and your glory.

3 For your favour is better than life; my lips will praise you.

4 So will I bless you in my life; by your name I will lift my hands.

5 As the richest fatness satisfies my soul, my lips shall praise with joyful song.

6 If I remember you on my bed, in the watches I meditate on you.

7 For you were my help, and in the shadow of your wings I will rejoice.

8 My soul clings to you; your right hand upholds me.

9 But they seek desolation for my soul; they shall go down into the lowest parts of the earth.

10 They shall be given over to the sword; their portion shall be for foxes.

11 And the king will rejoice in God; everyone who swears by him will exult, for the mouth of those speaking falsely will be silenced.


## Chapter 64

1 For the choirmaster, a psalm of David. Hear, O God, my voice in my prayer; from the fear of the enemy you will guard my life.

2 Hide me from the secret counsel of the workers of iniquity, from the company of those who do evil.

3 They sharpened their tongues like a sword, they bent their bitter word as a dart.

4 They shoot from the secret place at the blameless; suddenly they shoot without fear.

5 They devise an evil act against him; they conspire to set traps; they say, "Who will see them?"

6 They search for iniquity; they have completed a thorough search; the inward part of a man and his deep heart.

7 God suddenly raised an arrow; their wounds came.

8 They made him stumble by their tongue; all who see them will shake the head.

9 All the people feared and declared the work of God, and his deeds they discerned.

10 The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him, and all the upright in heart will praise.


## Chapter 65

1 A psalm, a song of David, for the director. To you, silence is praise, O God in Zion, and to you shall be paid a vow.

2 Hearing prayer, to you all flesh shall come.

3 The acts of my iniquities have prevailed over me; our transgressions, you will atone for them.

4 Blessed are those You choose and draw near to dwell in Your courts; they are satisfied with the goodness of Your house, Your holy temple.

5 Terrible things in justice will You answer us, O God of our salvation, confidence of all the ends of the earth and of the faraway sea.

6 By his power he establishes the hills, girded with strength.

7 the one who quiets the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the tumult of the peoples

8 The dwellers at the uttermost edges feared your signs; the goings out of morning and evening sing praise.

9 You visited the land and watered it; you greatly enriched it: God's river is full of water; you prepare their wheat, for thus you prepare it.

10 You drench her furrows; you settle her ridges; you soften her with showers; you bless her sprouting.

11 You have crowned the year with your goodness, and your paths overflow with fatness.

12 The pastures of the wilderness shall drip, and the little hills shall rejoice with gladness.

13 The lambs are clothed with pasture, and the valleys are covered with wheat; they cry out, yes, they sing.


## Chapter 66

1 For the music director, a song, a psalm: shout to God, all the earth.

2 Sing to the glory of his name; give honour to his praise.

3 Say to God, how awesome are your deeds; through the abundance of your strength, your enemies will feign obedience to you.

4 All the earth will worship you and sing praises to you; they will sing praises to your name. Selah.

5 Go and see the works of God, a fearful deed upon the children of man.

6 He turned the sea into dry land; through the river they passed on foot; there we rejoiced in him.

7 He who rules in His might forever, His eyes upon the nations watch: rebels shall not escape, Selah.

8 Bless, O folk, our God, and cause to be heard the voice of his praise.

9 who sets our soul among the living and does not allow our foot to slip

10 For you have tested us, God, and refined us like silver.

11 Have you brought us into the snare? You have placed affliction in our loins.

12 You set another over us as head; we came into fire and into water, and You brought us out to wealth.

13 I will come to your temple with burnt offerings; I will fulfill to you my vows.

14 whose lips have burst open, and my mouth speaks in distress.

15 Burnt offerings of fatlings I will bring up to you, with the incense of rams; I will make beeve with male goats, Selah.

16 Come, hear, and I will tell all who fear God what he has done for my soul.

17 To him my mouth cried and exalted under my tongue.

18 If I have seen affliction in my heart, the LORD will not hear.

19 Truly, God has heard; he has attended to the sound of my prayer.

20 Blessed be God who has not turned away my prayer nor his mercy from me.


## Chapter 67

1 For the music director, with stringed instruments; a psalm, a song. May God favor us and bless us; may he cause his face to shine upon us, Selah.

2 To make your way known in the land, your salvation among all nations.

3 The folk will praise you, O God; the folk will praise you, all of them.

4 Let the nations rejoice and exult, for you will judge the peoples with equity, and you will guide the nations in the land. Selah.

5 The folk will praise you, O God; the folk will praise you, all of them.

6 The land yields its increase; may God, our God, bless us.

7 May God bless us, and may all the ends of the earth fear him.


## Chapter 68

1 For the director of music, a psalm of David, a song. Let God arise, let his enemies be scattered, and let those who hate him flee before his face.

2 As smoke is blown away, so blow them away; as wax melts before fire, let the wicked perish from the presence of God.

3 And the righteous will rejoice, they will exult before God and will delight in joy.

4 Sing to God, make music to his name; praise him who rides upon the wilderness, his name is Yah, exult before his presence.

5 The chief of the fatherless and judge of the widows is God in his holy habitation.

6 God makes the solitary dwell in a home; he brings out prisoners with chains, but the rebellious dwell in dry land.

7 God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness, Selah.

8 The earth trembled, even the heavens dropped from the presence of God, this Sinai, from the presence of God, the God of Israel.

9 A shower of free offerings you rain down, O God; your inheritance, though weary, you have established.

10 Your living creatures dwell in her; in your goodness, O God, you prepare for the poor.

11 (my) Lord will give a word: the women who proclaim it are a great host.

12 Kings of the hosts will tremble, they will tremble, and the temple will divide the spoil.

13 If you lie between the hooks, wings of a dove covered in silver, and her pinions in golden yellow.

14 When the Almighty scattered kings there, it snowed on Zalmon.

15 mount of God, mount of Bashan, mount of the high ones, mount of Bashan

16 Why do you flee, O high hills? This mount has God desired for His dwelling; indeed, the LORD will abide there always.

17 A multitude of angels, thousands upon thousands, the LORD among them at Sinai in holiness.

18 You went up to the height, you took captive the captivity, you received gifts among humanity, even rebellious ones, so that the LORD God might dwell.

19 Blessed be the LORD day by day; he is our God, our deliverance. Selah.

20 God is for us a God of salvations, and to God the LORD belong the issues from death.

21 But God will crush the head of his enemies, the crown of hair of him who walks in their guiltiness.

22 (my) Lord said, "I will bring back from Bashan, I will bring back from the depths of the sea."

23 so that your foot may trample in blood, and the tongue of your dogs may have its portion from your enemies.

24 They saw your going, O God, the going of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.

25 The singers go before, the musicians after, among the damsels with timbrels.

26 In the congregations bless God; the LORD from the fountain of Israel.

27 There Benjamin, the youngest, rules; the captains of Judah pelted them; the captains of Zebulun, and the captains of Naphtali.

28 Your God has commanded your strength; strengthen, O God, what you have done for us.

29 From your temple in Jerusalem, kings will bring you a present.

30 Rebuke the wild beast of the reeds, the congregation of the mighty among the calves of the peoples, trampling pieces of silver; scatter the peoples who delight in war.

31 Princes from Egypt will come; Ethiopia will stretch out her hands to God.

32 Kingdoms of the world sing to God, make music to the LORD, Selah.

33 To the one riding on the heavens of ancient heavens—behold, he gives forth his voice, the voice of power.

34 Give strength to God over Israel his pride, and his power in the heavens.

35 The awesome God from your holy places, the God of Israel, he gives strength and power to the people; blessed be God.


## Chapter 69

1 For the choirmaster; upon Shoshannim; of David. Save me, O God, for the waters have come up to my soul.

2 I have sunk into miry deep where there is no standing; I have come into the depths of water and the flood overwhelms me.

3 I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched; my eyes fail while I wait for my God.

4 Many are the hairs of my head, those who hate me without cause; mighty are my destroyers, my enemies who lie; those whom I did not despoil, against them I must restore.

5 God, You have known my folly, and my sins have not been hidden from You.

6 May those who wait upon you not be put to shame because of me, O my Lord, God of hosts; may those who seek you be not confounded because of me, O God of Israel.

7 For your sake I have borne reproach; shame has covered my face.

8 I was a stranger to my brothers, and an alien to the sons of my mother.

9 For the zeal of your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of your reproachers have fallen upon me.

10 I wept and fasted with a soul of mine, and it became a reproach to me.

11 And I clothed myself with sackcloth, and became a byword to them.

12 Those who sit at the city gate speak of me, and I am the song of those who drink strong drink.

13 But as for me, my prayer to you, O the LORD, is at a time of favor; in your abundant steadfast love, O God, answer me with the faithfulness of your salvation.

14 Deliver me from the mire, that I may not sink; deliver me from my haters and from the deep waters.

15 Do not let the flood of waters sweep me away, do not let the deep swallow me, and do not let the pit close its mouth over me.

16 Answer me, O LORD, according to the goodness of your favour; according to the abundance of your tender mercies, turn to me.

17 Do not hide your face from your servant, for I am in distress; answer me quickly.

18 Draw near to my soul, redeem it for the sake of my enemies.

19 You know my reproach and my shame and my dishonour; all my adversaries are before You.

20 Reproach has broken my heart; I am faint. I looked for sympathy, but there was none; for comforters, I found not a one.

21 They gave me gall for my meat, and for my thirst they made me drink vinegar.

22 Let their table become a snare before them, and their peace a trap.

23 Their eyes are darkened from seeing, and their loins are continually unsteady.

24 Pour out your indignation on them, and let the fierceness of your anger overtake them.

25 Let their habitation be desolate; let no one dwell in their tents.

26 For you struck down the pursuer, and to the grief of your slain they will recount.

27 Assign punishment for their sin and let them not come into your justice.

28 May they be blotted out of the book of life and not be written with the righteous.

29 But I am poor and afflicted; your deliverance, O God, will lift me up.

30 I will praise the name of God with song and magnify him with thanksgiving.

31 And it shall be pleasing to the LORD more than a young bull with horns and hoofs.

32 The meek shall rejoice; those who seek God shall have their hearts encouraged.

33 For the LORD hears the poor, and he does not despise his prisoners.

34 Let the heavens and the earth praise him, the seas and every thing that moves in them.

35 For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah, and they will dwell there and possess it.

36 And the seed of his servants shall inherit it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.


## Chapter 70

1 For the choirmaster; of David; for commemoration. God, to deliver me; the LORD, make haste to my help.

2 Let those who seek my life be ashamed and confounded; let those who delight in my adversity turn back and be disgraced.

3 They will turn back because of the shame they have suffered, who say, "Aha, aha."

4 Let all who seek you rejoice and be glad in you; let those who love your salvation continually say, "The LORD is great!"

5 But I am poor and needy; O God, hasten to me; my help and my deliverer are You, O LORD; do not delay.


## Chapter 71

1 In you, O LORD, I have trusted; let me not be put to shame forever.

2 By your justice you will rescue me and deliver me; incline your ear to me and save me.

3 Be to me a rock of habitation to which I may continually come; you have commanded to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.

4 My God, deliver me from the hand of the wicked, from the power of the violent and cruel.

5 For you are my hope, O Lord God, my confidence from my youth.

6 On you I have leaned from the womb; from my mother's belly you are my support; in you my praise is alway.

7 I became a wonder to many, and you are my strong refuge.

8 May my mouth be filled with your praise, all the day with your glory.

9 Do not cast me away when old age comes; do not forsake me when my strength fails.

10 For my enemies said of me, and those who watch over my soul took counsel together.

11 saying, God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is no deliverer.

12 God, do not be far from me; my God, hasten to my help.

13 Let them be ashamed and confounded who seek my soul; let them be reproached and shamed who desire my adversity.

14 But I alway will hope, and increase upon all thy praise.

15 My mouth shall declare your righteousness all the day your salvation, for I do not know the number.

16 I will come with the force of my Lord God; I will mention your justice alone.

17 God has taught me from my youth, and even to this day I declare your wonders.

18 Even to old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I declare your strength to the generation, to everyone who will come your mighty power.

19 Your justice, O God, reaches to the heights; you have done great things, O God, who is like you?

20 You who have shown us many troubles and evils will revive us again, and from the depths of the earth you will bring us up again.

21 May the increase of my dignity return and comfort me.

22 I will also praise you with the psaltery, your truth, my God; I will sing to you with the harp, O Holy One of Israel.

23 My lips will shout for joy because I sing to you, and my soul which you have redeemed.

24 My tongue also shall continually speak of your righteousness, for they are ashamed who seek my adversity.


## Chapter 72

1 Give Solomon your justice, O God, and your righteousness to the king's son.

2 Judge your folk in justice and your afflicted with judgment.

3 Let the mountains bring peace to the people, and the little hills by justice.

4 He will judge the poor of the folk, save the children of the needy, and crush the oppressor.

5 They shall fear you with the sun and before the moon, generation after generation.

6 He will come down like rain upon the mown grass, like showers watering the earth.

7 A righteous one will flourish in his lifetime, and great peace will abound until the moon is no more.

8 And he shall come from sea to sea, and from the river to the uttermost parts of the earth.

9 Before him the coastlands will bow, and his enemies dust will lick.

10 The kings of Tarshish and the isles shall bring tribute; the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.

11 And to him shall all kings bow down; all nations shall serve him.

12 For he will deliver the poor when he cries, and the needy, when he has no helper.

13 He will have pity on the lowly and the poor, and the souls of the poor he will save.

14 from fraud and from violence he will redeem their soul, and their blood is precious in his sight.

15 May he live and receive from the gold of Sheba, and may one pray for him continually; all the day long may he be blessed.

16 Let a handful of wheat flourish in the land on the top of the mountains; its fruit shall shake like Lebanon; and those from the city shall blossom like grass of the earth.

17 May his name endure forever; may his name continue as long as the sun. In him all nations shall be blessed; they shall call him blessed.

18 Blessed be Jehovah, God, the God of Israel, who alone does wonders.

19 Blessed be the name of His glory without end, and may His glory fill the whole earth; amen and amen.

20 All prayers of David son of Jesse.


## Chapter 73

1 A psalm of Asaph. Only good is God to Israel, to those of pure heart.

2 But I was almost unable to endure; my steps had nearly slipped.

3 For I was envious of the boasters, the prosperity of the wicked I saw.

4 For there are no pangs in their death, and their body is strong.

5 In the grievance of a mortal man there is nothing, and with a man they do not interfere.

6 Therefore pride is their necklace; violence wraps them like a garment.

7 His eye has gone forth from the fatness of his countenance; the imaginations of his heart have passed over.

8 They sink down and speak maliciously; from on high they speak oppression.

9 They set their mouth in the heavens, and their tongue goes about on the earth.

10 Therefore his folk go to ruin, and pissing waters are drained for them.

11 And they will say, "How does God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?"

12 Behold, these are the wicked; ever at ease, they increase in riches.

13 Only in vain have I kept my heart pure, and washed my hands in innocence.

14 I was afflicted all the day, and my reproof was in the mornings.

15 If I say, I will speak like this, behold, I would betray the generation of your children.

16 And I considered to know this—a grievance in my eyes.

17 Until I come to the sanctuaries of God, I will understand their latter end.

18 Only in their divisions you will set for them; you have cast them down to desolation.

19 How did they become a desolation, suddenly waste! They ceased, they ended from terror!

20 Like a dream when one awakes, O my Lord, in the city their vain shew you despise.

21 For my heart bethinks itself and my reins are in anguish.

22 But I am brutish and do not know; I have been as a beast with you.

23 But I am always with you; you hold me by the right hand.

24 By your counsel you will guide me, and afterward receive me to glory.

25 Who is there for me in the heavens? And with you, I have no desire on the earth.

26 My flesh and my heart fail; but God is the rock of my heart and my portion forever.

27 For indeed your distant ones shall perish; you have utterly destroyed every one straying from you.

28 But as for me, drawing near to God is good; I have set my hope in the Lord God, to tell of all your works.


## Chapter 74

1 A Maschil of Asaph. O God, how long will you reject us alway? Will your anger smoke against the flock of your pasture?

2 Remember your congregation, which you acquired aforetime, the tribe of your inheritance, which you redeemed; this mount Zion, where you dwelt.

3 Lift your feet against the places of utter destruction, all the harm the enemy has done in the sanctuary.

4 Your enemies roared in the midst of your appointed assembly; they set up their signs as signs.

5 It will be known as one brought up above, in the thick of the wood, his ax.

6 Its carved work is broken by ax and hammer.

7 Send fire into your sanctuary, defile the dwelling of your name upon the land.

8 They said in their hearts, "Let us utterly destroy them together; let us burn all the meeting places of God in the land."

9 Our marks are not seen, there is no longer a prophet, and with us no one knows how long.

10 How long, O God, will the adversary reproach? Will the enemy continually taunt your name?

11 Why do you draw back your hand, even your right hand, from the midst of the burning?

12 O God, my king of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.

13 You split the sea with your strength; you broke the heads of the sea-monsters upon the waters.

14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food to the people inhabiting the wilderness.

15 You split open a fountain and a brook; you dried up mighty rivers.

16 To you the day, and to you the night season; you established the light and the sun.

17 You established all the borders of the land; summer and winter You formed them.

18 Remember this, O Jehovah, how the enemy has reproached, and a foolish people has scorned your name.

19 Do not deny the life of your soul a dove; do not forget the beast of your poor forever.

20 Look to the covenant, for the dark places of the land are full of habitations of violence.

21 May the oppressed not turn back ashamed; may the poor and needy praise your name.

22 Rise up, O God, plead Your cause; remember the reproach of every day from the fool.

23 Do not forget the sound of your enemies, the tumult of those who rise against you continually.


## Chapter 75

1 For the choirmaster; do not destroy; a psalm, a song of Asaph. We give thanks to you, God; we give thanks, and your name is near; they recount your wonders.

2 For I will take an appointed time; I will judge with equity.

3 The earth and all its inhabitants are shaken; I uphold its pillars. Selah.

4 I said to the revellers, Do not revel, and to the wicked, Do not lift up the horn.

5 Do not lift your horn on high; do not speak with a stiff neck.

6 For it is not from the east or from the west, and not from the desert of the mountains.

7 For God is judge: one He brings low, another He exalts.

8 For a cup in the hand of the LORD is full of wine, red wine mixed, and he pours from it; only its dregs they will drink, all the wicked of the earth.

9 But as for me, I will declare alway; I will sing psalms to the God of Jacob.

10 And all the horns of the wicked I will cut off; exalted will be the horns of the righteous.


## Chapter 76

1 For the director of music, with stringed instruments; a psalm of Asaph, a song. God is known in Judah; in Israel his renown is great.

2 And his covert was in Salem, and his dwelling place in Zion.

3 There he broke the burning arrows, shield and sword, war. Selah.

4 You are bright, O mighty one, from the mountains of prey.

5 The stouthearted were plundered; they slept their sleep, and none of the men of might found their hands.

6 At your rebuke, God of Jacob, they slumbered, both chariot and horse.

7 You are awesome; who can stand before Your anger since Your wrath?

8 From the heavens you made judgment heard; the earth feared and was still.

9 Arise, O God, to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth.

10 For the anger of a person will praise you; the remainder of furious acts you will gird.

11 They vowed and paid to the LORD your God, all who are round about him; they brought a present to the one who ought to be feared.

12 He gathers the spirit of princes; awesome to the kings of the earth.


## Chapter 77

1 For the director of music, by Asaph, a psalm. My voice to God and I cry aloud, my voice to God and He listens to me.

2 In time of my distress I sought the Lord; my hand was stretched out at night and would not cease; I refused to be comforted, my soul.

3 I remember God and I groan; I meditate and my spirit faints.

4 You have held my eyes in waking; I am troubled and cannot speak.

5 I reckon the days of old, the years of ancient times.

6 I remember my song in the night; I commune with my heart, and my spirit ponders.

7 The LORD will alway forsake, and will not any more continue to be favorable.

8 Has his favour ceased for evermore? Has he ceased to promise to generation and generation?

9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he in anger withheld his compassion? Selah.

10 And I said, "She has fainted, she is exhausted; the years of the right hand of the Most High."

11 The works of Jah, for I remember your wonders of old.

12 And I will meditate on all Your deeds, and speak of Your acts.

13 God in holiness is your way; who is a great God like our God?

14 You are God, doing wonders; you made your power known among the peoples.

15 You have redeemed your people with an outstretched arm, the children of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

16 The waters saw you, O God, the waters saw you; they trembled; the deep places shuddered with fear.

17 The clouds poured out water; the skies gave thunder; your arrows also went abroad.

18 The thunder of your voice in the rolling thing; lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and shook.

19 In the sea is your way, in the great waters, and your footprints are not known.

20 You led your flock safely by the hand of Moses and Aaron.


## Chapter 78

1 Maschil of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my law; incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

2 I will open my mouth with a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old.

3 What we have heard and known, and our forefathers have told us.

4 We will not hide from their children the praises of the LORD and his strength and his wonders that he has done.

5 He established a testimony in Jacob and placed a law in Israel, which he commanded our forefathers to make known to their children.

6 so that a later generation may know, children yet to be born arise and recount to their children.

7 And they will set their confidence in God, and not forget the works of God, and they will keep his commandments.

8 They shall not be like their forefathers, a rebellious and defiant generation, a generation that did not prepare its heart and was not faithful to God in spirit.

9 The sons of Ephraim, armed with bows, turned back on the day of battle.

10 They did not keep the covenant of God, and in his law they refused to walk.

11 They forgot his deeds and the wonders he had shown them.

12 Before their fathers he performed wonders in the land of Egypt in the field of Zoan.

13 He split the sea and brought them through, and made the waters stand like a heap.

14 And He led them by a cloud by day and in all the night by a light of fire.

15 He split rocks in the wilderness and gave drink abundantly from the depths.

16 And he brought forth streams from the (ragged) rock, and made waters descend as rivers.

17 And they added to sin against him, defying the Most High in the wilderness.

18 They tested God in their heart, asking food for their soul.

19 They spoke of God, saying, Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?

20 Behold, he struck the rock and water flowed, streams gushed forth—can bread also be given? Can he provide food for his people?

21 Therefore Jehovah heard and was provoked, and a burning flared in Jacob, and even wrath rose up against Israel.

22 For they did not believe in God, nor did they trust in His deliverance.

23 He commanded the skies above, and the doors of the heavens he opened.

24 And he rained manna upon them to eat, and gave them wheat of heaven.

25 A man ate the bread of the mighty; he sent provision to be satisfied.

26 He moved eastward in the heavens and drove with His strength southward.

27 He rained flesh upon them like dust, and winged birds like sand of the sea.

28 And he cast among his camp round about unto his dwelling places.

29 And they ate and were greatly satisfied; he brought them what they had desired.

30 They did not turn from their lust; their food was still in their mouths.

31 Yet God in anger rose against them and struck down their fat ones and laid low the choice young men of Israel.

32 Yet for all this they sinned still and did not believe in his wonders.

33 Their days are spent in vanity, and their years in trouble.

34 If they have killed him and sought him, then they shall return and seek God.

35 And they remembered that God is their rock, and the Almighty is their redeemer.

36 They flattered him with their mouth, and with their tongue they lied to him.

37 and their heart was not steadfast with him, and they were not faithful in his covenant.

38 Yet he, being compassionate, forgives iniquity and does not destroy; he often turns back his anger and does not stir up all his wrath.

39 And he remembered that they were flesh, a passing spirit that does not return.

40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, grieved him in the desert.

41 They turned back and tested God, and the Holy One of Israel provoked.

42 They did not remember his hand, the day when he redeemed them from the adversary.

43 when he set his signs in Egypt and his wonders in the field of Zoan

44 He turned their rivers to blood; they could not drink their flowing streams.

45 He sent among them swarms of flies, and they devoured them; and frogs, and they destroyed them.

46 And he gave their increase to the caterpillar, and their labour to the grasshopper.

47 May he strike their vine with hailstones and their sycamores with frost.

48 And he gave over their beasts to the hail, and their cattle to fiery bolts.

49 He sent among them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and rage, and trouble, sending a discharge of harmful messengers.

50 He plots a path to his anger; he does not spare their soul from death, and their life he delivers to the pestilence.

51 He struck down every firstborn in Egypt, the chief of their strength in the tents of Ham.

52 And he drove his people like a flock, and led them like a herd in the wilderness.

53 And he led them in assurance, and they feared not, and the sea covered their enemies.

54 And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary, this mountain which his right hand acquired.

55 He drove out the nations before them and cast them by the line of inheritance, and He made the tribes of Israel dwell in their tents.

56 They tested and rebelled against the LORD, the Most High, and did not keep his testimonies.

57 They turned back and acted treacherously like their forefathers; they became false like a deceitful bow.

58 They angered him with their high places and provoked him to jealousy with their carved images.

59 God heard and was greatly provoked, and He utterly rejected Israel.

60 And he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent where he dwelt among men.

61 He gave his strength to the captive and his glory into the hand of the adversary.

62 And he shut his people with the sword, and with his inheritance he was angry.

63 Its chosen ones the fire has devoured, and its virgins were not mourned.

64 His priests fell by the sword, and his widows did not weep.

65 The LORD awoke as from a slumber, like a mighty man crying out from wine-bibbing.

66 And he struck his adversaries backward, giving them the reproach of the ages.

67 And he rejected the tent of Joseph, and the tribe of Ephraim he did not choose.

68 And he chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loves.

69 He built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he founded forever.

70 And he chose David his servant and took him from the sheepfolds.

71 From following the ewes with young he brought him to pasture his folk, Jacob his nation, and Israel his inheritance.

72 And he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart, and by the skillfulness of his hands he guided them.


## Chapter 79

1 A psalm of Asaph. God, nations have entered your inheritance, they have defiled your holy temple, they have made Jerusalem a heap.

2 They have given the dead body of your servants as food to the birds of the heavens, the flesh of your faithful to the beasts of the earth.

3 They poured out their blood like water around Jerusalem, and there was none to bury.

4 We were a reproach to our neighbors, a derision and scorn to those round about us.

5 How long, O LORD, will You be angry alway? Your jealousy burns like fire.

6 Pour out your wrath on the nations who do not know you, and on the kingdoms who have not called upon your name.

7 For he has devoured Jacob, and laid waste his comely dwelling place.

8 Do not remember against us the sins of long ago; let your tender mercies quickly meet us, for we have sunk very low.

9 Help us, O God of our salvation, for the sake of the glory of your name; deliver us and atone for our sins for the sake of your name.

10 Why do the nations say, Where is their God? Let it be known before our eyes the vengeance for the blood of your servants that has been shed.

11 Let the groaning of the prisoner come before you, according to the greatness of your arm; increase the children of death.

12 And repay our neighbors sevenfold into their bosom the reproach with which they have reproached You, (my) Lord.

13 But we are your folk and the flock of your pasture; we will give thanks to you forever, to all generations; we will declare your praise.


## Chapter 80

1 To the choirmaster: Shushan-eduth, a psalm of Asaph. O shepherd of Israel, give ear, O leader of Joseph's flock, O enthroned above the cherubim, shine forth.

2 Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might and come to save us.

3 O God, restore us and cause Your face to shine upon us, that we may be saved.

4 O Jehovah, God of hosts, how long will you remain angry at the prayer of your people?

5 You fed them bread of tears, and gave them tears to drink in three measures.

6 You make us a cause of strife among our neighbors, and our enemies mock us.

7 God of hosts, restore us, and cause Your face to shine upon us, that we may be saved.

8 A vine from Egypt you carried; you drove out nations and planted it.

9 You turned aside before it; you planted its roots; it filled the land.

10 The mountains were covered with its shade, and its boughs were the cedars of God.

11 Her bough shall extend to the sea, and her young twigs to the river.

12 Why have you broken down her wall, so that all who pass by the way trample it?

13 A boar from the forest ravages it; the wild beast of the field grazes upon my land.

14 God of hosts, return, we pray; look down from heaven and see, and visit this vine.

15 The vineyard that your right hand planted, and the son whom you made strong for yourself.

16 Burned by fire, like chaff; they perish from the rebuke of your presence.

17 Let your hand be upon the man of your right hand, upon the son of humankind whom you made strong for yourself.

18 And not drawing back from you, you make us live, and in your name we are called.

19 O LORD God of hosts, restore us; make Your face shine upon us, and we shall be saved.


## Chapter 81

1 For the music director, on the Gittith. To Asaph. Sing to God our strength, shout aloud to the God of Jacob.

2 Lift up a melody and give the timbrel; the sweet harp with the psaltery.

3 Blow the cornet at the new moon, on the (time) appointed for our solemn feast day.

4 For it is a statute for Israel, a judgment for the God of Jacob.

5 A testimony against Joseph he set when he went out over the land of Egypt: a language I did not know I would hear.

6 I removed the burden from his shoulder; his hands were freed from the basket.

7 In distress you called and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah.

8 Hear, O my people, and I will testify to you, O Israel, if you will hearken to me.

9 There shall not be among you a strange god, and you shall not bow down to a foreign deity.

10 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt; open your mouth wide and I will fill it.

11 And my folk did not hearken to my voice, and Israel would not obey me.

12 And I let them go according to the imagination of their heart; they shall walk in their counsels.

13 If only my people would listen to me, Israel would walk in my ways.

14 Almost their adversaries I will subdue, and upon their enemies I will turn my hand.

15 Those who hate the LORD will lie to him, and their time will be forever.

16 He would feed him with the finest of the wheat, and from the rock I would satisfy you with honey.


## Chapter 82

1 A psalm of Asaph. God stands in the assembly of God; in the midst of angels he judges.

2 How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to the wicked? Selah.

3 Defend the weak and the fatherless; uphold the cause of the poor and the needy.

4 Rescue the poor and the needy from the hand of the wicked; deliver them.

5 They do not know, nor do they understand; in darkness they walk; all foundations of the earth are shaken.

6 I said, "You are gods, and all of you are sons of the Most High."

7 Surely you will die like a man, and fall like one of the rulers.

8 Arise, O God, judge the earth, for You shall inherit all the nations.


## Chapter 83

1 A song, a psalm for Asaph. God, do not remain silent; do not be quiet, O God, and do not be still.

2 For behold, your enemies are in tumult, and those who hate you have lifted up the head.

3 Against your people they devise a secret counsel, and they consult against your hidden ones.

4 They said, "Come, let us cut them off from the nation, so that the name of Israel will no longer be remembered."

5 For they have taken counsel together in heart; against you they make a covenant.

6 The coverings of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagarenes.

7 Gebal and Ammon and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre.

8 Even Assyria joined with them; they became a stronghold for Lot’s sons. Selah.

9 Do to them as to Midian, as to Sisera and to Jabin at the brook Kishon.

10 They perished at En-dor; they became dung for the ground.

11 Set them as Oreb and as Zeeb, and as Zebah and as Zalmunna, all their princes.

12 who said, "Let us possess for ourselves the pleasant places of God."

13 My God, make them like a wheel, like stubble before the wind.

14 Like a fire burning in the forest, and like a flame setting the mountains ablaze.

15 So you will drive them with your storm, and in your tempest you will terrify them.

16 Fill their faces with shame, and they will seek your name, O LORD.

17 Let them be ashamed and dismayed forever and ever; let them be confounded and perish.

18 And they shall know that your name, the LORD, alone is the Most High over all the earth.


## Chapter 84

1 For the choirmaster, upon the Gittith, a psalm of the sons of Korah. How lovely are thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts.

2 My soul faints with longing for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.

3 Even a bird has found a home, and a swallow a nest for her young, by your altars, O LORD of hosts, my King and my God.

4 Blessed are those who dwell in your house; they will yet praise you. Selah.

5 Blessed is the person whose strength is in you, whose paths are in their heart.

6 Those who pass through the valley of Baca make it a spring; indeed, the early rain covers it with blessings.

7 They go from strength to strength, appear before God in Zion.

8 O Jehovah, God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.

9 Our shield, look, O God, and behold the face of your anointed.

10 For one day in your courts is better than a thousand; I chose to stand at the threshold in the house of my God rather than dwell in the tents of wickedness.

11 For the LORD God is a sun and shield; the LORD gives grace and glory; he does not withhold good from those who walk in integrity.

12 The LORD of hosts: happy is the man who trusts in you.


## Chapter 85

1 For the music director, a psalm of the sons of Korah. The LORD favored your land; he restored the captivity of Jacob.

2 You have borne the iniquity of your people; you have covered all their sin. Selah.

3 You have gathered in all your wrath; you have turned from the fierceness of your anger.

4 Turn us back, O God of our salvation, and turn away Your anger from us.

5 Alway wilt thou be angry with us? wilt thou draw out thine anger to generation and generation?

6 Will you not indeed turn, revive us, and your people rejoice in you?

7 Show us your favour, O LORD, and grant us your salvation.

8 I will hear what God the LORD will speak, for he will speak peace to his people and to his faithful ones, and they shall not return to folly.

9 Only near those who fear him is salvation found, to dwell gloriously in our land.

10 Favour and faithfulness have met, justice and peace have kissed.

11 True verity will sprout from the ground and right will look down from the heavens.

12 Even the LORD will give what is good, and our land will yield its increase.

13 Justice will go before his face and prepare a way for his steps.


## Chapter 86

1 A prayer of David. Incline your ear, O Jehovah, answer me, for I am poor and needy.

2 Preserve my life, for I am faithful; save your servant, my God, who trusts in you.

3 Have mercy on me, (my) Lord, for to you I call (in) all (manner) day.

4 Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, my Lord, my soul I lift up.

5 For you, (my) Lord, are good and forgiving, and abounding in favour to all who call upon you.

6 Give ear, O Jehovah, to my prayer, and attend to the voice of my supplications.

7 In the day of my distress I call to you, for you answer me.

8 There is no one like you among the gods, O Lord, and there is no act like your deeds.

9 All the nations whom You have made shall come and worship before You, O Lord, and honor Your name.

10 For great are you and doing wonders; you, God, alone.

11 Teach me your way, O LORD; I will walk in your truth; unite my heart to fear your name.

12 I will praise you, my Lord God, with all my heart, and I will honor your name forever.

13 For your favour is great toward me, and you have delivered my soul from the grave below.

14 O God, the insolent have risen against me, and a company of tyrants seeks my soul, and they have not set you before them.

15 But you, O my Lord, are a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love and truth.

16 Look upon me and be gracious to me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your female servant.

17 Make with me a mark for good, that those who hate me may see and be ashamed, for you, the LORD, have helped me and comforted me.


## Chapter 87

1 A song, a psalm of the sons of Korah: its foundation is in the holy mountains.

2 The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

3 Greatly spoken of you, city of God, Selah.

4 I will mention Rahab and Babel among those who know me; behold, Philistia and Tyre with Cush: this one was born there.

5 And to Zion it shall be said, "This one and that one was born in her," and the Most High himself will establish her.

6 The LORD will count in the register of the peoples: “This one was born there.” Selah.

7 Singers and dancers alike say, All my fountains are in you.


## Chapter 88

1 A song, a psalm for the Korahites, to the director, upon Mahalath for the afflicted, a Maschil of Heman the Ezrahite. The LORD, God of my salvation, by day I cried out, and by night before You.

2 Let my prayer come before you; incline your ear to my cry.

3 For my soul is full of adversities, and my life has drawn near to the grave.

4 I am counted with those who go down to the pit; I am like a man without strength.

5 Among the dead, free like the slain lying in the grave, whom you remember no more, and they are cut off from your power.

6 He has set me in dark cisterns, in the depths.

7 Your anger has pressed down upon me, and all Your waves have overwhelmed me. Selah.

8 You have removed my close friends from me; you have made me an abomination to them; I am confined and cannot go out.

9 My eye grows dim from affliction; I call upon the LORD every day; I spread my hands to you.

10 Will You perform a marvellous thing for the dead? Will the deceased rise to praise You? Selah.

11 Shall your favour be declared in the grave, your faithfulness in destruction?

12 Will your wonders be known in the dark, and your justice in the land of forgetfulness?

13 But as for me, to you, O the LORD, I cry out, and in the morning my prayer comes before you.

14 Why, O the LORD, do you reject my soul? Why do you hide your face from me?

15 I am afflicted and perishing from childhood; I have borne your terrors; I am numb.

16 Your fierce wrath has passed over me; your terrors have overwhelmed me.

17 Water surrounds me all day long; they have come together against me.

18 You have removed from me lover and companion; my acquaintances are darkness.


## Chapter 89

1 A Maschil of Ethan the Ezrahite. The LORD's favour I will sing forever, to generation and generation I will declare your faithfulness with my mouth.

2 For I said, "alway shall mercy be built up; in the heavens you will establish your faithfulness."

3 I have made a covenant with my chosen one; I have sworn to David my servant.

4 I will establish your seed alway, and build your throne from generation to generation.

5 Let the heavens declare your wonderful deeds, O LORD, and your faithfulness in the assembly of the holy ones.

6 Who in the heavens can compare with the LORD? Who is like the LORD among the sons of the mighty?

7 A mighty God in the council of the holy ones, greatly feared by all around him.

8 The LORD God of hosts, who is like you, mighty Jah? And your faithfulness surrounds you.

9 You in the majesty of the sea, when the billows rise up, you subdue them.

10 You crushed Rahab as one slain; with the arm of Your strength You scattered Your enemies.

11 The heavens are yours, the earth also is yours; the habitable world and its fulness—you established them.

12 North and south you created them; Tabor and Hermon in your name will shout for joy.

13 Yours is the arm with might; Your hand grows strong, Your right hand is exalted.

14 Justice and judgment are the foundation of your throne; mercy and truth go before your face.

15 Blessed the people knowing the shout; the LORD in the light of your face they walk.

16 In your fame they will rejoice all day, and in your justice they will be exalted.

17 For you are the glory of their strength, and by your favor our horn is exalted.

18 For the LORD is our shield, and the Holy One of Israel is our king.

19 Then you spoke in a vision to your faithful ones: I have set help upon a hero; I have exalted a young man from the folk.

20 I have found David my bond-servant; with my holy anointing oil I have anointed him.

21 My hand will sustain him; indeed, my arm will strengthen him.

22 The enemy shall not exact from him, and a son of iniquity shall not oppress him.

23 And I will strike his adversaries from before him, and I will afflict his enemies.

24 My faithfulness and my steadfast love will be with him, and by my name his horn will be exalted.

25 And he shall put his hand into the sea, and his right hand into the rivers.

26 He will call to me, “My father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.”

27 I will indeed give him the eldest (son); he shall be highest among the kings of the earth.

28 I will keep my lovingkindness for him forever, and my covenant shall stand firm with him.

29 And I will set his seed for eternity, and his throne as the days of heaven.

30 If my children forsake my law and do not walk in my judgments,

31 If my statutes they profane, and my commandments they do not keep.

32 I will correct their rebellion with a rod, and their sin with stripes.

33 and my favour will not cease from him, nor will I be false to my faithfulness.

34 I will not profane my confederacy, and that which came out from my lips I will not change.

35 One thing I have sworn by my holiness: I will not lie to David.

36 His seed shall be forever, and his throne like the sun before me.

37 As the moon, alway; and a witness in the sky, faithful. Selah.

38 But you have cast off and rejected; you are furious with your anointed.

39 You have defiled your covenant with your servant; you have profaned his crown to the earth.

40 You have broken down all his walls, you have brought his strongholds to ruin.

41 All passersby mocked him; he became a reproach to his neighbors.

42 You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries; you have made all his enemies rejoice.

43 You will not return the edge of his sword, nor establish him in battle.

44 You have removed his clearness and cast his throne to the ground.

45 You have shortened the days of his youth; you have covered him with shame. Selah.

46 How long, O Jehovah, will You hide Yourself alway? Your wrath burns like fire.

47 Remember how short my time is; for what vanity have you created all mankind?

48 What man is he that liveth and shall not see death? shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah.

49 Where are your former mercies, my Lord? You swore to David by your faithfulness.

50 Remember, O my Lord, the reproach of Your bond-servants, the shame I bear in my bosom from many nations.

51 the reproach of your enemies, O LORD, the reproach of those who blaspheme the heel of your anointed

52 Blessed be the LORD alway, Amen and Amen.


## Chapter 90

1 A prayer of Moses, the man of God: O Lord, You have been our dwelling place throughout all generations.

2 Before the mountains were born and you brought forth the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting you are God.

3 You turn mankind back to contrition, and You say, "Return, O children of man."

4 For a thousand years in your sight are like yesterday when it is past, and like a watch in the night.

5 You sweep them away as with a flood; they are like sleep; in the morning they are like grass that changes.

6 In the morning it sprouts and passes; in the evening it is cut down and withers.

7 For we are consumed by your anger, and by your wrath we are terrified.

8 Our iniquities are before you, our sins in the light of your countenance.

9 For all our days are passed away in your wrath; we finish our years like a sigh.

10 The days of our years therein are seventy years; and if by might, eighty years; yet their strength is trouble and vanity, for it passes quickly and we fly away.

11 Who knows the power of your anger, and your wrath according to the fear due you?

12 So teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.

13 Turn back, O LORD, how long will it be? And relent concerning your servants.

14 Satisfy us in the morning with your kindness, and we will shout for joy and be glad all our days.

15 Make us glad according to the days you have afflicted us, the years we have seen adversity.

16 Let your work be shown to your servants, and your glory to their children.

17 And may the beauty of our Lord God be upon us, and the work of our hands confirm upon us; yes, the work of our hands confirm it.


## Chapter 91

1 He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

2 I say to the LORD, "My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust."

3 For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, from the pestilence of calamity.

4 With his pinions he will cover you, and under his wings you will find refuge; his faithfulness is a shield and buckler.

5 You will not fear the dread of night, from an arrow flying by day.

6 From pestilence in darkness he shall walk; from destruction at midday he shall destroy.

7 A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand, but it shall not come near you.

8 But you will look on with your eyes and see the recompense of the wicked.

9 For you, O Jehovah, are my trust; on high you have made your dwelling place.

10 No adversity will befall you, and no plague will approach your dwelling.

11 For his messengers he will command concerning you to guard you in all your ways.

12 On their hands they will bear you up, lest you strike your foot against a stone.

13 You will trample the (fierce) lion and the adder; you will tread underfoot the (young) lion and the dragon.

14 For he has set his love on me, and I will deliver him; I will exalt him, because he has known my name.

15 He will call upon me, and I will answer him; I am with him in distress; I will deliver him and honor him.

16 I will satisfy him with length of days and show him my salvation.


## Chapter 92

1 A psalm, a song for the sabbath day. It is good to give thanks to the LORD, and to sing praises to your name, O Most High.

2 To declare your mercy in the morning and your faithfulness at night.

3 On an instrument of ten strings and on psaltery, on solemn sound with harp.

4 For you, O LORD, have made me glad through your deeds; in the works of your hands I will sing for joy.

5 How great are your acts, O the LORD! How very deep are your thoughts!

6 A certain man does not know, and a fool does not understand this.

7 When the wicked sprout like grass and all workers of iniquity flourish, it is that they may be destroyed forever.

8 But you, O LORD, are forever on high.

9 For behold, your enemies, O LORD, for behold, your enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.

10 I lifted up my horn like a unicorn; my head is anointed with fresh oil.

11 My eye has seen the enemy rising up against me; my ear has heard the evil.

12 The righteous will flourish like a palm tree; he will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

13 Planted in the court of the LORD, in the courts of our God they flourish.

14 They will still bear fruit in old age; they will be full of sap and green.

15 To declare that the LORD is upright; my Rock, and there is no iniquity in him.


## Chapter 93

1 The LORD reigns, robed in majesty; the LORD is robed in strength and girded; indeed, the world is established, not to be moved.

2 Thy throne is established from of old; thou art from everlasting.

3 The floods lift up, O the LORD, the floods lift up their voice; the floods lift up their waves.

4 At the sound of many waters, mighty ones breaking the sea, mighty on high is the LORD.

5 Your testimonies are exceedingly faithful; your house is a fitting sanctuary, O LORD, for length of days.


## Chapter 94

1 God of vengeance, the LORD; God of vengeance, shine forth.

2 Rise up, O Judge of the earth; render recompense upon the proud.

3 How long, O Jehovah, shall the wicked triumph? How long shall the wicked exult?

4 They pour out arrogant words; all workers of iniquity boast.

5 Your folk, O the LORD, they have oppressed, and Your inheritance they have afflicted.

6 They murder the widow and the alien, and they kill the orphans.

7 They said, "He will not see, Jah, and the God of Jacob will not understand."

8 Understand, you brutish folk, and you fools, when will you become wise?

9 Will he who planted the ear not hear? Will he who formed the eye not see?

10 Will not the one who chastises Gentile nations surely rebuke? Will not the one who instructs a person in knowledge discern?

11 The LORD knows the thoughts of humankind, for they are vanity.

12 Blessed is the man whom you discipline, O LORD, and whom you teach from your law.

13 to grant him quiet from the days of trouble until the wicked is destroyed into the pit.

14 For the LORD will not forsake His people, nor abandon His inheritance.

15 For judgment shall return to even justice, and all the upright in heart shall follow it.

16 Who will rise up for me against evildoers? Who will take a stand for me against those who work iniquity?

17 If the LORD had not helped me, almost I had dwelt in silence.

18 If I say, "My foot is slipping," your mercy, O LORD, supports me.

19 In the abundance of my thoughts within me, your consolations delight my soul.

20 Can the seat of calamity be allied with you, forming grievance by decree?

21 They will condemn the soul of the righteous and hold innocent blood guilty.

22 And the LORD became for me a stronghold, and my God a rock of refuge.

23 He returned their affliction upon them and destroyed them in their adversity, destroyed them the LORD our God.


## Chapter 95

1 Come, let us sing to the LORD; let us shout to the rock of our salvation.

2 Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving; with psalms let us shout unto him.

3 For great is the LORD God, and a great king over all gods.

4 whose hand holds the deep places of the earth and the strength of the mountains

5 The sea is his, and he made it, and the dry land his hands formed.

6 Come, let us bow down and worship, let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.

7 For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand today—if you will hear his voice.

8 Do not harden your hearts as at the provocation, on the day of the trial in the wilderness.

9 your fathers tested me; they proved me, even saw my deeds.

10 Forty years long I loathed that generation, and said, a people of hearted mind they are, and they have not known my ways.

11 As I swore in My wrath, “They shall not enter My resting place.”


## Chapter 96

1 Sing to the LORD a new song; sing to the LORD, all the earth.

2 Sing to the LORD, bless his name; declare his salvation day after day.

3 Declare among the nations his glory, among all the peoples his wondrous works.

4 For great is the LORD and highly praised, awesome is He above all gods.

5 For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but the LORD made the heavens.

6 Beauty and majesty are before him, strength and glory in his sanctuary.

7 Give to the LORD, O families of the peoples, give to the LORD glory and strength.

8 Give to the LORD the glory of his name; bring an offering and come into his courts.

9 Worship the LORD in holy beauty; tremble before his presence, all the earth.

10 Say among the nations, The LORD reigns; indeed, the world is established; it shall not be moved; he will judge the peoples with equity.

11 Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad; let the sea thunder and all that is in it.

12 The field and all that is in it will rejoice; then all the trees of the forest will sing.

13 Before the LORD, for he has come, for he has come to judge the earth; he will judge the world in righteousness and the peoples in his faithfulness.


## Chapter 97

1 The LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice; let the many islands be glad.

2 Cloud and thick darkness are round about him; justice and judgment are the foundation of his throne.

3 Fire goes before him and burns up his enemies round about.

4 His lightning flashed across the world; the earth saw and trembled.

5 The mountains melted like wax before the LORD, before the presence of the Lord of all the earth.

6 Let the heavens declare his righteousness, and let all the peoples see his glory.

7 Let them be ashamed, all who serve a carved image, who boast in a thing of nought; bow down to him, all you gods.

8 Zion heard and rejoiced, and the daughters of Judah were glad because of your judgments, O LORD.

9 For you, O LORD, are most high above all the earth; you are exalted far above all gods.

10 Lovers of the LORD, hate evil; he who guards the souls of his faithful ones will deliver them from the hand of the wicked.

11 Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

12 Rejoice, O righteous, in the LORD, and give thanks to the remembrance of his holiness.


## Chapter 98

1 A psalm. Sing to the LORD a new song, for he has done wondrous things; his right hand and his holy arm have brought salvation.

2 The LORD has made known his salvation; he has revealed his righteousness in the sight of the nations.

3 He remembered his mercy and faithfulness toward the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth saw the deliverance of our God.

4 Shout to the LORD, all the earth; burst forth and sing for joy and make music.

5 Sing to the LORD with the harp, with the harp and the voice of melody.

6 With trumpets and the sound of the cornet shout aloud before the king the LORD.

7 Let the sea roar and all that is in it, the world and those who dwell in it.

8 Rivers clap the hand; together mountains rejoice.

9 Before the LORD, for he is come to judge the earth: he will judge the world in righteousness, and the peoples in equity.


## Chapter 99

1 The LORD reigns, let the peoples tremble; he who sits enthroned upon the cherubim—let the earth shake.

2 The LORD is great in Zion, and he is high above all the peoples.

3 They acknowledge your renowned and awesome name; holy is he.

4 The boldness of a king loves judgment; you have established equity; judgment and justice in Jacob you have done.

5 Exalt Jehovah our God, and worship at the footstool of his feet; he is holy.

6 Moses and Aaron among his priests, and Samuel who called upon his name— they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.

7 In a pillar of cloud he spoke to them; they kept his testimonies and the statute he gave them.

8 Jehovah our God, you answered them; God, you were their support, and avenger of their deeds.

9 Exalt Jehovah our God and bow down to His holy hill, for holy is Jehovah our God.


## Chapter 100

1 A psalm of thanksgiving: shout to the LORD, all the earth.

2 Serve the LORD with joy; come before his presence with singing.

3 Know that Jehovah, the Lord is God; he made us, we are his folk, the flock of his pasture.

4 Come into his gates with thanksgiving, into his courts with praise; give thanks to him, bless his name.

5 For good is the LORD, his mercy alway, and his truth unto age and age.


## Chapter 101

1 A psalm of David. I will sing of favour and judgment; to you, the LORD, I will sing.

2 I will consider the way of integrity; when will you come to me? I will walk within my house with integrity of heart.

3 I will not set before my eyes a word of wickedness; I hate the work of turning aside; it shall not cling to me.

4 A froward heart shall depart from me: I will not know wickedness.

5 In the backbiting of his friend he marks him, a man of haughty eyes and wide of heart—I cannot endure him.

6 My eyes are on the faithful of the land, to dwell with me; he who walks in the way of integrity—he shall serve me.

7 He who practices deceit shall not dwell among the court of my household; he who speaks lies shall not be established before my eyes.

8 In the morning, cut off all the wicked of the land, to destroy from the city of the LORD all who do iniquity.


## Chapter 102

1 A prayer of a poor man when he is faint, and before the LORD he pours out his complaint. Jehovah, hear my prayer; let my crying come to you.

2 Do not hide your face from me in the day of my trouble; turn your ear to me; when I call, answer me quickly.

3 For my days have ended in smoke, and my bones are like a burning hearth.

4 I was stricken like grass and my heart failed, for I forgot to eat my food.

5 From the sound of my groaning my bones cling to my flesh.

6 I became like a cormorant of the wilderness; I became like an owl of the waste places.

7 I have been watchful, and I remain like a solitary bird upon the roof.

8 All the day mine enemies reproach me; they that rail on me take oaths by me.

9 For ashes I have eaten as bread, and my drink with weeping I have mingled.

10 Because of your anger and your wrath, for you have lifted me up and cast me away.

11 My age is like a passing shadow, and I am withered like grass.

12 But you, O LORD, are enthroned forever, and your memorial endures to all generations.

13 You will rise, have compassion on Zion, for it is time to show her favor, for the appointed season has come.

14 For your servants cherish its stones, and its dust they hold dear.

15 And the Gentiles will fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth your glory.

16 For Jehovah built Zion; he was seen in his glory.

17 He turned to the prayer of the destitute and did not despise their prayer.

18 Let this be recorded for a future generation; the people yet to be created will praise the LORD.

19 For the LORD looked from the height of his holiness; from heaven he beheld the earth.

20 To hear the groaning of the prisoner, to free the children of death.

21 To declare in Zion the name of the LORD and his praise in Jerusalem.

22 When the folk are gathered together, and the kingdoms to serve the LORD.

23 He has afflicted me, and shortened my days along the way.

24 I say to my God: Do not take me in the middle of my days; your years are throughout all generations.

25 Before time you founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.

26 They will perish, but you will endure; indeed, all of them will wear out like a garment; like clothing you will change them, and they will pass away.

27 But you are he, and your years do not cease.

28 The children of your servants will dwell securely, and their seed will be established before you.


## Chapter 103

1 David. Bless the LORD, O my soul, and all my inward parts [bless] his holy name.

2 Bless the LORD, O my soul, and do not forget all his benefits.

3 who forgives all your sins, who heals all your diseases

4 The one who redeems from the pit my life, who crowns with kindness and tender mercy.

5 Who satisfies your mouth with good, so that your youth is renewed like the eagle.

6 The LORD does justice and judgments for all who are oppressed.

7 He made known his ways to Moses, his acts to the children of Israel.

8 The LORD is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.

9 Not alway shall he chide, neither for alway shall he keep anger.

10 Not according to our faults has he dealt with us, nor according to our sins has he rewarded us.

11 For as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is his mercy toward those who fear him.

12 As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.

13 As a father has compassion on his sons, so the LORD has compassion on those who fear him.

14 For he knows our frame; he remembers that we are dust.

15 Another, his days are like grass; as a flower of the field, so he flourishes.

16 For a wind passes over it, and it is gone; and its place knows it no more.
