17 The favour of the LORD is from alway to alway upon those who fear him, and his justice to children's children.

18 For keepers of his covenant and for rememberers of his commandments to perform them.

19 The LORD in the heavens established His throne, and over all His reign rules.

20 Bless Jehovah, His messenger angels, mighty ones of power, doing His work, to hear at the sound of His word.

21 Bless the LORD, all his hosts, you who serve him, doing his will.

22 Bless the LORD, all his works, in every place of his dominion; bless the LORD, O my soul.


## Chapter 104

1 Bless the LORD, O my soul; O LORD my God, Thou art very great; honour and majesty hast Thou put on.

2 Clothing Himself in light as a garment, stretching out the heavens like a curtain.

3 who makes the waters his upper chambers, who sets the thick clouds as his chariot, who walks upon the wings of the wind.

4 Making his messengers winds, his ministers a flaming fire.

5 He established the earth upon its foundations; it shall not be moved forever and ever.

6 The deep was clothed with a garment; upon the mountains the waters stood.

7 From the rebuking of you they will flee; from the sound of your thundering they will be terrified.

8 The hills ascend, the valleys descend, to the place which you established for them.

9 A boundary you set that they might not cross over, that they not return to cover the earth.

10 The one who sends springs into the streams, between the mountains they flow.

11 All the wild beasts of the field will drink; the wild asses will break their thirst.

12 The bird of the heavens shall dwell upon them; from between the branches they shall give forth their voice.

13 Watering the hills from his upper chambers, from the fruit of your deeds you satisfy the land.

14 Causing grass to grow for the cattle and herb for the service of humanity, to bring forth food from the earth.

15 And wine gladdens the heart of mortal man, to make the face shine more than oil; and bread strengthens the heart of mortal man.

16 The trees of the LORD are satisfied, the cedars of Lebanon which he planted.

17 where birds make their nests, the stork in the firs its home.

18 The high hills are for the wild goats; the rocky crags are a refuge for the conies.

19 He made the moon for appointed times; the sun knows its going down.

20 You set darkness and it becomes night, in which every beast of the forest stirs.

21 The young lions roar for prey, seeking their food from God.

22 The sun rises, they gather; and to their dwelling places they return.

23 Another goes out to his work and to his labor until evening.

24 How many are your deeds, O LORD! All of them you made with wisdom; the earth is full of your riches.

25 This is the great sea, broad of hands; there the creeping things without number, living creatures small and great.

26 There ships go, leviathan, whom you formed to play with.

27 All of them toward you shall look to give food in its due season.

28 You give to them, they gather; You open Your hand, they are satisfied with good.

29 You hide your face—they are terrified; you take away their breath—they perish and return to dust.

30 You send forth Your spirit, they are created, and You renew the face of the ground.

31 Let the glory of the LORD be forever; may the LORD rejoice in his works.

32 Who looks at the earth and it trembles, touches the mountains and they smoke.

33 I will sing to the LORD while I live; I will sing praises to my God while I exist.

34 May my meditation be pleasing to him; I will rejoice in the LORD.

35 Let sinners vanish from the earth and let the wicked be no more; bless the LORD, O my soul; hallelujah.


## Chapter 105

1 Give praise to the Lord, call in his name, make known among the peoples his deeds.

2 Sing to him, sing praise to him; speak in all his wonders.

3 Rejoice in his holy name; let the heart of those who seek the LORD be glad.

4 Seek Jehovah and his strength; seek his face continually.

5 Remember his wonders that he has done, his miracles and the judgments of his mouth.

6 Seed of Abraham his servant, sons of Jacob his chosen ones.

7 He is the LORD our God; in all the earth are his judgments.

8 He remembers his covenant always, a word he commanded to a thousand generations.

9 the covenant which he made with Abraham, and his oath to Isaac.

10 And he appointed it to Jacob for a statute, a perpetual covenant to Israel.

11 To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.

12 When they were few in number, very few, and sojourners in it.

13 And they went from nation to nation, from kingdom to another people.

14 A man did not let them oppress; he rebuked them, even kings.

15 Do not touch my anointed ones, and to my prophets do not bring harm.

16 And he called a famine upon the land; every staff of bread he broke.

17 He sent a man before them, Joseph, who was sold as a servant.

18 He was brought into iron fetters; his soul came to the point of death.

19 Until the time his word came, the promise of the LORD refined him.

20 A king sent and released him; a ruler of peoples set him free.

21 He made him lord of his household and ruler over all his possessions.

22 To bind its captains in its soul, and its elders it will instruct.

23 Israel went down to Egypt, and Jacob dwelled in the land of Ham.

24 And he made his folk very numerous, and made them mightier than their adversaries.

25 They turned their heart to hate their people, to deal craftily with their servants.

26 Moses sent his servant; Aaron whom he chose.

27 They placed his miraculous signs and wonders in Ham's land.

28 He sent darkness and it became dark, and they did not rebel against his word.

29 He turned their water to blood and killed their fish.

30 Their land swarmed with frogs, in the inner chambers of their kings.

31 He said, and there came a swarm of flies, lice of every manner in all their borders.

32 He gave their rain as hail, fire and flaming bolts upon their land.

33 He struck their vine and their fig tree, he broke down the tree of their border.

34 He said, and the locust came, and the cankerworm without number.

35 They ate every kind of grass in their land; they ate the produce of their ground.

36 He struck down every firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their strength.

37 And he brought them out with silver and gold, and there was no one stumbling among their tribes.

38 Egypt rejoiced at their departure, for the dread of them had fallen upon them.

39 He spread a cloud for a covering, and fire for burning to illuminate the night.

40 He asked, and brought quails, and bread from heaven he gave them to eat.

41 The edge opened and waters gushed forth; they went through the wilderness, a river.

42 For he remembered his holy promise, Abraham his servant.

43 And he brought forth his people with joy, his chosen ones with gladness.

44 And he gave them the lands of nations, and they inherited the toil of peoples.

45 Because they keep his statutes and observe his laws. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 106

1 Praise Jah, give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness is alway.

2 Who can declare the mighty acts of the LORD? Who can make heard all his praise?

3 Blessed are those who keep justice, who do righteousness at all times.

4 Remember me, O LORD, when You favor Your people; take care of me when You deliver them.

5 To behold the goodness of your chosen ones, to rejoice in the exceeding joy of your people, to glory with your inheritance.

6 We have sinned with our forefathers; we have done wrong, we have dealt wickedly.

7 Our fathers in Egypt did not understand your wonders; they did not remember the abundance of your kindness, and they rebelled at the sea, at the Red Sea.

8 And he saved them for the sake of his name, to make known his mighty power.

9 He rebuked the Sea of Reeds and it dried up, and he led them through the depths as through a wilderness.

10 And he saved them from the hand of the hater, and he redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.

11 Water covered their adversaries; not one of them was left.

12 They believed in his word; they sang his praise.

13 They hurried, they forgot his deeds; they did not wait for his counsel.

14 They greedily desired in the wilderness and tested God in the desert.

15 And he gave them their request, and sent leanness into their soul.

16 They were jealous of Moses in the camp, of Aaron the holy one of the LORD.

17 The earth opened and swallowed Dathan, and covered over the assembly of Abiram.

18 And burning came into the assembly, a flaming flame scorched the wicked.

19 They made a calf at Horeb and bowed down to a molten image.

20 They exchanged their glory for the figure of an ox that eats grass.

21 They forgot God their savior, who did great things in Egypt.

22 Wonders in the land of Ham, awesome deeds at the Red Sea.

23 And he said, to destroy them, had it not been for Moses, his chosen one, who stood in the breach before him, to turn back his wrath from destroying.

24 They rejected in the pleasant land; they did not believe his word.

25 They grumbled in their tents; they did not obey the voice of the LORD.

26 He raised his hand against them to cast them down in the wilderness.

27 and to cast their seed among the nations, and to scatter them throughout the lands.

28 They were joined to Baal-peor and ate sacrifices of the dead.

29 They angered by their doings, and a plague broke out among them.

30 And Phinehas stood and prayed, and the plague was checked.

31 And it was reckoned to him for justice, for generation and generation, forever.

32 They provoked anger at the waters of strife, and it went badly for Moses because of them.

33 For they rebelled against his spirit, and he spoke rashly with his lips.

34 They did not destroy the nations whom the LORD said to them.

35 They mingled among the Gentiles and learned their deeds.

36 They served their idols and were ensnared by them.

37 They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to the devils.

38 They poured out innocent blood, the blood of their sons and their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan, and the land was defiled with blood.

39 And they defiled themselves in their acts, and they committed adultery in their doings.

40 The LORD was angry with his people and loathed his inheritance.

41 And he gave them into the hand of the nations, and their enemies ruled over them.

42 Their enemies oppressed them, and they were subdued under their hand.

43 Many times he delivers them, but they rebel against his counsel, and they are brought low by their iniquity.

44 And he saw their distress, when he heard their cry.

45 And he remembered for them his covenant, and relented according to the abundance of his mercy.

46 And he gave them into compassion before all their oppressors.

47 Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your renowned name, to glory in your praise.

48 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting; and let all the people say, Amen. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 107

1 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness is alway.

2 Let the redeemed of the LORD say, who have been redeemed from the hand of the adversary.

3 and from the lands he gathered them, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the sea.

4 They wandered in the wilderness in the desolate region, the way of the inhabited city they did not find.

5 Hungry and thirsty, their soul within them faints.

6 They cried to the LORD in their distress; from their anguish he delivered them.

7 And he led them along a straight path to go to a city of dwelling.

8 Let them give thanks to the LORD for his mercy and his wonders to the children of men.

9 For he satisfies the appetite of the hungry, and the famished soul he fills with good.

10 Those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death, bound in affliction and iron.

11 For they have rejected the word of God and despised the counsel of the Most High.

12 And their heart was humbled by grievousness; they stumbled, and there was no helper.

13 They cried to the LORD in their distress; from their anguish he saved them.

14 He brings them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and breaks their bands.

15 Give thanks to the LORD for his kindness, and his wonders to humanity.

16 For he has broken the gates of brass and cut through the bars of iron.

17 Fools because of their transgression, and because of their iniquities they will be punished.

18 Their soul abhors all food, and they draw near to the gates of death.

19 They cried to the LORD in their distress; from their anguishes he saved them.

20 He sends his act and it heals them, and delivers them from their destructions.

21 Give thanks to the LORD for his kindness and his wonders to the children of man.

22 And they shall sacrifice offerings of thanksgiving and declare his deeds in song.

23 They that go down to the sea in ships, doing business in great waters.

24 They saw the acts of the LORD and his wonders in the deep.

25 And he said, and a stormy wind stood up and lifted its billows.

26 They go up to the heavens, they go down to the depths; their soul in distress is greatly distressed.

27 They reel and stagger like a drunkard, and all their wisdom is swallowed up.

28 They cried to the LORD in their distress, and from their anguish he brought them out.

29 He turned the storm into a calm, and the billows of it were stilled.

30 They rejoiced that they would be quiet, and he comforted them to their haven of delight.

31 Give thanks to the LORD for his mercy and his wonderful works to the children of men.

32 Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.

33 He set rivers in the wilderness and water-springs in the thirsty land.

34 A land of fruit turned to salt land because of the wickedness of its inhabitants.

35 He sets the wilderness as a pool of water, and the dry land as water-springs.

36 And they sat there hungry, and they lamented a city of dwelling.

37 They sowed fields and planted vineyards, and made fruit, increase.

38 And he blessed them, and they greatly multiplied, and their cattle did not diminish.

39 And they diminished and languished from oppression, adversity, and sorrow.

40 He pours out contempt on nobles and makes them wander in a waste land without a way.

41 He lifted the afflicted out of his trouble, and set his family like a flock.

42 Let the upright see and rejoice, and all iniquity shut its mouth.

43 Who is wise and will guard these things, and will consider the LORD's acts of favour?


## Chapter 108

1 A song, a psalm of David. My heart is steadfast, O God; I will sing and make music with all my glory.

2 Awake, O psaltery and harp; I will awaken the morning.

3 I will praise you among the folk, O the LORD, and I will sing to you among the nations.

4 For great is your lovingkindness above the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the skies.

5 Exalt God above the heavens, and your glory above all the earth.

6 So that your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand and answer me.

7 God spoke in his holiness: I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem and measure the valley of Succoth.

8 Gilead is mine, Manasseh is mine, Ephraim is the strength of my head, Judah is my lawgiver.

9 Moab is a wash-pot; upon Edom I will cast my shoe; over Philistia I will triumph.

10 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?

11 O God, have You cast us off? Will You not go out, O God, with our armies?

12 Give us help in distress, for the salvation of man is vain.

13 By God we shall do valiantly, and He shall trample our enemies.


## Chapter 109

1 For the director of music, a psalm of David. O God of my praise, do not be silent.

2 For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit have opened against me; they have spoken with me a lying tongue.

3 And words of hatred surrounded me, and they fought against me for no reason.

4 Instead of love they hate me, but I pray.

5 They have repaid me with adversity instead of good, and with hatred instead of my love.

6 Appoint a wicked man over him; let an adversary stand at his right hand.

7 When he is judged, the wicked will come forth, and his prayer will become a sin offering.

8 Let his days be few; let another take his office.

9 Let his sons be fatherless and his wife a widow.

10 And his children will wander afflicted and beg, and seek from desolate places.

11 A creditor demands all that is his, and strangers plunder his labour.

12 Let no one show him favour, and let him not be gracious to his orphans.

13 Let his latter end be to be cut off, in another generation let their name be blotted out.

14 Let the fault of his fathers be remembered by the LORD, and let the sin of his mother not be blotted out.

15 Let them be before the LORD always, and may he cut off their remembrance from the land.

16 Because he did not remember to do a good deed, and pursued a poor man and a needy one and a tender-hearted man to put them to death.

17 He loved cursing and it came upon him, and did not desire blessing, and it departed from him.

18 He clothed himself with cursing as with armour, and it entered his midst like water, and like oil in his bones.

19 Let it be to him as apparel that he wears, and as a girdle that he wears always.

20 This is the labour of my adversaries from the LORD and those who speak evil against my soul.

21 But you, God, (my) Lord, act for me because of your renown, for your mercy is good; deliver me.

22 For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.

23 I fade away like a lengthening shadow; I am shaken off like a locust.

24 My knees failed from fasting, and my body wasted from lack of fat.

25 But I was a reproach to them; when they saw me, they shook their heads.

26 Help me, O LORD my God; save me according to your mercy.

27 And they shall know that this is your hand, that you, the LORD, have done it.

28 Let them curse, but you bless; when they rise up, they shall be put to shame, and your servant shall rejoice.

29 Let my adversaries be clothed with confusion and covered with the shame of their disgrace.

30 I will greatly praise Jehovah with my mouth, and among many I will praise him.

31 For he stands at the right hand of the needy one, to save him from those who condemn his soul.


## Chapter 110

1 A psalm of David. The LORD saith unto my lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies the footstool of thy feet.

2 The LORD will send forth your rod of strength from Zion; rule in the midst of your enemies.

3 Your folk are free offerings in the day of your power, in holy majesty, from the womb of the morning; for you, the dew of your youth.

4 The LORD has sworn and will not relent: You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.

5 (my) Lord at your right hand will crush kings in the day of his wrath.

6 He will judge among the nations, full of corpses; he will shatter the chief on the land, many.

7 From the brook by the way he will drink; therefore he will lift the head.


## Chapter 111

1 Praise Jah, I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart, in the assembly of the upright and the congregation.

2 Great are the acts of the LORD; they are sought by all who delight in them.

3 Beauty and majesty are his work, and his justice endures forever.

4 He has made a memorial for his wonders; gracious and merciful is the LORD.

5 Spoil he gave to those who fear him; he will remember his covenant forever.

6 He declared the power of his deeds to his people, to give them the inheritance of the nations.

7 The acts of his hands are true and just; all his precepts are faithful.

8 They stand fast forever and ever, established in truth and uprightness.

9 He sent redemption to his people; he commanded his covenant forever: holy and awesome is his name.

10 The beginning of wisdom is fear of the LORD; good understanding to all who do them; his praise endures for ever.


## Chapter 112

1 Praise Jah, blessed is the man who fears the LORD, in his precepts he greatly delights.

2 A mighty one shall be in the land his seed; a generation of upright ones shall be blessed.

3 Riches and wealth are in his house, and his righteousness endures forever.

4 Light dawns in the darkness for the upright, gracious and merciful and righteous.

5 A good man is gracious and lends; he wisely directs his affairs with justice.

6 For alway he shall not be moved; the righteous shall be in memory for alway.

7 At news of evil he will not be afraid; his heart is firm, trusting in the LORD.

8 His heart is courageous; he will not fear until he sees the downfall of his adversaries.

9 He has scattered, giving to the poor; his justice endures forever; his horn is exalted in glory.

10 The wicked one will see and be enraged; his teeth he will gnash and melt away; the desire of the wicked will perish.


## Chapter 113

1 Praise Jah, praise the servants of the LORD, praise the name of the LORD.

2 Blessed be the name of the LORD from now and forever.

3 From the rising of the sun to its going down, the name of the LORD is renowned.

4 The LORD is high above all nations; his glory is above the heavens.

5 Who is like the LORD our God, who is exalted on high to dwell?

6 who brings me down to see [things] in the heavens and on the earth

7 He raises the poor from the dust, from the dung hill he lifts the needy.

8 to seat me with nobles, with the nobles of his folk.

9 He settles the barren woman in a home, a joyful mother of children. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 114

1 When Israel went out from Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange speech.

2 Judah became his consecrated thing; Israel, his dominion.

3 The sea saw and fled, the Jordan turned backward.

4 The hills skipped like rams, the little hills like lambs.

5 What to you, sea, that you flee? Jordan, that you turn back?

6 The mountains shall dance like rams, the little hills like lambs.

7 From before the Lord tremble, O earth, from before the God of Jacob.

8 Will the rock indeed turn to a pool of water, the flint to a spring of water?


## Chapter 115

1 Not to us, O Jehovah, not to us, but to your name give glory, for your favour, for your truth.

2 Why do the nations say, Where now is their God?

3 Our God in the heavens does all that he pleases.

4 Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.

5 Mouth they have, but they do not speak; eyes they have, but they do not see.

6 They have ears but cannot hear; they have noses but cannot smell.

7 Their hands cannot move, their legs cannot walk, they cannot speak through their throat.

8 Like them shall their makers become, all who trust in them.

9 Israel trusts in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.

10 The house of Aaron trusted in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.

11 Those who fear the LORD trust in the LORD; he is their help and their shield.

12 The LORD remembers us; he will bless, he will bless the house of Israel, he will bless the house of Aaron.

13 May he bless those who fear the LORD, the small together with the great.

14 May the LORD add to you, to you and to your children.

15 Blessed are you to the LORD, maker of the heavens and the earth.

16 The heavens are the LORD's heavens, but the earth he gave to the children of man.

17 The dead do not praise Jah, nor any who go down into silence.

18 But we will bless Jah from now on and forever; praise Jah.


## Chapter 116

1 I love because Jehovah hears my voice, my supplication.

2 For he inclined his ear to me, and in my days I will call.

3 The cords of death surrounded me, the distresses of the grave found me; trouble and sorrow I find.

4 In the name of the LORD I call; O LORD, deliver my soul.

5 Gracious is the LORD and righteous; our God is merciful.

6 The LORD preserves the simple; I was brought low, and he saved me.

7 Return, O my soul, to your rest, for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.

8 For you have delivered my soul from death, my eye from tears, my foot from falling.

9 I will walk before the LORD in the lands of the living.

10 I believed, therefore I spoke; I was greatly afflicted.

11 I said in my haste, every person is a liar.

12 What shall I return to the LORD for all his benefits to me?

13 I will take the cup of deliverance and call on the name of the LORD.

14 My vows to the LORD I will pay before his people.

15 Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his faithful.

16 O LORD, indeed I am your servant, I am your servant, the son of your female servant; you have loosed my bonds.

17 I will offer to you a sacrifice of thanksgiving, and in the name of the LORD I will call.

18 I will pay my vows to the LORD; behold now before all his people.

19 In the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem, praise the LORD.


## Chapter 117

1 Praise Jehovah, all nations; praise him, all peoples.

2 For his favour has prevailed over us, and the LORD's truth endures forever. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 118

1 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness endures forever.

2 Let Israel now say, for his mercy is alway.

3 Let the house of Aaron now say, "His loving-kindness is alway."

4 Let now those who fear the LORD say, for his kindness is alway.

5 From distress I called to Jah; the LORD answered me with abundant space.

6 The LORD is with me; I will not fear: what can a person do to me?

7 The LORD is my helper, and I will look in triumph on my enemies.

8 It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in a man.

9 It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in nobles.

10 All nations surrounded me in the name of the LORD; I will surely destroy them.

11 They encircle me, indeed they have surrounded me, in the name of the LORD, for I shall destroy them.

12 They surrounded me like bees; they were extinguished like a fire of thorns; in the name of the LORD I cut them off.

13 They pushed me to fall, but the LORD helped me.

14 My strength and song is Jah, and he has become my deliverance.

15 The sound of rejoicing and deliverance is in the tents of the righteous; the right hand of the LORD does valiantly.

16 The right hand of the LORD is exalted; the right hand of the LORD does valiantly.

17 I will not die, for I shall live, and I will declare the acts of the LORD.

18 Jah has severely disciplined me, but he has not given me over to death.

19 Open to me the gates of righteousness; I will enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.

20 This is the gate of the LORD; the righteous shall enter into it.

21 I thank you, for you have answered me, and have become my deliverance.

22 The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone.

23 This came about from the LORD; it is marvelous in our sight.

24 This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.

25 Please, O LORD, save now; I beg you, O LORD, make successful now.

26 Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the LORD; we bless you from the house of the LORD.

27 God, the LORD, and He has given light to us: bind the solemn feast with cords, even to the horns of the altar.

28 My God, I will give thanks to you; my God, I will exalt you.

29 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness is alway.


## Chapter 119

1 Blessed are those who are blameless in their way, who walk in the law of the LORD.

2 Blessed are those who keep his testimonies; with all their heart they seek him.

3 They did not commit iniquity; along his ways they walked.

4 You have commanded your precepts to be kept very much.

5 My ways are established to keep your statutes.

6 Then I will not be ashamed when I look upon all your precepts.

7 I will bless you with upright heart, when I learn your righteous judgments.

8 I will keep your statutes; do not forsake me utterly.

9 How can a boy keep his path according to your word?

10 With all my heart I have sought You; do not let me stray from Your precepts.

11 In my care I have treasured your word, so that I might not sin against you.

12 Blessed are you, the LORD, who teaches me your statutes.

13 With my lips I have declared all the judgments of your mouth.

14 Along your testimonies I have rejoiced, as much as all wealth.

15 I will meditate on your precepts and consider your ways.

16 I will delight in your statutes; I will not forget your word.

17 Deal kindly with your servant; I shall live and keep your word.

18 Open my eyes, and I will behold wonders from your law.

19 I am a sojourner in the land; do not hide your precepts from me.

20 My soul is consumed with longing for your judgments at all times.

21 You rebuked the arrogant, who are cursed, those who stray from (which was) commandment.

22 Remove from me reproach and contempt, for I have kept your testimonies.

23 Even the captains sat and spoke against me; your servant will meditate on your statutes.

24 Your testimonies also are my delight and my counselors.

25 My soul clings to the dust; revive me according to your word.

26 I told of my ways and you answered me; teach me your statutes.

27 Make me understand the way of your precepts, and I will meditate on your wonders.

28 My soul is poured out because of sorrow; confirm me according to your word.

29 Turn away from me the way of falsehood, and graciously give me your law.

30 I have chosen the way of faithfulness; your judgments I have set.

31 I have clung to your testimonies, O LORD; do not put me to shame.

32 I will run along your precepts because you will enlarge my heart.

33 Teach me, O LORD, the way of your statutes, and I will keep it to the end.

34 Make me understand, that I may keep your law and guard it with all my heart.

35 Lead me in the path of Your precepts, for in it I delight.

36 Incline my heart to your testimonies, and not to covetousness.

37 Turn my eyes away from seeing vanity; by your way give me life.

38 Establish your word to your servant, which is for your fear.

39 Remove my reproach, which I have borne, for your judgments are good.

40 Behold, I long for your commandments; in your righteousness give me life.

41 May Your favour come to me, O LORD, Your deliverance according to Your word.

42 And I will answer my detractors with a word, for I have trusted in your word.

43 Do not keep back from my mouth the word of truth; for in your judgment I have hoped.

44 And I will keep your law alway, for ever and ever.

45 And I will walk in freedom, for I have sought your commandments.

46 And I will speak of your testimonies before kings and will not be ashamed.

47 I will delight in your precepts, which I love.

48 And I will lift up my hands to your precepts which I have loved, and I will meditate on your statutes.

49 Remember the promise to your servant, the one you caused me to hope in.

50 This is my comfort in my affliction, that your word has revived me.

51 The arrogant have mocked me greatly, but from your law I have not turned aside.

52 I remembered your judgments of old, O LORD, and was comforted.

53 A terrible horror has seized me from the wicked, those who forsake your law.

54 Your statutes were my songs in the house of my sojourn.

55 I remembered your name, the LORD, in night, and I kept your law.

56 This became mine, because I kept your commandments.

57 My portion is the LORD; I have said that I would keep Your word.

58 I have sought your face with all my heart; be gracious to me according to your word.

59 I considered my ways and turned my feet back to your testimonies.

60 I hastened and did not delay in keeping your precepts.

61 The bands of the wicked have ensnared me, but I have not forgotten your law.

62 At midnight I will rise to give thanks to you for your righteous judgments.

63 I am a companion to all who fear you and to those who keep your commandments.

64 The LORD's favour has filled the earth; teach me your statutes.

65 You have dealt well with your servant, O LORD, according to your word.

66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge, for I have believed in your precepts.

67 Before I answer, I go astray; but now I have kept your word.

68 You are good and do good; teach me your statutes.

69 The arrogant have lied against me without cause; I, with all my heart, will keep your precepts.

70 Their heart is hardened like fat; I delight in your law.

71 It is good for me that I was afflicted, so that I might learn your statutes.

72 Good for me is the law of your word, more than thousands of gold and silver.

73 Your hands made me and fashioned me; give me understanding, that I may learn Your precepts.

74 Those who fear you will see me and rejoice, for I have waited for your word.

75 I know, O LORD, that Your judgments are just, and in faithfulness You have afflicted me.

76 Let your favour come to comfort me according to your word to your servant.

77 Let your compassions come to me, that I may live, for your law is my delight.

78 Let the arrogant be put to shame for falsely accusing me, but I will meditate on your precepts.

79 Let those who fear you turn to me, and let them know your testimonies.

80 Let my heart be blameless in your statutes, so that I may not be put to shame.

81 My soul has grown weary for your salvation; in your word I have hoped.

82 My eyes fail from your word, as I ask, "When will you comfort me?"

83 For I was like a bottle in smoke; your commandments I did not forget.

84 How long shall the life of your servant endure? When will you execute judgment on my persecutors?

85 The proud have dug a pit for me, of things not according to your law.

86 All your precepts are true; they pursue me with lies; help me.

87 Almost consumed upon the earth, yet I did not forsake your precepts.

88 According to your mercy, preserve my life, and I will keep the testimony of your word.

89 alway the LORD your word stands in the heavens

90 From age to age your faithfulness endures; you established the land and it stands.

91 Your judgments stand today, for all are your servants.

92 If your law had not been my delight, then I would have perished in my affliction.

93 Alway I will not forget thy precepts, for in them thou hast quickened me.

94 I am yours; save me, for I have sought your commandments.

95 The wicked have waited for me to destroy me, but I will consider your testimonies.

96 For every perfection I see an end; your commandment is exceedingly broad.

97 How I love your law; it is my meditation all the day.

98 From my enemies your precept makes me wise, for it is mine forever.

99 From all my teachers I gained insight, for Your testimonies are my meditation.

100 I have considered the elders, for I have kept your commandments.

101 From every evil way I have kept my feet, to keep your word.

102 I have not turned aside from your judgments, for you have taught me.

103 How sweet to my taste is your word, more than honey to the mouth.

104 From your precepts I gain understanding; therefore I hate every false way.

105 Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.

106 I have sworn and will confirm to keep the just ordinances of your righteousness.

107 I am exceedingly weary; O LORD, revive me according to your word.

108 May the free offerings of my mouth please the LORD, and teach me your judgments.

109 My soul is in my hand continually, and I have not forgotten your law.

110 The wicked have set a snare for me, yet I have not strayed from your precepts.

111 I have taken your testimonies as an everlasting possession, for they are the joy of my heart.

112 I set my heart to keep your statutes forever in return.

113 I hate pride; your law I love.

114 My covering and my shield, you are; for your word I wait.

115 Turn away from me, you evildoers, that I may keep the precepts of my God.

116 Sustain me according to your word, and I shall live; and do not put me to shame in my hope.

117 Support me, and I shall be saved, and I shall always observe your statutes.

118 You have rejected all who stray from your statutes, for they deal falsely with deceit.

119 Thou hast consumed all the wicked of the earth; therefore I love Thy testimonies.

120 My flesh bristled with dread of You, and I feared Your judgments.

121 I have done justice and righteousness; do not leave me to my oppressors.

122 Let Your servant find favor; let the arrogant not oppress me.

123 My eyes fail with longing for your salvation and for the word of your righteousness.

124 Deal with your servant according to your mercy, and teach me your statutes.

125 Your servant am I; make me understand, that I may know your testimonies.

126 A time to act for the LORD: they have broken your law.

127 Therefore I have loved your precepts more than gold, even more than fine gold.

128 Therefore I have esteemed all Your precepts concerning all things as right; I have hated every false way.

129 Thy testimonies are marvellous things; therefore doth my soul keep them.

130 The entrance of your words gives light; it gives understanding to the simple.

131 I opened my mouth and panted, for I longed for your precepts.

132 Look upon me and be gracious to me according to the judgment of those who love your name.

133 Order my steps by your word, and do not let any evil rule over me.

134 Deliver me from the oppression of a man, that I may keep your precepts.

135 Let Your face shine upon Your servant, and teach me Your statutes.

136 Streams of water flow from my eyes because they do not keep your law.

137 You are righteous, the LORD, and your judgments are upright.

138 You have commanded that which is altogether just, Your testimonies; very faithfulness.

139 My zeal has consumed me, because my adversaries have forgotten your words.

140 Your word is refined exceedingly and your servant loves it.

141 I am young and despised, but I have not forgotten your commandments.

142 Your justice is righteousness forever, and your law is truth.

143 Anguish and distress have found me; your precepts are my delight.

144 Your testimonies are altogether just forever; give me understanding, that I may live.

145 I called with all my heart; answer me, O LORD; your statutes I keep.

146 I have called to you; save me, and I will keep your testimonies.

147 Before the dawning of the day I cried out; I waited for your words.

148 My eyes anticipate the watches to meditate on your word.

149 Give ear to my voice according to your mercy, O LORD; preserve my life according to your justice.

150 Those who pursue heinous crime draw near; from your law they are far.

151 You are near, O the LORD, and all Your precepts are true.

152 Aforetime I knew from your testimonies that you have established them forever.

153 See my affliction and deliver me, for I have not forgotten your law.

154 Plead my cause, O LORD, and strive for me; by your word give me life.

155 Deliverance is far from the wicked, for your statutes they do not seek.

156 According to your great compassion, O LORD, according to your judgments give me life.

157 Many are my adversaries and those who afflict me, yet from your testimonies I have not turned aside.

158 I saw transgressors and was grieved because they did not keep your commandment.

159 See, for I have loved your commandments, O LORD; revive me according to your mercy.

160 The beginning of your word is truth, and forever all your righteous judgments are established.

161 Princes pursued me without cause, but my heart stands in awe of your words.

162 I rejoice at your word, as one who finds abundant spoil.

163 Deceit I have hated and abhorred; your law I have loved.

164 Seven times a day I praise you for your righteous judgments.

165 Great peace have those who love your law, and they have no stumbling-block.

166 I have waited for your salvation, O LORD, and your commandments I have kept.

167 My soul keeps your testimonies and I love them greatly.

168 I have kept your precepts and your testimonies, for all my ways are before you.

169 Let my cry come before you, O LORD; according to your word, give me understanding.

170 Let my supplication come before you according to your word; deliver me.

171 My lips will pour forth praise, when you teach me your statutes.

172 Let my tongue respond to your word, for all your commandments are justice.

173 Let your hand be ready to help me, for I have chosen your commandments.

174 I have longed for your salvation, O LORD, and your law is my delight.

175 Revive my soul and I will praise you, and your judgments will help me.

176 I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek Your servant, for I have not forgotten Your precepts.


## Chapter 120

1 A song of the steps: to the LORD in my distress I called, and he answered me.

2 O the LORD, deliver my soul from a lying lip, from a deceitful tongue.

3 What shall be given to you, and what shall be added to you, you deceitful tongue?

4 Sharp arrows of a warrior, with burning coals of juniper.

5 Woe to me, for I have sojourned in Mesech; I have dwelt with the tents of Kedar.

6 My soul has greatly dwelt with those who hate peace.

7 I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war.


## Chapter 121

1 A song of ascents: I lift my eyes to the mountains; from where does my help come?

2 My help is from the LORD, maker of heaven and earth.

3 Do not let your foot be moved; do not let your keeper slumber.

4 Behold, he does not slumber and does not sleep, the keeper of Israel.

5 The LORD is your keeper; the LORD is your shade at your right hand.

6 By day the sun will not strike you, nor the moon by night.

7 The LORD will keep you from all evil; he will keep your soul.

8 The LORD will keep your going out and your coming in from now and forever.


## Chapter 122

1 A song of the steps of David. I was glad when they said to me, Let us go to the house of the LORD.

2 Our legs were able to endure standing within your gates, Jerusalem.

3 Jerusalem is built as a city that is joined together.

4 There the tribes went up, the tribes of the LORD, as a testimony for Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD.

5 For there sat thrones for judgment, thrones for the house of David.

6 Ask for the welfare of Jerusalem; may those who love you prosper.

7 May your army be at peace, your palaces in prosperity.

8 For the sake of my brothers and my companions, I will now speak peace to you.

9 For the sake of the temple of the LORD our God, I will seek good for you.


## Chapter 123

1 A song of the steps: to you I lifted my eyes, O dweller in the heavens.

2 Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maidservant look to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the LORD our God, until He has mercy upon us.

3 Be gracious to us, O LORD, be gracious to us, for we are greatly filled with contempt.

4 Our soul is greatly filled with the scorn of those at ease, the contempt of the proud.


## Chapter 124

1 A song of the steps of David. If the LORD had not been for us, let Israel say now:

2 Had not the LORD been on our side when men rose against us,

3 Then the living would have swallowed us up, in the burning of their anger against us.

4 Then the waters flooded over our soul.

5 Then the swelling waters would have swept over our soul.

6 Blessed be the LORD who has not given us as prey to their teeth.

7 Our soul is escaped as a bird from the snare; the snare is broken, and we are escaped.

8 Help us by the name of the LORD, maker of the heavens and the earth.


## Chapter 125

1 A song of the steps: those trusting in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which does not totter, but endures forever.

2 Jerusalem is surrounded by hills, and the LORD is around his people, from now and forevermore.

3 For the sceptre of wickedness shall not rest upon the lot of the righteous, so that the righteous do not stretch forth their hands in iniquity.

4 The LORD has done good to the good, and to the upright in their hearts.

5 And those who distort their speech—Jehovah will lead them, the workers of affliction; peace upon Israel.


## Chapter 126

1 A song of the steps: when the LORD brought back the captivity of Zion, we were like dreamers.

2 Then our mouth will be filled with laughter and our tongue with rejoicing; then the nations will say, "The LORD has done great things with these."

3 The LORD has done great things for us; we were joyful.

4 Restore our captivity, O LORD, like the streams in the south.

5 Those who sow in tears shall reap with joy.

6 He shall go on his way weeping, bearing the precious seed; he shall come again with joy, bearing his sheaves.


## Chapter 127

1 A song of the steps for Solomon: If the LORD does not build the house, in vain do its builders labor in it; if the LORD does not guard the city, in vain does the watchman stay awake.

2 It is vain for you to rise early, to delay your sitting, eating the bread of sorrow; so he gives sleep to his beloved.

3 Behold, the heritage of the LORD is children; the reward is the fruit of the womb.

4 Like arrows in the hand of a warrior, so are the sons of youth.

5 Blessed is the man whose quiver is full of them; he shall not be ashamed when he speaks with his enemies at the gate.


## Chapter 128

1 A song of the steps: happy is everyone who fears the LORD, walking in his ways.

2 The labour of your hands, when you eat it, is your happiness and good for you.

3 Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine within your house; your children like olive plants around your table.

4 Behold, indeed thus shall every one who fears the LORD be blessed.

5 May the LORD bless you from Zion and grant you gladness in Jerusalem all your life.

6 See children of your sons, peace upon Israel.


## Chapter 129

1 A song of the steps: Greatly have they afflicted me from my youth, let Israel now say.

2 Many have afflicted me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.

3 Upon my back they have plowed furrows; they made long their furrows.

4 The LORD is righteous; he has cut off the cords of the wicked.

5 Let them be ashamed and turned backward, all who hate Zion.

6 Let them be like grass of the roofs, which a foretime scorches and withers.

7 so that the reaper does not fill his hand, nor he who gathers the grain fill his bosom.

8 And they that pass by do not say, "The LORD bless you," "We bless you in the name of the LORD."


## Chapter 130

1 A song of the steps: from the depths I called to you, the LORD.

2 (my) Lord, hear my voice; may your ears be attentive to the voice of my intreaty.

3 If faults are watched, O the LORD, who can stand?

4 For with you is forgiveness, so that you may be feared.

5 I waited for the LORD, my soul waited, and for his word I hoped.

6 My soul is for the Lord, more than the watchers for the morning, watchers for the morning.

7 Let Israel seek the LORD, for with the LORD is mercy, and with him abundant redemption.

8 But he will redeem Israel from all his faults.


## Chapter 131

1 A song of the steps of David. the LORD, my heart is not haughty, nor my eyes lofty, neither have I walked in great matters, nor in wonders beyond me.

2 If I had not quieted and stilled my soul like a weaned child with its mother, like a weaned child is my soul.

3 Israel shall cry to the LORD from now and alway.


## Chapter 132

1 A song of the steps: O LORD, remember David and all his afflictions.

2 which swore to the LORD, vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob.

3 If I enter the dwelling of my home, if I go up to the bed of my couch.

4 if I give sleep to my eyes, slumber to my eye-lids.

5 Until I find a place for the LORD, dwelling places for the Mighty One of Jacob.

6 Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of Jaar.

7 Let us go into his tabernacles; let us worship at his footstool.

8 Rise up, O LORD, to your resting place, you and the ark of your strength.

9 Your priests will clothe themselves in righteousness and your faithful ones will shout for joy.

10 For David your servant, do not turn away the face of your anointed.

11 The LORD has sworn to David a sure oath from which he will not turn back: “From the fruit of your womb I will set upon your throne.”

12 If your children keep my covenant and my testimony, then their children also forever shall sit upon your throne.

13 For Jehovah has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling place.

14 This is my resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.

15 Her food bless I will surely bless; her poor I will satisfy with bread.

16 And his priests clothe themselves in salvation, and his faithful shout aloud in joy.

17 There I will make a horn to grow for David; I have prepared a lamp for my anointed.

18 May his enemies be clothed with shame, but upon him may his crown flourish.


## Chapter 133

1 A song of the steps of David: behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to dwell together.

2 Like the good oil upon the head descending upon the beard, Aaron's beard, descending upon the edge of his garments.

3 Like dew upon Hermon that descends upon the hills of Zion, for there the LORD commanded the blessing, life for evermore.


## Chapter 134

1 A song of the steps: behold, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, who stand in the house of the LORD by night.

2 Lift up your hands in holiness and bless the LORD.

3 May the LORD bless you from Zion, the maker of the heavens and the earth.


## Chapter 135

1 Praise Jah, praise the name of the LORD, praise servants of the LORD.

2 who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God.

3 Praise Jah, for the Lord is good; sing to his name, for it is pleasant.

4 For Jacob is the choice of the LORD, Israel is his special treasure.

5 For I know that great is the LORD, and our Lord above all gods.

6 All that the LORD has desired, he has done in the heavens and on the earth, in the seas and in all their depths.

7 Lifting vapours from the uttermost edge of the land, making lightning for the rain, sending forth the wind from his storehouses.

8 who struck the firstborn of Egypt, from man to beast.

9 He sent his marks and miracles among Egypt, in Pharaoh and in all his servants.

10 who struck many nations and slew powerful kings.

11 to Sihon king of the Amorites and to Og king of Bashan and to all the kingdoms of Canaan

12 And he gave their land as an inheritance, an inheritance to Israel his people.

13 The LORD, your name is forever; the LORD, your remembrance to all generations.

14 For the LORD will judge his people, and upon his servants he will have compassion.

15 The idols of the Gentiles are silver and gold, the work of human hands.

16 They have a mouth, but they do not speak; they have eyes, but they do not see.

17 They have ears but do not hear; a nose they have, yet no breath is in their mouths.

18 Like them shall their makers become, all who trust in them.

19 O house of Israel, bless the LORD; O house of Aaron, bless the LORD.

20 The house of Levi, bless the LORD; you who fear the LORD, bless the LORD.

21 Blessed be the LORD from Zion, who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise Jah.


## Chapter 136

1 Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his kindness endures forever.

2 Give thanks to the God of gods, for his kindness endures forever.

3 Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his kindness is alway.

4 to him who alone does great wonders, for his kindness is alway.

5 To the maker of the heavens with wisdom: for his mercy endures forever.

6 To him who spread out the earth over the waters, for his kindness is everlasting.

7 to the maker of great lights, for his kindness is everlasting.

8 the sun to rule by day, for his kindness is forever.

9 the moon and the stars to govern the night, for his kindness is alway.

10 To the one who struck Egypt in their firstborn, for his kindness is everlasting.

11 And Israel went out from among them, for his kindness is everlasting.

12 with a strong hand and an outstretched arm, for his mercy endures forever.

13 who divided the Red Sea into parts, for his kindness is everlasting.

14 and brought Israel through it, for his kindness is everlasting.

15 And Pharaoh and his army the LORD swept into the Red Sea, for his kindness is forever.

16 For leading his folk through the wilderness, for his kindness is alway.

17 To him who struck great kings, for his kindness is alway.

18 And struck down mighty kings, for his kindness is alway.

19 To Sihon, king of the Amorites, for his mercy endures forever.

20 Og, king of Bashan, for his kindness is alway.

21 And gave their land as an inheritance, for his kindness endures forever.

22 An inheritance for Israel his servant, for his kindness is forever.

23 When we were brought low, he remembered us, for his lovingkindness is alway.

24 And he delivered us from our adversaries, for his kindness is everlasting.

25 Giving bread to all flesh, for his kindness is forever.

26 Give thanks to God of heaven, for his lovingkindness is forever.


## Chapter 137

1 By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat and also wept when we remembered Zion.

2 Upon the willows in the midst of her we hung our harps.

3 For there our captors asked us for words of song, and our tormentors for joy: “Sing us a song of Zion.”

4 How can we sing the song of the LORD on alien soil?

5 If I forget you, Jerusalem, may my right hand forget.

6 May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth if I do not remember you, if I do not raise up Jerusalem above the head of my joy.

7 Remember, O LORD, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem, who said, "Raze it, raze it down to its foundation."

8 Daughter of Babylon, the devastated, blessed is he who repays you your recompense that you have repaid us.

9 Blessed is the one who seizes and dashes your infants against the rock.


## Chapter 138

1 David. I will praise you with all my heart; before the angels I will sing to you.

2 I bow down toward your holy temple and give thanks to your name for your steadfast love and your faithfulness, because you have exalted your word above all your name.

3 In the day that I called, you answered me; you strengthened my soul with might.

4 All the kings of the earth will acknowledge you, O LORD, for they have heard the words of your mouth.

5 And they will sing along the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.

6 For high is the LORD, and the lowly He sees, and the lofty He knows from afar.

7 If I walk in the midst of trouble, you preserve my life; your hand strikes out against the fury of my adversaries, and your right hand saves me.

8 The LORD will fulfill for me; the LORD, Your steadfast favour endures perpetually—do not forsake the works of Your hands.


## Chapter 139

1 For the director: a psalm of David. O Jehovah, thou hast searched me and known me.

2 You know my sitting and my rising, you discern my thought from afar.

3 You have removed my way and my pasture, and (in) all my paths you have brought distress.

4 For there is not a word on my tongue; behold, O LORD, you know it altogether.

5 You hemmed me in behind and before, and set Your hand upon me.

6 Cunning from me is exalted; I cannot reach it.

7 Where can I go from your spirit, and where can I flee from your presence?

8 If I ascend to the heavens, there you are; and if I make low to the grave, behold, you are there.

9 I will take up the wings of the morning; I will dwell in the uttermost part of the sea.

10 Even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me.

11 And I said, "Only darkness will overcome me, and night is light for me."

12 Even the darkness is not dark to you, and the night is as bright as the day; darkness is as light.

13 For you formed my kidneys; you covered me in my mother's womb.

14 I thank you because your works are wonderful and awesome; my soul knows it very well.

15 My substance is not hidden from you, who formed me in secret; I was intricately woven in the depths of the earth.

16 Your eyes saw my unformed substance; in your scroll all of them were written, the days that were formed, when not one of them yet existed.

17 And to me, how precious are your thoughts, O God! How great is the sum of them!

18 If I would count them, they are more than sand; I awake, and still I am with you.

19 If God kills the wicked, then men of blood turn away from me.

20 For they speak against you wickedly; your enemies take your name in vain.

21 Do I not hate, O Jehovah, those who hate You, and loathe those who rise up against You?

22 I hate them with complete hatred; I count them my enemies.

23 Search me, God, and know my heart; test me and know my thoughts.

24 See if the path of sorrow is in me, and lead me in the way of alway.


## Chapter 140

1 For the director: a psalm of David. Deliver me, O LORD, from the evil man; from the man of violence preserve me.

2 who devise adversity in their heart; every day they stir up wars.

3 They have sharpened their tongues like the venom of an adder; under their lips is wrath. Selah.

4 Keep me, O LORD, from the hand of the wicked; preserve me from the violent man, who devise to trip my feet.

5 They have hidden a snare for me, and cords they have spread a net beside the path; traps they have set for me. Selah.

6 I said to the LORD, "You are my God; give ear, O LORD, to the voice of my supplications."

7 God, (my) Lord, the strength of my deliverance, a shield over my head in the day of battle.

8 Do not give, O LORD, the desire of the wicked; do not fulfill his wicked device, lest they be exalted. Selah.

9 The chief of my companions is the trouble of their speech; he will cover them.

10 He will throw them down; upon them (burning) coal will fall, in fire, in a deep pit they will not rise.

11 A man of slander will not be established in the land; a man of violence, evil will overthrow him.

12 For the LORD will plead the cause of the needy, the judgment of the poor.

13 Only the righteous will give thanks to your fame; the upright will dwell in your presence.


## Chapter 141

1 A psalm of David. O the LORD, I have called upon you; make haste to me; give ear to my voice when I cry to you.

2 May my prayer be set before you as (sweet) incense, the lifting up of my hands as a (meat) offering of evening.

3 Set a watch, O LORD, before my mouth; keep the door of my lips.

4 Do not let your heart consent to evil, to practice deeds of wickedness with men who work iniquity, nor delight in their dainties.

5 Let a righteous one strike me—it is a kindness; let him rebuke me—it is oil on my head; do not my head refuse it, for still my prayer is against their adversities.

6 Their judges fell into the hands of the rocky stronghold; and they would hear my words, for they were pleasant.

7 Like one who plows and cleaves the ground, our bones were scattered to the mouth of Sheol.

8 For to you, God my Lord, are my eyes; in you I take refuge; do not pour out my soul.

9 Keep me from the hand of the snare which they have laid for me, and from the traps of those who work iniquity.

10 The wicked will fall into their own net; I will pass through safely.


## Chapter 142

1 A Maschil of David, when he was in the cave, a prayer. My voice to the LORD I cry, my voice to the LORD I plead.

2 I pour out my complaint before him; I declare my distress before him.

3 When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, you knew my path; along this way I was going, they concealed a snare for me.

4 Look to the right and see—there is no one who recognizes me; all escape from me is gone, no one cares for my soul.

5 I cried to you, O Lord; I said, you are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.

6 Give ear to my cry, for I am greatly afflicted; deliver me from my pursuers, for they are too strong for me.

7 Bring my soul out of prison, that I may praise your name; the righteous will crown themselves because of your goodness to me.


## Chapter 143

1 A psalm of David. O the LORD, hear my prayer; give ear to my supplication; in your faithfulness answer me, in your righteousness.

2 Do not enter into judgment with your servant, for no living being will be justified before you.

3 For the enemy has pursued my soul, crushed my life to the ground, seated me in darkness like the eternally dead.

4 My spirit was overwhelmed within me; my heart within me was appalled.

5 I remembered days of old; I mused on all your deeds; in the works of your hands I meditated.

6 I have spread my hands to you; my soul is as a parched land toward you. Selah.

7 Hurry, answer me, O the LORD; my spirit is consumed; do not hide your face from me, lest I become like those who go down into the pit.

8 Make me hear your mercy in the morning, for in you I have trusted; make me know the way in which I should walk, for to you I have lifted up my soul.

9 Deliver me from my enemies, O LORD; to you I have fled for refuge.

10 Teach me to do your will, for you are my God; your good spirit will guide me in an even land.

11 For your name's sake, O LORD, preserve me; according to your justice, bring my soul out of distress.

12 By your mercy I will destroy my enemies, and you will annihilate all who oppose my life, for I am your servant.


## Chapter 144

1 Of David. Blessed be Jehovah, the Lord, my rock, who teaches my hands to battle, my fingers to war.

2 My favour and my fortress, my Misgab and my deliverer, my shield and in whom I take refuge, the one who subdues peoples under me.

3 O LORD, what is man that You know him, the son of a mortal that You think of him?

4 Man is like vanity; his days are as a passing shadow.

5 Jehovah, bow down the heavens and come down; touch the mountains so that they smoke.

6 Flash your lightning and scatter them; send your arrows and rout them.

7 Stretch out your hand from on high, pull me out and deliver me from many waters, from the hand of foreign sons.

8 Their mouth speaks falsehood, and their right hand is a right hand of deceit.

9 God, a new song I will sing to you; on a psaltery of ten strings I will make music to you.

10 who gives deliverance to kings, who delivers David his servant from the evil sword.

11 Deliver me and rescue me from the hand of the sons of the foreigner, whose mouth speaks vanity and their right hand a right hand of falsehood.

12 Our sons, like plants in their youth, our daughters, like cornerstones, carved in the form of a temple.

13 Our garners are full, yielding all manner of store from store; our flocks are thousands, even ten thousands, in our fields.

14 Our captains are devastated; there is no breach, and no one goes out; and there is no cry in our streets.

15 Blessed is the people whose God is the LORD, blessed is the people whose God is the LORD.


## Chapter 145

1 A praise for David: I will exalt you, my God, O king, and I will bless your name forever and ever.

2 Every day I will bless you and praise your name forever and ever.

3 Great is the LORD and highly to be praised, and to his majesty there is no finding out.

4 Generation after generation will praise your deeds, and they will declare your mighty acts.

5 The beauty of your glorious honour, and your wondrous deeds, I will ponder.

6 The might of Your awesome deeds I will declare, and Your awesome ones will speak.

7 They will utter a remembrance of your great goodness and joyfully sing of your rightness.

8 Jehovah, the Lord, is gracious and merciful, long-suffering and abundant in lovingkindness.

9 Good is the LORD to all and his compassion is over all that he has made.

10 All your works will praise you, O the LORD, and your faithful ones will bless you.

11 They shall speak of the glory of your kingdom and declare your power.

12 To declare to the children of man his mighty acts, and the glorious splendour of his kingdom.

13 Your kingdom is an empire of all ages, and your dominion endures in every generation.

14 The LORD upholds all who are falling and raises up all who are bowed down.

15 The eyes of all look to you, and you give them their food in due season.

16 You open your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing.

17 The LORD is righteous in all his ways and faithful in all his works.

18 The LORD is near to all who call upon him, to all who call upon him in truth.

19 He fulfills the desire of those who fear him; he hears their crying and saves them.

20 The LORD guards all who love him, but all the wicked he will destroy.

21 My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD, and all flesh shall bless his holy name forever and ever.


## Chapter 146

1 Praise Jah. Praise the LORD, O my soul.

2 I will praise the LORD during my life; I will sing to my God while I yet live.

3 Do not trust in nobles, in a son of man who has no salvation.

4 His breath departs; he returns to his land; in that day his thoughts perish.

5 Happy is the one whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD their God.

6 Making the heavens and the earth, the sea and all that is in them, keeping faith forever.

7 The LORD executes justice for the oppressed, gives bread to the hungry, and sets free the bound.

8 The LORD opens the eyes of the blind, the LORD raises up those who are bowed down, the LORD loves the righteous.

9 The LORD protects the stranger; he sustains the fatherless and the widow, but the way of the wicked he subverts.

10 The LORD will reign alway, your God, Zion, for generation and generation. Praise Jah.


## Chapter 147

1 Praise Jah, for it is good; sing praise to our God, for it is pleasant and fitting is praise.

2 The LORD builds Jerusalem; he gathers the outcasts of Israel.

3 He who heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.

4 He counts the number of the stars; to all of them he gives names.

5 Great is our Lord and mighty in power, and to his understanding there is no number.

6 The LORD encourages the humble; He brings the wicked down to the ground.

7 Answer the LORD with thanksgiving; sing to our God with the harp.

8 who covers the heavens with clouds, prepares rain for the earth, makes the mountains grow grass.

9 Giving food to the cattle, to the young of the raven that call.

10 He does not delight in the strength of the horse; he takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.

11 The LORD takes pleasure in those who fear him, in those who hope in his mercy.

12 Praise Jerusalem the LORD, glorify your God Zion.

13 For he has strengthened the bars of your gates; he has blessed your children within you.

14 He who sets your border at peace will satisfy you with the fat of wheat.

15 He sends his word to the earth; his word runs swiftly.

16 who gives snow like wool; he scatters hoar frost like ashes.

17 He casts his ice like morsels; who can stand before his cold?

18 He sends his word and melts them; he makes his wind blow, and the waters flow.

19 He declares his statutes and judgments to Jacob, his commandments to Israel.

20 He has not dealt so with any nation; as for judgments, they did not know them. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 148

1 Praise Jah, praise the LORD from the heavens, praise him in the heights.

2 Praise him, all his messengers; praise him, all his hosts.

3 Praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all you stars of light.

4 Praise him, heavens of the heavens, and the waters which are above the heavens.

5 Praise the name of the LORD, for he commanded and they were created.

6 And He established them forever and ever, a decree He gave that shall not pass away.

7 Praise Jehovah from the earth, sea-monsters and all depths.

8 Fire and hailstones, snow and vapour, stormy wind fulfilling his word.

9 The hills and every little hill, the tree of fruit and every cedar tree.

10 The living creature and every beast, the moving thing and the bird of wing.

11 Kings of the land and all nations, princes and all judges of the earth.

12 Young men and also maidens, old men with children.

13 Praise the name of the LORD, for exalted is his fame alone; his glory is over earth and heavens.

14 He raised a horn for His people, a praise for all His faithful, for the children of Israel, a nation close to Him. Praise the LORD.


## Chapter 149

1 Praise Jah, sing to the LORD a new song; his praise in the assembly of the faithful.

2 Israel shall rejoice in its Maker; the children of Zion shall exult in their king.

3 Praise his name in dancing; with timbrel and harp play to him.

4 For the LORD delights in his people; he will beautify the humble with deliverance.

5 Let the faithful exult in glory; they shall shout for joy upon their couches.

6 High praises of God in their throats and a two-edged sword in their hands.

7 To execute vengeance upon the nations, reproofs upon the peoples.

8 To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron.

9 To execute upon them a prescribed judgment: glory is for all his faithful; hallelujah.


## Chapter 150

1 Praise Jah, praise God in his holiness, praise him in the firmament of his strength.

2 Praise him for his mighty acts; praise him according to the abundance of his greatness.

3 Praise him with the sound of the cornet; praise him with the psaltery and harp.

4 Praise him with tabret and dancing; praise him with stringed instruments and organ.

5 Praise him with the cymbals of loud noise; praise him with the cymbals of shouting.

6 All that has breath shall praise the LORD. Praise the LORD.


# PROVERBS

## Chapter 1

1 Proverbs of Solomon son of David king of Israel.

2 To know wisdom and instruction, to understand sayings of understanding.

3 To take instruction in wisdom: justice, and judgment, and equity.

4 To give to the simple ones prudence, to the youth knowledge and discretion.

5 Let a wise man hear and increase in learning, and a man of understanding acquire wise counsels.

6 To understand a byword and an interpretation, the sayings of the wise and their riddles.

7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.

8 Hear, my son, the discipline of your father, and do not forsake the law of your mother.

9 For they are an ornament of grace upon your head and chains upon your neck.

10 My child, if sinners entice you, do not consent.

11 If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait for blood-guiltiness; let us ambush the innocent for nothing.”

12 Let us swallow them alive like Sheol, and whole, like those who go down to the pit.

13 All wealth found is enough; let our houses be filled with spoil.

14 Cast your lot among us; one bag shall be for all of us.

15 My child, do not walk in their way; keep your foot from their path.

16 For their feet run to evil, and they hasten to shed blood.

17 For the net-work is spread in vain, in the sight of every master of winged creatures.

18 And they lie in wait for their own blood, they set snares for their own souls.

19 So are the ways of everyone who makes dishonest gain; it takes the life of its owners.

20 Wisdom cries aloud in the streets, in the broad places she raises her voice.

21 At the beginning of the assemblies you shall proclaim, at the entrances of the city gates you shall speak your words.

22 How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity, and scorners delight in scorning, and fools hate knowledge?

23 Return to my correction; behold, I will pour out my spirit to you; I will declare my words to you.

24 Because I called and you refused, stretched out my hand and there was no one paying attention:

25 They rejected all my advice, and my reproof they did not accept.

26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your dread comes.

27 When your dread and your destruction come upon you like a storm, when distress and anguish come upon you.

28 Then they will call to me, but I will not answer; they will seek me, but they will not find me.

29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD.

30 They did not accept my counsel; they rejected all my rebuke.

31 And they will eat the reward of their way, and from their devices they will be filled.

32 For the backsliding of the simple will slay them, and the prosperity of fools will destroy them.

33 And he who listens to me will dwell in assurance, and will rest securely from dread of adversity.


## Chapter 2

1 My son, if you receive my words and keep my precepts with you,

2 Incline your ear to wisdom and apply your heart to understanding.

3 For if you call aloud for knowledge and raise your voice for understanding,

4 If you seek her as money, and search for her as hidden treasures.

5 Then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.

6 For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth comes knowledge and understanding.

7 For the upright there is wisdom, a shield to those who walk in integrity.

8 To keep his paths of judgment, and his way he will guard.

9 Then you will understand justice and judgment and equity—every good path.

10 For wisdom will enter your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.

11 Discretion will keep you, understanding will guard you.

12 To deliver you from an evil path, from a man speaking perverse things.

13 Those who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness.

14 They who rejoice to do evil shall exult in perverse things.

15 whose paths are crooked and who turn aside from their ways.

16 To deliver you from an adulteress, from a foreign woman whose speech is smooth.

17 She who forsakes the confederacy of her youth, and forgets the covenant of her God.

18 For her house has sunk down to death, and her paths lead to the dead.

19 All who enter her will not return, and will not attain the paths of life.

20 so that you may walk in the way of the good and keep the paths of the righteous

21 For the upright will dwell in the land, and the blameless will be established in it.

22 And the wicked will be cut off from the land, and the treacherous will be removed from it.


## Chapter 3

1 My son, do not forget my law, and let my commandments keep your heart.

2 For length of days and years of life and peace will be added to you.

3 Favour and truth shall not forsake you; bind them upon your neck; write them upon the tablet of your heart.

4 and finds favour and good discretion in the eyes of God and man.

5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not rely on your own understanding.

6 In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.

7 Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and turn from evil.

8 Let health be to your navel and marrow to your bones.

9 Honor the LORD from your wealth and from the first of all your produce.

10 May your barns be filled with abundance, and your wine presses overflow with new wine.

11 My son, do not despise the chastening of the LORD, neither be weary of his rebuke.

12 For whom Jehovah loves he reproves, even as a father reproves the son in whom he delights.

13 Happy is the person who finds wisdom, and the person who obtains understanding.

14 For her merchandise is better than the mart of silver, and her revenue than fine gold.

15 It is more precious than rubies; all your delights are not comparable to it.

16 Length of days in her right hand; in her left, riches and honour.

17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.

18 A tree of life is it to those who hold fast to it, and happy is everyone who takes hold of it.

19 The LORD founded the earth by wisdom; he established the heavens by understanding.

20 By his knowledge the depths were split open, and the skies dripped dew.

21 My son, do not let them depart from your eyes; keep sound wisdom and discretion.

22 And they shall be life to your soul and grace to your neck.

23 Then you will walk in assurance, and your foot will not stumble.

24 If you lie down, you will not fear; you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.

25 Do not fear sudden dread, nor the desolation of the wicked when it comes.

26 For the LORD will be your confidence, and he will keep your foot from being taken.

27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due when you have the power to act.

28 Do not say to your neighbor, “Go and come back; tomorrow I will give,” when you have it with you.

29 Do not plot evil against your fellow who dwells securely with you.

30 Do not afflict another without cause, if he has not repaid you with adversity.

31 Do not envy a man of violence, nor choose any of his ways.

32 For the deceiver is an abominable thing, the LORD's detestation; but with the upright He shares His secret counsel.

33 The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, but he blesses the dwelling of the righteous.

34 If to the scorner he is scoffing, so to the humble he gives favour.

35 The glorious shall inherit honour, but fools exalt shame.


## Chapter 4

1 Hear, children, the instruction of a father, and listen to gain understanding.

2 For I have given you a good doctrine; do not forsake my law.

3 For I was a child to my father, tender and an only son before my mother.

4 He encouraged me and said to me, "Let my advice strengthen your mind; keep my commandments and live."

5 Acquire skilful, acquire knowledge; do not forget and do not turn aside from my speech.

6 Do not forsake her and she will guard you; love her and she will keep you.

7 The beginning of wisdom is to get wisdom, and in all your getting, get understanding.

8 Embrace her, and she will exalt you; she will honor you when you embrace her.

9 Give to your head a band of ornament, a crown of beauty shall protect you.

10 Hear, my son, and accept my words, and your years of life will be many.

11 Along the way of wisdom I have brought you forth; I have led you in paths of equity.

12 When you walk, your pace will not be hindered, and if you run, you will not stumble.

13 Hold fast to discipline; do not let go; keep her, for she is your life.

14 Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not proceed along the way of evil men.

15 Avoid it, do not pass by it; turn away from it and pass on.

16 For they do not sleep unless they have done evil, and their sleep is stolen unless they have caused harm.

17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.

18 And the path of the righteous is like the bright light, going on and brightening until the day is firmly established.

19 The way of the wicked is darkness; they do not know at what they stumble.

20 My son, give attention to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.

21 Do not let them turn away from your eyes; keep them in the midst of your heart.

22 For they are life to those who find them, and to all flesh a healing.

23 Above all else guard your heart, for from it come forth the issues of life.

24 Put away from you froward speech, and perverse talk remove from you.

25 Let your eyes look directly ahead, and your eyelids gaze straight before you.

26 Mark out the path of your journey, and all your ways will be established.

27 Do not turn to the right or to the left; turn your foot from evil.


## Chapter 5

1 My son, attend to my wisdom; incline your ear to my understanding.

2 To keep your (wicked) devices, and your speech will preserve your cunning.

3 For honeycomb drips from the lips of a stranger, and smoother than oil is her mouth.

4 And its latter end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword.

5 Her legs go down to death; the steps of hers sustain the grave.

6 A path of life do not ponder; its ways wander, you will not know.

7 And now, children, listen to me; do not turn aside from my words.

8 Keep far from her path; do not draw near to the entrance of her house.

9 Lest you give your beauty to others, and your years to a cruel one.

10 Lest strangers be filled with your strength, and your labors go to the house of a foreigner.
