11 And you will groan in your latter end, when your body and your kin are consumed.

12 And you shall say, How I have hated chastening, and my heart has despised reproof.

13 And I did not listen to the voice of my teachers, nor incline my ear to my instructors.

14 Almost I had been in all evil, in the midst of the congregation and assembly.

15 Drink water from your own cistern and flowing water from within your own well.

16 Your fountains will burst forth abroad, streams of water in the broad places.

17 May they be yours alone, and not shared with outsiders.

18 May your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth.

19 Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the love of your youth, a precious hind and a graceful roe; let her breasts satisfy you always, and be intoxicated always in her love.

20 Why should you wander, my child, with a foreign woman and embrace the bosom of an outsider?

21 For the LORD sees a man's ways, and all his paths he weighs.

22 His iniquities will seize the wicked, and by the cords of his sin he will be held.

23 He shall die without discipline, and in the abundance of his folly he shall go astray.


## Chapter 6

1 My child, if you have become surety for your friend, if you have struck your hands for a stranger,

2 You are snared by the words of your mouth, you are taken by the speech of your lips.

3 Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, for you have come into the hand of your friend; go, humble yourself, and urgently plead with your friend.

4 Do not give sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eye-lids.

5 Rescued like a roe from the hand, and like a bird from the hand of the fowler.

6 Go to the ant, you sluggard; consider its ways and be wise.

7 which has no captain, ruler, or governor.

8 You prepare her food in the summer; you gather her harvest for eating.

9 How long will you lie down, you sluggard? When will you rise from your sleep?

10 Very little sleep, very little slumber, very little folding of the hands to lie down.

11 And your poverty comes like a runner, and your lack like a man with a shield.

12 A man of Belial, a wicked person, who walks in frowardness of mouth.

13 He mocks with his eyes, he incites with his feet, he points with his fingers.

14 Perverse things in his heart, he devises evil at every time; he will send out strife.

15 Therefore suddenly his calamity will come; in a moment he will be broken, and there is no healing.

16 Six things there are that the LORD hates, yea, seven that are an abomination to his soul.

17 Eyes proud, a tongue deceitful, and hands shedding innocent blood.

18 A heart devising thoughts of wickedness, feet hastening to run to evil.

19 A lying witness breathes out falsehoods, and one who sends strife is between brothers.

20 My child, keep your father's commandment and do not forsake your mother's teaching.

21 Bind them continually upon your heart; tie them around your neck.

22 When you walk, it will guide you; when you lie down, it will watch over you; and when you awake, it will speak with you.

23 For a lamp is the commandment and the law is light, and the way of life is the reproofs of discipline.

24 To keep you from the adulteress, from the foreign woman with her smooth talk.

25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her eyelids take hold of you.

26 For by means of a prostitute one is brought down to a loaf of bread, but the wife of another man captures a precious life.

27 Can a man carry fire in his bosom and his garments not be burned?

28 If a man walks on burning coals, his feet will not be scorched.

29 So is the one who comes to another's wife; none who touches her will go unpunished.

30 They will not despise the thief if he steals to satisfy his appetite when he is hungry.

31 And if he is caught, he shall pay sevenfold; all the wealth of his house shall he give.

32 An adulterer of a wife is a man lacking mind; he who destroys his own life will do it.

33 A plague and shame will find him, and his reproach will not be wiped away.

34 For the jealousy of a man's fury, and he will not pity in the day of vengeance.

35 He will not accept any bribe, nor be swayed though the bribe be large.


## Chapter 7

1 My son, keep my words, and store up my commandments with you.

2 Keep my commandments and live, and my law as the apple of your eye.

3 Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your care.

4 Say to wisdom, "You are my sister," and call understanding your kinswoman.

5 To keep you from an adulteress, from a foreign woman with smooth words.

6 For through the window of my court I gazed through my lattice.

7 And I saw among the simple ones, I discerned among the children a youth lacking heart.

8 Passing along the street beside her bulwark, and toward her house he makes his way.

9 At twilight in the evening of the day, in the blackness of night and darkness.

10 And behold, a woman met him, a harlot with attire, and guarded of heart.

11 It is a roaring lion and a rebel; in her household her feet will not remain.

12 once in the street, once in the broad plazas, and at every corner she lies in wait.

13 And she held fast to him and kissed him; she hardened her face and said to him.

14 My peace offerings are upon me; today I have fulfilled my vows.

15 Therefore I have gone out to meet you, to seek your face, and I have found you.

16 I spread a covering of tapestry upon my bed, with carved work of fine linen from Egypt.

17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

18 Come, let us drink our fill of love until the morning; let us delight in affection.

19 For there is no man in his house; he has gone away on a distant journey.

20 He took a bag of silver in his hand; on the day of reckoning he will come to his house.

21 With her abundance of fair speech she leads him astray; by the flattery of her lips she causes him to be driven away.

22 He goes after her suddenly, like an ox to the slaughter, like a fool to the stocks.

23 Until the arrow pierces his liver, like a bird rushing to the snare, and he does not know that it is for his soul.

24 And now, children, listen to me, and pay attention to the words of my mouth.

25 Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways, do not stray into her paths.

26 For she has felled many victims; indeed, all the slain by her are mighty.

27 Her house leads down to the grave, descending to the inner chambers of death.


## Chapter 8

1 Will not wisdom call, and understanding give her voice?

2 At the highest point above the path she takes her stand, in the house of the ways she stations herself.

3 Near the gates according to the city, at the entrance of the openings, they cry out.

4 To you I call, O people, and my voice is to the children of man.

5 Understand, you foolish, prudence, and you simple, understand care for.

6 Hear me, for I will speak of leaders, and the opening of my lips is upright.

7 For assuredly my taste utters truth, and on my lips is an abomination of wickedness.

8 Even the words of my mouth are altogether just, and there is no crookedness or perversity in them.

9 All of them are upright to those who understand, and right to those who find knowledge.

10 Take my instruction and not silver, and knowledge rather than fine gold.

11 For skilful wisdom is better than rubies, and all things desired cannot compare with it.

12 I am skilful wisdom, I dwell in prudence, and I find cunning thought.

13 The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, I hate.

14 To me belong counsel and wisdom; I have understanding, to me belongs strength.

15 By me kings reign and rulers decree justice.

16 By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of righteousness.

17 I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently will find me.

18 Riches and honour are with me, enduring wealth and righteousness.

19 My fruit is better than fine gold and my revenue than choice silver.

20 In the path of justice I will walk, in the midst of the paths of judgment.

21 To grant inheritance to those who love me there is, and their storehouses I will fill.

22 The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.

23 From of old I was poured out from the beginning, from aforetime of the earth.

24 When there were no deeps, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains of water, I was born.

25 Before the mountains were established, before the hills I was brought forth.

26 before he made the earth and the outside, and the beginning of the dust of the world.

27 When he established the heavens, there I was; when he drew a circle on the face of the deep.

28 When he made firm the clouds above, when the fountains of the deep grew strong (with the fierceness of his eyes).

29 When he appointed the sea with its decree, and the waters do not transgress his mouth, by his decree the foundations of the earth are established.

30 I was beside him as one brought up, and I was daily his delight, playing before him continually.

31 Playing in the habitable part of his world, and my delights are with the children of man.

32 And now, children, listen to me, and happy are those who keep my ways.

33 Hear instruction and be wise, and do not refuse discipline.

34 Happy is the person who listens to me, watching daily at my doors, to keep watch at the posts of my entrance.

35 For whoever finds me finds life, and obtains favor from the LORD.

36 But he who sins against me does violence to his own soul; all who hate me love death.


## Chapter 9

1 Every wise woman built her house; she cut out its seven pillars.

2 She has slaughtered her beast, mixed her wine, and also set her table.

3 She sent her damsels, calling upon the heights of the city.

4 Who is the foolish one who will turn aside? The one lacking understanding said to him.

5 Come, eat my bread, and drink my wine.

6 Leave the foolish and live, and walk in the way of understanding.

7 To reprove a scorner brings him confusion, and he who reproves a wicked man receives a blemish.

8 Do not rebuke a scorner, lest he hate you; rebuke a wise man, and he will love you.

9 Give to the wise, and he will grow wiser; teach the righteous, and he will add to his learning.

10 The beginning of wisdom is fear of the LORD, and knowledge of the holy ones is understanding.

11 For by me your days shall be multiplied, and years of life added to you.

12 If you have been wise, you have been wise for yourself; if you have scoffed, you will bear it alone.

13 A wife of foolishness is loud, simple, and knows nothing.

14 And she sat at the entrance of her house upon a lofty throne.

15 To call to those passing along the way, who straighten their paths.

16 Who is the foolish one who will turn aside? And the one lacking care who will say to him?

17 Stolen water is sweet, and bread of secret places is pleasant.

18 He does not know that the dead are there, in the depths of the grave, her guests.


## Chapter 10

1 Proverbs of Solomon: a wise son makes a father glad, but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother.

2 Treasures of iniquity will not profit, but justice will deliver from death.

3 The LORD will not starve the soul of the righteous, but He will drive away the calamity of the wicked.

4 A poor man's hand makes him poor, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.

5 A wise son gathers in summer, but a son who sleeps at harvest brings shame.

6 Blessings are for the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked will cover violence.

7 The memorial of the righteous is for blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.

8 A wise heart takes precepts, but a prating fool will be crushed.

9 Walking in integrity will go safely, but one who distorts his ways will be known.

10 One who gives a hostile look brings sorrow, and a fool with talkative lips will be struck down.

11 The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked covers over violence.

12 Hate stirs up strife, but love covers over all transgressions.

13 On the lips of the discerning wisdom is found, but a rod is for the back of him lacking heart.

14 The wise will store up knowledge, and the mouth of the fool is near destruction.

15 The wealth of a rich man is his strong city, the ruin of the poor is their poverty.

16 The labour of the righteous leads to life; the gain of the wicked to sin.

17 A path of life is the one who keeps discipline, but whoever forsakes reproof leads astray.

18 One who covers hate with lying lips, and one who brings forth a slander is a fool.

19 In abundance of words transgression does not cease, but one who restrains his speech is wise.

20 Choice silver is the tongue of the righteous; the heart of the wicked is as little.

21 The lips of the righteous feed many, but the foolish ones die for lack of heart.

22 The blessing of the LORD enriches, and he adds no sorrow with it.

23 Making a mock is sport to a fool, but wisdom is for a man of understanding.

24 The fear of the wicked will come upon him, and the desire of the righteous will be granted.

25 Like a storm passing and no wicked one remains, but the righteous is the foundation of the world.

26 Like vinegar to the tooth and smoke to the eyes, so is the lazy one to those who send him.

27 The fear of the LORD adds days, but the years of the wicked will be shortened.

28 The hope of the righteous is joy, but the expectation of the wicked perishes.

29 The way of the LORD is a strong fortress for the upright, but destruction to those who do iniquity.

30 The righteous shall never be moved, but the wicked shall not dwell in the land.

31 The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the tongue of perversity shall be cut off.

32 The lips of the righteous know acceptable speech, but the mouth of the wicked [knows] perverse things.


## Chapter 11

1 False balances are an abomination to the LORD, but a complete weight is his delight.

2 When pride comes, then comes shame; but with the humble is wisdom.

3 The integrity of the upright will guide them, but the perverseness of the treacherous will destroy them.

4 Wealth will not profit on the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.

5 The justice of the blameless will make his way straight, but by his wickedness the wicked will fall.

6 The justice of the upright will deliver them, but the wicked will be caught in the calamity of the treacherous.

7 When a wicked man dies, hope perishes; the expectation of the violent is lost.

8 The righteous is delivered from adversity, but the wicked comes into his place.

9 With the mouth a hypocrite destroys his neighbor, but through the knowledge of the righteous they are delivered.

10 At the fair of the righteous the city rejoices, and at the destruction of the wicked there is gladness.

11 By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted, but by the mouth of the wicked it is overthrown.

12 He who despises his neighbour is devoid of mind, but a man of understanding holds his peace.

13 One who goes about as a talebearer reveals a secret, but the one faithful in spirit conceals a matter.

14 Where there is no wise counsel, the people fall, but deliverance is in the abundance of counselors.

15 It is a disaster to become a guarantor for a stranger, and one who hates striking hands in pledge is secure.

16 A wife of grace sustains honour, but the violent are sustained by riches.

17 A man of kindness benefits his own soul, but the cruel troubles his own flesh.

18 The wicked one does a work of deceit, but the one who sows righteousness reaps a true reward.

19 Thus justice leads to life, but the one who pursues evil meets death.

20 The abominable thing to the LORD is the crooked of heart, but his delight is the blameless of way.

21 Hand to hand, the wicked will not go unpunished, but the seed of the righteous will escape.

22 A gold earring in a swine's snout: a woman fair and lacking discretion.

23 The desire of the righteous is only good; the expectation of the wicked is wrath.

24 There is one who scatters and yet gains more, and one who withholds what is right but only to poverty.

25 The generous soul shall be made fat, and the one who waters shall also be watered himself.

26 Withholding wheat, a nation will curse him, but a blessing belongs to the head of the one who breaks through.

27 One who seeks acceptance finds favour, but one who seeks evil, it shall come upon him.

28 He who trusts in riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like a leaf.

29 He who destroys his house inherits the wind, and a foolish servant belongs to the wise of heart.

30 The first-fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and the wise one takes souls.

31 Behold, the righteous is repaid in the land; how much more the wicked and the sinner.


## Chapter 12

1 Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but he who hates reproof is brutish.

2 Good is the acceptance of the LORD, but a man of wicked devices he condemns.

3 A person will not be established in wickedness, but the root of the righteous will not be moved.

4 A wife of valor is a crown to her husband, but a shameful wife is like decay in his bones.

5 The thoughts of the righteous are judgment, the counsels of the wicked are deceit.

6 The words of the wicked lie in ambush for blood; the speech of the upright delivers them.

7 The wicked are overturned and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.

8 According to his wisdom a man is praised, and a foolish heart will be despised.

9 Better is one lightly esteemed who has a servant, than one self-important and lacking food.

10 The righteous knows the appetite of his beast, but the pity of the wicked is cruel.

11 He who works his land will be satisfied with food, but he who pursues worthless things lacks sense.

12 The wicked desire the snare of evildoers, but the root of the righteous will yield.

13 Because of the transgression of speech, a snare of evil, the righteous escaped from distress.

14 From the fruit of a man's mouth one is satisfied with good, and the reward of a man's hands is given to him.

15 The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but one who listens to wise counsel is prudent.

16 A fool—his anger will be known that day, and his shame is naked.

17 A faithful witness declares what is just, but a false witness is deceit.

18 There is one who speaks rashly like the piercings of a sword, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.

19 A truthful speech endures perpetually, but a lying tongue lasts only until it is stilled.

20 Deceit is in the heart of those who plot evil, but for those who counsel peace there is joy.

21 No harm shall befall the righteous, but the wicked are full of evil.

22 The abominable thing to the LORD is lying lips, but those who do faithfulness are his delight.

23 A prudent person conceals knowledge, but the heart of fools proclaims folly.

24 The hand of the diligent shall rule, but slothfulness shall be for tribute.

25 Care in a man's heart weighs it down, but a good word brings it joy.

26 More than his fellow is the righteous one, but the way of the wicked leads them astray.

27 Deceit shall not catch his food, and man's wealth is a costly ransom.

28 In the path of righteousness is life, and the way of the path leads to death.


## Chapter 13

1 A wise son accepts a father's discipline, but a scoffer does not heed rebuke.

2 From the fruit of a man's mouth one eats good, but the soul of traitors feeds on violence.

3 Whoever guards his mouth preserves his life; he who breaks [his] lips [brings] destruction on himself.

4 The appetite of the sluggard craves and gets nothing, but the soul of the diligent is made rich.

5 The righteous hates a word of falsehood, and the wicked brings shame and disgrace.

6 Justice preserves integrity of way, and wickedness subverts the sinner.

7 There is one who grows rich yet has nothing; another who grows poor despite great wealth.

8 A rich man’s ransom is his wealth, but the poor does not respond to rebuke.

9 The bright of the righteous will rejoice, and the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished.

10 Only with pride comes strife, and wisdom with those who take counsel.

11 Wealth from vanity will diminish, but one who gathers by hand increases.

12 Delayed hope makes the heart sick, but a fulfilled desire is a tree of life.

13 Whoever despises an act will be punished, but whoever fears a commandment will be rewarded.

14 The law of the wise is a fountain of life, to turn from the snares of death.

15 Good discretion gives grace, but the way of the treacherous is enduring.

16 Everyone acts with knowledge, but a fool spreads folly.

17 A wicked messenger will fall into trouble, but a trustworthy ambassador brings healing.

18 Poverty and shame come to him who forsakes correction, but he who keeps reproof shall be honoured.

19 A desire is pleasant to a soul, but the abomination of fools is to turn from evil.

20 He who walks with the wise will become wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm.

21 Adversity pursues the wicked, but to the righteous he will repay good.

22 It is good to leave an inheritance to the children of one's children, and the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the righteous.

23 Much food comes from the plowing of the poor, and there is one swept away without justice.

24 He who withholds his rod hates his son, but the one who loves him diligently applies discipline.

25 The righteous eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the belly of the wicked will lack.


## Chapter 14

1 The wisdom of women builds her house, but folly with her hands tears it down.

2 He who walks in equity fears the LORD, but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.

3 According to the mouth of a fool comes a rod of pride, but the lips of the wise will guard them.

4 Where there are no oxen, the crib is clean; but much increase comes by the strength of an ox.

5 A faithful witness does not lie, but a lying witness speaks deceit.

6 The scoffer seeks wisdom and finds none; for insight, he is contemptible.

7 When you come upon a foolish man, go from his presence, for you did not know the lips of knowledge.

8 The wise by prudence understands his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.

9 The guilty mock at sin, but among the upright is acceptance.

10 The care for a soul knows its own bitterness, and in its joy no stranger will intermeddle.

11 The court of the wicked will be destroyed, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

12 There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end leads to death.

13 Even in laughter the heart is pained, and the end of it is heaviness.

14 The backslider in heart will be filled with his own ways, and a good man will be satisfied from his own ways.

15 The simple one believes every word, but the shrewd understands his course.

16 The wise one fears and turns from evil, but the fool rages and is confident.

17 Shortness of temper produces folly, and a man of wicked devices is hated.

18 The foolish inherit folly, and the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

19 The wicked bow before the good, and the wicked are at the gate of the righteous.

20 Even a fellow he hates, the poor man; but the rich man has many lovers, and many love the rich.

21 Whoever despises his neighbor sins, but he who is gracious to the poor, happy is he.

22 Will not those who plow iniquity surely go astray, while those who sow favour and truth reap faithful reward?

23 In all labor there is profit, but mere talk leads only to want.

24 The crown of the wise is riches; the folly of fools is folly.

25 A true witness delivers souls, but a lying witness is deceit.

26 In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence, and for his children he will be a refuge.

27 The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, to turn away from the snares of death.

28 In the abundance of people is the king's honour; but in the lack of people is the ruin of the prince.

29 Long-suffering great understanding, and shortness of spirit exalts folly.

30 The life of the flesh is a healing heart, but rottenness in the bones is envy.

31 One who oppresses the poor insults his Maker, but one who honors him shows favor to the needy.

32 In his adversity the wicked will be thrust aside, but the righteous takes refuge in his death.

33 Wisdom rests securely in the care of the discerning, but is made known among fools.

34 Justice exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to peoples.

35 The king's favour is toward a servant who is prudent, but his wrath will bring disgrace.


## Chapter 15

1 A soft answer turns away anger, but a harsh word stirs up wrath.

2 The tongue of the wise makes knowledge pleasant, but the mouth of fools pours out folly.

3 In every place the eyes of the LORD watch over the evil and the good.

4 A healing tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it is a breaking of the spirit.

5 A fool despiseth the chastening of his father, but he that keepeth reproof becometh prudent.

6 The court of the righteous has great strength, but the revenue of the wicked will be destroyed.

7 The lips of the wise spread knowledge, but the heart of fools does not do so.

8 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

9 The LORD hates the way of the wicked, but loves the one who pursues justice.

10 Chastisement is evil for one who forsakes the path; he who hates rebuke shall die.

11 The grave and destruction are before the LORD, even the hearts of the children of man.

12 A scoffer does not love one who reproves him; he will not go to the wise.

13 A cheerful heart gladdens the face, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is broken.

14 A discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouths of fools feed on folly.

15 All the days of the poor are evil, but good-heartedness is a continual feast.

16 A little with the fear of the LORD is better than a great storehouse and trouble therein.

17 Better is a dinner of herbs, and love there, than a fatted ox, and hatred with it.

18 A man of anger stirs up strife, but one slow to anger quiets contention.

19 The way of the sluggard is as a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is a highway.

20 A wise son gladdens a father, but a foolish man despises his mother.

21 Folly is joy to him that lacks understanding, but a man of wisdom is upright in his way.

22 He who frustrates the devices of the cunning, and makes void the counsel of the secret assembly, and by the abundance of counselors establishes success.

23 Joy comes to a man from an answer of his mouth; how good is a word in its proper time.

24 A path of life upward for the prudent, to turn aside from the grave below.

25 The LORD will sweep away the court of the proud and establish the border of the widow.

26 The abominable thing of the LORD is wicked thoughts, but pure words are His delight.

27 He who overthrows his household by dishonest gain, but hates a gift, will live.

28 The care of a righteous person considers how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours out evil things.

29 Far from the wicked is the LORD, but he hears the prayer of the righteous.

30 Bright of eyes rejoices the heart; good news fattens the bone.

31 An ear that hears life's correction dwells in the midst of the wise.

32 One who breaks discipline despises his soul, but one who hears reproof acquires understanding.

33 The fear of the LORD is instruction in wisdom, and before honour is humility.


## Chapter 16

1 Man devises preparations in mind, but from the LORD comes an answer of tongue.

2 Every way of a man is clean in his own eyes, but the LORD weighs the spirits.

3 Cast your deeds upon the LORD, and your thoughts will be established.

4 The LORD has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of adversity.

5 An abomination to the LORD is everyone proud of heart; hand to hand, he will not be unpunished.

6 By steadfast love and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the LORD one turns from evil.

7 When the LORD is pleased with a man's way, even his enemies make peace with him.

8 Very little with justice is better than abundance of revenue without judgment.

9 The care of a man considers his way, but the LORD directs his steps.

10 The divine sentence upon the lips of the king, in judgment his mouth shall not err.

11 The scales and balances of justice belong to the LORD; all the weights in the bag are his work.

12 An abomination to kings is doing wickedness, for by justice a throne is established.

13 The speech of righteousness is acceptable to kings, and one who speaks fairly is loved.

14 The king's anger is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.

15 In the light of the king's face is life, and his favor is like a cloud of the latter rain.

16 Acquire skilful wisdom, how much better it is than fine gold; and acquire understanding, chosen above silver.

17 A causeway of the upright turns from evil; he who guards his soul preserves his way.

18 Before destruction pride grows, and before a fall a high spirit rises.

19 Better is a lowly spirit than dividing spoil with the proud.

20 One who is prudent about a matter finds good, and whoever trusts in the LORD is blessed.

21 He who is wise in heart is called discerning, and sweetness of lips increases learning.

22 The fountain of life is the discretion of its master, but the discipline of fools is folly.

23 A wise heart will guide his mouth, and on his lips he will increase fair speech.

24 Honey-sweet sayings are like a honeycomb, sweet to the soul and healing to the bone.

25 There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end leads to death.

26 The appetite of the laborer labors for him, because his mouth urges him on.

27 A wicked man devises evil, and on his lips is a burning fire.

28 A man of perverse things stirs up strife, and a separatist incites contention.

29 A violent man entices his neighbor and leads him in a way that is not good.

30 Shutting his eyes to devise perverse things, snapping his lips, he completes evil.

31 A crown of beauty is found in the way of righteousness; gray hair is obtained thereby.

32 Better is a long-suffering person than a mighty warrior, and one who rules his spirit than one who captures a city.

33 The lot is cast into the bosom, but the whole judgment is from the LORD.


## Chapter 17

1 A morsel of bread with peace is better than a house full of sacrificial offerings with strife.

2 A bondman servant will rule over a son who brings shame, and among brothers he will share an inheritance.

3 A fining pot for silver and a furnace for gold, but the LORD is the one who tests hearts.

4 From evil, one who heeds a band of iniquity feeds on the tongue of mischief.

5 One who mocks the poor insults his Maker; one who rejoices in calamity will not be acquitted.

6 A crown of old men is children's children, and the beauty of children is their fathers.

7 It is not fitting for a fool to utter abundant talk, nor for a generous person to utter lying speech.

8 A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of its owner; wherever he turns, he prospers.

9 He who covers rebellion seeks love, but he who repeats an act separates a close friend.

10 Reproof is better for one who understands than striking a fool a hundred times.

11 But the most rebellious seeks evil, and a messenger of calamity will be sent against him.

12 Better to meet a bear robbed of her cubs than to encounter a man in his folly.

13 He repays adversity with good; adversity has not departed from his house.

14 One who starts a quarrel is like one who lets out water; before the dispute breaks out, abandon it.

15 To justify the wicked and to condemn the righteous is an abomination to the LORD; even both these things.

16 Why this price in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since there is no understanding?

17 In every time a friend loves, and a brother is born for adversity.

18 A low sort claps the hand, rashly becoming a pledge before his neighbour.

19 Loving transgression loves strife; he who widens his gate seeks destruction.

20 One who is crooked in heart will not find good, and one who turns on his tongue will fall into evil.

21 A fool brings sorrow to his father, and the father of folly will not rejoice.

22 A merry heart makes medicine good, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.

23 A bribe from the bosom of the wicked will be taken to pervert the paths of justice.

24 The face of the wise is in wisdom, but the eyes of the fool are at the edge of the earth.

25 The anger of a father is a foolish son, and bitterness to her who bore him.

26 Also, punishing the righteous is not good, striking nobles for equity.

27 He who restrains his words has knowledge, and a man of understanding is cool of spirit.

28 Even a fool, if he keeps silent, is considered wise; he who stops his lips is discerning.


## Chapter 18

1 He who lusts greedily seeks what is apart; in every enterprise he will be overthrown.

2 A fool does not delight in discretion, but only in revealing his own mind.

3 When the wicked comes, then comes contempt, and with dishonour comes reproach.

4 Deep water is the saying of a man’s mouth; a flowing brook is the fountain of wisdom.

5 To show partiality to the wicked is not good, perverting the righteous in judgment.

6 The lips of a fool enter into strife, and his mouth calls for stripes.

7 The mouth of a fool is his ruin, and the lips of his speech are a snare to his soul.

8 Words of a scorner are like wounds, and they go down to the innermost parts of the belly.

9 Even failing in his business, he is a brother to the man of destruction.

10 A tower of strength is the name of the LORD; the righteous runs into it and is set on high.

11 The riches of a man are the city of his strength, and like a high wall in his conceit.

12 Before destruction comes pride, and before honor comes humility.

13 Answering a matter before hearing it—this is folly and shame to him.

14 A man's spirit sustains his sickness, but a crushed spirit who can bear?

15 A discerning heart acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.

16 A gift from a person will make room for him and bring him before great ones.

17 The first to plead his cause is righteous; his companion examines him.

18 By lot it shall end the brawling, and between mighty ones it shall make separation.

19 Another brother breaks away from a city of strength, like a bar that flees from a palace.

20 From the fruit of a man's mouth his belly is satisfied; with the yield of his lips he is satisfied.

21 Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love it will eat its reward.

22 Whoever finds a wife finds good and obtains favor from the LORD.

23 The poor speaks with entreaties, but the rich answers with harshness.

24 A certain companion for friendship, and there is a lover more closely bound than a brother.


## Chapter 19

1 Better is a poor man walking in his integrity than one of crooked lips and he being a fool.

2 Also, without knowledge, a person fares badly, and one who rushes on foot sins.

3 The folly of a person perverts their way, and their heart rages against the LORD.

4 Wealth increases many companions, but the poor man is separated from his fellow.

5 A witness of deceit shall not be acquitted, and one who breathes out lies shall not escape.

6 Many seek the favor of a noble person, and everyone is a friend to a man of gifts.

7 All his brothers hate him; indeed, even his companions distance themselves from him; they pursue words against him.

8 Whoever loves care for the heart values discretion, to find good.

9 A witness of deceit shall not go unpunished, and he who breathes out lies shall perish.

10 Delicate delight is not fitting for a fool; much less for a servant to rule over princes.

11 The discretion of a man is long-suffering, and his glory is to pass over a transgression.

12 Roaring like a young lion in rage, a king, and his favor like dew upon grass.

13 A foolish son is a calamity to his father, and the contentions of a wife are a continual dripping.

14 A house and wealth are an inheritance from fathers, but from the LORD comes a woman of understanding.

15 Slothfulness casts into deep sleep, and a slack soul shall be hungry.

16 Whoever keeps the commandment keeps their life; he who despises his ways will die.

17 He that giveth to the poor lendeth to the LORD; and that which he hath given will He repay him.

18 Discipline your child, for there is hope, and do not set your soul on his death.

19 Hot displeasure bears punishment, unless you rescue, and still you will add more.

20 Hear advice and accept instruction, so that you may become wise in your latter end.

21 Many are the thoughts in a man's heart, but the counsel of the LORD will stand.

22 The desire of a man is his kindness, and a poor man is better than a lying man.

23 The fear of the LORD is to live, and he who has it will be satisfied and not visited with evil.

24 The lazy man buries his hand in the dish; he is not able to bring it back to his mouth.

25 Strike a scoffer, and the simple will become shrewd; reprove the discerning, and he will gain knowledge.

26 He who does violence to his father and drives away his mother is a son who brings shame and disgrace.

27 My son, cease to hear instruction, to stray from the words of knowledge.

28 A witness of Belial scoffs at justice, and the mouth of the wicked devours iniquity.

29 Judgments are prepared for scoffers, and stripes for the back of fools.


## Chapter 20

1 Wine is a banqueting drink and strong drink is a brawler; whosoever is led astray thereby is not wise.

2 Roaring like a young lion, the terror of a king, he who provokes him endangers his own soul.

3 It is glorious for a man to cease from strife, but every fool will quarrel.

4 From winter the sluggard does not plow; at harvest time, and nothing.

5 Deep water is counsel in the heart of a man, and a man of understanding will draw it forth.

6 An abundance of people will call a man his lovingkindness, but a faithful man, who will find?

7 Walking in his integrity, the righteous is blessed; happy are his children after him.

8 A king sitting on a throne of judgement, scattering with his eyes all evil.

9 Who can say, I have made my heart pure; I have purified myself from my sin?

10 A stone and a stone, an ephah and an ephah, both are an abomination to the LORD.

11 Even in his doings he disguises himself as a child, whether his work is pure or upright.

12 The ear that hears and the eye that sees—the LORD has made both of them.

13 Do not love sleep, lest you become poor; open your eyes and be satisfied with food.

14 Evil, evil will the buyer say, and when he departs from him, then he will boast.

15 There is gold and abundance of pearls, but a vessel of precious worth is the lips of knowledge.

16 Take his garment, for he has put up a stranger as security, and hold it in pledge for strangers.

17 Bread of deceit is sweet to a man, but afterward his mouth is filled with gravel.

18 Cunning purposes are established by counsel, and by wise counsels wage war.

19 He who reveals the secret goes about as a talebearer; do not associate with one who flatters with his lips.

20 Whoever curses his father or mother, his lamp will be extinguished in darkness.

21 An inheritance at first, but its end will not be blessed.

22 Do not say, "I will repay evil"; wait for the LORD, and he will save you.

23 An abomination to the LORD are a false stone and a false weight, and dishonest balances are not good.

24 From the LORD are the steps of a man; and how can a person understand his way?

25 A man ensnared by a consecrated thing is snared, and after vows he must inquire (and fulfill them).

26 A wise king scatters the wicked and sets a wheel over them.

27 The lamp of the LORD is the spirit of man, searching all the innermost parts of the body.

28 Favour and faithfulness preserve a king, and by favour he upholds his throne.

29 The beauty of young men is their strength, and the glory of old men is gray hair.

30 Blueness of wound and strokes of the innermost part of the body will flee from evil.


## Chapter 21

1 Streams of water are a king's heart in the hand of the LORD; wherever he pleases, he turns it.

2 Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but the LORD establishes the heart.

3 To do justice and judgment is chosen by the LORD above sacrifice.

4 Haughtiness of eyes and a broad heart, the lamp of the wicked, is sin.

5 The thoughts of the diligent surely tend to profit, but everyone who is hasty surely tends to lack.

6 The act of treasures by a lying tongue is vanity pursued by those seeking death.

7 The desolation of the wicked will destroy them, because they refuse to do justice.

8 Is a man's path crooked and strange, yet his work is pure and upright?

9 Better to dwell on a corner of the roof than with a contentious woman in a house of company.

10 The appetite of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor is not shown favor in his eyes.

11 By the punishment of a scoffer, a simple one becomes wise, and by instructing a wise one, he receives knowledge.

12 A wise man regards the house of the wicked, but he who turns the wicked to evil [is the wicked man's downfall].

13 He who closes his ear to the cry of the poor will himself cry and not be heard.

14 A gift in secret averts anger, and a bribe in the bosom brings fierce wrath.

15 Gladness for the righteous to do justice, and terror for those who work iniquity.

16 A common sort departs from the way of insight; in the assembly of the dead he rests.

17 A man of lack loves joy; he who loves wine and oil will not grow rich.

18 A wicked man is a ransom for the righteous, and the treacherous for the upright.

19 Better is a home in the wilderness than a wife of strife.

20 A treasure of desirable oil is in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish man will swallow it up.

21 One who pursues justice and kindness shall find life, justice, and honour.

22 A wise man ascends a city of warriors, and brings down the strength of its confidence.

23 He who guards his mouth and his tongue guards his soul from adversity.

24 A scornful man, his name is infamous, he acts in the rage of pride.

25 The desire of the sluggard kills him, for his hands refuse to work.

26 All day long he lusts after lust, but the righteous gives and does not withhold.

27 The offering of the wicked is an abomination, even more so when brought with heinous intent.

28 A deceitful witness will perish, but a man who listens to alway will speak.

29 A certain man has grown bold in his presence, but the upright man will prepare his way.

30 There is no skilful wisdom and there is no understanding and there is no counsel against the LORD.

31 A horse is prepared for the day of battle, but deliverance belongs to the LORD.


## Chapter 22

1 A good name is chosen above great riches; above silver and gold, favour.

2 Rich and poor meet together; the LORD has made them all.

3 The prudent sees adversity, but the simple pass on and are punished.

4 Because of humility, the fear of the LORD, [comes] riches and honour and life.

5 Thorns and snares are in the crooked way; he who guards his soul will keep far from them.

6 Train the child according to his way; even when he is old he will not turn aside from it.

7 The rich rules over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.

8 Sowing iniquity, he reaps affliction; the rod of his wrath will come to an end.

9 A generous eye will be blessed, for he gives of his food to the poor.

10 Dismiss a scoffer, and contention departs; then strife and reproach cease.

11 He who loves purity of heart, for the grace of his lips the king will be his friend.

12 The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, and He overthrows the words of the faithless.

13 The sluggard says: “A lion is in the open; I shall be pierced in the broad places of the streets.”

14 A deep pit is the mouth of strangers; the wrath of the LORD will fall there.

15 Folly is bound up in the heart of a child; the rod of discipline will remove it from him.

16 One who oppresses the poor to increase for himself gives to the rich only for lack.

17 Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise, and set your heart to my knowledge.

18 For pleasant it is when you keep them within your body; together they will be established upon your lips.

19 To have your trust in the LORD—this I have made known to you today, even you.

20 Did I not write to you with counsel and knowledge?

21 To make known to you the truth of reliable sayings, to return true sayings to those who sent you.

22 Do not rob the poor, for he is poor, and do not oppress the needy at the city gate.

23 For the LORD will contend with their adversary and destroy the soul of those who destroy them.

24 Do not associate with an angry man, and do not enter the house of a furious man.

25 Lest you become accustomed and take a snare to your soul.

26 Do not be among those who strike hands, among those who become guarantors for debts.

27 If you have nothing to pay, why should he take your bed from under you?

28 Do not move an ancient border that your fathers made.

29 If you see a man diligent in his work, he will stand before kings; he will not stand before obscure men.


## Chapter 23

1 When you sit to eat with a ruler, consider carefully what is before you.

2 Put a knife to your throat, if you are a person given to appetite.

3 Do not desire his dainty meats, for it is the bread of deceit.

4 Do not touch to make rich from your wisdom; cease.

5 Will you set your eyes on that which is not? For riches surely make themselves wings; they fly away like an eagle toward heaven.

6 Do not eat the bread of one who has an evil eye, nor desire his dainty foods.

7 For as he is in his appetite, so is his eating and drinking; he says to you, but his heart is not with you.

8 The morsel you have eaten you shall vomit up, and you will waste your pleasant words.

9 Do not speak to a fool, for he will scorn your words of wisdom.

10 Do not move an ancient landmark, and do not enter the fields of the fatherless.

11 For their Redeemer is strong; he will contend with you on their behalf.

12 Apply discipline to your heart, and your ear to words of knowledge.

13 Do not withhold discipline from a child; though you strike him with the rod, he will not die.

14 You will correct him with a rod, and his soul you will deliver from the grave.

15 My son, if your heart is wise, then my heart will rejoice even me.

16 And my kidneys will rejoice when your lips speak what is upright.

17 Do not let your heart envy sinners, but only fear the LORD all the day.

18 For if there is a latter end, your hope will not be cut off.

19 Hear now, you son, and be wise, and direct your mind in the way.

20 Do not be among those who banquet on wine, the gluttons of flesh.

21 For the glutton and drunkard will come to poverty, and rags shall clothe drowsiness.

22 Hear your father, who begot you, and do not despise your mother when she is old.

23 Buy truth and do not sell it; buy wisdom and discipline and understanding.

24 The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice, and he who begets a wise son will be glad in him.

25 Let your father and your mother rejoice, and let she who bore you be glad.

26 Give me your heart, my son, and let your eyes be on my ways.

27 For this pit is a deep harlot, and that well is a foreign woman.

28 Even she lies in wait like prey, and traitors increase against a man.

29 To whom the wail, to whom the groan, to whom the babbling, to whom the wounds for nothing, to whom the redness of the eyes?

30 For those who linger over wine, for those who go in quest of mixed wine.

31 Do not gaze at wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly.

32 Its latter end is like a serpent’s bite, and like an adder it will spread.

33 Your eyes will see strange women, and your heart will speak of perverse things.

34 You will be like one sleeping in the midst of the sea, and like one lying on the top of a mast.

35 They struck me, yet I was not ill; they chastised me, yet I did not know it; when shall I awake? I will seek him still.


## Chapter 24

1 Do not envy wicked men, nor desire to be with them.

2 For desolation they devise in their heart, and mischief their lips do utter.

3 With wisdom a house is built, and with understanding it is established.

4 And by knowledge inner chambers will be filled with all kinds of precious wealth and pleasant things.

5 A mighty man in strength, and a man of knowledge strong in power.

6 For by good advice you shall wage war, and deliverance comes with abundance of counselors.

7 The heights of wisdom are for a fool; at the gate he does not open his mouth.

8 Thinking to do evil, a master of wicked devices will be called.

9 A heinous crime is the folly of sin, and an abomination to a man is the scorner.

10 You have weakened in the day of adversity; your strength is limited.

11 Deliver those taken to death and those inclined to slaughter, if you can restrain.

12 For if you say, "Behold, we did not know this," does He not weigh hearts? He who guards your soul, He knows, and He will repay a person according to his deeds.

13 Eat, my son, honey, for it is good, and honeycomb sweet to your taste.

14 Know wisdom for your soul: if you have found it, there is a latter end, and your hope will not be cut off.

15 Do not let the wicked lie in wait at the dwelling of the righteous; do not destroy his resting place.

16 For seven times the righteous fall and rise again, but the wicked stumble in adversity.

17 When your enemy falls, do not rejoice; and when he stumbles, let not your heart be glad.

18 Lest Jehovah see and it be affliction in his eyes, and he turn his wrath from him.

19 Do not fret because of evildoers; do not envy the wicked.

20 For there will be no latter end for the wicked; the lamp of the wicked will be snuffed out.

21 Fear the LORD, my son, and king; do not associate with others.

22 For suddenly their calamity will rise, and who knows the destruction of them both?

23 These also are for the wise: to show partiality in judgment is not good.

24 Saying to the wicked, "You are righteous," the folk will despise him, peoples will abhor him.

25 And the reprovers will be shamed, and upon them will come a blessing of good.

26 Lips shall be kissed, returning upright words.

27 Prepare your work outside, and make it ready for yourself in the field; then afterward build your house.

28 Do not be a false witness against your neighbour, and twist with your lips.

29 Do not say, “As he has done to me, so will I do to him”; I will repay the man according to his deed.

30 I passed by the field of a slothful man and the vineyard of a man lacking sense.

31 And behold, it was all overgrown with thorns; nettles covered its face, and its stone wall was broken down.

32 And I myself took care to consider: I have seen the receiving of correction.

33 a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep

34 And your poverty comes walking, and your lack like an armed man.


## Chapter 25

1 These also are proverbs of Solomon which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied.

2 The glory of God is to conceal a matter, and the glory of kings is to search out a matter.

3 The heavens are height, and the earth is depth; the heart of kings is unsearchable.

4 Remove the dross from the silver, and the vessel shall go out to the refiner.

5 The wicked speaks before the king, and his throne is established in righteousness.

6 Do not exalt yourself before the king, and do not stand in the place of great men.

7 It is better that it be said to you, “Come up here,” than that you be humbled before a noble whom your eyes have seen.

8 Do not quickly go out to a dispute, lest what you do in its latter end, when your fellow puts you to shame.

9 Contend your cause with your neighbor, but the secret of another do not reveal.

10 Lest the one who hears it disgrace you, and your evil report not turn away.

11 Apples of gold in silver settings is a word fitly spoken.

12 A gold earring and a fine gold ornament is a wise reprover to a listening ear.

13 Like a cold snow in harvest time, a faithful messenger is to those who send him, for he refreshes the soul of his master.

14 Princes and wind and rain are lacking; a man boasts in a gift of falsehood.

15 With forbearing anger a ruler is persuaded, but a soft tongue breaks a bone.

16 If you find honey-comb, eat (only) as much as you are able, lest you become full and vomit it.

17 Do not wear out your neighbor’s house, lest he become weary of you and hate you.

18 A maul and a sword and a sharp arrow is a man who afflicts his neighbor, a witness of falsehood.

19 A bad tooth and a foot that cannot stand, trust that fails in a day of trouble.

20 Cloth is vinegar on nitre in a cold day, and a singer on an evil heart.

21 If your enemy is hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he is thirsty, give him water to drink.

22 For (burning) coal you heap on his head, and the LORD will repay you.

23 A wind from the north brings rain, and a countenance of anger is a tongue of secret slander.

24 Better is a corner of a roof than a wife and a house of contention.

25 Cold water to a weary soul, and good news from a distant land.

26 A spring that is stopped and a fountain that is corrupted: a righteous person falls before the wicked.

27 Eating too much honey is not good, and seeking one's own glory is not glory.

28 A city broken down, without a wall; a man without rule over his spirit.


## Chapter 26

1 Like snow in summer or rain during harvest, so honour is not fitting for a fool.

2 Like a bird wandering, like a swallow flying, so a curse undeserved does not come.

3 A scourge for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the back of fools.

4 Do not answer a fool according to his folly, lest you become like him also.

5 Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.

6 He who sends a message by the hand of a fool cuts off his own feet and drinks violence.

7 Like the legs of the lame that hang limp is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

8 Like a carbuncle stone in a sling, so is giving honour to a fool.

9 A bramble has come to rule by the hand of the drunkard, and a byword is spoken in the mouth of fools.

10 Like an archer who wounds everyone is he who hires a fool or hires passersby.

11 Like a dog that returns to its vomit, a fool repeats his folly.

12 You have seen a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

13 The sluggard says, "A (fierce) lion is along the highway; a (young) lion is in the broad places."

14 The door turns on its hinge, but the lazy man does not turn on his bed.

15 The lazy man buries his hand in the dish; he grows weary from bringing it back to his mouth.

16 The wise man thinks himself wiser than seven who give judgment.

17 Holding by the ears of a dog, passing by one who is angered over a controversy not his own.

18 Like a man scattering firebrands, arrows, and death.

19 Even a certain man mocks his neighbor, and says, "Am I not only joking?"

20 Without wood the fire will go out, and without a whisperer the strife will cease.

21 Coals for burning coals, and wood for fire, and a man for a contentious strife.

22 Words of a backbiter are as wounds, and they go down to the innermost parts of the belly.

23 Like silver dross covering earthenware are burning lips and a wicked heart.

24 The hater will be known, and within him he will place deceit.

25 Do not believe his voice when he cries aloud, for seven abominations are in his heart.

26 Hate shall be covered by deceit; his adversity shall be revealed in the assembly.

27 He who digs a pit will fall into it, and he who rolls a stone upon it will have it roll back upon him.

28 The babbler of deceit he hates, and a smooth mouth brings ruin.


## Chapter 27

1 Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what the day will bring forth.

2 A foreigner will praise you, not your mouth; an outsider, not your lips.

3 The grievousness of a stone and the weight of the sand, but heavier than both is the anger of a fool.

4 Cruel anger and overflowing wrath— who can stand before jealousy?

5 Better is open rebuke than hidden love.

6 Faithful are the wounds of a lover, but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.

7 A satisfied soul spurns honeycomb, but to a hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.

8 Like a bird wandering from its nest, so is a man wandering from his place.

9 Oil and incense gladden the heart, and the sweetness of a man's friend comes from hearty counsel.

10 Do not forsake your father's companion, nor go to your brother's house in your day of calamity; better is a neighbour nearby than a distant brother.

11 My son, be wise and bring joy to my heart, and I will answer the one who reproves me with a word.

12 The prudent see adversity and hide; the simple pass on and suffer.

13 Take his garment, for he has put up a foreigner as surety, and hold the pledge of the stranger.

14 He who blesses his fellow with a loud voice, early in the morning, it shall be counted as a curse to him.

15 A contentious woman is like a constant dropping on a very rainy day.

16 Her treasures He has hidden by His breath, and oil from His right hand He will call forth.

17 Iron with iron joins, and a man his fellow's face.

18 He who guards a fig tree will eat its fruit; he who guards his master will be honored.

19 As water reflects a face, so one person's heart reflects another person.

20 The grave does not get content, and the eyes of man do not get content.

21 A fining pot for silver and a furnace for gold, and a man according to his praise.

22 If you strike the fool in the mortar among the ground corn with the pestle, his folly will not depart from him.

23 You will surely know the face of your flock; set your heart on your herds.

24 For riches are not forever, nor does a crown endure to every generation.

25 The grass is revealed and the tender grass appears, and the herbs of the hills are gathered.

26 As lambs for your clothing, and goats for the price of a field.

27 and cheese of goats sufficient for your food, food for your household, and life for your young women.


## Chapter 28

1 The wicked flee though no one pursues, but the righteous are bold as a young lion.

2 For the rebellion of a land, its rulers are many; but by a person of understanding and knowledge, its stability will be prolonged.

3 A poor man and one who oppresses the needy is like a sweeping rain and there is no bread.

4 Those who forsake the law will praise the wicked, but those who keep the law will contend with them.

5 Wicked men do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD understand everything.

6 Better is a poor person walking in integrity than a rich one with crooked ways.

7 He who keeps the law is a discerning son, but he who keeps company with gluttons shames his father.

8 Whoever increases his wealth by usury for unjust gain will gather it for one who is kind to the poor.

9 He who turns his ear from hearing the law—his prayer also is an abomination.

10 One who leads the upright astray along an evil path will fall into his own pit, but the blameless will inherit good.

11 A rich man is esteemed wise in his own eyes, but one who is poor yet discerning closely examines him.

12 When the righteous rejoice, great is the glory; but when the wicked rise, a man is hard to find.

13 Whoever conceals his transgressions will not prosper, but one who confesses and forsakes them will find mercy.

14 Blessed is the person who fears alway; but he who hardens his heart shall fall into adversity.

15 A young lion roars and a bear growls; a wicked ruler dominates over a poor folk.

16 A ruler who lacks understanding is also a great oppressor, but he who hates dishonest gain will prolong his days.

17 A person oppressed in bloodguiltiness will flee to a pit—do not support him.

18 The one walking blamelessly will be saved, but the one crooked in his ways will fall into trouble.

19 He who works his land will be satisfied with food, but he who chases after emptiness will be satisfied with poverty.

20 A faithful person is greatly blessed, but one who rushes to get rich will not go unpunished.

21 To show partiality is not good; for because of a morsel of food a man will transgress.

22 A man of evil eye hastens after wealth, and does not know that poverty will come upon him.

23 He who reproves a man afterward will find more favor than he who flatters with the tongue.

24 He who robs his father and his mother, saying, "There is no transgression," is a companion to a man who destroys.

25 A wide appetite stirs up strife, but whoever trusts in the LORD will be satisfied.

26 He who trusts in his own heart is a fool, but he who walks in wisdom will be delivered.

27 He who gives to the poor shall not lack, but he who hides his eyes shall have many curses.

28 When the wicked rise up, a man is hidden; but when they perish, the righteous increase.


## Chapter 29

1 Any man of rebukes who hardens the back will suddenly be broken, and there is no remedy.

2 When the righteous increase, the people rejoice; but when the wicked rule, the folk groan.

3 A certain man who loves wisdom makes his father glad, but whoever associates with prostitutes wastes wealth.

4 A king by justice upholds the land, but one who exacts gifts will destroy it.

5 Every one who slides away from his fellow is a net spread upon his own steps.

6 By the transgression of an evil man comes a snare, but the righteous shall rejoice and be glad.

7 The righteous knows the cause of the poor; the wicked does not understand knowledge.

8 Men of scorn will kindle a city, but the wise will turn away anger.

9 A wise man judges a foolish man, but he who is angry and mocks has no quietness.

10 Men of blood hate the blameless, and the upright seek his soul.

11 All his spirit a fool gives out, but a wise one in the after restrains it.

12 A ruler who heeds a lying word—all his attendants are wicked.

13 A poor man and a deceitful man have met; the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.

14 A king who judges the poor in truth—his throne shall be established forever.

15 A rod and reproof give wisdom, but a boy left to himself brings shame to his mother.

16 When transgressions increase, rebellion grows, but the righteous will see [the wicked's] ruin.

17 Discipline your child and he will give you rest, and he will grant delights to your soul.

18 Where there is no vision the people are let loose, but happy is he who keeps the law.

19 By words shall not a servant be corrected, though he understand and there is no answer.

20 Have you seen a man rash in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

21 He who pampers his servant from childhood will have him become a dependent in the end.

22 A certain man stirs up strife, and a man of anger incurs many transgressions.

23 The excellency of man brings him low, but he who is of a humble spirit shall uphold honour.

24 One who shares with a thief hates his own soul; he hears a curse and does not report it.

25 Fear of man brings a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD will be exalted.

26 Many seek the favor of a ruler, but from the LORD comes a man's judgment.

27 The abomination of the upright is a wicked man, and the abomination of the wicked is the way of uprightness.


## Chapter 30

1 The acts of Agur son of Jakeh, the burden: the saying of the man to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal.

2 For I am brutish compared to a man, and I do not have human understanding.

3 And I have not learned wisdom, nor do I know the knowledge of the holy.

4 Who has ascended to the heavens and come down? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has bound the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name, and what is the name of his son, if you know?

5 Every word of God is pure; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.

6 Do not add to his advice, lest he rebuke you and you be found a liar.

7 Two things I have asked of you; do not deny me before I die.

8 Keep falsehood and lying words far from me; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with my daily bread.

9 Lest I be full and deny and say, Who is Jehovah? or lest I be poor and steal and take the name of my God.

10 Do not slander a servant, lest he curse you and you bear guilt.

11 A generation will curse its forefather, and will not bless its mother.

12 A generation pure in their own eyes, yet not washed from their filthiness.

13 A generation—how lofty are his eyes, and his eye-lids lifted up?

14 A generation whose teeth are swords and whose jaw teeth are knives, to devour the poor from the land and the needy from among mankind.

15 Two daughters of the horse-leech: give, give! Three have not had enough; four have not said, "Enough!"

16 Sheol, the barren womb, the earth that is not satisfied with water, and fire that never says, Enough.

17 An eye that mocks a father and scorns a mother’s obedience will be pecked out by the ravens of the stream and eaten by the young of the vulture.

18 Three things are too wonderful for me; I do not know them:

19 The way of the eagle in the air, the way of the serpent upon a rock, the way of a ship in the midst of the sea, and the way of a man with a virgin.

20 So is the way of an adulteress woman: she eats and wipes her mouth, and says, “I have not done iniquity.”

21 Under three things the land trembles, and under four it cannot bear up.

22 When a bondman rules, and a fool is filled with bread.

23 For an unloved woman when she is married, and a maidservant when she inherits her mistress.

24 Four they are, little ones of the land, yet they are exceedingly wise.

25 The ants are a folk not strong, yet they prepare their food in summer.

26 The coney is a folk not strong, and they set in the ragged rock their home.

27 There is no king for the grasshopper, yet it goes out altogether.

28 A spider is caught in the hands, and it is in the palaces of a king.

29 Three excel at step, and four excel at walk.

30 The old lion is mighty among the beasts, and it does not turn back from any.

31 A greyhound in the loins, or a he goat, and a royal king with him.

32 If you have acted foolishly by exalting yourself, or if you have devised plans, put your hand on your mouth.

33 For pressing milk produces butter, pressing the nose produces blood, and pressing anger produces strife.


## Chapter 31

1 The sayings of King Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him:

2 What son, and what son of my body, and what son of my vows?

3 Do not give your strength to women, nor your ways to those who destroy kings.

4 Do not drink wine, O kings, Lemuel, do not drink wine, O kings, nor strong drink, O rulers.

5 Lest he drink and forget the decree, and pervert the judgment of all the afflicted.

6 Give strong drink to the one perishing, and wine to the bitter of soul.

7 He will drink and forget his poverty, and his misery will not be remembered anymore.

8 Open your mouth for the mute, for the cause of all who are crushed.

9 Open your mouth for justice and judge the cause of the poor and needy.

10 A wife of valor—who can find her? Her worth is far beyond that of rubies.

11 Her husband's heart trusts in her, and he will not lack spoil.

12 She treated him well and not evil all the days of her life.

13 She seeks wool and flax, and works with the willing purpose of her hands.

14 She was like ships of merchants; from afar she brings her food.

15 She arose while yet night and gave meat to her (winter) household and a set task to her maids.

16 She considers a field and buys it; from the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.

17 She has girded her loins with strength and has strengthened her arms.

18 She has tasted that her merchandise is good; her lamp does not go out at night.

19 Her hands reached for the distaff and her palms supported the spindle.

20 She stretched out her hand to the poor, and she sent her palms to the needy.

21 She is not afraid for her household because of snow, for all her household is clothed in scarlet.

22 She made for her covering of tapestry; her apparel is linen and purple.

23 Known in her gates is her lord, when he sits with the elders of the land.

24 She made fine linen and sold it, and gave a girdle to the merchant.

25 Strength and beauty are her clothing, and she laughs at the day to come.

26 Her mouth opens with wisdom, and the teaching of kindness is on her tongue.

27 Watch the way of her home, and bread of idleness you shall not eat.

28 Her sons rose up and called her blessed; her husband praised her.

29 Many daughters have achieved might, but you have surpassed them all.

30 False is favour and vain is beauty: a woman who fears the LORD—she shall be praised.

31 Give her of the fruit of her hands, and let her works praise her in the gates.


# ECCLESIASTES

## Chapter 1

1 The words of the Preacher, the son of David, king in Jerusalem.

2 Utterly, utterly, says the Preacher, utterly, all is utterly vain.

3 What profit has a person in all his toil with which he toils under the sun?

4 A generation goes and a generation comes, but the earth stands forever.

5 And the sun rose and the sun set, and toward its place it pants, the one rising there.

6 Going toward the south and turning toward the north, the wind turns about, turning, turning; it goes on its circuits, and to its surrounding places the wind returns.

7 All the streams flow into the sea, yet the sea is not full; to the place where the streams flow, there they return to flow again.

8 All the words are wearisome; no man can speak; the eye is not satisfied with seeing, nor is the ear filled with hearing.

9 That which has been is what will be, and that which has been done is what will be done, and there is no new thing under the sun.

10 There is an act that says, "See, this is new"—it was already of old times, which was before us.

11 There is no memorial of the former; neither shall there be a memorial of the latter that are to come, with them being at the latter end.

12 I, the preacher, was king over Israel in Jerusalem.

13 I set my mind to seek and explore wisdom concerning all that is done under the heavens; it is a grievous task that God has given to humanity to toil in.

14 I have seen all the deeds that are done under the sun, and behold, all is vanity and vexation of spirit.

15 Crookedness cannot be made straight, and wanting cannot be numbered.

16 I spoke with my mind, saying, I indeed have greatly increased and added wisdom beyond all that was before me in Jerusalem, and my mind has seen much wisdom and knowledge.

17 I gave my mind to know wisdom and to know madness and folly; I learned that this also is a vexation of spirit.

18 For in abundance of wisdom there is much grief, and increasing knowledge increases sorrow.


## Chapter 2

1 I said in my heart, Come now, I will test you with pleasure; therefore enjoy good. But behold, this also is vanity.

2 I said of laughter, "It is derision," and of joy, "What does it accomplish?"

3 I considered in my mind to draw my body with wine, and my mind guiding with wisdom, and to hold folly, until I see what is good for the children of man, which they may do under the heavens, during the number of days of their life.

4 I enlarged my works; I built me houses, I planted me vineyards.

5 I made for myself gardens and orchards, and I planted in them every kind of fruit tree.

6 I made for myself water pools to irrigate from them a forest of trees.

7 I acquired bondmen and bondwomen and household servants; I also had great flocks of cattle and sheep, more than all who were before me in Jerusalem.

8 I gathered for myself also silver and gold, and the special treasure of kings and provinces; I made for myself male and female singers, and the delights of the sons of men, a woman and women.

9 And I grew and increased in all that had been before me in Jerusalem; even my wisdom remained with me.

10 And whatever my eyes asked for I did not withhold from them; I did not deny my heart any joy, for my heart rejoiced in all my toil, and this was my portion from all my toil.

11 I turned to all the deeds that my hands had done and to the labor I had labored to accomplish, and behold, all was vanity and a vexation of spirit, and there was no profit under the sun.

12 And I myself turned to see wisdom, madness, and folly—what person can come after the king to do what has already been done?

13 And I saw for myself that there is a profit in wisdom over folly, as the profit of light over darkness.

14 The wise person’s eyes are in their head, but the fool walks in darkness; and I myself also knew that one event befalls all of them.

15 And I said in my heart, Even as the event of the fool, so will it befall me; and why then have I been the more wise? And I spoke in my heart that this also is vanity.

16 For there is no memorial of the wise with the fool forever; already in the days to come all will be forgotten, and how the wise dies with the fool.

17 And I hated life, because the labor that is done under the sun is grievous to me, for all is vanity and vexation of spirit.

18 And I myself will hate all my toil with which I toil under the sun, which I must leave to another who will come after me.

19 And who knows whether the wise one will be wise or a fool, and will rule in all my toil which I toiled and in which I dealt wisely under the sun? Even this is vanity.

20 So I turned to cause my heart to despair concerning all the toil that I had toiled under the sun.

21 For there is a person whose toil is with wisdom and with knowledge and with equity, yet to a person who did not toil in it he gives its portion—also this is vanity and great affliction.

22 What does a person gain by all their toil and the vexation of their mind with which they toil under the sun?

23 For all his days are grief and sorrow is his business; even at night his heart does not rest; this also is vanity.

24 There is no good for a person to eat and to drink and to show his soul good in his toil; this also I have seen, that it is from the hand of God.

25 For who can eat and who can feel concern without me?

26 For the man whom He approves, God gives wisdom, knowledge, and joy; but to the sinner He gives the task of gathering and heaping up in order to give to the one approved before God. This also is vanity and a vexation of spirit.


## Chapter 3

1 There is a time for every purpose under the heavens.

2 a time to give birth and a time to die, a time to plant and a time to uproot what is planted

3 a time to kill and a time to heal, a time to break down and a time to build up.

4 a time to weep and a time to laugh, a time to mourn and a time to dance

5 A time to cast away stones, and a time to gather stones together; a time to embrace, and a time to refrain from embracing.

6 A time to seek and a time to lose, a time to keep and a time to cast away.

7 A time to rend and a time to sew, a time to be silent and a time to speak.

8 A time to love and a time to hate; a time of war and a time of peace.

9 What profit has he who works in that wherein he labors?

10 I have seen the business that God has given to the children of man to be afflicted with.

11 He has made everything beautiful in its time; also, eternity has He set in their heart, so that man cannot find out the work which God has done from the beginning even unto the end.

12 I know that there is no good in them, but to rejoice and to do good in their lifetime.

13 And also every person who eats and drinks and sees good in all his toil—it is a gift from God.

14 I know that whatever God does endures forever; there is nothing to add to it, nor anything to take away from it, and God has done it so that they stand in awe before his presence.

15 What is has already been, and what is to be has already been; and God seeks what has been driven away.

16 And again I saw under the sun: a place of judgment—there was wickedness, and a place of righteousness—there was wickedness.

17 I said in my mind, The God will judge the righteous and the wicked, for a time for every purpose and for every deed there.

18 I said in my heart concerning the estate of the children of man, that God tests them, and that they may see that they themselves are like beasts.

19 For the event of the sons of man and the event of the beast is one event; as the death of this, so is the death of that, and one spirit is for all; and the preeminence of man over the beast is nothing, for all is vanity.

20 All is going to one place; all was from the dust, and all returns to the dust.

21 Who knows the spirit of the children of man, whether it goes upward, and the spirit of the beast, whether it goes downward to the earth?

22 And I saw that there is no good for a person more than to rejoice in his deeds, for that is his portion; for who can bring him to see what will be after him?


## Chapter 4

1 I returned and saw all the oppressions done under the sun: the tears of the oppressed, with no comforter; and from the hand of their oppressors, power, yet no comforter for them.

2 And I praise the dead who have already died more than the living who are still alive.

3 And better than both of them is the one who has not yet been, who has not seen the evil deed that is done under the sun.

4 And I saw all grievance and all skill of the work, that it is a man's envy of his companion; also this is vanity and vexation of spirit.

5 The fool folds his hands and eats his own flesh.

6 Better is a handful of rest than a double handful of toil and vexation of spirit.

7 And I returned, and I saw vanity under the sun.

8 There is one, and no second; neither child nor brother has he; and there is no end to all his toil; neither is riches ever satisfied; and for whom am I toiling and depriving my soul of good? Even this is vanity and a grievous business.

9 Good is the pair rather than the one, for they have a good reward for their labour.

10 For if one falls, the other will raise his companion, but woe to one who falls when there is no second to raise him.

11 Even if two lie together, then there is warmth; but how can one be warm?

12 But if one of them prevails against the other, the two will stand against him; and the tripled cord will not quickly break.

13 Better is a poor and wise boy than an old and foolish king who no longer knows how to take warning.

14 For he came forth from the prison to reign, and indeed during his reign he was born poor.

15 I have seen all who live, walking under the sun, with the second boy who will stand in his place.

16 There is no end to all the people, for all that has been before them; even the latter ones will not rejoice in it, for this also is vanity and a vexation of spirit.


## Chapter 5

1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God, and draw near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools, for they do not know how to avoid doing evil.

2 Do not be hasty with your mouth, and let not your heart be quick to bring forth a word before God, for God is in heaven and you are on the earth; therefore let your words be few.

3 For the dream comes with much business, and the voice of a fool with great talk.

4 When you make a vow to God, do not delay to fulfill it, for there is no delight in fools; fulfill what you vow.

5 It is better not to vow than to vow and not pay.

6 Do not let your speech cause your body to sin, and do not claim before the messenger that it was an error; why should God be angered at your voice and destroy the work of your hands?
