7 For in the abundance of dreams there are vanities and many words, because he fears God.

8 If you see oppression of the poor and violent perverting of justice and righteousness in the province, do not marvel at the matter, for one higher than the high watches, and there are those higher than they.

9 And the profit of the land is in everything a king served by the field.

10 The lover of money will not be satisfied with money, and the lover of abundance will not gain revenue; this also is vanity.

11 When goods increase, those who eat them multiply, and what advantage is there for its owner except the sight of his eyes?

12 Sweet is the sleep of the laborer, whether he eats little or abundance; but abundance does not let the rich man sleep.

13 There is a grievous calamity that I have seen under the sun: wealth kept by its owner to his own harm.

14 And the riches perish in a grievous business, and he fathers a son, and there is nothing in his hand.

15 As he came from his mother's womb, naked shall he return as he came, and nothing shall he take by his toil that he can carry in his hand.

16 And this also is a grievous affliction, that all who come are in the same way going; and what profit has he who labors for the wind?

17 Even all his days he eats in darkness, with much anger and sickness and wrath.

18 Behold, what I have seen: it is good and fitting to eat and to drink and to see enjoyment in all one's toil which he toils under the sun during the number of days that God has given him, for that is his portion.

19 Also every person whom God has given riches and wealth and empowered to eat from it and to carry his portion and to rejoice in his toil—this is a gift of God.

20 For he will not greatly remember the days of his life, because God answers in the joy of his heart.


## Chapter 6

1 There is an affliction I have seen under the sun, and it is heavy upon the man.

2 A certain man to whom God gives riches and wealth and honour so that he lacks nothing for his soul of all that he desires, yet God does not enable him to eat from it, for a foreign man eats it—this is vanity and a grievous disease.

3 If a man beget a hundred children and live many years, so that the days of his years are many, yet his soul is not satisfied with good, and he also has no burial, I say that the untimely birth is better than he.

4 For in vanity he comes and in darkness he goes, and in darkness his name is covered.

5 Even the sun has not seen nor known, a quiet resting from this to that.

6 Even if he lived a thousand years twice over, and did not see good—surely all are going to one place.

7 All the man's toil is for the sake of his mouth, and yet the soul is not satisfied.

8 For what is the advantage of the wise over the fool, what to the poor one who knows how to walk before the living?

9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the soul: this also is vanity and vexation of spirit.

10 Whatever has been, its name was already called, and it is known that he is man; he cannot contend with one who is stronger than he.

11 For there are many acts increasing vanity; what more is there for a person?

12 For who knows what is good for a person in life, all the days of the life of vanity—which he spends like a shadow? And who can tell a person what will be after him under the sun?


## Chapter 7

1 A good name is better than good oil, and the day of death than the day of birth.

2 Better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feast, because it is the end of every person, and the living should take it to heart.

3 Better is anger than laughter, for by the sadness of the countenance the heart is made good.

4 The care of the wise is in the house of mourning, and the care of fools is in the house of joy.

5 Better to hear the rebuking of a wise person than a man listening to the song of fools.

6 Like the crackling of thorns under a pot, so is the laughter of a fool, and even this is vanity.

7 For oppression makes a wise person rage, and destroys the care of a gift.

8 Better is the end of a matter than its beginning; better is a patient spirit than a haughty spirit.

9 Do not let your spirit rush to anger, for anger rests in the bosom of fools.

10 Do not say, "Why were the former days better than these?" for you have not asked about this from wisdom.

11 Better is wisdom with an inheritance, and more for those who see the sun.

12 For in the shadow of wisdom is the shadow of silver, and the advantage of knowledge is that wisdom preserves its possessor.

13 See the act of God, for who can make right what he has made crooked?

14 In prosperity be joyful, but in adversity consider: God has made the one as well as the other, so that no person can find anything after him.

15 I have seen all things in the days of vanity: there is a righteous one perishing in his righteousness, and there is a wicked one prolonging his life in his wickedness.

16 Do not be overly righteous, and do not make yourself overly wise; why should you destroy yourself?

17 Do not be overly wicked, nor act the fool; why die before your time?

18 It is good that you take hold of this, and also from this do not let go of your hand, for the fear of God brings forth all of them.

19 Wisdom gives the wise person strength, more than the ten rulers who were in the city.

20 For there is no righteous man on earth who does good and does not sin.

21 Do not give your heart to all the things they may say, so that you do not hear your servant cursing you.

22 For also many times your heart has known that also others you have cursed.

23 All this I tested with wisdom: I said, I will be wise, but it was far from me.

24 Far off is what has been, and deep, deep—who can find it?

25 I turned, even my heart, to know, to search out, and to seek wisdom and reason, and to know the wickedness of folly and the foolishness of madness.

26 And I find that woman is more bitter than death: she is a snare, her heart is a net of curses; her hands are snares. The one who is good before God will escape from her, but the sinner will be caught by her.

27 See, this I found, says the preacher, one by one to find a reason.

28 Which yet my soul sought and did not find: one man among a thousand I found, but a woman in all these I did not find.

29 alone I have found this: that God made the man upright, but they have sought many inventions.


## Chapter 8

1 Who is like the wise, and who knows the interpretation of an act? The wisdom of a man illuminates his face, and the strength of his face changes.

2 I say, Keep the king's command, and that because of the oath of God.

3 Do not be hasty before his presence; do not stand in an evil matter, for whatsoever he desires he does.

4 Because the act of the king has power, and who can say to him, "What are you doing?"

5 One who keeps the commandment knows no evil thing, and the heart of the wise knows the due time and judgment.

6 For every purpose there is a time and a judgment, because the adversity of man is great upon him.

7 For he does not know what will be, for as it will be, who can tell him?

8 There is no person who has power over the spirit to restrain the spirit, and there is no power in the day of death, and there is no discharge in war, and wickedness will not deliver its master.

9 All this I have seen, and I gave my mind to every work that is done under the sun, in a time when one person rules over another to his harm.

10 Thus I saw the wicked buried, who came and went from the holy place, and they were forgotten in the city where they had done so; this also is vanity.

11 There is no decree carried out swiftly against the evil deed, therefore the hearts of the people are full of them to do evil.

12 Whoever sins and does evil yet prolongs his life—for I know that it will go well with those who fear God, who stand in awe before His presence.

13 But it will not be well with the wicked, and he will not prolong his days like a shadow, because he does not fear before God.

14 There is a vanity that is done upon the earth: there are righteous to whom it happens according to the deed of the wicked, and there are wicked to whom it happens according to the deed of the righteous; I said that this also is vanity.

15 I will praise the joy, for there is no good for the person under the sun except to eat and to drink and to rejoice, and it shall accompany him in his toil during the days of life which God has given to him under the sun.

16 As I have given my mind to know wisdom and to see the travail that is done on the earth—for not even by day or by night does he see sleep with his eyes—

17 I saw all the acts of God, that a person cannot find out the work that is done under the sun, because though a person labors to seek it, yet he will not find it; even though a wise person says he will know it, he will not be able to find it.


## Chapter 9

1 For all this I took to heart, and to explore all this: that the righteous and the wise and their works are in the hand of God; also love and hate—no person knows, all is before them.

2 All is as the lot of all, one event happens to the righteous and the wicked, to the good and the pure and the impure, to the one who sacrifices and the one who does not sacrifice; as the good, so the sinner; the one who swears an oath, as the one who fears an oath.

3 This is evil in all that is done under the sun: one event happens to all; also, the heart of the children of man is full of evil, and madness is in their hearts during their lives, and afterward they go to the dead.

4 For who among all the living has confidence? Indeed, a living dog is better than a dead lion.

5 For the living know that they will die, but the dead know no-thing, and they have no more reward, for their memory is forgotten.

6 Even their love, even their hate, even their envy is long gone, and they have no more part forever in anything that is done under the sun.

7 Go, eat your food with joy, and drink your wine with a glad heart, for already God has approved your deeds.

8 At all times let your garments be white, and do not lack oil upon your head.

9 See life with the wife whom you love, all the days of your vain life which are given to you under the sun, all the days of your vanity: for that is your portion in life and in your toil wherein you labor under the sun.

10 Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do with thy power, do it, for there is no work, nor device, nor knowledge, nor wisdom in the grave whither thou goest.

11 I turned and saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift, nor the battle to the strong, nor bread to the wise, nor riches to the intelligent, nor favour to those who know, for time and chance happen to them all.

12 For as people do not know their time, so fish are caught in an evil net, and birds are caught in a snare; so the children of people are snared in an evil time when it suddenly falls upon them.

13 Also this wisdom I have seen under the sun, and it is great to me.

14 A city, small, and few men in it; and there came against it a great king, and he besieged it and built against it great bulwarks.

15 And a poor wise man was found in it, and he delivered the city by his wisdom, but no person remembered that poor man.

16 Then I said, Wisdom is better than strength, but the poor man's wisdom is despised, and his words are not heard.

17 The words of the wise are heard with quietness rather than the cry of rulers among fools.

18 Better is wisdom than weapons of war, but one sinner destroys much good.


## Chapter 10

1 Dead flies make the perfumer’s oil stink and bubble; a little folly outweighs wisdom and glory.

2 A wise heart is at the right hand, but a foolish heart is at the left.

3 And also along the way his heart is lacking, and he says to everyone, "He is a fool."

4 If the ruler's anger rises against you, do not leave your place, for calmness will leave great offenses quiet.

5 There is an adversity I have seen under the sun: an error that proceeds from before the ruler.

6 Folly is given to lofty dignity, and the rich sit in a low place.

7 I saw bondmen riding on horses, and rulers walking like servants on the ground.

8 Whoever digs a pit will fall into it, and he who breaks through a fence will be bitten by a serpent.

9 He who moves stones will be wearied by them; he who splits wood will be endangered by it.

10 If the iron is blunt and he does not sharpen the edge, then he must use more strength; but wisdom has the advantage of success.

11 If the serpent bites without enchantment, then there is no profit for the master of the tongue.

12 The words of a wise man's mouth are gracious, but the lips of a fool consume him.

13 The beginning of his speech is folly, and the end of his mouth is madness.

14 But the fool multiplies words; the person does not know what will be, nor who will tell him what will happen after him.

15 The grievance of fools wearies them, who do not know how to go to a city.

16 How can the land be yours when your king is a boy and your princes devour in the morning?

17 Happy is the land whose king is of noble birth and whose princes eat at the proper time with restraint and not in drunkenness.

18 Through slothfulness the building sinks, and through idleness of hands the house leaks.

19 They make bread for laughter, and wine gladdens life, and money answers everything.

20 Even in your thought do not curse the king, nor in the inner chamber of your bed curse the rich, for a bird of the air carries the voice, and a winged creature tells the matter.


## Chapter 11

1 Send your bread upon the face of the waters, for in abundance of days you will find it.

2 Give a portion to seven and also to eight, for you do not know what calamity may come upon the land.

3 If the clouds are full of rain, they empty it upon the land; and if a tree falls to the south or to the north, in the place where the tree falls, there it will be.

4 He who guards the wind will not sow, and he who looks at the clouds will not reap.

5 Just as you do not know the way of the spirit in the bones within the womb, so you do not know the work of God, who makes all things.

6 In the morning sow your seed, and in the evening do not withhold your hand, for you do not know which will prosper, this or that, or whether both alike will be good.

7 And sweet is the light, and it is good for the eyes to see the sun.

8 But if a man lives many long years, let him rejoice in all of them; yet let him remember the days of darkness, for they will be many—indeed, all that comes is vanity.

9 Rejoice, O chosen young man, in your youth, and let your heart cheer you in the days of your young manhood; walk in the ways of your heart and in the sight of your eyes; but know that for all these things God will bring you into judgment.

10 Remove anger from your mind and turn away evil from your body, for childhood and youth are vanity.


## Chapter 12

1 Remember your Creator in the days of your youth, before the days of adversity come and the years arrive when you will say, "I have no delight in them."

2 until the sun and the light and the moon and the stars are darkened and the clouds return after the rain

3 In the day when the keepers of the house are shaken, and the men of power are bowed, and the grinders cease because they are few, and the women who look through the windows grow dim.

4 And the doors will be shut in the street when the sound of grinding is low, and one will rise to the voice of the bird, and all the daughters of song will be brought low.

5 Even from on high they will fear, and terrors will be along the way; the almond tree will reject, and the locust will be burdened, and desire will fail, because the person goes to his eternal home, and the mourners will go about in the street.

6 until the silver cord is snapped and the golden bowl is broken like a pitcher at the fountain, and the wheel is shattered at the cistern.

7 and the dust returns to the earth as it was, and the spirit returns to God who gave it.

8 Vanity of vanities, says the preacher; all is vanity.

9 Moreover, the preacher being wise still further taught the people knowledge, and weighed, investigated, and arranged many proverbs.

10 The preacher sought to find acceptable words, and wrote upright words of truth.

11 The advice of the wise is like goads, and like nails firmly fixed by the masters of assemblies; they are given from one shepherd.

12 Moreover, my son, be warned: making many books has no end, and much study brings weariness to the body.

13 The conclusion of the matter: all has been heard. Fear the LORD and keep his commandments, for this is the whole of humanity.

14 For God will bring every act to judgment, including every hidden thing, whether good or evil.


# SONG OF SOLOMON

## Chapter 1

1 The song of songs, which is Solomon's.

2 Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth, for your love is better than wine.

3 Your ointments have a pleasing scent, the fragrance of your ointments is delightful, your name is like poured out oil; therefore the maidens love you.

4 Draw me after you—we will run—bring me, O king, to your inner chambers; we will rejoice and delight in you; we remember your loves more than wine; the upright love you.

5 I am black and beautiful, O daughters of Jerusalem, like the tents of Kedar, like the curtains of Solomon.

6 Do not gaze upon me because I am dark, because the sun has scorched me; my mother's sons were angry with me; they made me keeper of the vineyards; my own vineyard I did not keep.

7 Tell me, whom my soul loves, how you will feed, how you will lead forth at midday? Why should I be as one veiled beside the flocks of your companions?

8 If you do not know, O beautiful one among women, go forth by the heels of the flock and pasture your kids by the shepherds' tents.

9 Like the company of horses in Pharaoh's chariot, I compare you, my love.

10 Your cheeks are lovely as doves in rows, your neck in chains.

11 Gold borders we will make for you with silver studs.

12 Until the king is in his place at table, my spikenard gives forth its scent.

13 A bundle of myrrh is my beloved to me; he shall lie between my breasts.

14 A cluster of henna is my beloved to me, from the vineyards of En-gedi.

15 Behold, you are beautiful, my love; behold, you are beautiful; your eyes are doves.

16 Behold, you are beautiful, my beloved, indeed delightful; indeed our couch is lush.

17 Our houses' beams are cedars, their rafters firs.


## Chapter 2

1 I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys.

2 Like a lily among the thorns, so is my love among the daughters.

3 Like an apple tree among the trees of the forest, so is my beloved among the sons; in his shade I delighted and sat, and his fruit is sweet to my taste.

4 He brought me into the house of wine, and his banner over me was love.

5 Sustain me with flagons, support me with apples, for I am lovesick.

6 His left hand is under my head, and his right hand embraces me.

7 I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes or by the hinds of the field, that you not stir up nor awaken love until it please.

8 The voice of my beloved, behold, he comes, leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.

9 My beloved is like a roe or a young roe of the harts; behold, he stands behind our wall, looking in through the windows, peering in through the lattices.

10 My beloved answered and said to me, "Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away."

11 For behold, the winter has passed, the rain has gone by and departed.

12 The flowers appear in the land, the time of singing has come, and the voice of the turtle dove is heard in our land.

13 The fig tree has enclosed her green figs, and the vines with tender grapes give forth their scent; arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.

14 My dove, in the clefts of the (ragged) rock, in the hiding place of the steep place, show me your countenance, let me hear your voice, for your voice is sweet and your countenance is lovely.

15 Catch us the foxes, the little foxes that damage the vines, for our vines are tender grapes.

16 My beloved is mine and I am his, who feeds among the lilies.

17 Until the day breathes and the shadows flee, turn, my beloved, like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.


## Chapter 3

1 On my couch at night I sought him whom my soul loves; I sought him but did not find him.

2 I will rise now and go about in the city, in the streets and in the broad places; I will seek him whom my soul loves; I sought him, but I did not find him.

3 The watchmen who are going about in the city found me: have you seen the one whom my soul loves?

4 Almost had I passed from them when I found the one my soul loves; I held him and would not let him go until I brought him to the house of my mother and to the chamber of her who bore me.

5 I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes or by the hinds of the field, that you do not stir up nor awaken love until it pleases.

6 Who is this coming up from the wilderness, like pillars of smoke, perfumed with myrrh and incense, from all the powders of the merchant?

7 Behold, the bed of Solomon; sixty mighty men are about it, from the mighty men of Israel.

8 All of them are armed with the sword, trained for battle, each man with his sword at his side, because of fear during the nights.

9 The king Solomon made a chariot from the woods of Lebanon.

10 Its pillars are silver, its bottom gold, its chariot purple, its midst paved with love, from the daughters of Jerusalem.

11 Go forth and see, daughters of Zion, the king Solomon, with the crown with which his mother crowned him on the day of his espousal, and on the day of the gladness of his heart.


## Chapter 4

1 Behold, you are beautiful, my love; behold, you are beautiful: your eyes are doves behind your locks; your hair is like a flock of goats that have come down from Mount Gilead.

2 Your teeth are like a flock of ewes that have come up from the washing, all of which are complete, and none is barren among them.

3 Like a crimson thread your lips, and your speech is lovely; like a piece of pomegranate your temple within your locks.

4 Your neck is like the tower of David, built as an armoury; a thousand shields hang upon it, all the shields of the mighty men.

5 Your two breasts are like two young roe twins of a roe, feeding among the lilies.

6 Until the day cools and the shadows flee, I will go to the mountain of myrrh and to the hill of frankincense.

7 You are altogether lovely, my love, and there is no blemish in you.

8 With me from Lebanon come, my bride; with me from Lebanon come; look from the top of Amana, from the top of Senir and Hermon, from the dens of the lions, from the mountains of the leopards.

9 You have ravished my heart, my sister, my bride; you have ravished my heart with one glance of your eyes, with one chain from your neck.

10 How beautiful are your loves, my sister, bride; how much better are your loves than wine; and your ointments' scent than all spices.

11 Honeycomb drips from your lips, my bride; honey and milk are under your tongue; the scent of your garments is like the scent of Lebanon.

12 A garden locked is my sister, bride; a spring sealed, a fountain sealed.

13 Your plantings are an orchard of pomegranates with the fruit of precious things, henna with spikenard.

14 spikenard and saffron, calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense, myrrh and aloes, with all chief spices.

15 A fountain of gardens, a well of living waters, flowing down from Lebanon.

16 Awake, O north wind, and come, O south wind; blow upon my garden, let the spices flow; let my beloved come into his garden and eat the fruit of his precious things.


## Chapter 5

1 I came to my garden, my sister, my bride; I gathered my myrrh with my spice; I ate my honeycomb with my honey; I drank my wine with my milk. Eat, O friends; drink, and be drunk, O beloved.

2 I am asleep but my heart is awake; the voice of my beloved knocks: Open for me, my sister, my love, my dove, my perfect one; for my head is filled with dew, my locks with droplets of the night.

3 I stripped off my garment—how can I put it back on? I washed my feet—how can I soil them again?

4 My beloved thrust his hand through the hole, and my bowels yearned for him.

5 I arose to open for my beloved, and my hands dripped with myrrh, my fingers with flowing myrrh upon the handles of the lock.

6 I opened for my beloved, but my beloved had withdrawn and passed on; my soul went out at his speaking; I sought him but did not find him; I called him but he did not answer me.

7 The watchmen who go about the city found me; they struck me, they wounded me; they took away my vail from upon me, the watchmen of the walls.

8 I have adjured you, daughters of Jerusalem, if you find my beloved, what will you tell him? That I am sick with love.

9 What is your beloved more than (a) beloved, O most beautiful among women? What is your beloved more than (a) beloved, that you have adjured us?

10 My beloved is radiant and ruddy, distinguished among ten thousand.

11 His head is finest gold, his locks are bushy, black as the raven.

12 His eyes are like doves beside channels of water, washing in milk, sitting fitly.

13 His jaw bones are like a bed of spice, towers of perfume; his lips are lilies, dropping flowing myrrh.

14 His hands are rings of gold, filled with beryl; his belly is a mosaic of ivory, overlaid with sapphires.

15 His legs are pillars of alabaster, set upon sockets of fine gold; his appearance is like Lebanon, choice as the cedars.

16 His taste is sweet and he is altogether beloved, this my lover and this my friend, daughters of Jerusalem.


## Chapter 6

1 Where has your beloved gone, O most beautiful among women? Where has your beloved turned, that we may seek him with you?

2 My beloved has gone down to his garden, to the beds of spices, to pasture in the gardens and to gather lilies.

3 I am my beloved's and my beloved is mine, the one who shepherds among the lilies.

4 You are beautiful, my love, like Tirzah, lovely like Jerusalem, awesome as the standards.

5 Turn your eyes away from me, for they have overwhelmed me; your hair is like a flock of goats that have come down from Gilead.

6 Your teeth are like a flock of ewes that have come up from the washing, all of which are paired, and none of which is barren.

7 Like a piece of pomegranate your temples behind your locks.

8 Sixty queens, eighty concubines, and damsels without number.

9 One is my dove, my perfect one; one is she to her mother; she is the pure one to her who bore her. Daughters saw her and praised her; queens and concubines, and they praised her.

10 Who is this who gazes forth like the morning, beautiful as the moon, bright as the sun, fearsome as the stars?

11 I went down to the garden of nuts to see the greenness of the valley, to see the vine flourish, the pomegranates blossom.

12 I did not know my soul; my noble people set me in chariots.

13 Return, return, O Shulamite; return, return, that we may look upon you. Why do you look upon the Shulamite, as upon the dances of a company?


## Chapter 7

1 How lovely are your feet in shoes, O daughter of nobles! The joints of your thighs are like jewels, the work of a cunning workman.

2 Your navel is a round goblet, let liquor not fail; your belly is a heap of wheat, set among the lilies.

3 Your two breasts are like two young roe twins of a roe.

4 Your neck is like the tower of ivory, your eyes like fish-pools of Heshbon by the gate of Bath-rabbim, your nose like the tower of Lebanon looking toward Damascus.

5 Your head upon you is like Carmel, and the flowing of your head like purple; a king is bound in the galleries.

6 How beautiful and how delightful is love in delights.

7 Your stature is like a palm tree, and your breasts like clusters of grapes.

8 I said, I will go up into the palm tree, I will take hold of its boughs; and let your breasts be like clusters of the vine, and the scent of your nose like apples.

9 And your palate is like the best wine going straight to my beloved, smoothing the lips of those who are asleep.

10 I am my beloved's and his desire is for me.

11 Come, my beloved, let us go out to the country; let us lodge in the villages.

12 Let us go early to the vineyards, let us see if the vine has budded, if the tender grape has opened, if the pomegranates are in bloom; there I will give my beloved to you.

13 The mandrakes have given forth scent, and at our entrance are all precious fruits, both new and old; my beloved, I have stored up for you.


## Chapter 8

1 Who will make you like a brother to me, one who nursed the breasts of my mother? If I found you outside, I would kiss you; indeed, they would not despise me.

2 I will lead you, I will bring you to the court of my mother; she will teach you. I will give you to drink of the spiced wine, of the juice of my pomegranate.

3 His left hand under my head and his right hand embracing me.

4 I have sworn to you, daughters of Jerusalem: what will you stir up, and what will you awaken love until it pleases?

5 Who is this coming up from the wilderness, leaning on her beloved? Under the apple tree I awakened you; there your mother was in labor with you; there she who bore you was in labor.

6 Put me as a seal on your heart, as a seal on your arm, for love is as strong as death, jealousy is as unyielding as the grave; its sparks are sparks of fire, a blazing flame of the LORD.

7 Many waters cannot quench love, nor can floods drown it; if a man should give all the wealth of his house for love, they would utterly despise him.

8 Our little sister has no breasts; what shall we do for our sister on the day when she is spoken of?

9 If she is a walled city, we will build upon her a silver habitation; but if she is a door, we will guard upon her a cedar board.

10 I am a wall, and my breasts are like towers; then I became in his eyes as one who brings peace.

11 A vineyard belonging to Solomon was at Baal-hamon; he gave the vineyard to keepers; each man was to bring for its fruit a thousand pieces of silver.

12 My vineyard, which is mine, is before me; a thousand [shall be] for you, Solomon, and two hundred for those who guard its fruit.

13 You who sit in the gardens, companions listen to your voice; make me hear it.

14 Flee, O my beloved, and be like the roe or the young hart upon the mountains of spices.


# ISAIAH

## Chapter 1

1 The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.

2 Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth, for the LORD has spoken: I have raised and exalted children, but they have rebelled against me.

3 The bull knows its owner, and the ass its crib; Israel does not know, my people do not consider.

4 Woe to the sinful nation, a people burdened with iniquity, a brood of evildoers, children acting corruptly; they have forsaken the LORD, spurned the Holy One of Israel, turned backward.

5 Why do you continue to rebel when you are only beaten again? Your whole head is sick and your whole heart is faint.

6 From the sole of the foot even to the head there is no soundness in it, only wound and bruise and stripe, not pressed out and not bound up and not softened with oil.

7 Your land is desolate, your cities are burned with fire, foreigners devour your land before you, and it is desolate like the overthrow of foreigners.

8 And the daughter of Zion will remain like a booth in a vineyard, like a cottage in a garden of cucumbers, like a city under siege.

9 If the LORD of hosts had not left us a very few survivors, we would have been like Sodom, we would resemble Gomorrah.

10 Hear the word of the LORD, you rulers of Sodom; give ear to the law of our God, you people of Gomorrah.

11 Why should I want the abundance of your sacrifices? says the LORD. I am full of burnt offerings of rams and fat of fed beasts; and the blood of bulls and sheep and goats I do not desire.

12 When you come to appear before me, who has required this from your hand, to trample my courts?

13 You shall not continue to bring a vain oblation; incense of abomination it is to me; new moon and every sabbath, calling an assembly—I cannot endure iniquity and a solemn assembly.

14 Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hates; they have become a burden to me; I am weary of bearing them.

15 When you spread your hands, I will hide my eyes from you; even though you make many prayers, I do not listen; your hands are full of blood.

16 Wash, purify yourselves, remove the evil of your doings from before my eyes, cease from evil.

17 Learn to do well; seek justice, relieve the oppressed, defend the fatherless, plead for the widow.

18 Come now, let us reason together, says the LORD: if your sins are like scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; if they are red like crimson, they shall be as wool.

19 If you are willing and obey, the goodness of the land you shall eat.

20 But if you refuse and rebel, a sword will consume you, for the word of the LORD has spoken.

21 How has the faithful city become a prostitute? Full of judgment, justice once lodged in her, but now murderers.

22 Your silver has become dross, your drink diluted with water.

23 Your captains are rebels and companions of thieves; everyone loves a bribe and pursues rewards; they do not defend the fatherless, and the cause of the widow does not come before them.

24 Therefore saith the Lord the LORD of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel, Woe, I will ease me from mine adversaries, and avenge me of mine enemies.

25 I will turn my hand against you, and I will purely refine your dross, and I will remove all your tin.

26 And I will restore your judges as at the beginning, and your counselors as at the first; afterward you shall be called the city of righteousness, a faithful city.

27 Zion shall be redeemed with judgment, and her returnees with justice.

28 And the affliction of transgressors and sinners together, and those forsaking the LORD shall perish.

29 For they will be ashamed of the mighty ones that they desired, and they will be embarrassed because of the gardens that they chose.

30 When you are like a withering elm without leaves, and like a garden that has no water.

31 And the tow shall be for the spark, and its work for the flame; both of them shall burn together, and none shall quench.


## Chapter 2

1 The word that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.

2 And it shall be in the latter days that the mount of the house of the LORD shall be established on the top of the mountains, and it shall be raised above the little hills, and all the nations shall flow to it.

3 And many folk shall go and say, Let us go up to the mount of the LORD, to the house of the God of Jacob, and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for out of Zion shall go forth the law, and the word of the LORD from Jerusalem.

4 And he shall judge between the nations and reprove the many peoples, and they shall beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning-hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.

5 House of Jacob, come, let us walk in the light of the LORD.

6 For you have rejected your people, the house of Jacob, because they are filled with customs from aforetime and with soothsayers like the Philistines, and they make alliances with the children of foreigners.

7 Her land was filled with silver and gold, and there was no end to her storehouses; her land was filled with horses, and there was no end to her chariots.

8 And his land was filled with idols; they worshiped the work of their hands, that which their fingers made.

9 And man bowed down and man was brought low, but do not exalt them.

10 Enter the rock and hide in the dust from the fear of the LORD and from the glory of his majesty.

11 The eyes of man's loftiness shall be humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be brought low, and the LORD alone shall be exalted in that day.

12 The age of the LORD of armies is against every haughty and exalted one, and against every proud and lofty one.

13 And against all the cedars of Lebanon, the tall and the lifted up, and against all the oaks of Bashan.

14 and upon all the high hills and upon all the uplifted little hills

15 And upon every high tower and upon every walled wall.

16 And upon all ships of Tarshish, and upon all pictures of desire.

17 And the loftiness of man will be brought low, and the haughtiness of men will be humbled, and the LORD alone will be exalted on that day.

18 And the idols will utterly perish.

19 And they go into caves of the rocks and into holes of the dust, from the fear of the LORD and from the glory of his majesty, when he rises to shake the earth.

20 In that age, people will cast away their silver idols and their gold idols, which they made to worship, to the moles and to the bats.

21 To enter into the clefts of the rocks and into the outmost branches of the ragged rocks, because of the fear of the LORD and the dread of his majesty, when he arises to shake the earth.

22 Cease from [the] man whose breath is in his nostril, for in what is he esteemed?


## Chapter 3

1 For behold, the Lord Jehovah of hosts is removing from Jerusalem and from Judah stay and staff, every stay of bread and every stay of water.

2 a mighty man and a man of war, a judge and a prophet and a diviner and an elder

3 a captain of fifty and one who lifts up faces, and a counselor, a wise man of secrets, and one discerning of enchantments.

4 And I will appoint babes as their princes, and babes shall rule over them.

5 And the folk draw near, man to another and man to his fellow; the youth shall be insolent toward the old man, and the base toward the honoured.

6 When a man takes hold of his brother in the house of his father, saying, You have a garment; you shall be our ruler, and this ruin shall be under your hand.

7 In that age he shall say, I will not be a healer; in my house there is no food and no garment; you shall not make me ruler of the people.

8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen, because their tongue and their doings are against the LORD, to provoke the eyes of his glory.

9 Their faces testify against them, and their sin declares itself like Sodom; they do not hide it. Woe to their soul, for they have brought evil upon themselves.

10 Say of the righteous that it is good, for the reward of their doings they shall eat.

11 Woe to the wicked for evil, for the recompense of his hands shall be done to him.

12 My people, their oppressors are children, and women rule over them; my people, your leaders mislead, and the path of your ways they swallow.

13 The LORD stands to judge and takes His place to contend with the peoples.

14 The LORD comes to enter judgment with the elders of his people and their rulers: you have consumed the vineyard; the spoil of the poor is in your houses.

15 You crush my people and grind the faces of the poor, says the Lord God of hosts.

16 And the LORD said, Because the daughters of Zion are haughty, and walk with outstretched neck and flirting eyes, walking with mincing steps, and tinking with their feet,

17 And (my) Lord will strike the crown of the head of the daughters of Zion, and the LORD will expose their secret parts.

18 In that age the LORD will remove the beauty of the tinkling ornaments, the cauls, and the round tires like the moon.

19 The drops, the chains, the bracelets, and the mufflers.

20 The ornaments, and the ornaments of the legs, and the headbands, and the houses of the soul, and the charms.

21 The rings and the earrings of the face.

22 The changeable suits of apparel, and the mantles, and the vails, and the crisping pins.

23 the mirrors, the fine linen garments, the turbans, and the veils.

24 Instead of perfume there will be stink, and instead of a girdle a rent, and instead of well-set hair baldness, and instead of a stomacher a girding of sackcloth, because of beauty.

25 Your few will fall by the sword, and your strength in battle.

26 And her doors will mourn and lament, and she will sit on the ground.


## Chapter 4

1 And in that day seven women shall take hold of one man, saying, Our food will we eat, and our garments will we wear; only let your name be called upon us; take away our reproach.

2 In that age the branch of the LORD shall be for beauty and for glory, and the fruit of the earth shall be for majesty and for splendour, to the remnant of Israel.

3 And the one left in Zion and the one remaining in Jerusalem will be called holy; everyone written down for life in Jerusalem.

4 If (my) Lord will wash the filth of the daughters of Zion, and purge the blood-guiltiness of Jerusalem from her midst by a spirit of judgment and by a spirit of burning.

5 And the LORD created over every settled place of Mount Zion and over its assembly a cloud by day and smoke, and the brightness of a flaming fire by night, for over all glory there is a defense.

6 And a booth shall be for shade by day from the heat, and for a refuge and for a covert from storm and from rain.


## Chapter 5

1 I will sing to my beloved a song of my beloved concerning his vineyard: my beloved had a vineyard on a fertile hill.

2 And he fenced it, and gathered out the stones, and planted it with choice wine; and he built a tower in the midst of it, and also hewed a winepress therein; and he looked for it to yield grapes, but it yielded wild grapes.

3 Now therefore, Jerusalem and Judah, judge between me and my vineyard.

4 What more could I do for my vines that I have not done in it? Why, when I expected it to produce (ripe) grapes, did it produce wild grapes?

5 And now let me tell you what I am about to do to my vines: remove its hedge, so that it becomes a treading down; break down its wall, so that it becomes a trampling under foot.

6 And I will make it a waste, not to be pruned nor hoed, and it shall grow adamant and thorns; and upon the thick clouds I will command that they rain no rain upon it.

7 For the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel and the man of Judah the plant of his delight; and he waited for justice but behold oppression; for righteousness but behold a cry.

8 Woe to those who join house to house, field to field, until there is no place left, and you alone are made to dwell in the midst of the land.

9 In my ears, the LORD of hosts has sworn: Surely many houses shall become desolate, great and fine houses without inhabitant.

10 For ten yoke of vineyard will make one bath, and a homer of seed will make an ephah.

11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning to pursue strong drink, who linger into the twilight to kindle themselves with wine.

12 And there shall be harp and psaltery, tambourine and flute and wine at their feasts; yet they do not regard the work of the LORD, nor see the deed of his hands.

13 Therefore my people go into exile for lack of knowledge, and their glory are famished men, and their multitude thirsts for water.

14 Therefore the grave has enlarged her appetite and opened her mouth without restraint, and down goes its beauty, its multitude, its tumult, and its revelry.

15 Another has bowed down and every man is brought low, and the eyes of the haughty are humbled.

16 The LORD of hosts was exalted in judgment, and God the Holy One was sanctified in righteousness.

17 And the lambs will graze in their manner, and the desolate places of the fat ones the swords of the oppressors will consume.

18 Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood, and the sin of the cart with thick ropes.

19 Those who say, "Let him hasten, let him speed up his act, so that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel come near and come, that we may know."

20 Woe to those who say to evil, "Good," and to good, "Evil," who set dark as light and light as dark, who set bitter as sweet and sweet as bitter.

21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes and discerning in their own sight.

22 Woe to those mighty men who are heroes at drinking wine, and champions at mixing strong drink.

23 Justifying the wicked for the sake of a bribe, and removing the justice of the righteous from him.

24 Therefore, as stubble is consumed by the tongue of fire and chaff by the flame, so their root will become like rottenness and their blossom will rise like dust, because they have rejected the law of the LORD of hosts and scorned the speech of the Holy One of Israel.

25 Therefore the LORD was angry with his people, and stretched out his hand against them and struck them; so the mountains trembled, and their dead bodies were like torn flesh in the midst of the streets. Yet for all this, his anger has not turned back, and his hand is still stretched out.

26 And he will raise a banner to the nations from afar, and whistle to it from the end of the earth, and behold, swiftly it will come hastily.

27 There is no weariness and no stumbling in him; he does not slumber and does not sleep; nor is the girdle of his loins opened, nor is the latchet of his sandal untied.

28 whose arrows are sharp and all his bows are bent; the hooves of his horses are accounted as flint, and his wheels as a whirlwind.

29 Their roaring is like a lion; they roar like young lions. They growl and seize the prey, and carry it away safely, and none can deliver.

30 And in that age he shall roar over him like the roaring of the sea, and he shall look toward the land and behold—darkness, distress, and light has gone dark in its heavens.


## Chapter 6

1 In the year of King Uzziah's death, I saw my Lord sitting on a throne, high and exalted, and his train filled the temple.

2 Seraphs stood above him; each had six wings: with two he covered his face, and with two he covered his legs, and with two he flew.

3 And one called to another and said, Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts; the fulness of all the earth is his glory.

4 The cubits of the basons moved from the sound of the caller, and the temple was filled with smoke.

5 And I said, Woe is me, for I am undone, because I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwell in the midst of a people of unclean lips, for my eyes have seen the King, the LORD of hosts.

6 Then one of the seraphs flew to me, with a live coal in his hand, which he took from the altar with tongs.

7 And he touched my mouth and said, "Behold, this has touched upon your lips, and your iniquity is removed, and your sin is atoned for."

8 And I heard the voice of my Lord saying, "Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?" And I said, "Here I am; send me."

9 And he said, Go and say to this folk, Hear indeed but do not understand, and see indeed but do not perceive.

10 Make the heart of this people fat, their ears heavy, and their eyes shut—lest they see with their eyes, hear with their ears, and their heart understand, and they turn and be healed.

11 And I said, "Until when, (my) Lord?" And He said, "Until cities are wasted from lack of inhabitant, and houses from lack of person, and the land is utterly desolate."

12 And the LORD removed the common sort, and great was the desolation in the midst of the land.

13 Yet in it a tenth shall remain, and it shall return and be consumed, like the elm or the oak, of which, when cast, the holy seed is its stock.


## Chapter 7

1 In the age of Ahaz son of Jotham son of Uzziah king of Judah, Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel went up to Jerusalem to war against it, but could not fight against it.

2 And it was told to the house of David, saying, Aram has settled upon Ephraim; and his heart and the heart of his people trembled, like the trembling of trees of the forest before the wind.

3 And the LORD said to Isaiah, Go out now to meet Ahaz, you and Shear-jashub your son, to the end of the conduit of the upper pool, to the highway of the field of the washerman.

4 And you shall say to him: "Beware and be calm; do not fear, and let not your heart bethink themselves faintly because of the two tails of the smoking fire-brands, the fierce wrath of Rezin and Aram and the son of Remaliah."

5 Because Aram has plotted evil against you, and Ephraim and the son of Remaliah, saying:

6 We will go up against Judah and harass her, and break through her midst, and set a king in her midst, even the son of Tabeal.

7 Thus says the LORD God: It shall not stand, nor shall it come to pass.

8 For the head of Aram is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin; yet sixty-five years more, and Ephraim will be broken from being a people.

9 The chief of Ephraim is Samaria, and the chief of Samaria is the son of Remaliah; if you do not believe, surely you shall not be established.

10 And Jehovah continued to speak to Ahaz, saying:

11 Ask for a sign from the LORD your God; ask for something deep or high above.

12 Ahaz said, I will not ask, neither will I tempt the LORD.

13 Then he said, “Listen now, house of David, is it too little for you to weary men, that you also weary my God?”

14 Therefore my Lord himself will give you a sign: behold, the virgin is with child and bears a son, and calls his name Immanuel.

15 Butter and honey-comb he shall eat, that he may know to refuse the evil and choose the good.

16 For before the boy knows to reject evil and choose good, the land that you dread because of her two kings will be forsaken.

17 The LORD will bring on you and on your people and on the house of your father days such as have not come since the day that Ephraim departed from Judah, even the king of Assyria.

18 And it shall come to pass in that day, the LORD will whistle for the fly that is in the uttermost part of the rivers of Egypt, and for the bee that is in the land of Assyria.

19 And they came and rested in all the desolate brooks, and in the holes of the ragged rocks, and in all the thorns, and in all the bushes.

20 On that day the LORD will shave with a razor hired from beyond the river, from the king of Assyria, the head and the hair of the legs, and even the beard will be removed.

21 And it shall come to pass in that age, a man shall keep alive a cow and two sheep.

22 And it shall be, from the abundance of milk they produce, he will eat butter, for butter and honey he will eat, every one remaining in the midst of the land.

23 And it shall come to pass in that age, every place where there shall be a thousand vines for a thousand silverlings, it shall become briers and thorns.

24 With arrows and archery he will come there, for the whole earth will be thorns and briers.

25 And all the hills that could be cleared with the mattock shall not be visited for fear of briers and thorns; it shall be a place for sending oxen and for sheep to tread.


## Chapter 8

1 And the LORD said to me, Take for yourself a large roll, and write on it with a man's graving tool: Maher-sha-lal-bash-baz.

2 And I will call as witnesses for me faithful witnesses, Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.

3 I approached the prophetess, and she conceived and bore a son. Then the LORD said to me, "Name his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz."

4 Before the child knows to call father and mother, the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria will be carried off before the king of Assyria.

5 And the LORD continued to speak to me again, saying:

6 Because this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah that flow gently, and rejoices over Rezin and the son of Remaliah.

7 Therefore, behold, (my) Lord is raising against them the waters of the river, mighty and many—Assyria and all its glory—and it will rise up over all its channels, and will march across all its banks.

8 And it will sweep through Judah, it will overflow and pass, until it reaches the neck; and the stretching out of its wings will be the fullness of the breadth of your land, Immanuel.

9 Be shattered, O peoples, and listen, all you far-off lands; gird yourselves and be shattered; gird yourselves and be shattered.

10 Take counsel, but it will be broken; speak a word, but it will not stand, for God is with us.

11 For thus said the LORD to me, with a strong hand, and he instructed me not to walk in the way of this people, saying:

12 You shall not say, "A conspiracy," to all that this people calls a conspiracy, and you shall not fear their dread, nor be in terror of it.

13 You shall sanctify the LORD of hosts; he shall be your dread, and he shall be your terror.

14 And it will become a sanctuary and a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense for both houses of Israel, a snare and a trap for the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

15 And many will stumble over them, and fall, and be broken, and be snared, and be taken.

16 Bind up the testimony, seal the law among my disciples.

17 And I waited for the LORD, who hid his face from the house of Jacob, and I looked to him.

18 Here I am, and the children whom the LORD has given me as signs and wonders in Israel, from the LORD of hosts who dwells on Mount Zion.

19 And when they say to you, “Seek to the familiar spirits and to the wizards who chirp and mutter,” shall not a people seek to their God? Should one seek for the living to the dead?

20 To the law and to the testimony: if they do not speak according to this word, there is no morning for them.

21 And a desperate and hungry man will pass through it, and when he is hungry he will become furious and curse his king and his gods, and turn upward.

22 And he looked toward the land, and behold, adversity and darkness, dimness of anguish, and he will be driven into thick gloom.


## Chapter 9

1 For there is no dimness for her who is straitened, whereas at the former time he brought low the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, but at the latter time he has made heavy and glorified the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.

2 The folk walking in darkness have seen a great light; those sitting in the land of shadow of death, light has shone upon them.

3 You have multiplied the nation; you have not increased the joy. They rejoice before you as with the joy of harvest, as men rejoice when they divide the spoil.

4 For the yoke of his burden and the rod upon his shoulder, the staff of his oppressor, he has shattered like the day of Midian.

5 For all battle, with its tumult and shaking, and garments rolled in blood, shall be for burning, fuel for fire.

6 For a child is born to us, a son is given to us, and the government is upon his shoulder, and his name is called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

7 The government and prosperity have no end upon the throne of David and his kingdom, to establish it and sustain it with justice and righteousness, from now and forever; the zeal of the LORD of hosts will accomplish this.

8 The LORD sent a word against Jacob, and it fell upon Israel.

9 And all the folk of Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria shall know, in their pride and in the greatness of heart, saying:

10 We fell to (altars of) brick, but we will build with hewn stone; they cut down sycamores, but we will replace them with cedars.

11 And the LORD exalted the adversaries of Rezin against him, and He brought together his enemies.

12 Aram from aforetime and Philistines from after, and they have consumed Israel altogether with mouth; in all this, not turned back is his anger, and yet his hand is stretched out.

13 And the folk did not return to the one who struck them, and they did not seek the LORD of hosts.

14 The LORD cut off from Israel head and tail, branch and bulrush in one day.

15 An aged man with an uplifted countenance, he is the chief; but a prophet teaching falsehood, he is the tail.

16 And the blessers of this folk are deceivers, and their blessed ones are swallowed up.

17 Therefore the LORD will not rejoice over his chosen young men, nor have compassion on his orphans and his widows, for altogether they are godless and evil, and every mouth speaks folly; yet for all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is still outstretched.

18 For the fault has burned like a fire, it has consumed the adamant and thorns; it has kindled in the thickets of the forest, and the pride of smoke has been lifted up.

19 In the wrath of the LORD of hosts the land is burned, and the people are like fuel for fire; man does not spare his brother.

20 He cut off on the right hand, and the hungry ate on the left, but was not satisfied; each man ate the flesh of his own arm.

21 Manasseh provokes Ephraim, and Ephraim provokes Manasseh; together they are allied against Judah. In spite of all this, his anger has not turned back, and his hand remains outstretched.


## Chapter 10

1 Woe to those who decree decrees of iniquity, and write grievousness with their writings.

2 To pervert the cause of the needy and to rob the judgment of the poor of my people, that widows may be their spoil and that they may plunder the fatherless.

3 What will you do on the day of visitation and of destruction from afar? To whom will you flee for help, and where will you leave your glory?

4 Without bowing under the prisoner, and under the slain they will fall; in all this, His anger has not turned back, and His hand is still stretched out.

5 Woe to Assyria, the rod of my anger, and the staff in their hand is my wrath.

6 I will send him against a hypocritical nation, and against the people of my wrath I will command him, to take spoil as prey and to seize plunder as booty, treading them down like clay in the streets.

7 But he does not bethink himself thus, nor does his mind plan thus, for in his heart he purposes to destroy and to exterminate nations not few.

8 For he will say, "Are not my captains kings?"

9 Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Damascus like Samaria?

10 As my hand has found the kingdoms of the idols, and their carved images, from Jerusalem and from Samaria.

11 As I have done to Samaria and her idols, so will I do to Jerusalem and her images.

12 And it shall come to pass, when the LORD has finished all his work on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, I will punish the fruit of the greatness of the heart of the king of Assyria and the beauty of the haughtiness of his eyes.

13 For he said, By the strength of my hand I have done it, and by my wisdom, for I am skillful; I have removed the borders of peoples, plundered their treasures, and like a mighty one brought down inhabitants.

14 I found as a nest the wealth of the peoples, and as one gathering abandoned eggs, all the earth I gathered, and there was none stirring a wing or opening a mouth or chirping.

15 Shall the ax boast over the one who chops with it? Or shall the saw magnify itself over the one who wields it? As if a rod should wave over those who lift it, like a staff lifting those who are not wood.

16 Therefore the Lord, the LORD of hosts, will send leanness into his fatness, and under his glory a burning shall burn like the burning of fire.

17 And Israel’s light shall be a fire, and their Holy One a flame; and it shall burn and consume their thorns and their briers in one day.

18 And the glory of its forest and its fruitful field, from soul even to flesh, shall be consumed, and it shall be like the fleeing of a fugitive.

19 And the rest of the trees of the forest will be few, and a young man will record them.

20 And it shall come to pass in that age, the remnant of Israel and the escaped of the house of Jacob shall no longer lean upon their striker, but shall lean upon the LORD, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.

21 The remnant of Jacob will return, a remnant to the mighty God.

22 But if your folk Israel be as the sand by the sea, a remnant shall return in it: destruction is decreed, overflowing with righteousness.

23 For the consumption is determined and the LORD God of hosts has ordained a devastation upon all the earth.

24 Therefore thus says the LORD God of hosts: Do not fear, My people dwelling in Zion, on account of Assyria; with a rod he shall strike you, and his staff lift up against you in the way of Egypt.

25 For yet a very little while, and the indignation will be finished, and my wrath will be upon their destruction.

26 And the LORD of hosts will stir up against him a scourge like the blow of Midian at the rock of Oreb, and his rod over the sea, and he will lift it up against the way of Egypt.

27 And it shall come to pass in that day, the burden shall be removed from upon your shoulder, and his yoke from upon your neck, and the yoke shall be destroyed because of the anointing oil.

28 He came to Ai, passed through Migron, to Mikmas he committed his weapons.

29 They passed the ford; Geba is our lodging; Ramah is alarmed; Gibeah of Saul flees.

30 Cry aloud, daughter of Gallim; give ear, Laishah, O poor Anathoth.

31 Madmenah has wandered; the inhabitants of Gebim have become bold.

32 Even today at Nob, he will wave his hand against the mount of Zion, the hill of Jerusalem.

33 Behold, the LORD of hosts is lopping off the bough with terror, and the tall of stature shall be cut down, and the high shall be brought low.

34 And the thickets of the forest will be felled with iron, and Lebanon with the mighty one will fall.


## Chapter 11

1 A rod will come out from the stem of Jesse, and a branch from his roots will bear fruit.

2 And upon him will rest the spirit of the LORD, the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and fear of the LORD.

3 And he will smell with fear of the LORD, and will not judge by the sight of his eyes, nor reprove by the hearing of his ears.

4 And he shall judge the needy with justice, and decide with equity for the oppressed of the land; and he shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall put to death the wicked.

5 And justice shall be the girdle of his loins, and faithfulness the girdle of his reins.

6 And a wolf shall dwell with a lamb, and a leopard shall lie down with a kid; and a calf and a young lion and a fat beast together, and a young child shall lead them.

7 And a cow and a bear shall graze together; their young ones shall lie down; and a young lion as an ox shall eat straw.

8 A nursing child will play over the hole of an adder, and over the den of a cockatrice the toddler will put his hand.

9 They shall not harm or destroy in all my holy mountain, for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.

10 And it shall be in that day: the root of Jesse, who stands as a banner to the peoples, to him shall the nations seek, and his resting place shall be glorious.

11 And it shall come to pass in that day, the LORD will add the second time His hand to redeem the remnant of His people which remains from Assyria, and from Egypt, and from Pathros, and from Cush, and from Elam, and from Shinar, and from Hamath, and from the islands of the sea.

12 And he will raise a banner for the nations, and gather the outcasts of Israel, and the scattered ones of Judah he will collect from the four corners of the earth.

13 And the envy of Ephraim shall depart, and the adversaries of Judah shall be cut off; Ephraim shall not envy Judah, and Judah shall not vex Ephraim.

14 And they shall despoil the Philistines from the west side together, they shall plunder the children of the east; Edom and Moab shall be the sending forth of their hand, and the children of Ammon shall be their obedience.

15 And the LORD will utterly destroy the tongue of the sea of Egypt, and will wave his hand over the river with mighty power, and will strike it into seven streams, and cause men to pass over dryshod.

16 And there shall be a highway for the remnant of his people who remain from Assyria, as there was for Israel in the day of their coming up from the land of Egypt.


## Chapter 12

1 And you shall say in that day: I will give thanks to the LORD, for though you were angry with me, your anger has turned back and you have comforted me.

2 Behold, God is my deliverance; I will trust and will not fear, for the LORD is my strength and song, and has become my salvation.

3 And you shall draw water in joy from the fountains of salvation.

4 And you shall say on that day, give thanks to the LORD, call upon his name, declare among the peoples his deeds, make mention that his name is exalted.

5 Sing to the LORD, for he has done excellent things; this is known throughout the world.

6 Shout for joy and rejoice, O inhabitant of Zion, for great in your midst is the Holy One of Israel.


## Chapter 13

1 The burden of Babylon, which Isaiah the son of Amoz saw.

2 On the mount raise a banner, lift up the voice to them, wave the hand, and let them enter the gates of the nobles.

3 I have commanded My consecrated ones, indeed I have summoned My mighty ones in My wrath, the exultant of My pride.

4 A sound of tumult on the mountains, like a great people; a sound of the tumult of kingdoms, nations assembled; the LORD of hosts is mustering an army for war.

5 Coming from a distant land, from the uttermost edge of the heavens, the LORD and the instruments of his wrath, to destroy the whole earth.

6 Howl, for the day of the LORD is near; like destruction from the Almighty it comes.

7 Therefore every hand will become powerless, and every heart of man will melt.

8 The ambassadors are dismayed, pains seize them like a woman in labor; each man stares at his neighbor, their faces are faces of flame.

9 Behold, an age of the LORD is coming, with harshness and rage and fierce wrath, to make the land a desolation, and its sinners he will destroy from it.

10 For the stars of the heavens and their constellations will not give their light; the sun will be dark when it rises, and the moon will not shed its light.

11 I will punish the habitable world for its calamity and the wicked for their sin, and I will bring down the arrogance of the proud and humble the haughtiness of the tyrants.

12 I esteem a certain man more than fine gold, and humanity more than the golden wedge of Ophir.

13 Therefore I make the heavens tremble and the earth shakes from its place at the wrath of the LORD of hosts and at the day of the fierceness of his anger.

14 And it shall be like a roe driven away, and like sheep with none to gather them; every man shall turn to his people, and each man shall flee to his land.

15 Everything found shall be destroyed, and everything gathered shall fall by the sword.

16 Their babes will be dashed to pieces before their eyes; their houses plundered and their wives ravished.

17 Behold, I am raising up against them the Medes, who will not regard silver, nor desire gold.

18 The bows of the young men are shattered, and the fruit of the womb shows no mercy to children—its eyes spare not.

19 And Babylon shall become a glorious kingdom, the beauty and pride of the Chaldeans; as when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah.

20 It shall not sit forever, nor dwell from generation to generation; an Arabian shall not pitch tent there, nor shall shepherds lie down there.

21 And there shall lie down wild beasts of the desert, and their homes shall be full of doleful creatures; and the daughters of the owl shall dwell there, and satyrs shall dance there.

22 Wild beasts of the islands answer in its desolate houses, and dragons in palaces of delight; and her time is near to come, and her days shall not be prolonged.


## Chapter 14

1 For the LORD will have mercy on Jacob and will choose Israel once more, and will let them rest upon their own land; and the sojourner will join them and will be attached to the house of Jacob.

2 And the peoples shall take them and bring them to their place, and the house of Israel shall possess them in the land of the LORD as male servants and female servants; they shall take captive those whose captives they were, and they shall rule over their oppressors.

3 And it shall be, when the LORD gives you rest from your sorrow and from your rage and from the hard service with which you were made to serve,

4 And you shall take up this proverb concerning the king of Babylon and say, How has the oppressor ceased? The golden city has ceased.

5 The LORD broke the rod of the wicked, the sceptre of rulers.

6 Striking folk in wrath, a blow without ceasing, treading in anger the nations, persecuted without restraint.

7 The whole land was quiet and still; they broke out in singing.

8 Even the fir trees and the cedars of Lebanon rejoiced over you, since you lay down, the one who felled will not come up against us.

9 The grave trembles before you, rousing the dead, all the chief ones of the earth; it has raised up from their thrones all the kings of the nations.

10 All of them shall answer and say to you, You also have been weakened like us, to our astonishment you have been brought down.

11 Down to the grave went your pride, the noise of your psalteries; under you are spread the worm and the worm that covers you.

12 How you have fallen from the heavens, O lucifer son of the morning! You have been cut down to the earth, you who once laid waste to the nations!

13 But you said in your heart: I will ascend to heaven, above the stars of God I will raise my throne, and I will sit on the mount of assembly in the far north.

14 I will ascend the heights of thick cloud; I will make myself like the Most High.

15 But to the grave you will descend, to the border of the pit.

16 Your lookouts are watching for you, gazing toward you: Is this the man who caused the earth to tremble, who shook kingdoms?

17 He made the world as a wilderness, and overthrew its cities; he did not open the house for his prisoners.

18 All the kings of the nations, all of them, lie in glory, each man in his house.

19 But you, from your grave you cast out like a rejected branch, clothed with the slain pierced by the sword, those descending to the stones of the pit, like a trampled corpse.

20 You shall not be buried with them in burial, for your land you have destroyed, your people you have slain; the seed of evildoers shall not be called for ever.

21 Prepare for their children a slaughter because of the iniquity of their fathers, so that they may not rise up and possess the land and fill the face of the habitable part with cities.

22 And I will rise up against them, saith the LORD of hosts, and cut off from Babylon name and remnant, son and son's son, saith the LORD.

23 And I will make her a possession of the bittern and pools of standing water, and I will sweep her with the besom of destruction, saith the LORD of hosts.

24 The LORD of hosts has sworn, saying, "Surely as I have purposed, so it shall be, and as I have planned, it shall stand."

25 To break Assyria in my land, and upon my mountains I will crush him; and his yoke shall depart from them, and his burden shall be taken off from their shoulder.

26 This is the counsel devised against all the earth, and this is the outstretched hand against all the nations.

27 For the LORD of hosts has purposed, and who can annul it? And His hand is stretched out, and who can turn it back?

28 In the year of the death of King Ahaz, this prophecy occurred.

29 Rejoice not, O Philistia, altogether, because the rod that strikes you is broken; for out of the root of the serpent will come forth an adder, and its fruit will be a fiery serpent flying.

30 The firstborn of the poor shall feed, and the needy in safety shall lie down, but I will kill your root by famine, and your remnant shall be slain.

31 Howl, O gate; cry out, O city; all of you, Philistia, are melting, for smoke comes from the north, and there is no one spared among their appointed times.

32 And what will the messengers of the nation answer, when the LORD establishes Zion and in her the poor of his people take refuge?


## Chapter 15

1 A prophecy concerning Moab: in one night the city of Moab is destroyed, in one night Kir of Moab is destroyed.

2 Bajith and Dibon went up to the heights for weeping; upon Nebo and Medeba Moab wailed; on all her heights baldness, every beard cut off.

3 In her streets they girded with sackcloth, upon her roofs and in her broad places all of it descends with weeping.

4 Heshbon cried out and Elealeh, even to Jahaz the sound of their voice was heard; therefore the armed men of Moab cried out, his soul moaned within him.

5 My care for Moab cries out; her fugitives reach as far as Zoar, the heifer of the third part; for by the ascent of Luhith they go up with weeping; because the way of Horonaim brings a cry of destruction.

6 For the waters of Nimrim will be desolations, for the grass has withered, the tender grass has perished, there was no green thing.

7 Therefore, abundance will be made, and their account will be carried upon the brook of the willows.

8 For the cry has encircled the border of Moab; a howling to Eglaim, and a howling to Beer-elim.

9 For the waters of Dimon are full of blood; indeed, I will bring upon Dimon additions for the escaped remnant of Moab, a young lion, and for the remnant of the land.


## Chapter 16

1 Send a lamb to the ruler of the land from Sela, from the wilderness, to the mount of the daughter of Zion.

2 And it shall be as a bird that fleeth, as a nest forsaken, so shall the daughters of Moab be, fugitives at the fords of Arnon.

3 Bring counsel, make a judgment; set your shadow in the midst of noon as a defense for the outcasts; do not uncover the wanderer.

4 Let my outcasts dwell with you, Moab; be a hiding place for them from the destroyer; for the extortioner has ceased, destruction has ended, the trampler has vanished from the earth.

5 And established in mercy shall be the throne, and he who sits upon it in truth in the tent of David, judging and seeking justice and swift righteousness.

6 We have heard the arrogancy of Moab, very arrogant; his pride, and his arrogancy, and his wrath; his lies are not so.

7 Therefore Moab shall wail for Moab; altogether shall he wail: for the foundations of Kir-haraseth shall you mourn, surely stricken.

8 For the fields of Heshbon languish, and the vine of Sibmah; the lords of the nations have broken down its choice vines. They reached to Jaazer; they wandered into the wilderness; its shoots spread abroad and crossed over the sea.

9 Therefore I will weep with sore weeping for Jaazer the vine of Sibmah; my tears shall go to Heshbon and Elealeh; for upon your summer and upon your harvest shouting has fallen.

10 And gathered in shall be the joy and rejoicing from the fruitful field, and in the vineyards no shouting, no shouting shall be heard; wine in the winepresses no treaders shall tread—utter silence I have made.

11 Therefore my bowels for Moab shall sound like a harp, and my inward parts for Kir-hareseth.

12 And it shall come to pass when Moab is seen to be wearied on the high place, and he comes to his sanctuary to pray but is not able.

13 This is the act that the LORD spoke concerning Moab from of old.

14 Now the LORD spoke, saying, "In three years, as the years of a hired man, the glory of Moab will be humbled despite all its great abundance, and the remnant will be very small, not glorious."


## Chapter 17

1 Prophecy concerning Damascus: Behold, Damascus is removed from being a city and becomes a heap of ruins.

2 The forsaken towns of Aroer shall become folds for flocks, and they shall lie down with none to frighten.

3 And the fortress shall be forsaken from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus, and the remnant of Aram; they shall be as the glory of the children of Israel, saith the LORD of hosts.

4 And it shall be on that day, the glory of Jacob shall diminish, and the fatness of his body shall grow lean.

5 And it shall be like gathering the harvest of stalks, his arm reaping ears of corn; and it shall be like one gathering ears in the valley of Rephaim.

6 And olive gleanings shall be left in it, like the shaking of an olive tree, two or three berries at the top of a bough, four or five in its outmost branches, saith the LORD, the God of Israel.

7 In that age, the man will regard his Maker, and his eyes will look to the Holy One of Israel.

8 He shall not regard the altars, the work of his hands, nor the things his fingers have made, nor the Asherim nor the idols.

9 On that day, his fortified cities will become like the desolation of forest and field, which they abandoned because of the children of Israel, and there will be desolation.

10 For you have forgotten the God of your salvation and the rock of your strength have you not remembered; therefore you plant pleasant plants and sow a foreign vine.

11 On the day your plant is set you shall flourish, and in the morning your seed shall bloom; a heap at harvest in the day of inheritance, and as the pain of a man.

12 Woe, the abundance of many peoples, like the rumbling of the seas they roar; and the rushing of nations, like the rushing of mighty waters, they will be driven away.

13 The nations roar like the roaring of many waters, but he will rebuke them, and they will flee far away, chased like chaff on the mountains before the wind, and like a rolling thing before the whirlwind.

14 In the evening time, and behold, terror; before morning it is not—this is our portion and the lot of those who plunder us.


## Chapter 18

1 Woe to the land of the shadow of wings, beyond the rivers of Cush.

2 He who sends ambassadors by sea, in vessels of bulrushes upon the face of the waters: Go, you swift messengers, to a nation tall and smooth, to a people terrible from its beginning onward, a nation trodden under foot, whose land the rivers divide.

3 All inhabitants of the world and dwellers on the earth, as the lifting of a banner on the mountains you shall see, and as the blowing of a trumpet you shall hear.

4 For thus says the LORD to me: I will be quiet, and I will look from my dwelling place, like clear heat in sunshine, like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.

5 Before the harvest, the bud is a shriveling sour grape, and the flower will become a withered blossom; the sprigs with the pruning hooks are cut down, and the neglected branches are removed.

6 They shall be left together for the ravenous bird of the hills and for the beasts of the earth; the ravenous bird shall summer upon him, and all the beasts of the earth shall winter upon him.

7 At that time a present shall be brought to the LORD of hosts from a people of mingled speech and of trampling under foot, and from a nation terrible from its beginning onward, a nation stretched and trodden down, whose land the rivers divide, to the place of the name of the LORD of hosts, Mount Zion.


## Chapter 19

1 A prophecy concerning Egypt: Behold, the LORD rides upon a swift cloud and comes to Egypt; and the idols of Egypt will tremble before him, and the heart of Egypt will melt within it.

2 I will strike Egypt with Egypt, and they will fight, one against his brother and one against his neighbor, city against city, kingdom against kingdom.

3 And the air of Egypt will be deflated within him, and his counsel will be swallowed; so they will seek to the idols, to the charmers, to the familiar spirits, and to the wizards.

4 I will deliver Egypt into the hand of harsh masters, and a strong king shall rule over them, says the Lord, the LORD of hosts.

5 And the waters will be cut off from the sea, and the river will dry up and be lost.

6 And the rivers dried up and flowed weakly, the streams of the fortified places dried up, reed and flag withered.

7 The paper reed withers beside the brook, at the mouth of the brook, and every thing sown beside the brook dries up, is scattered, and is no more.
