8 But we are the fishers, and all who cast the angle into the river mourn, and those who spread the net upon the face of the waters are desolate.

9 And ashamed are the workers of flax, those beating fulling-mats, and the weavers of network.

10 And her foundations shall be crushed, all who make wages shall be grieved in soul.

11 Only the captains of Zoan are fools, the wise counselors of Pharaoh give senseless counsel; how can you say to Pharaoh, I am a son of wise men, a son of kings of old?

12 Where are your wise men now? Let them tell you, let them know what counsel the LORD of hosts has determined against Egypt.

13 The captains of Zoan are foolish, the captains of Noph are deluded; they have misled Egypt, the chief of her tribes.

14 The LORD has brought a spirit of confusion into her midst, and the Egyptians have staggered in all their undertakings like a drunkard reeling in his vomit.

15 And there shall be no work for Egypt, which they make: a head and a tail, a branch and a rush.

16 In that day Egypt will be like women, and tremble with fear because of the waving of the hand of the LORD of hosts, which he will wave over it.

17 The land of Judah will become a terror to Egypt; everyone who mentions it will fear because of the purpose of the LORD of hosts, which he has purposed against it.

18 On that day five cities in the land of Egypt will speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD of hosts; one of them will be called the City of Destruction.

19 In that age there will be an altar to the LORD in the midst of the land of Egypt and a pillar at her border to the LORD.

20 And it shall be a mark and a witness to the LORD of hosts in the land of Egypt, for they shall cry to the LORD because of oppressors, and he will send to them a savior and a defender, and he will deliver them.

21 And the LORD will become known to the Egyptians, and the Egyptians will acknowledge the LORD on that day, and they will serve with sacrifice and offering, and vow a vow to the LORD and fulfill it.

22 And the LORD will strike Egypt, striking and healing, and they will return to the LORD, and he will be entreated by them and heal them.

23 In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria and Assyria will come into Egypt and Egypt into Assyria and Egypt will serve Assyria.

24 In that age Israel will be one-third for Egypt and for Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the land.

25 Whom the LORD of hosts blessed, saying, "Blessed be My people Egypt, and the work of My hands Assyria, and My inheritance Israel."


## Chapter 20

1 In the year Tartan came to Ahdod, when Sargon king of Assyria sent him, and he fought against Ahdod and captured it.

2 At that time the LORD spoke by the hand of Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, Go, remove the sackcloth from your loins and your shoes from your feet. And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.

3 And the LORD said, As my servant Isaiah has gone naked and barefoot, three years a sign and a wonder upon Egypt and upon Cush.

4 So shall the king of Assyria lead away the captives of Egypt and the exiles of Ethiopia, young and old, naked and barefoot, and with buttocks uncovered, the nakedness of Egypt.

5 And ashamed and confounded be their expectation from Cush and their glory from Egypt.

6 And the inhabitant of this land shall say in that day, "Behold, such is our expectation, which we trusted there for help to deliver us from the presence of the king of Assyria; and how shall we escape?"


## Chapter 21

1 A prophecy concerning the desert by the sea: like whirlwinds from the south it comes, from a land of terror.

2 A difficult vision has been declared to me: the traitor betrays, and the plunderer plunders. Come up against me, Elam; my stronghold, Medes! All groaning I have ended.

3 Therefore my loins are filled with great pain; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am bowed down from hearing, I am dismayed from seeing.

4 My heart wanders; horror terrifies me; the twilight I longed for has been turned into trembling for me.

5 Set the table, watch from the watchtower, eat, drink; arise, O captains, [and] brandish the shield.

6 For thus said the LORD to me, Go, set the watchman; let him declare what he sees.

7 And he saw a chariot with a yoke of horsemen, a chariot of an ass, a chariot of a camel, and gave much heed, great regard.

8 A (young) lion calls from the watch tower: "My Lord, I am standing alway(s) by day, and on my watch I stand every night season."

9 And behold, a chariot of a man, a pair of horsemen; and he answered and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon, and all the carved images of her gods he has shattered to the ground.

10 My threshing and the son of my threshing floor, whom I heard from the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, I have declared to you.

11 A prophecy concerning Dumah: One calls to me from Seir, “Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?”

12 The watcher said, "Morning and also night: if you inquire, inquire; return, return."

13 An oracle concerning Arabia: in the thicket of Arabia you will lodge, you travelling companies of Dedanites.

14 To meet the thirsty they gave water, the inhabitants of the land of Tema with his bread met the fugitive.

15 For they fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the heaviness of war.

16 For thus says the LORD to me: Within a year, according to the years of a hired worker, all the glory of Kedar will cease.

17 And the remnant of the number of the archers of the sons of Kedar shall be few, for the LORD God of Israel has spoken.


## Chapter 22

1 A prophecy concerning the valley of vision: What ails you now, that you have all gone up to the housetops?

2 Stir filled the noisy city, the exultant city; your slain are not slain by the sword, nor dead from battle.

3 All your rulers have fled together; they were bound by the archers; all who were found of you were bound together and fled far away.

4 Therefore I said, Withdraw from me; let me weep bitterly with overflowing weeping; do not urge me to be comforted for the desolation of the daughter of my people.

5 For a day of destruction, treading down, and perplexity to the LORD God of hosts in the valley of vision, a crashing against the wall and crying to the hill.

6 Elam took up the quiver with a chariot of man and horsemen; Kir uncovered the shield.

7 Your chosen valleys were full of chariots, and the horsemen stirred up the gates.

8 And he uncovered the covering of Judah, and on that day she looked toward the weapon of the house of the forest.

9 And you saw that the breaches in the city of David were many, and you gathered the waters of the lower pool.

10 And you counted the houses of Jerusalem, and you broke down the houses to fortify the wall.

11 And you made a ditch between the walled [areas] for the water of the old fish-pool, but you did not look to the one who made it, and the former from afar you did not see.

12 The LORD God of hosts called on that day for weeping and for mourning and for baldness and for wearing sackcloth.

13 And behold, gladness and joy, the killing of the herd and the slaughtering of the flock, eating flesh and drinking wine, eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.

14 And the LORD of hosts revealed in my hearing: "Shall the iniquity of this be forgiven you until you die?" said the Lord GOD of hosts.

15 Thus says the Lord God of hosts: Go, come to this steward, to Shebna who is over the house:

16 What are you doing here, and to whom do you belong here, that you have hewn for yourself here a grave, carving your grave on high, chiseling your dwelling in the rocky stone?

17 Behold, the LORD will shake you with a mighty shaking, a captivity of every man, and will wrap you with wrapping.

18 He will toss you around like a ball into a broad land, there you will die, and there will be the chariots of your glory, the shame of the house of your master.

19 And I will drive you from your garrison and from your office I will cast you down.

20 And it shall be in that day, that I will call to my servant Eliakim son of Hillkiah.

21 I will clothe him with your robe and your girdle I will strengthen; your dominion I will place in his hand, and he shall be a chief to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.

22 And I will place the key of the court of David on his shoulder, and he shall open and none shall shut, and he shall shut and none shall open.

23 And you shall fasten it with a nail in a sure place, and it shall become a glorious seat for the family of his father.

24 And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father's house, the offspring and the issue, all the vessels of small things, from the vessels of the basins even to all the vessels of the pitchers.

25 In that day, says the LORD of hosts, the nail that was fastened in a sure place will fail, and it will be cut down and fall, and the burden that was on it will be cut off, for the LORD has spoken.


## Chapter 23

1 A prophecy concerning Tyre: wail, O ships of Tarshish, for it has been destroyed, its house no longer has an entrance; from the land of the Kittim it has been revealed to them.

2 Be silent, you inhabitants of the coast; the merchant of Sidon, passing over the sea, has filled you.

3 And in abundant waters, the seed of Shihor, the harvest of the river, its produce, became merchandise of nations.

4 Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea, the stronghold of the sea, says: I have not labored, nor given birth, nor brought up young men, nor raised virgins.

5 As the bruit concerning Egypt, they will tremble at the report of Tyre.

6 Cross over to Tarshish; wail, you inhabitants of the coastlands.

7 Is this your proud city, whose origins are from ancient times, whose feet have carried her from afar to sojourn?

8 Who has planned this against Tyre, the crowning city, whose merchants are princes, whose traders are the world's leading men?

9 The LORD of hosts has purposed to defile the pride of all beauty, to bring low every distinguished one of the earth.

10 Your land is like the Nile; daughter of Tarshish, there is no more strength.

11 His hand stretched out against the sea, he stirred up kingdoms; the LORD has commanded concerning Canaan to destroy her strongholds.

12 And he said, You shall no longer rejoice, O maid, daughter of Zidon; arise, pass over to Kittim; even there you shall have no rest.

13 Behold, the land of the Chaldeans, this people was not; Asshur founded it for wild beasts of the desert; they erected, they raised up its palaces, he made it a ruin.

14 Howl, you ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed.

15 And it shall come to pass in that day, that Tyre shall be forgotten seventy years, according to the days of one king; after the end of seventy years it shall be for Tyre as the song of a prostitute.

16 Take the harp, go about the city, forgotten harlot; skillfully play, multiply song, that you may be remembered.

17 After seventy years the LORD will punish Tyre, and she will return to her hire, and play the prostitute with all the kingdoms of the earth on the face of the ground.

18 Her merchandise and her hire shall be consecrated to the LORD; it shall not be stored up or hoarded, for her merchandise shall belong to those who dwell before the LORD, to eat until they are satisfied and for clothing to cover the old.


## Chapter 24

1 Behold, the LORD is shattering the land and scattering its inhabitants, distorting the face of it.

2 And it shall be: like folk so the priest, like the servant so his master, like the handmaid so her mistress, like the buyer so the seller, like the lender so the borrower, like the creditor so the one indebted to him.

3 The land shall be utterly emptied and utterly plundered, for the LORD has spoken this word.

4 The earth mourns, withers; the world languishes, withers; the lofty ones of the people of the land languish.

5 And the land is defiled under its inhabitants, for they have transgressed law, changed statute, broken perpetual covenant.

6 Therefore the land was cursed and its inhabitants became guilty; therefore the inhabitants of the land were scorched, and only a very few men remained.

7 New wine mourns, the vine languishes, all the merry-hearted sigh.

8 The joy of tabrets ceases, the noise of the exultant ends, the mirth of the harp ceases.

9 With song they do not drink wine; bitter is strong drink to those who drink it.

10 The city of confusion is broken; every house is shut up from entering.

11 Crying over the wine in the streets, all joy has ceased, the mirth of the land has been banished.

12 The city is left to desolation, and destruction strikes the gate.

13 For thus it shall be in the midst of the land among the peoples, like shaking of an olive tree, like grapegleanings after the vintage is complete.

14 They shall lift up their voices, rejoicing; with the majesty of the LORD they shall cry aloud from the sea.

15 Therefore in the lands the LORD is honored, among the isles of the sea the name of the LORD God of Israel is renowned.

16 From the uttermost part of the land we have heard songs, beauty to the righteous; and I said, leanness to me, leanness to me, woe to me; treacherously the treacherous have dealt, treacherously with treachery the treacherous have dealt.

17 Dread and a pit and a snare are upon you, O dweller of the land.

18 The one who flees from the sound of dread will fall into the pit, and the one who climbs up from the midst of the pit will be caught in the snare, for windows from on high have opened and the foundations of the earth have trembled.

19 The earth is shaken and shattered, the earth is split and broken, the earth is shaken and reeling.

20 The land staggers like a drunkard and sways like a cottage, and its rebellion weighs heavily upon it, so it falls and cannot rise again.

21 And it shall come to pass in that day, the LORD will punish the host on high, and the kings of the land upon the land.

22 And they will be gathered together, prisoner upon cistern, and they will be shut up in prison, and after many days they will be attended to.

23 And the moon will turn pale and the sun will be ashamed, for the LORD of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before his elders there will be glory.


## Chapter 25

1 The LORD my God, you are exalted; I will praise your name, for you have done wonders, counsels from afar, faithfulness, truth.

2 For you have turned a city into a heap, a fortified city into a ruin; a castle of strangers from the city, forever, will not be rebuilt.

3 Therefore shall folk of mighty nations fear you, city of Gentiles shall dread you.

4 For you were a fortress to the poor, a fortress to the needy in distress, a refuge from the storm, shade from the heat, for the blast of the ruthless is like a storm against a wall.

5 As desolation in a dry place, the noise of foreigners you will silence; desolation in the shadow of a thick cloud, the song of tyrants you will answer.

6 And the LORD of hosts will make for all the nations on this mountain a banquet of rich foods, a banquet of aged wines, of rich foods full of marrow, of aged wines well refined.

7 And he shall destroy on this mount the face of the covering that covereth all the peoples, and the vail that is spread over all the nations.

8 Death will be swallowed up forever, and the Lord God will wipe away tears from every face, and the reproach of his people he will remove from all the earth, for the LORD has spoken.

9 And he will say in that day, Behold, this is our God, we have waited for him, and he will save us; this is the LORD, we have waited for him; we will rejoice and be glad in his salvation.

10 For the hand of the LORD rests on this mountain, and Moab shall be trampled under it, like threshing straw on a dunghill.

11 And he spreads his hands in his midst as the swimmer spreads out to swim, and brings low his pride together with the spoils of his hands.

12 And the most strong hold, Misgab, your walled walls, he has laid low, brought down to the ground even to the dust.


## Chapter 26

1 On that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah: a strong city is ours; salvation he sets as walls and bulwark.

2 Open the gates, that a righteous nation keeping faith may enter.

3 A framed design you will guard, peace, peace, for in you they have trusted.

4 Trust in the LORD forever, for in the LORD is the rock of eternity.

5 For the inhabitants of the height have made the exalted city haughty; he will bring it down, he will bring it down even to the earth, he will reach it even to the dust.

6 The foot shall trample it, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.

7 The path of the righteous is aright; the way of the righteous you make level.

8 Even the path of your ordinances, O LORD, we have waited for you; your name and your memory are the desire of our soul.

9 My soul has longed for you at night; indeed, my spirit within me seeks you earnestly, for as your judgments come upon the earth, the inhabitants of the habitable world learn righteousness.

10 Though favor is shown to the wicked, he will not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness he deals perversely and will not behold the majesty of the LORD.

11 Jehovah, exalted is your hand; they will not see, they will look and be ashamed, the envy of the people, the fire of your enemies will consume them.

12 O LORD, grant us prosperity, for indeed all our works you have accomplished for us.

13 Jehovah our God, lords besides you have ruled over us; only you we will remember by name.

14 The dead do not live; the deceased do not rise— therefore You visited and destroyed them, and caused every memorial of them to perish.

15 You have added to the nation, O LORD; You have added to the nation, You are glorified; You have removed far all the ends of the earth.

16 LORD, in distress they sought you; they poured out a whispered prayer when your discipline was upon them.

17 Like a woman with child who draws near to giving birth, she writhes and cries out in her pangs, so were we before you, O LORD.

18 We have groaned in labor, as though we had brought forth air; we have not achieved deliverance for the earth, nor have the inhabitants of the habitable part of the world fallen.

19 Your dead shall live, my dead bodies shall rise; awake and sing, you who dwell in the dust, for your dew is a dew of light, and the earth shall cause the dead to fall.

20 Go, my people, enter your chambers and shut the doors behind you; hide for a brief moment until the wrath passes.

21 For behold, the LORD is going out from his place to punish the iniquity of the inhabitants of the earth upon him; and the earth will disclose her blood, and will no longer cover her slain.


## Chapter 27

1 In that age the LORD will punish with His sword, His hard, great, and strong sword, the leviathan serpent fleeing, and the leviathan serpent coiling, and He will kill the dragon that is in the sea.

2 On that day, sing to her, a vineyard of delight.

3 I am the LORD who guards her, every moment I refresh her, lest anyone visit her; night and day I keep her.

4 I have no anger; who will appoint me a time of battle? I would confront it with thorny adamant; I would burn it together.

5 Or he will take hold of my force, make peace with me; peace he will make with me.

6 Those who come shall take root, Jacob; Israel shall blossom and sprout, and the face of the habitable part shall be filled with fruit.

7 As he was smote with the blow, so he smote him; as he slew those slain, so he was slain.

8 In measure, when sending her away, you contended with her; he removed her with his fierce wind in the day of the east wind.

9 Therefore by this Jacob’s iniquity will be purged, and this is the whole fruit of removing his sin: by making all the altar stones into chalk stones, broken in pieces, the pillars and the idols will not stand.

10 For the fortified city is solitary, a habitation deserted and forsaken like the wilderness; there the calf shall feed, and there it shall lie down and consume its branches.

11 When its harvest is gathered, the women who formed it will be broken; for it is a people without understanding, therefore its Maker will not pity it, and its Former will show it no mercy.

12 And it shall come to pass in that day, the LORD will strike from the channel of the river to the brook of Egypt, and you will be gathered one by one, O children of Israel.

13 And it shall come to pass in that day, a great trumpet shall be blown, and the lost ones in the land of Assyria and the outcasts in the land of Egypt shall come, and they shall worship the LORD on the holy mountain in Jerusalem.


## Chapter 28

1 Woe to the crown of pride, the drunkards of Ephraim, and to the fading flower, the beauty of his glorious adornment, which is on the head of the fertile valley, those overcome by wine.

2 Behold, strong and mighty for (my) Lord, like a storm of hailstones, a storm of destruction, like a storm of mighty waters flooding, he has laid waste the land by his power.

3 The crown of pride, the drunkards of Ephraim, shall be trampled underfoot.

4 And the fading flower of its beauty, which is on the head of the fat valley, will be like the first-ripe fig before summer, which one sees, and while it is still in his hand, he swallows it.

5 In that day the LORD of hosts shall be a crown of beauty and a diadem of glory to the remnant of his people.

6 And for the spirit of justice, for him who sits on the judgment, and for strength, turning back the warriors from the gate.

7 These also erred through wine and through strong drink wandered; priest and prophet wandered through strong drink, from wine they were swallowed, from strong drink they wandered, they erred in vision, judgment they missed.

8 For all tables are full of vomit and filth, without any place.

9 Whom does he teach knowledge, and whom does he make to understand the report? Those weaned from milk, cut off from breast.

10 For precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, little upon little, little upon little.

11 For with stammering lip and another language he will speak to this people.

12 which he said to them, "This is the resting place for the weary, and this is the refreshing," but they were unwilling to listen.

13 And it shall be to them the word of the LORD: commandment upon commandment, commandment upon commandment, line upon line, line upon line, little upon little, little upon little, so that they go forward and stumble backward and are broken and snared and captured.

14 Therefore, hear the word of the LORD, O men of scorn, rulers of this people who are in Jerusalem.

15 Because you have said, "We have made a covenant with death, and with the grave we have made an agreement; when the scourge passes through, it will not come upon us, for we have made lying our refuge, and in falsehood we have hidden ourselves."

16 Therefore thus says the LORD God: Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone, a tried stone, a precious corner foundation; the one who believes will not hurry.

17 I will make justice the line and righteousness the plummet; hail will sweep away the refuge of lies, and waters will overwhelm the hiding place.

18 And your confederacy with death shall be annulled, and your agreement with Sheol shall not stand; when the scourge passes through, you shall become a thing trodden under foot.

19 According to his passing he will take you, for in the morning, morning, he will pass in the day and in the night; and it will be, only trouble understanding news.

20 For short is the bed from stretching out, and the covering is narrow when gathering in.

21 For the LORD will rise like Mount Perazim, He will rage like the valley of Gibeon, to do His work, strange is His work, and to perform His labor, foreign is His labor.

22 And now do not scoff, lest your bonds strengthen, for an utter end has been decreed; I have heard from the LORD God of hosts concerning the whole land.

23 Hearken and hear my voice; give ear and hear my word.

24 Is the plowman to plow all day long to sow, continually preparing and breaking his ground?

25 If he has not set his face, and scattered fitches, and cast cummin, and appointed wheat as principal, and barley as measured, and fitches as its border.

26 and his God will instruct him in judgment.

27 For fitches are not threshed with a cart wheel, nor is a wagon wheel rolled over cummin; for fitches are beaten with a rod, and cummin with a staff.

28 Bread is crushed, for it is not forever threshed; the wheel of his cart crushes, but his horsemen do not crush it.

29 Even this has gone forth from the LORD of hosts: he has purposed counsel, he has magnified wisdom.


## Chapter 29

1 Woe, Ariel, Ariel, the city of David's feasting: year after year, let the (solemn) feasts come round.

2 I will bring distress upon Ariel; she shall be heavy with mourning and lamentation, and to me she will be like Ariel.

3 And I will turn a ball upon you, and I will set a mount upon you, and I will establish fortifications against you.

4 You will speak low from the ground, and from the dust your speech will whisper; your voice will be like a spirit speaking from the earth, and from the dust your word will chirp.

5 And it shall be as fine dust the abundance of your foreigners, and as chaff that passes the abundance of the ruthless; and it shall be suddenly, at an instant.

6 From the LORD of hosts you shall be visited with thunder and earthquake and loud noise, storm and tempest, and the flame of devouring fire.

7 And it shall be as a dream vision of night: the abundance of all the nations that war against Ariel, and all their bulwarks and the one who oppresses her.

8 And it shall be as when the hungry man dreams, and behold, he is eating, but when he awakes, his soul is empty; and as when the thirsty man dreams, and behold, he is drinking, but when he awakes, he is still faint, and his soul is craving: so shall be the abundance of all the nations that fight against the mountain of Zion.

9 Be stunned and amazed, be astonished and bewildered; they stagger but not from wine, they reel but not from strong drink.

10 For the LORD has poured out upon you a spirit of deep sleep, and has closed your eyes; he has covered the prophets and your rulers, the seers.

11 Your vision of all this shall be as the words of a sealed book, which when they give to one who knows [how] to read, saying, "Read this, please," he says, "I cannot, for it is sealed."

12 And the scroll shall be given to him who has not learned the scroll, saying, "Read now this," and he shall say, "I have not learned the scroll."

13 (my) Lord said, "Because this people draw near with their mouth and their lips, but their heart is far from me, and their fear of me is a commandment taught by men,"

14 Therefore, behold, I will again do a marvellous thing with this folk, a marvellous and wonderful thing; and the wisdom of their wise shall perish, and the understanding of their skilful shall be hidden.

15 Woe to those who deeply hide counsel from the LORD, and their deeds are in darkness, and they say, "Who sees us, and who knows us?"

16 How can you turn things upside down as though the clay could be considered like the potter, or the thing made say to its maker, "He did not make me," or the framed thing say to its framer, "He did not understand"?

17 Is it not yet a very small thing, and he will return to Lebanon, the fruitful field; and the fruitful field will be reckoned to the forest.

18 And in that age the deaf shall hear the words of a scroll, and out of darkness and out of obscurity the eyes of the blind shall see.

19 And the meek shall increase their joy in the LORD, and the poor among men shall rejoice in the Holy One of Israel.

20 For the oppressor is no more, and the scoffer has ceased, and all those who watch for iniquity are cut off.

21 Those who make a man sin by a word, and lay a snare for the one who reproves in the gate, and turn aside the righteous with emptiness.

22 Therefore, thus says the LORD to the house of Jacob, whom he redeemed, even Abraham: not now shall Jacob be ashamed, neither now shall his face be pale.

23 When they see my children, the work of my hands, in their midst, they will sanctify my name; they will sanctify the Holy One of Jacob and revere the God of Israel.

24 And those who err in spirit shall gain understanding, and the contentious shall learn fair speech.


## Chapter 30

1 Woe to rebellious sons, says the LORD, to make counsel but not from me, and to pour out a molten image but not my spirit, in order to heap sin upon sin.

2 Those who go down to Egypt without asking my mouth, to take refuge in the strength of Pharaoh and to trust in the shadow of Egypt.

3 And it will be to you a stronghold; a force in which Pharaoh is put to shame, and trust in the shade of Egypt for reproach.

4 For his captains were in Zoan, and his messengers came to Hanes.

5 All the people will not profit them, neither for help nor for benefit, but for shame and also for reproach.

6 A burden concerning the beasts of the south: through a land of trouble and anguish, from which come the lion and the lioness, the viper and the fiery serpent, they carry on the shoulders of donkeys their riches, and on the hunches of camels their treasures, to a people that will not profit.

7 Egypt is altogether vain and empty; therefore I have called this: proud strength has ceased.

8 Now come, write it on a board and inscribe it on a scroll, that it may be for a future day, a witness forever and ever.

9 For it is a rebellious folk, sons of falsehood, sons who refuse to hear the law of the LORD.

10 who say to the seers, "Do not see," and to the beholders, "Do not behold for us what is right; speak to us smooth things; behold deceit."

11 Turn away from the path, depart from the way, remove from before us the Holy One of Israel.

12 Therefore, thus says the Holy One of Israel: Because you have rejected this word and have trusted in oppression and deceit and have relied on them.

13 Therefore this iniquity will be to you like a breach ready to fall, bulging out in a high wall, whose breaking comes suddenly, in an instant.

14 And she shall be broken like the breaking of a potter's vessel, shattered without pity, so that among the bursting of it no pot-sherd is found to take fire from the hearth or to draw water from the cistern.

15 For thus says the Lord GOD, the Holy One of Israel: In returning and lighting down you shall be saved; in quietness and confidence shall be your strength, but you were not willing.

16 And you said, No, for we will flee upon horses; therefore you shall flee. And, We will ride upon swift steeds; therefore those who pursue you shall be swift.

17 One thousand will flee at the rebuke of one, at the rebuke of five you will flee, until you are left like a beacon on the top of a mountain and like a banner on a hill.

18 Therefore the LORD waits to show you mercy, and therefore he exalts himself to have compassion on you, for the LORD is a God of justice; blessed are all who wait for him.

19 For the folk in Zion will dwell in Jerusalem; you shall not weep—gracious will the LORD be to you at the sound of your cry, when He hears, He will answer you.

20 And the LORD will give you bread of affliction and water of oppression, but your teacher will no longer be hidden, and your eyes will see your teacher.

21 And your ear shall hear a word behind you, saying, "This is the way, walk in it," when you believe and when you turn aside.

22 You shall defile the covering of your carved images of silver, and the ornament of your molten image of gold; you shall cast them away like menstrual cloth; go, say to him!

23 And he will give rain for your seed, which you sow upon the land, and bread from the fruit of the land, and it will be fat and rich; your cattle will feed on that day in a large pasture.

24 And the oxen and the young asses that till the ground shall eat provender on the night of unleavened bread, which is winnowed with the shovel and the fan.

25 And it shall come to pass that upon every high mountain and every lifted-up hill, streams of water-courses shall flow, in the day of great slaughter, when towers fall.

26 And the light of the moon will be as the light of the sun, and the light of the sun will be sevenfold, as the light of seven days, on the day when the LORD binds up the affliction of his people and heals the stroke of its wound.

27 Behold, the name of the LORD comes from afar, his anger burning and a heavy burden; his lips are full of indignation, and his tongue is like a devouring fire.

28 And his spirit is like a flooding river reaching to the neck, for shaking the nations with the sieve of falsehood, and a misleading bridle upon the jaws of the peoples.

29 The song will be for you as the night of the solemn feast, and joy of heart as when one goes with a pipe to come to the mountain of the LORD, to the Rock of Israel.

30 And the LORD will make the sound of his glory heard, and the resting of his arm he will cause to be seen, in burning indignation and a devouring flame, a sweeping storm, and hailstones.

31 Because of the voice of the LORD Assyria is shattered, by the rod he strikes.

32 And it shall come to pass, every passage of a tribe grounded, upon which the LORD rests, with tabrets and with harps, and in battles of waving, is fought.

33 For Tophet, prepared heretofore, even for the king it is made ready; deep and wide is its fire-pile, with much wood and fire; the breath of the LORD, like a stream of brimstone, kindles it.


## Chapter 31

1 Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, who rely on horses, who trust in chariots because they are many, and in horsemen because they are very strong, yet do not look to the Holy One of Israel, nor seek the LORD.

2 And he also is wise and brings disaster; he does not revoke his words. He rises against the house of those who do evil, and against the help of those who work iniquity.

3 Egypt is man and not God, and their horses are flesh and not spirit; the LORD will stretch out his hand, and the helper will stumble and the helped will fall, and together all of them will be consumed.

4 For thus says the LORD to me: As a young lion and a lion roar over its prey, when a multitude of shepherds is called against it, he does not fear their voice nor is dismayed by their tumult, so shall the LORD of hosts come down to fight upon Mount Zion and upon its little hill.

5 Like birds fluttering, so will the LORD of hosts protect Jerusalem; he will protect and deliver, pass over and preserve.

6 Return to the one from whom the sons of Israel have deeply revolted.

7 For on that day a man will reject the idols of his silver and the idols of his gold, the works of your hands which are his sin.

8 And Assyria shall fall by a sword not of man, and a sword not of mortal shall devour him; and from the sword he shall flee, and his chosen young men shall become tribute.

9 And their (ragged) rock from fear shall pass, and their captains from ensign shall flee, saith the LORD, who hath kindled fire for him in Zion and furnace for him in Jerusalem.


## Chapter 32

1 Even a king shall reign for justice, and rulers shall rule for judgment.

2 And a man shall be as a hiding place from the wind, and a covert from the tempest, as rivers of water in a dry place, as the shade of a great rock in a weary land.

3 And the eyes of those who see will not be able to see, and the ears of those who hear will not be able to hear.

4 And the faint hearted shall bethink themselves to understand, and the tongue of stammerers shall hasten to speak clearly.

5 No longer shall Nabal be called liberal, nor churl said to be bountiful.

6 For he speaks folly and his heart devises iniquity, to practice hypocrisy and to speak error against the LORD, to empty the soul of the hungry and to withhold drink from the thirsty.

7 And his instruments are instruments of evil: he deviseth heinous crimes, to compass with lying words and to speak wrongfully against the poor in judgment.

8 And a noble will devise noble things, and by noble things he will stand.

9 You women at ease, hear my voice; you daughters confident, give ear to my word.

10 In little more than a year you shall tremble, you complacent women; for the vintage shall fail, and the gathering shall not come.

11 Tremble, O complacent ones; quake, O confident ones; strip and make bare, and gird the loins.

12 They shall beat their breasts for the pleasant fields, for the fruitful vine.

13 Over the land of my people thorns and briers will come up, for upon every house of mirth the city will be in ruins.

14 For the castle is forsaken, the multitude of the city is abandoned; the tower and the fortress shall become caves forever, a joy of wild asses, a pasture of flocks.

15 Until the Spirit from on high is poured upon us, and the wilderness becomes a fruitful field, and the fruitful field is counted as a forest.

16 And justice will dwell in the wilderness, and righteousness will abide in the fruitful field.

17 And the act of justice will be peace, and the service of justice will be quietness and confidence forever.

18 And my people shall dwell in a dwelling of peace, and in habitations of trust, and in resting places of quiet.

19 And hail fell with the forest, and in the low place the city was brought low.

20 Blessed are you who sow beside all waters, sending forth the foot of the ox and the ass.


## Chapter 33

1 Woe to the one who plunders though no one has plundered him, and the one who betrays though no one has betrayed him; when you finish plundering, you will be plundered; when you complete your betrayals, they will betray you.

2 O LORD, grant us grace; for to you we have looked: be the strength to us in the morning, even our deliverance in time of trouble.

3 At the sound of the tumult nations are driven away; at your self-exaltation peoples are shattered.

4 And your prey will be gathered, a gathering of the caterpillar, like the running to and fro of locusts swarming.

5 The LORD is exalted, for He dwells on high; He has filled Zion with judgment and justice.

6 And faith of your times shall be a treasure of deliverances, wisdom and knowledge; the fear of the LORD is his treasure.

7 Behold, the valiant ones cry out abroad; the messengers of peace bitterly weep.

8 The highways are forsaken, the traveller has ceased from the path; covenant is broken, cities are rejected, no man is regarded.

9 The land mourns and languishes; Lebanon is ashamed and withers; Sharon has become like a wilderness, and Bashan and Carmel shake off their leaves.

10 Now I will rise, says the LORD; now I will be exalted; now I will be lifted up.

11 You conceive chaff, you bring forth stubble; your breath is a fire that shall consume you.

12 And the peoples shall be as burnings of lime, as thorns cut down that are burned in the fire.

13 Hear, you who are far, what I have done; and you who are near, know my might.

14 The sinners in Zion are afraid; trembling has seized the godless: who of us can dwell with the devouring fire? who of us can dwell with the eternal burning?

15 He who walks in justice and speaks what is right, who rejects dishonest gain, waves his hands from taking bribes, stops his ears from hearing bloodshed, and shuts his eyes from seeing evil.

16 He dwells in the highest places, his stronghold is the rocky fortresses; his food is provided, his waters are sure.

17 Your eyes shall behold the king in his beauty; they shall see a land very far off.

18 Your heart will ponder dread: Where is the scribe, where is the weigher, where is the one who counts the towers?

19 You will not see the proud folk; a people of deep speech, beyond hearing, scorned of speech, without understanding.

20 Behold Zion, the city of our appointed assembly; your eyes shall see Jerusalem, a comely habitation, a tent that shall not be taken down, nor ever be moved; its stakes shall remain forever, and none of its cords shall be broken.

21 But there the LORD will be mighty for us, a place of broad rivers and streams, where no galley with oars will go, nor any mighty ship pass through it.

22 For the LORD is our judge, the LORD is our lawgiver, the LORD is our king; he will save us.

23 Your tackle hangs loose; they cannot strengthen the base of the mast, nor spread the sail. At that time abundant spoil is divided; the lame take the prey.

24 Yet let not the dweller say, "I am sick"; the people dwelling in it bear their iniquity.


## Chapter 34

1 Approach, O Gentiles, and hear; and you peoples, give heed: let the earth and all that is in it hear, the world and every offspring thereof.

2 For the LORD's wrath is against all the nations, and his indignation against all their hosts; he has devoted them to destruction, he has given them over to slaughter.

3 And their slain shall be cast out, and their corpses shall give off a stink, and the mountains shall melt with their blood.

4 And all the host of the heavens shall dissolve, and the scroll of the heavens shall be rolled up, and all their host shall wither like a leaf from the vine and like a fig from the fig tree.

5 For my sword has drunk its fill in the heavens; behold, it will descend upon Edom and upon the people devoted to me for judgment.

6 The sword of the LORD is full of blood, it is covered with fat, with the blood of lambs and goats, with the fat of the kidneys of rams; for the LORD has a sacrifice in Bozrah, and a great slaughter in the land of Edom.

7 And unicorns shall go down with them, young bulls with mighty bulls; and their land shall be filled with blood, and their dust trodden with fat.

8 For a day of vengeance belongs to the LORD, a year of recompense for the controversy of Zion.

9 And her streams will be turned to pitch, and her dust to brimstone, and her land will become burning pitch.

10 Night and day it shall not be quenched; its smoke shall ascend alway; from generation to generation it shall be desolate, alway alway, no one passing through it.

11 And the cormorant and bittern shall possess it, and the (great) owl and raven shall dwell in it; and He will stretch over it the line of confusion and the stones of emptiness.

12 Her nobles—there is no royal one to call [them]; and all her princes will be nothing.

13 Her palaces shall become thorn-pots, nettle and bramble in her fortresses; her dwelling shall be a haunt of jackals, a courtyard for ostriches.

14 And the wild beasts of the desert shall meet with the wild beasts of the islands, and the satyr shall call to his fellow; indeed, there the screech owl shall settle, and shall find for her a place of rest.

15 There the great owl nested and laid eggs and hatched and brooded in its shadow; only there the vultures gathered, each with her mate.

16 Seek from the scroll of the LORD and read: not one of these has been missing; a woman has not visited her mate. For his mouth commanded, and his spirit gathered them.

17 And he cast a lot for them, and his hand divided it by line; alway they shall inherit it, from generation to generation they shall dwell therein.


## Chapter 35

1 Let the desert and the barren land rejoice, and let the wilderness exult and bloom like a rose.

2 Bloom and rejoice, indeed exult with joy and shout; the glory of Lebanon is given to her, the beauty of Carmel and Lasharon; they will see the glorious splendour of the LORD, the majestic beauty of our God.

3 Strengthen the hands that are feeble, and the knees that stumble, strengthen.

4 Say to those of faint heart, "Be strong, do not fear; behold, your God will come with vengeance, even God with recompense; he will come and save you."

5 Then the eyes of the blind will be opened, and the ears of the deaf will be unstopped.

6 Then the lame shall leap like a hart, and the tongue of the mute shall sing; for waters break forth in the wilderness, and streams in the desert.

7 And the parched ground shall become a pool, and the thirsty land springs of water; in the dwelling place of jackals, where they lay, there shall be grass with reeds and rushes.

8 And there will be a highway and a way, and the way of holiness will be called for it; the unclean will not pass along it, but it will be for them—a way— and fools will not wander.

9 There will be no young lion there, nor will a destroyer of wild beasts go up on it; it will not be found there, and the redeemed will walk.

10 And the redeemed of the LORD will return and come to Zion with rejoicing, and everlasting joy upon their heads; they will obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing will flee away.


## Chapter 36

1 In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and captured them.

2 The king of Assyria sent Rabshakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah at Jerusalem with a great army, and he stood by the conduit of the upper pool on the causeway of the fuller's field.

3 And Eliakim son of Hillkiah, who was over the house, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph the recorder, went out to him.

4 Then Rabshakeh said to them, "Say now to Hezekiah, 'Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria, "What is this confidence in which you have trusted?"'"

5 I said, "Only this: advice and strength for war are the words of my lips. Now on whom have you relied that you have rebelled against me?"

6 Behold, you have relied on the staff of this broken reed, on Egypt, which if a man leans on it, it will go into his hand and pierce him; so is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who rely on him.

7 But if you say to me, "We have trusted in the LORD our God," is it not He whose high places and altars Hezekiah has removed, saying to Judah and to Jerusalem, "Before this altar you shall worship"?

8 And now, please intercede, my lord the king of Assyria, and I will give you two thousand horses, if you are able to provide riders for them.

9 How can you restore the face of one governor, your servant, among the least of my lord’s servants, and have trusted in Egypt for chariots and horsemen?

10 And now, must I have come up against the land to destroy it without the LORD? The LORD said to me, Go up against this land and destroy it.

11 Eliakim and Shebna and Joah said to the Rabshakeh, “Speak, please, to your servants in the Syrian language, for we understand; but do not speak to us in the Jews' language in the hearing of the people who are on the wall.”

12 And Rabshakeh said, "Did my lord send me to your lord and to you to speak these words? Is it not to the men sitting on the wall, to eat their dung and to drink their piss with you?"

13 Then Rabshakeh stood and called aloud in the Jews' language, saying, "Hear the words of the great king, the king of Assyria."

14 Thus says the king: Do not let Hezekiah give you confidence, for he will not be able to deliver you.

15 Do not let Hezekiah deceive you by the LORD, saying, "The LORD will surely deliver us; this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria."

16 Do not listen to Hezekiah, for thus says the king of Assyria: Make peace with me and come out to me, and each man will eat his vine and his fig tree, and each man will drink water from his cistern.

17 Until I bring you to a land like your own land, a land of wheat and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.

18 Beware lest Hezekiah persuade you, saying, The LORD will deliver us; have the gods of the nations delivered each his land from the hand of the king of Assyria?

19 Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim, that they have delivered Samaria out of my hand?

20 Who among all the gods of the lands has delivered his land from my hand, that the LORD should deliver Jerusalem from my hand?

21 They remained silent and did not answer him any word, because the king's commandment was, "Do not answer him."

22 Eliakim son of Hillkiah, who was over the house, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph the recorder, came to Hezekiah with torn garments and told him the words of Rabshakeh.


## Chapter 37

1 When King Hezekiah heard it, he tore his garments, put on sackcloth, and went into the house of the LORD.

2 He sent Eliakim, who was over the palace, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, wearing sackcloth, to Isaiah son of Amoz the prophet.

3 They said to him, Thus says Hezekiah, A day of distress, rebuke, and blasphemy is this day, for the children have come to the birthplace but there is no strength to give birth.

4 Perhaps the LORD your God has heard the words of the Rabshakeh, whom his master the king of Assyria sent to taunt the living God, and will rebuke him for the words which the LORD your God has heard; therefore lift up a prayer for the remnant that is left.

5 The servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah.

6 Isaiah said to them, Thus you shall say to your lord, Thus says the LORD, Do not fear because of the words that you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.

7 I am giving into him air, and he will hear a report and return to his land, and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his land.

8 Rabshakeh returned and found King Asshur fighting against Libnah, for he had heard that he had departed from Lachish.

9 And Tirhakah king of Cush heard, saying, He has gone out to fight against you; and he heard and sent ambassadors to Hezekiah, saying:

10 Thus shall you say to Hezekiah king of Judah: Do not let your God in whom you trust deceive you by saying, Jerusalem will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.

11 Behold, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, to devote them to destruction; and will you be delivered?

12 Will the gods of the nations, who destroyed my fathers, deliver them from Gozan and from Haran and from Rezeph and from the sons of Eden who are in Telassar?

13 Where is the king of Hamath and the king of Arpad and the king of the city Sepharvaim? Where are Hena and Ivah?

14 Hezekiah took the letters from the hand of the messengers and read them, then went up to the house of the LORD and spread them out before the LORD.

15 Hezekiah prayed to the LORD, saying:

16 The LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, who is enthroned above the cherubim, you alone are God of all the kingdoms of the earth; you made the heavens and the earth.

17 Incline your ear, O LORD, and hear; open your eyes, O LORD, and see; and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he sent to taunt the living God.

18 Indeed, the LORD, the kings of Assyria have destroyed all the lands and their land.

19 And they shall put their gods into the fire, for they are not gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone, and they shall destroy them.

20 And now, O LORD our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you, the LORD, are alone.

21 Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, Thus says the LORD, God of Israel, to whom you have prayed concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria:

22 This is the act that the LORD has spoken concerning him: She has despised you, she has mocked you, the virgin daughter of Zion; behind you she has shaken her head, daughter of Jerusalem.

23 Whom have you reviled and blasphemed, and against whom have you raised your voice, and lifted up your eyes in haughtiness toward the Holy One of Israel?

24 By the hand of your servants you have taunted the LORD, and said, “By the abundance of my chariots I will go up to the height of the mountains, to the sides of Lebanon; I will cut down the height of its cedars, the choice of its firs; I will enter the farthest height of its border, the forest of its fruitful field.”

25 I have called and drunk water, and I will waste with the sole of my feet every river of fortified places.

26 Have you not heard that from afar I planned it long ago, and formed it in ancient times? Now I have brought it to pass, that you may lay waste fortified cities with crashing waves.

27 And her inhabitants, short of hand, are weak and ashamed; they are grass of the field and green tender herb, the grass on the housetops, before the standing corn.

28 I have known your lying down and your rising up, and your going out and your coming in, and your agitation toward me.

29 Because you have raged against me and your arrogance has come up to my ear, I will put my hook in your nose and my bridle on your lips, and I will turn you back by the way by which you came.

30 And this shall be the mark to you: in this year you shall eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs of the same; but in the third year you shall sow and reap and plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.

31 And the remnant of the house of Judah that is left shall take root downward and bear fruit upward.

32 For the remnant shall go out from Jerusalem, and the escaped from Mount Zion; the zeal of the LORD of hosts shall do this.

33 Therefore, thus says the LORD concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come against it with shield, nor cast up a bank against it.

34 He shall return by the way that he came, and to this city he shall not come, says the LORD.

35 And I will defend this city to deliver it, for my sake and for the sake of David my servant.

36 And an ambassador of the LORD went out and struck in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and eighty-five thousand, and they rose up in the morning and behold all of them were dead bodies.

37 And he departed and went and returned, Sennacherib king of Assyria, and he dwelt in Nineveh.

38 He was worshiping in the court of Nisroch his god, and Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons struck him with the sword, and they escaped to the land of Ararat, and Esar-haddon his son reigned in his place.


## Chapter 38

1 In those days Hezekiah became mortally ill, and Isaiah son of Amoz the prophet came to him and said to him, “Thus says the LORD, ‘Set your house in order, for you shall die and not live.’”

2 Hezekiah turned his face toward the wall and prayed to the LORD.

3 Then Hezekiah said, "Now, O Jehovah, remember, I beseech thee, how I have walked before thee in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight." And Hezekiah wept with great weeping.

4 The act of the LORD came to Isaiah, saying:

5 Go and say to Hezekiah, Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; behold, I will add to your days fifteen years.

6 and from the hand of the king of Assyria I will deliver you and this city, and I will defend this city.

7 And this shall be the sign to you from the LORD, that the LORD will do this thing which he has spoken.

8 I am turning back the shadow on the stairway of Ahaz by ten steps, by which the sun has gone down; so the sun returned ten steps on the stairway by which it had gone down.

9 A writing of Hezekiah king of Judah when he was sick and lived from his disease.

10 I said in the cutting off of my life, I shall go to the gates of the grave; I was allotted the rest of my years.

11 I said, I shall not see Jah, the Lord, most vehement, in the land of the living; I shall not behold another person again with the inhabitants of the world.

12 My age departs and vanishes from me like a shepherd's tent; I am cut off like a weaver cutting my life from the warp; from day until night you make an end of me.

13 I have been like a young lion until morning; so he breaks all my bones; from day until night he makes an end of me.

14 Like a crane, like a swallow, so do I chatter; I moan like a dove; my eyes fail toward the height; O Lord, oppressed—plead my cause.

15 What can I say and speak to him, and he has done it? I will lament all my years because of the bitterness of my soul.

16 (my) Lord upon them shall live, and in all of them the life of my spirit; and revive me and quicken me.

17 Behold, for peace I had great bitterness, and you have lovingly delivered my soul from the pit of destruction, for you have cast behind your back all my sins.

18 For the grave does not thank you, death does not praise you; those who go down to the pit do not wait for your truth.

19 He who lives will praise you as I do this day; a father will make known to his children your faithfulness.

20 The LORD will save me, and our songs we will play on stringed instruments all the days of our life upon the house of the LORD.

21 Isaiah said, "Take a cake of figs and spread it on the boil, and he will live."

22 Hezekiah said, "What is the sign that I will go up to the temple of the LORD?"


## Chapter 39

1 At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan king of Babylon sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, for he had heard that he was ill and had recovered.

2 Hezekiah rejoiced over them and showed them the house of his silver and the house of his gold and the house of his spices and the house of the good oil and all the house of his vessels and all that was found in his storehouses; there was not a thing which Hezekiah did not show them in his house and in all his dominion.

3 Isaiah the prophet came to king Hezekiah, and said to him, What did these men say, and from where did they come to you? And Hezekiah said, They came to me from a distant land, from Babylon.

4 And he said, What have they seen in your house? And Hezekiah said, All that is in my house have they seen; there was no thing which I did not show them in my treasuries.

5 Isaiah said to Hezekiah, "Hear the word of the LORD of hosts:"

6 Behold, days are coming when all that is in your house and all that your fathers have stored up until this day will be carried to Babylon; not a thing will be left, says the LORD.

7 And of your children who will come forth from you, whom you will father, they will be taken and become eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

8 Hezekiah said to Isaiah, "Good is the word of the LORD that you have spoken," and he said, "For there shall be peace and truth in my days."


## Chapter 40

1 Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.

2 Speak to the heart of Jerusalem, and comfort her, and call to her that her appointed time of service is fulfilled, that her punishment for iniquity is accepted, that she has received from the hand of the LORD double for all her sins.

3 A voice cries out in the wilderness, "Prepare the way of the LORD, make straight in the desert a highway for our God."

4 Every valley shall be raised up, and every hill and mountain shall be made low; the crooked shall become straight, and the rough places a plain.

5 And the glory of the LORD will be revealed, and all flesh together will see, for the mouth of the LORD has spoken.

6 A voice says, "Proclaim!" and I say, "What shall I proclaim?" All flesh is grass, and all its favour is like the flower of the field.

7 The grass withers, the flower fades, because the breath of the LORD blows upon it; surely the people are grass.

8 The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God stands forever.

9 Upon a high hill go up, O herald of Zion; lift up your voice with strength, O herald of Jerusalem; lift it up, do not fear; say to the cities of Judah, "Behold, your God!"

10 Behold, the LORD God comes with strength, and His arm rules for Him; behold, His reward is with Him, and His work before Him.

11 Like a shepherd tending his flock, he will tend; with his arm he will gather the lambs, and in his bosom he will carry them; he will lead the nursing ones.

12 Who measured the water in the hollow of his hand and established the heavens with a span, and gauged all the dust of the earth in a great measure, and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in balances?

13 Who has directed the spirit of the LORD, or who has been his counselor to instruct him?

14 Whom did he consult, and who made him understand? Who taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge and the way of understanding?

15 Behold, the Gentiles are as a drop in the bucket, and as small dust on the balances are accounted; behold, the isles are as a mere particle.

16 And Lebanon is not sufficient for burning, nor its living creatures sufficient for burnt offering.

17 All the nations are as nothing before him, counted as less than nothing and emptiness.

18 And to whom will you liken God, and what similitude will you compare to him?

19 The carved image, a craftsman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold, and a silversmith forges silver chains.

20 The skilled craftsman selects a gift of wood that will not rot, seeking skillfully to prepare a carved image that will not fall.

21 Will you not know? Will you not hear? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood the foundations of the earth?

22 The One sitting above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants like locusts; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them out like a tent to dwell in.

23 who appoints nobodies as nobles and makes the judges of the earth a thing of nought

24 Neither were they planted, nor were they sown, nor did their stock take root in the earth; and He also blew on them, and they dried up, and a storm carried them away like stubble.

25 And to whom will you compare me, that I should be equal? says the Holy One.

26 Lift up your eyes high and see who created these, the one who brings out their host by number; he calls them all by name, because of the abundance of strength and mighty power, not one is missing.

27 Why do you say, Jacob, and speak, Israel, "My way is hidden from the LORD, and from my God my judgment passes away"?

28 Have you not known? Have you not heard? The LORD, God of the world, Maker of the farthest edges of the earth, does not grow faint nor weary; there is no searching of his understanding.

29 Giving ability to the weak, and to the one without strength, abundance increases.

30 And the young men will grow weary and faint, and the choice young men will stumble and fall.

31 But those who wait for the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles; they shall run and not be weary; they shall walk and not faint.


## Chapter 41

1 Be silent toward me, O islands and nations; let them renew their strength. Let them come near, then let them speak; together we will draw near to judgment.

2 Who raised up one from the east, whom justice follows at his feet? He gives nations before him and kings he subdues; he gives their sword as dust, his bow as driven stubble.

3 He pursues them and passes on safely, by a path his feet had not gone.

4 Who has wrought and done it, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the LORD, am first, and with the last I am He.

5 The islands saw and feared, the uttermost parts of the earth trembled; they drew near and came.

6 Each man shall help his fellow and say to his brother, "Be strong."

7 The craftsman strengthens the goldsmith, and the one who smooths with the hammer strengthens the one who strikes the anvil; he says of the soldering, It is good, and fastens it with nails so that it will not move.

8 But you, Israel, are my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, seed of Abraham my beloved.

9 Whom I have held from the uttermost coast of the land, and from its nobles I have called you, and I said to you, You are My servant, I have chosen you, and not rejected you.

10 Do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God; I have strengthened you, indeed I have helped you, indeed I uphold you with my right hand of justice.

11 Indeed, they shall be ashamed and also confounded, all those who rage against you; they shall be as nothing and perish, the men of your contention.

12 You will seek them but not find them, your men of contention will be as nothing and your men of war as if they did not exist.

13 For I am the LORD your God, holding your right hand, who says to you, “Do not fear, I have helped you.”

14 Do not fear, worm of Jacob, few of Israel; I have helped you, says the LORD, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.

15 Behold, I have made you a sharp threshing instrument, master of two-edged teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and tread down the hills; you shall turn the mountains into chaff.

16 You will drive them away and a blast will carry them off, and a storm will scatter them; but you will rejoice in the LORD, in the Holy One of Israel you will exult.

17 The poor and the needy seek water but there is none; their tongue fails from thirst; I the LORD will answer them, the God of Israel will not forsake them.

18 I will open rivers on high places and springs among valleys; I will turn the wilderness into a pool of water and the dry land into water springs.

19 I will place in the wilderness the cedar, the shittah, the myrtle, and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the fir, the pine, and the box together.

20 so that they may see and know, may consider and understand together, that the LORD has done this, and Israel's Holy One has created it.

21 Draw near your cause, says the LORD; bring forth your strong ones, says the King, Jacob.

22 Let them come near and declare to us what will happen: let them tell the former things, what they were, that we may consider them and know their outcome, or let them announce the things to come.

23 Declare the former things, that we may know that you are gods; indeed, do good or do evil, that we may be dismayed together.

24 Behold, you are from nothing, and your deeds are from nought; an abomination will choose you.

25 I have stirred up one from the north, and he has come; from the rising of the sun he shall call upon my name. He shall come upon rulers as upon mortar, and as the potter treads clay.

26 Who declared from the beginning and knew, and from times past and said, "Righteous"? Indeed, no one declares, no one proclaims, no one hears your words.

27 The first concerning Zion: “Behold, there they are!” And concerning Jerusalem, I will appoint a herald.

28 And I looked, but there was no man, and from these there was no counselor; I asked them, and they returned an answer.

29 Behold, they are all vanity, nothingness their works; their drink offerings are wind and waste.


## Chapter 42

1 Behold, my servant I will uphold; my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.

2 He will not cry out or raise his voice, nor make his voice heard abroad.

3 A bruised reed he will not break, and a smoldering wick he will not quench; he will faithfully bring forth justice.

4 He shall not fail nor be discouraged until he has established justice in the land, and the islands shall wait for his law.

5 Thus said God the LORD, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread the earth and its offspring, who gives breath to the people upon it and spirit to those who walk in it:

6 I am the LORD; I have called you in righteousness, I will take hold of your hand, I will keep you, and I will give you as a covenant to a people, as a light to the nations.

7 To open blind eyes, to bring out prisoners from prison, from the house of darkness those who sit in darkness.

8 I am the LORD, that is my name; and my glory I will not give to another, nor my praise to carved images.

9 Behold, the former things have come, and new things I declare; before they spring forth, I announce them to you.

10 Sing to the LORD a new song, his praise from the end of the earth, you who go down to the sea and all that fills it, isles and their inhabitants.

11 Let the desert and its towns lift up, the villages of Kedar dwell; let the inhabitants of the rock shout for joy, from the top of the mountains they shall triumph.

12 Ascribe glory to the LORD, and declare his praise in the islands.

13 The LORD goes forth like a mighty man; like a warrior he stirs up jealousy; he shouts, yes, he roars; he prevails over his enemies.

14 I have been silent from of old; I have been still, I have restrained myself; like a woman in labor I will groan, I will pant and together I will gasp.

15 I will make desolate the hill country and the little hills and all their grass; I will turn rivers into country and pools into dry ground.

16 And I will lead the blind along a way they did not know, in paths they did not know I will guide them; I will make darkness before them into light and crooked things into straightness—these words I have done, and I have not forsaken them.

17 They shall turn back, be ashamed, those who trust in carved images, who say to a molten image, "You are our gods."

18 The deaf have heard, and the blind have looked to see.

19 Who is blind but my servant, or deaf as the messenger I send? Who is blind as the impartial, and who is blind as the servant of the LORD?

20 You have seen many things, but you do not observe; your ears are open, but you do not hear.

21 The LORD delights in the justice of his cause; he will magnify law and make it glorious.

22 And they are a folk despised and trampled, driven from every hole, and in the prison houses they are hidden; they have become prey, and there is no deliverer; spoil, and no one says “Restore!”

23 Who among you will give heed to this, listen and pay attention hereafter?

24 Who gave Jacob for spoil and Israel to the plunderers? Was it not the LORD, against whom we sinned, and in whose ways they refused to walk, and whose law they would not obey?

25 He poured out on him the fury of his anger and the strength of battle, and it blazed all around him, but he did not know; it burned him, but he did not take to heart.


## Chapter 43

1 Now therefore thus says the LORD who created you, Jacob, and formed you, Israel: Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are Mine.

2 When you pass through the waters, I will be with you, and the rivers will not overflow you; when you walk in the fire, you will not be burned, and the flame will not consume you.

3 For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your savior; I give Egypt as your ransom, Cush and Seba in your stead.

4 Because you were precious in my sight, you were honored, and I loved you; therefore I will give people in place of you, and nations in place of your soul.

5 Do not fear, for I am with you; from the east I will bring your seed, and from the west I will gather you.

6 Say to the north, Give up, and to the south, Do not withhold; bring my sons from afar and my daughters from the end of the earth.

7 All who are called by my name, and for my glory I have created them, formed them, even made them.

8 Bring out the folk who are blind though they have eyes, and who are deaf though they have ears.

9 All the nations are gathered together and assembled as peoples; who among them declares this, and makes us hear former things? Let them bring their witnesses and justify themselves, so that we may hear and say, "It is true."

10 You are my witnesses, saith the LORD, and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me, and understand that I am he: before me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me.

11 I, I am the LORD, and there is no savior besides me.

12 I have declared and saved and made heard, and there is no foreigner among you; and you are my witnesses, saith the LORD, and I am God.

13 Even from of old I am He, and there is no one from My power who can deliver; I act, and who can reverse it?

14 Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I sent to Babylon and brought down all their nobles, and the Chaldeans, whose rejoicing is in their ships.

15 I am the LORD your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.

16 Thus says the LORD, who gives a way in the sea and a path in the mighty waters.

17 He who brings out chariot and horse, a mighty host with strength—they lie down together and will not rise; they are extinguished like flax.

18 Do not remember the former things, and do not consider the ancient things.

19 I am doing a new thing; now it springs forth; will you not know it? I will even make a way in the wilderness, rivers in the desert.

20 Let the wild beast of the field, the sea-monster and the daughters of the owl, honor me, for I have given water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen.

21 A folk I formed for Myself shall declare My praise.

22 But you have not called upon me, Jacob, because you have wearied of me, Israel.

23 You have not brought me your sheep for burnt offerings, nor honored me with your sacrifices; I have not burdened you with grain offerings, nor wearied you with frankincense.

24 You did not buy me with money, nor have you satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices; rather, you have burdened me with your sins, you have wearied me with your iniquities.

25 I, even I, am he who blots out your transgressions for my sake, and your sins I will not remember.

26 Remember me, that we may be judged together; answer me, that you may be justified.

27 Your first father sinned, and your intercessors rebelled against me.

28 And I will profane the captains of holiness, and I will give Jacob to destruction and Israel to reviling.


## Chapter 44

1 And now, hear, Jacob my servant, and Israel, whom I have chosen.

2 Thus says the LORD your Maker, who formed you from the womb: Fear not, My servant Jacob, and Jeshurun whom I have chosen.

3 For I will pour water on the thirsty, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour my spirit on your offspring, and my blessing on your descendants.

4 And they shall grow among the grass like willows beside the water-courses.

5 One will say to the LORD, I am yours; another will call on the name of Jacob; one will write on his hand, ‘To the LORD,’ and name himself by the name of Israel.

6 Thus says the Lord, the king of Israel and his redeemer, the Lord of hosts: I am the first and I am the last, and besides me there is no God.

7 And who is like Me? Let him declare and make it known to Me, since I appointed a people for all time; let them declare to them the signs and the things that are coming.

8 Do not fear and do not be afraid. Have I not long ago made you hear and declared it? And you are my witness. Is there a God besides me? And there is no rock; I know none.

9 Those who fashion carved images are altogether confusion, and their precious things are of no benefit; their witnesses see nothing and know nothing, so that they may be put to shame.

10 Who has fashioned a god or cast a carved image for no profit?

11 Behold, all his companions shall be ashamed, and the craftsmen are but men; they shall all be gathered together, they shall stand, they shall fear, they shall be ashamed together.

12 The artificer wrought iron with the ax head, labored with the coals, and shaped it with the hammer; he labored at it with the strength of his arm, yet grew hungry and had no strength, did not drink water and grew faint.

13 An artificer cuts wood, stretches out a line, marks it with a line, shapes it with a plane and with a compass, and makes it in the figure of a man, according to the beauty of a person, to dwell in a house.

14 To cut cedars for him, and he takes a cypress and an oak, and strengthens himself with forest timber, plants an ash, and the rain makes it grow.

15 And it came to pass that a man would take from the common sort for fuel, and he would warm himself; yea, he kindleth it, and baketh bread; yet he would make a god and worship it, fashioning a carved image and bowing down to it.

16 Half of it he burns in the fire—on half of it he eats flesh, roasts a roast, and is satisfied; he also warms himself and says, “Ah, I am warm, I have seen fire.”

17 And the rest of it he makes into a god, his carved image; he falls down to it, bows down, and prays to it, saying, Deliver me, for you are my god.

18 They do not know nor do they understand, for their eyes have been shut from seeing, and their hearts from understanding.

19 He does not consider, nor is there knowledge or discretion to say, "Half of it I burned in the fire; yes, I baked bread on its coals; I roasted flesh and ate. Shall I make the remainder into an abomination? Shall I bow down to a tree's stock?"

20 Feeding on ashes, a heart hardened turns away and does not save his life, nor says, Is not the lie in my right hand?

21 Remember these, Jacob, and Israel, for you are my bond-servant; I formed you, you are my bond-servant, Israel, you shall not be forgotten by me.

22 I have wiped away your transgressions like a thick cloud and your sins like a cloudy mist; return to me, for I have redeemed you.

23 Heaven and earth rejoice together, for the LORD has acted; let the mountains shout for joy, the forest and every tree in it, for the LORD has redeemed Jacob and shown his glory in Israel.

24 Thus says the LORD your Redeemer and your Creator from the womb: I am the LORD, doing everything, stretching out the heavens alone, spreading out the earth by myself.

25 He who frustrates the signs of liars and mocks diviners, who turns wise men backward and makes their knowledge foolish.

26 He confirms the promise of his servant and fulfills the counsel of his messengers, who said to Jerusalem, "You shall be inhabited," and to the cities of Judah, "You shall be rebuilt," and her desolate places I will raise up.

27 Who says to the deep, "Be dry," and your rivers I will dry up.

28 The one who says to Cyrus, "My shepherd," and all my delight he will fulfill, and to say to Jerusalem, "You will be rebuilt," and the temple will be established.


## Chapter 45

1 Thus says the LORD to his anointed, to Cyrus, whom I have strengthened in his right hand, to subdue nations before him and to loose the loins of kings; to open before him the two-leaved doors, so that the gates shall not be shut.

2 I will go before you and make the crooked places straight; I will break the bronze gates and cut the iron bars.

3 And I will give you the treasures of darkness and hidden riches in secret places, so that you may know that I, the LORD, am the one who calls you by name, the God of Israel.

4 For the sake of my servant Jacob and Israel my chosen, I have called you by name, I have surnamed you, and you did not know me.

5 I am the LORD, and there is no other; besides me there is no God; I will strengthen you, and you have not known me.

6 So that they may know from the rising of the sun and from the west that there is no one besides me: I am the LORD, and there is no other.

7 Forming light and creating darkness, making peace and creating evil: I am the LORD, doing all these.

8 Let the heavens above tremble and the skies drip justice; let the earth open and salvation sprout forth, and righteousness spring up together; I the LORD have created it.

9 Woe to him who strives with his Maker, a potsherd with the potsherds of earth; shall the clay say to its potter, "What are you making?" and "Your work has no hands"?

10 Woe to him who says to a father, "What have you begotten?" and to a woman, "With what do you labor?"

11 Thus says the LORD, the Holy One of Israel, and his Maker: You inquire of me concerning my children, and concerning the work of my hands you command me.

12 I made the earth and created humankind upon it; my hands stretched out the heavens, and I commanded all their host.

13 I am the one who raised him up in justice, and I will make straight all his ways; he will build my city and release my captives, not for price nor for bribe, says the LORD of hosts.
