14 Thus says the LORD: The labour of Egypt and the merchandise of Cush and the Sabaeans, men of stature, shall come over to you and shall be yours; after you they shall pass; in chains they shall come over; they shall fall down before you, they shall make supplication to you: Only in you is God, and there is no other, no El besides.

15 Indeed, you are a God who hides, O God of Israel, Savior.

16 Let them be ashamed and also confounded, all of them; together let them go in confusion, the artificers of idols.

17 Israel is saved by the LORD, the salvation of alway; you shall not be ashamed nor confounded to eternity.

18 For thus says the LORD, creator of the heavens—he is God, former of the earth and maker of it, he established it, not as a waste did he create it, for inhabiting did he form it— I am the LORD, and there is no other.

19 I did not speak in secret, in a place of earth's darkness; I did not say to Jacob's seed, Seek emptiness from me; I am the LORD, speaking justice, declaring things that are right.

20 Gather together and come; assemble yourselves, you fugitives of the nations; they do not know, those who carry their wooden carved image and pray to a god who cannot save.

21 Declare and bring forth; yes, let them take counsel together; who has made this heard from aforetime, declared it long ago? Is it not I, the LORD? And there is no other God besides me, a righteous God and a savior; there is none besides me.

22 Turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth, for I am God and there is no other.

23 By myself I have sworn; from my mouth has gone forth in righteousness a word that shall not return: for to me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.

24 Only in the LORD, he says, are righteousness and strength; to him they shall come, and all who are angry against him shall be ashamed.

25 In the LORD shall all the seed of Israel be justified and shall glorify.


## Chapter 46

1 Bel bows down, Nebo stoops; their idols are burdened upon the beast and the cattle: your burdens are carried away, a load for the weary.

2 They crouched, they fell together; they could not carry away the burden, and their soul went into captivity.

3 Hear me, O house of Jacob, and all the remnant of the house of Israel, you who are borne from the womb, who are carried from the belly.

4 Even to old age I am he, and even to gray hairs I will carry; I made, and I will bear, and I will carry, and I will save.

5 To whom will you liken me and make equal, and to whom will you compare me, that I should be the same?

6 They melt gold from the bag and weigh silver with the balance; they hire a goldsmith to make it into an idol, and they bow down and worship it.

7 They lift him upon a shoulder, they carry him and set him down in his place; he stands and does not move from his space; indeed, he cries out to him, but he does not answer, nor delivers him from his distress.

8 Remember this and be consoled; sinners, take it to heart.

9 Remember the former things of old, for I am God and there is no other; I am God and no one is like me.

10 Declaring the end from the beginning, and from aforetime things not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and all My purpose I will do.

11 Calling from the east a ravenous bird, from a far country a man of my purpose—even I have spoken, indeed I will bring it; I have purposed it, indeed I will do it.

12 Hear me, you strong of heart, far from justice.

13 I have brought near my justice; it is not far off, and my salvation will not tarry; I will set deliverance in Zion, my glory to Israel.


## Chapter 47

1 Come down and sit in the dust, virgin daughter of Babylon; sit on the ground, there is no throne, daughter of the Chaldeans, for you shall no longer be called tender and delicate.

2 Take a mill (stone) and grind flour, uncover your locks, bare the leg, uncover the thigh, cross the rivers.

3 Your nakedness shall be uncovered and your shame exposed; I will take vengeance and show no man pity.

4 Our Redeemer, the LORD of hosts is his name, the Holy One of Israel.

5 Sit silently, and go into darkness, daughter of the Chaldeans; you shall no longer be called lady of kingdoms.

6 I was angry with my people, I profaned my inheritance, and I gave them into your hand; you showed no compassion to them; you laid your yoke very heavily upon the old.

7 And you said, "I will be a lady forever," until you placed these things on your mind, you did not remember her latter end.

8 And now hear this, O delicate one who sits in assurance, who says in her heart, “I and nothing else; I shall never sit as a widow, nor know loss of children.”

9 In an instant, in one day, loss of children and widowhood will come upon you, in full measure, because of the abundance of your sorceries, because of the great strength of your enchantments.

10 You trusted in your adversity, saying, "No one sees me"; your wisdom and your knowledge have corrupted you, and you said in your heart, "I am, and there is none beside me."

11 And adversity will come upon you that you do not know its dawn, and mischief will fall upon you that you cannot ransom; and suddenly there will come upon you a desolation that you do not know.

12 Stand now with your enchantments and with the abundance of your sorceries, in which you have labored from your youth; perhaps you may be able to profit, perhaps you may prevail.

13 You are wearied by the abundance of your counsel; let the astrologers stand now and save you, those who gaze at the stars, who announce the months, from what is coming upon you.

14 Behold, they are like stubble; a burning has consumed them; fire shall not deliver their life from the hand of the flame; there is no burning coal for their bread, nor a fire to sit before.

15 Thus your lovers have become from your youth; each has wandered from his quarter; there is no one to save you.


## Chapter 48

1 Hear this, O house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel and have come forth from the waters of Judah; you who swear by the name of the LORD and make mention of the God of Israel, but not in truth and not in righteousness.

2 For from the city of holiness they are called, and upon the God of Israel they rely; the LORD of hosts is his name.

3 Long ago I declared the former things; out of my mouth they went forth, and I proclaimed them. Suddenly I acted, and they came to pass.

4 I know that you are stubborn, your neck a sinew of iron, and your brow brass.

5 And I declared to you from the beginning, before it came to pass I made you hear, lest you should say, My idol wrought them, and my graven image and my molten image commanded them.

6 Have you heard? See, all of it—will you not declare it? I announce new things to you from now on, things kept hidden that you did not know.

7 Now they are created, and not from of old, and before this day you have not heard, lest you say, "Behold, I knew."

8 You have not heard, you have not known, from that time your ear has not been opened, for I know that you will surely betray, a rebel from the womb is called for you.

9 For the sake of my renown I will prolong my anger, and for the sake of my praise I will refrain concerning you, so as not to destroy you.

10 Behold, I have refined you, but not with silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.

11 For my sake, for my sake I will do it, for how could I be profaned? And my glory I will not give to another.

12 Hear me, O Jacob, and Israel, my called one: I am he; I am the first, and I am also the last.

13 My hand founded the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens; when I call to them, they stand up together.

14 Gather together, all of you, and hear: who among them has declared these things? The LORD has loved him; he will do his pleasure in Babylon, and his arm [will be] against the Chaldeans.

15 I, even I, have spoken; indeed, I have called him; I have brought him, and I will make his way prosper.

16 Draw near to me; hear this: I have not spoken in secret from the beginning; from the time it was, there I am. And now the LORD God has sent me, and his spirit.

17 Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you for your benefit, who leads you in the way you should go.

18 If only you had heeded the commandments, your peace would have been like a river, and your justice like the waves of the sea.

19 And your seed shall be as the sand, and your offspring as the gravel; his name shall not be cut off nor destroyed from before me.

20 Go out from Babylon, flee from the Chaldeans, with a voice of rejoicing declare, proclaim it, bring it forth to the end of the earth; say, The LORD has redeemed his servant Jacob.

21 And they did not thirst in the desolate places; he led them; water from the rock he made to trickle for them; he split the rock and waters gushed forth.

22 There is no peace, says the LORD, to the wicked.


## Chapter 49

1 Hear me, islands, and you peoples from afar, the LORD called me from the womb, from my mother’s womb he named my name.

2 He set my mouth like a sharp sword, in the shadow of his hand he concealed me, and he made me a polished arrow in his quiver he hid me.

3 And he said to me, You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified.

4 But I said in vain I have labored, for a thing of nought and vanity my strength is spent; surely my judgment is with the LORD and my work is with my God.

5 And now, says the LORD, the one forming me from the womb to be his servant, to return Jacob to him, and that Israel should not be gathered, I shall be honored in the eyes of the LORD, and my God has become my strength.

6 He said, "It is too light that you should be My servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob and restore Israel; I will also make you a light to the nations, to be My salvation to the end of the earth."

7 Thus says the LORD, the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to the one despised in soul, to the abhorred nation, to the servant of rulers: kings shall see and rise up, princes shall prostrate themselves, for the sake of the LORD who is faithful, the Holy One of Israel who has chosen you.

8 Thus says the LORD: In a time of acceptance I answered you, and in a day of salvation I helped you; and I will preserve you, and give you for a covenant of the people, to raise up the land, to cause to inherit desolate inheritances.

9 saying to the prisoners go out, to those in darkness appear; along paths they will graze, and on every high place their pasture.

10 They will not grow hungry or thirsty, nor will the heat or sun strike them, for their compassionate one will guide them, and to springs of water he will lead them.

11 And I will make every hill a way, and my highways will be raised up.

12 Behold, these shall come from afar; and behold, these from the north and from the west; and these from the land of Sinim.

13 Rejoice, O heavens, and shout for joy, O earth; let the mountains burst into singing, for the LORD has comforted his people and will have compassion on his afflicted.

14 And Zion said, "Jehovah has forsaken me, and (my) Lord has forgotten me."

15 Can a woman forget her sucking child, or withhold compassion from the son of her womb? Even these may forget, but I will not forget you.

16 Behold, upon my palms I have engraved you; your walls are continually before me.

17 Your children hasten; those who destroy and lay you waste go out from you.

18 Look about you with your eyes and see, all of them have gathered, they have come to you; as I live, declares the LORD, all of them as ornaments you shall put on, and gird yourselves as a bride.

19 For your desolate places and your destruction and the land of your destruction—for now you will burst forth from the inhabitant, and those who swallowed you will be far away.

20 They will yet say in your hearing, 'Your children, who have survived after loss of others: "Tight is the place for me; make room for me, that I may dwell."'

21 And you shall say in your heart, “Who has borne me these, seeing I am childless and bereaved, banished and divorced? And these—where are they?” I am left alone; where are they?

22 Thus says (my) Lord God: Behold, I will raise my hand to the nations, and to the peoples I will lift up my banner; and they shall bring your sons in their arms, and your daughters shall be carried on the shoulder.

23 And kings shall be your foster fathers and their princesses your nursing mothers; they shall bow down to you with their faces to the ground and lick the dust of your feet; and you shall know that I am the LORD, those who wait for me shall not be put to shame.

24 Can booty be taken from a mighty man, or will the captive of a righteous man be delivered?

25 For thus says the LORD: Even the captive of the mighty shall be taken, and the prey of the tyrant shall escape; and I will plead your cause, and I will save your children.

26 I will make you eat your own flesh, and with their blood they will be drunk like new wine, and every body shall know that I, the LORD, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.


## Chapter 50

1 Thus says the LORD: Where is the bill of your mother's divorce that I gave her? Or to which of my creditors have I sold you? Indeed, you were sold because of your faults, and your mother was sent away because of your transgressions.

2 Why did I come and there was no man? I called and there was no one answering. Is my hand too short for redemption? Or is there no ability in me to deliver? Behold, by my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make rivers a wilderness; their fish stink from lack of water and die in thirst.

3 Clothe the heavens with blackness and make sackcloth their covering.

4 (my) Lord God has given me the tongue of those who are taught, to know how to sustain with a word him who is weary; he awakens me morning by morning, he awakens my ear to listen as those who are taught.

5 The Lord GOD hath opened mine ear and I did not rebel backward I did not go away

6 My body I gave to those who strike, and my jaw to those who pluck; my face I did not hide from reproach and spitting.

7 And my Lord. God. helped me, therefore I was not ashamed; therefore I set my face like flint, and I knew that I would not be put to shame.

8 My vindicator is near; who will contend with me? Let us confront one another. Who is my adversary? Let him approach me.

9 Behold, my Lord God will help me; who is he that shall condemn me? Behold, they all shall wear out like a garment; the moth shall eat them up.

10 Who among you fears the LORD, heeding the voice of his servant, who walks in darkness and has no light? Let him trust in the name of the LORD and rely on his God.

11 Behold, you are all kindlers of fire, girding yourselves with sparks; go by the light of your own fire, and by the sparks you have kindled—this from my hand shall be to you: you shall lie down in sorrow.


## Chapter 51

1 Hearken to me, you who pursue justice, you who seek the LORD: look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the quarry from which you were dug.

2 Look to Abraham your forefather and to Sarah who bore you, for I called him alone and blessed him and made him many.

3 For the LORD has comforted Zion; He has comforted all her desolate places, and has made her wilderness like Eden, and her desert like the garden of the LORD: gladness and joy shall be found in her, thanksgiving and the voice of melody.

4 Hearken to me, my people, and my nation, give ear to me; for law shall go forth from me, and my judgment shall be a light to nations.

5 My deliverance is near, my righteousness has gone forth; my arm will judge the peoples; the isles await me, and for my arm they wait.

6 Lift up your eyes to the heavens and look at the earth beneath: for the heavens have dissolved like smoke, and the earth wears out like a garment, and its inhabitants perish in like manner; but my deliverance will last forever, and my righteousness will not fail.

7 Hearken to me, you who know justice, my people in whose heart is my law; fear not the reproach of men, neither be dismayed at their revilings.

8 For like a garment the moth consumes them, and like wool the worm consumes them, but my justice will be forever, and my deliverance for generation to generation.

9 Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the LORD; awake as in the days of old, in generations of ancient times; is it not You who crushed Rahab, who pierced the dragon?

10 Is it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep, who made the depths of the sea a way for the redeemed to pass through?

11 And the redeemed of the LORD shall return and come to Zion with rejoicing, and joy everlasting upon their heads; they shall obtain gladness and joy; sorrow and sighing shall flee away.

12 I, even I, am he who comforts you; why should you fear a mere mortal who dies, or a human being who is like grass?

13 You have forgotten the LORD your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth; you feared continually all day long the wrath of the oppressor, as though he were preparing to destroy; and where is the wrath of the oppressor?

14 The captive exile hastens to be released, that he should not die in the pit, nor lack his bread.

15 I myself am the LORD your God who quiets the sea when its waves roar; the LORD of hosts is his name.

16 I have placed my counsel in your mouth, and with the shadow of my hand have I shielded you, to plant the heavens and to found the earth, and to say to Zion, "My people, you are."

17 Awake, awake, rise up, Jerusalem, you who have drunk from the hand of the LORD the cup of His anger; the dregs of the cup of astonishment you have drunk.

18 She has no leader among all the sons she bore, nor any to take her by the hand among all the sons she reared.

19 Two are calling you— who will pity you? Desolation, affliction, famine, and sword— who will comfort you?

20 Your children lie stunned, fallen at the head of every street like a wild bull in a net, full of the wrath of the LORD, the rebuke of your God.

21 Therefore, hear now this, O afflicted one, drunk, but not with wine.

22 Thus says your Lord, the LORD, and your God who pleads for His people: Behold, I have taken from your hand the cup of trembling, the goblet, the cup of My fury; you shall not drink it again.

23 I will place it in the power of your enemies who said to your soul, "Bow down and we will pass over," and you laid your body like the earth and as a street to those who pass by.


## Chapter 52

1 Awake, awake, put on your strength, Zion; put on the garments of your beauty, Jerusalem, city of the holy; for there shall no more come into you the uncircumcised and the unclean.

2 Shake yourself from the dust, rise up, O Jerusalem, loose the bonds from your neck, O daughter of Zion.

3 For thus says the LORD: Without money you were sold, and not with money will you be redeemed.

4 For thus says the LORD my Lord: My people went down at first to Egypt to sojourn there, and Asshur oppressed them without cause.

5 Now therefore, what have I here, says the LORD, that my people are taken away for nothing? Those who rule over them make them wail, says the LORD, and my name is continually despised all the day.

6 Therefore my people shall know my name, therefore in that day that it is I who speak—behold, here I am.

7 How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the messenger who proclaims peace, who brings good news, who proclaims salvation, who says to Zion, "Your God reigns!"

8 The voice of your watchmen is lifted up, they shout aloud together; for eye to eye they will see when the LORD returns to Zion.

9 Break forth, shout together, O decayed places of Jerusalem, for the LORD has comforted His people; He has redeemed Jerusalem.

10 The LORD has bared his holy arm before the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

11 Turn away, turn away, depart from there, unclean! Do not touch; depart from among her, purify yourselves, you who carry the vessels of the LORD.

12 For you will not go out in haste, nor will you flee; for the LORD will go before you, and the God of Israel will gather you.

13 Behold, my servant shall act wisely; he shall be exalted and lifted up, and highly esteemed.

14 As many were appalled at you, so marred was his appearance from that of a man, and his form from the children of men.

15 Thus he shall sprinkle many nations; kings shall shut their mouths because of him; for that which had not been told them they shall see, and that which they had not heard they shall consider.


## Chapter 53

1 Who has believed our report, and to whom has the arm of the LORD been revealed?

2 He shall spring up before him as a tender plant and as a root out of a dry ground; he hath no form nor comeliness, and when we see him, there is no beauty that we should desire him.

3 Despised and shunned by men, a man of sorrows, acquainted with disease, and like one who hides his face from us, despised, and we held him not in account.

4 Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows; yet we esteemed him stricken, smitten by God and afflicted.

5 But he was pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities; the chastisement of our peace was upon him, and by his wound we are healed.

6 We have all gone astray like sheep; each to his own way; and the LORD has caused the iniquity of us all to meet upon him.

7 He was oppressed and he was afflicted, yet he did not open his mouth; like a lamb to the slaughter he is brought, and like a ewe before her shearers is dumb, and he did not open his mouth.

8 From oppression and from judgment he was taken, and his generation—who can ponder it? For he was cut off from the land of the living because of the transgression of my people, a wound upon them.

9 And he gave his grave with the wicked and with the rich in his death, although he had done no violence and there was no deceit in his mouth.

10 And the LORD was pleased to crush him, to make him suffer; if his soul offers guiltiness, he shall see seed, shall prolong days, and the purpose of the LORD shall prosper by his hand.

11 From the grievance of his soul he shall see, he shall be satisfied; by his knowledge my servant the righteous one shall justify many, and their sins he shall bear.

12 Therefore I will apportion him prey among the many, and with the mighty he will divide spoil, because he exposed his soul to death and was numbered with the offenders; and he bore the fault of many, and interceded for the offenders.


## Chapter 54

1 Shout for joy, O barren woman who has not given birth; break forth in singing and cry aloud, you who have not travailed, for many are the children of the desolate one, more than the children of her who has a husband, says the LORD.

2 Enlarge the place of your tent, and let the curtains of your dwellings be stretched out; do not withhold; lengthen your cords, and strengthen your stakes.

3 For you shall spread out to the right and to the left, and your seed shall inherit nations, and they shall resettle the desolate cities.

4 Do not fear, for you shall not be ashamed; do not be humiliated, for you shall not be disgraced; for you shall forget the shame of your youth, and the reproach of your widowhood you shall remember no more.

5 For your husband is your Maker, the LORD of hosts is His name, and your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel, God of all the earth He shall be called.

6 For like a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit you are called by the LORD, and like a wife of youth though she is rejected, says your God.

7 In a moment, suddenly, I abandoned you, and with great tender mercies I will gather you.

8 In little wrath I hid my face from you for a moment, but with everlasting kindness I will have mercy on you, said the LORD, your Redeemer.

9 For the waters of Noah this is to me, by which I swore never again to bring the waters of Noah over the earth; so I have sworn not to be angry with you or rebuke you.

10 For the mountains may shift and the hills may be removed, but my mercy from you will not shift, and my covenant of peace will not be shaken, says the LORD who has mercy on you.

11 Afflicted, storm-tossed, not comforted—behold, I will set your stones with fair colours, and lay your foundations with sapphires.

12 And I will set your suns as agate, and your gates to carbuncle stones, and all your borders to desirable stones.

13 And all your children shall be taught by the LORD, and great shall be the peace of your children.

14 In righteousness you will be established; you will be far from oppression, for you will not fear, and from ruin, for it will not approach you.

15 Behold, a sojourner shall sojourn, not of my people; who sojourns with you shall fall upon you.

16 I created the craftsman who blows fire into coals and produces a tool for his work, and I created the destroyer to cause ruin.

17 No weapon formed against you shall succeed, and every tongue that rises up against you in judgment you shall condemn; this is the inheritance of the servants of the LORD, and their righteousness is from me, declares the LORD.


## Chapter 55

1 Ho, everyone thirsty, come for water, and the one who has no silver, come, buy and eat; come, buy wine and milk without money and without price.

2 Why do you weigh out money for that which is not food, and your labour for that which does not satisfy? Listen carefully to me, and eat what is good, and delight yourselves in fatness, your souls.

3 Incline your ears and come to me; hear, that your souls may live; and I will make with you an everlasting covenant, even the faithful mercies of David.

4 Behold, I have given him as a witness to the nations, a leader and commander to the peoples.

5 Behold, a nation that you do not know will call upon you, and a nation that does not know you will run to you, for the sake of the LORD your God and for the Holy One of Israel, because He has glorified you.

6 Seek Jehovah while he may be found; call upon him while he is near.

7 Let the wicked forsake his way and the unrighteous man his thoughts, and let him return to the LORD that he may have mercy upon him, and to our God for he is abundant to forgive.

8 For my purposes are not your purposes, nor are your ways my ways, says the LORD.

9 For as the heavens are high above the earth, so are my ways above your ways, and my thoughts above your thoughts.

10 For as the rain and the snow come down from the heavens, and there they do not return, except after watering the earth and causing it to bring forth and to sprout, and giving seed to the sower and bread to the eater.

11 So shall my word be that goes out from my mouth: it shall not return to me empty, but shall accomplish what I please, and succeed in what I send it.

12 For with joy you will go out, and in peace you will be led; the mountains and the hills will break forth before you into singing, and all the trees of the field will clap the hand.

13 Instead of the thorn the fir tree will come up; instead of the brier the myrtle tree will come up; and it will be to the Lord for renown, for an everlasting sign that will not be cut off.


## Chapter 56

1 Thus says the LORD: Keep justice and do righteousness, for my deliverance is about to come and my justice to be revealed.

2 Blessed is the certain man who does this, and the son of a man who holds fast to it, keeping the sabbath from profaning it, and keeping his hand from doing any evil.

3 Let not the foreigner who has joined himself to the LORD say, "The LORD will surely separate me from his people," and let not the eunuch say, "Behold, I am a dry tree."

4 For thus says the LORD to the eunuchs who keep my sabbaths and choose what I delight in and hold fast my covenant:

5 I will give them in my temple and within my walls a hand and a name better than sons and daughters; an everlasting name I will give them, which shall not be cut off.

6 And the children of the alien who join themselves to the LORD to serve him and to love the name of the LORD to be his servants, everyone who keeps the sabbath from profaning it and holds fast to my covenant.

7 And I will bring them to my holy mountain and make them joyful in my house of prayer; their burnt offerings and their sacrifices will be accepted on my altar, for my house will be called a house of prayer for all nations.

8 The Lord God said: "I will still gather the scattered of Israel; I will gather them together with those already gathered."

9 Every living creature of the field, come to eat; yea, all the beasts of the forest.

10 All are blind, all dogs are mute, unable to bark; they are snorers, lying down, lovers of slumber.

11 And the dogs are greedy of appetite, they do not know satisfaction; they are shepherds who do not understand; they all turn to their own way, each to his gain from his own quarter.

12 Come, they say, I will take wine, and we will fill ourselves with strong drink; and tomorrow shall be like today, great beyond measure.


## Chapter 57

1 The righteous one perishes and no person gives thought; and men of kindness are gathered in by one who does not understand, because on account of the evil the righteous one is gathered in.

2 He shall come in peace, they shall rest upon their beds, each one going in the uprightness of his way.

3 But you, come near here, sons of the afflicted, seed of the adulterer and the fornicator.

4 On whom do you delight, on whom do you open the mouth, lengthen the tongue? Are you not children of transgression, a seed of falsehood?

5 The consolations among the gods under every green tree, those who slaughter the children in the streams under the branches of the rocks.

6 In the dividing of the valley, your portion, they, even they, are your lot; also to them you poured out a drink offering, you brought up a sacrifice—shall I be comforted over these?

7 On a high hill and lifted up you placed your bed, and also there you went up to offer a sacrifice.

8 After the door and the doorpost you placed your memorial, for from me you departed and went up; you widened your bed and made a covenant for yourself with them; you loved their bed, your hand reached out.

9 You increased your anointing for the king and multiplied your perfumes; you sent your messengers far away, and brought yourself down to the grave.

10 In your abundant ways you grew weary, yet you did not say, "I am faint"; the life of your hand found it, therefore you did not grow weak.

11 And whom did you fear and dread, that you would lie, and me you did not remember, did not take to heart? Was I not the one who has been silent from of old, and you do not fear me?

12 I will declare your justice, and your deeds will not profit you.

13 When you cry out, your company will save you, but a wind will carry off and take away all of them as vanity; but the one who takes refuge in me will inherit the land and possess my holy mountain.

14 Clear the way, clear the way, prepare a path; remove the stumbling block from the path of my people.

15 For thus says the high and exalted One who inhabits eternity, whose name is holy: I dwell in the height and in holiness, and with the contrite and lowly in spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly and to revive the heart of the contrite.

16 For I will not always contend, neither will I forever be angry; for the spirit before me will faint, and the souls I have made.

17 Because of the fault of his covetousness I was angry and struck him; I hid and was angry, but he went rebellious in the way of his heart.

18 I have seen his ways, and I will heal him, and I will lead him, and I will restore comforts to him and to his mourners.

19 Creating the lips—peace, peace to the one far away and to the one near—says the LORD—and I will heal him.

20 And the wicked are like the sea driven out, for it cannot be quiet, and its waters cast up mire and clay.

21 There is no peace, says my God, to the wicked.


## Chapter 58

1 Cry aloud, do not hold back, raise your voice like a trumpet; declare to my people their rebellion, and to the house of Jacob their sins.

2 And continually they seek me, and delight to know my ways, as a nation that has done justice and has not forsaken the judgment of their God; they ask of me righteous judgments, they delight in the approaching of God.

3 Why do we fast, and you do not see? We afflict our appetite, and you do not notice. Behold, in the day of your fasting you find delight, and all your labour you oppress.

4 You fast for strife and contention, to strike with the fist of wickedness; you do not fast as on a day to make your voice heard on high.

5 Is such a fast as this what I choose, a day for a man to humble his soul? Is it to bow his head like a bulrush, to spread sackcloth and ashes? Is this what you call a fast, a day acceptable to the LORD?

6 Is not this the fast I choose: to loosen the bands of wickedness, to undo the heavy burdens, to let the oppressed go free, and to break every yoke?

7 Indeed, share your bread with the hungry, and bring the cast-out poor into your home; when you see one naked, clothe him, and do not hide yourself from your own flesh.

8 Then your light will break forth like the morning, your health will quickly spring forth, and your righteousness will go before your face; the glory of the LORD will gather you.

9 Then you will call and the LORD will answer; you will cry for help and he will say, "Here I am"; if you remove from among you the yoke, the pointing of the finger, and the speaking of iniquity.

10 And you shall satisfy the appetite of the hungry and the soul of the afflicted you shall gladden, and your light shall rise in the darkness and your gloominess like midday.

11 And the LORD will always lead you, and satisfy your soul in scorched places, and strengthen your bones; and you will be like a watered garden, and like a spring whose waters do not fail.

12 And from you shall arise decayed places of old, foundations of generation upon generation shall be raised, and you shall be called Builder of the Breach, Restorer of paths to dwell in.

13 If you turn back your foot from the sabbath, from doing your desires on the holy day, and call the sabbath a delight, the consecrated of the LORD honorable, and honor it by not doing your own ways, by not pursuing your own desires, nor speaking a word.

14 Then you will delight in the Lord, and I will make you ride on the heights of the land, and I will make you eat the inheritance of Jacob your father, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.


## Chapter 59

1 Indeed, the arm of the LORD is not too short to save, nor is His ear too heavy to hear.

2 But your iniquities have been the divider between you and your God, and your sins have hidden his face from you, so that he does not hear.

3 For your hands are defiled with blood, and your fingers with iniquity; your lips have spoken deceit, your tongue mutters iniquity.

4 There is no one calling for justice, and no one judging with faithfulness; trusting in nothing, they speak vanity; conceiving trouble, they give birth to iniquity.

5 They hatch the eggs of the cockatrice, and the webs of the spider will be woven; he who eats of their eggs will die, and what is crushed will break out into a viper.

6 Their webs will not become garments, nor will they cover themselves with their deeds; their deeds are deeds of iniquity, and works of violence are in their hands.

7 Their legs hasten to evil, they rush to shed innocent blood; their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity, desolation and destruction in their highways.

8 The way of peace they have not known, and there is no justice in their paths; their paths have been crooked for them; all who walk on them do not know peace.

9 Therefore judgment is far from us, and justice does not reach us; we hope for light, but behold, darkness; for brightness, but we walk in gloom.

10 We grope like the blind at a wall, and as though we had no eyes we feel our way; we stumble at midday as in the twilight, in desolate places like the dead.

11 We groan like bears, all of us, and like doves we moan; we wait for justice but there is none, for deliverance—it is far from us.

12 For our rebellions and sins against you are many, and our sins have answered against us; for our rebellions are with us, and our iniquities are known.

13 Transgressing and denying the LORD, turning back from following our God, speaking oppression and rebellion, conceiving and uttering from the heart words of falsehood.

14 And justice is pushed backward, and righteousness stands far away; for truth has stumbled in the public square, and integrity cannot enter.

15 And assuredness was missing, and turning from evil was plundered; and the LORD saw, and it displeased Him in His sight that there was no justice.

16 And he saw that there was no man, and he was appalled that there was no one intervening; so his own arm brought him salvation, and his righteousness was his support.

17 He put on justice as a breastplate, and a helmet of deliverance on his head; he clothed himself with garments of vengeance as clothing, and wrapped himself with zeal as a mantle.

18 According to the recompense of his deeds, so will he repay: hot displeasure to his adversaries, recompense to his enemies; to the islands a recompense will he repay.

19 And from the west they shall fear the name of the LORD, and from the east, the rising of the sun, his glory; for he shall come like a flood, the spirit of the LORD driving upon it.

20 And the Redeemer shall come to Zion, and to those who turn from rebellion in Jacob, saith the LORD.

21 And I, this is my confederacy with them, says the LORD: my spirit that is upon you and my words which I have put in your mouth shall not depart from your mouth, nor from the mouth of your seed, nor from the mouth of the seed of your seed, says the LORD, from now and forever.


## Chapter 60

1 Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the LORD has risen upon you.

2 For behold, darkness will cover the earth and gross darkness the nations, but upon you the LORD will shine, and His glory will be seen upon you.

3 And nations shall walk by your light, and kings by the brightness of your rising.

4 Lift up your eyes all around and see: they are all gathered, they come to you. Your sons are coming from afar, and your daughters on the side will be carried.

5 Then you shall see and be radiant, and your heart shall tremble and expand, for the abundance of the sea shall be turned to you, the wealth of the nations shall come to you.

6 A multitude of camels will cover you, dromedaries from Midian and Ephah; all of them from Sheba will come; they will bear gold and frankincense, and will proclaim the praises of the LORD.

7 All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered to you, the rams of Nebaioth shall minister to you; they shall go up with acceptance on my altar, and I will glorify my beautiful house.

8 Who are these that fly like a cloud, and like doves to their clefts?

9 For me the islands wait, and the ships of Tarshish first, to bring your children from afar, their silver and their gold with them, for the name of the LORD your God, and for the Holy One of Israel, because He has glorified you.

10 And the sons of the alien shall build your walls, and their kings shall serve you: for in my wrath I struck you, but in my favour I have had mercy on you.

11 And your gates shall be open continually day and night; they shall not be shut, to bring to you the wealth of the nations and their kings led in procession.

12 For the nation and the kingdom that will not serve you will perish, and the nations will be utterly destroyed.

13 The glory of Lebanon shall come to you, fir and pine and box together, to glorify the place of my sanctuary, and the place of my feet I will honor.

14 And your oppressors' children shall come to you, bowing down upon the palms of your feet; all who despised you shall prostrate themselves at your feet and call you the city of the LORD, Zion, the holy one of Israel.

15 Instead of being forsaken and hated, with no one passing through, I will make you an everlasting excellency, a joy of generation to generation.

16 And you shall suck the milk of nations, and the breast of kings you shall nurse; and you shall know that I am the LORD your Savior, and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.

17 Instead of brass I will bring gold, and instead of iron I will bring silver, and instead of wood I will bring brass, and instead of stones I will bring iron; and I will appoint your oversight peace, and your overseer righteousness.

18 No longer shall there be heard in your land injustice, desolation and destruction within your borders; your walls shall be called deliverance, and your gates praise.

19 Your sun shall no longer serve as light by day, nor the brightness of the moon shine upon you; but the LORD shall be your everlasting light, and your God shall be your glory.

20 Your sun shall no longer rise, nor shall your moon be gathered in, for the LORD will be for you as an everlasting light, and the days of your mourning shall be complete.

21 And your people, all of them righteous, will possess the land forever, a planting of the work of my hands to be glorified.

22 The small one will become a thousand, and the insignificant a mighty nation; I the LORD, in its time, will hasten it.


## Chapter 61

1 The air of my Lord Jehovah is upon me, because Jehovah has anointed me to bring good news to the meek; He has sent me to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and opening of the prison to the bound.

2 To proclaim the year of the LORD's favour and the day of our God's vengeance, to comfort all who mourn.

3 To appoint to the mourners of Zion, to give them beauty instead of ashes, oil of joy instead of mourning, a garment of praise instead of a faint spirit; and they shall be called terebinths of righteousness, the planting of the LORD, for glory.

4 And they shall rebuild the decayed places of ancient desolation, raise up the ruined cities, and renew the waste towns from generation to generation.

5 And foreigners shall stand and feed your flock, and alien men shall be your husbandmen and vine dressers.

6 But you shall be called priests of the LORD, ministers of our God; it shall be said to you, the wealth of the nations you shall consume, and in their glory you shall boast.

7 Instead of your shame and confusion, you will possess a double portion; their land will be twice as much, everlasting joy will be theirs.

8 For I am the LORD who loves justice, hates robbery with a burnt offering, and I will give their reward in truth, and make an everlasting covenant with them.

9 And his seed shall be known among the Gentiles, and his offspring among the peoples; all who see them shall recognize them, that they are a seed whom the LORD has blessed.

10 I will greatly rejoice in the LORD, my soul shall be joyful in my God; for he hath clothed me with garments of salvation, he hath covered me with the robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom decketh himself with ornaments, and as a bride adorneth herself with her jewels.

11 For as the earth brings forth its spring, and as a garden causes its sown things to grow, so the LORD my Lord will cause justice and praise to spring up before all the nations.


## Chapter 62

1 For the sake of Zion I will not keep silent, and for the sake of Jerusalem I will not keep quiet, until her righteousness goes forth as a clear shining, and her deliverance as a burning torch.

2 And the Gentiles shall see your righteousness, and all kings your glory; and you shall be called by a new name, which the mouth of the LORD shall assign.

3 You will be a crown of beauty in the hand of the LORD, a royal diadem in the palm of your God.

4 You shall no longer be called Forsaken, and your land shall no longer be called Desolate, but you shall be called Hephzi-bah, and your land Beulah, for the LORD delights in you, and your land shall be married.

5 When a chosen young man marries a virgin, so shall your sons marry you; and as a bridegroom rejoices over a bride, so shall your God rejoice over you.

6 Upon your walls, Jerusalem, I have set watchmen all day and all night continually; they shall not be silent, you who remind the LORD—do not keep silence.

7 Do not give him silence or rest until he establishes Jerusalem as praise in the land.

8 The LORD has sworn by his right hand and by the arm of his strength: “Surely I will no longer give your wheat as food to your enemies, and foreigners shall not drink your wine that you have labored for.”

9 For those who gather it will eat it and praise Jehovah, and those who collect it will drink it in the holy courts.

10 Go through the gates, clear the way for the people, prepare the highway, remove the stones, raise a banner over the nations.

11 Behold, the LORD proclaims to the end of the earth, Say to daughter Zion, Behold, your salvation comes, Behold, his reward is with him and his work before him.

12 And they shall call them the holy people, the redeemed of the LORD; and you shall be called Sought Out, a city not forsaken.


## Chapter 63

1 Who is this coming from Edom, in crimsoned apparel from Bozrah, this one majestic in his clothing, striding in the greatness of his strength? I, speaking in justice, mighty to save.

2 Why is your apparel red, and your garments like one treading in a winepress?

3 I trod the winepress alone, and from the peoples no man was with me; and I trampled them in my anger, and I trod them in the fierceness of my wrath; and their blood was sprinkled on my apparel, and all my vestments I have stained.

4 For a day of vengeance is in my heart, and the year of my redemption has come.

5 I looked for help, but there was no helper; I was appalled, and there was no one to sustain; so my own arm brought me salvation, and my fury was the one that sustained me.

6 and I will yoke nations in my anger, and I will make them drunk with my wrath, and I will bring down their strength to the ground.

7 I will recount the LORD's acts of favour, the praise due to the LORD, for all that the LORD has done for us, and the abundance of goodness to the house of Israel, which He bestowed according to His compassion and according to the abundance of His kindnesses.

8 Yet my folk they are, sons who will not deal falsely; and he became their savior.

9 In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the messenger of his presence saved them; in his love and in his pity he redeemed them; and he lifted them up and carried them all the days of old.

10 And they rebelled and grieved his holy spirit, so he turned against them as an enemy; he himself fought against them.

11 Moses remembered the days of old, his people: where is the one who brought up from the sea the shepherd of his flock? Where is the one who put within him the spirit of his holiness?

12 Who caused his glorious arm to go at the right hand of Moses, splitting the waters before them to make for himself an everlasting name.

13 He leads them through the depths like a horse in the wilderness; they do not stumble.

14 Like a beast in the valley, the Spirit of the LORD will cause him to rest; so You led Your people to make for Yourself a glorious name.

15 Look down from heaven and see, from the dwelling of your holiness and your glory: where is your zeal and your mighty power? The abundance of your inner parts and your tender mercies are held back from me.

16 For you are our father, for Abraham does not know us, nor does Israel recognize us; you, the LORD, are our father, our redeemer from of old is your name.

17 Why do you make us wander from your ways, hardening our heart from your fear? Return for the sake of your servants, the tribes of your inheritance.

18 While the small inherit your holy folk, our adversaries trample your sanctuary.

19 We have long been as those over whom you did not rule, those not called by your name.


## Chapter 64

1 Had you torn open the heavens and come down, the mountains would have flowed from your presence.

2 Like fire kindling brushwood, like fire causing water to boil, make your name known to your adversaries; from your presence, nations shall tremble.

3 When you did awesome things we did not expect, you came down, and the mountains flowed away before your presence.

4 From ancient times no one has heard, no one has paid attention, no eye has seen a god besides you who acts on behalf of those who wait for him.

5 You meet the one who rejoices and does righteousness, those who remember you in your ways. Behold, you were angry, and we sinned; in them we have continued, and shall we be saved?

6 We have all become unclean, and our righteous deeds are like a filthy garment; we all wither like a leaf, and our iniquities, like the wind, carry us away.

7 And none calls on your name, awakening to hold on to you, for you have hidden your face from us and melted us in the hand of our iniquity.

8 And now, O LORD, You are our Father; we are the clay, and You are our Potter; and the work of Your hands—we are all.

9 Do not be exceedingly angry, O Jehovah, neither remember sin perpetually; behold, look, we beseech thee, at thy people, all of us.

10 The cities of your holiness became a wilderness, Zion became a wilderness, Jerusalem a desolation.

11 Our temple, the holy place of our glory, which our ancestors praised you in, has become a burning with fire, and all our precious things have become a desolation.

12 Will You refrain on account of these things, O the LORD? Will You keep silent and afflict us exceedingly?


## Chapter 65

1 I was sought, though not asked; I was found, though not sought; I said, "Here I am, here I am," to a nation not calling in my name.

2 I have spread out my hands all day to a rebellious people, walking in a way that is not good, after their own thoughts.

3 The people who continually anger me to my face, sacrificing in gardens and burning incense on bricks.

4 Those who sit in graves and lodge in monuments, who eat swine's flesh, and broth of abominable things is in their vessels.

5 Those who say, “Come near to you; do not approach me, for I have consecrated you,” these are smoke in my anger, a fire burning all day long.

6 Behold, it is written before me: I will not keep silence, except I have recompensed; yea, I will recompense into their bosom.

7 Your sins and the sins of your ancestors together, says the LORD, who offered incense on the mountains and insulted me on the hills; therefore I will measure their former work into their lap.

8 Thus says the LORD: As new wine is found in a cluster, and one says, "Do not destroy it, for there is a blessing in it," so I will do for the sake of my servants, not to destroy altogether.

9 And I will bring forth from Jacob seed, and from Judah a possessor of the mountains; and My chosen shall possess it, and My servants shall dwell there.

10 And Sharon shall be a fold for flocks, and the valley of Achor a resting place for cattle, for my people that have sought me.

11 But you who forsake the LORD, who forget the hill of my holy sanctuary, who set a table for that troop and fill a drink-offering for number,

12 I appointed you to the sword, and all of you shall bow to the slaughter, because I called and you did not answer, I spoke and you did not listen, and you did evil in my eyes, and chose what I did not desire.

13 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Behold, my servants will eat, but you will hunger; behold, my servants will drink, but you will thirst; behold, my servants will rejoice, but you will be ashamed.

14 Behold, my servants will rejoice with joy of heart, but you will cry out with pain of heart and with breaking of spirit you will wail.

15 And you shall leave your name for a curse to my chosen, and the LORD God shall slay you, and to his servants he shall call a different name.

16 He who is blessed in the land will be blessed by the God of truth, and he who swears in the land will swear by the God of truth, for the former troubles are forgotten and hidden from my eyes.

17 For behold, I am creating new heavens and a new earth, and the former ones shall not be remembered, nor come upon the heart.

18 But be glad and rejoice forever, for I am creating Jerusalem as joy and her people as gladness.

19 And I will restore Jerusalem and rejoice in my people, and there shall no longer be heard in her a voice of weeping and a voice of crying.

20 There shall no longer be from there an infant who dies at a hundred years old, nor an old man who does not reach his full age; for the child shall be a hundred years old when he dies, and the sinner a hundred years old shall be accursed.

21 And they shall build houses and dwell in them, and plant vineyards and eat their fruit.

22 They shall not build and another inhabit; they shall not plant and another eat; for like the days of the tree shall be the days of my people, and the work of their hands shall enjoy my chosen.

23 They shall not labor in vain, nor bring forth children to terror, for they are the offspring of the LORD’s blessed, and their descendants are with them.

24 And it shall be, before they call, I will answer; while they are yet speaking, I will hear.

25 The wolf and the lamb shall feed together, and the young lion shall eat straw like the beeve, and the serpent’s food shall be dust: they shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain, says the LORD.


## Chapter 66

1 Thus says the LORD, the heavens are my throne and the earth is the stool for my feet; where is this house that you would build for me, and where is this place of my rest?

2 And all these my hand has made, and so all these came to be, says the LORD; but to this one will I look: to the poor and crushed in spirit, and who trembles at my word.

3 Whoever slaughters the bull strikes a man, sacrificing the lamb breaks a dog's neck, presenting a grain offering brings blood of a swine, remembering frankincense invokes affliction; yea, they also have chosen their own ways, and their soul delights in their detestable things.

4 I also choose delusion for them, and I will bring fear upon them, because I called and there was no answer, I spoke and they did not hear, and they did what was evil in my sight, and they chose what I did not desire.

5 Hear the word of the LORD, you who tremble at his word: your brothers who hate you and cast you out for my name’s sake say, “Let the LORD be glorified, that we may see your joy”; but they shall be put to shame.

6 A sound of tumult from the city, a sound from the temple, a sound of the LORD repaying recompense to his enemies.

7 Before she was in labor, before her pang came, she gave birth to a male.

8 Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen the like of these? Can a land be born in one day, or a nation be brought forth at once? For as soon as Zion was in labor, she also gave birth to her children.

9 Shall I bring to the birth and not cause to bring forth? says the LORD. Shall I who cause to bring forth shut the womb? says your God.

10 Rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad in her, all who love her; rejoice with her in joy, all who mourn over her.

11 so that you may nurse and be satisfied from her comforting breast, so that you may drink deeply and delight in her glorious abundance.

12 For thus says the LORD: Behold, I will extend to her a river of peace and a stream of the nations' glory, and you shall nurse; you shall be carried at the side and dandled upon the knees.

13 As a man whom his mother comforts, so I will comfort you, and in Jerusalem you will be comforted.

14 And you shall see and rejoice in your heart, and your bones shall flourish like tender grass, and the hand of the LORD shall be known to His servants, and indignation to His enemies.

15 For behold, the LORD will come with fire, and his chariots like the whirlwind, to render his anger with fury and his rebuke with flames of fire.

16 For the LORD will judge with fire and with his sword will strike down all flesh, and many will be slain by the LORD.

17 Those who sanctify and purify themselves to the gardens, following after the midst, eating swine flesh and the abomination and the mouse, together they shall be cut off, says the LORD.

18 For I know their works and their thoughts; the time is coming to gather all nations and tongues, and they shall come and see my glory.

19 And I will set in them a mark, and I will send from them fugitives to the nations, to Tarshish, Pul, and Lud, who draw the bow, to Tubal and Javan, the distant islands that have not heard my fame nor seen my glory, and they shall declare my glory among the nations.

20 And they shall bring your brothers from all the nations as a gift to the LORD on horses and in chariots and on litters and on mules and on swift beasts to Mount My holiness, Jerusalem, says the LORD, just as the children of Israel bring the offering in a clean vessel to the house of the LORD.

21 And from them I will also take priests and Leviites, says the LORD.

22 For as the new heavens and the new earth that I make remain before me, declares the LORD, so shall your seed and your name remain.

23 According to the month in its month, and according to the sabbath in the sabbath, every flesh shall come to bow down before me, says the LORD.

24 And they shall go out and see the corpses of the men who have transgressed against me, for their worm shall not die and their burning shall not be quenched, and they shall be an abhorring to all flesh.


# JEREMIAH

## Chapter 1

1 The acts of Jeremiah son of Hillkiah, one of the priests who was in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin.

2 The word of the LORD came to him in the days of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.

3 In the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, until the end of eleven years of Zedekiah son of Josiah king of Judah, until the exile of Jerusalem in the fifth month.

4 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

5 Before I formed you in the belly, and before you came out of the womb, I sanctified you; a prophet to the nations I gave you.

6 And I said, Ah, (my) Lord God, behold, I do not know how to speak, for I am a lad.

7 And Jehovah said to me, Do not say I am a lad, for to all to which I shall send you, you shall go, and all which I command you, you shall speak.

8 Do not fear their presence, for I am with you to deliver you, says the LORD.

9 The LORD sent His hand and touched my mouth, and the LORD said to me, "Behold, I have put My words in your mouth."

10 See, I have appointed you this day over the nations and over the kingdoms, to uproot and to break down, to destroy and to overthrow, to build and to plant.

11 The word of the LORD came to me, saying, What do you see, Jeremiah? And I said, I see a rod of an almond tree.

12 The LORD said to me, "You have done well to see, for I am watching over my word to perform it."

13 The word of the LORD came to me a second time, saying, “What do you see?” And I said, “I see a boiling pot; its face is from the face of the north.”

14 And the LORD said to me, From the north disaster shall break out on all the inhabitants of the land.

15 For behold I am calling all the families of the kingdoms of the north, says the LORD, and they shall come and each one shall set his throne at the entrance of the gates of Jerusalem and against all its walls all around and against all the cities of Judah.

16 And I spoke my judgments to them concerning all their evil, in that they had forsaken me and burned incense to other gods and bowed down to the works of their hands.

17 And you shall gird your loins and arise and speak to them all that I command you; do not be dismayed before their presence, lest I dismay you before them.

18 And I, behold, I have made you this day a fortified city, an iron pillar, and bronze walls against all the land, to the kings of Judah, to its officials, to its priests, and to the people of the land.

19 And they shall fight against thee, but they shall not prevail against thee; for I am with thee, saith the LORD, to deliver thee.


## Chapter 2

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem: Thus says the LORD: I remember for your sake the favour of your youth, the love of your espousals, how you went after me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.

3 The consecrated of Israel to the LORD, the first of its increase: all who devour it shall be held guilty, adversity shall come upon them, says the LORD.

4 Hear the word of the LORD, O house of Jacob, and all families of the house of Israel.

5 Thus says the LORD: What iniquity did your fathers find in me that they went far from me and went after vanity and became vain?

6 They did not say, "Where is the LORD who brought us up from the land of Egypt, who led us through the wilderness, through a land of desert and pit, through a land of drought and shadow of death, through a land which no man passed through and where no person dwelt?"

7 I brought you to the fruitful land to eat its fruit and its goodness, but you came and defiled my land and made my inheritance an abomination.

8 The priests did not say, "Where is the LORD?" and those holding the law did not know me, and the shepherds rebelled against me, and the prophets prophesied by Baal and after useless things they went.

9 Therefore once more will I contend with you, saith the LORD, and with the children of your children will I contend.

10 For they have passed through the isles of Kittim and see, and Kedar send and observe closely; see, has there ever been such a thing?

11 Has a nation exchanged gods, though they are no gods, but My people have exchanged their glory for that which does not profit?

12 Be appalled, O heavens, at this, and shudder, be very desolate, says the LORD.

13 For two adversities my people have done: they have forsaken me, the fountain of living waters, to hew out for themselves cisterns, broken cisterns, which cannot hold water.

14 Is the bondman Israel, though he be home-born, why has he become spoil?

15 Over him the young lions will roar, uttering their voice, and they will make his land a desolation, their cities without inhabitant.

16 Even the children of Noph and Tahpanhes shall shepherd you, the crown of the head.

17 Have you not brought this on yourself by forsaking Jehovah your God when he led you in the way?

18 And now, what have you to do with the way of Egypt, to drink the waters of Shihor? And what have you to do with the way of Assyria, to drink the waters of the river?

19 He will discipline you for your adversity and rebuke you for your backslidings; know and see that it is evil and bitter that you have forsaken the LORD your God and that fear of me is not in you, says the Lord God of hosts.

20 For alway I broke your yoke, I loosed your bands; yet you said, No, but on every high hill and under every green tree I will go, playing the harlot.

21 Yet I planted you a choice wine, altogether a true seed; how then have you turned to Me into a degenerate vine of a foreign tree?

22 But if you wash with nitre and increase for yourself soap, your iniquity is marked before me, says the Lord God.

23 How can you say, "I have not become defiled," and claim, "I have not gone after the Baalim"? See your way in the valley; consider what you have done: a swift dromedary wandering her paths.

24 A wild ass accustomed to the wilderness, in the desire of its breath who can turn it back? All who seek it do not weary; in its month they find it.

25 Keep your foot from straying, and your young men from thirst; and you said, "I am weary; no, I have loved strangers, and after them I will go."

26 As a thief is ashamed when caught, so are the house of Israel ashamed— their kings, their princes, their priests, and their prophets.

27 They say to a tree, You are my father, and to a stone, You gave birth to me; for they have turned their back to me, and not their face; but in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise and save us.

28 But where are the gods you made for yourself? Let them rise up, if they can save you in the time of your distress; for the number of your cities, Judah, were your gods.

29 Why do you contend with me? altogether you have transgressed against me, says the LORD.

30 In vain I struck your children; they did not receive discipline; your sword devoured your prophets like a destroying lion.

31 This generation, behold the word of the LORD: Have I been a wilderness to Israel, a land of darkness? Why then do my people say, We have broken loose, we will come no more to you?

32 Shall a virgin forget her ornament, a bride her attire? Yet my people have forgotten me, days without number.

33 How well you direct your way to seek love! Therefore you have even taught the wicked women your ways.

34 Also in your wings are found the blood of the lives of innocent poor; not in the secret search were they found, but for all these things.

35 And you said, “I am innocent; surely his anger has turned from me.” Behold, I will enter into judgment with you because of your saying, “I have not sinned.”

36 Why do you go so far to change your way? You will also be ashamed of Egypt as you were ashamed of Assyria.

37 Indeed from this one you will depart, and your hands upon your own head, for the LORD has rejected your trusts, and you will not succeed with them.


## Chapter 3

1 Say: When a man sends away his wife and she goes from him and becomes another man’s wife, will he return again to her? Would not the land be utterly defiled? But you have played the whore with many lovers; and yet return to me, says the LORD.

2 Lift up your eyes to the high places and see, where have you not sat upon the ways? Like an Arabian in the wilderness, you have adorned the land with your whoredom and with your wickedness.

3 The shower was withheld and the latter rain did not come; an impudent brow of a woman you became, refusing to be ashamed.

4 Is it not from now on that you are my father, the guide of my youth?

5 Will he keep forever? Will he maintain perpetually? Behold, I have spoken; you have done adversities and prevailed.

6 And the LORD said to me in the days of King Josiah, Have you seen what backsliding Israel has done? She has gone upon every high hill and under every green tree and played the harlot there.

7 And I said, After she has done all these things, she will return to me; but she did not return, and her treacherous sister Judah saw it.

8 I saw that for all the causes of her backsliding Israel had committed adultery, I had sent her away and given her the bill of her divorce; yet her sister Judah did not fear, but she also went and committed adultery.

9 And it came to pass because of the sound of her whoredom, that she defiled the land and committed adultery with stone and with wood.

10 Yet in all this, Judah has not returned to me with her whole heart, but falsely, declares the LORD.

11 And the LORD said to me, Backsliding Israel has justified herself more than treacherous Judah.

12 Go and proclaim these words toward the north, and say, Return, O backsliding Israel, says the LORD; I will not make my face against you, for I am merciful, says the LORD, I will not keep anger forever.

13 Only know your sin, that you have rebelled against the LORD your God, and have scattered your ways to strangers under every green tree, and to my voice you did not listen, says the LORD.

14 Return, O rebellious children, says the LORD, for I am married to you; and I will take you one from a city and two from a family, and bring you to Zion.

15 And I will give you shepherds according to my heart, and they shall shepherd you with knowledge and understanding.

16 And it shall come to pass when you multiply and increase in the land in those days, says the LORD, that they shall no longer say, "The ark of the covenant of the LORD," nor shall it come to mind, nor shall they remember it, nor shall they miss it, nor shall it be made again.

17 At that time they will call Jerusalem the throne of the LORD, and all the nations will gather to it, to the name of the LORD in Jerusalem, and they will no longer follow the stubbornness of their evil heart.

18 In those days the house of Judah shall walk with the house of Israel, and they shall come together from the land of the north to the land that I gave to your fathers.

19 Yet I said, How shall I put you among the children and give you a pleasant land, a beautiful inheritance of the hosts of nations? And I said, You shall call me, My father, and shall not turn away from following me.

20 Indeed, a wife has betrayed her companion; so have you dealt treacherously with me, house of Israel, says the LORD.

21 A voice upon the high places is heard, the sound of the weeping and supplication of the sons of Israel, because they have perverted their way and forgotten the LORD their God.

22 Return, O children of backsliding, I will heal your turning away; behold, we come to you, for you, the LORD, are our God.

23 Indeed, without a cause the multitude of the hills is in vain; indeed, in the LORD our God is the salvation of Israel.

24 And the shame has devoured the work of our fathers from our youth, their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

25 We lie in our shame, and our dishonor covers us, for we have sinned against the LORD our God—we and our fathers—from our youth even to this day, and we have not obeyed the voice of the LORD our God.


## Chapter 4

1 If thou return, Israel, saith the LORD, unto me thou shalt return; and if thou take thine abominable filth from before my face, then thou shalt not wander.

2 And you shall swear by the LORD truly, with justice and with righteousness, and the nations shall bless themselves by him and in him shall they glory.

3 For thus says the LORD to the man Judah and to Jerusalem: plow for yourselves fallow ground, and do not sow among thorns.

4 Circumcise yourselves to the LORD and remove the foreskins of your hearts, O men of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, lest my anger go forth like fire and burn with none to quench it, because of the wickedness of your deeds.

5 Declare in Judah and in Jerusalem make heard: blow the cornet in the land; call out fully and say: gather and let us go into the fortified cities.

6 Raise a banner toward Zion; flee for safety, do not stay, for I am bringing calamity from the north, and great destruction.

7 The (young) lion has come up from the thicket, the destroyer of nations has departed; he has left his place to make your land a waste; your cities will lie desolate without inhabitant.

8 Therefore gird sackcloth, mourn and wail, for the fierce wrath of the LORD has not turned back from them.

9 And it shall come to pass in that age, saith the LORD, that the heart of the king and the heart of the rulers shall fail, and the priests shall be appalled, and the prophets shall be dismayed.

10 And I said, Ah, (my) Lord God, surely You have greatly troubled this people and Jerusalem, saying, Peace shall be with you, and the sword has reached even to the soul.

11 At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem a wind of high places in the wilderness the way of the daughter of my people not to winnow and not to cleanse.

12 A spirit from these will come to me; now also I will speak judgments to them.

13 Behold, he ascends like stormy clouds, his chariots are like the whirlwind, his horses are swifter than eagles. Woe to us, for we are utterly destroyed.

14 Wash yourself from the evil of your heart, Jerusalem, that you may be saved; how long will you harbor within you the thoughts of your wickedness?

15 For a voice proclaims from Dan, and announces affliction from the hill of Ephraim.

16 Call to account the nations; behold, proclaim against Jerusalem, watchmen are coming from a far country, and they shall lift up their voice against the cities of Judah.

17 Like keepers of the field, they were round about her, for she hath rebelled against me, saith the LORD.

18 Your ways and your doings have brought this upon you: this is your evil, for it is bitter, for it has reached your heart.

19 My belly, my bowels, the walls of my heart are in turmoil; my heart does not keep silent, because the sound of the trumpet, the shout of war, alarms my soul.

20 Destruction upon destruction is called, for the whole earth is devastated; suddenly my tents are destroyed, in an instant my curtains.

21 How long will I see a banner? I will hear the sound of a trumpet?

22 For my folk do not know me; they are foolish children, not understanding; they are wise to do evil, but to do good they do not know.

23 I saw the earth and behold, without form and void, and to the heavens and their light was not.

24 I saw the hills, and behold, they were trembling, and all the little hills were shaking.

25 I looked, and behold, there was no person; and every bird of heaven had fled.

26 I saw, and behold, the fruitful field was a wilderness, and all its towns were broken down before the LORD, before the fierceness of his anger.

27 For thus says the LORD: Desolate shall be all the land, but an end I will not make.

28 Therefore the land shall mourn, and the heavens above shall grow dark, because I have spoken, I have purposed, and I will not relent, neither will I turn back from it.

29 At the sound of horseman and archer every city flees; they enter into thickets and climb upon rocks; every city is forsaken, and no man dwells in them.

30 And you, O devastated one, what will you do? Though you clothe yourself with scarlet, though you deck yourself with ornaments of gold, though you enlarge your eyes with paint, in vain you make yourself beautiful; your lovers despise you, they seek your life.

31 For I have heard a cry like a woman in labor, distress like a woman giving birth; the cry of Daughter Zion gasping, stretching out her hands: Woe is me now, for my soul is weary of murderers.


## Chapter 5

1 Walk about the streets of Jerusalem and see now, and know, and search its broad places, if you can find a man, if there is one who does justice, who seeks faithfulness; then I will pardon her.

2 Though they say, "As the LORD lives," surely they swear falsely.

3 Jehovah, are not thine eyes upon faithfulness? thou hast smitten them, yet they have not weakened; thou hast consumed them, they refuse to receive correction; they have made their faces harder than a rock, they have refused to return.

4 But I said, Surely they are poor; they are foolish, because they do not know the way of the LORD, the judgment of their God.

5 I will go to the great ones and speak to them, for they know the way of the LORD, the judgment of their God; but together they have broken the yoke, they have burst the bands.

6 Therefore the young lion has attacked them from the forest, the wolf of the wilderness has ravaged them, the leopard keeps watch against their cities; all who go out from there will be torn to pieces, for their transgressions are many, their rebellions are strengthened.

7 Where is the evil of this to you, your children have forsaken me and sworn by no gods; I satisfied them, and they committed adultery and crowded to the house of a prostitute.

8 They were well-fed lusty horses; each one neighed after his neighbor's wife.

9 For these things will I not punish? saith the LORD; and if a nation such as this, shall not my soul be avenged?

10 Ascend upon her battlements and consume utterly, but do not make an end altogether; remove her branches, for they are not to the LORD.

11 For treacherously have Israel's house and Judah's house dealt with me, says the LORD.

12 They lied about the LORD and said, "It is not He"; "Adversity will not come upon us, nor will we see sword or famine."

13 And the prophets will be to the wind, and the speech is not in them; so it will be done to them.

14 Therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts: Because you have spoken this word, behold, I will make my words in your mouth a fire, and this people the wood, and it shall consume them.

15 Behold, I am bringing upon you a nation from afar, O house of Israel, declares the LORD— a mighty nation, an ancient nation, a nation whose language you do not know, neither shall you understand what they speak.

16 His quiver is like an open grave; all of them are mighty men.

17 And they shall eat your harvest and your bread, which your sons and your daughters should eat; they shall eat your sheep and your oxen; they shall eat your vine and your fig tree; they shall impoverish your fortified cities in which you trust with the sword.

18 Even in those days, says the LORD, I will not utterly consume you.

19 And when you say, “Why has the LORD our God done all this to us?” you shall say to them, “Just as you have forsaken me and served foreign gods in your land, so shall you serve strangers in a land that is not yours.”

20 Declare this in the house of Jacob and proclaim it in Judah, saying:

21 Hear now this, O foolish folk, and without understanding: they have eyes, yet do not see; they have ears, yet do not hear.

22 Fear ye not me, saith the LORD; shall ye not tremble before me, who have set the sand a perpetual border for the sea, a decree which it cannot pass, though its waves toss and cannot prevail?

23 And this folk had a rebellious and defiant heart; they turned aside and went away.

24 They did not bethink themselves in their breast: Let us now fear the LORD our God, who gives rain and the latter rain in its due season, who preserves for us the weeks of the harvest according to his statute.

25 Your faults have turned away these things, and your sins have withheld the good from you.

26 For in my people are found the wicked, who lie in wait like fowlers, setting up a trap to destroy men.

27 Like a cage full of birds, so their houses are full of deceit; therefore they have grown great and rich.

28 They have grown fat and sleek; they have committed evil deeds; they do not judge the cause of the fatherless, nor do they execute justice for the poor.

29 For these things will I not punish? saith the LORD; if in a nation such as this my soul will not avenge?

30 Desolation and horror have become in the land.

31 The prophets prophesy falsely, and the priests rule by their power, and my people love it so; what will you do in the end?


## Chapter 6

1 Afflict, O sons of Benjamin, from within Jerusalem, and blow the trumpet in Tekoa, and raise a signal of fire at Beth-haccerem, for evil is beheld from the north and a great breach.

2 The graceful and delicate, I likened to a daughter of Zion.

3 To her shall come shepherds with their flocks; they shall pitch their tents around her; each man shall pasture his flock at his hand.

4 Set apart a battle against her; rise up, and let us go up at midday. Woe to us, for the day has turned; the shadows of evening have lengthened.

5 Arise and let us go up at night and destroy her palaces.

6 For thus says the LORD of hosts: Cut down trees and cast up a mound against Jerusalem; she is the city of oppression, all of her is extortion within her.

7 As a cistern spreads its waters, so she has spread her calamity; violence and destruction are heard in her continually, before me there is ever sickness and wound.

8 Jerusalem, be removed, lest I cut off my soul from you, lest I make you a desolation, a land not inhabited.

9 Thus says the LORD of hosts: They shall thoroughly glean the remnant of Israel like a vine; turn your hand again like a grape-gatherer over the baskets.

10 Against whom shall I speak and bear witness, that they may hear? Behold, their ear is uncircumcised, and they cannot heed; behold, the word of the LORD has become to them a reproach; they do not delight in it.
