11 And I am full of the anger of the LORD; I am weary with holding back; pour it out upon the child in the street and upon the assembly of the young men together; for even man with woman shall be taken, old man with fullness of days.

12 And their houses shall be turned over to others, fields and wives together, for I will stretch out my hand against the inhabitants of the land, says the LORD.

13 From the least of them even to the greatest, everyone is greedy for dishonest gain; and from prophet even to priest, everyone practices deceit.

14 They healed the hurt of my people lightly, saying, "Peace, peace," and there is no peace.

15 They are ashamed because they have done an abominable thing; indeed, they are not ashamed and they do not know shame. Therefore they shall fall among those who fall at the time of their punishment; they shall stumble, says the LORD.

16 Thus says the LORD: Stand upon the ancient paths, look and see which way is the good way, and walk in it, and find rest for your souls; but they said, We will not walk.

17 And I will raise up over you watchmen; give heed to the sound of the trumpet, but they said, We will not give heed.

18 Therefore, hear, O nations, and know, O assembly, what is in them.

19 Hear, O earth, behold I bring disaster upon this people, the fruit of their thoughts, because they have not listened to my words and have rejected my law.

20 Why should I have frank-incense from Sheba, or a good reed from a far country? Your burnt offerings are not acceptable to me, and your sacrifices do not please me.

21 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Behold, I am setting before this people stumbling-blocks, and they shall stumble in them, fathers and sons together, neighbor and his friend.

22 Thus says the LORD: Behold, a people comes from the land of the north, and a great nation stirs from the farthest parts of the earth.

23 Archers and spearmen hold firm; he is cruel and shows no mercy; their voice roars like the sea; they ride upon horses; arrayed like men for battle against you, daughter of Zion.

24 We have heard of his fame; our hands are powerless. Distress has seized us, pain like that of childbirth.

25 Do not go out to the field, and do not travel the way, for the sword of the enemy is terror all around.

26 Daughter of my people, gird yourself with sackcloth and roll in ashes; make mourning for yourself as for an only child, a lamentation most bitter, for suddenly the destroyer will come upon us.

27 I have made you a tower, a fortress among my people, that you may know and test their way.

28 All of them are rebellious rebels, going about as talebearers, brass and iron; all of them are corrupters.

29 The bellows burn fiercely with fire; lead is vainly refined, for the wicked are not removed.

30 Rejected silver, they are called, because the LORD has rejected them.


## Chapter 7

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Stand in the gate of the house of the LORD and proclaim there this word and say, Hear the word of the LORD, all Judah who enter by these gates to worship the LORD.

3 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Improve your ways and your deeds, and I will let you dwell in this place.

4 Do not trust in lying words, saying, "The temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD, they are."

5 But if you will indeed correct your ways and your doings, if you will execute justice between a man and his neighbor,

6 You shall not oppress an alien, a fatherless child, or a widow; you shall not shed innocent blood in this place, and you shall not go after other gods to your own harm.

7 And I will settle you in this place, in the land that I gave to your forefathers, from of old even to everlasting.

8 Behold, you are trusting in you upon the advice of the lie, so that it does not help.

9 Stealing, committing murder, and adultery, swearing falsely, and offering incense to Baal, and going after other gods which you have not known.

10 And you shall come and stand before me in the temple which is called by my name and say, We are delivered, in order to do all these abominations.

11 Is this den of robbers the house which is called by my name, in your eyes? Behold, I myself have seen it, says the LORD.

12 Go now to my place that is in Shiloh, where I established my name at first, and see what I did to it because of the evil of my people Israel.

13 Now therefore, because you have done all these deeds, says the LORD, I spoke to you persistently but you did not listen, and I called to you but you did not answer.

14 And I will do to the temple that is called by my name, in which you trust, and to the place that I gave to you and to your fathers, as I did to Shiloh.

15 And I will cast you out from My presence, as I cast out all your brothers, all the seed of Ephraim.

16 But you, do not pray on behalf of this folk, and do not raise to them a cry or prayer, and do not intercede with me, for I am not listening to you.

17 Do you not see what they are doing in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?

18 The sons gather wood and the fathers kindle the fire and the women knead dough to make cakes for the queen of the heavens and to pour out drink offerings to other gods in order to provoke me.

19 Is it I whom they have angered, saith the LORD? Is it not themselves, for the shame of their countenance?

20 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: behold, my anger and my wrath are poured out upon this place, upon man and upon beast, and upon the tree of the field and upon the fruit of the ground; and it shall burn, and it shall not be quenched.

21 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Add your burnt offerings to your sacrifices and eat the flesh.

22 For I did not speak to your fathers and did not command them on the day I brought them out from the land of Egypt concerning burnt offerings and sacrifice.

23 But this act I commanded them, saying, Obey my voice, and I will be your God, and you shall be my people, and you shall walk in every way that I command you, so that it may go well with you.

24 And they did not hear nor incline their ear, but walked in the counsel of their evil imagination, and became backward and not forward.

25 From the day your forefathers went out from the land of Egypt until this day, I have sent to you all my servant prophets, sending them daily.

26 They did not listen to me, nor did they incline their ear; they stiffened their neck and behaved worse than their fathers.

27 And you shall speak to them all these words, but they will not listen to you; and you shall call to them, but they will not answer you.

28 And you shall say to them, This is the nation that did not heed the voice of the LORD their God, nor take discipline; faithfulness has perished and is cut off from their mouth.

29 Shave off your consecration and cast it away; raise a lamentation on the high places, for the LORD has rejected and forsaken the generation of his wrath.

30 For the people of Judah have done evil in my sight, declares the LORD: they have set their detestable things in the temple that bears my name, to defile it.

31 And they built the high places of Topheth, which is in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to burn their children and their daughters in the fire, which I did not command, nor did it come into my mind.

32 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when no longer will Topheth and the valley of the son of Hinnom be called so, but rather Valley of Slaughter, and they will bury in Topheth because of no place.

33 And the dead body of this people shall become food for the birds of the air and for the beasts of the earth, and there shall be none to frighten them away.

34 I will cause the sound of joy and the sound of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, to cease from the cities of Judah and the streets of Jerusalem; for the land shall become a desolate place.


## Chapter 8

1 At that time, says the LORD, I will take the bones of the kings of Judah, and the bones of its officials, and the bones of the priests, and the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, from their graves.

2 And they shall spread them out to the sun and to the moon and to all the host of heaven, which they have loved and which they have served and after which they have walked and which they have sought and to which they have bowed down; they shall not be gathered and they shall not be buried, they shall be dung upon the face of the ground.

3 And death is chosen rather than life by all the remnant that remains of this evil family, in every place where I have driven them, declares the LORD of hosts.

4 Then you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD: Shall they fall and not arise? Shall he turn back and not return?

5 Why has this folk turned away, Jerusalem, with perpetual backsliding? They hold fast to deceit; they refuse to return.

6 I listened and heard: not so do they speak; there is no man repenting of his evil, saying, "What have I done?" All of them have turned like a horse rushing into battle.

7 Even the stork in the heavens knows her appointed times, and the turtledove, the swift, and the crane observe the time of their coming; but my people do not know the judgment of the LORD.

8 How can you say, We are wise, and the law of the LORD is with us? Indeed, the lying pen has made falsehood, scribes of deceit.

9 The wise are shamed, they are dismayed and taken; behold, because of the word of the LORD they have rejected; and what wisdom is in them?

10 Therefore I will give their wives to others, their fields to those who will inherit them; for from the least to the greatest, everyone is greedy for dishonest gain; from the prophet to the priest, everyone practices deceit.

11 They treated the affliction of the daughter of my people lightly, saying, "Peace, peace," but there is no peace.

12 They are ashamed because they have done an abomination; indeed, they are not ashamed and they do not know; therefore they will fall among those who fall at the time of their visitation; they will stumble, says the LORD.

13 I will surely gather them, saith the LORD; there are no grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig tree; the leaf is withered; and what I have given them shall pass away from them.

14 Why do we sit still? Gather together, and let us go into the walled cities, and let us perish there, for the LORD our God has made us perish and has given us poisoned water to drink, because we have sinned against the LORD.

15 Waiting for peace, but no good comes; for a time of healing, and behold, trouble.

16 From Dan is heard the snorting of his horses; at the sound of the neighing of his stallions the whole earth trembles; they come and devour the land and all that is in it, the city and its inhabitants.

17 For behold, I will send among you serpents, adders, which have no enchantment, and they will bite you, says the LORD.

18 I have comforted myself concerning sorrow, but my heart is deeply grieved.

19 Behold, the sound of the crying of the daughter of my people from a distant land: Is the LORD not in Zion? Is her king not in her? Why have they provoked me to anger with their carved images, with the vanities of foreigners?

20 The harvest has passed, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.

21 For the affliction of the daughter of my people I am broken, I am desolate, I am utterly seized.

22 Is there no balm in Gilead? Is there no healer there? Why then has not the health of the daughter of my people come up?


## Chapter 9

1 Who will give my head water and my eye a fountain of tears, that I may weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people?

2 Who will give me a lodging place in the wilderness, that I might leave my people and depart from them, for they are all adulterers, a solemn assembly of traitors?

3 They have bent their tongue like a deceitful bow, and not in faithfulness have they grown strong in the land; from evil to evil they proceed, and they do not know me, declares the LORD.

4 Beware of another, and do not trust any brother, for every brother will surely deceive, and every companion will surely slander.

5 Also, each man would deceive his neighbor, and truthful words they would not speak; they have taught their tongues to speak lies; they have labored to do wrong.

6 Your dwelling among deceit, in deceit from refusing to know me, says the LORD.

7 Therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, I will melt them and test them, for how shall I do regarding the daughter of my people?

8 An arrow is the tongue of him with deceit he speaks; with his mouth peace to his fellow he speaks, but in his midst he sets a wait.

9 Shall I not punish them for these things? saith the LORD: shall not my soul avenge itself on such a nation?

10 I will lift up weeping and lamentation on the mountains, and on the pastures of the wilderness a lamentation, for they are burned up without a man passing through, and they do not hear the sound of cattle; from the bird of the heavens even to the beast, they have wandered away, they are gone.

11 And I will give Jerusalem for heaps, a den of serpents; and I will give the cities of Judah for desolation, so that no one dwells there.

12 Who is the man wise and understanding of this, to whom the word of the LORD has spoken, that he may declare why the land has perished and is burned like a wilderness without one passing through?

13 And the LORD said, Because they have forsaken my law which I set before them, and have not obeyed my voice, nor walked in it.

14 They went after the lust of their heart and after the Baalim whom their fathers had taught them.

15 Therefore thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am feeding this people wormwood and making them drink waters of poison.

16 And I will scatter them among the nations whom they do not know, they and their fathers, and I will send the sword after them until I have finished them.

17 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Consider, and call for the mourning women to come; and send for the wise women to come.

18 And speedily let lamentation come upon us; our eyes shall shed tears, and our eyelids flow with water.

19 For a voice of lamentation is heard from Zion: How are we ruined! We are greatly ashamed, for we have forsaken the land, for they have cast out our dwellings.

20 For the women are to hear the word of the LORD, and your ears are to receive the word of his mouth; and teach your daughters lamentation, and each woman her neighbor a lament.

21 For death has come up through our windows, entered our palaces, to cut off the child from the outside, the young men from the streets.

22 Thus saith the LORD: And the dead body of man shall fall as dung upon the face of the field, and as a handful after the reaper, and there is none to gather.

23 Thus says the LORD: Let not the wise person boast in their wisdom, and let not the strong person boast in their strength, and let not the rich person boast in their riches.

24 But let the one who boasts boast in this: to be wise and know me, for I am the LORD who does favour, judgment, and justice in the land, for in these I delight, says the LORD.

25 Behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will punish all who are circumcised in uncircumcision.

26 Against Egypt and against Judah and against the Edomites and against the sons of Ammon and against Moab and against all those who cut the corners of their hair, who live in the wilderness: for all the nations are uncircumcised, and all the house of Israel are uncircumcised in heart.


## Chapter 10

1 Hear the word that the LORD has spoken against you, house of Israel.

2 Thus says the LORD, "Do not learn the custom of the nations, and do not be dismayed by the signs of the heavens, for the nations are dismayed by them."

3 For the customs of the nations are vanity, for a tree from the forest, its cutting, the work of the hands of a craftsman with an ax.

4 With silver and gold they beautify it; with nails and hammers they secure them, but it cannot save.

5 Like a palm tree of beaten work they stand and do not speak; carried they must be carried, for they cannot walk; do not fear them, for they cannot harm, nor can they do good.

6 There is no one like you, O Lord; you are great, and your name is great in might.

7 Who would not revere you, O king of the nations? For to you it belongs; because among all the wise ones of the nations and in all their realms there is none like you.

8 And in one instant they shall be burned and wither; the chastening of vanities is a tree.

9 Silver from Tarshish is brought, and gold from Uphaz, the work of the artificer and the hands of the goldsmith; blue and purple are their clothing; all of them are the work of skilled men.

10 The LORD is a God of truth; he is the living God and an eternal king; from his wrath the earth trembles, and the nations cannot endure his indignation.

11 Thus shall you say to them: "The gods who did not make heaven and earth—these shall perish from the earth and from beneath the heavens."

12 Making the earth by his power, establishing the world by his wisdom, and stretching out the heavens by his understanding.

13 At the sound of his giving, the abundance of waters in the heavens; he raises vapours from the uttermost edges; he brings forth lightning for the rain; he sends out the wind from his storehouses.

14 Every man is dumb from knowledge; every craftsman is put to shame by the carved image, for false is its molten image, and there is no spirit in them.

15 Vanity they are, acts of error; at the time of their account they will perish.

16 Not like these is the inheritance of Jacob, for He is the former of all, and Israel is the tribe of His inheritance; the LORD of hosts is His name.

17 Gather up your bundle from the ground, you who dwell under siege.

18 For thus says the LORD: Behold, I am about to make the inhabitants of the land tremble this once, and I will bring distress upon them so that they may be found.

19 Woe is me because of my affliction; my wound is grievous. Yet I said, "Surely this is sickness, and I will bear it."

20 My covering is plundered and all its cords are torn; my children have departed from me and are no more; there is no one to stretch out my covering again or to raise my curtains.

21 For the shepherds have become senseless and have not sought Jehovah; therefore they have not prospered, and all their pasture has been scattered.

22 A voice, a report: behold, it comes—great trembling from the land of the north, to make the cities of Judah a desolation, a dwelling of serpents.

23 I know, O LORD, that a man's way is not in himself, nor is it in a man who walks to direct his step.

24 Jehovah, correct me, but only in justice; do not in your anger, lest you diminish me.

25 Pour out Your anger upon the nations who do not know You, and upon the families who have not called upon Your name; for they have devoured Jacob, and consumed him, and laid waste his dwelling place.


## Chapter 11

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Hear the words of this covenant and speak them to the men of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

3 And you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Cursed is the man who does not obey the words of this covenant.

4 which I commanded your forefathers on the day I brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the furnace of iron, saying, "Obey my voice and do according to all that I command you, and you shall be my people, and I will be your God."

5 To confirm the oath that I swore to your ancestors, to give them a land flowing with milk and honey, as this day; and I answered and said, Amen: the LORD.

6 And the LORD said to me, Call all these words in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, saying, Hearken to the words of this covenant, and do them.

7 For I have testified against your fathers from the day I brought them out of the land of Egypt even to this day, rising early and testifying, saying, "Obey my voice."

8 They did not listen and did not incline their ear; each man walked in the stubbornness of his evil heart, so I brought upon them all the words of this covenant that I commanded to do, but they did not do.

9 And the LORD said to me, A conspiracy has been found among the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

10 They returned to the faults of their former forefathers, who refused to listen to my words, and they went after other gods to serve them; the house of Israel and the house of Judah broke my covenant which I made with their forefathers.

11 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Behold, I am bringing adversity upon them, which they will not be able to escape; and they will cry out to me, but I will not listen to them.

12 And the cities of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem will go and cry to the gods to whom they are offering incense, but those gods will not save them in the time of their adversity.

13 For your gods were as numerous as the cities of Judah, and for the number of Jerusalem’s streets you placed altars to shame, altars to burn incense to Baal.

14 But you, do not pray for this folk, and do not raise for them cry or prayer, for I am not listening at the time they cry to me for their trouble.

15 What has my beloved to do in my house, having wrought the many wicked devices? Though holy flesh passes from upon you, when your evil comes, then you will rejoice.

16 An olive tree, lush and fair in fruit and form, the LORD has called your name; at the sound of great tumult he set fire upon it, and its branches are scorched.

17 And the LORD of hosts who planted you has spoken evil concerning you for the evil of the house of Israel and the house of Judah which they have done to provoke My anger to burn incense to Baal.

18 And the LORD made me know, and I knew; then You showed me their doings.

19 But I was like a gentle lamb led to slaughter, and I did not know that they devised cunning against me, saying, Let us destroy the tree with its food, and cut it off from the land of the living, and his name be remembered no more.

20 And the LORD of hosts judges righteously, testing kidneys and heart; I will see your vengeance upon them, for to you I have revealed my cause.

21 Therefore, thus says the LORD concerning the men of Anathoth who seek your life, saying, "You shall not prophesy in the name of the LORD, or you will die by our hand."

22 Therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, I am about to punish them; the young men shall die by the sword, their sons and their daughters shall die by famine.

23 And no remnant shall be left to them, for I will bring calamity upon the men of Anathoth, the year of their visitation.


## Chapter 12

1 You are righteous, O LORD, when I bring my case before You; yet I will speak judgments with You: Why does the way of the wicked prosper? They are all at ease who deal very treacherously.

2 You planted them and they took root; they grow and also bear fruit. You are near in their mouth, but far from their kidneys.

3 But you, O LORD, know me; you see me and test my heart toward you: set them apart like sheep for slaughter, set them apart for the day of slaughter.

4 How long will the land mourn and the grass of every field wither because of the evil of those who dwell in it? The beasts and the birds have been swept away, for they said, "He will not see our latter end."

5 Because you have run with the horsemen and they have wearied you, how will you contend with horses? And in a land of peace you are confident, but how will you do in the pride of the Jordan?

6 For even your brothers and your father’s house have betrayed you; they have also raised a cry after you; do not trust them, though they speak good to you.

7 I have forsaken my house, I have abandoned my heritage; I have given into the hand of her enemies the dearly beloved of my soul.

8 My heritage became to me like a lion in the forest; it cried out against me—therefore I hate it.

9 Is the ravenous bird my heritage to me? The ravenous bird is round about her: come, gather all the beasts of the field, they shall devour.

10 Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard; they have trampled my field; they have given my pleasant field over to a desolate wilderness.

11 She has been laid most desolate, she mourns for me; desolation covers all the earth, for there is no man who takes it to heart.

12 On every high place in the wilderness came marauders, for a sword belonging to the LORD is devouring from the end of the land to the end of the land; there is no peace for any flesh.

13 Sow wheat and reap thorns; you have labored but it profits nothing; you shall be ashamed of your fruit because of the fierce wrath of the LORD.

14 Thus says the LORD concerning all the evil neighbors who touch the heritage that I caused my people, Israel, to inherit: Behold, I am about to uproot them from their land, and the house of Judah I will uproot from among them.

15 And after I have uprooted them, I will return and have compassion on them, and I will bring back each to his inheritance and each to his land.

16 And it shall be, if they diligently learn the ways of my people, to swear by my name, “As the LORD lives,” just as they taught my people to swear by Baal, then they shall be built within my people.

17 But if they will not listen, then I will utterly destroy and abandon that nation, says the LORD.


## Chapter 13

1 Thus says the LORD to me: Go and buy for yourself a linen girdle, and put it on your loins; and do not bring it into the water.

2 And I bought the girdle according to the word of the LORD, and I put it on my loins.

3 And the word of the LORD came to me a second time, saying:

4 Take the girdle that you bought, which is on your loins, and arise, go to the Euphrates and hide it there in a hole of the rock.

5 And I went and buried it in the Euphrates, as the LORD commanded me.

6 After many days had passed, the LORD said to me, "Go, take for yourself the girdle from the Euphrates, which I commanded you to hide there."

7 I went to the Euphrates and dug and took the girdle from the place where I had hidden it, and behold, the girdle was ruined; it was not fit for anything.

8 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

9 Thus says the LORD, so will I destroy the arrogancy of Judah and the great arrogancy of Jerusalem.

10 This folk, the evil ones who refuse to listen to my words, who walk in the lust of their heart, and go after other gods to serve them and bow down to them, will be like this girdle, which will not succeed in anything.

11 For as a girdle cleaves to the loins of a man, so I caused the whole house of Israel and the whole house of Judah to cleave to me, says the LORD, to be my people, for name, and for praise, and for beauty; but they would not hearken.

12 And you shall say to them this word: Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Every vessel shall be filled with wine. And they shall say to you, Do you indeed not know that every vessel will be filled with wine?

13 Thus says the LORD, Behold, I will fill all the inhabitants of the land, and the kings who sit upon David's throne, and the priests, and the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, with drunkenness.

14 I will scatter them, every man from his brother, and fathers together with sons, declares the LORD; I will neither pity nor spare nor have mercy; I will destroy them.

15 Hear and give ear; do not be proud, for Jehovah has spoken.

16 Give glory to the LORD your God before darkness comes, and before your feet stumble on the mountains of twilight; and you wait for light, but He makes it into the shadow of death, into thick darkness.

17 But if you will not listen to her, in secret my soul will weep because of pride, and my eye will weep tears and flow down, for the flock of the LORD has been taken captive.

18 Say to the king and the queen, “Humble yourselves, for your principalities have fallen, the crown of your majesty.”

19 The cities of the south are shut up, and no one opens; Judah is utterly exiled, wholly carried away captive.

20 Lift up your eyes and see those coming from the north. Where is the flock that was given to you, your beautiful flock?

21 What will you say when he visits you, though you have taught these against you, captains at the head? Will not pangs seize you like a woman in labor?

22 And if you say in your heart, "Why have these things befallen me?" because of the abundance of your iniquity your skirts are uncovered and your heels are harmed.

23 Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? Then you also could do good, you who are taught to do evil.

24 And I will scatter them like stubble before the wind of the wilderness.

25 This is your lot, the portion of your measure from me, says the LORD, because you have forgotten me and trusted in falsehood.

26 And I also uncovered your skirt over your face, and your shame was exposed.

27 Your adultery and your neighing, the lewdness of your whoredom, your abominable filth—I have seen it on the hills in the wild; woe to you, Jerusalem; you will not be cleansed; how long yet?


## Chapter 14

1 The word that came from the LORD to Jeremiah concerning the dearths.

2 Judah mourns, its gates languish; they are bowed down to the earth; the cry of Jerusalem has gone up.

3 And their cattle they sent for water, they came to the pits, they found no water; they returned their vessels empty; they were ashamed and confounded, and covered their heads.

4 Because of the land it was parched, for there was no rain in the earth; the husbandmen were ashamed, they covered their heads.

5 For even the hind in the wild gave birth and abandoned, for there was no green.

6 And the wild asses stood on the high places, they snorted air like serpents, their eyes failed for lack of grass.

7 If our sins have punished us, O LORD, act for the sake of your name, for our backslidings to you are many, we have sinned.

8 Israel's hope, their deliverer in time of distress: why should you be as a stranger in the land, or as a traveler turning aside to lodge?

9 Why should you be as a man dismayed, like a mighty man who cannot save? But you are in our midst, O LORD, and your name is called upon us; do not forsake us.

10 Thus says the LORD to this people: They have loved to wander; their legs have not stayed still, and the LORD has not been pleased with them; now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins.

11 And the LORD said to me, Do not pray for this people for good.

12 When they fast, I do not hear their cry; when they offer burnt offering and gift, I do not accept them; for by sword and by famine and by pestilence I am destroying them.

13 And I said, Ah, (my) Lord God, behold, the prophets say to them, You shall not see sword, nor shall famine be among you, for true peace I will give to you in this place.

14 And the LORD said to me, "False are the prophets who prophesy in my name; I did not send them, nor did I command them, nor did I speak to them—false vision, the divination of their heart, they are prophesying to you."

15 Therefore, thus says the LORD concerning the prophets who prophesy in my name, though I did not send them, and who say, "Sword and famine shall not be in this land": by sword and by famine these prophets shall be consumed.

16 And the folk whom they prophesy to shall be cast out in the streets of Jerusalem because of the famine and the sword, and there shall be none to bury them, their wives and their sons and their daughters, and I will pour out upon them their evil.

17 And you shall say to them this word: My eyes shall weep with tears night and day and not cease, for the virgin daughter of my people is severely wounded with a grievous breach.

18 If I go out into the field, and behold, the slain by the sword, and if I enter the city, and behold, the sick from famine, for both prophet and priest have traded to the land, and they do not know.

19 Have you utterly rejected Judah? Is your soul utterly disgusted with Zion? Why have you struck us down so that we have no healing? We looked for peace, but there is no good; for a time of healing, and behold, trouble.

20 We know, O the LORD, our iniquity, the iniquity of our forefathers; indeed, we have sinned against you.

21 Do not despise for the sake of your name, do not dishonor the throne of your glory; remember, do not annul your covenant with us.

22 Are there showers in the vanities of the nations, or will the heavens give showers? Is it not you, O LORD our God, whom we hope in? For you have made all these things.


## Chapter 15

1 And the LORD said to me, Even if Moses and Samuel stood before me, my soul would not be toward this people: send them away from before my presence, and let them go out.

2 And when they say to you, “Where are we going?” you shall say to them, “Thus says the LORD: Whoever is for death, to death; and whoever is for the sword, to the sword; and whoever is for famine, to famine; and whoever is for captivity, to captivity.”

3 And I will punish them with four kinds, saith the LORD: with the sword to slay, and with dogs to drag, and with the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth to eat and to destroy.

4 And I will give them to all the kingdoms of the earth because of Manasseh the son of Hezekiah king of Judah for what he did in Jerusalem.

5 For who will have pity on you, Jerusalem, and who will mourn for you, and who will turn aside to ask about your welfare?

6 Thou hast forsaken me, saith the LORD, backward shallst thou go; and I stretched out my hand upon thee, and destroyed thee; I am weary with repenting.

7 I scattered them with a fan at the gates of the land; I destroyed my people; they did not return from their ways.

8 Her widows became more numerous than the sand of the seas; I brought upon her, against the mother of a young man, a despoiler at midday; I suddenly brought upon her anguish and terrors.

9 The one who bore seven is weary; her breath fails—her sun sets while it is still day; she is ashamed and confounded. I will give their remnant to the sword before their enemies, declares the LORD.

10 Woe to me, my mother, for she bore me a man of strife and a man of contention to the whole earth; I have not lent nor have I borrowed, yet all of them curse me.

11 The LORD said, Surely I will set you free for good; surely I will cause the enemy to plead with you in a time of adversity and in a time of trouble.

12 Is iron broken, iron from the north and bronze?

13 Your able and your storehouses I will give as spoil, not for a price, because of all your sins and throughout all your borders.

14 And I will drive your enemies into a land you do not know, for a fiery anger burns in my nostrils against you.

15 You, O LORD, know; remember me and visit me, and avenge me of those who persecute me; do not, in the long-suffering of Your anger, take me away; know the reproach that I bear because of You.

16 Your words were found and I ate them, and they became to me joy and rejoicing of heart, because your name is called upon me, the LORD of hosts.

17 I did not sit in the assembly of those who mock, nor did I rejoice; because of your hand I sat alone, for indignation has filled me.

18 Why is my grief perpetual and my wound incurable, refusing to be healed? It has become to me like waters not to be trusted.

19 Therefore, thus says the LORD: If you return, then I will restore you before my face; if you bring out the precious from the vile, you will be as my mouth; they will return to you, but you will not return to them.

20 And I have appointed thee for this folk a brasen wall of fortification, and they shall fight against thee but shall not prevail against thee, for I am with thee to save thee and to deliver thee, saith the LORD.

21 And I delivered you from the hand of the wicked, and I rescued you from the power of the oppressors.


## Chapter 16

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 You shall not take a wife, and there shall be no sons or daughters for you in this place.

3 For thus says the LORD concerning the children and the daughters born in this place, and concerning their mothers who bear them and their fathers who beget them in this land:

4 They shall die the deaths of diseases; they shall not be lamented, nor buried; they shall become dung upon the face of the ground; and by sword and by famine they shall be consumed; and their dead bodies shall become food for the birds of the heavens and for the beasts of the earth.

5 For thus says the LORD, Do not enter the house of mourning, nor go to weep for them, nor console them, for I have taken away my peace from this people, says the LORD, even my kindness and my tender mercies.

6 And the great and the small shall die in this land; they shall not be buried, nor shall anyone weep for them, nor cut themselves, nor make themselves bald for them.

7 And they shall not break bread for them in mourning to comfort them for the dead, nor shall they give them a cup of consolation for their father and for their mother.

8 and a house of feasting you shall not enter to sit with them to eat and to drink.

9 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I will remove from this place, before your eyes and in your days, the voice of gladness and the voice of joy, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride.

10 And it shall be, when you tell this folk all these words, and they say to you, “Why has the LORD spoken all this great evil against us? What is our fault, and what is our sin that we have sinned against the LORD our God?”

11 And you shall say to them, Because your fathers have forsaken me, says the LORD, and have gone after other gods, and have served them and have worshiped them, and me they have forsaken, and my law they have not kept.

12 But you have acted worse than your forefathers, and behold, you are each walking after the stubbornness of his evil heart, so as not to listen to me.

13 And I will cast you from off the land upon the land which you and your fathers have not known, and there you shall serve other gods day and night, where I will show you no favour.

14 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when no longer will one say, "As the LORD lives, who brought up the people of Israel from the land of Egypt."

15 But as the LORD lives, who brought up the sons of Israel from the land of the north and from all the lands where he had banished them, I will bring them back to their land that I gave to their fathers.

16 Behold, I am sending many fishers, declares the LORD, and they shall fish them; and afterward I will send many hunters, and they shall hunt them from upon every mountain and from upon every hill and from the holes of the ragged rocks.

17 For my eyes are on all their ways; they are not hidden from my presence, and their iniquity is not concealed from before my eyes.

18 I will repay their first sin and their guilt, for they have filled my inheritance with the corpses of their detestable and abominable things, defiling my land.

19 The LORD is my strength, my stronghold, and my refuge in the day of distress; to You nations shall come from the ends of the earth and say, "Only falsehood have our fathers inherited, vanity, and there is no benefit in them."

20 Will [a] man [of low degree] make God [of] himself, yet they [are] not God [at all]?

21 Therefore, behold, I will this once make them know my hand and my might, and they shall know that my name is the LORD.


## Chapter 17

1 The punishment of Judah is written with an iron pen, with the point of a diamond; it is engraved on the tablet of their heart and on the horns of your altars.

2 As they remember their children, so they remember their altars and their groves by the green trees upon the high hills.

3 The hills in the land, your strength and all your treasures I will give as spoil, your high places for your sin throughout all your borders.

4 You will let go and you will be removed from your inheritance that I gave you, and I will make you serve your enemies in the land that you do not know, because the fire of my wrath has burned against you forever.

5 Thus says the LORD: Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength, and whose heart turns away from the LORD.

6 And he will be like a shrub in the desert, and will not see when good comes; he will dwell in parched places in the wilderness, a salt land that does not remain inhabited.

7 Blessed is the man that trusts in the LORD, and whose confidence is the LORD.

8 It will be like a tree planted by water, sending its roots by a river; it will not feel the heat, its leaf will remain green; in a year of drought it will not worry, nor will it cease producing fruit.

9 The heart is more deceitful than anything and beyond cure—who can understand it?

10 I am the LORD who searches the heart and tests the kidneys, to give to each person according to the fruit of their deeds.

11 The partridge gathers but does not hatch; he who amasses riches unjustly—half of his days he will forsake them, and in the end he will be a fool.

12 A throne of glory, high above the beginning, the place of our sanctuary.

13 The hope of Israel, the LORD, all those that forsake you shall be ashamed, they shall be written in the earth, because they have forsaken the fountain of living waters, even the LORD.

14 Heal me, O LORD, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved: for you are my praise.

15 Behold, they say to me, "Where is the word of the LORD? Let it come now."

16 But I did not covet your shepherd's wages, nor did I desire a day of trouble; you know the utterance of my lips was before your face.

17 Do not become to me a source of terror; you are my refuge in the day of adversity.

18 Let my persecutors be shamed and not let me be shamed; let them be dismayed and not let me be dismayed; bring upon them a day of adversity and with double destruction destroy them.

19 Thus says the LORD to me: Go and stand in the gate where the kings of Judah enter and go out, and in all the gates of Jerusalem.

20 And you shall say to them, Hear the word of the LORD, all kings of Judah, and all Judah, and all inhabitants of Jerusalem, who enter by these gates.

21 Thus says the LORD: Take heed to your souls; do not carry a burden on the sabbath day, nor bring it in by the gates of Jerusalem.

22 You shall not carry a burden out of your homes on the sabbath day, and no work shall you do; and you shall hallow the sabbath day as I commanded your fathers.

23 And they did not listen, nor did they incline their ear; they made their neck stiff, so as not to hear and not to receive discipline.

24 And it shall come to pass, if ye diligently hearken unto me, saith the LORD, not to bring in a burden through the gates of this city on the sabbath day, and to hallow the sabbath day, not to do therein any manner of work,

25 And rulers shall enter the gates of this city, kings and princes sitting on the throne of David, riding in chariots and on horses, they and their princes, every man of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and this city shall dwell forever.

26 And they came from the cities of Judah and from the towns around Jerusalem and from the land of Benjamin and from the low plain and from the hill country and from the south, bringing burnt offerings and sacrifices and grain offerings and frankincense and thank offerings to the house of the LORD.

27 But if you do not listen to me to consecrate the sabbath day and not to carry a burden and to enter by the gates of Jerusalem on the sabbath day, then I will kindle a fire in its gates and it will consume the palaces of Jerusalem and not be quenched.


## Chapter 18

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Arise, go down to the potter's house, and there I will cause you to hear my words.

3 And I went down to the potter's house, and behold, he was working at the wheel.

4 And the vessel made of clay was ruined under the hand of the potter, so he returned and made it another vessel, as seemed right in the eyes of the potter to make.

5 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

6 Is not the LORD able to deal with you as this potter, O house of Israel? says the LORD. Behold, as the clay in the potter's hand, so are you in my hand, O house of Israel.

7 In an instant I will speak concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, to uproot and to pull down and to destroy.

8 And the nation turns from its evil, which I spoke concerning it, then I relent concerning the evil which I thought to do to it.

9 And suddenly I will speak concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, to build and to plant.

10 and he will do the evil in my sight so as not to listen to my voice, and I will regret the good that I said I would do for him.

11 Now therefore say to the man Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem: Thus says the LORD: Behold, I am forming adversity against you and devising a device against you; turn back now each from his evil way and make good your ways and your doings.

12 And they will say, "We will follow our own devices and every man will do the lust of his evil heart."

13 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Ask now among the nations, Who has heard such a horrible thing, as the virgin of Israel has greatly done?

14 Will he forsake the edge of the field of snow of Lebanon, or will the alien waters, cold, flowing, be cut off?

15 For my people have forgotten me, they sacrifice falsely; I made them stumble in their ways, in the ancient paths, to walk in pathless ways, in a road not paved.

16 To make their land a desolation forever; everyone passing over it will be appalled and shake his head.

17 Like an east wind I will scatter them before their enemy; I will show them the back and not the face in the day of their calamity.

18 They said, "Come, let us devise cunning plans against Jeremiah, for the law shall not perish from the priest, nor counsel from the wise, nor word from the prophet. Come, let us strike him with the tongue, and not listen to any of his words."

19 Hear me, O LORD, and listen to the voice of my adversaries.

20 Shall adversity be repaid for good, because they have dug a pit for my life? Remember how I stood before your presence to speak good on their behalf, to turn away your wrath from them.

21 Therefore give their children over to famine and deliver them to the sword; let their wives become childless and widows, and let their men be dead men, their young men struck down by the sword in battle.

22 A cry will be heard from their homes, for you will bring against them a troop suddenly; for they have plotted to ensnare me, and snares they have hidden for my feet.

23 And you, O LORD, know all their counsel against me for death; do not atone for their iniquity, and their sin from your presence do not wipe out; they are a stumbling block before you—deal with them in the time of your anger.


## Chapter 19

1 Thus says the LORD, Go and buy a potter's earthen bottle, and take some of the elders of the people and some of the elders of the priests.

2 And you shall go out to the valley of the son of Hinnom, which is by the entrance of the Potsherd Gate, and there you shall proclaim the words that I will speak to you.

3 And you shall say, Hear the word of the LORD, kings of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem: Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am bringing disaster on this place, so that whoever hears of it, their ears will tingle.

4 Because they have forsaken me and have profaned this place, and have burned incense in it to other gods whom they did not know, they and their fathers and the kings of Judah, and have filled this place with innocent blood.

5 And they built the high places of Baal to burn their children in the fire as burnt offerings to Baal, which I did not command and did not speak and did not come upon my mind.

6 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when this place will no longer be called Topheth and the valley of the son of Hinnom, but the valley of slaughter.

7 And I will punish the counsel of Judah and Jerusalem in this place, and I will cause them to fall by the sword before their enemies and by the hand of those who seek their life, and I will give their dead bodies as food to the birds of the heavens and to the beasts of the earth.

8 I will make this city a desolation and a hissing; everyone who passes by it will be appalled and hiss at all its plagues.

9 And I will make them eat the body of their sons and the body of their daughters, and each man will eat the body of his neighbor, during the siege and the distress with which their enemies and those who seek their lives will distress them.

10 And you shall break the bottle in the sight of the men who walk with you.

11 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Just as a potter's vessel is broken and cannot be repaired, so I will break this people and this city. They will bury in Topheth because there is no place to bury.

12 So will I do to this place, says the LORD, and to the inhabitants thereof, and will make this city like Topheth.

13 And the courts of Jerusalem and the courts of the kings of Judah will be defiled like the place of Topheth, for all the courts where they burned incense on their roofs to all the host of heaven and poured out drink offerings to other gods.

14 Then Jeremiah came from Topheth, where the LORD had sent him to prophesy, and he stood in the court of the temple of the LORD, and spoke to all the people.

15 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am bringing upon this city and upon all its towns all the adversity that I have spoken against it, because they have stiffened their neck so as not to hear my words.


## Chapter 20

1 And Pashur the son of Immer the priest, who was overseer and leader in the house of the LORD, heard Jeremiah prophesying these words.

2 Then Pashur struck Jeremiah the prophet and put him in the stocks that were in the upper gate of Benjamin at the house of the LORD.

3 And it came to pass on the morrow that Pashur brought Jeremiah forth out of the stocks; then said Jeremiah unto him, The LORD hath not called thy name Pashur, but Magormissabib.

4 For thus says the LORD: I am going to make you a terror to yourself and to all your lovers, and they will fall by the sword before your eyes; and all Judah I will give into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will carry them to Babylon and strike them down with the sword.

5 And I will give into the hand of their enemies all the riches of this city, all its labour, all its precious things, and all the treasuries of the kings of Judah; they shall plunder them and take them and carry them to Babylon.

6 And you, Pashur, and all the inhabitants of your house shall go into captivity, and you shall come to Babylon, and there you shall die, and there you shall be buried, you and all your lovers, to whom you have prophesied falsely.

7 You have enticed me, O LORD, and I am crushed; You have overpowered me and prevailed; I have become a derision all the day—everyone mocks me.

8 For as often as I speak, I cry out, proclaiming violence and destruction; for the word of the LORD has become to me a reproach and derision all day long.

9 And I said, I will not mention him nor speak any more in his fame; but there was in my mind as a burning fire shut up in my bones, and I was weary with holding back and could not.

10 For I heard the defaming of many, fear all around: “Report, and we will report on him!” All who were at peace with me watched for my halting, saying, “Perhaps he will be enticed, then we will prevail over him, and take our vengeance on him.”

11 And the LORD made me like a strong warrior; therefore my pursuers will stumble and not prevail; they will be greatly shamed, for they did not act wisely—a perpetual reproach that will not be forgotten.

12 And the LORD of hosts, who tests the righteous, sees the kidneys and the heart: I will see your vengeance upon them, for to you I have revealed my cause.

13 Sing to the LORD, praise Jehovah, for he has delivered the life of the needy one from the hand of the wicked.

14 Cursed be the day on which I was born; let not the day on which my mother bore me be blessed.

15 Cursed be the man who brings news to my father, saying, "A male child has been born," and rejoices him with joy.

16 And that man was like the city which the LORD overthrew and did not relent, and he heard a cry in the morning and a shout at the time of midday.

17 who did not let me die from the womb; my mother became my grave, and the womb that conceived me [is] alway.

18 Why have I come forth from the womb to see grievance and sorrow, and that my days end in shame?


## Chapter 21

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD when King Zedekiah sent to him Pashhur the son of Malchiah and Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah the priest, saying:

2 Please seek the Lord on our behalf, for Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon is fighting against us; perhaps the Lord will do with us according to all his wonders and remove him from us.

3 Jeremiah said to them, "Thus you shall say to Zedekiah:"

4 Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Behold, I will cause to return the weapons of war that are in your hands, with which you are fighting against the king of Babylon and the Chaldeans who are besieging you outside the wall, and I will gather them into the midst of this city.

5 I myself will fight against you with outstretched hand and with mighty arm, and with anger, with wrath, and with great indignation.

6 And I will strike the inhabitants of this city, and the man and the beast, with a great pestilence; they shall die.

7 And after this saith the LORD I will give Zedekiah king of Judah and his servants and the people and those left in this city from the pestilence from the sword and from the famine into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their lives and he will strike them by the sword he will not pity them nor have compassion nor show mercy.

8 And to this people you shall say, Thus says the LORD, Behold, I set before you the way of life and the way of death.

9 Whoever stays in this city will die by the sword, by famine, and by plague, but whoever goes out and surrenders to the Chaldeans who are besieging you will live, for his life will be spared as plunder.

10 For I have set my face against this city for evil and not for good, declares the LORD: it shall be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall burn it with fire.

11 And to the house of the king of Judah, hear the word of the LORD.

12 The court of David: thus says the LORD, Execute justice in the morning and deliver the robbed from the hand of the oppressor, lest My anger go out like fire and burn with none to quench it because of the evil of their deeds.

13 Behold, I am against you, O dweller in the valley, the rock of the plain, declares the LORD, who say, Who will come against us? or who will enter our dwelling places?

14 I will punish you according to the fruit of your doings, says the LORD; and I will kindle a fire in its forest, and it shall devour all that is round about it.


## Chapter 22

1 Thus says the LORD, Go down to the house of the king of Judah, and speak there the word that follows.

2 And you shall say, “Hear the word of the LORD, O king of Judah who sits upon the throne of David, you and your servants and your people who enter by these gates.”

3 Thus says the LORD: Do justice and righteousness, and deliver the victim from the hand of the oppressor; and do not wrong the alien, the fatherless, and the widow; do not shed innocent blood in this place.

4 But if you truly carry out this act, then kings from David’s line shall enter the gates of this house, sitting on his throne, riding in chariots and on horses, he and his people.

5 But if you will not listen to these words, by myself have I sworn, declares the LORD, that this house shall become a desolate place.

6 For thus says the LORD concerning the house of the king of Judah: You are Gilead to me, the head of Lebanon; if not, I will make you a wilderness, cities not inhabited.

7 I will consecrate against you destroyers, each one and his weapons, and they shall cut down the choicest of your cedars and cast them into the fire.

8 And many nations will pass by this city, and each man will say to his companion, Why has the LORD done thus to this great city?

9 They will say, Because they have forsaken the covenant of the LORD their God, and have bowed down to other gods and served them.

10 Do not weep for the dead, nor mourn for him; weep bitterly for the one who goes away, for he shall not return again to see his native land.

11 For thus says the LORD to Shallum son of Josiah king of Judah, who reigned instead of Josiah his father, who went out from this place: He shall not return there again.

12 For in the country to which they are exiled there he shall die, and that land he shall never see again.

13 Woe to him who builds his house by injustice and his upper chambers by unjust judgment, who makes his neighbor serve him without pay and does not give him his work.

14 who says, I will build for myself a house of measuring and spacious upper chambers, and cut out for him windows, and panel it with cedar and paint it with vermillion.

15 Will you reign because you compete with the cedar tree? Did not your father eat and drink and execute judgment and justice? Then it was good for him.

16 To judge the cause of the poor and the needy is good; is not this to know me? says the LORD.

17 For your eyes and your heart are not on anything but your dishonest gain, and on shedding innocent blood, and on oppression and violence to do it.

18 Therefore, thus says the LORD concerning Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah: They shall not lament for him, saying, "Alas, my brother!" or "Alas, my sister!" They shall not lament for him, saying, "Alas, lord!" or "Alas, glory!"

19 The burial of an ass will be buried; carry and cast out beyond the gates of Jerusalem.

20 Go up to Lebanon and cry out, and in Bashan raise your voice, cry from the Abarim, for all your lovers are shattered.

21 I spoke to you in your prosperity, you said, I will not listen; this is your way from your youth, that you have not listened to my voice.

22 All your shepherds will be scattered by the wind, and your lovers will go into captivity; then you will be ashamed and disgraced because of all your wickedness.

23 In Lebanon, among the cedars, how are you in pain with your coming pangs, the pain of a woman in labor?

24 As I live, saith the LORD, though Coniah the son of Jehoiakim king of Judah were the signet upon my right hand, yet would I pluck thee thence.

25 I will give you into the hand of those who seek your life, and into the hand of those from whose presence you are fleeing, and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of the Chaldeans.

26 And I will cast you out, and your mother who bore you, into another land where you were not born, and there you will die.

27 And upon the land, whose life they long to return, there they shall not return.

28 The grievous idol is despised, scattered is this man Coniah; as a vessel in which there is no delight, why are he and his seed carried away and cast upon the earth which they did not know?

29 Land, land, land, hear the word of the LORD.

30 Thus says the LORD, Write this man down as childless, a man who will not succeed in his days; for there will be no man of his seed who will sit on the throne of David or rule still in Judah.


## Chapter 23

1 Woe to the shepherds who destroy and scatter the flock of my pasture, declares the LORD.

2 Therefore, thus says the LORD, God of Israel, concerning the shepherds who shepherd my people: You have scattered my flock and driven them away, and have not attended to them. Behold, I will attend to you for the evil of your deeds, declares the LORD.

3 But I will gather the remnant of my flock from all the lands where I have driven them, and I will bring them back to their dwellings, and they shall be fruitful and multiply.

4 And I will raise up shepherds over them, and they shall feed them; and they shall fear no more, nor be dismayed, nor be lacking, (hath) said the LORD.

5 Behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will raise up for David a righteous Branch, and he shall reign as king and act wisely, and execute justice and righteousness in the land.

6 In his days Judah shall be saved and Israel shall dwell securely, and this is the name by which he shall be called: the LORD our righteousness.

7 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when they shall no longer say, "As the LORD lives, who brought up the children of Israel from the land of Egypt."

8 But as the LORD lives, who brought up and brought back the seed of the house of Israel from the land of the north and from all the lands where I scattered them, they shall dwell again on their own land.

9 My heart is broken within me; all my bones tremble; I am like a man overcome, and like a mighty man overcome with wine, because of the LORD and because of the words of his holiness.

10 For the land is full of adulterers; because of the curse the land mourns, the habitations of the wilderness are dried up; their course is evil, and their strength is not right.

11 For both prophet and priest are wicked; even in my temple I have found their evil, says the LORD.

12 Therefore their way will be to them like slippery places in the dark; they will be thrust aside and fall therein, for I am bringing upon them adversity, the year of their visitation, declares the LORD.

13 And in the prophets of Samaria I saw folly: they prophesy by Baal and cause my people Israel to err.

14 And in the prophets of Jerusalem I saw a horrible thing: they commit adultery and walk in falsehood; they strengthen the hands of evildoers, so that no one turns from his wickedness. All of them are to me like Sodom, and her inhabitants like Gomorrah.

15 Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts concerning the prophets: Behold, I will feed them with wormwood and give them poison to drink; for from the prophets of Jerusalem has gone forth profaneness to all the land.

16 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you; they are deluding you. They speak visions of their own heart, not from the mouth of the LORD.

17 They say, saying to those who despise me, "Thus says the LORD: Peace will be yours," and to everyone who walks in the stubbornness of his heart they say, "No disaster will come upon you."

18 For who has stood in the counsel of the LORD, that he should see and hear his word? Who has paid attention and listened?

19 Behold, a storm of the LORD, [even] wrath, is gone forth, and a tempestuous storm shall whirl upon the head of the wicked.

20 The wrath of the LORD will not return until He has done and until He has established the intents of His heart; in the latter days you will consider this with understanding.

21 I did not send the prophets, yet they ran; I did not speak to them, yet they prophesied.

22 But if they stand in my counsel and hear my words to my people, and turn them from their evil way and from the wickedness of their deeds,

23 I am a God near at hand, declares the LORD, and not a God afar off.

24 Can a man hide himself in secret places and I not see him? says the LORD. Do I not fill the heavens and the earth? says the LORD.

25 I have heard what the prophets have prophesied in my name falsely, saying, "I dreamed, I dreamed."

26 How long will there be in the heart of the prophets who prophesy falsehood, the prophets of deceitful heart?

27 Those who plan to make my folk forget my name by their dreams, which each man recounts to his companion, just as their ancestors forgot my name in Baal.

28 What has the chaff to do with the wheat? says the LORD. Let the prophet who has a dream tell the dream, but let the one to whom my word is given speak my true word.

29 Is not my word like fire, says the LORD, and like a hammer that breaks the rock?

30 Therefore, behold, I am against the prophets, says the LORD, who steal my words, each from his neighbor.

31 Behold, I am against the prophets, saith the LORD, that take their tongue and utter a saying.

32 Behold, I am against the prophets of lying dreams, declares the LORD, who tell them and lead My people astray by their lies and their lightness, and I did not send them nor command them, and they will not profit this people at all, declares the LORD.

33 And if this folk or the prophet or the priest shall ask you, saying, “What is the burden?” then you shall say to them, “What burden? I have forsaken you,” says the LORD.

34 And the prophet and the priest and the people who say, "A burden of the LORD," I will punish that man and his household.

35 Thus you shall say, each to his fellow and a man to his brother: What has the LORD answered, and what has the LORD spoken?

36 You shall no longer remember the burden, for the burden will become each man’s own word, and you have turned the words of the living God, the LORD of hosts, our God.

37 Thus you shall say to the prophet: What has the LORD answered you, and what has the LORD spoken?

38 If you say, "This is a burden of the LORD," therefore thus says the LORD: "Because you have said, 'This thing is a burden of the LORD,' and I sent to you, saying, 'You shall not say, A burden of the LORD,'"

39 Therefore, behold, I will cast you out and abandon you and the city that I gave to you and your ancestors from before my face.

40 And I will set upon you the shame of alway and the shame of alway which shall not be forgotten.


## Chapter 24

1 The LORD showed me, and behold, two baskets of figs were set before the temple of the LORD, after Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had carried away Jeconiah son of Jehoiakim king of Judah, and the rulers of Judah, and the craftsmen, and the smiths, from Jerusalem, and brought them to Babylon.

2 One basket of very good figs, like the figs of the firstripe; and one basket of very bad figs, which cannot be eaten for badness.

3 And the LORD said to me, What do you see, Jeremiah? And I said, Figs, the good figs very good, and the bad figs very bad, which cannot be eaten because of badness.

4 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

5 Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Like the good figs, so will I regard the captives of Judah whom I have sent from this place to the land of the Chaldeans for good.

6 I will set my eyes upon them for good, and I will bring them back to this land; I will build them and not tear down, and I will plant them and not uproot.

7 I will give them care for to know me, for I am the LORD, and they shall be my people, and I myself will be their God, for they will return to me with all their care.

8 Like bad figs that cannot be eaten because of their badness, so says the LORD, so will I give Zedekiah king of Judah and his captains and the remnant of Jerusalem who remain in this land and those who dwell in the land of Egypt.

9 And I will give them over to adversity among all the kingdoms of the earth, for reproach and a byword, for taunting and for cursing, in every place where I will drive them.

10 I will send among them the sword, famine, and pestilence until they are consumed from the land that I gave to them and to their forefathers.


## Chapter 25

1 The act that came upon Jeremiah concerning all the people of Judah in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah—this was the first year of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.

2 That Jeremiah the prophet spoke concerning all the people of Judah and to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, saying:

3 From thirteen years of age for Josiah son of Amon king of Judah, and until this day, this is twenty-three years: the word of the LORD has come to me, and I have spoken to you persistently, but you have not listened.

4 And the LORD sent to you all his servant prophets, rising early and sending, but you did not listen, nor did you incline your ears to hear.

5 Say, “Turn now each from his evil way and from the wickedness of your doings, and dwell upon the land that the LORD gave to you and to your fathers from ancient time even to perpetual age.”

6 Do not go after other gods to serve and bow down to them, and do not provoke me by the deeds of your hands, so that I do not bring disaster on you.

7 Yet you have not listened to me, says the LORD, that you might provoke me to anger with the work of your hands, to your own hurt.

8 Therefore thus says the LORD of hosts: Because you have not listened to my words,

9 Behold, I am sending and will take all the families of the north, declares the LORD, and will bring them against this land and against its inhabitants and against all these nations around it; and I will utterly destroy them, and make them a desolation and a hissing and perpetual waste places.

10 I will destroy from them the voice of joy and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voice of the millstone and the light of the lamp.

11 And this whole land shall become a desolate waste, and these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.

12 And it shall come to pass, at the completion of seventy years, I will punish the king of Babylon and that nation, saith the LORD, for their iniquity, and the land of the Chaldeans; and I will make it perpetual desolations.

13 On that land all my words which I spoke concerning it, all that is written in this book which Jeremiah prophesied concerning all the nations.

14 For they too served in them many nations and great kings, and I repaid them according to their deeds and the work of their hands.

15 For thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, to me: Take from my hand this cup of the wine of wrath and make all the nations drink it, to whom I send you.

16 And they shall drink and stagger and rave because of the sword which I am sending among them.

17 And I took the cup from the hand of the LORD and made all the nations drink, whom the LORD sent me to.

18 Jerusalem and the cities of Judah and their kings and their rulers, to give them for destruction and for desolation and for hissing and for cursing, as at this day.

19 Pharaoh king of Egypt and his servants and his captains and all his people.

20 and all the mixed people, and all the kings of the land of Uz, and all the kings of the land of the Philistines, and Ashkelon, and Azzah, and Ekron, and the remnant of Ahdod:

21 Edom, Moab, and the sons of Ammon.

22 and all the kings of Tyre and all the kings of Sidon and the kings of the island who are beyond the sea

23 And Dedan and Tema and Buz and all those who cut the corners.

24 and all the kings of Arabia, and all the kings of the mixed people who dwell in the wilderness.

25 and all the kings of Zimri, and all the kings of Elam, and all the kings of Madai

26 And all the kings of the north, those near and those far off, every one to his brother, and all the kingdoms of the earth upon the face of the ground, and the king of Sheshach shall drink after them.

27 And you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD of armies, the God of Israel, Drink and become drunk and vomit and fall and do not rise again because of the sword which I am sending among you.

28 And it shall come to pass, if they refuse to take the cup from your hand to drink, then you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD of hosts: You shall surely drink.

29 Indeed, in the city called by my name I begin to do harm—will you indeed go unpunished? You will not go unpunished, for I am calling the sword against all who dwell in the land, declares the LORD of hosts.

30 But you, prophesy to them all these words, and say to them, The LORD will roar from on high, and from his holy habitation he will give forth his voice; he will roar mightily against his fold; a shout, like those who tread grapes, will answer against all who dwell on the earth.

31 A noise has come to the end of the earth, for the LORD has a controversy with the nations; he will enter into judgment with all flesh; he has given the wicked to the sword, says the LORD.

32 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, a calamity is going out from nation to nation, and a great storm is stirring from the remote parts of the earth.

33 And the slain of the LORD in that age shall be from the end of the earth even to the end of the earth; they shall not be lamented, nor gathered, nor buried, but shall become dung upon the face of the ground.

34 Howl, you shepherds, and cry out; roll in ashes, you leaders of the flock; for your days of slaughter are fulfilled, and your dispersions are complete, and you shall fall like a vessel of preciousness.

35 And flight shall perish from the shepherds, and escape from the leaders of the flock.

36 A cry of the bleating and a howling of the lords of the flock, for the LORD has laid waste their pasture.

37 And the pleasant places of peace were ruined because of the fierce wrath of the LORD.

38 Like a young lion he has abandoned his den, for their land has become a desolation because of the fury of the one enraged and because of the fierceness of his anger.


## Chapter 26

1 In the beginning of the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, this word came from the LORD saying:

2 Thus says the LORD: Stand in the court of the house of the LORD, and speak to all the cities of Judah who come to worship in the house of the LORD all the words that I have commanded you to speak to them; do not omit a word.

3 Perhaps they will listen and turn from their evil way, and I will relent concerning the calamity that I intend to bring upon them because of the wickedness of their deeds.

4 And you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD, If you will not listen to me, to walk in my law which I have set before you,

5 to heed the words of my servant prophets whom I send to you, rising up early and sending them, yet you have not heeded.

6 I will make this temple like Shiloh, and I will give this city for a curse to all the nations of the earth.

7 The priests and the prophets and all the people heard Jeremiah speaking these words in the temple of the LORD.

8 And it came to pass, when Jeremiah had made an end of speaking all that the LORD had commanded him to speak unto all the people, that the priests and the prophets and all the people laid hold on him, saying, Thou shalt surely die.

9 Why have you prophesied in the name of the LORD, saying, "Like Shiloh shall this temple be, and this city shall be destroyed without inhabitant?" And all the people were gathered to Jeremiah in the house of the LORD.

10 The captains of Judah heard these words and went up from the king's house to the house of the LORD and sat in the entrance of the new gate of the LORD.

11 The priests and the prophets said to the officials and to all the people: A judgment of death for this man, because he has prophesied against this city, as you have heard in your ears.

12 Jeremiah said to all the officials and to all the people, saying, "The LORD sent me to prophesy concerning this house and concerning this city all the words that you have heard."

13 And now, improve your ways and your deeds, and listen to the voice of the LORD your God, that the LORD may relent concerning the calamity which he spoke against you.

14 But I am at your disposal; treat me as seems good and right in your eyes.

15 Only know that if you put me to death, you bring the blood of the innocent upon yourselves and upon this city and its inhabitants, for in truth the LORD sent me to you to speak in your hearing all these words.

16 And the captains and all the people said to the priests and to the prophets: This man shall not be condemned to death, for he has spoken to us in the name of the LORD our God.

17 And certain men from the elders of the land rose up and said to all the congregation of the people, saying:

18 The Morashthite was prophesying in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah, and he said to all the people of Judah: Thus says the LORD of hosts: Zion shall be plowed as a field, and Jerusalem shall become heaps, and the mountain of the temple as the high places of a forest.

19 Did not Hezekiah king of Judah and all Judah cause him to be put to death, did he not fear the LORD, and did the LORD not relent concerning the adversity that he had spoken against them? But we are doing great evil against our own lives.

20 And also a certain man prophesied in the name of the LORD, Uriah son of Shemaiah from Kirjath-jearim, and he prophesied against this city and against this land according to all the words of Jeremiah.

21 King Jehoiakim and all his warriors and all the captains heard his words, and the king sought to put him to death; and Uriah heard and was afraid and fled and went to Egypt.

22 Then King Jehoiakim sent men of Egypt, even Elnathan the son of Achbor, and men with him, to Egypt.

23 They brought Uriah from Egypt and brought him to King Jehoiakim, who struck him with the sword and cast his dead body into the graves of the people's sons.

24 Only Ahikam son of Shaphan was with Jeremiah, to prevent handing him over to the people to put him to death.


## Chapter 27

1 In the beginning of the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, the word came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Thus said the LORD to me: Make for yourself bands and yokes, and put them on your neck.

3 And you shall send them by the hand of messengers who are coming to Jerusalem, to Zedekiah king of Judah, to the king of Edom, to the king of Moab, to the king of the sons of Ammon, to the king of Tyre, and to the king of Sidon.

4 And you shall command them to their masters, saying, Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, Thus shall you say to your masters:

5 I made the earth, the person, and the cattle which are upon the face of the land with my great power and with my outstretched arm, and I gave it to whomever seemed right in my eyes.

6 And now I have given all these lands into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon my servant, and even the beasts of the field I have given to him to serve him.

7 And all the nations shall serve him, and his son, and his son's son, until the time of his land also comes; and many nations and great kings shall serve him.

8 And it shall be that the nation and the kingdom which will not serve Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and which will not put its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon, I will punish that nation with sword, with famine, and with pestilence, declares the LORD, until I have finished them by his hand.

9 But you, do not listen to your prophets, your diviners, your dreamers, your sorcerers, or your soothsayers, who say to you, "You shall not serve the king of Babylon."

10 For they have prophesied to you falsely, in order to drive you from upon your land, and I have banished you and you are lost.

11 And the nation that brings its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serves him, I will let remain on its land, says the LORD, and it shall serve and dwell in it.

12 And to Zedekiah king of Judah I spoke according to all these words, saying: Bring your necks under the yoke of the king of Babylon, and serve him and his people, and live.

13 Why should you and your folk perish by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence, as the Lord has spoken to the nation that will not serve the king of Babylon?

14 Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you, saying, “You shall not serve the king of Babylon,” for they prophesy falsely to you.
