15 For I did not send them, saith the LORD, and they prophesy in my name falsely, to drive you away, and ye shall perish, even you and the prophets that prophesy unto you.

16 And I spoke to the priests and to all the people, saying, Thus says the LORD, Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you, saying, Behold, the vessels of the temple of the LORD will soon be brought back from Babylon; for they prophesy falsely to you.

17 Do not listen to them; serve the king of Babylon and live. Why should this city become a desolate place?

18 But if they are prophets, and if there is a word of the LORD with them, let them now intercede with the LORD of hosts that the vessels which are left in the house of the LORD and in the house of the king of Judah and in Jerusalem may not go to Babylon.

19 For thus says the LORD of hosts concerning the pillars, and concerning the sea, and concerning the bases, and concerning the remnant of the vessels that remain in the city:

20 whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon did not take away when he carried off Jeconiah son of Jehoiakim king of Judah from Jerusalem to Babylon, and all the nobles of Judah and Jerusalem.

21 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, concerning the vessels that remain in the house of the LORD and in the house of the king of Judah and in Jerusalem:

22 To Babylon they shall be brought, and there they shall remain until the day I visit them, declares the LORD; and I will bring them up and restore them to this place.


## Chapter 28

1 In that year, at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah, in the fourth year, in the fifth month, Hananiah son of Azur the prophet, who was from Gibeon, spoke to me in the house of the LORD, in the presence of the priests and all the people, saying:

2 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: I have broken the yoke of the king of Babylon.

3 Yet two years more and I will restore to this place all the vessels of the house of the LORD that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon took from this place and carried to Babylon.

4 And Jeconiah the son of Jehoiakim king of Judah, and all the captives of Judah who came to Babylon, I will bring back to this place, says the LORD, for I will break the yoke of the king of Babylon.

5 Jeremiah the prophet said to Hananiah the prophet before the priests and all the people who were standing in the house of the LORD.

6 Jeremiah the prophet said, "Amen, may the Lord do so; may the Lord confirm your word that you have prophesied to bring back the vessels of the house of the Lord and all the captives from Babylon to this place."

7 But please hear now this word that I am speaking into your ears and into the ears of all the people.

8 The prophets who were before me and before you of old prophesied to many lands and to great kingdoms concerning war, and concerning evil, and concerning famine, and concerning pestilence.

9 The prophet who prophesies peace—when the word of the prophet comes, the prophet whom the LORD has truly sent will be known.

10 Then Hananiah the prophet took the yoke from off the neck of Jeremiah the prophet and broke it.

11 Hananiah said before all the people, "Thus says the LORD: 'Even so will I break the yoke of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon within two years from off the neck of all the nations.' And Jeremiah the prophet went his way."

12 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah after Hananiah the prophet broke the yoke from off the neck of Jeremiah the prophet, saying:

13 Go and say to Hananiah, Thus says the LORD, You have broken wooden staves, and you have made iron staves in their place.

14 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: I have put an iron yoke on the neck of all these nations, to serve Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon; and they shall serve him. I have also given him the wild animals of the field.

15 Jeremiah the prophet said to Hananiah the prophet, Hear now, Hananiah: the LORD has not sent you, and you have caused this people to trust in a lie.

16 Therefore, thus says the LORD, Behold, I will send you away from the presence of the ground; this year you will die, because you have spoken rebellion against the LORD.

17 And Hananiah the prophet died that year in the seventh month.


## Chapter 29

1 And these are the words of the book that Jeremiah the prophet sent from Jerusalem to the remainder of the elders of the exile, and to the priests, and to the prophets, and to all the people whom Nebuchadnezzar had exiled from Jerusalem to Babylon.

2 After Jeconiah the king, the queen, the chamberlains, the captains of Judah and Jerusalem, the craftsman, and the smith departed from Jerusalem.

3 By the hand of Elasah son of Shaphan and Gemariah son of Hillkiah, whom Zedekiah king of Judah sent to Babylon to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, saying:

4 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, to all the captivity, whom I have removed from Jerusalem to Babylon:

5 Build houses and live in them, plant gardens and eat their fruit.

6 Take wives and bear sons and daughters; take wives for your sons and give your daughters to husbands, so that they may bear sons and daughters; multiply there and do not decrease.

7 Seek the welfare of the city where I have exiled you, and pray to the LORD on its behalf, for in its welfare you will have welfare.

8 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Do not let your prophets who are among you and your diviners deceive you, and do not listen to the dreams that you are dreaming.

9 For they prophesy falsely to you in my name; I did not send them, says the LORD.

10 For thus says the LORD: Because Babylon has completed seventy years, I will attend to you and fulfill my good promise to restore you to this place.

11 For I know the thoughts which I think toward you, says the LORD, thoughts of peace and not of adversity, to give you an end and hope.

12 You shall call to me and walk and pray to me, and I will hear you.

13 And you will seek me and find me, when you seek me with all your heart.

14 And I will find you, saith the LORD, and I will bring again your captivity, and I will gather you from all the nations, and from all the places, where I have driven you, saith the LORD, and I will bring you again into the place whence I caused you to be exiled.

15 For you said, "Jehovah, the Lord, has raised up prophets for us in Babylon."

16 For thus says the LORD to the king who sits on the throne of David and to all the people who dwell in this city, your brothers who did not go out with you into captivity:

17 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, I am sending among them the sword, the famine, and the pestilence, and I will make them like the figs that are so bad they cannot be eaten because of evil.

18 And I will pursue them with sword, famine, and pestilence, and I will make them a horror, a desolation, a hissing, and a reproach among all the kingdoms of the earth, to all the nations where I have driven them.

19 Because they have not listened to my words, declares the LORD, whom I sent to them by my servant prophets, rising up early and sending, yet you did not listen, declares the LORD.

20 But you, hear the word of the LORD, all the removing that I have sent from Jerusalem to Babylon.

21 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, to Ahab son of Kolaiah and to Zedekiah son of Maaseiah, who prophesy falsely to you in my name: Behold, I will give them into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and he will strike them before your eyes.

22 A curse shall be taken from them for all the captives of Judah who are in Babylon, saying, "May the LORD make you like Zedekiah and like Ahab, whom the king of Babylon burned in the fire."

23 Because they have committed folly in Israel and committed adultery with their neighbors' wives and have spoken a lying word in my name that I did not command them. I know, and I am witness, declares the LORD.

24 And to Shemaiah the Nehelamite you shall say, saying:

25 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Because you have sent letters by the hand of Shemiah to all the people who are in Jerusalem, and to Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah the priest, and to all the priests, saying:

26 The LORD has appointed you priest instead of Jehoiada the priest to have oversight of the temple of the LORD over every madman and prophet, and you shall confine him in the prison and in the stocks.

27 And now, why have you not rebuked Jeremiah the Anathothite, who prophesies to you?

28 For on this account Babylon sent to us, saying: It is long; build houses and dwell, and plant gardens and eat their fruit.

29 Zephaniah the priest read the book in the hearing of Jeremiah the prophet.

30 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:

31 Send to all the exiles, saying, Thus says the LORD to Shemaiah the Nehelamite: Because Shemaiah has prophesied to you, though I did not send him, and has caused you to trust in falsehood—

32 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Behold, I am about to punish Shemaiah the Nehelamite and his offspring; there shall not be any man of his seed dwelling among this people, nor shall he see the good that I am doing for my people, declares the LORD, because he has turned away in rebellion against the LORD.


## Chapter 30

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Thus says Jehovah, God of Israel: Write for yourself all the words that I have spoken to you in a scroll.

3 For behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will restore the fortunes of my people Israel and Judah, says the LORD, and I will bring them back to the land that I gave to their ancestors, and they shall inherit it.

4 And these are the words that the LORD spoke to Israel and to Judah.

5 For thus says the LORD: A voice of trembling we have heard, fear and no peace.

6 Inquire, I pray you, and see, if a male can bring forth a child: why have I seen every man with his hands upon his loins, as a woman in travail, and all faces turned pale?

7 Woe, for that day is great, there is none like it; it is a time of distress for Jacob, but from it he shall be saved.

8 And it shall come to pass in that day, says the LORD of hosts, I will break the yoke from upon your neck and your bonds I will burst, and strangers shall no more serve in it.

9 And they shall serve the LORD their God and David their king, whom I will raise up for them.

10 But you, bondman Jacob, do not fear, says the LORD, and do not be dismayed, Israel, for behold, I am saving you from afar, and your seed from the land of captivity; and Jacob shall return and be quiet and at ease, and there shall be none to make afraid.

11 For I am with thee, saith the LORD, to save thee: though I will make a full end of all the nations whither I have scattered thee, yet of thee I will not make a full end, but I will correct thee in judgment, and will by no means leave thee unpunished.

12 For thus says the LORD: Your affliction is incurable, the wound of your injury.

13 There is no healing for your wound, no cure for your affliction.

14 All your lovers have forgotten you; they do not seek you. For the blow of an enemy has struck you, a cruel chastisement, because of the abundance of your iniquities, your sins have increased.

15 Why do you cry out over your affliction, your pain? Because of the abundance of your iniquity your sins have increased; I have done these things to you.

16 Therefore all your devourers shall be devoured, and all your adversaries shall go into captivity; your spoilers shall become spoil, and all your plunderers I will give as plunder.

17 For I will bring health to you, and from your wounds I will heal you, says the LORD; for they called you an outcast: She is Zion, there is no one seeking her.

18 Thus says the LORD: Behold, I will restore the captive dwelling places of Jacob and have mercy on his habitations; and the city shall be built upon its heap, and the palace according to its judgment shall be inhabited.

19 And confession and a voice of rejoicing shall go out from them; I will multiply them, and they shall not be few; I will honor them, and they shall not be slighted.

20 And his sons shall be as aforetime, and his congregation before me shall be established, and I will visit upon all his oppressors.

21 And his prince shall be one of them, and his ruler shall go out from among him; I will bring him near, and he shall approach me, for who is he who has pledged his heart to draw near to me? declares the LORD.

22 And you shall be my people, and I will be your God.

23 Behold, a storm of the LORD, a furious tempest, has gone forth; upon the head of the wicked it whirls.

24 The fierce wrath of the LORD will not turn back until He has done and fulfilled the intents of His heart; in the latter days you will consider this.


## Chapter 31

1 At that time, says the LORD, I will be the God of all the families of Israel, and they shall be my people.

2 Thus says the LORD: I found favour in the wilderness for the people, the remnant of the sword; go to appease Israel.

3 From afar the LORD appeared to me, saying, I have loved you with an everlasting love; therefore I have drawn you with kindness.

4 Again I will build you, and you shall be built, O virgin of Israel; again you will adorn your tabrets and go forth dancing in the dance.

5 You will again plant vines on the hills of Samaria; planters will plant and defile.

6 For there is a day; the watchmen have called on the mountain of Ephraim: “Arise, and let us go up to Zion, to the LORD our God.”

7 For thus says the LORD, Rejoice in Jacob with gladness, and raise shouts of joy at the forefront of the nations; proclaim, give praise, and say, Save your people, the remnant of Israel, O the LORD.

8 Behold, I am bringing them from the land of the north and gathering them from the remote parts of the earth, among them the blind and the lame, the pregnant woman and she who gives birth, a great congregation returning here.

9 With weeping they shall come, and with supplications I will lead them; I will cause them to walk beside streams of water in a straight path in which they shall not stumble, for I have been a father to Israel, and Ephraim is my firstborn.

10 Hear the word of the LORD, O nations, and declare it in the distant islands; say, “He who scattered Israel will gather him, and will keep him as a shepherd keeps his flock.”

11 For the LORD has redeemed Jacob and ransomed him from the hand of one stronger than him.

12 And they shall come and sing in the height of Zion, and flow to the goodness of the LORD for wheat and for new wine and for anointed oil, and for young calves and cattle; and their soul shall be like a well-watered garden, and they shall not sorrow anymore.

13 Then the maid will rejoice in dancing, young men and old together; I will turn their mourning into gladness, comfort them, and give them joy from their sorrow.

14 I will satisfy the appetite of the priests with fatness, and my people with my goodness will be satisfied, says the LORD.

15 Thus says the LORD: A voice is heard in Ramah—lamentation, the sound of bitter weeping—Rachel weeping for her children; she refuses to be comforted for her children, because they are no more.

16 Thus says the LORD: “Keep your voice from crying out and your eyes from tears, for there is reward for your labor,” declares the LORD, “and they shall return from the land of the enemy.”

17 And there is hope for your latter end, says the LORD; and your children shall return to their border.

18 I have heard Ephraim mourning: "You disciplined me, and I was corrected; like a calf not trained; bring me back, and I will return, for you, O LORD, are my God."

19 For after I turned back I regretted, and after I was made aware I struck my thigh; I was ashamed and indeed humiliated because I bore the reproach of my youth.

20 Is Ephraim my dear son, a child of delight? For as often as I speak of him, I still remember him; therefore my bowels are stirred for him; I will surely have mercy on him, says the LORD.

21 Set up for yourself waymarks, make for yourself high heaps; set your heart upon the highway, the way you went; return, O virgin of Israel, return to these cities.

22 How long will you persist, O rebellious daughter? For the LORD has created a new thing on the earth: a female will encompass a man.

23 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Again they shall speak this word in the land of Judah and in its cities, when I restore their captivity: “May the LORD bless you, O habitation of justice, O holy mountain.”

24 And Judah shall dwell therein, and all her cities together, husbandmen, and they shall move with the flock.

25 For I have satisfied the weary appetite, and I have filled every hungry soul.

26 Therefore I have been aroused and I see, and my sleep was sweet to me.

27 Behold, the days are coming, says the LORD, and I will sow the house of Israel and the house of Judah with the seed of man and the seed of beast.

28 And it shall come to pass, as I have watched over them to pluck up and to break down and to destroy and to ruin and to do evil, so will I watch over them to build and to plant, (hath) said the LORD.

29 In those days they will no longer say, "The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge."

30 But each man will die for his own sin; the person who eats the sour grape—his teeth will be on edge.

31 Behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah.

32 Not like the covenant which I made with their fathers in the day I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt, which they broke my covenant, though I was a husband to them, says the LORD.

33 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the LORD: I will put my law within them, and I will write it upon their heart; and I will be to them for God, and they shall be to me for a people.

34 And they shall no longer teach, each man his companion and each man his brother, saying, Know the LORD, for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest, declares the LORD, for I will forgive their iniquity and their sin I will no longer remember.

35 Thus says the LORD, who gives the sun for light by day and the fixed order of the moon and the stars for light by night, who stirs up the sea so that its waves roar, the LORD of hosts is his name.

36 If these statutes depart from before me, declares the LORD, then also the seed of Israel will cease from being a nation before me all the days.

37 Thus says the LORD: If the heavens above can be measured and the foundations of the earth below can be searched out, even I will reject all the seed of Israel for all that they have done, declares the LORD.

38 Behold, days, says the LORD, when the city shall be rebuilt for the LORD from the tower of Hananeel to the corner gate.

39 And the line went out still further opposite him, over the hill of Gareb, and it turned to Goath.

40 And all the valley of the corpses and the ashes, and all the fields as far as the brook Kidron, as far as the corner of the Horse Gate toward the east, shall be holy to the LORD; it shall not be uprooted or thrown down anymore forever.


## Chapter 32

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah, which was the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar.

2 Then the army of the king of Babylon laid siege against Jerusalem, and Jeremiah the prophet was confined in the prison which was in the house of the king of Judah.

3 Which Zedekiah king of Judah had confined, saying, Why do you prophesy, saying, Thus says the LORD, Behold, I am giving this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall take it?

4 Zedekiah king of Judah shall not escape from the hand of the Chaldeans, but shall surely be given into the hand of the king of Babylon, and his mouth shall speak with his mouth, and his eyes shall see his eyes.

5 And in Babel shall he cause Zedekiah to go, and there shall he be until I visit him, saith the LORD; for ye shall fight against the Chaldeans, ye shall not prosper.

6 Jeremiah said, The word of the LORD came to me, saying:

7 Behold, Hanameel the son of Shallum your uncle is coming to you, saying, Buy for yourself the field which is in Anathoth, for to you belongs the right of redemption to buy it.

8 Hanameel the son of my uncle came to me according to the word of the LORD, to the court of the prison, and said to me, "Buy now my field which is at Anathoth, which is in the land of Benjamin, for you have the right of inheritance and the right of redemption; buy it for yourself." Then I knew that this was the word of the LORD.

9 I bought the field from Hanameel, my uncle's son, who was in Anathoth, and I weighed out to him the silver, seventeen shekels of silver.

10 And I wrote in the bill and sealed it, and called witnesses, and weighed the money in the balances.

11 And I took the bill of purchase, the sealed one, the commandment and the statutes, and the open one.

12 I gave the bill of purchase to Baruch son of Neriah son of Maaseiah, in the presence of Hanameel my uncle, in the presence of the witnesses who signed the bill of purchase, and in the presence of all the Jews sitting in the court of the prison.

13 And I will command Baruch before their presence, saying:

14 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Take these scrolls, the scroll of this purchase and the sealed one and the open scroll, and put them in an earthen vessel, so that they may last many days.

15 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Houses, fields, and vineyards shall yet again be bought in this land.

16 And I prayed unto the LORD, after I had given the bill of the purchase unto Baruch the son of Neriah, saying:

17 Ah, (my) Lord God, behold, You have made the heavens and the earth by Your great power and by Your outstretched arm; not one thing is too hard for You.

18 who shows favour to thousands and repays the iniquity of the fathers upon the bosom of their children after them, the great God, the mighty, the LORD of hosts, His name.

19 Great is the counsel and abundant the work, whose eyes are open upon all the ways of humankind, to give to each person according to their way and the fruit of their deeds.

20 The marks and wonders that you have set in the land of Egypt to this day, and in Israel and among mankind, and you have made for yourself a name as of this day.

21 You brought out your folk, Israel, from the land of Egypt with signs and wonders, with a strong hand and an outstretched arm, and with great terror.

22 And you gave them this land, which you swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.

23 They came and took possession of it, but they did not listen to your voice, nor did they walk in all the law which you commanded them to do; they did not do it, and you called upon them the whole of this evil.

24 Behold, the mounters have come to the city to capture it, and the city has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans who are fighting against it because of the sword and because of the famine and because of the pestilence, and that which you spoke has come to pass, and behold, you are seeing it.

25 And you said to me, (my) Lord God, Buy for yourself the field with silver and take witnesses as witnesses, and the city has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

26 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:

27 Behold, I am the LORD, God of all flesh; is there any thing too hard for me?

28 Therefore thus says the LORD Behold I am giving this city into the hand of the Chaldeans and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and he shall capture it

29 Then the Chaldeans who were fighting against this city came and set this city on fire and burned it, and the houses on whose roofs they burned incense to Baal and poured out drink offerings to other gods, to provoke me to anger.

30 For the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah have done only evil in my sight from their youth, for the sons of Israel have done nothing but provoke me by the works of their hands, declares the LORD.

31 For my anger and my wrath this city has been before me from the day they built it until this day, to remove it from before my face.

32 For all the evil of the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah that they did to displease me, their kings and their rulers, their priests and their prophets, and each man of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

33 They turned their stiff neck to me, not their face, and I taught them diligently, but they did not listen to receive instruction.

34 They placed their abominable filth in the temple where my name is called to defile it.

35 They built the high places of Baal, which are in the valley of Ben-Hinnom, to pass their sons and their daughters to Molech, which I did not command them, nor did it come into my heart to do this abomination, to cause Judah to sin.

36 Therefore thus says the LORD, the God of Israel, concerning this city of which you say, "It will be given into the hand of the king of Babylon by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence":

37 Behold, I will gather them from all the lands where I have driven them in My anger and in My fury and in great wrath, and I will bring them back to this place and I will make them dwell securely.

38 And they shall be mine folk, and I will be their God.

39 I will give them one heart and one way to fear me all their days, for good to them and to their children after them.

40 And I will make with them an everlasting covenant that I will not turn away from following after them to do good to them, and I will put my fear in their heart so that they will not turn away from me.

41 I will rejoice over them to do them good, and I will plant them in this land in truth, with all my heart and with all my soul.

42 For thus says the LORD: Just as I brought upon this people all the great adversity, so I will bring upon them all the good that I am speaking concerning them.

43 And the country will be acquired in the land of this place, which you say is desolate, without man and beast; it has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

44 Fields with silver they shall buy, and write in the scroll and seal, and the witnesses shall witness, in the land of Benjamin and in the surroundings of Jerusalem and in the cities of Judah and in the cities of the hill country and in the cities of the low country and in the cities of the south, for I will restore their captivity, says the LORD.


## Chapter 33

1 The act of the LORD came to Jeremiah a second time while he was still confined in the court of the prison, saying:

2 Thus says the Lord who made it, the Lord who formed it to establish it—Jehovah is his name.

3 Call to me and I will answer you, and I will declare to you great and fortified things which you have not known.

4 For thus says the LORD God of Israel concerning the houses of this city and the houses of the kings of Judah, which are broken down to the mounds and to the sword:

5 coming to fight against the Chaldeans and to fill them with the dead bodies of the man whom I struck in my anger and in my wrath, and from whose city I hid my face because of all their evil.

6 I am raising up for her health and healing, and I will heal them; and I will reveal to them abundance of peace and truth.

7 And I will restore the captivity of Judah and the captivity of Israel, and I will rebuild them as at first.

8 I will cleanse them from all their faults with which they sinned against me, and I will pardon their sins with which they sinned against me and with which they rebelled against me.

9 And it shall be to me for a name of joy, for praise and for beauty to all the nations of the earth, who shall hear of all the good that I am doing for them, and they shall fear and tremble because of all the good and because of all the peace that I am doing for her.

10 Thus says the LORD: Yet again shall it be heard in this place, of which you say, It is desolate from lack of man and lack of beast, even in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, that are desolate from lack of man and lack of inhabitant and lack of beast.

11 The voice of joy and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voice of those saying, "Give thanks to the LORD of hosts, for good is the LORD, for everlasting is his mercy," bringing thanksgiving to the house of the LORD, for I will restore the captivity of the land as at first, says the LORD.

12 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Yet again there shall be in this place that is desolate, without man and without beast, and in all its cities, shepherds making their flocks lie down.

13 In the hill country cities, in the lowland cities, in the southern cities, in the land of Benjamin, around Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah, sheep shall yet pass again under the hand of the one who counts, says the LORD.

14 Behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will perform the good word that I spoke concerning the house of Israel and concerning the house of Judah.

15 In those days and at that time I will cause a righteous branch to spring up for David, and he will execute justice and righteousness in the land.

16 In those days Judah shall be saved and Jerusalem shall dwell securely, and this is what shall be called to her: "The LORD our righteousness."

17 For thus says the LORD: There shall not be cut off from David a man sitting upon the throne of the house of Israel.

18 And to the priests, the Levites, no man shall be cut off from before me, offering up a burnt offering and presenting a gift and sacrificing continually all the days.

19 The act of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:

20 Thus says the LORD: If you break My covenant of the day and My covenant of the night, so that there is not day and night in their due time,

21 Even my covenant will be broken with David my servant, so that he will not have a son reigning on his throne, and with the Levites the priests, my ministers.

22 whose heavenly host cannot be numbered and whose sandy sea cannot be measured, so I will multiply the offspring of David my servant and the Levites who minister to me.

23 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:

24 Have you not seen what this people have spoken, saying, “The two families that the LORD chose, He has rejected them”? Thus they despise My people, no longer to be a nation before them.

25 Thus says the LORD, If not my covenant with day and night, the statutes of heaven and earth, I have not set.

26 Even the seed of Jacob and David my servant I will not despise in taking a ruler from his seed, for the seed of Abraham Isaac and Jacob, because I will restore their captivity and have mercy on them.


## Chapter 34

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and all his forces, and all the kingdoms of the earth under his dominion, and all the peoples, were warring against Jerusalem and against all its cities, saying:

2 Thus says the Lord God of Israel, Go and say to Zedekiah king of Judah, and say to him, Thus says the Lord, Behold, I am giving this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will burn it with fire.

3 But you shall not escape from his hand, for you shall surely be captured and delivered into his hand; your eyes shall see the eyes of the king of Babylon, and his mouth shall speak with your mouth, and you shall go to Babylon.

4 Only listen to the word of the LORD, Zedekiah king of Judah: thus says the LORD concerning you: you shall not die by the sword.

5 You will die in peace, and as with the burnings for your fathers, the former kings who were before you, so they will burn for you; and they will lament for you, saying, Ah lord! For I have spoken the word, declares the LORD.

6 Jeremiah the prophet spoke to Zedekiah king of Judah all these words in Jerusalem.

7 And the army of the king of Babylon fought against Jerusalem and against all the cities of Judah that remained: Lachish and Azekah, for they were left among the cities of Judah, fortified cities.

8 The act that came to Jeremiah from the LORD after King Zedekiah made a covenant with all the people who were in Jerusalem to proclaim liberty to them.

9 To send a man his male servant and a man his female servant, the Hebrew and the Hebrew woman, free, so that they do not enslave them, a Jew his brother man.

10 And all the captains and all the people who entered into the covenant heard to send every man his male servant and every man his female servant free, not to impose servitude upon them anymore, and they obeyed and sent them away.

11 But afterward they turned and brought back the male servants and female servants whom they had set free, and subdued them as male servants and female servants.

12 And the act of the LORD came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

13 Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: I made a covenant with your fathers in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage, saying:

14 After seven years you shall release every man his Hebrew brother who is sold to you, and he has served you six years, and you shall send him free from you; but your fathers did not listen to me, and they did not incline their ear.

15 Today you returned and did what was right in my sight by proclaiming liberty, each to his neighbor, and you made a covenant before me in the house upon which my name is called.

16 You turned back and profaned my name, and each man returned his male servant and each man returned his female servant whom you had released free to their souls, and you brought them under control to be for you as male servants and as female servants.

17 Therefore, thus says the LORD: You have not obeyed me by proclaiming liberty, each to his brother and each to his neighbor; behold, I am proclaiming liberty for you, says the LORD, to the sword, to pestilence, and to famine; and I will make you a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

18 I will give the men who passed by my covenant and did not uphold the words of the covenant which they made before me, the calf which they made into two parts, and they passed between its pieces.

19 The captains of Judah and the captains of Jerusalem, the chamberlains and the priests and all the people of the land, those passing between the parts of the calf.

20 I will give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life, and their dead bodies will become food for the birds of the air and for the beasts of the earth.

21 And Zedekiah king of Judah and his captains I will give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their life and into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon who are coming up against you.

22 Behold, I command, saith the LORD, and I will bring them back to this city, and they shall fight against it, and take it, and burn it with fire; and I will give the cities of Judah to be desolate without inhabitant.


## Chapter 35

1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD in the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, saying:

2 Go to the house of the Rechabites and speak to them, and bring them into the house of the LORD, to one of the chambers, and give them wine to drink.

3 And I took Jaazaniah son of Jeremiah son of Habazaniah, and his brothers, and all his sons, and all the house of the Rechabites.

4 And I brought them into the house of the LORD, to the chamber of Hanan the son of Igdaliah, the man of God, which was by the chamber of the princes, which was above the chamber of Maaseiah the son of Shallum, the keeper of the threshold.

5 And I set before the sons of the house of Rechab cups full of wine and cups, and I said to them, "Drink wine."

6 They said, We will not drink wine, for Jonadab the son of Rechab our father commanded us, saying, You shall not drink wine, you nor your sons, for ever.

7 You shall not build a house, nor sow seed, nor plant a vineyard, and it shall not be yours, for in tents you shall dwell all your days, that you may live many days upon the face of the land where you are sojourners.

8 And we obeyed the voice of Jehonadab son of Rechab our father in all that he commanded us, not to drink wine all our days, we and our wives and our sons and our daughters.

9 and not to build houses for our dwelling, nor shall vineyard nor field nor seed be for us.

10 And we stayed in the tents, and we listened and did according to all that Jonadab our father commanded us.

11 When Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon went up against the land, we said, "Come, let us go to Jerusalem because of the army of the Chaldeans and because of the army of Aram," and we dwelt in Jerusalem.

12 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah, saying:

13 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Go and say to the man of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem: Will you not receive correction to obey my words? declares the LORD.

14 The acts of Jehonadab son of Rechab have been fulfilled, which he commanded his sons not to drink wine, and they have not drunk to this day, for they have obeyed the commandment of their father; but I have spoken to you, rising early and speaking, and you have not obeyed me.

15 I sent to you all my servant prophets, rising up early and sending, to say, Return now, each one from his evil way, and make good your deeds, and do not go after other gods to serve them, and dwell upon the land that I gave to you and to your fathers; but you did not incline your ear, nor did you listen to me.

16 For the sons of Jehonadab son of Rechab have kept the commandment of their father which he commanded them, but this people have not listened to me.

17 Therefore thus says the LORD, the God of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am bringing upon Judah and upon all the inhabitants of Jerusalem all the adversity that I have spoken against them, because I spoke to them but they did not listen, and I called to them but they did not answer.

18 And to the house of the Rechabites Jeremiah said, Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Because you have obeyed the command of your father Jonadab, and have kept all his precepts and done according to all that he commanded you,

19 Therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Jonadab the son of Rechab shall not lack a man to stand before me all the days.


## Chapter 36

1 In the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, this word came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying:

2 Take for yourself a scroll of a book and write upon it all the words that I have spoken to you concerning Israel and concerning Judah and concerning all the nations, from the day I spoke to you in the days of Josiah even to this day.

3 Perhaps the house of Judah will hear all the evil that I am planning to do to them, so that each man may turn from his evil way, and I will forgive their iniquity and their sin.

4 Jeremiah called Baruch son of Neriah, and Baruch wrote from the mouth of Jeremiah all the words of the LORD which he spoke to him upon a scroll of a book.

5 Jeremiah commanded Baruch, saying, "I am confined; I cannot enter the temple of the LORD."

6 You shall come and read the roll which you wrote at my command, the words of the LORD, in the hearing of the people in the house of the LORD on the day of the fast, and also in the hearing of all Judah who come from their cities.

7 Perhaps their supplication will turn away the anger and wrath of the LORD from this people, so that each man turns from his evil way, for great is the anger and heat that the LORD has spoken against this people.

8 Baruch son of Neriah did all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him, to read the words of the LORD from the scroll in the house of the LORD.

9 In the fifth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, in the ninth month, a fast was proclaimed before the LORD for all the people in Jerusalem and for all the people who came from the towns of Judah to Jerusalem.

10 Baruch read the words of Jeremiah from the scroll in the temple of the LORD, in the chamber of Gemariah son of Shaphan the scribe, in the upper court, at the entrance of the new gate of the house of the LORD, so that all the people could hear.

11 And Micah son of Gemariah son of Shaphan heard all the words of the LORD from above the scroll.

12 He went down to the chamber of the scribe in the king's court, and there all the officials were sitting: Elishama the scribe, Dalaiah son of Shemaiah, Elnathan son of Achbor, Gemariah son of Shaphan, Zedekiah son of Hananiah, and all the officials.

13 And Micah told them all the words that he had heard, when Baruch read the book in the audience of the people.

14 All the captains sent Jehudi son of Nethaniah son of Shelemiah son of Cushi to Baruch, saying, Take the roll that you read in the ears of the people and come; so Baruch son of Neriah took the roll in his hand and came to them.

15 They said to him, “Sit now and call Baruch in our hearing,” so Baruch called in their hearing.

16 When they heard all the words, each man feared for his neighbor, and they said to Baruch, “Tell the king all these words.”

17 And they asked Baruch, saying, Please tell us, how have you written down all these words from his mouth?

18 Baruch said to them, "From his mouth he will speak to me all these words, and I am writing them on the scroll with ink."

19 The captains said to Baruch, "Go, hide yourself, you and Jeremiah; let no man know where you are."

20 They came into the king's court and deposited the roll in the chamber of Elishama the scribe, and they reported in the king's ears all the words.

21 Then the king sent Jehudi to take the roll, and he took it from the chamber of Elishama the scribe; and Jehudi read it in the ears of the king and in the ears of all the princes standing beside the king.

22 And the king was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month, and the hearth was burning before him.

23 And it came to pass, when Jehudi had read three leaves and four, that he tore it with the penknife of the scribe and cast it into the fire upon the hearth, until the whole roll was consumed upon the fire that was on the hearth.

24 They were not afraid, nor did they tear their garments, the king and all his servants who heard all these words.

25 And also Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah spoke with the king to prevent the burning of the roll, but he did not listen to them.

26 The king commanded Jerahmeel son of the king and Seraiah son of Azriel and Shelemiah son of Abdeel to take Baruch the scribe and Jeremiah the prophet, but the LORD hid them.

27 The act of the LORD came to Jeremiah after the king burned the roll and the words which Baruch wrote at the mouth of Jeremiah.

28 Take for yourself another roll and write on it all the former words that were on the first roll which Jehoiakim king of Judah burned.

29 And concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah you shall say, Thus says the Lord, You have burned this roll, saying, Why have you written on it, saying, The king of Babylon will come and destroy this land and remove from it man and beast?

30 Therefore thus says the LORD concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: He shall have no one sitting on the throne of David, and his dead body shall be cast out to the heat by day and to the frost by night.

31 I will punish them—their child, their servants, and them—for their sin, and I will bring upon them, upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and upon every man of Judah all the evil that I spoke to them, but they did not listen.

32 Jeremiah took another roll and gave it to Baruch son of Neriah the scribe, and Baruch wrote on it at Jeremiah’s dictation all the words of the book that Jehoiakim king of Judah had burned in the fire, and many similar words were added besides.


## Chapter 37

1 Zedekiah son of Josiah became king in place of Coniah son of Jehoiakim, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had made king in the land of Judah.

2 And he and his servants and the people of the land did not listen to the words of the LORD that he spoke by the hand of Jeremiah the prophet.

3 Then King Zedekiah sent Jehucal son of Shelemiah and Zephaniah son of Maaseiah the priest to the prophet Jeremiah, saying, Pray now for us to the LORD our God.

4 Jeremiah. came and went among the people, and they did not put him in prison.

5 And the army of Pharaoh went out from Egypt, and the Chaldeans who were besieging Jerusalem heard the report about them, and they withdrew from upon Jerusalem.

6 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah the prophet, saying:

7 Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Thus shall you say to the king of Judah, who sent you to me to inquire of me: Behold, the army of Pharaoh that came out to help you will return to its own land, Egypt.

8 Then the Chaldeans will return and fight against this city, capture it, and burn it with fire.

9 Thus says the LORD: Do not set your souls to say, "The Chaldeans will surely go away from us," for they will not go away.

10 But if you strike down all the army of the Chaldeans who are fighting against you, and only pierced men remain among them, then every man in his tent will rise up and burn this city with fire.

11 And it came to pass, when the army of the Chaldeans went up from Jerusalem because of the army of Pharaoh,

12 Jeremiah went out from Jerusalem to go to the land of Benjamin, to divide among the people from there.

13 He was at the Benjamin Gate, where the captain of the ward was, whose name was Irijah son of Shelemiah son of Hananiah, and he arrested Jeremiah the prophet, saying, "You are defecting to the Chaldeans."

14 Jeremiah said, "False: I am not defecting to the Chaldeans," but he did not listen to him; then Irijah seized Jeremiah and brought him to the captains.

15 The captains became angry with Jeremiah and struck him and put him in the prison house, the house of Jonathan the scribe, for they had made it the prison.

16 For Jeremiah came to the dungeon court and to the cabin; and Jeremiah remained there many days.

17 Then the king Zedekiah sent and took him, and the king questioned him in his palace secretly and said, "Is there any word from the LORD?" And Jeremiah said, "There is," and he said, "You will be given into the hand of the king of Babylon."

18 Jeremiah said to King Zedekiah, "What have I sinned against you, or against your servants, or against this people, that you have given me into the prison house?"

19 Your prophets who prophesy to you say, "The king of Babylon will not come against you or against this land."

20 And now, please listen, my lord the king, to my supplication before you; do not send me back to the house of Jonathan the scribe, lest I die there.

21 Then King Zedekiah commanded, and they committed Jeremiah to the court of the prison; and there was given to him a loaf of bread daily from the street of the bakers, until all the bread in the city was consumed; and Jeremiah remained in the court of the prison.


## Chapter 38

1 Shephatiah son of Mattan, Gedaliah son of Pashur, Jucal son of Shelemiah, and Pashur son of Malchiah heard the words that Jeremiah spoke to all the people saying:

2 Thus says the LORD, He who remains in this city will die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence; but he who goes out to the Chaldeans will have his life for prey and live.

3 Thus says the LORD: This city shall surely be given into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon, and he shall take it.

4 The captains said to the king, Let this man die, for he thus weakens the hands of the men of war who remain in the city and the hands of all the people by speaking such words to them, because this man does not seek the welfare of this people but for evil.

5 King Zedekiah said, "Behold, he is in your hand, for the king cannot do anything to you."

6 They took Jeremiah and cast him into the cistern of Malchijah son of the king, which was in the court of the prison, and they let down Jeremiah by ropes; and there was no water in the cistern, only mire, and Jeremiah sank into the mire.

7 Ebed-melech the Cushite, a certain chamberlain who was in the court of the king, heard that they had put Jeremiah into the dungeon, and the king was sitting in the gate of Benjamin.

8 And Ebed-melech came forth from the king's court and spoke to the king, saying:

9 My lord the king, these men have done evil in all that they did to Jeremiah the prophet, whom they cast into the pit, and he would have died there from famine, for there was no more bread in the city.

10 Then the king commanded Ebed-melech the Cushite, saying, Take thirty men with you from here and bring up Jeremiah the prophet from the cistern before he dies.

11 Ebed-melech took the men with him and went into the king's house under the treasury, and took from there old rags and worn-out clothes, and let them down by ropes to Jeremiah in the cistern.

12 Ebed-melech the Cushite said to Jeremiah, "Please put the old cast clouts and rotten rags under your armholes beneath the cords," and Jeremiah did so.

13 They pulled Jeremiah up with ropes and brought him out of the pit, and Jeremiah remained in the prison court.

14 Then King Zedekiah sent and took Jeremiah the prophet to him, to the entrance of the third [court] which [was] in the house of the LORD; and the king said to Jeremiah, "I am asking you a word: do not hide anything from me."

15 Jeremiah said to Zedekiah, "If I tell you, will you not put me to death? And if I give you counsel, you will not listen to me."

16 King Zedekiah swore to Jeremiah in secret, saying, "As the LORD lives who made for us this life, I will not put you to death, nor will I hand you over to these men who seek your life."

17 Jeremiah said to Zedekiah, Thus says the LORD, God of Armies, God of Israel: If you go out to the princes of the king of Babylon, your life will be spared and this city will not be burned with fire, and you will live, you and your household.

18 But if you do not go out to the captains of the king of Babylon, then this city will be given into the hand of the Chaldeans, and they will burn it with fire, and you will not escape from their hand.

19 King Zedekiah said to Jeremiah, "I am afraid of the Jews who have defected to the Chaldeans, lest they deliver me into their hand and abuse me."

20 Jeremiah said, "He will not give it; please hear the voice of the LORD to which I speak to you, and it will be well with you, and your soul will live."

21 But if you refuse to go out, this is the word that the LORD has shown me:

22 And behold, all the women who were left in the house of the king of Judah were found by the princes of the king of Babylon, and they said: "Your men of peace have enticed you and prevailed against you; they have sunk your feet in the mire, they have turned backward."

23 And all your wives and your sons shall be brought out to the Chaldeans, and you shall not escape from their hand, for you shall be seized by the hand of the king of Babylon, and this city shall be burned with fire.

24 Zedekiah said to Jeremiah, "Let no man know of these words, and you shall not die."

25 And if the captains hear that I have spoken with you and come to you and say to you, "Tell us, we pray, what you have said to the king; do not hide it from us, and we will not put you to death," and what the king has said to you—

26 And you shall say to them, I am casting my supplication before the king, so that he does not return me to the house of Jonathan to die there.

27 All the captains came to Jeremiah and questioned him, and he told them all the words that the king had commanded; so they held their peace from him, because the word was not heard.

28 Jeremiah remained in the court of the prison until the day that Jerusalem was captured, and it happened when Jerusalem was captured.


## Chapter 39

1 In the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah, in the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came with all his army to Jerusalem, and they besieged it.

2 In the eleventh year of Zedekiah, in the fourth month, on the ninth day of the month, the city was breached.

3 Then came all the captains of the king of Babylon, and they sat in the middle gate: Nergal-sharezer, Samgar-nebo, Sarsechim, Rab-saris, Nergal-sharezer, Rab-mag, and all the rest of the captains of the king of Babylon.

4 When Zedekiah king of Judah and all the warriors saw them, they fled and went out by night from the city by way of the king's garden, by the gate between the walls, and they went out by way of the Arabah.

5 The army of the Chaldeans pursued them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho, and they took him and brought him up to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and he spoke with him judgments.

6 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and all the nobles of Judah the king of Babylon slaughtered.

7 And he put out Zedekiah's eyes and bound him with brass fetters and brought him to Babylon.

8 And the Chaldeans burned the king's palace and the people's houses with fire, and broke down the walls of Jerusalem.

9 And the remnant of the people who were left in the city, and those who had defected to him, and the rest of the people who were left, Nebuzaradan captain of the guard exiled to Babylon.

10 And from the poor folk who had no-thing, Nebuzaradan captain of the guard left some in the land of Judah, and he gave them vineyards and winepresses on that day.

11 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon commanded Jeremiah by the hand of Nebuzaradan captain of the guard, saying:

12 Take him and set your eyes upon him, and do not do to him anything bad, for as he speaks to you, so shall you do with him.

13 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard, Nebushazban the Rab-saris, Nergal-sharezer the Rab-mag, and all the officers of the king of Babylon sent and took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the threshold.

14 They sent and took Jeremiah from the court of the prison and gave him to Gedaliah the son of Ahikam the son of Shaphan to release him to the house, and he lived among the people.

15 And the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah while he was confined in the prison court, saying:

16 Go and say to Ebed-melech the Cushite, "Thus says the LORD of hosts, God of Israel: Behold, I will bring my words upon this city for adversity and not for good, and they shall come to pass before you in that day."

17 And I will deliver you in that day, says the LORD, and you shall not be given into the hands of the men from whose presence you fear.

18 For I will deliver you, and by the sword you shall not fall, and your life shall be for prey, because you have trusted in me, says the LORD.


## Chapter 40

1 The act that came to Jeremiah from the LORD after Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard had released him from Ramah, having taken him while he was bound in chains among all the captives of Jerusalem and Judah being exiled to Babylon.

2 The captain of the guards took Jeremiah and said to him, "The LORD your God has spoken this evil against this place."

3 And the LORD brought about and did as He had spoken, because you sinned against the LORD and did not obey His voice, and this became your doom.

4 And now, behold, this day I have released you from the chains on your hand: if it seems good in your eyes to come with me to Babylon, come, and I will keep my eye upon you; but if it seems evil in your eyes to come with me to Babylon, refrain; see, the whole land is before you; go wherever it seems good and right in your eyes to go.

5 While he had not yet returned, he said, Return to Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan, whom the king of Babylon has appointed over the cities of Judah, and dwell with him among the people; or go wherever it seems right to you to go. So the captain of the guard gave him provisions and a gift, and let him go.

6 Jeremiah came to Gedaliah son of Ahikam at Mizpeh and lived among the people who remained in the land.

7 All the captains of the forces who were in the field, they and their men, heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah son of Ahikam over the land, and that he had appointed with him men and women and children and the poorest of the land, those who had not been exiled to Babylon.

8 They came to Gedaliah at Mizpeh, even Ishmael son of Nethaniah, and Johanan and Jonathan sons of Careah, and Seraiah son of Tanhumeth, and the sons of Ephai the Netophathite, and Jezaniah son of the Maachathite, they and their men.

9 Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan swore to them and to their men, saying, "Do not fear from the service of the Chaldeans. Dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it will be well with you."

10 And I, behold, I am dwelling in Mitspah, to stand before the Chaldeans who will come to us; and you, gather wine and summer fruit and oil, and put them in your vessels, and dwell in your cities which you have taken.

11 And also all the Jews who were in Moab and among the children of Ammon and in Edom and who were in all the lands heard that the king of Babylon had left a remnant of Judah and that he had set over them Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan.

12 And all the Jew returned from all the place where they had been driven away, and came into the land of Judah to Gedaliah at Mizpeh, and gathered wine and summer fruit in great abundance.

13 And Johanan son of Careah and all the captains of the forces that were in the field came to Gedaliah at Mizpeh.

14 They said to him, "Do you know that Baalis king of the Ammonites has sent Ishmael son of Nethaniah to strike your life?" But Gedaliah son of Ahikam did not believe them.

15 Johanan son of Careah spoke secretly to Gedaliah at Mizpah, saying, "Let me go, I pray you, and I will strike down Ishmael son of Nethaniah, and no man will know; why should he take your life, so that all the Judeans gathered to you are scattered and the remnant of Judah perishes?"

16 Gedaliah son of Ahikam said to Johanan son of Careah, Do not do this thing, for you are speaking falsely about Ishmael.


## Chapter 41

1 In the seventh month, Ishmael son of Nethaniah son of Elishama came with ten men to Gedaliah son of Ahikam at Mizpah, and they ate bread together there at Mizpah.

2 Ishmael son of Nethaniah arose, he and the ten men who were with him, and they struck Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan with the sword and killed him, whom the king of Babylon had appointed in the land.

3 And all the Jews who were with him at Mizpah, and the Chaldeans who were found there, even the men of war, Ishmael struck down.

4 On the second day after they killed Gedaliah, no man knew.

5 And there came certain men from Shechem, from Shiloh, and from Samaria, eighty men, having their beards shaved and their garments torn, and gashing themselves, with a gift and frankincense in their hands, to bring to the house of the LORD.

6 Ishmael son of Nethaniah went out to meet them from Mitspah, walking and weeping; and when he met them, he said to them, "Come to Gedaliah son of Ahikam."

7 And it happened, when they came into the midst of the city, that Ishmael son of Nethaniah slaughtered them in the midst of the pit, he and the men who were with him.

8 Ten men were found among them, and they said to Ishmael, "Do not kill us, for we have hidden treasures in the field—wheat, barley, oil, and honey." So he spared them and did not put them to death among their brothers.

9 And the cistern into which Ishmael had cast all the corpses of the men whom he had struck down by the hand of Gedaliah—this was the cistern that King Asa had made because of Baasha king of Israel; that one Ishmael son of Nethaniah filled with corpses.

10 Ishmael took captive all the remnant of the people who were at Mitspah, the king's daughters and all the people who remained at Mitspah, whom Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard had appointed under Gedaliah son of Ahikam; and Ishmael son of Nethaniah took them captive and departed to go over to the Ammonites.

11 Johanan son of Careah and all the captains of the forces who were with him heard of all the evil that Ishmael son of Nethaniah had done.

12 They took all the men and went to fight against Ishmael son of Nethaniah, and they found him at the great waters which were in Gibeon.

13 And it happened, when all the people who were with Ishmael saw Johanan the son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces who were with him, they rejoiced.

14 And all the people who had been with Ishmael from Mizpah turned back and returned and went to Johanan son of Kareah.

15 And Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped with eight men from before Johanan, and went to the sons of Ammon.

16 Johanan son of Careah and all the captains of the forces who were with him took all the remnant of the people whom they had brought back from Mizpah, after he had struck Gedaliah son of Ahikam, men, women, children, and officers whom they had brought back from Gibeon.

17 They went and lived in the habitation which was by Bethlehem, to go down to Egypt.

18 Because of the Chaldeans, for they were afraid of them, because Ishmael son of Nethaniah had struck down Gedaliah son of Ahikam, whom the king of Babylon had appointed over the land.


## Chapter 42

1 Then drew near all the captains of the forces, and Johanan son of Kareah, and Jezaniah son of Hoshaiah, and all the people, both small and great.

2 And they said to Jeremiah the prophet, Please let our supplication be accepted before you, and pray for us to the LORD your God, for all the remnant that is left, for we are few compared to how many we were, as your eyes can see us.

3 And the LORD your God will declare to us the way in which we should walk and the thing that we should do.

4 Jeremiah the prophet said to them, I have heard; behold, I am praying to the LORD your God according to your word, and whatever matter the LORD answers you, I will declare to you; I will not withhold from you any matter.

5 And they said to Jeremiah, "May the LORD be witness between us, faithful and true, that we will surely do according to all the word that the LORD your God sends to us."

6 Whether good or evil, we will listen in the voice of the LORD our God to whom we send you, so that it may go well with us when we listen in the voice of the LORD our God.

7 After ten days, the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah.

8 And he called to Johanan son of Careah and to all the captains of the forces who were with him and to all the people, from the least to the greatest.

9 He said to them, Thus says the LORD, God of Israel, whom you sent to him to make supplication before him:

10 If you will indeed remain in this land, then I will build you up and not tear down, and plant you and not uproot; for I relent concerning the calamity that I have brought upon you.

11 Do not fear before the king of Babylon, whom you fear before his presence; do not fear from him, says the LORD, for I am with you to save you and to deliver you from his hand.

12 And I will give you compassion and have mercy on you, and I will bring you back to your land.

13 But if you say, "We will not dwell in this land," so as not to obey the voice of the LORD your God,

14 saying, Not to the land of Egypt will we go, where no war is seen, nor sound of trumpet heard, nor for bread do we hunger, and there we will dwell.

15 And now therefore hear the word of the LORD, remnant of Judah: thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, if you set your faces to go into Egypt and you go to sojourn there:

16 And the sword that you fear will overtake you in the land of Egypt, and the famine that you dread will cling to you there in Egypt, and there you will die.

17 And all the men who set their faces to come into Egypt to sojourn there shall die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence, and there shall be neither remnant nor fugitive from the evil that I will bring upon them.

18 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: As my wrath and my anger were poured out on the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so my anger will be poured out on you when you go to Egypt, and you will become a curse and a desolation and a curse and a reproach, and you will no longer see this place.

19 The LORD spoke to you remnant of Judah, Do not go to Egypt; know for certain, for I testify against you today.

20 For according to your own appetites you sent me to the LORD your God, saying, Pray for us to the LORD our God; and according to all that the LORD our God shall say, so declare to us, and we will do.

21 And I declared to you today, but you did not obey the voice of the LORD your God nor all that I sent to you.

22 And now you shall know that by the sword, by famine, and by pestilence you shall die in the place where you desired to go to dwell.


## Chapter 43

1 And it came to pass, when Jeremiah had made an end of speaking unto all the people all the words of the LORD their God, which the LORD their God had sent unto them, even all these words,

2 Then Azariah the son of Hoshaiah, and Johanan the son of Kareah, and all the proud men said to Jeremiah, "You speak falsely; the LORD our God has not sent you to say, 'You must not go to Egypt to sojourn there.'"

3 Baruch the son of Neriah is cursed, who persuaded you from us, in order to deliver us into the hand of the Chaldeans to kill us and to exile us to Babylon.

4 And Johanan son of Careah and all the captains of the forces and all the people did not obey the voice of the LORD to dwell in the land of Judah.

5 Johanan son of Careah and all the captains of the forces took all the remnant of Judah who had returned from all the nations where they had been driven to sojourn in the land of Judah.

6 the men and the women and the little children and the daughters of the king and all the persons whom Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard had left, even Gedaliah son of Ahikam son of Shaphan and Jeremiah the prophet and Baruch son of Neriah

7 They came into the land of Egypt because they did not listen to the voice of the LORD, and they came as far as Tahpanhes.

8 The act of the LORD came to Jeremiah in Tahapanes, saying:

9 Take large stones in your hand and hide them in the clay in the brickkiln at the entrance of Pharaoh's house in Tahpanhes, before the eyes of the men, the Jews.

10 And you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: I am about to send and take Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, my servant, and I will set his throne above these stones where I have hidden it, and he will spread his royal pavilion over them.

11 And it shall come, and he shall strike the land of Egypt, death to death, captivity to captivity, and sword to sword.

12 I will kindle a fire in the temples of the gods of Egypt, and it will burn them and carry them away; and the LORD will cover the land of Egypt as a shepherd covers his garment, and he will go out from there in peace.

13 And he shall break the pillars of Beth-shemesh which are in the land of Egypt, and the temples of the gods of Egypt he shall burn with fire.


## Chapter 44

1 The act that came to Jeremiah concerning all the Jews dwelling in the land of Egypt, dwelling in Migdol and in Tahpanhes and in Noph and in the land of Pathros, saying:

2 Thus says the LORD of armies, the God of Israel: You have seen all the adversity that I have brought on Jerusalem and on all the cities of Judah, and behold, they are a desolate place today, and there is no one dwelling in them.

3 Because of the evil they did to provoke me by going to burn incense and serve other gods whom neither you nor your fathers have known.

4 And I sent to you all my servant prophets, rising early and sending, to say, "Please do not do this abomination that I hate."

5 They did not listen and did not incline their ear to turn from their evil to cease burning incense to other gods.

6 And my anger and my wrath were poured out on the cities of Judah and on the streets of Jerusalem, and they became a desolate waste as of this day.

7 Now therefore thus says the LORD, God of hosts, the God of Israel: Why are you doing a great evil to your own souls, to cut off from yourselves man and woman, infant and nursing child, from the midst of Judah, so as to leave no remnant to you?

8 to provoke me to anger by the acts of your hands, to burn incense to gods other than me in the land of Egypt, where you are going to sojourn, in order to cut you off, and in order that you may become a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth.

9 Have you forgotten the evils of your fathers and the evils of the kings of Judah and the evils of their wives and your own evils and the evils of your wives, which they committed in the land of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?

10 They have not been humbled to this day, nor have they feared, nor walked in my law and in my statutes which I set before you and before your fathers.

11 Therefore, thus says the LORD of armies, the God of Israel: Behold, I am setting my face against you for adversity, and to cut off all Judah.

12 I will take the remnant of Judah who have set their faces to come into the land of Egypt to sojourn there, and they shall all be consumed in the land of Egypt; they shall fall by the sword, they shall be consumed by the famine; from the least even to the greatest, by the sword and by the famine they shall die; and they shall become a curse, a desolation, a reproach.

13 I will punish those who dwell in the land of Egypt, just as I punished Jerusalem with the sword, with famine, and with pestilence.

14 And there will be no fugitive or survivor among the remnant of Judah who have come to settle in the land of Egypt, nor will they return to the land of Judah, toward which they lift up their souls to return and dwell there, for they will not return unless fugitives.

15 All the men who knew that their wives were making offerings to other gods, and all the women who stood in a great assembly, and all the people who lived in the land of Egypt in Pathros, answered Jeremiah, saying:

16 The advice that you spoke to us in the name of the LORD—we will not listen to you.

17 For we will surely perform all the word that has gone forth from our mouth, to offer sacrifice to the queen of heaven and to pour out drink offerings to her, as we have done, we and our fathers, our kings and our princes, in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem; and we have been filled with bread and have prospered, and we have seen no adversity.

18 And from then on we ceased to burn incense to the queen of the heavens and to pour out drink offerings to her; we have lacked everything, and by sword and by famine we have been consumed.

19 And when we make offerings to the queen of the heavens and pour out drink offerings to her, do we do so without our men, making cakes for her and pouring out drink offerings to her?

20 Jeremiah said to all the people, to the men and to the women and to all the people who were answering him, saying, a word:

21 Did the LORD not remember the incense that you and your fathers, your kings and your princes and the people of the land offered in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, and did it come upon his mind?

22 And the LORD can no longer endure because of the evil of your deeds, because of the abominations that you have committed, so your land has become a desolation, a waste, and a curse, without inhabitant, as at this day.

23 Because you burned incense, and because you sinned against the LORD and did not obey the voice of the LORD, nor walk in his law, nor in his statutes, nor in his testimonies, therefore this adversity has happened to you, as it is today.

24 Jeremiah said to all the people and to all the women, Hear the word of the LORD, all Judah who are in the land of Egypt.

25 Thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: You and your wives have spoken with your mouths and fulfilled with your hands, saying, “We will surely perform our vows that we have vowed, to make offerings to the queen of heaven and to pour out drink offerings to her.” You will surely establish your vows and surely perform your vows.

26 Therefore, hear the word of the LORD, all Judah who dwell in the land of Egypt: I swear by my great name, says the LORD, that my name will no longer be invoked by the mouth of any man of Judah, saying, "As the LORD God lives," in all the land of Egypt.

27 I am watching over them for adversity and not for good, and all the men of Judah who are in the land of Egypt will be consumed by sword and by famine until they are gone.

28 And the fugitives of the sword shall return from the land of Egypt to the land of Judah, the few in number; and all the remnant of Judah who come into the land of Egypt to sojourn there shall know whose word stands firm, mine or theirs.

29 And this shall be a sign to you, says the LORD, that I am visiting you in this place, so that you may know that my words will surely come upon you for adversity.

30 Thus says the LORD, Behold, I will give Pharaoh-hophra king of Egypt into the hand of his enemies and into the hand of those who seek his life, as I gave Zedekiah king of Judah into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, his enemy and the one who sought his life.


## Chapter 45

1 The word that Jeremiah the prophet spoke to Baruch son of Neriah, when he wrote these words on a scroll from the mouth of Jeremiah in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah, saying:

2 Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: to you, Baruch.

3 I said, Alas, now for me, because Jehovah has added grief to my pain; I have grown weary with my groaning; and rest I have not found.

4 Thus you shall say to him: Thus says the LORD: Behold, what I have built I am destroying, and what I have planted I am uprooting, and the whole land.

5 But you, do not seek great things for yourself, for behold, I am bringing adversity upon all flesh, declares the LORD, and I will give you your life as prey in every place where you go.


## Chapter 46

1 The act of the LORD came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the nations.

2 Against Egypt, concerning the army of Pharaoh-necho king of Egypt, who was by the river Euphrates at Carchemish, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon defeated in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah.

3 Arm your buckler and shield, and advance to battle.

4 Harness the horses, mount the horsemen, take your stations with helmets, polish the spears, put on the brigandines.

5 Why have I seen them dismayed, turning back, their warriors stricken, fleeing apace without turning back? Terror all around, says the LORD.

6 Let not the swift flee, nor the mighty escape; northward by the Euphrates River they stumble and fall.

7 Who is this that rises like a river, whose waters rage like floods?

8 Egypt will rise like a river, and its waters will surge like the floods; and he will say, I will rise, I will cover the land, I will destroy the city and its inhabitants.

9 Rise up, horses, and boast, chariots; let the warriors come forth—Cush and Put holding the shield, and Ludim holding the bow.

10 That day shall be a day of vengeance for the LORD God of hosts, to avenge himself on his adversaries; the sword shall devour and be sated and drink its fill of their blood, for the LORD God of hosts holds a sacrifice in the land of the north by the river Euphrates.

11 Go up to Gilead and take balm, virgin daughter of Egypt; in vain you have multiplied medicines—there is no healing for you.

12 Hear, O nations, your shame, and the cry of you has filled the earth; for mighty one has stumbled upon mighty, together both have fallen.

13 The word that the LORD spoke to Jeremiah the prophet about Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon coming to strike the land of Egypt.

14 Declare in Egypt and proclaim in Migdol, and cause it to be heard in Noph and in Tahpanhes: say, "Take your stand and prepare yourself, for the sword has devoured those around you."

15 Why has your mighty one been swept away? He did not stand, because the LORD drove him back.

16 He made many stumble; they fell, one man upon his fellow, and they said, “Arise, and let us return to our people and to the land of our birth, because of the oppressing sword.”

17 Pharaoh king of Egypt made a tumultuous noise there, he caused the appointed time to pass.

18 As I live, saith the king, the LORD of hosts, whose name is Jehovah, he shall come as Tabor is among the mountains, and as Carmel is by the sea.

19 Make thee vessels of captivity, O dweller, daughter of Egypt; for Noph shall become a desolation, and shall be emptied from [lack of] inhabitant.

20 A beautiful heifer is Egypt; destruction from the north has come, has come.

21 Even her hired ones in her midst are like stall-fed calves; for even they have turned, they flee together; they do not stand; for the day of their calamity has come upon them, the time of their visitation.

22 Her voice goes like a serpent; for with might they go, and they come against her with axes, like woodcutters of trees.

23 Cut down the forest, saith the LORD, for it cannot be searched; because they are more numerous than grasshoppers, and they have no number.

24 The daughter of Egypt is shamed, given into the hand of a northern people.

25 The LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, says: I am about to punish Amon of No, and Pharaoh, and Egypt and her gods and her kings, even Pharaoh and those who trust in him.

26 And I will give them into the hand of those who seek their life, and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of his servants; and afterward they shall dwell as in the days of old, says the LORD.

27 But you, Jacob, do not fear, my servant, nor be dismayed, Israel, for behold, I will save you from afar, and your offspring from the land of their captivity; and Jacob shall return and be at rest and quiet, and none shall make him afraid.

28 You shall not fear, my servant Jacob, declares the LORD, for I am with you; for I will make a full end of all the nations where I have driven you, but I will not make a full end of you, and I will correct you in judgment, yet not leave you unpunished.


## Chapter 47

1 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the Philistines, before Pharaoh struck Azzah.

2 Thus says the LORD: Behold, waters rise from the north and become a flooding river, and they shall overflow the land and all that is in it, the city and its inhabitants; and the people shall cry out, and all the inhabitants of the land shall wail.

3 From the sound of the stamping of his mighty ones' hoofs, from the trembling of his chariot, the rumbling of his wheels, fathers did not turn to sons because of the feebleness of hands.

4 Against the day to destroy all the Philistines, to cut off from Tyre and from Sidon every remnant of help; for the LORD will destroy the remnant of the isle of Caphtor.

5 Utterly has the baldness come to Gaza, Ashkelon is like a remnant of their valley; how long wilt thou cut thyself?

6 Woe, sword, to the LORD! How long will you not be quiet? Cease! Do not gird yourself! Rest and be still!

7 How can you be silent, O Jehovah, when he has commanded her? Against Ashkelon and against the coast of the sea there he has appointed her.


## Chapter 48

1 To Moab: Thus says the LORD of armies, the God of Israel: Alas for Nebo, for it is laid waste; Kiriathaim is put to shame, it is taken; Misgab is put to shame and broken.

2 There is no more praise for Moab; in Heshbon they devised affliction against her: “Come, let us cut her off from being a nation.” Even Madmen will be silenced; after you the sword will go.
