3 A cry of destruction from Horonaim, desolation and great destruction.

4 Moab is broken; her little ones have caused a cry to be heard.

5 For at the ascent of Luhith there will be weeping, for at the descent of Horonaim the cry of distress from affliction will be heard.

6 Flee, save your lives, and be as a shrub in the wilderness.

7 Because you trusted in your works and in your treasures, you also shall be captured; and Chemosh shall go into exile, his priests and his princes together.

8 And the destroyer shall come upon every city, and no city shall escape; the valley shall perish and the plain be destroyed, as the LORD has spoken.

9 Give a blossom to Moab, for she will surely flee, and her towns will become desolation, without inhabitant in them.

10 Cursed is the one who does the business of the LORD deceitfully, and cursed is the one who keeps back his sword from blood.

11 Moab has been at ease from his youth, and he has remained on his dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into captivity; therefore his taste remains in him, and his scent has not changed.

12 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, and I will send against him destroyers who shall empty his vessels and shatter his bottles.

13 And Moab is ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Beth-el, their trust.

14 How can you say, We are mighty men and men of war?

15 Moab is plundered, her towns have gone up, and her chosen young men have gone down to the slaughter, says the King, the LORD of hosts, whose name is the LORD.

16 The calamity of Moab is near to coming, and his adversity hastens greatly.

17 Stripped from him are all who were round about, and everyone who knew his fame said, How broken is the rod of strength, the staff of glory!

18 Come down from your glory, you who dwell in Dibon, to thirst; for the destroyer of Moab has come up against you, he has destroyed your strongholds.

19 Along the way, stand and look, O inhabitant of Aroer; ask the fleeing one, inquire what has happened.

20 Moab is shamed, for it is broken down; wail and cry out. Declare by the Arnon that Moab is destroyed.

21 And judgment came to the land of the plain, to Holon and to Jahazah and upon Mephaath.

22 And over Dibon and over Nebo and over Beth-diblathaim.

23 And against Kiriathaim and against Beth-gamul and against Beth-baal-meon.

24 And on Kerioth and on Bozrah and on every city of the land of Moab, far and near.

25 The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, saith the LORD.

26 They have made him drunk, for he has magnified himself against the LORD; and Moab has been dashed with his vomit, and he also has become a derision.

27 Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves? For whenever you spoke of him, you shook your head.

28 Forsake the cities and dwell in the rock, O inhabitants of Moab; and be like a dove nesting in the sides of the mouth of the pit.

29 We have heard the arrogancy of Moab; he is exceedingly proud: his excellency, and his pride, and the swelling of his heart.

30 I know His anger, saith the LORD, but they have not done so; their lies are not so.

31 Therefore I wail for Moab; for all Moab I cry out; I moan for the men of Kir-hareseth.

32 From the overflowing of Jaazer I will weep for you, O vine of Sibmah; your branches passed over the sea, they reached to the sea of Jaazer. Upon your summer fruit and upon your vintage, the spoiler has fallen.

33 And the joy and rejoicing shall be gathered from the fruitful field and from the land of Moab; the wine shall cease from the winepresses, no shouting, no shouting, no shouting.

34 From the cry of Heshbon to Elealeh, to Jahaz they gave their voice; from Zoar to Horonaim, a heifer of the third year; for even the waters of Nimrim shall become desolate.

35 And I will cause to cease from Moab, saith the LORD, him that offereth upon the high places, and him that burneth incense to his gods.

36 Therefore my care for Moab is like pipes; my care for the men of Kir-hareseth is like pipes; therefore the abundance has perished.

37 For every head is bald and every beard is shorn, on all hands there are gashes, and on the loins sackcloth.

38 On all the rooftops of Moab and in all her streets there is lamentation, for I have broken Moab like a vessel in which there is no delight, declares the LORD.

39 How it is broken down! Wail! How Moab has turned the back with shame! So Moab shall be a derision and a terror to all those around him.

40 For thus says the LORD: Behold, he shall swoop like an eagle and spread his wings toward Moab.

41 Kerioth was captured and the fortresses were seized; and the courage of the warriors of Moab in that day will be like the heart of a woman in labor.

42 And Moab shall be destroyed from among the folk, for against the LORD he has magnified himself.

43 Dread and pit and snare are upon you, O dweller of Moab, says the LORD.

44 Because of the fear they will fall into the pit, and the one who climbs out of the pit will be caught in the snare; for I will bring upon them the year of their visitation, says the LORD.

45 In the defence of Heshbon they stood, from the might of Nes; for fire went out from Heshbon, and a flame from the midst of Sihon, and it consumed the corner of Moab and the crown of the sons of Shon.

46 Woe to you, Moab; the folk of Chemosh are destroyed, for your sons have been taken captive and your daughters into captivity.

47 And I will restore the captivity of Moab in the latter end of the days, says the LORD. Behold, the judgment of Moab.


## Chapter 49

1 To the Ammonites thus says the LORD: Is Israel without sons or without heirs? Why then has their king taken possession of Gad, and his people dwell in its cities?

2 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will cause a war cry to be heard against Rabbah of the Ammonites, and it shall become a desolate heap, and her daughters shall be consumed by fire; and Israel shall dispossess those who dispossessed him, says the LORD.

3 Lament, Heshbon, for Ai is destroyed; cry out, daughters of Rabbah, gird on sackcloth, mourn and writhe on the walls, for their king shall go into captivity, his priests and his rulers together.

4 Why do you boast in the valleys, you overflowing valley, daughter turned back, trusting in your treasuries, who will come against me?

5 I am bringing upon you a dread, declares the LORD of armies, from all around you, and you shall be driven, each man before his face, and there is no one gathering the wanderer.

6 And afterward I will bring back the captive state of the children of Ammon, says the LORD.

7 To Edom, thus says the LORD of hosts: Is wisdom no more in Teman? Has counsel perished from the sons thereof? Is their wisdom corrupted?

8 Flee, turn aside, go deep to dwell, O inhabitants of Dedan, for calamity of Esau I have brought upon him, the time of his visitation.

9 If grapegleanings had come to you, they would not have left (gleaning) of the grapes; if thieves in night, they would have destroyed only as (much as is) sufficient.

10 For I have stripped Esau, I have uncovered his secret places, and he is not able to hide; his children and his brothers and his neighbors are destroyed, and he is not.

11 I will care for your orphans, and your widows must rely on me.

12 For thus says the LORD: Behold, those who were not sentenced to drink the cup will drink it; but as for you, you will surely be punished, you will not go unpunished, for you shall surely drink.

13 For by Me hath it been said, saith the LORD, that Bozrah shall become a desolation, a reproach, a waste, and a curse; and all her cities shall be perpetual desolate places.

14 A report I have heard from the LORD, a messenger is sent among the nations: assemble and come against her, and rise up to war.

15 For behold, I have made you small among the nations; you are despised by man.

16 Your terrible pride has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, who hold the height of the hill; though you make your nest as high as an eagle, from there I will bring you down, says the LORD.

17 And Edom will become a desolation; everyone passing over her will be appalled and hiss at all her plagues.

18 As when the LORD overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and its neighbors, no man shall dwell there, nor shall a son of man sojourn in it.

19 Behold, like a young lion coming up from the pride of the Jordan to a permanent dwelling, for I will drive him away from it; and who is the chosen one to whom I shall appoint it? For who is like me, and who will summon me? And who is the shepherd who can stand before me?

20 Therefore, hear the counsel that the LORD has taken against Edom, and the purposes that he has devised against the inhabitants of Teman: Will not their little ones be dragged away? Will not their dwellings be made desolate?

21 From the sound of their panic the earth trembled; a cry was heard at the Sea of Reeds, the sound of her voice.

22 Behold, the eagle will mount up and soar, and spread its wings over Bozrah; and the courage of the mighty men of Edom in that day will be like the courage of a woman in labor.

23 Damascus is put to shame, Hamath and Arpad are dismayed, because they have heard an evil report; they are melted away: in the sea there is care; to be still is not possible.

24 Damascus has become feeble, she turns to flee, and fear has gripped her; anguish has seized her like a woman in labor.

25 How is it that the city, the city of my joy, is not forsaken?

26 Therefore the chosen young men will fall in her streets, and all the warriors will be silenced on that day, says the LORD of hosts.

27 I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus, and it will consume the palaces of Benhadad.

28 Against Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon struck, thus says the LORD: Arise, go up against Kedar and plunder the sons of the east.

29 Their tents and their flocks they will take, their curtains and all their vessels and their camels they will carry away for them, and they will cry out against them terror from every side.

30 Flee, flee far off, hide deep; ye that dwell in Hazor, saith the LORD; for Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath taken counsel against you, and hath conceived a device.

31 Rise up, go up against a nation dwelling in assurance, declares the LORD: he has no double doors nor bar; alone he dwells.

32 And their camels shall become prey and the abundance of their cattle a spoil, and they shall be scattered to every wind, those with the corners of their hair cut, and from all their quarters I will bring their calamity, says the LORD.

33 Hazor will become a den for dragons, a desolation forever; no man will dwell there, nor will a human being sojourn in it.

34 The act of the LORD came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning Elam, at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah, saying:

35 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, I will break the bow of Elam, the chief of their strength.

36 I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four corners of the heavens, and I will scatter them to all the winds; and there will be no nation to which the exiles of Elam shall not come.

37 I will smite Elam before their enemies and before those who seek their lives, and I will bring disaster upon them, even the fury of my anger, declares the LORD; and I will send the sword after them until I have consumed them.

38 I will set my throne in Elam, and I will destroy from there king and captains, says the LORD.

39 And it shall come to pass in the latter days that I will turn the captivity of Elam, saith the LORD.


## Chapter 50

1 The act that the LORD spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, by the hand of Jeremiah the prophet.

2 Declare among the nations and proclaim; raise up a banner; proclaim and do not conceal: Babylon is captured; Bel is put to shame; Merodach is dismayed; her idols are put to shame; her images are dismayed.

3 For a nation has come up from the north and set her land to desolation, and there is no inhabitant remaining in it, from man to beast they have fled, they are gone.

4 In those days and at that time, says the LORD, the children of Israel and the children of Judah shall come together, walking and weeping, and they shall seek the LORD their God.

5 Zion shall enquire along the way toward his face: Come, and let us join ourselves to the LORD in an everlasting covenant that shall not be forgotten.

6 My people are scattered sheep; their shepherds have led them astray, causing them to wander from mountain to hill, forgetting their resting place.

7 All who found them devoured them, and their adversaries said, “We are not guilty,” because they sinned against the LORD, the habitation of justice, the hope of their ancestors, the LORD.

8 They are driven from the midst of Babylon, and from the land of the Chaldeans, and become as rams before the flock.

9 For behold, I am raising up and bringing against Babylon an assembly of great nations from the land of the north, and they shall array themselves against her; from there she shall be captured; her arrows are like those of an expert warrior, they shall not return empty.

10 And the Chaldeans shall become prey; all who plundered them shall be satisfied, says the LORD.

11 Because you rejoice, because you exult, O plunderers of my heritage, because you frisk like a heifer in the grass and neigh like stallions.

12 Your mother is utterly ashamed, she who bore you is disgraced; behold, the latter end of the nations is a wilderness, a dry land and a desert.

13 Because of the wrath of the LORD it shall not be inhabited, but shall be utterly desolate; everyone who passes by Babylon shall be appalled and hiss at all her plagues.

14 Set yourselves against Babylon on every side, all you archers; shoot at her, do not spare any arrow, for she has sinned against the LORD.

15 Shout against her all around; she has offered her hand; her foundations have fallen, her walls are destroyed, for the vengeance of the LORD is upon her; he has avenged himself on her according to what she has done.

16 Cut off the sower from Babylon, and seize the sickle at harvest time; because of the sword, each man shall turn to his people, and each man shall flee to his land.

17 A scattered sheep is Israel, lions have driven it away; the first one who devoured it was the king of Assyria, and this last one who has broken its bones is Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.

18 Therefore, thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I will visit upon the king of Babylon and upon his land, as I have visited upon the king of Assyria.

19 And I will bring Israel back to his comely dwelling; and he shall feed on Carmel and Bashan, and on the hill of Ephraim and Gilead, and his soul shall be satisfied.

20 In those days and at that time, says the LORD, the iniquity of Israel shall be sought and shall not be found, and the sins of Judah shall not be discovered, for I will pardon those I leave.

21 Against the land of Merathaim, go up against her, and against the inhabitants of Pekod; destroy and devote them after them, says the LORD; do all that I have commanded you.

22 A sound of war in the land and great destruction.

23 How is the hammer broken and shattered, the one that struck the whole earth? How has Babylon become a desolation among the nations?

24 I laid a snare for you, and you were also taken, O Babylon, and you did not know; you were found and also caught, because you strove against the LORD.

25 The LORD opened his storehouse and brought out the instruments of his anger, for it was a work for the Lord Jehovah of hosts in the land of the Chaldeans.

26 Come against her from the end; open her storehouses; empty her like heaps of grain and destroy her. Let no remnant remain in her.

27 Slay all her young bulls; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them, for their day has come, the time of their visitation.

28 A sound of fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon, to declare in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God, the vengeance of his temple.

29 Sound the alarm against Babylon, you archers; surround her on every side; let no one escape; repay her according to her act, according to all she has done so do to her, for she has acted insolently against the LORD, against the Holy One of Israel.

30 Therefore her chosen young men will fall in her streets, and all her warriors will be cut down on that day, says the LORD.

31 Behold, I am against you, O proud one, says the LORD of hosts; for your day has come, the time of your visitation.

32 And the proud man will stumble and fall, and there is no one to raise him up, and I will kindle a fire in his towns, and it will consume all who are around him.

33 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The children of Israel and the children of Judah have been oppressed together, and all their captors have held them fast, refusing to let them go.

34 Their Redeemer is strong, the LORD of armies is his name; he will zealously plead their cause, to bring peace to the earth and to trouble the inhabitants of Babylon.

35 A sword against the Chaldeans, declares the LORD, and against the inhabitants of Babylon, and against her rulers and against her wise men.

36 A sword against the diviners, and they shall become fools; sword against her mighty ones and they are cut down.

37 Sword against his horses and against his chariots and against all the mixed people who are in the midst of her, and they shall become women; sword against her storehouses and they shall be plundered.

38 Desolation upon her waters and they dried up, for she is a land of carved images, and they delight in idols.

39 Therefore, wild beasts of the desert will dwell with wild beasts of the islands, and daughters of the owl will dwell in it, and it will not be inhabited forever, nor will it be settled from generation to generation.

40 When God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighboring cities, says the LORD, no man shall dwell there, nor shall any human being sojourn in her.

41 Behold, a folk comes from the north, and a great nation and many kings shall be stirred up from the coasts of the earth.

42 They hold bow and spear; they are cruel and show no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea; they ride on horses, arrayed like a man for war against you, daughter Babylon.

43 The king of Babylon heard the report and his hands fell helpless; distress seized him, pain as of a woman in labor.

44 Behold, like a young lion he comes up from the pride of the Jordan to a habitation of strength; for I will suddenly move from upon her, and who is the chosen one, to whom I shall appoint her? For who is like me, and who will summon me, and who is the shepherd that can stand before me?

45 Therefore, hear the counsel of the LORD that he has counseled against Babylon, and his thoughts that he has devised against the land of the Chaldeans: surely the young of the flock will drag them away, surely he will make desolate their dwelling place.

46 From the sound of Babylon's capture the earth trembled and a cry was heard among the nations.


## Chapter 51

1 Thus says the LORD: Behold, I am stirring up a destroying wind against Babylon and against the inhabitants of the midst of those who rise up against me.

2 I will send foreigners against Babylon and they will empty her land, for they will be upon her from every side in a day of adversity.

3 Do not let the archer advance with his bow, nor mount in his brigandine; show no pity to his young men, utterly destroy all his army.

4 And the slain shall fall in the land of the Chaldeans, and those pierced through in her streets.

5 For Israel and Judah have not forsaken their God, the LORD of hosts, for their land is full of guiltiness, the sin of Israel.

6 Flee from the midst of Babylon, and save each man his life; do not be destroyed in her iniquity, for it is a time of vengeance for the LORD, a recompense he repays to her.

7 A golden cup in the hand of the LORD is Babylon, making all the nations drunk; from her wine the nations have drunk, therefore the nations are mad.

8 Suddenly Babel has fallen and is broken; wail over her, take balm for her sorrow, perhaps she may be healed.

9 We would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed. Leave her, and let each man go to his own land, for her judgment reaches to the heavens and is lifted up to the skies.

10 Bring forth the justice of Jehovah; come, let us recount in Zion the deeds of Jehovah our God.

11 Sharpen the arrows, fill the quivers; the LORD has stirred the spirit of the kings of the Medes against Babylon, to destroy her, for it is the vengeance of the LORD, the vengeance of his temple.

12 Raise a banner against the walls of Babylon, maintain the watch, set up the guards, prepare the ambushes, for the LORD has both purposed and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.

13 Upon abundant water-floods, great store-houses; your end has come, the cubit of your dishonest gain.

14 The LORD of hosts has sworn by his soul: "Surely I will fill you with people like the locust, and they shall roar against you with shouting."

15 Making the earth by his power, establishing the world by his wisdom, and stretching out the heavens by his understanding.

16 At the sound of his giving, a rumbling of waters in the heavens; he raises vapours from the uttermost parts of the earth; he makes lightning for the rain; he brings forth wind from his storehouses.

17 Every person is cut off from knowledge; every maker of a carved image is put to shame, for false is its molten image, and there is no spirit in them.

18 Vanity altogether are they, acts of error; in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

19 Not like these is the inheritance of Jacob, for the Maker of all is He, and the tribe of His inheritance; the LORD of hosts is His name.

20 You are a battle ax for me, weapon of war; I will shatter nations in you, and I will destroy kingdoms in you.

21 And I will dash in pieces by you the horse and its rider, and I will dash in pieces by you the chariot and its rider.

22 I will scatter against you man and woman, I will scatter against you old and young, I will scatter against you young man and virgin.

23 I will scatter against you the shepherd and his flock, the husbandman and his yoke, the captain and the rulers.

24 And I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea for all their evil that they did to Zion before your eyes, declares the LORD.

25 Behold, I am against you, O mount of destruction, declares the LORD, the destroyer of all the earth; I will stretch out My hand against you, and roll you down from the rocks, and make you a mountain of burning.

26 And they shall not take from you a stone for a bulwark nor a stone for foundations, for desolations everlasting you shall be, says the LORD.

27 Lift up a banner in the land, blow the trumpet among the nations, consecrate a war against her, summon the kingdoms against her, Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz; appoint a captain against her, bring up the horse like the creeping locust.

28 Consecrate against her the nations, the kings of Media, its governors and all its rulers, and every land of his dominion.

29 The land trembled and shook, for the LORD has purposed against Babylon, to make the land of Babylon a desolation without inhabitant.

30 The mighty men of Babel ceased from fighting; they sat in strongholds; their strength was weakened; they became as women; they set fire to her dwelling places; her bars were broken.

31 He runs to meet a runner, and a herald to meet a herald, to report to the king of Babylon that his city has been captured at the farthest edge.

32 And the fords were seized, and the ponds they burned with fire, and the men of war were dismayed.

33 For thus says the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel: O daughter Babylon, you are like a threshing floor at the time of treading; still a little while, and the time of harvest will come upon her.

34 Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has devoured me; he has crushed me, he has made me an empty vessel; he has swallowed me like a dragon, he has filled his belly with my delicacies.

35 My cruelty and my near kin upon Babylon, says the dweller in Zion, and my blood to the inhabitants of Chaldea, says Jerusalem.

36 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Behold, I will contend with your adversary and avenge your vengeance; I will make desolate your sea and dry up your fountain.

37 And Babel shall become heaps, a resting place of jackals, a desolation and a hissing, without an inhabitant.

38 Together like (young) lions they will roar, they have grown strong like whelps of (young) lions.

39 In their heat I will set their drink, and I will make them drunk, so that they may rejoice and sleep an eternal sleep, and not awake, says the LORD.

40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with chief ones.

41 How is Sheshach taken, the praise of all the earth? How is Babylon become a desolation among the nations?

42 The sea has come up over Babylon, its waves in tumult have covered her.

43 Her cities became a desolation, a land of drought and wilderness, a land in which no man dwells, nor does any son of man pass through it.

44 I will punish Bel in Babylon, and I will bring out of his mouth that which he hath swallowed up; and the nations shall not come to him any more; also the wall of Babylon is fallen.

45 Go out from among her, my people, and deliver every man his soul from the fierce wrath of the LORD.

46 Lest your heart faint and you fear at the report heard in the land, and calamity comes in the year of report, and after it in the following year of report, and violence is in the land, and one ruler overcomes another.

47 Therefore, behold, days are coming when I will punish the carved images of Babylon, and all her land will be ashamed, and all her slain will fall within her.

48 Shout against Babylon, O heavens and earth, and all that is in them; for from the north the destroyer comes against her, declares the LORD.

49 Even Babel has fallen, the slain of Israel; yes, at Babel have fallen the slain of all the earth.

50 Fugitives from the sword, go, do not stand; remember the LORD from afar, and Jerusalem come upon your minds.

51 We are ashamed, for we have heard reproach; confusion has covered our faces, because strangers have come upon the sanctuaries of the house of the LORD.

52 Therefore, behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when I will punish her carved images, and throughout her land the wounded will groan.

53 For if Babel should ascend to the heavens, and if she should fortify the height of her strength, from me shall come spoilers against her, declares the LORD.

54 A cry aloud from Babylon and a great breaking from the land of the Chaldeans.

55 For the LORD has destroyed Babylon and silenced the loud voice from her; her waves roar like many waters, the sound of their tumult has been given.

56 For a destroyer has come upon Babylon; her warriors are taken, their bows are broken, for the LORD is a God of recompense; he will surely repay.

57 I will make drunk her captains, and her wise men, her governors, and her princes, and her mighty ones; and they shall sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, says the King, the LORD of hosts, whose name is the LORD.

58 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The walls of Babylon, broad as they are, shall be utterly overthrown, and her gates, so high, shall be set on fire; and the peoples shall labor only for emptiness, and the nations only for fire, and they shall be weary.

59 The act that Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Maaseiah, when he went with Zedekiah king of Judah to Babylon in the fourth year of his reign, and Seraiah was chief of the officials.

60 Jeremiah wrote in a scroll all the adversity that was to come upon Babylon, all these words that are written concerning Babylon.

61 Jeremiah said to Seraiah, "When you come to Babylon, and see, then you shall read all these words."

62 And you shall say, Jehovah, the Lord, You said concerning this place, to destroy it, so that there should be no inhabitant in it, neither man nor beast, for it shall be desolate wastes forever.

63 And when you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it and throw it into the middle of the Euphrates.

64 And you shall say, Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise again because of the calamity that I am bringing upon her; they shall be weary; thus far the words of Jeremiah.


## Chapter 52

1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem; the name of his mother was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.

3 Because of the anger of the LORD, it was against Jerusalem and Judah until He cast them from His presence; and Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.

4 In the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came with all his army against Jerusalem, and they encamped against it, and built a siege fort all around it.

5 The city entered the siege until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.

6 In the fourth month, on the ninth day of the month, the famine became severe in the city, and there was no bread for the people of the land.

7 The city was breached and all the men of war fled, going out from the city by night by way of the gate between the two walls which was by the king’s garden, while the Chaldeans were around the city, and they went by way of the Arabah.

8 The army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho, and all his army was scattered from him.

9 They seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and he spoke with him in judgments.

10 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and also slaughtered all the leaders of Judah at Riblah.

11 And he put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him in brass fetters, and the king of Babylon brought him to Babylon, and he was kept in custody until the day of his death.

12 In the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month, in the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard came, he stood before the king of Babylon at Jerusalem.

13 And he burned the temple of the LORD and the royal palace, and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every great house he burned with fire.

14 And all the walls of Jerusalem round about, all the army of the Chaldeans that was with the captain of the guard broke down.

15 And the poorest of the people, and the rest of the people who remained in the city, and those who had defected to the king of Babylon, and the remnant of the multitude—Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard exiled.

16 And from the poorest of the land Nebuzaradan the captain of the guards left vine dressers and farmers.

17 And the pillars of brass that belonged to the temple of the LORD, and the bases and the sea of brass that were in the temple of the LORD, the Chaldeans broke in pieces and carried their brass to Babylon.

18 And the caldrons, and the shovels, and the snuffers, and the bowls, and the spoons, and all the vessels of brass which they used in service—they took.

19 And the basins and the firepans and the bowls and the pots and the candlesticks and the spoons and the bowls, those of gold being gold and those of silver being silver, the captain of the guards took.

20 The two pillars, the one sea, and the twelve oxen of bronze which were under the bases that the king Solomon had made for the temple of the LORD, the weight of the bronze for them was not determined; all these vessels.

21 The pillars were eighteen cubits apiece, and a line of twelve cubits encircled each pillar; their thickness was four fingers.

22 And a chapiter of brass upon it, and the height of the chapiter one, five cubits, and network and pomegranates upon the chapiter round about, all brass; and so for the second pillar, and pomegranates.

23 Ninety-six pomegranates were in the air, all the pomegranates one hundred on the network round about.

24 The captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest and the three keepers of the threshold.

25 And from the city he took one chamberlain who had been overseer of the men of war, and seven men from the king's presence who were found in the city, and the scribe, captain of the host, who mustered the people of the land, and sixty men of the people of the land who were found in the midst of the city.

26 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.

27 The king of Babylon struck them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and Judah was exiled from his land.

28 This is the folk whom Nebuchadnezzar exiled in the year seven: Jews, three thousand and twenty-three.

29 In the year eighteen of Nebuchadnezzar, from Jerusalem, souls eight hundred thirty-two.

30 In the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guards exiled the Jews, seven hundred forty-five persons; every person, four thousand six hundred.

31 In the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the year of his reign, lifted up the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah and brought him forth from the house of prison.

32 And he spoke good things to him, and gave his seat above the seat of the kings who were with him in Babylon.

33 And he changed his prison apparel and ate bread before him continually all the days of his life.

34 And his allowance, a daily allowance, was given him by the king of Babylon, a daily portion every day, all the days of his life.


# LAMENTATIONS

## Chapter 1

1 How desolate the city sits, once full of people! She has become like a widow; great among the nations, princess among the provinces, she has become a tribute.

2 She weeps bitterly in the night and her tears are on her cheek; she has no comforter among all her lovers; all her companions have dealt treacherously with her, they have become her enemies.

3 Judah has gone into exile because of affliction and great servitude; she dwells among the nations but found no place of rest; all her pursuers overtook her between the straits.

4 The ways of Zion mourn for lack of those who come to the appointed assembly; all her gates are desolate, her priests sigh; her virgins are in distress, and she is in bitterness.

5 Her adversaries have become the head; her enemies prosper, because the LORD has afflicted her on account of the multitude of her transgressions; her children have gone into captivity before the adversary.

6 Zion went out in all her beauty; her princes were like harts that found no pasture, and they walked without strength before the pursuer.

7 Jerusalem remembered the days of her affliction and misery, all her pleasant things that were from the days of old, when her people fell by the hand of the adversary and there was no helper for her; her adversaries saw her, they mocked at her sabbath.

8 Jerusalem has grievously offended; therefore she has become removed: all who honored her have despised her, because they have seen her nakedness; she also sighed and turned backward.

9 Her filthiness is in the hem of her skirt; she did not remember her latter end; she descended marvels; there is no comforter for her; see, O LORD, my affliction, for the enemy has magnified himself.

10 His hand has spread the adversary over all her pleasant things, because she saw nations enter your sanctuary, whom you commanded should not enter your congregation.

11 All her people groan, seeking bread; they have given their valuables for food to sustain life; see, O the LORD, and behold, that I have become wretched.

12 Not to you, all passers-by, look and see: Is there any grief like my grief, which has come upon me, with which the LORD has afflicted me in the day of his fierce anger?

13 From on high he sent fire into my bones and it descended; he spread a net for my feet, he turned me backward; he made me desolate, continually ill.

14 The yoke of my transgressions is fastened upon his hand; they are bound upon my neck; he has sapped my strength; my Lord has delivered me into the hand of those from whom I am not able to rise.

15 The LORD has crushed all my mighty men in the midst of me; the LORD has proclaimed a set time against me to crush the young men of Judah; the LORD has trodden the virgin daughter of Judah.

16 For these things I weep: my eye, my eye streams with water, because far from me is the one who comforts, who restores my soul; my children are desolate, because the enemy has prevailed.

17 Zion spread her hands; there is no one to comfort her; the LORD has commanded concerning Jacob that his adversaries surround him; Jerusalem has become unclean among them.

18 The LORD is righteous, for I have rebelled against his word; hear now, all peoples, and behold my sorrow: my virgins and my young men have gone into captivity.

19 I called to my lovers, but they deceived me; my priests and elders in the city have failed, for they sought food for themselves but could not sustain their own souls.

20 See, O LORD, for distressing is my pain; my inward parts are stirred, my heart is turned within me, for bitter, bitter is my suffering; without, the sword bereaves; within, there is death.

21 Hear, for I am groaning—there is no comforter for me; all my enemies have heard of my adversity; they rejoiced because you have done it. You have brought the day you proclaimed, and they shall be like me.

22 Let all their adversity come before you, and bring upon them what you brought on me for all my transgressions; for my groanings are many and my heart is in anguish.


## Chapter 2

1 How can the LORD in His anger reject the daughter of Zion, cast down from heaven to earth the glory of Israel, and not remember His footstool in the day of His wrath?

2 (my) Lord has swallowed up in His wrath all the pleasant places of Jacob; He has thrown down to the ground the strongholds of the daughter of Judah; He has profaned the kingdom and its princes.

3 The fierce anger cut off every horn of Israel; he drew back his right hand from before the enemy, and he burned in Jacob like a flaming fire, consuming all around.

4 He bent his bow like an enemy; he stood with his right hand as an adversary, and slew all that were pleasant to the eye; in the tent of the daughter of Zion he poured out his wrath like fire.

5 The LORD was as an enemy; He has swallowed up Israel, He has swallowed up all her palaces, He has destroyed His strongholds; He has increased in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.

6 He heated like a garden, its tabernacle destroyed, its appointed time forgotten; the LORD in Zion rejected the appointed feast and sabbath, and in the rage of his anger despised king and priest.

7 The LORD has abandoned His altar, defiled His sanctuary; He has delivered the fortified palaces into the hand of the enemy. A cry resounds in the house of the LORD as on the day of a solemn assembly.

8 Jehovah, the Lord thought to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion; he stretched out a line; he did not turn his hand from swallowing; and the bulwark and wall together mourned.

9 Her gates have sunk into the earth; he has destroyed and broken her bars; her king and her rulers are among the nations, there is no law; also her prophets have found no vision from the LORD.

10 The elders of Daughter Zion sit on the ground; they have thrown dust on their heads, girded themselves with sackcloth; the virgins of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.

11 My eyes are spent with tears, my belly churns, my liver is poured out on the ground because of the destruction of the daughter of my people, as the babe and the infant faint in the streets of the city.

12 To their mothers they will say, Where is the corn and the wine? when they faint in the broad places of the city, pouring out their souls into the bosom of their mothers.

13 What can I witness for you, to what can I compare you, O daughter of Jerusalem? What can I equate with you and comfort you, virgin daughter of Zion? For great as the sea is your affliction; who can heal you?

14 Your prophets have seen for you false and worthless things, and they did not reveal your iniquity to turn you back; they saw for you burdens of falsehood and cause of banishment.

15 All who pass along the way clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem: Is this the city of perfect beauty, the joy of all the earth?

16 All your enemies opened their mouths against you, they hissed and gnashed the tooth, they said, "We have swallowed her; surely this day we have found, we have seen."

17 The LORD has done what he planned; he has fulfilled his word that he commanded from ancient times. He has overthrown and shown no pity, and he has made the enemy rejoice over you; he has exalted the horn of your adversaries.

18 Their heart cried to the LORD: "Wall of Daughter Zion, pour out tears like a river day and night; give yourself no rest, do not cease, daughter of your eye."

19 Arise, cry out at the beginning of the watches; pour out your heart like water before the LORD; lift your hands to him for the life of your infants who faint from hunger at the head of every street.

20 See, O LORD, and behold to whom such things have been done: shall women eat their own children, the babes of span-long? Shall priest and prophet be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?

21 The streets have sunk into the earth, boy and old man lie fallen; my virgins and young men have fallen by the sword; you killed in the day of your wrath; you slaughtered without pity.

22 Call out as on a day of appointed solemnity my terrors from all around, for on the day of the LORD’s fierce anger there is no fugitive or remnant, whom I had cradled and raised, my enemy has destroyed them all.


## Chapter 3

1 I am the man who has seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.

2 He led me and made me walk in darkness and not in light.

3 Only in me he will return; his hand will turn continually.

4 He has worn my body and my skin; he has broken my bones.

5 He has built against me and surrounded me with gall and hardship.

6 He set me in dark places like the eternally dead.

7 He shut me in, and I cannot go out; he has made my fetter of brass heavy.

8 Even though I cry out and wail, he blocks my prayer.

9 He has walled my ways with hewn stone; he has made crooked my paths.

10 A bear lurks for me, a lion in secret.

11 He has turned aside my ways and torn me in pieces; he has made me desolate.

12 He bent his bow and set me as a mark for the arrow.

13 He brought the sons of his quiver into my reins.

14 I was a derision to all my people, their song all the day.

15 He has filled me with bitterness, He has made me drink wormwood.

16 He hath broken my teeth with gravel, he shattered me with ashes.

17 My soul has rejected peace; I have forgotten prosperity.

18 And I said, My victory is lost, my hope from the LORD is gone.

19 Remember my affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the poison.

20 Remember! My soul continually remembers it and is bowed down within me.

21 This I will recall to my heart; therefore I will wait.

22 The LORD's acts of favour have not ceased, for His mercies have not come to an end.

23 New every morning; great is your faithfulness.

24 My soul said, The LORD is my portion; therefore I will wait for him.

25 The LORD is good to those who wait for him, to the soul that seeks him.

26 It is good to wait quietly for the deliverance of the LORD.

27 It is good for every one that he bear the yoke in his youth.

28 He sits alone and is still, because he has taken upon him.

29 Let him put his mouth in the dust; perhaps there is hope.

30 Let him give his cheek to the one who strikes him; let him be filled with reproach.

31 For the LORD will not forsake forever.

32 But though he causes grief, he will have compassion according to the abundance of his steadfast love.

33 For he does not afflict from his heart, nor grieve the children of men.

34 To subdue under his feet every prisoner of the land.

35 To pervert the judgment of a man before the face of the Most High.

36 To pervert a person in his cause—my Lord has not seen.

37 Who has spoken and it came to pass, (my) Lord did not command?

38 From the mouth of the Most High does not come the evil and the good.

39 Why should any man who is alive, every one, complain of his fault?

40 Let us search our ways and examine, and return to the LORD.

41 We lift up our heart to the hand toward God in the heavens.

42 We have transgressed and rebelled; you did not pardon.

43 You pursued us in anger and did not spare, You killed without pity.

44 He covered you with a cloud to prevent the passing of prayer.

45 You make us offscouring and refuse among the nations.

46 They opened their mouths against us, all our enemies.

47 Dread and a pit were for us, the lifting and the breaking.

48 Streams of water flow from my eyes because of the breaking of the daughter of my people.

49 My eye has flowed and there is no cessation.

50 Until Jehovah does look down and see from the heavens.

51 My eye is worn out from grief for my soul, because of all the daughters of my city.

52 My enemies hunt me like a bird without cause.

53 My life is worn out in the dungeon, and they have cast a stone upon me.

54 Water has covered my head; I said, I am cut off.

55 I called your name, the LORD, from the lowest cistern.

56 My voice you heard; do not hide your ear from my breathing, from my crying.

57 When I called You in the day, You said, "Do not fear."

58 You have contended, O my Lord, for the contention of my soul; You have redeemed my life.

59 You have seen, O LORD, my wrong; You have judged my cause.

60 You have seen all their vengeance, all their devices against me.

61 You have heard their reproach, O LORD, all their devices against me.

62 The lips of those who rise against me, and their meditation, are upon me all the day.

63 I have observed their sitting down and their rising up; their music is mine.

64 Give them their due, O LORD, according to the deeds of their hands.

65 You will give them hardness of heart; your curse will be upon them.

66 Pursue in anger and destroy them, from beneath the heavens of the LORD.


## Chapter 4

1 How the gold has become dim! How the fine gold is changed! The holy stones are poured out at the head of every street.

2 The precious sons of Zion, esteemed as fine gold, how are they reckoned as earthen vessels, the work of a potter's hands?

3 Even jackals offer the breast and nurse their young, but the daughter of my people has become cruel, like ostriches in the wilderness.

4 The tongue of the infant cleaves to the roof of its mouth from thirst; the young children ask for food, but there is none to break it for them.

5 Those who ate dainty foods are desolate in the streets; those who were brought up in scarlet embrace dung hills.

6 The fault of my people's daughter is greater than the sin of Sodom; she was overthrown as in an instant, yet hands were not weakened there.

7 The skin of her Nazarites was whiter than snow, purer than milk; their bones were ruddier than rubies, their polishing like sapphire.

8 Their countenance is blacker than coal; they are not recognized in the streets; their skin clings to their bones; it has become dry like wood.

9 Better were those slain by the sword than those slain by famine, for they wasted away pierced through, from the fruits of the field.

10 The hands of compassionate women have cooked their children; they became their food in the breaking of my people.

11 The LORD has completed his wrath; he has poured out his fierce anger. He has kindled a fire in Zion that has consumed its foundations.

12 The kings of the earth and the inhabitants of the world did not believe that an enemy and a distress would come into the gates of Jerusalem.

13 Because of the punishment of her prophets, the iniquity of her priests, who shed in her midst the blood of the innocent.

14 The blind wander abroad, defiled with blood, unable to touch their clothing.

15 Turn, unclean one; they called to them: Turn away, keep away, do not touch, for they have been utterly rejected and cast out; they said among the Gentiles: They will no longer dwell there.

16 The face of the LORD has scattered them; he will no longer look upon them; they did not respect the priests, nor show favor to the elders.

17 Our eyes have failed in looking for our help which was vanity; in our watching we watched for a nation that could not save.

18 Our steps have turned from walking in our broad places; our end has drawn near, our days are fulfilled, for our end has come.

19 Our pursuers were swifter than eagles in the heavens; on the mountains they hunted us; in the wilderness they lay in wait for us.

20 The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of the LORD, was caught in their pits, of whom we said, "In his shade we will live among the nations."

21 Rejoice and be glad, daughter of Edom, in the land of Uz; indeed, the cup will pass over to you; you will drink and be exposed.

22 The fault of Zion has been fully punished, daughter of Zion, he will no longer carry you away into exile; the LORD has punished your fault, daughter of Edom, he has laid bare your sin.


## Chapter 5

1 Remember, O LORD, what has befallen us; look upon our reproach.

2 Our heritage is turned over to strangers, our homes to foreigners.

3 We were orphans without a father; our mothers were like widows.

4 Our water we drank for silver; our wood came at a price.

5 Our pursuers are upon our necks; we are weary, and no rest is given to us.

6 We gave the hand to Egypt and to Assyria, to be satisfied with bread.

7 Our fathers sinned and are no more; we bear their iniquities.

8 Bondmen ruled over us; there was none to deliver from their hand.

9 With our lives we got our bread because of the sword of the wilderness.

10 Our skin has become like an oven's heat from the fury of famine.

11 Women in Zion were defiled, virgins in the towns of Judah.

12 Captains in their hand were hanged; the faces of elders were not honored.

13 The chosen young men have borne the grinding; the boys have stumbled at the wood.

14 The elders from the gate have ceased, the young men from their song.

15 Our joy and mirth have ceased, our dancing is turned to mourning.

16 Fallen is the crown of our head; alas for us, for we have sinned.

17 Because of this our heart is sick and our eyes have grown dim.

18 Foxes walk on the mount of Zion that is laid waste.

19 You, O Jehovah, will reign forever; your throne endures from generation to generation.

20 Why do you forget us alway, and forsake us for the length of days?

21 Turn us back to you, O LORD; renew our days as of old.

22 Indeed, you have utterly rejected us; you are exceedingly angry with us.


# EZEKIEL

## Chapter 1

1 In the thirtieth year, in the fourth month, on the fifth day of the month, while I was among the captives by the river Chebar, the heavens were opened and I saw visions of God.

2 In the fifth month, it was the fifth year of the captivity of King Jehoiachin.

3 The word of the LORD came to Ezekiel the son of Buzi the priest in the land of the Chaldeans by the river Chebar, and the hand of the LORD was upon him there.

4 And I looked, and behold, a stormy wind came from the north, a great cloud and a burning fire flashing forth, and its brightness was all around, and in the midst of the fire, like the appearance of amber, from the midst of the fire.

5 And from the midst of it, a similitude of four living creatures; and this was their appearance: a similitude of a person to them.

6 And four faces each one, and four wings each one for them.

7 Their legs were straight legs, and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calf's foot, and their sparkles were like the appearance of burnished copper.

8 And they had human hands under their wings on their four sides; and the four of them had their faces and their wings.

9 Their wings are joined woman to her sister; they do not turn when they go; each one goes straight ahead toward the face of his fellow.

10 And the fashion of their faces was the face of a man; and the face of a lion to the right of them four, and the face of an ox to the left of them four, and the face of an eagle for them four.

11 Their faces and their wings were separated above; each had two wings joined to the other, and two wings covering their bodies.

12 And each man shall go straight ahead in the direction the wind would go; they shall not turn aside in their going.

13 And the fashion of the living creatures: their appearance was like burning coals of fire, like the appearance of torches; it moved among the living creatures, and brightness was from the fire, and from the fire came forth lightning.

14 And the living creatures ran back and forth like the appearance of a flash of lightning.

15 And I saw the living creatures, and behold, one wheel was on the ground beside the living creatures, for each of their four faces.

16 The appearance of the wheels and their workmanship was like beryl; and the likeness of each one was like the other; and their appearance and their workmanship was as a wheel in the midst of a wheel.

17 They go on their four sides; in going they do not turn aside.

18 As for their rims, they were high and awesome; and the rims of all four were full of eyes all around.

19 And when the living creatures moved, the wheels moved beside them; and when the living creatures rose from the earth, the wheels rose.

20 Wherever the spirit goes they go, there the spirit goes, and the wheels rise beside them, because the spirit of the living creature is in the wheels.

21 When they go, they go; when they stand, they stand; and when they are lifted up from the earth, the wheels are lifted up beside them, for the spirit of the living creature is in the wheels.

22 And the fashion of the firmament over the heads of the living creature was like the appearance of terrible crystal, extended over their heads from above.

23 Under the firmament, their wings were straight, outstretched one toward the other; for each man two wings covered them, and for each man two wings covered them, their dead bodies.

24 And I heard the sound of their wings like the sound of many waters, like the voice of the Almighty when they walked; the sound of tumult like the sound of a host; and when they stood, they let down their wings.

25 And there was a sound from above the firmament that was over their heads, when they stopped the spreading of their wings.

26 And above the firmament that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne, like the appearance of a sapphire stone; and upon the likeness of the throne was the likeness of the appearance of a man upon it above.

27 And I saw as it were the appearance of amber, like the look of fire round about within it, from the appearance of his loins upward; and from the appearance of his loins downward I saw as it were the appearance of fire, and its brightness round about.

28 As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud on the day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness round about; this was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD. And I saw, and I fell upon my face; and I heard a voice speaking.


## Chapter 2

1 And he said to me, Son of man, stand on your feet, and I will speak with you.

2 And the spirit came into me as he spoke to me, and it set me on my feet, and I heard the one speaking to me.

3 Then he said to me, O mortal, I am sending you to the children of Israel, to rebellious nations who have rebelled against me; they and their fathers have transgressed against me to this very day.

4 And the children are hard of face and stubborn of heart; I am sending you to them, and you shall say to them, Thus says the Lord God.

5 But they, if they hear or if they refuse, are a rebellious house, and they will know that a prophet has been among them.

6 But you, son of man, do not fear them and do not fear their words, though briers and thorns are with you and you dwell among scorpions; do not fear their words and do not be dismayed at their faces, for they are a rebellious house.

7 And you shall speak my words to them, whether they listen or whether they refuse, for they are rebellious.

8 And you, son of man, hear what I am speaking to you: do not be rebellious like the rebellious house; open your mouth and eat what I am giving you.

9 And I saw, and behold, a hand was stretched out to me, and behold, in it was a scroll of a book.

10 And he spread it out before me, and it was written on the front and on the back; and written on it was lamentation, mourning, and woe.


## Chapter 3

1 And he said to me, O mortal, eat what you find: eat this roll, and go, speak to the house of Israel.

2 And I opened my mouth, and he caused me to eat this roll.

3 And he said to me, O man, eat this roll that I give you and fill your belly and your bowels with it. So I ate, and it was in my mouth as honey for sweetness.

4 And he said to me, Son of man, go, come to the house of Israel and speak my words to them.

5 For you are not sent to a people of deep speech and heavy tongue, but to the house of Israel.

6 Not to many nations, people of deep language and heavy tongue, whose words you do not hear, would I send you, yet they would listen to you.

7 And the house of Israel will not be willing to listen to you, because they are not willing to listen to me, for the whole house of Israel are strong-browed and hard-hearted.

8 Behold, I have made your face strong against their faces, and your brow strong against their brows.

9 I have made your brow like the hardest flint, so you will not fear them nor be dismayed by their presence, for they are a rebellious house.

10 And he said to me, Son of man, take into your heart all my words that I speak to you and hear with your ears.

11 Go, enter to the carried away, to the sons of your people, and speak to them and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: Whether they hear or whether they cease.

12 A blast lifted me up, and behind me I heard the sound of a great commotion: “Blessed is the glory of the LORD from his place.”

13 And the sound of the wings of the living creatures touching one another, and the sound of the wheels beside them, and the sound of a great commotion.

14 And a wind lifted me up and took me away, and I went in bitterness of spirit in the heat of my anger; and the hand of the LORD was upon me strongly.

15 I came to the exiles at Tel-abib, by the river Chebar, where they were living, and I sat among them there seven days, overwhelmed.

16 After seven days, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

17 Son of man, I have appointed you a watchman for the house of Israel; when you hear a word from my mouth, you shall warn them from me.

18 When I say to the wicked, You will surely die, and you do not warn him and do not speak to warn the wicked from his wicked way to keep him alive, he, the wicked one, will die in his iniquity, and his blood I will require at your hand.

19 But you, because you warned the wicked and he did not turn from his iniquity or from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity, but you have delivered your soul.

20 When a righteous one turns back from his justice and does iniquity, and I place a stumbling-block before him, he shall die; because you did not warn him in his sin he shall die, and his righteous acts which he did shall not be remembered; and his blood from your hand I will seek.

21 But you, since you warned the righteous that he might not sin, and he did not sin, he shall surely live because he was warned, and you have delivered your own soul.

22 And the hand of the LORD was upon me there, and he said to me, "Rise, go out to the valley, and there I will speak with you."

23 I rose and went out to the plain, and there the glory of the LORD was standing, like the glory that I had seen by the river Chebar, and I fell on my face.

24 And air came into me and made me stand on my legs, and he spoke to me and said to me, Go, be confined within your house.

25 And you, son of man, behold, they have placed ropes upon you and bound you with them, and you shall not go out among them.

26 And I will make your tongue stick to the roof of your mouth so that you will be mute and unable to rebuke them, for they are a rebellious house.

27 When I speak with you, I will open your mouth, and you shall say to them, Thus says the Lord God: The one who hears will hear, and the one who refrains will refrain, for they are a rebellious house.


## Chapter 4

1 And you, son of man, take for yourself a brick and lay it before your face, and engrave upon it the city Jerusalem.

2 And you shall set a siege against it, and build a fort against it, and cast up a mound against it, and set camps against it, and place battering rams against it all around.

3 And you, take for yourself an iron pan and set it as an iron wall between you and the city; set your face toward it, and it shall be in a state of siege, and you shall besiege it—an sign for the house of Israel.

4 And you, lie on your left side and place the fault of the house of Israel upon it; for the number of the days that you lie on it, you shall bear their iniquity.

5 And I have given you their two iniquities, a certain number of days, three hundred and ninety days, and you shall bear the iniquity of the house of Israel.

6 And you shall finish these, and lie on your side a second time, and bear the fault of the house of Judah forty days: a day for a year, a day for a year, I assign to you.

7 And set your face toward the besieged Jerusalem, and bared is your arm, and you shall prophesy against her.

8 And behold, I have put bands on you, and you shall not turn from your side to your side until the days of your siege are completed.

9 And you, take for yourself wheat, barley, beans, lentile, millet, and fitches, rie; put them into one vessel, and make them into food for you, according to the number of days that you lie upon your side: three hundred and ninety days you shall eat it.

10 And your food that you shall eat shall be by weight, twenty shekels a day; from time to time you shall eat it.

11 And water by measure you shall drink, the sixth of the hin, from time to time you shall drink.

12 And you shall eat a barley cake, and it shall be baked with human dung before their eyes.

13 And the LORD said, Thus shall the people of Israel eat their bread unclean among the nations, where I will drive them.

14 And I said, Ah, (my) Lord God, behold, any appetite of mine has not been defiled, and (dead) carcase nor that which was torn have I eaten from my youth even until now, and no flesh of an abomination has entered into my mouth.

15 Then he said to me, See, I have given you cow dung instead of the dung of man, and you shall make your bread upon them.

16 And he said to me, O son of man, behold, I am breaking the staff of bread in Jerusalem, and they shall eat bread by full weight and in care, and drink water by measure and in astonishment.

17 so that they lack bread and water, and are appalled, each man with his brother, and waste away because of their iniquity.


## Chapter 5

1 But you, son of man, take yourself a sharp sword, take a barber’s razor for yourself, and pass it over your head and your beard; then take balances for yourself and divide them.

2 A third with fire shall burn in the midst of the city when the days of the siege are fulfilled, and you shall take the third part and strike with the sword round about it, and the third part you shall scatter to the wind, and I will draw a sword after them.

3 And you shall take from there a very few by number and gather them in your wings.

4 And from them yet you shall take, and you shall cast them into the midst of the fire, and you shall burn them in the fire; from it a fire shall go out into all the house of Israel.

5 Thus says the Lord God, Jerusalem I placed among the nations, and lands round about her.

6 She made my judgments more wicked than the nations, and my statutes more than the lands around her, for she rejected my judgments and did not walk in my statutes.

7 Therefore thus says the LORD God: Because of your abundance among the nations, you have not walked in my statutes, nor have you done my judgments, nor have you done the judgments of the nations which are on every side of you.

8 Therefore, thus says the LORD God, "Behold, I am against you also, and I will execute judgments among you in the sight of the nations."

9 I will do to you what I have not done, and I will never do the like again, because of all your abominations.

10 Therefore the chiefs will eat the sons in your midst and the sons will eat their fathers, and I will execute judgments on you and scatter all your remnant to every wind.

11 Therefore, as I live, says the LORD God, because you have defiled my sanctuary with all your detestable things and with all your abominations, therefore I will also withdraw; my eye shall not spare, and I will have no pity.

12 A third of you will die by pestilence and waste away by famine among you, and a third will fall by the sword all around you, and a third I will scatter to every wind, and I will draw a sword after them.

13 And my anger shall be completed, and I will cause my wrath toward them to rest, and I will be comforted; and they shall know that I the LORD have spoken in my zeal, when I have spent my wrath upon them.

14 And I will give you for a desolate place and for shame among the nations that are about you, before the eyes of everyone who passes by.

15 And you shall become a reproach and a taunt, a chastisement and a desolation among the nations around you, when I execute judgments against you in anger and in fury and in furious rebukes; I the LORD have spoken.

16 When I send out against them the arrows of the raging famine, which were for destruction, with which I will send to destroy you, and the dearth will increase upon you, and I will break for you the staff of bread.

17 I will send against you famine and a deadly beast that will bereave you, and plague and blood shall pass through you, and I will bring the sword against you, I the LORD have spoken.


## Chapter 6

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face toward the mountains of Israel and prophesy against them.

3 And you shall say, O mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God to the mountains and the hills, to the ravines and the valleys: Behold, I will bring a sword upon you, and I will destroy your high places.

4 Your altars shall be desolate and your idols broken; I will cast your lifeless bodies before your idols.

5 I will give the corpses of the children of Israel before their idols, and I will scatter your bones around your altars.

6 In all your dwelling places the cities shall be laid waste and the high places shall be desolate, so that your altars shall be laid waste and ruined, and your idols shall be broken and destroyed, and your images cut down, and your works wiped out.

7 And the slain shall fall among you, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

8 I will leave survivors of the sword among the nations, when you are scattered through the lands.

9 And your fugitives shall remember me among the nations where they are carried captive, because I have broken their adulterous heart that turned away from me, and their eyes that went lusting after their idols; and they shall loathe themselves in my presence for the evils they committed in all their abominations.

10 And they shall know that I am the LORD; I did not speak in vain to bring this calamity upon them.

11 Thus says the Lord God: Clap with your hand and stamp with your foot and say, Alas! because of all the abominable things of the house of Israel, who will fall by sword, famine, and pestilence.

12 The one far off will die by pestilence, the one near will fall by the sword, and the one left and the one guarded will die by famine; and I will spend my anger on them.

13 You shall know that I am the LORD when their slain are among their idols, around their altars, on every high hill, at all the tops of the mountains, under every green tree, and under every thick oak, at every place where they offered a sweet savour to all their idols.

14 I will stretch out my hand against them and make the land desolate and a desolation from the wilderness of Diblah throughout all their dwelling places, and they shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 7

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 And you, son of man, thus says the Lord God to the land of Israel: an end, the end has come upon the corners of the earth.

3 Now the end is upon you, and I will send my anger against you, and judge you according to your ways, and bring upon you all your abominations.

4 I will not look upon you with pity, nor will I show you compassion; your ways I will bring upon you, and your abominations will be in your midst, and you will know that I am the LORD.

5 Thus says the LORD God: One affliction, one affliction—behold, it is coming.

6 The end has come, the end has come; it has awakened against you; behold, it comes.

7 The morning has come to you, O dweller in the land; the time has come, the day of tumult is near, yet there is no sounding again from the mountains.

8 Now I will pour out my wrath near you and spend my anger upon you, and I will judge you according to your ways, and I will place upon you all your abominations.

9 I will not look upon you with pity, nor will I show compassion; according to your ways I will repay you, and your abominations will be in your midst, and you will know that I am the LORD who strikes.

10 Behold, the day, behold, it is coming; the morning has gone forth, the rod has blossomed, pride has budded.

11 The violence has arisen from a tribe of iniquity, not from them, nor from their multitude, nor from any of theirs, and there is no rest among them.

12 The time has come, the day has arrived; let not the buyer rejoice, and let not the seller mourn, for fierce wrath is upon all its multitude.

13 For the one who is sold shall not return to that which was sold, and while they yet live their lives shall not return; for the vision concerning all her multitude shall not return, and each man in his iniquity shall not strengthen his life.

14 Blow the trumpet and let all be prepared, but no one goes to the battle, for my fierce wrath is against all her multitude.

15 The sword without and the pestilence and the famine within the house: he who is in the field by the sword shall die, and he who is in the city the famine and the pestilence shall consume.

16 And their fugitives shall escape and become like doves of the valleys, all of them trembling, each for his iniquity.

17 All hands will hang limp, and all knees will melt into water.

18 They shall gird sackclothes and horror shall cover them, and upon every face shame and upon every head baldness.

19 Their silver they will cast in the streets, and their gold will become an unclean thing; their silver and their gold will not be able to deliver them in the day of the LORD's wrath; their soul will not be satisfied, nor will their belly be filled, for it became a stumbling-block of their iniquity.

20 And the beauty of his ornament he set for majesty, and the images of their abominations, their detestable filth, they made in it; therefore I have given it to them for uncleanness.

21 and I gave it into the hand of strangers for a prey, and to the wicked of the earth for a spoil, and they shall profane it

22 And I will turn my face from them, and they shall profane my secret place; and destroyers shall enter into it and profane it.

23 Make a chain, for the earth is full of bloodguiltiness, and the city is full of violence.

24 I will bring evil from the Gentiles, and they shall inherit their houses; and I will cause the pride of the mighty to cease, and they shall desecrate their sanctuaries.

25 Destruction has come, and they seek peace, but there is none.

26 Mischief upon mischief shall come, and report upon report shall be; they will seek vision from prophecy, and law shall perish from the priest, and counsel from the elders.

27 The king shall mourn, and the ruler shall be clothed with desolation, and the hands of the people of the land shall tremble; according to their way I will do to them, and according to their judgments I will judge them; and they shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 8

1 In the sixth year, in the sixth month, on the fifth day of the month, I was sitting in my house, and the elders of Judah were sitting before me, and the hand of the LORD my God fell upon me there.

2 And I looked, and behold, a fashion like the appearance of fire, from the appearance of his loins and downward, fire; and from his loins upward, like the appearance of brightness, like the colour of amber.

3 He sent a figure of a hand and took me by a lock of my head, and a spirit lifted me up between the earth and the heavens and brought me to Jerusalem in visions of God, to the entrance of the inner gate facing north, where the seat of the image of jealousy that provokes jealousy is located.

4 And behold, there the glory of the God of Israel, like the appearance which I saw in the valley.

5 Then he said to me, Son of man, raise now your eyes toward the north; so I raised my eyes toward the north, and behold, north of the altar gate, the image of this jealousy at the entrance.

6 Then he said to me, "Son of man, do you see the great abominations that the house of Israel is doing here, to keep far from my sanctuary? And yet again, you will see even greater abominations."

7 And he brought me to the entrance of the court, and I looked, and behold, a hole in the wall.

8 And he said to me, O man, dig now through the wall; so I dug through the wall, and behold, there was an opening.

9 And he said to me, "Come and see the abominable things they are doing here."

10 And I went in and saw, and behold, every form of creeping thing and beast, the abomination, and every idol of the house of Israel, engraved upon the wall all around.

11 Seventy men of the elders of the house of Israel, and Jaazaniah son of Shaphan standing in their midst, standing before them, each man with his censer in his hand, and a thick cloud of the sweet incense ascending.

12 And he said to me, "Have you seen, son of man, what the elders of the house of Israel are doing in the darkness, each in the chamber of his image? For they say, 'The LORD does not see us; the LORD has forsaken the land.'"

13 And he said to me, "You will again see still greater abominable things that they are doing."

14 He brought me to the entrance of the gate of the house of the LORD which was toward the north, and there, behold, were women sitting, weeping for Tammuz.

15 Then he said to me, “Have you seen, son of man? You will again see even greater abominations than these.”

16 And he brought me into the inner court of the house of the LORD, and behold, at the entrance of the temple of the LORD, between the porch and the altar, about twenty-five men, their backs to the temple of the LORD and their faces eastward, and they were bowing eastward to the sun.

17 And he said to me, "Have you seen, son of man? Is it too light a thing for the house of Judah to commit the abominations that they have done here, that they have filled the land with violence and have returned to provoke my anger? Behold, they put the branch before their nose."

18 I also will act in my wrath: my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity; they will cry in my ears with a loud voice, but I will not hear them.


## Chapter 9

1 And a loud voice called in my hearing, saying, "Approach, you who have charge over the city, and each man with his weapon of destruction in his hand."

2 And behold, six men coming from the way of the upper gate which looks toward the north, and each man with a weapon of slaughter in his hand; and among them was one man clothed in linen, with the scribe’s inkhorn at his waist; and they came forward and stood beside the brasen altar.

3 And the glory of the God of Israel went up from over the cherub where it had been, to the threshold of the house; and he called to the man clothed in linen, whose inkhorn was at his loins.

4 And Jehovah said, Pass through the midst of the city, through the midst of Jerusalem, and set a mark upon the foreheads of the men who sigh and groan over all the abominations that are committed in the midst of it.

5 And to these he said in my hearing, "Pass through the city after him and strike; do not spare, and do not have pity."

6 Old men, chosen young men, virgins, little children, and women—you shall utterly destroy; do not come near any man upon whom is the mark, but begin at my sanctuary. So they began with the elders who were before the temple.

7 Then he said to them, Defile the temple and fill the courts with corpses; go out, and they went out and struck the city.

8 Then it happened, while they were striking the city, that I alone was left, and I fell on my face and cried out and said, "Ah, Lord God! Are you destroying all the remnant of Israel by pouring out your wrath on Jerusalem?"

9 And he said to me, The iniquity of the house of Israel and Judah is very, very great, and the land is full of bloods, and the city is full of perverseness; because they say, The LORD has forsaken the land, and the LORD does not see.

10 And I also will not spare my eye nor have compassion; their way I have placed upon their heads.

11 And behold, the man clothed in linen, with the inkhorn at his loins, returned an answer, saying, I have done as you commanded me.


## Chapter 10

1 And I looked, and behold, upon the firmament that was above the heads of the cherubim, there appeared over them as it were a sapphire stone, in the appearance of the likeness of a throne.

2 Then he said to the man clothed in linen, Go between the wheels, under the cherub, and fill your fists with burning coals from between the cherubim, and scatter them over the city. And he went in my sight.

3 And the cherubims stood on the right side of the temple when the man came in, and the cloud filled the inner court.

4 The glory of the LORD rose up from above the cherub to the threshold of the temple, and the house was filled with the cloud, and the court was filled with the brightness of the glory of the LORD.

5 And the sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard as far as the outer court, like the voice of God Almighty when he speaks.

6 And it happened, when he commanded the man clothed in linen, saying, Take fire from between the wheels, from between the cherubim, that he went in and stood beside the wheel.

7 The cherub stretched out his hand from among the cherubims to the fire that was among the cherubims, and took and put it into the hands of the linen-clad man; he took and went out.

8 And a figure of the hand of a man was seen under the wings of the cherubims.

9 And I looked, and behold, four wheels by the cherubims: one wheel by one cherub, and one wheel by one cherub; and the appearance of the wheels was like the colour of a beryl stone.

10 Their appearance was alike, each one like a wheel within a wheel.
