11 As they go to their four sides they go; they do not turn aside as they go, for the place where the head turns, there they go; they do not turn aside as they go.

12 And all their bodies, and their backs, and their hands, and their wings, and the wheels—full of eyes round about, their wheels.

13 To the wheels, a wheel called the rolling thing in my hearing.

14 And each had four faces: the first face was the face of a cherub, the second face was the face of a man, the third was the face of a young lion, and the fourth was the face of an eagle.

15 The cherubims were lifted up; that was the living creature which I saw by the river Chebar.

16 And when the cherubims went, the wheels went beside them; and when the cherubims lifted up their wings to rise up from the earth, the wheels did not turn aside from beside them.

17 When they stand, they will stand; and when they rise up, they will exalt them, for the spirit of the living one is in them.

18 And the glory of the LORD went forth from above the threshold of the house and stood upon the cherubim.

19 The cherubims lifted up their wings and rose from the earth before my eyes as they went out, and the wheels were beside them; and they stood at the entrance of the east gate of the house of the LORD, and the glory of the God of Israel was over them above.

20 It is the living creature that I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chebar, and I knew that they are cherubim.

21 Each had four faces, and each had four wings, and human hands were under their wings.

22 And the fashion of their faces was the same faces which I saw by the river Chebar, their appearance and themselves; each man toward the face of his front they went.


## Chapter 11

1 And a spirit lifted me up and brought me to the gate of the house of the LORD, the east gate facing eastward; and behold, at the entrance of the gate twenty-five men, and I saw among them Jaazaniah son of Azur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, rulers of the people.

2 And he said to me, Son of man, these are the men who devise iniquity and counsel wicked advice in this city.

3 Those who say, "It is not near; we will not see disaster. She is the pot, and we are the flesh."

4 Therefore prophesy against them, prophesy, son of man.

5 Then the air of the LORD fell upon me, and he said to me, "Say, 'Thus says the LORD: "So you have said, O house of Israel, and the steps of your spirit I have known."'

6 You have greatly increased your slain in this city, and filled its streets with corpses.

7 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Your slain whom you have set in the midst of her, they are the flesh and she is the caldron, but you I will bring out from the midst of her.

8 The sword you feared, I will bring a sword upon you, says the LORD God.

9 And I will bring you out from among her, and I will give you into the hand of strangers, and I will execute judgments on you.

10 By the sword you shall fall, at the border of Israel I will judge you, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

11 She shall not be a pan for you, and you shall be flesh in her midst; at the border of Israel I will judge you.

12 You shall know that I am the LORD, whose statutes you have not followed and whose judgments you have not done, but you have done the customs of the nations that are round about you.

13 And it came to pass as he prophesied, that Pelatiah son of Benaiah died. Then I fell upon my face and cried with a loud voice, and said, Ah Lord God, will you indeed bring to an end the remnant of Israel?

14 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

15 Son of man, your brothers, your brothers, the men of your kindred, and all the house of Israel, all of them, to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, “Keep far from the LORD; the land has been given to us as a possession.”

16 Therefore say, Thus says the LORD God, Because I have sent them far among the nations, and because I have scattered them through the lands, yet I have become to them a sanctuary in small measure through the lands where they have gone.

17 Therefore say, Thus says the LORD God, I will gather you from the nations and collect you from the lands where you were scattered and I will give you the land of Israel.

18 And they shall come there and remove all its abominable filth and all its abominations from it.

19 I will give them one heart and put a new spirit within you, and I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh.

20 so that they will walk in my statutes and keep my judgments and do them, and they will be my people and I will be their God.

21 And to the care for their detestable filth and their abominations their heart continually goes; their way I have placed at their head, says the LORD God.

22 The cherubims lifted up their wings and the wheels were beside them, and the glory of God of Israel was over them above.

23 The glorious honour of the LORD ascended from the midst of the city and stood upon the mountain which is toward the east of the city.

24 And a wind took me up and brought me to the Chaldeans, to the exiles, in the vision by the Spirit of God, and the vision that I had seen went up from me.

25 And I spoke to the carried away all the words of the LORD which he showed me.


## Chapter 12

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, you dwell in the midst of a rebellious house; they have eyes to see but do not see, ears to hear but do not hear, for they are a rebellious house.

3 And you, son of man, make for yourself vessels of captivity and go into exile by day before their eyes; and go from your place to another place before their eyes; perhaps they will see, for they are a rebellious house.

4 And you shall bring out your stuff by day in their sight, as stuff is brought out for removing, and you shall go out yourself at even in their sight, as those that go forth into captivity go forth.

5 According to their appearance, make for yourself a breach in the wall; and you shall go out through it.

6 Before their eyes, carry on your shoulder into the twilight; cover your face, and you shall not see the land, for I have made you a sign to the house of Israel.

7 So I did as I was commanded: I brought out my things like the things of an exile by day, and in the evening I dug through the wall by hand; in the twilight I carried them out on my shoulder in their sight.

8 The word of the LORD came to me in the morning, saying:

9 Son of man, do not the rebellious house of Israel say to you, "What are you doing?"

10 Say to them, Thus says the Lord God, The prince is the burden in Jerusalem, and all the house of Israel who are among them.

11 Say, I am a sign to you: as I have done, so shall it be done to them; in captivity, in removal, they shall go.

12 And the prince who is among them shall bear [his burden] on the shoulder in the twilight, and go out through the wall; they shall dig through the wall to bring him out; his face shall be covered because he shall not see the land with his eyes.

13 And I will spread my net upon him, and he will be caught in my snare; and I will bring him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans, and there he will not see, and there he will die.

14 And all who are around him to help him, and all his bands, I will scatter to every wind; and I will draw out a sword after them.

15 And they shall know that I am the LORD, because I have scattered them among the nations and dispersed them throughout the lands.

16 And I will spare from them a few people from the sword, from famine, and from pestilence, so that they may recount all their abominations among the nations where they have gone, and they may know that I am the LORD.

17 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

18 Son of man, your bread you shall eat trembling, and your water you shall drink with quaking and with care.

19 And you shall say to the folk of the land, Thus says the Lord God to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, to the land of Israel: Their bread they shall eat in care, and their waters they shall drink in astonishment, so that the land of it may become desolate from all that is full thereof, from the violence of all the inhabitants in it.

20 And the cities that are inhabited will be laid waste, and the land will become desolate, and you will know that I am the LORD.

21 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

22 Son of man, what is this proverb to you, the land of Israel, saying, The days are prolonged and every vision fails?

23 Therefore say to them, Thus says the Lord God, I have put an end to this proverb; it shall no longer be used as a byword in Israel. But say to them, The days are coming, and the fulfillment of every vision.

24 For there shall no longer be any false vision or divination within the house of Israel.

25 For I am the LORD; I will speak the word and it will be done; it shall no longer be delayed, for in your days, O rebellious house, I will speak the word and I will do it, says the Lord GOD.

26 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

27 Son of man, behold, the house of Israel says, "The vision that he sees is for many days, and for distant times he prophesies."

28 Therefore say to them, Thus says the LORD God, No longer shall any of my words be delayed, for I will speak a word and it shall be done, declares the LORD God.


## Chapter 13

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, prophesy against the prophets of Israel who prophesy, and say to the prophets of their own heart, Hear the word of the LORD.

3 Thus says the LORD God: Woe to the prophets who are foolish, who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing.

4 Like foxes in desolate places, your prophets of Israel were.

5 You did not go up through the breaches, nor build a wall for the house of Israel to stand in battle on the day of the LORD.

6 They have seen vanity and the lying divination, saying, "The word of the LORD," but the LORD has not sent them, and they expect to confirm the word.

7 Have ye not seen a false vision and spoken a lying divination, saying, The LORD said, when I had not spoken?

8 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Because you have spoken falsely and prophesied lies, therefore I am against you, declares the LORD God.

9 My hand shall be against the prophets who prophesy falsely and who divine deceitfully, in the assembly of my people they shall not be, in the register of the house of Israel they shall not be written, and into the land of Israel they shall not come; and you shall know that I am the LORD God.

10 Because they misled my people, saying "Peace," and there is no peace, and he builds a wall, but they are foolishly plastering it.

11 Say to those who daub with untempered mortar that it shall fall; when the shower falls, I will send down great hailstones, and a storm wind shall break it.

12 And behold, the wall has fallen; will it not be said to you, "Where is the daubing you daubed?"

13 Therefore thus says the LORD God: I will break forth with tempestuous winds in my wrath, and there will be a flooding rain in my anger, and stones of great hail in furious wrath to consume altogether.

14 And I will tear down the wall that you plastered with unsavoury things and bring it to the ground, and its foundation will be exposed; and it will fall, and you will perish in its midst, and you will know that I am the LORD.

15 I will spend my anger on the wall, and on its untamped mortar I will pour it; and I will say to you, "There is no wall, and there are no untamped things upon it."

16 The prophets of Israel prophesy to Jerusalem and the seers to her of a vision of peace, but there is no peace, says the Lord God.

17 But you, son of man, set your face against the daughters of your people who prophesy from their own heart, and prophesy against them.

18 And you shall say, Thus says the LORD God, Woe to those who sew pillows for all armholes and make kerchiefs for the head of every stature to ensnare lives; will they ensnare the lives of my people, but save their own lives?

19 And they profaned me among my folk by handfuls of barley and by morsels of bread, to slay souls that should not die and to keep alive souls that should not live, by your lies to my people who listen to falsehood.

20 Therefore, thus says my Lord God: Behold, I am against your pillows, by which you hunt lives to make them fly; I will tear them from upon your arms, and I will send away the lives which you hunt, even souls to make them fly.

21 I will tear your kerchiefs and deliver my people from your hands, and they shall no longer be in your hands for a snare, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

22 Because with lies you have made the heart of the righteous sad, whom I have not made sad, and you have strengthened the hands of the wicked, so that he does not turn from his evil way, to preserve him alive.

23 Therefore, you shall no longer see false visions or practice divination, and I will deliver my people from your hand, and you shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 14

1 And certain men from the elders of Israel came to me and sat before me.

2 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

3 Son of man, these men have set their idols in their hearts and put the stumbling block of their iniquity before their faces; shall I be inquired of by them?

4 Therefore, speak to them and say, Thus says the LORD God, Any man, each man of the house of Israel who sets his idol in his heart and puts the stumbling-block of his iniquity before his face, and who then comes to the prophet— I, the LORD, will answer him according to the abundance of his idols.

5 so that I may take the house of Israel in their heart, which has turned from me to their idols, all of them.

6 Therefore say to the house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: Return and turn away from your idols and from all your abominations turn your faces.

7 For any person from the house of Israel or from the alien sojourning in Israel who turns away from following me and sets up his idols in his heart and puts before his face the stumbling-block of his iniquity, and yet goes to the prophet to inquire of me, I the LORD will answer him by myself.

8 And I will set My face against that man, and make him a sign and a proverb, and cut him off from the midst of My people, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

9 And if the prophet is deceived and speaks a word, I the LORD have deceived that prophet; I will stretch out my hand against him and destroy him from among my people Israel.

10 They shall bear their iniquity as the iniquity of him that seeks, like the iniquity of the prophet it shall be.

11 So that the house of Israel may no longer stray from after me, nor defile themselves any more with all their transgressions; and they shall be my people, and I will be their God, says the Lord the LORD.

12 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

13 Son of man, when a land commits grievous trespass against me, I will stretch out my hand against it, break its staff of bread, send famine into it, and cut off from it both man and beast.

14 And if these three men, Noah, Daniel, and Job, were in her midst, by their righteousness they would deliver their own souls, declares the Lord God.

15 If a wild beast were to pass through the land and lay it waste, and it would become desolate so that no one would pass through because of the wild beast.

16 These three men in the midst of her, as I live, declares the Lord God, if sons or daughters they save, they alone shall be delivered, but the land shall be desolation.

17 Or I will bring a sword against that land, and I will say, "Let a sword pass through the land," and I will cut off from it both man and beast.

18 And those three men in the midst of her, as I live, says the Lord God, they shall not deliver sons and daughters; they alone shall be delivered.

19 Or a pestilence I will send into that land, and I will pour out my wrath upon it with blood, to cut off from it man and beast.

20 But Noah and Job in the midst of her, as I live, declares the Lord GOD, neither son nor daughter shall they deliver; they by their righteousness shall deliver their own souls.

21 For thus says the LORD God: Indeed, my four judgments—sword, famine, wild beast, and plague—I am sending against Jerusalem to cut off from it man and beast.

22 And behold, a remnant has escaped, the survivors, sons and daughters; behold, they are coming out to you, and you will see their ways and their deeds, and you will be comforted concerning the evil that I brought on Jerusalem, all that I brought upon her.

23 And they will comfort you when you see their way and their deeds, and you will know that not in vain I have done to her all that I have done, says the LORD God.


## Chapter 15

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, what is the vine wood compared to all the trees, the branch that was among the trees of the forest?

3 Can wood be taken from it to do a work? If they take from it a peg to hang any vessel upon it?

4 Behold, the burning has been given to consume: the fire has consumed its two edges, and its middle is scorched—will it succeed for work?

5 Behold, when it was unblemished, it was not to be used for work; yet if fire has consumed it, and it is burned, then it may still be used for work.

6 Therefore thus says the LORD God: As the vine tree among the trees of the forest which I have given to the fire for food, so I have given the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

7 And I will set my face against them, though they came out from the fire, the fire shall yet consume them, and you shall know that I am the LORD when I set my face against them.

8 And I will give the land to desolation, because they have grievously trespassed, says the LORD God.


## Chapter 16

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, declare to Jerusalem her abominations.

3 Thus says the Lord God to Jerusalem: Your birth and your nativity are of the land of the Canaanite, your father the Amorite and your mother the Hittite.

4 Your birth and your kindred on the day you were born: your navel was not cut, and in water you were not washed to supple, and you were not salted, and you were not swaddled.

5 No eye pitied you to do any of these things for you, to spare you, but you were cast out into the face of the field in loathing of your soul, in the day you were born.

6 And I passed over you and saw you wallowing in your blood, and I said to you, “In your blood, live!” and I said to you, “In your blood, live!”

7 As a million, like a sprouting of the field I gave you; and you increased and grew, and came to excellent ornaments; your breasts were formed and your hair grew, but you were naked and bare.

8 I passed over you and saw you, and behold, your time was a time of loves; so I spread my wing over you and covered your nakedness; and I swore to you and entered into covenant with you, says the Lord God, and you became mine.

9 And I washed you with water, and rinsed your blood from you, and anointed you with oil.

10 And I clothed you with broidered work, and I shod you with badger, and I bound you with fine linen, and I covered you with silk.

11 I adorned you with ornament, and I put bracelets on your hands and a chain on your neck.

12 And I put a jewel on your nose, and earrings on your ears, and a beautiful crown on your head.

13 You were adorned with gold and silver, and your clothing was of silk and broidered work; fine flour, honey, and oil were yours, and you prospered exceedingly to royalty.

14 And your name went out among the nations because of your beauty, for it was perfect through the beauty I had bestowed on you, says the LORD God.

15 You trusted in your beauty and played the whore because of your fame, pouring out your whoredom on everyone who passed by.

16 And you took of your apparel and made for yourself high places adorned with divers colors; on them you committed harlotry; such things shall not come nor shall they continue.

17 You took your beautiful things, the gold and silver I had given you, and made images of men for yourself, and you prostituted yourself with them.

18 And you took your broidered apparel and covered them with it, and set my oil and my incense before them.

19 And my bread which I gave you—fine flour and oil and honey—you ate, and I gave it before them for a sweet savour; and it came to pass, says the Lord God.

20 You took your sons and your daughters whom you bore to me and sacrificed them to be eaten; was your whoredom a small thing?

21 You slaughtered my children and gave them in passing them over to them.

22 And all your abominations and your fornications I did not remember from the days of your youth, when you were naked and bare, writhing in your blood, you were.

23 And it came to pass after all your adversity: Woe, woe to you, says the LORD God.

24 You built for yourself a back and made for yourself a high place in every street.

25 You built a high place at every head of the way and increased your fornication, defiling your beauty, spreading your legs for every passerby, multiplying your whoredom.

26 And you committed fornication with the Egyptians, your neighbors, mighty in flesh, and multiplied your whoredom to provoke me.

27 And behold, I have stretched out my hand against you and diminished your appointed portion, and delivered you to the soul of those who hate you, the daughters of the Philistines, who are ashamed of your lewd way.

28 And you prostituted yourself to the sons of Asshur without being satisfied, and you committed prostitution and still were not satisfied.

29 You increased your fornication to the land of Canaan toward the Chaldeans, and even in this you were not satisfied.

30 How weak is your heart, says the LORD God, in doing all these things, the deeds of a dominating prostitute.

31 When you built your vaulted place at the head of every way and made your high place in every broad place, you were not like a prostitute, because you scorned hire.

32 The adulteress, under her husband, takes strangers.

33 To all prostitutes they give gifts, but you gave your gift to all your lovers, bribing them to come to you from all around for your fornication.

34 There was a contrary from the women in your whoredom, and after you there did not go a whoring; and you gave a hire, and a hire was not given to you, and you were for a contrary.

35 Therefore, O Shimei, hear the word of the LORD:

36 Thus says the Lord God: Because your filthiness was poured out and your nakedness was uncovered through your fornication with all your idols, the abominations of yours, and because of the blood of your children whom you gave to them,

37 Therefore, behold, I will gather against you all your lovers to whom you have made promises, and all those you have loved and all those you have hated; I will gather them against you from all around and expose your nakedness to them, and they shall see all your nakedness.

38 And I will judge you by the judgments of adulteresses and of blood-shedders, and I will give you blood of wrath and jealousy.

39 I will give you into their hand, and they shall destroy your back and break down your high places; they shall strip off your clothes and take away your beautiful ornaments; and they shall leave you naked and bare.

40 And the assembly shall come up against you and stone you with stones and pierce you with their swords.

41 They will burn your homes with fire and execute judgments against you before the eyes of many women; I will put an end to your prostitution, and you will no longer give your hire.

42 And I will set down my anger upon you, and my jealousy shall turn away from you, and I will be still and will not be angry anymore.

43 Because you did not remember the days of your youth, and you provoked me with all these things, I also have placed your way upon your head, says the Lord God; yet you shall not commit this lewdness with all your abominations.

44 Behold, every ruler over you shall rule, saying, "As a mother, her daughter."

45 You are the daughter of your mother, who loathed her husband and her sons, and you are the sister of your sisters, who loathed their husbands and their sons; your mother was a Hittite and your father an Amorite.

46 And your older sister, Samaria, she and her daughters, who dwells on your left, and your younger sister, Sodom, and her daughters, who dwells on your right.

47 You did not walk along their ways, and you almost did not commit their abominations, yet you became more corrupt than they in all your ways.

48 As I live, saith the LORD God, Sodom thy sister with her daughters hath not done as thou hast done, thou and thy daughters.

49 Behold, this was the iniquity of Sodom your sister: pride, fulness of bread, and abundance of idleness was in her and in her daughters; and the hand of the poor and the needy she did not strengthen.

50 And they exalted themselves and committed an abomination before me, so I removed them as I saw fit.

51 Samaria did not commit half of your sins, but you have multiplied your abominations more than they, and you have justified your sisters by all your abominations which you have done.

52 You also carry your shame, which you brought upon yourself by pleading the case of your sister; because of your sins, from which you turned away in disgust, they will be declared righteous compared to you; indeed, you too must feel shame and bear your shame because of your sisters’ righteousness.

53 And I will restore their captivity, the captivity of Sodom and her daughters, and the captivity of Samaria and her daughters, and your captivity in their midst.

54 So that you may bear your shame and be ashamed of all that you have done, in your comfort over them.

55 And your sisters Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former estate, and Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former estate, and you and your daughters shall return to your former estate.

56 And Sodom, your sister, was not mentioned in your mouth in the day of your pride.

57 Before your adversity was exposed, like the time of the shame of the daughters of Aram and all those around her, the daughters of the Philistines who despise you from every side.

58 You have borne your heinous crimes and your abominations, declares the LORD.

59 For thus says the LORD God: I will deal with you as you have done, because you have despised the oath in breaking the covenant.

60 I will remember My covenant with you in the days of your youth, and I will establish for you an everlasting covenant.

61 And you shall remember your ways and be ashamed when you take your older sisters from you and the younger from you; and I will give them to you for daughters, but not because of your covenant.

62 And I will confirm my covenant with you, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

63 So that you may remember and be ashamed, and never again have an opening of the mouth because of your shame, when I have forgiven you for all that you have done, says the LORD God.


## Chapter 17

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, utter a riddle and speak a parable to the house of Israel.

3 Then say: Thus says the Lord God: The great eagle, great of wings, long of pinions, full of feathers, which had broidered plumage, came to Lebanon and took the highest branch of the cedar.

4 He took the top of his young twig and brought it to the land of Canaan, to a merchant city.

5 He took of the seed of the land and placed it in a field of seed; he took it beside abundant waters and set it like a willow tree.

6 And it grew and became a vine spreading low in stature, its branches turned toward him, and its roots were beneath it; and it became a vine and produced branches and sent out boughs.

7 And there was one eagle, great, with great wings and many feathers; and behold, this vine bent its roots toward it and stretched its branches to it, to be watered from the furrows of its planting.

8 To a good field, to abundant waters, it was planted, to bring forth a branch and bear fruit, to become a goodly vine.

9 Say, Thus says the Lord God: Shall it prosper? Shall he not pull up its root and cut off its fruit, so that all its springing buds dry up? And not by a strong arm or by a great people shall it be lifted from its root.

10 And look, a planted shoot—will it prosper? Will not the east wind utterly dry it up, and on the beds of its springing it will wither?

11 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

12 Say now to the rebellious court, Have you not known what these things mean? Say, Behold, the king of Babylon came to Jerusalem, and he took its king and its rulers, and brought them to himself to Babylon.

13 And he took of the royal seed and made a covenant with him, bringing him under an oath, and took the mighty of the land.

14 To become a lowly kingdom, not to exalt itself, to keep its covenant for its survival.

15 He rebelled against him by sending his ambassadors to Egypt to obtain horses and a great people; will he succeed? Will he who does this escape? He has broken the covenant and yet expects to escape.

16 As I live, saith the LORD God, surely in the country of the king who made him king, whose oath he despised and whose covenant he broke, with him in the midst of Babylon he shall die.

17 Nor by great forces and a numerous assembly will Pharaoh do it in battle, in raising a bank and building a fort, to destroy many lives.

18 And he despised the curse, to break the covenant; behold, he has given his hand, and all these things he has done—he shall not escape.

19 Therefore, thus says the LORD God, As I live, surely my curse, because they despised it, and my covenant that they broke, I will put on his head.

20 And I will spread my net over him, and he will be caught in my snare; and I will bring him to Babylon, and there I will judge him for his transgression with which he has trespassed against me.

21 And all his bands of all his wings shall fall by the sword, and the fugitives to every wind shall be scattered, and you shall know that I the LORD have spoken.

22 Thus says the Lord God: I myself will take from the top of the high cedar a tender young twig and plant it myself on a high and exalted mountain.

23 On a high mountain of Israel I will plant it, and it will lift up a branch and bear fruit and become a majestic cedar; and under it all birds of every wing will dwell, in the shade of its branches they will dwell.

24 And all the trees of the field shall know that I the LORD have brought down the high tree, have exalted the low tree, have dried up the green tree, and have made the dry tree flourish; I the LORD have spoken, and I will do it.


## Chapter 18

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 What right do you have, you who rule with this proverb in the land of Israel, saying, "The fathers eat sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge"?

3 As I live, saith the LORD God, surely you shall no longer use this proverb in Israel.

4 Behold, all lives are mine: as the life of the father, so the life of the son is mine: the soul that sins—it shall die.

5 and any man who is righteous and does justice and righteousness

6 He has not eaten upon the mountains, nor lifted his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, and has not defiled his neighbor's wife, nor approached a woman during her menstrual separation.

7 He has not oppressed anyone; he restores to the debtor his pledge, robs no one, gives his bread to the hungry, and covers the naked with a garment.

8 He shall not give usury, nor take increase; from iniquity he shall withdraw his hand; he shall execute true judgment between man and man.

9 He shall walk in my statutes and keep my judgments by doing truth; being righteous, he shall live—thus says the Lord God.

10 And he begot a son, a robber shedding blood, who commits any one of these things.

11 Yet he did none of all these, for he also ate on the mountains and defiled the wife of his companion.

12 The poor and the needy suffer violence; he has violently taken away their spoil; the pledge he does not restore; to idols he lifts up his eyes; he has committed an abomination.

13 He gave money at interest and took unjust gain; he shall not live: he has done all these abominable things—he shall surely die; his blood shall be upon him.

14 And behold, he begot a son, and he saw all the sin of his father which he had done, and he feared, and did not do likewise.

15 On the mountains he did not eat, and his eyes did not lift to the idols of the house of Israel; the wife of his companion he did not defile.

16 and no man oppressed, a pledge he did not take, and robbery he did not rob, his bread to the hungry he gave, and a naked man he covered with a garment.

17 From the poor he withdrew his hand, usury and unjust gain he did not take; he executed my judgments, walked in my statutes; he shall not die for the iniquity of his father—he shall surely live.

18 his father, because he practiced extortion, seized violently the property of another, and did not do good among his people; behold, he died in his iniquity.

19 And you shall say, Why does not the son bear the iniquity of the father, when the son has done judgment and justice, kept all my statutes, and done them? He shall surely live.

20 The soul that sins, it shall die; a son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, nor a father bear the iniquity of the son; the righteousness of the righteous shall be upon him, and the wickedness shall be upon him.

21 And the wicked, if he turns from all his sins and keeps all my statutes and does justice and righteousness, he shall surely live; he shall not die.

22 All his rebellions that he has committed will not be remembered to him; in his justice that he has done he will live.

23 Is it the death of the wicked that the Lord God desires? declares the LORD. Is it not that he turns from his ways and lives?

24 But when a righteous one turns back from his righteousness and commits iniquity, doing according to all the abominations that the wicked one does, shall he live? All his righteousness that he has done shall not be remembered; in his trespass that he trespassed and in his sin that he sinned, in them he shall die.

25 And you shall say, "The way of the Lord is not right"; hear now, O house of Israel, is my way not right? Are not your ways the ones that are not right?

26 When a righteous one turns from his justice and does iniquity and dies in them, for the iniquity that he has done he shall die.

27 When the wicked turns from the fault which he has done and does judgment and justice, he shall live his soul.

28 And he shall consider, and turn away from all his transgressions that he has committed; he shall live, he shall not die.

29 And the house of Israel shall say, The way of the Lord is not right; are my ways not right, O house of Israel? Are not your ways the ones that are not right?

30 Therefore, I will judge you each according to your ways, house of Israel, declares the Lord God: repent and turn away from all your transgressions, so that your sin will not become a stumbling-block to you.

31 Cast away from you all your transgressions by which you have rebelled, and make for yourselves a new heart and a new spirit; and why will you die, O house of Israel?

32 For I have no pleasure in the death of the dead, says the LORD God; return and live.


## Chapter 19

1 But you, raise a lamentation over the princes of Israel.

2 And you shall say, What did your mother do, a lioness among lions, in the midst of young lions she couched, she reared her whelps?

3 And one of her young ones went up, a young lion; he learned to seize prey, a man he devoured.

4 The Gentiles heeded him when he was seized in the pit; they brought him with chains to the land of Egypt.

5 And she saw that her expectation was lost, her hope had perished, so she took one of her whelps and made him a young lion.

6 He walked among lions as a young lion; he learned to seize prey, a person he devoured.

7 And he knew their desolate houses and their cities he destroyed; and the land and all that is in it was laid waste at the sound of his roaring.

8 They set Gentiles around about him from every province, and they spread their net upon him; in their pit he was caught.

9 They gave him into ward with chains and brought him to the king of Babylon; they brought him into holds so that his voice would no longer be heard upon the mountains of Israel.

10 Your mother is like a vine in your blood, planted by abundant waters; she bore fruit and branches.

11 Her rods of strength became sceptres of rulers, and her stature was exalted above the midst of thick boughs; he was seen in his height among the abundance of his branches.

12 In fury she was cast to the ground; the east wind dried up her fruit; her strong tribe was broken and withered, fire consumed it.

13 And now planted in the desert, in a land of drought and thirst.

14 And fire went out from a rod of her branches; her fruit it devoured, and there was in her no rod of strength, no sceptre for ruling; a lamentation it is, and it became a lamentation.


## Chapter 20

1 In the seventh year, in the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month, certain men from the elders of Israel came to inquire of the LORD, and sat before me.

2 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

3 Son of man, speak to the elders of Israel and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: Do you come to seek me? As I live, declares the Lord God, I will not be sought by you.

4 Will you judge them, son of man? Will you judge them? Declare to them the abominations of their fathers.

5 And you shall say to them, Thus says the Lord God, In the day when I chose Israel, and swore by My hand to the seed of the house of Jacob, and made Myself known to them in the land of Egypt, I swore by My hand to them, saying, I am the LORD your God.

6 On that day I raised my hand to them to bring them out of the land of Egypt to a land that I had sworn to them, flowing with milk and honey, its beauty surpassing all lands.

7 I said to them, “Each of you cast away the detestable things that are the abominations of your eyes, and do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.”

8 They rebelled against me and refused to listen to me; each man did not cast away the abominable filth of his eyes, and they did not abandon the idols of Egypt, so I said I would pour out my wrath upon them to spend my anger against them in the midst of the land of Egypt.

9 And I acted for the sake of my name, so as not to profane it before the nations among whom they were, before whose eyes I made myself known to them by bringing them out of the land of Egypt.

10 I brought them out from the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.

11 And I gave them my statutes and made known to them my judgments, which a person shall do and live by them.

12 And I also gave them every sabbath as a sign between me and them, to know that I am the LORD who sanctifies them.

13 The house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness, they did not walk in my statutes, and they rejected my judgments which a person should do and live by them, and my sabbaths they greatly profaned; so I said I would pour out my wrath on them in the wilderness to consume them.

14 And I did it for the sake of my name, so as not to profane it before the nations, before whose sight I brought them out.

15 I also raised my hand to them in the wilderness, not to bring them into the land that I had given, flowing with milk and honey, the beauty of all lands.

16 Because they rejected my judgments, and my statutes they did not walk in them, and my sabbaths they profaned; for after their idols their heart walks.

17 My eye spared them from their destruction, and I did not utterly consume them in the wilderness.

18 And I said to the children in the wilderness, Do not walk in the statutes of your fathers, and do not keep the judgments of them, and do not defile yourselves with their idols.

19 I am the LORD your God; walk in my statutes and keep my judgments and do them.

20 And you shall keep my sabbaths and they shall be a sign between me and you, so that you may know that I am the LORD your God.

21 The children rebelled against me and did not follow my statutes, nor keep my judgments to do them, which if a man does, he shall live by them; they profaned my sabbaths. So I said I would pour out my anger on them and spend my wrath on them in the wilderness.

22 And I turned back My hand and acted for the sake of My name, so that it would not be profaned before the eyes of the nations, before whose eyes I brought them out.

23 I also raised my hand to them in the wilderness to scatter them among the nations and disperse them throughout the lands.

24 Because my judgments they did not perform, and my statutes they rejected, and my sabbaths they profaned, and after the idols of their fathers their eyes were.

25 And I also gave them statutes that were not good and ordinances by which they could not live.

26 And I defiled them by their gifts, in causing to pass every firstling of the womb, that I might make them desolate; in order that they might know that I am the LORD.

27 Therefore, son of man, speak to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God, Yet once more your fathers have committed trespass against me by their treachery.

28 I brought them to the land concerning which I lifted up my hand to give it to them, and they saw every high hill and all thick tree, and there they offered their sacrifices and there they presented the provocation of their offerings and there they made pleasant the savour of their sweet odours and there they poured out their drink offerings.

29 And I said to them, "What is the high place to which you go?" So its name is called Bamah to this day.

30 Therefore say to the house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: After the way of your fathers you have become unclean, and after their detestable things you have played the prostitute.

31 And by offering your gifts, making your children pass through the fire, you have defiled yourselves with all your idols to this day; and shall I be inquired of by you, house of Israel? As I live, declares the Lord God, I will not be inquired of by you.

32 And the offering according to your spirit shall not be, which you say: "We will be like the nations, like the families of the lands, to serve wood and stone."

33 As I live, saith the LORD God, surely with a mighty hand, and with an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out, will I reign over you.

34 And I will bring you out from among the folk, and I will gather you from among the lands where you are scattered, with a strong hand and with an outstretched arm and with poured-out wrath.

35 And I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.

36 As I judged your forefathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so I will judge you, says the LORD God.

37 I will make you pass under the rod and bring you into the bond of the covenant.

38 And I will take the rebels and transgressors against me out of the land of their sojournings, but they shall not enter the land of Israel; and you shall know that I am the LORD.

39 But as for you, house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: each of you, go serve your idols; yet afterward, if you will not listen to me, you shall no longer profane my holy name with your gifts and with your idols.

40 For on my holy hill, the mountain height of Israel, declares the Lord GOD, there will serve me all the house of Israel, all of them, in the land; there I will accept them, and there I will require my gifts and the first of your oblations, all your holy things.

41 A savour of sweet odour I will accept you, when I bring you out from the nations and gather you from the lands where you were scattered, and I will be sanctified in you before the eyes of the nations.

42 You will know that I am the LORD when I bring you to the land of Israel, to the land that I raised my hand to give to your fathers.

43 And you will remember there your ways and all your deeds with which you defiled yourselves, and you will loathe yourselves in your presence because of all your evil things that you have done.

44 And you shall know that I am the LORD, when I act toward you for the sake of my name, not according to your evil ways nor according to your corrupt deeds, O house of Israel, declares the Lord GOD.

45 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

46 Son of man, set your face toward the south and look toward the south, and prophesy against the forest of the south country.

47 And you shall say to the forest of the south, Hear the word of the LORD; thus says the Lord GOD, Behold, I will kindle in you a fire, and it shall devour every green tree in you and every dry tree; the blazing flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from the south to the north shall be scorched in it.

48 And all flesh shall see that I the LORD have kindled it; it shall not be quenched.

49 And I said, Ah, (my) Lord God, they say to me, Is he not speaking bywords?


## Chapter 21

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face toward Jerusalem and speak against the sanctuaries and prophesy against the land of Israel.

3 And you shall say to the land of Israel, Thus says the LORD, Behold, I am against you; I will draw my sword from its sheath and cut off from you both righteous and wicked.

4 Because I have cut off from you both righteous and wicked, therefore my sword will come out of its sheath against all flesh from south to north.

5 And all flesh shall know that I am the LORD; I have drawn my sword from its sheath—it shall not return again.

6 And you, son of man, groan with the breaking of your loins and in bitterness groan before their eyes.

7 And it shall be, when they say to you, “Why do you sigh?” you shall say, “Because of the bruit: it comes, and every heart shall melt, and all hands shall be feeble, and every spirit shall faint, and all knees shall be turned to water; behold, it comes, and it shall be,” says the Lord God.

8 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

9 Son of man, prophesy and say: Thus says the Lord: Say, sword, sword, I have sharpened and also polished.

10 To slaughter a slaughter was decreed, to make for her a bright glittering lightning or a joyful dart, a rod of my son, a rejected tree.

11 He gave it to the branch to grasp in the palm; she was sharpened, a sword, and she was polished, to give her into the hand of the slayer.

12 Cry and wail, O son of man, for it has happened among my people, it is among all the princes of Israel; those who inhabit are delivered to the sword among my people; therefore strike upon the thigh.

13 For it is a trial; and what if even the despising rod shall be no more? says the Lord God.

14 And you, son of man, prophesy and strike hand against hand, and let the sword be tripled, the sword of the slain; it is the sword of the great slain, the sword that enters into them.

15 So that the heart may melt and stumbling blocks be multiplied at all their gates, I have set the point of the sword. Ah! It is made like lightning, polished for slaughter.

16 Gather yourself, go to the right; set yourself, go to the left, wherever your face is appointed.

17 And I also will strike hand upon hand, and I will set down my wrath; I the LORD have spoken.

18 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

19 And you, son of man, set yourself two ways for the sword of the king of Babylon to come; from one land they will go out, and a hand shall point at the head of the way to the city.

20 Set a path for the sword to come against Rabbah of the Ammonites and against Judah in Jerusalem.

21 For the king of Babylon stood at the parting of the way, at the head of the two ways, to practice divination; he shook the arrows, consulted the idols, and looked at the liver.

22 On his right was the divination, Jerusalem, to place a captain for opening the mouth to slaughter, to raise the voice with alarm, to place captains at the gates, to pour out a mound, to build a fort.

23 And it shall be vanity in their sight, oaths of cursing for them, and He—remembering iniquity for punishment.

24 Therefore, thus says my Lord God: Because your transgressions have been brought to mind, exposing your sins, your iniquities in all their deeds, therefore, for being remembered, you shall be seized by the hand.

25 But you, a slain wicked ruler of Israel, whose day has come, the time of whose final iniquity has arrived.

26 Thus says the LORD God: Remove the diadem and take off the crown; this shall not be this—bring low the high, and exalt the low.

27 Overturn, overturn, overturn; I will make it overturn; even this did not happen until the one to whom the judgment belongs came, and I gave it to him.

28 But you, son of man, prophesy and say: Thus says the Lord GOD to the Ammonites and to their reproach; say: Sword, sword, drawn for slaughter, polished to flash.

29 When you see a false vision and a lying divination to set a lying mark on the neck of the slain, the wicked, whose day has come in the time of final punishment.

30 Return to the place where you were born, in the land of your nativity I will judge you.

31 I will pour out my rage upon you with the fire of my wrath, and I will deliver you into the hand of men who are burning, craftsmen of destruction.

32 You shall be a fire to consume you; your blood shall be in the midst of the land; you shall not be remembered, for I the LORD have spoken.


## Chapter 22

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 And you, son of man, will you judge, will you judge the city of blood? Then declare to her all her abominations.

3 And you shall say, Thus says the LORD God, A city shedding blood in her midst, when her time has come, and making idols upon her to defile her.

4 Because of your blood-guiltiness which you have shed, and because of your idols which you have made, you have defiled yourself, and you have drawn near your days, and you have come even to your years; therefore I have given you for a reproach to the nations, and for a mocking to all the lands.

5 Those near and those far from you will mock you, defiled in name and great in tumult.

6 Behold, the chiefs of Israel, each one according to his arm, were in you for the purpose of shedding blood.

7 Father and mother have been dishonored in you; the alien they have oppressed in your midst; the fatherless child and widow they have wronged in you.

8 You have profaned my consecrated things and have desecrated my sabbaths.

9 Slanderous men have been in you to shed blood; in you they have eaten upon the mountains; in your midst they have committed lewdness.

10 You have uncovered the shame of a father; you have defiled yourself with the filthiness of a menstruous woman.

11 And any man who commits an abomination with his neighbor’s wife, and any man who defiles his daughter-in-law with a heinous crime, and any man who lies with his sister, his father’s daughter, among you—

12 You took a bribe to shed blood; you took usury and unjust gain, and you extorted your fellow by oppression, and you forgot me, says the LORD.

13 And behold, I have struck my hand upon your dishonest gain that you made, and upon your blood that was in your midst.

14 Will your heart endure, will your hands be strong in the days when I do this to you? I the LORD have spoken, and I will do it.

15 And I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the lands, and I will consume your filthiness from you.

16 And you shall be profaned in yourself before the nations, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

17 The word of the LORD came to me, saying:

18 Son of man, my people Israel have all become dross of silver, brass, tin, iron, and lead in the midst of a furnace.

19 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Because you have altogether become dross, therefore behold, I am gathering you into the midst of Jerusalem.

20 Like a gathering of silver, brass, iron, lead, and tin into the midst of a furnace to blow fire upon it, so I will gather you in my anger and in my wrath, and I will place and smelt you.

21 And I gathered you and I breathed on you with my burning wrath, and you were melted in its midst.

22 As silver is melted in the midst of a furnace, so you shall be melted in the midst of her, and you shall know that I the LORD have poured out my wrath upon you.

23 The word of the LORD came to me, saying:

24 Son of man, say to her, You are a land not cleansed, not rained upon in the day of indignation.

25 The confederacy of her prophets in her midst is like a roaring lion tearing prey; lives they devour, riches and honour they take; her widows are multiplied in her midst.

26 My priests have wronged my law and profaned my consecrated things, making no distinction between the consecrated and the common, and failing to inform others between the unclean and the clean, and from my every sabbath they have hidden their eyes, and I am profaned among them.

27 Her captains in her midst are like wolves tearing prey, shedding blood to destroy lives for the sake of dishonest gain.

28 And her prophets prophesy unsavoury things, seeing lying, and divining deceitful things for them, saying, Thus says the Lord God; but the LORD has not spoken.

29 The folk of the land committed oppression and robbery, and they wronged the poor and needy, and oppressed the sojourner without justice.

30 I sought from them a man who would build a wall and stand in the breach before me for the land, so that I would not destroy it, but I found none.

31 Therefore I have poured out my indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath; I have brought their way upon their heads, says the LORD God.


## Chapter 23

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, two women, daughters of one mother, were.

3 They played the harlot in Egypt during their youth; there their breasts were squeezed, and there they received the marks of their virginity.

4 And their names were Aholah the older and Aholibah her sister, and they became mine and bore sons and daughters; and their names were Samaria Aholah and Jerusalem Aholibah.

5 Then Aholah committed harlotry under me and doted on her lovers, the nearby Assyrians.

6 Clothed in blue, captains and princes, all of them choice young men, horsemen riding horses.

7 She gave her fornication to them, the chosen of the sons of Assyria, all of them, and with everyone she coveted among all her idols she became defiled.

8 And she did not forsake her fornications from the Egyptians, for in her youth they lay with her, and they bruised the breasts of her virginity, and they poured out their whoredom upon her.

9 Therefore I delivered her into the hands of her lovers, into the hands of the Assyrians upon whom she lusted.

10 They exposed her nakedness, took her sons and daughters, and killed her with the sword; so she became a name among women, and judgments were executed upon her.

11 And Aholibah saw her sister’s inordinate love and her fornications, the whoredoms of her sister.

12 To the sons of Assyria, governors and princes, those clad in all sorts, horsemen riding horses, choice young men, all of them.

13 And I saw that it was defiled, one way for both of them.

14 She added more whoredom and saw men portrayed on the wall, images of the Chaldeans, engraved in vermillion.

15 Girded with a girdle on their loins, with bands of dyed attire on their heads, all of them captains in appearance, resembling the likeness of Babylonians, Chaldeans by birth.

16 And she lusted after them when her eyes saw them, and she sent messengers to them in Chaldea.

17 And the Babylonians came to her to the bed of lovers, and they defiled her with their whoredom, and she was defiled by them, and her soul rejected them.

18 She uncovered her fornication and exposed her nakedness, and my soul became disgusted with her just as my soul had become disgusted with her sister.

19 She increased her fornication, to remember the days of her youth, when she played the harlot in the land of Egypt.

20 And she lusted after her paramours, whose bodies were the flesh of donkeys and whose issue was the issue of horses.

21 You have remembered the heinous crime of your youth, when you acted in Egypt with your breasts, for the sake of the breasts of your youth.

22 Therefore, Aholibah, thus says the LORD God: I am about to stir up your lovers against you, even those whose appetite you have turned away from, and I will bring them against you from every side.

23 The sons of Babylon and all the Chaldeans, Pekod and Shoa and Koa, all the sons of Asshur, they are with the choice young men, governors and princes, all of them, captains and nobles, all horsemen.

24 And they shall come against you with chariots, wagons, and wheels, and with an assembly of peoples; they shall set buckler, shield, and helmet against you all around, and I will set judgment before them, and they shall judge you by their judgments.

25 I will set my jealousy against you, and they shall deal with you in fury: your nose and your ears shall be removed, and your latter end shall fall by the sword; your sons and your daughters shall be taken, and your remnant shall be consumed by fire.

26 And they shall strip off your apparel and take away your beautiful things.

27 I will stop your heinous crimes from you and your whoredom from the land of Egypt, and you will not lift your eyes to them, nor remember Egypt anymore.

28 For thus says the LORD God, I am about to give you into the hand of one whom you hate, into the hand of one whose presence your soul detests.

29 They will treat you with intense hatred, take all your labors, and leave you bare and naked; your nakedness, your whoredom, your heinous crimes, and your fornication will be exposed.

30 Doing these things to you because of your prostitution after the Gentiles, because of your being defiled by their idols.

31 You have walked in the way of your sister, and I will put her cup into your hand.

32 Thus says the LORD God, You shall drink your sister's cup, deep and wide; it shall be a laughingstock and a scorn, much to hold.

33 Be filled with drunkenness and sorrow, the cup of astonishment and desolation, the cup of your sister Samaria.

34 And you shall drink it and break it, and you shall crush its potsherds, and your breasts shall be torn off, for I have spoken, says the Lord God.

35 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back, you also bear your lewdness and your fornication.

36 And the LORD said to me, Son of man, will you judge Aholah and Aholibah? Declare to them their abominations.

37 For they have committed adultery and blood is in their hands, and they have committed adultery with their idols, and even the sons whom they bore to me they passed over to them to devour.

38 This too they have done to me: they have defiled my sanctuary on that day and have profaned my sabbaths.

39 And when they slaughtered their children to idols, they came on that day into my sanctuary to profane it; and behold, thus they did in the midst of my temple.

40 Even if you send for men from afar, who were sent messengers, and behold, they have come—for whom you have washed, for whom you have worn out your eyes and adorned your ornament.

41 And you will sit on a stately bed, and a table set before it, and my incense and my oil you will place upon it.

42 The sound of a carefree multitude was with her; and with men from the common multitude, drunkards were brought from the wilderness; they put bracelets on their hands and beautiful crowns on their heads.

43 Then I said of her who was worn out by adulteries, Now they will commit her fornications with her, and she with them.

44 He came to her as one comes to a whore; so they came to Aholah and to Aholibah, wife of heinous crime.

45 And these men are righteous; they shall judge them by the judgment of adulteresses and the judgment of murderers, for they are adulteresses and blood is in their hands.

46 For thus says the LORD God: Bring up an assembly against them and give them over to trouble and spoil.

47 And the assembly shall stone them with stones, and they shall pierce them through with their swords; their sons and their daughters they shall kill, and their houses they shall burn with fire.

48 I will put an end to heinous crime in the land, and all women will be restrained, and they will not commit lewdness as you have done.

49 And they shall recompense your lewdness upon you, and you shall bear the sins of your idols; then you shall know that I am the LORD God.


## Chapter 24

1 And the word of the LORD came to me in the ninth year, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, saying:

2 Son of man, mark this day, even this very day, on which the king of Babylon laid siege against Jerusalem.

3 And take up a parable concerning the rebellious house; you shall say to them, Thus says the Lord God, Set on the caldron, set it on, and pour water into it.

4 Gather its parts to it, every good piece: thigh and shoulder, the choicest of bones, fill.

5 Take the choice of the flock, and also place the cauldron of bones beneath it; boil its boiling well, and also let its bones be boiled within it.

6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the city of blood, a caldron whose scum is in it, and its scum has not gone out from it; piece by piece bring it out; no lot has fallen upon it.

7 For her blood was in her midst, upon the bare rock she placed it; she did not pour it upon the ground to cover it with dust.

8 To kindle anger to avenge vengeance, I have placed her blood upon the bare rock, so that it may not be covered.

9 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Woe to the city of bloodguilt; I myself will make the pile for fire greater.

10 Burn the wood, kindle the fire, finish the flesh, stir the pot of ointment, and let the bones be scorched.

11 And place it empty on its burning coals, so that it may become hot and its brass may burn, and its filthiness may be melted in its midst, its scum consumed.

12 She has wearied herself with toil, and her abundant scum does not go out from her; her scum remains in the fire.

13 In your filthiness is heinous crime, because I cleansed you but you were not cleansed; from your filthiness you shall not be purified again, until I have set My wrath upon you.

14 I am the LORD; I have spoken, and I will do it; I will not go back, nor will I have pity, nor will I relent; according to your ways and your acts you will be judged, says the Lord Jehovah.

15 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

16 Son of man, behold, I am taking from you the delight of your eyes by plague, and you shall not mourn or weep, nor shall your tears come.

17 Mourn in silence for the dead; do no mourning; put your beauty on your head, and put your shoes on your feet; do not cover your beard; and do not eat the bread of men.

18 And I spoke to the folk in the morning, and my wife died in the evening; and I did in the morning as I was commanded.

19 And the folk said to me, Will you not tell us what these things mean to us, for you are doing them?

20 And I said to them, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

21 Say to the house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: I am profaning my sanctuary, the pride of your strength, the delight of your eyes, and the pitieth of your soul; and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword.

22 You shall do as I have done; you shall not cover the upper lip, and you shall not eat the bread of men.

23 Your tires shall be upon your heads, and your shoes upon your feet: you shall not mourn nor weep; do not mourn, do not weep; but you shall waste away in your iniquities and groan, each man to his brother.

24 And Ezekiel shall be a sign to you: according to all that he has done, so shall you do; when it comes, you shall know that I am the LORD God.

25 And you, son of man, is it not on the day when I take from them their stronghold, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes and the burden of their soul, their children and their daughters?

26 On that day the fugitive will come to you to cause a hearing of ears.

27 On that day your mouth will be opened to the fugitive, and you will speak and no longer be silent, and you will be a sign to them, and they will know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 25

1 The act of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.

3 And you shall say to the sons of Ammon, Hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God: Because you have said, Aha, against my sanctuary, when it was profaned, and against the land of Israel, when it was made desolate, and against the house of Judah, because they went into captivity;

4 Therefore, behold, I will give you for an inheritance to the people of the east; they shall dwell in your fortresses and set their dwellings in you; they shall eat your fruit and they shall drink your milk.

5 I will make Rabbah a dwelling place for camels and the Ammonites a resting place for flocks, and you will know that I am the LORD.

6 Because my Lord God says: Inasmuch as you clapped your hand and stamped with your foot and rejoiced with all your spite against the land of Israel,

7 Therefore, behold, I am stretching out My hand against you and giving you over to the nations, and cutting you off from the peoples, and causing you to perish from the lands; I will destroy you, and you will know that I am the LORD.

8 Thus says the LORD God: Because Moab and Seir said, Behold, the house of Judah is as all the nations.

9 Therefore, behold, I will open the shoulder of Moab from its cities, from its towns, from its edge, the beauty of the land, Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim.

10 to the sons of the east against the sons of Ammon and I gave it as a possession so that the sons of Ammon would not be remembered among the nations

11 And in Moab I will execute judgments, and they shall know that I am the LORD.

12 Thus says the LORD God: Because Edom took vengeance upon the house of Judah and incurred guilt by avenging themselves upon them:

13 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: I will stretch out my hand against Edom and cut off from it man and beast, and I will make it a desolation from Teman; and Dedan shall fall by the sword.

14 I will execute my vengeance on Edom by my people Israel, and they shall deal with Edom according to my anger and my wrath; and they shall know my vengeance, says the Lord God.

15 Thus says the Lord God: Because the Philistines have avenged with vengeance, and have taken revenge despite the soul, to destroy with lasting hatred,

16 Therefore thus says the LORD God: Behold, I will stretch out my hand against the Philistines and cut off the Cherethites and destroy the remnant of the coast of the sea.

17 And I will execute great acts of vengeance upon them in my hot displeasure, and they shall know that I am the LORD when I lay my vengeance upon them.


## Chapter 26

1 In the eleventh year, on the first day of the month, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, because Tyre has said against Jerusalem, "Aha, the gateway of the peoples is broken; she is turned over to me; I shall be filled, now that she is laid waste,"

3 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: I am against you, Tyre, and will bring up many nations against you, like the sea bringing up its waves.

4 And they shall destroy the walls of Tyre and demolish her towers, and I will scrape her dust from her and make her a bare rock.

5 It shall become a spreading of curses in the midst of the sea, for I the LORD have spoken, declares the Lord God, and it shall become spoil for the nations.

6 And her daughters that are in the field shall be slain with the sword, and they shall know that I am the LORD.

7 For thus says the LORD: Behold, I am bringing against Tyre Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, king of kings, from the north, with horses, and chariots, and horsemen, and a multitude, and a great people.

8 Your daughters in the field he will kill by the sword, and he will set against you a fort and pour out against you a bank, and he will raise against you a shield.

9 And engines of war he shall set against your walls, and with his swords he shall cast down your towers.

10 From the abundance of his horses their dust will cover you; from the sound of horseman and wheel and chariot your walls will tremble when he enters your gates like those entering a breached city.

11 With the hoofs of his horses he will trample all your streets; your people he will kill with the sword, and he will bring down to the ground the pillars of your strength.

12 They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise, destroy your walls and crush your precious houses; your stones, your timber, and your dust they will cast into the water.

13 And I will silence the abundance of your songs, and the sound of your harps shall be heard no more.

14 And I will make you a higher place of a (ragged) rock, a spreading of things appointed to utter destruction; you shall no longer be built, for I the LORD have spoken, declares the LORD God.

15 Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Shall not the islands tremble at the sound of your fall, at the cry of the slain, at the slaughter in your midst?

16 Then all the captains of the sea will descend from their thrones, remove their cloaks, strip off their embroidered garments, put on trembling, sit on the ground, tremble every instant, and be appalled over you.

17 They will take up a lamentation over you and say to you, “How you have perished, O dweller of the sea, the city so praised, once mighty in the west, she and her inhabitants, who instilled terror upon all her neighbors!”

18 Now tremble, O isles, in the day of your ruin; and let the isles that are in the sea be dismayed at your departure.

19 For thus says the LORD God, when I make you a ruined city, like the cities that are not inhabited, when I bring up the deep upon you, and the great waters cover you:

20 And I will bring you down with those who go down to the pit, to the people of old; and I will set you in the lower parts of the earth, like ancient desolations, with those who go down to the pit, that you may never be inhabited again; and I will give beauty to the land of the living.

21 In terror you will be given, and there will be no more of you; you will be sought but never found again, says the Lord God.


## Chapter 27

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 And you, son of man, take up a lamentation for Tyre.

3 And you shall say to Tyre, who sits at the entries of the sea, merchant of the peoples to many isles: Thus says the Lord the LORD: O Tyre, you have said, I am perfect in beauty.

4 Your borders are in the midst of the seas; your builders have perfected your beauty.

5 With fir trees from Senir you built for you all the boards; they took cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.

6 Of oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your benches of ivory inlaid in boxwood from the coasts of Kittim.

7 Broidered work from Egypt was your spreading to be for you a banner; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was your covering.

8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; your wise men, O Tyre, were in you, they were your pilots.

9 The elders of Gebal and her wise men were in you, repairing your breaches; all the ships of the sea and their mariners were in you, attending to your market.

10 Persia, Lydia, and Put were in your army, warriors of your warfare; shield and helmet were in you; they gave your beauty.

11 The sons of Arvad were with your army on your walls all around, and the Gammadim were in your towers; they hung their shields on your walls all around; they completed your beauty.

12 Tarshish traded with you because of the abundance of all wealth; silver, iron, tin, and lead were the wares you received.

13 Javan, Tubal, and Mesech were your traders; in the person of man and vessels of brass they supplied your market.

14 From the court of Togarmah they gave horses, horsemen, and mules as your fair.

15 The merchants of Dedan were your traders; many coastlands were your trade partners—ebony and ivory they returned as your tribute.

16 Aram was your merchant by reason of the abundance of your wares; in emeralds, purple, and broidered work, and fine linen, and coral, and agate, they gave your ware.

17 Judah and the land of Israel were your merchants, with wheat from Minnith, Pannag, honeycomb, oil, and balm they supplied your market.

18 Damascus was your merchant because of the abundance of your works, because of the abundance of all riches, with wine of Helbon and white wool.

19 Vedan and Javan gave goods in your wares; wrought iron, cassia, and cane were traded in your market.

20 Dedan was your merchant in apparel of precious cloth for chariots.

21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar—they were your merchants—your trade was in lambs, rams, and goats.

22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah were your merchants; with chief spice, and with every precious stone and gold, they paid your fair.

23 Haran, Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur with Chilmad were your merchants.

24 They are the merchants of your all sorts, with clothes of blue and broidered work, and with chests of rich apparel, bound with cords and packed in your merchandise.

25 Ships of Tarshish carried your merchandise; you were filled and greatly enriched in the midst of the seas.

26 Through many waters they brought you; the east wind has shattered you in the midst of the seas.

27 Your wealth, your fair, your market, your mariners, and your pilots, those who held your calkers and your merchants, and all your warriors who were in you, and all your assembly who were in your midst, will fall into the midst of the sea on the day of your ruin.

28 At the cry of your shipmasters, the suburbs will tremble.

29 And all who handle the oar, the mariners and all the pilots of the sea, will come down from their ships to stand upon the land.

30 And they shall cry aloud against you with their voices, and wail bitterly, and cast dust on their heads; in ashes they shall roll.

31 And they shall make baldness for you, and gird sackcloth, and weep for you with bitter soul, a bitter lamentation.

32 And your sons shall take up a lamentation for you and wail over you: Who is like Tyre, destroyed in the midst of the sea?

33 When your wares went out by sea, you satisfied many peoples; with the abundance of your riches and your merchandise, you enriched the kings of the earth.

34 A time of breaking from the seas in the depths of waters; your merchandise and all your company in your midst have fallen.

35 All the inhabitants of the isles are appalled at you; their kings are horrified, their faces are stormy.

36 Merchants among the folk have hissed at you; you have become a terror, and you are no more forever.


## Chapter 28

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, say to the prince of Tyre, Thus says the Lord God: Because your heart is proud and you have said, A god I am, the seat of gods, I dwell in the midst of the seas, yet you are a man and not God, though you have set your heart like the heart of God,

3 Behold, you are wise—there is no secret that confounds you.

4 By your wisdom and by your understanding you have gained power, and you have made gold and silver in your treasuries.

5 By the abundance of your wisdom through your traffic you increased your riches, and your heart was lifted up by your riches.

6 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Because you have bethought yourself of having a heart like the heart of God,

7 Therefore, behold, I will bring against you strangers, ruthless men of a nation; and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom, and they shall defile your brightness.

8 To the pit they will bring you down; you shall die the death of the slain in the midst of the sea.

9 You say, "I am God" before your killer, but you are a man and not a god in the hand of those who pierce you.

10 The death of the uncircumcised shall die by the hand of strangers, for I have spoken, says the LORD God.

11 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

12 Son of man, take up a lamentation for the king of Tyre, and say to him, Thus says the Lord God: You are the seal of perfection, full of wisdom and perfect in beauty.

13 In Eden, the garden of God, were all precious stones your covering: sardius, topaz, and diamond, beryl, onyx, and jasper, sapphire, emerald, and carbuncle, and gold; the work of your tabrets and pipes was in you, prepared in the day you were created.

14 You were a cherub anointed for covering, and I placed you on the holy mount of God; you walked in the midst of fiery stones.

15 You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created until iniquity was found in you.

16 By the abundance of your merchandise, violence filled your midst and you sinned; so I profaned you from the mount of God and destroyed you, O cherub shielding, from among the stones of fire.

17 Your heart was lifted up because of your beauty, you corrupted your wisdom because of your brightness; I cast you to the ground, I gave you before kings to look upon you.

18 Because of the abundance of your iniquities through the iniquity of your merchandise, you have profaned your sanctuaries; so I have brought fire from among you—it has consumed you—and I have made you ashes upon the land before the eyes of all who see you.

19 All who knew you among the peoples are appalled at you; you have become a terror; you will be no more forever.

20 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

21 Son of man, set your face toward Sidon and prophesy against her.

22 And you shall say, Thus says the Lord God, Behold, I am against you, O Sidon, and I will be glorified in the midst of you, and they shall know that I am the LORD when I execute judgments in her, and I will be sanctified in her.

23 I will send pestilence and blood into her streets, and the slain will fall in her midst by the sword against her from all around, and they will know that I am the LORD.

24 And the house of Israel shall no longer have a brier to prick or a thorn to cause pain from all who are around them, those who treated them with contempt; and they shall know that I am the LORD God.

25 Thus says the Lord God: when I gather the house of Israel from the nations where they have been scattered, I will be sanctified among them in the sight of the nations, and they will dwell on their land that I gave to my servant Jacob.

26 They will live on it securely and build houses and plant vineyards and dwell in safety, when I execute judgment on all who wrong them from every side, and they will know that I am the LORD their God.


## Chapter 29

1 In the tenth year, on the tenth day of the twelfth month, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face against Pharaoh king of Egypt and prophesy against him and against all Egypt.

3 Speak and say, Thus says the LORD God, Behold, I am against you, Pharaoh king of Egypt, the great dragon that lies in the midst of his rivers, who says, My river is my own, and I made it.

4 I will put hooks in your jaws and fasten the fish of your rivers to your scales, and I will bring you up from the midst of your rivers, with all the fish of your rivers clinging to your scales.
