5 I will leave you in the wilderness, you and all the fish of your rivers; you shall fall on the face of the field, not gathered and not collected; to the beasts of the earth and to the birds of the heavens I have given you for food.

6 And all the inhabitants of Egypt will know that I am the LORD, because they have been a staff of reed to the house of Israel.

7 When they seize you, you will flee, and you will break for them every shoulder, and when they lean on you, you will be broken, and you will set up for them every loin.

8 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Behold, I am bringing upon you a sword, and I will cut off from you man and beast.

9 And the land of Egypt shall be a desolation and a waste, and they shall know that I am the LORD, because the river said, "I am, and I have made it."

10 Therefore, behold, I am against you and against your rivers, and I will give the land of Egypt as a desolation, a desolate waste, from Migdol to Syene, even to the border of Cush.

11 No man nor beast may pass through it, nor shall it be inhabited for forty years.

12 I will give the land of Egypt as a desolation in the midst of desolate lands, and her cities in the midst of ruined cities they will be desolate forty years, and I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and disperse them through the lands.

13 For thus says the LORD God, after forty years I will gather Egypt from the nations where they were scattered.

14 I will bring back the captivity of Egypt, and I will return them to the land of Pathros, to their land of birth, and there they shall be a lowly kingdom.

15 From the kingdoms you shall be lowly, and you shall no longer rise up over the nations, and I will diminish you so that you may not rule among the nations.

16 And the house of Israel shall no more have a confidence of sin to cause them to stray after their idols, and they shall know that I am the LORD God.

17 In the twenty-seventh year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

18 Son of man, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon imposed a great service upon his forces toward Tyre: every head balded and every shoulder rubbed bare, yet neither he nor his forces received wages from Tyre for the service which he performed upon her.

19 Therefore, thus says the Lord God: Behold, I give the land of Egypt to Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and he shall take its abundance and seize its spoil and plunder its booty, and it shall be the wages for his army.

20 For the labour which he did for me, I have given him the land of Egypt, says the LORD God.

21 In that day I will cause a horn to flourish for the house of Israel, and to you I will give an opening of the mouth among them, and they shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 30

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, prophesy and say: Thus says the Lord God: Wail, alas for the day!

3 For near is the day and near is the day of the LORD, a day of cloud, a time of nations shall be.

4 And the sword came into Egypt, and there was great pain in Cush when the slain fell in Egypt, and they took its abundance, and its foundations were broken down.

5 Chush, Phut, Lud, all the mixed multitude of Arabia, Chub, and the sons of the covenant land—all of them shall fall by the sword.

6 Thus says the LORD: The supporters of Egypt will fall, and the pride of its strength will be brought low; from Migdol to Syene by the sword they will fall in her, declares the Lord GOD.

7 And they shall be desolate among desolate lands, and their cities shall be in the midst of ruined cities.

8 And they shall know that I am the LORD when I bring a fire upon Egypt and all her helpers are broken.

9 On that day messengers will go out from before me in ships to terrify Ethiopia securely, and there will be great pain among them on the day of Egypt, for indeed it is coming.

10 Thus says the Lord God, I will cause the abundance of Egypt to cease by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.

11 He and his people with him, tyrants of nations, are brought to destroy the land, and they empty their swords upon Egypt, and fill the land with slain.

12 I will make the rivers a waste and sell the land into the hand of evildoers, and I will lay waste the land and its fullness by the hand of strangers; the LORD has spoken.

13 Thus says the (my) Lord God, I will destroy the idol and cause the thing of nought to cease from Noph, and a ruler from the land of Egypt shall be no more, and I will put fear in the land of Egypt.

14 I will make Pathros desolate, and I will set a fire in Zoan, and I will execute judgment in No.

15 I will pour out my wrath upon Sin, the stronghold of Egypt, and I will cut off the multitude of No.

16 I will bring a burning upon Egypt: Sin will be in great anguish, No will be destroyed, and Noph will have adversaries every day.

17 The chosen of Aven and Pi-beseth by the sword shall fall, and they into captivity shall be taken away.

18 On that day, when I break the bars of Egypt in Tahpanhes, her strength’s pride will cease; she will be covered by a cloud, and her daughters will go into captivity.

19 I will execute judgment on Egypt, and they will know that I am the LORD.

20 In the eleventh year, in the first month, on the seventh day of the month, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

21 Son of man, I have broken the arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and behold, it has not been bound to apply healing medicine or to put on a roller to bind it, to strengthen it for grasping the sword.

22 Therefore thus says the Lord God: I am against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and I will break his arms, the strong one and the broken one, and I will cause the sword to fall from his hand.

23 And I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and disperse them through the lands.

24 I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon and place my sword in his hand, and I will break the arms of Pharaoh, and he will groan with the groaning of the slain before him.

25 I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, and the arms of Pharaoh will fall; and they will know that I am the LORD, when I place my sword in the hand of the king of Babylon, and he turns it against the land of Egypt.

26 I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations and disperse them throughout the lands, and they shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 31

1 And it came to pass in the eleventh year, in the third month, on the first day of the month, that the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, say to Pharaoh king of Egypt and to his multitude: To whom are you equal in your greatness?

3 Behold, Assyria is a cedar in Lebanon, beautiful in bough, and a forest from the shade, and high in stature, and among thick boughs was its highest branch.

4 Watering made it grow, the deep lifted it up; its rivers went round about its plantation, and its conduits were sent forth to all the trees of the field.

5 Therefore its height was exalted above all the trees of the field, and its boughs multiplied and its branches were lengthened by abundant waters when it was sent forth.

6 In its boughs all birds of the air nested, and under its branches all living creatures of the field gave birth; and in its shade all many nations dwelt.

7 And in its greatness, in the length of its branches, because its root was by abundant waters.

8 Cedars could not compare with it in the garden of God, firs were not like its boughs, chestnut trees were not like its sprigs; no tree in the garden of God was like it in beauty.

9 Beautifully had he made him, in the abundance of his branches; and all the trees of Eden, that were in the garden of God, envied him.

10 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: Because it towered in its height and set its top among thick boughs, and its heart was proud in its loftiness,

11 I will give him into the hand of a mighty nation; he has done, he shall do to him; according to his wickedness I have cast him out.

12 Foreign tyrants of the nations cut him down; they abandoned him on the mountains and in all his valleys his branches fell, and his boughs were broken in all the streams of the land; and all the peoples of the land came down from his shade and abandoned him.

13 Upon its ruin dwell all birds of the air, and upon its branches are all beasts of the field.

14 So that none of the trees of the waters may exalt themselves in their stature, nor set their top among the thick boughs, nor stand up to them in height, all the trees of the waters; for they are all given to death, to the earth beneath, among the children of men, to those who go down to the pit.

15 Thus says the Lord God, in the day of his going down to the grave I caused the deep to mourn for him, I restrained its rivers, and the many waters were held back; I made Lebanon dark for him, and all the trees of the field fainted for him.

16 At the sound of his fall I made nations tremble, when I brought him down to Sheol, to the pit, and all the trees of Eden, the choice and best of Lebanon, all who drank water, were comforted in the land of the underworld.

17 They also went down with him to the grave, slain by the sword; those who were his arm dwelt in his shadow among the nations.

18 Whom have you been made like in glory and greatness among the trees of Eden? You shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the earth beneath, and among the uncircumcised you shall lie with the slain by the sword—Pharaoh and all his multitude, says the Lord God.


## Chapter 32

1 In the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, on the first day of the month, the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, raise a lamentation over Pharaoh king of Egypt and say to him: You were like a lion among the nations, but you are like a dragon in the seas; you surged in your rivers, you stirred the waters with your feet, and you trampled their rivers.

3 Thus says the Lord God: I will spread my net over you in the assembly of many nations, and they shall lift you up in my net.

4 And I will leave you upon the land, on the face of the field, and I will cause to dwell upon you every bird of the heavens, and I will fill from you every living creature of the earth.

5 And I will give your body upon the mountains, and fill the valleys with your heights.

6 I will make the land of your swimming drink blood from you to the mountains, and the streams will be filled from you.

7 I will cover the heavens with your cloud and darken their stars; I will hide the sun in a cloud, and the moon will not give its light.

8 I will darken all the lights of the light in the heavens over you, and I will set darkness upon your land, says the Lord God.

9 And I will cause the care for many nations to be vexed when I bring your affliction among the peoples upon lands that you have not known.

10 I will make many folk appalled at you, and their kings shall shudder at you with horror when I wave my sword before their faces; they shall tremble every man for his life in the day of your ruin.

11 For thus says the LORD God: the sword of the king of Babylon will come upon you.

12 With the swords of mighty men I brought down your abundance, the tyrants of the nations, all of them; and they despoiled the pride of Egypt, and the multitude of her was destroyed.

13 And I will destroy all cattle from upon the many waters, and the foot of man shall tread them no more, nor shall the hoofs of beasts tread them.

14 Then I will cause their waters to settle and their rivers to flow like oil, says the LORD God.

15 When I make the land of Egypt a desolation, and the land full of its fullness is laid waste, because I strike all who dwell in it, then they will know that I am the LORD.

16 A lamentation, yes, lament it; daughters of the nations, lament over it; over Egypt and over all her multitude, lament over it, says the LORD God.

17 And it came to pass in the twelfth year, on the fifteenth day of the month, that the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

18 Son of man, lament for the multitude of Egypt and bring it down, her and the daughters of the nations, the mighty ones, to the land below, to those who go down to the pit.

19 From whom have you been pleased? Go, lie with the uncircumcised.

20 Among the slain by sword they shall fall; the sword is given: draw it and all its multitude.

21 The mighty ones shall speak to him from the midst of the grave, with his helpers; they have gone down, they lie uncircumcised, slain by the sword.

22 There Assyria and all her company, round about her graves, all of them slain, fallen by the sword.

23 Her graves were given at the edge of the pit, and her assembly was all around her burial; all of them slain by the sword, who caused terror in the land of the living.

24 There Elam and all her multitude are round about her grave, all of them slain, fallen by the sword, who went down uncircumcised to the nether land, who caused their terror in the land of the living, and who bore their shame with those who descend to the pit.

25 Among the slain they have given her a bed, in the midst of all his multitude, with her graves round about him; all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword, whose terror was given in the land of the living, and they bore their shame with those who go down to the pit; among the slain he is given.

26 There Meshech, Tubal, and all her multitude, round about her graves, all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword, who set their terror in the land of the living.

27 And they shall not lie with the mighty, the fallen of the uncircumcised, who went down to the grave with their weapons of war; their swords were placed under their heads, and their iniquities are upon their bones, for they were a terror to the inhabitants of the land of the living.

28 But you shall be broken among the uncircumcised and lie with the slain of the sword.

29 There Edom, its kings and all its princes, who in their might were given to the slain by the sword, lie with the uncircumcised and with those who go down to the pit.

30 There the princes of the north, all of them, and all the Sidonians, went down to the slain, in the terror of their might, ashamed, lying with the uncircumcised, slain by the sword, and they bore their shame, those who went down to the pit.

31 Pharaoh shall see them and will regret all the slain of the sword of Pharaoh and all his host, says the Lord God.

32 For I have caused his terror in the land of the living; Pharaoh and all his multitude shall be laid among the uncircumcised, slain by the sword, says the Lord God.


## Chapter 33

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, speak to the people of your nation and say to them, When a land sins against me and I bring the sword upon it, and the people of the land take one man from among their borders and appoint him as a watchman,

3 And he sees the sword coming upon the land, and blows the trumpet and warns the people.

4 And if the hearer hears the sound of the trumpet but does not take heed, and the sword comes and takes him, his blood will be on his own head.

5 When he hears the sound of the trumpet but does not take warning, his blood shall be upon him; but if he takes warning, he shall deliver his soul.

6 And the watchman, when he sees the sword coming and does not blow the trumpet, so that the people do not take warning, and the sword comes and takes a life, that person is taken in their iniquity; but their blood I will require at the watchman's hand.

7 But you, son of man, I have made you a watchman for the house of Israel; and you shall hear a word from my mouth and warn them from me.

8 When I say to the wicked, "You will surely die," and you do not speak to warn the wicked from his way, he, the wicked, will die in his iniquity, and I will require his blood from your hand.

9 But you, because you have warned the wicked from his way to turn from it, and he did not turn from his way, he shall die in his iniquity, but your soul you have delivered.

10 But you, son of man, say to the house of Israel, Thus you have said, Indeed our transgressions and our sins are upon us, and by them we are wasting away; how then can we live?

11 As I live, says the LORD God, I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked, but rather that the wicked turn from his way and live; turn, turn from your evil ways, and why will you die, house of Israel?

12 And you, son of man, say to your people: The righteousness of the righteous will not save him in the day of his transgression, and the wickedness of the wicked will not cause him to stumble in the day he turns from his wickedness, and the righteous will not be able to live in his righteousness in the day he sins.

13 When I say to the righteous that he shall surely live, and he trusts in his own justice and commits iniquity, none of his righteous acts shall be remembered, and in the iniquity he has committed he shall die.

14 And when I say to the sinner, “You shall surely die,” and he turns from his punishment for sin and does justice and righteousness,

15 If a wicked person restores a pledge, repays what he had robbed, walks in the statutes of life so as not to do iniquity, he shall live and not die.

16 All his sins shall not be remembered to him; he has done justice and righteousness—he shall live.

17 And the sons of your people will say, "The way of my Lord is not made right," but it is their own way that is not made right.

18 When a righteous one turns back from his justice and does iniquity and dies in them.

19 When a wicked person turns from their wickedness and does what is just and right, they will live by these.

20 And you shall say, "The way of my Lord is not right." I will judge you, each according to his ways, house of Israel.

21 In the twelfth year, in the tenth month on the fifth day of the month, during our captivity, a fugitive came to me from Jerusalem, saying, The city has been struck.

22 And the hand of the LORD was upon me in the evening before the fugitive came, and he opened my mouth until the morning when he came to me, and my mouth was opened and I was no longer mute.

23 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

24 Son of man, those who dwell in the desolate places of the land of Israel say, "Abraham was one and he inherited the land, but we are many; the land has been given to us as a possession."

25 Therefore say to them, Thus says the Lord God: You eat with the blood, and lift up your eyes to your idols, and shed blood; and shall you possess the land?

26 You stand upon your swords, you commit abomination, and each man defiles his neighbor's wife; and shall you possess the land?

27 Thus you shall say to them, Thus says the Lord God, As I live, surely those in the desolate places shall fall by the sword, and him who is in the open field I have given to the beast to devour, and those in the fortresses and in the caves shall die by the plague.

28 And I will make the land desolate and a desolation, and the pride of her power shall cease, and the mountains of Israel shall be desolate, without anyone passing through.

29 And they shall know that I am the LORD, when I make the land a desolation and a waste because of all their abominations that they have committed.

30 But you, son of man, your people are talking about you by the walls and at the doors of the houses, and each man says to his brother, “Come, let us hear what the word is that comes forth from the LORD.”

31 And they come to you as folk come, and they sit before you, my people, and they hear your words, but them they do not perform; for with much love they speak with their mouth, but after their gain their heart goes.

32 And you are to them like a song of great love, a lovely voice and a skillful player, and they hear your words but do not do them.

33 And when it comes, behold, it has come, and they shall know that a prophet has been among them.


## Chapter 34

1 The act of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel; prophesy and say to them, to the shepherds: Thus says the Lord God: Woe to the shepherds of Israel who have been feeding themselves! Should not the shepherds feed the sheep?

3 You shall eat the fat, and you shall wear the wool; the well-fed animal you shall slaughter; the sheep you shall not pasture.

4 You did not strengthen the weak, nor heal the sick, nor bind up the broken, nor bring back the driven away, nor seek the lost; but with force and cruelty you ruled over them.

5 They were scattered for lack of a shepherd and became food for every wild beast of the field and were scattered.

6 My sheep shall stray on every mountain and on every high hill and on all the face of the land, and my sheep shall be scattered, with none seeking and none searching.

7 Therefore, shepherds, hear the word of the LORD:

8 As I live, saith the LORD God, surely because My flock became prey and My flock was consumed by every wild beast of the field, for the shepherds did not seek My flock, and the shepherds fed themselves but My flock they did not feed.

9 Therefore, O shepherds, hear the word of the LORD:

10 Thus says the Lord God: Behold, I am against the shepherds, and I will require my flock at their hand, and I will cause them to cease from tending the flock, and the shepherds shall no longer feed them, and I will deliver my flock from their mouth, and they shall no longer be food for them.

11 For thus says the LORD God, I am going to search for my flock and seek them out.

12 As a shepherd seeks out his flock when he is among his scattered sheep, so I will seek out my sheep and rescue them from all the places where they have been scattered, on a day of cloud and thick darkness.

13 And I will bring them out from among the folk, and gather them from the lands, and bring them to their own land, and feed them upon the mountains of Israel, by the rivers, and in all the dwelling places of the land.

14 In a good pasture I will feed them, and on the high mountains of Israel will be their habitation; there they will lie down in a comely habitation, and in rich pasture they will feed on the mountains of Israel.

15 I will feed my flock and I will cause them to lie down, says the LORD God.

16 I will seek the lost and restore the scattered, and bind up the injured and strengthen the sick, and the fat and the strong I will destroy; I will pasture them with judgment.

17 And I will judge between sheep and sheep, the rams and the he-goats, says the LORD God.

18 Are the few of you too light a thing, that you trample with your feet the good pasture, and the rest of your pastures you tread down with your feet, and the water of the deep you drink, and with your feet you foul what remains?

19 And my sheep, you shall feed in the place where you have trodden with your feet, and you shall drink from the place where you have fouled with your feet.

20 Therefore, thus says the LORD God to them: Behold, I myself will judge between the fat sheep and the lean sheep.

21 Because with side and shoulder you pushed and with your horns you gored all the weak ones until you had driven them out abroad.

22 And I will save my flock, and they shall no longer be prey; and I will judge between sheep and sheep.

23 And I will raise up over them one shepherd, and he will shepherd them, my servant David; he will shepherd them, and he will be their shepherd.

24 And I, the LORD, will be their God, and my servant David a prince among them; I, the LORD, have spoken.

25 And I will make with them a covenant of peace, and I will cause the wild beast to cease from the land, and they shall dwell in the wilderness safely, and they shall lie down in the forests.

26 And I will give them and the places round about my hill for a blessing, and I will cause the rain to descend in its due season; it shall be showers of blessing.

27 The tree of the field will yield its fruit, and the land will give its increase, and they will dwell on their land securely, and they will know that I am the LORD when I break the bars of their yoke and deliver them from the hand of those who enslave them.

28 And they shall no more be prey to the nations, and the beasts of the land shall not devour them; and they shall dwell securely and none shall make them afraid.

29 And I will raise up for them a plantation for renown, and they shall no longer be gathered in famine from the land, nor shall they bear the reproach of the nations anymore.

30 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God with them, and they are my people, the house of Israel, says the Lord GOD.

31 And you my flock, the flock of my pasture, are men; I am your God, says the Lord the LORD.


## Chapter 35

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face against Mount Seir and prophesy against it.

3 And you shall say to him, Thus says the LORD God, Behold, I am against you, Mount Seir, and I will stretch out my hand against you, and I will make you desolate and an astonishment.

4 Your cities I will make a waste, and you shall become a desolation, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

5 Because of your ancient hatred, and because you delivered the sons of Israel to the power of the sword in their time of calamity, in the time of their final punishment.

6 Therefore, as I live, saith the LORD God, blood-guiltiness I will make of thee, and blood-guiltiness shall pursue thee; since blood-guiltiness thou hast hated, blood-guiltiness shall pursue thee.

7 I will make Mount Seir utterly desolate waste and most desolate waste, and I will cut off from it passer-through and returner.

8 And I have filled his hills with his slain; on your hills, and in your valleys, and in all your streams, the slain of the sword shall fall.

9 I will make you a perpetual desolation, and your cities shall not be inhabited; and you shall know that I am the LORD.

10 Because you said, "The two nations and the two lands shall be mine, and I will possess them," but the LORD was there.

11 Therefore, as I live, saith the LORD God, I will do according to thy wrath and according to thy jealousy, which thou hast shown in thy hatred against them; and I will make myself known among them, when I shall execute judgment upon thee.

12 And you shall know that I am the LORD, I have heard all your blasphemies which you spoke against the mountains of Israel, saying, "We have been given them to consume."

13 You have magnified against me with your mouth and brought charges against me with your words; I have heard.

14 Thus says the LORD God: As the whole earth rejoices, I will make desolation for you.

15 As the joy you felt over the inheritance of Israel when it became desolate, so will I do to you: you will become desolate, Mount Seir, and all Edom altogether; and they will know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 36

1 But you, son of man, prophesy to the mountains of Israel and say, O mountains of Israel, hear the word of the LORD.

2 Thus says the Lord God: Because the enemy has said concerning you, "Aha, the heights are ours forever as a heritage,"

3 Therefore, prophesy and say, Thus says the Lord God, Because of being desolate and being devoured from (round) about, to become a heritage for the remnant of the nations, and you were taken up on the edge of the tongue, a defaming of the people.

4 Therefore, O hills of Israel, hear the word of the Lord GOD: thus says the Lord GOD to the mountains and the hills, to the ravines and the valleys, to the desolate wastes and the cities that have been left desolate, which have become prey and derision to the remnant of the nations round about:

5 Therefore, thus says the Lord God: Because my jealousy has spoken in a fiery blaze against the remnant of the nations and against all Edom, who gave my land to themselves as a possession with wholehearted joy and despiteful soul, to plunder it as spoil.

6 Therefore prophesy concerning the land of Israel and say to the hills and to the little hills, to the valleys and to the brooks: Thus says the Lord the LORD: Behold, I have spoken in my jealousy and in my wrath, because you have borne the reproach of the nations.

7 Therefore, thus says the LORD God: I have raised my hand—surely the nations around you, they shall bear their shame.

8 But you, O mountains of Israel, shall yield your branches and bear your fruit for my people Israel, for they are about to come.

9 For behold, I am for you, and I will turn to you, and you shall be tilled and sown.

10 And I will multiply upon you man, all the house of Israel, all of it, and the cities shall be inhabited and the waste places shall be rebuilt.

11 I will greatly increase upon you people and cattle; they shall multiply and be fruitful, and I will restore your former estate and make it prosper more than at your beginning, and you shall know that I am the LORD.

12 And I will bring against you a man, even My people Israel, and they shall possess you, and you shall become their inheritance, and you shall no longer bereave them.

13 Thus says the Lord God: Because you say to them, "You have eaten men, and have made your nation childless,"

14 Therefore you shall devour people no more, nor make your nation stumble anymore, says the LORD God.

15 I will no longer let you hear the shame of the nations, nor bear the reproach of the peoples; you shall stumble no more, declares the Lord GOD.

16 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

17 Son of man, the house of Israel has dwelt upon the land and defiled it with their way and with their acts; their way before me has become like the uncleanness of a menstruous woman.

18 I poured out my anger on them for the blood they shed on the land, and with their idols they defiled it.

19 I scattered them among the Gentiles and they were dispersed throughout the lands; according to their manner and their deeds I judged them.

20 He came to the nations where they had gone, and they profaned my consecrated name, when it was said of them, "These are the people of the LORD, yet they came out from his land."

21 And I relented for the sake of my consecrated name, which the house of Israel profaned among the nations where they went.

22 Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: Not for your sake do I act, house of Israel, but for the sake of my holy name, which you profaned among the nations where you came.

23 And I will make holy my great name which was profaned among the nations, which you have profaned in their midst; and the nations shall know that I am the LORD, says the Lord GOD, when I am sanctified in you before their eyes.

24 And I will take you from the Gentiles and gather you from all the lands, and bring you into your own land.

25 And I will sprinkle clean water upon you, and you shall be cleansed from all your filthiness and from all your idols I will cleanse you.

26 I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit within you; I will remove the heart of stone from your flesh and give you a heart of flesh.

27 And my spirit I will put within you, and I will cause you to walk in my statutes and my judgments you will keep and do.

28 And you shall dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers, and you shall be to me a people, and I myself will be to you God.

29 And I will save you from all your filthiness, and I will call to the grain and multiply it, and I will not set famine upon you.

30 I will greatly increase the fruit of the tree and the increase of the field, so that you will no longer receive the reproach of famine among the nations.

31 You shall remember your evil ways and your deeds that were not good, and you shall loathe yourselves in your own sight because of your iniquities and your abominations.

32 Not for your sake do I act, saith the LORD God; let it be known to you: be ashamed and confounded because of your ways, house of Israel.

33 Thus says the Lord God: in the age of your cleansing from all your sins, I will cause the cities to be inhabited and the desolate places rebuilt.

34 And the desolate land shall be cultivated, instead of its being a waste, before the eyes of all who pass by.

35 And they will say, This land that was desolate has become like the garden of Eden, and the ruined, desolate, and demolished cities are inhabited.

36 And the nations that remain round about you shall know that I the LORD have built the ruined places, I have planted the desolate places; I the LORD have spoken, and I will do it.

37 Thus says the Lord God, Yet again I will be sought by the house of Israel to act for them; I will multiply them like a flock, man.

38 Like consecrated flocks, like flocks in Jerusalem at her appointed seasons, so shall the cities laid waste be filled with flocks of people, and they shall know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 37

1 The hand of the LORD was upon me, and he brought me out by the spirit of the LORD, and set me down in the middle of the valley, and it was full of bones.

2 And he caused me to pass over them round about, round about, and behold, very many upon the face of the plain, and behold, very dry.

3 Then he said to me, O mortal, will these bones live? And I answered, O Lord God, you know.

4 Then he said to me, Prophesy to these bones, and say to them, O dry bones, hear the word of the LORD:

5 Thus says (my) Lord God to the bones: Behold, I am bringing upon you spirit and you shall live.

6 And I will give you sinew and bring up flesh upon you and cover you with skin and put spirit within you, and you shall live; and you will know that I am the LORD.

7 And I prophesied as I was commanded, and as I prophesied, there was a sound, and behold, a quaking; and the bones came near, bone to its bone.

8 And I looked, and behold, sinews were on them, and flesh came up upon them, and skin covered them from above, but there was no breath in them.

9 And he said to me, Prophesy to the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus says the Lord God, Come from the four winds, O air, and breathe on these slain, that they may live.

10 And I prophesied as I was commanded, and the breath entered them, and they lived and stood on their feet, a vast army, exceedingly great.

11 And he said to me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel; behold, they say, Our bones are dried up, and our hope is lost; we are cut off.

12 Therefore prophesy and say to them, Thus says the LORD God, Behold, I will open your graves and raise you from your graves, my people, and bring you into the land of Israel.

13 And you shall know that I am the LORD when I open your graves and bring you up out of your graves, my people.

14 And I will put my spirit in you, and you shall live, and I will place you in your land, and you shall know that I the LORD have spoken and done it, says the LORD.

15 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

16 And you, son of man, take one stick and write on it “For Judah and the people of Israel”; and take another stick and write on it “For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim and all the house of Israel.”

17 And bring them near to you, one to the other, into one tree, and they shall become one in your hand.

18 And when your people's children say to you, "Will you not declare to us what these things mean to you?"

19 Speak to them: Thus says the LORD God: I am about to take the stick of Joseph, which is in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel, and I will join them to it, even the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in my hand.

20 And the sticks on which you write shall be in your hand before their eyes.

21 Thus says the LORD God, Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the nations where they have gone, and gather them from all around, and bring them into their land.

22 And I will make them one nation in the land, in the mountains of Israel, and one king will be king over all of them, and they will no longer be two nations, nor will they be divided into two kingdoms anymore.

23 They shall no longer defile themselves with their idols, nor with their abominable filth, nor with any of their rebellions; and I will save them from all their dwelling places in which they sinned, and I will purify them, and they shall be my people, and I will be their God.

24 And my servant David shall be king over them, and one shepherd shall be for all of them, and they shall walk in my judgments and keep my statutes and do them.

25 They shall dwell on the land that I gave to my servant Jacob, where your fathers dwelt, and they shall dwell on it, they and their children and their children's children, forever, and David my servant shall be prince over them forever.

26 I will make with them a covenant of peace, an everlasting covenant it shall be with them; I will establish them and multiply them, and I will set my sanctuary in their midst forever.

27 And my dwelling shall be over them, and I will be their God, and they shall be to me a people.

28 And the Gentiles shall know that I am the LORD who sanctifies Israel, my sanctuary being in their midst forever.


## Chapter 38

1 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

2 Son of man, set your face against Gog, land of Magog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal, and prophesy against him.

3 And you shall say, Thus says the LORD God, Behold, I am against you, Gog, chief prince of Meshech and Tubal.

4 And I will bring you back, and I will put hooks in your jaws, and I will bring you out, even you and all your army, horses and horsemen, all of them clothed in full array, a great multitude, all of them bearing shields and bucklers, all of them wielding swords.

5 Persia, Cush, and Put were with them; all of them were armed with shield and helmet.

6 Gomer and all his bands, the house of Togarmah, the remote parts of the north and all his bands, many peoples with you.

7 Prepare yourself and set in order for yourself all your assembly that is gathered about you, and you shall be to them a watch.

8 After many days you shall be visited; in the latter years you shall come into the land brought back from the sword, gathered from many peoples, upon the mountains of Israel, which had been a continual waste; it was brought out from the peoples, and they shall dwell securely, all of them.

9 You shall come up as a desolation, you shall enter as a cloud to cover the land; you, and all your bands, and many peoples with you.

10 Thus says the Lord God: In that day, thoughts will come into your mind, and you will devise an evil thought.

11 And you shall say, I will go up against the land of unwalled towns, I will come upon those who dwell securely, all of them dwelling without a wall, and without bar or gate.

12 To take prey and spoil, to seize the spoil; to stretch out your hand against desolate places now inhabited, and against a people gathered from the nations, possessing cattle and goods, dwelling in the midst of the land.

13 Sheba, Dedan, and the traders of Tarshish, with all her villages, will say to you: Have you come to take spoil? Is your gathering of your company for plunder, to carry off silver and gold, to seize cattle and riches, to carry off great spoil?

14 Therefore, prophesy, son of man, and say to Gog, Thus says the Lord God: Will you not know in that day, when my people Israel dwell securely?

15 And you shall come from your country, from the northern quarters, you and many peoples with you, all of them riders on horses, a great company and a vast host.

16 You shall advance against my people Israel like a cloud covering the land in the latter days, and I will bring you upon my land, so that the nations may know me through your sanctification before their eyes, O Gog.

17 Thus says the Lord God, You are the one who spoke in former days by the hand of my servants the prophets of Israel, who prophesied for years to bring you against them.

18 And it shall be in that day, in the day of Gog’s coming against the land of Israel, declares the Lord the LORD, My wrath shall come up in My anger.

19 And in my jealousy, with the fire of my wrath, I have spoken: surely in that day there will be a great earthquake upon the land of Israel.

20 And the fish of the sea and the birds of the heavens and the beasts of the field and all the creeping things that creep upon the ground and all mankind that are upon the face of the earth shall tremble before me, and the mountains shall be overthrown and the stairways shall fall and every wall of the land shall fall.

21 And I will call for a sword against him upon all the hills, declares the Lord God: a sword, each man against his brother it shall be.

22 I will judge him with pestilence and blood, and I will pour upon him and upon his bands and upon the many peoples who are with him a flooding rain and great hailstones, fire and brimstone.

23 And I will be exalted and I will be sanctified, and I will be known in the sight of many nations, and they will know that I am the LORD.


## Chapter 39

1 But you, son of man, prophesy against Gog, and say, Thus says the Lord God, Behold, I am against you, Gog, prince of Rosh, Meshech, and Tubal.

2 And I will bring you back and carry you and raise you up from the northern quarter, and I will bring you to the mountains of Israel.

3 I will strike your bow from your left hand, and your arrows from your right hand I will cast down.

4 You shall fall upon the mountains of Israel, you and all your bands and the peoples who are with you; I will give you to the birds of prey, every winged creature, and to the beasts of the field to be devoured.

5 You shall fall upon the face of the field, for I have spoken, says the LORD God.

6 And I will send a fire against Magog and against those who dwell securely in the isles, and they will know that I am the LORD.

7 And I will make known my holy name among my people Israel, and I will no longer profane my holy name; and the nations shall know that I am the LORD, holy in Israel.

8 Behold, it comes and shall be, saith the LORD God; this is the day whereof I have spoken.

9 And the inhabitants of the cities of Israel will go out and burn with weapon, and with buckler and shield, and with bow and arrows, and with hand-staff and spear, and they will burn them with fire for seven years.

10 They shall not take wood from the field nor cut timber from the forests, for with weapons they shall burn fire, and they shall take their plunder and plunder their plunderers, says the Lord God.

11 And it shall come to pass on that day that I will give to Gog a place there for a grave in Israel, the valley of those who pass by east of the sea; and it shall stop those who pass by, and there they shall bury Gog and all his multitude, and they shall call it the valley of Hamogog.

12 And the house of Israel shall bury them in order to cleanse the land, seven months.

13 And all the people of the land shall bury him, and it shall be to them a renowned day, the day of my glory, declares the Lord God.

14 And men of continual service shall distinguish those passing through the land, burying the passers, the remaining ones, upon the face of the land, for purification; from the end of seven months they shall search.

15 And travelers will pass through the land, and see a human bone, and build beside it a marker, until the buriers bury him in the valley of Hamogog.

16 And also the name of the city shall be Hamonah, and they shall cleanse the land.

17 And you, son of man, thus says the Lord God: Say to every bird of wing and to all the beasts of the field: Gather yourselves together and come; assemble from all around to my sacrifice, which I am sacrificing for you, a great sacrifice upon the mountains of Israel; and you shall eat flesh and drink blood.

18 You shall eat the flesh of mighty men and drink the blood of the earth's princes; rams, lambs, and he-goats, bulls, fatlings of Bashan, all of them.

19 You shall eat fat till you are full, and drink blood till drunkenness, from my sacrifice which I have slaughtered for you.

20 And you will be satisfied on my table: horse and chariot, warrior and every man of war, declares the Lord God.

21 And I will set my glory among the nations, and all the nations shall see the judgment that I have executed and the hand that I have set upon them.

22 And the house of Israel shall know that I am the LORD their God from that day forward.

23 And the nations shall know that the house of Israel went into exile for their iniquity, because they trespassed against me; therefore I hid my face from them and delivered them into the hand of their enemies, and they all fell by the sword.

24 According to their filthiness and their transgressions I dealt with them, and I hid my face from them.

25 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold, I will restore the captive Jacob and I will have mercy on all the house of Israel, and I will be zealous for my holy name.

26 And they shall bear their dishonour and all their transgressions with which they trespassed against me, when they dwell securely on their land, without fear.

27 When I bring them back from the folk and gather them from the lands of their enemies, I will be honored in them before the eyes of the many nations.

28 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, when I have exiled them among the nations and gathered them unto their land, and I will leave none of them there any longer.

29 And I will no longer hide my face from them, for I have poured out my spirit upon the house of Israel, says the Lord God.


## Chapter 40

1 In the twenty-fifth year of our captivity, at the beginning of the year, on the tenth day of the month, fourteen years after the city was struck, on this very day, the hand of the LORD was upon me, and he brought me there.

2 In visions of God he brought me to the land of Israel and set me down upon a very high mountain, and upon it was a structure like a city to the south.

3 And he brought me there, and behold, a man whose appearance was like the appearance of brass, with a linen thread in his hand and a measuring reed, and he was standing in the gate.

4 The man spoke to me, saying, Son of man, see with your eyes and hear with your ears, and set your heart to all that I show you, for you have been brought here to be shown; declare to the house of Israel all that you see.

5 And behold, a wall was outside the house all around; and in the man's hand was a measuring reed, six cubits by the cubit and a hand-breadth; and he measured the breadth of the building, one reed; and the height, one reed.

6 He came to the gate whose entrance faced east, went up and measured the threshold of the gate, one reed wide, and the width of one threshold, one reed wide.

7 And the little chamber: one reed in length, and one reed in breadth; and between the little chambers five cubits; and the threshold of the gate next to the porch of the gate from the temple: one reed.

8 And he measured the porch of the gate from the house, one reed.

9 And he measured the porch of the gate, eight cubits; and its posts, two cubits; and the porch of the gate was toward the temple.

10 The little chambers of the gate toward the east were three on this side and three on that side, one measure for the three of them, and one measure for the posts on this side and on that.

11 And he measured the breadth of the entrance of the gate, ten cubits; the length of the gate, thirteen cubits.

12 And the border before the chambers was one cubit, and a cubit was the border on that side; and the chamber was six cubits on this side and six cubits on that side.

13 He measured the gate from the roof of the chamber to its roof, a breadth of twenty-five cubits, entrance opposite entrance.

14 He made the posts sixty cubits, even unto the post of the court, round about the gate.

15 And before the gate, in front of the inner porch of the gate, fifty cubits.

16 And its windows were closed toward the inner chambers and toward their lintels, within the gate all around; likewise for the arches, and the windows all around inside, and toward the post of palm trees.

17 And he brought me into the outer court, and behold, chambers and pavement were made for the court all around, all around: thirty chambers next to the pavement.

18 And the pavement to the corner of the gates opposite the length of the gates, the lower pavement.

19 He measured the breadth from the front of the lower gate to the front of the inner court, a hundred cubits on the east and on the north.

20 The gate whose face is toward the north of the outer court he measured, its length and its breadth.

21 It had three chambers on this side and three chambers on that side; its posts and arches were according to the measure of the first gate: fifty cubits its length and twenty-five cubits its breadth.

22 Its windows, its porch, and its palm trees were according to the measure of the gate that faces east; and they went up to it by seven steps, and its porch was before them.

23 And the gate of the inner court facing north and east, he measured from gate to gate a hundred cubits.

24 And he led me toward the south, and behold, a gate toward the south; and he measured its posts and its porches according to those measurements.

25 And it had windows and its porches all around, like those windows; the length was fifty cubits and the breadth twenty-five cubits.

26 And there were seven steps going up to it, and its porch was before them; and it had palm trees, one on this side and one on that side, upon its posts.

27 And the gate of the inner court toward the south, he measured from the gate to the gate toward the south, one hundred cubits.

28 And he brought me into the inner court by the south gate, and he measured the south gate according to these measures.

29 Its chambers, its posts, and its porches were according to these measurements, and it had windows all around; the length was fifty cubits and the breadth twenty-five cubits.

30 And the arches round about, round about: length twenty-five cubits and breadth five cubits.

31 And its arches were toward the outer court, with palm trees on its posts, and eight steps.

32 And he brought me into the inner court by the way of the east, and he measured the gate according to these measures.

33 Its chambers, its posts, and its porches were according to these measurements, and it had windows all around; the length was fifty cubits and the breadth twenty-five cubits.

34 and its arches were toward the outer court, and palm trees were on its posts, on this side and on that side, and eight steps were its going up

35 And he brought me to the gate of the north, and measured according to those measures.

36 And the windows were all around it, all around, its length fifty cubits and its breadth five and twenty cubits.

37 The outer court, and palm trees by the gate on this side and on that side, and eight steps.

38 And a chamber and its entrance were by the posts of the gates; there they shall wash the burnt offering.

39 In the porch of the gate, two tables were on this side and two tables on that side, to slaughter upon them the burnt offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering.

40 On the outer side of the gateway, at the entrance of the north gate, were two tables, and on the other side, which belonged to the porch of the gate, were two tables.

41 Four tables from here and four tables from there on the side of the gate: eight tables upon which they shall slaughter.

42 And four tables for the ascent, hewn stone, a length of one cubit and a half and a breadth of one cubit and a half, and a height of one cubit; to them shall belong the vessels with which they shall slaughter the ascent and the sacrifice upon them.

43 And the pots were one hand-breadth, fitted in the house round about, round about, and to the tables of the flesh of the offering.

44 And outside the inner gate, the chambers of the officers in the inner court, which is by the side of the north gate, their faces toward the south; one by the side of the east gate, facing toward the north.

45 And he spoke to me, This is the chamber whose face is toward the south, for the priests who keep the charge of the temple.

46 The chamber whose entrance faces north is for the priests, the keepers of the charge of the altar, who are the sons of Zadok, those of the sons of Levi who come near to the LORD to minister to him.

47 He measured the court, a hundred cubits in length and a hundred cubits in breadth, square, and the altar before the temple.

48 He brought me into the porch of the temple, and he measured the porch: five cubits from this side and five cubits from that side; and the breadth of the gate was three cubits from its front and three cubits from its back.

49 The length of the porch was twenty cubits and the breadth eleven cubits, and the steps that went up to it, and pillars beside the lintels, one on this side and one on that side.


## Chapter 41

1 And he brought me to the temple and measured the posts: six cubits wide from one side and six cubits wide from the other side, the breadth of the tabernacle.

2 The breadth of the entrance was ten cubits, and the shoulders of the entrance were five cubits on one side and five cubits on the other; he measured its length, forty cubits, and its breadth, twenty cubits.

3 Then he went inside and measured the lintel of the entrance: two cubits, and the entrance six cubits, and the breadth of the entrance seven cubits.

4 He measured its length twenty cubits and its breadth twenty cubits toward the face of the temple, and he said to me, This is the most holy sanctuary.

5 He measured the wall of the house, six cubits; and the breadth of the side chamber, four cubits, round about, round about the house, round about.

6 The side chambers had side chambers, three and thirty times, and they entered into the wall of the house for the side chambers all around, all around, to be held, but they would not be held in the wall of the house.

7 And it widened and rose upward, upward, toward the chambers, for the house was wound about, upward, upward, round about round about the house; therefore the breadth of the house upward, and so the lowest ascended to the highest to the middle.

8 And I saw the height of the house all around; the foundations of the side chambers were a full reed of six great cubits.

9 The breadth of the wall, that which belongs to the side chamber on the outside, five cubits, and that which is set apart, the house of its side chambers which belongs to the house.

10 And the breadth between the chambers was twenty cubits round about, round about the house, round about.

11 The entrance of the side chamber was an opening to the north, and an opening to the south; and the breadth of the place of the chamber was five cubits all around.

12 The building that was before the separate place, toward the sea side, was seventy cubits wide, and the wall of the building was five cubits wide all around, all around, and its length was ninety cubits.

13 And he measured the court: its length was a hundred cubits; and the separate place, and the building, and its walls, their length was a hundred cubits.

14 The breadth of the front of the house and the separate place toward the east was a hundred cubits.

15 And he measured the length of the building facing the separate place which was behind it, and its galleries on this side and on that side, one hundred cubits, with the inner temple and the porches of the court.

16 The basins and the windowless and ancient galleries all around, three times around opposite the threshold, a wooden frame all around, all around, and the ground up to the windows, and the windows being covered.

17 Over the entrance and to the inner house and outward and to all the wall round about round about in the inner and in the outer measuring.

18 And they shall make cherubims and palm trees, and a palm tree between cherub and cherub, and two faces to the cherub.

19 The face of a man was toward the palm tree from its mouth, and the face of a young lion was toward the palm tree from its mouth; they were made for all the house all around.

20 From the earth up to above the entrance the cherubim and palm trees were made, and the wall of the temple.

21 As for the temple, the door posts were square, and the face of the sanctuary had the appearance as the appearance of the other.

22 The altar was wood, three cubits high and two cubits its length; its corners, length, and walls were of wood; and he said to me, This is the table that is before the LORD.

23 And two doors for the temple and for the holy place.

24 And two doors for the doors, two folding doors for the doors, two for one door, and two doors for the other.

25 And they shall make for them cherubim and palm trees, like those executed on the walls, and a wooden beam on the face of the porch from the outside.

26 And windows closed, and palm trees from window to window, toward the corners of the porch, and the side chambers of the house and the thick beams.


## Chapter 42

1 And he brought me out into the outer court, along the way toward the north, and brought me to the chamber which faced the separate place and which faced the building toward the north.

2 Before the length of a hundred cubits, the entrance of the north, and the breadth fifty cubits.

3 Opposite the twenty for the inner court and opposite the pavement for the outer court, a gallery corresponding to the galleries in the thirties.

4 Before the chambers was a ten-cubit-wide walkway leading to the inner room, with a one-cubit-wide path, and their entrances faced north.

5 And the upper chambers were short because galleries took from them, from the lower and middle parts, in building.

6 For they are threefold, and have no pillars like the pillars of the courts; therefore they are removed from the lower ones and from the middle ones from the earth.

7 And a wall outside opposite the chambers, along the outer court toward the front of the chambers, its length fifty cubits.

8 The length of the chambers belonging to the outer court was fifty cubits, and behold, in front of the temple was a hundred cubits.

9 And from below these chambers was the entrance on the east side, as one enters them from the outer court.

10 In the breadth of the wall of the court, the way of the gate eastward, facing the building, were chambers.

11 And the way before them was like the appearance of the chambers, which were toward the north; according to their length, so was their width; and all their exits, and their arrangements, and their entrances.

12 And as for the entrances of the chambers, the way of the south, a gate at the head of the way, the way before the wall toward the east, when one comes into them.

13 Then he said to me, These are the chambers of the north, the chambers of the south, which are before the separate place: these are the chambers of holiness, where the priests who are near to the LORD shall eat the most holy things; there they shall put the most holy things, and the oblation, and the sin offering, and the trespass offering; for the place is holy.

14 When the priests enter, they shall not go out from the sanctuary into the outer court, and there they shall leave their garments in which they minister, for they are holy; they shall put on other garments, and approach what belongs to the people.

15 And he finished measuring the inner temple, and he brought me out by the way of the gate whose faces were toward the east, and he measured it all around, round about.

16 Measured the east wind with a reed of measure, five reeds with a reed of measure, round about.

17 He measured the north wind with five hundred reeds according to the measuring rod, all around.

18 The south wind he measured: five hundred reeds by the measuring reed.

19 He went around to the west side, measured five hundred reeds by the measuring reed.

20 To its four sides a wall surrounds it all around, five hundred in length and five hundred in breadth, to separate between the holy and the common.


## Chapter 43

1 And he led me to the gate, the gate facing the way of the east.

2 And behold, the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east, and his voice was like the sound of many waters, and the earth was lit up by his glory.

3 And the appearance was like the appearance that I had seen when I came to destroy the city, and the visions were like the vision that I had seen by the river Chebar, and I fell upon my face.

4 And the glory of the LORD entered the temple by the gate whose face is toward the east.

5 And a wind lifted me and brought me to the inner court, and behold, the house was filled with the glory of the LORD.

6 And I heard one speaking to me from the temple, and a man was standing beside me.

7 And he said to me, O man, the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel forever, and the house of Israel shall no more defile my holy name, they and their kings, by their whoredom and by the corpses of their kings at their high places.

8 When they placed their thresholds next to my thresholds and their doorposts beside my doorposts, with a wall between me and them, they defiled my holy name by their abominations that they committed, and I consumed them in my anger.

9 Now they shall remove their whoredoms and the carcases of their kings from me, and I will dwell in their midst alway.

10 You, son of man, tell the house of Israel about the temple, and let them be ashamed of their iniquities; and let them measure the pattern.

11 And if they are ashamed of all that they have done, make known to them the form of the temple and its fashion, and its goings out and its comings in, and all its forms, and all its ordinances, and all its law; write it before their eyes, that they may keep all its form and all its ordinances and do them.

12 This is the law of the temple: on the top of the mountain, all its border round about, most holy; behold, this is the law of the temple.

13 And these are the measures of the altar in cubits: a cubit and a cubit and a hand-breadth; and the base shall be a cubit, and the breadth a cubit, and its border to its brim round about a span; this is the back of the altar.

14 From the bottom of the land to the lower court two cubits, and the breadth of a cubit; and from the smaller court to the larger court four cubits, and the breadth of the cubit.

15 And the altar was four cubits, and from the altar upward the horns were four.

16 Twelve [units of] length by twelve [units of] breadth, square on its four sides.

17 The court was fourteen in length and fourteen in breadth, squared on its four sides, and the border around it was half a cubit, and its surrounding ledge was one cubit; its steps faced the east.

18 Then he said to me, Son of man, thus says the Lord God: These are the statutes of the altar on the day it is made, to offer up burnt offerings upon it and to sprinkle blood upon it.

19 And you shall give to the priests, the Levites, who are of the seed of Zadok, those who come near to me, declares the Lord God, to minister to me: a young bull, a son of the herd, for a sin offering.

20 And you shall take some of its blood and put it on the four horns thereof, and on the four corners of the court, and on the border round about: and you shall purge it, and make atonement for it.

21 And you shall take the bull of the purifying offering and burn it at the appointed place of the temple outside the sanctuary.

22 And on the second day you shall offer a sin offering of a male goat without blemish, and they shall purify the altar as they purified it with the bull.

23 When you have finished sinning, you shall bring a young bull without blemish and a ram from the flock without blemish.

24 You shall bring them before the presence of the LORD, and the priests shall throw salt on them and offer them as a burnt offering to the LORD.

25 For seven days you shall make a goat of purification for sin each day, and they shall make a young bull and a ram from the flock, both without blemish.

26 Seven days they shall make atonement for the altar and cleanse it, and so consecrate it.

27 When the days are completed, then on the eighth day and onward the priests shall make your burnt offerings and your peace offerings upon the altar; and I will accept you, says the Lord God.


## Chapter 44

1 And he brought me back along the way of the outer gate of the sanctuary facing east, and it was shut.

2 And the LORD said to me, This gate shall be shut; it shall not be opened, and no man shall come in by it, because the LORD, the God of Israel, has come in by it; therefore it shall be shut.

3 The prince, he shall sit in it to eat food before the LORD; from the way of the porch of the gate he shall come in, and by his way he shall go out.

4 He brought me along the way of the north gate to the face of the house, and I saw, and behold, the glory of the LORD filled the house of the LORD, and I fell upon my face.

5 And the LORD said to me, Son of man, set your mind, see with your eyes, and with your ears hear all that I speak to you concerning all the statutes of the house of the LORD and all the law; and set your mind to the entrance of the house, to all the goings out of the sanctuary.

6 And you shall say to the rebellious, to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God, It is enough for you, O house of Israel, of all your abominations.

7 When you bring foreigners, those whose hearts and flesh are uncircumcised, to defile my sanctuary and to profane my temple by offering my bread, the fat and the blood, they break my covenant with all your abominations.

8 You did not keep my holy charge, and you have appointed yourselves to keep my charge in my sanctuary for you.

9 Thus says the LORD God: Every alien with uncircumcised heart and uncircumcised flesh shall not come into my sanctuary, even every alien who is among the people of Israel.

10 But the Levites who went far from me, when Israel strayed from me after their idols, shall bear their fault.

11 And they shall serve in my sanctuary, having charge at the gates of the house and ministering in the house; they shall slaughter the burnt offering and the sacrifice for the people, and they shall stand before them to minister to them.

12 Because they served idols before their faces and became to the house of Israel a stumbling-block of sin, therefore I have lifted up My hand against them, declares the Lord God, and they shall bear their iniquity.

13 They shall not come near to me to serve as priests or approach any of my consecrated things, even the most holy things; but they shall bear their disgrace and the abominations they have committed.

14 And I will appoint them keepers of the charge of the temple, for all its service and for all that is to be done in it.

15 And the priests, the Levites, the sons of Zadok, who kept the charge of my sanctuary when the children of Israel went astray from me, they shall come near to me to minister to me, and they shall stand before me to offer to me fat and blood, declares the Lord God.

16 They shall come to my sanctuary and they shall come near to my table to serve me, and they shall keep my charge.

17 And it shall be, when they enter by the gates of the inner court, they shall wear linen garments; and no wool shall come up upon them while they minister by the gates of the inner court and within.

18 Their heads shall have bonnets of linen, and their loins shall have breeches of linen; they shall not gird themselves with any thing that causeth sweat.

19 When they go out to the outer court, to the outer court to the people, they shall put off the garments in which they minister, and lay them in the chambers of the sanctuary, and put on other garments, so that they do not sanctify the people with their garments.

20 And their heads they shall not shave, nor let their locks grow long; they shall keep their heads covered.

21 And wine they shall not drink, all the priests, when they enter into the inner court.

22 They shall not take for themselves widows or divorced women as wives, but only virgins from the offspring of the house of Israel; and a widow who is a widow of a priest they may take.

23 And my folk they shall teach, between consecrated and common, and between unclean and clean they shall make known.

24 And concerning their controversy they shall stand before me for judgment, and he shall judge him according to my judgments; and my laws and my statutes at all my appointed times they shall keep, and my sabbaths they shall sanctify.

25 He shall not defile himself for the dead of another person, except for his father or his mother, or his son or his daughter, or his brother or his sister who has no husband.

26 And after his cleansing, seven days shall be numbered for him.

27 And on the day he comes into the sanctuary, to the inner court, to minister in the sanctuary, he shall present his sin offering, says the Lord GOD.

28 And it shall be to them as heritage; I am their inheritance; a possession you shall not give them in Israel; I am their possession.

29 The gift, the purifying for sin, and the offering for guilt—they shall eat them; and any devoted thing in Israel shall belong to them.

30 The beginning of all firstfruits and every heave offering of all kinds of offerings, all your heave offerings, shall belong to the priest; and the beginning of your dough you shall give to the priest, to bring a blessing upon your household.

31 All carcasses and torn animals, whether of birds or beasts, the priests shall not eat.


## Chapter 45

1 When you apportion the land as an inheritance, you shall elevate an offering to the LORD, a holy portion from the land, a length of twenty-five thousand in length and a breadth of ten thousand; it is holy in all its borders round about.

2 Let there be from this to the consecrated thing five hundred by five hundred square on every side, and fifty cubits as a suburb for it on every side.

3 And from this measure you shall measure: length twenty-five thousand and breadth ten thousand, and in it shall be the sanctuary, most holy.

4 A consecrated thing from the land shall be for the priests, the ministers of the sanctuary, for those who come near to serve the LORD; and it shall be for them a place for houses and a sanctuary for the sanctuary.

5 Twenty-five thousand in length and ten thousand in breadth for the Levites, the servants of the temple, shall have for possession twenty chambers.

6 The possession of the city shall be five thousand in breadth and twenty-five thousand in length, beside the holy oblation, for all the house of Israel.

7 And for the prince, on the one side and on the other side of the heave offering of the sanctuary and of the possession of the city, facing the heave offering of the sanctuary and facing the possession of the city, from the west side westward and from the east side eastward; and the length answering to one of the portions from the western border to the eastern border.

8 The land shall be his possession in Israel, and my princes shall no longer oppress my people; and the land they shall give to the house of Israel according to their tribes.

9 Thus says the Lord God: It is enough for you, O rulers of Israel; remove violence and oppression, and execute justice and righteousness; remove your exactions from my people, says the Lord God.

10 Just balances, a just ephah, and a just bath shall be yours.

11 The ephah and the bath shall be equal in measure: the tenth of the homer shall be the bath, and the tenth of the homer shall be the ephah; their measure shall be the same.

12 And the shekel shall be twenty gerahs; twenty shekels, five and twenty shekels, ten shekels shall be one maneh for you.

13 This is the offering that you shall raise: one-sixth of an ephah from a homer of wheat, and one-sixth of an ephah from a homer of barley.

14 And the appointed measure of oil is the bath of oil, a tenth of a cor; ten baths make one homer, for ten baths make one homer.

15 And one sheep from the flock, from the two hundred from the watered pastures of Israel, for a gift, and for a burnt offering, and for peace offerings, to make atonement for them, says the LORD.

16 All the people of the land shall contribute to this offering for the prince in Israel.

17 And the prince shall provide the burnt offerings, the grain offerings, and the drink offerings on the feasts, the new moons, and the Sabbaths, all the appointed times of the house of Israel; he shall make the sin offerings, the grain offerings, the burnt offerings, and the peace offerings to make atonement for the house of Israel.

18 Thus says the LORD God: In the first month, on the first of the month, you shall take a bull, a young bull without blemish, and make atonement for the sanctuary.

19 The priest shall take some of the blood of the punishment and put it on the side post of the house and on the four corners of the court of the altar and on the side post of the gate of the inner court.

20 And so you shall do on the seventh day of the month for a man who errs and for the simple one, and you shall atone for the temple.

21 On the first, on the fourteenth day of the month, there shall be for you the passover, a solemn feast of seven days: unleavened bread shall be eaten.

22 And the prince shall provide on that day a bull for a sin offering on his own behalf and on behalf of all the people of the land.

23 For seven days of the feast he shall make a burnt offering to the LORD: seven bulls and seven rams, perfect each day, for the seven days, and a sin offering, a male goat, each day.

24 A gift of an ephah for the bull and an ephah for the ram shall he prepare, and oil, the hin for the ephah.

25 On the seventh day, the fifteenth day of the month, during the feast, he shall do these things for seven days: as the sin offering, so the burnt offering, and as the grain offering, and as the oil.


## Chapter 46

1 Thus says the Lord God: The gate of the inner court facing east shall be shut six days of the work; but on the sabbath day it shall be opened, and on the day of the month it shall be opened.

2 The prince shall enter by way of the porch of the gate from outside and shall stand at the post of the gate, and the priests shall make his burnt offering and his peace offering; he shall worship at the threshold of the gate and then go out, and the gate shall not be shut until the evening.

3 And the folk of the land shall worship at the entrance of that gate on the sabbaths and on the new moons before the LORD.

4 And the burnt offering that the prince shall present to the LORD on the sabbath day: six lambs without blemish and one ram without blemish.

5 And a grain offering of one ephah for the ram, and for the lambs a grain offering according to the hand of each one, and oil of one hin for each ephah.

6 On the day of the new moon, a young bull without blemish, and six lambs, and a ram, all without blemish, shall be offered.

7 An ephah for the young bull and an ephah for the ram shall make a grain offering, and for the lambs as his hand can reach, and oil, one hin for each ephah.

8 When the prince comes, he shall enter by the way of the porch of the gate, and by his way he shall go out.

9 When the folk of the land come before the LORD at the appointed feasts, the one entering by way of the north gate to worship shall go out by way of the south gate, and the one entering by way of the south gate shall go out by way of the north gate; they shall not return by way of the gate by which they came in, but shall go out straight ahead.

10 And the prince among them shall go in when they go in, and shall go out when they go out.

11 On the (solemn) feast days and at the (appointed) seasons, the grain offering shall be an ephah for the bull and an ephah for the ram, and for the lambs a gift according to one's ability, and oil: one hin for each ephah.

12 When the prince offers a free-will offering, a burnt offering or a peace offering as a free-will offering to the LORD, they shall open for him the gate facing east, and he shall make his burnt offering and his peace offerings as he does on the sabbath day; then he shall go out, and they shall close the gate after his going out.

13 A lamb of one year old without blemish you shall make as a burnt offering to the LORD each morning continually.

14 And a gift you shall make on it morning morning a sixth of the ephah and oil a third of the hin to moisten the fine flour a gift to the LORD statutes alway continually.

15 The lamb, the gift, and the oil in the morning, morning, a continual ascent.

16 Thus says the Lord God: When the prince gives a gift to any of his sons, it shall be their possession; their holding shall be by inheritance.

17 But if he gives a gift from his inheritance to one of his servants, it shall remain with them until the year of liberty, then it shall return to the prince; only his inheritance shall belong to his sons for them.

18 And the prince shall not take from the people's inheritance to wrong them, from his own possession he shall provide for his sons, so that my people shall not be scattered, every man from his possession.

19 And he brought me by the way which was at the shoulder of the gate, into the chambers of the priests facing north; and behold, there was a place in the hinder part thereof westward.

20 Then he said to me, This is the place where the priests shall boil the trespass offering and the sin offering, and bake the grain offering, so that they do not carry them out into the outer court, to sanctify the people.

21 And he brought me out into the outer court and led me past the four corners of the court, and behold, a court at the corner of the court, a court at the corner of the court.

22 At the four corners of the court, court joinings, forty cubits long and thirty cubits wide, each with the same measure, were cut off.

23 And a row was around them on every side, around all four, and boiling-places were made under the rows on every side.

24 Then he said to me, These are the boiling courts, where the temple servants shall boil the people's sacrifice there.


## Chapter 47

1 And he brought me back to the entrance of the temple, and behold, waters were issuing from under the threshold of the temple eastward, for the front of the temple faced east; and the waters went down from under the temple's right side, south of the altar.

2 And he brought me out by the way of the north gate, and led me around by the way outside, to the outer gate by the way of the gate facing east; and behold, waters were gushing out from under the right shoulder.

3 When the man went out toward the east with a line in his hand, he measured one thousand cubits and led me through the waters, waters reaching the ankles.

4 He measured a thousand, and he led me through the waters, waters to the knees; he measured a thousand, and he led me through the waters of the loins.

5 And he measured a thousand brooks which I could not pass through, for the waters were waters of swimming, a brook which cannot be crossed.

6 And he said to me, "Have you seen, son of man?" Then he led me and brought me back to the bank of the stream.

7 When I turned back, behold, upon the bank of the stream, a very great tree, on this side and on that.

8 And he said to me, These waters go forth toward the eastern country, and go down into the Arabah, and go into the sea, into the sea of the waters that were made to issue out, and the waters shall be healed.

9 And it shall be that every living soul which swarms shall live wherever the streams come, and the fish shall be very many, because these waters have come there and they shall be healed; everything that comes into the river shall live.
