10 And it shall be that fishers shall stand beside it from En-gedi to En-eglaim; they shall be a place for spreading nets. Their fish shall be according to their kinds, like the fish of the Great Sea, exceedingly many.

11 But its swamps and marshes shall not be healed; they shall be given over to salt.

12 Along the river, on its banks, on this side and on that, every tree of food shall grow; its leaf shall not wither and its fruit shall not fail, monthly it shall bear new fruit, for its waters come from the sanctuary; its fruit shall be for food and its leaf for medicine.

13 Thus says the LORD God: Behold, the border which you shall inherit, even the land, for the twelve tribes of Israel: Joseph [receives] portions.

14 You shall inherit it, each as his brother, this land which I lifted my hand to give to your fathers, and it shall fall to you as an inheritance.

15 And this is the border of the land toward the north side, from the great sea, the way to Hethlon, as one comes to Zedad.

16 Hamath, Berothah, Sibraim, which is between the border of Damascus and the border of Hamath, Hazar-hatticon, which is by the border of Hauran.

17 And the border shall be from the sea, Hazar-enon, the border of Damascus, northward northward, and the border of Hamath, and the northern quarter.

18 The eastern quarter from between Hauran and from between Damascus and from between Gilead and from between the land of Israel, the Jordan, from the border to the eastern sea to the end, and the eastern quarter.

19 And the southern quarter southward from Tamar to the waters of Meribath-kadesh, the brook to the Great Sea, and the southern side southward.

20 And the western quarter shall be the Great Sea from the border to the entrance opposite Hamath; this is the western quarter.

21 And you shall divide this land among you by the tribes of Israel.

22 And you shall allot that inheritance to you and to the sojourners who reside among you, who have begotten sons among you, and they shall be to you as native-born among the sons of Israel; with you they shall receive their inheritance among the tribes of Israel.

23 And when the alien sojourns with him in the tribe, there you shall give him his inheritance, saith the LORD God.


## Chapter 48

1 And these are the names of the tribes: from the northern border, at the way of Hethlon, going toward Hamath, Hazar-enan, the border of Damascus northward, at Hamath, and it shall be for him the eastern side, the sea, Dan one.

2 And on the border of Dan from the east side to the west side Asher one.

3 And on the border of Asher, from the eastern end to the western end, [is] Naphtali one.

4 And on the border of Naphtali, from the eastern side to the western side, [is] one Manasseh.

5 And next to the border of Manasseh, from the eastern side to the western side, [is] Ephraim, one [portion].

6 And toward the border of Ephraim, from the eastern side to the western side, Reuben one.

7 And on Reuben's border from the eastern side to the western side, Judah is one.

8 On the border of Judah, from the eastern side to the western sea, shall be the offering that you shall offer: twenty-five thousand in breadth, and in length equal to one of the portions, from the eastern side to the western sea; and the sanctuary shall be in the midst of it.

9 The heave offering that you shall lift up to the LORD: its length twenty-five thousand and its breadth ten thousand.

10 And for these shall be the heave offering of the consecrated thing to the priests: northward twenty-five thousand, and westward breadth ten thousand, and eastward breadth ten thousand, and southward length twenty-five thousand, and the sanctuary of the LORD shall be in the midst of it.

11 To the priests who are consecrated from the sons of Zadok, who kept my charge, who did not go astray when the sons of Israel went astray, just as the Leviites went astray.

12 And it shall be their oblation from the gift of the land, most holy, bordering on the territory of the Leviites.

13 And the Leviite beside the border of the priest, length twenty five thousand and breadth ten thousand; all the length twenty five thousand, and the breadth ten thousand.

14 They shall not sell from it, nor exchange nor alienate the chief part of the land, for it is holy to the LORD.

15 and five thousand in breadth, facing twenty-five thousand, it shall be common ground for the city, for dwelling and for suburbs, and the city shall be in the midst of it

16 And these are its measurements: the northern side five hundred and four thousand, the southern side five hundred and four thousand, the eastern side five hundred and four thousand, and the western side five hundred and four thousand.

17 And the suburb of the city shall be two hundred fifty cubits on the north, two hundred fifty on the south, two hundred fifty on the east, and two hundred fifty on the west.

18 And the remainder along the length, opposite the holy offering, ten thousand cubits eastward and ten thousand cubits westward, shall be opposite the holy offering, and it shall serve as revenue for the workers of the city.

19 And the servant of the city shall serve him from all the tribes of Israel.

20 All the offering, five and twenty thousand by five and twenty thousand, a fourth part you shall elevate as the offering of the consecrated thing, unto the possession of the city.

21 The remainder for the prince from this side and from that side shall be before the holy offering and the possession of the city, facing east twenty-five thousand in measure, and westward facing twenty-five thousand, opposite the portions for the prince; and it shall be the holy offering with the sanctuary of the house in the midst of it.

22 And from the possession of the Leviite and from the possession of the city, in the midst among that which belongs to the prince, between the border of Judah and between the border of Benjamin, shall belong to the prince.

23 and the rest of the tribes from the east side to the west side, Benjamin one.

24 And on the border of Benjamin, from the eastern side to the western side, [is] Simeon one.

25 And on the border of Simeon from the eastern side to the western side, Issachar one.

26 And on the border of Issachar, from the side toward the east even to the side toward the west, [is] one [portion] of Zebulun.

27 And on the border of Zebulun, from the side toward the east to the side toward the west, Gad one.

28 And as for the border of Gad, toward the southern flank southward, the border shall be from Tamar, the waters of Meribath-kadesh, a ravine to the Great Sea.

29 This is the land that you shall divide as inheritance to the tribes of Israel, and these are their portions, says the LORD God.

30 These are the outgoings of the city: on the north side, four thousand and five hundred cubits in measure.

31 The gates of the city are named for the tribes of Israel: three gates toward the north, one gate of Reuben, one gate of Judah, one gate of Levi.

32 And toward the east side five hundred and four thousand, and three gates: one gate of Joseph, one gate of Benjamin, one gate of Dan.

33 And the south quarter four thousand and five hundred cubits in measure, and three gates: one gate of Simeon, one gate of Issachar, one gate of Zebulun.

34 The westward side five thousand four hundred; its gates three: one gate for Gad, one gate for Asher, one gate for Naphtali.

35 about eighteen thousand [and] the name of the city from the day [is] the LORD [is] there


# DANIEL

## Chapter 1

1 In the year three of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and laid siege against it.

2 The LORD gave into his hand Jehoiakim king of Judah and part of the vessels of the house of God, and he brought them to the land of Shinar, into the house of his god; and the vessels he brought into the storehouse of his god.

3 The king said to Ashpenaz, chief of his chamberlains, to bring some of the children of Israel, and of the royal seed, and of the most noble youths.

4 Boys who had no blemish, goodly in appearance and skilful in all wisdom, knowing knowledge and understanding science, and in whom was ability to stand in the palace of the king, and to instruct them in the book and the language of the Chaldeans.

5 And the king appointed them a daily provision from the king's food and from the wine of his banqueting, to nourish them three years, and at the end of them they were to stand before the king.

6 And among them from the children of Judah were Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah.

7 The captain of the eunuchs gave them names: to Daniel he gave the name Belteshazzar, to Hananiah Shadrach, to Mishael Meshak, and to Azariah Abed-nego.

8 Daniel resolved in his mind not to defile himself with the king's portion of meat or with the wine of his banqueting, and he requested from the chief of the chamberlains that he might not defile himself.

9 And God gave Daniel into favour and tender mercies before the prince of the eunuchs.

10 Then said the captain of the eunuchs to Daniel, I fear my lord the king, who has appointed your food and your drink: why should he see your faces looking more haggard than the youths of your sort? so would you endanger my head to the king.

11 Then Daniel said to Melzar, whom the captain of the eunuchs had appointed over Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah:

12 Please test your servants ten days and let them give us pulses to eat and water to drink.

13 Let our appearance and the appearance of the young men who eat the king's portion of meat be considered before you; and as you see, deal with your servants.

14 He listened to them in this matter, and tested them for ten days.

15 And some ten days later, their appearance was better and their bodies healthier than all the youths who ate the king's portion of meat.

16 Then Melzar carried away their portion of meat and their banqueting wine and gave them pulse.

17 And these four boys, God gave them knowledge and wisdom in all learning and science, and Daniel understood all visions and dreams.

18 And at the end of the days which the king had appointed for bringing them in, the captain of the eunuchs brought them before Nebuchadnezzar.

19 The king spoke with them, but none of them was found like Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah; so they stood before the king.

20 And in every matter of wisdom and understanding that the king sought from them, he found them ten times more capable than all the magicians and astrologers who were in all his realm.

21 And Daniel continued until the first year of Cyrus the king.


## Chapter 2

1 In the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams, and his spirit was troubled, and his sleep broke from him.

2 The king said to summon the magicians, the astrologers, the sorcerers, and the Chaldeans to tell the king his dreams, and they came and stood before the king.

3 The king said to them, "I dreamed a dream, and my spirit is troubled to understand the dream."

4 The Chaldeans spoke to the king in the Syrian language: "O king, live forever; declare the dream, and the interpretation we will give."

5 The king answered and said, "This matter is mine: if you do not make known to me the dream and its interpretation, you shall be cut into pieces, and your houses shall be laid in ruins."

6 But if you will show the dream and its interpretation, gifts, rewards, and great honour you shall receive from me; therefore, show the dream and its interpretation.

7 They answered a second time and said, Let the king tell his servants the dream, and we will declare the interpretation.

8 The king answered and said, I know certainly that you are stalling for time, because you have seen that the matter is fixed from me.

9 If you do not make the dream known to me, there is one decree for you; for you have prepared lying and corrupt words to speak before me until the time changes. Therefore tell me the dream, and I shall know that you can show me its interpretation.

10 Answer before the king and say, There is no man on earth who can make known to the king the matter, forasmuch as every great and ruling king has not asked such a word of any magician, astrologer, or Chaldean.

11 And the matter that the king asked is precious, and there is no other who can reveal it before the king, except the gods who dwell with flesh.

12 Because of this the king was furious and greatly enraged and said to bring all the wise men of Babylon.

13 And the decree went out, and the wise were being killed, and Daniel and his companions were sought to be killed.

14 Then Daniel replied with counsel and wisdom to Arioch, the king's captain, who had gone out to destroy the wise men of Babylon.

15 Arioch answered and said to Daniel, "Why is this decree issued in haste from before the king?" Then Arioch made the matter known to Daniel.

16 But Daniel requested of the king a time, that he might show the interpretation to the king.

17 Then Daniel went to his house and made the matter known to his companions Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah.

18 And mercy from before God of heaven was sought concerning this secret, that Daniel and his companions might not perish with the rest of the wise of Babylon.

19 Then to Daniel in a vision of the night the secret was revealed; then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.

20 Daniel answered and said, "May the name of God be blessed from everlasting to everlasting, for wisdom and might are his."

21 But he changes times and seasons, removes kings and establishes kings; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding.

22 He reveals the deep and the hidden; He knows what is in the darkness, and light is with Him.

23 To you, God of my fathers, I give thanks and praise, for you have given me wisdom and might, and have now made known to me what we desired of you; for you have made known to us the matter that we asked of the king.

24 Because of this Daniel spoke to Arioch, whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise of Babylon: “Do not destroy the wise of Babylon; bring me before the king, and I will declare the interpretation to the king.”

25 Then Arioch in haste brought Daniel before the king and thus said to him that there is a certain man from the children of the captivity of Judah who can make known the interpretation to the king.

26 The king answered and said to Daniel, whose name is Belteshazzar, Can you make known to me the dream that I have seen and its interpretation?

27 Daniel answered before the king and said, "The secret that the king has asked cannot be revealed to the king by wise men, astrologers, or magicians."

28 But God in heaven has revealed secrets and made known to King Nebuchadnezzar what shall be in the latter days: your dream and the visions of your head upon your bed are these.

29 You are the king; upon your bed your thoughts have risen: what will be hereafter? And you have revealed the secret, what will be.

30 But as for me, this secret has not been revealed to me because of any wisdom that is in me more than all living beings, but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king, and the thoughts of your heart may be known.

31 You are the king; I saw and behold, one form exceedingly great, that form so mighty and its countenance surpassing, stood before you, and its form was terrifying.

32 It is an image, its head of fine gold, its breast and arms of silver, its belly and thighs of brass.

33 His legs are iron, his feet are iron and clay.

34 I saw until a stone cut out without hands struck the image on its feet of iron and clay and crushed them.

35 Then the iron, the clay, the brass, the silver, and the gold were crushed together, and became like chaff from the summer threshingfloor, and the wind carried them away so that no place was found for them; but the stone that struck the image became a great mountain and filled the whole earth.

36 This dream and its interpretation let me declare before the king.

37 You are the king, king of kings, to whom the God of heaven has given the kingdom, power, might, and glory.

38 And over all the sons of man, the beasts of the field, and the fowl of the heavens, he gave into your hand and caused you to rule over all of them: you are the head of gold.

39 After you, another kingdom shall arise, and a third kingdom of brass, which shall rule over all the earth.

40 And the kingdom shall be strong as iron, inasmuch as iron crushes and shatters all, and as iron that breaks all these, it shall crush and break.

41 And as for what you saw of the feet and toes, partly of potter's clay and partly of iron, it means a kingdom will be divided; but there will be some of the strength of iron in it, because you saw the iron mixed with miry clay.

42 And the toes of the feet were partly iron and partly clay; the kingdom will be strong in part, and in part it will be broken.

43 You have seen iron mixed with miry clay, mixed to become seed of man, but they will not adhere, this one to that one: indeed, iron does not mix with clay.

44 In their days when these kings reign, the God of heaven will establish a kingdom that shall not be destroyed, and this kingdom shall not be left to another people; it will crush and consume all these kingdoms, and it shall stand forever.

45 Because you saw a stone cut from a mountain not by hands, and it struck the iron, brass, clay, silver, and gold; the great God has made known to the king what will come after this: the dream is certain and its interpretation is trustworthy.

46 Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face and worshiped Daniel, and offerings and sweet savours he commanded to be presented to him.

47 The king answered Daniel and said, "Truly, your God is God of gods and Lord of kings and a revealer of secrets, since you have been able to reveal this secret."

48 Then the king Daniel honored, and great gifts gave him, and made him rule over all the province of Babylon, and governor over all the wise of Babylon.

49 And Daniel requested from the king, and he appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego; and Daniel was at the gate mouth of the king.


## Chapter 3

1 Nebuchadnezzar the king made an image of golden form, its height sixty cubits, its breadth six cubits; he set it up in the plain of Dura in the province of Babylon.

2 And King Nebuchadnezzar sent to the princes, governors, captains, judges, treasurers, counsellors, sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces, to assemble for the dedication of the image that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.

3 Then the princes, governors, captains, judges, treasurers, counsellors, sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces were gathered for the dedication of the image that Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up, and they stood before the image that Nebuchadnezzar had set up.

4 And a herald proclaims aloud: "Come, my peoples, nations, and languages."

5 At the time when you hear the sound of the horn, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, dulcimer, and all kinds of musick, you shall fall down and worship the golden image that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up.

6 But whoever does not fall down and worship at that hour shall be cast into the midst of the fiery furnace.

7 For this cause, at that time, when all the peoples, nations, and languages hear the sound of the horn, flute, harp, sackbut, psaltery, and every kind of musick, they fall down and worship the golden image that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up.

8 At that time certain Chaldean men came near and accused the Jews.

9 Answer and say to Nebuchadnezzar the king, O king, live forever.

10 O king, you have issued a decree that all people, man or woman, who hear the sound of the horn, the flute, the harp, the sackbut, the psaltery, the dulcimer, and every kind of musick, shall fall down and worship the golden image.

11 But whoever does not fall down and worship will be thrown into the midst of a fiery furnace.

12 There are certain Jews whom you appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon, Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego—these certain men, O king, have not regarded the decree of the king, nor do they serve the king's god, nor do they worship the golden image which you have set up.

13 Then Nebuchadnezzar in rage and fury said to bring Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego; then these certain men were brought before the king.

14 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said to them, Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego, is it true that you do not serve my gods, and that you do not worship the golden image which I have set up?

15 Now if you are ready, when you hear the sound of the horn, cornet, sackbut, psaltery, dulcimer, and all kinds of music, fall down and worship the image which I have made; but if you do not worship, in that same hour you shall be cast into the midst of the fiery furnace. And who is the God who can deliver you out of my hand?

16 Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego answered the king Nebuchadnezzar: We have no need to answer you concerning this matter.

17 Look, there is our God whom we serve, able to deliver us from the fiery furnace and from the power of the king.

18 But if not, let it be known to you, O king, that we do not serve your gods, nor will we worship the golden image which you have set up.

19 Then Nebuchadnezzar was filled with fury, and the form of his visage was changed against Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego; he answered and spoke to heat the furnace seven times hotter than was customary.

20 And to certain mighty men, powerful in strength, he said to cast Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego into the fiery furnace.

21 Then these certain men were cast into the midst of the fiery furnace, with their coats, their hose, their hats, and their garments.

22 On account of this, because the king's commandment was urgent, and the certain men had been bound, Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego, the furnace was heated exceedingly, and the flame of fire slew those men.

23 And the certain men, Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego, fell into the midst of the fiery furnace.

24 Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and arose in haste; he declared to his counsellors, "Is it not three certain men whom we cast into the midst of the fiery furnace?" Then answered the counsellors to the king, "True, O king."

25 He answered and said, Behold, I see four certain men loose, walking in the midst of the fiery furnace, and there is no damage in them; and the form of the fourth is like a son of God.

26 Then Nebuchadnezzar came near to the mouth of the furnace of fire; he answered and said, Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego, servants of God Most High, come out and enter here; then Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego came out from the midst of the fire.

27 Then the princes, governors, captains, and royal counsellors gathered and saw those certain men whose bodies were unharmed by the fire, whose hair was not singed, whose coats were not changed, and whose smell of fire was not upon them.

28 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said, Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego, who sent his angel and delivered his servants who trusted in him, and they changed the king's commandment and yielded their bodies, that they might not serve nor worship any god except their own God.

29 And from me let a decree go out that any people, nation, or language that speaks against the God of Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego shall be cut into pieces and his house shall be made a dunghill, because there is no other god who is able to deliver in this way.

30 Then the king promoted Shadrach, Meshak, and Abed-nego in the province of Babylon.


## Chapter 4

1 Nebuchadnezzar the king to all peoples, nations, and languages that dwell in all the earth: peace be multiplied to you.

2 Signs and wonders that my God, the Most High God, has done with me, to display before me.

3 How great are his signs, and how mighty are his wonders! His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and his dominion is from generation to generation.

4 I, Nebuchadnezzar, was at peace in my house and flourishing in my palace.

5 A dream terrifies me and I am afraid; thoughts upon my bed alarm me, and visions of my head frighten me.

6 Therefore I made a decree to bring in all the wise men of Babylon before me, that they might make known to me the interpretation of the dream.

7 Then the magicians, the astrologers, and the Chaldeans came, and I told them the dream, but its interpretation they did not make known to me.

8 And to Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, by the name of my God, and in whom is the spirit of the holy gods, I will tell the dream.

9 Belteshazzar, chief magician, I know that you have a mind of holy gods and that no secret is too difficult for you; tell the vision of my dream that I saw and its interpretation.

10 And visions of my head upon my bed I saw, and look, a tree in the midst of the earth and its height great.

11 The tree grew large and strong, its height reached heaven, and its sight extended to the end of all the earth.

12 Its leaves were beautiful and its fruit great, and meat for all was in it; in its shade lived beasts of the field, and in its boughs birds of heaven, and from it all flesh was fed.

13 I was looking in the visions of my head upon my bed, and behold, a watcher, a holy one, came down from heaven.

14 Cry aloud thus: Cut down the tree and chop off its branches, strip off its leaves and scatter its fruit; let the beast flee from beneath it and the birds from its boughs.

15 But leave the stump of its roots in the earth, bound with a band of iron and brass, in the tender grass of the field; let it be wet with the dew of heaven, and let its portion be with the beasts in the grass of the earth.

16 His heart is changed from that of a man and the heart of a beast is given to him, and seven times shall pass over him.

17 By the decree of the watchers, by the word of the holy ones, the matter is issued, so that the living may know that the Most High rules over the kingdom of man and gives it to whomever he wills, and sets up the lowly.

18 This dream I saw, O king Nebuchadnezzar; Belteshazzar, declare the interpretation, because all the wise of my kingdom have not been able to make known to me the interpretation, but you, in whom is the spirit of the holy God.

19 Then Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, was dismayed for an hour, and his thoughts alarmed him; the king answered and said, Belteshazzar, do not let the dream and its interpretation alarm you; Belteshazzar answered and said, My lord, let the dream be to your enemies, and its interpretation to those that hate you.

20 The tree that you saw, which grew and became strong, its height reached to heaven, and its sight extended over all the earth.

21 Its leaves were beautiful and its fruit abundant, and meat for all was in it; in its shade lived beasts of the field, and in its branches dwelt birds of heaven.

22 You are the king who has grown and become strong, and your greatness has grown and reached to heaven, and your dominion to the end of the earth.

23 And the watcher, the holy one, came down from heaven; he said, "Cut down the tree and destroy it, but leave its stump with the root in the earth, bound with a band of iron and brass, amid the tender grass of the field; and let him be wet with the dew of heaven, and let his portion be with the beasts of the field, until seven times pass over him."

24 This is the interpretation, O king, and it is a decree from the Lord that has been issued against the king.

25 You shall be driven from men, and your dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field; they shall make you eat grass like oxen, and you shall be wet with the dew of heaven, and seven times shall pass over you, until you know that the Most High rules in the kingdom of men, and gives it to whomever he wills.

26 And the decree that they said to leave the stump of the root of the tree, your kingdom shall be established for you, from the moment that you know that the sovereignty is of heaven.

27 Therefore, O king, let my counsel be acceptable to you: break off your sins by righteousness, and your iniquities by showing mercy to the poor, that there may be a lengthening of your tranquility.

28 all of it pertains to King Nebuchadnezzar

29 At the end of twelve months, he was walking in the royal palace of Babylon.

30 The king answered and said, Is not this great Babylon that I have built for the house of my kingdom by the strength of my power and for the glory of my majesty?

31 While the word was yet in the king's mouth, a voice fell from heaven, saying, "O Nebuchadnezzar, king, the kingdom is departed from you."

32 And you shall be driven from men, and your dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field; they shall make you eat grass like oxen, and seven times shall pass over you, until you know that the Most High rules in the kingdom of men and gives it to whomever he wills.

33 At that hour a word went out concerning Nebuchadnezzar, and from man he was driven, and with the grass he ate like oxen, and with the dew of heaven his body was wet, until his hair grew like eagles and his nails like birds.

34 But at the end of my days, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High, and praised and honored the one who lives forever, whose dominion is an everlasting dominion and whose kingdom is from generation to generation.

35 And all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing, and he does according to his will in the host of heaven and among the inhabitants of the earth, and none can stay his hand, or say to him, "What have you done?"

36 At that season, my reason returned to me, and the glory of my majesty and brightness returned to me, and my counsellors and lords sought me, and I was established in my kingdom, and greater majesty was added to me.

37 Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and exalt and honor the king of heaven, whose works are all truth and whose ways are judgment, and who is able to humble those who walk in pride.


## Chapter 5

1 Belshazzar the king made a great feast for his royal lords, a thousand in number, and drank wine before the thousand.

2 Belshazzar gave orders to bring the gold and silver vessels that Nebuchadnezzar his father had taken from the temple in Jerusalem, so that the king and his lords, his wives, and his concubines might drink from them.

3 Then were brought the golden vessels that had come out of the temple-house of God in Jerusalem, and the king and his lords, his wives, and his concubines drank from them.

4 They drank wine, and praised the gods of gold, silver, brass, iron, wood, and stone.

5 In that hour, the fingers of a man's hand appeared and wrote opposite the candlestick upon the plaster of the wall of the king's palace, and the king saw the hand that wrote.

6 Then the king, his brightness and his thought were alarmed, and the joints of his loins were loosened, and his knees knocked one against another.

7 The king called aloud to bring in the astrologers, the Chaldeans, and the wise of Babylon; the king answered and said to the wise of Babylon, Whoever among men shall read this writing and show me its interpretation, he shall be clothed in purple, a chain of gold shall be put about his neck, and he shall have authority as third ruler in the kingdom.

8 Then all the wise men of the king came in, but they were not able to read the writing or make its interpretation known to the king.

9 Then King Belshazzar was greatly terrified, and his countenance changed, and his lords were perplexed.

10 The queen answered before the king and his lords, "O king, live forever! Let not your thought trouble you, nor let your countenance change."

11 There is a certain man in your kingdom in whom is the spirit of holy gods, and in the days of your father light and understanding and wisdom like the wisdom of gods was found in him, and King Nebuchadnezzar your father, your father the king, appointed him chief of the magicians, astrologers, and Chaldeans.

12 Because an excellent spirit, knowledge, and understanding, an interpreter of dreams, and a shewer of hard sentences, and a dissolver of doubts, were found in Daniel—he to whom the king had given the name Belteshazzar—now let Daniel be called, and he will declare the interpretation.

13 Then Daniel was brought before the king; the king answered and said to Daniel, "Are you Daniel, of the children of the captivity of Judah, whom the king, my father, brought from Judah?"

14 I have heard of you that the spirit of God is in you, and that light and understanding and excellent wisdom are found in you.

15 Now my wise men and astrologers have been brought before me to read this writing and make known to me its interpretation, but they are not able to reveal the interpretation of the matter.

16 And I have heard of you that you are able to give interpretations and resolve doubts; now if you can read the writing and make known its interpretation to me, you shall be clothed with purple, have a golden chain around your neck, and rule as third in the kingdom.

17 Then Daniel answered and said before the king, Your gift be to you, and your reward give to another; but the writing I will read to the king, and the interpretation I will make known to him.

18 O king, the Most High God gave Nebuchadnezzar your father the kingdom, greatness, glory, and majesty.

19 And from the greatness that he gave to him, all peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him; whomever he wanted, he killed, and whomever he wanted, he kept alive, and whomever he wanted, he raised up, and whomever he wanted, he brought low.

20 And when his heart was lifted up and his spirit hardened toward arrogance, he was brought down from his royal throne and his glory was taken from him.

21 And he was driven from the children of men; his heart was made like the beasts, and his dwelling was with the wild donkeys. They fed him grass like oxen, and his body was wet with the dew of heaven, until he knew that the Most High God rules over the kingdom of men, and appoints over it whom he will.

22 Old, son Belshazzar, you have not humbled your heart, even though all this you knew.

23 And against the Lord of heaven you have exalted yourself, and the vessels from his house you brought before you, and you and your lords, your wives and your concubines have drunk wine, and gods of silver and gold, brass, iron, timber, and stone, which do not see, nor hear, nor know, you praised; but the God in whose hand is your breath and all your ways, him you did not honor.

24 Then a hand was sent from before him, and this writing was inscribed.

25 And this writing is prescribed: Mene, Mene, Tekel, and divide Upharsin.

26 This is the interpretation of the word: Mene, God has numbered your kingdom and brought it to an end.

27 You have been weighed in the balances and found wanting.

28 Divide the kingdom of your realm, and it shall be given to the Medes and Persians.

29 Then Belshazzar said, and they clothed Daniel in purple, with a chain of gold around his neck, and proclaimed concerning him that he should be third ruler in the kingdom.

30 That night Belshazzar the king was killed.

31 And Darius the Median received the kingdom, being sixty-two years old.


## Chapter 6

1 Set up before Darius and establish over the kingdom princes one hundred and twenty, who shall be over all the kingdom.

2 And over them was set a president, one of whom was Daniel, that these princes might give account, so that the king should not suffer loss.

3 Then Daniel prospered over the presidents and princes, because a superior spirit was in him, and the king appointed him over all the kingdom.

4 Then the presidents and the princes sought occasion against Daniel concerning the kingdom, and all manner of error and corruption they could not find, forasmuch as he was faithful, and no error or corruption was found in him.

5 Then these men said, We shall not find any occasion against this Daniel unless we find it against him concerning the law of his God.

6 Then the presidents and princes conspired against the king and said thus to him, Darius the king, live for ever.

7 All the presidents of the kingdom, governors, princes, counsellors, and captains convened to establish a decree of the king and to strengthen a statute, that any petition from any man or god, for thirty days, except from the king, should be cast into the den of lions.

8 According to the command of the king, establish the decree and the writing, so that the decree of the Medes and Persians may not be changed.

9 Because of this, King Darius issued a decree and made a binding writing.

10 And Daniel, when he knew that a writing was prescribed upon his house, and the window was opened in his chamber facing Jerusalem, at three seasons in a day he kneeled upon his knees and prayed and gave thanks before his God, as he had done from aforetime.

11 Then certain men came near and found Daniel praying and pleading before his God.

12 Then they came near and said before the king, "O king, did you not sign a decree that every man who should petition any god or man within thirty days, except you, O king, would be cast into the den of lions?" The king answered and said, "The commandment is established according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which cannot be changed."

13 Then they answered and said before the king, Daniel who is of the children of the captivity of Judah does not set the king's command nor the decree which you wrote, and three times in the day he makes petition to his God.

14 Then the king, when he heard the matter, was greatly distressed and set his heart on Daniel’s deliverance, and until the going down of the sun he made strenuous effort to save him.

15 Then those certain men rushed in before the king and said to the king, "Know, O king, that the decree of the Medes and Persians is that every decree and statute which the king establishes must not be changed."

16 Then the king said, Let Daniel be taken and cast into the den of lions. The king answered and said to Daniel, Your God whom you serve continually, he will deliver you.

17 And it was a stone, one, placed over the mouth of the den, and sealed with the signet of the king and with the signet of his lords, that the purpose of Daniel might not be changed.

18 Then the king went to his palace and spent the night fasting; no instruments of music were brought before him, and his sleep fled from him.

19 Then the king arose very early in the morning in haste to the den of lions.

20 And as he came near the den, toward Daniel, with a distressed voice, the king answered and said to Daniel, Daniel, servant of the living God, does your God, whom you serve continually, have power to deliver you from the lions?

21 Then Daniel answered the king, O king, live forever.

22 My God sent his angel and shut the mouth of the lions and they did not hurt me, because before him my innocency was found; and also before the king, no hurt have I done.

23 Then the royal king was greatly distressed and said to raise Daniel from the den, and Daniel was raised from the den and every hurt was not found upon him because he trusted in his God.

24 Then the king said, Let the men who accused Daniel be brought and cast into the lions’ den, their children and their women; and they did not reach the bottom of the den before the lions overpowered them and crushed all their bones.

25 Then King Darius wrote to all peoples, nations, and languages that are in all the earth: "Peace be greatly increased to you."

26 Before me set forth a decree that in all the dominion of my kingdom men tremble and fear before Daniel's God, who is the living God enduring forever, and his kingdom which shall not be destroyed, and his dominion unto the end.

27 He delivers and rescues, performing signs and wonders in heaven and on earth, who delivered Daniel from the power of the lion.

28 And Daniel prospered in the kingdom of Darius and in the kingdom of Cyrus the Persian.


## Chapter 7

1 In the year of Belshazzar king of Babylon, Daniel had a dream and visions of his head upon his bed; then he wrote down the dream, the sum of the matter.

2 Daniel answered and said, "I was looking in my vision by night, and behold, four winds of heaven were stirring up the great sea."

3 And four beasts, great ones, came up from the sea; each was different from the other.

4 It had the appearance of a lion with eagle's wings; I watched until its wings were plucked and it was lifted from the earth, and on feet like a man's it was raised, and a man's heart was given to it.

5 And another beast, a second one, appeared like a bear; and on one side it stood up, and three ribs were in its mouth between its teeth: and thus they said to it, "Arise, devour much flesh."

6 After this I was looking and behold, another like a leopard, and to it four wings of a fowl; and the beast had four heads, and dominion was given to it.

7 After this I was looking in visions of the night and behold a beast of terror and dread and strength exceedingly, and its teeth of iron, it was devouring and crushing and with its feet trampling the rest, and it was different from all the beasts that were before it, and it had ten horns.

8 I was watching the horns, and behold, another small horn came up, and three of the former horns were uprooted before it; and in this horn were eyes like human eyes, and a mouth speaking great things.

9 I saw until thrones were placed and the Ancient of Days took his seat; his garments were white as snow, and the hair of his head was like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames; its wheels were a burning fire.

10 A river of fire was flowing and going out from before him; a thousand thousands served him and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him; the judgement sat and the books were opened.

11 I beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spoke; I beheld even till the beast was slain, and its body was given to the burning flame.

12 And whatsoever more of the beasts, their dominions were prolonged, and a lengthening of life was given to them until a season and a time.

13 I was looking in visions of the night, and I saw with the clouds of heaven one like a son of man coming, and he came near to the Ancient of Days, and they brought him near before him.

14 And to him was given dominion, and glory, honour, and kingdom, and all peoples, nations, and languages shall serve him: his dominion is an everlasting dominion that shall not pass away, and his kingdom is one that shall not be destroyed.

15 My spirit was terrified, I, Daniel, in the midst of the body, and the vision of my head alarmed me.

16 I came near unto one of those that stood by, and asked him the truth of all this; so he told me, and made known to me the interpretation of the things.

17 These are four great beasts, which four kings will arise from the earth.

18 And the holy ones of the Most High will receive the kingdom, and they will possess the kingdom for ever and ever.

19 Then I looked again at the fourth beast, which was different from all beasts, having iron teeth and nails of brass; it was devouring, breaking in pieces, and trampling the rest with its feet.

20 And concerning the ten horns that were on its head, and the other which came up, and before whom three fell; even of that horn that had eyes, and a mouth that spoke great things, whose look was more stout than its fellows.

21 I saw, and that horn made war with the holy ones, and prevailed against them.

22 Until the ancient of days came, and judgement was given to the holy ones on high, and the season arrived, and the kingdom was granted to the holy ones.

23 Thus said the fourth beast: kingdom shall be upon earth, which shall differ from all kingdoms, and shall devour all the earth, and tread it down, and break it in pieces.

24 And the ten horns are ten kings who will arise, and another will arise after them, and he will be different from the earlier ones, and three kings he will subdue.

25 And he shall speak great words against the Most High, and shall wear out the holy ones of the Most High; and he shall think to change times and laws, and they shall be given into his hand until a time, and times, and the dividing of time.

26 And judgement shall be set, and his dominion shall be taken away to destruction and ruin until the end.

27 And the kingdom and dominion, and the greatness of the kingdoms under all heaven, was given to the people of the saints of the Most High; their kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey him.

28 Until the end of this matter the thing troubled me, Daniel; my thoughts alarmed me, and my countenance changed, but the matter remained in my heart.


## Chapter 8

1 In the third year of the reign of King Belshazzar, a vision appeared to me, I Daniel, after the vision appeared to me at the beginning.

2 And I saw in a vision, and it came to pass when I saw, I was in Shushan the palace, which is in Elam the province; and I saw in a vision, and I was by the river Ulai.

3 I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, a ram standing before the river, and it had two horns, and the horns were high, one higher than the other, and the higher one was growing last.

4 I saw the ram pushing westward and northward and southward, and no living creatures could stand before it, and there was no one to deliver from its hand, and it did according to its will and became great.

5 And I was observing, and behold, a he-goat came from the west over the face of the whole earth, and it did not touch the ground; and the he-goat had a notable horn between its eyes.

6 And he came to the ram, the lord of the horns, which I had seen standing before the river, and he ran toward him in the fury of his power.

7 And I saw him approaching the ram, and he was enraged at him, and struck the ram and broke his two horns; and there was no strength in the ram to stand before him; and he cast him to the ground and trampled him, and there was no one to deliver the ram from his hand.

8 And the male goat grew very great, but when he became strong, the great horn was broken, and four notable ones arose under it toward the four sides of heaven.

9 And from one of them came forth one horn, small at first, and it grew exceedingly toward the south and toward the east and toward the beautiful.

10 And it grew as far as the host of heaven, and it cast down to the earth the host and the stars and trampled them.

11 And until the captain of the host was magnified, and the daily sacrifice was taken away, and the foundation of his sanctuary was cast down.

12 An appointed time shall be given upon the daily offering because of transgression, and truth shall be cast down to the earth, and it shall act and succeed.

13 And I heard one holy one speaking, and another holy one said to the one speaking to him, “Until when will the vision of the alway, and the rebellion causing desolation, be given, so that the consecrated thing and the host are trodden under foot?”

14 He said to me, Until evening, morning, two thousand and three hundred; then the sanctuary will be vindicated.

15 And I, Daniel, saw the vision and sought knowledge, and behold, there stood before me one in the appearance of a man.

16 And I heard a human voice between the banks of Ulai, and he called and said, "Gabriel, make this one understand the vision."

17 I came near to where he stood, and when he came, I was afraid and fell on my face; and he said to me, Understand, son of man, for the vision is for an end time.

18 While he was speaking with me, I fell on my face to the ground, and he touched me and made me stand on my feet.

19 Behold, I will inform you of what will be at the latter end of the indignation, for at the appointed time there is an end.

20 The ram that you saw, the lord of the horns, is the kings of the Medes and Persia.

21 And the shaggy he-goat is the king of Javan, and the large horn between its eyes is the first king.

22 And the broken one, four of its supports stood up; four empires will arise from a nation, but not by its power.

23 And in the latter end of their kingdom, when the transgressors have reached their full measure, a king of fierce countenance and understanding dark sayings shall arise.

24 And he shall grow strong but not by his own power, and he shall cause wonders to perish and shall prosper and succeed, and shall destroy the mighty and the holy people.

25 And by his discretion he shall cause deceit to prosper in his hand, and in his heart he shall magnify himself, and by prosperity he shall destroy many; and against the prince of princes he shall stand, but without hand he shall be broken.

26 The vision of the evening and the morning that was spoken of is true, but seal up the vision, for it pertains to many days.

27 And I, Daniel, was weakened and sick for days; then I arose and attended to the king's business, and I was appalled by the vision, but there was no one who understood it.


## Chapter 9

1 In the first year of Darius son of Ahasuerus, of the seed of the Medes, who reigned over the kingdom of the Chaldeans:

2 In the first year of his reign, I, Daniel, was studying the books, discerning the number of years that the word of the LORD had come to Jeremiah the prophet, for the completion of the desolations of Jerusalem seventy years.

3 I set my face to the LORD my God to seek prayer and intreaties by fasting and sackcloth and ashes.

4 And I prayed to the LORD my God and confessed, saying, "Please, my Lord, the great and awesome God, who keeps the covenant and steadfast love for those who love Him and keep His commandments."

5 We have sinned, and have committed iniquity, and have done wickedly, and have rebelled, even by departing from your precepts and from your judgments.

6 And we did not listen to your servant prophets who spoke in your name to our kings, our rulers, our ancestors, and to all the people of the land.

7 To you, O Lord, belongs the justice, but to us the shame of face, as it is this day, to the men of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to all Israel, both near and far, in all the lands where you have driven them, because of their transgression that they committed against you.

8 Jehovah, to us belongs the shame of face for our kings, for our rulers, and for our forefathers, because we have sinned against you.

9 To (my) Lord our God belong the compassion and the forgiveness, for we have rebelled against him.

10 We did not listen to the voice of the LORD our God, to walk in the laws which he set before us by the hand of his servants the prophets.

11 And all Israel have transgressed thy law, and turned aside so as not to hearken to thy voice; and thou hast poured upon us the curse and the oath which are written in the law of Moses the servant of God, because we have sinned against him.

12 He established the word that he spoke against us and against our judges who judged us, to bring upon us great adversity, such as had not been done under all the heavens as was done in Jerusalem.

13 As it is written in the law of Moses, all this adversity has come upon us, yet we have not entreated the face of the LORD our God to turn from our iniquity and to act wisely in your truth.

14 And the LORD watched over the adversity and brought it upon us, for righteous is the LORD our God in all His deeds that He has done, but we did not listen to His voice.

15 And now, (my) Lord, God (gods) our, who brought your folk out from land Egypt by force strong, and made for yourself renown as at this day, we have sinned, we have done wickedly.

16 My Lord, according to all Your righteousness, let Your anger and Your wrath turn back from Your city Jerusalem, Your holy mountain; for because of our sins and the iniquities of our fathers, Jerusalem and Your people have become a reproach to all who are around us.

17 And now, hear, O our God, the prayer of Your servant and his supplication, and cause Your face to shine upon Your desolate sanctuary for the sake of the Lord.

18 Turn Your ear, O God, and hear; open Your eyes and see our desolations and the city which is called by Your name; for we do not present our supplications before You on account of our righteous acts, but because of Your great mercies.

19 (my) Lord, hear (my) Lord, forgive (my) Lord, listen and act; do not delay for your sake, God, because your name is called upon your city and upon your people.

20 And while I was still speaking and praying and confessing my sin and the sin of my people Israel and presenting my supplication before the LORD my God for the holy mountain of my God,

21 While I was still speaking in prayer, the man Gabriel, whom I had seen in the vision at the beginning, swiftly flew and touched me at the time of the evening offering.

22 And he came and spoke with me and said, Daniel, now I have come out to give you understanding.

23 At the beginning of your supplications the word went out, and I have come to tell you, for you are greatly beloved; so understand the word and consider the vision.

24 Seventy weeks are decreed upon your people and upon your holy city, in order to finish the transgression and to make purification for iniquity, and to bring in everlasting righteousness, and to seal up vision and prophecy, and to anoint the most holy.

25 And you shall know and understand from the going forth of the word to restore and rebuild Jerusalem until the anointed prince: seven weeks, and sixty-two weeks; and it shall be restored and rebuilt, the broad place and the wall, even in troublous times.

26 After the weeks of sixty-two, the anointed shall be cut off and have nothing, and the people of the coming prince shall destroy the city and the consecrated, and its end shall come with a flood, and until the end war is decreed upon desolations.

27 And he shall confirm a covenant with many for one week, and in the middle of the week he shall cause sacrifice and offering to cease, and upon the wing of abominable filth shall be the desolator, and until the consummation and what is determined is poured out upon the desolator.


## Chapter 10

1 In the third year of Cyrus king of Persia, a matter was revealed to Daniel who was called by the name Belteshazzar—this matter was true and a great host, and understanding of the matter was given to him in the vision.

2 In those days I, Daniel, was mourning for three weeks of days.

3 I did not eat choice bread, nor did flesh or wine enter my mouth, nor did I anoint myself, until three weeks of days were completed.

4 And on the twenty-fourth day of the first month, I was by the great river, that is Hiddekel.

5 And I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, a man clothed in linen, and his loins were girded with pure gold from Uphaz.

6 His corpse was like beryl, his face like the appearance of lightning, his eyes like torches of fire, his arms and his feet like the gleam of burnished brass, and the sound of his words like the sound of a multitude.

7 And I, Daniel, alone saw the vision, and the men who were with me did not see the vision, but a great fear fell upon them, and they fled to hide themselves.

8 And I alone remained, and I saw this great vision, and no strength remained in me; and my glory was turned upon me to destruction, and I retained no strength.

9 And I heard the sound of his words, and when I heard the sound of his words, I was lying prostrate with my face to the ground.

10 And behold, a hand touched me, and it moved me upon my knees and the palms of my hands.

11 Then he said to me, Daniel, you greatly beloved man: understand the words that I am speaking to you, and stand on your standing, for now I have been sent to you. And while he was speaking to me this word, I stood trembling.

12 Do not fear, Daniel, for from the first day that you set your heart to understand and to humble yourself before your God, your words were heard, and I have come because of your words.

13 And a captain of the kingdom of Persia withstood me twenty-one days, and behold, Michael, one of the chief captains, came to help me, and I remained there beside the kings of Persia.

14 And I came to make you understand the vision that will yet happen to your folk in the latter end of the days, for the vision is yet for days.

15 And while he was speaking with me according to these words, I turned my face to the ground and was speechless.

16 And behold, a fashion like a son of man touching upon my lips; so I opened my mouth and spoke, and said to the one standing before me, my lord, because of the vision my sorrows are turned upon me, and I have not retained strength.

17 How can this servant of my lord speak with this lord of mine, and now no strength remains in me, nor breath is left in me.

18 And again he touched me and strengthened me as a man strengthens.

19 Do not fear, O man of great delight, peace be to you; strengthen yourself and be strong. And when he spoke to me, I grew strong, and I said, “Let my lord speak, for you have strengthened me.”

20 And he said, Do you know why I have come to you? And now I will return to fight with the captain of Persia; and I am going forth, and behold, the prince of Javan is coming.

21 But I will tell you what is inscribed in the true scripture: there is no one who strengthens himself with me against these, except Michael your prince.


## Chapter 11

1 And I, in the first year of Darius the Mede, arose to support and strengthen him.

2 And now, assuredly, I will tell you: indeed, there are still three kings standing before Persia, and the fourth will enrich himself with great riches more than all; and according to his strength through his riches, he will stir up all against the kingdom of Javan.

3 And a king, strong, shall stand, and rule from dominion, great, and do according to his own will.

4 When he stands up, his kingdom will be broken and shattered toward the four winds of the heavens, not for his posterity nor according to his dominion which he ruled, for his kingdom will be torn away and given to others besides these.

5 And the king of the south shall strengthen, and one of his captains; and he shall strengthen himself and rule, with great dominion from his rule.

6 At the end of years they will join together, and the daughter of the king of the south will come to the king of the north to make agreements, but the strength of the arm will not be held, and his own arm will not stand; she and her attendants and the one who fathered her will be given over at the appointed times.

7 And a branch shall stand from its root, its base like him; and he shall come to the army and come into the stronghold of the king of the north, and shall act against them and prevail.

8 And even their gods with their drink offerings and with their precious vessels of silver and gold he will take as captives to Egypt, and he will stand for years against the king of the north.

9 And he entered the realm of the king of the south, and returned to his own land.

10 And his sons shall be stirred up, and shall assemble a multitude of great forces, which shall come, and overflow, and pass through; then shall he return, and be stirred up, even to his fortress.

11 Then the king of the south shall be moved with anger, and shall come forth and fight with him, even with the king of the north; and he shall set forth a great multitude, but the multitude shall be given into his hand.

12 And when the multitude is taken away, his heart shall be lifted up; he shall cast down tens of thousands, but he shall not prevail.

13 Then the king of the north will return and muster a great multitude greater than the former, and after an appointed time of years he will come with a large army and with great wealth.

14 In those times many will rise against the king of the south, and the sons of the violent among your people will lift themselves up to establish the vision, but they will stumble.

15 And the king of the north shall come and cast up a bank and take a fortified city, and the arms of the south shall not stand, nor the people of his chosen ones, and there shall be no power to withstand.

16 And he who comes to him does as he desires, and there is none who stands before him, and he stands in the land of the beautiful, and all is accomplished by his hand.

17 He will set his face to come with all the strength of his kingdom, and deal uprightly with him and do; but a daughter of women he will give to him to destroy her, and she will not stand and will not be for him.

18 Then he shall turn his face to the coastlands and shall capture many; but a commander shall make his reproach cease for him, and shall turn his reproach back upon him.

19 And he will turn his face toward the fortresses of his land, and stumble and fall, and not be found.

20 And he shall stand on his base, passing over the glory of the realm, and within a few days he shall be broken, neither in anger nor in battle.

21 And he stood on his base despised, and they did not give to him the honour of kingship, but he came peaceably and seized the kingdom by flattery.

22 And the arms of the flood shall be swept away and broken before him, even the prince of the covenant.

23 And from an alliance with him he will act with deceit, and will rise and grow strong with a very small people.

24 With abundance and in the fat of every province he shall come, and he shall do what his fathers and the fathers of his fathers have not done: spoil and prey and riches he shall scatter for them; and against fortresses he shall devise his devices until a time.

25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army, and the king of the south shall be stirred up to battle with a very great and mighty army, but he shall not stand because they shall devise thoughts against him.

26 And those who eat a portion of meat will break it, and his army will sweep it away, and many slain will fall.

27 And the two of them, the kings, bethink themselves to do mischief; at one table they speak lying; but it shall not prosper, for there is yet an end unto the appointed time.

28 And he will return to his land with great wealth, and his heart will be upon the holy covenant, and he will act and return to his land.

29 At the appointed time he will return and come to the south, but it will not be as it was formerly or afterward.

30 Then ships of the Chittim shall come and humble him; and he shall turn back and be enraged at the covenant of holiness, and do [so]; and he shall turn again and consider those who forsake the covenant of holiness.

31 And from him arms will arise, and they will profane the fortified sanctuary and remove the daily offering, and they will set up the abominable filth that causes desolation.

32 And the violators of the covenant will act deceitfully, but the people who know their God will gain strength and act.

33 And those who are wise among the people will make many understand, yet they will fall by sword and by flame, by captivity and by spoil, for a time.

34 When they stumble, they will be helped with almost no help, and many will join them with flattery.

35 And some of the wise shall stumble, to refine them, and to purify, and to make them white, until the time of the end; for it is yet toward the appointed season.

36 And he shall do according to his own will, and exalt and magnify himself above every god, and above the God of gods he shall speak wonders, and he shall prosper until the indignation is accomplished; for what is decreed shall be done.

37 And he shall not regard the gods of his fathers, nor the desire of women, nor any god, for he shall magnify himself above all.

38 And to the God of fortresses, on his base he will honor; and to the god whom their fathers did not know, he will honor with gold and with silver and with precious stone and with delights.

39 And he shall deal with the fortresses of a stronghold, making alliance with a foreign god, who shall greatly increase in glory; and he shall cause them to rule over the many, and shall divide the land for a price.

40 And at the time of the end, the king of the south shall push against him, and the king of the north shall storm against him with chariots, horsemen, and many ships; he shall invade lands and sweep through them like a flood.

41 He entered the land of beauty, and many fell, but these escaped from his hand: Edom and Moab and the chief of the sons of Ammon.

42 He shall stretch out his hand against the lands, but the land of Egypt shall not be a deliverance.

43 And he ruled over the treasures of gold and silver, and over all the precious things of Egypt, and the Libyans and Ethiopians were under his command.

44 And reports alarm him from the east and from the north, and he goes out in great wrath to destroy and to annihilate many.

45 And he will pitch the covering of his palace between the seas to the mount of the beauty of holiness, and he will come to his end, and there is no helper for him.


## Chapter 12

1 At that time Michael the great prince who stands for your people will arise, and there will be a time of distress such as has not occurred since nations came into being until that time; and at that time your people will escape, everyone found written in the book.

2 And many of those who sleep in the dust of the ground will awake, these to everlasting life, and those to everlasting shame and abhorring.

3 And those who are wise will shine with the brightness of the firmament, and those who justify the many will shine as the stars forever and ever.

4 But you, Daniel, shut up the words and seal the book until the time of the end; many will run to and fro, and knowledge will increase.

5 And I, Daniel, saw, and behold, two others were standing, one on the bank of the river and one on the bank of the river.

6 And he said to the man clothed in linen, who was above the waters of the river: How long shall be the end of the wonders?

7 And I heard the man clothed in linen, who was above the waters of the river, as he raised his right hand and his left to heaven and swore by the life of the age, that when a time, times, and a half is accomplished, all these things will be finished when the hand of the holy people is shattered.

8 And I heard but did not understand, and I said, "My lord, what is the end of these things?"

9 Go, Daniel, for the words are sealed and shut until the time of the end.

10 Many shall be purified and made white and refined, but the wicked shall act wickedly; and none of the wicked shall understand, but those who are wise shall understand.

11 And from the time that the daily sacrifice is taken away and the abominable filth is set up, one thousand two hundred and ninety days.

12 Blessed is the one who waits and arrives at the age of one thousand three hundred and thirty-five days.

13 But you go to the end and rest and rise for your lot at the end of the age.


# HOSEA

## Chapter 1

1 The word of the LORD that came to Hosea son of Beeri in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash, king of Israel.

2 The beginning of the LORD's speaking by Hosea, and the LORD said to Hosea, Go, take for yourself a wife of whoredom and children of whoredom, for the land commits whoredom from following the LORD.

3 And he went and took Gomer daughter of Diblaim, and she conceived and bore him a son.

4 And the LORD said to him, "Name him Jezreel, for yet a little while and I will punish the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and I will end the kingdom of the house of Israel."

5 And it shall come to pass on that day, that I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.

6 She conceived again and bore a daughter, and He said to him, Name her Lo-ruhamah, for I will no longer show mercy to the house of Israel, but I will utterly take them away.

7 I will show favor to the house of Judah and save them by the LORD their God, but I will not save them by bow, by sword, by war, by horses, or by horsemen.

8 And when she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived and bore a son.

9 And he said, Call his name Lo-ammi, for you are not my people, and I will not be your God.

10 And the number of the children of Israel will be as the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured or numbered, and instead of it being said to them, “You are not my people,” it will be said to them, “You are children of the living God.”

11 And the sons of Judah and the sons of Israel will gather together and set for themselves one chief and go up from the land, for great is the day of Jezreel.


## Chapter 2

1 Say to your brothers, "My people," and to your sisters, "My beloved."

2 Plead with your mother, plead, for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband; and let her put away her whoredom from before her face, and her adultery from between her breasts.

3 Lest I strip her naked and set her as on the day she was born, and make her like a wilderness, and set her like a dry land, and kill her with thirst.

4 And I will not have mercy on her children, for they are children of whoredom.

5 For their mother has played the whore; she who bore them is disgraced. For she said, “I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my linen, my oil and my drink.”

6 Therefore, behold, I will block your way with thorns, and I will fence in her wall, and her paths you will not find.

7 And she shall pursue her lovers but not overtake them, and she shall seek them but not find them, and she shall say, I will go and return to my former husband, for it was better for me then than now.

8 And she did not know that I was the one who gave her the corn, the wine, and the anointed oil, and multiplied silver and gold for her, which they used for Baal.

9 Therefore I will return and take my wheat in its season and my wine in its appointed time, and I will rescue my wool and my flax to cover her nakedness.

10 And now I will uncover her lewdness before the eyes of her lovers, and no man will deliver her from my hand.

11 And I will put an end to all her mirth, her feast, her new moon, and every sabbath, and all her appointed times.

12 I will make desolate her vine and her fig tree, of which she said, "These are my reward that my lovers have given me," and I will set them as a forest, and the beasts of the field will devour them.

13 I will punish her for the days of the Baalim, when she burned incense to them, adorned with her earrings and her jewels, and went after her lovers, but me she forgot, says the LORD.

14 Therefore, behold, I am enticing her, and I will lead her into the wilderness, and I will speak to her heart.

15 And I will give her the increase of her vineyards from there, and the Valley of Achor as an entrance to hope; and there she will respond as in the days of her youth, and as in the day she came up from the land of Egypt.

16 And it shall be at that day, saith the LORD, that you shall call me Ishi, and shall call me no more Baali.

17 And I will remove the names of the Baalim from their mouths, and they shall no longer be remembered by their name.

18 I will make a covenant with them in that day, with the wild beasts of the field, and with the birds of the heavens, and with the creeping things of the earth; and I will break the bow, and the sword, and war out of the land, and will cause them to lie down safely.

19 And I will betroth you to me alway in righteousness and in judgment and in mercy and in tender love.

20 And I will betroth you to me in faithfulness, and you shall know the LORD.

21 And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the LORD, I will answer the heavens, and they shall answer the earth.

22 And the land will answer the wheat and the sweet wine and the anointed oil, and they will answer Jezreel.

23 And I will sow her for Me in the land, and I will have compassion on her who had not obtained compassion, and I will say to those who were not My people, “You are My people,” and they will say, “You are my God.”


## Chapter 3

1 And the LORD said to me, “Go again, love a woman, beloved of a companion and an adulteress, as the love of the LORD toward the sons of Israel, though they turn to other gods and love flagons of wine.”

2 And I bought her for myself for fifteen silver coins and a homer and a half-homer of barley.

3 And I said to her, "You shall wait many days for me; do not play the harlot, and do not be another man's; and I will also be for you."

4 For many days the people of Israel have sat without a king and without a ruler, without sacrifice and without pillar, without ephod and without idols.

5 Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek the LORD their God and David their king, and they shall fear the LORD and His goodness in the latter days.


## Chapter 4

1 Hear the word of the LORD, O children of Israel, for the LORD has a controversy with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no truth and no kindness and no knowledge of God in the land.

2 They swear falsely, and commit murder, and steal, and commit adultery; they break out, and blood touches blood.

3 Therefore the land will mourn, and all who dwell in it will languish, along with the living creatures of the field and the birds of the heavens, and even the fish of the sea will be gathered up.

4 Only let no man contend or reprove another, for your people are like those who strive with a priest.

5 And you will stumble today, and the prophet will also stumble with you at night, and I will strike your mother.

6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge, because you have rejected knowledge, I will reject you from being priest to me, and you have forgotten the law of your God, I also will forget your children.

7 As their glory, so they have sinned against me; their glory is in ignominy.

8 The punishment of my people they shall eat, and for their iniquity their soul shall bear.

9 And the folk shall be as the priest, and I will punish him for his ways, and his doings I will repay to him.

10 They will eat and not be satisfied; they will commit adultery and not conceive, because they have forsaken Jehovah to keep.

11 Whoredom and wine and sweet wine take away the heart.

12 My folk ask counsel of their stick, and their staff declares to them; for a spirit of whoredom has led them astray, and they have played the whore beneath their gods.

13 On the tops of the mountains they sacrifice, and on the hills they burn incense, under oak, poplar, and elm, because good is its shade; therefore your daughters play the harlot, and your brides are violated.

14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore, nor your daughters-in-law when they commit adultery, for they themselves go aside with the prostitutes and sacrifice with the cult prostitutes, and a people without understanding will be ruined.

15 If Israel is committing harlotry, let not Judah become guilty; and do not go to Gilgal, nor go up to Beth-aven, nor swear, "As Jehovah the Lord lives."

16 For rebellious Israel is like a heifer; now the LORD will pasture them like a lamb in a broad place.

17 Ephraim is joined to idols; leave him alone.

18 Their drink is turned aside; they behave like drunkards; they love, they give, the ignominy of their shield.

19 Her spirit has oppressed her in her wings, and they are ashamed from their sacrifices.


## Chapter 5

1 Hear this, O priests, and give ear, O house of Israel, and give heed, O royal house; for judgment is against you, for you have been a snare to Mizpah and a net spread over Tabor.

2 They have slaughtered the revolters; I am the chastisement for them all.

3 I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; now Ephraim has committed harlotry, Israel is defiled.

4 Their works will not allow them to return to their God, for a spirit of whoredom is within them, and they do not know the LORD.

5 And Israel's pride will answer to his face, and Israel and Ephraim will stumble in their iniquity; Judah also has stumbled with them.

6 They will go with their flocks and with their herds to seek the LORD, but they will not find him; he has withdrawn from them.

7 They have betrayed the LORD, for they have borne alien children; now the month will devour their portions.

8 Blow the cornet at Gibeah, the trumpet at Ramah; cry aloud at Beth-aven, after you, Benjamin.

9 Ephraim shall be for desolation in the day of rebuke; in the tribes of Israel I have declared him faithful.

10 The captains of Judah were like those who remove a border; upon them I will pour out my wrath like water.

11 Ephraim oppresses, judgment is crushed, because he has willingly gone after a commandment.

12 But I am like a moth to Ephraim, and like rottenness to the house of Judah.

13 Ephraim saw his sickness and Judah saw his wound; Ephraim went to Assyria and sent to King Jareb, but he is not able to heal you nor to bind up from you the wound.

14 For I am like a (fierce) lion to Ephraim, and like a (young) lion to the house of Judah; I myself will tear and go away, I will carry off and there will be no deliverer.

15 I will go; I will return to my home, until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face; in their distress they will earnestly seek me.


## Chapter 6

1 Come, let us return to the LORD, for he has torn and he will heal us; he has struck and he will bind us up.

2 He will revive us from the days; on the third day he will raise us up, and we will live in his presence.

3 We shall know, let us press on to know Jehovah, as the morning light surely finds its going forth, and He shall come to us like the rain, like the latter rain watering the ground.

4 What shall I do to you, Ephraim? What shall I do to you, Judah? Your favour is like a cloud of morning and like the dew that goes away at morning.

5 Therefore I have appointed prophets, I have slain them with the words of my mouth, and my judgments go forth as light.

6 For I desire favour and not sacrifice, and knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.

7 But they transgressed the covenant like Adam; there they dealt faithlessly with me.

8 Gilead is a city of evildoers, stained with blood.

9 And as troops of robbers wait for a man, so the company of priests murder in the way by consent; for they commit lewdness.

10 In the house of Israel I have seen a horrible thing: there Ephraim commits whoredom; Israel is defiled.

11 Judah also has a harvest set for you when I restore the captivity of my people.


## Chapter 7

1 When I healed Israel, then the iniquity of Ephraim was revealed and the evils of Samaria, for they commit deceit; a thief comes in, a band of robbers ravages abroad.

2 They do not bethink themselves in their heart: I remember all their wickedness; now their deeds surround them; they are before my face.

3 They make the king rejoice in their wickedness, and the rulers in their lies.

4 They are all adulterers, like an oven heated by a baker, who ceases from stirring the fire from the time the dough is kneaded until it is leavened.

5 In the days of our king, the princes began their wrath from wine; he stretched out his hand toward the mockers.

6 For their heart is like an oven when they lie in ambush; all night their baker sleeps; in the morning it burns like a flaming fire.

7 They are all heated like an oven; they devour their judges; all their kings have fallen; there is no one among them who calls to me.

8 Ephraim among the peoples is mixed; Ephraim is a cake unturned.

9 Strangers ate his strength, and he did not know; even gray hairs have been scattered upon him, and he did not know.
