10 And the arrogancy of Israel shall answer to his face, and they shall not return to the LORD their God, nor seek him for all this.

11 And Ephraim became like a dove without heart; they called to Egypt, to Assyria they went.

12 As they go, I will spread my net over them like a bird of the heavens; I will bring them down; I will discipline them according to the report of their assembly.

13 Woe to them, for they have strayed from me; destruction to them, for they have transgressed against me. I will redeem them, but they have spoken lies about me.

14 They did not cry out to me with their heart, though they wail upon their beds for wheat and sweet wine; they turn aside from me.

15 But I disciplined, I strengthened their arms, and toward me they intend evil.

16 They return, but not upward; they have become like a deceitful bow. Their princes shall fall by the sword because of the rage of their tongue; this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.


## Chapter 8

1 Upon your mouth a trumpet like an eagle against the house of the LORD because they have transgressed my covenant and against my law they have rebelled.

2 To me they cry, "O God of Israel, we know you."

3 Israel has forsaken the good; the enemy pursues him.

4 They made kings without me, they appointed princes without my knowledge; their silver and their gold they used to make idols for themselves, so that they may be cut off.

5 Your bullock has rejected it, Samaria; my anger is kindled against them; how long will they not be able to achieve cleanness?

6 For Israel has made him, a craftsman, a smith; he is not God: indeed, the calf of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.

7 For they sow air, and reap a storm; the standing corn has no spring, lacking flour—though it produce, strangers will devour it.

8 Israel is swallowed up; now they are among the nations like a vessel in which there is no delight.

9 For they are gone up to Assyria, a wild ass alone by himself; Ephraim has hired lovers.

10 Even though they are given among the Gentiles, now I will gather them; they trembled slightly from the burden of the king, the rulers.

11 Because Ephraim multiplied altars for sinning, they became for him altars for sinning.

12 I will write to him the great things of my law; yet my law they counted as a stranger.

13 Their offerings they sacrifice flesh and eat; the LORD does not accept them; now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins; they shall return to Egypt.

14 Israel forgot its maker and built temples, and Judah multiplied fortified towns, but I will send fire upon its cities and it will consume its palaces.


## Chapter 9

1 Do not rejoice, Israel, with the joy of the peoples, for you have played the harlot from upon your God; you have loved the reward upon every threshing floor of wheat.

2 A threshing floor and winepress will not feed them, and the new wine will fail them.

3 They shall not dwell in the land of the LORD, and Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and in Assyria they shall eat unclean food.

4 They will not pour out wine for the LORD, nor will they offer sacrifices to him; their bread is like the bread of mourners to them; all who eat it are defiled, for it is bread for their own souls; it shall not come into the house of the LORD.

5 What will you do for the day of appointed assembly, and for the day of the feast of the LORD?

6 For behold, they have gone because of destruction; Egypt will gather them, Memphis will bury them; nettles shall possess the precious things of their silver, and thorns shall be in their tents.

7 The days of visitation have come, the days of recompense have come; Israel knows: the prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit is mad, because of the abundance of your iniquity, and great is the hatred.

8 Ephraim, with my God, watches; a prophet is a fowler's snare on all his ways, hatred in the house of his God.

9 They have deepened their corruption, as in the days of Gibeah; he will remember their iniquity, he will punish their sins.

10 Like (ripe) grapes in the wilderness I found Israel, like the firstripe (fruit) on a fig (tree) at its beginning I saw your fathers; they came to Baal-peor and were defiled by the shameful thing, and became detestable like those they loved.

11 Ephraim, like a bird, their glory fleeth—away from birth, and from the womb, and from conception.

12 But if their children grow, I will bereave them from man; indeed, woe to them when I turn away from them.

13 Ephraim, when I saw them, was like Tyre, a pleasant dwelling place; but Ephraim will bring forth his children to the murderer.

14 Give them, O LORD, what will You give them? Give them a womb of emptiness and breasts that shrivel.

15 All their evil at Gilgal, for there I hated them because of the wickedness of their deeds; from my house I will drive them out; I will no longer love them; all their princes are rebels.

16 Ephraim is struck; their root is dried up; they will produce no fruit; even when they bear children, I will slay the beloved of their womb.

17 God will reject them because they did not obey him, and they will wander among the nations.


## Chapter 10

1 A vine of Israel, he gave it fruit; according to the abundance of its fruit it increased altars; according to the goodness of its land it made excellent standing images.

2 Their heart shall be divided; now they shall bear guilt; he shall strike down their altars, destroy their pillars.

3 For now they will say, There is no king for us, for we have not feared Jehovah, and what will the king do for us?

4 They speak words of lying vanity, covenant-breaking; judgment has sprouted like poison from the furrows of the field.

5 The folk of Samaria shall tremble for the calf, even for Beth-aven; because his people mourn for him, and the Chemarims shall exult over him, for his glory is departed from him.

6 It also shall be carried to Assyria as a gift to King Jareb; Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.

7 Samaria is cut off, her king, like foam upon the face of the waters.

8 And they will be destroyed by the heights of evil, the sin of Israel; thorn and thistle will grow upon their altars, and they will say to the mountains, Cover us, and to the hills, Fall upon us.

9 From the days of Gibeah you have sinned, O Israel; there they stood. The battle in Gibeah against the sons of iniquity did not overtake them.

10 When I desire, I will chastise them; and peoples shall be gathered against them when they are bound for their two iniquities.

11 And Ephraim is a trained heifer, I loved her for threshing; but I passed over the good of her neck; I will yoke Ephraim; Judah shall plow; Jacob shall harrow for him.

12 Sow for yourselves justice, reap according to mercy, plow for yourselves fallow ground, for it is time to seek the LORD until he comes and rains righteousness upon you.

13 You devised iniquity, you worked unrighteousness; you reaped the reward of lies, because you trusted in your way, in the abundance of your warriors.

14 There will be a rushing tumult among your people, and all your fortresses will be destroyed, like the desolation of Shalman at Beth-Arbel in the day of battle—mother dashed to pieces with her children.

15 Thus Beth-el has dealt with you because of the evil of your deeds: at morning’s light the king of Israel is utterly destroyed.


## Chapter 11

1 When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.

2 Call to them; thus they go from their presence; to the Baalim they sacrifice, and to the carved images they burn incense.

3 But I healed Ephraim; I took them up in my arms, and they did not know that I healed them.

4 With the cords of a person I will draw them, by the bands of love; and I will be to them as one who lifts the yoke from their jaws, and I will gently lower to them to eat.

5 He will not return to the land of Egypt, and Assyria will be his king, because he refused to return.

6 And the sword will strike the city and consume its strength and devour their devices.

7 And my people are hung up for backsliding; to the Most High they cry out together, but he will not exalt them.

8 How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I surrender you, Israel? How can I hand you over like Admah? How can I make you like Zeboim? My care for you is overturned within me, my repenting is stirred together.

9 I will not execute the fury of my anger; I will not turn to destroy Ephraim, for I am God and not man, holy in your midst; I will not come into the city.

10 After the LORD they shall go, roaring like a young lion, for He will roar, and the sons from the sea shall tremble.

11 They shall flutter like a bird from Egypt and like a dove from the land of Assyria, and I will restore them to their homes, declares the LORD.

12 Ephraim surrounds me with lies, the house of Israel with deceit, and Judah still walks with God and with faithful holy ones.


## Chapter 12

1 Ephraim shepherds the wind and pursues the east wind all the day; he multiplies deceit and destruction; they make a covenant with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.

2 And the LORD has a controversy with Judah, and to punish Jacob according to his ways, according to his doings He will repay him.

3 In the belly he supplanted his brother, and in his strength he contended with God.

4 And he struggled with the ambassador and was overcome; he wept and pleaded with him; at Beth-el he will find him, and there he will speak with us.

5 The LORD God of hosts, the LORD is his name.

6 But you shall return to your God, keep kindness and justice, and wait for your God always.

7 A Canaanite merchant, in his hand false balances, he loves to oppress.

8 Then Ephraim said, "Surely I have acquired force for myself; I have found goods; all my labour will not find iniquity that is sin."

9 And I am the LORD your God from the land of Egypt; I will again cause you to dwell in tents as in the days of the appointed feast.

10 And I spoke against the prophets, and I multiplied vision; and by the hand of the prophets I delivered my message.

11 If Gilead is full of affliction, they have only brought forth vanity; in Gilgal they sacrificed bulls; indeed, their altars are like heaps upon the furrows of the field.

12 Jacob fled to the country of Aram, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept.

13 By a prophet the LORD brought Israel up from Egypt, and by a prophet he guarded them.

14 Ephraim has provoked with bitter things, and his bloodguiltiness shall be upon him; his Lord will return his reproach to him.


## Chapter 13

1 When Ephraim spoke, there was trembling; he exalted himself in Israel, but he sinned through Baal and died.

2 And now they again add to sin, making for themselves a molten image from their silver, according to their own skilfulness; all of it is the work of craftsmen. They say: "Let men who sacrifice bullocks kiss it."

3 Therefore they will be like a morning cloud and like dew that goes early, like chaff swirling from a threshing floor and like smoke from a chimney.

4 I am the LORD your God from the land of Egypt, and besides me you shall not know a god, and there is no savior besides me.

5 I knew you in the desert, in the land of great drought.

6 When they were fed, they became full; they were filled, and their heart lifted up; therefore they forgot me.

7 And I became to them like a (fierce) lion, like a leopard along the way of Assyria.

8 I will meet them like a bereaved bear and tear open the caul of their heart; there I will devour them like a lioness; the wild beast of the field will rend them.

9 You have destroyed yourself, Israel; but in me is your help.

10 Where now is your king, that he may save you in all your cities, and your judges of whom you said, Give me a king and rulers?

11 I gave you a king in my wrath, and I took him away in my anger.

12 The fault of Ephraim is stored up, his sin is hidden.

13 Pains like those of childbirth will come upon him; he is a son who lacks wisdom, for a time of breaking forth of children, he will not stand.

14 From the grave I will ransom them, from death I will redeem them; where is your plague, death? where is your destruction, grave? repentance shall be hidden from my eyes.

15 For he is among brothers he shall cause to multiply, there comes from the east a wind, the blast of the LORD from the wilderness ascending, and his fountain shall be ashamed and his spring shall be dried up; he shall lay waste the treasure of every precious vessel.

16 Samaria will incur guilt, because she has rebelled against her God; with the sword they will fall, her little ones will be dashed, and her pregnant women will be ripped open.


## Chapter 14

1 Return, Israel, to the LORD your God, for you have stumbled in your iniquity.

2 Take with you words and return to the LORD; say to him: "Forgive all iniquity and accept good, and we will repay the young bulls with our lips."

3 Assyria shall not save us; on horse we will not ride; and we will no longer say, "Our gods," to the work of our hands, in which the orphan is shown mercy.

4 I will heal their turning away, I will love them freely, for my anger has turned away from them.

5 I will be as the dew to Israel; he shall blossom as the lily, and his roots shall strike as Lebanon.

6 His (tender) branches spread out, and his beauty is like the olive tree, and his scent like Lebanon.

7 They return, the dwellers in its shade, they live on wheat and flourish like the vine; its memory is like wine of Lebanon.

8 Ephraim, what more to me with idols? I have answered and I will regard him; I am like a flourishing fir; from me your fruit is found.

9 Who is wise and will understand these things? Who is discerning and will know them? For the ways of the LORD are upright; the righteous will walk in them, but transgressors will stumble in them.


# JOEL

## Chapter 1

1 The word of the LORD that came to Joel son of Pethuel.

2 Hear this, elders, and give ear, all inhabitants of the land: has this happened in your days, or in the days of your fathers?

3 Tell it to your sons, and your sons to their sons, and their sons to another generation.

4 The remnant of the palmer-worm the locust hath left did eat, and the remnant of the locust the caterpillar hath left did eat, and the remnant of the caterpillar the cankerworm did eat.

5 Awake, you drunkards, and weep; and wail, all you who drink wine, on account of the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.

6 For a nation has come up against my land, mighty and without number; its teeth are the teeth of a lion, and it has the jaw teeth of a lioness.

7 He set my vine to desolation and my fig tree to bark; stripped it bare and cast it off; its branches turned white.

8 Mourn, O maid, girt with sackcloth for the husband of your youth.

9 The cessation of the grain offering and drink offering from the house of the LORD has caused the priests, the ministers of the LORD, to mourn.

10 The field is laid waste, the ground mourns, for the wheat is laid waste, the wine has dried up, the anointed oil languishes.

11 The husbandmen are ashamed, the vine dressers wail, for the wheat and for the barley; because the harvest of the field is perished.

12 The vine has withered and the fig tree has languished; pomegranate, palm, and apple tree—all the trees of the field have withered—because joy has dried up from the children of men.

13 Gird yourselves and lament, O priests; wail, you ministers of the altar; come, spend the night in sackcloth, you ministers of my God, for a grain offering and a drink offering are withheld from the house of your God.

14 Sanctify a fast, call a solemn assembly, gather all the inhabitants of the land into the house of the LORD your God, and cry out to the LORD.

15 Alas for the day, for near is the day of the LORD; like destruction from the Almighty, it comes.

16 Is not food cut off before our eyes, joy and gladness from the house of our God?

17 Their seed rots under their clods, their storehouses are devastated, their barns are destroyed, for the wheat has been put to shame.

18 How the cattle are in distress, the herds of beeves are perplexed, because there is no pasture for them; also the flocks of sheep are guilty.

19 To you, O LORD, I call, for fire has consumed the wilderness dwellings, and a flame has scorched every tree of the field.

20 Even the beasts of the field will long for you, for the water channels have dried up, and fire has consumed the pastures of the wilderness.


## Chapter 2

1 Blow the cornet in Zion and shout on the mount of the consecrated; let all inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of the LORD is coming; indeed, it is near.

2 A day of darkness and gloom, a day of cloud and thick darkness, like the dawn spreading over the mountains, a people vast and mighty; like him there has never been from ancient times, and after him there will be no more even to generation and generation.

3 Before him a fire devours, and behind him a flame blazes; the land before him is like the garden of Eden, and after him a desolate wilderness, and no deliverance is possible for him.

4 Its appearance is like the appearance of horses, and like horsemen so they run.

5 Like the sound of chariots on the tops of the mountains they leap, like the crackling of a flame devouring stubble, like a mighty people set in battle array.

6 From his presence the peoples tremble; every face gathers blackness.

7 Like warriors they charge, like soldiers they scale the wall; each advances along his own way and they do not turn aside from their paths.

8 And no man shall push his brother; each man shall walk in his path, and by the weapon they shall fall, they shall not wound.

9 In the city they will wail, on the wall they will run, into the houses they will climb, through the windows they will enter like a thief.

10 Before him the earth trembled, the heavens shook; the sun and moon grew dark, and the stars withdrew their brightness.

11 And the LORD has given His voice before His army, for His camp is very great, for mighty is He who performs His word; for great is the day of the LORD and exceedingly terrible—who can endure it?

12 And even now, saith the LORD, return ye unto me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with lamentation.

13 Tear your hearts and not your garments, and turn back to the LORD your God, for He is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in kindness, and relenting concerning the calamity.

14 Who knows if He will turn and relent and leave behind Him a blessing, a grain offering and a drink offering for the LORD your God?

15 Blow the cornet in Zion, sanctify a fast, proclaim a solemn assembly.

16 Gather the folk, sanctify the congregation, assemble the elders, gather the infants and the nursing ones from the breast; let the bridegroom go out from his chamber and the bride from her closet.

17 Between the porch and the altar let the priests who minister to the LORD weep and say: Spare your people, O LORD, and give not your heritage to reproach, so that the nations should say among the peoples, Where is their God?

18 And the LORD was jealous for his land and had pity on his people.

19 Then the LORD answered and said to his people, Behold, I am sending to you the wheat and the wine and the anointed oil, and you shall eat it fully, and I will no longer give you as a reproach among the nations.

20 And I will remove the northern army far from you and drive it into a parched and desolate land, its front toward the eastern sea and its rear toward the western sea; and its stench will rise, its ill savour will ascend, for it has magnified its task.

21 Do not fear, land; rejoice and be glad, for the LORD has done great things.

22 Do not fear the beasts of the field, for the pastures of the wilderness have become lush; the tree has borne its fruit, the fig tree and the vine have given their strength.

23 Children of Zion, rejoice and be glad in the LORD your God, for he has given you the early rain for righteousness, and he sends down to you rain, the early rain and the latter rain, as before.

24 And the threshing places were filled with wheat, and the winepresses overflowed with sweet wine and anointed oil.

25 I will repay you the years that the locust, the cankerworm, the caterpillar, and the palmer-worm—my great army which I sent among you—consumed.

26 You shall eat your fill and be satisfied, and you shall praise the name of the LORD your God who has done wonders with you, and my people shall never be put to shame.

27 And you shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and I am the LORD your God, and there is no other, and my people shall never be put to shame.

28 And afterward I will pour out my spirit on all flesh, and your sons and your daughters will prophesy, your elders will dream dreams, your young men will see visions.

29 And also on the bondmen and on the handmaidens in those days I will pour out my spirit.

30 And I will give signs in the heavens and on the earth: blood and fire and pillars of smoke.

31 The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the coming of the great and awesome day of the LORD.

32 And it shall come to pass that (in) all manner who call by name on the LORD shall be delivered, for in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be deliverance, as the LORD has said, and among the remnant whom the LORD calls.


## Chapter 3

1 For behold, in those days and at that time, when I restore the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem.

2 I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the Valley of Jehoshaphat, and I will enter into judgment with them there concerning my people and my inheritance Israel, whom they scattered among the nations, and divided my land.

3 And upon my folk they cast lots, and the child they gave for whoredom, and the girl they sold for wine and drank.

4 What are you to me, Tyre and Sidon, and all the coasts of Philistia? Will you repay me? If you repay me, swiftly I will return your recompense upon your own heads.

5 You took my silver and my gold, and carried my choicest treasures to your temples.

6 And the sons of Judah and the sons of Jerusalem sold them to the sons of the Grecians, in order to remove them far from their border.

7 Behold, I will stir them up from the country where you sold them, and I will return your recompense upon your own heads.

8 And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the Judahites, and they will sell them to the Sabeans, to a distant nation, for the LORD has spoken.

9 Proclaim this among the nations: sanctify war; rouse the warriors; let all men of battle draw near and come up.

10 Make your plowshares into swords and your pruning-hooks into spears; the weak shall say, I am strong.

11 Hasten and come, all the nations round about, and gather yourselves there; cause your mighty ones to come down, O LORD.

12 Let the Gentiles bestir themselves and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat, for there I will sit to judge all the nations round about.

13 Send the sickle, for the harvest is ripe; come, tread, for the winepress is full; the fats overflow, for their calamity is great.

14 Multitude, multitude in the valley of decision, for the day of the LORD is near in the valley of decision.

15 The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars gather in their light.

16 And the LORD roars from Zion and gives His voice from Jerusalem, and the heavens and the earth quake; but the LORD is a refuge for His people and a stronghold for the children of Israel.

17 You shall know that I am the LORD your God dwelling in Zion, my holy hill; Jerusalem shall be holy, and foreigners shall never again pass through her.

18 And it shall come to pass in that age, the mountains shall drop new wine, and the little hills shall flow with milk; and all the watercourses of Judah shall stream with water; and a spring shall come forth from the house of the LORD, and shall water the valley of Shittim.

19 Egypt will become a desolation and Edom a wilderness of desolation because of the violence against the sons of Judah, for they have shed innocent blood in their land.

20 Judah shall dwell alway, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.

21 And I will clear them of bloodguiltiness; I will not clear them. The LORD dwells in Zion.


# AMOS

## Chapter 1

1 The words of Amos, who was among the herdsmen of Tekoa, who saw concerning Israel in the days of Uzziah king of Judah and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash king of Israel, two years before the earthquake.

2 And the LORD said from Zion and from Jerusalem He will give His voice, and the pastures of the shepherds will mourn, and the top of Carmel will wither.

3 Thus says the LORD: "For three transgressions of Damascus and for four, I will not revoke it: for threshing Gilead with iron threshing instruments."

4 And I will send fire upon the house of Hazael, and it will consume the palaces of Benhadad.

5 I will break the bar of Damascus, and cut off the dweller from the valley of Aven, and him that holds the sceptre from the house of Eden; and the people of Aram shall go into exile to Kir, says the LORD.

6 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Gaza, and for four, I will not reverse it: because they carried away captives wholly to deliver them to Edom.

7 And I will send a fire against the wall of Gaza, and it will consume her palaces.

8 I will cut off the inhabitant from Ahdod and the one who holds the sceptre from Ashkelon, and I will turn my hand against Ekron, and the remnant of the Philistines shall perish, says the Lord God.

9 Thus says the LORD, for three transgressions of Tyre and for four I will not turn it back: because they delivered up the captives wholly to Edom and did not remember the covenant of brothers.

10 I will send fire against the wall of Tyre, and it will consume her palaces.

11 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Edom, and for four, I will not turn back: because he pursued his brother with the sword, and destroyed his compassion; his anger tore perpetually, and his wrath kept eternal.

12 I will send a fire into Teman, and it shall devour the palaces of Bozrah.

13 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of the sons of Ammon, and for four, I will not revoke it: because they ripped open the pregnant women of Gilead, to enlarge their border.

14 And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Rabbah, and it will consume her palaces, with an alarm in the day of battle, in a storm in the day of tempest.

15 And the king shall go into captivity, he and his rulers together, says the LORD.


## Chapter 2

1 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Moab, and for four, I will not turn back: for having burned the bones of the king of Edom to lime.

2 I will send a fire upon Moab, and it shall consume the palaces of Kerioth, and Moab shall die in the tumult, with an alarm at the sound of the trumpet.

3 I will cut off the ruler from among her, and all her princes I will destroy with him, says the LORD.

4 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Judah and for four I will not turn it back: because they have rejected the law of the LORD and have not kept his commandments, and their lies have led them astray after which their fathers walked.

5 I will send fire against Judah, and it will consume the palaces of Jerusalem.

6 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Israel, and for four, I will not reverse it: because of selling the righteous for silver, and the poor because of a pair of shoes.

7 Trampling on the dust of the earth on the heads of the poor, and turning aside the way of the meek, a man and his father go to the young woman, to profane my holy name.

8 And upon treacherous garments they cast beside every altar, and wine of the punished they drink in the temple of their gods.

9 And I destroyed the Amorite before them, whose height was the height of cedars and whose strength was the strength of oaks; and I destroyed his fruit from above and his roots beneath.

10 I brought you up from the land of Egypt and led you through the wilderness forty years to possess the land of the Amorite.

11 And I raised up prophets from your children and Nazarites from your young men; is it not indeed this, O children of Israel? says the LORD.

12 And you made the Nazarite drink wine, and you commanded the prophet, saying, You shall not prophesy.

13 Behold, I am pressing down beneath you, as a cart presses down when full of sheaves.

14 And flight shall perish from the swift, and the strong shall not retain his strength, nor shall the mighty deliver his life.

15 The archer will not stand, the swift of foot will not escape, and the rider of the horse will not save his life.

16 And he who is courageous among the mighty shall flee naked in that day, says the LORD.


## Chapter 3

1 Hear the act which the LORD has spoken concerning you, O children of Israel, against all the family which I brought up from the land of Egypt, saying:

2 You only have I known of all the families of the land; therefore I will punish you for all your faults.

3 Can two walk together unless they have agreed to meet?

4 Does a young lion roar in the forest when there is no prey? Does a young lion send out its voice from its den if it has not seized its prey?

5 Does a bird fall into a snare on the ground when there is no trap for it? Does a snare rise from the earth when it has caught nothing?

6 If a trumpet is blown in a city, will the people not tremble? If adversity comes upon a city, has not the LORD done it?

7 For (my) Lord God will do no act unless He reveals His secret counsel to His servant prophets.

8 A (young) lion roars—who will not fear? The LORD has spoken—who will not prophesy?

9 Proclaim upon the palaces in Ahdod and upon the palaces in the land of Egypt, and say: Assemble upon the hills of Samaria and see the tumults in her midst and the oppressed within her.

10 They know not how to do right, saith the LORD, they that store up violence and robbery in their palaces.

11 Therefore thus says the LORD God: An adversary all around the land will come, and from you he will bring down your strength, and your palaces will be plundered.

12 Thus says the LORD: As the shepherd rescues from the mouth of the lion two legs or a piece of an ear, so the children of Israel who dwell in Samaria shall be rescued, at the corner of a bed and on a couch in Damascus.

13 Hear, and testify in the house of Jacob, declares the Lord GOD of hosts:

14 For on the day I punish Israel for his transgression, I will also punish the altars of Beth-el; the horns of the altar will be cut off and fall to the ground.

15 I will strike the winter house along with the summer house; the ivory houses shall perish, and the many houses shall come to an end, says the LORD.


## Chapter 4

1 Hear this word, you cows of Bashan who are on the mountain of Samaria, who oppress the weak, crush the poor, and say to their masters, "Bring and let us drink."

2 The LORD God has sworn by his holiness: Surely, days are coming upon you when they will take you away with hooks, and your remnant with fishhooks.

3 And breaches shall go forth, each against her, and you will cast her into Harmon, says the LORD.

4 Come to Beth-el and transgress; to Gilgal multiply your transgression; and bring your sacrifices in the morning, your tithes every three days.

5 But offer a thanksgiving of unleavened bread, and proclaim free offerings; make them heard, for such is your love, O children of Israel, says the Lord God.

6 And I also gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities and lack of bread in all your places, yet you did not return to me, declares the LORD.

7 I also withheld the rain from you when there were yet three months to the harvest; I caused it to rain on one city but on another city I did not cause it to rain; one piece of land was rained upon while the piece of land on which I did not cause it to rain dried up.

8 And two or three towns stagger to one town for water, but are not satisfied; and you have not returned to me, declares the LORD.

9 I struck you with blasting and with the yellow blight; your gardens and your vineyards and your fig trees and your olive trees the palmer-worm devoured; yet you did not return to me, declares the LORD.

10 I sent pestilence among you along the way of Egypt; I struck down your young men with the sword, along with the captivity of your horses; and I caused the stink of your camps to rise up, even into your nostrils; yet you did not return to me, declares the LORD.

11 I have overthrown you as God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and you became like a firebrand plucked from a burning, yet you did not return to me, says the LORD.

12 Therefore, thus will I do to you, Israel: because this I will do to you, prepare to meet your God, Israel.

13 For behold, the one forming the hills and creating wind, revealing to a person his thought, making morning darkness and treading upon the heights of the earth—the LORD, God of hosts, is his name.


## Chapter 5

1 Hear this act which I bring against you, a lamentation, O house of Israel.

2 Fallen, she shall not rise again, the virgin of Israel; forsaken upon her land, there is none to raise her up.

3 For thus says the Lord God: the city that departs a thousand shall leave a hundred, and the one that departs a hundred shall leave ten to the house of Israel.

4 For thus says the LORD to the house of Israel: Seek me and live.

5 Do not seek Beth-el, and do not enter Gilgal, and do not pass through Beer-shebah, for Gilgal will surely be exiled, and Beth-el will become affliction.

6 Seek the LORD and live, lest he break out like fire against the house of Joseph, and it devour, with none to quench it in Beth-el.

7 They turn justice into wormwood and cast righteousness to the ground.

8 He who made the Pleiades and Orion, who turns the shadow of death into morning and makes day dark as night, who calls for the waters of the sea and pours them out upon the face of the land—the LORD is His name.

9 The one who kindles desolation against the strong will bring desolation upon the fortress.

10 They hate at the gate the one who reproves, and they abhor the one who speaks uprightly.

11 Therefore, because you have trampled upon the poor and taken a burden of grain from him, you have built houses of hewn stone, but you shall not dwell in them; vineyards of pleasantness you have planted, but you shall not drink their wine.

12 For I know your many rebellions and your mighty sins—oppressors of the righteous, taking bribes, and turning aside the needy at the gate.

13 Therefore the prudent will keep silent at that time, for it is an evil time.

14 Seek good and not evil, that you may live; and so the LORD, the God of hosts, will be with you, as you have said.

15 Hate evil and love good, and establish justice in the gate; perhaps the LORD God of hosts may be gracious to the remnant of Joseph.

16 Therefore thus says the LORD God of hosts, my Lord: In every broad place there is lamentation, and in every street they cry, "Alas! Alas!" And they call the husbandman to mourning, and those skilled in lamentation to wailing.

17 And in all vineyards there is lamentation, for I will pass through your midst, says the LORD.

18 Woe to those who desire the day of the LORD! Why would this be for you? The day of the LORD is darkness and not light.

19 As a man flees from the presence of the lion and meets the bear, and he comes into the house and leans his hand upon the wall, and the serpent bites him.

20 Is not the day of the LORD dark and not light, and gloomy and not bright?

21 I hate, I reject your (solemn) feast (day), and I do not smell with pleasure your (solemn) assembly (meeting).

22 But if you bring me ascent offerings and your gifts, I will not accept them; nor will I look upon the peace offering of your fat beasts.

23 Remove from me the noise of your songs, and I will not hear the melody of your psalteries.

24 Let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.

25 The offerings and oblation you brought me in the wilderness forty years, O house of Israel.

26 You shall take up the tabernacle of your Molech, and the image of your vain shew, the star of your god, whom you made for yourselves.

27 And I will exile you beyond Damascus, says the LORD, whose name is God of hosts.


## Chapter 6

1 Woe to those who are complacent in Zion and those who feel secure on the mount of Samaria, the noted among the chief of the nations, to whom the house of Israel comes.

2 Cross over to Calneh and see, and go from there to Hamath the great, and go down to Gath of the Philistines: are they better than these kingdoms? Or is their border greater than your border?

3 Those who prepare for a day of trouble, and bring near the seat of violence.

4 Those who lie on beds of ivory and lounge on couches, eating lambs from the flock and calves from the stall.

5 Those who chant to the sound of the psaltery, and like David invent for themselves instruments of song.

6 Those drinking wine from bowls and anointing themselves with the chief of oils, yet they are not grieved over the affliction of Joseph.

7 Therefore now they shall go into exile at the head of the captives, and the banquet of the dissolute shall pass away.

8 The Lord God has sworn by his very life, says the LORD, the God of hosts: I abhor the pride of Jacob, and his palaces I hate; so I will deliver up the city and all that is in it.

9 And it shall come to pass, if ten men are left in one house, they shall die.

10 And his well-beloved shall lift him up and burn him to remove bones from the house, and say to the one at the sides of the house, “Is there yet any with you?” and he shall say, “No,” and he shall say, “Hush, for one must not invoke the name of the LORD.”

11 Indeed, look, the LORD commands, and He will strike the great house with drops, and the small house with clefts.

12 Do horses delight in (ragged) rock? Does one plow with beeves? For you have turned justice into gall and the reward of righteousness into wormwood.

13 Those who rejoice for no reason, who say, “Have we not by our own strength taken for ourselves horns?”

14 For behold I am raising up against you, O house of Israel, declares the LORD, the God of hosts, a nation; and they shall oppress you from the entrance of Hamath to the valley of the Arabah.


## Chapter 7

1 Thus the LORD God showed me: behold, he formed locusts at the beginning of the latter growth, after the king's mowings.

2 And it shall come to pass, when the grass of the field has ended, that I will say, O Lord God, forgive, I pray; how shall Jacob stand? for he is small.

3 The LORD relented concerning this; “It shall not be,” said the LORD.

4 Thus the LORD God showed me, and behold, the Lord GOD called to contend by fire; it consumed the great deep and devoured the portion.

5 And I said, "(My) Lord God, cease, I beg You; how can Jacob stand? For he is small."

6 The LORD relented concerning this; it shall not be, said my Lord Jehovah.

7 Thus He showed me: and behold, (my) Lord was standing beside a wall with a plumb-line in His hand.

8 And Jehovah said to Amos, What do you see? And I said, A plumb-line. And the Lord said, Behold, I am setting a plumb-line among my people Israel; I will not pass by them again.

9 And the high places of Isaac shall be desolate, and the sanctuaries of Israel shall be laid waste, and I will rise against the house of Jeroboam with the sword.

10 Amaziah the priest of Beth-el sent to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying, Amos has conspired against you in the midst of the house of Israel; the land is not able to bear all his words.

11 For thus Amos said: "By the sword shall Jeroboam die, and Israel shall surely be exiled from his land."

12 Amaziah said to Amos, "Prophet, go, flee to the land of Judah, and there eat bread and there prophesy."

13 Beth-el shall no longer continue to prophesy, for it is a sanctuary of a king and a court of a kingdom.

14 Amos answered and said to Amaziah, I am not a prophet, neither am I a son of a prophet; for I am a herdman, and a dresser of sycamore trees.

15 And the LORD took me from behind the sheep, and the LORD said to me, Go, prophesy to my people Israel.

16 Now therefore hear the word of the LORD: you say, You shall not prophesy against Israel, neither shall you drop (your message) against the house of Isaac.

17 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Your wife shall play the whore in the city, and your sons and your daughters shall fall by the sword; your land shall be divided by the measuring line; and you yourself shall die upon an unclean land; and Israel shall surely be exiled from his land.


## Chapter 8

1 Thus the LORD showed me, and behold, a basket of summer fruit.

2 And he said, What do you see, Amos? And I said, A basket of summer fruit. Then the LORD said to me, The end has come upon my people Israel; I will not again pass by them.

3 And the songs of the temple shall become wailing on that day, says the Lord GOD: many a corpse in every place, cast forth hush.

4 Hear this, you who trample the needy and bring the poor of the land to ruin.

5 saying, When will the new moon be gone, that we may sell corn? and the sabbath, that we may open the wheat [for sale], to make the ephah small and to make the shekel great, and to falsify the balances of deceit?

6 That we may buy the poor for silver and the needy for a pair of shoes, and sell the refuse of the wheat.

7 The LORD has sworn by the excellency of Jacob: Surely I will never forget any of their deeds.

8 Because of this, should not the land tremble and mourn, all who dwell in it? And it shall rise up altogether like the river, and be cast out, like the river of Egypt.

9 And it shall come to pass in that day, says the Lord GOD, that I will bring down the sun at midday and will cause darkness upon the land in the day of light.

10 I will turn your (solemn) feast (days) into mourning and all your songs into lamentation, and I will bring up sackcloth upon every pair of loins and baldness upon every head; and I will make it like mourning for an only child, and its (latter) end like a day of bitterness.

11 Behold, days are coming, declares the Lord God, and I will send famine upon the land: not famine for bread, nor thirst for water, but for hearing the words of the LORD.

12 They will wander from sea to sea and from north to east, roaming to seek the word of the LORD, but they will not find it.

13 On that day the fair virgins and the chosen young men will faint from thirst.

14 Those who swear by the sin of Samaria and say, "As the life of your God, O Dan," and, "As the life of the way of Beer-shebah," will fall and never rise again.


## Chapter 9

1 I saw my Lord standing beside the altar, and He said, "Strike the upper lintel so that the doorposts shake, and shatter them on the heads of all of them; and I will slay their remnant with the sword; not one fugitive of theirs shall escape, nor shall any of them elude capture."

2 If they dig into the grave, from there my hand will take them; and if they ascend to heaven, from there I will bring them down.

3 And if they hide in the top of Carmel, from there I will search and take them; and if they hide from my presence in the bottom of the sea, from there I will command the serpent and it will bite them.

4 And if they are taken captive before their enemies, from there I will command the sword and it will kill them; and I will set my eye upon them for adversity and not for good.

5 The Lord God of hosts touches the land and it dissolves; all who dwell in it mourn. Its entirety rises like the River and subsides like the River of Egypt.

6 The one who builds his upper chambers in the air, and establishes his troop upon the earth, who calls for the waters of the sea and pours them out upon the face of the land—the LORD is his name.

7 Are you not like the Ethiopians to me, O children of Israel? declares the LORD. Did I not bring up Israel from the land of Egypt, and the Philistines from Caphtor, and the Syrians from Kir?

8 Behold, the eyes of the Lord Jehovah are upon the sinful kingdom, and I will destroy it from off the face of the land; but yet not utterly destroy the house of Jacob, saith Jehovah.

9 For behold I am commanding, and I will shake the house of Israel among all the nations, as grain is shaken in a sieve, and not a pebble of the earth shall fall.

10 With the sword they shall die, all the sinners of My people who say, "Do not let evil come near us or confront us."

11 On that day I will raise up the fallen booth of David and repair its breaches, and its ruins I will rebuild, and I will restore it as in days of old.

12 that they may possess the remnant of Edom and all the nations, upon whom my name is called, saith the LORD who doeth this.

13 Behold, days are coming, declares the LORD, when the plowman shall overtake the reaper and the treader of grapes the one who sows seed; and the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and all the hills shall melt.

14 And I will restore the captivity of My people Israel, and they shall build cities that are waste and dwell therein, and plant vineyards and drink their wine, and make gardens and eat their fruit.

15 And I will plant them on their land, and they shall never again be uprooted from their land which I have given them, says the LORD your God.


# OBADIAH

## Chapter 1

1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord God to Edom: A report we have heard from the Lord, and a messenger is sent among the nations: Arise, and let us rise up against her for battle.

2 Behold, I have made you small among the nations; you are greatly despised.

3 Your pride has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, whose seat is high, who says in his heart, "Who will bring me down to the ground?"

4 If you soar like the eagle and set your nest among the stars, from there I will bring you down, saith the LORD.

5 If thieves came to you, if raiders by night—how are you ruined? Would they not steal (much as is) sufficient? If grape pickers came to you, would they not leave (gleaning) (of the) grapes?

6 How Esau was searched out, his hidden things laid bare.

7 All the men of your covenant have sent you to the border; the men at peace with you have deceived you and prevailed against you; those who eat your bread have set a trap under you. There is no understanding in him.

8 Is it not in that day, says the LORD, that I will destroy wisdom from Edom and understanding from the mountain of Esau?

9 And your mighty men shall be dismayed, O Teman, so that every man may be cut off from the mount of Esau by slaughter.

10 From the violence against your brother Jacob, shame will cover you, and you will be cut off forever.

11 In the day that you stood aloof, in the day that foreigners carried away his wealth, and strangers entered his gates and cast lots for Jerusalem, even you were like one of them.

12 Do not look upon your brother's day of distress with joy, and do not rejoice over the children of Judah in the day of their calamity, and do not enlarge your mouth in the day of trouble.

13 Do not enter the gate of my people in the day of their calamity, do not look on their adversity in the day of their destruction, and do not seize their strength in the day of their calamity.

14 Do not stand at the crossway to cut off his fugitives, and do not betray his remnant in the day of distress.

15 For near is the day of the LORD upon all the nations; as you have done, it shall be done to you; your recompense shall return upon your own head.

16 For as you drank upon my holy hill, so shall all the nations drink continually, and they shall drink and stagger and be as though they had never been.

17 And on Mount Zion there shall be deliverance, and it shall be holy, and the house of Jacob shall inherit their possessions.

18 And the house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau stubble; and they shall kindle upon them and consume them, and there shall be no remnant to the house of Esau, for the LORD has spoken.

19 The south shall inherit Mount Esau, and the low country shall possess the Philistines; and they shall inherit the field of Ephraim and the field of Samaria; and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.

20 And the captives of this army, the Israelites who are Canaanites as far as Zarephath, and the captives of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad, shall inherit the cities of the south.

21 And deliverers shall ascend the mount of Zion to judge the mount of Esau, and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.


# JONAH

## Chapter 1

1 The act of the LORD came to Jonah son of Amittai, saying:

2 Arise, go to Nineveh the great city and proclaim against her, for their evil has risen before me.

3 Jonah arose to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD, and he went down to Joppa and found a ship going to Tarshish; he paid its hire and went down into it to go with them to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD.

4 And the LORD cast a great wind upon the sea, and there was a mighty storm on the sea, and the ship was like to be broken.

5 The mariners feared, and each cried out to his gods; they cast the vessels that were in the ship into the sea to lighten it from them, but Jonah went down to the quarters of the ship, lay down, and fell sound asleep.

6 The shipmaster approached him and said to him, "How can you be soundly asleep? Get up, call to your God! Perhaps God will take thought for us so that we do not perish."

7 And they said, each to his fellow, "Come, let us cast lots, that we may know on whose account this adversity has come upon us." So they cast lots, and the lot fell upon Jonah.

8 And they said to him, “Please tell us, against whom is this adversity coming upon us? What is your occupation, and where do you come from? What is your land, and from what people are you?”

9 He said to them, I am a Hebrew, and I fear Jehovah, God of the heavens, who made the sea and the dry land.

10 The men feared a great fear and said to him, "What is this that you have done?" For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the LORD, because he had told them.

11 And they said to him, "What shall we do to you so that the sea may become quiet from upon us?" for the sea was going and storming.

12 Then he said to them, “Lift me up and throw me into the sea, and the sea will quiet down from upon you; for I know that because of me this great storm is upon you.”

13 And the men dug to return to the dry land, but they could not, for the sea was going and raging against them.

14 And they cried to the LORD and said, "Please, O LORD, do not let us perish by the life of this man, and do not lay innocent blood upon us, for you, O LORD, have done as you pleased."

15 Then they lifted up Jonah and cast him into the sea, and the sea ceased from its raging.

16 And the men feared a great fear of the LORD, and they sacrificed a sacrifice to the LORD and vowed vows.

17 And Jehovah appointed a great fish to swallow Jonah, and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.


## Chapter 2

1 Jonah prayed to the LORD his God from the belly of the fish.

2 And he said, I cried from adversity to the LORD and he answered me; from the belly of Sheol I cried, you heard my voice.

3 And you cast me into the deep, in the heart of the seas, and a flood surrounds me; all your billows and your waves have passed over me.

4 But I said, "I am cast out from before your presence; yet I will look again toward your holy temple."

5 Water has surrounded me up to the soul; the deep encircles me; seaweed is wrapped around my head.

6 To the bottoms of the mountains I descended; the earth with its bars was against me forever, but You, LORD my God, brought up my life from the pit.

7 When my soul fainted within me I remembered the LORD, and my prayer came to You, to Your holy temple.

8 Those who guard vain lies shall forsake their mercy.

9 But as for me, with voice of thanksgiving I will sacrifice to you what I have vowed; deliverance belongs to the LORD.

10 And the LORD spoke to the fish, and it vomited Jonah onto the dry land.


## Chapter 3

1 And the act of the LORD came to Jonah a second time, saying:

2 Arise, go to Nineveh, the great city, and proclaim to it the preaching that I am speaking to you.

3 Jonah arose and went to Nineveh according to the word of the LORD, and Nineveh was a great city to God, a journey of three days.

4 Jonah began to enter the city, a journey of one day, and he cried out and said, "Yet forty days and Nineveh will be overthrown."

5 And the men of Nineveh believed in God and called for a fast and put on sackcloth, from their greatest to their least.

6 Then the act reached the king of Nineveh, and he rose from his throne and removed his robe from upon him and covered himself with sackcloth and sat in the ashes.

7 And he cried out and said, In Nineveh by the decree of the king and his great ones, saying, The man and the beast, the beeve and the flock, shall not taste any thing, they shall not feed, and water they shall not drink.

8 Let both man and beast be covered with sackcloth; and let them cry mightily to God, and let each turn from his evil way and from the violence that is in their hands.

9 Who knows? Perhaps God will turn and relent from his fierce wrath, and turn back from the anger of his face, and we will not perish.

10 And God saw their deeds, that they turned from their evil way; and God relented from the evil which He said He would do to them, and did not do it.


## Chapter 4

1 And it displeased Jonah greatly, and he was angry.

2 And he prayed to the LORD and said, "Please, Jehovah, is not this my word while I was still upon my land? Therefore I hastened to flee to Tarshish, for I knew that you are a gracious and merciful God, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, and relenting from calamity."

3 And now, O LORD, take, I pray, my life from me, for death is better to me than life.

4 And the LORD said, Was your wrath good for you?

5 Jonah went out from the city and sat down on the east side of the city, and he made a booth for himself there and sat under its shade until he should see what would become of the city.

6 And the LORD God appointed the gourd and it went up over Jonah to be a shade over his head to deliver him from his adversity, and Jonah rejoiced over the gourd with great joy.

7 And God appointed a worm when the morning dawned the next day, and it struck the gourd so that it withered.

8 When the sun rose, God appointed a scorching east wind; the sun beat upon Jonah’s head so that he fainted, and he requested his soul to die, saying, “Better is my death than my life.”

9 Then God said to Jonah, "Is it right for you to be angry about the gourd?" And he said, "It is right for me to be angry to the point of death."

10 And the LORD said, You have had pity on the gourd, which you did not labor over nor grow, which came into being in a night and in a night perished.

11 And I, should I not spare Nineveh the great city, in which there are more than one hundred and twenty thousand persons who do not know between their right hand and their left hand, and also much cattle?


# MICAH

## Chapter 1

1 The word of the LORD that came to Micah the Morashthite in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.

2 Hear, all peoples; listen, O earth and all that is in it: may the Lord God be witness against you from his holy temple.

3 For behold, the LORD is going out from his place, and will come down and tread upon the earth.

4 And the mountains shall melt under him, and the valleys shall split apart like wax before the fire, like waters poured down a steep place.

5 Because of Jacob's transgression all this has come, and because of Israel's sins; is not Jacob's transgression Samaria, and is not Judah's high place Jerusalem?

6 I will make Samaria a heap of the field, a planting of a vineyard; I will pour out her stones into the valley, and I will lay bare her foundation.

7 And all their carved images shall be cut down, and all their rewards shall be burned with fire, and all their idols I will make a desolation, because from the hire of a prostitute she gathered, and to the hire of a prostitute they shall return.

8 Therefore I will lament and wail; I will go stripped and naked; I will make lamentation like the dragons, and mourning like the daughters of the owl.

9 For I am wounded by her wounds; she has come to Judah, reached to the gate of my people, to Jerusalem.

10 Do not declare it in Gath; do not weep at all; in Beth-le-aphrah roll yourself in the dust.

11 Pass away, inhabitant of Saphir, quite naked, ashamed; the resident of Zaanan does not come forth for the lament of Beth-ezel; it takes from you its standing.

12 For the inhabitant of Maroth waited anxiously for good, because evil has come down from the LORD to the gate of Jerusalem.

13 Harness the chariot to the mule, inhabitant of Lachish; she is the chief sin of the daughter of Zion, for in you were found the transgressions of Israel.

14 Therefore you shall send back presents to Moresheth-gath; the houses of Achzib will be a lie to the kings of Israel.

15 Yet I will bring an heir to you, O inhabitant of Mareshah; the glory of Israel shall come to Adullam.

16 Shave and shear yourself for the children of your delight; enlarge your baldness like the eagle, for they are gone from you.


## Chapter 2

1 Woe to those who devise affliction and work evil upon their beds; by the light of morning they do it, for they have the power in their hand.

2 They will desire fields and seize them, and houses and take them; they will oppress a man and his household, and a person and his inheritance.

3 Therefore, thus says the LORD: Behold, I am planning adversity against this family, from which you will not free your necks, nor walk erect, for it is a time of calamity.

4 In that age they shall raise a proverb against you and utter a lamentation, saying, "We are utterly destroyed; the LORD has taken away my people's inheritance; how He removes it from me, returning our fields to others to divide."

5 Therefore you shall have no one casting a measuring line by lot in the assembly of the LORD.

6 Do not prophesy, they prophesy; they shall not prophesy to these, so that disgrace shall not depart.

7 Is it said, O house of Jacob, that the Spirit of the LORD is straitened? Are these His doings? Does not My word do good to him that walks uprightly?

8 But recently My people rose up as an enemy; from before the garment you stripped off the robe from those passing securely, returning from war.

9 The women of my people you drive out from their pleasant houses; from their little children you take away my glory forever.

10 Arise and go, for this is not the resting place; because of uncleanness it will destroy you, a painful destruction.

11 If a man walking in a spirit of falsehood were to prophesy to you, pouring out wine and strong drink, he would be the one pouring out this people.

12 I will surely gather Jacob all together; I will surely assemble the remnant of Israel; they shall be together like sheep in Bozrah, like a flock in the midst of its fold; they shall swarm from the presence of men.

13 The breaker has gone up before them; they have broken through and passed on, the gate they have gone out by; their king passes on before them, and the LORD at their head.


## Chapter 3

1 And I said, "Please listen, O chiefs of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel! Is it not for you to know justice?"

2 You who hate good and love evil, who strip their skin from off them and their flesh from off their bones.

3 And they ate the flesh of my people, and stripped their skin from them, and broke their bones, and scattered them like meat in a pot, and flesh in the midst of a caldron.

4 Then they will cry to the LORD, but he will not answer them; and he will hide his face from them at that time, because of their evil deeds.

5 Thus says the LORD concerning the prophets who mislead my people: those who bite with their teeth and cry "peace," but against the one who does not put into their mouths they sanctify war against him.

6 Therefore at night there will be darkness for you from the vision, and gloom for you from divination; the sun will set on the prophets, and the day will grow dark over them.

7 And the seers are shamed, the diviners are disgraced; they all cover the upper lip, because there is no answer from God.

8 But I have been filled with power, the spirit of the LORD, with judgment and might, to declare to Jacob his rebellion, and to Israel his sin.

9 Hear this now, O heads of the house of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel, who despise justice and pervert all that is right.

10 Building Zion with blood, and Jerusalem with iniquity.

11 Her leaders judge for a bribe, her priests teach for a price, and her prophets divine for money, yet they rely on the LORD, saying, Is not the LORD among us? No disaster will come upon us.

12 Therefore because of you, Zion will be plowed like a field, and Jerusalem will become a heap of ruins, and the temple mount will be high places of forest.


## Chapter 4

1 And it shall come to pass in the latter days that the mount of the house of the LORD shall be established as the chief of the mountains, and it shall be raised above the little hills, and peoples shall flow to it.

2 And many nations shall go and say, Come, let us go up to the mount of the LORD and to the house of the God of Jacob, and he will teach us from his ways and we will walk in his paths; for from Zion shall go forth law and the word of the LORD from Jerusalem.

3 And he shall judge between many nations and reprove mighty peoples afar off; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning-hooks; nation shall not lift up sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.

4 And each shall sit under his vine and under his fig tree, and none shall make afraid, for the mouth of the LORD of hosts has spoken.

5 For all the nations will walk each in the name of his god, but we will walk in the name of the LORD our God forever and ever.

6 In that age, says the LORD, I will gather the one who limps and collect the one driven away, and whom I have afflicted.

7 And I will set the lame as a remnant, and the outcast as a strong nation; and the LORD will reign over them on Mount Zion from now and forever.

8 And you, O tower of the flock, O stronghold of the daughter of Zion, to you shall come the former dominion, the kingdom of the daughter of Jerusalem.

9 Now why do you shout aloud, as in pain like a woman giving birth, though there is no king in you? Has your counselor perished?

10 Groan and writhe, daughter of Zion, like a woman in labor, for now you shall go out from the city and dwell in the country, and you shall come to Babylon; there you shall be rescued; there the LORD will redeem you from the hand of your enemies.

11 Now many Gentiles are gathered against you, saying, "Let us surely afflict Zion with our eyes."

12 But they do not know the purposes of the LORD, nor understand his counsel, for he has gathered them like a sheaf to the threshing floor.

13 Rise and thresh, daughter of Zion, for I will make your horn iron and your hooves I will make bronze, and you shall crush many peoples; and I will devote their gain to the LORD, and their riches to the Lord of all the earth.


## Chapter 5

1 Now you shall be struck, daughter of troops, siege is laid against us; with a rod they shall strike the jaw of the ruler of Israel.

2 But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, though you are small among the clans of Judah, from you shall come forth for Me one who is to be ruler in Israel, whose origins are from of old, from ancient times.

3 Therefore he will give them up until the time she who is in labor gives birth; then the rest of his brothers will return to the people of Israel.

4 And he shall stand and shepherd in the strength of the LORD, in the majesty of the name of the LORD his God; and they shall dwell, for now he shall be great to the uttermost parts of the land.

5 And this shall be peace: when Assyria comes into our land and tramples upon our palaces, then we will raise up against him seven shepherds and eight princes of men.

6 They will pasture the land of Assyria with the sword and the land of Nimrod at its entrances, and he will deliver us from Assyria when he enters our land and when he tramples within our borders.

7 And that had escaped of Jacob shall be in the midst of many peoples as dew from the LORD as showers upon grass, which waits not for man nor tarries for the sons of men.

8 And that had escaped of Jacob shall be among the nations in the midst of many peoples, as a young lion among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among flocks of sheep, who, when he passes through, both treads down and tears, and there is none to deliver.

9 Lift your hand against your enemies, and all your adversaries will be cut off.

10 And it shall come to pass on that day, says the LORD, that I will cut off your horses from your midst and destroy your chariots.

11 I will destroy the cities of your land, and I will tear down all your fortresses.

12 I will cut off sorcery from your hand, and there will be no more soothsayers for you.

13 I will cut off your carved images and your standing pillars from among you, and you shall no longer bow down to the works of your hands.

14 I will destroy your groves from among you and I will lay waste your cities.

15 And I will execute vengeance on the nations with anger and wrath, who did not listen.


## Chapter 6

1 Hear now what the LORD says: Arise, contend with the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.

2 Hear, O hills, the LORD's controversy, and you enduring foundations of the earth; for the LORD has a controversy with his people, and with Israel he will contend.

3 My folk, what have I done to you, and wherein have I wearied you? Answer me.

4 For I brought you up from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of bondage, and I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.

5 My folk, remember now what counsel Balak king of Moab devised, and what Balaam son of Beor answered him, from Shittim to Gilgal, so that you may know the righteous acts of the LORD.

6 With what shall I come before the LORD, bow before the high God? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with bullocks of a year old?

7 Will Jehovah be pleased with thousands of rams, with myriads of streams of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my transgression, the fruit of my womb for the sin of my soul?

8 He hath told thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee, but to do justice, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?

9 The voice of the LORD cries to the city, and wisdom shall see your name: Hear the rod, and who has appointed it.

10 Yet the woman of the house of wickedness stores up iniquity, and a measure of leanness is cursed.

11 Shall I carry iniquity in balances and a bag of deceitful stones?

12 whose rich are full of violence, and whose inhabitants speak lies, their tongue is deceit in their mouth.

13 Therefore I also have made you sick by striking you, making you desolate because of your sins.

14 You will eat and not be satisfied, and casting down will be among your inward parts; you will flee and not escape, and whoever escapes I will give to the sword.

15 You shall sow but not reap; you shall tread the olive but not anoint with oil; (new, sweet) wine but not drink wine.

16 And you shall keep the statutes of Omri and all the deeds of the house of Ahab, and you shall walk in their counsels, so that I may make you a desolation and your inhabitants a hissing; thus you shall bear the reproach of my people.


## Chapter 7

1 Woe is me, for I have become like the gathering of summer fruit, like the grapegleanings after the vintage; there is no cluster to eat, the firstripe fruit my soul desired.

2 The faithful have perished from the land, and there is no upright person among humankind; all of them lie in wait for blood, each one hunts his brother with a net.

3 Against the evil of the hands to do good, the ruler demands recompense, and the judge in recompense, and the great one speaks the iniquity of his soul—he exalts it.

4 The best of them is like a brier; the upright is worse than a thorn hedge. The day of your watchmen, your visitation, has come; now their perplexity has arrived.

5 Do not trust a companion, do not rely on a friend; from the one lying in the bosom of your embrace, guard the openings of your mouth.

6 For a son disgraces his father, a daughter rises against her mother, a bride against her mother-in-law; a man’s enemies are the men of his own household.

7 But I will wait for the LORD; I will put my hope in the God of my salvation; my God will hear me.

8 Do not rejoice, my enemy, over me, for though I have fallen, I will rise; though I sit in darkness, the LORD is light for me.

9 I will bear the indignation of the LORD because I have sinned against him, until he pleads my cause and executes judgment for me; he will bring me forth to the light; I shall see his justice.

10 May my enemy be seen and covered with shame, she who said to me, "Where is the LORD, your God?" My eyes will look upon her; now she will be trampled down like mud in the streets.

11 In the age of building your walls, that age shall remove far off the appointed decree.

12 An age is coming when he will enter from Assyria and the fortified cities, and from fortified places to the river, and from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain.

13 And the land will become desolate because of the deeds of its inhabitants.

14 See your folk with your staff, the flock, your inheritance; they dwell alone in the midst of Carmel; let them feed in Bashan and Gilead as in days of old.

15 As the days of your going out from the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders.

16 Let the Gentiles see and be ashamed of all their might; let them put a hand on the mouth, their ears become deaf.

17 They shall lick dust like a serpent, like crawling things of the earth they shall tremble from their holes; to the LORD our God they shall fear, and they shall be afraid of you.

18 Who is a God like you, bearing fault and passing over transgression for the remnant of his inheritance? He does not keep his anger forever, for he delights in kindness.

19 He will return, He will have compassion upon us; He will tread underfoot our iniquities; and He will cast into the depths of the sea all their sins.

20 Grant faithful assurance to Jacob and loving kindness to Abraham, as You swore to our ancestors in days of old.


# NAHUM

## Chapter 1

1 An oracle concerning Nineveh: the book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.

2 God, jealous and avenging is the LORD; the LORD is avenging and master of wrath; the LORD avenges His adversaries and keeps wrath for His enemies.

3 The LORD is slow to anger and great in power, and will by no means clear the guilty; the LORD has His way in the whirlwind and the storm, and the cloud is the dust of His feet.

4 He rebukes the sea and dries it up, and all the rivers he makes desolate; Bashan languishes and Carmel, and the flower of Lebanon languishes.

5 The hills trembled from his presence, and the little hills melted; the earth heaved before his face, and the habitable part and all who dwell in it.

6 Who can stand before his wrath, and who can endure in the fierceness of his anger? His anger burns like fire, and the rocks are shattered before him.

7 The LORD is good, a fortress in a day of trouble, and He knows those who take refuge in Him.

8 But with an overflowing flood He will make an utter end of her place, and He will pursue His enemies into darkness.

9 Why do you devise plans against the LORD? He will make a complete end; trouble will not rise up a second time.

10 For they are completely ensnared like thorns, and like their drink they are drunk; they are consumed like dry stubble.

11 From you comes one who devises evil against the Lord, a counsellor of Belial.

12 Thus says the LORD: Though they are complete and many, yet they will be cut off and pass away; and I have punished you, I will not punish you anymore.

13 And now I will break his bar from off you, and I will snap your bonds.

14 And the LORD has commanded concerning you: No more shall your name be sown. From the house of your gods I will cut off carved image and molten image; I will make your grave, for you are vile.

15 Behold, upon the mountains the feet of the herald announcing peace; celebrate your feasts, Judah, fulfill your vows, for never again shall the wicked pass through you, all of him is cut off.


## Chapter 2

1 The scatterer has come up before your face. Guard the fortress, watch the road, strengthen your loins, make your strength very great.

2 For the LORD has restored the excellency of Jacob, as the excellency of Israel, for they have emptied them out, and their vine branches they have destroyed.

3 The shield of his mighty men is red; the men of valor are clothed in scarlet. The chariots flash with fire in the day of his preparation, and the cypress spears are brandished.

4 Abroad the chariot shall exult, they shall rattle in the broad places; their appearance like the torch, like lightning they shall dart.

5 He remembers his nobles; they stumble in their walk; they hurry to its wall, and the defense is prepared.

6 The gates of the rivers were opened, and the palace trembled.

7 And the altar was established, the burnt offering was set up, and her maidservants were moaning like the voice of doves, beating upon their breasts.

8 Though Nineveh has been like a pool of water from her days, yet they flee away. Stand, stand, they cry, but no one turns back.

9 They have despoiled silver, they have despoiled gold; and there is no end to the fashion; glorious is the sum of all precious vessels.

10 Empty and void and ruined, a heart dismayed and knees smiting together, and much pain in all the loins, and the faces of all are gathered into blackness.

11 Where is the den of the lions and the feeding place for the young lions, where the lion and the old lion went, the whelp of the lion, and none to make afraid?

12 A young lion tears prey with its whelps and feeds the lioness; it fills its den with prey and its dwelling place with ravin.

13 Behold, I am against thee, saith the LORD of hosts, and I will burn in smoke thy chariot, and the sword shall devour thy young lions; and I will cut off thy prey from the land, and the voice of thy messengers shall no more be heard.


## Chapter 3

1 Woe to the bloody city, all of it full of lies and robbery; prey does not depart.

2 A sound of the whip and a sound of the rattling of the wheel, the horse rushing and the chariot bounding.

3 The horseman charges, the sword flashes, the spear glitters; there is a multitude of slain, a great heap of corpses, no end of bodies; they stumble over their bodies.

4 Because of the abundance of her whoredom, the well-favoured mistress of sorceries, who sells nations through her whoredom and families through her sorceries.

5 Behold, I am against thee, saith the LORD of hosts, and I will discover thy skirts upon thy face, and I will shew the nations thy nakedness, and the kingdoms thy shame.

6 And I will cast abominable filth upon you, and treat you as vile, and make you a spectacle.

7 And all your lovers will shun you, and say, "Nineveh is ravaged; who will mourn for her?" From where shall I seek comforters for you?

8 Shall No be more populous than the cities sitting in the rivers, waters round about her, whose bulwark is the sea, and whose wall is from the sea?

9 Chush and Egypt with abundant strength, Phut and the Libyans, were your helpers.

10 She also went into captivity into exile, and her infants were dashed at the head of every street; and upon her honored ones they cast lots, and all her great ones were bound with chains.

11 You also will be hired, you will be hidden; you also will seek force from the enemy.

12 All your fortresses are fig trees with early figs; if they are shaken, they will fall into the mouth of the eater.

13 Behold, your folk are women in your midst; the gates of your land have been opened wide to your enemies; fire has consumed your bars.

14 Draw water for the siege, strengthen your fortresses; go into the clay and trample the mire; strengthen the brickkiln.

15 There the burning will devour you, the sword will cut you off, it will consume you like the cankerworm; be devoured like the cankerworm, be devoured like the locust.

16 You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of the heavens; the cankerworm spreads and flies away.

17 Your crowned are like locusts, and your captains like great grasshoppers, which settle on the hedges in the day of cold; the sun rises and they depart, and the place of them is not known where.

18 Fallen are your shepherds, O king of Assyria; your nobles have settled; your people have been scattered on the mountains, and there is no one gathering them.

19 There is no healing for your affliction, a wound incurable is your destruction; all who hear your report will clap the hand over you, for upon whom has not your evil continually passed?


# HABAKKUK

## Chapter 1

1 The burden which Habakkuk the prophet saw.

2 How long, O LORD, must I cry out for help and you do not listen? I cry out to you, "Violence!" and you do not save.

3 Why do you make me see affliction and regard grievance, while desolation and violence are before me, and strife arises and contention prevails?

4 Therefore the law is paralyzed, and justice never goes forth, for the wicked man besmirches the righteous; therefore justice goes forth perverted.

5 See, you Gentiles, and look, and be utterly amazed: for a deed is being done in your days—though you would not believe it if told.

6 For behold, I am raising up the Chaldeans, that bitter and hasty nation, who march across the breadth of the earth to seize for themselves dwelling places that are not theirs.

7 He is fearful and terrible; his judgment and his rising go forth from him.

8 His horses are swifter than leopards, fiercer than wolves of the night; his horsemen gallop far, his horsemen come from afar, they fly like an eagle swift to devour.

9 All of them come with violence; their front faces are set toward the east, and they gather captives like sand.

10 And he scoffs at kings, and rulers are his scorn; he mocks every fortress, and gathers dust and captures it.

11 Then the spirit passed on and went by, and he is guilty—this is his power to his God.

12 Is not thou from aforetime, O LORD my God, the Holy One, immortal? O LORD, for judgment thou hast established him; yea, O Rock, thou hast founded him for correction.

13 You are of purer eyes than to look upon evil, and you cannot look upon wrongdoing. Why do you look upon those who deal treacherously, and remain silent when the wicked swallows up one more righteous than he?

14 And the common sort did as the fish of the sea, as a creeping thing which has no ruler over it.

15 All of it he lifts up with a hook; he drags it with his net, and gathers it into his dragnet; therefore he rejoices and is glad.

16 Therefore he will sacrifice to his net and burn incense to his drag, because his portion is fat and his food is rich.

17 Shall he then empty his net and continually destroy nations without mercy?


## Chapter 2

1 I will stand on my watch and take my post on the fortress; I will see what he will speak to me, and what I shall reply to my rebuke.
