2 And the LORD answered me and said, Write the vision and make clear on the boards, so that the reader may run through it.

3 For still a vision for the appointed time; it hastens to the end and will not lie; if it delays, wait for it; for it will surely come, it will not tarry.

4 Behold, his soul is puffed up, it is not upright within him; but the righteous shall live by his faith.

5 Indeed, because wine betrays him, a proud man does not stay at home; he enlarges his appetite like Sheol, and like death he is never satisfied, but gathers to himself all nations and collects to himself all peoples.

6 Are not all these a parable against him, and a taunting riddle about him? And will he not say, Woe to him who increases what is not his—how long? And lays thick clay upon himself?

7 Will not your creditors suddenly rise, and your oppressors awaken, and you become spoil for them?

8 For you have plundered many nations; all the remnant of the peoples shall plunder you, because of the blood of man and the violence done to the land, to the city and all who dwell in her.

9 Woe to him who gains dishonest profit for his household, to place his nest on high, to escape from the hand of disaster.

10 You have devised shame for your household, the ends of many peoples, and the sin of your soul.

11 For a stone from the wall will cry out, and a beam from the wood will answer it.

12 Woe to him who builds a city with blood and establishes a town in iniquity.

13 Is it not from the LORD of hosts that the peoples toil in fire and the nations in emptiness?

14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.

15 Woe to him who gives his fellow to drink, pouring out his bottle and even intoxicating, in order to look upon their nakedness.

16 You have drunk dishonour instead of glory; drink also yourself and be exposed: the cup of the LORD’s right hand will come around to you, and shameful spewing upon your glory.

17 For the violence against Lebanon will cover you, and the destruction of cattle will make you desolate, because of the blood of man and the violence against the land, the city, and all who dwell in her.

18 What good is a carved (graven) image when its maker has formed it? A molten (image) and a deceitful teacher, because the maker trusts in his own work, to make idols, things of nought.

19 Woe to him who says to a piece of wood, "Awake!" and to a silent stone, "Arise!"—behold, it is overlaid with gold and silver, and there is no breath within it.

20 But the LORD is in his holy temple; silence before his face, all the earth.


## Chapter 3

1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet on Shigionoth.

2 O Jehovah, I have heard the report of you; I am afraid: O Jehovah, revive your work in the midst of years, in the midst of years make it known; in wrath remember mercy.

3 God comes from Teman and the Holy One from mount Paran. Selah. His glory covers the heavens, and his praise fills the earth.

4 And brightness shall be as the light; horns are at his hand, and there his hiding of strength.

5 Before him goes pestilence, and burning heat departs at his feet.

6 He stood and measured the earth; he looked and shook the nations; the everlasting mountains were shattered, the ancient hills bowed; his ways are everlasting.

7 Because of affliction I saw the tents of Cushan; the curtains of the land of Midian trembled.

8 Is the LORD angry at the rivers? Is your wrath against the rivers, or your rage against the sea, that you ride on your horses, your chariots of deliverance?

9 Your bow was made quite bare; oaths were sworn over the rods of promise. Selah. You split the earth with rivers.

10 The hills saw you and trembled, the flood of waters passed by; the deep gave forth its voice, on high he lifted his hands.

11 Sun and moon stood still in their dwelling; at the light of your arrows they marched, at the brightness of the flash of your spear.

12 In your rage you tread the land; in your anger you trample the nations.

13 You went forth for the salvation of your people, for the salvation of your anointed; you struck the head from the house of the wicked, laying bare the foundation to the neck. Selah.

14 You pierced with his rods the head of the band, who came as a storm to scatter us; their rejoicing was as to devour a poor man in secret.

15 You marched through the sea with your horses, through the heap of many waters.

16 I heard and my belly trembled, at the sound of the ringing of my lips; rottenness entered my bones, and beneath me I trembled, because I must wait for the day of trouble to rise against the people who invade us.

17 For the fig tree does not blossom and there is no fruit on the vines; the olive tree fails its work, and the fields do not produce food; the sheep fold is cut off and there is no herd in the stalls.

18 But I in the LORD will rejoice, I will joy in the God of my salvation.

19 God, (my) Lord, my strength, and He sets my feet like hinds, and on my heights He makes me walk; for the Director, on my stringed instruments.


# ZEPHANIAH

## Chapter 1

1 The word of the LORD that came to Zephaniah son of Cushi son of Gedaliah son of Amariah son of Hezekiah in the days of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah.

2 I will utterly destroy all from the face of the ground, says the LORD.

3 I will gather person and cattle, I will gather bird of the heavens and fish of the sea, and the stumbling-blocks of the wicked, and I will cut off person from upon the face of the land, saith the LORD.

4 I will stretch out my hand against Judah and against all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and I will cut off from this place the remnant of Baal, the name of the Chemarims along with the priests.

5 and those who bow down on the roofs to the host of heaven, and those who swear allegiance to the LORD and those who swear by their king.

6 and those who turn back from following after Jehovah, and who do not seek Jehovah nor inquire of him.

7 Cease from before the Lord, for the day of the LORD is near; for the LORD has prepared a sacrifice, he has consecrated his guests.

8 And it shall come to pass on the day of the LORD's sacrifice, that I will punish the princes and the sons of the king and all who wear foreign apparel.

9 And I will punish on that day every one who leaps over the threshold, who fill their master's house with violence and deceit.

10 And it shall come to pass in that day, says the LORD, a cry at the fish gate, and a howling from the second, and great destruction from the hills.

11 Howl, you who dwell in Maktesh, for all the folk of Canaan are destroyed; all who bear silver are cut off.

12 At that time I will search Jerusalem with lamps and punish the men who settle on their dregs, who say in their hearts, “The LORD will not do good, nor will he do evil.”

13 Their wealth will become spoil, their houses desolation; they will build houses but not inhabit them, plant vineyards but not drink their wine.

14 The great day of the LORD is near, near and hastening greatly; the voice of the day of the LORD is bitter, there the warrior cries out.

15 A day of anger this day, a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and desolation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of cloud and thick darkness.

16 A day of trumpet and alarm against the fortified cities and against the high towers.

17 I will bring distress upon the common sort, and they shall walk as blind men because they have sinned against the LORD; their blood shall be poured out like dust, and their flesh as dung.

18 Even their silver and their gold cannot save them in the day of the LORD's wrath, and in the fire of his jealousy the whole earth shall be consumed, for he will make an end, utterly, of all the inhabitants of the earth.


## Chapter 2

1 Gather yourselves together, yes, gather, O nation not desired.

2 Before the appointed time is born, a day has passed like chaff; before the fierce wrath of the LORD comes upon you, before the day of the wrath of the LORD comes upon you.

3 Seek the LORD, all you humble of the land, who have done His judgment; seek righteousness, seek humility; perhaps you will be hidden in the day of the wrath of the LORD.

4 For Azzah shall be forsaken, and Ashkelon shall become a desolation; Ahdod at midday shall be driven out, and Ekron shall be uprooted.

5 Woe to those dwelling in the coastland of the sea, O nation of the Cherethites! The word of the LORD is against you, O Canaan, land of the Philistines: I will utterly destroy you so that there will be no inhabitant.

6 And the coast of the sea will become folds, shepherds' cottages, and sheepfolds.

7 A band of the remnant of the house of Judah shall pasture upon them; in the evening they shall lie down in the houses of Ashkelon, for the LORD their God will visit them and restore their captive people.

8 I have heard the reproach of Moab and the revilings of the sons of Ammon, who reproached my people and magnified themselves against their border.

9 Therefore, as I live, saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, surely Moab shall be as Sodom, and the children of Ammon as Gomorrah, a possession of nettles and salt pits, and a desolation forever; the remnant of my people shall plunder them, and the rest shall inherit them.

10 This is their due for their arrogancy, because they mocked and magnified themselves against the people of the LORD of hosts.

11 The LORD is to be feared above them, for He has utterly destroyed all the gods of the earth; and every man from his place shall bow down before Him, all the isles of the nations.

12 Even you Ethiopians are slain by my sword.

13 And he shall stretch out his hand toward the north and destroy Assyria, and shall make Nineveh a desolation, a dry wilderness.

14 And flocks will lie down in the midst of her, every living thing of the nation; both cormorant and bittern will lodge in the upper lintels thereof; a voice of singing in the window, desolation at the threshold; for cedar work is laid bare.

15 The city that dwelt in assurance, saying in her heart, I am and none beside me: how has she become a desolation, a couching place for beasts? Every passerby hisses, shakes his hand.


## Chapter 3

1 Alas, a rebellious and defiled city!

2 She did not listen to the voice, she did not accept discipline; she did not trust in the LORD, she did not draw near to her God.

3 Her captains within her are roaring lions; her judges are evening wolves; they do not leave enough for the morning.

4 Her prophets are treacherous men, her priests profane the consecrated, they violate the law.

5 The LORD is righteous in her midst; He does no iniquity; every morning He gives her judgment to the light; He fails not; but the unjust knows no shame.

6 I have cut off nations; their bulwarks are desolate; I have laid waste their streets so that no one passes by; their cities are destroyed, without people, without inhabitants.

7 I said, "Surely you will fear me, accept chastisement; so her dwelling place will not be cut off, all that I have appointed upon her." Indeed, they rose early, they acted corruptly in all their deeds.

8 Therefore wait ye for me, saith the LORD, for the day when I will rise up as a witness: for my judgment is to gather the nations and assemble the kingdoms, to pour upon them my indignation, all the fierce wrath of my anger; for in the fire of my jealousy shall the whole earth be devoured.

9 For then I will convert the peoples to a clear language, so that they all call on the name of the LORD to serve him with one shoulder.

10 Beyond the rivers of Cush, my suppliants, the daughter of my town, shall bring my offering.

11 On that age you shall not be ashamed of any of your deeds with which you have transgressed against me, for then I will remove from within you the boastful pride of your swelling, and you shall no longer be haughty upon my holy mountain.

12 And I will leave in your midst a poor and humble people, and they will take refuge in the name of the LORD.

13 The remnant of Israel shall do no wickedness, nor speak lies, neither shall a deceitful tongue be found in their mouth; for they shall feed and lie down, and none shall make them afraid.

14 Rejoice, Daughter Zion; shout aloud, Israel; be glad with all your heart, Daughter Jerusalem.

15 The LORD has removed your judgment; your enemy is turned aside; the King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst; you shall not fear evil anymore.

16 On that day it will be said to Jerusalem: Do not fear; to Zion: Let not your hands be weak.

17 The LORD your God in your midst is a mighty one who saves; he rejoices over you with joy, he is silent in his love, he exults over you with singing.

18 I will gather those who grieve over the appointed feast, who are from you; the reproach was a burden upon her.

19 Behold, I will do all your hurt at that time, and I will save the limping and gather her that is banished, and I will make them a praise and a name in all the earth for their shame.

20 At that time I will bring you in, and at the time I gather you, I will give you renown and praise among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your fortunes before your eyes, says the LORD.


# HAGGAI

## Chapter 1

1 In the second year of King Darius, on the first day of the sixth month, the word of the LORD came by the hand of the prophet Haggai to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, saying:

2 Thus says the LORD of hosts: This people say, It is not time to come, the time to build the house of the LORD.

3 The word of the LORD came by the hand of Haggai the prophet, saying:

4 Is it time for you to dwell in your paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins?

5 And now thus says the LORD of hosts: Take to heart your ways.

6 You have sown much and brought in little; you eat but have not enough, you drink but are not satisfied, you clothe yourselves but are not warm, and the wage-earner earns wages only for a bag with holes.

7 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Set your heart upon your ways.

8 Go up to the mountain and bring back timber, and build the house; then I will take pleasure in it, and I will be glorified, says the LORD.

9 Look, I turned to the many, but there was little; and you brought it to the house, and I blew upon it—why? says the LORD of hosts—because of my house which lies in ruins, while you run each man to his own house.

10 Therefore against you the heavens withhold dew, and the land withholds its fruit.

11 I called a desolation upon the land and upon the mountains and upon the wheat and upon the new wine and upon the anointed oil and upon what the ground brings forth and upon man and upon cattle and upon all the labour of hands.

12 Then Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel and Joshua the son of Jehozadak the high priest and all the remnant of the people obeyed the voice of the LORD their God and obeyed the words of Haggai the prophet, whom the LORD their God had sent; and the people feared before the face of the LORD.

13 Then said Haggai the messenger of the LORD by the message of the LORD to the people, Thus saith the LORD, I am with you.

14 The LORD stirred the spirit of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the spirit of Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people; and they came and worked on the house of the LORD of hosts, their God.

15 In the twenty-fourth day of the month, in the sixth month, in the second year of King Darius.


## Chapter 2

1 In the seventh month, on the twenty-first of the month, the word of the LORD came by the hand of Haggai the prophet, saying:

2 Say now to Zerubbabel son of Shalthiel governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak the priest, and to the remnant of the people, saying:

3 Who among you remains, who saw this temple in its former glory? And how do you see it now? Is it not in your eyes as nothing?

4 And now strengthen Zerubbabel, saith the LORD, and strengthen Joshua son of Jehozadek the high priest, and strengthen all the people of the land, saith the LORD, and do ye work; for I am with you, saith the LORD of hosts.

5 The act that I made with you when you came out of Egypt, and my spirit standing among you, do not fear.

6 For thus says the LORD of hosts: Yet once more, a little while, and I will shake the heavens and the earth and the sea and the dry land.

7 And I will shake all the nations, and the desired things of all the nations shall come, and I will fill this house with glory, says the LORD of hosts.

8 Silver is mine, and gold is mine, saith the LORD of hosts.

9 Great shall be the glory of this latter house more than the former, says the LORD of hosts, and in this place I will give peace, declares the LORD of hosts.

10 On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, came the word of the LORD to Haggai the prophet, saying:

11 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Ask now the priests for a ruling concerning the law:

12 If a man carries holy flesh in the fold of his garment and touches with his fold bread or pottage or wine or oil or any food, will it become holy? And the priests answered and said, No.

13 Haggai said, "If any unclean person touches any of all these things, will they be unclean?" And the priests answered and said, "They will be unclean."

14 Haggai answered and said, "So is this folk and so is this nation before me, declares the LORD, and so is all the work of their hands, and whatever they offer there is unclean."

15 And now bethink yourselves, from this day and upward, before a stone was set upon a stone in the temple of the LORD.

16 When one came to a heap of twenty measures, there were but ten; when one came to the wine vat to draw fifty measures, there were but twenty.

17 I struck you with blasting and yellowing and with hailstones all the work of your hands, yet you did not turn to me, declares the LORD.

18 Set your hearts on this day onward, from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, from the day when the temple of the LORD was founded; set your hearts.

19 Is the seed still in the barn? Until now the vine and the fig tree and the pomegranate and the olive tree have not borne fruit. From this day on I will bless you.

20 And the word of the LORD came a second time to Haggai on the twenty-fourth day of the month, saying:

21 Say to Zerubbabel governor of Judah: I will shake the heavens and the earth.

22 I will overturn the throne of kingdoms and destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations, and I will overturn chariots and their riders, and horses and their riders will fall, each by the sword of his brother.

23 In that day, says the LORD of hosts, I will take you, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, my servant, says the LORD, and I will make you like a signet ring, for I have chosen you, says the LORD of hosts.


# ZECHARIAH

## Chapter 1

1 In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of the LORD came to Zechariah son of Berechiah son of Iddo the prophet, saying:

2 The LORD was wrathful with your fathers, wrathful.

3 And you shall say to them, Thus says the LORD of hosts, Return to me, says the LORD of hosts, and I will return to you, says the LORD of hosts.

4 Do not be like your forefathers, to whom the former prophets called, saying, Thus says the LORD of hosts: Return now from your evil ways and your evil deeds; but they did not listen or pay attention to me, declares the LORD.

5 Your fathers, where are they? And the prophets—do they live forever?

6 But my acts and my statutes that I commanded my servants the prophets—did they not overtake your fathers? And they returned and said, “As the LORD of hosts intended to do to us according to our ways and our deeds, so he has dealt with us.”

7 In the age of twenty and four to the tenth month, it is the month Sebat, in the year two of Darius, was the word of the LORD to Zechariah son of Berachiah son of Iddo the prophet, saying:

8 I saw in the night, and behold, a man riding upon a red horse; and he stood among the myrtle trees which were in the bottom; and behind him were red, sorrel, and white horses.

9 And I said, What are these, my lord? And the messenger who was speaking with me said to me, I will show you what these are.

10 And the man standing among the myrtle trees answered and said, These are the ones whom the LORD has sent to walk through the land.

11 And they answered the messenger of the LORD who was standing among the myrtle trees and said We have walked through the land and behold all the land is sitting and resting

12 And the messenger of the LORD answered and said, O LORD of hosts, how long wilt thou not have mercy on Jerusalem and on the cities of Judah, against which thou hast had indignation these seventy years?

13 And the LORD answered the messenger who spoke with me with good words, words of comfort.

14 And the messenger who spoke within me said to me, "Call out, saying, Thus says the LORD of hosts, With great jealousy I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion."

15 And I am full of indignation against the nations that are at ease, for I was only a little angry, but they helped toward adversity.

16 Therefore, thus says the LORD, I have returned to Jerusalem with compassion; my temple shall be rebuilt in it, declares the LORD of hosts, and a line shall be stretched over Jerusalem.

17 Yet again proclaim, thus says the LORD of hosts: My cities will yet again overflow with good, and the LORD will yet again take pity on Zion and yet again choose Jerusalem.

18 And I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, four horns.

19 And I said to the messenger who was speaking with me, "What are these?" And he said to me, "These are the horns that scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem."

20 And the LORD showed me four craftsmen.

21 Then I said, "What are these coming to do?" And he said, saying, "These are the horns that scattered Judah, so that no man lifted up his head; and these have come to terrify them, to cast down the horns of the nations that lifted up a horn against the land of Judah to scatter it."


## Chapter 2

1 And I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, a man, and in his hand a measuring line.

2 And I said, "Where are you going?" and he said to me, "To measure Jerusalem, to see what is its breadth and what is its length."

3 And behold, the messenger speaking with me was going out, and another messenger was going out to meet him.

4 He said to him, Run, speak to the lad, saying, Jerusalem will dwell unwalled towns because of the abundance of man and beast in her midst.

5 And I will be to her, saith the LORD, a wall of fire round about, and for glory will I be in the midst of her.

6 Woe, woe, flee from the land of the north, says the LORD, for I have scattered you to the four winds of the heavens, says the LORD.

7 Woe to Zion, flee, O daughter dwelling in Babylon.

8 For thus says the LORD of hosts: After glory he has sent me to the nations that plunder you, for he who touches you touches the apple of his eye.

9 For behold, I am waving My hand against them, and they shall become spoil for their servants, and you shall know that it is the LORD of hosts who has sent me.

10 Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion, for behold, I am coming to dwell in your midst, says the LORD.

11 And many nations will join themselves to the LORD in that day and will become my people, and I will dwell in your midst, and you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you.

12 And the LORD will take Judah as His portion upon the land of holiness, and He will again choose Jerusalem.

13 Be silent, all flesh, before the LORD, for He stirs from His holy habitation.


## Chapter 3

1 And I saw Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of the LORD, and the adversary standing at his right hand to oppose him.

2 And the LORD said to the adversary, May the LORD rebuke you, O adversary, and may the LORD who has chosen Jerusalem rebuke you: is not this a burning brand plucked from the fire?

3 And Joshua was clothed in filthy garments, and he was standing before the angel.

4 Then he answered and said to those standing before him, Remove the filthy garments from upon him; and he said to him, Behold, I have removed your iniquity from upon you, and I will clothe you with changeable suits of apparel.

5 And I said, Let them put a clean mitre upon his head; so they put the clean mitre upon his head, and clothed him with garments; and the angel of the LORD stood by.

6 And the LORD's messenger testified to Joshua, saying:

7 Thus says the LORD of hosts: If you will walk in my ways and keep my charge, then you shall also govern my house and keep my courts; and I will give you places to walk among these who are standing here.

8 Hear now, O Joshua the high priest, you and your companions who sit before you, for they are men of wonder; behold, I am bringing forth my servant the Branch.

9 For behold the stone that I have set before Joshua, on one stone are seven eyes; behold, I will engrave the graving thereof, says the LORD of hosts, and I will remove the iniquity of that land in one day.

10 In that age, says the LORD of hosts, you shall call each man to his neighbor, under vine and under fig tree.


## Chapter 4

1 And the messenger who was speaking with me returned and awakened me, like a man awakened from his sleep.

2 And he said to me, What do you see? And I said, I have seen, and behold, a candlestick all of gold, and its bowl upon the top of it, and seven lamps thereon, and seven pipes cast for the lamps which are upon the top of it.

3 And two olive trees upon it, one on the right side of the bowl and one on the left side of it.

4 And I answered and said to the ambassador who was speaking in me, saying, What are these, my lord?

5 Then the ambassador who was speaking with me answered and said to me, "Do you not know what these are?" And I said, "No, my lord."

6 Then he answered and said to me, saying, This is the word of the LORD to Zerubbabel, saying, Not by might nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.

7 Who are you, great hill, before Zerubbabel, to become a plain? And you shall bring forth the cornerstone, with shouts of "Grace, grace to it!"

8 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

9 The hands of Zerubbabel will found this house, and his hands will complete it; and you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you.

10 For who despises the day of small things, and they shall rejoice and see the tin stone in the hand of Zerubbabel—these seven are the eyes of the LORD, which range over all the earth.

11 Then I answered and said to him, What are the two olive trees, the one on the right of the candlestick and the one on the left of it?

12 And I answered the second time and said to him, What are the two olive branches that are in the hands of the two golden pipes, which pour golden oil from beside them?

13 And he said to me, saying, "Do you not know what these are?" And I said, "No, my lord."

14 And he said, These are the two sons of Yizhar, standing before the Lord of all the earth.


## Chapter 5

1 Then I turned and lifted my eyes and looked, and behold, a flying scroll.

2 And he said to me, "What do you see?" And I said, "I see a flying roll: twenty cubits in length and ten cubits in breadth."

3 And he said to me, This is the curse that goes out over the face of all the land, for everyone who steals by means of this shall be cleared, and everyone who swears by means of this shall be cleared.

4 I have cast her out, saith the LORD of armies, and she shall enter into the house of the thief and into the house of him that sweareth falsely by my name; and she shall dwell in the midst of his house, and consume it with its timber and its stones.

5 The ambassador who was speaking to me went out and said to me, "Please lift up your eyes and see what is going out here."

6 And I said, What is it? And he said, This is the ephah going out; and he said, This is their eye in every land.

7 And behold, a talent of lead lifted up, and this woman sitting in the midst of the ephah.

8 And he said, "This is the fault," and cast it into the middle of the ephah, and he cast the stone of lead upon its mouth.

9 And I lifted up my eyes and saw, and behold, two women coming out, and wind in their wings; for they had wings like the wings of a stork, and they lifted up the ephah between the earth and the heavens.

10 And I said to the messenger speaking in me, "Where are they leading the ephah?"

11 And he said to me, "To build for her a house in the land of Shinar, and it shall be prepared and set there upon its base."


## Chapter 6

1 Then I turned and lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, four chariots coming out from between two mountains, and the mountains were mountains of bronze.

2 In the first chariot were red horses, and in the second chariot black horses.

3 And in the third chariot white horses, and in the fourth chariot strong reddish-brown horses.

4 And I answered and said to the angel who was speaking with me, "What are these, my lord?"

5 And the angel answered and said to me, These are four winds of the heavens, going out from standing before the Lord of all the earth.

6 The black horses that go out to the land of the north, and the white horses go out after them, and the gray horses go out to the land of the south.

7 And the mighty went out and sought to walk in the land, and he said, "Go, walk in the land," and they walked in the land.

8 He cried out to me and spoke to me, saying, "See, those going out to the northern land have set my spirit at rest in the northern land."

9 And the word of the LORD came to me, saying:

10 Take from the carried away, from Heldai and from Tobijah and from Jedaiah, and you shall come on that day and go to the house of Josiah son of Zephaniah, who came from Babylon.

11 And you shall take silver and gold and make crowns, and set them on the head of Joshua son of Jehozadak, the chief priest.

12 And you shall say to him, saying, Thus says the LORD of hosts, saying, Behold, a man whose name is Branch, and he shall spring up from his place and build the temple of the LORD.

13 And he will build the temple of the LORD, and he will bear the honour, and he will sit and rule upon his throne, and he will be a chief ruler upon his throne, and a counsel of peace will be between them both.

14 And the crowns shall be for Helem, and for Tobijah, and for Jedaiah, and for Hen son of Zephaniah, for a memorial in the temple of the LORD.

15 And those far off will come and build in the temple of the LORD, and you will know that the LORD of hosts has sent me to you; and it will be, if you diligently obey the voice of the LORD your God.


## Chapter 7

1 In the year four of Darius the king, the word of the LORD came to Zechariah on the fourth day of the ninth month, the month of Chisleu.

2 And he sent to Beth-el Sharezer and Regem-melech and the men with him to entreat the favor of the face of the LORD.

3 Say to the priests who are in the house of the LORD of hosts, and to the prophets, saying, Should I weep in the fifth month, practicing abstinence as I have done these many years?

4 And the word of the LORD of hosts came to me, saying:

5 Say to all the people of the land and to the priests, When you fasted and mourned in the fifth and in the seventh month, even these seventy years, was it a fast to me, even to me?

6 When you eat and when you drink, are you not the ones eating and you the ones drinking?

7 Are not the acts which the LORD proclaimed by the hand of the former prophets, when Jerusalem was dwelling securely and her towns round about her, and the south and the low country were inhabited?

8 And the word of the LORD came to Zechariah, saying:

9 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Judge true judgment, and show kindness and mercies, each to his brother.

10 Do not oppress the widow, the orphan, the alien, and the destitute, and do not devise evil against one another in your heart.

11 They refused to pay attention, turned a rebellious shoulder, and made their ears heavy so as not to hear.

12 And their heart they set adamantly not to heed the law and the words which the LORD of hosts sent by His spirit through the former prophets, and great wrath came from the LORD of hosts.

13 And as when I called, they did not hear, so they will call and I will not listen, says the LORD of hosts.

14 And I will drive them among all the nations which they have not known, and the land behind them shall be desolate, without traveler or return, and they shall make the land of preciousness a desolation.


## Chapter 8

1 The word of the LORD of hosts came, saying:

2 Thus says the LORD of hosts: I am zealous for Zion with great zeal, and with great wrath I am zealous for her.

3 Thus says the LORD, I have returned to Zion and will dwell among Jerusalem; and Jerusalem will be called the city of truth, and the mountain of the LORD of hosts the holy mountain.

4 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Old men and old women shall yet sit in the broad places of Jerusalem, each with his staff in hand because of great age.

5 And the broad places of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in her streets.

6 Thus says the LORD of hosts: If it is extraordinary in the eyes of the remnant of this people in those days, it will also be extraordinary in my eyes, declares the LORD of hosts.

7 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold, I am saving my people from the land of the east and from the land of the going down of the sun.

8 And I will bring them and they will dwell among Jerusalem, and they will be to me a people and I will be to them God in truth and in justice.

9 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Strengthen your hands, you who in these days hear these words from the mouth of the prophets, since the day the temple of the LORD of hosts was founded, to build the house.

10 For before those days there was no wage for man nor wage for beast, nor was there any peace for the outgoing and the incoming from the adversary, and I will send every man away from his fellow.

11 And now I am not as in former days to the remnant of this people, says the LORD of hosts.

12 For the seed of peace the vine shall yield her fruit and the land shall yield her increase and the heavens shall give their dew and I will cause the remnant of this people to inherit all these things.

13 And it shall be as you were a curse among the Gentiles, O house of Judah and house of Israel, so will I save you, and you shall be a blessing; do not fear, let your hands be strengthened.

14 For thus says the LORD of hosts: As I intended to bring disaster upon you when your fathers angered me, says the LORD of hosts, and I did not relent,

15 Even so have I purposed in those days to do good to Jerusalem and to the house of Judah; do not fear.

16 These are the acts you shall do: speak true each one to his neighbour, true and peaceable judgment judge in your gates.

17 Also, do not plot adversity against your neighbor in your heart, and do not love a false oath, for I hate all these things, says the LORD.

18 And the word of the LORD of hosts came to me, saying:

19 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The fast of the fourth month, and the fast of the fifth, and the fast of the seventh, and the fast of the tenth shall be to the house of Judah joy and gladness, and joyful festivals; therefore love truth and peace.

20 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Yet again peoples and inhabitants of many towns will come.

21 And the inhabitants of one city will go to another, saying, "Let us surely go to seek the face of the LORD and to seek the LORD of hosts; I myself will also go."

22 And many folk and mighty nations shall come to seek the LORD of hosts in Jerusalem and to entreat the face of the LORD.

23 Thus says the LORD of hosts: In those days, ten men from all the languages of the nations shall take hold, each one of the wing of a Jewish man, saying, "We will go with you, for we have heard that God is with you."


## Chapter 9

1 The burden of the word of the LORD concerning the land of Hadrach, and Damascus its resting place, for the eye of the LORD is upon mankind and all the tribes of Israel.

2 Even Hamath will be dismayed by it, as will Tyre and Sidon, for they are very wise.

3 And Tyre built a bulwark for herself, and gathered silver like dust, and fine gold like the mire of the streets.

4 Behold, (my) Lord will dispossess her and strike her power in the sea; and she will be consumed by fire.

5 Ashkelon will see and fear, and Gaza will tremble greatly, and Ekron, because her expectation is ashamed; and a king will perish from Gaza, and Ashkelon will not be inhabited.

6 And the bastard shall dwell in Ashdod, and I will cut off the arrogancy of the Philistine.

7 And I will remove his blood from his mouth and his abominable filth from between his teeth, and he also shall be left to our God, and he shall become like a chief friend in Judah, and Ekron like the Jebusites.

8 And I will grant relief to my household from the garrison, from passer-by and returner, and a taskmaster shall not pass over them again, for now I have seen with my own eyes.

9 Daughter Zion, shout aloud with joy; daughter Jerusalem, cry out in triumph: behold, your king comes to you, righteous and victorious, humble and riding on an ass, and on a colt, the foal of a she-ass.

10 I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the horse from Jerusalem; the bow of battle will be cut off, and he will speak peace to the nations; and he will rule from sea to sea and from the river to the uttermost parts of the earth.

11 Even you, by the blood of your covenant, I have sent your prisoners from the pit where there is no water.

12 Return to the stronghold, you prisoners of hope; even today, I declare, I will restore double to you.

13 For I have bent Judah for me, I have filled Ephraim; I will stir your sons, O Zion, against your sons, O Javan, and I will make you like the sword of a mighty one.

14 And the LORD will be seen over them, and His arrow will go forth like lightning, and my Lord God will blow the trumpet and march in the storms of the south.

15 The LORD of hosts will defend them, and they will eat and trample on sling stones; they will drink and become noisy like wine, like the corners of the altar with wine.

16 And the LORD their God will save them on that day as the flock of his people, for [they are] stones of a crown, lifted up over his land.

17 What is the good of it and what is its beauty? Wheat, choice young men, and sweet wine, virgins.


## Chapter 10

1 Ask the LORD for rain in the time of the latter rain; the LORD makes lightning, and He gives rain, each man grass in the field.

2 For the idols have spoken vanity, and the diviners have seen falsehood; and dreams of lying speak vanity; they comfort with vanity: therefore they are gone away like a flock, they suffer because there is no shepherd.

3 My anger is kindled against the shepherds, and I will punish the chief ones; for the LORD of hosts has visited his flock, the house of Judah, and has made them like his glorious horse in battle.

4 From him comes the bulwark, from him the nail, from him the bow of war; from him goes forth every ruler together.

5 And they will be like mighty men treading in the mire of the streets in battle, and they will fight, for the LORD is with them, and they will put to shame those who ride on horses.

6 I will strengthen the house of Judah and save the house of Joseph, and I will restore them because I have compassion on them, and they will be as though I had not rejected them, for I am the LORD their God and I will answer them.

7 And the Ephraimites shall be mighty, and their hearts shall rejoice like wine; their children shall see and be glad, their hearts shall triumph in the LORD.

8 I will whistle for them and gather them, for I have redeemed them; they will increase as they once increased.

9 I will sow them among the nations, and in distant lands they will remember me; they will live with their children and return.

10 And I will bring them back from the land of Egypt and from Assyria I will gather them, and to the land of Gilead and Lebanon I will bring them, but there will be no place found for them.

11 And he passed through the sea in distress and struck the sea with waves and dried up all the depths of the river; and the pride of Assyria was brought down and the rod of Egypt removed.

12 And I will strengthen them in the LORD, and in his name they shall walk, saith the LORD.


## Chapter 11

1 Open your doors, Lebanon, that fire may devour your cedars.

2 Lament the fir, for the cedar has fallen, the mighty have been plundered; wail, oaks of Bashan, for the forest has fallen.

3 A howling of shepherds, for their glory has been plundered; a roaring of young lions, for the majesty of the Jordan has been plundered.

4 Thus says the LORD my God, Feed the flock of slaughter.

5 Their buyers slaughter them and are not held guilty, and their sellers say, "Blessed be Jehovah, I am rich," and their shepherds have no pity on them.

6 For I will no longer have pity on the inhabitants of the land, declares the LORD; behold, I am bringing upon humanity, each person by the hand of his fellow and by the hand of his king, and they shall strike the land, and I will not deliver from their hand.

7 And I pastured the flock doomed to slaughter, the poor of the flock; and I took for myself two staffs, one I called Beauty, and one I called Destruction, and I pastured the flock.

8 I removed the three shepherds in one month, and my soul was weary of them, and their soul also was weary of me.

9 And I said, I will not care for you: you shall die the death, and the wasted shall waste away, and the survivors shall eat each woman the flesh of her own kin.

10 And I took my rod, the beauty, and broke it, to annul my confederacy which I had made with all the folk.

11 On that day it was broken, and so the shepherds who kept watch over the flock knew that it was the word of the LORD.

12 Then I said to them, "If it seems good to you, give me my wages; and if not, refrain." So they weighed out my wages, thirty pieces of silver.

13 And the LORD said to me, Cast it to the potter, the goodly price that I was prized at by them. And I took the thirty pieces of silver and cast them into the house of the LORD, to the potter.

14 And I broke my second rod, the bands, to annul the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.

15 And the LORD said to me, Take for yourself yet again the instrument of a foolish shepherd.

16 For indeed, I am raising up a shepherd in the land who will not attend to the lost, nor seek the scattered, nor heal the injured, nor care for the standing, but will eat the flesh of the healthy and tear their hoofs.

17 Woe to the idol, the shepherd who forsakes the flock—a sword against his arm and against his right eye: his arm shall utterly wither, and his right eye shall completely go blind.


## Chapter 12

1 A prophecy concerning Israel: the LORD, who stretches out the heavens and establishes the earth, and forms the spirit of man within him, declares.

2 Behold, I am making Jerusalem a cup of trembling to all the peoples round about, and also Judah will be besieged on account of Jerusalem.

3 And it shall come to pass in that day, I will make Jerusalem a burdensome stone for all the peoples; all who burden themselves with her will be completely shattered, and all the nations of the earth will be gathered against her.

4 In that day, says the LORD, I will strike every horse with panic and its rider with madness, but on the house of Judah I will keep watch, and all the horses of the peoples I will strike with blindness.

5 And the captains of Judah said in their hearts, "The strength of those who dwell in Jerusalem is in the LORD of hosts, their God."

6 On that day I will make the captains of Judah like a pan of fire among trees and like a torch of fire among sheaves, and they will consume on the right and on the left all the nations round about, and Jerusalem will yet dwell in her place, in Jerusalem.

7 And the LORD will save the tents of Judah first, so that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitant of Jerusalem may not be magnified over Judah.

8 On that day the LORD will defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and the one who is stumbling among them on that day will be like David, and the house of David will be like God, like the angel of the LORD before them.

9 And it shall come to pass in that age, I will seek to destroy every nation that comes against Jerusalem.

10 And I will pour out on the house of David and on the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and supplication; and they will look to me, to him whom they pierced, and they will mourn for him as one mourns for an only son, and grieve bitterly over him as one grieves bitterly over the firstborn.

11 On that day the mourning in Jerusalem will be as great as the mourning for Hadad-rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.

12 The land will mourn, family by family apart, the family of the house of David apart and their women apart, the family of the house of Nathan apart and their women apart.

13 the family of the house of Levi alone and their wives alone the family of the Shimites alone and their wives alone

14 All the surviving families, families apart, and their wives apart.


## Chapter 13

1 On that day a fountain will be opened for the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem for purification from sin and uncleanness.

2 And it shall come to pass in that age, says the LORD of hosts, I will cut off the names of the idols from the land, and they shall no longer be remembered; and also the prophets and the spirit of uncleanness I will remove from the land.

3 And it shall be, if a certain man prophesies again, that his father and mother, his begetters, shall say to him, "You shall not live, because you have spoken falsely in the name of the LORD"; and his father and mother, his begetters, shall pierce him through when he prophesies.

4 And it shall come to pass in that age, the prophets shall be ashamed, each one of his vision, in his prophesying; neither shall they wear a hairy garment to deceive.

5 And he said, I am not a prophet; I am a man, a worker of the ground, for a man has possessed me from my youth.

6 And he said to him, "What are these wounds in your hands?" and he said, "Those with which I was wounded in the house of my lovers."

7 Sword, awake against my shepherd and against the man my fellow, declares the LORD of hosts: strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered, and I will turn my hand against the little ones.

8 And it shall come to pass in all the land, says the LORD, two parts therein shall be cut off and perish, and the third part shall remain therein.

9 I will bring the third part into the fire and refine them as silver is refined, and test them as gold is tested; they will call on my name, and I will answer them. I will say, "It is my people," and they will say, "The LORD is my God."


## Chapter 14

1 Behold, a day is coming for the LORD, and your spoil will be divided in your midst.

2 I will gather all the nations to Jerusalem for battle, and the city will be captured, the houses plundered, and the women ravished; half the city will go into captivity, but the remnant of the people will not be cut off from the city.

3 And the LORD will go out and fight against those nations, as when He fought in the day of battle.

4 And his legs shall stand on that day upon the Mount of Olives, which is before Jerusalem toward the east, and the Mount of Olives shall be split in its middle from east to west, a very great valley; and half of the mountain shall move toward the north, and half of it toward the south.

5 And you shall flee through the valley of the mountains, for the valley of the mountains shall reach to Azal, and you shall flee as you fled from before the earthquake in the days of Uzziah king of Judah, and the LORD my God shall come, all the holy ones with him.

6 And it shall come to pass in that day that there shall be no light; the precious lights shall congeal.

7 And it shall be one day, that day shall be known to the LORD, not day nor night, but at evening tide there shall be light.

8 And it shall come to pass in that age, living waters shall go out from Jerusalem, half of them toward the eastern sea and half of them toward the western sea, in summer and in winter it shall be.

9 And the LORD will be king over all the earth; in that day the LORD will be one and his name one.

10 All the land shall turn as a wilderness from Geba to Rimmon southward of Jerusalem, and the plain shall extend and settle beneath it from the gate of Benjamin to the place of the first gate, to the gate of the corners, and from the tower of Hananeel to the king’s winepresses.

11 And they will dwell in it, and there will no longer be a curse, and Jerusalem will dwell securely.

12 This shall be the plague with which the LORD will strike all the peoples that have warred against Jerusalem: their flesh shall rot while they stand on their feet, and their eyes shall rot in their holes, and their tongue shall rot in their mouth.

13 And it shall come to pass on that day, there shall be a great tumult from the LORD among them, and one man shall seize the hand of his fellow, and his hand shall rise against the hand of his fellow.

14 And also Judah will fight in Jerusalem, and the riches of all the nations round about—gold and silver and clothing—will be gathered in great abundance.

15 And so shall the plague be upon the horse, the mule, the camel, and the ass, and upon all the beasts that are in their camps, like this plague.

16 And it shall come to pass, that every one of all the nations that come against Jerusalem who is left shall go up from year to year to worship the King, the LORD of hosts, and to keep the Feast of Booths.

17 And it shall be that the family of the earth who does not go up to Jerusalem to worship the king, the LORD of hosts, upon them there will be no rain.

18 But if the family of Egypt does not go up and come, then upon them there shall be the plague with which the LORD strikes the nations who do not go up to celebrate the feast of Booths.

19 This will be the punishment of Egypt and the punishment of all the nations who do not go up to celebrate the feast of the booths.

20 On that day there shall be bells of the horse consecrated to the LORD, and the pots in the house of the LORD shall be like the basins before the altar.

21 And every caldron in Jerusalem and in Judah shall be holy to the LORD of hosts; and all who sacrifice shall come and take of them and boil in them; and there shall no longer be a Canaanite in the house of the LORD of hosts on that day.


# MALACHI

## Chapter 1

1 An oracle: the word of the LORD to Israel by the hand of Malachi.

2 I have loved you, said the LORD, and you said, In what way have you loved us? Is not Esau a brother to Jacob? says the LORD. Yet I have loved Jacob.

3 I hated Esau, and I made his hills desolate and his inheritance a wilderness of dragons.

4 If Edom says, We are laid waste, but we will return and rebuild the desolate places, thus says the Lord of hosts: They shall build, but I will throw down; and they shall be called the border of wickedness, and the people against whom the Lord has indignation forever.

5 And your eyes shall see, and you shall say, "The LORD will be magnified beyond the border of Israel."

6 A son honors a father and a servant honors his master; but if I am a father, where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is my fear? says the LORD of hosts to you priests who despise my name. And you say, “How have we despised your name?”

7 Bringing onto my altar defiled bread, you say, “How have we defiled you?” By saying, “The table of the LORD is despised.”

8 And when you offer a blind animal for sacrifice, is it not evil? And when you offer a lame or sick animal, is it not evil? Offer it now to your governor; will he be pleased with you or show you favour? says the LORD of hosts.

9 And now, please seek the face of God, that He may be gracious to us; from your hand this has come—will the LORD of hosts accept you favorably?

10 Who among you will shut the doors and not kindle the altar in vain? I have no delight in you, says the LORD of hosts, and I will not accept an offering from your hand.

11 From the rising of the sun even to its going down my name is great among the nations, and in every place a pure oblation is offered to my name, for great is my name among the nations, says the LORD of hosts.

12 But you profane it, saying, "The table of (my) Lord is defiled, and its fruit is despised in its eating."

13 And you shall say, "What a weariness!" and you shall snort at him, says the LORD of hosts; and you shall bring the stolen, and the lame, and the sick, and you shall bring the offering; shall I accept this from your hand? says the LORD.

14 But cursed is the deceiver who has a male in his flock and yet vows and sacrifices a blemished animal to (my) Lord, for I am a great king, says the LORD of hosts, and my name is feared among the nations.


## Chapter 2

1 And now to you, priests, (is) the ordinance (which was) commanded.

2 If you will not listen, and if you will not take to heart to give honour to my name, says the LORD of hosts, then I will send the curse among you and I will curse your blessings; indeed, I have already cursed them because you do not take it to heart.

3 Behold, I am about to rebuke you and scatter your seed, and I will spread dung on your faces, the dung of your solemn feasts, and you shall be taken away to it.

4 And you shall know that I have sent to you this commandment, so that my covenant may be with Levi, says the LORD of hosts.

5 My covenant was with him, life and peace, and I gave them to him with reverence; he feared me, and before my name he was humbled.

6 The law of truth was in his mouth, and iniquity was not found on his lips; he walked with me in peace and in equity, and turned many from iniquity.

7 For the lips of a priest preserve knowledge, and law they seek from his mouth, for he is a messenger of the LORD of hosts.

8 But you have turned away from the way; you have caused many to stumble in the law; you have corrupted the covenant of Levi, says the LORD of hosts.

9 And I also have made you despised and abased among all the people, according to my word, because you do not keep my ways and show partiality in the law.

10 Is not one chief ours? Did not one God create us? Why does each man betray his brother, profaning the covenant of our forefathers?

11 Judah has dealt faithlessly, and an abomination has been committed in Israel and in Jerusalem; for Judah has profaned the consecrated thing of the LORD which he loved, and has married the daughter of a foreign god.

12 May the LORD cut off the man who does this, both the one who cries out and the one who answers, from the tents of Jacob, and the one who brings a grain offering to the LORD of hosts.

13 And this second thing you do: you cover the altar of the LORD with tears, with overflowing and with groaning, because he no longer regards the gift nor accepts acceptance from your hand.

14 And you shall say, "Why? Because the LORD has testified between you and the wife of your youth, that you have dealt faithlessly with her, though she is your companion and the wife of your covenant."

15 Yet not one has he made, though a remnant of spirit remains to him; and why one? Because he seeks offspring from God. So guard yourselves in your spirit, and do not act faithlessly toward the wife of your youth.

16 For detesting divorce, says the LORD, the God of Israel, and for covering violence with his garment, says the LORD of hosts, you shall guard yourselves in your spirit, and not deal treacherously.

17 You have wearied the LORD with your words, and you say, “How have we wearied?” By saying, “Everyone who does evil is good in the eyes of the LORD, and in them he delights.” Or where is the God of justice?


## Chapter 3

1 Behold, I am sending my messenger, and he will clear the way before me; and suddenly the Lord whom you seek will come to his temple, and the messenger of the covenant whom you desire—behold, he is coming, says the LORD of hosts.

2 Who can endure the day of his coming, and who can stand when he appears? For he is like a refiner's fire and like soap.

3 And he will sit refining and purifying silver, and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and like silver, and they will be to the LORD those who bring the offering in righteousness.

4 And the offering of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasing to the LORD as in the days of old and as in former years.

5 I will come near to you for judgment, and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, against the adulterers, against those who swear falsely, and against those who defraud the hired laborer of his wages, the widow, and the orphan, and pervert the rights of the stranger, who do not fear me, says the LORD of hosts.

6 For I am the LORD, I do not change; and you, sons of Jacob, are not consumed.

7 From the days of your fathers you have turned away from my statutes and have not kept them; return to me and I will return to you, says the LORD of hosts. And you said, In what shall we return?

8 Will a man of low degree set bounds to God, when you set bounds to me, and say, “In what way have we set bounds to you?” The tithe and the offering?

9 You are cursed with a curse, for you rob me, the whole nation of you.

10 Bring all the tithe into the storehouse so that there may be meat in my house, and test me now in this, says the LORD of hosts, if I will not open for you the windows of heaven and pour out for you blessing until there is no more need.

11 I will rebuke you concerning the food, and it will not destroy for you the fruit of the ground, and the vine in the field will not miscarry for you, says the LORD of hosts.

12 And all the nations will delight in you, for you will be a desirable land, says the LORD of hosts.

13 Your words have been strong against me, says the LORD. Yet you say, What have we spoken against you?

14 You said falsely, "Serve God?" and, "What profit that we have kept His charge and walked mournfully before the LORD of hosts?"

15 Now we call the arrogant blessed; indeed, those who do wickedness are built up; indeed, they test God and escape.

16 Then those who feared the LORD spoke one to another, and the LORD listened and heard, and a book of remembrance was written before him for those who feared the LORD and for those who considered his name.

17 And they shall be mine, says the LORD of hosts, on the day when I make them my special treasure, and I will have mercy on them as a man has mercy on his son who serves him.

18 And you shall return and see between the righteous and the wicked, between one serving God and one who has not served him.


## Chapter 4

1 For behold the age is coming, burning like an oven, and all the proud and all who commit wickedness will be stubble; and the age that is coming shall set them ablaze, says the LORD of hosts, so that it leaves them neither root nor branch.

2 And for you who revere my name, the sun of righteousness will rise with healing in its wings, and you will go out and leap like calves from the stall.

3 And you shall trample down the wicked, for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet on the day that I am making, says the LORD of hosts.

4 Remember the law of Moses my servant, which I commanded him at Horeb for all Israel, the statutes and judgments.

5 Behold, I am sending to you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the day of the LORD, the great and terrible.

6 He will turn the care of fathers to their children and the care of children to their fathers, lest I come and strike the land with utter destruction.


# MATTHEW

## Chapter 1

1 The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, the Son of Abraham.

2 Abraham begat Isaac, and Isaac begat Jacob, and Jacob begat Judas and his brothers.

3 And Judas begat Perez and Zara of Thamar, and Perez begat Hezron, and Hezron begat Aram.

4 And Aram begat Aminadab, and Aminadab begat Nahshon, and Nahshon begat Salmon.

5 And Salmon begat Booz of Rahab, and Booz begat Obed of Ruth, and Obed begat Jesse.

6 And Jesse begat David the king, and David the king begat Solomon of her that had been the wife of Uriah.

7 And Solomon begat Roboam, and Roboam begat Abia, and Abia begat Asa.

8 And Asa begat Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat begat Joram, and Joram begat Ozias.

9 And Ozias begat Joatham, and Joatham begat Achaz, and Achaz begat Ezekias.

10 And Ezekias begat Manasses, and Manasses begat Amon, and Amon begat Josias.

11 And Josias begat Jechonias and his brothers at the time of the deportation to Babylon.

12 And after the deportation to Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel, and Salathiel begat Zerubbabel.

13 And Zerubbabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begat Azor.

14 And Azor begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud.

15 And Eliud begat Eleazar, and Eleazar begat Matthan, and Matthan begat Jacob.

16 Jacob, however, born Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom Jesus was born, the one who is said Christ.

17 So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations, and from David to the deportation to Babylon are fourteen generations, and from the deportation to Babylon to Christ are fourteen generations.

18 Now the birth of Jesus Christ was thus: after His mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together, she was found to be with child by the Holy Spirit.

19 Then Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man and not wanting to make a public example of her, willed to release her secretly.

20 But while he thought on these things, see, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, Joseph, Son of David, do not fear to receive Mary your wife, for that which is born in her is of the Holy Spirit.

21 She will bear a Son, and you will call His name Jesus, for He will save His people from their sins.

22 Now all this happened so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet would be fulfilled, saying,

23 See, the virgin will conceive and bear a Son, and they will call His name Emmanuel, which is interpreted, God with us.

24 When Joseph was aroused from sleep, he did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him, and he received his wife.

25 And he did not know her until she had borne her firstborn Son, and he called His name Jesus.


## Chapter 2

1 Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the day of Herod the king, see, magi from the east came to Jerusalem.

2 Saying, where is the one who has been born King of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.

3 Hearing this, King Herod was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.

4 And gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked of them where the Christ is born.

5 And they said to him, "In Bethlehem of Judea, for thus it has been written through the prophet."

6 And you, Bethlehem, earth of Judah, are not the least among the governors of Judah; for out of you shall go out a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.

7 Then Herod, having secretly called the magi, enquired diligently of them the time of the appearing star.

8 And having sent them to Bethlehem, he said, "Go and carefully ask about the child; and when you have found him, report to me, so that I also, having come, may worship him."

9 After hearing the king, they went, and see, the star which they had seen in the east went before them until, coming, it stood over where the child was.

10 Seeing the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.

11 And coming into the house, they found the child with Mary his mother, and falling down they worshipped him; and opening their treasures, they offered to him gifts: gold, and frankincense, and myrrh.

12 And having been warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they withdrew by another way into their own region.

13 When they had withdrawn, behold, an angel of the Lord appears to Joseph in a dream, saying, "Arise, take the child and his mother and flee into Egypt, and be there until I tell you; for Herod is about to seek the child to destroy him."

14 But having arisen, he received the child and his mother by night and withdrew into Egypt.

15 And he was there until the death of Herod, so that what was spoken by the Lord through the prophet might be fulfilled, saying, "Out of Egypt I called my Son."

16 Then Herod, having seen that he had been mocked by the magi, was greatly enraged, and having sent out, he killed all the male children in Bethlehem and in all its borders, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had diligently inquired from the magi.

17 Then that which was said through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying:

18 A voice in Rama was heard: lamentation and weeping and much mourning; Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be encouraged, because they are not.

19 But when Herod had died, see, an angel of the Lord appears in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

20 Say: "Rise, take the child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those seeking the soul of the child have died."

21 And when he had been raised up, he received the child and his mother and came into the land of Israel.

22 But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there; and having been warned in a dream, he withdrew into the region of Galilee.

23 And come, he dwelt in a city said Nazareth, that the spoken through the prophets might be fulfill that, Nazarene he shall be call.


## Chapter 3

1 In those days John the Baptist comes, preaching in the wilderness of Judea.

2 and say, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has drawn near."

3 For this is the one having been spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying, "A voice of one crying in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.'"

4 Now John himself had his garment made from camel's hair and a leather belt about his waist, and his food was locusts and wild honey.

5 Then Jerusalem and all Judea and all the region around the Jordan were going out to him.

6 and were being baptized in the Jordan by him, confessing their sins.

7 Seeing many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, "Brood of vipers, who showed you to flee from the coming wrath?"

8 Produce therefore fruit worthy of repentance.

9 And do not seem to say within yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our Father'; for I say to you that God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

10 Now also the axe lies at the root of the trees; therefore every tree not doing good fruit is cut off and cast into fire.

11 I baptize you with water for repentance, but the one coming after me is stronger than I, whose sandals I am not sufficient to bear; he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.

12 His winnowing fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly purge his threshing floor and gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn up with unquenchable fire.

13 Then Jesus comes from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptized by him.

14 John was forbidding him, saying, "I have need to be baptized by you, and you come to me?"

15 Jesus answered and said to him, "Let it be so now, for thus it is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness." Then he allowed him.

16 And after being baptized, Jesus immediately went up from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and coming upon him.

17 And see, a voice from the heavens saying, "This is my Son, the beloved, in whom I am pleased."


## Chapter 4

1 Then Jesus was led up into the wilderness by the Spirit to be tested by the devil.

2 And after fasting forty days and forty nights, afterward he hungered.

3 And coming near to him, the tester said, "If you are the Son of God, say that these stones become bread."

4 But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of God.

5 Then the devil receives him into the holy city and stands him on the pinnacle of the temple.

6 He says to him, "If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down; for it is written: 'He will command his angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands, lest you strike your foot against a stone.'"

7 Jesus said to him again, "It is written: 'You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.'"

8 Again the devil receives him onto an exceedingly high mountain and shows him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.

9 And he says to him, "All these things I will give to you, if, having fallen, you worship me."

10 Then Jesus says to him, "Depart, Satan; for it is written: 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and Him only shall you serve.'"

11 Then the devil leaves him, and see, angels came near and served him.

12 When Jesus heard that John had been delivered up, he withdrew into Galilee.

13 And leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is upon the sea coast, in the borders of Zebulun and Naphtali.

14 so that might be fulfilled the word having been spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying

15 Earth of Zebulun and earth of Naphtali, way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.

16 The people sitting in darkness saw a great light, and to those sitting in a region and shadow of death, light rose upon them.

17 From that time Jesus began to preach and say, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has drawn near."

18 As Jesus was walking alongside the sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon who is called Peter and Andrew his brother, casting a casting net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

19 And he says to them, "Come after me, and I will make you fishers of men."

20 And they immediately left the nets and followed him.

21 And advancing from there, he saw another two brothers, James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, restoring their nets, and he called them.

22 And they, immediately leaving the boat and their father, followed him.

23 And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing all disease and all sickness among the people.

24 His fame spread throughout all Syria, and they offered to him all those having various diseases and being constrained by torment, and being demon-possessed, and moonstruck, and paralytic, and he healed them.

25 And many crowds followed Him from Galilee and Decapolis and Jerusalem and Judea and beyond the Jordan.


## Chapter 5

1 When he saw the crowds, he went up into the mountain, and when he had sat down, his disciples came near to him.

2 And opening his mouth, he taught them, saying.

3 Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

4 Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be encouraged.

5 Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.

6 Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be satisfied.

7 Blessed are the merciful, for they shall have mercy.

8 Blessed are the clean in heart, for they shall see God.

9 Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God.

10 Blessed are the ones who have been persecuted for the sake of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

11 Blessed are you when they reproach you and persecute you and say all evil words against you, lying, for my sake.

12 Rejoice and be glad, for your reward is great in heaven; for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.

13 You are the salt of the earth; but if the salt becomes tasteless, with what will it be salted? It is no longer able for anything, except to be cast out and to be trampled by men.

14 You are the light of the world. A city lying on a mountain is not able to be hidden.

15 Neither do they burn a lamp and put it under the bushel, but on the lampstand; and it shines to all those in the house.

16 So let your light shine before men, so that they may see your good works and glorify your Father who is in the heavens.

17 Do not suppose that I came to destroy the law or the prophets; I did not come to destroy, but to fulfill.

18 For amen I say to you, until heaven and earth pass away, one jot or one tittle shall by no means pass away from the law until all things happen.

19 Whoever therefore shall loose one of the least of these commandments and teach men so, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.

20 For I say to you that unless your righteousness abounds much more than that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of the heavens.

21 You have heard that it was said to the men of old, "You shall not murder," and whoever murders will be guilty of the judgment.

22 But I say to you that all who are angry with his brother in vain will be guilty of the judgment; and whoever says to his brother 'Raca' will be guilty of the council; and whoever says 'Foolish' will be guilty of the hell of fire.

23 If therefore you offer your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you,

24 Leave your gift there before the altar and depart first, be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.

25 Be agreeing with your adversary quickly while you are in the way with him, lest the adversary deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the servant, and you be cast into prison.

26 Amen I say to you, you will by no means go out from there until you repay the last farthing.

27 You have heard that it was said to those of old, "You shall not commit adultery."

28 But I say to you that all who look at a woman with desire for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

29 And if your right eye offends you, pluck it out and cast it from you; for it profits you that one of your members should be destroyed, and not that your whole body should be cast into hell.

30 And if your right hand offends you, cut it off and cast it from you; for it is profitable for you that one of your members be destroyed, and not that your whole body be cast into hell.

31 But it was said, "Whoever shall release his wife, let him give her a divorcement."

32 But I say to you that whoever releases his wife except for the cause of sexual immorality makes her commit adultery, and whoever marries a released woman commits adultery.

33 Again, you have heard that it was said to the old: 'You shall not forswear yourself, but shall repay to the Lord your oaths.'

34 But I say to you, do not swear at all, neither by heaven, because it is the throne of God.

35 neither by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; neither by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great king.

36 nor swear by your head, for you are not able to make one hair white or black.

37 Let your word be 'Yes, yes' and 'No, no.' For anything beyond these is from the evil one.

38 You have heard that it was said, "An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth."

39 But I say to you, not to resist the evil; but whoever smites you on your right cheek, turn to him also the other.

40 To the one wanting to judge you and take your tunic, let him have the garment also.

41 And whoever compels you one mile, depart with him two.

42 Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away the one who wants to borrow from you.

43 You have heard that it was said, "You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy."

44 But I say to you, love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who mistreat you and persecute you.

45 so that you may be Sons of your Father who is in heaven, because He causes His sun to rise on the evil and the good, and He sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.

46 For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the publicans do the same?

47 And if you greet only your brothers, what abundant thing do you do? Do not even the publicans do so?

48 Be therefore you perfect, just as your Father who is in heaven is perfect.


## Chapter 6

1 Give heed not to do your alms before men, to be beheld by them; otherwise you have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.

2 Therefore, when you do alms, do not sound a trumpet before you, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the lanes, so that they may be glorified by men. Amen, I say to you, they receive their reward.

3 But when you do alms, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.

4 so that your alms may be in secret; and your Father who looks in secret will repay you in the open.

5 And when you pray, you shall not be as the hypocrites; for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may appear to men. Amen, I say to you, they receive their reward.

6 But you, when you pray, enter into your secret chamber, and having shut your door, pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who looks in secret will repay you in the open.

7 But when you pray, do not use vain repetitions as the heathen do; for they think that they will be heard for their much speaking.

8 Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.

9 Pray therefore in this way: Our Father who is in heaven, sanctify your name.

10 Let your kingdom come; let your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

11 Give us today our daily bread.

12 And forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.

13 And do not bring us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one, for yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory to the ages. Amen.

14 For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

15 But if you do not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

16 But when you fast, do not become like the hypocrites, of a sad countenance; for they disfigure their faces that they may appear to men to be fasting. Amen, I say to you, they receive their reward.

17 But you, when fasting, anoint your head and wash your face.

18 so that you may not appear to men to be fasting, but to your Father who is in secret; and your Father who looks in secret will repay you in the manifest.

19 Do not treasure up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where moth and corrosion destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.

20 But treasure up for yourselves treasure in heaven, where neither moth nor corrosion destroys, and where thieves do not break in nor steal.

21 For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

22 The lamp of the body is the eye; if therefore your eye is single, your whole body will be bright.

23 But if your eye is evil, your whole body will be dark. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is that darkness!

24 No one is able to serve two masters; for he will hate the one and love the other, or he will hold fast to one and despise the other. You are not able to serve God and mammon.

25 For this reason I say to you, do not be anxious about your soul, what you eat and what you drink, nor about your body, what you put on. Is not the soul more than the food and the body more than the garment?

26 Look at the birds of heaven, because they do not sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and your heavenly Father nourishes them. Are you not worth more than they?
