34 Amen, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.

35 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

36 But concerning that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of the heavens, except my Father only.

37 But just as the days of Noah were, so also will be the coming of the Son of Man.

38 For just as they were in the days before the flood, eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark.

39 and they did not know until the flood came and took all away; so will be the coming of the Son of man.

40 Then two will be in the field: the one is taken and the other is left.

41 Two women grinding at the mill: one is taken and one is left.

42 Watch therefore, for you do not know what hour your Lord comes.

43 But know this, that if the householder had known what watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have watched and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.

44 Therefore you also must be ready, because the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.

45 Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his master has appointed over his household to give them their food in due time?

46 Blessed is that servant whom his master when he comes will find doing so.

47 Amen I say to you that he will appoint him over all his possessions.

48 But if that evil servant says in his heart, 'My master delays to come,'

49 And begins to strike his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with those who are drunk.

50 The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not know.

51 And will cut him asunder, and put his part with the hypocrites; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.


## Chapter 25

1 Then the kingdom of heaven will be likened to ten virgins, who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.

2 five of them were wise, and five were foolish.

3 The foolish ones, having taken their lamps, did not take oil with them.

4 But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

5 While the bridegroom delayed, all slumbered and slept.

6 In the middle of the night a cry came: See, the bridegroom is coming, go out to meet him.

7 Then all those virgins arose and adorned their lamps.

8 And the foolish said to the wise, Give us from your oil, for our lamps are quenched.

9 The wise answered, saying, Not so, lest there not suffice for us and you: go rather to those selling, and buy for yourselves.

10 While they were departing to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready entered with him into the wedding, and the door was shut.

11 Later then come also the remaining virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.

12 And he, answering, said, Amen, I say to you, I do not know you.

13 Watch therefore, because you do not know the day nor the hour in which the Son of man comes.

14 For just as a man going abroad called his own servants and delivered to them his possessions.

15 And to one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, to each according to his own ability, and he went abroad at once.

16 Then the one who took the five talents went and worked with them and made another five talents.

17 Likewise, the one who gained two also gained another two.

18 But the one who took the one talent, departing, dug in the earth and hid his master's silver.

19 But after a long time, the master of those servants comes and settles accounts with them.

20 And the one who had taken the five talents came near and offered another five talents, saying, "Master, you delivered five talents to me; see, I have gained another five talents upon them."

21 His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant; you have been faithful over a few things; I will appoint you over many things; enter into the joy of your master.'

22 Then the one who had taken the two talents came near and said, 'Master, you delivered two talents to me; see, I have gained another two talents besides them.'

23 His master said to him, "Well done, good and faithful servant; you have been faithful over a few things, I will appoint you over much; enter into the joy of your master."

24 Then the one who had taken the one talent also came near and said, 'Master, I knew you, that you are a hard man, reaping where you did not sow and gathering from where you did not scatter.

25 And fearing, I departed and hid your talent in the earth; see, you have what is yours.

26 His master answered and said to him, "Evil and slothful servant, you knew that I reap where I did not sow and gather where I did not scatter seed."

27 You must therefore cast my silver to the exchangers, and when I came I would have received mine own with usury.

28 Take therefore the talent from him, and give it to the one having the ten talents.

29 For to everyone who has, it will be given, and he will abound, but from him who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

30 And cast the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

31 But when the Son of man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.

32 All the nations will be gathered before him, and he will separate them from one another just as the shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

33 And he will stand the sheep on his right and the goats on the left.

34 Then the king will say to those on his right, "Come, you who are blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world."

35 For I hungered and you gave me to eat; I thirsted and you gave me drink; I was a stranger and you gathered me.

36 naked and you clothed me; I was weak and you visited me; I was in prison and you came to me.

37 Then the righteous will answer him, saying, "Lord, when did we see you hungry and nourish you, or thirsty and give you drink?"

38 When did we see you a stranger and gather you, or naked and clothe you?

39 When did we see you weak or in prison and come to you?

40 The king answering will say to them, 'Amen, I say to you, as much as you did to one of the least of these my brothers, you did to me.'

41 Then he will say also to those on the left: 'Go from me, you who are cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.'

42 For I hungered, and you gave me no food; I thirsted, and you gave me no drink.

43 I was a stranger, and you did not gather me; naked, and you did not clothe me; weak and in prison, and you did not visit me.

44 Then they also will answer, saying, "Lord, when did we see you hungering or thirsting or a stranger or naked or weak or in prison, and did not serve you?"

45 Then he will answer them, saying, 'Amen, I say to you, inasmuch as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to me.'

46 And these will depart into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.


## Chapter 26

1 And it happened when Jesus finished all these words, he said to his disciples.

2 You know that after two days the Passover takes place, and the Son of Man is delivered up to be crucified.

3 Then the high priests and the scribes and the elders of the people were gathered into the courtyard of the high priest who is called Caiaphas.

4 And they advised that they should seize Jesus by deceit and kill him.

5 But they said, "Not during the feast, so that there will be no uproar among the people."

6 Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

7 A woman came near to him having an alabaster jar of very precious ointment and poured it on his head as he reclined.

8 When the disciples saw it, they were indignant, saying, "Why this destruction?"

9 For this ointment could have been sold for much and given to the poor.

10 Knowing this, Jesus said to them, "Why do you provide trouble to the woman? For she has worked a good work toward me.

11 For you always have the poor with you, but me you do not have always.

12 For by casting this ointment upon my body, she did it to bury me.

13 Amen, I say to you, wherever this gospel is preached in the whole world, what she has done will also be spoken of as a memorial of her.

14 Then one of the twelve, the one called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.

15 He said, What are you willing to give me, and I will deliver him to you? And they stood for him thirty pieces of silver.

16 And from that time he sought an opportunity so that he might betray Him.

17 On the first day of the unleavened bread, the disciples came near to Jesus, saying to him, "Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?"

18 But he said, "Depart into the city to a certain man, and say to him, 'The Teacher says, "My time is near; I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples."'"

19 And the disciples did as Jesus appointed to them, and they prepared the Passover.

20 When evening came, he reclined at table with the twelve.

21 And as they were eating, he said, "Amen, I say to you that one of you will betray me."

22 And grieving greatly, they each began to say to him, "Surely not I, Lord?"

23 The one who dipped his hand with me in the dish will betray me.

24 The Son of man departs as it is written concerning him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed; it would have been good for him if that man had never been born.

25 Then Judas, the one betraying him, answered and said, "Surely not I am, Rabbi?" He said to him, "You yourself have said."

26 While they were eating, Jesus took the bread, and having blessed it, he broke it and gave it to the disciples and said, "Take, eat; this is my body."

27 And taking the cup and giving thanks, He gave it to them, saying, "Drink from it, all of you."

28 For this is my blood of the new covenant, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.

29 I say to you, from now on I will not drink of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in the kingdom of my Father.

30 And after singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

31 Then Jesus says to them, "All of you will be offended in me during this night; for it is written, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.' "

32 But after I am raised, I will go before you into Galilee.

33 But Peter answered him, "Even if all are offended in you, I will never be offended."

34 Jesus said to him, Amen I say to you that in this night, before a rooster calls, you will deny me three times.

35 Peter says to him, "Even if I must die with you, I will never deny you." Likewise also all the disciples said.

36 Then Jesus comes with them to a field called Gethsemane, and he says to the disciples, "Sit here until I depart and pray there."

37 Having received Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to grieve and be full of heaviness.

38 Then he says to them, "My soul is deeply grieved, even to death; remain here and watch with me."

39 Going a little farther, he fell on his face, praying and saying, "My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as you will."

40 And he comes to the disciples and finds them sleeping, and says to Peter, "So, were you not able to watch with me one hour?"

41 Watch and pray, that you do not enter into temptation; the Spirit indeed is ready, but the flesh is weak.

42 Again he departed a second time and prayed, saying, "My Father, if this cup cannot pass away from me unless I drink it, may your will be done."

43 And having come, he finds them sleeping again, for their eyes were weighed down.

44 And having left them, having departed again, he prayed a third time, saying the same word.

45 Then he comes to his disciples and says to them, "Sleep on now and take your rest. See, the hour has drawn near, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners."

46 Rise, let us go; see, the one betraying me has drawn near.

47 And while he was yet speaking, see, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great crowd with swords and clubs from the high priests and elders of the people.

48 Now the one betraying him gave them a sign, saying, "Whomever I kiss, he is; seize him."

49 And immediately coming near to Jesus, he said, "Rejoice, Rabbi," and kissed him.

50 Jesus said to him, "Friend, for what purpose are you present?" Then, coming near, they laid hands on Jesus and seized him.

51 And see, one of those with Jesus stretched out his hand, withdrew his sword, and striking the servant of the high priest, took away his ear.

52 Then Jesus says to him, "Turn away your sword into its place; for all who take the sword will be destroyed by the sword."

53 Do you seem that I am not able to encourage my Father and He will present much more than twelve legions of angels to me.

54 How then would the Scriptures be fulfilled, that it must happen this way?

55 In that hour Jesus said to the crowd, "As against a robber you have gone out with swords and clubs to seize me. Daily I sat teaching in the temple, and you did not seize me."

56 But all this has happened that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled; then all the disciples, leaving him, fled.

57 And those who had seized Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered.

58 But Peter followed Him from afar, as far as the courtyard of the high priest; and entering within, he sat with the servants to see the end.

59 But the chief priests and the elders and the whole council sought false witness against Jesus in order to put him to death.

60 And they did not find any, and though many false witnesses came near, they did not find any.

61 Later two false witnesses came forward and said, "This man said, 'I am able to destroy the temple of God and to build it in three days.'"

62 And rising, the high priest said to him, "Do you answer nothing? What do these testify against you?"

63 But Jesus was silent. And the high priest answered and said to him, "I adjure you by the living God, that you tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God."

64 Jesus says to him, "You have said. Nevertheless, I say to you, from now on you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven."

65 Then the high priest tore his garments, saying, "He has blasphemed! What further need do we have of witnesses? See, now you have heard his blasphemy."

66 What do you think? And they answered and said, He is guilty of death.

67 Then they spit in his face and struck him with their fists, and others slapped him.

68 "Prophesy to us, Christ: who is the one that struck You?" they said.

69 But Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard, and one maidservant came near to him, saying, "You also were with Jesus the Galilean."

70 But he denied before all, saying, "I do not know what you say."

71 And when he had gone out to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those there, "This man was with Jesus the Nazarene."

72 And again he denied with an oath, "I do not know the man."

73 After a little while those standing there came near and said to Peter, "Truly you also are one of them, for your speech makes you evident."

74 Then he began to curse and swear, "I do not know the man!" And immediately a rooster called.

75 And Peter remembered the word of Jesus which had said to him, "Before a rooster calls, you will deny me three times," and going out, he wept bitterly.


## Chapter 27

1 When early came, all the high priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus so as to put him to death.

2 And having bound him, they led him away and delivered him to Pontius Pilate the governor.

3 Then see, Judas, the one betraying him, that he was condemned, having regretted, he turned away the thirty silver pieces to the high priests and the elders.

4 Saying, I have sinned in betraying innocent blood. But they said, What is that to us? You see to it.

5 And having thrown the silver in the temple, he withdrew, and having departed, he hanged himself.

6 The high priests, taking the silver, said, "It is not lawful to cast them into the Corban, because it is the price of blood."

7 And when they had taken counsel, they bought with them the potter's field for the burial of strangers.

8 Therefore that field was called the field of blood to this day.

9 Then was fulfilled that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying, "And they took the thirty silver pieces, the price of him who was honored, whom they of the sons of Israel honored."

10 And they gave them for the field of the potter, as the Lord appointed me.

11 Now Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked him, saying, "Are you the king of the Jews?" Jesus said to him, "You say so."

12 And while he was being accused by the high priests and the elders, he answered no one.

13 Then Pilate says to him, "Do you not hear how many things they testify against you?"

14 And he did not answer him to not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.

15 Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release to the crowd one prisoner whom they wanted.

16 They then had a notable prisoner called Barabbas.

17 When they had gathered together, Pilate said to them, "Whom do you want me to release to you: Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?"

18 He knew that it was because of envy that they had delivered Him.

19 While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, "Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered much today in a dream because of him."

20 But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.

21 The governor answered and said to them, 'Whom do you want from the two that I should release to you?' Then they said, 'Barabbas.'

22 Pilate says to them, "What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?" They all say to him, "Let him be crucified!"

23 The governor said, "Why, what evil did he do?" But they cried out all the more, saying, "Let him be crucified."

24 When Pilate saw that he was profiting no one, but rather an uproar was happening, he took water and washed his hands before the crowd, saying, "I am innocent of the blood of this righteous man; you will see to it."

25 And all the people answered and said, "His blood be on us and on our children."

26 Then he released Barabbas to them; but after scourging Jesus, he delivered him up to be crucified.

27 Then the soldiers of the governor, having received Jesus into the praetorium, gathered the whole cohort against him.

28 And stripping him, they put a scarlet robe around him.

29 And having plaited a crown of thorns, they laid it on His head, and a reed in His right hand, and having knelt before Him, they mocked Him, saying, "Rejoice, King of the Jews."

30 And spitting on him, they took the reed and struck him on the head.

31 And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the robe and put his own garments on him, and led him away to crucify him.

32 And as they were going out, they found a man of Cyrene by the name Simon; they compelled this man to take his cross.

33 And having come to a place called Golgotha, which is called "Place of a Skull."

34 They gave him vinegar mingled with gall to drink, and after tasting it, he did not want to drink.

35 And having crucified him, they divided his garments among themselves, casting lots, so that what was said by the prophet might be fulfilled: "They divided my garments among themselves, and for my clothing they cast lots."

36 And those sitting watched him there.

37 and they laid over His head the charge against Him, which was written: "This is Jesus, the King of the Jews."

38 Then two robbers are crucified with him, one on the right and one on the left.

39 And those who passed by blasphemed him, moving their heads.

40 and saying, "You who destroy the temple and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, go down from the cross."

41 Likewise also the chief priests, mocking, together with the scribes and elders, said.

42 He saved another; he is not able to save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him now go down from the cross, and we will believe in him.

43 He has trusted in God; let Him deliver him now if He wants him, for He said, "I am the Son of God."

44 The robbers who were crucified with him also reproached him in the same way.

45 And from the sixth hour there came darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.

46 And about the ninth hour Jesus cried aloud with a great voice, saying, "Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?" that is, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"

47 But some of those standing there, when they heard it, were saying, "He calls Elijah."

48 And immediately one of them ran and took a sponge, filled it with vinegar, and put it around a reed and gave him a drink.

49 But the remaining said, "Leave him alone; let us see if Elijah comes to save him."

50 And Jesus, having cried out again with a great voice, yielded up his spirit.

51 And behold, the veil of the temple was split into two from above to below; and the earth was shaken, and the rocks were split.

52 And the tombs were opened, and many bodies of the sleeping holy ones were raised.

53 And going out of the tombs after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city and were manifested to many.

54 When the centurion and those who were keeping Jesus with him saw the earthquake and the things that happened, they feared greatly, saying, "Truly this was the Son of God."

55 And there were many women there, from afar beholding, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him.

56 Among them were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

57 When it was evening, a rich man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea, and who himself had become a disciple of Jesus, came.

58 This man went near to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus; then Pilate commanded the body to be given back.

59 And taking the body, Joseph wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.

60 And he put it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn in the rock; and having rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb, he departed.

61 And there was Mary the Magdalene and the other Mary sitting opposite the tomb.

62 The next day, which is after the preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.

63 Say, "Sir, we remember that that deceiver said while still living, 'After three days I am raised.'"

64 Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night and steal him and say to the people, "He has been raised from the dead," and the last error will be worse than the first.

65 But Pilate said to them, "You have a watch; depart, make it secure as you know."

66 So they went and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone with the watch.


## Chapter 28

1 Now late on the Sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to behold the tomb.

2 And see, a great earthquake happened, for an angel of the Lord, having gone down from heaven and having come near, rolled away the stone from the door and sat on top of it.

3 His countenance was like lightning, and his garment white as snow.

4 And from fear of him the keepers were shaken and became like dead men.

5 The angel answered and said to the women, "Do not be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus who has been crucified."

6 He is not here, for He has been raised just as He said; come, see the place where the Lord was lying.

7 And quickly go and say to his disciples that he has been raised from the dead, and see, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him; see, I have told you.

8 And having gone out quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to report to his disciples.

9 As they were going to report to his disciples, see, Jesus met them, saying, "Rejoice!" And they came near and seized his feet and worshipped him.

10 Then Jesus says to them, "Do not fear; depart, report to my brothers that they should depart into Galilee, and there they will see me."

11 Now as they were going, see, some of the watch came into the city and reported to the high priests all that had happened.

12 And having gathered with the elders, taken counsel, they gave sufficient silver to the soldiers.

13 Say, "His disciples came at night and stole him while we were sleeping."

14 And if this is heard by the governor, we will persuade him and make you carefree.

15 And those who took the silver did as they were taught, and this word was spread abroad among Jews until this day.

16 Then the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had appointed them.

17 And seeing him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.

18 And having come near, Jesus spoke to them, saying, "All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth."

19 Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, baptizing them into the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.

20 teaching them to keep all things, whatever I commanded you; and see, I am with you all the days until the consummation of the age. Amen.


# MARK

## Chapter 1

1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

2 As it is written in the prophets: See, I am sending my angel before your face, who will prepare your way ahead of you.

3 A voice crying in the wilderness: prepare the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.

4 John appeared, baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

5 All the region of Judea and those of Jerusalem were going out to him, and they were all being baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.

6 Now John was dressed in camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and was eating locusts and wild honey.

7 And he preached, saying, "He who is stronger than I comes after me, of whom I am not sufficient, having stooped, to loose the strap of his sandals."

8 I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.

9 And it happened in those days that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

10 And immediately, while going up from the water, he saw the heavens being split and the Spirit, like a dove, going down upon him.

11 and a voice came from the heavens: "You are my Son, the Beloved; in you I am well pleased."

12 And immediately the Spirit cast him out into the wilderness.

13 And he was there in the wilderness forty days being tested by Satan, and he was with the beasts, and the angels were serving him.

14 After John was delivered, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God.

15 and saying, "The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God has drawn near. Repent and believe in the gospel."

16 As he was walking along the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a casting net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

17 And Jesus said to them, "Come after me, and I will do to make you become fishermen of men."

18 And immediately, having left their nets, they followed him.

19 And advancing a little from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, and them in the boat restoring the nets.

20 And immediately he called them, and they, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, departed after him.

21 And they enter into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath He entered into the synagogue and taught.

22 They were astonished at his teaching, for he was teaching them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.

23 And there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit, and he cried out.

24 Let alone. What is there to us and to you, Jesus the Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you, who you are—the Holy One of God.

25 And Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be muzzled and come out of him."

26 And convulsing him, the unclean spirit cried out with a loud voice and went out from him.

27 They were all astonished, so that they began to dispute among themselves, saying, "What is this? What is this new teaching? For with authority He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him."

28 And the news about him went out immediately into the whole region around Galilee.

29 And immediately, going out from the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

30 But Simon's mother-in-law was reclining, sick with a fever, and immediately they tell him about her.

31 And coming near, he raised her, having seized her hand, and the fever immediately left her, and she began to serve them.

32 When evening came, after the sun had set, they were bringing to Him all those who were sick and those who were demon-possessed.

33 And the whole city was gathered together at the door.

34 And he healed many who were sick with various diseases and cast out many demons, and he would not allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.

35 And rising very early before day, he went out and departed to a wilderness place, and there he prayed.

36 And Simon followed after him, and those who were with him.

37 And having found him, they say to him, "All are seeking you."

38 And he says to them, "Let us go into the nearby towns so that I may preach there also, for this reason I have gone out."

39 He was preaching in their synagogues throughout all Galilee and casting out the demons.

40 And a leper comes to him, imploring him and kneeling before him and saying to him, "If you are willing, you are able to cleanse me."

41 Jesus, having compassion, stretched out his hand and touched him, and says to him, "I am willing; be cleansed."

42 And immediately when He spoke, the leprosy departed from him and he was cleansed.

43 And groaning inwardly at him, immediately he cast him out.

44 He said to him, "See that you say nothing to anyone; but depart, show yourself to the priest and offer for your purification what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them."

45 But he went out and began to preach much and to spread abroad the word, so that he was no longer able to openly enter into the city, but was outside in wilderness places; and they came to him from every quarter.


## Chapter 2

1 Again he entered Capernaum after some days, and it was heard that he was at home.

2 And immediately many gathered, so that there was no longer room even at the door, and he spoke the word to them.

3 And they come to him, bearing a paralytic, taken up by four men.

4 And not being able to come nigh to him because of the crowd, they uncovered the roof where he was, and breaking it up, they let down the bed on which the paralytic reclined.

5 When Jesus saw their faith, he says to the paralytic, "Child, your sins are forgiven."

6 But some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts.

7 Why does this man speak such blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but one, God?

8 Jesus immediately recognized in His Spirit that they were reasoning among themselves, and He said to them, "Why are you reasoning these things in your hearts?"

9 What is easier to say to the paralytic, "Your sins are forgiven," or to say, "Rise, take up your bed, and walk"?

10 But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority to forgive sins on earth, he says to the paralytic,

11 I say to you, rise and take your bed and depart to your house.

12 He got up immediately, and taking his bed, went out in front of all of them, so that all were amazed and glorified God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this."

13 And he went out again beside the sea, and all the crowd was coming to him, and he was teaching them.

14 And passing by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax office, and he says to him, "Follow me"; and rising, he followed him.

15 And it happened, while he was reclining at table in his house, that many publicans and sinners were reclining with Jesus and his disciples; for there were many, and they followed him.

16 When the scribes and the Pharisees saw him eating with the publicans and sinners, they said to his disciples, "Why does he eat and drink with publicans and sinners?"

17 And when Jesus heard, he says to them, "Those who are strong have no need of a physician, but those who are sick; I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance."

18 The disciples of John and those of the Pharisees were fasting, and they come and say to him, "Why do the disciples of John and those of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?"

19 And Jesus said to them, "Are the sons of the bridechamber able to fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long a time as they have the bridegroom with them, they are not able to fast."

20 But the days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.

21 And no one sews a patch of new cloth onto an old garment, otherwise the new fullness takes from the old, and a worse division happens.

22 And no one casts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise, the new wine bursts the wineskins, and the wine pours out, and the wineskins are destroyed, but new wine must be put into new wineskins.

23 And it happened that He was passing through the grain fields on the Sabbath, and His disciples began to make their way plucking the ears of grain.

24 And the Pharisees said to him, "See, what are they doing on the Sabbath that is not lawful?"

25 And he said to them, "Did you never read what David did when he had need and he and those with him were hungry?"

26 He entered into the house of God in the time of Abiathar the high priest and ate the bread of the purpose, which it is not lawful for anyone to eat except the priests, and he also gave it to those who were with him.

27 And he said to them, "The Sabbath came into being for the sake of man, not man for the sake of the Sabbath."

28 So the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.


## Chapter 3

1 And he entered again into the synagogue, and a man was there having a withered hand.

2 And they were watching Him closely to see if He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.

3 And He says to the man having the withered hand, "Raise yourself into the middle."

4 And He says to them, "Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save a soul or to kill?" But they were silent.

5 And looking around at them with wrath, being grieved at the blindness of their heart, He says to the man, "Stretch out your hand." And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored whole as the other.

6 The Pharisees went out and immediately began to form a council with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

7 And Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judea.

8 And from Jerusalem and from Idumaea and beyond the Jordan, and those around Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, having heard how many things he was doing, came to him.

9 And he said to his disciples that a small boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they would not press on him.

10 For he had healed many, so that as many as had scourges were falling upon him in order to touch him.

11 And the unclean spirits, whenever they beheld Him, fell down before Him and cried out, saying, "You are the Son of God."

12 And he rebuked them much that they should not make him manifest.

13 He went up into the mountain and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they came to him.

14 And he did twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them to preach.

15 and to have authority to heal the diseases and to cast out the demons.

16 And he laid on Simon the name Peter.

17 And James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James, and he laid on them the names Boanerges, which is Sons of Thunder.

18 and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite.

19 and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed Him; and they went into a house.

20 And a crowd comes together again, so that they were not able even to eat bread.

21 When his family heard about it, they went out to seize him, for they were saying, "He has lost his mind."

22 And the scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, "He has Beelzebul," and, "By the ruler of the demons he casts out the demons."

23 And after calling them, he said to them in parables, "How can Satan cast out Satan?"

24 And if a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

25 And if a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

26 And if Satan has risen up against himself and is divided, he is not able to stand, but has an end.

27 No one is able to enter the strong man's house and plunder his vessels unless he first binds the strong man, and then he will plunder his house.

28 Amen I say to you that all sins will be forgiven the sons of men, and whatever blasphemies they may blaspheme.

29 But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit does not have forgiveness forever, but is guilty of eternal judgment.

30 for they were saying, "He has an unclean spirit."

31 Then his brothers and his mother came and, standing outside, sent for him, calling him.

32 A crowd was sitting around him, and they said to him, "See, your mother and your brothers are outside seeking you."

33 And he answered them, saying, "Who is my mother or my brothers?"

34 And looking around at those sitting in a circle about him, he says, "See, my mother and my brothers."

35 For whoever does the will of God, this person is my brother and my sister and my mother.


## Chapter 4

1 And again he began to teach by the sea, and a large crowd gathered to him, so that he entered into the boat and sat down on the sea, and all the crowd was on the land by the sea.

2 He was teaching them many things in parables, and he was saying to them in his teaching,

3 Hear, see, the one sowing went out to sow.

4 And it happened while sowing that some seed fell beside the way, and the birds of heaven came and devoured it.

5 Another seed fell on the stony ground where it did not have much earth, and immediately it sprang up because it did not have depth of earth.

6 But when the sun rose, it was scorched, and because it had no root, it withered away.

7 And another fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

8 and another fell into the good soil and was giving fruit that went up and grew, and it bore in one thirty, and in one sixty, and in one hundred.

9 And he said to them, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear."

10 When he was alone, those around him with the twelve asked him about the parable.

11 And he said to them, "To you has been given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God, but to those outside, all things happen in parables."

12 so that looking they may look but not see, and hearing they may hear but not understand, lest they turn back and their sins be forgiven them.

13 And he says to them, "Do you not know this parable, and how will you know all the parables?"

14 The one sowing the word sows.

15 These are the ones beside the way where the word is sown, and when they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in their hearts.

16 And these likewise are the ones sown on stony ground, who when they hear the word immediately receive it with joy.

17 and they have no root in themselves but are temporary; then, when tribulation or persecution happens because of the word, they are immediately offended.

18 These are the ones sown among the thorns; these are the ones who hear the word.

19 the anxieties of this age and the deceit of riches and the desires for remaining things enter and choke the word and it becomes unfruitful.

20 And these are those who were sown on the good earth, who hear the word and receive it and bear fruit, one thirty, one sixty, and one a hundred.

21 And he said to them, "Does a lamp come to be put under a bushel or under a bed, not to be laid on a lampstand?"

22 For there is nothing secret that will not be revealed, nor anything that has been hidden that will not come to light.

23 If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.

24 And he said to them, "Look at what you hear: with the measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be added to you who hear."

25 For whoever has will be given to him, and whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.

26 And he said, "Thus is the kingdom of God as if a man should cast the seed upon the earth."

27 and he sleeps and is roused night and day, and the seed sprouts and grows up—how, he himself does not know.

28 For the earth bears fruit of its own accord, first the grass, then the ear, then the full wheat in the ear.

29 when the fruit is delivered, he immediately sends the sickle, because the harvest is present.

30 And he was saying, "To what should we liken the kingdom of God, or by what parable should we arrive at it?"

31 Like a grain of mustard, which when it is sown upon the earth is smaller than all the seeds that are upon the earth.

32 And when it is sown, it goes up and becomes greater than all the herbs, and it produces great branches, so that the birds of the sky are able to nest under its shadow.

33 And with many such parables he spoke the word to them as they were able to hear.

34 But without a parable he did not speak to them, but privately to his own disciples he determined everything.

35 And he says to them on that day, when evening came, "Let us pass through to the other side."

36 And leaving the crowd, they receive him as he was in the boat, and there were also other boats with him.

37 And a great storm of wind arose, and the waves beat against the boat, so that it was already being filled.

38 And he was in the stern, sleeping on the pillow, and they aroused him and said to him, "Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?"

39 He got up, rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, "Be silent! Be muzzled!" Then the wind ceased, and a great calm came.

40 He said to them, "Why are you so fearful? How do you not have faith?"

41 Then they feared a great fear and said to one another, "Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?"


## Chapter 5

1 And they came to the other side of the sea, to the region of the Gadarenes.

2 And as he was going out of the boat, immediately a man from the tombs met him, with an unclean spirit.

3 He had his dwelling among the tombs, and no one was able to bind him even with chains.

4 because he had often been bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been plucked apart by him and the fetters shattered, and no one was able to tame him.

5 Always, night and day, in the mountains and in the tombs, he was crying out and cutting himself with stones.

6 And when he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and worshipped him.

7 Crying out with a loud voice, he said, "What do I have to do with you, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me."

8 For He was saying to it, "Go out of the man, you unclean spirit."

9 And he asked him, "What is your name?" And he answered, saying, "My name is Legion, for we are many."

10 And he kept begging him urgently not to send them away outside the region.

11 And there was a great herd of swine feeding near the mountains.

12 And all the demons begged him, saying, "Send us into the swine, so that we may enter into them."

13 And immediately Jesus permitted them, and the unclean spirits came out and entered the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep place into the sea—there were about two thousand—and they were choked in the sea.

14 The ones feeding the swine fled and announced it in the city and in the fields, and they went out to see what it was that had happened.

15 And they come to Jesus and behold the one who had been demon-possessed sitting and clothed and being sober-minded, the one who had had the legion; and they were afraid.

16 And those who had seen it declared to them how it happened to the one who had been demon-possessed, and about the swine.

17 And they began to beg him to depart from their borders.

18 And when he entered the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed begged him to be with him.

19 But Jesus did not allow him, but said to him, "Go home to your own people and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you."

20 And he departed and began to preach in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him, and all marveled.

21 And when Jesus had crossed over again in the boat to the other side, a great crowd gathered around him, and he was beside the sea.

22 And see, one of the synagogue rulers comes, by name Jairus, and upon seeing him falls at his feet.

23 He kept imploring him insistently, saying, "My little daughter is at the point of death; please come and lay your hands on her so that she may be made well and live."

24 And he departed with him, and a large crowd followed him and thronged him.

25 And a woman was there who had a flow of blood for twelve years.

26 And having suffered much under many physicians and spent all that she had and benefited nothing but rather having come into a worse condition.

27 Hearing about Jesus, she came in the crowd behind and touched his garment.

28 For she kept saying, "Even if I touch his garments, I will be made well."

29 And immediately the spring of her blood withered, and she knew in her body that she was healed of the scourge.

30 And immediately Jesus, recognizing in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd and said, "Who touched my garments?"

31 And his disciples were saying to him, "You see the crowd thronging you, and yet you say, 'Who touched me?'"

32 And he looked around to see the woman who had done this.

33 The woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell before him and told him all the truth.

34 And he said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well. Depart in peace, and be whole of your scourge."

35 While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler's house, saying, "Your daughter has died. Why do you still trouble the teacher?"

36 But Jesus, immediately hearing the word being spoken, says to the synagogue ruler, "Do not fear; only believe."

37 And he did not allow anyone to follow him except Peter and James and John the brother of James.

38 And he comes to the house of the synagogue ruler and beholds an uproar, with people weeping and wailing loudly.

39 After entering, he says to them, "Why are you troubled and weeping? The child has not died, but is sleeping."

40 And they laughed him to scorn; but he, having cast out all, takes the father of the child and the mother and those with him, and enters where the child was reclining.

41 Seizing the hand of the child, he says to her, "Talitha cumi," which is interpreted, "Girl, I say to you, raise yourself."

42 And immediately the girl rose and walked, for she was twelve years old, and they were amazed with a great amazement.

43 And he commanded them much that no one should know this, and he said to give her something to eat.


## Chapter 6

1 And he went out from there and came into his own homeland, and his disciples follow him.

2 When the Sabbath came, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard were astonished, saying, "Where did this man get these things? And what is the wisdom that has been given to him, that even such powers come through his hands?"

3 Is this not the carpenter, the Son of Mary, and the brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon, and are not His sisters here with us? And they were offended at Him.

4 And Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not dishonored except in his homeland, and among his kinsmen, and in his own household."

5 And he was not able to do any power there, except that he laid hands on a few sick people and healed them.

6 And he marveled because of their unbelief, and he went about the villages round about teaching.

7 He calls to himself the twelve and began to send them out two by two and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

8 He commanded them that no one take nothing for the way except a rod only—no bag, no bread, no bronze in the belt.

9 but bound on sandals and not putting on two tunics.

10 And he said to them, "Wherever you enter into a house, remain there until you go out from that place."

11 And as many as may not receive you nor hear you, as you go out from there, shake off the dust under your feet as a testimony against them. Truly I say to you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that city.

12 And going out, they preached that people should repent.

13 And they cast out many demons and anointed many sick people with oil and healed them.

14 King Herod heard, for his name had become well known, and he said, "John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him."

15 Some said, "He is Elijah," and others said, "He is a prophet," or like one of the prophets.

16 When Herod heard, he said, "John whom I beheaded—this one has been raised from the dead."

17 For Herod himself had sent and seized John and bound him in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip, because he had married her.

18 For John said to Herod, "It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife."

19 But Herodias held it against him and wanted to kill him, and was not able.

20 For Herod feared John, knowing him to be a righteous and holy man, and he kept him safe; and when he heard him, he did much, and he heard him gladly.

21 And when a convenient day came on which Herod on his birthday did a supper for his great men and the captains and the first men of Galilee,

22 And when the daughter of Herodias herself entered and danced and pleased Herod and those reclining with him, the king said to the girl, "Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you."

23 He swore to her, "Whatever you ask me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom."

24 She went out and said to her mother, "What should I ask for?" And she said, "The head of John the Baptist."

25 She entered immediately with haste to the king and asked, saying, "I want you to give me at once on a platter the head of John the Baptist."

26 The king, having become deeply grieved, did not want to reject her because of the oaths and those reclining with him.

27 And immediately the king, having sent an executioner, commanded that his head be brought.

28 He went and beheaded him in the prison, and brought his head on a platter and gave it to the girl, and the girl gave it to her mother.

29 And having heard this, his disciples came and took his corpse and put it in a tomb.

30 The apostles gathered to Jesus and reported to him everything, both what they had done and what they had taught.

31 He said to them, "Come by yourselves to a private wilderness place and rest a while," for many were coming and departing, and they did not even have opportunity to eat.

32 So they departed by boat to a remote place by themselves.

33 When the crowds saw them departing, many recognized him and ran together on foot from all the cities to that place, and they went before them and came together to him.

34 And going out, Jesus saw a great crowd and had compassion on them, because they were like sheep not having a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

35 And when much time had already passed, his disciples came near to him and said, "This place is a wilderness, and it is already a late hour."

36 Release them so that departing into the fields and villages round about they may buy bread for themselves, for they do not have what to eat.

37 He answered and said to them, "You give them something to eat." And they said to him, "Shall we go and buy bread for two hundred denarii and give it to them to eat?"

38 He says to them, "How many loaves do you have? Depart and see." And having known, they say, "Five, and two fish."

39 And he commanded them to make all recline in companies on the green grass.

40 So they reclined in ranks, in groups of hundreds and of fifties.

41 And taking the five loaves and the two fish, having received sight into heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves and gave to his disciples that they might set them before the people, and he divided the two fish among all.

42 And all ate and were satisfied.

43 And they took up twelve baskets full of fragments, and also of the fish.

44 And the ones who ate the loaves were about five thousand men.

45 Immediately he compelled his disciples to enter the boat and go before him to the other side, toward Bethsaida, while he himself released the crowd.

46 Bidding them farewell, he departed to the mountain to pray.

47 When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.

48 He saw them being tormented as they were rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, and about the fourth watch of the night he comes to them, walking on the sea, and he wanted to pass by them.

49 But when they saw him walking on the sea, they thought it was a spirit, and they cried out.

50 For all saw him and were troubled, and immediately he spoke with them and says to them, "Be of good cheer, I am; do not fear."

51 He went up to them into the boat, and the wind ceased, and they were utterly amazed among themselves and marveled.

52 For they did not understand about the bread; for their heart was callous.

53 And crossing over, they came to the land of Gennesaret and drew to the shore.

54 When they had gone out of the boat, the people immediately recognized him.

55 They ran throughout that whole region and began to carry about on beds those who were sick, wherever they heard that he was.

56 And wherever he entered, into villages or cities or fields, in the marketplaces they put the sick and begged him that they might touch even the hem of his garment, and as many as touched him were made well.


## Chapter 7

1 And the Pharisees gather around him, and some of the scribes who have come from Jerusalem.

2 Seeing some of his disciples eating bread with common hands—that is, unwashed—they blamed them.

3 For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands with a fist, seizing the tradition of the elders.

4 And from the marketplace, unless they baptize, they do not eat, and there are many other things that they received to observe: washings of cups and pots and brazen vessels and beds.

5 Then the Pharisees and the scribes ask him, "Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashed hands?"

6 He answered and said to them, "Isaiah rightly prophesied about you hypocrites, as it is written: 'This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.'"

7 In vain they worship me, teaching as teachings the commandments of men.

8 Laying aside the commandment of God, you seize the tradition of men — washings of pots and cups, and you do many other such like things.

9 And he said to them, "You do well at rejecting the commandment of God so that you may keep your tradition."

10 For Moses said, "Honor your father and your mother," and, "The one who reviles father or mother must certainly die."

11 But you say, if a man says to his father or mother, "Corban" (that is, a gift): whatever you might have profited from me.

12 And you no longer allow him to do anything for his father or his mother.

13 Nullifying the word of God by your tradition, which you have delivered; and you do many such things.

14 After calling the whole crowd, he said to them, "Hear me, all of you, and understand."

15 no one from outside the man entering into him is able to defile him, but the things that go out from him, those are what defile the man.

16 If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.

17 When he had entered a house away from the crowd, his disciples asked him about the parable.

18 And he says to them, "So also you are senseless. Do you not understand that everything from the outside entering into the man is not able to defile him?"

19 because it does not enter his heart but into his belly and goes out into the draught, cleansing all foods.

20 "He was saying that what goes out of the man—that defiles the man."

21 For from within, out of the heart of man, come forth evil reasonings, adulteries, fornications, murders.

22 thefts, greed, wickedness, deceit, licentiousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness.

23 All these evil things go out from within and defile a person.

24 And rising from there, he departed into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entering into a house, he wanted no one to know, and he was not able to escape notice.

25 For a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit heard about him, and she came and fell at his feet.

26 The woman was Greek, a Syrophoenician by descent, and she asked him to cast the demon out of her daughter.

27 Jesus said to her, "First let the children be satisfied, for it is not good to take the children's bread and cast it to the dogs."

28 But she answered and says to him, "Yes, Lord; for even the dogs under the table eat from the children's crumbs."

29 And he said to her, "Because of this word, depart; the demon has gone out of your daughter."

30 And having departed to her house, she found the demon had gone out and the daughter lying on the bed.

31 And again going out from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, he came to the sea of Galilee, through the middle of the borders of Decapolis.

32 And they bear to him a mute who has an impediment in his speech, and they beg him to lay his hand on him.

33 Receiving him away from the crowd by himself, he cast his fingers into his ears, and spitting, he touched his tongue.

34 Then, receiving sight and looking up to heaven, he groaned and says to him, "Ephphatha," which means, "Be opened."

35 And immediately his ears were opened, and the bond of his tongue was loosed, and he spoke rightly.

36 And he commanded them to tell no one; but the more he commanded them, the more abundantly they preached.

37 And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, "He has done all things well; he causes both the deaf to hear and the mute to speak."


## Chapter 8

1 In those days, with a very great crowd being present and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples and says to them.

2 I have compassion on the crowd, because they have already remained with me three days and have nothing to eat.

3 If I release them fasting to their own houses, they will faint on the way, for some of them have come from afar.

4 His disciples answered him, "Where will anyone be able to satisfy these people with bread here in a wilderness?"

5 He asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" And they said, "Seven."

6 And he commanded the crowd to recline on the earth, and taking the seven loaves, having given thanks, he broke them and gave them to his disciples so that they might set them before the people, and they set them before the crowd.

7 and they had a few little fish, and having blessed them, he said to set these also before them.

8 So they ate and were satisfied, and they took up seven baskets full of leftover fragments.

9 And those who ate were about four thousand, and he released them.

10 And immediately, having entered into the boat with his disciples, he came to the parts of Dalmanutha.

11 And the Pharisees went out and began to dispute with him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, testing him.

12 And sighing deeply in his spirit, he says, "Why does this generation seek a sign? Truly I say to you, no sign will be given to this generation."

13 After leaving them, he entered again into the boat and departed to the opposite shore.

14 And they forgot to take bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.

15 And he commanded them, saying, "See, look out for the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod."

16 And they reasoned with one another, saying, "We have no bread."

17 And Jesus, knowing, says to them, "Why are you reasoning because you do not have bread? Do you not yet understand nor comprehend? Do you still have your heart callous?"

18 Having eyes, do you not look? And having ears, do you not hear? And do you not remember?

19 When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments did you take? They said to him, "Twelve."

20 When I broke the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments did you take? They said, "Seven."

21 And he said to them, "How do you not understand?"

22 He comes to Bethsaida, and they bear a blind man to him and beg him to touch him.

23 Taking hold of the hand of the blind man, he led him out of the village, and after spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on him, he asked him if he could see anything.

24 Looking up, he said, "I see men, for I see them walking like trees."

25 Then again he laid his hands on his eyes, and made him look up, and he was restored and saw everyone clearly.

26 He sent him to his house, saying, "Do not enter the village, and do not tell anyone in the village."

27 Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea Philippi, and on the way he asked his disciples, saying to them, "Who do men say that I am?"

28 They answered, "John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and others, one of the prophets."

29 And he says to them, "But you, who do you say that I am?" Answering, Peter says to him, "You are the Christ."

30 And He rebuked them that they should tell no one about Him.

31 He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer much and be rejected by the elders, high priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise.

32 He was speaking the word with boldness, and Peter, taking him aside, began to rebuke him.

33 But when he turned back and saw his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, "Depart behind me, Satan, for you are not thinking the things of God, but the things of men."

34 And calling the crowd together with his disciples, he said to them, "Whoever wants to come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me."

35 For whoever wants to save his soul will destroy it, but whoever destroys his soul for my sake and for the gospel will save it.

36 For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and suffers the loss of his soul?

37 Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?

38 For whoever is ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man will also be ashamed of him when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.


## Chapter 9

1 And he said to them, "Amen, I say to you, that there are some of those standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God having come in power."

2 And after six days Jesus receives Peter and James and John and offers them up into a high mountain by themselves alone, and he was transfigured before them.

3 And his garments became gleaming white, exceedingly white as snow, such that no fuller on earth is able to make them white.

4 And Elijah appeared to them together with Moses, and they were conversing with Jesus.

5 And answering, Peter says to Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here, and let us do three tabernacles: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."

6 For they did not know what to speak, for they were sore afraid.

7 And a cloud came overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, "This is My beloved Son; hear Him."

8 And suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone except Jesus alone with them.

9 As they were going down from the mountain, he commanded them not to declare to anyone what they had seen until the Son of Man should rise from the dead.

10 They seized the word within themselves, disputing what it means to rise from the dead.

11 And they asked him, saying, "The scribes say that Elijah must come first."

12 He answered and said to them, "Elijah indeed comes first and restores all things; and how is it written about the Son of Man that he must suffer much and be set at nought?"

13 But I say to you that Elijah also has come, and they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it is written about him.

14 And coming to the disciples, he saw a great crowd around them and scribes disputing with them.

15 And immediately, all the crowd, seeing him, was greatly alarmed, and running to him, they greeted him.

16 And he asked the scribes, "Why are you disputing with them?"

17 One of the crowd answered and said, "Teacher, I brought my son to you, who has a mute spirit."

18 And wherever it overtakes him, it bursts him, and he foams, and he gnashes his teeth, and he withers away; and I said to your disciples that they should cast it out, and they were not able.

19 Answering, he says to him, "O unbelieving generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bear him to me."

20 They bore him to Him, and having seen Him, immediately the spirit rent him, and having fallen upon the earth, he wallowed, foaming.

21 He asked his father, "How long is it since this happened to him?" And he said, "From childhood."

22 Often it has even cast him into fire and into water to destroy him. But if you are able to do anything, help us, having compassion on us.

23 But Jesus said to him, "If you are able to believe, all things are able for the one who believes."

24 And immediately, crying out with tears, the father of the child said, "I believe, Lord; help my unbelief."

25 When Jesus saw that a crowd was coming running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, "You dumb and mute spirit, I command you, go out of him and never enter him again."

26 And after crying out and rending him much, it went out, and he became as if dead, so that many said, "He has died."

27 But Jesus, seizing his hand, raised him up, and he arose.

28 And after he entered a house, his disciples asked him privately, "Why were we unable to cast it out?"

29 And he said to them, "This kind cannot go out by any means except by prayer and fasting."

30 And going out from there, they were passing through Galilee, and he did not want anyone to know it.

31 For he was teaching his disciples and saying to them, "The Son of Man is delivered into the hands of men, and they will kill him, and having been killed, on the third day he will rise."

32 But they did not know the word, and they feared to ask him.

33 He came to Capernaum, and after coming into the house he asked them, "What were you reasoning among yourselves on the way?"

34 But they were silent, for they had reasoned with one another on the way who was greatest.

35 And sitting down, he called the twelve and says to them, "If anyone wants to be first, he will be last of all and servant of all."

36 And taking a child, he stood it in the middle of them, and taking it up in his arms, he said to them.

37 Whoever receives one of such children in my name receives me; and whoever receives me does not receive me but the one who sent me.

38 John answered him, saying, "Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, who does not follow us, and we forbade him because he does not follow us."

39 But Jesus said, "Do not forbid him, for no one who will do a power in my name will be able to quickly revile me."

40 Whoever is not against you is for you.

41 For whoever gives you a cup of water to drink in my name, because you are Christ's, amen I say to you, he will certainly not lose his reward.

42 And whoever offends one of the small ones who believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone be hung around his neck and that he be cast into the sea.

43 And if your hand offends you, cut it off; it is good for you to enter life maimed, rather than having two hands to depart into hell, into the unquenchable fire.

44 where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.

45 And if your foot offends you, cut it off; it is good for you to enter life lame than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the unquenchable fire.

46 where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.

47 And if your eye offends you, cast it out; it is good for you to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye rather than, having two eyes, to be cast into the hell of fire.

48 where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.

49 For all will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be salted with salt.

50 Salt is good, but if the salt loses its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.


## Chapter 10

1 Rising from there, he comes into the borders of Judea by the other side of the Jordan, and crowds go with him again, and as he was accustomed, he again taught them.

2 The Pharisees came near and asked him if it is lawful for a man to release his wife, testing him.

3 And answering, he said to them, "What did Moses command you?"

4 They said, "Moses permitted a book of divorcement to be written and to release her."

5 And answering, Jesus said to them, "Because of your hardness of heart he wrote this commandment for you."

6 But from the beginning of creation, God made them male and female.

7 For this reason a man will leave his Father and mother and cleave to his wife.

8 And the two will be one flesh, so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

9 Therefore what God has joined together, let no man separate.

10 And in the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.

11 He says to them, "Whoever releases his wife and marries another commits adultery against her."

12 If a woman releases her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.

13 And they were bringing children to him so that he might touch them, but the disciples were rebuking those who were bringing them.

14 When Jesus saw this, he was indignant and said to them, "Let the little children come to me, and do not forbid them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.

15 Amen, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a child will never enter it.

16 And having taken them up in his arms, he put his hands on them and blessed them.

17 As he was going out on the way, one ran to him and knelt before him and asked him, "Good teacher, what must I do that I may inherit eternal life?"

18 But Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except one — God."

19 You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, do not defraud, honor your father and mother.'

20 But he answered and said to him, "Teacher, all these I have kept from my youth."

21 Jesus looked at him, loved him, and said to him, "You lack one thing: go, sell whatever you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven, and come, follow me, taking up the cross."

22 But he, lowering his face at the word, departed grieving, for he had many possessions.

23 Looking around, Jesus says to his disciples, "How hardly will those who have money enter into the kingdom of God."

24 The disciples were astonished at his words, but Jesus again answered and says to them, Children, how hard it is for those who have been persuaded in their money to enter into the kingdom of God.

25 It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.

26 And they were even more astonished, saying to one another, "And who is able to be saved?"

27 Looking at them, Jesus says, "With men it is impossible, but not with God; for all things are able with God."

28 And Peter began to say to him, "See, we have left all things and have followed you."

29 Jesus answered and said, "Amen, I say to you, no one is there who has left house or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or fields for my sake and for the gospel's sake."

30 if he does not take a hundredfold now in this time houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and fields with persecutions, and in the coming age eternal life.

31 But many who are first will be last, and the last first.

32 And they were on the way going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was going before them, and they were astonished, and those who followed were afraid, and having taken again the twelve, he began to say to them the things that were about to happen to him.

33 For see, we go up to Jerusalem and the Son of Man will be delivered to the high priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death and deliver him to the nations.

34 They will mock Him and scourge Him and spit on Him and kill Him, and on the third day He will rise.

35 And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, go before him, saying, "Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask."

36 He said to them, "What do you want me to do for you?"

37 But they said to him, "Grant us that we may sit one at your right hand and one at your left in your glory."

38 But Jesus said to them, "You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink and to be baptized with the baptism with which I am baptized?"

39 They said to him, "We are able." But Jesus said to them, "The cup that I drink you will drink, and the baptism that I am baptized with you will be baptized with."

40 But to sit at my right hand and at my left is not mine to give, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared.

41 And hearing this, the ten began to be indignant about James and John.

42 Jesus called them and said to them, "You know that those who seem to rule the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

43 It will not be this way among you, but whoever wants to become great among you will be your servant.

44 And whoever among you wants to be first will be the servant of all.

45 For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his soul as a ransom in place of many.

46 They come to Jericho, and as He was going out from Jericho, along with His disciples and a considerable crowd, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind man, was sitting beside the way begging.

47 And hearing that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out and say, "Son of David, Jesus, have mercy on me."

48 And many rebuked him to be silent, but he cried out much more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

49 And standing still, Jesus said to call him; and they call the blind man, saying to him, "Be of good cheer; get up; he is calling you."

50 But he, having cast away his garment, rose up and came to Jesus.

51 And answering, Jesus says to him, "What do you want me to do for you?" And the blind man said to him, "Lord, let me receive sight."

52 And Jesus said to him, "Depart. Your faith has made you well." And immediately he received sight and followed Jesus in the way.


## Chapter 11

1 And when they draw near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, toward the Mount of Olives, he sends two of his disciples.

2 And he says to them, "Depart into the village opposite you, and immediately, as you enter it, you will find a colt bound, on which no man has ever sat; having loosed it, lead it.

3 And if anyone says to you, "What are you doing this for?" say, "The Lord has need of it," and immediately he will send it here.

4 They departed and found the colt tied at the door outside on the street, and they untied it.

5 And some of those standing there said to them, "What are you doing, loosing the colt?"

6 And they said to them just as Jesus had commanded, and they let them go.

7 And they led the colt to Jesus and laid their garments on it, and he sat on it.

8 Many spread their garments on the way, and others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the way.

9 Then those who went before and those who followed cried out, saying, "Hosanna! Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord!"

10 Blessed is the coming kingdom in the name of the Lord of our father David; Hosanna in the highest.

11 And Jesus entered into Jerusalem and into the temple, and when he had looked around at everything, the hour already being evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

12 And on the next day, when they had gone out from Bethany, he hungered.

13 And seeing a fig tree from afar having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he would find anything on it; and when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

14 Answering, Jesus said to her, "May no one ever eat fruit from you again," and his disciples were hearing this.

15 And they come to Jerusalem, and after entering the temple, Jesus began to cast out those who were selling and buying in the temple, and he overthrew the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling doves.

16 And he would not allow anyone to carry a vessel through the temple.

17 He was teaching and saying to them, "Is it not written, 'My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations'? But you have made it a cave of robbers."

18 The scribes and the high priests heard this and were seeking how they might destroy him, for they feared him, because all the crowd was astonished at his teaching.

19 And when it was late, he went out of the city.

20 And early in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots.

21 And Peter, reminded, says to him, "Rabbi, see, the fig tree which you cursed has withered."

22 Jesus answered and says to them, "Have faith in God."

23 Amen, for I say to you that whoever says to this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' and does not discern in his heart but believes that what he says happens, it will be his, whatever he says.

24 Therefore I say to you, all things whatever you ask while praying, believe that you take them, and it will be to you.

25 And when you stand praying, forgive if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father who is in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.

26 But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father who is in heaven forgive your trespasses.

27 And they came again to Jerusalem, and while he was walking in the temple, the high priests and the scribes and the elders came to him.

28 And they say to him, "By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority to do these things?"

29 But Jesus, answering, said to them, "I will also ask you one word, and you answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things."

30 The baptism of John, was it from heaven or from men? Answer me.

31 And they were reckoning among themselves, saying, "If we say 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'"

32 But if we say, 'From men,' they feared the people, for all held that John was indeed a prophet.

33 And answering, they say to Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus, answering, says to them, "Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things."


## Chapter 12

1 He began to say to them in parables: a man planted a vineyard and put a hedge around it and dug a wine vat and built a tower and let it out to husbandmen and went abroad.

2 And he sent a servant to the husbandmen at the time, so that he might take from the husbandmen some of the fruit of the vineyard.

3 But those who took him beat him and sent him away empty.

4 And again he sent another servant to them, and him they stoned and wounded in the head, and sent away shamefully treated.

5 And again he sent another; him they killed, and many others—some they beat, and some they killed.
