6 He still had one Son, his beloved; he sent him last of all to them, saying, 'They will respect my Son.'

7 But the husbandmen said among themselves, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'

8 And after taking him, they killed him and cast him out of the vineyard.

9 What then will the Lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the husbandmen and give the vineyard to others.

10 Have you not read this Scripture: "The stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner"?

11 This was from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes.

12 And they sought to seize him, but feared the crowd, for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them, and they left him and departed.

13 And they send some of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to him, so that they might catch him by a word.

14 They came and said to him, "Teacher, we know that you are true and you care about no one, for you do not look at the face of men, but you teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar or not?"

15 Should we give, or should we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, "Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius so that I may see it."

16 They brought one, and he says to them, "Whose image and inscription is this?" They said to him, "Caesar's."

17 And answering, Jesus said to them, "Repay Caesar's things to Caesar and the things of God to God." And they marveled at him.

18 And Sadducees come to him, who say that there is no resurrection, and they asked him, saying,

19 Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife behind, and leaves no children, his brother should take his wife and raise up seed for his brother.

20 There were seven brothers, and the first took a wife, and dying he did not leave behind seed.

21 The second took her and died, and he also left no seed; and the third likewise.

22 And the seven took her and did not leave seed; last of all, the woman also died.

23 In the resurrection, therefore, when they rise, whose wife will she be? For the seven had her as wife.

24 Jesus answered and said to them, "Is it not for this reason that you are deceived, because you do not know the Scriptures nor the power of God?"

25 For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in the heavens.

26 Concerning the dead, that they are raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, at the bush, how God said to him, 'I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?

27 God is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living; you therefore are greatly deceived.

28 One of the scribes came near, heard them disputing, and knowing that Jesus had answered them well, asked him, "Which commandment is the first of all?"

29 Jesus answered him, "The first of all the commandments is, 'Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one.

30 And you will love the Lord your God with your whole heart and with your whole soul and with your whole mind and with your whole strength. This is the first commandment.

31 And a second, like it, you will love your neighbor as yourself; there is no other commandment greater than these.

32 The scribe said to him, "Well, teacher, you have said the truth that God is one, and there is no other besides him."

33 And to love him with the whole heart and with the whole understanding and with the whole soul and with the whole strength, and to love the neighbor as oneself, is much more than all the burnt offerings and the sacrifices.

34 Jesus, seeing that he answered discreetly, said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God," and no one dared to ask him anything anymore.

35 And answering, Jesus said, teaching in the temple, "How do the scribes say that the Christ is the Son of David?"

36 For David himself said by the Holy Spirit, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies as a footstool under your feet."'

37 David himself calls him 'Lord.' So how can he be his son? And the large crowd was hearing him gladly.

38 He said to them in his teaching, "Look out for the scribes who want to walk around in robes and greetings in the marketplaces.

39 and chief seats in the synagogues and seats of honor at the suppers.

40 Those who devour the houses of widows and for a pretense pray far [long] prayers will take abundant judgment.

41 Jesus sat down opposite the treasury and beheld how the crowd cast bronze into the treasury, and many rich people cast much.

42 And one poor widow came and cast in two mites, which is a farthing.

43 And calling his disciples, he says to them, "Amen, I say to you that this poor widow has cast in more than all those casting into the treasury."

44 For they all cast out of what abounds to them, but she out of her want cast all that she had, her whole living.


## Chapter 13

1 As he was going out of the temple, one of his disciples says to him, "Teacher, see what manner of stones and what manner of buildings!"

2 Jesus answered and said to him, "Do you look at these great buildings? Not one stone will be left upon another that will not be destroyed."

3 As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew were asking him privately.

4 Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be completed?

5 Then Jesus, answering them, began to say, "Look, do not let anyone deceive you."

6 For many will come upon my name, saying that I am, and will deceive many.

7 But when you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be troubled; for these things must happen, but the end is not yet.

8 For nation will be raised against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be earthquakes in various places, and there will be famines and troubles; these are the beginning of birth pains.

9 But you, look out for yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils, and you will be beaten in synagogues, and you will stand before governors and kings for my sake, as a testimony to them.

10 And the gospel must first be preached to all the nations.

11 And when they lead you away, delivering you up, do not take thought beforehand about what you should speak, nor imagine; but whatever is given to you in that hour, speak that—for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

12 Brother will deliver brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise up against parents and put them to death.

13 You will be hated by all because of my name, but the one who endures to the end will be saved.

14 But when you see the abomination of desolation, which was spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it must not, let the reader understand—then let those in Judea flee into the mountains.

15 But the one on the housetop must not go down into the house nor enter to take anything out of his house.

16 And the one who is in the field must not turn back to take his garment.

17 But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days.

18 But pray that your flight may not happen in winter.

19 For those days will be a tribulation such as has not happened from the beginning of creation which God created until now, and will never happen.

20 And if the Lord had not shortened the days, no flesh would be saved; but for the sake of the chosen, whom he chose, he shortened the days.

21 And then if anyone says to you, "See, here is the Christ," or "See, there," do not believe it.

22 For false Christs and false prophets will be raised up, and they will give signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

23 But you look; see, I have foretold you all things.

24 But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light.

25 The stars of the heaven will be falling away, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.

26 and then they will see the Son of man coming in clouds with much power and glory.

27 And then he will send his angels and gather together his chosen from the four winds, from the tip of the earth to the tip of heaven.

28 Now learn the parable from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.

29 In the same way, when you see these things happening, know that it is near, at the doors.

30 Amen, I say to you that this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things happen.

31 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

32 But concerning that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, except the Father.

33 Look, watch and pray, for you do not know when the time is.

34 Like a man going on a far journey, having left his house and given authority to his servants, to each his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to watch.

35 Watch therefore, for you do not know when the master of the house comes—either late, or at midnight, or at cockcrowing, or early in the morning.

36 "so that when he comes suddenly, he will not find you sleeping."

37 But what I say to you I say to all: watch.


## Chapter 14

1 The Passover and the Unleavened Bread were two days away, and the high priests and the scribes were seeking how, having seized him by deceit, they might kill him.

2 But they were saying, "Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar among the people."

3 And while he was in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he reclined, a woman came having an alabaster jar of costly spikenard ointment, and shattering the alabaster jar, she poured it over his head.

4 But there were some who were indignant among themselves and saying, "Why has this destruction of the ointment happened?"

5 It could have been sold for over three hundred denarii and given to the poor, and they groaned inwardly at her.

6 But Jesus said, "Leave her alone; why do you provide labor for her? She has worked a good work on me.

7 For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want you are able to do good to them, but you do not always have me.

8 She did what she had; she took beforehand to anoint my body for the burying.

9 Amen I say to you, wherever this gospel is preached in the whole world, what she has done will also be spoken of as a memorial of her.

10 And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, departed to the high priests in order to deliver him to them.

11 And when they heard, they rejoiced and promised to give him silver; and he sought how he might conveniently deliver him.

12 On the first day of the unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples say to him, "Where do you want us to go and prepare so that you may eat the Passover?"

13 And he sends two of his disciples and says to them, "Depart into the city, and a man bearing a pitcher of water will meet you; follow him."

14 And wherever you enter, say to the householder, 'The Teacher says, "Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?" '

15 And he himself will show you a great upper room, spread and ready; prepare it for us there.

16 And his disciples went out and came into the city and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

17 When evening came, he came with the twelve.

18 And while they were reclining and eating, Jesus said, "Amen, I say to you that one of you will betray me—the one eating with me."

19 And they began to be grieved and to say to him, one after another, "Surely not I?" and another, "Surely not I?"

20 He answered and said to them, "One of the twelve who dips with me in the dish."

21 The Son of man departs as it has been written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed; it would be good for him if that man had not been born.

22 While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, gave it to them, and said, "Take, eat; this is my body."

23 And taking the cup, having given thanks, he gave it to them, and all drank from it.

24 And he said to them, "This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many."

25 Amen, I say to you that I will by no means drink of the fruit of the vine from now until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.

26 And after singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

27 And Jesus says to them that all of you will be offended because of me in this night, for it is written, "I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered."

28 but after I am raised, I will go before you into Galilee.

29 But Peter said to him, "Even if all are offended, yet not I."

30 And Jesus says to him, "Amen I say to you that today, in this night, before a rooster calls twice, you will deny me three times."

31 But he was saying all the more, "If I must die with you, I will never deny you." And likewise they all were saying.

32 And they come to a field whose name is Gethsemane, and he says to his disciples, "Sit here while I pray."

33 He receives Peter, James, and John with himself, and began to be alarmed and to be full of heaviness.

34 And he says to them, "My soul is deeply grieved, even to death; remain here and watch."

35 And going before a little, he fell on the earth and prayed that, if it is able, the hour might pass away from him.

36 And He said, "Abba, Father, all things are able for You; remove this cup from Me—yet not what I will, but what You will."

37 Then he comes and finds them sleeping, and he says to Peter, "Simon, are you sleeping? Were you not able to watch one hour?"

38 Watch and pray so that you do not enter into temptation; the Spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.

39 And departing again, he prayed, saying the same word.

40 And having returned, he found them again sleeping, for their eyes were weighed down, and they did not know what to answer him.

41 And he comes a third time and says to them, "Sleep the remaining time and rest—it is enough; the hour has come. See, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of the sinners."

42 Rise, let us go; see, the one betraying me has drawn near.

43 And immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, comes, and with him a great crowd with swords and clubs from the high priests and the scribes and the elders.

44 Now the one who was betraying him had given them a token, saying, "Whomever I love, it is he; seize him and lead him away assuredly."

45 And having come, he immediately came near to him and says, "Rabbi, Rabbi," and kissed him.

46 But they laid their hands on him and seized him.

47 But one of those who were present, having drawn his sword, struck the servant of the high priest and took away his ear.

48 Then Jesus answered and said to them, "Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs, to seize me?"

49 Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me. But this happened so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.

50 And they all forsook him and fled.

51 And one young man was following him, clothed with a linen cloth over his naked body, and the young men seized him.

52 But he, leaving the linen cloth, fled naked from them.

53 And they led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the high priests and the elders and the scribes come together to him.

54 And Peter followed him from afar, right into the courtyard of the high priest, and was sitting with the servants and warming himself at the light.

55 The chief priests and the whole council were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, and they were not finding any.

56 For many were bearing false witness against him, but their testimonies were not equal.

57 Some people rose up and began to bear false witness against him, saying,

58 We heard him saying, "I will destroy this temple made by hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands."

59 and not even in this way was their testimony equal.

60 And rising up, the high priest into the middle asked Jesus, saying, "Do you answer nothing? What are these testifying against you?"

61 But he was silent and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him and said to him, "You are the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"

62 Jesus said, "I am, and you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming with the clouds of heaven."

63 So the high priest, tearing his tunics, says, "What further need do we have of witnesses?

64 You heard the blasphemy. What does it appear to you? They all condemned him to be guilty of death.

65 And some began to spit on him and cover his face and strike him and say to him, "Prophesy!" And the servants were casting blows on him with the palms of their hands.

66 And while Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maidservants of the high priest came.

67 And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, "You also were with the Nazarene, Jesus."

68 But he denied it, saying, "I do not know nor understand what you are saying." And he went outside into the porch, and a rooster called.

69 And the maidservant, seeing him again, began to say to those standing nearby, "This man is one of them."

70 But he denied it again, and after a little while those standing by said to Peter again, "Truly you are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech agrees."

71 Then he began to bind himself with an oath and to swear, "I do not know this man whom you are talking about."

72 And the rooster called a second time, and Peter remembered the word that Jesus had said to him, "Before the rooster calls twice, you will deny me three times," and he laid on and wept.


## Chapter 15

1 And immediately, early in the morning, the chief priests with the elders and scribes and the whole council, having done a consultation, bound Jesus and carried him away and delivered him to Pilate.

2 Pilate asked him, "Are you the king of the Jews?" And answering, he said to him, "You say so."

3 And the chief priests were accusing him of many things.

4 Pilate again asked him, saying, "Do you not answer? See how many things they testify against you."

5 But Jesus no longer answered anything, so that Pilate marveled.

6 But at the feast he used to release to them one prisoner whom they asked.

7 Now one called Barabbas was bound with the insurrectionists who had committed murder in the dissension.

8 The crowd cried aloud and began to ask for what he always did for them.

9 Pilate answered them, saying, "Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews?"

10 He knew that the high priests had delivered him because of envy.

11 The chief priests, however, stirred up the crowd so that he would release Barabbas to them instead.

12 And Pilate answered and said to them again, "What then do you want me to do to him whom you call the king of the Jews?"

13 But they cried out again, "Crucify him."

14 But Pilate was saying to them, "What evil, then, has he done?" But they cried out all the more, "Crucify him!"

15 And Pilate, willing to do what was sufficient for the crowd, released Barabbas to them and, after scourging Jesus, delivered him over to be crucified.

16 Then the soldiers led him away within the courtyard, which is the Praetorium, and they call together the whole cohort.

17 And they put a purple robe on him, and after plaiting a thorny crown, they put it around him.

18 And they began to greet him, "Rejoice, King of the Jews!"

19 They kept striking his head with a reed and were spitting on him, and bending their knees, they worshipped him.

20 And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own garments on him, and they lead him out so that they might crucify him.

21 They compel a certain passerby, Simon a Cyrenian, coming from the field, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to take his cross.

22 And they bear him to Golgotha, a place which is interpreted, "Place of a skull."

23 They gave him wine mingled with myrrh to drink, but he did not take it.

24 And having crucified him, they divided his garments, casting lots upon them to determine who should take what.

25 It was the third hour, and they crucified him.

26 And the inscription of his charge was written on him: "The king of the Jews."

27 And with him they crucify two robbers, one on his right and one on his left.

28 And the Scripture that says, "And He was reckoned with the lawless," was fulfilled.

29 And those who passed by blasphemed him, moving their heads and saying, "Ah, you who destroy the temple and build it in three days."

30 Save yourself and go down from the cross.

31 Likewise also the high priests, mocking among themselves with the scribes, were saying, "He saved others, but he is not able to save himself."

32 Let the Christ, the king of Israel, go down now from the cross, so that we may see and believe. Even those who were crucified with him reproached him.

33 And when the sixth hour came, darkness came over the whole earth until the ninth hour.

34 And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a great voice, saying, "Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?" which is interpreted, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"

35 And some of those present, having heard it, said, "See, he calls Elijah."

36 One ran, filled a sponge with vinegar, put it on a reed, and gave him a drink, saying, "Leave him alone; let us see if Elijah comes to take him down."

37 But Jesus, letting out a great voice, gave up the ghost.

38 And the veil of the temple was split in two from top to bottom.

39 When the centurion who was standing opposite him saw that he cried out in this way and gave up the ghost, he said, "Truly this man was the Son of God."

40 There were also women be holding from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James the small and of Joses, and Salome.

41 who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him and served him; and many other women had come up with him to Jerusalem.

42 And when evening had come, since it was the preparation, that is, the day before the Sabbath.

43 Joseph from Arimathea, an honorable counsellor, who was also himself waiting for the kingdom of God, came; having dared, he entered to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.

44 Pilate marveled that he had already died, and calling the centurion, he asked him if he had died long ago.

45 And having learned from the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.

46 He bought a linen cloth, and casting Him down, wrapped Him in the linen, and placed Him in a tomb which had been hewn out of rock; and he rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.

47 But Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses were observing where he was placed.


## Chapter 16

1 When the Sabbath had passed, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might come and anoint him.

2 And very early on the first day of the week, they come to the tomb when the sun had risen.

3 They were saying to one another, "Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?"

4 And when they looked up, they behold that the stone has been rolled away, for it was very great.

5 And entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right, clothed in a white robe, and they were alarmed.

6 But he says to them, "Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has been raised; he is not here. See the place where they put him."

7 But depart, say to his disciples and to Peter that he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him, just as he told you.

8 And going out quickly, they fled from the tomb, for trembling and trance had seized them, and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.

9 Having risen early on the first day of the Sabbath, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.

10 She went and reported to those who had been with him, as they were mourning and weeping.

11 But they, having heard that he was alive and had been beheld by her, disbelieved.

12 After these things he was revealed in a different form to two of them as they were walking, going into the countryside.

13 And when they had departed, they reported to the remaining, but they did not believe even them.

14 Later, while they were reclining, he revealed himself to the eleven and reproached their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who had beheld him raised.

15 And he said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation."

16 The one who believes and is baptized will be saved, but the one who disbelieves will be condemned.

17 And these signs will attain to those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues.

18 They will take up serpents, and if they drink anything deadly, it will not hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will have good health.

19 Then the Lord, after speaking to them, was taken up into heaven and sat at the right hand of God.

20 They went out and preached everywhere, the Lord working together and confirming the word through the accompanying signs. Amen.


# LUKE

## Chapter 1

1 Since many have attempted to set in order a declaration concerning the matters that have been fully assured among us.

2 just as those who from the beginning were eye-witnesses and servants of the word delivered them to us.

3 It seemed good to me also, having carefully traced all things from the beginning, to write to you in order, most excellent Theophilus.

4 so that you may recognize the certainty of the words you were instructed in.

5 It happened in the days of Herod the king of Judea, there was a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia, and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elisabeth.

6 Both of them were righteous before God, going in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.

7 And they had no child, because Elisabeth was barren, and both were advanced in their days.

8 Now it happened that while he was executing the priest's office in the order of his course before God.

9 According to the custom of the office of the priesthood, he obtained by lot to burn incense when he entered into the temple of the Lord.

10 And all the multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of the incense.

11 And an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing at the right side of the altar of incense.

12 And Zacharias, having seen, was troubled, and fear fell upon him.

13 The angel said to him, "Do not fear, Zacharias, because your supplication has been heard, and your wife Elisabeth will bear a son to you, and you will call his name John."

14 It will be joy and exultation to you, and many will rejoice at his birth.

15 For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and he will by no means drink wine or strong drink, and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother's womb.

16 And he will turn back many of the sons of Israel to the Lord their God.

17 He himself will go before him in the Spirit and power of Elijah, to turn back the hearts of fathers to children and the disobedient to the prudence of the righteous, to prepare a people prepared for the Lord.

18 Zacharias said to the angel, "How will I know this? For I am an old man and my wife is advanced in years."

19 The angel answered and said to him, "I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and I was sent to speak to you and to preach this good news to you."

20 See, you will be silent and not able to speak until the day these things happen, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time.

21 And the people were expecting Zacharias and were marveling at his delaying in the temple.

22 When he went out, he was not able to speak to them, and they recognized that he had seen a vision in the temple; and he was beckoning to them and remained mute.

23 And it happened that when the days of his ministry were fulfilled, he departed to his house.

24 After these days Elizabeth his wife conceived and hid herself five months, saying

25 For so the Lord has done for me in the days when he beheld to take away my reproach among men.

26 In the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth.

27 to a virgin betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, from the house of David, and the name of the virgin was Mary.

28 And entering, the angel said to her, "Rejoice, O highly favored one, the Lord is with you, blessed are you among women."

29 But when she saw him, she was troubled at his word and reasoned what manner of greeting this might be.

30 The angel said to her, "Do not fear, Mary, for you have found grace with God."

31 And see, you will conceive in your womb and bear a Son, and you will call his name Jesus.

32 He will be great and will be called Son of the Most High, and the Lord God will give him the throne of David his Father.

33 And he will reign over the house of Jacob for the ages, and of his kingdom there will be no end.

34 Mary said to the angel, "How will this be, since I do not know a man?"

35 And the angel answered and said to her, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore also the holy one who is born will be called the Son of God."

36 And see, Elisabeth your kinswoman, she also has conceived a son in her old age; and this is the sixth month for her who is called barren.

37 for not a single word from God will be impossible.

38 Then Mary said, "See, the handmaid of the Lord; be it done to me according to your word," and the angel departed from her.

39 Rising up, Mary went with diligence into the hill country in those days, to a city of Judah.

40 And she entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elisabeth.

41 And it happened, when Elisabeth heard the greeting of Mary, the infant leaped in her belly, and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

42 She spoke out with a great voice and said, "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your belly."

43 And how is this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

44 For see, when the voice of your greeting came to my ears, the infant leaped in my belly with exultation.

45 Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of the things spoken to her from the Lord.

46 And Mary said, "My soul magnifies the Lord."

47 And my spirit rejoiced in God my Savior.

48 For he has looked upon the humiliation of his handmaid; for see, from now on all generations will call me blessed.

49 because the Able One has done great things for me, and holy is his name.

50 and his mercy is to generations of generations for those who fear him.

51 He has done powerful deeds with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the intent of their hearts.

52 He cast down potentates from thrones and exalted the humble.

53 He has filled those who hunger with good things, and those who are rich he has sent away empty.

54 He has helped his servant Israel, remembering mercy.

55 just as he spoke to our fathers—to Abraham and to his seed forever.

56 But Mary remained with her about three months and returned to her house.

57 Now Elisabeth's time of bearing was fulfilled, and she gave birth to a son.

58 Her neighbours and her kinsmen heard that the Lord had magnified his mercy toward her, and they rejoiced with her.

59 On the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they were calling him by the name of his father Zacharias.

60 His mother answered and said, "No, but he will be called John."

61 And they said to her, "There is no one among your kindred who is called by this name."

62 They made signs to his father as to what he wanted him to be called.

63 And having asked for a writing table, he wrote, saying, "John is his name." And all marveled.

64 And immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue, and he spoke, blessing God.

65 Fear came on all those who lived around them, and all these words were spoken about throughout the whole hill country of Judea.

66 All who heard put them in their heart, saying, "What then will this child be?" And the hand of the Lord was with him.

67 And Zacharias his father was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied, saying,

68 Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, because he visited and performed redemption for his people.

69 and raised a horn of salvation for us in the house of David his servant

70 as he spoke through the mouth of his holy prophets from the ages.

71 Salvation from our enemies and from the hand of all who hate us.

72 to do mercy with our Fathers, and to remember his holy covenant.

73 the oath which he swore to Abraham our father, to grant to us

74 Having been delivered from the hand of our enemies, we might serve him fearlessly.

75 in holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life.

76 And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways.

77 to give knowledge of salvation to his people in the forgiveness of their sins.

78 Because of the tender mercy of our God, with which the rising dawn from on high has visited us.

79 to appear to those sitting in darkness and the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.

80 And the child grew and became strong in spirit, and was in the wilderness until the day of his showing to Israel.


## Chapter 2

1 Now it happened in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus for all the world to be registered.

2 This registration first took place while Cyrenius was governing Syria.

3 And all went to register, each to his own city.

4 Now Joseph also went up from Galilee, from the city of Nazareth, into Judea, into the city of David which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David.

5 to register with Mary, who was betrothed to him, being great with child.

6 And it happened that while they were there, the days were completed for her to bear.

7 and she bore her Son, the firstborn, and wrapped Him in swaddling clothes and laid Him in the manger, because there was no place for them in the guest room.

8 And there were shepherds in the same region, abiding in the field and keeping watch by night over their flock.

9 And see, an angel of the Lord stood by them and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they feared with great fear.

10 The angel said to them, "Do not fear, for see, I preach good news to you of great joy which will be for all the people."

11 For to you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.

12 And this will be a sign to you: you will find an infant wrapped in swaddling clothes lying in the manger.

13 Suddenly there appeared with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God and saying,

14 Glory in the highest to God, and on earth peace among men of good pleasure.

15 And it happened, as the angels departed from them into heaven, the men, the shepherds, said to one another, "Let us pass through indeed to Bethlehem and see this word that has happened, which the Lord made known to us."

16 And they came hastening and found both Mary and Joseph and the infant lying in the manger.

17 When they saw this, they made known the word that had been spoken to them concerning this child.

18 All those who heard marveled at the things spoken by the shepherds to them.

19 But Mary kept all these words, pondering them in her heart.

20 The shepherds turned back, glorifying and praising God for all that they had heard and seen, just as it was spoken to them.

21 And when eight days were completed to circumcise the child, his name was called Jesus, the name called by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

22 And when the days of their purification were filled according to the law of Moses, they led him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord.

23 just as it is written in the law of the Lord: "Every male opening the womb will be called holy to the Lord."

24 and to give a sacrifice according to what is said in the law of the Lord, a yoke of turtle-doves or two chicks of doves.

25 Now see, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon, and this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the comfort of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.

26 And it had been warned to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he saw the Lord's Christ.

27 He came in the Spirit into the temple, and when the parents brought in the child Jesus to do for him according to the custom of the law.

28 And he received him into his arms and blessed God and said,

29 Now you are releasing your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace.

30 For my eyes have seen your salvation.

31 which you have prepared in the face of all the peoples.

32 a light for revelation to the nations and glory for your people Israel.

33 Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things being spoken about him.

34 Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, "Behold, this child is appointed for the fall and resurrection of many in Israel and for a sign that is spoken against."

35 And a sword will pass through your own soul as well, so that the reasonings of many hearts may be revealed.

36 And Anna was a prophetess, a daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher; she had advanced in many days, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity.

37 And this widow was about fourscore and four years old, who did not depart from the temple, serving day and night with fasts and supplications.

38 At that same hour she stood by and gave thanks to the Lord and spoke about him to all who were waiting for redemption in Jerusalem.

39 And when they had finished all the things according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.

40 The child grew and grew strong in the Spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

41 And his parents went to Jerusalem every year at the feast of the Passover.

42 And when he became twelve years old, as they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,

43 And when they had completed the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus remained in Jerusalem; and his parents did not know it.

44 But supposing him to be in the company, they went a day's journey and sought him among their relatives and among their acquaintances.

45 Not finding him, they returned to Jerusalem, seeking him.

46 After three days they found him sitting in the temple in the middle of the teachers, both hearing them and asking them questions.

47 All those who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

48 When they saw him they were astonished, and his mother said to him, "Child, why have you done this to us? See, your father and I have been seeking you in anguish."

49 And he said to them, "Why were you seeking me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?"

50 And they did not understand the word that he spoke to them.

51 And he went down with them and came to Nazareth and was submitting to them. And his mother kept all these words in her heart.

52 And Jesus kept advancing in wisdom and stature and grace with God and men.


## Chapter 3

1 Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, when Pontius Pilate was governor of Judea, and Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip was tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was tetrarch of Abilene—

2 During the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.

3 And he came into all the region around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

4 As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, saying, "A voice of one crying in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of the Lord; make his paths straight.'"

5 Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be brought low, and the crooked places will become straight, and the rough ways smooth.

6 And all flesh will see the salvation of God.

7 He was saying therefore to the crowds going out to be baptized by him, "Fruit of vipers, who showed you to flee from the coming wrath?"

8 Therefore do fruit worthy of repentance, and do not begin to say among yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our Father'; for I say to you that God is able from these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

9 Even now the axe is lying at the root of the trees; therefore every tree not doing good fruit is cut off and cast into the fire.

10 And the crowds were asking him, saying, "What then should we do?"

11 Answering, he says to them, "The one who has two tunics, let him share with the one who has none; and the one who has food, let him do likewise."

12 Publicans also came to be baptized and said to him, "Teacher, what should we do?"

13 He said to them, Practice no more than what has been appointed to you.

14 Some soldiers also asked him, saying, "And we, what shall we do?" And he said to them, "Do violence to no one, and accuse no one falsely, and be content with your wages."

15 While the people were expecting and all were reasoning in their hearts about John, whether he might be the Christ.

16 John answered all of them, saying, "I baptize you with water, but he who is stronger than I is coming, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to loose; he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire."

17 whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly purge his threshing floor, and will gather the wheat into his barn, but will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.

18 With many other exhortations he was preaching the gospel to the people.

19 Herod the tetrarch was rebuked by him because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, and because of all the evil things that Herod had done.

20 He added this also to all: he shut up John in prison.

21 Now when all the people were baptized, and Jesus having been baptized and praying, heaven was opened.

22 And the Holy Spirit descended in bodily form like a dove upon Him, and a voice came from heaven, saying, "You are My beloved Son, in You I am well pleased."

23 And Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry, being the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli.

24 The son of Mathat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Janna, the son of Joseph.

25 the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai

26 The son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semei, the son of Joseph, the son of Judas.

27 of Joannas, of Rhesa, of Zerubbabel, of Salathiel, of Neri

28 of Melchi, of Addi, of Cosam, of Elmodam, of Er

29 the son of Joses, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Mathat, the son of Levi

30 The son of Simeon, the son of Judas, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim.

31 Melea of Mattatha of Nathan of David.

32 Jesse son of Obed son of Booz son of Salmon son of Nahshon.

33 the son of Aminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah.

34 of Jacob, of Isaac, of Abraham, of Thara, of Nahor

35 the son of Saruch, the son of Ragau, the son of Phalec, the son of Eber, the son of Sala.

36 Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech.

37 of Methuselah, of Enoch, of Jared, of Mahalaleel, of Cainan.

38 The son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.


## Chapter 4

1 Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.

2 For forty days he was being tested by the devil, and he ate nothing in those days, and when they were completed, afterward he became hungry.

3 The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread."

4 And Jesus answered and said to him, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word of God.'"

5 And the devil, leading him up to a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

6 The devil said to him, "I will give you all this authority and their glory, for it has been delivered to me, and I give it to whomever I want."

7 So if you worship before me, all will be yours.

8 And answering, Jesus said to him, "Depart behind me, Satan, for it is written: 'You will worship the Lord your God, and you will serve him only.'"

9 And he led him into Jerusalem and stood him on the pinnacle of the temple and said to him, "If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here."

10 For it is written that He will command His angels concerning you, to keep you.

11 and that they will take you in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.

12 And answering, Jesus said to him, "It is said, 'You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.'"

13 When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until a time.

14 And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and a report went out about him throughout the whole surrounding region.

15 He was teaching in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

16 He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and entered the synagogue on the Sabbath day, as was his custom, and stood up to read.

17 And a book of the prophet Isaiah was given to him, and having opened the book, he found the place where it was written.

18 The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor; he has sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to preach forgiveness to the captives and recovery of sight to the blind, to send forth the bruised in freedom.

19 to preach the acceptable year of the Lord.

20 And having rolled up the book and given it back to the servant, he sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed, gazing at him.

21 Then he began to say to them, "Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your ears."

22 All testified about him and marveled at the words of grace that were going out of his mouth, and they said, "Is this not the Son of Joseph?"

23 And he said to them, "Surely you will say to me this parable: 'Physician, heal yourself.' As many things as we heard happening in Capernaum, do also here in your homeland.

24 He said, "Amen, I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his own homeland."

25 But in truth I say to you, there were many widows in the days of Elijah in Israel, when the heaven was shut up for three years and six months, so that a great famine came upon all the earth.

26 And Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Sarepta of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

27 And many lepers were under Elisha the prophet in Israel, and no one of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian.

28 All were filled with wrath in the synagogue upon hearing these things.

29 Rising up, they cast him out of the city and led him to the brow of the mountain on which their city was built, so as to cast him down headlong.

30 But he, having passed through the middle of them, went on his way.

31 And he went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbaths.

32 And they were astonished at his teaching, because his word was with authority.

33 And in the synagogue there was a man having a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a great voice.

34 "Let us alone, Jesus the Nazarene—have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God."

35 Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be muzzled and come out of him." And the demon threw him into the middle and came out of him, hurting no one.

36 And amazement came upon all, and they conversed with one another, saying, "What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they go out."

37 And a report about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

38 Rising from the synagogue, he entered the house of Simon, and Simon's mother-in-law was constrained by a great fever, and they asked him about her.

39 And standing over her, he rebuked the fever, and it left her; and immediately she rose up and began to serve them.

40 As the sun was setting, all those who had people sick with various diseases led them to him. And he laid his hands on each one of them and healed them.

41 Demons also went out from many, crying out and saying, "You are the Christ, the Son of God." And rebuking them, he did not allow them to speak, because they knew him to be the Christ.

42 When the day came, he went out and traveled to a wilderness place, and the crowds were seeking him and came to him and held him fast so that he would not go away from them.

43 But he said to them that I must preach the gospel to the other cities as well, the kingdom of God, because for this purpose I have been sent.

44 And he was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.


## Chapter 5

1 Now it happened that as the crowd pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

2 He saw two boats standing by the lake, and the fishermen, having turned out from them, were washing the nets.

3 Having entered one of the boats, which was Simon's, he asked him to put out a little from the land; and sitting down, he taught the crowds from the boat.

4 When he ceased speaking, he said to Simon, "Bring up into the deep and let down your nets for a draught."

5 Answering, Simon said to him, "Master, we labored throughout the whole night and took no one, but at your word I will let down the net."

6 And having done this, they enclosed a great multitude of fish, but their net was tearing.

7 They beckoned to their partners in the other boat to come and help them, and they came and filled both boats so that they began to sink.

8 When Simon Peter saw this, he fell before Jesus' knees, saying, "Go out from me, because I am a sinful man, Lord."

9 For amazement held him and all those who were with him at the draught of the fish which they had seized.

10 Likewise also James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, "Do not fear; from now on you will be taking men captive."

11 And bringing the boats down to the shore, having left everything behind, they followed him.

12 And it happened that while he was in one of the cities, behold, a man full of leprosy; and seeing Jesus, he fell on his face and prayed to him, saying, "Lord, if you are willing, you are able to cleanse me."

13 And stretching out his hand, he touched him, saying, "I am willing; be cleansed." And immediately the leprosy departed from him.

14 And he commanded him to tell no one, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer for your purification just as Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.

15 But the word about him was spreading even more, and large crowds came together to hear and be healed by him from their weaknesses.

16 But he was going aside into the wilderness and praying.

17 It happened on one of the days while he was teaching, and Pharisees and teachers of the law, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and Jerusalem, were sitting, and the power of the Lord was present for him to heal them.

18 And see, men bearing on a bed a man who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in and put him before him.

19 But when they could not find a way to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the housetop and let him down through the tiling, with his bed, into the middle in front of Jesus.

20 And seeing their faith, he said to him, "Man, your sins are forgiven you."

21 The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, "Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?"

22 Now Jesus, recognizing their reasoning, answered and said to them, "Why are you reasoning in your hearts?"

23 Which is easier, to say, "Your sins are forgiven," or to say, "Get up and walk"?

24 But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins, he said to the paralyzed man, I say to you, raise yourself and take your bed and go to your house.

25 And immediately rising up before them, taking what he was reclining on, he departed to his house, glorifying God.

26 And a trance took hold of all, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, "We have seen strange things today."

27 After these things he went out and beheld a publican named Levi sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, "Follow me."

28 And leaving everything, he rose and followed him.

29 Levi did a great feast for him in his house, and there was a large crowd of publicans and others who were reclining with them.

30 And their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, "Why do you eat and drink with publicans and sinners?"

31 Answering, Jesus said to them, "Those who are healthy have no need of a physician, but those who are sick."

32 I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

33 They said to him, "Why do the disciples of John fast often and make supplications, likewise also those of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?"

34 He said to them, "You are not able to make the sons of the bridechamber fast while the bridegroom is with them, are you?"

35 The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days.

36 He also told them a parable: "No one lays a patch from a new garment onto an old garment; otherwise, both the new splits and the patch from the new does not agree with the old.

37 Also, no one casts new wine into old wineskins. Otherwise, the new wine will burst the wineskins, and it will be poured out, and the wineskins will be destroyed.

38 But new wine must be put into new wineskins, and both are kept.

39 And no one who drinks old wine immediately wants new, for he says that the old is kind.


## Chapter 6

1 Now it happened on a second Sabbath after the first that He was passing through grain fields, and His disciples were plucking the ears of grain and eating them, rubbing them in their hands.

2 But some of the Pharisees said to them, "Why do you do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath?"

3 And Jesus answered and said to them, "Did you not even read this, what David did when he himself and those who were with him were hungry?"

4 as he entered into the house of God and took and ate the bread of the purpose and gave also to those with him, which is not lawful to eat except only for the priests.

5 And he said to them that the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.

6 Now it happened on another Sabbath that he entered the synagogue and was teaching, and there was a man there whose right hand was dry.

7 So the scribes and the Pharisees were watching him closely, to see if he would heal on the Sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.

8 He knew their reasoning and said to the man who had the dry hand, "Rise and stand in the middle." And he rose and stood.

9 Then Jesus said to them, "I will ask you: what is lawful on the Sabbaths—to do good or to do evil, to save a soul or to destroy it?"

10 After looking around at all of them, he said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He did so, and his hand was restored whole as the other.

11 But they were filled with folly and communed with one another what they might do to Jesus.

12 Now it happened in those days that he went out to the mountain to pray, and he was continuing all night in prayer to God.

13 And when it became day, he called to his disciples, and having chosen twelve from them, he also named them apostles.

14 Simon, whom He also named Peter, and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew.

15 Matthew and Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called the zealot.

16 Judas of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a betrayer.

17 And going down with them, he stood on a level place, and a crowd of his disciples and a great multitude of the people from all of Judea and Jerusalem and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases.

18 And those who were vexed by unclean spirits were healed.

19 And all the crowd were seeking to touch him, because power was going out from him and he healed everyone.

20 And he, lifting up his eyes to his disciples, was saying, "Blessed are the poor, because yours is the kingdom of God."

21 Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be satisfied. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.

22 Blessed are you when men hate you, and when they separate you and reproach you and cast out your name as evil because of the Son of Man.

23 Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, for see, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same things to the prophets.

24 Woe to you who are rich, for you have received your comfort.

25 Woe to you who are filled, for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.

26 Woe to you when all men say well of you, for their fathers did the same to the false prophets.

27 But I say to you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you.

28 Bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.

29 To the one who strikes you on the cheek, provide also the other, and from the one who takes your garment, do not forbid also the tunic.

30 Give to all who ask you, and do not ask again from the one who takes what is yours.

31 And as you want men to do to you, you also do likewise to them.

32 If you love those who love you, what grace is that to you? For even the sinners love those who love them.

33 And if you do good to those who do good to you, what grace is that to you? For even sinners do the same thing.

34 And if you lend to those from whom you hope to receive in return, what grace is there for you? For even the sinners lend to sinners so that they may receive back the same amount.

35 But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing in return; and your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High, for He is kind to the unthankful and evil.

36 Therefore be merciful, just as your Father also is merciful.

37 Do not judge, and you will not be judged; do not condemn, and you will not be condemned; release, and you will be released.

38 Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, they will pour into your lap. For with the same measure you use, it will be measured back to you.

39 Then he told them a parable: "A blind person cannot guide a blind person, can he? Will they not both fall into a ditch?"

40 A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully restored will be like his teacher.

41 Why do you look at the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not consider the beam that is in your own eye?

42 Or how can you say to your brother, 'Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,' while you yourself do not see the beam in your own eye? Hypocrite, first take the beam out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.

43 For there is not a good tree doing rotten fruit, nor a rotten tree doing good fruit.

44 For each tree is known by its own fruit, for people do not gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bush.

45 The good man brings forth good from the good treasure of his heart, and the evil man brings forth evil from the evil treasure of his heart; for out of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaks.

46 Why then do you call me 'Lord, Lord,' and not do what I say?

47 All who come to me and hear my words and do them—I will show you what they are like.

48 He is like a man building a house who dug and deepened and put a foundation on the rock, but when a flood happened, the river dashed against that house and was not able to shake it, for it had been founded on the rock.

49 But the one who hears and does not do is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the river dashed, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.


## Chapter 7

1 When he had fulfilled all his words into the ears of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

2 And a certain centurion's servant, who was honored by him, was sick and about to die.

3 Hearing about Jesus, he sent Jewish elders to him, asking him to come and save his servant.

4 Those who came to Jesus were earnestly imploring Him, saying that the one for whom He would provide this is worthy.

5 for he loves our nation and built the synagogue for us.

6 But Jesus was going with them. Already, while He was not far away from the house, the centurion sent friends to Him, saying to Him, "Lord, do not trouble Yourself, for I am not sufficient for You to enter under my roof."

7 Therefore I did not even count myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.

8 For I myself am a man appointed under authority, having soldiers under me, and I say to this one, 'Go,' and he goes, and to another, 'Come,' and he comes, and to my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it.

9 When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turning to the crowd that was following him, he said, "I tell you, not even in Israel have I found such great faith."

10 And when those who had been sent returned to the house, they found the sick servant healthy.

11 And it happened on the next day that he went into a city called Nain, and many of his disciples went with him, and a large crowd.

12 As he drew near to the gate of the city—and see, a dead son, the only begotten, was being carried out for his mother, and she was a widow, and a sufficient crowd of the city was with her.

13 And seeing her, the Lord had compassion on her and said to her, "Do not weep."

14 And coming near, he touched the bier, and those bearing it stood still, and he said, "Young man, I say to you, arise."

15 And the dead man sat up and began to speak, and he gave him to his mother.

16 And fear took hold of all, and they were glorifying God, saying that a great prophet has been raised among us, and that God has visited his people.

17 And this word went out about him in the whole of Judea and in all the surrounding region.

18 His disciples reported to John about all these things.

19 And having called two of his disciples, John sent them to Jesus, saying, "Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect another?"

20 When the men came to him, they said, "John the Baptist has sent us to you, saying, 'Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect another?'"

21 In that same hour he healed many from diseases and scourges and evil spirits, and to many blind he granted sight.

22 And answering, Jesus said to them, "Go, report to John what you see and hear: that the blind receive sight, the lame walk, lepers are cleansed, the mute hear, the dead are raised, and the poor are preached the gospel.

23 Blessed is he who is not offended in me.

24 When John's angels had departed, he began to say to the crowds about John, "What did you go out into the wilderness to behold? A reed shaken by the wind?

25 But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Behold, those who exist in glorious clothing and delicacy are in royal palaces.

26 But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet.

27 This is the one about whom it is written, "See, I am sending my angel before your face, who will prepare your way before you."

28 For I say to you, a greater prophet among those born of women than John the Baptist is no one; but the one who is smallest in the kingdom of God is greater than he.

29 And all the people who heard, even the publicans, justified God, being baptized with the baptism of John.

30 But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God for themselves, not having been baptized by him.

31 Then the Lord said, "To what then shall I liken the men of this generation, and what are they like?"

32 They are like children sitting in the marketplace and calling to one another, saying, "We piped to you, and you did not dance; we lamented to you, and you did not weep."

33 For John the Baptist has come, eating no bread and drinking no wine, and you say, "He has a demon."

34 The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'See, a gluttonous man and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners.'

35 Yet wisdom was justified by all her children.

36 Now one of the Pharisees asked him to eat with him, and entering into the Pharisee's house, he reclined.

37 And see, a woman in the city who was a sinner, having recognized that he reclines in the house of the Pharisee, having received an alabaster jar of ointment.

38 And standing behind at his feet, weeping, she began to rain tears upon his feet, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, and she kissed his feet, and she anointed them with the ointment.

39 When the Pharisee who had called him saw it, he said within himself, "This man, if he were a prophet, would know who and what manner of woman this is who is touching him, that she is a sinner."

40 And answering, Jesus said to him, "Simon, I have something to say to you." And he said, "Teacher, say it."

41 Two debtors were in debt to a certain creditor; the one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

42 But since they had nothing to repay, he forgave both of them. Which of them, then, will love him more?

43 Simon answered and said, "I suppose the one to whom he forgave more." And he said to him, "You have judged rightly."

44 Turning toward the woman, he said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? I entered your house; you did not give me water for my feet, but she rained tears on my feet and wiped them with the hair of her head."

45 You gave me no kiss, but from the time I entered, she has not ceased kissing my feet.

46 You did not anoint my head with oil, but she anointed my feet with ointment.

47 Therefore I say to you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much; but he who is forgiven little loves little.

48 And he said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."

49 And those reclining with him began to say among themselves, "Who is this who even forgives sins?"

50 He said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."


## Chapter 8

1 Soon afterwards He began going throughout the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God, and the twelve were with Him.

2 and some women who had been healed of evil spirits and weaknesses: Mary who is called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out.

3 and Joanna, the wife of Chuza, Herod's steward, and Susanna, and many other women who served him from their own possessions.

4 Now when a large crowd was gathering together and people from every city were coming to him, he said through a parable.

5 The sower went out to sow his seed, and while he was sowing, some seed fell along the way, and it was trampled, and the birds of the heaven devoured it.

6 And other seed fell on the rock, and when it grew, it withered because it had no moisture.

7 And other seed fell in the middle of the thorns, and the thorns, springing up together with it, choked it.

8 Other fell on the good earth, and having grown, it produced fruit a hundredfold; saying these things, he called out, "The one who has ears to hear, let him hear."

9 His disciples were asking him, saying, "What might this parable be?"

10 He said, "To you it has been given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God, but to the remaining ones in parables, so that looking they may not look, and hearing they may not understand."

11 This is the parable: the seed is the word of God.

12 Those along the way are the ones who hear; then the devil comes and takes the word away from their heart, so that they may not believe and be saved.

13 But those on the rock: when they hear, they receive the word with joy, and these have no root—they believe for a time, and in a time of temptation they depart.

14 The seed which fell among the thorns, these are those who hear, and as they go they are choked by anxieties, riches, and pleasures of living, and do not bring fruit to perfection.

15 But the seed in the good earth—these are those who, having heard the word in a good and honest heart, hold it fast and bear fruit with patience.

16 No one, having kindled a lamp, covers it with a vessel or puts it under a bed, but lays it on a lampstand so that those who enter may look at the light.

17 For nothing is secret that will not become manifest, nor anything hid that will not be known and come to light.

18 Look therefore how you hear; for whoever has, it will be given to him, and whoever does not have, even what he seems to have will be taken from him.

19 Now his mother and his brothers came to him, but they were not able to come at him because of the crowd.

20 It was reported to Him, saying, "Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see You."

21 He answered and said to them, "My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God and do it."

22 And it happened on one of the days that he himself entered into a boat, and his disciples with him, and he said to them, "Let us pass through to the other side of the lake," and they were led out.

23 Now as they were sailing he fell asleep, and a storm of wind went down onto the lake, and they were filling with water and in danger.

24 Coming near, they aroused him, saying, "Master, Master, we are perishing." But when he was raised, he rebuked the wind and the wave of the water, and they ceased, and there happened a calm.

25 He said to them, "Where is your faith?" But they, having feared, marveled, saying to one another, "Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?"

26 And they arrived at the region of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee.

27 When he had gone out onto the land, a man from the city met him, who had demons for a long time and did not wear a garment and did not remain in a house but in the tombs.

28 When he saw Jesus, he cried out and fell down before him, and in a loud voice he said, "What do you have to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me."

29 For he was commanding the unclean spirit to go out from the man. For over a long time it had caught him, and he was bound with chains and fetters, being kept under guard; and tearing the bonds apart, he was driven by the demon into the wilderness.

30 Jesus asked him, saying, "What is your name?" And he said, "Legion," because many demons had entered him.

31 He was begging him not to command them to depart into the abyss.

32 Now there was there a sufficient herd of swine feeding on the mountain, and they were begging him to permit them to enter into those, and he permitted them.

33 When the demons had gone out from the man, they entered into the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep place into the lake and was choked.

34 When those who were feeding them saw what had happened, they fled and, after departing, reported it in the city and in the fields.

35 Then they went out to see what had happened, and they came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting clothed and sober-minded at the feet of Jesus, and they were afraid.

36 And those who had seen it also reported to them how the man who had been demon-possessed was healed.

37 All the multitude of the region around the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, because they were constrained with great fear; but he entered into the boat and returned.

38 Then the man from whom the demons had gone out prayed to be with Him, but Jesus released him, saying

39 Return to your house and declare what God has done for you. And he departed, preaching throughout the whole city what Jesus had done for him.

40 It happened that when Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all expecting him.

41 And see, a man came whose name was Jairus, and he existed as a ruler of the synagogue, and falling at the feet of Jesus, he begged him to enter into his house.

42 because his only begotten daughter was about twelve years old, and she was dying. But as he was departing, the crowds were choking him.

43 And a woman who was in a flow of blood for twelve years, having spent her whole living on physicians, was not able to be healed by anyone.

44 Coming near behind, she touched the hem of his garment, and immediately the flow of her blood stopped.

45 Jesus said, "Who touched me?" But while all were denying it, Peter and those with him said, "Master, the crowds constrain you and press you, and you say, 'Who touched me?'"

46 But Jesus said, "Someone touched me, for I knew that power had gone out from me."

47 Seeing that she did not escape notice, the woman came trembling and fell down before him, and she reported to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him and how she was healed immediately.

48 He said to her, "Be of good cheer, daughter; your faith has made you well. Go in peace."

49 While he was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue ruler's house, saying to him, "Your daughter has died; do not trouble the teacher."

50 But Jesus, hearing this, answered him, saying, "Do not fear; only believe, and she will be made well."

51 After entering the house, he did not allow anyone to enter except Peter, James, John, and the father and mother of the servant girl.

52 But all were weeping and mourning for her, but he said, "Do not weep; she did not die but sleeps."

53 And they laughed him to scorn, knowing that she had died.

54 And he cast out all, and having seized her hand, called, saying, "Child, arise."

55 Her spirit returned, and she immediately rose up, and he appointed that something be given her to eat.

56 Her parents were amazed, but he commanded them to tell no one what had happened.


## Chapter 9

1 And calling together his twelve disciples, he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal diseases.

2 He sent them to preach the kingdom of God and to heal the weak.

3 He said to them, "Take nothing for the way: no rod, no bag, no bread, no silver, and have no two tunics."

4 Into whatever house you enter, remain there and go out from there.

5 And whoever does not receive you, as you go out from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet as a testimony against them.

6 Going out, they passed through the villages, preaching the gospel and healing everywhere.

7 Herod the tetrarch heard all that was happening by him, and he was perplexed because it was said by some that John has been raised from the dead.

8 But some said that Elijah had appeared, and others that one of the old prophets had risen.

9 And Herod said, "John I beheaded; but who is this about whom I hear such things?" And he sought to see him.

10 The apostles returned and declared to him all that they had done, and taking them with him he went aside privately into a wilderness place of a city called Bethsaida.

11 But the crowds, knowing it, followed him; and he received them and spoke to them about the kingdom of God, and he healed those who had need of healing.

12 Now the day began to bow, and the twelve came near and said to him, "Release the crowd, so that they may depart into the villages and fields round about to stay and find victuals, for we are here in a wilderness place."

13 He said to them, "You give them something to eat." But they said, "There are not more than five loaves and two fish for us, unless we go and buy food for all this people."

14 For there were about five thousand men. Then he said to his disciples, "Have them sit down in companies of about fifty each."

15 And they did so and reclined all of them.

16 And taking the five loaves and the two fish, having looked up to heaven, he blessed them and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.

17 And they all ate and were satisfied, and they took up the leftover fragments, twelve baskets.

18 And it happened that while he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he asked them, saying, "Who do the crowds say that I am?"

19 They answered and said, "John the Baptist; but others said Elijah; and others said that one of the old prophets has risen."

20 He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter answered and said, "The Christ of God."

21 But he rebuked them and commanded them to tell this to no one.

22 Saying that the Son of Man must suffer much and be rejected by the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed and on the third day be raised.

23 He was saying to all, "If anyone wants to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow me."

24 For whoever wants to save his soul will destroy it, but whoever destroys his soul for my sake will save it.

25 For what does it profit a man, gaining the whole world but destroying himself, or suffering loss?

26 For whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Son of Man will be ashamed of him when he comes in his glory and the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.

27 But I say to you truly, there are some of those standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God.

28 Now it happened that after these words about eight days, and taking along Peter and John and James, He went up into the mountain to pray.

29 And it happened while he was praying, the form of his face became other, and his clothing became white, glistening.

30 And see, two men were conversing with him, who were Moses and Elijah.

31 Those who were seen in glory spoke of his departure which he was about to fulfill in Jerusalem.

32 But Peter and those with him were weighed down with sleep, and having become fully awake, they saw his glory and the two men who were standing with him.

33 And it happened, as they were departing from him, Peter said to Jesus, "Master, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah"—not knowing what he was saying.

34 While he was saying these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them; and they feared as they entered into the cloud.

35 And a voice came from the cloud, saying, "This is my beloved Son; hear him."

36 And as the voice came, Jesus was found alone; and they were silent and reported to no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.

37 On the next day, as they were going down from the mountain, a great crowd met him.

38 And see, a man from the crowd cried aloud, saying, "Teacher, I beg you, look upon my son, for he is my only begotten son."

39 And see, a spirit takes him, and suddenly he cries out and it rends him with foaming, and it hardly departs from him, shattering him.

40 And I prayed to your disciples that they might cast it out, and they were not able.

41 Answering, Jesus said, "O unbelieving and perverted generation, how long will I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here."

42 As he was still coming near, the demon burst him and threw him down; but Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit and healed the servant and gave him back to his father.

43 They were all astonished at the majesty of God, and while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples.

44 Put these words into your ears, for the Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.

45 But they did not know this word, and it was hidden from them so that they might not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this word.

46 A reasoning entered among them as to who would be the greatest of them.

47 But Jesus, seeing the reasoning of their heart, took hold of a child and stood it beside himself.
