48 And he said to them, "Whoever receives this child in my name receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me; for the one who is smallest among all of you, this one will be great."

49 John answered and said, "Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him because he does not follow with us."

50 And Jesus said to him, "Do not forbid him, for whoever is not against us is for us."

51 And it happened, when the days of his taking up were coming, that he set his face to go to Jerusalem.

52 And he sent angels before his face, and going, they entered into a village of the Samaritans to prepare for him.

53 But they did not receive him, because his face was going toward Jerusalem.

54 When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, "Lord, do you want us to say for fire to go down from heaven and consume them, just as Elijah also did?"

55 But turning, he rebuked them and said, "You do not know what kind of Spirit you are of."

56 For the Son of man did not come to destroy the souls of men, but to save; and they went to another village.

57 As they were going on the way, someone said to him, "I will follow you wherever you depart, Lord."

58 Jesus said to him, "Foxes have holes, and the birds of heaven have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to bow his head."

59 He said to another, "Follow me." But he said, "Lord, permit me first to depart and bury my father."

60 But Jesus said to him, "Let the dead bury their own dead; but you, having departed, declare the kingdom of God."

61 Then another also said, "I will follow you, Lord, but first permit me to bid farewell to those in my house."

62 But Jesus said to him, "No one who has laid his hand on the plough and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God."


## Chapter 10

1 After these things the Lord appointed seventy others as well and sent them two by two ahead of his face into every city and place where he himself was about to come.

2 So he said to them, "The harvest is much, but the workers are few; therefore pray the Lord of the harvest that he might cast out workers into his harvest."

3 Depart. See, I send you as lambs in the middle of wolves.

4 Do not carry a money bag, nor a bag, nor sandals, and greet no one along the way.

5 Whenever you enter any house, first say, "Peace to this house."

6 If indeed a son of peace is there, your peace will rest in him; but if not, it will return to you.

7 In that same house remain, eating and drinking what they provide, for the worker is worthy of his reward; do not depart from house to house.

8 And into whatever city you enter and they receive you, eat what is set before you.

9 Heal the weak in it and say to them, 'The kingdom of God has drawn near to you.'

10 Into whatever city you enter and they do not receive you, go out into its streets and say.

11 Even the dust from your city that clings to us we wipe off against you. Yet know this: the kingdom of God has drawn near to you.

12 But I say to you that it will be more tolerable for Sodom on that day than for that city.

13 Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the powers which were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

14 Yet it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.

15 And you, Capernaum, exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.

16 The one who hears you hears me, and the one who rejects you rejects me; but the one who rejects me rejects the one who sent me.

17 Then the seventy returned with joy, saying, "Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name."

18 And he said to them, "I beheld Satan falling as lightning from heaven."

19 See, I give you the authority to tread upon serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing will harm you.

20 However, do not rejoice in this, that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice rather that your names have been written in the heavens.

21 In that hour, Jesus rejoiced in the Spirit and said, "I confess to you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and prudent and have revealed them to children; yes, Father, for so it was a good pleasure before you."

22 Turning to the disciples, he said, "All things have been delivered to me by my Father, and no one knows who the Son is except the Father, and who the Father is except the Son, and anyone to whom the Son wills to reveal him."

23 And turning to the disciples privately, he said, "Blessed are the eyes that look at what you look."

24 For I tell you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you look at and did not see, and to hear what you hear and did not hear.

25 And see, a certain lawyer rose up, putting him to the test and saying, "Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

26 But he said to him, In the law, what is written? How do you read?

27 He answered and said, "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind, and your neighbor as yourself."

28 He said to him, "You have answered correctly; do this and you will live."

29 But the one wanting to justify himself said to Jesus, "And who is my neighbor?"

30 Answering, Jesus said, "A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho and fell into the hands of robbers, who stripped him and laid blows on him and departed, leaving him half dead.

31 But by chance a certain priest was going down that way, and when he saw him, he passed by on the opposite side.

32 Likewise also a Levite, happening to come to that place and seeing him, passed by on the opposite side.

33 But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was and, when he saw him, had compassion.

34 And he came near and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine; and setting him on his own beast, he led him to an inn and took care of him.

35 And on the next day, when he went out, he cast out two denarii, gave them to the host, and said to him, 'Take care of him, and whatever more you spend, I will repay you when I come again.'

36 Which of these three seems to you to have become neighbor to the one who fell into the robbers.

37 He said, "The one who did mercy with him." Then Jesus said to him, "Go, and you also do likewise."

38 Now as they were going along, he entered a certain village, and a woman named Martha received him into her house.

39 She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus' feet and was listening to his word.

40 But Martha was cumbered about much serving, and she came to him and said, Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Tell her therefore to help me.

41 Jesus answered and said to her, "Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about much."

42 But one thing is needed, and Mary has chosen the good portion, which will not be taken away from her.


## Chapter 11

1 And it happened that while he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said to him, "Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples."

2 He said to them, when you pray, say: Father of us, the one in the heavens, sanctify your name; come your kingdom; become your will, as in heaven also on the earth.

3 Give us each day our daily bread.

4 Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive all who owe us, and do not bring us into temptation, but deliver us from evil.

5 And he said to them, "Which of you will have a friend, and will go to him at midnight and say to him, 'Friend, lend me three loaves'?"

6 because a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him.

7 And he from within, answering, says, 'Do not provide me trouble; the door has already been shut, and my children are with me in the bed; I am not able, having risen, to give to you.'

8 I say to you, even if he will not get up and give him anything because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity, he will rise and give him as much as he needs.

9 And I say to you: ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.

10 For all who ask receive, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks it will be opened.

11 Which father among you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a serpent instead of a fish?

12 Or if he asks for an egg, will he give him a scorpion?

13 If you then, existing as evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the Father from heaven give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.

14 He was casting out a demon, and it was mute; and when the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke, and the crowds marveled.

15 But some of them said, "He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons."

16 And others, testing him, kept seeking from him a sign from heaven.

17 He, knowing their thoughts, said to them, "Every kingdom divided against itself is made desolate, and house falls on house."

18 And if Satan has been divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand?—because you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.

19 And if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.

20 But if I cast out demons by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.

21 When the strong man, fully armed, keeps his own courtyard, his possessions are in peace.

22 But when one stronger than he comes upon him and overcomes him, he takes his armor in which he trusted and distributes his spoils.

23 The one not being with me is against me, and the one not gathering with me scatters.

24 When the unclean spirit goes out of the man, it passes through waterless places seeking rest, and finding none, it says, "I will return to my house from which I came out."

25 and when it comes, it finds it swept and adorned.

26 Then it goes and receives seven other spirits more evil than itself, and entering, it dwells there, and the last state of that man becomes worse than the first.

27 It happened that while he was saying these things, a certain woman from the crowd lifted up her voice and said to him, "Blessed is the belly that bore you and the breasts that you nursed."

28 "Blessed rather are those who hear the word of God and keep it."

29 As the crowds were gathering thick together, he began to say, "This generation is an evil generation; it seeks a sign, and a sign will not be given to it except the sign of Jonah the prophet."

30 For just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineve, so also will the Son of Man be to this generation.

31 The queen of the south will be raised in the judgment with the men of this generation and will condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and look, something greater than Solomon is here.

32 The men of Nineveh will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and see, much more than Jonah is here.

33 No one, having kindled a lamp, puts it in a secret place nor under a bushel, but on a lampstand, so that those who enter may look at the light.

34 The lamp of the body is the eye; when therefore your eye is single, your whole body is bright; but when it is evil, your body is dark.

35 Therefore watch that the light in you is not darkness.

36 If therefore your whole body is bright, having no dark part, it will be wholly bright as when a lamp enlightens you with its lightning.

37 While he was speaking, a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him; so he entered and reclined at the table.

38 The Pharisee, seeing this, marveled that he was not first baptized before the dinner.

39 The Lord said to him, "Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and the platter, but your within is full of extortion and wickedness."

40 Foolish ones, did not the one who made the outside also make the within?

41 But give what is within as alms, and behold, all things are clean to you.

42 But woe to you Pharisees, because you tithe mint and rue and every herb, and pass over judgment and the love of God—these you ought to have done, without neglecting the others.

43 Woe to you Pharisees, because you love the chief seat in the synagogues and the greetings in the marketplaces.

44 Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because you are like unseen tombs, and the men who walk over them do not know it.

45 But some of the lawyers answered and said to him, "Teacher, when you say these things, you also mistreat us."

46 He said, "Woe also to you lawyers, because you lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and you yourselves do not touch the burdens with one of your fingers."

47 Woe to you, because you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

48 So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers, because they indeed killed them, and you build their tombs.

49 Therefore also the wisdom of God said, 'I will send prophets and apostles to them, and some of them they will kill and persecute.'

50 so that the blood of all the prophets that was poured out from the foundation of the world may be sought out from this generation.

51 from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zacharias, who was destroyed between the altar and the house. Yes, I say to you, it will be sought out from this generation.

52 Woe to you lawyers, because you have taken away the key of knowledge; you yourselves did not enter, and you hindered those who were entering.

53 While he was saying these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to hold things grievously against him and to provoke him to speak about many things.

54 They were lying in wait for him, seeking to catch something from his mouth so that they could accuse him.


## Chapter 12

1 In the meantime, when so many thousands of the crowd had gathered together that they trampled one another, he began to say to his disciples first, "Give heed to yourselves from the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy."

2 Nothing is covered that will not be revealed, and nothing is secret that will not be known.

3 Therefore everything that you said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and what you spoke in the ear in the secret chambers will be preached on the housetops.

4 I say to you, my friends, do not fear those who kill the body and after that have nothing more abundant to do.

5 But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear the one who, after killing, has authority to cast into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear him.

6 Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings? And not one of them is forgotten before God.

7 But even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Therefore do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.

8 I say to you, all who confess in me before men, the Son of Man will also confess in him before the angels of God.

9 But the one who denies me before men will be denied before the angels of God.

10 And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but to the one who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven.

11 But when they offer you to the synagogues and the beginnings and the authorities, do not be anxious about how or what you will defend or what you will say.

12 For the Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you must say.

13 Some of the crowd said to him, "Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me."

14 But he said to him, "Man, who appointed me a judge or a divider over you?"

15 He said to them, "See and keep yourselves from greed, because a person's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions."

16 He then told them a parable, saying, "A certain rich man's land brought forth abundantly."

17 And he reasoned within himself, saying, "What will I do, since I do not have a place to gather my fruits?"

18 And he said, "I will do this: I will cast down my barns and build greater ones, and I will gather there all my fruits and my good things."

19 And I will say to my soul, "Soul, you have much good things lying in store for many years; rest, eat, drink, be merry."

20 But God said to him, 'Fool, this night your soul is required of you; and what you have prepared, whose will it be?'

21 So is the one who treasures up for himself and is not rich toward God.

22 He said to his disciples, "For this reason I tell you, do not be anxious about your soul, what you will eat, nor about your body, what you will put on."

23 The soul is much more than food, and the body than clothing.

24 Consider the ravens: they do not sow nor reap; they have no secret chamber nor barn, and God nourishes them. How much more do you differ from the birds?

25 Which of you by being anxious is able to add one cubit to his stature?

26 If then you are not able to do even the least, why are you anxious about the remaining things?

27 Consider the lilies, how they grow; they do not labor, nor do they spin; yet I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory clothed himself like one of these.

28 If then God so clothes the grass of the field, which is today and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?

29 You also, do not seek what you will eat or what you will drink, and do not be of doubtful mind.

30 For all these things the nations of the world seek, but your Father knows that you need these things.

31 But seek the kingdom of God, and all these things will be added to you.

32 Do not fear, little flock, because your Father was pleased to give you the kingdom.

33 Sell your possessions and give alms. Make for yourselves money bags that do not decay, a treasure that faileth not in the heavens, where no thief draws near nor moth corrupts.

34 For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

35 Let your loins be girded and your lamps burning.

36 You also must be like people waiting for their master, when he will depart from the wedding, so that when he comes and knocks they may open to him immediately.

37 Blessed are those servants whom the master will find watching when he comes; amen I say to you that he will gird himself and have them recline, and passing along will serve them.

38 And if he comes in the second watch, and comes in the third watch, and finds them so, those servants are blessed.

39 But know this, that if the householder had known what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched and would not have let his house be broken into.

40 Therefore you also be ready, because the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not seem to expect.

41 Peter said to him, "Lord, are you telling this parable for us—or also for everyone?"

42 The Lord said, Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his master will appoint over his household to give them their portion of food at the proper time?

43 Blessed is that servant whom his master when he comes will find doing so.

44 Truly I say to you that he will appoint him over all his possessions.

45 But if that servant says in his heart, 'My master delays his coming,' and begins to strike the male servants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink and be drunk.

46 The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not know, and will cut him asunder and put his portion with the unbelieving.

47 That servant who knew the will of his own master and did not prepare nor do according to his will will be beaten with many blows.

48 But the one who did not know, yet did things worthy of a beating, will be beaten with few blows; and from everyone to whom much has been given, much will be sought; and the one to whom they committed much, they will ask him even more abundantly.

49 I came to cast fire upon the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!

50 But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how I am constrained until it is finished!

51 Do you seem that I have come to give peace on the earth? I tell you, no, but division.

52 For from now on there will be five in one house divided, three against two and two against three.

53 Father will be divided against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her bride and bride against her mother-in-law.

54 He also said to the crowds, "When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, 'A shower is coming,' and so it happens."

55 And when you see the south wind blowing, you say, 'A burning heat will be,' and it happens.

56 Hypocrites, you know how to test the face of the earth and the sky, but how do you not test this time?

57 And why do you not even judge what is righteous from yourselves?

58 For as you depart with your adversary before the ruler on the way, give profit to be delivered from him, lest he hale you to the judge, and the judge delivers you to the officer, and the officer casts you into prison.

59 I say to you, you will certainly not go out from there until you repay even the last mite.


## Chapter 13

1 Now at that very time there were some present reporting to him about the Galileans whose blood Pilate mingled with their sacrifices.

2 And answering, Jesus said to them, "Do you think that these Galileans were sinners beyond all the Galileans because they have suffered such things?"

3 No, I tell you, but unless you repent, you will all likewise be destroyed.

4 Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them, do you suppose that they were debtors beyond all men dwelling in Jerusalem?

5 No, I tell you, but unless you repent, you will all likewise be destroyed.

6 And he was telling this parable: "A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it and found none.

7 He said then to the vine-dresser, 'See, for three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree and I find none; cut it off! Why does it even abolish the ground?'

8 He answered, saying to him, "Sir, leave it alone this year also until I dig around it and cast dung."

9 If then it produces fruit, but if not, in the future you will cut it down.

10 He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.

11 And see, there was a woman having a spirit of weakness for eighteen years, and she was bowed together and was unable to look up completely.

12 Jesus saw her, called to her, and said to her, "Woman, you are released from your weakness."

13 And he laid his hands on her, and immediately she was lifted up and she was glorifying God.

14 The synagogue ruler, answering and indignant because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, said to the crowd, "There are six days in which it is necessary to work; therefore, coming in them, be healed, and not on the day of the Sabbath."

15 The Lord answered him and said, "Hypocrite! Does not each of you on the Sabbath loose his ox or his donkey from the manger and lead it away to give it drink?"

16 Yet this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan bound—see, for ten and eight years—must she not have been loosed from this bond on the day of the Sabbath?

17 As he was saying these things, all those opposing him were put to shame, and all the crowd was rejoicing over all the glorious things happening through him.

18 And he said, "What is the kingdom of God like, and to what shall I liken it?"

19 It is like a grain of mustard, which a man took and cast into his garden, and it grew and became a great tree, and the birds of heaven nested in its branches.

20 And again he said, "To what shall I liken the kingdom of God?"

21 It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal until the whole was leavened.

22 And he was passing through cities and villages, teaching and making a journey to Jerusalem.

23 But He said to him, "Lord, are there few who are being saved?" And He said to them,

24 Strive to enter through the narrow gate, for many, I say to you, will seek to enter and will not be able.

25 From the time the householder has risen and shut the door, and you begin to stand outside and knock on the door, saying, 'Lord, Lord, open to us,' then he will answer and say to you, 'I do not know where you are from.'

26 Then you will begin to say, "We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets."

27 And he will say, I tell you, I do not know where you are from. Depart from me, all you workers of unrighteousness.

28 There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but yourselves being cast out.

29 And they will come from east and west and from north and south, and will recline in the kingdom of God.

30 And see, there are last ones who will be first, and there are first ones who will be last.

31 On that day some Pharisees came near, saying to him, "Go out and go from here, because Herod wants to kill you."

32 And he said to them, "Go, say to that fox, 'See, I cast out demons and accomplish cures today and tomorrow, and on the third day I am perfected.'"

33 Nevertheless, I must go on today and tomorrow and the day following, for it cannot be that a prophet should be destroyed outside Jerusalem.

34 Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her, how often I wanted to gather your children together, in the way a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you did not want it!

35 See, your house is left to you desolate. For I tell you, you will not see me until the time comes when you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'


## Chapter 14

1 And it happened when he came into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on the Sabbath to eat bread, and they were watching him.

2 And see, a man who had the dropsy was before him.

3 And answering, Jesus said to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?"

4 They were quiet, and taking hold of him, he healed him and released him.

5 And answering them he said, "Which of you—whose donkey or ox falls into a well—will not immediately draw it up on the Sabbath day?"

6 And they were not able to answer back to him in response to these things.

7 Then he said a parable to those who had been called, giving heed to how they were choosing the seats of honor, saying to them,

8 When you are called by someone to a wedding, do not sit down in the seat of honor, lest someone more honored than you has been called by him.

9 and the one who invited both you and him will come and say to you, 'Give this man your place,' and then you will begin with shame to hold the last place.

10 But when you are called, go and recline in the last place, so that when the one who called you comes, he may say to you, 'Friend, go up higher'; then there will be glory for you before those who recline with you.

11 For all who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.

12 He was also saying to the one who had called him, "When you do a dinner or a supper, do not call your friends nor your brothers nor your kinsmen nor rich neighbors, lest they also bid you again and a recompense be done to you."

13 But when you do a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind.

14 And you will be blessed, because they do not have anything to repay you, for you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.

15 Hearing these things, a certain one of those reclining with him said to him, "Blessed is the one who will eat bread in the kingdom of God."

16 But he said to him, "A certain man made a great supper and called many."

17 And he sent his servant at the hour of the supper to say to those who had been called, "Come, for all things are now ready."

18 They all began to refuse one after another. The first said to him, 'I have bought a field, and I must go out and see it. I ask you, have me excused.'

19 Another said, 'I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to test them. I ask you, have me refused.'

20 Another said, 'I have married a wife, and for this reason I am not able to come.'

21 That servant came and reported these things to his master. Then the householder, becoming angry, said to his servant, 'Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in here the poor and maimed and lame and blind.'

22 The servant said, 'Sir, it has become as you commanded, and there is still place.'

23 And the master said to the servant, 'Go out into the ways and hedges, and compel them to enter, so that my house may be filled.'

24 For I say to you that no one of those men who were called will taste my supper.

25 Now great crowds were going with him, and turning he said to them.

26 If anyone comes to me and does not hate his own father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters—yes, and even his own soul—he is not able to be my disciple.

27 And whoever does not bear his own cross and come after me is not able to be my disciple.

28 For which of you, wanting to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost whether he has the means for finishing?

29 so that when he has laid a foundation and is not able to finish, all who behold begin to mock him.

30 Saying, 'This man began to build and was not able to finish.'

31 What king, going to encounter another king in war, does not first sit down and plan whether he is able with ten thousand to meet the one coming against him with twenty thousand?

32 But if not, while he is still far away, having sent an ambassage, he asks for the things pertaining to peace.

33 So therefore every one of you who does not bid farewell to all his own possessions cannot be my disciple.

34 Salt is good, but if the salt becomes tasteless, with what will it be seasoned?

35 It is not fit for earth nor for dung; they cast it out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.


## Chapter 15

1 All the tax collectors and the sinners were drawing near to him to hear him.

2 The Pharisees and the scribes were murmuring, saying, "This man welcomes sinners and eats with them."

3 Then he told them this parable, saying:

4 Which man among you, having a hundred sheep and losing one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the lost one until he finds it?

5 And having found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

6 And when he comes into the house, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was destroyed.'

7 I say to you that there will be joy in heaven over one repentant sinner rather than over ninety-nine righteous persons who have no need of repentance.

8 Which woman, having ten drachmas, if she loses one drachma, does not kindle a lamp and sweep the house and seek diligently until she finds it?

9 And having found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.'

10 I say to you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.

11 A certain man said, he had two sons.

12 And the younger of them said to his father, 'Father, give me the part of the goods that falls to me'; and he divided his living between them.

13 And not many days later, the younger son gathered everything together and went abroad into a far region, and there he scattered his goods, living riotously.

14 And when he had spent everything, a severe famine happened throughout that region, and he began to lack.

15 And having gone, he joined himself to one of the citizens of that region, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.

16 And he desired to fill his belly with the husks which the swine were eating, and no one gave to him.

17 But when he came to himself, he said, 'How many hired servants of my Father abound in bread, while I am being destroyed by famine!'

18 Rising, I will go to my Father and say to him, Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you.

19 and I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me like one of your hired servants.

20 And he rose and came to his father. But while he was still far away, his father saw him and had compassion, and running he fell upon his neck and kissed him.

21 But the son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.'

22 But the Father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe and put it on him, and give a ring for his hand and sandals for his feet.

23 Bring the fattened calf, sacrifice it, and let us eat and rejoice.

24 because this son of mine was dead and has revived, and was destroyed and has been found, and they began to rejoice.

25 But his elder son was in the field, and as he came and drew near the house, he heard music and dancing.

26 Calling one of his servants, he asked what these things might be.

27 And he said to him, that your brother has come, and your father has sacrificed the fatted calf, because he has received him back healthy.

28 But he became angry and did not want to enter; so his father went out and pleaded with him.

29 But he answered and said to his Father, 'See, these many years I serve you and I never passed away your commandment; and you never gave me a goat, so that I might rejoice with my friends.'

30 But when this son of yours, who devoured your living with prostitutes, came, you sacrificed the fatted calf for him.

31 But he said to him, 'Child, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.'

32 But it was necessary to rejoice and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and has revived, and was lost and has been found.


## Chapter 16

1 He also said to his disciples: There was a certain rich man who had a steward, and this man was accused to him of squandering his possessions.

2 Calling him, he said to him, "What is this I hear about you? Repay the account of your stewardship, for you will no longer be able to be steward."

3 Then the steward said to himself, 'What will I do, because my master is taking away the stewardship from me? I am not able to dig; I am ashamed to beg.'

4 I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from the stewardship they will receive me into their houses.

5 And having called each one of his master's debtors, he said to the first, "How much do you owe my master?"

6 He said, "A hundred measures of oil." And he said to him, "Take your letter and sit down quickly and write fifty."

7 Then he said to another, 'And you, how much do you owe?' And he said, 'A hundred measures of wheat.' And he said to him, 'Take your letter and write eighty.'

8 The master praised the steward of unrighteousness because he did wisely, for the sons of this age are wiser than the sons of light in their generation.

9 And I say to you, do for yourselves friends from the mammon of unrighteousness, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tabernacles.

10 The one who is faithful in the least is also faithful in much, and the one who is unrighteous in the least is also unrighteous in much.

11 If then you have not been faithful in unrighteous mammon, who will entrust to you the true?

12 If you have not been faithful in what belongs to a stranger, who will give you what is yours?

13 No servant is able to serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will hold fast to one and despise the other. You are not able to serve God and mammon.

14 Now the Pharisees, who were covetous, heard all these things and derided him.

15 He said to them, "You are the ones who justify yourselves before men, but God knows your hearts, because what is high among men is an abomination before God."

16 The law and the prophets were until John; from that time the kingdom of God is preached, and everyone presses into it.

17 But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one tittle of the law to fall.

18 All who release his wife and marry another commits adultery, and all who marry a woman released from a husband commits adultery.

19 Now there was a certain rich man, who was clothed in purple and fine linen, rejoicing sumptuously every day.

20 And a certain poor man named Lazarus had been cast at his gate, full of sores.

21 desiring to be satisfied with the crumbs falling from the table of the rich man, but even the dogs came and licked his sores.

22 It happened that the poor man died and was carried away by the angels into the bosom of Abraham; the rich man also died and was buried.

23 And in Hades, lifting up his eyes, existing in torments, he sees Abraham from afar and Lazarus in his bosom.

24 And he called out, saying, "Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am in anguish in this flame."

25 Abraham said, 'Child, remember that you received your good things in your life, and Lazarus likewise the evil things, but now he is comforted here while you are in anguish.'

26 And besides all these things, between us and you a great gulf is fixed, so that those who want to come over from here to you are not able, nor may those from there cross over to us.

27 He said, "Therefore I ask you, Father, that you send him to my father's house."

28 For I have five brothers, so that he may testify to them, in order that they also may not come into this place of torment.

29 Abraham says to him, 'They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.'

30 But he said, 'No, father Abraham, but if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.'

31 He said to him, 'If they do not hear Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded even if someone rises from the dead.'


## Chapter 17

1 He said to the disciples, "It is impossible for stumbling blocks not to come, but woe to the one through whom they come."

2 It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea than that he should offend one of these little ones.

3 Give heed to yourselves: if your brother sins against you, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.

4 And if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times in a day he turns back to you, saying, 'I repent,' you shall forgive him.

5 The apostles said to the Lord, "Add faith to us."

6 The Lord said, if you had faith like a grain of mustard, you could say to this sycamine tree, 'Be uprooted and planted in the sea,' and it would obey you.

7 Which of you, having a servant plowing or shepherding, who will say to him upon his entrance from the field, 'Pass by at once and recline'?

8 But will he not say to him, "Prepare something that I may dine, and gird yourself and serve me until I eat and drink, and after these things you will eat and drink"?

9 Does he have grace for that servant because he did the things appointed for him? I do not think so.

10 In the same way you also, when you have done all the things appointed to you, say, "We are unprofitable servants; we have done what we were obligated to do."

11 And it happened while he was going to Jerusalem that he was passing through the middle of Samaria and Galilee.

12 As he was entering a certain village, ten leprous men met him, who stood afar off.

13 And they raised their voices, saying, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us."

14 When he saw them, he said, "Go and show yourselves to the priests," and as they were departing, they were cleansed.

15 But one of them, seeing that he was healed, returned with a loud voice, glorifying God.

16 And he fell on his face at his feet, giving thanks to him, and he was a Samaritan.

17 Jesus answered, "Were not ten cleansed? But the nine — where?"

18 Were they not found returning to give glory to God except this stranger?

19 Rise and go; your faith has made you well.

20 Being asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, he answered them and said, "The kingdom of God does not come with observation."

21 Nor will they say, 'See here!' or 'See there!' For indeed, the kingdom of God is within you.

22 He said to the disciples, "Days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it."

23 And they will say to you, "See, here!" or "See, there!" Do not depart nor pursue them.

24 For just as the lightning that lightens out of the part under heaven shines to the part under heaven, so also will the Son of Man be in His day.

25 But first he must suffer much and be rejected by this generation.

26 Just as it happened in the days of Noah, so also it will be in the days of the Son of Man.

27 They were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.

28 Likewise, just as it happened in the days of Lot—they were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building.

29 But on the day Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and destroyed them all.

30 According to these things, it will be the day the Son of Man is revealed.

31 On that day, the one who will be on the housetop, and his vessels in the house, must not go down to take them away, and the one in the field likewise must not turn back to the things behind.

32 Remember Lot's wife.

33 Whoever seeks to save his soul will destroy it, and whoever destroys it will make it live.

34 I tell you, on that night there will be two on one bed: one will be received and the other will be left.

35 Two women will be grinding together; one will be taken and the other left.

36 Two men will be in the field; one will be received and the other will be left behind.

37 They answered and said to him, "Where, Lord?" But he said to them, "Where the body is, there the eagles will be gathered."


## Chapter 18

1 He was also telling them a parable about the need to always pray and not lose heart.

2 Saying, there was a certain judge in a certain city who did not fear God and did not respect man.

3 There was a widow in that city, and she kept coming to him, saying, "Avenge me against my adversary."

4 And he did not want for a time, but after these things he said within himself, Even though I do not fear God nor respect man.

5 Because this widow provides me with labor, I will avenge her, so that she may not come continually and keep me under.

6 And the Lord said, "Hear what the unrighteous judge says."

7 Will not God bring about vengeance for His chosen who cry to Him day and night, and He is patient with them?

8 I say to you that He will do their vengeance with speed. Yet the Son of Man having come, will He find faith upon the earth?

9 He also told this parable to some who were persuaded in themselves that they were righteous and despised the remaining others.

10 Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a publican.

11 The Pharisee stood and prayed these things to himself: "God, I give thanks to you that I am not like the rest of men—ravenous, unrighteous, adulterers—or even like this publican.

12 I fast twice a week, I tithe all that I acquire.

13 And the publican, standing from afar, would not even lift up his eyes to heaven, but was striking his chest, saying, "God, be merciful to me, the sinner."

14 I say to you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but the one who humbles himself will be exalted.

15 Now they were bringing even the infants to him so that he might touch them; but when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

16 Jesus called them to him and said, "Let the little children come to me and do not forbid them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these."

17 Amen, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child will never enter it.

18 Then a certain ruler asked him, saying, "Good teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?"

19 Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except one: God."

20 You know the commandments: do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your Father and your mother.

21 He said, "All these I have kept from my youth."

22 When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, "One thing you still lack: sell all that you have and distribute to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven, and come, follow me."

23 But when he heard this, he became deeply grieved, for he was very rich.

24 When Jesus saw him become deeply grieved, he said, "How hardly will those who have money enter the kingdom of God."

25 For it is easier for a camel to enter through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter into the kingdom of God.

26 Those who heard said, "And who can be saved?"

27 But he said, The things impossible with men are possible with God.

28 Then Peter said, "See, we have left all and followed you."

29 But he said to them, "Truly I say to you, there is no one who has left house or parents or brothers or wife or children for the sake of the kingdom of God."

30 who will not receive manifold more in this time, and in the coming age eternal life.

31 Receiving the twelve, he said to them, "See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things written through the prophets will be finished concerning the Son of Man."

32 For he will be delivered to the nations, and will be mocked, and mistreated, and spit on.

33 And having scourged him, they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise.

34 And they understood no one of these things, and this word was hidden from them, and they did not know the things being said.

35 Now it happened, as he drew near to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting by the way begging.

36 Hearing a crowd passing through, he asked what this might be.

37 And they reported to him that Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.

38 He cried out, saying, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me."

39 Those who went before rebuked him, telling him to be silent; but he cried out much more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

40 Then Jesus stood and commanded him to be led to him; and when he drew near, he asked him.

41 Saying, "What do you want me to do?" And he said, "Lord, that I may receive sight."

42 And Jesus said to him, "Receive sight; your faith has made you well."

43 And immediately he received sight and followed him, glorifying God; and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise to God.


## Chapter 19

1 Having entered, he was passing through Jericho.

2 And see a man named Zacchæus, and he was a chief among the publicans, and this man was rich.

3 He was seeking to see who Jesus is, and he could not because of the crowd, since he was small in stature.

4 And he ran on ahead and went up into a sycamore tree to see him, because he was about to pass through that way.

5 When he came to the place, Jesus looked up, received sight, saw him, and said to him, "Zacchaeus, hasten and go down, for today I must remain at your house."

6 So he hurried and came down, and received him joyfully.

7 All who saw it began to murmur, saying, "He has gone in to stay at the house of a sinful man."

8 And Zacchæus stood and said to the Lord, "See, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have falsely accused anyone of anything, I repay fourfold."

9 Then Jesus said to him, "Today salvation has happened to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham."

10 For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was destroyed.

11 As they were hearing these things, he added and spoke a parable, because he was near Jerusalem and they seemed to think that the kingdom of God was about to appear immediately.

12 Then a certain nobleman went to a far region to take for himself a kingdom and then return.

13 Having called ten of his servants, he gave them ten minas and said to them, "Occupy until I come."

14 And his citizens hated him and sent an ambassage after him, saying, "We do not want this man to reign over us."

15 And it happened when he had come again after taking the kingdom, he said that those servants to whom he had given the silver should be called to him, so that he might know what each had gained by trading.

16 And the first came, saying, 'Master, your mina has gained ten minas.'

17 He said to him, "Well done, good servant; because you were faithful in the least, be having authority over ten cities."

18 And the second came, saying, 'Master, your mina has made five minas.'

19 He said to him also, "And you, become ruler over five cities."

20 "And another came, saying, 'Master, see, your mina which I had, being laid up in a handkerchief.'"

21 For I was fearing you, because you are an austere man: you take up what you did not put down, and you reap what you did not sow.

22 He said to him, 'Out of your own mouth I judge you, evil servant. You knew that I am an austere man, taking what I did not put down and reaping what I did not sow.'

23 And why did you not give my silver to the table, and I, having come, would have practiced it with usury?

24 And to those who were present he said, "Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has the ten minas."

25 "Sir, he has ten minas," they said to him.

26 For I say to you that to all who have, more will be given; but from the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.

27 Nevertheless—those enemies of mine who did not want me to reign over them—lead them here and slay them before me.

28 And having said these things, he was going ahead, going up into Jerusalem.

29 And it happened that as He drew near to Bethphage and Bethany, toward the mountain called of Olive Trees, He sent two of His disciples.

30 Saying, depart into the village opposite you, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, on which no one of men has ever sat; having loosed it, lead it.

31 And if anyone asks you, "Why are you loosing it?" thus you will say to him, "The Lord has need of it."

32 So the ones who had been sent departed and found it just as he had told them.

33 And as they were loosing the colt, its owners said to them, "Why are you loosing the colt?"

34 But they said, "The Lord has need of him."

35 And they led him to Jesus, and casting their garments upon the colt, they set Jesus on it.

36 As he was going, they spread their garments on the road.

37 Now as He drew near to the descent of the Mount of Olives, all the multitude of the disciples began rejoicing to praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen.

38 Saying, "Blessed is the coming king in the name of the Lord; peace in heaven and glory in the Most High."

39 Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to him, "Teacher, rebuke your disciples."

40 And answering, he said to them, "I tell you that if these become silent, the stones will cry out."

41 And as he drew near, seeing the city, he wept over it.

42 saying, "If you, even you, had known on this your day the things that lead to your peace—but now they have been hidden from your eyes."

43 For the days will come upon you when your enemies will cast up a trench around you, and will surround you and constrain you on every side.

44 and they will lay you even with the ground, and your children within you, and they will not leave in you one stone upon another, because you did not know the time of your visitation.

45 And entering into the temple, he began to cast out those selling and buying in it.

46 Saying to them, "It is written, 'My house is a house of prayer,' but you have made it a cave of robbers."

47 And he was teaching daily in the temple, but the high priests and the scribes were seeking to destroy him, as well as the foremost of the people.

48 And they could not find what they should do, for all the people were very attentive to him, listening.


## Chapter 20

1 And it happened on one of those days, while he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, that the high priests and the scribes stood by with the elders.

2 And they said to him, saying, "Tell us by what authority you do these things, or who is the one who gave you this authority."

3 And answering, he said to them, "I also will ask you one word, and you tell me."

4 The baptism of John—was it from heaven or from men?

5 And they reasoned with themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'"

6 But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.

7 And they answered that they did not know where it came from.

8 And Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things."

9 He began to say to the people this parable: "A certain man planted a vineyard and let it out to husbandmen, and went abroad for a sufficient time.

10 And at the time he sent a servant to the husbandmen, so that they might give him some of the fruit of the vineyard; but the husbandmen beat him and sent him away empty.

11 Again he added to send another servant, but they, having beaten and dishonored him also, sent him forth empty.

12 And he added to send a third; and they also wounded this one and cast him out.

13 The owner of the vineyard said, 'What will I do? I will send my beloved Son; it may be that when they see him, they will respect him.'

14 When the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, "This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, so that the inheritance may become ours."

15 And after casting him out of the vineyard, they killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?

16 He will come and destroy those husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others. But when they heard this, they said, "By no means!"

17 But he looked at them and said, "What then is this which is written: 'The stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner'?"

18 Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken, but on whomever it falls, it will grind him to powder.

19 And the high priests and the scribes sought to lay their hands on him in that very hour, and they feared the people, for they knew that he had spoken this parable against them.

20 And they watched and sent spies, who feigned themselves to be righteous, so that they might take hold of his word, in order to deliver him to the rule and authority of the governor.

21 And they asked him, saying, "Teacher, we know that you speak and teach rightly, and you do not take the face of anyone, but you teach the way of God in truth.

22 Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?

23 Considering their craftiness, he said to them, "Why are you testing me?"

24 Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription does it have?" And answering, they said, "Caesar's."

25 And he said to them, "Repay therefore the things of Caesar to Caesar and the things of God to God."

26 And they were not able to take hold of his word before the people, and marveling at his answer, they were silent.

27 Then some of the Sadducees, who speak against the resurrection being, came near and asked him.

28 Saying, "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, having a wife, and he dies childless, so that his brother may take the wife and raise up seed for his brother."

29 Then there were seven brothers. The first took a wife and died childless.

30 And the second man took her as wife, and he also died childless.

31 The third took her, and likewise also the seven left no children and died.

32 Later, after all, the woman also died.

33 In the resurrection, therefore, which of them does the woman become, for the seven had her as a wife.

34 Jesus answered and said to them, "The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage."

35 But those who are counted worthy to obtain that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

36 For they can no longer die, for they are equal to angels and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.

37 But that the dead are raised, even Moses informed at the bush, when he says the Lord is the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.

38 But God is not of the dead, but of the living, for all live to him.

39 Some of the scribes answered, "Teacher, you have spoken well."

40 But they no longer dared to ask him anything.

41 He said to them, "How do they say that the Christ is the Son of David?"

42 And David himself says in the book of Psalms: 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand."'

43 until I put your enemies as a footstool for your feet.

44 David therefore calls him Lord, and how is he his Son?

45 While all the people were hearing, he said to his disciples.

46 Give heed to the scribes, who want to walk in robes and love greetings in the marketplaces and chief seats in the synagogues and seats of honor at the suppers.

47 Those who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers will take abundant judgment.


## Chapter 21

1 And looking up, he saw the rich casting their gifts into the treasury.

2 Then he saw a certain poor widow casting two mites there.

3 And He said, "Truly I say to you that this poor widow has cast in more than all."

4 For all these cast into the gifts of God out of their abundance, but she out of her lack cast in all the living that she had.

5 As some were saying about the temple that it was adorned with good stones and gifts, he said,

6 These things which you behold, the days will come in which not one stone will be left upon another stone that will not be destroyed.

7 They asked him, saying, "Teacher, when therefore will these things be, and what is the sign when these things are about to happen?"

8 He said, "Watch out that you are not led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, 'I am he,' and, 'The time has drawn near.' Do not go after them."

9 But when you hear of wars and disorders, do not be frightened, for these things must happen first, but the end will not come immediately.

10 Then he was saying to them, "Nation will be raised up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom."

11 There will be great earthquakes in various places, and famines and pestilences; and there will be fearful sights and great signs from heaven.

12 But before all these, they will lay their hands on you and persecute you, delivering you to synagogues and prisons, being led before kings and governors for the sake of my name.

13 But this will turn out for you as a testimony.

14 Then put into your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to defend yourselves.

15 For I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all who oppose you will not be able to speak against or resist.

16 You will also be delivered up even by parents and brothers and kinsmen and friends, and they will put some of you to death.

17 And you will be hated by all because of my name.

18 But not a hair from your head will be destroyed.

19 In your patience acquire your souls.

20 But when you see Jerusalem being surrounded by armies, then know that her desolation has drawn near.

21 Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those in her middle must depart out, and those in the regions must not enter into her.

22 because these are days of vengeance, for all that have been written to be fulfilled.

23 Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing in those days, for there will be great distress on the earth and wrath upon this people.

24 And they will fall by the mouth of the sword and will be led away captive into all the nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled by the nations until the times of the nations are fulfilled.

25 And there will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth anguish of nations in perplexity at the roaring of the sea and the waves.

26 Men fainting from fear and expectation of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

27 And then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and much glory.

28 But when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.

29 And he said a parable to them: See the fig tree and all the trees.

30 As soon as they put forward [leaves], by looking at them you know by yourselves that the summer is already near.

31 So also you, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.

32 Amen, I say to you that this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things happen.

33 Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.

34 Give heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting and drunkenness and worldly anxieties, and that day come upon you suddenly.

35 For it will come upon all those sitting on the face of all the earth like a snare.

36 Therefore watch at all times, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that are about to happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.

37 He was teaching in the temple during the days, but going out at night he would abide on the mountain called Olivet.

38 All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.


## Chapter 22

1 Now the feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was drawing near.

2 The high priests and the scribes were seeking how they might kill him, for they feared the people.

3 Then Satan entered into Judas, who is called Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.

4 And he departed and conversed with the high priests and the captains about how he would deliver him to them.

5 And they rejoiced and agreed to give him silver.

6 And he confessed and sought an opportunity to deliver Him to them without a crowd.

7 Then came the day of unleavened bread on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

8 And he sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover for us, so that we may eat."

9 But they said to him, "Where do you want us to prepare?"

10 He said to them, "See, as you enter into the city, a man bearing a pitcher of water will meet you; follow him into the house where he enters."

11 And say to the householder of the house, 'The Teacher says to you, "Where is the guest room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?"'

12 And he will show you a large upper room, spread and ready; prepare it there.

13 So they departed and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

14 And when the hour came, he reclined, and the twelve apostles with him.

15 And he said to them, "I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer."

16 For I say to you that I will no longer eat of it until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.

17 He received a cup, gave thanks, and said, "Take this and divide it among yourselves."

18 For I say to you that I will certainly not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.

19 And taking bread, having given thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, "This is my body which is given for you; do this in remembrance of me."

20 Likewise also the cup after dining, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you."

21 But see, the hand of the one who betrays me is with me on the table.

22 And the Son of man goes according to what has been determined, but woe to that man by whom he is betrayed.

23 And they began to dispute among themselves as to which of them it might be who was about to do this.

24 Now there also arose a dispute among them as to which of them seemed to be greatest.

25 But he said to them, "The kings of the nations lord over them, and those who have authority over them are called benefactors."

26 But you are not this way; rather, the greatest among you must become like the younger, and the one who is considered leader like the one who serves.

27 For who is greater, the one reclining or the one serving? Is it not the one reclining? But I am among you as the one serving.

28 But you are the ones who have remained with me in my temptations.

29 I also appoint to you a kingdom, just as my Father appointed to me.

30 so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

31 The Lord said, "Simon, Simon, see, Satan has desired to sift you as wheat."

32 But I prayed for you, that your faith may not fail; and you, when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.

33 And he said to him, "Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death."

34 But he said, "I tell you, Peter, a rooster will not call today before you deny three times that you do not know me."

35 And he said to them, "When I sent you without money bag and bag and sandals, did you lack anything?" And they said, "Nothing."

36 He said to them, "But now, the one who has a money bag, let him take it, and likewise a bag; and the one who does not have one, let him sell his garment and buy a sword."

37 I tell you that this that has been written must still be finished in me: 'And he was reckoned with the lawless.' For also the things concerning me have an end.

38 They said, "Lord, see, here are two swords." And he said to them, "It is sufficient."

39 After going out, he went according to his custom to the Mount of Olives, and his disciples also followed him.

40 When he came to the place, he said to them, "Pray that you not enter into temptation."

41 And he withdrew from them about a stone's cast, and putting his knees down, he prayed.

42 Father, if you will, remove this cup from me; yet not my will, but yours be done.

43 An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

44 And being in agony, he prayed more earnestly; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.

45 He rose from prayer, came to the disciples, and found them sleeping from sorrow.

46 And he said to them, "Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation."

47 While he was still speaking, see, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the twelve, was going before them and drew near to Jesus to kiss him.

48 But Jesus said to him, "Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?"

49 Those around him, seeing what was about to happen, said to him, "Lord, should we strike with the sword?"

50 And one of them struck the servant of the high priest and took away his right ear.

51 And answering, Jesus said, "Allow them as far as this," and touching his ear, he healed him.

52 Jesus said to the high priests and captains of the temple and elders who had come against him, "Have you gone out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?"

53 Day after day I was with you in the temple, and you did not stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the authority of darkness.

54 Having seized him, they led him away and brought him into the house of the high priest; but Peter was following from afar.

55 When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter was sitting in the middle of them.

56 A certain maidservant, seeing him sitting by the light and gazing at him, said, "This man also was with him."

57 But he denied him, saying, "Woman, I do not know him."

58 And after a little while, another person who saw him said, "You also are one of them." But Peter said, "Man, I am not."

59 About an hour later, another person confidently affirmed, saying, "Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean."

60 But Peter said, "Man, I do not know what you are saying." And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster called out.

61 And the Lord turned and looked at Peter, and Peter was reminded of the word of the Lord, how He had said to him, "Before the rooster calls, you will deny Me three times."

62 And Peter went out and wept bitterly.

63 And the men who were restraining Jesus mocked him as they beat him.

64 Having covered Him, they were striking His face and asking Him, saying, "Prophesy, who is the one who struck You?"

65 And they were saying many other blasphemous things against him.

66 When day came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, gathered together and led him into their council, saying,

67 If you are the Christ, tell us. But he said to them, if I tell you, you will not believe.

68 If I also ask, you will certainly not answer me or release me.

69 From now on the Son of Man will be sitting at the right hand of the power of God.

70 They all said, "Are you then the Son of God?" And he said to them, "You say that I am."

71 And they said, "What further need do we have of testimony? For we ourselves have heard from his mouth."


## Chapter 23

1 Rising up, all their multitude led him to Pilate.

2 They began to accuse him, saying, "We found this man perverting the nation and forbidding tribute to be given to Caesar, saying that he himself is Christ, a king."

3 Then Pilate asked him, saying, "Are you the king of the Jews?" And he answered him, "You say."

4 But Pilate said to the high priests and the crowds, I find no cause in this man.

5 But they were the more fierce, saying, He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee to this place.

6 When Pilate heard Galilee, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.

7 And when he recognized that he was under Herod's authority, he sent him back to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem in those days.

8 When Herod saw Jesus he rejoiced greatly, for he had been wanting to see him for a long time because he heard much about him, and he hoped to see some sign performed by him.

9 So he questioned him with many words, but he answered him nothing.

10 But the chief priests and the scribes stood, mightily accusing him.

11 And Herod, with his army, despised and mocked him, then clothed him in bright clothing and sent him back to Pilate.

12 And Pilate and Herod became friends with each other on that day, for before they had existed in enmity toward each other.

13 But Pilate, having called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,

14 He said to them, "You offered this man to me as one who turns away the people, and see, I have examined him in your presence and found no fault in this man concerning the things of which you accuse him."

15 But neither did Herod, for I sent you back to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been practiced by him.

16 After disciplining him, I will release him.

17 But he had a necessity to release one to them during the feast.

18 They all cried out at once, saying, "Take this man away, and release Barabbas to us!"

19 who was cast into prison for a certain dissension that happened in the city, and for murder.

20 Again Pilate called to them, wanting to release Jesus.

21 But they cried out, saying, "Crucify, crucify Him!"

22 He said to them a third time, "What evil has this man done? I found no cause of death in him; therefore, after disciplining him, I will release him."

23 And they pressed upon him with loud voices, asking him to crucify him. And their voices and those of the chief priests prevailed.

24 So Pilate gave sentence that their petition happen.

25 He released for them the one who had been cast into prison for dissension and murder, whom they asked for, and delivered Jesus over to their will.

26 And as they led him away, they took hold of a certain Simon, a Cyrenian, who was coming from the field, and laid the cross on him to bear it behind Jesus.

27 Now a great multitude of the people and of women were following him, mourning and lamenting him.

28 Turning to them, Jesus said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children."

29 See, days are coming in which they will say, "Blessed are the barren women, and the bellies that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed."

30 Then they will begin to say to the mountains, "Fall on us!" and to the hills, "Cover us!"

31 If they do these things in the green wood, what will happen in the dry?

32 And two other evildoers were also being led with him to be killed.

33 And when they departed to the place called Skull, there they crucified him and the evildoers, one on the right and one on the left.

34 And Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing." And dividing his garments, they cast lots.

35 And the people stood beholding, but the rulers also derided him, saying, "He saved others; let him save himself, if this is the Christ, the chosen of God."

36 The soldiers also came near and mocked him, offering him vinegar.

37 and saying, "If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself."

38 There was also an inscription written over Him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: "This is the King of the Jews."

39 One of the hanged evildoers was blaspheming him, saying, "If you are the Christ, save yourself and us."

40 But the other answered, rebuking him, saying, "Do you not even fear God, since you are under the same judgment?"

41 We indeed justly receive what is worthy of what we practiced, but this man practiced no wrong.

42 And he said to Jesus, "Remember me, Lord, when you come in your kingdom."

43 And Jesus said to him, "Amen, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise."

44 And it was about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.

45 And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was split in the middle.

46 And calling out with a loud voice, Jesus said, "Father, into your hands I commit my spirit." And having said this, he gave up the ghost.

47 When the centurion saw what had happened, he glorified God, saying, "Indeed this man was righteous."

48 And all the crowds that had come together for this sight, beholding the things that had happened, returned striking their own chests.

49 But all his acquaintances and the women who had followed him from Galilee were standing at a distance, seeing these things.

50 And see, a man named Joseph, who existed as a counsellor, a good and righteous man.

51 This man had not consented to their counsel and deed, from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who was also himself waiting for the kingdom of God.

52 This man came near to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.

53 And having cast him down, he wrapped him in linen and put him in a tomb hewn in stone, where no one had yet been lying.

54 It was the day of preparation, and the Sabbath was beginning to dawn.

55 Then the women who had come together with him from Galilee also followed and beheld the tomb and how his body was put.

56 But having returned, they prepared spices and ointments, and on the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.


## Chapter 24

1 On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came to the tomb, bearing the spices which they had prepared, and some others with them.

2 But they found the stone rolled away from the tomb.

3 And entering, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

4 And it happened while they were perplexed about this, see, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.

5 While the women were terrified and bowing their faces to the earth, they said to them, "Why do you seek the living among the dead?"

6 He is not here, but has been raised. Remember how he spoke to you while he was still in Galilee.

7 Saying that the Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men and be crucified and on the third day rise.

8 And they remembered his words.

9 And returning from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.

10 Now the Magdalene Mary and Joanna and Mary the mother of James and the remaining women with them were saying these things to the apostles.

11 And their words appeared before them like an idle tale, and they disbelieved them.

12 But Peter arose and ran to the tomb, and stooping to look in, he sees the linen wrappings lying alone, and he departed to his home marveling at what had happened.

13 Behold, two of them were going on that day to a village receiving sixty stades from Jerusalem, whose name is Emmaus.

14 And they communed with each other about all these things that had happened.

15 And it happened, while they were communing and disputing, that Jesus himself drew near and went with them.

16 But their eyes were seized so that they would not recognize him.

17 He said to them, "What are these words that you discuss with one another as you walk and are sad?"

18 Answering, the one whose name was Cleopas said to him, "Do you alone sojourn in Jerusalem and not know the things that have happened in it in these days?"

19 And he said to them, "What things?" And they said to him, "The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene, who was a man, a prophet, able in work and word before God and all the people."

20 and how the high priests and our rulers delivered him up to the judgment of death and crucified him.

21 But we were hoping that he was the one about to redeem Israel. Yet besides all this, today is the third day since these things happened.

22 But also some women among us amazed us, having been at the tomb early.

23 and not finding his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who say that he is alive.

24 And some of those with us went off to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see.

25 And he said to them, "O foolish and slow in heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken."

26 Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?

27 And beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he interpreted for them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

28 They drew near to the village where they were going, and he pretended to be going farther.

29 They constrained him, saying, "Remain with us, because it is toward evening and the day has bowed," and he entered to remain with them.

30 And it happened that when he sat down with them, he took the bread, blessed it, and breaking it, he gave it to them.

31 Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, and he vanished out of sight from them.

32 And they said to one another, "Was not our heart burning within us as he was speaking to us on the way, as he was opening the Scriptures to us?"

33 And rising up at that same hour, they returned to Jerusalem and found the eleven and those with them gathered together.

34 Saying that the Lord was truly raised and appeared to Simon.

35 And they declared the things on the way, and how he was known to them in the breaking of the bread.

36 While they were speaking about these things, Jesus himself stood in their midst and said to them, "Peace to you."

37 But they were frightened and terrified, and seemed to behold a spirit.

38 And he said to them, "Why are you troubled, and why do reasonings arise in your hearts?"

39 See my hands and my feet, that I myself am. Touch me and see, because a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you behold me having.

40 Then he said this, and showed them his hands and his feet.

41 While they were still disbelieving from joy and marveling, he said to them, "Do you have anything to eat here?"

42 and they gave him a piece of broiled fish and some of a honeycomb.

43 And taking it before them, he ate.

44 He said to them, "These are the words which I spoke to you while I was still with you, that all the things written in the law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms concerning me must be fulfilled."

45 Then he opened their mind to understand the Scriptures.

46 And he said to them that thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead on the third day.

47 And preach repentance and forgiveness of sins in his name to all the nations, beginning from Jerusalem.

48 But you are witnesses of these things.
