30 Now we know that you know all things and have no need for anyone to ask you; by this we believe that you have gone out from God.

31 Jesus answered them, "Now do you believe?"

32 See, an hour is coming and has now come, when you will be scattered, each to his own place, and will leave me alone; yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

33 I have spoken these things to you so that in me you may have peace. In the world you have tribulation, but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.


## Chapter 17

1 Jesus spoke these things, and after lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son, so that your Son may also glorify you.

2 just as you gave him authority over all flesh, so that all whom you have given him, he would give them eternal life.

3 Now this is eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.

4 I glorified you on the earth, having perfected the work which you have given me to do.

5 And now, glorify me, you Father, with yourself, with the glory which I had before the world was, with you.

6 I revealed your name to the men whom you gave me out of the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.

7 Now they know that everything you have given me is from you.

8 I have given them the words which you gave me, and they received them and truly knew that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.

9 I ask on behalf of them; I do not ask on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those whom you have given me, because they are yours.

10 And all my things are yours, and your things are mine, and I have been glorified in them.

11 I am no longer in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you, holy Father; keep them in your name which you have given to me, so that they may be one as we are one.

12 While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name; those whom you have given me I have kept, and no one of them has been destroyed except the Son of destruction, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

13 But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy fulfilled in themselves.

14 I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

15 I do not ask that you take them out of the world, but that you keep them from the evil one.

16 They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

17 Sanctify them by your truth; your word is truth.

18 Just as you sent me into the world, I also sent them into the world.

19 And for them I sanctify myself, so that they themselves may also be sanctified in truth.

20 I ask not only on behalf of these, but also on behalf of those who will believe in me through their word.

21 that all may be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be one in us, so that the world may believe that you sent me.

22 And I have given them the glory which you gave me, so that they may be one, just as we are one.

23 I am in them and you are in me, so that they may be perfected into one, and so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them just as you loved me.

24 Father, those whom you have given to me, I want that where I am, they also may be with me, so that they may behold my glory which you have given me, because you loved me before the foundation of the world.

25 Righteous Father, the world has not known you, but I have known you, and these have known that you sent me.

26 I made known to them your name, and I will continue to make it known, so that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.


## Chapter 18

1 After saying these things, Jesus went out with his disciples across the brook Cedron, where there was a garden into which he and his disciples entered.

2 Now Judas, the one who was about to betray him, also knew the place, because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.

3 Then Judas, having taken the cohort and servants from the high priests and Pharisees, comes there with lanterns and lamps and weapons.

4 Jesus, therefore, knowing all the things that were coming upon him, went out and said to them, "Who are you seeking?"

5 They answered him, "Jesus the Nazarene." Jesus says to them, "I am he." Now Judas, who was betraying him, also stood with them.

6 When therefore he said to them, "I am," they departed backward and fell on the ground.

7 Then he asked them again, "Who do you seek?" And they said, "Jesus the Nazarene."

8 Jesus answered, "I told you that I am he; so if you seek me, let these go their way."

9 so that the word which he spoke might be fulfilled: "Of those whom you have given me, I have destroyed no one."

10 Simon Peter, having a sword, drew it and struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear; and the servant's name was Malchus.

11 Then Jesus said to Peter, "Cast your sword into the sheath; the cup that the Father has given me, shall I not drink it?"

12 The cohort, the captain, and the servants of the Jews seized Jesus and bound him.

13 They led him away first to Annas, for he was the father‑in‑law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

14 It was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it profits for one man to be destroyed for the sake of the people.

15 Now Simon Peter followed Jesus, along with the other disciple. That disciple was known to the high priest, and he went in with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.

16 But Peter was standing outside at the door. Therefore the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and said to the doorkeeper and brought Peter in.

17 Then the maidservant, who was the doorkeeper, says to Peter, "Are you also one of this man's disciples?" That man says, "I am not."

18 Now the servants and the attendants had made a charcoal fire because it was cold, and they were warming themselves. And Peter was also standing with them and warming himself.

19 So the high priest asked Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.

20 Jesus answered him, "I have spoken boldly to the world; I always taught in the synagogue and in the temple, where the Jews always come together, and I have spoken nothing in secret."

21 Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard what I spoke to them. See, these know what I said.

22 And when he said these things, one of the servants who was present gave a slap to Jesus, saying, "Do you answer the high priest in this way?"

23 Jesus answered him, "If I spoke evil, testify about the evil; but if well, why do you beat me?"

24 Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.

25 Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. So they said to him, "Are you also one of his disciples?" He denied it and said, "I am not."

26 One of the servants of the high priest, a kinsman of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, says, "Did I not see you in the garden with him?"

27 Again therefore Peter denied, and immediately the rooster called.

28 Then they lead Jesus from Caiaphas into the Praetorium; it was early, and they themselves did not enter the Praetorium, so that they would not be defiled, but that they might eat the Passover.

29 So Pilate went out to them and said, "What accusation do you bear against this man?"

30 They answered and said to him, "If this man were not an evildoer, we would not have delivered him to you."

31 Pilate said to them, "Take him yourselves and judge him according to your law." Then the Jews said to him, "It is not lawful for us to kill anyone."

32 so that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he said, signifying what kind of death he was about to die.

33 Then Pilate entered into the Praetorium again and called Jesus and said to him, "You are the king of the Jews?"

34 Jesus answered him, "Do you say this from yourself, or did others say it to you about me?"

35 Pilate answered, "Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered you to me. What have you done?"

36 Jesus answered, "My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have striven so that I would not be delivered to the Jews. But now my kingdom is not from here."

37 Then Pilate said to him, "So you are a king?" Jesus answered, "You say that I am a king. For this I have been born, and for this I have come into the world, that I might testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears my voice."

38 Pilate says to him, "What is truth?" And having said this, he went out again to the Jews and says to them, "I find no cause in him."

39 You have a custom that I release one for you at the Passover; do you will then that I release for you the king of the Jews?

40 So they all cried out again, saying, "Not this man, but Barabbas!" Now Barabbas was a robber.


## Chapter 19

1 Then Pilate took Jesus and scourged him.

2 The soldiers plaited a crown out of thorns and laid it on his head, and clothed him in a purple garment.

3 And they said, "Rejoice, King of the Jews!" And they gave him strikes with the palm of the hand.

4 So Pilate went out again and says to them, "See, I am leading him out to you so that you may know that I find no cause in him."

5 Then Jesus went out, wearing the thorny crown and the purple garment. And he says to them, "See the man."

6 When the high priests and the servants saw him, they cried out, saying, "Crucify! Crucify!" Pilate says to them, "Take him yourselves and crucify him, for I find no cause in him."

7 The Jews answered him, "We have a law, and according to our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God."

8 When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.

9 And he entered into the praetorium again and says to Jesus, "Where are you from?" But Jesus gave him no answer.

10 So Pilate says to him, "Do you not speak to me? Do you not know that I have authority to crucify you and have authority to release you?"

11 Jesus answered, "You would have no authority over me unless it had been given to you from above; therefore the one who delivers me to you has the greater sin."

12 From then on Pilate sought to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, "If you release this man, you are not a friend of Caesar; everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar."

13 So Pilate, having heard this word, led Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called the Pavement (in Hebrew, Gabbatha).

14 Now it was the preparation day of the Passover, about the sixth hour, and he says to the Jews, "See, your king."

15 But they cried out, "Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Shall I crucify your king?" The high priests answered, "We have no king but Caesar."

16 Then he delivered him to them to be crucified; so they received Jesus and led him away.

17 And bearing his own cross, he went out to the place called "of the skull," which is said in Hebrew, "Golgotha."

18 There they crucified him, and with him two others, one on either side, and Jesus in the middle.

19 Pilate also wrote a title and put it on the cross. It was written: "Jesus the Nazarene, the King of the Jews."

20 Therefore, many of the Jews read this title, because the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city, and it was written in Hebrew, in Greek, and in Latin.

21 So the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, "Do not write 'The King of the Jews,' but that he said, 'I am the King of the Jews.'"

22 Pilate answered, "What I have written, I have written."

23 So the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, a part for each soldier, and the tunic. Now the tunic was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

24 They said therefore to one another, "Let us not split it, but let us obtain by lot for it whose it will be," so that the Scripture might be fulfilled which says, "They divided my garments among themselves, and for my clothing they cast lots." So the soldiers indeed did these things.

25 Now by the cross of Jesus stood his mother, and his mother's sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.

26 Jesus therefore, seeing his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, says to his mother, "Woman, see your son."

27 Then he says to the disciple, "See, your mother!" And from that hour the disciple took her into his own home.

28 After this, knowing that everything had already been finished, in order that the Scripture might be perfected, Jesus says, "I thirst."

29 A vessel full of vinegar was lying there, and they filled a sponge with vinegar, put it around hyssop, and offered it to his mouth.

30 Therefore when Jesus took the vinegar, he said, "It is finished," and bowing his head, he delivered up his spirit.

31 The Jews therefore, so that the bodies would not remain on the cross on the Sabbath—since it was the preparation day, for that Sabbath day was a great one—asked Pilate that their legs might be broken and they be taken away.

32 Then the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first man and of the other man crucified with him.

33 But when they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead, so they did not break his legs.

34 But one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.

35 And the one who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true, and he knows that he speaks the truth, so that you also may believe.

36 For these things happened so that the Scripture would be fulfilled: "Not a bone of his will be shattered."

37 And again another Scripture says, "They will look on the one whom they pierced."

38 After these things Joseph from Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus but hidden because of fear of the Jews, asked Pilate that he might take the body of Jesus, and Pilate permitted it; so he came and took the body of Jesus.

39 And Nicodemus also came, who had come to Jesus at night the first time, bearing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds.

40 They took the body of Jesus and bound it in linen wrappings with the spices, as is the custom of the Jews to bury.

41 Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had ever yet been put.

42 Therefore, on account of the Jewish preparation day, since the tomb was nearby, they put Jesus there.


## Chapter 20

1 On the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene comes to the tomb early, while it was still dark, and looks at the stone having been taken away from the tomb.

2 So she runs and comes to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and says to them, "They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we do not know where they have put him."

3 So Peter and the other disciple went out and were coming to the tomb.

4 The two were running together, and the other disciple outran Peter quickly and came first to the tomb.

5 Stooping to look, he looks at the linen wrappings lying there; however, he did not enter.

6 Then Simon Peter came following him and entered the tomb, and he beholds the linen wrappings lying there.

7 and the handkerchief, which was on his head, not lying with the linen wrappings but wrapped in a place by itself.

8 Then the other disciple, who had come first to the tomb, also entered, and he saw and believed.

9 For they did not yet know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.

10 Then the disciples departed back to their own homes.

11 But Mary stood outside by the tomb, weeping; so as she wept, she stooped to look into the tomb.

12 And she beholds two angels in white sitting, one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

13 And those say to her, "Woman, why are you weeping?" She says to them, "Because they have taken my Lord, and I do not know where they have put him."

14 And having said these things, she turned to the rear and beholds Jesus standing, and she did not know that it was Jesus.

15 Jesus says to her, "Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?" She, supposing him to be the gardener, says to him, "Sir, if you have borne him away, tell me where you put him, and I will take him."

16 Jesus says to her, "Mary." Having turned, she says to him, "Rabboni" (which is said to mean "Teacher").

17 Jesus says to her, "Do not touch me, for I have not yet gone up to my Father; but go to my brothers and say to them, 'I am going up to my Father and your Father, and my God and your God.'"

18 Mary Magdalene comes, reporting to the disciples that she has seen the Lord and that he said these things to her.

19 Therefore, when it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and the doors were shut where the disciples were gathered due to fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle and said to them, "Peace to you."

20 And after saying this, he showed them his hands and his side; the disciples then rejoiced when they saw the Lord.

21 So Jesus said to them again, "Peace to you. Just as the Father has sent me, I also send you."

22 And when he had said this, he breathed on them and said, "Take the Holy Spirit."

23 If you forgive the sins of any, they are forgiven them; if you seize upon the sins of any, they are seized upon.

24 Thomas, one of the twelve, the one who is called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

25 So the other disciples were saying to him, "We have seen the Lord." But he said to them, "Unless I see in his hands the pattern of the nails and put my finger into the pattern of the nails and put my hand into his side, I will never believe."

26 And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them. Jesus comes, the doors being shut, and stood in the middle and said, "Peace to you."

27 Then He says to Thomas, "Bear your finger here and see My hands, and bear your hand and cast it into My side, and do not be unbelieving, but faithful."

28 Thomas answered and said to him, "My Lord and my God!"

29 Jesus said to him, "Because you have seen me, Thomas, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed."

30 Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book.

31 So these things have been written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.


## Chapter 21

1 After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the Sea of Tiberias, and he revealed himself in this way.

2 Simon Peter was together, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael from Cana of Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples.

3 Simon Peter says to them, "I am departing to go fishing." They say to him, "We also are coming with you." They went out and got into the boat immediately, and that night they seized nothing.

4 When early morning had already come, Jesus stood on the shore; the disciples, however, did not know that it was Jesus.

5 Therefore Jesus says to them, "Children, do you have anything to eat?" They answered him, "No."

6 He said to them, "Cast the net on the right side of the boat and you will find." So they cast, and then they were not able to draw it because of the multitude of fish.

7 Then that disciple whom Jesus loved says to Peter, "It is the Lord." So Simon Peter, hearing that it was the Lord, girded his fisher coat (for he was naked) and cast himself into the sea.

8 But the other disciples came in the boat, for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits away, dragging the net of fish.

9 So when they turned out onto the earth, they look at a coal fire lying there, and a fish lying on it, and bread.

10 Jesus says to them, "Bear from the fish which you have now seized."

11 Simon Peter went up and drew the net onto the land, full of great fish—one hundred and fifty-three—and although there were so many, the net was not split.

12 Jesus says to them, "Come, dine." But no one of the disciples dared ask him, "Who are you?" knowing that it is the Lord.

13 Jesus comes and takes the bread and gives it to them, and the fish likewise.

14 This was now the third time Jesus was revealed to his disciples after being raised from the dead.

15 So when they had dined, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me much?" He said to him, "Yes, Lord; you know that I love you." He said to him, "Feed my lambs."

16 He says to him again, a second time, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?" He says to him, "Yes, Lord, you know that I love you." He says to him, "Shepherd my sheep."

17 He says to him a third time, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?" Peter was grieved because he said to him a third time, "Do you love me?" And he said to him, "Lord, you know all things; you know that I love you." Jesus says to him, "Feed my sheep."

18 Amen, amen, I say to you, when you were new, you girded yourself and walked where you wanted; but when you become old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will gird you and bear you where you do not want.

19 He said this to signify by what kind of death he would glorify God; and having said this, he says to him, "Follow me."

20 Then Peter, turning back, saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following—the one who also had reclined at the supper on his chest and said, "Lord, who is the one betraying you?"

21 Peter, seeing this man, says to Jesus, "Lord, what about this one?"

22 Jesus says to him, "If I want him to remain until I come, what is that to you? You follow me."

23 So this word went out among the brothers, that that disciple would not die; and Jesus did not say to him that he would not die, but, "If I want him to remain until I come, what is that to you?"

24 This is the disciple who testifies about these things and who has written these things, and we know that his testimony is true.

25 There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if each one were written, I suppose that not even the world itself could contain the books that would be written. Amen.


# ACTS

## Chapter 1

1 The first word I made concerning all things, O Theophilus, which Jesus began to do and to teach.

2 until the day he was taken up, having given commandment through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen.

3 To these he also presented himself living after his suffering with many infallible proofs, being seen by them during forty days and speaking about the kingdom of God.

4 Then, while staying with them, he commanded them not to leave Jerusalem, but to wait for the promise of the Father, which you heard from me.

5 For John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit not many days from now.

6 Therefore, those who had come together asked him, saying, "Lord, are you restoring the kingdom to Israel at this time?"

7 He said to them, "It is not for you to know the times or the seasons which the Father has put under His own authority.

8 But you will take power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses both in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the last part of the earth.

9 And after saying these things, while they were looking, He was lifted up, and a cloud took Him out of their sight.

10 And while they were gazing into heaven as He was going, behold, two men in white clothing stood by them.

11 They said, "Men, Galileans, why do you stand looking at the heaven? This Jesus, who has been taken up from you into the heaven, will come in the same way as you beheld him going into the heaven."

12 Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day's journey away.

13 And when they entered, they went up into the upper chamber where they were abiding, both Peter and James and John and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James of Alphaeus and Simon the Zealot and Judas of James.

14 All these were devoting themselves unanimously to prayer and supplication, along with women and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brothers.

15 In those days, rising up in the middle of the disciples, Peter said, there was a crowd of names together about a hundred twenty.

16 Men, brothers, the Scripture had to be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit foretold through the mouth of David concerning Judas, who became a guide to those who seized Jesus.

17 because he had been numbered with us and obtained the share of this ministry.

18 This man acquired a field from the reward of unrighteousness, and becoming headlong he burst asunder in the middle and all his affections poured out.

19 And it became known to all those dwelling in Jerusalem, so that that field was called in their own language Aceldama, which is, Field of Blood.

20 For it is written in the book of Psalms, 'Let his habitation become desolate, and let no one dwelling in it be, and let another take his visitation.'

21 Therefore it is necessary that of the men who have come together with us all the time in which the Lord Jesus entered and went out among us,

22 beginning from the baptism of John until the day when he was taken up from us, one of these must become a witness of his resurrection together with us.

23 And they stood up two: Joseph who is called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.

24 And they prayed and said, "You, Lord, who know the hearts of all men, appoint one of these two whom you have chosen."

25 to take the lot of this ministry and apostleship from which Judas transgressed to go to his own place.

26 And they gave their lots, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.


## Chapter 2

1 When the day of Pentecost came, they were all unanimously in one place.

2 And suddenly there came a sound from heaven like that of a mighty rushing breath, and it filled the whole house where they were sitting.

3 And divided tongues as of fire appeared to them and sat upon each one of them.

4 And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.

5 There were Jews dwelling in Jerusalem, devout men from every nation under heaven.

6 When this voice came, the multitude came together and were confounded, because each one heard them speaking in his own language.

7 All were amazed and marveled, saying to one another, "See, are not all these who are speaking Galileans?"

8 And how do we hear, each of us, in our own native language in which we were born?

9 Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and those dwelling in Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.

10 Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the part of Libya which is toward Cyrene, and the Romans dwelling there, both Jews and proselytes.

11 Cretans and Arabians—we hear them speaking in our own tongues the great things of God.

12 All were amazed and greatly perplexed, saying to one another, "What could this mean?"

13 But others, mocking, were saying that they are filled with new wine.

14 So Peter, standing with the eleven, lifted up his voice and said to them, "Men of Judea and all who dwell in Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and hearken to my words.

15 For they are not drunk, as you think, for it is the third hour of the day.

16 But this is what was said through the prophet Joel.

17 And it will be in the last days, says God, I will pour out from my Spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters will prophesy, and your young men will see visions, and your elders will dream dreams.

18 Even on My male servants and on My female servants I will pour out from My Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.

19 And I will give wonders in the heaven above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and vapour of smoke.

20 The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the great and notable day of the Lord comes.

21 And it will be that all who call upon the name of the Lord will be saved.

22 Men of Israel, hear these words: Jesus the Nazarene, a man demonstrated to you by God through powers and wonders and signs which God did through him in your midst, just as you yourselves know.

23 This man, delivered up by the determined counsel and foreknowledge of God, you took and, having crucified by the hands of lawless men, killed.

24 God raised him up, loosing the pains of death, because it was not possible for him to be seized by it.

25 David says about him, 'I foresaw the Lord before me continually, for he is at my right hand, so that I may not be shaken.'

26 Therefore my heart rejoiced and my tongue rejoiced; and moreover my flesh will also nest in hope.

27 For you will not forsake my soul in Hades, nor will you let your Holy One see corruption.

28 You have made known to me the ways of life; you will fill me with gladness in your presence.

29 Men, brothers, it is lawful to speak with boldness to you about the patriarch David, that he both died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this day.

30 Therefore being a prophet and knowing that God had sworn with an oath to him, of the fruit of his loin according to the flesh he would raise up the Christ to sit on his throne.

31 Foreseeing this, he spoke about the resurrection of the Christ, that his soul was not left in Hades, nor did his flesh see corruption.

32 God raised up this Jesus, of whom we all are witnesses.

33 Having been exalted to the right hand of God, and having taken the promise of the Holy Spirit from the Father, he has poured out this which you now look at and hear.

34 For David did not go up into the heavens, but he himself says, "The Lord said to my Lord, 'Sit at my right hand,'"

35 until I put your enemies as a footstool for your feet.

36 Assuredly, therefore, let all the house of Israel know that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.

37 When they heard this, they were pricked in their heart and said to Peter and the remaining apostles, "What shall we do, men and brothers?"

38 Peter said to them, "Repent and each of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will take the gift of the Holy Spirit."

39 For the promise is for you and your children and for all those who are far away, as many as the Lord our God may call.

40 With many other words he earnestly testified and was exhorting them, saying, "Be saved from this crooked generation."

41 Those who gladly welcomed his word were baptized, and on that day about three thousand souls were added.

42 They were devoting themselves to the teaching of the apostles and to the fellowship and to the breaking of bread and to the prayers.

43 Fear came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs happened through the apostles.

44 All the believers were in one place and had all things in common.

45 And they sold their possessions and goods and divided them among all, according as anyone had need.

46 Devoting themselves day by day unanimously in the temple and breaking bread from house to house, they partook of food with exultation and singleness of heart.

47 praising God and having grace toward the whole people, and the Lord was adding those being saved day by day to the church.


## Chapter 3

1 Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.

2 Now a certain man, lame from his mother's womb, was being carried, whom they placed daily at the gate of the temple called Beautiful, to ask for alms from those entering into the temple.

3 He, seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, asked to receive alms.

4 But Peter, gazing at him together with John, said, "Look at us."

5 But he gave heed to them, expecting to take something from them.

6 Peter said, "Silver and gold do not exist for me, but what I have I give to you: in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, get up and walk."

7 And seizing him by the right hand, he raised him immediately, and his feet and ankle bones were established.

8 And leaping up, he stood and began to walk, and entered with them into the temple, walking and leaping and praising God.

9 And all the people saw him walking and praising God.

10 They recognized him that this was the one who was sitting for alms at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with amazement and trance at what had happened to him.

11 While the man who had been healed was seizing Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the porch which is called Solomon's, greatly wondering.

12 When Peter saw this, he answered the people, "Men of Israel, why do you marvel at this, or why do you gaze at us, as though by our own power or godliness we have made him walk?"

13 The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, the God of our Fathers, glorified his servant Jesus, whom you delivered and denied in the presence of Pilate when he had judged to release him.

14 But you denied the Holy and Righteous One, and asked for a murderer to be forgiven to you.

15 You killed the pioneer of life, whom God raised from the dead, of which we are witnesses.

16 And by faith in his name, this man whom you behold and know, his name has established, and the faith that comes through him has given him this perfect soundness in the presence of all of you.

17 And now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance, as also your rulers did.

18 But God fulfilled in this way what He foretold through the mouth of all His prophets, that the Christ would suffer.

19 Therefore repent and turn back, so that your sins may be blotted out, in order that times of revival may come from the face of the Lord.

20 and he should send the one who had been proclaimed beforehand to you—Jesus Christ.

21 whom heaven must receive until the time of the restitution of all things, of which God spoke through the mouth of all his holy prophets from the age.

22 Moses indeed said to the Fathers, "The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet from among your brothers like me; you will hear him in everything whatever he says to you."

23 And every soul who will not hear that prophet shall be destroyed from among the people.

24 And all the prophets from Samuel and those in order, as many as spoke, also foretold these days.

25 You are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant which God appointed to our fathers, saying to Abraham, "And in your seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed."

26 God, after causing his servant Jesus to rise, sent him to bless you by turning each of you from your wickednesses.


## Chapter 4

1 While they were speaking to the people, the priests and the captain of the temple and the Sadducees came up to them.

2 They were grieved because they taught the people and proclaimed the resurrection from the dead in Jesus.

3 And they laid hands on them and put them in custody until the next day, for it was already evening.

4 But many of those who heard the word believed, and the number of the men came to about five thousand.

5 The next day, their rulers, elders, and scribes gathered together in Jerusalem.

6 Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the high-priestly family.

7 They stood them in the middle and asked, "By what power or by what name did you do this?"

8 Then Peter, filled with the Holy Spirit, said to them, "Rulers of the people and elders of Israel,

9 If we are being examined today for a benefit to a weak man, by what means this man has been made well,

10 Be it known to all of you and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, whom you crucified, whom God raised from the dead, in this name this man stands before you whole.

11 This is the stone that was despised by you, the builders, which has become the chief cornerstone.

12 And there is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved.

13 Beholding the boldness of Peter and John, and having overtaken that they are unlearned and unskilled men, they marveled, and recognized them, that they had been with Jesus.

14 Looking at the man who had been healed standing with them, they had nothing to say against him.

15 And commanding them to depart outside the council, they conferred with one another.

16 saying, "What shall we do to these men? For indeed a known sign has happened through them, manifest to all those dwelling in Jerusalem, and we are not able to deny it."

17 But to keep it from spreading further among the people, let us threaten them with a threat not to speak anymore in this name to any man.

18 They called them in and commanded them not to utter anything at all nor to teach in the name of Jesus.

19 But Peter and John answered and said to them, "Whether it is righteous before God to listen to you rather than to God, judge.

20 For we are not able not to speak the things which we have seen and heard.

21 After threatening them further, they released them, finding no way they could punish them because of the people, since all were glorifying God for what had happened.

22 For the man on whom this sign of healing had happened was more than forty years old.

23 After being released, they came to their own people and reported everything that the high priests and the elders had said to them.

24 And hearing this, they unanimously raised their voice to God and said, "Master, you are the God who made heaven and earth and the sea and everything in them."

25 who through the mouth of your servant David said, "Why did the nations rage and the peoples imagine vain things?"

26 The kings of the earth stood present, and the rulers gathered together against the Lord and against his Christ.

27 For truly in this city they gathered together against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, along with the nations and the peoples of Israel.

28 to do whatever your hand and your counsel predestined to happen.

29 And now, Lord, behold their threats and give to your servants to speak your word with all boldness.

30 while you stretch out your hand for cure and signs and wonders to happen through the name of your holy servant Jesus.

31 And when they had prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and they spoke the word of God with boldness.

32 The multitude of those who believed were of one heart and one soul, and no one said that any of the things which existed was his own, but all things were common to them.

33 And with great power the apostles were repaying the testimony of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.

34 For there was not any lacking among them, for as many as were possessors of fields or houses, selling them, were bearing the prices of the things being sold.

35 They put them at the feet of the apostles, and it was distributed to each as anyone had need.

36 Joses, who was called Barnabas by the apostles (which is interpreted as Son of Comfort), a Levite and a Cypriot by birth.

37 He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and put it at the apostles' feet.


## Chapter 5

1 But a man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession.

2 And he kept back part of the price, his wife being aware of it, and after bearing a certain part, he put it at the feet of the apostles.

3 But Peter said, "Ananias, why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit and to keep back part of the price of the field?

4 While it remained unsold, did it not remain yours? And after it was sold, was it not in your own authority? Why did you put this matter in your heart? You did not lie to men but to God.

5 Hearing these words, Ananias fell down and gave up the ghost, and great fear came upon all who heard these things.

6 When they had risen, the new men wrapped him in a short shroud, and bringing him forth, they buried him.

7 After a space of about three hours had happened, his wife entered, not knowing what had happened.

8 And Peter answered her, "Tell me, did you sell the field for so much?" And she said, "Yes, for so much."

9 But Peter said to her, "Why is it that you agreed together to test the Spirit of the Lord? See, the feet of those who buried your husband are at the door, and they will bring you forth."

10 And immediately she fell at his feet and gave up the ghost, and when the young men entered they found her dead, and they brought her forth and buried her beside her husband.

11 And great fear came upon the whole church and upon all who heard these things.

12 Through the hands of the apostles, many signs and wonders happened among the people; and they were all unanimously together in Solomon's porch.

13 But none of the remaining dared to join them; however, the people magnified them.

14 And more than ever, multitudes of men and women were added, believing in the Lord.

15 So that they brought forth the weak into the streets and put them on beds and pallets, that as Peter came by at least his shadow might overshadow some of them.

16 Also the multitude from the cities round about were coming together to Jerusalem, bearing the weak and those vexed by unclean spirits, all of whom were healed.

17 But the high priest rose up, and all those with him, which is the sect of the Sadducees, were filled with zeal.

18 And they laid their hands on the apostles and put them in public keeping.

19 During the night an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the prison, led them out, and said.

20 Go and stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this life.

21 Having heard this, they entered the temple early in the morning and began to teach. Now when the high priest and those with him had come, they called together the council and all the senate of the sons of Israel, and they sent to the prison to have them brought.

22 But when the officers came, they did not find them in the prison; so when they had returned, they reported,

23 saying that we found the prison shut with all certainty, and the keepers standing outside before the doors, but when we opened it, we found no one within.

24 When they heard these words, both the priest and the captain of the temple and the high priests were perplexed about them, wondering what this would be.

25 But someone came and reported to them, saying, "See, the men you put in the prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people."

26 Then the captain, having departed with the servants, led them, not with violence, for they feared the people, lest they be stoned.

27 After leading them in, they stood them before the council, and the high priest asked them.

28 Saying, "Did we not strictly command you not to teach in this name? And see, you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching, and you intend to bring upon us the blood of this man."

29 But Peter and the apostles answered, "We must hearken to God rather than to men."

30 The God of our Fathers raised Jesus, whom you killed by hanging Him on a tree.

31 God exalted this one as a pioneer and Savior with his right hand, to give repentance to Israel and forgiveness of sins.

32 And we are his witnesses of these words, and the Holy Spirit also, whom God has given to those who hearken to him.

33 When they heard this, they were cut to the heart and plotted to kill them.

34 Then a certain Pharisee in the council, named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law who was honored by all the people, commanded that the apostles be put outside for a little while.

35 He said to them, "Men, Israelites, give heed to yourselves as to these men — what are you about to do?"

36 For before these days Theudas rose up, saying that he himself was somebody, to whom a number of men, about four hundred, cleaved; who was killed, and all, as many as were persuaded by him, were scattered and came to nothing.

37 After this man, Judas the Galilean rose up in the days of the registration and led away a considerable number of people after him; that man also perished, and all who were persuaded by him were scattered.

38 And now I say to you, depart from these men and allow them, for if this counsel or this work is from men, it will be destroyed.

39 But if it is from God, you are not able to destroy it, lest you be found to be fighting against God as well.

40 They were persuaded by him, and after calling the apostles and beating them, they commanded them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and released them.

41 So they went out from the face of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer dishonor for the sake of his name.

42 And every day, in the temple and from house to house, they did not cease teaching and preaching the gospel of Jesus the Christ.


## Chapter 6

1 Now in those days, as the disciples were multiplying, a grumbling happened of the Grecians against the Hebrews, because their widows were being neglected in the daily ministry.

2 The twelve called the multitude of the disciples and said, "It is not pleasing that we, leaving the word of God, should serve tables."

3 Therefore, brothers, select seven men from among you who are testified to be full of the Holy Spirit and wisdom, whom we will appoint over this need.

4 But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.

5 The word pleased all the multitude, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas, a proselyte of Antioch.

6 They stood them before the apostles, and after praying, they laid their hands on them.

7 And the word of God grew, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem was greatly multiplied, and a great crowd of the priests were obeying the faith.

8 Stephen, full of faith and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.

9 But some of those from the synagogue called the Libertines, Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of those from Cilicia and Asia, rose up and were disputing with Stephen.

10 And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.

11 Then they suborned men saying, "We have heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God."

12 They stirred up the people and the elders and the scribes, and standing by, they caught him and led him into the council.

13 They stood up false witnesses saying, "This man does not cease speaking blasphemous words against this holy place and the law."

14 For we have heard him saying that this Jesus the Nazarene will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses delivered to us.

15 And gazing at him, all those sitting in the council saw his face like the face of an angel.


## Chapter 7

1 The high priest asked, "Surely these things are not so?"

2 He said, "Brothers and fathers, listen: the God of glory appeared to our father Abraham while he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran.

3 And he said to him, "Go out from your earth and from your kindred, and come to the earth which I will show you."

4 After going out from the land of the Chaldeans, he dwelt in Charran; and from there, after his father died, He deported him into this land in which you now dwell.

5 And he did not give him an inheritance in it, not even a footprint, and he promised to give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, though he had no child.

6 God spoke in this way: that his seed would be a foreigner in a stranger's land, and they would enslave and harm it for four hundred years.

7 And the nation to which they will serve I will judge, God said, and after these things they will go out and serve me in this place.

8 And he gave him the covenant of circumcision; and so he gave birth to Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day, and Isaac to Jacob, and Jacob to the twelve patriarchs.

9 The patriarchs, being jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt, and God was with him.

10 And he delivered him out of all his tribulations and gave him grace and wisdom before Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he appointed him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.

11 Now a famine came over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great tribulation, and our fathers found no sustenance.

12 When Jacob heard that there was wheat in Egypt, he sent forth our fathers first.

13 And on the second occasion Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph's kindred became manifest to Pharaoh.

14 Joseph sent and summoned his Father Jacob and all his kindred, seventy-five souls in all.

15 Then Jacob went down to Egypt, and he died, as did our Fathers.

16 And they were removed to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.

17 But as the time of the promise which God swore to Abraham was drawing near, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt.

18 Until a different king rose who did not know Joseph.

19 He dealt subtly with our kind and harmed our fathers, causing them to cast out their infants so that they would not live.

20 At that time Moses was born, and he was fair in the sight of God; he was brought up for three months in his father's house.

21 When he was set aside, the daughter of Pharaoh took and brought him up for herself as a son.

22 And Moses was disciplined in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was able in words and in works.

23 When the time of forty years was being fulfilled for him, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the sons of Israel.

24 And seeing someone being wronged, he defended him and did vengeance for the one being oppressed by striking the Egyptian.

25 He was supposing that his brothers would understand that God was giving them salvation through his hand, but they did not understand.

26 The next day he appeared to them as they were striving and set them at one again, saying, Men, you are brothers; why do you do wrong to one another?

27 But the one doing wrong to his neighbor thrust him away, saying, 'Who appointed you a ruler and a judge over us?'

28 Do you want to kill me in the same way you killed the Egyptian yesterday?

29 But Moses fled at this word and became a foreigner in the land of Midian, where he fathered two sons.

30 And when forty years were fulfilled, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai in a flame of fire, in a bush.

31 But when Moses saw it, he marveled at the vision; and as he came near to consider, the voice of the Lord came to him.

32 I am the God of your Fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob; but Moses, having become trembling, did not dare to consider it.

33 The Lord said to him, "Loose the sandal of your feet, for the place on which you stand is holy ground."

34 I have certainly seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and I have come down to deliver them; and now come, I will send you to Egypt.

35 This Moses, whom they denied, saying, 'Who appointed you a ruler and a judge?'—this one God sent as a ruler and deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

36 This man led them out, having done wonders and signs in the land of Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the wilderness for forty years.

37 This is the Moses who said to the sons of Israel, 'The Lord your God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers; you will listen to him.'

38 This is the one who happened in the church in the wilderness with the angel speaking to him on Mount Sinai and with our Fathers, who received living oracles to give to us.

39 Our fathers did not want to become obedient, but thrust him away and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.

40 Saying to Aaron, "Make gods for us who will go before us, for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt—we do not know what has happened to him."

41 And they made a calf in those days and led up a sacrifice to the idol and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.

42 But God turned and delivered them to serve the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: Did you offer slain beasts and sacrifices to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel?

43 You took up the tabernacle of Moloch and the star of your God Remphan, the patterns which you made to worship them, and I will deport you beyond Babylon.

44 The tabernacle of the testimony was among our fathers in the wilderness, just as the one speaking to Moses appointed to make it according to the pattern that he had seen.

45 Our fathers, who came after, brought it in with Jesus during the possession of the nations which God drove out from before the face of our fathers until the days of David.

46 He found grace in the sight of God and asked to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.

47 But Solomon built a house for him.

48 But the Most High does not dwell in temples made by hands, just as the prophet says.

49 Heaven is my throne, and the earth is a footstool for my feet. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is the place of my rest?

50 Did not my hand do all these things?

51 Stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit; as your fathers did, so do you.

52 Which of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? And they killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers.

53 You who took the law as ordinances of angels and did not keep it.

54 When they heard these things, they were cut to their hearts and gnashed their teeth at him.

55 Being full of the Holy Spirit, he gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.

56 He said, "See, I behold the heavens open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God."

57 But they cried out with a loud voice, stopped their ears, and rushed upon him unanimously.

58 And having cast him out of the city, they stoned him; and the witnesses put away their garments at the feet of a young man called Saul.

59 And they were stoning Stephen as he called upon God and said, "Lord Jesus, receive my spirit."

60 Then putting his knees down, he cried out with a loud voice, "Lord, do not hold this sin against them." And having said this, he fell asleep.


## Chapter 8

1 Saul was consenting to his death. On that day a great persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all were scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.

2 Devout men carried Stephen and made great lamentation over him.

3 But Saul was ravaging the church, entering house after house, dragging off both men and women and delivering them to prison.

4 Those who had been scattered abroad passed through, preaching the word.

5 Then Philip, having gone down to a city of Samaria, was preaching Christ to them.

6 And the crowds gave heed unanimously to the things being said by Philip when they heard and saw the signs which he did.

7 For many of those who had unclean spirits, crying with a loud voice, went out, and many who were paralyzed and lame were healed.

8 And there was great joy in that city.

9 Now a certain man named Simon had previously existed in the city, using sorcery and amazing the nation of Samaria, saying that he himself was someone great.

10 All of them, from the least to the greatest, gave heed to him, saying, "This man is the great power of God."

11 But they were giving heed to him because for a sufficient time he had amazed them with his sorceries.

12 But when they believed Philip preaching the gospel concerning the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.

13 But Simon himself also believed, and having been baptized, he was constantly devoting himself to Philip, and as he beheld signs and great powers taking place, he was amazed.

14 The apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, and they sent Peter and John to them.

15 Those who had gone down prayed for them that they might take the Holy Spirit.

16 For as yet He had not fallen upon any of them; they only existed baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.

17 Then they were laying their hands on them, and they were taking the Holy Spirit.

18 When Simon saw that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money.

19 Saying, "Give this authority to me also, so that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit."

20 But Peter said to him, "May your silver be with you for destruction, because you supposed that the gift of God is acquired by money."

21 There is no portion or lot for you in this word, for your heart is not upright before God.

22 Repent, therefore, of this your malice, and pray to God that perhaps the thought of your heart might be forgiven you.

23 For I see you being in the gall of bitterness and the band of unrighteousness.

24 Then Simon answered and said, "Pray to the Lord for me, so that none of the things you have said come upon me."

25 After testifying and speaking the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, and they preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.

26 An angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, "Rise and go toward noon to the road going down from Jerusalem to Gaza; this is a wilderness."

27 So he got up and went, and see, an Ethiopian man, a eunuch and potentate of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasure, had come to worship in Jerusalem.

28 He was returning and sitting in his chariot, and was reading the prophet Isaiah.

29 The Spirit said to Philip, "Come near and join this chariot."

30 Then Philip ran to him and heard him reading the prophet Isaiah, and said, "Do you really understand what you are reading?"

31 He said, "For how could I be able, unless someone guides me?" And he encouraged Philip to go up and sit with him.

32 The place of the Scripture which he was reading was this: "As a sheep is led to slaughter, and as a lamb before the one shearing it is mute, so he does not open his mouth."

33 In his humiliation his judgment was taken away, and who will declare his generation? For his life is taken from the earth.

34 The eunuch answered and said to Philip, "I pray you, about whom does the prophet say this—about himself or about some other person?"

35 Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, he preached the gospel to him about Jesus.

36 As they were going along the way, they came to some water, and the eunuch says, "See, water! What forbids me to be baptized?"

37 Then Philip said, "If you believe with all your heart, it is lawful." And he answered and said, "I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God."

38 He commanded the chariot to stand still, and both went down into the water, both Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.

39 But when they went up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more; for he went on his way rejoicing.

40 Philip was found in Azotus, and as he passed through he preached the gospel in all the cities until he came to Caesarea.


## Chapter 9

1 But Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, came near to the high priest.

2 He asked from him letters to Damascus for the synagogues, so that if he found any who were of the Way, both men and women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.

3 Now as he went, it happened that he was drawing near to Damascus, and suddenly a light from heaven shone around him.

4 And falling to the earth, he heard a voice saying to him, "Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?"

5 He said, "Who are you, Lord?" And the Lord said, "I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting; it is hard for you to kick against the stings."

6 Trembling and astonished, he said, "Lord, what do you want me to do?" And the Lord said to him, "Rise and enter the city, and it will be spoken to you what you must do."

7 But the men who were journeying with him stood speechless, hearing the voice but beholding no one.

8 Saul got up from the ground, and with his eyes opened, he could see no one, and leading him by the hand, they brought him into Damascus.

9 And he was three days without looking, and did not eat or drink.

10 There was a certain disciple in Damascus named Ananias, and the Lord said to him in a vision, "Ananias," and he said, "See, I am here, Lord."

11 The Lord said to him, "Rise and go to the lane called Immediate, and seek in the house of Judas a man named Saul of Tarsus, for see, he is praying."

12 And he saw in a vision a man named Ananias enter and lay his hands on him so that he might receive sight.

13 Then Ananias answered, "Lord, I have heard from many about this man how much evil he has done to your holy ones in Jerusalem."

14 And here he has authority from the high priests to bind all those who call upon your name.

15 The Lord said to him, "Go, for this man is a chosen vessel to me to bear my name before both the Gentiles and their kings and the sons of Israel."

16 For I will show him how much he must suffer for my name.

17 Ananias departed and entered the house, and laying his hands on him he said, "Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me so that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit."

18 And immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he received his sight at once; and getting up, he was baptized.

19 And taking food, he was strengthened; and Saul was with the disciples in Damascus for some days.

20 And immediately he preached Christ in the synagogues, that this One is the Son of God.

21 All those who were hearing were amazed and were saying, "Is this not the one who destroyed in Jerusalem those who call upon this name, and had come here for this purpose, that he might lead them bound to the high priests?"

22 But Saul was all the more strengthened and confounded the Jews dwelling in Damascus, knitting together that this is the Christ.

23 But when enough days had been fulfilled, the Jews advised together to kill him.

24 But their plot became known to Saul; and they also watched the gates day and night so that they might kill him.

25 But the disciples took him by night and let him down through the wall, letting him down in a basket.

26 When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he attempted to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

27 But Barnabas took hold of him and led him to the apostles, and declared to them how he had seen the Lord on the way, and that He had spoken to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.

28 And he was with them, entering and going out in Jerusalem and speaking boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus.

29 He spoke boldly and disputed with the Grecians; but they attempted to kill him.

30 When the brothers recognized him, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.

31 Then the churches throughout the whole of Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace, being built up and walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, and they were multiplied.

32 Now it happened that as Peter was passing through all [the regions], he went down also to the holy ones dwelling in Lydda.

33 But he found a certain man there named Aeneas, who had been lying on a bed for eight years and was paralyzed.

34 And Peter said to him, "Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; rise and spread your own bed." And immediately he rose.

35 All the dwellers of Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned back to the Lord.

36 In Joppa there was a certain disciple named Tabitha, which when interpreted is called Dorcas; she was full of good works and alms which she did.

37 But it became in those days that she, having become weak, died; and after washing her, they placed her in an upper chamber.

38 Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, having heard that Peter was there, sent two men to him, urging him not to delay passing through to them.

39 Rising, Peter came together with them; when he came, they led him up into the upper chamber, and all the widows presented themselves to him, weeping and showing the tunics and garments that Dorcas used to make while she was with them.

40 Cast out all of them, Peter put his knees and prayed, and turning back to the body he said, "Tabitha, rise." She opened her eyes, and seeing Peter she sat up.

41 Then, giving her a hand, he raised her up; and calling the holy ones and the widows, he presented her alive.

42 Now it became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed on the Lord.

43 It happened that he remained for many days in Joppa with a certain Simon, a tanner.


## Chapter 10

1 Now there was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion of the cohort called Italian.

2 devout and fearing God with all his household, doing many alms to the people and praying always to God.

3 He saw in a vision openly, about the ninth hour of the day, an angel of God entering toward him and saying to him, "Cornelius."

4 Then he gazed at him and became terrified and said, "What is it, Sir?" He said to him, "Your prayers and your alms have gone up as a memorial before God."

5 And now send men to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter.

6 He is entertained by Simon the tanner, whose house is by the sea; he will speak to you what you must do.

7 When the angel who was speaking to Cornelius had departed, he called two of his servants and a devout soldier from those who were devoted to him.

8 And having declared all these things to them, he sent them to Joppa.

9 On the next day, as they were going on a journey and drawing near to the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray, about the sixth hour.

10 Now became very hungry and wanted to taste something, and while they were preparing, a trance fell upon him.

11 And he beholds heaven opened and a vessel like a great sheet descending upon him, bound at four beginnings and let down to the earth.

12 In which there existed all the fourfooted beasts of the earth, and the beasts, and the reptiles, and the birds of the heaven.

13 A voice came to him: "Rise, Peter, sacrifice and eat."

14 But Peter said, "Not so, Lord, for I have never eaten anything common or unclean."

15 And a voice spoke again a second time to him, "What God has cleansed, do not defile."

16 But this happened three times, and the vessel was taken up again into heaven.

17 But while Peter was perplexed within himself as to what the vision which he had seen might be, see, the men who were sent from Cornelius, having made enquiry for Simon's house, stood at the gate.

18 They called and asked whether Simon, who is called Peter, was being entertained there.

19 While Peter was thinking about the vision, the Spirit said to him, "See, three men are seeking you.

20 But rise, go down and go with them, doubting nothing, because I have sent them.

21 Peter went down to the men sent from Cornelius to him and said, "See, I am the one you are seeking; what is the reason for which you are present?"

22 And they said, Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous and God-fearing man, well spoken of by the whole nation of the Jews, was warned by a holy angel to summon you to his house and to hear words from you.

23 After calling them in, he entertained them; and the next day Peter went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa came together with him.

24 On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Now Cornelius was expecting them, having called together his kinsmen and his necessary friends.

25 And as it happened that Peter entered, Cornelius, meeting him, fell at his feet and worshiped him.

26 But Peter raised him, saying, "Rise; and I myself am a man."

27 As he talked with him, he entered and found many who had come together.

28 He said to them, "You know how unlawful it is for a Jewish man to join or come near a foreigner, but God has shown me not to call any man common or unclean.

29 And so I came without objection when I was summoned, and I ask then for what reason you summoned me.

30 Cornelius said, "From the fourth day until this hour I was fasting and praying at the ninth hour in my house; and see, a man stood before me in bright clothing.

31 And he says, "Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God."

32 Therefore send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter; he is being entertained in the house of Simon the tanner, by the sea, and when he comes he will speak to you.

33 Therefore I sent for you immediately, and you have done well to come. Now therefore we are all present before God to hear all the things that have been commanded you by God.

34 Having opened his mouth, Peter said, "In truth I perceive that God is not a respecter of persons."

35 But in every nation the one who fears Him and works righteousness is acceptable to Him.

36 The word which he sent to the sons of Israel, preaching the gospel of peace through Jesus Christ—this one is Lord of all.

37 You yourselves know the word that happened throughout the whole of Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John preached.

38 Jesus of Nazareth, how God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all those oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.

39 And we are witnesses of all that he did both in the region of the Jews and in Jerusalem, whom they killed by hanging him on a tree.

40 God raised this one on the third day and gave him to become manifest.

41 not to all the people, but to witnesses who had been chosen before by God, to us who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.

42 And he commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one determined by God to be the judge of the living and the dead.

43 All the prophets testify to this, that through his name everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins.

44 While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who were hearing the word.

45 The faithful from among the circumcision were amazed, as many as had come together with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the nations.

46 For they heard them speaking in tongues and magnifying God. Then Peter answered.

47 Surely no one is able to forbid the water for these to be baptized, who have taken the Holy Spirit just as we also have.

48 He commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then they asked him to continue for some days.


## Chapter 11

1 Now the apostles and the brothers who were throughout Judea heard that the nations also had received the word of God.

2 And when Peter went up to Jerusalem, those from the circumcision disputed with him.

3 They were saying, "You entered to men who have uncircumcision and ate with them."

4 Then Peter began to expound to them in order, saying,

5 I was in the city of Joppa praying, and I saw in a trance a vision: something like a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners, and it came as far as me.

6 Gazing into it and considering, I saw the fourfooted beasts of the earth, and the beasts, and the reptiles, and the birds of the heaven.

7 I heard a voice saying to me, 'Rise, Peter, sacrifice and eat.'

8 I said, "Not so, Lord, for nothing common or unclean has ever entered my mouth."

9 Then a voice answered me a second time from heaven, "What God has cleansed, do not defile."

10 This happened three times, and then all was drawn up again into heaven.

11 See, immediately three men stood by the house in which I was, sent from Caesarea to me.

12 The Spirit told me to come together with them, discerning no one. And these six brothers also came with me, and we entered the house of the man.

13 And he reported to us how he saw the angel standing in his house and saying to him, "Send men to Joppa and summon Simon who is called Peter."

14 He will speak words to you by which you and all your household will be saved.

15 Then as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them, just as also upon us in the beginning.

16 Then I remembered the word of the Lord, how he used to say, 'John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.'

17 If then God gave them the same gift as also to us who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I to be able to forbid God?

18 When they heard these things, they were quiet and glorified God, saying, "Then indeed God has given repentance to the nations, leading to life."

19 Those who were scattered abroad from the tribulation that happened over Stephen passed through to Phoenix and Cyprus and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except only Jews.

20 But there were some of them, men from Cyprus and Cyrene, who, when they had entered Antioch, spoke to the Grecians, preaching the gospel of the Lord Jesus.

21 And the hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number who believed turned back to the Lord.

22 The word was heard in the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem concerning them, and they sent forth Barnabas to pass through as far as Antioch.

23 When he came and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged all to remain with the Lord with purpose of heart.

24 For he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and a considerable crowd was added to the Lord.

25 And Barnabas went out to Tarsus to seek Saul.

26 And when he found him, he led him to Antioch. And it happened that for a whole year they were gathered together in the church and taught a sufficient crowd, and the disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

27 In those days, prophets went down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

28 One of them, named Agabus, stood up and signified through the Spirit that a great famine was about to come upon the whole world, which also happened under Claudius Caesar.

29 The disciples, each as anyone prospered, determined to send aid to the brothers dwelling in Judea.

30 This they did, sending it to the elders by the hand of Barnabas and Saul.


## Chapter 12

1 At that time, Herod the king laid hands on some of those from the church to harm them.

2 He killed James the brother of John with a sword.

3 Seeing that it was pleasing to the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter as well. Now those were the days of unleavened bread.

4 Having seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him, intending after the Passover to lead him up to the people.

5 So Peter was kept in the prison, but prayer was being made without ceasing by the church to God for him.

6 But when Herod was about to bring him forward, that night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and keepers before the door were guarding the prison.

7 And see, an angel of the Lord stood by, and a light shone in the prison; and striking Peter on the side, he raised him, saying, "Rise quickly." And the chains fell away from his hands.

8 The angel said to him, "Gird yourself and bind on your sandals." He did so, and he says to him, "Clothe yourself with your garment and follow me."

9 Going out, he followed him and did not know that what was happening through the angel was true, but he thought he was seeing a vision.

10 And having passed through the first and second prison, they came to the iron gate that bears into the city, which opened to them of its own accord; and having gone out, they advanced one lane, and immediately the angel departed from him.

11 And Peter, having come to himself, said, "Now I know truly that the Lord has sent forth his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod and all the expectation of the people of the Jews."

12 Having considered this, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who is called Mark, where many were gathered together and praying.

13 When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a maidservant named Rhoda came near to answer.

14 And having recognized Peter's voice, from joy she did not open the gate, but having run in, she reported that Peter was standing before the gate.

15 They said to her, "You are mad," but she confidently affirmed that it was so. But they said, "It is his angel."

16 But Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the door, they saw him and were amazed.

17 After beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, he declared how the Lord had led him out of the prison. Then he said, "Report these things to James and the brothers." And after going out, he went to another place.

18 When day happened, there was no small stir among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter.

19 Herod, after seeking him and not finding him, examined the keepers and commanded them to be led away; and going down from Judea to Caesarea, he stayed there.

20 Now Herod was highly displeased with the Tyrians and Sidonians, but they came to him unanimously and, having persuaded Blastus the king's chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their region was nourished from the king's country.

21 On a set day Herod, having put on royal clothing and sat upon the judgment seat, made an oration to them.

22 The crowd was crying out, "The voice of a god, and not of a man!"

23 Immediately, an angel of the Lord struck him, because he did not give the glory to God, and having become eaten of worms, he gave up the ghost.

24 But the word of God grew and multiplied.

25 Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, having fulfilled their ministry, and also took with them John, who was called Mark.


## Chapter 13

1 Now there were in Antioch, in the existing church, prophets and teachers: both Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius the Cyrenian, Manaen who had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

2 While they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, "Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them."

3 Then, after fasting and praying and laying their hands on them, they released them.

4 So these men, having been sent away by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, and from there they sailed away to Cyprus.

5 When they were in Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews, and they also had John as a servant.

6 When they had passed through the island as far as Paphos, they found a certain magi, a false prophet, a Jew whose name was Barjesus.

7 He was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, a prudent man, who called for Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God.

8 But Elymas the magician resisted them (for that is how his name is interpreted), seeking to pervert the proconsul from the faith.

9 But Saul, also known as Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit and gazing intently at him,

10 You are full of all deceit and all mischief, son of the devil, enemy of all righteousness—will you never cease perverting the straight ways of the Lord?

11 And now see, the hand of the Lord is upon you, and you will be blind, not looking at the sun until a time. And immediately mist and darkness fell upon him, and going about he sought some to lead him by the hand.

12 Then the proconsul, seeing what had happened, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

13 So having been led up from Paphos, those around Paul came to Perga of Pamphylia. But John, having departed from them, returned to Jerusalem.

14 But having passed through from Perga, they came to Antioch of Pisidia, and having entered the synagogue on the Sabbath day, they sat down.

15 After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue rulers sent to them, saying, "Men, brothers, if there is any word of comfort for the people, say it."

16 Rising up, Paul, and beckoning with his hand, said, "Men of Israel and those who fear God, hear."

17 The God of this people Israel chose our Fathers and exalted the people in the sojourning in the land of Egypt, and with a high arm he led them out from it.
