25 For circumcision indeed profits if you practice the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.

26 If then the uncircumcision keeps the ordinances of the law, will not his uncircumcision be reckoned as circumcision?

27 And the uncircumcision which is by nature, when it fulfills the law, will judge you who are a transgressor of the law through the letter and circumcision.

28 For the one who is a Jew outwardly is not a Jew, nor is outward circumcision in the flesh circumcision.

29 But the Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter; whose praise is not from men, but from God.


## Chapter 3

1 What then is the abundant advantage of the Jew, or what is the benefit of circumcision?

2 Much in every way. First of all, they were entrusted with the oracles of God.

3 What then? If some disbelieved, their unbelief will not abolish the faith of God, will it?

4 May it never be! But let God be true, and every man a liar, as it is written: "That you may be justified in your words, and overcome when you are judged."

5 But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what shall we say? God, who brings upon wrath, is not unrighteous, is he?—I speak as a man.

6 Of course, not! Otherwise, how would God judge the world?

7 For if the truth of God abounded in my lie to His glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?

8 And not as we are blasphemed and as some say that we say, "Let us do evil so that good may come"? Their judgment is just.

9 What then are we better? Not at all; for we have previously proved both Jews and Greeks to be all under sin.

10 as it is written: there is not even one righteous person.

11 There is no one who understands, there is no one who seeks out God.

12 All have avoided, together they have become unprofitable; there is none who does kindness, there is not so much as one.

13 Their throat is an open tomb; they use deceit with their tongues; the poison of asps is under their lips.

14 Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.

15 Sharp are their feet to pour out blood.

16 Destruction and misery are in their ways.

17 and they have not known the way of peace.

18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.

19 We know that whatever the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be stopped and all the world may become guilty before God.

20 Therefore, no flesh will be justified before him by works of the law; for through the law comes knowledge of sin.

21 But now, apart from the law, the righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified to by the law and the prophets.

22 But the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ is for all who believe, for there is no difference.

23 For all have sinned and lack the glory of God.

24 Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus.

25 God set forth as a propitiation through faith in his blood for a proof of his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past.

26 in the forbearance of God, as a proof of his righteousness at the present time, for him to be righteous and the one who justifies the one who is of the faith of Jesus.

27 Then what is the boasting? It is excluded. Through what law? That of works? No, but through the law of faith.

28 We reckon, therefore, that a man is justified by faith without works of the law.

29 The God of the Jews only? Is he not also of the nations? Yes, of the nations also.

30 Since there is only one God, who will justify the circumcision by faith and the uncircumcision through faith.

31 Do we then abolish the law through faith? By no means! On the contrary, we establish the law.


## Chapter 4

1 What then shall we say that Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh, has found?

2 For if Abraham was justified by works, he has boasting, but not before God.

3 For what does the Scripture say? "Abraham believed God, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness."

4 Now to the one who works, the reward is not reckoned according to grace but according to debt.

5 But to the one who does not work but believes on him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is reckoned for righteousness.

6 just as David also says the blessing of the man to whom God reckons righteousness apart from works.

7 Blessed are those whose lawlessness is forgiven and whose sins are covered.

8 Blessed is the man against whom the Lord will never reckon sin.

9 This blessing, then, is for the circumcision or also for the uncircumcision, for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham as righteousness.

10 How then was it reckoned to him, being in circumcision or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

11 And he took the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while in uncircumcision, so that he might be the father of all who believe while in uncircumcision, so that righteousness might be reckoned to them also.

12 and the Father of circumcision to those who are not only of the circumcision, but also to those who walk orderly in the steps of the faith in the uncircumcision of our Father Abraham.

13 For the promise to Abraham and to his seed that he would be heir of the world was not through law, but through the righteousness of faith.

14 For if those of the law are heirs, faith is emptied and the promise is abolished.

15 For the law works wrath, for where there is no law there is no transgression.

16 For this reason, it is from faith, so that it might be according to grace, for the promise to be firm to all the seed—not only to that which is from the law, but also to that which is from the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all.

17 as it is written: "I have made you a father of many nations" — before God, whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls the things that are not as though they were.

18 He believed in hope beyond hope, so that he might become the Father of many nations, according to what had been said: "So shall your seed be."

19 And not being weak in faith, he did not consider his own body already made dead, being about a hundred years old, or the deadness of Sara's womb.

20 Yet concerning the promise of God, he did not discern through unbelief, but was strengthened in faith, giving glory to God.

21 and having been fully assured that what He has promised, He is able also to do.

22 Therefore also it was reckoned to him as righteousness.

23 It was not written for his sake only that it was reckoned to him.

24 but also for us, to whom it is about to be reckoned—for us who believe in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.

25 who was delivered over for our trespasses and was raised for our justification.


## Chapter 5

1 Therefore, having been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.

2 through whom also we have obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand and boast in hope of the glory of God.

3 Not only this, but we also boast in our tribulations, knowing that tribulation works patience.

4 And patience produces proof, and proof produces hope.

5 And hope does not shame us, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit who was given to us.

6 For while we were still weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly.

7 For scarcely will anyone die for a righteous person; for perhaps someone might even dare to die for a good person.

8 But God commends his own love toward us, in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

9 Much more therefore, having now been justified by his blood, we will be saved through him from the wrath.

10 For if while being enemies we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more, having been reconciled, we will be saved by his life.

11 And not only that, but we also boast in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now taken reconciliation.

12 Therefore, just as sin entered the world through one man, and death through sin, in this way death passed through to all men, because all sinned.

13 For until the law, sin was in the world, but sin is not imputed when there is no law.

14 But death reigned from Adam until Moses, even over those who did not sin in the likeness of the transgression of Adam, who is a pattern of the one who was to come.

15 But the gift is not like the trespass. For if by the trespass of the one the many died, much more the grace of God and the gift by the grace of the one man, Jesus Christ, abounded to the many.

16 And the gift is not as through one who sinned; for the judgment from one led to condemnation, but the gift from many trespasses leads to justification.

17 For if death reigned through the one trespass of the one man, much more will those who take the abundance of grace and the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one, Jesus Christ.

18 Consequently, therefore, as through one trespass came condemnation for all men, so also through one ordinance came justification of life for all men.

19 For just as through the disobedience of the one man the many were appointed sinners, so also through the obedience of the one the many will be appointed righteous.

20 Now the law came in secretly so that the trespass might abound; but where sin abounded, grace abounded much more.

21 So that just as sin reigned in death, so also grace might reign through righteousness to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.


## Chapter 6

1 What then shall we say? Are we to continue in sin so that grace may abound?

2 By no means! We who died to sin, how shall we still live in it?

3 Or do you not know that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?

4 We have been buried with him through baptism into death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we also should walk in newness of life.

5 For if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death, we will also be in the likeness of his resurrection.

6 knowing this, that our old man was crucified with him so that the body of sin might be abolished, for us no longer to serve sin.

7 The one who has died is justified from sin.

8 But if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.

9 knowing that Christ, having been raised from the dead, dies no more; death no longer lords over him.

10 For the death he died, he died to sin once for all; but the life he lives, he lives to God.

11 Likewise also you, reckon yourselves to be dead indeed to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.

12 Do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its desires.

13 Do not present your members as weapons of unrighteousness to sin, but present yourselves to God as those who are alive from the dead, and your members as weapons of righteousness to God.

14 For sin will not lord over you, because you are not under law but under grace.

15 What then? Will we sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!

16 Don't you know that to whomever you present yourselves as servants for obedience, you are servants to whomever you obey—whether of sin for death, or of obedience for righteousness?

17 But grace be to God, that you were servants of sin, but you obeyed from the heart the pattern of teaching to which you were delivered.

18 Having been set free from sin, you were enslaved to righteousness.

19 I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you presented your members as servants to uncleanness and to lawlessness for lawlessness, so now present your members as servants to righteousness for sanctification.

20 For when you were servants of sin, you were free in regard to righteousness.

21 What fruit then did you have at that time in the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.

22 But now, having been set free from sin and having been enslaved to God, you have your fruit leading to sanctification, and the end is eternal life.

23 For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.


## Chapter 7

1 Or don't you know, brothers—for I speak to those who know the law—that the law lords over a person as long as he lives?

2 A married woman is bound to her living husband by law, but if the husband dies, she is released from the law of the husband.

3 So then, while the husband is living, she will be called an adulteress if she becomes joined to another man; but if the husband dies, she is free from the law, so that she is not an adulteress when she becomes joined to another man.

4 So, my brothers, you also have been put to death to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may be joined to another, to him who was raised from the dead, in order that we might bear fruit for God.

5 For when we were in the flesh, the sufferings of sins, which were through the law, were working in our members to bear fruit for death.

6 Now we have been abolished from the law, having died by that in which we were held fast, so that we serve in newness of Spirit and not in oldness of letter.

7 What then shall we say? The law is sin? By no means! But I would not have known sin except through the law, for I would not have known desire except the law said, "You shall not desire."

8 Sin, seizing the occasion through the commandment, produced in me all kinds of desire; for apart from the law sin is dead.

9 I was living without law once, but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.

10 and the commandment which was for life, this was found for me to be a source of death.

11 For sin, taking occasion through the commandment, deceived me and through it killed me.

12 So then, the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and righteous and good.

13 Did the good then become death to me? By no means! But it was sin, so that sin might appear as sin by working death for me through the good, so that sin might become a surpassing sinner through the commandment.

14 For we know that the law is spiritual, but I am fleshly, sold under sin.

15 For I do not know what I am working; for I do not practice what I want, but I do what I hate.

16 But if I do what I do not want, I consent to the law that it is good.

17 Now it is no longer I who work it, but the sin dwelling in me.

18 For I know that good does not dwell in me, that is, in my flesh; for to will is present with me, but to work what is good I do not find.

19 For I do not do the good that I want, but the evil I do not want, this I practice.

20 But if I do what I do not want, it is no longer I who work it, but the sin dwelling in me.

21 I find then the law that for me who wants to do the good, evil is present with me.

22 For I delight in the law of God according to the inner man.

23 I look then at another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind and leading me away captive to the law of sin which is in my members.

24 Wretched man that I am, who will deliver me from this body of death?

25 I give thanks to God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then, I myself serve the law of God with my mind, but with the flesh the law of sin.


## Chapter 8

1 There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus, who do not walk according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.

2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set me free from the law of sin and of death.

3 For what was impossible for the law, in that it was weak through the flesh, God, having sent His own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and concerning sin, condemned sin in the flesh.

4 so that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us who do not walk according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.

5 For those who are according to the flesh think about things of the flesh, but those according to the Spirit think about things of the Spirit.

6 For the mindset of the flesh is death, but the mindset of the Spirit is life and peace.

7 For the mindset of the flesh is enmity against God, for it does not submit to the law of God, nor indeed can it.

8 But those who are in the flesh are not able to please God.

9 You, however, are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God dwells in you; but if anyone does not have the Spirit of Christ, this one is not his.

10 But if Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the Spirit is life because of righteousness.

11 But if the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit who dwells in you.

12 Therefore, brothers, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh.

13 For if you live according to the flesh, you are about to die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.

14 For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.

15 For you did not take a spirit of bondage again into fear, but you took a Spirit of adoption, in whom we cry out, "Abba, Father."

16 The Spirit Himself testifies to our spirit that we are children of God.

17 And if children, then heirs—heirs of God and fellow-heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with him so that we may also be glorified together.

18 For I reckon that the sufferings of the present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory that is about to be revealed in us.

19 For the earnest expectation of the creation waits for the revelation of the sons of God.

20 For the creation was subjected to vanity, not willingly, but because of the one who subjected it in hope.

21 For also the creation itself will be set free from the bondage of corruption into the freedom of the glory of the children of God.

22 For we know that all creation groans together and travails in pain together until now.

23 Not only this, but also we ourselves, having the firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the adoption, the redemption of our body.

24 For in hope we were saved, but hope that is seen is not hope, for who hopes for what he sees?

25 But if we hope for what we do not look at, we wait for it with patience.

26 Likewise also the Spirit helps our weaknesses, for we do not know what we ought to pray for, but the Spirit itself makes intercession for us with unutterable groanings.

27 And he who searches the hearts knows what the mindset of the Spirit is, because he intercedes for the holy ones according to God.

28 And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.

29 Because those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, so that he would be the firstborn among many brothers.

30 And those whom he predestined, these he also called; and those whom he called, these he also justified; and those whom he justified, these he also glorified.

31 What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

32 He who did not spare his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how will he not also with him freely give us all things?

33 Who will accuse God's chosen ones? God is the one who justifies.

34 Who is the one who condemns? Christ is the one who died—more than that, who was raised—who is also at the right hand of God, who also intercedes for us.

35 Who will separate us from the love of Christ—tribulation, or anguish, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?

36 As it is written, "For your sake we are being put to death all day long; we were reckoned as sheep for slaughter."

37 But in all these things we are more than conquerors through the one who loved us.

38 For I am persuaded that neither death nor life nor angels nor beginnings nor powers nor things present nor things about to come

39 neither height nor depth nor any other creation will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.


## Chapter 9

1 I am speaking the truth in Christ; I am not lying, as my conscience bears witness with me in the Holy Spirit.

2 For I have great sorrow and unceasing pain in my heart.

3 For I was praying that I myself might be a curse, cut off from the Christ for the sake of my brothers, my kinsmen according to the flesh.

4 These are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises.

5 Their fathers, and from them is the Christ according to the flesh, the one who is God over all, blessed forever. Amen.

6 Not that the word of God has fallen away, for not all those from Israel are Israel.

7 Nor is it that all are children because they are seed of Abraham; but, "In Isaac your seed will be called."

8 That is, not the children of the flesh are these children of God, but the children of the promise are reckoned as the seed.

9 For this is the word of promise: "At this time I will come, and Sara will have a son."

10 Not only that, but also Rebecca, having conceived by one man, our father Isaac,

11 For the children not yet being born nor having practiced anything good or evil, so that God's purpose according to election might remain, not from works but from the one who calls.

12 It was said to her, "The greater will serve the lesser."

13 Just as it is written, "I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau."

14 What then shall we say? Is there unrighteousness with God? By no means!

15 For he says to Moses, "I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion."

16 So then, it is not of the one who wants nor of the one who runs, but of God who has mercy.

17 For the Scripture says to Pharaoh: "For this very purpose I raised you up, so that I might show my power in you, and so that my name might be declared in all the earth."

18 Therefore then he wills to have mercy on whom he wills, and he hardens whom he wills.

19 You will say then to me, why does he still blame? For who has resisted his purpose?

20 On the contrary, O man, who are you who answer back to God? Will the thing formed say to the one who formed it, "Why did you make me like this?"

21 Or does the potter not have authority over the clay from the same lump to make one vessel for honor and another for dishonor?

22 And if God, being willing to show his wrath and to make known his power, bore with much patience vessels of wrath fitted for destruction.

23 and to make known the riches of his glory upon vessels of mercy, which he prepared beforehand for glory.

24 He also called us, not only from the Jews but also from the nations.

25 as he also says in Hosea: "I will call the one who is not my people 'my people,' and the one who is unloved 'loved.'"

26 And it shall be in the place where it was said to them, "You are not my people," there they will be called sons of the living God.

27 Isaiah cries out concerning Israel, "If the number of the sons of Israel is as the sand of the sea, the remnant will be saved."

28 For the Lord will complete and cut short the matter in righteousness, because the Lord will make a short word upon the earth.

29 And as Isaiah foretold, unless the Lord of Sabaoth had forsaken us a seed, we would have become as Sodom, and we would have been likened as Gomorrah.

30 What then shall we say? That nations who did not pursue righteousness have overtaken righteousness—a righteousness that is from faith.

31 Israel, while pursuing the law of righteousness, did not attain to the law of righteousness.

32 Why? Because they did not seek it by faith, but as if by works of the law, for they stumbled over the stone of stumbling.

33 As it is written: "See, I put in Zion a stone of stumbling and a rock of stumbling, and all who believe on him will not be put to shame."


## Chapter 10

1 Brother, the good pleasure of my heart and the supplication to God for Israel is for salvation.

2 For I testify to them that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.

3 For not knowing the righteousness of God, and seeking to establish their own righteousness, they did not submit to the righteousness of God.

4 For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.

5 For Moses writes about the righteousness that is from the law: The man who does these things will live by them.

6 But the righteousness from faith says this: "Do not say in your heart, 'Who will go up into heaven?' (that is, to bring Christ down).

7 Or who will go down into the abyss? (That is, to lead Christ up from the dead.)

8 But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart—that is, the word of faith which we are preaching.

9 For if you confess with your mouth Jesus as Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

10 For with the heart one believes for righteousness, and with the mouth one confesses for salvation.

11 For the Scripture says, all who believe on him will not be shamed.

12 For there is no difference between Jew and Greek, for the same Lord is rich toward all who call upon him.

13 For all who call upon the name of the Lord will be saved.

14 How then will they call upon him in whom they have not believed? And how will they believe in him of whom they have not heard? And how will they hear without someone preaching?

15 And how will they preach unless they are sent? Just as it is written: "How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the gospel of peace, who preach the gospel of good things."

16 But not all obeyed the gospel, for Isaiah says, "Lord, who has believed our report?"

17 So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of God.

18 But I say, did they not hear? Indeed, their sound went out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.

19 But I say, did Israel not know? First, Moses says, "I will provoke you to emulation by those who are not a nation; by a senseless nation I will anger you."

20 But Isaiah is very bold and says, "I was found by those who were not seeking me; I became manifest to those who were not asking for me."

21 But to Israel he says, "All day long I have stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people."


## Chapter 11

1 I say then, God did not thrust away his people? By no means! For I also am an Israelite, from the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.

2 God has not thrust away his people whom he foreknew. Or do you not know what the Scripture says in the passage about Elijah, how he intercedes with God against Israel, saying...

3 Lord, they killed your prophets and dug down your altars, and I alone was left, and they seek my soul.

4 But what does the answer of God say to him? "I have left for myself seven thousand men who have not bowed a knee to Baal."

5 So then, at this present time also a remnant has happened according to the election of grace.

6 And if by grace, it is no longer from works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace. And if from works, it is no longer grace; otherwise work would no longer be work.

7 What then? What Israel seeks, this it did not obtain, but the election obtained it, and the remaining were callous.

8 Just as it is written, God gave them a Spirit of slumber, eyes for not looking and ears for not hearing, until this very day.

9 And David says, "Let their table become a snare and a trap and a stumbling block and a recompense to them."

10 Let their eyes be darkened so they cannot see, and always bow down their back.

11 I say then, did they stumble that they might fall? By no means! But by their trespass, salvation has come to the nations, so as to provoke them to emulation.

12 If their trespass is the riches of the world, and their diminishing is the riches of the nations, how much more will their fullness be?

13 For I say to you, the nations, insofar as I am an apostle of the nations, I glorify my ministry.

14 if somehow I may provoke my flesh to emulation and save some of them.

15 For if their casting away is the reconciliation of the world, what will their receiving be but life from the dead?

16 If the firstfruits is holy, then the lump is also holy; and if the root is holy, then the branches are also holy.

17 Now if some of the branches were broken off, and you, being a wild olive tree, were grafted in among them and became a companion of the root and of the fatness of the olive tree,

18 Do not boast against the branches. But if you boast against them, you do not bear the root, but the root bears you.

19 You will say then, "The branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in."

20 They were broken off by unbelief, but you stand by faith. Do not be highminded, but fear.

21 For if God did not spare the natural branches, nor will he spare you.

22 See then the kindness and severity of God: severity toward those who fell, but kindness toward you, if you continue in his kindness; otherwise you also will be cut off.

23 And they also, if they do not continue in unbelief, will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.

24 For if you were cut off from the naturally wild olive tree and were grafted contrary to nature into the good olive tree, how much more will these, who are natural, be grafted into their own olive tree.

25 I do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, of this mystery, so that you may not be wise in your own estimation, because a partial blindness has happened to Israel until the fullness of the nations enters.

26 And thus all Israel will be saved, just as it is written: "The deliverer will come from Zion, and he will turn away ungodliness from Jacob."

27 And this is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins.

28 According to the gospel, enemies for your sake; but according to election, beloved for the sake of the Fathers.

29 For the gifts and the calling of God are without regret.

30 Just as you also at one time disobeyed God, but now have been shown mercy by the disobedience of these.

31 In the same way these also have now been disobedient, so that through your mercy they themselves also may receive mercy.

32 For God concluded all in disobedience so that he might have mercy on all.

33 Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments and unfathomable his ways!

34 For who has known the mind of the Lord, or who has become his counsellor?

35 Or who has first given to Him, and it will be repaid to him?

36 For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be the glory forever. Amen.


## Chapter 12

1 Therefore I encourage you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your reasonable worship.

2 Do not conform to this age, but be transfigured by the renewing of your mind, so that you may test what is the good, acceptable, and perfect will of God.

3 For I say, through the grace that was given to me, to everyone who is among you, not to think more highly than one must think, but to think so as to be sober-minded, as God has divided a measure of faith to each.

4 For just as we have many members in one body, but not all the members have the same function.

5 In the same way, though we are many, we are one body in Christ, and each individually members of one another.

6 Having then gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, whether prophecy, according to the proportion of faith.

7 if ministry, in the ministry; or the one who teaches, in the teaching.

8 if it is the one who encourages, in the comfort; the one who shares, in simplicity; the one who rules, in diligence; the one who has mercy, in cheerfulness.

9 Let love be unfeigned. Abhor what is evil; join to what is good.

10 With brotherly love, be kindly affectioned to one another; in honor prefer one another.

11 In diligence, not slothful; be fervent in spirit, serving the time.

12 Rejoicing in hope, enduring in tribulation, devoting yourselves to prayer.

13 Share with the needs of the holy ones, pursue hospitality.

14 Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.

15 Rejoice with those who rejoice, and weep with those who weep.

16 Think the same thing toward one another, not thinking high things, but being carried away with the humble; do not be wise in your own estimation.

17 Do not repay evil for evil to anyone. Provide what is good in the sight of all people.

18 If possible, so far as it depends on you, be at peace with all people.

19 Beloved, do not avenge yourselves, but give place to wrath, for it is written, "Vengeance is mine; I will repay," says the Lord.

20 If then your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him drink; for by doing this you will heap coals of fire on his head.

21 Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.


## Chapter 13

1 Let every soul submit to the surpassing authorities, for there is no authority except from God, and the existing authorities have been appointed by God.

2 Therefore the one resisting the authority resists the ordinance of God; and those who resist will take judgment on themselves.

3 For the rulers are not a fear to good works but to evil. Do you want, however, not to fear the authority? Do good, and you will have praise from it.

4 For he is God's servant to you for good, but if you do evil, fear, for he does not wear the sword in vain; for he is God's servant, an avenger for wrath to the one who practices evil.

5 Therefore it is necessary to submit, not only because of wrath but also because of conscience.

6 For this reason you also pay tribute, for they are ministers of God, devoting themselves to this very thing.

7 Repay to all their debts: to the one the tribute, the tribute; to the one the end, the end; to the one the fear, the fear; to the one the honor, the honor.

8 Owe no one anything, except to love one another; for the one who loves the other has fulfilled the law.

9 For the commandments, "You shall not commit adultery," "You shall not murder," "You shall not steal," "You shall not bear false witness," "You shall not desire," and if there is any other commandment, are summed up in this word: in "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."

10 Love does no evil to a neighbor; therefore love is the fullness of the law.

11 And knowing this, that the hour has already come for us to arise from sleep, for now our salvation is nearer than when we believed.

12 The night has advanced; the day has drawn near. Therefore, let us put away the works of darkness and put on the weapons of light.

13 As in the daytime, let us walk decently, not in revelry and drunkenness, not in bed and licentiousness, not in strife and zeal.

14 But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not make provision for the flesh, to satisfy its desires.


## Chapter 14

1 Receive the one who is weak in faith, not for discernment of reasonings.

2 One person believes he may eat all things, but the one who is weak eats only herbs.

3 The one who eats must not despise the one who does not eat, and the one who does not eat must not judge the one who eats, for God has received him.

4 Who are you who judges another's servant? To his own master he stands or falls; and he will stand, for God is able to make him stand.

5 One person judges one day above another, another judges every day alike; let each be fully assured in his own mind.

6 The one who regards the day regards it for the Lord; and the one who does not regard the day does not regard it for the Lord. The one who eats eats for the Lord, for he gives thanks to God; and the one who does not eat does not eat for the Lord and gives thanks to God.

7 For none of us lives to himself, and no one dies to himself.

8 For if we live, we live for the Lord; and if we die, we die for the Lord; therefore, whether we live or die, we are the Lord's.

9 For this reason Christ died and rose and revived, in order that he might lord over both the dead and the living.

10 But you, why do you judge your brother? Or also you, why do you despise your brother? For we will all present ourselves before the judgment seat of Christ.

11 For it is written, "I live, says the Lord, that to me every knee will bow and every tongue will confess to God."

12 Therefore each of us will give an account of himself to God.

13 Therefore let us no longer judge one another, but rather judge this: not to put a stumbling block or an obstacle before a brother.

14 I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is common of itself, but to the one who reckons something to be common, to that person it is common.

15 But if because of food your brother is grieved, you are no longer walking according to love; do not destroy that person for whom Christ died by your food.

16 So do not let your good be blasphemed.

17 For the kingdom of God is not food and drink, but righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.

18 For the one serving Christ in these things is acceptable to God and approved by men.

19 So then let us pursue the things of peace and the things of building up one another.

20 Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All things indeed are clean, but it is evil for the man who eats with a stumbling block.

21 It is good not to eat flesh nor drink wine nor anything by which your brother stumbles or is offended or is weak.

22 Do you have faith? Have it for yourself before God. Blessed is the one who does not judge himself in what he puts to the test.

23 But the one who discerns is condemned if he eats, because it is not from faith; and all that is not from faith is sin.


## Chapter 15

1 We who are able ought to bear the infirmities of the impossible and not please ourselves.

2 Let each of us please our neighbor for his good, to build him up.

3 For even Christ did not please himself, but as it is written: "The reproaches of those who reproached you fell upon me."

4 For whatever was written beforehand was written for our instruction, so that through the patience and comfort of the Scriptures we might have hope.

5 Now may the God of patience and comfort give you to think the same among one another according to Christ Jesus.

6 so that with one mind and one mouth you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

7 Therefore receive one another, just as also Christ received us, to the glory of God.

8 I say that Jesus Christ has become a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises of the Fathers.

9 And the nations for mercy will glorify God, just as it is written: "For this reason I will confess you among the nations and sing psalms to your name."

10 And again he says, "Rejoice, nations, with his people."

11 And again, praise the Lord, all nations, and praise him, all peoples.

12 And again Isaiah says, "The root of Jesse will come, even the one who rises to rule the nations; on him the nations will set their hope."

13 Now may the God of hope fulfill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you may abound in hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.

14 I am persuaded, my brothers, also I myself concerning you, that you yourselves are full of goodness, having been filled with all knowledge, being able also to admonish one another.

15 I have written the more boldly to you, brothers, in part as putting you in mind, because of the grace given to me by God.

16 for me to be a minister of Jesus Christ to the nations, ministering the gospel of God, so that the offering of the nations may happen, sanctified by the Holy Spirit, acceptable.

17 I have then boasting in Christ Jesus in the things toward God.

18 For I will not dare to speak of anything that Christ has not worked through me for the obedience of the nations, by word and by work.

19 by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God, so that from Jerusalem and round about as far as Illyricum I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.

20 So I laboured to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, so that I might not build on another man's foundation.

21 But just as it is written: "Those who were not told about him will see, and those who have not heard will understand."

22 For this reason also I was hindered many times from coming to you.

23 But now, no longer having a place in these regions, and having a great desire to come to you for many years,

24 If I go to Spain, I will come to you, for I hope while passing through to see you and to be escorted there by you, once I have first been filled in part from you.

25 But now I am going to Jerusalem to serve the holy people.

26 For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make some fellowship for the poor among the holy ones in Jerusalem.

27 For they were pleased, and they are their debtors; for if the nations shared in their spiritual things, they owe it also to minister to them in fleshly things.

28 Therefore, after accomplishing this and having sealed this fruit to them, I will depart through you to Spain.

29 But I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.

30 I encourage you, brothers, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers to God on my behalf.

31 that I may be delivered from the disobedient in Judea, and that my ministry for Jerusalem may become acceptable to the holy ones.

32 so that in joy I will come to you through the will of God and be refreshed with you.

33 Now may the God of peace be with all of you. Amen.


## Chapter 16

1 I commend to you Phebe our sister, who is a servant of the church which is in Cenchrea.

2 that you may receive her in the Lord worthily of the holy, and present her in whatever matter she needs from you; for she also became a succourer of many, and of myself as well.

3 Greet Priscilla and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus.

4 who for my soul laid down their own neck, to whom not only I give thanks but also all the churches of the nations.

5 Greet the church that is in their house. Greet Epenetus, my beloved, who is the firstfruits of Achaia for Christ.

6 Greet Mary, who has labored much for us.

7 Greet Adronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and my fellow prisoners, who are notable among the apostles, and who also have been in Christ before me.

8 Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.

9 Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.

10 Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those of the household of Aristobulus.

11 Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet those from the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.

12 Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis the beloved, who has labored much in the Lord.

13 Greet Rufus, the chosen one in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

14 Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brothers who are with them.

15 Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the holy ones with them.

16 Greet one another with a holy kiss. The churches of Christ greet you.

17 Now I encourage you, brothers, to watch those who cause divisions and stumbling blocks contrary to the teaching which you learned, and avoid them.

18 For such people do not serve our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly, and through flattery and blessing they deceive the hearts of the harmless.

19 Your obedience has come abroad to all. I rejoice therefore over you, but I want you to be wise in what is good and harmless in what is evil.

20 And the God of peace will shatter Satan under your feet in speed. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

21 Timothy, my fellow worker, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, greet you.

22 I, Tertius, who wrote the letter in the Lord, greet you.

23 Gaius, my stranger, and the whole church greet you. Erastus, the steward of the city, and Quartus the brother greet you.

24 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you. Amen.

25 Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept silent for eternal times.

26 but now having been revealed and through the prophetic Scriptures, according to the command of the eternal God, for obedience of faith among all the nations, having been made known.

27 To the only wise God through Jesus Christ be glory forever. Amen. [The epistle to the Romans was written from Corinth by Phebe, the servant of the church in Cenchrea.]


# 1 CORINTHIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, called as an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes the brother.

2 To the church of God which is in Corinth, to those sanctified in Christ Jesus, called holy people, with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place, both theirs and ours.

3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

4 I give thanks to my God always for you concerning the grace of God that was given to you in Christ Jesus.

5 For in every way you were made rich in him, in all speech and all knowledge.

6 just as the testimony of Christ was confirmed among you.

7 So that you do not lack in any gift, as you wait for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.

8 who will also confirm you to the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

9 Faithful is God, through whom you were called into fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

10 Now I encourage you, brothers, through the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you all say the same thing and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be restored in the same mind and in the same judgment.

11 For it has been declared to me concerning you, my brothers, by those of Chloe, that there are strifes among you.

12 I say this, that each of you says, "I indeed am of Paul," and "I of Apollos," and "I of Cephas," and "I of Christ."

13 Has Christ been divided? Paul was not crucified for you, was he, or were you baptized into the name of Paul?

14 I give thanks to God that I baptized no one among you except Crispus and Gaius.

15 so that no one may say that I baptized into my own name.

16 I also baptized the house of Stephanas; for the rest, I do not know if I baptized anyone else.

17 For Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel, not in wisdom of word, so that the cross of Christ might not be emptied.

18 For the word about the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.

19 For it is written, "I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the understanding of the prudent."

20 Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the disputer of this age? Has God not made foolish the wisdom of this world?

21 For since, in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom did not know God, God was pleased through the foolishness of preaching to save those who believe.

22 for indeed Jews ask for a sign and Greeks seek wisdom.

23 But we preach Christ crucified, a stumbling block to Jews and foolishness to Greeks.

24 But to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God.

25 For the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

26 Look at your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many are able, not many are noble.

27 But the foolish things of the world God chose so that he might shame the wise, and the weak things of the world God chose so that he might shame the strong.

28 and the base things of the world and the things that are despised God chose, and the things that are not, so that the things that are he might abolish.

29 so that no flesh may boast in his presence.

30 But from him you are in Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God, both righteousness and sanctification and redemption.

31 so that, just as it is written, "Let the one who boasts boast in the Lord."


## Chapter 2

1 I also, when I came to you, brothers, came not proclaiming the testimony of God to you with superiority of word or wisdom.

2 For I judged not to know anything among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified.

3 And I was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling.

4 And my word and my preaching were not in enticing words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power.

5 so that your faith would not be in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.

6 Yet we speak wisdom among the perfect, but not the wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age, who are being abolished.

7 But we speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, the hidden wisdom which God predestined before the ages for our glory.

8 No one of the rulers of this age has known, for if they had known, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.

9 But just as it is written: "What no eye has seen and no ear has heard and what has not gone up into the heart of man—what God has prepared for those who love him."

10 But to us God has revealed it through His Spirit, for the Spirit searches all things, even the depths of God.

11 For who among men knows the things of a man except the spirit of the man which is in him? So also no one knows the things of God except the Spirit of God.

12 But we did not take the spirit of the world, but the Spirit that is from God, so that we might know the things freely given to us by God.

13 which things we also speak, not in words taught by human wisdom, but in words taught by the Holy Spirit, comparing spiritual things with spiritual.

14 Now the natural man does not receive the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness to him, and he is not able to know them because they are spiritually examined.

15 But the spiritual person examines all things, yet he himself is examined by no one.

16 For who has known the mind of the Lord, who will instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.


## Chapter 3

1 And I, brothers, was not able to speak to you as to spiritual people, but as to fleshly people, as to children in Christ.

2 I gave you milk to drink, not food, for you were not yet able, and you are still not able even now.

3 For you are still fleshly, for where there is jealousy and strife and divisions among you, are you not fleshly and walking according to man?

4 For since someone says, "I indeed am of Paul," and another, "I am of Apollos," are you not fleshly?

5 What then is Paul? What is Apollos? They are only servants through whom you believed, even as the Lord gave to each one.

6 I planted, Apollos gave drink, but God made it grow.

7 So neither the one planting nor the one giving drink is anything, but the God who grows is.

8 The one who plants and the one who gives drink are one, but each will take his own reward according to his own labor.

9 For we are God's fellow workers; you are God's husbandry, God's building.

10 According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise masterbuilder I have put a foundation; another builds upon it, but let each one look how he builds upon it.

11 For no one is able to put another foundation besides the one that lies, which is Jesus Christ.

12 But if anyone builds upon this foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or stubble,

13 Each one's work will become manifest, for the day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire, and the fire will test what kind each one's work is.

14 If anyone's work that he built upon remains, he will take a reward.

15 If anyone's work burns up, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, yet so as through fire.

16 Do you not know that you are God's temple and that the Spirit of God dwells in you?

17 If anyone corrupts the temple of God, God will corrupt that person; for the temple of God is holy, which you are.

18 Let no one deceive himself. If anyone seems to be wise among you in this age, let him become foolish, so that he may become wise.

19 For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God; for it has been written, "He who takes the wise in their craftiness."

20 And again the Lord knows the reasonings of the wise, that they are vain.

21 Therefore let no one boast in men, for all things are yours.

22 Whether Paul or Apollos or Cephas or the world or life or death or things present or things about to come—all are yours.

23 You, however, are of Christ, and Christ is of God.


## Chapter 4

1 Let a man so reckon us as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.

2 Now it is required of stewards that one be found faithful.

3 To me, however, it is of least importance that I be examined by you or by a human day; indeed, I do not even examine myself.

4 For I consider nothing about myself, but I am not justified in this; the one who examines me is the Lord.

5 So do not judge anything before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both enlighten the secret things of the darkness and reveal the counsels of the hearts; and then praise will happen to each from God.

6 Now, brothers, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit, so that you may learn from us not to go beyond what is written, so that no one of you will be puffed up in favor of one against the other.

7 For who discerns you? And what do you have that you did not take? But if you also took it, why do you boast as though you did not take it?

8 You are already satisfied, you have already become rich, you have reigned without us, and I would that you did reign, so that we also might reign with you.

9 For I think God has demonstrated us, the apostles, as last, appointed to death, because we have become a spectacle to the world, both to angels and to men.

10 We are foolish for Christ's sake, but you are wise in Christ; we are weak, but you are strong; you are glorious, but we are dishonored.

11 To this present hour we both hunger and thirst and are naked and are struck and have no certain dwelling place.

12 We labor, working with our own hands; when reviled, we bless; when persecuted, we bear with it.

13 Being blasphemed, we encourage; we have become the filth of the world, the offscouring of all things, until now.

14 I am not writing these things to make you feel ashamed, but I am admonishing you as my beloved children.

15 For though you have ten thousand schoolmasters in Christ, yet you do not have many fathers; for in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.

16 Therefore I encourage you to become imitators of me.

17 For this reason I sent you Timothy, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord; he will remind you of my ways in Christ, just as I teach everywhere in every church.

18 But before I come to you, some have become puffed up.

19 Now I will come to you quickly, if the Lord wills, and I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.

20 For the kingdom of God is not in word but in power.

21 What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or with love and a spirit of meekness?


## Chapter 5

1 It is actually reported that there is fornication among you, and such fornication as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one should have his father's wife.

2 And you are puffed up, and did you not rather mourn, so that the one who has done this work might be put away from your midst?

3 For indeed, although I am absent in body, yet I am present in the Spirit, I have already judged, as though I were present, the one who has worked this thing in such a manner.

4 In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when you have gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ.

5 Deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, so that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

6 Your boasting is not good. Do you not know that a small leaven leavens the whole lump?

7 Purge out the old leaven, that you may be a new lump, as you are unleavened; for indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed for us.

8 Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, nor with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

9 I wrote to you in my letter not to mix with fornicators.

10 and surely not with the fornicators of this world, or with the covetous, or the ravenous, or the idolaters; since then you would need to go out of the world.

11 But now I wrote to you not to mix with anyone who is called a brother, if he is a fornicator or covetous or an idolater or a railer or a drunkard or ravenous—with such a one not even to eat.

12 For what is it to me to judge those outside? Do you not judge those within?

13 God judges those outside; and you will put away the evil from among yourselves.


## Chapter 6

1 Dare any of you, having a matter against another, be judged before the unrighteous, and not before the holy?

2 Do you not know that the holy ones will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy of the smallest courts?

3 Do you not know that we will judge angels, let alone worldly matters?

4 If then you have judgments concerning this life, set those to judge who are least esteemed in the church.

5 I say this to your shame: is there not even one wise man among you who will be able to discern between his brothers?

6 But brother goes to law against brother, and this before unbelievers.

7 Therefore it is already at all a diminishing among you, because you have judgments with yourselves. Why not rather do wrong? Why not rather be defrauded?

8 But you do wrong and defraud, and this toward brothers.

9 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived: neither fornicators, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor the effeminate, nor sodomites,

10 neither thieves, nor the covetous, nor drunkards, nor railers, nor the ravenous will inherit the kingdom of God.

11 And such were some of you, but you were washed, but you were sanctified, but you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus and in the Spirit of our God.

12 All things are lawful for me, but not all things profit. All things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the authority of any.

13 Food is for the belly, and the belly is for food, but God will abolish both this and these. The body is not for fornication, but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.

14 But God raised the Lord and will also raise us up by his power.

15 Do you not know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I therefore take away the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? By no means!

16 Or do you not know that the one joining himself to a prostitute is one body? For "the two," he says, "will become one flesh."

17 But the one who is joined to the Lord is one Spirit.

18 Flee fornication. Every sin that a man does is outside the body, but the one who fornicates sins against his own body.

19 Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God, and you are not your own?

20 For you were bought with a price; therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God's.


## Chapter 7

1 Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman.

2 But because of fornication, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.

3 The husband should repay the owed benevolence to his wife, and likewise also the wife to her husband.

4 The wife does not have authority over her own body, but the husband does; likewise, also the husband does not have authority over his own body, but the wife does.

5 Do not defraud one another, except whenever there is agreement for a time so that you may empty yourselves for the fast and for the prayer, and again come together so that Satan does not test you because of your excess.

6 Now I say this by way of permission, not by way of command.

7 For I want all men to be as I myself am, but each has his own gift from God, one in this way, another in that way.

8 But I say to the unmarried and to the widows: it is good for them if they remain as I also.

9 But if they cannot contain themselves, let them marry; for it is better to marry than to burn.

10 To those who have married I command—not I, but the Lord—[that] a wife not be separated from her husband.

11 If, however, she does separate, let her remain unmarried or be reconciled to her husband; and a husband must not abandon his wife.

12 To the remaining I say (not the Lord): if any brother has an unbelieving wife and she consents to dwell with him, let him not divorce her.

13 And a wife who has an unbelieving husband, and he consents to dwell with her, let her not leave him.

14 For the unbelieving husband is sanctified by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified by the husband; otherwise your children would be unclean, but now they are holy.

15 But if the unbelieving one separates, let him separate; the brother or the sister is not enslaved in such cases, but God has called us in peace.

16 For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?

17 Only, to each as God has divided, each as the Lord has called—so let him walk. And thus I appoint in all the churches.

18 Let the one who was called when circumcised not become uncircumcised; let the one who was called in uncircumcision not be circumcised.

19 Circumcision is no one, and uncircumcision is no one, but keeping the commandments of God.

20 Let each remain in that calling in which he was called.

21 If as a servant you were called, do not let it be a care to you; but if also you are able to become free, use it rather.

22 For the one called as a servant in the Lord is a freeman of the Lord; likewise also the one called as a free man is a servant of Christ.

23 You were bought at a price; do not become servants of men.

24 In whatever condition each was called, brothers, let them remain there with God.

25 Now concerning the virgins, I have no command of the Lord, but I give my opinion as one who has been shown mercy by the Lord to be faithful.

26 I suppose, therefore, this to be good to exist because of the present necessity, that it is good for a man to be thus.

27 You are bound to a wife, do not seek release; you are loosed from a wife, do not seek a wife.

28 But even if you marry, you have not sinned; and if a virgin marries, she has not sinned; but such people will have tribulation in the flesh, and I am sparing you.

29 Now I say this, brothers: the time is shortened, so that from now on even those who have wives may be as though they had none.

30 and those who weep as though not weeping, and those who rejoice as though not rejoicing, and those who buy as though not holding fast.

31 And those who use this world, as not abusing it—for the fashion of this world is passing away.

32 Now I want you to be free from concern. The unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord.

33 But the one having married is anxious about the things of the world, how he may please his wife.

34 The wife and the virgin are divided; the unmarried woman is anxious about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in Spirit, but she who is married is anxious about the things of the world, how she may please her husband.

35 Now I say this for your own profit, not to lay a snare on you, but for what is honorable and to attend upon the Lord without distraction.

36 But if anyone supposes that he is behaving himself uncomely toward his virgin if she be past marriageable age, and thus it ought to happen, let him do what he wants; he does not sin; let them marry.

37 But he who stands settled in his heart, not having necessity but having authority over his own will, and has judged this in his own heart—to keep his own virgin—does well.

38 So then, he who gives his daughter in marriage does well, but he who does not give her in marriage does better.

39 A wife is bound by law as long as her husband lives; but if her husband sleeps, she is free to marry whom she wants, only in the Lord.

40 But she is more blessed if she remains as she is, according to my opinion; and I think I also have the Spirit of God.


## Chapter 8

1 Now concerning food offered to idols: we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.

2 But if anyone seems to know something, he does not yet know anything as he must know.

3 But if anyone loves God, this person is known by Him.

4 Concerning the eating of things offered to idols, we know that nothing is an idol in the world, and that there is no God other than the one.

5 For even if there are so-called gods, whether in heaven or on earth, as indeed there are many gods and many lords,

6 Yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom are all things, and we exist for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we exist through him.

7 But not all have knowledge; some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat food offered to idols as such, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

8 Food does not present us to God; for neither if we eat do we abound, nor if we do not eat do we lack.

9 But look lest this authority of yours become a stumbling block to those who are weak.

10 For if someone sees you, who have knowledge, reclining in an idol temple, will his conscience, since it is weak, not be built up to eat things offered to idols?

11 And the weak brother is destroyed by your knowledge, for whom Christ died.

12 Thus, by sinning against the brothers and striking their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

13 Therefore, if food offends my brother, I will never eat meat, so as not to offend my brother.


## Chapter 9

1 Am I not an apostle? Am I not free? Have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?

2 If I am not an apostle to others, yet I am to you, for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.

3 This is my defense to those who examine me.

4 Do we not have authority to eat and drink?

5 Do we not have authority to go about with a sister as a wife, as also the remaining apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?

6 Only I and Barnabas do not have the authority to refrain from working.

7 Who serves as a soldier at his own wages? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat from its fruit? Or who shepherds a flock and does not eat from the milk of the flock?

8 I am not speaking these things according to man; does not the law also say these things?

9 For in the law of Moses it is written: "You shall not muzzle an ox while it is threshing." Is it for the oxen that God cares?

10 He says this surely for our sake, for it was written for our sake, because the one who plows ought to plow in hope, and the one who threshes ought to thresh in hope of sharing in the harvest.

11 If we sowed spiritual things for you, is it too great if we reap your fleshly things?

12 If others share in your authority, do we not have more right? Yet we did not use this right, but we endure all things so that we may give no hindrance to the gospel of Christ.

13 Do you not know that those who work in the holy things eat from the temple, and those who wait at the altar are partakers with the altar?

14 In the same way, the Lord appointed that those who proclaim the gospel live from the gospel.

15 But I did not use any of these things, and I have not written these things so that it would happen this way for me; for it is better for me to die than for anyone to empty my boasting.

16 If indeed I preach the gospel, it is no reason for boasting to me, for necessity is laid upon me; indeed, woe is to me if I do not preach the gospel.

17 For if I practice this willingly, I have a reward; but if against my will, I have been entrusted with a stewardship.

18 What then is my reward? That when I preach the gospel, I may put the gospel of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the gospel.

19 For though I am free from all people, I have enslaved myself to all, so that I may gain the greater number.

20 I became to the Jews as a Jew, so that I might gain Jews; to those under the law as under the law, so that I might gain those under the law.

21 To the lawless I became like a lawless person, though not being lawless toward God but lawful toward Christ, so that I might gain the lawless.

22 I became weak to the weak, that I might gain the weak; I have become all things to all men, that I might by all means save some.

23 I do this for the sake of the gospel, so that I may become a companion of it.

24 Do you not know that those who run in a stade all run, but one takes the prize? Run in such a way that you may overtake.

25 But everyone who competes exercises self-control in all things; they do it then in order to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible one.

26 I therefore run in such a way, not as uncertainly; I fight in such a way, not as beating the air.

27 I keep under my body and bring it into subjection, lest by any means, having preached to another, I myself should become a reprobate.


## Chapter 10

1 I do not want you to not know, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea.

2 And all were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.

3 And all ate the same spiritual food.

4 And all drank the same spiritual drink, for they were drinking from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

5 But with the most of them God was not pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.

6 Now these things became patterns for us, so that we would not be lustful for evil, just as they also desired.

7 Do not become idolaters as some of them were, as it is written: "The people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play."

8 Neither let us fornicate, as some of them fornicated, and fell in one day twenty-three thousand.

9 Let us not put the Christ to the test, as some of them tested him and were destroyed by the serpents.

10 do not murmur, as some of them murmured, and were destroyed by the destroyer.

11 Now all these things happened as patterns to them, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have arrived.

12 So let the one who seems to stand watch out lest he fall.

13 No temptation has taken you except what is human; but God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tested beyond what you are able, but will also do, along with the temptation, the way out, so that you are able to bear it.

14 Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

15 I say as to wise men; you judge what I say.

16 The cup of blessing that we bless, is it not the fellowship of the blood of Christ? The bread that we break, is it not the fellowship of the body of Christ?

17 Because there is one loaf, we who are many are one body, for we all partake of the one loaf.

18 Look at Israel according to the flesh: are not those who eat the sacrifices partners of the altar?

19 What then do I say? That an idol is anything, or that what is offered to idols is anything?

20 But I say that the things which the nations sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons and not to God, and I do not want you to become partners with demons.

21 You cannot drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cannot partake of the table of the Lord and the table of demons.

22 Do we provoke the Lord to emulation, are we stronger than Him?

23 All things are lawful for me, but not all things are profitable; all things are lawful for me, but not all things build up.

24 No one should seek his own good, but each that of the other.

25 Eat all that is sold in the shambles, examining nothing for conscience' sake.

26 For the earth is the Lord's, and its fullness.

27 If anyone of the unbelieving calls you and you want to go, eat all that is committed to you, examining nothing for conscience' sake.

28 But if someone says to you, "This is offered to idols," do not eat for the sake of the one who informed you and for the sake of conscience; for the earth is the Lord's, and its fullness.

29 But I say conscience, not your own but the other person's; for why is my freedom judged by another's conscience?

30 But if I partake by grace, why am I blasphemed for that for which I give thanks?

31 Therefore, whether you eat or drink or do anything, do all to the glory of God.

32 Do not be a stumbling block to Jews and to Greeks and to the church of God.

33 Just as I also please all people in all things, not seeking my own profit but that of the many, so that they may be saved.


## Chapter 11

1 Become imitators of me, just as I also am of Christ.

2 Now I praise you, brothers, because you remember all things of mine and hold fast the traditions just as I delivered them to you.

3 Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.

4 Every man who prays or prophesies with something on his head dishonors his head.

5 But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered shames her head, for it is one and the same with her who is shaved.

6 For if a woman is not covered, let her also be sheared; but if it is shameful for a woman to be sheared or shaved, let her be covered.

7 For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, since he exists as the image and glory of God; but woman is the glory of man.

8 For indeed man is not from woman, but woman from man.

9 For indeed man was not created for the woman, but woman for the man.

10 For this reason the woman ought to have authority on her head because of the angels.

11 Nevertheless, neither is man apart from woman, nor woman apart from man, in the Lord.

12 For just as the woman came from the man, so also the man comes through the woman, and all things come from God.

13 Judge for yourselves: is it fitting for a woman to pray to God uncovered?

14 Does not nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?

15 But if a woman has long hair, it is glory to her, because the hair is given to her instead of a covering.

16 But if anyone seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, nor do the churches of God.

17 But when I command this, I do not praise you because you come together not for the better but for the less.

18 First of all, I hear that when you come together in the church, there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.

19 For there must also be sects among you, so that those who are approved may become manifest among you.

20 When you come together in the same place, it is not possible to eat the Lord's supper.

21 For each one takes his own supper beforehand when eating, and one indeed hungers and another is drunk.

22 Do you not have houses to eat and drink? Or do you despise the church of God and shame those who have nothing? What should I say to you? Shall I praise you in this? I do not praise you.

23 For I received from the Lord what I also delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.

24 After giving thanks, he broke it and said, "Take, eat; this is my body which is broken for you. Do this in remembrance of me."

25 Likewise also the cup after dining, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood; do this, as often as you drink, in remembrance of me."

26 For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.

27 So that whoever eats this bread or drinks the cup of the Lord unworthily will be guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.

28 But let a man test himself, and so let him eat from the bread and drink from the cup.

29 For the one eating and drinking unworthily eats and drinks judgment to himself, not discerning the body of the Lord.

30 That is why many among you are weak and sick, and a considerable number fall asleep.

31 For if we discerned ourselves, we would not be judged.

32 But being judged by the Lord, we are disciplined so that we will not be condemned along with the world.

33 So then, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

34 But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, so that you do not come together for judgment. And the remaining matters I will appoint when I come.


## Chapter 12

1 Now, brothers, I do not want you to be ignorant concerning spiritual gifts.

2 You know that you were nations, led away to mute idols as you were being led.

3 Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking by the Spirit of God says "Jesus is a curse," and no one is able to say "Jesus is Lord" except by the Holy Spirit.

4 There are differences of gifts, but the same Spirit.

5 There are differences of ministries, and the same Lord.

6 There are differences of operations, but it is the same God who works all things in all.

7 But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for profit.

8 For to one is given a word of wisdom through the Spirit, and to another a word of knowledge according to the same Spirit.

9 To another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healings by the same Spirit.

10 To another, operations of powers; to another, prophecy; to another, discernments of spirits; to another, various kinds of tongues; and to another, interpretation of tongues.

11 But all these things are worked by the one and the same Spirit, dividing to each one individually just as he wills.

12 For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the one body, being many, are one body, so also is Christ.

13 For indeed we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether servants or free, and we were all given to drink of one Spirit.

14 For the body is not one member, but many.

15 If the foot says, "I am not a hand, I am not of the body," it is not for this reason not a part of the body.

16 And if the ear says, "Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body," it is not for this reason not of the body.

17 If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?

18 But now God has put each one of the members in the body, just as he wanted.

19 But if all were one member, where would the body be?

20 But now, many members, yet one body.

21 But the eye is not able to say to the hand, "I have no need of you," nor again the head to the feet, "I have no need of you."

22 But much more, the members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.

23 And the parts which seem to be more dishonored of the body, we put around more abundant honor, and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.

24 But our honorable [parts] have no need; instead God mixed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the [part] that lacks.

25 that there may be no division in the body, but the members may be anxious for one another.

26 If one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; if one member is glorified, all the members rejoice with it.

27 But you are the body of Christ and individual members.

28 And God put some in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then powers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, kinds of tongues.

29 Not all are apostles; not all are prophets; not all are teachers; not all perform powers.

30 Not all have gifts of healing, do they? Not all speak in tongues, do they? Not all interpret, do they?

31 But be zealous for the better gifts, and yet I show you a more surpassing way.


## Chapter 13

1 If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but do not have love, I have become a roaring bronze or a clanging cymbal.
