2 If I have prophecy and know all mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith so as to remove mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.

3 And if I feed all my possessions and if I deliver my body to be burned, but do not have love, I profit nothing.

4 Love is patient, love is kind; love is not zealous, love does not vaunt itself, is not puffed up.

5 does not behave rudely, does not seek its own, is not easily provoked, does not reckon evil.

6 it does not rejoice over unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth.

7 it bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.

8 Love never falls away; but if there are prophecies, they will be abolished; if tongues, they will cease; if knowledge, it will be abolished.

9 We know in part, and we prophesy in part.

10 But when the perfect comes, then that which is in part will be abolished.

11 When I was a child, I used to speak as a child, I used to think as a child, I used to reason as a child; but when I became a man, I abolished the things of the child.

12 For now we look through a mirror in an enigma, but then face to face; now I know in part, but then I will recognize fully, just as also I have been recognized.

13 Now these three remain: faith, hope, love; but the greatest of these is love.


## Chapter 14

1 Pursue love, and be zealous for the spiritual things, but rather that you may prophesy.

2 For the one who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God, for no one hears, but he speaks mysteries in the Spirit.

3 But the one who prophesies speaks to people for building up and comfort and consolation.

4 The one who speaks in a tongue builds himself up, but the one who prophesies builds up the church.

5 I want all of you to speak in tongues, but even more that you would prophesy; for the one who prophesies is greater than the one who speaks in tongues, unless he interprets, so that the church may receive building up.

6 Now, brothers, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I profit you unless I speak to you either in revelation or in knowledge or in prophecy or in teaching?

7 Yet even lifeless things that give a voice, whether a pipe or a harp, if they do not give a difference in the sounds, how will it be known what is piped or what is harped?

8 If, however, an uncertain voice is given by a trumpet, who will prepare for war?

9 So also you, through the tongue, if you do not give an easy to be understood word, how will that which is spoken be known? For you will be speaking into the air.

10 There are, it so happens, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them is mute.

11 If then I do not know the power of the voice, I will be a barbarian to the one speaking, and the one speaking will be a barbarian to me.

12 So also you, since you are zealots for the Spirit, seek that you may abound toward the building up of the church.

13 Therefore the one speaking in a tongue should pray that he may interpret.

14 For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind is unfruitful.

15 What then is it? I will pray with the Spirit, and I will pray also with the mind; I will sing psalms with the Spirit, and I will sing psalms also with the mind.

16 Otherwise, if you bless in the Spirit, how will the one who fills the place of the unskilled person say the "Amen" at your thanksgiving, since he does not know what you are saying?

17 You indeed give thanks well, but the other is not built up.

18 I give thanks to my God, speaking in tongues more than all of you.

19 But in the church, I want to speak five words with my mind so that I may also instruct another person rather than ten thousand words in a tongue.

20 Brothers, do not become children in your understanding, but be babes in malice, yet become mature in your understanding.

21 In the law, it is written: "By men of other tongues and by other lips I will speak to this people, and even so they will not hear me," says the Lord.

22 So then tongues are a sign, not for those who believe, but for the unbelieving; but prophecy is not for the unbelieving, but for those who believe.

23 If, then, the whole church comes together in the same place and all speak with tongues, and unskilled persons or unbelievers enter, will they not say that you are mad?

24 But if all prophesy, and an unbeliever or an unskilled person should enter, he is rebuked by all and examined by all.

25 and so the secrets of his heart become manifest, and so, falling on his face, he will worship God, reporting that God is indeed among you.

26 What then is it, brothers? When you come together, each of you has a psalm, has a teaching, has a tongue, has a revelation, has an interpretation—let all be done for building up.

27 If anyone speaks in a tongue, let it be by two or at the most three, and in turn, and let one interpret.

28 if there is no interpreter, let him be silent in the church; let him speak to himself and to God.

29 Let two or three prophets speak, and let the others discern.

30 But if a revelation is made to another person who is sitting, the first one should be silent.

31 For you can all prophesy one by one, so that all may learn and all may be encouraged.

32 And the spirits of prophets are subject to prophets.

33 For God is not of disorder, but of peace, as in all the churches of the holy ones.

34 Your women should be silent in the churches, for they are not permitted to speak, but to submit, as also the law says.

35 But if they want to learn anything, let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for women to speak in church.

36 Did the word of God go out from you? Or did it come to you only?

37 If anyone seems to be a prophet or is spiritual, let him recognize that the things I write to you are the Lord's commandments.

38 But if anyone does not know, let him not know.

39 So then, brothers, be zealous to prophesy, and do not forbid speaking in tongues.

40 Let all things happen decently and in order.


## Chapter 15

1 Now I make known to you, brothers, the gospel which I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.

2 through which you are also being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you—unless you believed in vain.

3 For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received, that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures.

4 and that he was buried, and that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures.

5 and that he was seen by Cephas, then by the twelve.

6 Then he was seen by over five hundred brothers once for all, among whom the majority remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.

7 Then he was seen by James, then by all the apostles.

8 and last of all he appeared to me also, as to one born prematurely.

9 For I am the least of the apostles, who am not sufficient to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.

10 But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain, but I labored more abundantly than all of them, yet not I, but the grace of God that was with me.

11 Whether, then, I or those men, so we preach and so you believed.

12 But if Christ is preached, that He has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?

13 But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then Christ has not been raised.

14 But if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is empty, and your faith is also empty.

15 Moreover, we are found to be false witnesses of God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, whom he did not raise if indeed the dead are not raised.

16 For if the dead are not raised, then Christ has not been raised either.

17 But if Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.

18 As a result, those who have fallen asleep in Christ have also been destroyed.

19 If in this life we have hope in Christ only, we are more miserable than all men.

20 But now Christ has been raised from the dead; he became the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.

21 For since death came through a man, also the resurrection of the dead comes through a man.

22 For just as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be given life.

23 But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, then those of Christ at his coming.

24 Then comes the end, when He delivers the kingdom to God the Father, when He abolishes all rule and all authority and power.

25 For he must reign until he puts all enemies under his feet.

26 The last enemy to be abolished is death.

27 For he has put all things under his feet. But when he says that all things have been submitted, it is evident that he who has submitted all things to him is excepted.

28 But when all things are subjected to Him, then the Son Himself also will be subjected to Him who subjected all things to Him, so that God may be all in all.

29 Otherwise, what will those who are baptized for the dead do if the dead are not raised at all? Why then are they baptized for the dead?

30 And why are we in danger every hour?

31 Daily I die, by our boasting which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord.

32 If, according to man, I fought with wild beasts at Ephesus, what profit is it to me? If the dead are not raised, let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.

33 Do not be deceived: evil communications corrupt kind manners.

34 Awake justly and do not sin, for some have ignorance of God; I say this to your shame.

35 But someone will say, "How are the dead raised? And with what kind of body do they come?"

36 Foolish one, what you sow is not given life unless it dies.

37 And what you sow is not the body that will happen, but a naked grain—perhaps of wheat or of some of the remaining kinds.

38 But God gives it a body as He wanted, and to each of the seeds its own body.

39 Not all flesh is the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fish, and another of birds.

40 There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly ones is one thing, and the glory of the earthly ones is another.

41 There is another glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.

42 So also is the resurrection of the dead: it is sown in corruption, it is raised in incorruption.

43 It is sown in dishonor, it is raised in glory; it is sown in weakness, it is raised in power.

44 A natural body is sown, a spiritual body is raised. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.

45 So also it is written, "The first man Adam became a living soul"; the last Adam became a life-giving Spirit.

46 But not the spiritual first, but the natural, then the spiritual.

47 The first man is of the earth, earthy; the second man is the Lord from heaven.

48 Just as the earthy man is, so also are those who are earthy; and just as the heavenly man is, so also are those who are heavenly.

49 And just as we have worn the image of the earthy, we will also wear the image of the heavenly.

50 Now this I say, brothers, that flesh and blood are not able to inherit the kingdom of God, nor does corruption inherit incorruption.

51 See, I tell you a mystery: we will not all sleep, but we will all be changed.

52 In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet; for the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.

53 For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

54 When this corruptible has put on incorruption and this mortal has put on immortality, then the word that has been written will happen: "Death has been swallowed up in victory."

55 Where, death, is your sting? Where, Hades, is your victory?

56 The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.

57 But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

58 Therefore, my beloved brothers, be settled, unmovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not vain in the Lord.


## Chapter 16

1 Now concerning the collection for the holy people, as I appointed for the churches of Galatia, so you also do.

2 On the first day of the week, each of you is to put something aside and treasure up as he may prosper, so that when I come, collections may not happen then.

3 And when I come, I will send those whom you approve with letters to carry away your grace to Jerusalem.

4 But if it is worthy that even I myself should go with you, they will go.

5 But I will come to you when I pass through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia.

6 But I will perhaps remain with you, or even winter, so that you may escort me wherever I go.

7 For I do not want to see you now in passing, but I hope to continue some time with you, if the Lord permits.

8 But I will continue in Ephesus until Pentecost.

9 For a great and effectual door has been opened to me, and many oppose me.

10 But if Timothy comes, look that he happen fearlessly with you, for he works the work of the Lord as I also do.

11 Therefore let no one despise him; escort him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me, for I am waiting for him with the brothers.

12 Now concerning Apollos our brother, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; and it was certainly not his will to come now, but he will come when he has opportunity.

13 Watch, stand in the faith, quit like men, grow strong.

14 Let all your affairs happen in love.

15 Now I encourage you, brothers—you know the household of Stephanas, that it is firstfruits of Achaia, and they appointed themselves for ministry to the holy ones.

16 so that you also submit to such people and to all who work together and labor.

17 I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because these have filled up your lack.

18 They have refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore recognize such people.

19 The churches of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you much in the Lord, together with the church that is in their house.

20 All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

21 The greeting is in my hand—Paul's.

22 If anyone does not love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be accursed. Maran-atha.

23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

24 My love be with all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen. [The first letter to the Corinthians was written from Philippi, sent through Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus and Timothy.]


# 2 CORINTHIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Timothy the brother, to the church of God being in Corinth, together with all the holy ones being in the whole of Achaia.

2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort.

4 the one who comforts us in all our tribulation, so that we may be able to comfort those in any tribulation through the comfort with which we ourselves are encouraged by God.

5 For just as the sufferings of Christ abound toward us, so also our comfort abounds through Christ.

6 Now if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation, which is worked out in patience of the same sufferings which we also suffer; or if we are encouraged, it is for your comfort and salvation, and our hope is firm concerning you.

7 knowing that just as you are partners in the sufferings, so also in the comfort.

8 For we do not want you to be unaware, brothers, concerning our tribulation which happened to us in Asia, that we were weighed down beyond measure, beyond our power, so that we despaired even of living.

9 But we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, so that we would not be persuaded in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead.

10 He delivered us from so great a death, and delivers us; in him we have hoped that he will even yet deliver us.

11 While you also help together by your supplication on our behalf, so that from many faces the gift that is ours may be given thanks for by many on our behalf.

12 For our boasting is this, the testimony of our conscience, that in simplicity and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God, we conducted ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.

13 For we are not writing another thing to you than what you read and also recognize, and I hope that you will recognize even to the end.

14 just as you also recognized us in part, that we are your boasting even as you also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

15 And with this confidence I intended to come to you first, so that you might have a second grace.

16 and to pass through you into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and by you to be escorted into Judea.

17 Therefore, when I was planning this, I did not use lightness, did I? Or the things I plan, do I plan according to the flesh, so that with me there would be both "yes, yes" and "no, no"?

18 Faithful is God, because our word spoken to you did not happen "yes" and "no."

19 For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you through us - through me and Silvanus and Timothy - did not become yes and no, but has become yes in him.

20 For as many promises as God has, in him is the 'Yes'; therefore also the 'Amen' is through him to God for glory through us.

21 But the one who confirms us with you into Christ and has anointed us is God.

22 who also sealed us and gave the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.

23 But I call upon God as a witness against my soul that sparing you I have not yet come to Corinth.

24 Not that we lord it over your faith, but we are fellow workers of your joy, for you stand by faith.


## Chapter 2

1 But I judged this for myself, not to come to you again in sorrow.

2 For even if I grieve you, who is the one who rejoices me except the one who is grieved by me?

3 And I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I come I may not have sorrow from those from whom I ought to rejoice, having confidence in all of you, that my joy is the joy of all of you.

4 For out of much tribulation and anguish of heart I wrote to you with many tears, not that you would be grieved, but that you might know the love which I have more abundantly toward you.

5 But if anyone has grieved, he has not grieved me, but in part—so as not to burden all of you.

6 This punishment by the majority is sufficient for such a person.

7 Contrariwise, you should rather forgive and comfort him, lest such a man be swallowed up by excessive sorrow.

8 Therefore I encourage you to confirm your love for him.

9 For this reason also I wrote, so that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all things.

10 But if you forgive anything, I also forgive; for indeed what I have forgiven—if I have forgiven anything—I have done for your sakes in the face of Christ.

11 so that we may not be defrauded by Satan, for we are not ignorant of his thoughts.

12 When I came to Troas for the gospel of Christ, and a door was opened to me in the Lord,

13 I have had no relief in my spirit because I did not find Titus my brother; but after bidding them farewell, I went out to Macedonia.

14 But grace to God, who always triumphs over us in Christ and reveals the fragrance of his knowledge through us in every place.

15 For we are the sweet fragrance of Christ to God among those who are being saved and among those who are being destroyed.

16 To some a fragrance of death for death, but to others a fragrance of life for life; and for these things who is sufficient?

17 For we are not as many, corrupting the word of God; but as from sincerity, but as from God, in the sight of God we speak in Christ.


## Chapter 3

1 Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as some do, commendatory letters to you or from you?

2 You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men.

3 being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of hearts of flesh.

4 We have such confidence through Christ toward God.

5 We are not sufficient of ourselves to reckon anything as from ourselves, but our sufficiency is from God.

6 He has also made us able servants of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.

7 But if the ministry of death, engraved in letters on stones, happened with glory, so that the sons of Israel were not able to gaze into the face of Moses because of the glory of his face, which is being abolished.

8 How much more will the ministry of the Spirit be in glory?

9 For if the ministry of condemnation is glory, much more does the ministry of righteousness abound in glory.

10 For indeed what had been glorified has not been glorified in this respect, because of the surpassing glory.

11 For if that which is abolished came through glory, much more that which remains is in glory.

12 Since we have such hope, we use great boldness.

13 And not as Moses put a veil over his own face to prevent the sons of Israel from gazing at the end of that which was being abolished.

14 Their thoughts were calloused; for to this very day the same veil remains at the reading of the old covenant, not being unveiled, because it is abolished in Christ.

15 But to this day, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.

16 But whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.

17 Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.

18 But we all, with unveiled face, beholding as in a glass the glory of the Lord, are transfigured into the same image from glory to glory, just as from the Lord, the Spirit.


## Chapter 4

1 Therefore, having this ministry as we have received mercy, we do not lose heart.

2 We have renounced the secret things of shame, not walking in craftiness nor handling the word of God deceitfully, but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man's conscience in the sight of God.

3 But even if our gospel is covered, it is covered among those who are being destroyed.

4 In them, the god of this age has blinded the thoughts of the unbelieving, so that the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God, would not shine on them.

5 For we do not preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for the sake of Jesus.

6 For God, the one who said, "Let light shine out of darkness," has shone in our hearts for the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

7 But we have this treasure in vessels of earth, so that the surpassing power may be from God and not from us.

8 In all things afflicted, but not distressed; being at a loss, but not despairing.

9 persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not destroyed.

10 always carrying about the deadness of the Lord Jesus in the body, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.

11 For we who are always living are being delivered to death for Jesus' sake, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our mortal flesh.

12 As a result, death is at work in us, but life in you.

13 Since we have the same Spirit of faith, according to what is written, "I believed, therefore I spoke," we also believe, therefore we also speak.

14 knowing that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us through Jesus and present us with you.

15 For all things are for your sakes, so that grace, having abounded through the many, may cause thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God.

16 Therefore we do not lose heart, but even if our outward man is corrupted, yet the inward man is renewed day by day.

17 For our light tribulation, which is but for a moment, works for us an exceedingly surpassing and eternal burden of glory.

18 not watching for us the things that are looked at but the things that are not looked at, for the things that are looked at are temporary, but the things that are not looked at are eternal.


## Chapter 5

1 For we know that if our earthly house of the tabernacle is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house made without hands, eternal in the heavens.

2 For in this we groan, longing to be clothed upon with our habitation which is from heaven.

3 if indeed, having put on clothing, we will not be found naked.

4 For indeed we who are in the tabernacle groan, being weighed down, because we do not want to be stripped but to be clothed upon, so that the mortal may be swallowed up by life.

5 Now he who has worked us for this very thing is God, who also has given us the earnest of the Spirit.

6 Therefore, we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord.

7 For we walk by faith, not by sight.

8 But we are confident and are pleased rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.

9 Therefore we also labour, whether being at home or being absent, to be acceptable to him.

10 For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ, so that each may receive according to what he has done through the body, whether good or evil.

11 Knowing, therefore, the fear of the Lord, we persuade men, but we are revealed to God, and I hope also to have been revealed in your consciences.

12 For we do not commend ourselves again to you, but are giving you occasion for boasting on our behalf, so that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance but not in heart.

13 For if we are amazed, it is for God; if we are sober-minded, it is for you.

14 For the love of Christ constrains us, having judged this, that if one died for all, then all died.

15 And he died for all, so that those who live might no longer live for themselves but for the one who died and was raised for them.

16 So from now on, we know no one according to flesh; even if we have known Christ according to flesh, yet now we no longer know him in that way.

17 Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation; the old things have passed away—see, all things have become new.

18 Now all these things are from God, who reconciled us to Himself through Jesus Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation.

19 that is, God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning their trespasses to them, and putting the word of reconciliation in us.

20 We are therefore ambassadors on behalf of Christ, as God is encouraging through us; we pray on behalf of Christ: be reconciled to God.

21 For the one who did not know sin, he made sin for us, so that we might become the righteousness of God in him.


## Chapter 6

1 Working together then, we also encourage you not to receive the grace of God in vain.

2 For he says, "At an acceptable time I heard you, and in a day of salvation I helped you." See, now is the acceptable time; see, now is the day of salvation.

3 Giving no one any offence in anything, so that the ministry may not be blamed.

4 Rather, in all things we commend ourselves as servants of God in much patience, in tribulations, in necessities, in anguish.

5 in plagues, in prisons, in disorders, in labors, in watches, in fasts

6 in pureness, in knowledge, in patience, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in unfeigned love;

7 in the word of truth, in the power of God, through the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and the left.

8 through glory and dishonor, through evil report and good report; as deceivers and yet true.

9 As unknown yet recognized, as dying and see we live, as disciplined yet not put to death.

10 as grieving yet always rejoicing, as poor yet making many rich, as having nothing yet holding fast all things.

11 We have spoken openly to you, Corinthians; our heart is made broad.

12 You are not distressed by us, but you are distressed in your own affections.

13 As the very same recompense — I speak as to children — make yourselves broad also.

14 Do not be unequally yoked together with unbelieving; for what fellowship does righteousness have with lawlessness, and what fellowship does light have with darkness?

15 And what concord does Christ have with Beliar? Or what portion does a believer share with an unbeliever?

16 What agreement does God's temple have with idols? For we are the temple of the living God, just as God said: "I will dwell in them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they will be my people."

17 Therefore go out from their midst and be separate, says the Lord, and do not touch anything unclean; and I will receive you.

18 And I will be a Father to you, and you will be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord Almighty.


## Chapter 7

1 Therefore having these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of flesh and spirit, accomplishing holiness in fear of God.

2 Contain us: we have done wrong to no one, we have corrupted no one, we have defrauded no one.

3 I do not speak this in condemnation, for I have foretold that you are in our hearts to die together and to live together.

4 Great boldness is mine toward you; great boasting is mine on your behalf; I have been filled with comfort; I abound much more with joy in all our tribulation.

5 For even when we came into Macedonia, our flesh had no relief, but we were afflicted in every way—strife outside, fears within.

6 But God, who comforts the humble, comforted us by the coming of Titus.

7 Not only in his coming, but also in the comfort with which he was encouraged over you, announcing to us your earnest desire, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced all the more.

8 For even if I grieved you with the letter, I do not regret it, even if I did regret it; for I look at that letter, even if it grieved you for an hour.

9 Now I rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved into repentance; for you were grieved according to God, so that you might suffer loss in no way from us.

10 For the sorrow that is according to God works a repentance without regret leading to salvation, but the sorrow of the world works death.

11 For see, this very thing, that you grieved according to God, what great diligence it worked in you, yes defense, yes indignation, yes fear, yes earnest desire, yes zeal, yes vengeance! In all things you commended yourselves to be pure in the matter.

12 So even if I wrote to you, it was not for the sake of the one who did wrong, nor for the sake of the one who was wronged, but for the sake of revealing your diligence for us toward you in the sight of God.

13 Therefore we have been encouraged by your comfort, and we rejoiced all the more at the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been rested by all of you.

14 For if I have boasted to him about anything on your behalf, I was not put to shame, but just as we spoke all things to you in truth, so also our boasting before Titus became truth.

15 and his affections are more abundantly toward you, as he remembers the obedience of you all, how you received him with fear and trembling.

16 I rejoice that in everything I am confident in you.


## Chapter 8

1 Now we make known to you, brothers, the grace of God that has been given in the churches of Macedonia.

2 that in much proof of tribulation the abundance of their joy, and their deep poverty, abounded to the riches of their simplicity.

3 For I testify that according to their power, and even beyond their power, they were of their own accord.

4 They earnestly begged us with much entreaty to receive the grace and the fellowship of the ministry to the saints.

5 and not as we hoped, but they gave themselves first to the Lord and to us by the will of God.

6 So we urged Titus that, just as he had previously begun, he would also complete this grace among you.

7 But just as you abound in all things—in faith, in word, in knowledge, in all diligence, and in the love from you toward us—so you may also abound in this grace.

8 I do not say this by way of command, but by testing the genuineness of your love through the diligence of others.

9 You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that because of you he became poor, being rich, so that you through his poverty might be rich.

10 I give an opinion in this matter, for this is profitable for you, who began a year ago not only to do, but also to be willing.

11 Now also accomplish the doing, so that just as there was the eagerness to be willing, so also there may be the accomplishment from what you have.

12 For if the eagerness is set before you, it is acceptable according to what one has, not according to what he does not have.

13 For it is not that there may be relief for another and tribulation for you, but by equality, at the present time, your abundance fills their lack.

14 so that their abundance might become a supply for your lack, in order that equality might come about.

15 just as it is written: "The one who gathered much did not abound, and the one who gathered little did not have lack."

16 But thanks be to God, who gives the same diligence for you in the heart of Titus.

17 For he received the comfort, but being more diligent, he went out of his own accord to you.

18 But we have sent with him the brother whose praise in the gospel is throughout all the churches.

19 And not only this, but also having been chosen by the churches, he is our traveling companion with this grace that is being served by us for the glory of the Lord himself and your eagerness.

20 We avoid this, that no one should blame us in this abundance that is being served by us.

21 providing good things, not only before the Lord but also before men.

22 We have sent with them our brother whom we have tested in many things often to be diligent, but now much more diligent, with great confidence in you.

23 Whether through Titus, my partner and fellow worker for you, or our brothers, apostles of the churches, glory of Christ.

24 Therefore, show them the proof of your love and of our boasting about you, in the presence of the churches.


## Chapter 9

1 For indeed, concerning the ministry to the holy, it is abundant for me to write to you.

2 For I know your eagerness, which I boast about on your behalf to the Macedonians, that Achaia has been prepared since last year, and your zeal has provoked the majority.

3 I sent the brothers, so that our boasting about you might not be emptied in this matter, so that, just as I was saying, you might be prepared.

4 Otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find you unprepared, we, not to say you, would be shamed in this confidence of boasting.

5 Therefore I considered it necessary to urge the brothers to go before you and prepare beforehand this blessing of yours that was previously announced, so that it may be ready, as a blessing and not as greed.

6 Moreover, the one who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and the one who sows with blessings will also reap with blessings.

7 Each must purpose in his heart, not from sorrow or from necessity, for God loves a cheerful giver.

8 And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things at all times, having all contentment, you may abound for every good work.

9 As it is written: "He has scattered, he has given to the poor; his righteousness remains forever."

10 And the one who supplies seed to the sower and bread for eating will give and multiply your seed and grow the fruit of your righteousness.

11 in everything being made rich in all simplicity, which works through us thanksgiving to God.

12 For the ministry of this service is not only supplying the needs of the holy ones, but also abounding through much thanksgiving to God.

13 By the proof of this ministry glorifying God through the subjection of your confession to the gospel of Christ and the simplicity of the fellowship toward them and toward all.

14 And also their supplication for you, as they long for you because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.

15 Thanks be to God for His unspeakable gift.


## Chapter 10

1 Now I myself, Paul, encourage you by the meekness and clemency of Christ—I who, when present face-to-face, am humble among you, but when absent, am confident toward you.

2 But I beg that when present I may not be bold with the confidence with which I reckon to dare against some who reckon us as walking according to the flesh.

3 For though we walk in the flesh, we do not serve as soldiers according to the flesh.

4 For the weapons of our warfare are not fleshly, but able through God for the destruction of strongholds.

5 casting down imaginations and every high thing that lifts itself up against the knowledge of God, and leading captive every thought into the obedience of Christ.

6 and having ready to avenge all disobedience when your obedience is fulfilled.

7 You look at things according to the face. If anyone has persuaded himself that he is of Christ, let him reckon this again from himself, that just as he is of Christ, so also are we of Christ.

8 For even if I boast somewhat more abundantly about our authority, which the Lord gave us for building up and not for your destruction, I will not be ashamed.

9 so that I may not seem to be terrifying you by my letters.

10 "For his letters, they say, are heavy and strong, but his bodily presence is weak and his speech is despised."

11 Let such a one reckon this, that what we are in word by letters when absent, such we are also in deed when present.

12 For we do not dare to make ourselves of the number or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves, but they, measuring themselves by themselves and comparing themselves with themselves, do not understand.

13 But we will not boast beyond measure, but according to the measure of the rule which God divided to us as a measure, to reach even as far as you.

14 For we are not stretching beyond ourselves as if we did not reach to you, for we attained even to you in the gospel of Christ.

15 We are not boasting beyond our measure in the labors of others, but we have hope that, as your faith grows, we may be magnified among you according to our own rule to an abundance.

16 to preach the gospel in the regions beyond you, not to boast in another man's rule of things made ready.

17 But the one who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.

18 For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.


## Chapter 11

1 Would that you bore with me a little in my foolishness; but indeed you do bear with me.

2 For I am zealous for you with a godly zeal, for I espoused you to one man, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.

3 But I fear that, as the serpent deceived Eve by his craftiness, so your thoughts might be corrupted from the simplicity that is toward Christ.

4 If indeed the coming one preaches another Jesus whom we did not preach, or you take a different Spirit which you did not take, or another gospel which you did not receive, you bear with it well.

5 For I reckon that I am in no way lacking these superlative apostles.

6 But even if I am an unskilled person in word, yet I am not in knowledge, but in every way we have been revealed in all things to you.

7 I did sin by humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you freely.

8 I robbed other churches, taking wages for your ministry.

9 While present with you and lacking, I was not burdensome to anyone; for the brothers when they came from Macedonia supplied my lack, and in everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and will keep myself.

10 The truth of Christ is in me that this boasting will not be sealed up against me in the regions of Achaia.

11 Why? Because I do not love you? God knows.

12 And what I do and will do is so that I may cut off the occasion of those who want an occasion, so that in what they boast about they may be found just as we are.

13 For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Christ.

14 And no marvel, for Satan himself transforms into an angel of light.

15 No great thing, then, if even his servants also transform themselves as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.

16 Again I say, let no one seem to me to be foolish; but if otherwise, even as a fool receive me, so that I also may boast a little.

17 What I speak, I do not speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness in this substance of boasting.

18 Since many boast according to the flesh, I also will boast.

19 For you gladly bear with the foolish, being wise yourselves.

20 For you bear with it if someone enslaves you, if someone devours you, if someone takes from you, if someone lifts himself up, if someone beats you in the face.

21 I speak as if dishonor, that we have been weak; but in whatever anyone dares—I speak in foolishness—I also dare.

22 Hebrews? I am. Israelites? I am. Seed of Abraham? I am.

23 Are they servants of Christ? I speak as one who raves—I am more so: in labors more abundant, in plagues beyond measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths often.

24 Five times I received forty lashes minus one from the Jews.

25 Three times I was beaten, once I was stoned, three times I made shipwreck, a night and a day I spent in the deep.

26 in journeys often, in dangers from rivers, in dangers from robbers, in dangers from my own kind, in dangers from nations, in dangers in the city, in dangers in the wilderness, in dangers on the sea, in dangers among false brothers.

27 in labor and painfulness, in watchings often, in famine and thirst, in fasts often, in cold and nakedness.

28 Besides the things that come upon me, my daily anxiety for all the churches.

29 Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is offended, and I do not burn?

30 If I must boast, I will boast about the things of my weakness.

31 God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, knows, who is blessed forever, that I do not lie.

32 In Damascus, the ethnarch of King Aretas kept the city of the Damascenes, wanting to seize me.

33 And through a window I was let down in a basket through the wall and escaped his hands.


## Chapter 12

1 To boast, indeed, is not profitable for me; for I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.

2 I know a man in Christ fourteen years ago, whether in the body I do not know or outside the body I do not know, God knows, such a one was snatched up to the third heaven.

3 And I know such a man—whether in the body or apart from the body, I do not know, God knows.

4 He was snatched away into paradise and heard unspeakable words which it is not lawful for a man to speak.

5 About such a person I will boast, but concerning myself I will not boast, except in my weaknesses.

6 For if I want to boast, I will not be foolish, for I will speak the truth; but I spare, so that no one may reckon me beyond what he looks at from me or hears anything from me.

7 And because of the surpassing of the revelations, so that I would not exalt myself, a thorn in the flesh was given to me, a messenger of Satan, to strike me, so that I would not exalt myself.

8 Three times I begged the Lord for this, that it might depart from me.

9 He has said to me, "My grace suffices for you, for my power is perfected in weakness." Most gladly, therefore, I will rather boast in my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest upon me.

10 Therefore I am pleased in weaknesses, in insults, in necessities, in persecutions, in anguish, for the sake of Christ; for whenever I am weak, then I am able.

11 I have become a fool boasting! You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you, for I was lacking in nothing compared to the "super-apostles," even though I am nothing.

12 Truly the signs of an apostle were worked among you in all patience, by signs and wonders and powers.

13 For what is it in which you were overcome over the remaining churches, except that I myself was not burdensome to you? Forgive me this unrighteousness.

14 See, for the third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be burdensome to you, for I do not seek what is yours but you; for the children ought not to treasure up for the parents, but the parents for the children.

15 I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls, even if loving you more, I am loved less.

16 But be it so; I did not burden you, but being crafty, I took you by deceit.

17 Did I defraud you through any of those whom I sent to you?

18 I encouraged Titus, and I sent the brother with him. Did Titus defraud you? Did we not walk in the same Spirit? Did we not walk in the same steps?

19 Again, do you seem that we are defending ourselves before God? In Christ we speak all things, beloved, for your building up.

20 I fear that when I come, I may not find you as I want, and I may be found by you as you do not want: strifes, jealousies, outbursts of wrath, factions, backbitings, whisperings, conceits, disorders.

21 when I return again, my God may not humble me among you, and I may mourn over many of those who have already sinned and have not repented of the uncleanness, fornication, and licentiousness which they have practiced.


## Chapter 13

1 This is the third time I am coming to you; every matter shall be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses.

2 I have foretold and I foretell, as being present the second time, and now being absent I write to those who have sinned already and to all the rest, that if I come again I will not spare.

3 Since you seek proof of Christ speaking in me, who is not weak toward you but is mighty in you.

4 For indeed he was crucified from weakness, but he lives from the power of God. For indeed we are weak in him, but we will live with him from the power of God toward you.

5 Test yourselves to see whether you are in the faith; test yourselves. Or do you not recognize yourselves that Jesus Christ is in you—unless indeed you are reprobate?

6 But I hope that you will know that we are not reprobate.

7 But I pray to God that you do no evil, not so that we may appear approved, but that you may do what is good, while we may be as reprobates.

8 For we are not able to do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.

9 For we rejoice when we are weak and you are able; this also we pray for, your perfection.

10 For this reason I am writing these things while absent, so that when present I may not use severity according to the authority which the Lord gave me for building up and not for destruction.

11 Finally, brothers, rejoice, be restored, be encouraged, think the same thing, be at peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.

12 Greet one another with a holy kiss.

13 All the holy ones greet you.

14 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit, be with all of you. Amen. [The second epistle to the Corinthians was written from Philippi of Macedonia by Titus and Lucas.]


# GALATIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle not from men nor through a man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead.

2 All the brothers with me to the churches of Galatia.

3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.

4 who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.

5 To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

6 I marvel that you are so quickly being removed from the one who called you in the grace of Christ to a different gospel.

7 This is not another, except that there are some who trouble you and want to turn the gospel of Christ.

8 But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel to you contrary to what we preached to you, let him be accursed.

9 As we have foretold and now again I say, if anyone preaches a gospel to you contrary to what you received, let him be a curse.

10 For am I now persuading men, or God? Or do I seek to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I would not be a servant of Christ.

11 Now I make known to you, brothers, the gospel which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.

12 For I did not receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but through a revelation of Jesus Christ.

13 For you have heard of my former conduct in the Jews' religion, how I was persecuting the church of God beyond measure and destroying it.

14 And I was advancing in the Jews' religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly a zealot for the traditions of my fathers.

15 But when God, who separated me from my mother's womb and called me through His grace, was pleased.

16 to reveal His Son in me so that I might preach the gospel of Him among the nations, immediately I did not consult with flesh and blood.

17 Nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I departed into Arabia and again returned to Damascus.

18 Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter and continued with him fifteen days.

19 But other of the apostles I saw none except James the brother of the Lord.

20 Now what I am writing to you—see, before God—that I am not lying.

21 Then I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

22 But I was not known by face to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.

23 Only they were hearing that the one who formerly persecuted us now preaches the gospel of the faith which he formerly destroyed.

24 And they glorified God in me.


## Chapter 2

1 Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus along also.

2 But I went up according to a revelation, and I communicated to them the gospel which I preach among the nations, but privately to those who seemed to be something, lest somehow I should be running or had run in vain.

3 But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

4 But this was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who came in secretly to spy out our freedom which we have in Christ Jesus, so that they might enslave us.

5 To them we did not give place in subjection even for an hour, so that the truth of the gospel might remain with you.

6 But from those who seem to be something—whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God does not take a man's face; for to me, those who seem to be something added nothing in conference.

7 But contrariwise, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the gospel of the uncircumcision, just as Peter was of the circumcision.

8 For the one working in Peter for the apostleship of the circumcision also worked in me for the nations.

9 knowing the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, who seemed to be pillars, gave the right hand of fellowship to me and to Barnabas, so that we might go to the nations, and they to the circumcision.

10 only to remember the poor, which very thing I also was diligent to do.

11 But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face because he was blameworthy.

12 For before certain people came from James, he used to eat with the nations. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those of the circumcision.

13 And the rest of the Jews dissembled with him, so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy.

14 But when I saw that they were not walking uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said to Peter in front of all, "If you, being a Jew, live after the manner of Gentiles and not as do the Jews, why do you compel the nations to live as the Jews?"

15 We are Jews by nature, and not sinners from among the Gentiles.

16 knowing that a man is not justified by works of the law unless through the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ and not by works of the law, because by works of the law no flesh will be justified.

17 But if while seeking to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found to be sinners, is Christ then a servant of sin? By no means!

18 For if I build again the things I destroyed, I commend myself as a transgressor.

19 For through the law I died to the law, so that I might live to God.

20 I have been crucified with Christ; it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me; and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and delivered Himself up for me.

21 I do not reject the grace of God, for if righteousness comes through the law, then Christ died freely.


## Chapter 3

1 O foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to be persuaded by the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was written beforehand as crucified among you?

2 I want to learn only this from you: did you take the Spirit by works of the law or by the hearing of faith?

3 Are you so foolish? Having begun by the Spirit, are you now accomplished by the flesh?

4 You suffered so much in vain—if indeed it was in vain.

5 So then the one who supplies the Spirit to you and works powers among you, is this by the works of the law or by the hearing of faith?

6 Just as Abraham believed God and it was reckoned to him as righteousness.

7 You know, therefore, that those who are of faith are sons of Abraham.

8 Foreseeing that God justifies the nations by faith, the Scripture preached the gospel beforehand to Abraham, saying, "All nations will be blessed in you."

9 Therefore, those who are of faith are blessed together with the faithful Abraham.

10 For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse, for it is written: "Cursed is everyone who does not continue in all the things written in the book of the law to do them."

11 But it is evident that no one is justified before God by the law, because "the righteous will live by faith."

12 But the law is not from faith; but the man who does these things will live by them.

13 Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us, for it is written, "Cursed is everyone who is hanged on a wood."

14 so that the blessing of Abraham might happen to the nations in Christ Jesus, so that we might take the promise of the Spirit through faith.

15 Brothers, I speak in human terms: no one rejects or adds to a human covenant once it has been confirmed.

16 Now the promises were said to Abraham and to his seed. It does not say, "and to seeds," as concerning many, but as concerning one, "and to your seed," who is Christ.

17 But I say this: a covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law that came into being four hundred and thirty years later does not annul it, so as to abolish the promise.

18 For if the inheritance comes from the law, it no longer comes from a promise; but God freely gave it to Abraham through a promise.

19 Why then the law? It was added because of transgressions until the seed to whom the promise had been made should come, appointed through angels by the hand of a mediator.

20 But the mediator is not of one, but God is one.

21 So the law is against the promises of God? By no means! For if a law had been given that was able to give life, indeed righteousness would have been from the law.

22 But Scripture concluded all under sin, so that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

23 But before faith came, we were kept under the law, confined until the faith which was about to be revealed.

24 So then, the law has been our schoolmaster leading us to Christ, so that we might be justified by faith.

25 But when faith has come, we are no longer under a schoolmaster.

26 For you are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.

27 For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

28 There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither servant nor free, there is neither male nor female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

29 And if you are Christ's, then you are Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise.


## Chapter 4

1 Now I say, as long as the heir is a child, he differs in no way from a servant, though he is lord of all.

2 but he is under stewards and managers until the time appointed by the Father.

3 So also we, when we were children, were enslaved under the rudiments of the world.

4 But when the fullness of the time came, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under the law.

5 so that He might redeem those under the law, that we might receive the adoption.

6 Because you are sons, God sent forth the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying out, "Abba, Father."

7 So you are no longer a servant, but a Son; and if a Son, then an heir of God through Christ.

8 But at that time, not knowing God, you served those who by nature are not gods.

9 But now that you have come to know God—or rather, have been known by God—how is it that you turn back again to the weak and poor rudiments, to which you want to serve all over again?

10 You observe days and months and times and years.

11 I fear for you, that perhaps I have labored in vain for you.

12 Become as I am, because I also am as you are, brothers; I pray to you, you have done me no wrong.

13 But you know that because of a weakness of the flesh I preached the gospel to you previously.

14 You did not despise or reject my temptation in my flesh, but you received me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus.

15 What then was your blessing? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have broken out your eyes and given them to me.

16 So I have become your enemy by speaking truth to you.

17 They are zealous for you, not rightly, but they want to exclude you so that you may be zealous for them.

18 It is good to be zealous in a good cause always, and not only when I am present with you.

19 My little children, with whom I again travail in birth until Christ is formed in you.

20 I want to be present with you now and change my voice, because I am at a loss over you.

21 Say to me, you who want to be under the law, do you not hear the law?

22 For it is written that Abraham had two Sons, one from the maidservant and one from the free woman.

23 But the one born from the maidservant is according to the flesh, and the one from the free woman is through the promise.

24 These things are allegorized, for these women are two covenants: one from Mount Sinai, giving birth to bondage, which is Hagar.

25 For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she answers to the present Jerusalem, for she serves with her children.

26 But the Jerusalem above is free, which is the mother of us all.

27 For it is written, Rejoice, barren woman who does not bear; burst out and cry, she who does not travail in childbirth, because the children of the wilderness are many more than those of her who has a husband.

28 But we, brothers, are children of promise according to Isaac.

29 But just as then the one born according to the flesh persecuted the one born according to the Spirit, so also now.

30 But what does the Scripture say? Cast out the maidservant and her son, for the son of the maidservant shall by no means inherit with the son of the free woman.

31 Therefore, brothers, we are not children of a maidservant, but of the free woman.


## Chapter 5

1 For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm, then, and do not hold yourselves again under a yoke of bondage.

2 See, I Paul say to you that if you circumcise yourselves, Christ will profit you nothing.

3 I testify again to every man who is circumcised that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

4 You have been abolished from Christ, you who are justified in law, you have fallen away from grace.

5 For we through the Spirit, by faith, wait for the hope of righteousness.

6 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision is able to accomplish anything, but faith working through love.

7 You were running well. Who hindered you from being persuaded by the truth?

8 This persuasion is not from the one who calls you.

9 A little leaven leavens the whole lump.

10 I am persuaded concerning you in the Lord that you will think nothing else, but the one who is troubling you will bear the judgment, whoever he may be.

11 But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been abolished.

12 I wish those who trouble you would cut themselves off.

13 For you were called to freedom, brothers; only do not use your freedom as an occasion for the flesh, but through love serve one another.

14 For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."

15 But if you bite and devour one another, look out that you are not consumed by one another.

16 But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not carry out the desire of the flesh.

17 For the flesh desires what is against the Spirit, and the Spirit what is against the flesh; these oppose each other, so that you do not do the things you want.

18 But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under law.

19 Now manifest are the works of the flesh, which are adultery, fornication, uncleanness, licentiousness.

20 idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, zeal, wrath, strife, division, sects

21 envy, murder, drunkenness, revelry, and things like these, which I foretell to you, just as I also foretold, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.

22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith.

23 Against such things there is no law: meekness, self-control.

24 But those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh together with its sufferings and desires.

25 If we live by the Spirit, let us also walk orderly by the Spirit.

26 Let us not become desirous of vain-glory, provoking one another and envying one another.


## Chapter 6

1 Brothers, if a man also be taken beforehand in any trespass, you who are spiritual restore such a one in the Spirit of meekness, watching yourself lest you also be tested.

2 Bear one another's burdens, and so fill up the law of Christ.

3 For if anyone seems to be someone when he is no one, he deceives himself.

4 Let each test his own work, and then he will have his boasting only in himself and not in the other.

5 For each will bear his own burden.

6 Let the one who is instructed in the word share all good things with the one who instructs.

7 Do not be deceived: God is not mocked; for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.

8 Because the one who sows into his own flesh will reap corruption from the flesh, but the one who sows into the Spirit will reap eternal life from the Spirit.

9 Let us not lose heart in doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not faint.

10 Therefore, as we have time, let us work good toward all, especially toward those of the household of faith.

11 See with how great letters I have written to you with my hand.

12 All who want to make a fair show in the flesh compel you to be circumcised, only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.

13 For even those who are circumcised do not themselves keep the law, but they want you to be circumcised so that they may boast in your flesh.

14 But far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

15 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision is able to do anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

16 And as many as walk orderly according to this rule, peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.

17 From now on, let no one cause me trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus in my body.

18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen. [Written to the Galatians from Rome.]


# EPHESIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, to the holy ones who are in Ephesus and faithful in Christ Jesus.

2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 Blessed is the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly realms in Christ.

4 just as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, for us to be holy and blameless before him in love.

5 He predestined us for adoption through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will.

6 to the praise of the glory of his grace, by which he made us accepted in the Beloved.

7 In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of trespasses, according to the riches of his grace.

8 which he abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence.

9 having made known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he purposed in him.

10 for the stewardship of the fullness of time, to sum up all things in Christ, the things in the heavens and the things on the earth.

11 In him we also obtained an inheritance, having been predestined according to the purpose of the one who works all things in accordance with the counsel of his will.

12 we who have first trusted in Christ should be for the praise of his glory.

13 In whom also you, having heard the word of the truth, the gospel of your salvation, in whom also having believed you were sealed with the Holy Spirit of promise.

14 who is the guarantee of our inheritance, for the redemption of the purchased possession, for the praise of his glory.

15 For this reason, even I, having heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and the love you have for all the holy ones,

16 I do not cease giving thanks for you, making remembrance of you in my prayers.

17 May the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, give you a Spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of Him.

18 having the eyes of your mind enlightened, so that you may know what is the hope of his calling, and what are the riches of the glory of his inheritance among the holy ones.

19 and what is the surpassing greatness of his power toward us who believe according to the working of the power of his strength.

20 which he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places.

21 far above all beginning and authority and power and dominion and all name being named, not only in this age but also in the one about to come.

22 And he subjected all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church.

23 which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all things in all.


## Chapter 2

1 And you being dead in your trespasses and sins.

2 in which you once walked according to the age of this world, according to the ruler of the authority of the air, of the Spirit now working in the Sons of disobedience.

3 Among whom also we all once conducted ourselves in the desires of our flesh, doing the wills of the flesh and of the minds, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest.

4 But God, being rich in mercy because of the great love with which he loved us,

5 And being dead in trespasses, he made us alive together with Christ — by grace you have been saved.

6 And raised us up together and seated us together in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus.

7 so that he might show in the coming ages the surpassing riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.

8 For it is by grace you have been saved through faith, and this is not from yourselves; it is the gift of God.

9 not from works, so that no one may boast.

10 For we are his thing that is made, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand so that we might walk in them.

11 Therefore remember that you were once nations in the flesh, those who are said to be "uncircumcision" by what is said to be "circumcision" in the flesh made by hands.

12 that at that time you were without Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel and strangers to the covenants of the promise, having no hope and without God in the world.

13 But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far away have become near by the blood of Christ.

14 For he himself is our peace, the one who has made both one and has loosed the middle wall of the hedge.

15 by abolishing in his flesh the enmity, the law of commandments in decrees, so that he might create the two in himself into one new man, doing peace.

16 and reconcile both in one body to God through the cross, having killed the enmity in himself.

17 And having come, he preached peace to you who were far away and to those who were near.

18 For through him we both have access by one Spirit to the Father.

19 Therefore then you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but fellow citizens of the holy ones and household members of God.

20 built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Jesus Christ himself being the cornerstone.

21 In him the whole building, being fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord.

22 in whom you also are being built together into a habitation of God in the Spirit.


## Chapter 3

1 For this reason I, Paul, the prisoner of Christ Jesus on behalf of you nations.

2 if indeed you heard about the stewardship of the grace of God that was given to me for you.

3 that by revelation he made known to me the mystery, as I wrote beforehand briefly.

4 By reading this, you are able to understand my insight into the mystery of Christ.

5 In other generations it was not made known to the sons of men, as it has now been revealed to his holy apostles and prophets in the Spirit.

6 the nations be fellow-heirs and of the same body and partakers of his promise in Christ through the gospel

7 I became a servant according to the gift of the grace of God that was given to me according to the working of his power.

8 To me, the less than the least of all the holy ones, this grace was given, to preach the gospel among the nations the unsearchable riches of Christ.

9 and to enlighten all as to what is the fellowship of the mystery which has been hidden from the ages in God, who created all things through Jesus Christ.

10 so that the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places through the church.

11 according to the purpose of the ages which he did in Christ Jesus our Lord.

12 In whom we have boldness and access in confidence through the faith of him.

13 Therefore I ask that you not lose heart at my tribulations on your behalf, which is your glory.

14 For this reason I bow my knees to the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

15 from whom all family in heaven and on earth is named

16 that he would give you according to the riches of his glory to grow strong with power through his Spirit in the inner man.

17 that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith

18 rooted and grounded in love, so that you may have power to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth.

19 and to know the surpassing love of Christ beyond knowledge, so that you may be fulfilled to all the fullness of God.

20 Now to him who is able to do far more abundantly beyond all that we ask or understand, according to the power that works within us.

21 To him be glory in the church in Christ Jesus to all generations, forever and ever. Amen.


## Chapter 4

1 Therefore I, the prisoner in the Lord, encourage you to walk worthily of the calling to which you were called.

2 With all humility and meekness, with patience, bearing with one another in love.

3 being diligent to keep the unity of the Spirit in the band of peace.

4 one body and one Spirit, just as also you were called in one hope of your calling.

5 one Lord, one faith, one baptism

6 one God and Father of all, who is over all and through all and in all of you.

7 But to each one of us grace has been given according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

8 Therefore it says, "Having gone up to a height, he led captivity captive and gave gifts to men."

9 Now what does "he went up" mean, except that he also first went down into the lower parts of the earth?

10 He who went down is the same as he who went up far above all the heavens, so that he might fulfill all things.

11 And he gave some apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, some shepherds and teachers.

12 for the perfecting of the holy people for the work of ministry, for the building up of the body of Christ.

13 until we all arrive at the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, at a perfect man, at the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ.

14 No longer may we be children, tossed to and fro and carried about by every wind of teaching, in the sleight of men, in craftiness for the wile of error.

15 But speaking truth in love, let us grow up into him in all things, who is the head, Christ.

16 From whom the whole body, being fitted together and knit together through every joint of the supply according to the working in measure of each one part, produces the increase of the body for the building of itself in love.

17 Therefore I say and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the remaining nations also walk, in the vanity of their mind.

18 having been darkened in their understanding, being alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart.

19 who, having become past feeling, have delivered themselves over to licentiousness for the working of all uncleanness in greed.

20 But you did not learn Christ in this way.

21 if indeed you have heard him and have been taught in him, just as truth is in Jesus.

22 to put away, in regard to your former conduct, the old man who is corrupt according to the desires of deceit.

23 be renewed in the spirit of your mind

24 and to put on the new man, which was created according to God in righteousness and holiness of the truth.

25 Therefore, having put away the lie, speak truth each one with his neighbor, for we are members of one another.

26 Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your wrath.

27 and do not give place to the devil.

28 Let the one who steals steal no longer, but rather let him labor, working with his hands what is good, so that he may have something to share with the one who has need.

29 Let no rotten word come out of your mouth, but if any is good for edification of the need, that it may give grace to those who hear.

30 And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.

31 Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and blasphemy be taken away from you, along with all malice.

32 Become kind to one another, pitiful, forgiving each other, just as also God in Christ forgave you.


## Chapter 5

1 Become imitators of God, as beloved children.

2 Walk in love, just as Christ also loved us and delivered Himself up for us as an offering and a sacrifice to God for a fragrant aroma.

3 But fornication and all uncleanness or greed must not even be named among you, as is fitting for holy people.

4 and filthiness and foolish talking or jesting, which are not convenient, but rather thanksgiving.

5 For be knowing this, that all fornicator or unclean or covetous, who is an idolater, does not have an inheritance in the kingdom of Christ and God.

6 Let no one deceive you with vain words, for because of these things the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience.

7 Therefore, do not become partakers with them.

8 For you were once darkness, but now light in the Lord; walk as children of light.

9 for the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth.

10 testing what is acceptable to the Lord.

11 and do not share with the unfruitful works of darkness, but rather also rebuke them.

12 For the things happening in secret by them are shameful even to say.

13 But all things that are being rebuked by the light are revealed; for everything that is being revealed is light.

14 Therefore he says, "Wake up, sleeper, and rise from the dead, and Christ will give light to you."

15 Look carefully then how you walk, not as fools but as wise.

16 redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

17 Therefore do not be foolish, but understand what the will of the Lord is.

18 And do not be drunk with wine, in which is excess, but be fulfilled with the Spirit.

19 speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord

20 always giving thanks for all in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God and Father.

21 submitting to one another in the fear of God.

22 Wives, submit to your own husbands as to the Lord.

23 For the husband is the head of the wife, as also Christ is the head of the church, and he is the Savior of the body.

24 But as the church submits to Christ, so also the wives submit to their own husbands in all things.

25 Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and delivered himself up for her.

26 that he might sanctify her, having cleansed her by the washing of water with the word.

27 that he might present the church to himself glorious, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she might be holy and blameless.

28 Thus husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself.

29 For no one ever hated his own flesh, but brings it up and cherishes it, just as also the Lord does the church.

30 For we are members of his body, of his flesh and of his bones.

31 Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and cleave to his wife, and the two shall be one flesh.

32 This mystery is great; but I say this with reference to Christ and the church.

33 Nevertheless, also you, each one individually, must love his own wife as himself; and the wife must fear her husband.


## Chapter 6

1 Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is righteous.

2 Honor your Father and your mother, which is the first commandment with a promise.

3 so that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.

4 And fathers, do not anger your children, but bring them up in the discipline and admonition of the Lord.

5 Servants, obey your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in the simplicity of your heart, as to Christ.

6 not with eye-service, as people pleasers, but as servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the soul.

7 Serving with benevolence as to the Lord and not to men.

8 knowing that whatever good each one does, he will receive this from the Lord, whether servant or free.

9 And masters, do the same to them, releasing the threat, knowing that your own Lord is in heaven, and there is no respect of persons with him.

10 Finally, my brothers, be strengthened in the Lord and in the power of his strength.

11 Put on the whole armor of God, so that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

12 because our wrestle is not against blood and flesh, but against the beginnings, against the authorities, against the rulers of the darkness of this age, against the spiritual things of wickedness in the heavenly places.

13 Therefore take up the full armor of God, so that you may be able to resist in the evil day, and having worked all things, stand.

14 Stand therefore, having girded your loins with truth and having put on the breastplate of righteousness.

15 and having bound on your feet with the preparation of the gospel of peace.

16 In all circumstances, take up the shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the burning darts of the evil one.

17 And take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.

18 Praying with all prayer and supplication at all times in the Spirit, and watching with all perseverance and supplication for all the holy ones.
