19 and also for me, that a word may be given to me in the opening of my mouth, in boldness to make known the mystery of the gospel.

20 for which I am an ambassador in chains, so that in it I may speak boldly as I must speak.

21 But that you also may know the things concerning me, what I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make known to you everything.

22 I sent him to you for this very purpose, so that you might know the things concerning us and that he might encourage your hearts.

23 Peace to the brothers, and love with faith from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

24 Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ in incorruption. Amen. [The epistle to the Ephesians was written from Rome by Tychicus.]


# PHILIPPIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ, to all the holy ones in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, together with the overseers and servants.

2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 I give thanks to my God for all your remembrance.

4 always in all my supplication for all of you doing the supplication with joy

5 in your fellowship for the gospel from the first day until now

6 Being persuaded of this very thing, that the One who began a good work in you will accomplish it until the day of Jesus Christ.

7 It is righteous for me to think this about all of you, because I have you in my heart, you all being my companions of grace both in my bonds and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.

8 For God is my witness how I long for all of you with the affection of Jesus Christ.

9 And this I pray, that your love may abound more and more in knowledge and all judgment.

10 so that you may test the things that differ, in order that you may be pure and blameless for the day of Christ.

11 fulfilling the fruit of righteousness which comes through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.

12 Now I want you to know, brothers, that what has happened to me has come rather for the furtherance of the gospel.

13 so that my bonds became manifest in Christ among the whole praetorium and all the remaining people.

14 And most of the brothers, having been persuaded in the Lord by my bonds, dare more abundantly to speak the word fearlessly.

15 Some indeed preach Christ even out of envy and strife, but others also out of good pleasure.

16 Some proclaim Christ from strife, not sincerely, supposing to bring tribulation upon my bonds.

17 But the others from love, knowing that I am placed for the defense of the gospel.

18 What then? In every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed, and in this I rejoice—yes, and I will rejoice.

19 For I know that this will turn out for my salvation through your supplication and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ.

20 According to my earnest expectation and hope that I will be ashamed in nothing, but with all boldness, as always, even now, Christ will be magnified in my body, whether through life or through death.

21 For to me, to live is Christ and to die is gain.

22 If to live in the flesh is fruit of my work, I do not know what I will choose.

23 I am constrained by the two, having the desire to depart and to be with Christ, which is much better.

24 But to continue in the flesh is more necessary because of you.

25 And being persuaded of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your furtherance and joy in the faith.

26 so that your boasting may abound in Christ Jesus through my coming again to you.

27 Only let your manner of life be worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of your affairs, that you stand steadfast in one spirit, with one soul laboring together for the faith of the gospel.

28 and not being terrified in anything by those who oppose you, which is a proof of destruction to them, but of salvation to you, and this from God.

29 for it has been granted to you, on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in him, but also to suffer for him.

30 having the same contest that you saw in me and now hear in me.


## Chapter 2

1 Therefore if there is any comfort in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any affection and mercy.

2 Fulfill my joy, so that you think the same thing, having the same love, being like-minded, thinking the one thing.

3 No one out of strife or vain-glory, but in humility consider others surpassing yourselves.

4 Each of you should watch not only your own interests, but also the interests of others.

5 For let this be thought among you which was also in Christ Jesus.

6 He, existing in the form of God, did not consider being equal with God as robbery.

7 but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, happening in the likeness of men.

8 And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to the point of death, even the death of the cross.

9 Therefore God also highly exalted Him and graciously gave Him the name that is above every name.

10 so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of heavenly beings and earthly beings and beings under the earth.

11 and every tongue will confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

12 Therefore, my beloved, just as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

13 For it is God who works in you both to will and to work for His good pleasure.

14 Do all things without grumbling and without arguing.

15 so that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without fault in the midst of a crooked and perverted generation, among whom you appear as lights in the world.

16 holding fast the word of life, for a boast to me in the day of Christ, that I did not run in vain nor labor in vain.

17 But even if I am being offered as a drink offering upon the sacrifice and ministry of your faith, I rejoice and rejoice with you all.

18 Likewise you also rejoice and rejoice with me.

19 But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I also may be of good comfort when I know the things concerning you.

20 For I have no one likeminded who will naturally be anxious for the things concerning you.

21 For all seek their own interests, not those of Christ Jesus.

22 But you know his proven character, that as a child serves his Father, so he served with me for the gospel.

23 I hope to send him immediately whenever I look at my circumstances.

24 I am persuaded in the Lord that I myself also will come quickly.

25 But I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, and fellowsoldier, but your apostle and minister to my need.

26 Since I was longing for all of you and was full of heaviness, because you heard that he was weak.

27 He indeed was weak to the point of death, but God had mercy on him; and not only on him, but also on me, so that I might not have sorrow upon sorrow.

28 Therefore I sent him more carefully, so that when you see him again you may rejoice, and I myself may be less sorrowful.

29 Therefore, wait for him in the Lord with all joy, and hold such people as honored.

30 because for the work of Christ he drew near to death, not regarding his soul, in order to fill up your lack of the ministry toward me.


## Chapter 3

1 Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is not bothersome for me, but it is certain for you.

2 Look out for the dogs, look out for the evil workers, look out for the concision.

3 For we are the circumcision, who serve God in the Spirit and boast in Christ Jesus and have no confidence in the flesh.

4 Although I myself have confidence even in the flesh—if anyone else seems to have confidence in the flesh, I more.

5 circumcised on the eighth day, of the kind of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews, according to the law a Pharisee.

6 persecuting the church out of zeal, having become blameless according to the righteousness that is in the law.

7 But whatever things were gain to me, these I have considered loss because of Christ.

8 But indeed I also consider all things to be loss because of the surpassing value of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for whom I have suffered the loss of all things and consider them to be dung, so that I may gain Christ.

9 and be found in him, not having my own righteousness from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God based on faith.

10 to know him and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship of his sufferings, being conformed to his death

11 if somehow I may arrive at the resurrection from the dead.

12 Not that I have already taken it or have already been perfected, but I press on if I may also overtake that for which I was also overtaken by Christ Jesus.

13 Brothers, I do not reckon myself to have overtaken it, but forgetting the things which are behind and reaching forth to the things which are before.

14 I press on toward the mark for the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ Jesus.

15 Therefore, as many as are perfect, let us think this way; and if you think anything otherwise, God will also reveal this to you.

16 Nevertheless, in what we have attained, let us walk orderly by the same rule, thinking the same thing.

17 Fellow followers together, become imitators of me, and watch those who walk in such a way as the pattern you have in us.

18 For many walk, of whom I often told you, and now even weeping I tell you, as the enemies of the cross of the Christ.

19 Their end is destruction, whose god is their belly and whose glory is in their shame, those who think about earthly things.

20 For our citizenship exists in heaven, from which we also wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ.

21 who will transform the body of our humiliation into its becoming conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.


## Chapter 4

1 Therefore, my brothers, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord, beloved.

2 I encourage Euodia and I encourage Syntyche to think the same thing in the Lord.

3 Yes, I also ask you, genuine yokefellow, seize them—these women who laboured with me in the gospel, along with Clement and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life.

4 Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice!

5 Let your gentleness be known to all men; the Lord is near.

6 Be anxious about nothing, but in every matter, by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving, let your petitions be made known to God.

7 And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will keep your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.

8 Finally, brothers, whatever is true, whatever is grave, whatever is righteous, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is of good report, if there is any virtue and if any praise, reckon on these things.

9 The things which you have learned and received and heard and seen in me, practice these, and the God of peace will be with you.

10 But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at last you have flourished again to think on my behalf, which indeed you were thinking, but you lacked opportunity.

11 Not that I speak according to want, for I have learned to be content in the circumstances in which I am.

12 I know how to be humbled, and I know how to abound. In everything and in all things I have been instructed both to be satisfied and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer lack.

13 I can do all things through the one strengthening me, Christ.

14 Nevertheless, you did well to share with me in my tribulation.

15 And you yourselves also know, Philippians, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I went out from Macedonia, no church shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving except you only.

16 For even in Thessalonica you sent once and again a supply for my need.

17 Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the fruit that abounds to your word.

18 But I have received all things and abound; I am fulfilled, having received from Epaphroditus what came from you, a fragrant aroma, an acceptable sacrifice, pleasing to God.

19 And my God will fulfill all your need according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.

20 Now to our God and Father be the glory for the ages of the ages. Amen.

21 Greet all the holy ones in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you.

22 All the holy ones greet you, especially those from Caesar's house.

23 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen. Written to the Philippians from Rome by Epaphroditus.


# COLOSSIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy, the brother.

2 To the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 We give thanks to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, always praying for you.

4 Hearing of your faith in Christ Jesus and your love for all the holy ones.

5 because of the hope being laid up for you in the heavens, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel.

6 The gospel that is present among you, just as it is also in all the world, and it is bearing fruit, just as it also is among you from the day you heard and recognized the grace of God in truth.

7 just as you also learned from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on your behalf.

8 who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.

9 Therefore we also, from the day we heard, do not cease praying for you and asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding.

10 to walk worthily of the Lord, to all pleasing, bearing fruit and growing in every good work in the knowledge of God.

11 being strengthened with all power according to the might of his glory for all patience and patience with joy

12 Giving thanks to the Father, who made us able to share in the portion of the lot of the holy ones in the light.

13 who delivered us from the authority of darkness and removed us into the kingdom of the Son of his love.

14 in whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins.

15 He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.

16 For in him all things were created, the things in the heavens and the things on the earth, the visible and the invisible, whether thrones or dominions or beginnings or authorities; all things have been created through him and for him.

17 And he is before all things, and all things cohere in him.

18 And he is the head of the body, the church; he is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that in all things he might have the preeminence.

19 For in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell.

20 and through him to reconcile all things to himself, making peace through the blood of his cross—through him, whether the things on earth or the things in the heavens.

21 And you, once being alienated and enemies in mind by your evil works, he has now reconciled.

22 in the body of his flesh through death to present you holy and blameless and irreproachable before him.

23 if indeed you continue in the faith, founded and settled and not being moved away from the hope of the gospel, which you heard, which was preached in all creation under heaven, of which I, Paul, became a servant.

24 Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you, and I fill up the lack of the tribulations of Christ in my flesh for his body, which is the church.

25 I became a servant according to the stewardship of God that was given to me for you, to fulfill the word of God.

26 The mystery which was hidden from the ages and from the generations, but now has been revealed to his holy ones.

27 God willed to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the nations, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.

28 We proclaim Him, admonishing all men and teaching all men with all wisdom, so that we may present all men perfect in Christ Jesus.

29 To this end I also labor, striving according to his working which works in me in power.


## Chapter 2

1 For I want you to know how great a contest I have for you and for those in Laodicea and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh.

2 so that their hearts may be encouraged, being knit together in love and resulting in all the riches of the full assurance of understanding, resulting in the knowledge of the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ.

3 In whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden.

4 I say this so that no one may deceive you with persuasive words.

5 For even if I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in the Spirit, rejoicing and looking at your order and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.

6 Therefore, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, walk in him.

7 rooted and built up in Him and confirmed in the faith, just as you were taught, abounding in it with thanksgiving.

8 Look, lest there be anyone who spoils you through philosophy and vain deceit, according to the tradition of men, according to the rudiments of the world, and not according to Christ.

9 For in Him dwells all the fullness of the Godhead bodily.

10 And you have been fulfilled in him, who is the head of all beginning and authority.

11 In whom you were also circumcised with a circumcision made without hands, in the putting off of the body of the sins of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ.

12 Having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised together through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.

13 And you, being dead in your trespasses and in the uncircumcision of your flesh, he made alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses.

14 having blotted out the handwriting of decrees that was against us, which was contrary to us, and He has taken it out of the middle, nailing it to the cross.

15 Having put off the beginnings and the authorities, he made an example with boldness, triumphing over them in it.

16 Therefore let no one judge you in food or in drink or in respect of a feast or a new moon or Sabbaths.

17 which is a shadow of the things about to come, but the body is of Christ.

18 Let no one defraud you of your reward, delighting in humility and religion of the angels, intruding into things which he has not seen, in vain puffed up by the mind of his flesh.

19 and not seizing the head, from whom all the body, being supplied and knit together through the joints and bands, grows the increase of God.

20 Therefore, if you died with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are you subject to ordinances?

21 Do not touch, do not taste, do not handle.

22 which all things are for corruption by their using, according to the commandments and teachings of men.

23 These are things which indeed have a word of wisdom in will worship and humility and severity to the body, not in any honor for the satisfying of the flesh.


## Chapter 3

1 If then you have been raised together with Christ, seek the things above, where Christ is, sitting at the right hand of God.

2 Think about the things above, not the things on the earth.

3 For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.

4 When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.

5 Make dead, therefore, your members which are on the earth—fornication, uncleanness, passion, evil desire, and greed, which is idolatry.

6 Because of these things the wrath of God is coming upon the sons of disobedience.

7 among whom you also once walked, when you were living among them.

8 Now you must also put away all these: wrath, anger, malice, blasphemy, and filthy communication out of your mouth.

9 Do not lie to one another, having put off the old man with his deeds.

10 and have put on the new self which is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of the one who created it

11 where there is no Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, servant, free, but Christ is all and in all.

12 Therefore, put on, as chosen of God, holy and loved, affection of mercy, kindness, humility, meekness, patience.

13 bearing with one another and forgiving each other if anyone has a quarrel against another; just as Christ forgave you, so also do you.

14 And over all these put on love, which is the bond of perfection.

15 And the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which you were called in one body; and become thankful.

16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you abundantly in all wisdom, teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

17 And whatever you do in word or in work, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.

18 Wives, submit to your own husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.

19 Husbands, love your wives and do not be bitter toward them.

20 Children, obey your parents in all things, for this is acceptable to the Lord.

21 Fathers, do not provoke your children, so that they will not be dismayed.

22 Servants, obey your masters according to the flesh in all things, not with eye-service as people-pleasers, but with simplicity of heart, fearing God.

23 And all, whatever you do, work from the soul, as for the Lord and not for men.

24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.

25 And the one who does wrong will receive what he did wrong, and there is no respect of persons.


## Chapter 4

1 Masters, provide your servants with what is righteous and equality, knowing that you also have a Lord in heaven.

2 Devote yourselves to prayer, watching in it with thanksgiving.

3 Praying together also for us, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also bound.

4 So that I may reveal it, as I must speak.

5 Walk in wisdom toward those outside, redeeming the time.

6 Let your word always be seasoned with grace, like salt, so that you may know how you must answer each person.

7 Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant and fellow servant in the Lord, will make known to you all the things concerning me.

8 I sent him to you for this very purpose, that he may know the things concerning you and that he may encourage your hearts.

9 With Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is from among you; they will make known to you all the things here.

10 Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, greets you, and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas (concerning whom you received commandments—if he comes to you, receive him).

11 and Jesus, who is called Justus, who are from the circumcision—these are the only fellow workers for the kingdom of God, who have become a comfort to me.

12 Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, greets you, always striving for you in his prayers that you may stand perfect and fully assured in all the will of God.

13 For I testify to him that he has much zeal for you and for those in Laodicea and those in Hierapolis.

14 Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas greet you.

15 Greet the brothers in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church in his house.

16 And when the letter has been read among you, see to it that it is also read in the church of the Laodiceans, and that you also read the one from Laodicea.

17 Say to Archippus, "Look to the ministry which you received in the Lord, that you may fulfill it."

18 The greeting with my hand, Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen. Written to the Colossians from Rome by Tychicus and Onesimus.


# 1 THESSALONIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, to the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

2 We always give thanks to God for all of you, doing remembrance of you in our prayers.

3 Without ceasing remembering your work of faith and labor of love and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ before our God and Father.

4 knowing, brothers loved by God, your election.

5 For our gospel was not done to you in word only, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and in much assurance, just as you know what kind of men we were among you for your sake.

6 And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, having received the word in much tribulation with the joy of the Holy Spirit.

7 So you became a pattern to all those who believe in Macedonia and in Achaia.

8 From you the word of the Lord has sounded forth, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out, so that we have no need to say anything.

9 For they themselves report what kind of entrance we had to you, and how you turned back to God from idols to serve a living and true God.

10 and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who delivers us from the coming wrath.


## Chapter 2

1 For you yourselves know, brothers, that our entrance to you has not been in vain.

2 But even after suffering before and being mistreated, just as you know, in Philippi we spoke boldly in our God to speak the gospel of God to you in much conflict.

3 For our comfort does not come from error or from uncleanness, nor in deceit.

4 But just as we have been tested by God to be entrusted with the gospel, so we speak, not as pleasing men, but God who tests our hearts.

5 For we never came with words of flattery, as you know, nor in a pretense of greed—God is witness.

6 nor seeking glory from men, neither from you nor from others, when we could have been a burden as apostles of Christ.

7 But we became gentle among you, as a nurse cherishes her own children.

8 Being affectionately desirous of you, we are pleased to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own souls, because you have become beloved to us.

9 For you remember, brothers, our labor and toil, working night and day so as not to burden any of you, we preached to you the gospel of God.

10 You are witnesses, and God also, how holily and justly and blamelessly we behaved among you who believe.

11 just as you know how each one of you, as a father his own children, we were encouraging and comforting you

12 and we bear witness to you walking worthily of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.

13 Therefore also we give thanks to God without ceasing, because when you received the word of hearing from us of God, you received it not as the word of men but as it truly is, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.

14 For you became imitators, brothers, of the churches of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus, because you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, just as they themselves also suffered from the Jews.

15 Who killed the Lord Jesus and their own prophets and persecuted you, and they are not pleasing to God and are contrary to all men.

16 forbidding us to speak to the nations that they might be saved, so as to fill up their sins always; but the wrath has attained them to the end.

17 But we, brothers, having been bereaved of you for a short time in presence, not in heart, were all the more diligent to see your face with great desire.

18 Therefore we wanted to come to you—I, Paul, once and again—and Satan hindered us.

19 For who is our hope or joy or crown of boasting—is it not even you—in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at his coming?

20 For you are our glory and joy.


## Chapter 3

1 So, no longer bearing it, we were pleased to be left behind in Athens alone.

2 And we sent Timothy, our brother and God's servant and our fellow worker in the gospel of Christ, to strengthen and encourage you concerning your faith.

3 so that no one would be moved by these tribulations; for you yourselves know that we are appointed for this.

4 For indeed when we were with you, we foretold you that we are about to be afflicted, just as also it happened, and you know.

5 For this reason, when I could bear it no longer, I sent to learn about your faith, for fear that the tempter had tested you and our labor might have happened in vain.

6 But now that Timothy has come to us from you and preached the gospel to us concerning your faith and love, and that you always have a good remembrance of us, longing to see us just as we also long to see you.

7 Therefore we have been encouraged, brothers, concerning you, in all our tribulation and necessity through your faith.

8 For now we live, if you stand firm in the Lord.

9 For what thanksgiving can we repay to God concerning you for all the joy with which we rejoice because of you before our God?

10 night and day, praying most earnestly, to see your face and to restore the things lacking in your faith.

11 Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, guide our way to you.

12 May the Lord make you abound and overflow in love toward one another and toward all people, just as we also do toward you.

13 to strengthen your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ with all his holy ones.


## Chapter 4

1 Finally then, brothers, we ask and encourage you in the Lord Jesus, just as you received from us how you must walk and please God, that you may abound more.

2 For you know what commands we gave you through the Lord Jesus.

3 For this is the will of God, your sanctification, that you abstain from fornication.

4 Each of you know how to acquire his own vessel in sanctification and honor.

5 not in the passion of desire, just like the nations that do not know God.

6 that no one go beyond and defraud his brother in any matter, because the Lord is an avenger of all these things, just as we foretold you and testified.

7 For God did not call us to uncleanness, but in sanctification.

8 Therefore the one who rejects is not rejecting man but God, who also gives His Holy Spirit to us.

9 Now concerning brotherly love, you have no need for anyone to write to you, for you yourselves are taught of God to love one another.

10 For indeed you do this toward all the brothers throughout the whole of Macedonia; but we encourage you, brothers, to abound even more.

11 And to labour to be quiet, and to practice your own affairs, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you.

12 that you may walk decently toward those outside and have need of no one.

13 I do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you may not grieve as also the rest who have no hope.

14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose, so also God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep through Jesus.

15 For this we say to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who remain until the coming of the Lord, will by no means attain those who have fallen asleep.

16 For the Lord himself will go down from heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel, and with the trumpet of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.

17 Then we who are alive, who remain, will be snatched together with them in clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.

18 Therefore encourage one another with these words.


## Chapter 5

1 But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need to have anything written to you.

2 For you yourselves carefully know that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night.

3 For when they say, "Peace and certainty," then sudden destruction stands by them, just as pain comes upon a woman who has a child in the womb, and they will not escape.

4 But you, brothers, are not in darkness so that the day would overtake you like a thief.

5 All of you are sons of light and sons of the day; we are not of the night nor of the darkness.

6 So then let us not sleep as the remaining also, but let us watch and be sober.

7 For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk, get drunk at night.

8 But we who are of the day, let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and the helmet of the hope of salvation.

9 For God has not put us into wrath, but into the possession of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.

10 of the one who died for us, so that whether we watch or sleep, we might live together with him.

11 Therefore encourage one another and build one another up, just as you are doing.

12 Now we ask you, brothers, to know those who labor among you and who rule over you in the Lord and who admonish you.

13 Consider them beyond all measure in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.

14 We encourage you, brothers: admonish the unruly, comfort the feebleminded, hold fast to the weak, be patient toward all.

15 See that no one repays evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good, both toward one another and toward all.

16 Rejoice always.

17 Pray without ceasing.

18 In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.

19 Do not quench the Spirit.

20 Do not despise prophecies.

21 Test all things; hold fast the good.

22 Abstain from all form of evil.

23 Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you wholly, and may your entire spirit, soul, and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

24 Faithful is the one calling you, who also will do it.

25 Brothers, pray for us.

26 Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.

27 I adjure you by the Lord that the letter be read to all the holy brothers.

28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen. [The first letter to the Thessalonians was written from Athens.]


# 2 THESSALONIANS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul and Silvanus and Timothy to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

3 We ought to give thanks to God always for you, brothers, as it is fitting, because your faith grows exceedingly and the love of each one of all of you abounds toward one another.

4 So we ourselves boast in you among the churches of God about your patience and faith in all your persecutions and the tribulations that you bear with.

5 a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, to the end that you may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you also suffer.

6 if indeed it is righteous before God to repay with tribulation those who afflict you.

7 and relief to you who are afflicted, along with us, at the revelation of the Lord Jesus from heaven with his mighty angels.

8 in flaming fire, giving vengeance to those who do not know God and to those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ.

9 who will be punished with eternal destruction away from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his strength.

10 when he comes to be glorified among his holy ones and to be marveled at among all those who have believed—because our testimony was believed among you—on that day.

11 To this end we also pray always for you, that our God may count you worthy of the calling and fulfill all the good pleasure of goodness and the work of faith with power.

12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and Lord Jesus Christ.


## Chapter 2

1 Now we ask you, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being assembled together to him.

2 not to be quickly shaken from your mind, nor troubled—neither by a spirit, nor by a word, nor by a letter as if from us, as that the day of Christ is present.

3 Let no one deceive you in any way, for that day will not come unless the apostasy comes first and the man of sin is revealed, the son of destruction.

4 He who opposes and exalts himself against every one called God or an object of worship, so that he sits as God in the temple of God, demonstrating himself that he is God.

5 Do you not remember that while I was still with you, I told you these things?

6 And now you know what holds fast, for him to be revealed in his own time.

7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only the one who now restrains will do so until he is taken out of the way.

8 And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will consume with the Spirit of His mouth and will abolish by the appearing of His coming.

9 whose coming is in accordance with the working of Satan, with all power and signs and wonders of a lie,

10 and in all deceit of unrighteousness among those who are being destroyed, because they did not receive the love of the truth in order for them to be saved.

11 And for this reason God will send them a working of error, so that they might believe the lie.

12 so that all who have not believed the truth but have been pleased with unrighteousness may be judged.

13 But we ought to give thanks to God always for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation in sanctification of the Spirit and faith in the truth.

14 to which he called you through our gospel for the possession of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

15 So then, brothers, stand firm and seize the traditions that you were taught, whether by word or by our letter.

16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave eternal comfort and good hope through grace,

17 encourage your hearts and strengthen you in all word and good work.


## Chapter 3

1 As for the rest, pray, brothers, about us, that the word of the Lord may run and be glorified, just as also with you.

2 and that we may be delivered from wrong and evil men, for faith is not for all.

3 But the Lord is faithful, who will strengthen you and keep you from evil.

4 We are persuaded in the Lord concerning you that what we command you, you both are doing and will do.

5 May the Lord guide your hearts into the love of God and into the patience of Christ.

6 Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to avoid every brother who walks disorderly and not according to the tradition which he received from us.

7 For you yourselves know how you must imitate us, because we did not behave in a disorderly manner among you.

8 We did not eat anyone's bread freely, but with labor and toil, working night and day so as not to burden any of you.

9 Not that we do not have authority, but to give ourselves as a pattern for you to imitate.

10 For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: that if anyone does not want to work, neither let him eat.

11 For we hear that some among you are walking disorderly, not working at all, but being busybodies.

12 Now we command and encourage such people through our Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and eat their own bread.

13 But you, brothers, do not lose heart in doing well.

14 But if anyone does not obey our word through this letter, note that person and do not mix with him, so that he may be put to respect.

15 And do not consider him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.

16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with all of you.

17 The greeting is in my hand, Paul, which is a sign in every letter; I write in this way.

18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you. Amen. [The second letter to the Thessalonians was written from Athens.]


# 1 TIMOTHY

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the command of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ, our hope.

2 Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord, to Timothy, a genuine child in the faith.

3 As I encouraged you to remain in Ephesus while I was going to Macedonia, so that you could command certain people not to teach other doctrine.

4 nor to give heed to myths and endless genealogies, which provide controversies rather than the stewardship of God in faith.

5 Now the end of the command is love from a clean heart and a good conscience and unfeigned faith.

6 Some, having erred, have turned away to vain jangling.

7 wanting to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they are saying nor the things about which they constantly affirm.

8 But we know that the law is good, if a person uses it lawfully.

9 knowing this, that law is not laid down for a righteous person, but for the lawless and unruly, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers.

10 fornicators, sodomites, menstealers, liars, perjured persons, and if there is any other thing that opposes the healthy teaching.

11 according to the gospel of the glory of the blessed God which I was entrusted with.

12 I have grace for the one having strengthened me, Christ Jesus our Lord, that he considered me faithful, putting me into ministry.

13 I was once a blasphemer, a persecutor, and an insolent man, but I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and unbelief.

14 And the grace of our Lord was exceedingly abundant, with faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.

15 Faithful is the word and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am the first.

16 But for this reason I was shown mercy, so that in me first Jesus Christ might display all his patience, as a pattern for those who are about to believe in him for eternal life.

17 Now to the King of the ages, incorruptible, invisible, the only wise God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.

18 This command I commit to you, child Timothy, according to the prophecies that went before concerning you, so that you may serve as a soldier in them the good warfare.

19 having faith and a good conscience, which some have thrust away and made shipwreck concerning the faith.

20 Among these are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have delivered to Satan so that they may be disciplined not to blaspheme.


## Chapter 2

1 Therefore I encourage, first of all, that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all men.

2 For kings and all those who are in authority, so that we may lead a quiet and peaceable living in all godliness and dignity.

3 For this is good and acceptable in the presence of God our Savior.

4 who desires all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.

5 For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.

6 He gave Himself as a ransom for all, the testimony in its own times.

7 For this I was put as a preacher and an apostle (I am speaking truth in Christ, I am not lying), a teacher of the nations in faith and truth.

8 Therefore I will that the men pray in every place, lifting up holy hands without wrath and reasoning.

9 likewise the women, in apparel of good behaviour, with reverence and soberness, to adorn themselves, not with broidered hair or gold or pearl or costly clothing.

10 but it is fitting for women who promise godliness through good works.

11 Let a woman learn in quietness with all subjection.

12 I do not permit a woman to teach or to usurp authority over a man, but to be in quietness.

13 For Adam was formed first, then Eve.

14 And Adam was not deceived, but the woman, having been deceived, has come into transgression.

15 She will be saved through childbearing, if they remain in faith and love and sanctification with soberness.


## Chapter 3

1 Faithful is the word: if anyone desires the office of overseer, he desires a good work.

2 So an overseer must be blameless, the husband of one wife, discreet, of good behaviour, hospitable, apt to teach.

3 not given to wine, not a striker, not given to filthy lucre, but gentle, peaceable, without covetousness.

4 ruling his own house well, having children in subjection with all dignity.

5 If then someone does not know how to rule his own house, how will he take care of God's church?

6 Not a novice, lest having been puffed up he fall into the judgment of the devil.

7 He must also have a good testimony from those outside, so that he may not fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.

8 Servants likewise must be grave, not double-tongued, not giving heed to much wine, not given to filthy lucre.

9 having the mystery of the faith in a clean conscience.

10 And let these also be tested first, then let them serve, being irreproachable.

11 Likewise, wives must be dignified, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.

12 Servants must be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

13 For those having served well purchase for themselves a good degree and much boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.

14 I write these things to you, hoping to come to you quickly.

15 But if I am slack, so that you may know how one must conduct oneself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

16 And without controversy, great is the mystery of godliness: God was revealed in flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.


## Chapter 4

1 But the Spirit expressly says that in latter times some will depart from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and teachings of demons.

2 Among those who speak lies in hypocrisy, having their own conscience seared with a hot iron.

3 forbidding marriage and requiring them to abstain from foods that God created for receiving with thanksgiving by the faithful and those who have known the truth.

4 For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be refused if it is taken with thanksgiving.

5 It is sanctified for through the word of God and intercession.

6 By laying down these things to the brothers, you will be a good servant of Jesus Christ, being nourished up in the words of the faith and of the good teaching which you have attained.

7 But refuse profane and old wives' myths, and train yourself for godliness.

8 For bodily exercise profits for a little while, but godliness profits for all things, having a promise of the present life and of the life that is about to come.

9 Faithful is the word and worthy of all acceptation.

10 For to this end also we labor and are reproached, because we have hoped in a living God, who is Saviour of all men, especially of the faithful.

11 Command these things and teach them.

12 Let no one despise your youth, but become a pattern for the faithful in word, in conduct, in love, in Spirit, in faith, in purity.

13 Until I come, give heed to reading, to comfort, to teaching.

14 Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you through prophecy with the laying on of the hands of the council of elders.

15 Imagine these things; be in them, so that your furtherance may be manifest in all things.

16 Give heed to yourself and to the teaching; continue in them, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.


## Chapter 5

1 Do not rebuke an elder, but exhort him as a father, and the younger men as brothers.

2 Older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, with all purity.

3 Honor widows who are indeed widows.

4 If any widow has children or nephews, let them first learn to show piety to their own house and to repay their forefathers, for this is good and acceptable before God.

5 But the woman who is indeed a widow and desolate has hoped in God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.

6 But the one who lives in pleasure is dead even while she lives.

7 And command these things so that they may be blameless.

8 If anyone does not provide for his own, and especially for his own household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.

9 Let a widow be taken into the number, being not less than sixty years old, the wife of one husband.

10 testified by good works, if she brought up children, if she lodged strangers, if she washed the feet of the holy ones, if she helped those who were afflicted, if she followed every good work.

11 But refuse younger widows, for when they grow wanton against Christ, they want to marry.

12 having judgment because they rejected the first faith.

13 At the same time also, being idle, they learn to go around houses, and not only idle but also tattlers and busybodies, speaking things which must not be spoken.

14 I will, therefore, that younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give no occasion to the adversary for reproach.

15 For some have already turned away to follow Satan.

16 If any faithful man or faithful woman has widows, let them help them, and do not let the church be weighed down, so that it may help the widows who are indeed widows.

17 Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching.

18 For the Scripture says, "You will not muzzle an ox while it is threshing," and "The worker is worthy of his reward."

19 Do not receive an accusation against an elder except on the basis of two or three witnesses.

20 Rebuke those who sin in the presence of all, so that the remaining may also have fear.

21 I testify before God and the Lord Jesus Christ and the chosen angels that you keep these things without prejudice, doing nothing with partiality.

22 Do not lay hands quickly on anyone, nor share in the sins of strangers; keep yourself pure.

23 No longer drink water only, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent weaknesses.

24 The sins of some men are evident, going before them to judgment, but for others they follow after.

25 Likewise, good works are also evident, and those that are otherwise cannot be hidden.


## Chapter 6

1 All who are under a yoke as servants are to consider their own masters worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.

2 Those who have believing masters should not despise them because they are brothers, but rather serve them because they are faithful and beloved—those who receive the benefit. Teach and encourage these things.

3 If anyone teaches other doctrine and does not come near the healthy words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that is according to godliness,

4 He is puffed up, knowing nothing, but doting about controversies and strifes of words, from which happen envy, strife, blasphemy, evil surmising.

5 Withdraw from such men—perverse disputing, corrupt in mind and deprived of the truth, who suppose godliness to be gain.

6 But godliness with contentment is great gain.

7 For we brought nothing into the world, since it is evident that we are not able to bring anything out either.

8 But having food and clothing, with these we will be content.

9 But those who will be rich fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and hurtful desires, which sink men into destruction and ruin.

10 For the love of money is a root of all evils, which some desiring have erred from the faith and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

11 But you, O man of God, flee these things, and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, and meekness.

12 Strive the good contest of faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and confessed the good confession before many witnesses.

13 I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and of Christ Jesus, who testified the good confession before Pontius Pilate.

14 Keep the commandment spotless and blameless until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ.

15 He will show at the proper times—the blessed and only Potentate, the King of those who reign and Lord of those who lord over.

16 the only one who has immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no one among mankind has seen or is able to see, to him be honor and eternal power. Amen.

17 Command the rich in this present age not to be highminded, nor to hope in the uncertainty of riches, but in the living God, who provides us abundantly with all things for enjoyment.

18 Do good, be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate.

19 storing up for themselves a good foundation for that which is about to come, so that they may take hold of eternal life.

20 Timothy, keep that which is committed to you, turning away from the profane empty talk and opposition of the falsely so called knowledge.

21 Some, who promise concerning the faith, have erred. Grace be with you. Amen (the first to Timothy was written from Laodicea, which is the chiefest city of Phrygia Pacatiana).


# 2 TIMOTHY

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.

2 Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord, to Timothy, my beloved child.

3 I have grace for God, whom I serve from my forefathers with a clean conscience, as without ceasing I have remembrance of you in my supplications night and day.

4 Longing to see you, remembering your tears, so that I may be filled with joy.

5 Taking remembrance of the unfeigned faith that is in you, which first dwell in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded that it also dwells in you.

6 For this cause I remind you to stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.

7 For God did not give us a Spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.

8 Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but be a partaker of afflictions for the gospel according to the power of God.

9 who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before eternal times.

10 but has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Jesus Christ, who has abolished death and has brought life and incorruption to light through the gospel.

11 to which I was put as a preacher and an apostle and a teacher of the nations.

12 For this reason I also suffer these things, but I am not ashamed; for I know whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to keep my deposit for that day.

13 Hold to the form of healthy words that you heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.

14 Guard the good deposit committed to you through the holy Spirit who dwells in us.

15 You know this, that all those in Asia turned away from me, among whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.

16 May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain.

17 But having been in Rome, he diligently sought me and found me.

18 May the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day—and how much he served in Ephesus you know very well.


## Chapter 2

1 You therefore, my child, strengthen yourself in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

2 And the things you heard from me through many witnesses, commit these to faithful men who will be sufficient to teach others also.

3 Therefore endure hardship as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.

4 No one serving as a soldier entangles himself with the affairs of this life, so that he may please the one who chose him to be a soldier.

5 If anyone also competes as an athlete, he is not crowned unless he competes lawfully.

6 The laboring husbandman must first partake of the fruits.

7 Understand what I say; for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.

8 Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, from the seed of David, according to my gospel.

9 In which I endure hardship even to the point of bonds as an evildoer, but the word of God is not bound.

10 For this reason I endure all things for the sake of the chosen, so that they also may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

11 The saying is faithful: for if we died with him, we will also live with him.

12 If we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us.

13 If we disbelieve, he remains faithful—he is not able to deny himself.

14 Remind them of these things, testifying before the Lord not to strive about words for no profitable purpose, to the overthrow of those who hear.

15 Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a worker unashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.

16 But avoid profane empty talk, for they will advance much in ungodliness.

17 Their word will have pasture like a canker; among whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus.

18 Who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection has already happened, and they overthrow the faith of some.

19 Nevertheless, the stedfast foundation of God stands, having this seal: the Lord knows those who are His, and let all who name the name of Christ depart from unrighteousness.

20 In a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and of earth, and some for honor and some for dishonor.

21 If therefore anyone purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified and profitable to the master, prepared for every good work.

22 But flee youthful desires, and persecute righteousness, faith, love, peace, with those who call upon the Lord from a clean heart.

23 But refuse foolish and unlearned controversies, knowing that they birth strife.

24 A servant of the Lord must not strive, but be gentle towards all, apt to teach, patient.

25 In meekness disciplining those that oppose themselves, if perhaps God may give them repentance leading to the knowledge of the truth.

26 and they may recover self from the snare of the devil, having been taken captive by him for his will.


## Chapter 3

1 But know this, that in last days fierce times will be present.

2 For men will be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemous, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy.

3 without natural affection, implacable, devil, incontinent, fierce, despiser of those that are good.

4 Betrayers, heady, puffed up, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God.

5 Having a form of godliness but having denied its power; also turn away from these.

6 For among these are those who creep into houses and lead captive silly women heaped up with sins, being led by various desires.

7 always learning and never able to come to a knowledge of the truth.

8 Just as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses, so also these men resist the truth — people corrupted in mind, reprobate concerning the faith.

9 But they will not advance much, for their folly will be manifest to all, as also theirs became.

10 But you have attained my teaching, my manner of life, my purpose, my faith, my long-suffering, my love, my endurance.

11 the persecutions, the sufferings which happened to me in Antioch, in Iconium, in Lystra—what persecutions I bore—and from all of them the Lord delivered me.

12 And all who want to live godly in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.

13 Evil men and seducers will advance to the worse, deceiving and being deceived.

14 But you, remain in what you learned and were assured of, knowing from whom you learned it.

15 and that from infancy you have known the holy letters which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.

16 All Scripture is given by inspiration of God and is profitable for teaching, for evidence, for correction, for discipline in righteousness.

17 so that the man of God may be perfect, completely accomplished for all good work.


## Chapter 4

1 I testify therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who is about to judge the living and the dead, according to his appearing and his kingdom.

2 Preach the word; stand by it in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all patience and teaching.

3 For there will be a time when they will not bear with healthy teaching, but they will heap teachers to themselves according to their own desires, having itching ears.

4 And they will turn away their hearing from the truth, and will be turned aside to myths.

5 But you, be sober in all things, endure hardship, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.

6 For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure stands by.

7 I have striven the good contest. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.

8 Remaining, there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will repay to me on that day—and not only to me, but also to all who love his appearing.

9 Be diligent to come to me quickly.

10 For Demas has forsaken me, having loved the present age, and has gone to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia; Titus to Dalmatia.

11 Luke is only with me. Take up Mark and lead him with yourself, for he is profitable to me for ministry.

12 But I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.

13 Bring the cloak I left in Troas with Carpus, and the books, especially the parchments, when you come.

14 Alexander the coppersmith showed me much evil; may the Lord repay him according to his works.

15 Keep also yourself from him, for he strongly resists our words.

16 In my first defense no one came together with me, but all forsook me—may it not be reckoned against them.

17 But the Lord was present with me and strengthened me, so that through me the preaching might be fully assured and all the nations might hear, and I was delivered from the mouth of a lion.

18 And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work and will save me into his heavenly kingdom, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.

19 Greet Prisca and Aquila and the house of Onesiphorus.

20 Erastus remained in Corinth, but I left Trophimus in Miletus being weak.

21 Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, and Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers.

22 The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen. [The second letter to Timothy, who was chosen as the first overseer of the church of the Ephesians, was written from Rome when Paul was presented a second time before Caesar Nero.]


# TITUS

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the faith of God's chosen and the knowledge of the truth that leads to godliness.

2 in hope of eternal life, which the unlying God promised before eternal times.

3 And he revealed his word at the proper times in the preaching with which I was entrusted, according to the command of God our Savior.

4 To Titus, my genuine child according to our common faith: grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.

5 For this reason I left you in Crete, so that you might set in order the things that are lacking and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you.

6 If anyone is irreproachable, the husband of one wife, having faithful children, not under accusation of excess or unruly.

7 For the overseer must be irreproachable as God's steward, not self-willed, not soon angry, not given to wine, not a striker, not given to filthy lucre.

8 but hospitable, love of good men, discreet, righteous, holy, temperate.

9 holding fast to the faithful word according to the teaching, so that he may be able both to encourage in the healthy teaching and to rebuke those who speak against it.

10 For there are many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision.

11 whose mouths must be stopped, who overthrow whole houses, teaching things they must not, for the sake of shameful gain.

12 One of them, a prophet of their own, said: "Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, idle gluttons."

13 This testimony is true, for which cause rebuke them sharply, that they may be healthy in the faith.

14 not giving heed to Jewish myths and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.

15 All things indeed are clean to the clean, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is clean; instead, both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

16 They confess to know God, but by their works they deny him, being abominable and disobedient, and reprobate toward every good work.


## Chapter 2

1 But you must speak what is fitting for sound teaching.

2 Old men are to be sober, grave, discreet, being healthy in the faith, in the love, in the patience.

3 Likewise, aged women are to be reverent in behaviour, not slanderers, not enslaved to much wine, teachers of good things.

4 so that they teach the new women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children.

5 discreet, pure, keepers at home, good, submitting to their own husbands, so that the word of God may not be blasphemed.

6 Urge the younger men likewise to be sober-minded.

7 In all things provide yourself as a pattern of good works, with uncorruptness in teaching, dignity, incorruption.

8 Sound speech that cannot be condemned, so that the one from the contrary may respect you, having no evil to say about you.

9 Servants should be subject to their own masters, to be acceptable in all things, not talking back.

10 not keeping back, but showing all good faith, so that in all things they may adorn the teaching of God your Savior.

11 For the saving grace of God has appeared to all men.

12 Disciplining us so that, having denied ungodliness and worldly desires, we might live soberly and justly and godly in the present age.

13 waiting for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.

14 who gave himself for us, in order that he might redeem us from all lawlessness and cleanse for himself a peculiar people, zealous for good works.

15 Speak these things and encourage and rebuke with all command; let no one despise you.


## Chapter 3

1 Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities, to hearken, and to be ready for every good work.

2 to blaspheme no one, to be peaceable, gentle, showing all meekness toward all men.

3 For we also once were foolish, disobedient, being deceived, serving various desires and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, hating one another.

4 But when the kindness and love of God our Savior appeared,

5 not by works of righteousness which we did, but according to his own mercy he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Spirit.

6 which He poured out on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior.

7 that having been justified by his grace, we should become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

8 Faithful is the word, and concerning these things I will that you affirm constantly, so that those who have believed God may be careful to rule over good works; these things are good and profitable to men.

9 But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, strife, and quarrels about the law, for they are unprofitable and vain.

10 Reject a heretic man after one and a second admonition.

11 knowing that such a person is subverted and sins, being self-condemned.

12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, be diligent to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to winter there.

13 Diligently escort Zenas the lawyer and Apollos, so that nothing may be lacking for them.

14 But also let our people learn to rule good works for the necessary needs, so that they may not be unfruitful.

15 All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen. [Written to Titus, ordained the first overseer of the church of the Cretans, from Nicopolis of Macedonia.]


# PHILEMON

## Chapter 1

1 Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy the brother, to Philemon our beloved and fellow worker.

2 to Apphia, the beloved, and to Archippus, our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house.

3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

4 I give thanks to my God always, making mention of you in my prayers.

5 Hearing of your love and the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and toward all the holy ones.

6 so that the fellowship of your faith may become effectual in the knowledge of every good thing that is in you for Christ Jesus.

7 For we have much joy and comfort over your love, because the affections of the holy ones have been rested through you, brother.

8 Therefore, having much boldness in Christ to command you what is convenient,

9 For this reason of love I rather encourage you, being such a one as Paul, an old man, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

10 I encourage you for my child Onesimus, whom I have born in my bonds.

11 He who was once unprofitable to you but now is profitable both to you and to me, I have sent back.

12 But as for you, receive him, that is, my very affections.

13 whom I wanted to hold fast for myself, so that on your behalf he might serve me in the bonds of the gospel.

14 But I wanted to do nothing without your consent, so that your good might not be by compulsion but willingly.

15 Perhaps for this reason he was separated for a time, so that you might receive him eternally.

16 No longer as a servant, but more than a servant, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.

17 If then you have me as a partner, receive him as me.

18 But if he has done you any wrong or owes anything, impute this to me.

19 I, Paul, write with my own hand: I will repay it, not to say to you that you also owe me yourself besides.

20 Yes, brother, may I have joy from you in the Lord; refresh my affections in the Lord.

21 Persuaded of your obedience, I wrote to you, knowing that you will also do more than what I say.

22 At the same time, also prepare a lodging for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be granted to you.

23 Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you.

24 Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, Luke, my fellow workers.

25 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen. [Written to Philemon from Rome through Onesimus, a servant.]


# HEBREWS

## Chapter 1

1 God, having spoken long ago at many times and in various ways to the Fathers by the prophets, has in these last days spoken to us by his Son,

2 whom He appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the ages.

3 Who, being the brightness of the glory and the express image of his substance, and bearing all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself done purification of our sins, sat down on the right of the Majesty on high.

4 having become as much better than the angels as he has inherited a name more excellent than theirs.

5 For to which of the angels did He ever say, "You are My Son, today I have begotten You"? And again, "I will be to Him a Father, and He will be to Me a Son."

6 And again, when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, "Let all God's angels worship him."

7 And to the angels on the one hand he says, "The one who makes his angels spirits and his ministers a flame of fire."

8 But to the Son: "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, and the scepter of righteousness is the scepter of your kingdom.

9 You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of exultation beyond your partners.

10 And you, Lord, at the beginning founded the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.

11 They will be destroyed, but you remain; and they will all decay like a garment.

12 And like a covering you will fold them up, and they will be changed; but you are the same, and your years will not fail.

13 But to which of the angels has he ever said, "Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies as a footstool for your feet"?

14 Are not all ministering spirits sent for ministry for the sake of those who are about to inherit salvation?


## Chapter 2

1 Therefore we must give more earnest heed to the things that were heard, lest we let them slip.

2 For if the word spoken through angels happened as firm, and all transgression and disobedience took a just reward.

3 How shall we escape if we neglect so great a salvation, which, having taken its beginning to be spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard.

4 God also bearing witness with them by signs and wonders and various powers and by distributions of the Holy Spirit according to His will.

5 For it was not to angels that he subjected the world about which we speak, the one that is to come.

6 And someone testified somewhere, saying, "What is man that you remember him, or the Son of Man that you visit him?"

7 You made him a little lower than the angels; you crowned him with glory and honor and appointed him over the works of your hands.

8 You have subjected all things under his feet. For in subjecting all things to him, he left nothing unruly to him. But now we do not yet see all things subjected to him.

9 But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels, crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God He might taste death for everyone.

10 For it was fitting for him, for whom are all things and through whom are all things, in bringing many sons to glory, to perfect the pioneer of their salvation through sufferings.

11 For both the one who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all of one; for this cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers.

12 Saying, "I will report your name to my brothers; in the middle of the church I will sing a hymn to you."

13 And again, I will be persuaded in him; and again, see, I and the children whom God gave to me.

14 Since then the children share flesh and blood, he himself likewise partook of the same things, so that through death he might abolish the one who has the power of death, that is, the devil.

15 and deliver those who through fear of death were all their lives subject to bondage.

16 For surely he does not take hold of angels, but he takes hold of the seed of Abraham.

17 Therefore he had to be made like his brothers in every respect, so that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.

18 For in that he himself has suffered, having been tested, he is able to help those who are being tested.


## Chapter 3

1 Therefore, holy brothers, partners of a heavenly calling, consider the apostle and high priest of our confession, Christ Jesus.

2 Faithful to the one who made him, just as Moses was in all his house.

3 For this man has been counted worthy of much more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who prepared it has much more honor than the house.

4 For every house is prepared by someone, but the one who prepared all things is God.

5 And Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of the things that would be spoken.

6 but Christ was faithful as a Son over his house, whose house we are, if indeed we hold fast our boldness and the boasting of our hope firm to the end.

7 Therefore, just as the Holy Spirit says: "Today, if you hear his voice,

8 Do not harden your hearts as in the provocation, on the day of temptation in the wilderness.

9 Your fathers tested me, they examined me and saw my works for forty years.

10 Therefore I was grieved at that generation and said, 'They always go astray in their heart, and they did not know my ways.'

11 As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter my rest.

12 Look carefully, brothers, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.

13 But encourage one another each day, as long as it is called "Today," so that none of you may be hardened by sin's deceit.

14 For we have become partners of Christ, if indeed we hold fast the beginning of the substance firm until the end.

15 While it is said, "Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the provocation."

16 For some who heard provoked him, but not all who went out from Egypt through Moses.

17 But with whom was he grieved for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose carcasses fell in the wilderness?

18 And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, if not to those who disobeyed?

19 And we look that they were not able to enter because of unbelief.


## Chapter 4

1 Let us fear therefore, lest, while the promise of entering into his rest remains, any of you should seem to have failed to reach it.

2 All of us have had the gospel preached to us, just as they also did, but the word of hearing did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in those who heard.

3 For we who have believed enter into that rest, just as he has said, "As I swore in my wrath, 'They shall not enter into my rest,'" although the works were done from the foundation of the world.

4 For he has said somewhere about the seventh day thus: "And God rested on the seventh day from all his works."

5 And in this case again they will enter into my rest.

6 Therefore, since it remains for some to enter it, and those who formerly had the gospel preached to them did not enter because of disobedience.

7 Again He determines a certain day, "Today," in David, saying after so long a time, as it has been said, "Today, if you hear His voice, do not harden your hearts."

8 For if Jesus had caused them to rest, he would not be speaking afterward about another day.

9 Therefore, a Sabbath rest is left for the people of God.

10 For the one who entered his rest has himself also ceased from his works, just as God did from his own.

11 Therefore let us be diligent to enter that rest, so that no one may fall into the same example of disobedience.

12 For the word of God is living and effectual and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the division of soul and spirit, of joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

13 And there is no creation that is not manifest in his sight, but all things are naked and opened to the eyes of him to whom we must give an account.

14 Therefore, since we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us seize the confession.

15 For we do not have a high priest who is unable to have compassion on our weaknesses, but one who has been tested in all things in likeness, without sin.

16 Therefore let us come near with boldness to the throne of grace, so that we may receive mercy and find grace for timely help.


## Chapter 5

1 For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed on behalf of men in things relating to God, so that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.

2 He is able to have compassion on those who are ignorant and are deceived, since he himself is surrounded by weakness.

3 For this reason, he ought to offer sacrifices for sins, just as for the people, so also for himself.

4 And no one takes the honor for himself, but the one called by God, just as also Aaron was.

5 So also Christ did not glorify himself to become a high priest, but the one who spoke to him, "You are my Son; today I have begotten you."

6 just as he also says in another place, "You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchisedec."

7 who in the days of his flesh, having offered up both supplications and requests to the one able to save him from death with a strong cry and tears, and having been heard because of his fear.

8 Although being a Son, He learned obedience from what He suffered.

9 And having been perfected, he became the cause of eternal salvation to all who obey him.

10 having been called by God a high priest according to the order of Melchisedec

11 Concerning this, we have much to say and it is hard to be uttered in speaking, since you have become dull in your hearing.

12 For indeed, though you owe it to be teachers because of the time, you again have need of someone to teach you what are the rudiments of the beginning of the oracles of God, and you have happened to have need of milk and not of steadfast food.
