13 For all who partake of milk are unskilful in the word of righteousness, for he is a child.

14 But solid food is for the perfect, for those who have their senses trained by practice to discern good and evil.


## Chapter 6

1 Therefore, having left the word of the beginning of Christ, let us be borne toward perfection, not casting down again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God.

2 of the teaching about washings, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.

3 And this we will do if indeed God permits.

4 For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, having tasted the heavenly gift and having become partners of the Holy Spirit,

5 and who have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come.

6 and when they have fallen away, to renew them again to repentance, while they crucify afresh the Son of God for themselves and make a public example of him.

7 The earth that drinks the rain that often comes upon it and bears fit herbs for those for whose sake it is also dressed partakes of blessing from God.

8 Bringing forth thorns and briers, it is reprobate and near a curse, whose end is into burning.

9 But we are persuaded of better things concerning you, beloved, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak this way.

10 For God is not unrighteous so as to forget your work and the labor of the love that you showed toward his name, having served the saints and continuing to serve them.

11 But we desire each of you to show the same diligence for the assurance of hope until the end.

12 so that you may not become dull, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherit the promises.

13 For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater to swear by, he swore by himself.

14 Saying, "Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you."

15 And so, having been patient, he obtained the promise.

16 For people indeed swear by the greater, and the oath is the end of all opposition for confirmation to them.

17 In which God, desiring more abundantly to show to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable nature of his counsel, confirmed it with an oath.

18 so that by two unchangeable matters in which it is impossible for God to lie we might have strong comfort, who have fled to seize the hope set before us.

19 We have this anchor of the soul, both certain and firm, which enters into the inner place of the veil.

20 where a forerunner entered for us, Jesus, having become a high priest forever according to the order of Melchisedec.


## Chapter 7

1 For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him.

2 To whom also Abraham divided a tenth from all things, being first interpreted "king of righteousness," and then also "king of Salem," which is "king of peace."

3 Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God, he remains a priest perpetually.

4 Behold how great this man was, to whom even Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth out of the spoils.

5 And those from among the sons of Levi who take the office of the priesthood have a commandment to tithe the people according to the law, that is, their brothers, even though they have gone out from the loin of Abraham.

6 But the one not counting by descent from them has paid tithes to Abraham and has blessed the one having the promises.

7 But without any opposition, the lesser is blessed by the better.

8 And here mortal men receive tithes, but there one receives them of whom it is testified that he lives.

9 So to say, through Abraham even Levi, who takes the tenth, has paid tithes.

10 For he was still in the loins of his father when Melchisedec met him.

11 If then perfection were through the Levitical priesthood—for the people were established under it—what further need that another priest should rise according to the order of Melchisedec, and not be said according to the order of Aaron?

12 For when the priesthood is removed, of necessity there also happens a change of the law.

13 for he of whom these things are said partook of another tribe, from which no one has attended at the altar.

14 For it is evident that our Lord has risen out of Judah, a tribe about which Moses spoke nothing concerning the priesthood.

15 And it is even far more abundant evident if according to the likeness of Melchisedec there rises another priest.

16 who has become a priest not according to the law of a fleshly commandment but according to the power of an endless life.

17 For he testifies, "You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchisedec."

18 For there is an annulling of the former commandment because of its weakness and uselessness.

19 For the law perfected no one, but a bringing in of a better hope is, through which we draw near to God.

20 And by as much as it was not without an oath, for on the one hand those have become priests without an oath,

21 But he with an oath through the one saying to him, "The Lord swore and will not regret: 'You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchisedec.'"

22 Jesus has become the surety of a better covenant.

23 And on the one hand the much ones have become priests, being forbidden to abide because of death.

24 But because he remains forever, he has an unchangeable priesthood.

25 Therefore he is also able to save completely those who come near to God through him, since he always lives to intercede for them.

26 For such a high priest was fitting for us, holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and has become higher than the heavens.

27 who does not have a daily necessity, as the high priests do, to offer up sacrifices first for their own sins, then for those of the people; for this he did once for all when he offered up himself.

28 For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath, which came after the law, appoints a Son, who has been made perfect forever.


## Chapter 8

1 Now the main point of what is being said is this: we have such a high priest, who sat down at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens.

2 a minister of the holy things and of the true tabernacle which the Lord pitched, not man.

3 For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; therefore it is necessary for this one also to have something to offer.

4 For if he were on earth, he would not even be a priest, since there are priests who offer the gifts according to the law.

5 who serve a pattern and shadow of the heavenly things, just as Moses was warned when he was about to accomplish the tabernacle: for "See," he says, "that you do all things according to the pattern that was shown to you on the mountain."

6 But now he has obtained an excellent ministry, inasmuch as he is the mediator of a better covenant, which has been established on better promises.

7 For if that first had been blameless, no place would have been sought for a second.

8 Finding fault with them, he says, "See, days are coming, says the Lord, and I will complete a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah."

9 not according to the covenant which I made with their Fathers in the day when I took hold of their hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue in my covenant, and I neglected them, says the Lord.

10 For this is the covenant which I will appoint to the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord: giving my laws into their mind, and on their hearts I will write them; and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people.

11 And each shall not teach his neighbor, and each his brother, saying, Know the Lord, for all will know me, from their small to their great.

12 For I will be merciful towards their unrighteousness, and I will by no means remember their sins and their lawlessness any longer.

13 By calling it new, he has made the first decay; now that which is decaying and growing old is near disappearance.


## Chapter 9

1 Now the first tabernacle also had ordinances of worship and the worldly holy place.

2 For a tabernacle was prepared, the first, in which were the lampstand and the table and the showbread, which is called holy.

3 Now after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies.

4 having a golden censer and the ark of the covenant covered on every side with gold, in which was a golden pot having the manna and Aaron's rod that sprouted and the tablets of the covenant.

5 Far above it were cherubims of glory overshadowing the mercyseat; concerning which it is not now to speak in particular.

6 Now when these things were thus prepared, the priests always go into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the worship services.

7 But into the second only the high priest goes once a year, not without blood, which he offers for himself and for the errors of the people.

8 The Holy Spirit was declaring that the way of the holy ones had not yet been revealed while the first tabernacle still had its standing.

9 This parable refers to the present time during which gifts and sacrifices are offered, which are not able to perfect the conscience of the one who serves.

10 consisting only of foods and drinks and various washings and ordinances of the flesh, imposed until the time of reformation.

11 But Christ, having come as a high priest of the good things that are about to come, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle not made by hands (that is, not of this creation).

12 nor through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, he entered once for all into the holy places, having found eternal redemption.

13 For if the blood of bulls and goats and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who are defiled sanctifies for the purification of the flesh.

14 how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself blameless to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God.

15 And because of this, he is the mediator of a new covenant, so that, a death having happened for the redemption of the transgressions under the first covenant, those who have been called may take the promise of the eternal inheritance.

16 For where a covenant is, there is a necessity to bear the death of the one who appointed it.

17 For a covenant is firm over dead people, since it is never able to be enforced while the one who appointed it lives.

18 Hence not even the first was consecrated apart from blood.

19 For after every commandment had been spoken according to the law by Moses to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people.

20 saying, "This is the blood of the covenant that God commanded for you."

21 And likewise he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry.

22 And almost all things are cleansed with blood according to the law, and without bloodshed forgiveness does not happen.

23 Therefore it was necessary for the copies of the things in the heavens to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

24 For Christ did not enter a holy place made by hands, an antitype of the true one, but into heaven itself, now to be manifested in the presence of God for us.

25 not that he should often offer himself, just as the high priest enters into the holy places year by year with the blood of another.

26 Then He must have often suffered from the foundation of the world, but now once at the end of the ages He has been revealed to put away sin through His sacrifice.

27 And just as it is laid up for men to die once, and after this, judgment.

28 So Christ, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, apart from sin, to those who wait for him for salvation.


## Chapter 10

1 For the law, having a shadow of the good things about to come, not the image itself of the matters, can never, by the same sacrifices which they offer continually year by year, perfect those who come near.

2 Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, once purged, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?

3 But in these sacrifices there is a remembrance of sins every year.

4 It is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.

5 Therefore, when entering into the world, he says: "Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you restored for me."

6 You were not pleased with burnt offerings and offerings for sin.

7 Then I said, "See, I have come; in the volume of a book it is written concerning me, to do your will, O God."

8 Above, saying, "Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and (offerings) for sin you have not desired nor been pleased with"—which are offered according to the law.

9 Then he says, "See, I come to do your will, God." He kills the first in order that the second may stand.

10 By that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

11 And every priest stands ministering day after day and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.

12 But He, having offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, sat down at the right hand of God.

13 For the remaining time, waiting until his enemies are put as a footstool for his feet.

14 For by one offering He has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.

15 The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after foretelling,

16 This is the covenant that I will appoint with them after those days, says the Lord: giving my laws on their hearts, and on their minds I will write them.

17 And their sins and their lawlessness I will remember no more.

18 But where forgiveness of these things is, there is no longer an offering for sin.

19 Therefore, brothers, having boldness for the entrance into the holy places by the blood of Jesus,

20 which he consecrated for us as a new and living way through the veil, that is, his flesh.

21 and a great priest over the house of God

22 Let us come near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with clean water.

23 Let us hold fast the unwavering confession of hope, for the one who promised is faithful.

24 And let us consider one another for the contention of love and good works.

25 not forsaking our own assembling together, as is the custom of some, but encouraging one another, and so much more as you look, seeing the day drawing near.

26 For if we wilfully sin after taking the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins.

27 But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery zeal that is about to eat the adversaries.

28 Anyone who rejected the law of Moses dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.

29 How much worse punishment do you suppose they will be counted worthy of who have trampled the Son of God underfoot, and have considered the blood of the covenant by which they were sanctified as a common thing, and have insulted the Spirit of grace?

30 For we know the one who said, "Vengeance is mine; I will repay," says the Lord, and again, "The Lord will judge his people."

31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

32 But remember the former days in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great fight of sufferings.

33 On one hand, being made a gazing stock by reproaches and tribulations; on the other, having become partners of those who conducted themselves in such a way.

34 For you also had compassion on my bonds and accepted the extortion of your existing goods with joy, knowing that you have for yourselves a better and abiding possession in the heavens.

35 Do not cast away therefore your boldness, which has a great reward.

36 For you have need of patience, so that after doing the will of God you may receive the promise.

37 For yet a little while, the one who is coming will come and will not delay.

38 But the righteous will live by faith, and if he draws back, my soul is not pleased with him.

39 But we are not those who draw back to destruction, but of faith to the possession of the soul.


## Chapter 11

1 Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of matters not seen.

2 For in this the elders were testified of.

3 By faith we understand that the ages were framed by the word of God, so that what is looked at did not happen from what appears.

4 By faith Abel offered a greater sacrifice than Cain to God, through which he was testified to be righteous, God testifying concerning his gifts, and through it, though he died, he still speaks.

5 By faith Enoch was removed so as not to see death, and he was not found, because God removed him; for before his change he was testified to have pleased God.

6 Without faith it is impossible to please him, for the one who comes near to God must believe that he exists and that he becomes a rewarder to those who seek him out.

7 By faith Noah, having been warned about things not yet seen, moved with fear, prepared an ark for the salvation of his household; through which he condemned the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes by faith.

8 By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed by going out to the place that he was about to receive as an inheritance, and he went out, not knowing where he was going.

9 By faith he sojourned in the land of promise as in a foreign country, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the fellow heirs of the same promise.

10 For he was waiting for the city that has foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

11 By faith also Sara herself received power for the foundation of seed, and she bore a child beyond the time of age, since she considered the one who had promised faithful.

12 Therefore also from one man they were born (even this man being as good as dead), just as the stars of heaven in multitude and as the sand by the lip of the sea which is innumerable.

13 According to faith, all these died, not having taken the promises, but saw them afar off and were persuaded and greeted them, and confessed that they are strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

14 For those who say such things manifest that they are seeking a homeland.

15 And if indeed they were remembering that place from which they went out, they would have had time to return.

16 But now they desire a better one, that is, a heavenly; therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.

17 By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested, and the one who received the promises was offering his only begotten son.

18 It was spoken to him, "In Isaac your seed will be called."

19 He reckoned that God was able to raise even from the dead, and therefore he also received him back in a figure.

20 By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things about to come.

21 By faith Jacob, as he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

22 By faith Joseph, when he was dying, remembered about the departure of the Sons of Israel, and gave a command concerning his bones.

23 By faith Moses, when he was born, was hidden three months by his fathers, because they saw the child was fair, and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.

24 By faith, Moses, after he had become great, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter.

25 having chosen rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to have the temporary enjoyment of sin.

26 he considered the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking to the reward.

27 By faith he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king, for he endured as seeing the invisible one.

28 By faith he performed the Passover and the sprinkling of the blood, so that the destroyer of the firstborn might not touch them.

29 By faith they came over the Red Sea as through dry land, of which the Egyptians, taking an assaying, were swallowed up.

30 By faith, the walls of Jericho fell after being surrounded for seven days.

31 By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.

32 And what more shall I say? For the time will fail me to declare of Gedeon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.

33 Those who through faith subdued kingdoms, worked righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions.

34 They quenched the power of fire, fled the mouth of the sword, were strengthened from weakness, became strong in war, and bowed down camps of strangers.

35 Women took their dead by resurrection, but others were tortured, not accepting the redemption, so that they might obtain a better resurrection.

36 And others took an assaying of mockings and scourges, and further, of bonds and prison.

37 They were stoned, they were sawed asunder, were tempted, died by murder with the sword; they went about in sheepskins, in goat skins, being destitute, afflicted, mistreated.

38 The world was not worthy of them; they wandered in wildernesses and mountains and caves and in the holes of the earth.

39 And all of these, having been testified to through faith, did not receive the promise.

40 God having provided something better concerning us, so that they would not be perfected apart from us.


## Chapter 12

1 Therefore, since we also have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us, let us lay aside every weight and the easily entangling sin, and let us run with patience the contest set before us.

2 looking to Jesus, the pioneer and finisher of faith, who for the joy set before him endured a cross, despising shame, and sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.

3 For consider him who has endured such opposition from sinners against himself, so that you may not faint in your souls, becoming weary.

4 You have not yet resisted to the point of blood, striving against sin.

5 And you have forgotten the comfort which reasons with you as with sons: "My son, do not despise the discipline of the Lord, nor faint when rebuked by him."

6 For whom the Lord loves, He disciplines, and He scourges every son whom He receives.

7 If you endure discipline, God offers it to you as to sons; for what son is there whom a father does not discipline?

8 If you are without discipline, of which all have become partners, then you are bastards and not sons.

9 We have had our fathers of the flesh as those who corrected us, and we respected them; shall we not much more submit to the Father of spirits and live?

10 For they disciplined us for a few days as seemed best to them, but he disciplines us for our profit, so that we may partake of his holiness.

11 All discipline for the present does not seem to be joy but sorrow, yet later it repays the peaceable fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.

12 Therefore, lift up the hands that hang down and the knees that are paralyzed.

13 and make straight paths for your feet, so that the lame limb may not be turned away, but rather be healed.

14 Pursue peace with all, and the sanctification without which no one will see the Lord.

15 Looking diligently lest anyone be lacking the grace of God, lest any root of bitterness growing up above cause trouble and by it many become defiled.

16 Let no one be a fornicator or profane like Esau, who for one meal sold his birthright.

17 For you know that even afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for repentance, though he sought it out with tears.

18 For you have not come near a mountain that may be felt after and that burns with fire, and blackness, and darkness, and tempest.

19 And the sound of a trumpet and the voice of words, which those who heard refused that no word should be added to them.

20 For they could not bear what was commanded: if even a beast touches the mountain, it will be stoned or shot through with arrows.

21 And so terrible was the appearing that Moses said, I am sore afraid and trembling.

22 But you have come near to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to myriads of angels.

23 to a general assembly and church of the firstborn who are registered in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.

24 and a mediator of a new covenant, Jesus, and by the blood of sprinkling that speaks better than that of Abel.

25 Look that you do not refuse the one who speaks. For if those did not escape when they refused the one who warned them on earth, much more we who turn away from the one who warns from heaven.

26 whose voice then shook the earth; but now he has promised, saying, "Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also heaven."

27 But the phrase "Yet once more" declares the removal of the things being shaken, as of things that have been done so that the things not shaken may remain.

28 Therefore, receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, through which we may serve God acceptably with reverence and fear.

29 For our God is a consuming fire.


## Chapter 13

1 Let brotherly love remain.

2 Do not forget to entertain strangers, for by this some have entertained angels without knowing it.

3 Remember the prisoners as being bound with them, and those who suffer adversity as being yourselves also in the body.

4 The wedding is to be held in honor among all, and the bed undefiled; but God will judge fornicators and adulterers.

5 Let your way be without covetousness, being satisfied with the present; for he has said, "I will never let go of you, and I will never forsake you."

6 So we can confidently say, "The Lord is my helper; I will not fear. What can man do to me?"

7 Remember those who lead you, who spoke the word of God to you; beholding the end of their conduct, imitate their faith.

8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today, and for the ages.

9 Do not be carried about by various and strange teachings, for it is good for the heart to be confirmed by grace, not by foods, in which those who walked were not profited.

10 We have an altar from which those who serve the tabernacle have no authority to eat.

11 For of those living creatures whose blood is brought into the holy places for sin by the high priest, their bodies are burned up outside the camp.

12 Therefore Jesus also suffered outside the gate in order to sanctify the people through his own blood.

13 Therefore, let us go out to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach.

14 For we do not have here a city that remains, but we are seeking the one that is about to come.

15 Through him then let us offer up a sacrifice of praise always to God, that is, the fruit of lips confessing his name.

16 Do not forget to do good and fellowship, for with such sacrifices God is pleased.

17 Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they keep watch over your souls as those who will give an account, so that they may do this with joy and not with groaning, for this is unprofitable to you.

18 Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience in all things, wanting to conduct ourselves well.

19 But I especially encourage you to do this, that I may be restored to you more quickly.

20 Now may the God of peace, who led up from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep by the blood of the eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus.

21 Restore you in every good work to do his will, doing in you what is acceptable before him through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.

22 I encourage you, brothers, bear with the word of comfort, for I wrote to you in few words.

23 Know the brother Timothy as having been released, with whom if he comes more quickly I will see you.

24 Greet all of your leading ones and all the holy ones. Those from Italy greet you.

25 Grace be with you all. Amen. [It was written to the Hebrews from Italy through Timothy.]


# JAMES

## Chapter 1

1 James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes in the Diaspora: Rejoice.

2 Consider it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations.

3 knowing that the trial of your faith works patience.

4 But let patience have its perfect work, so that you may be perfect and entire, lacking in nothing.

5 But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all generously and without reproach, and it will be given to him.

6 But let him ask in faith, without doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.

7 For that man must not suppose that he will take anything from the Lord.

8 A double minded man is unstable in all his ways.

9 Let the humble brother boast in his high position.

10 The rich man in his humiliation, because like a flower of grass he will pass away.

11 For the sun rises with the burning heat and withers the grass, and its flower falls away, and the beauty of its appearance is destroyed; so also the rich man will fade away in his pursuits.

12 Blessed is the man who endures temptation, because when he has become approved he will take the crown of life which the Lord promised to those who love him.

13 No one who is being tested should say, "I am being tested by God," for God cannot be tempted by evil, and He Himself tests no one.

14 But each one is tested when he is drawn away and allured by his own desire.

15 Then desire, having seized, bears sin; and sin, when finished, begets death.

16 Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers.

17 Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, going down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variableness or shadow of turning.

18 Having willed it, he begot us by the word of truth, so that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.

19 Therefore, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath.

20 For the wrath of a man does not work the righteousness of God.

21 Therefore, putting aside all turpitude and abundance of malice, receive in meekness the engrafted word which is able to save your souls.

22 Become doers of the word, and not only hearers, beguiling yourselves.

23 For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man considering his natural face in a mirror.

24 For he considered himself, departed, and immediately forgot what kind he was.

25 But the one who stoops to look into the perfect law of freedom and abides, having not become a forgetful hearer but a doer of work, this one will be blessed in his deed.

26 If anyone seems to be religious among you, not bridling his tongue but deceiving his heart, this person's religion is vain.

27 Religion that is clean and undefiled before God and the Father is this: to visit the comfortless and widows in their tribulation, to keep oneself spotless from the world.


## Chapter 2

1 My brothers, do not have the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the glory, with respect of persons.

2 For if a man with a gold ring in bright clothing enters your synagogue, and a poor man in vile clothing also enters,

3 and you look upon the one who wears the bright clothing and say to him, "Sit here well," and to the poor person you say, "Stand there" or "Sit here under my footstool."

4 And did you not discern within yourselves and became judges of evil reasoning?

5 Listen, my beloved brothers: did not God choose the poor of this world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom which He promised to those who love Him?

6 But you dishonored the poor. Do not the rich oppress you and themselves draw you into courts?

7 Don't they blaspheme the good name that was called upon you?

8 However, if you finish the royal law according to the Scripture, "You shall love your neighbor as yourself," you do well.

9 If you have respect to persons, you work sin, being rebuked by the law as transgressors.

10 For whoever keeps the whole law but stumbles in one point has become guilty of all.

11 For the one who said, "Do not commit adultery," also said, "Do not murder." Now if you do not commit adultery but you murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.

12 So speak and so do as those who are about to be judged by the law of freedom.

13 For judgment is without mercy to the one who does not do mercy; mercy boasts against judgment.

14 What is the profit, my brothers, if someone says he has faith but does not have works? That faith is not able to save him.

15 But if a brother or a sister exist naked and are lacking daily food,

16 If one of you says to them, "Depart in peace, be warmed and satisfied," but does not give them the things which are needful for the body, what is the profit?

17 So also faith, if it does not have works, is dead by itself.

18 But someone will say, you have faith, and I have works; show me your faith by your works, and I will show you by my works my faith.

19 You believe that God is one; you do well. Even the demons believe and tremble.

20 Do you want to know, O vain man, that faith without works is dead?

21 Was not Abraham our Father justified by works when he offered up Isaac his Son upon the altar?

22 You look that the faith was working together with his works, and from the works the faith was perfected.

23 And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, "Abraham believed God, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness," and he was called a friend of God.

24 You see, then, that a man is justified by works and not by faith alone.

25 Likewise, was not Rahab the prostitute also justified by works when she received the messengers and sent them out by another way?

26 Just as the body without the Spirit is dead, so also faith without works is dead.


## Chapter 3

1 My brothers, do not be many teachers, knowing that we will take a greater judgment.

2 For we all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in word, this man is perfect, able to bridle the whole body as well.

3 See, we cast bridles into the mouths of horses for them to obey us, and we turn about their whole body.

4 See also the boats, though they are so great and are driven by hard winds, are turned about by a very small rudder wherever the impulse of the helmsman wills.

5 So also the tongue is a small member and boasts great things. See how great a wood a small fire kindles.

6 And the tongue is a fire, a world of unrighteousness. Thus the tongue is appointed among our members, defiling the whole body and setting on fire the course of generation, and is set on fire by hell.

7 All nature—both of beasts and of birds, of reptiles and of things in the sea—is tamed and has been tamed by human nature.

8 No one among men is able to tame the tongue, an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.

9 With it we bless God and Father, and with it we curse men who have been made after the similitude of God.

10 From the same mouth come blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to happen this way.

11 Does a spring send forth sweet and bitter from the same cave?

12 My brothers, a fig tree is not able to do olive trees, or a vine figs; likewise, no spring is able to do salt and sweet water.

13 Who is wise and endued with knowledge among you? Let him show his works by good conduct in the meekness of wisdom.

14 But if you have bitter zeal and strife in your hearts, do not boast and lie against the truth.

15 This wisdom is not the wisdom that goes down from above, but is earthly, natural, devilish.

16 For where zeal and strife are, there is disorder and every evil matter.

17 But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, easy to be intreated, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and unfeigned.

18 And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who do peace.


## Chapter 4

1 Where do wars and strife come from among you? Is it not from your pleasures that serve as soldiers in your members?

2 You desire and do not have. You murder and are zealous and are not able to obtain. You strive and wage war. You do not have because you do not ask.

3 You ask and do not take, because you ask wrongly, so that you may spend it on your pleasures.

4 Adulterers and adulteresses, do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Therefore whoever wills to be a friend of the world appoints himself an enemy of God.

5 Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, "The Spirit who dwells in us longs with envy"?

6 But he gives greater grace; therefore it says, "God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble."

7 Submit therefore to God; resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

8 Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.

9 Be afflicted, and mourn, and weep; let your laughter be turned into mourning, and your joy into heaviness.

10 Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.

11 Do not slander one another, brothers. The one who slanders a brother and judges his brother slanders the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.

12 There is one lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy; but who are you who judges the other?

13 Come now, you who say, "Today or tomorrow we will go into this city, and we will spend a year there, and we will trade and make a profit."

14 You do not know what will happen tomorrow. For what is your life? It is a vapour that appears for a little while and then vanishes.

15 Instead of you saying, "If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that."

16 But now you boast in your boastings; all such boasting is evil.

17 The one who knows the good to do and does not do it, to him it is sin.


## Chapter 5

1 Come now, you rich, weep, howling over your miseries that are coming upon you.

2 Your riches have become corrupted, and your garments have become motheaten.

3 Your gold and your silver have cankered, and their rust will be a testimony against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have treasured up treasure in the last days.

4 See, the reward of the workers who reaped your regions—the one defrauded by you—cries out, and the cries of those who reaped have entered into the ears of the Lord Sabaoth.

5 You have lived in pleasure on the earth and lived in luxury; you have nourished your hearts as in a day of slaughter.

6 You have condemned, you have murdered the righteous; he does not resist you.

7 Therefore be patient, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. See, the husbandman waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient over it until it takes the early and latter rain.

8 Be patient and strengthen your hearts, because the coming of the Lord has drawn near.

9 Do not groan against one another, brothers, so that you may not be condemned; see, the Judge stands before the doors.

10 Take, my brothers, an example of affliction and patience from the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.

11 See, we call blessed those who endure. You have heard of the patience of Job, and you have seen the end of the Lord, that the Lord is very pitiful and merciful.

12 But above all, my brothers, do not swear, neither by heaven nor by earth nor by any other oath, but let your yes be yes and your no be no, so that you may not fall into hypocrisy.

13 Let anyone among you who endures hardship pray; let anyone who is cheerful sing psalms.

14 Is anyone among you weak? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.

15 And the prayer of faith will make well the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up; and if he has been committing sins, it will be forgiven him.

16 Confess your trespasses to one another and pray for one another so that you may be healed; the supplication of a righteous person works powerfully.

17 Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain, and it did not rain on the earth for three years and six months.

18 And again he prayed, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth sprouted its fruit.

19 Brothers, if anyone among you is deceived from the truth and someone turns him back,

20 Let him know that the one who turns back a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.


# 1 PETER

## Chapter 1

1 Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen strangers of the diaspora in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.

2 According to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in the sanctification of the Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: grace and peace be multiplied to you.

3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his much mercy has begotten us again into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.

4 into an inheritance incorruptible and undefiled and that fades not away, having been kept in heavens for us

5 those who are being kept by the power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.

6 In this you rejoice, though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been grieved by various temptations.

7 so that the trial of your faith, which is much more precious than gold that perishes, though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise and honor and glory at the revelation of Jesus Christ.

8 Whom not having seen you love, and in whom now not seeing, yet believing, you rejoice with joy unspeakable and glorified.

9 receiving the end of your faith, the salvation of souls.

10 Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace that would come to you sought out and searched diligently.

11 Searching for what time or what sort of time the Spirit of Christ within them was declaring, testifying beforehand about the sufferings belonging to Christ and the glories that would follow these things.

12 It was revealed to them that they were not serving themselves but you in these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven — things into which angels desire to stoop to look.

13 Therefore, girding up the loins of your mind, being completely sober, set your hope fully on the grace that is being brought to you in the revelation of Jesus Christ.

14 As children of obedience, not being conformed to your former desires in your ignorance.

15 But as the one who called you is holy, you also be holy in all your conduct.

16 For it is written, "Be holy, because I am holy."

17 If you call upon the Father, who without respect of persons judges according to each one's work, conduct yourselves in fear during the time of your sojourning.

18 knowing that you were redeemed from your vain conduct received by tradition from fathers, not with corruptible silver or gold.

19 but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot.

20 Foreknown before the foundation of the world, but revealed in the last times for your sake.

21 you who through him believe in God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.

22 Having purified your souls in your obedience to the truth through the Spirit for unfeigned brotherly love, love one another fervently from a clean heart.

23 having been begotten, not from corruptible seed but from incorruptible, through the living and abiding word of God into the age.

24 Because all flesh is like grass, and all the glory of man is like the flower of grass—the grass withers, and its flower falls away.

25 And the word of the Lord remains forever; and this is the word that was preached to you.


## Chapter 2

1 Therefore, putting away all malice and all deceit and hypocrisy and envy and all backbiting,

2 As new born infants, long for the sincere, reasonable milk, so that by it you may grow.

3 if you have tasted that the Lord is kind.

4 coming near to a living stone, rejected indeed by men but chosen and honored with God.

5 You yourselves, as living stones, are being built into a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

6 For it also contains in Scripture, "See, I put in Zion a stone, a chosen and honored cornerstone, and the one who believes on him will never be put to shame.

7 Therefore, the honor is for you who believe, but for those who disobey, "The stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner."

8 a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense; they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were put.

9 But you are a chosen kind, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for possession, so that you may show forth the virtues of the one who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.

10 Once you were not a people, but now you are the people of God; you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy.

11 Beloved, I encourage you as foreigners and strangers to abstain from fleshly desires which serve as soldiers against the soul.

12 Have your conduct good among the nations, so that when they slander you as evildoers, having observed your good works, they may glorify God on the day of visitation.

13 Submit, then, to every human institution for the sake of the Lord, whether to a king as one surpassing,

14 whether to governors as those sent through him for vengeance on evildoers and praise to them that do well.

15 For this is the will of God, that by doing good you muzzle the ignorance of foolish men.

16 as free people, and not having your freedom as a cloak for malice, but as servants of God.

17 Honor all people, love the brotherhood, fear God, honor the king.

18 Servants, submit yourselves to your masters with all fear, not only to the good and gentle, but also to the crooked.

19 For this is grace, if for the sake of conscience toward God someone bears sorrows while suffering wrongfully.

20 For what renown is there if, sinning and being struck, you endure? But if, doing good and suffering, you endure, this is grace before God.

21 For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving us an example, so that you might follow in his steps.

22 He committed no sin, nor was deceit found in his mouth.

23 who, when he was reviled, did not revile in return; when he suffered, he did not threaten, but entrusted himself to him who judges justly.

24 He himself bore our sins in his body on the wood, so that, being dead to sins, we might live to righteousness; by whose stripe you were healed.

25 For you were like sheep being deceived, but now you have turned back to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.


## Chapter 3

1 Likewise, wives submit to your own husbands, so that even if some disobey the word, they may be gained without a word through the conduct of their wives.

2 They have beheld your pure conduct in fear.

3 Let your adornment not be the outward adorning of plaiting hair and wearing gold or putting on garments.

4 but the secret man of the heart, in the incorruptible adornment of a meek and peaceable spirit, which is very costly in the sight of God.

5 For in this way also the holy women who hoped in God used to adorn themselves, submitting to their own husbands.

6 As Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, you have become her children when you do good and without fearing any terror.

7 Husbands, likewise, live with your wives according to knowledge, giving honor to the wife as to the weaker vessel, as being fellow-heirs of the grace of life, so that your prayers may not be cut off.

8 And finally, all of you be of one mind, having compassion one of another, love as brothers, be tenderhearted, be courteous.

9 Not repaying evil for evil or railing for railing, but contrariwise blessing, knowing that you were called for this purpose, that you might inherit a blessing.

10 for the one who wants to love life and see good days, let him cease his tongue from evil and his lips from speaking deceit.

11 Let him avoid evil and do good; let him seek peace and pursue it.

12 For the eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are open to their supplication; but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil.

13 And who will harm you if you become imitators of the good?

14 But even if you should suffer for righteousness, you are blessed; do not fear their fear, nor be troubled.

15 Sanctify the Lord God in your hearts, and always be ready for a defense to all who ask you a word concerning the hope that is in you, with meekness and fear.

16 having a good conscience, so that in the matter in which they slander you as evildoers, those who mistreat your good conduct in Christ may be shamed.

17 For it is better, if the will of God should will it, to suffer while doing good than while doing evil.

18 For Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, so that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh but given life in the Spirit.

19 In which also he went and preached to the spirits in prison.

20 who formerly disobeyed when the patience of God was waiting once in the days of Noah while the ark was being prepared, in which a few—that is, eight souls—were saved through water.

21 Baptism, which corresponds to this, now saves you—not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the pledge of a good conscience toward God, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

22 who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, with angels and authorities and powers having been subjected to him.


## Chapter 4

1 Since therefore Christ suffered in the flesh for us, you also arm yourselves with the same intent, because the one who has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin.

2 so to no longer live the rest of your time in the flesh for human desires, but for the will of God.

3 For the time that has passed away is sufficient for us to have worked the will of the nations, having gone in licentiousness, desires, excess of wine, revelries, banqueting, and unlawful idolatries.

4 In this they are entertained, since you do not run together with them into the same excess of debauchery, blaspheming.

5 They will repay the word to the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.

6 For this reason also the gospel was preached to the dead, so that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the Spirit.

7 The end of all things has drawn near; therefore be sober-minded and watchful in prayers.

8 Above all, have earnest love among yourselves, for love will cover a multitude of sins.

9 Be hospitable to one another without grumbling.

10 Each as they have taken a gift, serving it among themselves as good stewards of the various grace of God.

11 If anyone speaks, let it be as oracles of God; if anyone serves, let it be as from the strength that God gives, so that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.

12 Beloved, do not be surprised at the fiery trial among you happening for your testing, as if a stranger were happening to you.

13 But insofar as you share in the sufferings of Christ, rejoice, so that also at the revelation of his glory you may rejoice with great joy.

14 If you are reproached in the name of Christ, you are blessed, because the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you; on the one hand He is blasphemed according to them, but on the other hand He is glorified according to you.

15 For let none of you suffer as a murderer or a thief or an evildoer or as a busybody.

16 But if as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but let him glorify God in this matter.

17 For the time has come for judgment to begin from the house of God; and if it begins with us first, what will be the end of those who disobey the gospel of God?

18 And if the righteous is scarcely saved, where will the ungodly and sinner appear?

19 Therefore let those who suffer according to the will of God also commit their souls to a faithful Creator in well-doing.


## Chapter 5

1 I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow presbyter and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and also a partner in the glory that is about to be revealed.

2 Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, looking diligently not by constraint but wilfully, nor yet for filthy lucre's sake but willingly.

3 Not as lording over the lot, but becoming patterns for the flock.

4 And when the chief shepherd has been revealed, you will receive the crown of glory that fadeth not away.

5 Likewise, younger people, submit to elders. And all of you, submitting to one another, be clothed with humility, because God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble.

6 Humble yourselves, therefore, under the powerful hand of God, so that He may exalt you at the proper time.

7 Cast all your anxiety upon him, because he cares for you.

8 Be sober, watch, because your adversary the devil walks about as a roaring lion, seeking someone to swallow.

9 Resist him, steadfast in the faith, knowing that the same sufferings are accomplished in your brotherhood throughout the world.

10 and the God of all grace, who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, will himself restore, strengthen, confirm, and establish you.

11 To him be glory and power for the ages of the ages. Amen.

12 Through Silvanus, a faithful brother as I reckon, I have written to you in few words, encouraging and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.

13 She who is in Babylon, elected together with you, greets you, and Mark my Son.

14 Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace be to all of you who are in Christ Jesus. Amen. [Peter's first catholic epistle.]


# 2 PETER

## Chapter 1

1 Simeon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have obtained a faith of like preciousness with us through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ.

2 Grace and peace be multiplied to you in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.

3 as all of his divine power has given to us the things pertaining to life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by glory and virtue.

4 Through these he has given to us his great and precious promises, so that through them you may become partners of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world by desire.

5 And for this very reason, giving all diligence, supply to your faith virtue, and to virtue knowledge.

6 and in knowledge, self-control; and in self-control, patience; and in patience, godliness.

7 And in godliness, brotherly love; and in brotherly love, love.

8 For if these things exist and abound in you, they appoint you neither idle nor unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.

9 For if these things are not present, he is blind, unable to see far off, having taken forgetfulness of the purification of his long-ago sins.

10 Therefore, brothers, be all the more diligent to make your calling and election firm, for by doing these things you will never stumble.

11 For in this way the entrance into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ will be abundantly supplied to you.

12 Therefore, I will not neglect to always remind you of these things, although you know and are strengthened in the present truth.

13 But I consider it right, as long as I am in this tabernacle, to arouse you by remembrance.

14 knowing that the putting away of my tabernacle is shortly to come, just as our Lord Jesus Christ also declared to me.

15 And I will also be diligent always for you to have the remembrance of these things after my decease.

16 For we did not make known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ by following cleverly devised myths, but we became eyewitnesses of his majesty.

17 For having received honor and glory from God the Father, a voice such as this having been borne to him by the excellent glory, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."

18 And we heard this voice borne from heaven when we were with him on the holy mountain.

19 And we have the more firm word of prophecy, to which you do well to give heed as to a lamp appearing in a dark place, until the day dawns and the day star rises in your hearts.

20 First of all, know this: no prophecy of Scripture comes from one's own interpretation.

21 For prophecy was never borne by the will of man, but holy men of God spoke as they were borne along by the Holy Spirit.


## Chapter 2

1 But false prophets also arose among the people, just as false teachers will be among you, who will privily bring in destructive sects, denying the master who bought them, bringing upon themselves swift destruction.

2 And many will follow their destruction, because of whom the way of the truth will be blasphemed.

3 And in greed they will buy and sell you with feigned words, whose judgment of a long time does not linger, and their destruction does not slumber.

4 For if God did not spare angels when they sinned, but cast them down to hell, delivering them in chains of darkness to be kept for judgment.

5 and did not spare the old world, but kept Noah, the eighth, a preacher of righteousness, bringing a flood upon the world of the ungodly.

6 and turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to ashes, condemned them to overthrow, having made them an example of those who are about to act ungodly.

7 and he delivered righteous Lot, who was oppressed by the conduct of the wicked in licentiousness.

8 For by seeing and hearing, the righteous man dwelling among them tormented his righteous soul day after day with lawless works.

9 The Lord knows how to deliver the devout from temptation, and to keep the unrighteous being punished for the day of judgment.

10 especially those who go after the flesh in the desire of uncleanness and despise dominion; daredevils, self-willed, they are not afraid to blaspheme glories.

11 Angels, who are greater in strength and power, do not bear a blasphemous judgment against them from the Lord.

12 These people, like brute living creatures, natural ones having happened for capture and corruption, blaspheming in things they do not know, will be corrupted in their own corruption.

13 They receive the reward of unrighteousness, considering pleasure in the day to be delicate living; they are spots and blemishes, sporting themselves in their deceits while feasting with you.

14 Having eyes full of an adulteress and that cannot cease from sin, alluring unstable souls, having a heart trained in greed, children of a curse.

15 Leaving the straight way, they were deceived, having followed the way of Balaam of Beor, who loved the reward of unrighteousness.

16 But he had a rebuke for his own iniquity: a mute ass, uttering with a man's voice, forbade the madness of the prophet.

17 These are waterless springs, clouds driven by a storm, for whom the darkness of gloom has been kept forever.

18 uttering great swelling words of vanity, they allure through desires of the flesh and licentiousness those who had indeed escaped from those who conduct themselves in error.

19 While promising them freedom, they themselves exist as servants of corruption; for by whatever someone is overcome, to this he has also been enslaved.

20 For if, having escaped the pollutions of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, and then again being entangled in them, they are overcome, the last things have become worse for them than the first.

21 For it would be better for them not to have recognized the way of righteousness than, after recognizing it, to turn back from the holy commandment delivered to them.

22 But it has happened to them according to the true proverb: a dog, having turned back to its own vomit, and a sow that was washed, to wallowing in the mire.


## Chapter 3

1 Beloved, I am now writing to you a second letter, in which I arouse your pure mind by means of remembrance.

2 to remember the words foretold by the holy prophets and the commandment of our Lord and Savior through our apostles.

3 knowing this first, that mockers will come in the last of the days, going according to their own desires.

4 and saying, "Where is the promise of his coming? For since the Fathers fell asleep, all things continue in this way from the beginning of creation."

5 This fact escapes them, since they willingly ignore that long ago heavens existed and earth was formed out of water and through water by the word of God.

6 by which the world at that time was overflowed with water and destroyed.

7 But the present heavens and the earth, by his word, are being stored up for fire, being kept until the day of judgment and destruction of ungodly men.

8 But do not let this one fact escape your notice, beloved, that one day with the Lord is as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day.

9 The Lord is not slack concerning his promise, as some count slackness, but is patient toward us, not willing that any should be destroyed, but that all should come to repentance.

10 But the day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night, in which the heavens will pass away with a great noise, and the rudiments, being fervent with heat, will be dissolved; and the earth and the works that are in it will be burned up.

11 Since all these things are being loosed, what manner of persons must you exist in holy conduct and godliness?

12 Expecting and hastening the coming of the day of God, on account of which the heavens, being burned, will be loosened and the rudiments, being intensely hot, will melt.

13 But according to his promise we expect new heavens and a new earth, in which righteousness dwells.

14 Therefore, beloved, since you are expecting these things, be diligent to be found by him in peace, spotless and blameless.

15 And consider the patience of our Lord as salvation, just as also our beloved brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.

16 as also in all his letters, speaking in them about these matters, in which there are some things hard to be understood, which the unlearned and unstable wrest, as they do also the remaining Scriptures, to their own destruction.

17 Therefore, beloved, since you foreknow these things, keep yourselves so that you are not carried away with the error of the wicked and fall away from your own stedfastness.

18 But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and to the day of eternity. Amen.


# 1 JOHN

## Chapter 1

1 That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have beheld and our hands have felt after, concerning the Word of the life.

2 And the life was revealed, and we have seen and testify and report to you the eternal life, which was with the Father and was revealed to us.

3 We proclaim to you what we have seen and heard, so that you also may have fellowship with us; and our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.

4 We write these things to you so that our joy may be fulfilled.

5 And this is the promise which we have heard from him and announce to you: that God is light and in him there is no darkness at all.

6 If we say that we have fellowship with Him and walk in the darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth.

7 But if we walk in the light as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ his Son cleanses us from all sin.

8 If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

9 If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

10 If we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.


## Chapter 2

1 My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. And if anyone sins, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous.

2 And he is the propitiation for our sins, and not for ours only, but also for the whole world.

3 And by this we know that we have known him, if we keep his commandments.

4 The one who says, "I know him," and does not keep his commandments is a liar, and the truth is not in him.

5 Whoever keeps his word, truly in him the love of God is perfected. In this we know that we are in him.

6 The one who says he remains in him ought to walk himself also just as he walked.

7 Brothers, I am not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you have had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you have heard from the beginning.

8 Again I write a new commandment to you, which is true in Him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already appearing.

9 The one who says he is in the light and hates his brother is in the darkness until now.

10 The one who loves his brother remains in the light, and there is no stumbling block in him.

11 The one who hates his brother is in the darkness and walks in the darkness, and he does not know where he is departing because the darkness has blinded his eyes.

12 I am writing to you, little children, because your sins have been forgiven you on account of his name.

13 I am writing to you, Fathers, because you have known Him who is from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one. I am writing to you, children, because you have known the Father.

14 I wrote to you, Fathers, because you know Him who is from the beginning. I wrote to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God remains in you, and you have overcome the evil one.

15 Do not love the world nor the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

16 For all that is in the world—the desire of the flesh and the desire of the eyes and the boasting of living—is not from the Father, but is from the world.

17 And the world is passing away, and its desire, but the one who does the will of God remains forever.

18 Children, it is the last hour, and just as you heard that the antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have arisen; from this we know that it is the last hour.

19 They went out from us, but they were not from us; for if they had been from us, they would have remained with us, but this happened so that it might be revealed that they are not all from us.

20 and you have anointing from the Holy One, and you know all things.

21 I did not write to you because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it, and because every lie is not of the truth.

22 Who is the liar but the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This one is the antichrist, the one who denies the Father and the Son.

23 All who deny the Son do not have the Father.

24 Therefore, let what you heard from the beginning remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son and in the Father.

25 And this is the promise which He promised to us: eternal life.

26 I have written these things to you concerning those who are deceiving you.

27 And you, the anointing that you took from him remains in you, and you have no need for anyone to teach you; but as his same anointing teaches you about all things, and is true and is not a lie, and just as it taught you, you remain in him.

28 And now, little children, remain in him, so that when he is revealed we may have boldness and not be ashamed before him at his coming.

29 If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who does righteousness has been born of him.


## Chapter 3

1 See what manner of love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God; for this reason the world does not know us, because it did not know Him.

2 Beloved, now we are children of God, and it has not yet been revealed what we will be, but we know that when he is revealed we will be like him, because we will see him just as he is.

3 And all who have this hope in him purify themselves, just as he is pure.

4 All who do sin also do lawlessness, and sin is lawlessness.

5 And you know that he was revealed in order that he might take away our sins, and sin is not in him.

6 All who remain in him do not sin; all who sin have not seen him nor known him.

7 Little children, let no one deceive you; the one who does righteousness is righteous, just as he is righteous.

8 The one who does sin is of the devil, because from the beginning the devil sins. For this purpose the Son of God was revealed, that he might loose the works of the devil.

9 All who are born from God do not practice sin, because his seed remains in him, and he is not able to sin because he has been born from God.

10 In this the children of God are manifest and the children of the devil: all who do not do righteousness are not from God, and those who do not love their brother.

11 For this is the message which you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.

12 Not as Cain, who was of the evil one and slew his brother; and why did he slay him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother's righteous.

13 Do not marvel, my brothers, if the world hates you.

14 We know that we have departed from death into life because we love the brothers; the one not loving the brother remains in death.

15 All who hate their brother are murderers, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in them.

16 In this we have known love, because God laid down his soul for us, and we ought to lay down our souls for the brothers.

17 Whoever has the world's living and beholds his brother having need and shuts his affection from him, how does the love of God remain in him?

18 My little children, let us not love with word nor with tongue, but with work and truth.

19 And in this we know that we are of the truth, and will persuade our hearts before him.

20 For if our heart blames us, God is greater than our heart and knows all things.

21 Beloved, if our heart does not blame us, we have boldness toward God.

22 And whatever we ask we take from him, because we keep his commandments and do the things that are pleasing before him.

23 And this is his commandment: that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he gave us a commandment.

24 And the one who keeps his commandments remains in him, and he in him; and by this we know that he remains in us, from the Spirit which he gave us.


## Chapter 4

1 Beloved, do not believe every Spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.

2 By this you know the Spirit of God: all the spirit that confesses Jesus Christ having come in the flesh is from God.

3 And every spirit that does not confess Jesus Christ as having come in the flesh is not from God; and this is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and now is already in the world.

4 You are from God, little children, and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.

5 They are of the world; therefore they speak from the world, and the world hears them.

6 We are from God. The one who knows God hears us; whoever is not from God does not hear us. By this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.

7 Beloved, let us love one another, because love is from God, and all who love have been born of God and know God.

8 The one who does not love does not know God, because God is love.

9 In this the love of God was revealed in us, that God has sent his only begotten Son into the world so that we might live through him.

10 In this is love, not that we have loved God, but that He loved us and sent His Son as a propitiation for our sins.

11 Beloved, if God loved us in this way, we also ought to love one another.

12 No one has ever beheld God; if we love one another, God remains in us and his love is perfected in us.

13 By this we know that we remain in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.

14 And we have beheld and testify that the Father has sent the Son as Savior of the world.

15 Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God remains in him, and he in God.

16 We have come to know and believe the love that God has in us. God is love, and the one who remains in love remains in God, and God remains in him.

17 In this is love perfected with us, so that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because just as he is, so also are we in this world.

18 Fear is not in love, but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment, and the one who fears has not been perfected in love.

19 We love him because he first loved us.

20 If anyone says, "I love God," and hates his brother, he is a liar; for the one who does not love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen?

21 And this is the commandment we have from him, that the one who loves God also loves his brother.


## Chapter 5

1 All who believe that Jesus is the Christ have been born of God, and all who love the one who fathered also love the one who has been born of him.

2 In this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and keep his commandments.

3 For this is the love of God, that we keep his commandments; and his commandments are not heavy.

4 For everyone born of God overcomes the world, and this is the victory that has overcome the world: our faith.

5 Who is the one who overcomes the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?

6 This is the one who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ; not by the water only but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.

7 For there are three who testify in heaven: the Father, the Word, and the Holy Spirit; and these three are one.

8 And three are those who testify on the earth: the Spirit, the water, and the blood; and these three are in agreement.

9 If we take the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater, because this is the testimony of God which he has testified concerning his Son.

10 The one who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself; the one who does not believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has testified about his Son.

11 And this is the testimony: that God has given us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.

12 The one having the Son has the life; the one not having the Son of God does not have the life.

13 These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, so that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may believe in the name of the Son of God.

14 And this is the boldness that we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.

15 And if we know that He hears us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the petitions that we have asked from Him.

16 If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not leading to death, he will ask and he will give him life—for those who sin not leading to death. There is a sin leading to death; I do not say that he should ask concerning that.

17 All unrighteousness is sin, and there is sin that does not lead to death.

18 We know that all who are born of God do not sin, but the one born of God keeps himself, and the evil one does not touch him.

19 We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the evil one.

20 We know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we know the true one, and we are in the true one, in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.

21 Little children, keep yourselves from idols. Amen.


# 2 JOHN

## Chapter 1

1 The elder to the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in truth, and not only I but also all who know the truth.

2 Because of the truth that remains in us and will be with us forever.

3 May grace, mercy, and peace be with us from God the Father and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

4 I rejoiced greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, just as we received a commandment from the Father.

5 Now I am asking you, lady—not as though I am writing a new commandment to you, but one we had from the beginning: that we should love one another.

6 And this is the love: that we walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, just as you heard from the beginning, that you walk in it.

7 For many deceivers have entered into the world, who do not confess Jesus Christ coming in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.

8 Look to yourselves, so that we may not destroy what we have worked for, but may receive a full reward.

9 All who transgress and do not remain in the teaching of Christ do not have God; the one who remains in the teaching of Christ has both the Father and the Son.

10 If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not take him into your house and do not greet him.

11 The one who says to him, "Rejoice," shares in his evil works.

12 Having much to write to you, I did not wish to do so with paper and ink, but I hope to come to you and speak mouth to mouth, so that our joy may be fulfilled.

13 The children of your chosen sister greet you. Amen.


# 3 JOHN

## Chapter 1

1 The elder to Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.

2 Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be healthy, just as your soul prospers.

3 I rejoiced greatly when brothers came and testified to your truth, just as you walk in truth.

4 I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are walking in truth.

5 Beloved, you do a faithful thing in whatever work you do for the brothers and for the strangers.

6 They testified of your love before the church. You will do well to escort them worthily of God.

7 For they went out for the sake of the name, taking nothing from the Gentiles.

8 We therefore ought to receive such people, so that we may become fellow workers with the truth.

9 I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to have the preeminence among them, does not receive us.

10 Therefore, if I come, I will remind him of the works which he does, prating against us with evil words, and not being content with these, he himself neither receives the brothers, and he forbids those who are willing and casts them out of the church.

11 Beloved, do not imitate evil but good. The one who does good is from God; the one who does evil has not seen God.

12 Demetrius has been testified to by all and by the truth itself; and we also testify, and you know that our testimony is true.

13 I had much to write, but I do not want to write to you with ink and reed.

14 I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face to face. Peace be with you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.


# JUDE

## Chapter 1

1 Judas, a servant of Jesus Christ and a brother of James, to those who are sanctified in God the Father and kept in Jesus Christ, called.

2 Mercy, peace, and love be multiplied to you.

3 Beloved, while doing all diligence to write to you concerning our common salvation, I had a necessity to write to you, encouraging you to earnestly contend for the faith once delivered to the holy ones.
